eng: The Pharisees and Sadducees came to Jesus. In order to test him they asked him to show them a sign from heaven.
local: Wee daale nɛ, Farasiitena na Sadukiitena kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ kaa teese e ɔ nɔɔman nyi waa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia wola wɔ, na kawola nyi ɔ lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ampaŋ.

eng: But he replied, At evening you say, It will be nice weather because the sky is bright red.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, De lii balinɔɔ te ŋɔma yasɛɛ na ɛ ne tale naa yako nyi, tɛɛ de naa na weese waa gyɔ.

eng: And in the morning you say, There will be bad weather today because the sky is cloudy. You know how to make sense of the skys appearance. But you are unable to recognize the signs that point to what the time is.
local: Te nebo mɔ na, ɛ ne tale kɛɛ ŋɔma dinɔɔ yako nyi gyɛŋ ɔga waa ŋɛɛ. Ɛ ne tale kɛɛ ŋɔma na ɛ kawola kpene keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ. Mɔna nombia ŋan ne yɛɛ debaŋ kei man nɛ, ɛ bɛɛ tale wola ŋe man ya.

eng: An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign. But it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign. Then he left them and went away.
local: Ɛmɛɛ gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ, ɛ yɛɛ balaŋ kumɛɛwɔ te ɛ bee yee Wurubuarɛ ya. Ɛ ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia ɛ kena, mɔna mɔɔ gyae maa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia kamasɛ wola ŋon kpu na ŋan be kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Gyona dɔɔ nɛ ya. Keŋte Yesu leewɔ be gyaŋ laŋ tina wɔ yela.

eng: When the disciples arrived on the other side of the lake, they had forgotten to bring bread.
local: Yesu na ɔ kaseela ka toŋ gyu boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ wolee bodobodo ka mo bo.

eng: Jesus said to them, Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Keŋte Yesu faree wɔ nyiaa, ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose man nideli lee Farasiitena na Sadukiitena wose man. Nawolo nyi be dinɔɔ yɛɛ ŋgba tee keŋ bɛɛ mo do bodobodo man te kɛɛ koro nɛ.

eng: They discussed this among themselves and said, We didnt bring any bread.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Keŋ de ta mo bodobodo kɔŋ dɔɔ ya te ɔ ne yako mena nombia kɛŋa yaa.

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, You people of weak faith! Why are you discussing among yourselves the fact that you dont have any bread?.
local: Na Yesu ta gyeŋ kpene keŋ bee kolosi na dɔŋa nɛ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kelɛɛdi ta boo ya. Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne kolosi nyi ɛ ta mo bodobodo tela ya?.

eng: Dont you understand yet? Dont you remember the five loaves that fed the five thousand and how many baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ sɛɛ ɛ te nyii ta me nombia man yee? Ɛ bɛɛ tɔɔse bodobodose anoŋ ŋan mɔɔ kɛrɛɛ fa balaŋ kakpoŋse banoŋ, na ŋan bɔɔ dii kaase te ɛ be tɔɔsee wulu doŋase nɛ yee?.

eng: And the seven loaves that fed the four thousand and how many large baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Yaa ɛ bɛɛ tɔɔse bodobodose nyetooro ŋan balaŋ kakpoŋse banaara be dii, na ŋan bɔɔ dii kaase te ɛ be tɔɔsee wulu doŋase nɛ yee?.

eng: Dont you know that I wasnt talking about bread? But be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ta gyem nyi na bodobodo wose man nombia yaa mee kolosi fa ŋon ya? Mɔna ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose man nideli lee Farasiitena na Sadukiitena wose man. Be dinɔɔ yɛɛ ŋgba tee keŋ bɛɛ mo do bodobodo man te kɛɛ koro nɛ.

eng: Then they understood that he wasnt telling them to be on their guard for yeast used in making bread. No, he was telling them to watch out for the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ gyeŋawɔ nyi, na tee keŋ ne yeli bodobodo na koro nɛ yaa ɔ ne yako wɔ nyi baa kɛɛ ba wose man nideli na ke ya. Mɔna Farasiitena na Sadukiitena nombia ŋan bɛɛ wolo nɛ yaa ɔ ne yako wɔ.

eng: Now when Jesus came to the area of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, Who do people say the Human One is.
local: Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ ka gyoo Kaesaria Filipi tɛɛle man nɛ, ɔ bɔɔse ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Balaŋ ne kolosi ma wose man nombia na be kpa maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon yɛna amɔ.

eng: They replied, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others Jeremiah or one of the other prophets.
local: Keŋte ɔ kaseela baŋ tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Balaŋ baale kpa nyaŋ yɛna Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon. Baale mɔ kpa nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Elia, te baale mɔ kpa nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Gyeremia, yaa Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ man ŋolo.

eng: He said, And what about you? Who do you say that I am.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ mɔ de? Ɛ kpa maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon yɛna amɔ.

eng: Simon Peter said, You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.
local: Botɔɔ te Simɔn Pita tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna Kristo, nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ. Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ Bu.

eng: Then Jesus replied, Happy are you, Simon son of Jonah, because no human has shown this to you. Rather my Father who is in heaven has shown you.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, Gyona bu Simɔn, nyaŋ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi na deniwalaŋ lese na nombia kɛŋa wola neŋ ya, mɔna me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ lese na ŋa wola neŋ.

eng: I tell you that you are Peter. And Ill build my church on this rock. The gates of the underworld wont be able to stand against it.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako neŋ nyiaa, nyaŋ yɛna Pita, te n yele kenaŋ asɛɛ yɛna nyi boe. Nyaŋ boe kei dɔɔ te maa baake me balaŋ pou yilaa. Te yeŋ gba koraŋ be gyae ke tale di be dɔɔ ya.

eng: Ill give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven. Anything you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. Anything you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: Maa mo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ safowaase do n nyiŋmaa man. Kpene kamasɛ keŋ nyaŋ ke gyɔ ke dowoe tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, baa gyɔ ke dowoe adido, te kpene kamasɛ keŋ nyaŋ ke booli ke tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, baa booli ke adido mɔ.

eng: Then he ordered the disciples not to tell anybody that he was the Christ.
local: Keŋte Yesu faree ɔ kaseela baŋ nyi, be na yako walaŋ kamasɛ nyi ŋon yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ ya.

eng: From that time Jesus began to show his disciples that he had to go to Jerusalem and suffer many things from the elders, chief priests, and legal experts, and that he had to be killed and raised on the third day.
local: Lee debaŋ kenaŋ pou nɛ, Yesu fiasɛɛ ɔ na lese nombia ŋan asɛɛ fa ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Kaboena nyi maa gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man, na me kaa naa diyem nideli Gyudatena kegyiise na Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ nyiŋmaa man. Baa ko maŋ, mɔna weeya atoorote keŋ man na, Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu maŋ lee yeŋ man.

eng: Then Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him: God forbid, Lord! This wont happen to you.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita moo Yesu lee yenaŋ te ɔ tɔɔ e nyi, Me Gbeŋgyoo, na yako mena ya. Wurubuarɛ dɛɛ mo kɛŋa gyu lem. Kɛŋa na kɔŋ n dɔɔ korakora ya.

eng: But he turned to Peter and said, Get behind me, Satan. You are a stone that could make me stumble, for you are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ Pita te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Ɔbɔnsam, ta lee me gyaŋ! N ne gyae n ke tɔ maŋ gbɛɛ. Nawolo nyi na Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii te n na gyueŋ ya, mɔna denibalaŋ kegyaebii te n na gyueŋ.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, All who want to come after me must sayno to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Akpaa ŋolo ne gyae waa sila maŋ na, ɔ na yɛɛ kpene keŋ ŋon gbagba ne gyae ya, mɔna waa mo ɔ ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ seele, na waa sila maŋ wee kamasɛ.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae waa lɛɛ ŋon gbagba nyeedoŋ na, ke gyae ka lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man. Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ nyeedoŋ da lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man lee maŋ Yesu na me nombia dɔɔ nɛ, waa bese nyiŋ ke.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives? What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Akpaa ŋolo de nyiŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ pou te ɔ nyeedoŋ da lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man na, woŋ tɔnɔɔ te waa nyiŋ? Yaa woŋ te walaŋ waa tale mo kyɛɛkee na ɔ nyeedoŋ?.

eng: For the Human One is about to come with the majesty of his Father with his angels. And then he will repay each one for what that person has done.
local: Maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon na me kpilala dɛɛ lee adido kɔŋ me kya Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri man, na me kaa tɔ walaŋ kamasɛ kom lee ɔ tom keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ man.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see the Human One coming in his kingdom.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, baale seŋɛɛ ɛ man kɛbo keŋ bɔɔ gyae baa yeŋ ya, kelii debaŋ keŋ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa lese ma wose wola me gyoorobiiri man.

eng: He called his twelve disciples and gave them authority over unclean spirits to throw them out and to heal every disease and every sickness.
local: Yesu baake ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ, te ɔ faa wɔ doŋ keŋ baa mo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ lee balaŋ man, na baa kyɔ kaweese kamasɛ.

eng: Here are the names of the twelve apostles: first, Simon, who is called Peter; and Andrew his brother; James the son of Zebedee; and John his brother.
local: Kpilala kufu bala baŋ yela yaa. Gyaŋgbate ŋon yɛna Simɔn, ŋon ɔ yele daale mɔ yɛna nyi Pita nɛ, na ɔ naabu Anderiase, na Sebedeo bu Gyemisi na ɔ naabu Gyɔn.

eng: Philip; and Bartholomew; Thomas; and Matthew the tax collector; James the son of Alphaeus; and Thaddaeus.
local: na Filipo na Batolomeo na Tomase na Mateo ŋon nafɔ ɔ yɛɛ lempoolɛɛre e nɛ, na Alfeose bu Gyemisi na Tadeose.

eng: Simon the Cananaean; and Judas, who betrayed Jesus.
local: na Simɔn ŋon ɔ ne gyae ɔ balaŋ nideli nɛ, na Gyudase Isekarɔte ŋon waa kaa lese Yesu fa nɛ.

eng: Jesus sent these twelve out and commanded them, Dont go among the Gentiles or into a Samaritan city.
local: Mena kpilala kufu bala kɛwɔ te Yesu kpilawɔ te ɔ faree wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ na gyu balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ gyaŋ ya, te ɛ na gyu Samariatena mɔ donɔɔ kamasɛ man ya.

eng: Go instead to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Mɔna ɛ taŋgbɛɛ gyu Iseraetena baŋ gyaŋ. Be yɛɛ ŋgba namense baŋ bɔɔ yoo nɛ.

eng: As you go, make this announcement: The kingdom of heaven has come near.
local: Ɛ gyu na ɛ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia fa wɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ ta benaa.

eng: Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse those with skin diseases, and throw out demons. You received without having to pay. Therefore, give without demanding payment.
local: Ɛ kyɔ kaweesetena na ɛ ke gyuusu balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ yekee nɛ, na ɛ ke kyɔ baŋ bɛɛ wee kawee yayam nɛ, na ɛ ke gegi feliŋkumɛɛ lee balaŋ man. Ba moo mena doŋ kei fa ŋon bo yakaa keŋ ɛ te tɔ kom ya, mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ mo fa wɔ yakaa keŋ ɛ na lɛɛ wɔ kolo ya.

eng: Workers deserve to be fed, so dont gather gold or silver or copper coins for your money belts to take on your trips.
local: Ɛ na mo kɔba na kɔba tela ya.

eng: Dont take a backpack for the road or two shirts or sandals or a walking stick.
local: Ɛ na mo akpo ya, te ɛ na mo kegba alate yaa nkyokota alate yaa gbutu alate tela ya. Nawolo nyi kaboena nyi baa tɔ tomyɛɛre kamasɛ kom.

eng: Whatever city or village you go into, find somebody in it who is worthy and stay there until you go on your way.
local: Donɔɔ yaa akuraa kamasɛ keŋ man ɛ gyoo nɛ, ɛ taŋgbɛɛ bɔɔse walaŋ ŋon ɔ kaboena te waa lɛɛ ŋon nɛ dɔɔ, na ɛ ke kyaa ɔ gyaŋ kaboena e ka mo lee donɔɔ kenaŋ man.

eng: When you go into a house, say, Peace.
local: Ɛ gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ kenaŋ man nɛ, ɛ do dekpaŋalaŋmantena baŋ nɔɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ wosefɛɛreŋ dɛɛ kyaa ɛ man.

eng: If the house is worthy, give it your blessing of peace. But if the house isnt worthy, take back your blessing.
local: Nyi akpaa dekpaŋalaŋmantena benaŋ de lɛɛ ŋon nideli na, ɛ sola Wurubuarɛ wosefɛɛreŋ fa wɔ. Te akpaa ba te lɛɛ ŋon nideli ya na, ɛ yela wosefɛɛreŋ keŋ ka bese kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ.

eng: If anyone refuses to welcome you or listen to your words, shake the dust off your feet as you leave that house or city.
local: Mɔna donɔɔ yaa akuraa kamasɛ keŋ ke man balaŋ de bɛɛ ba te lɛɛ ŋon ya, yaa be te nyii ɛ nombia ŋan ya nɛ, nyi akpaa ɛ na lee botɔɔ na ɛ kpiisi ɛ nawɔɔ aŋasa lɔ wɔ, na kawola nyi be ta fom Wurubuarɛ.

eng: I assure you that it will be more bearable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah on Judgment Day than it will be for that city.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa di balaŋ nombia nɛ, waa naase mena balaŋ benaŋ diyem kela Sodom na Gomoratena diyem keŋ bɔɔ naa kaalaŋ nɛ.

eng: Look, Im sending you as sheep among wolves. Therefore, be wise as snakes and innocent as doves.
local: Ɛ kɛɛ, mee kpila ŋon baa ŋgba namense nɛ, nyi yaa gyu gbougbouse man. Mena dɔɔ ɛ yela ɛ sia ke kara ŋgba doma nɛ, na ɛ ke yɔkɔse ɛ wose ŋgba awuluŋna nɛ.

eng: Watch out for people—because they will hand you over to councils and they will beat you in their synagogues.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose man nideli lee balaŋ wose man. Nawolo nyi baa mo ŋon gyu kegyiise siaman na baa di ɛ nombia, na baa tuku ŋon bɔ ɔsom denɛɛ man.

eng: They will haul you in front of governors and even kings because of me so that you may give your testimony to them and to the Gentiles.
local: Lee maŋ dɔɔ, baa mo ŋon gyu tɛɛle kegyiise na gyooneŋ siaman. Kei gyae ke yela ɛ ke nyiŋ gbɛɛ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan fa wɔ, na baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ.

eng: Whenever they hand you over, dont worry about how to speak or what you will say, because what you can say will be given to you at that moment.
local: Mɔna nyi akpaa be kyaŋ ŋon ka seŋ be siaman nɛ, ɛ na wɔlɛɛ na kpene keŋ ɛ ke yako, na mena keŋ ɛ ke yako ke ya. Debaŋ kenaŋ de lii nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa wola ŋon kpene keŋ ɛ ke yako.

eng: You arent doing the talking, but the Spirit of my Father is doing the talking through you.
local: Nawolo nyi na ɛmɛɛ ne gyae la ɛ ke kolosi ya. Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne gyae la waa ta ɛ dɔɔ kolosi.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to be executed. A father will turn his child in. Children will defy their parents and have them executed.
local: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ nɛ, naa lolo bia baa lese dɔŋa fa yeŋ. Kya waa lese ɔ bu fa yeŋ, te bia mɔ baa koro seŋ ba lolala nyeeya dɔɔ na baa lese wɔ fa yeŋ.

eng: Everyone will hate you on account of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Lee maŋ dɔɔ balaŋ pou baa kɔla ŋon. Mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ kakyeŋ seŋ keŋkeŋ ɔ kelɛɛdi man kelii ɔto nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ ɔ nyee.

eng: Whenever they harass you in one city, escape to the next, because I assure that you will not go through all the cities of Israel before the Human One comes.
local: Nyi akpaa be naase ŋon diyem donɔɔ daale man na, ɛ yeu gyu donɔɔ wɔle man. Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke yɛɛ tom Iserae donɔɔse ŋan man pou taŋ, pɛte maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa bese kɔŋ ya.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, and slaves arent greater than their master.
local: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kasee abɔɔ nɛ ɔ ba kela ŋon ɔ na wola e nɛ ya, te gbeŋi mɔ ba kela ɔ gbeŋgyoo ya.

eng: Its enough for disciples to be like their teacher and slaves like their master. If they have called the head of the house Beelzebul, its certain that they will call the members of his household by even worse names.
local: Kaboena nyi kaseele waa yɛɛ ŋgba ɔ wolale nɛ, na gbeŋi mɔ ke yɛɛ ŋgba ɔ gbeŋgyoo nɛ. Te akpaa bɛɛ baake dekpaŋalaŋte ŋon nyi ŋon yɛna feliŋkumɛɛ gyoo Bɛlisɛbu na, woŋ dɔɔ te bɔɔ mo yela kumɛɛ gyakaa ɔ dekpaŋalaŋmantena baŋ dɔɔ ya?.

eng: Therefore, dont be afraid of those people because nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na yee wɔ ya. Nawolo nyi kolo be kyaa keŋ bɔɔ wuu ke dɔɔ keŋ nyi bɔɔ gyae baa gyuusu ke ya, te nombia aweeseŋ ŋaale mɔ be kyaa keŋ be gyae ka lee debɔɔ ya.

eng: What I say to you in the darkness, tell in the light; and what you hear whispered, announce from the rooftops.
local: Nombii kamasɛ keŋ me yako ŋon nelim na ɛ yako ke weeseteŋteŋ, te nombii kamasɛ keŋ me kolosi ke adadawoya man do ɛ deŋela man na, ɛ mo ke gyɔ dekpeŋkpeŋ.

eng: Dont be afraid of those who kill the body but cant kill the soul. Instead, be afraid of the one who can destroy both body and soul in hell.
local: Ɛ na yee balaŋ baŋ baa tale ko deniwalaŋ kaageŋ bo, mɔna bɔɔ tale yɛɛ kolo kpu bela nɛ ya. Mɔna ɛ yee Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ko walaŋ taŋ na, ɔ dana doŋ nyi waa fuŋ e na ɔ woo pou ka do boalaŋ keŋ bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man.

eng: Arent two sparrows sold for a small coin? But not one of them will fall to the ground without your Father knowing about it already.
local: Akpelee koya yɛɛ aŋmoŋ? Na kaperɛ te bɛɛ yɔɔ akpeleese bala yee? Mɔna akpaa Wurubuarɛ te fa gbɛɛ ya na, be man dokoloŋ gba be gyae waa yala yeŋ ya.

eng: Even the hairs of your head are all counted.
local: Ɛ nyeenyɔɔse koraŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ gyeŋ ŋe yaanɔɔle.

eng: Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na yee ya, nawolo nyi ɛ dana tɔnɔɔ kela akpeleese burum.

eng: Therefore, everyone who acknowledges me before people, I also will acknowledge before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ man do denibalaŋ siaman nyi me yɛɛ ɔ Gbeŋgyoo e nɛ, maŋ mɔ maa lɛɛ e do me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ siaman.

eng: But everyone who denies me before people, I also will deny before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ te lɛɛ maŋ do denibalaŋ siaman nyi me yɛɛ ɔ Gbeŋgyoo e ya nɛ, maŋ mɔ maa bɛɛ e me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ siaman.

eng: Dont think that Ive come to bring peace to the earth. I havent come to bring peace but a sword.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, ɛ na gyueŋ nyi wosefɛɛreŋ yaa ma moowɔ kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ ya. Koa, na wosefɛɛreŋ ya, mɔna yoo te ma moowɔ kɔŋ, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nɛ.

eng: Ive come to turn a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law .
local: Bia baala baa koro seŋ tia be kyaɔ, na bia alebia mɔ ka koro seŋ tia be naɔ, na bia ala mɔ ka koro seŋ tia ba awuna.

eng: Peoples enemies are members of their own households .
local: Walaŋ dekpaŋalaŋ man balaŋ gbagba ne gyae la baa kɔla e.

eng: Those who love father or mother more than me arent worthy of me. Those who love son or daughter more than me arent worthy of me.
local: Akpaa ŋolo ne kɔŋ me gyaŋ ɔ kaa sila maŋ te ɔ ne gyae ɔ kya yaa ɔ naa kela maŋ na, ɔ tekaboena nyi waa yɛɛ me kaseele ya. Te walaŋ ŋon mɔ ɔ ne gyae ɔ bu tobaale yaa ɔ bu alebu kela maŋ na, ɔ tekaboena nyi waa yɛɛ me kaseele ya.

eng: Those who dont pick up their crosses and follow me arent worthy of me.
local: Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ta mo ɔ ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ ka seŋɛɛ fa kaale na yeŋ nɛ na waa sila maŋ ya na, ɔ tekaboena nyi waa yɛɛ me kaseele ya.

eng: Those who find their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ mo ŋon gbagba doŋ ɔ ne kɛo ɔ nyeedoŋ nɛ, ke gyae ka lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man. Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ mo ɔ wose fa yeŋ lee maŋ dɔɔ nɛ, waa bese nyiŋ ɔ nyeedoŋ keŋ.

eng: Those who receive you are also receiving me, and those who receive me are receiving the one who sent me.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ ŋon na maŋ yaa ɔ lɛɛwɔ, te ŋon ɔ lɛɛ maŋ na Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ yaa ɔ lɛɛwɔ.

eng: Those who receive a prophet as a prophet will receive a prophets reward. Those who receive a righteous person as a righteous person will receive a righteous persons reward.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre, nyi keŋ ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e dɔɔ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre kom te Wurubuarɛ waa mo tɔ walaŋ ŋonaŋ. Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nyi keŋ ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman dɔɔ nɛ, walaŋ ŋon ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ kom te Wurubuarɛ waa mo tɔ walaŋ ŋonaŋ.

eng: I assure you that everybody who gives even a cup of cold water to these little ones because they are my disciples will certainly be rewarded.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ fa balaŋ kɛwɔ man ŋolo loŋ defɛɛsa leenɔɔ tɔɔ nyi keŋ ɔ yɛɛ me kaseele e dɔɔ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa tɔ e kom.

eng: A record of the ancestors of Jesus Christ, son of David, son of Abraham.
local: Gyoo Defidi na Aberaham be naanabii Yesu Kristo duuluŋ man balaŋ yaa.

eng: Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.
local: Aberaham lolana Asiki te Asiki mɔ lola Gyekɔpo te Gyekɔpo mɔ lola Gyuda na ɔ naabia.

eng: Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah, whose mother was Tamar. Perez was the father of Hezron. Hezron was the father of Aram.
local: Gyuda lolana Peresi na Sera baŋ be naa yɛna Tama nɛ, te Peresi mɔ lola Heserɔn, te Heserɔn mɔ lola Ram.

eng: Aram was the father of Amminadab. Amminadab was the father of Nahshon. Nahshon was the father of Salmon.
local: Ram lolana Aminadabo te Aminadabo mɔ lola Nasɔn te Nasɔn mɔ lola Salimɔn.

eng: Salmon was the father of Boaz, whose mother was Rahab. Boaz was the father of Obed, whose mother was Ruth. Obed was the father of Jesse.
local: Salimɔn na ɔ wɛɛle Rahabo lolana Boase te Boase na ɔ wɛɛle Ruute mɔ lolana Obedi te Obedi mɔ lolana Gyese.

eng: Jesse was the father of David the king. David was the father of Solomon, whose mother had been the wife of Uriah.
local: Gyese mɔ lolana gyoo Defidi te gyoo Defidi mɔ lolana Solomɔn. Solomɔn ɔ naa yɛna Yuria lialo ŋon gyoo Defidi ba mo yala nɛ.

eng: Solomon was the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam was the father of Abijah. Abijah was the father of Asaph.
local: Solomɔn mɔ lolana Rehoboam te Rehoboam mɔ lolana Abigya te Abigya mɔ lolana Asa.

eng: Asaph was the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat was the father of Joram. Joram was the father of Uzziah.
local: Asa lolana Gyehosafate te Gyehosafate mɔ lolana Gyehoram te Gyehoram mɔ lolana Yusia.

eng: Uzziah was the father of Jotham. Jotham was the father of Ahaz. Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah.
local: Yusia lolana Gyotam te Gyotam mɔ lolana Ahase te Ahase mɔ lolana Hesekia.

eng: Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh. Manasseh was the father of Amos. Amos was the father of Josiah.
local: Hesekia lolana Manase te Manase mɔ lola Amɔn te Amɔn mɔ lola Gyosia.

eng: Josiah was the father of Jechoniah and his brothers. This was at the time of the exile to Babylon.
local: Gyosia lolana Gyekonia na ɔ naabia debaŋ keŋ bɔɔ kyaŋ Iseraetena yoo man mo gyu Babilɔn tɛɛle man nɛ.

eng: After the exile to Babylon: Jechoniah was the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the father of Zerubbabel.
local: Ba ke kyaŋ Iseraetena gyu Babilɔn tɛɛle man wɔle nɛ te Gyekonia lola Selateli te Selateli mɔ lola Serubabeli.

eng: Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud. Abiud was the father of Eliakim. Eliakim was the father of Azor.
local: Serubabeli lolana Abiudi te Abiudi mɔ lola Eliakim te Eliakim mɔ lola Asɔ.

eng: Azor was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Achim. Achim was the father of Eliud.
local: Asɔ lolana Sadoki te Sadoki mɔ lola Akim te Akim mɔ lola Eliudi.

eng: Eliud was the father of Eleazar. Eleazar was the father of Matthan. Matthan was the father of Jacob.
local: Eliudi mɔ lolana Eliasa te Eliasa mɔ lola Matan te Matan mɔ lola Gyekɔpo.

eng: Jacob was the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary—of whom Jesus was born, who is called the Christ.
local: Gyekɔpo mɔ lolana Gyosɛfo. Te Gyosɛfo yala Mɛɛre ŋon ɔ ba lola Yesu ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Kristo nɛ. Ŋon yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: So there were fourteen generations from Abraham to David, fourteen generations from David to the exile to Babylon, and fourteen generations from the exile to Babylon to the Christ.
local: Mena dɔɔ lee Aberaham dɔɔ kelii gyoo Defidi dɔɔ yɛɛ kalola kegyakaare kufu anaara. Te lee gyoo Defidi dɔɔ kelii debaŋ keŋ bɔɔ kyaŋ Iseraetena gyu Babilɔn nɛ yɛɛ kalola kegyakaare kufu anaara. Te lee debaŋ keŋ bɔɔ kyaa Babilɔn kelii debaŋ keŋ bɔɔ lola Kristo mɔ nɛ yɛɛ kalola kegyakaare kufu anaara.

eng: This is how the birth of Jesus Christ took place. When Mary his mother was engaged to Joseph, before they were married, she became pregnant by the Holy Spirit.
local: Yesu Kristo kalola wose man nombia yaa. Gyosɛfo na Yesu ɔ naa Mɛɛre yakowɔ see nyi baa yala dɔŋa. Mɔna Gyosɛfo ta kpeo ta e ya, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ yela Mɛɛre kyaŋa fulu.

eng: Joseph her husband was a righteous man. Because he didnt want to humiliate her, he decided to call off their engagement quietly.
local: Na Gyosɛfo yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman, te ɔ bɛɛ gyae nyi waa do Mɛɛre desɛŋ balaŋ man dɔɔ ya nɛ, ɔ yɛɛ wa agyueŋ nyi waa yo bɛɛ e keŋ ŋolo na ŋolo ba gyem ya.

eng: As he was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, Joseph son of David, dont be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because the child she carries was conceived by the Holy Spirit.
local: Debaŋ keŋ Gyosɛfo na mo mena agyueŋ kei nɛ, te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo kaa lese ɔ wose wola e dosɛɛ man, te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Defidi ɔ naanabii Gyosɛfo, na yee Mɛɛre ke yala ya. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ feliŋ yele la te ɔ kyaŋa fulu kei.

eng: She will give birth to a son, and you will call him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.
local: Waa lola bu baale na n ka do e yele nyi Yesu. Nawolo nyi ŋon ne gyae la waa lese ɔ balaŋ lee be nombiakumɛɛ man.

eng: Now all of this took place so that what the Lord had spoken through the prophet would be fulfilled.
local: Kɛŋa pou kɔŋawɔ mena, na kpene keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya dɔɔ yako see nɛ ke kɔŋ mena ampaŋ. Nombia ŋenaŋ yɛna nyiaa.

eng: Look! A virgin will become pregnant and give birth to a son, And they will call him, Emmanuel.
local: Alebu ŋon ɔ te doo ta na baale see ya nɛ waa kyaŋ fulu na waa lola bu baale na baa baake e nyi Imanuɛle. Keŋ nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ kyaa de man.

eng: When Joseph woke up, he did just as an angel from God commanded and took Mary as his wife.
local: Mena dɔɔ Gyosɛfo kefoŋ nɛ, ɔ moo Mɛɛre yala ŋgba mena keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon be yako e nɛ.

eng: But he didnt have sexual relations with her until she gave birth to a son. Joseph called him Jesus.
local: Mɔna ŋon na Mɛɛre te doo ŋgba alo na baale nɛ ya kaboena ɔ lola bu baale. Te Gyosɛfo doo e yele nyi Yesu.

eng: Then the Spirit led Jesus up into the wilderness so that the devil might tempt him.
local: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon yela Yesu gyuu depampaa dinaa daale man. Botɔɔ te ɔbɔnsam kɛɛse e kɛɛ.

eng: After Jesus had fasted for forty days and forty nights, he was starving.
local: Yesu ke bake ɔ nɔɔ weeya sɔŋola nelim na ka weese wɔle nɛ, tanam kyaŋa e nideli.

eng: The tempter came to him and said, Since you are Gods Son, command these stones to become bread.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔbɔnsam kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa, Akpaa nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon na, yela boya kɛŋa ka bese weenɛɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread, but by every word spoken by God .
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako daa nyiaa, Na weenɛɛ ŋaageŋ ne faa na walaŋ nyeedoŋ ya, mɔna Wurubuarɛ nɔɔwoya kamasɛ mɔ ne fa walaŋ nyeedoŋ. .

eng: After that the devil brought him into the holy city and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔbɔnsam moo Yesu gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man, te ɔ moo e gyu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ nyeeman adido adido.

eng: Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down; for it is written, I will command my angels concerning you, and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Akpaa nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon na, fuŋ n wose lee kɛbo ke lɔ tɛɛle man, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako daa nyiaa, Nyaŋ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ waa kpila ɔ kpilala, na be kaa gyee neŋ nideli, na baa kɔlee neŋ keŋ nyi n be gyae n ka mo n nae gyɔ na boe koraŋ gba ya. .

eng: Jesus replied, Again its written, Dont test the Lord your God .
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see na bese yako daa nyiaa, Na kɛɛse n Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ŋon kɛɛ ya. .

eng: Now when Jesus had come down from the mountain, large crowds followed him.
local: Yesu ke tisi lee bula keŋ dɔɔ kɔŋ nɛ, balaŋ burum kaa sila e.

eng: Then the devil brought him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔbɔnsam besewɔ mo Yesu gyu bula dinaa daale nyee dɔɔ, te ɔ wola e tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyoorobiiri na ka kyeo pou.

eng: He said, Ill give you all these if you bow down and worship me.
local: Keŋte ɔ yako Yesu nyiaa, Akpaa n buŋ me nawɔɔ tɛɛ som maŋ na, maa mo abɔɔ kɛŋa pou fa neŋ.

eng: A man with a skin disease came, kneeled before him, and said, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: Keŋte akuŋtuŋte ŋolo kaa buŋawɔ ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ waase e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, akpaa n ne gyae na nyaŋ ke tale kyɔ maŋ me kawee.

eng: Jesus responded, Go away, Satan, because its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him .
local: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, Ɔbɔnsam ta lee me siaman, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako daa nyiaa, de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ waageŋ te kaboena nyi dee buŋ e na dɛɛ som e.

eng: Jesus reached out his hand and touched him, saying, I do want to. Become clean. Instantly his skin disease was cleansed.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tenee ɔ nyiŋmaa tina e te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Mɛɛ gyae, n kawee dɛɛ taŋ. Debokenaŋ man te balee ŋon kawee taŋawɔ.

eng: The devil left him, and angels came and took care of him.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔbɔnsam leewɔ Yesu gyaŋ laŋ, te Wurubuarɛ kpilala kɔŋawɔ kaa kɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the gift that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Keŋte Yesu tɔɔ e kiiŋ nyi, Na yele walaŋ na walaŋ ke nyii ya, mɔna mo n wose gyu kawola Wurubuarɛ sae ŋon, na n ke deesi fa Wurubuarɛ ŋgba mena keŋ Mosesi ba wola ŋon nɛ, na ke di adansɛɛ fa walaŋ kamasɛ nyi n kawee te taŋ.

eng: Now when Jesus heard that John was arrested, he went to Galilee.
local: Yesu ke nyii nyi ba te kyaŋ Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon tɔ deni nɛ, te ɔ besewɔ gyu Galelia tɛɛle man.

eng: When Jesus went to Capernaum, a centurion approached.
local: Yesu ke gyu ka gyoo Kapanum donɔɔ man nɛ, Romantena yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋolo kɔŋawɔ kaa waase nyiaa.

eng: He left Nazareth and settled in Capernaum, which lies alongside the sea in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali.
local: Yesu ka lee Nasarɛte donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ ke kyaa Kapanum donɔɔ man. Donɔɔ kei kyaa Galelia depaa nɔɔman, te ka benaa na Sebulɔn na Nafitali tɛɛlese.

eng: pleading with him, Lord, my servant is flat on his back at home, paralyzed, and his suffering is awful.
local: Me Gbeŋgyoo lɛɛ maŋ, nawolo nyi me tobaale te tekerii ɔ doo deni man ɔ ne naa diyem nideli.

eng: Be careful that you dont practice your religion in front of people to draw their attention. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father who is in heaven.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa Ɛ kɛɛ nideli nyi ɛ be yɛɛ deeli balaŋ man, na baa nyiŋ naa ŋon ya. Akpaa ɛ yɛɛ mena na, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke nyiŋ kom ketɔ kamasɛ lee ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ gyaŋ ya.

eng: This fulfilled what Isaiah the prophet said.
local: Kei yela kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya be yako see lee tɛɛle kenaŋ wose man nɛ kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Yesaya yakowɔ see nyiaa.

eng: Jesus responded, Ill come and heal him.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, Maa kɔŋ na me kaa kyɔ e ɔ kawee.

eng: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in the territory of Judea during the rule of King Herod, magi came from the east to Jerusalem.
local: Ba lola Yesu bo Bɛtelɛhɛm donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ man. Debaŋ kenaŋ na gyoo Herode ne diina gyoori. Be ka lola Yesu nɛ, te di weeya ala ya te siakaralatena baale baŋ ba gyeŋ kyolobiise wose man nombia nɛ lee weese dɛɛlee yenaŋ kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man.

eng: Whenever you give to the poor, dont blow your trumpet as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets so that they may get praise from people. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: Mena dɔɔ akpaa ɛ yɛɛ deeli fa diyemte ŋolo na, ɛ na mo ke gyɔ dekpeŋkpeŋ ŋgba mena keŋ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ beo ba wose nɛ ne yɛɛ Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ na abɔnterese man, na balaŋ kenyiŋ kɛlɛɛ wɔ nɛ ya. Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, be taŋ nyiŋ ba kom ketɔ pou.

eng: Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali, alongside the sea, across the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles.
local: Ɛmɛɛ balaŋ baŋ ɛ kyaa Sebulɔn na Nafitali tɛɛlese ŋan ŋe kyaa benaa na Galelia depaa, botɔɔ keŋ balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ boe nɛ, na Gyɔɔdan boo diŋgyiŋ nɛ, ɛ tei kɛbo.

eng: But the centurion replied, Lord, I dont deserve to have you come under my roof. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: Botɔɔ te yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon yako e nyiaa, Me gbeŋgyoo, na haa n wose ya. Nawolo nyi ma tekaboe nyi nya kɔŋ me dɛɛ ya. Mena dɔɔ nyaŋ betaa gyuusu n nɔɔ faree kawee keŋ, me tobaale ŋon waa koro.

eng: At that time Herod the ruler heard the news about Jesus.
local: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ man te gyoo Herode ŋon ɔ ne kɛɛ Galelia tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ, nyii Yesu wose man nombia.

eng: They asked, Where is the newborn king of the Jews? Weve seen his star in the east, and weve come to honor him.
local: kaa bɔɔse balaŋ nyiaa, Aleŋ te ba lola bu ŋon waa kaa bese Gyudatena gyoo nɛ? Da te naa kyolobii ŋolo weese dɛɛlee yenaŋ keŋ na wola daa nyi be ta lola e, mena dɔɔ te de kɔŋawɔ de kaa som e.

eng: Dont judge, so that you wont be judged.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ na yɛɛ ɛ wose balaŋ nombiadiira na ɛ ke bu wɔ fɔɔ ya, na Wurubuarɛ mɔ be di ɛ nombia na waa bu ŋon fɔɔ ya.

eng: But when you give to the poor, dont let your left hand know what your right hand is doing.
local: Mɔna akpaa n yɛɛ deeli fa diyemte ŋolo na, na yele n dɔɔ kpaakpaa gba koraŋ ke nyii ya.

eng: the people who lived in the dark have seen a great light, and a light has come upon those who lived in the region and in shadow of death.
local: Balaŋ baŋ be doo ditiŋtɛɛ man nɛ te naa keŋmaŋee dinaa daale. Balaŋ baŋ mɔ be kyaa botɔɔ keŋ yeŋ ne di gyoori nɛ, keŋmaŋee te kɔŋ fa wɔ.

eng: Im a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Nawolo nyi maŋ gbagba mɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon me doo baale tɛɛ, te yoonɔɔtena mɔ doo me tɛɛ. Me yako ŋolo nyi waa gyu yenaŋ daale na ɔ ne gyu, te me yako ŋolo nyi waa kɔŋ na ɔ ne kɔŋ. Kpene kamasɛ keŋ me yako be man ŋolo nyi waa yɛɛ na, keŋ te ɔ ne yɛɛ.

eng: He said to his servants, This is John the Baptist. Hes been raised from the dead. This is why these miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: Keŋte ɔ yako ɔ tewulɛɛ nyiaa, gyae ke yɛɛ Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon bese la foŋ lee yeŋ man kɔŋ nɛ. Mena dɔɔ te ɔ ne tale yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia nɛ.

eng: When Jesus finished teaching his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in their cities.
local: Yesu ke kolosi nombia kɛŋa fa ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ taŋ nɛ, ɔ leewɔ botɔɔ gyu donɔɔse akaŋ ŋan kyaa Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ ke kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia fa wɔ, te ɔ wola wɔ ŋe man nideli.

eng: When King Herod heard this, he was troubled, and everyone in Jerusalem was troubled with him.
local: Gyoo Herode na baŋ pou be kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, takaa wɔ nideli.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he went up a mountain. He sat down and his disciples came to him.
local: Yesu kena balaŋ burum baŋ nɛ, ɔ gyeŋa bula daale gyu ke kyaa ke dɔɔ. Keŋte ɔ kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ kaa kyaa kilisi e.

eng: Youll receive the same judgment you give. Whatever you deal out will be dealt out to you.
local: Nawolo nyi gbɛɛ keŋ man ɛ ka mo di ɛ tebia nombia na, mena mɔ te Wurubuarɛ waa mo di ɛ nombia. Nombii keŋ ɛ ka mo yɛɛ ɛ tebia nɛ, keŋ ke te Wurubuarɛ mɔ waa mo yɛɛ ŋon.

eng: so that you may give to the poor in secret. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: Yela kedoo walaŋ ŋon n be yɛɛ deeli keŋ fa e nɛ, ɛmɛɛ na e ɛ bala nsana. Dekɔŋ mena na, n kya Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ne naa kpene keŋ n be yɛɛ weese nɛ, waa tɔ neŋ kom.

eng: From that time Jesus began to announce, Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: Lee debaŋ kenaŋ te Yesu fiasɛɛ ɔ ne kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia nyiaa, Ɛ kyɛɛkee lee ɛ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na ɛ ke kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ ta benaa.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he was impressed and said to the people following him, I say to you with all seriousness that even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Yesu ke nyii nɔɔwoya kɛŋa nɛ, doo ɔ nɔɔ te ɔ yako balaŋ dikpii keŋ be silana e nɛ nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, Iserae tɛɛle kei dɔɔ pou nɛ ma te naa ta walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di ŋgba balee kei nɛ ya.

eng: Herod had arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip.
local: Nawolo nyi gyoo Herode gbagba yele la be kyaŋa Gyɔn te be bake e, te ba moo e ke tɔ deni lee ɔ kegyia Filipo wɛɛle Herodia dɔɔ.

eng: At that time Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry so they were picking heads of wheat and eating them.
local: Te di weeya ala ya nɛ, Yesu na ɔ kaseela gyuuwɔ ka mo wɔɔ daale man. Na weenɛɛ ŋan seŋɛɛ wɔɔ kenaŋ man nɛ yɛɛ ŋgba kefaa nɛ. Tanam kyaŋa ɔ kaseela baŋ te ba kpera ŋaale posaa bɛɛ tao. Na wee kenaŋ yɛɛ Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee ke.

eng: Now when John heard in prison about the things the Christ was doing, he sent word by his disciples to Jesus, asking.
local: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ na ba te kyaŋ Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre tɔ deni. Ɔ ke nyii kpene keŋ Yesu ne yɛɛ nɛ, ɔ kpila ɔ kaseela nyi.

eng: He gathered all the chief priests and the legal experts and asked them where the Christ was to be born.
local: Mena dɔɔ gyoo Herode baake Gyudatena saese kegyiise na ba mmaraa wolala pou yilaa te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Aleŋ te baa lola nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: He taught them, saying.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ gyuusu ɔ nɔɔ ɔ na wola wɔ nyiaa.

eng: Why do you see the splinter thats in your brothers or sisters eye, but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: Weera dɔɔ te n ne naa woŋsilibii keŋ doo n dɔɔ sibii man, te n be famenɛ na daakpee keŋ doo nyaŋ gbagba n sibii man ya?.

eng: When you pray, dont be like hypocrites. They love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners so that people will see them. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyi, Akpaa ɛ ne fane nɛ, ɛ na yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ beo ba wose ne yɛɛ nɛ ya. Nawolo nyi bɛɛ gyae Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man, na abɔnterese man keta seŋee fane na balaŋ kenyiŋ naa wɔ. Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi be taŋ tɔ wɔ ba kom pou.

eng: As Jesus walked alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, because they were fishermen.
local: Wee daale Yesu keta Galelia boo nɔɔ nɔɔ nɛ, ɔ naa Simɔn ŋon ɔ yele daale bela yɛna Pita nɛ, na ɔ naabu Anderiase na bɛɛ lɔ asawu boo keŋ man, nawolo nyi be yɛɛ kpɛɛ kyaŋarawɔ.

eng: I say to you that there are many who will come from east and west and sit down to eat with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, balaŋ burum baa lee weese dɛɛlee na weese dɛɛyalae kɔŋ, na baŋ na Aberaham na Asiki na Gyekɔpo kaa kyaa Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man na baa di bom.

eng: Thats because John told Herod, Its against the law for you to marry her.
local: Kpene keŋ dɔɔ gyoo Herode be yɛɛ mena yɛna nyi, Gyɔn yako e nyi gbɛɛ be doo nyi waa mo ɔ kegyia Filipo wɛɛle Herodia yala ya.

eng: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to him, Look, your disciples are breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Farasiitena baŋ kena mena nɛ be yako Yesu nyiaa, Kɛɛ! N kaseela ne yɛɛ kpene keŋ da mmaraa bɛɛ fa gbɛɛ nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ da kefɛɛfowee ya.

eng: Are you the one who is to come, or should we look for another.
local: baa gyu ke bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi nyaŋ ke kɔŋ nɛ yaa, dɛɛ kɛɛ walaŋ wɔle gbɛɛ.

eng: They said, In Bethlehem of Judea, for this is what the prophet wrote.
local: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Bɛtelɛhɛm donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋolo yakowɔ see lee Bɛtelɛhɛm wose man nyiaa.

eng: Happy are people who are hopeless, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ba mo ɛ wose pou lɔ Wurubuarɛ dɔɔ nɛ ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi ɛmɛɛ te Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ɛ wui.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Let me take the splinter out of your eye, when theres a log in your eye?.
local: Sena te n ke tale yako n dɔɔ nyi, Me dɔɔ yela maa lese woŋsilibii lee n sibii man, keŋ nyaŋ gbagba be famenɛ na daakpee keŋ doo n sibii man ya.

eng: But when you pray, go to your room, shut the door, and pray to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: Mɔna akpaa ɛ ne gyae ɛ ke fane na, ɛ gyu ɛ denɛɛ man na ɛ ketɔ disim na ɛ ke fane fa ɛ kya ŋon ɛ bɛɛ naa e na ɛ sia fonɛɛ ya nɛ. Akpaa ɛ yɛɛ mena na, ɛ kya ŋon ɔ ne naa kpene keŋ ɛ be yɛɛ weese nɛ, waa tɔ ŋon kom.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kaa sila maŋ, na maa yela ɛ ke yilaa balaŋ fa maŋ.

eng: But the children of the kingdom will be thrown outside into the darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Te ɛmɛɛ Gyudatena baŋ nafɔ kaboena nyi yaa kyaa Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man nɛ, baa lese ŋon debɔɔ ka see ditiŋtɛɛ keŋ man. Botɔɔ te ɛ ke kyaŋ ɛ nyeeya wii nideli.

eng: Although Herod wanted to kill him, he feared the crowd because they thought John was a prophet.
local: Gyoo Herode gyaewɔ nyi nafɔ waa ko Gyɔn mɔna ɔ ne yee Gyudatena baŋ, nawolo nyi bɛɛ lɛɛ Gyɔn di nyi ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e.

eng: But he said to them, Havent you read what David did when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu mɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ te kala ta lee Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man kpene keŋ gyoo Defidi be yɛɛ debaŋ keŋ tanam be kyaŋ ŋon na ɔ balaŋ baŋ nɛ yee?.

eng: Jesus responded, Go, report to John what you hear and see.
local: Be ke gyu ke bɔɔse Yesu nɛ te ɔ tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ bese gyu ke yako Gyɔn kpene keŋ ɛ be nyii na ɛ deŋela, na ŋan ɛ be naa na ɛ sia.

eng: You, Bethlehem, land of Judah, by no means are you least among the rulers of Judah, because from you will come one who governs, who will shepherd my people Israel.
local: Wurubuarɛ kpa, Ɛmɛɛ Bɛtelɛhɛmtena baŋ ɛ kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ ɛ yɛɛ gyoorobiawɔ. Nawolo nyi ɛ man te gyoo dinaa ŋon waa kɛɛ me balaŋ Iseraetena dɔɔ nɛ waa lee.

eng: Happy are people who grieve, because they will be made glad.
local: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ne di walaŋsa nɛ ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ waa kyɔlɛɛ ŋon.

eng: You deceive yourself! First take the log out of your eye, and then youll see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Nyaŋ walaŋ ŋon n na beo n wose, kaboena nyi nya taŋgbɛɛ lese daakpee keŋ doo n sibii man nɛ, pɛna n kena nideli na n ka lese woŋsilibii keŋ doo n dɔɔ sibii man nɛ.

eng: When you pray, dont pour out a flood of empty words, as the Gentiles do. They think that by saying many words theyll be heard.
local: Akpaa ɛ ne fane na, ɛ na kolosi nɔɔwoya burum ŋan ŋɔɔ taŋ tɔnɔɔ, ŋgba mena keŋ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ ya ne yɛɛ nɛ ya. Baŋ gyeŋ nyi akpaa be kolosi nɔɔwoya burum na, te Wurubuarɛ waa nyii wɔ.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: Debokenaŋ man te be tinaa ba asawuse ŋan yela te be kaa sila Yesu.

eng: Jesus said to the centurion, Go; it will be done for you just as you have believed. And his servant was healed that very moment.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon nyiaa Ta gyu dɛɛ, mena keŋ n be lɛɛ maŋ di nɛ dɔɔ gyae ke kɔŋ mena fa neŋ. Debokenaŋ man te ɔ tobaale ŋon kawee taŋawɔ.

eng: But at Herods birthday party Herodias daughter danced in front of the guests and thrilled Herod.
local: Wee daale gyoo Herode kee di ɔ kalolawee nɛ, Herodia bu alebu kɔŋawɔ kaa sɔe balaŋ baŋ pou siaman, te yɛɛ gyoo Herode kyeo.

eng: He went into Gods house and broke the law by eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat.
local: Ɔ gyoowɔ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man te ɔ ka moo weenɛɛ ŋan bɔɔ mo deesi fa Wurubuarɛ, keŋ nafɔ tekaboena nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ te yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ sae e ya waa di ya nɛ, te ɔ diiwɔ te ɔ moo ŋaale mɔ fa ɔ balaŋ baŋ be silana e nɛ.

eng: Those who were blind are able to see . Those who were crippled are walking. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. The poor have good news proclaimed to them .
local: Siayɛlɛɛsatena ne naa, ɔtakasese ne tɛɛ, baŋ bɛɛ wee kawee yayam nɛ kaweese te taŋ, te deŋela atɔɔsatena ne nyii. Woya ne foŋii, te bɛɛ yako Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan mɔ fa ayematena.

eng: Then Herod secretly called for the magi and found out from them the time when the star had first appeared.
local: Mena dɔɔ gyoo Herode moo Siakaralatena baŋ lee yenaŋ, te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ na waa gyeŋ debaŋ kitii keŋ kyolobii ŋon ba lee.

eng: Happy are people who are humble, because they will inherit the earth.
local: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ne yɔkɔse ɛ wose nɛ ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi ɛmɛɛ te tɛɛle kei gyae kaa bese ɛ wui.

eng: Dont give holy things to dogs, and dont throw your pearls in front of pigs. They will stomp on the pearls, then turn around and attack you.
local: Ɛ na mo kpene keŋ ke yɛɛ kolo kpaakpaa ke Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ fa babaase ya, na mena ya na baa besena bo kɔŋ ɛ dɔɔ na baa doŋee ŋon. Ɛ na mo ɛ kegyaŋase kpaakpaa nyiŋan mɔ fuŋ lɔ kekuiriise ya, na mena ya na baa sakatee ŋa bo.

eng: Dont be like them, because your Father knows what you need before you ask.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na yɛɛ ŋgba be kɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ ya, nawolo nyi ɛ kya Wurubuarɛ wulaa ɔ gyeŋ kpene keŋ ne hia ŋon koraŋ gba pɛte ɛ ne bɔɔse e.

eng: Continuing on, he saw another set of brothers, James the son of Zebedee and his brother John. They were in a boat with Zebedee their father repairing their nets. Jesus called them and.
local: Ɔ ke gyu ɔ siaman kyomii nɛ, ɔ naa Sebedeo bia Gyemisi na ɔ naabu Gyɔn na baŋ na be kya Sebedeo doo degbele man bee desina ba asawuse te Yesu baake wɔ.

eng: Jesus went home with Peter and saw Peters mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu korowɔ gyu Pita dɛɛ. Ɔ ke gyu nɛ, na Pita wa awu doo ɔ na wee kpaŋalaŋ.

eng: Then he swore to give her anything she asked.
local: Mena dɔɔ gyoo Herode kaŋa ntam yako alebu ŋon nyiaa, Kpene kamasɛ keŋ n gyae nyi maa fa neŋ na, yako na maa fa neŋ.

eng: Or havent you read in the Law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple treat the Sabbath as any other day and are still innocent?.
local: Yaa ɛ ta bese ta kala lee Wurubuarɛ mmaraa tɔne keŋ man nyi, saese baŋ be kyaa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ, deesiwɔ fa Wurubuarɛ kefɛɛfowee keŋ mɔna ŋolo te bu wɔ fɔɔ nyi ba te walaa ke yee?.

eng: Happy are those who dont stumble and fall because of me.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ aŋmaareŋ nyi maŋ yɛna ŋon Wurubuarɛ be kpili ya nɛ, ŋon na nyeebam.

eng: He sent them to Bethlehem, saying, Go and search carefully for the child. When youve found him, report to me so that I too may go and honor him.
local: Be ke tiranɔɔ fa e taŋ nɛ, ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ gyu na ɛ ke kɛo bu ŋon nideli. Akpaa ɛ naa e na ɛ bese kaa yako maŋ, na maŋ mɔ ke gyu ka som e. Keŋte ɔ lɔɔ wɔ gbɛɛ te be laŋa Bɛtelɛhɛm donɔɔ man.

eng: Happy are people who are hungry and thirsty for righteousness, because they will be fed until they are full.
local: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ loo gyakaa nideli nyi ɛ ke yɛɛ kpene keŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ waa fa ŋon abɔɔ ŋan ɛ ne gyae lee ɛ konɔɔ man nɛ.

eng: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened to you.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ sola Wurubuarɛ kolo na ɛ nyiŋmaa gyae ke kaŋ ke. Ɛ kɛo kolo lee ɔ gyaŋ na ɛ ke nyiŋ ke, te ɛ gyɔ e disim na waa toro fa ŋon.

eng: Pray like this: Our Father who is in heaven, uphold the holiness of your name.
local: Ɛ ne gyae ɛ ke fane na, ɛ yako nyiaa, De kya ŋon n kyaa adido, dɛɛ fane nyi balaŋ pou baa lese n yele.

eng: immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
local: debokenaŋ man te be tinaa degbele keŋ na be kya yela, te be sila Yesu laŋ.

eng: He touched her hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served them.
local: Keŋte Yesu kyaŋa e ɔ nyiŋmaa man te kpaŋalaŋ keŋ taŋawɔ. Keŋte ɔ korowɔ kɛɛ be dɔɔ.

eng: At her mothers urging, the girl said, Give me the head of John the Baptist here on a plate.
local: Keŋte alebu ŋon ɔ naa feefuwɔ do ɔ bu nyi waa yako nyiaa, Mɛɛ gyae n ke kara Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre nyee do tasa taŋtaŋalaŋ man fa maŋ debokeiman.

eng: But I tell you that something greater than the temple is here.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, walaŋ ŋon ɔ kela Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ nɛ kyaa kɛbo.

eng: When Johns disciples had gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What did you go out to the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Gyɔn kpilala baŋ ka bese bee gyu nɛ, Yesu kolosi Gyɔn wose man nombia fa balaŋ dikpii keŋ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ ke gyu Gyɔn gyaŋ depampaa dinaa keŋ man nɛ, woŋ te ɛ gyaewɔ nyi ɛ kena? Ŋale keŋ feliŋ ne gyɔ ke wosi nɛ?.

eng: When they heard the king, they went; and look, the star they had seen in the east went ahead of them until it stood over the place where the child was.
local: Be ke nyii gyoo ŋon nɔɔwoya taŋ nɛ, ba korowɔ mo be gbɛɛ bee gyu. Te kyolobii ŋon bɔɔ naa weese dɛɛlee yenaŋ nɛ, tɛɛwɔ be siaman menɛɛ te ke ka seŋawɔ botɔɔ keŋ bu ŋon doo nɛ adido.

eng: Happy are people who show mercy, because they will receive mercy.
local: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ne wii balaŋ waraŋase nɛ ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ waa wii ɛmɛɛ mɔ waraŋase.

eng: For everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. And to everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ bɔɔse e kolo na ɔ nyiŋmaa ne kaŋ ke. Ŋon ɔ gyae kolo lee ɔ gyaŋ na ɔ ne nyiŋ ke, te ŋon mɔ ɔ gyɔ e disim na waa toro fa e.

eng: Bring in your kingdom so that your will is done on earth as its done in heaven.
local: Lese n gyoori debɔɔ, yela baa yɛɛ n kegyaebii tɛɛle dɔɔ, ŋgba mena keŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ adido nɛ.

eng: Jesus traveled throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues. He announced the good news of the kingdom and healed every disease and sickness among the people.
local: Keŋte Yesu kila Galelia tɛɛle keŋ pou, na ɔ na wola wɔ Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man. Ɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia kpaakpaa lee Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man, te ɔ kyɔɔ kaweese kpookpoo balaŋ wose man.

eng: That evening people brought to Jesus many who were demon-possessed. He threw the spirits out with just a word. He healed everyone who was sick.
local: Kelii balinɔɔ nɛ, ba moo balaŋ baŋ feliŋkumɛɛ doo be man nɛ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ. Ɔ moo nombii dokoloŋ kooŋ bo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan pou, te ɔ kyɔɔ kaweesetena baŋ mɔ pou.

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests he commanded that they give it to her.
local: Nombia ŋan takaa gyoo ŋon, mɔna ntam keŋ ɔ be kam na balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ yilaa botɔɔ dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ yela be yɛɛ alebu ŋon kegyaebii fa e.

eng: If you had known what this means, I want mercy and not sacrifice , you wouldnt have condemned the innocent.
local: Wurubuarɛ yakowɔ ɔ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see man nyiaa, Mɛɛ gyae bo nyi yaa wii balaŋ waraŋase, na kedeesa te mɛɛ gyae ya. Nyi yeŋ nyii nombia kɛŋa man nɛ, nafɔ ɛ be gyae ɛ ke bu balaŋ baŋ be nombia dei nɛ fɔɔ ya.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who wear refined clothes are in royal palaces.
local: Akpaa na keŋ ya na, woŋ te ɛ gyuuwɔ ɛ kaa kɛɛ? Walaŋ ŋon ɔ doona abɔɔ kpaakpaa nyiŋan yaa? Baŋ bee mia mena nɛ, ne kyaa bo gyooneŋ dɛɛ.

eng: When they saw the star, they were filled with joy.
local: Ba kena kyolobii ŋon mena nɛ, yɛɛ wɔ gyoŋ nideli.

eng: Happy are people who have pure hearts, because they will see God.
local: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ dukum kamasɛ be doo ɛ konɔɔse man ya nɛ ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi ɛmɛɛ ne gyae la ɛ kena Wurubuarɛ.

eng: Who among you will give your children a stone when they ask for bread?.
local: Ɛ man woŋti kyaa la keŋ nyi ɔ bu da sola e weenɛɛ na waa mo boe fa e?.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Fa daa gyɛŋ wee kei weenɛɛ.

eng: News about him spread throughout Syria. People brought to him all those who had various kinds of diseases, those in pain, those possessed by demons, those with epilepsy, and those who were paralyzed, and he healed them.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu yele leewɔ Siria tɛɛle keŋ dɔɔ pou. Keŋte balaŋ moo kaweesetena kpookpoo, ŋgba baŋ bɛɛ wee keleuleuse na ɔtakasese na baŋ ba yenaase ba yekee nɛ kɔna e, te ɔ kyɔɔ wɔ te ɔ gberaa feliŋkumɛɛ lee baale mɔ man.

eng: This happened so that what Isaiah the prophet said would be fulfilled: He is the one who took our illnesses and carried away our diseases.
local: Ɔ ke yɛɛ kei yela nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya be yako see nɛ kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Yesaya yakowɔ nyiaa, Ŋon lɛɛ na de kaweese pou, te ɔ moo de kaale mɔ seele.

eng: Then he had John beheaded in prison.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔ yela be gyuuwɔ ke kara Gyɔn nyee deni keŋ man.

eng: The Human One is Lord of the Sabbath.
local: Nawolo nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon yɛna kefɛɛfowee keŋ gbeŋgyoo.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Ɛ yako maŋ maa nyii, woŋ te nafɔ ɛ gyuuwɔ nyi ɛ kaa kɛɛ? Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre? Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi, ŋon ɛ be naa nɛ gba kela Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre.

eng: They entered the house and saw the child with Mary his mother. Falling to their knees, they honored him. Then they opened their treasure chests and presented him with gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
local: Be ke gyu ka gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man nɛ, be naa bu ŋon na ɔ naa Mɛɛre keŋte ba buŋawɔ som bu ŋon. Be gyuusu ba akpose te ba lese kɔba yayam, na oliifi na adudo kyɛɛ bu ŋon.

eng: Happy are people who make peace, because they will be called Gods children.
local: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ne yele wosefɛɛreŋ ne kɔŋ balaŋ nsana nɛ ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi ɛmɛɛ te baa baake nyi Wurubuarɛ bia.

eng: Or give them a snake when they ask for fish?.
local: Yaa ɛ man woŋti kyaa la keŋ nyi ɔ bu da sola e kpebii na waa mo dom fa e?.

eng: Forgive us for the ways we have wronged you, just as we also forgive those who have wronged us.
local: Mo de nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ daa, ŋgba mena keŋ daa mɔ na mo kyɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ tia daa nɛ.

eng: Large crowds followed him from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and from the areas beyond the Jordan River.
local: Mena dɔɔ balaŋ burum lee Galelia, na Dekapolise tɛɛle man na Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man na Gyudia tɛɛle man, na Gyɔɔdan diŋgyiŋ kaa sila e.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowd, he ordered his disciples to go over to the other side of the lake.
local: Yesu kena nyi balaŋ burum te kilisi e nɛ, ɔ yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyi baa toŋ gyu boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ.

eng: They brought his head on a plate and gave it to the young woman, and she brought it to her mother.
local: Keŋte ba moo Gyɔn nyee keŋ do tasa taŋtaŋalaŋ man kɔna alebu ŋon, te ɔ moo ke gyina ɔ naa.

eng: Jesus left that place and went into their synagogue.
local: Yesu ka lee botɔɔ nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ Gyudatena ɔsom deni man.

eng: He is the one of whom it is written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: Nawolo nyi Gyɔn kei yɛna ŋon bɔɔ yako ɔ wose man nombia Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see man nɛ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa, mee kpila me kpilale ke ta n siaman, ŋon ne gyae la waa desina n gbɛɛ fa neŋ.

eng: Because they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they went back to their own country by another route.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, Wurubuarɛ faree wɔ dosɛɛ man nyi be na bese gyu ka mo gyoo Herode gyaŋ bela ya, mena dɔɔ ba moo gbɛɛ wɔle kpoo man gyu be dɛɛ.

eng: Happy are people whose lives are harassed because they are righteous, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ bɛɛ naase ŋon diyem nyi ɛ ne yɛɛ kpene keŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman dɔɔ nɛ, ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi ɛmɛɛ te Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ɛ wui.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give good things to those who ask him.
local: Akpaa ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ koraŋ gyeŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa ka mo fa ɛ bia na, de yaa sena te ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ be gyae waa mo abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan kela kɛŋa nɛ fa baŋ bɛɛ sola e nɛ ya.

eng: And dont lead us into temptation, but rescue us from the evil one.
local: Na yele dɛɛ yala kekɛɛsekɛɛ man ya. Mɔna lese daa lee walaŋkum ŋon nyiŋmaa man. .

eng: A legal expert came and said to him, Teacher, Ill follow you wherever you go.
local: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ mmaraa wolale ŋolo kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, yenaŋ kamasɛ keŋ n ke gyu na, maa sila neŋ gyu.

eng: But Johns disciples came and took his body and buried it. Then they went and told Jesus what had happened.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle te Gyɔn kaseela gyuuwɔ ka mo Gyɔn woo ŋon ke wuu, te be gyuuwɔ ke yako Yesu.

eng: I assure you that no one who has ever been born is greater than John the Baptist. Yet whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ lolo tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, ŋolo be kyaa keŋ nyi ɔ kela Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man nɛ, walaŋ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ kemaasa e koraŋ nɛ, ɔ kela Gyɔn kei.

eng: A man with a withered hand was there. Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they asked, Does the Law allow a person to heal on the Sabbath.
local: Na balee ŋolo kyaa botɔɔ keŋ ɔ baam ba yem. Lee mena keŋ bɛɛ gyae gbɛɛ na baa mo nombiikum seele Yesu dɔɔ nɛ, be bɔɔse e nyi da mmaraa ne fa daa gbɛɛ nyi dɛɛ kyɔ walaŋ kawee da kefɛɛfowee?.

eng: When the magi had departed, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, Get up. Take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you, for Herod will soon search for the child in order to kill him.
local: Siakaralatena baŋ ke laŋ wɔle nɛ, de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo kaa lese ɔ wose wola Gyosɛfo dosɛɛ man te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Koro mo bu ŋon na ɔ naa na ɛ ke yeu gyu Igyipite tɛɛle man, na ɛ ke kyaa botɔɔ kelii debaŋ keŋ maa yako ŋon nyi yaa koro botɔɔ. Nawolo nyi gyoo Herode waa kɛo bu ŋon waa ko.

eng: Therefore, you should treat people in the same way that you want people to treat you; this is the Law and the Prophets.
local: Wurubuarɛ mmaraase na Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra nombia ŋan pou bɔɔ wolo nɛ ŋa nyee yɛna nyi, kpene keŋ n ne gyae nyi balaŋ baa yɛɛ fa neŋ na, keŋ te kaboena nyi nyaŋ mɔ nya yɛɛ fa wɔ.

eng: Happy are you when people insult you and harass you and speak all kinds of bad and false things about you, all because of me.
local: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ balaŋ na tee ŋon te bɛɛ naase ŋon diyem te bɛɛ kɛrɛɛ ɛ nɔɔse yako nombiakumɛɛ gyakaa ɛ dɔɔ, nawolo nyi keŋ e silana maŋ dɔɔ nɛ, ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam.

eng: If you forgive others their sins, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
local: Nawolo nyi akpaa ɛ mo balaŋ nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ na, ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ mɔ waa mo ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ ŋon.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens, and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human One has no place to lay his head.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, Nyanyase dana bɔɔneŋ te gyebuse mɔ dana asaase, mɔna Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon be taŋ botɔɔ keŋ waa mo ɔ nyee teeli ya.

eng: When Jesus heard about John, he withdrew in a boat to a deserted place by himself. When the crowds learned this, they followed him on foot from the cities.
local: Yesu ke nyii Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre yeŋ nɛ, ɔ gyoowɔ debgele man laŋ yenaŋ daale gyu ke kyaa waageŋ kooŋ. Balaŋ dikpii keŋ ke nyii nyi ɔ te lam nɛ, te ba leewɔ be donɔɔse man, mo na be nawɔɔ sila e.

eng: From the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven is violently attacked as violent people seize it.
local: Leenaŋ Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre debaŋ kaa lii na gyɛŋ kei nɛ, balaŋ na mo kale na doŋ nombia bo gyaŋee na Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ nombia ŋan, te balaŋ baŋ mɔ bɛɛ gyae ke nɛ, na mo siadoŋ bo baa gyoo ke man.

eng: Jesus replied, Who among you has a sheep that falls into a pit on the Sabbath and will not take hold of it and pull it out?.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ man woŋti te akpaa ɔ dana nam dokoloŋ te ɔ yale gyoo bɔɔ man kefɛɛfowee na, waa bɛɛ nyi ɔ ba lese e ya?.

eng: Joseph got up and, during the night, took the child and his mother to Egypt.
local: Mena dɔɔ Gyosɛfo korowɔ nelim kenaŋ te ɔ moo bu ŋon na ɔ naa te be yeuwɔ gyu Igyipite tɛɛle man.

eng: Go in through the narrow gate. The gate that leads to destruction is broad and the road wide, so many people enter through it.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ na ɛ ka mo denanɔɔ bɔbɔɔkyee keŋ man gyoo Wurubuarɛ man. Nawolo nyi denanɔɔ keŋ ka keerɛɛ te ke gbɛɛnaa fɛlɛɛ te ke dɔɔ keta be taŋ baŋ ya nɛ, na mo balaŋ bo gyu kayeyɛɛ keŋ be taŋ ɔto ya nɛ man. Mɔna ke man te balaŋ burum dɔŋɛɛ.

eng: Be full of joy and be glad, because you have a great reward in heaven. In the same way, people harassed the prophets who came before you.
local: Ɛ yela ke yɛɛ ŋon gyoŋ nideli, nawolo nyi kom keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa tɔ ŋon adido nɛ ka boe. Mena keŋ bɛɛ naase ŋon diyem nɛ, mena ke te be naase Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ bɔɔ kyaa pɛte ɛ be kɔŋ nɛ diyem.

eng: But if you dont forgive others, neither will your Father forgive your sins.
local: Mɔna akpaa ɛ ta mo balaŋ nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ ya na, ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ mɔ be gyae waa mo ɛmɛɛ mɔ wui nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ ŋon ya.

eng: Another man, one of his disciples, said to him, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu silala baŋ man ŋolo mɔ yako e nyiaa, Me gbeŋgyoo, yela maa taŋgbɛɛ gyu kewuu me kya pɛna me kaa sila neŋ.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion for them and healed those who were sick.
local: Yesu ke tisi lee degbele keŋ man, te ɔ kena balaŋ dikpii keŋ nɛ, be nombia yɛɛ e waraŋase te ɔ kyɔɔ kaweesetena baŋ pou bɔɔ sila wɔ gyu nɛ.

eng: All the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John came.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra, na Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan pou kolosi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man nombia, pɛte Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre kɔŋawɔ.

eng: How much more valuable is a person than a sheep! So the Law allows a person to do what is good on the Sabbath.
local: Deniwalaŋ kela nam. Mena dɔɔ gbɛɛ doo nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ deeli kefɛɛfowee.

eng: He stayed there until Herod died. This fulfilled what the Lord had spoken through the prophet: I have called my son out of Egypt .
local: te be kyaawɔ botɔɔ kaboena gyoo Herode yeŋawɔ. Kɛŋa pou kɔŋawɔ mena na kpene keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo be tɛɛ ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre dɔɔ yako see nɛ ke kɔŋ mena ampaŋ. Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Igyipite tɛɛle man te ma lese me bu lee.

eng: But the gate that leads to life is narrow and the road difficult, so few people find it.
local: Te denanɔɔ bɔbɔɔkyee keŋ ke gbɛɛnaa yɛɛ peperee te ke dɔɔ keta dana baŋ nɛ, na moona balaŋ gyu nyeedoŋ keŋ be taŋ ɔto ya nɛ man. Mɔna balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ naa ke te bɛɛ moo ke man gyoo nɛ, be ta boo ya.

eng: You are the salt of the earth. But if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Its good for nothing except to be thrown away and trampled under peoples feet.
local: Ɛ yɛɛ ŋgba yabia nɛ fa balaŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ. Mɔna akpaa yabia gyoŋ keŋ da lee ke man na, n be gyae n ke tale yela ka ke yɛɛ gyoŋ bela ya. Dekɔŋ mena na yabia kenaŋ be dei fa kolo na kolo ke yɛɛ bela ya. Bee fuŋ ke bo leki, na balaŋ ke sakatee ke laŋ.

eng: And when you fast, dont put on a sad face like the hypocrites. They distort their faces so people will know they are fasting. I assure you that they have their reward.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyi, Akpaa ɛ ne bake ɛ nɔɔ nɛ, ɛ na nyaŋe ɛ sia ŋgba mena keŋ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ beo ba wose nɛ ne yɛɛ nɛ ya. Bɛɛ nyaŋe be sia na balaŋ ke nyiŋ naa nyi bɛɛ bake be nɔɔ. Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi be taŋ nyiŋ ba kom ketɔ.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, Sila maŋ, na n ke yela woya kewuu ba woya.

eng: That evening his disciples came and said to him, This is an isolated place and its getting late. Send the crowds away so they can go into the villages and buy food for themselves.
local: Ke lii balinɔɔ nɛ, Yesu kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, tɛɛ ne yuu te kɛbo mɔ yɛɛ ŋale nsana ke. Lɔ balaŋ baŋ gbɛɛ, na baa gyu akuraase ŋan benaa nɛ man na be kaa gyae kolo lɛɛ di.

eng: If you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah who is to come.
local: Akpaa ɛ ke sɛɛ lɛɛ mena nombia kɛŋa di na, ŋon Gyɔn yɛna Elia ŋon Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋolo be yako see nyi waa bese kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: Then Jesus said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and it was made healthy, just like the other one.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako balee ŋon nyiaa, Tenee n baam keŋ. Keŋte balee ŋon mɔ tenee ke, te ɔ baam keŋ yɛɛ doŋ ŋgba alate keŋ nɛ.

eng: When Herod knew the magi had fooled him, he grew very angry. He sent soldiers to kill all the children in Bethlehem and in all the surrounding territory who were two years old and younger, according to the time that he had learned from the magi.
local: Gyoo Herode kena nyi Siakaralatena baŋ ta mo adao di ɔ dɔɔ, keŋ be ta bese kɔŋ kaa mo ɔ gyaŋ bela ya nɛ ɔ gyeŋa baŋ nideli. Keŋte ɔ kpila ɔ yoonɔɔtena nyi baa gyu Bɛtelɛhɛm donɔɔ man, na ke donɔɔsebia ŋan benaa na ke nɛ, na baa koe bia baala baŋ pou be ta bee ta kela kulutooneŋ ala ya nɛ, nawolo nyi mena debaŋ kenaŋ man te Siakaralatena baŋ yako e nyi be naa kyolobii ŋon.

eng: Watch out for false prophets. They come to you dressed like sheep, but inside they are vicious wolves.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose man nideli lee akɛrɛɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ wose man. Akpaa be kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ na, bɛɛ yɔkɔse ba wose ŋgba namense nɛ mɔna be konɔɔse man na be yɛɛ ŋgba gbougbouse nɛ.

eng: You are the light of the world. A city on top of a hill cant be hidden.
local: Ɛ yɛɛ ŋgba keŋmaŋee nɛ fa balaŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ. Donɔɔ keŋ ke gyakaa bula dɔɔ nɛ bɛɛ weese ya.

eng: When you fast, brush your hair and wash your face.
local: Mɔna akpaa n bake n nɔɔ nɛ, faafo n sia na n ke tiu nuŋ na n ke fulaa n nyee.

eng: When Jesus got into a boat, his disciples followed him.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu gyoowɔ degbele man, te ɔ kaseela baŋ mɔ gyoowɔ kpu na e.

eng: But Jesus said to them, Theres no need to send them away. You give them something to eat.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Tekaboena nyi baa ta ya. Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ gyae kolo fa wɔ na baa di.

eng: Let the person who has ears, hear.
local: Nyaŋ ŋon n dana deŋela anenyiiŋ dei na, tei.

eng: The Pharisees went out and met in order to find a way to destroy Jesus.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ Farasiitena baŋ leewɔ gyu kebake, lee gbɛɛ keŋ man baa mo na baa ko Yesu.

eng: This fulfilled the word spoken through Jeremiah the prophet.
local: Kei yele la kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Gyeremia be yako see nɛ kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa.

eng: You will know them by their fruit. Do people get bunches of grapes from thorny weeds, or do they get figs from thistles?.
local: Be nombia ŋan bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ te ɛ ka mo gyeŋ wɔ. Ŋolo bɛɛ kɔ mango bia lee akutu daŋ man ya, mena mɔ te bɔɔ ne kɔ apaŋ lee abotoo woŋse man ya.

eng: Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a basket. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand, and it shines on all who are in the house.
local: Ŋolo bɛɛ kyaŋee kanea na waa mo kolo wuu ke dɔɔ ya, mɔna ɔ na mo ke bo gyakaa kolo dɔɔ adido, na ke nyiŋ naa fa balaŋ baŋ pou be kyaa dekpaŋalaŋ man nɛ.

eng: Then you wont look like you are fasting to people, but only to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
local: N yɛɛ mena na balaŋ ba gyem nyi n ne bake n nɔɔ ya. Mɔna n kya ŋon n bɛɛ naa e na n sia fonɛɛ ya, te ɔ ne naa kpene kamasɛ keŋ n ne yɛɛ weese nɛ waa tɔ neŋ kom.

eng: A huge storm arose on the lake so that waves were sloshing over the boat. But Jesus was asleep.
local: Karatetee dokoloŋ te feliŋ dinaa daale korowɔ ne gyɔɔ loŋ keŋ dɔɔ, te gyɔɔ loŋ do degbele keŋ man. Debaŋ kenaŋ na Yesu te doo dosoroŋ.

eng: They replied, We have nothing here except five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ yako e nyiaa, Bodobodose abiliŋ anoŋ na kpebia bala pɛ te de dana.

eng: To what will I compare this generation? It is like a child sitting in the marketplaces calling out to others.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, woŋ te maa mo gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ maa na? Sena te be wɔɛ? Be yɛɛ ŋgba bia kyaa la abɔntere man bee yoi nɛ, na bɛɛ fae yeesa yako dɔŋa nyiaa.

eng: Jesus knew what they intended to do, so he went away from there. Large crowds followed him, and he healed them all.
local: Yesu kena mena nɛ, ɔ wɔŋa ɔ wose lee yenaŋ. Balaŋ burum sila e te ɔ kyɔɔ be man kaweesetena baŋ pou.

eng: A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and much grieving. Rachel weeping for her children, and she did not want to be comforted, because they were no more.
local: De nyii yeesa na kewii na kayeyɛɛ dinaa Rama tɛɛle man. Rahɛle ne wii ɔ bia te ɔ te bɛɛ nyi be na kyɔlɛɛ e ya, nawolo nyi ɔ bia pou te munu.

eng: In the same way, every good tree produces good fruit, and every rotten tree produces bad fruit.
local: Mena mɔ te daŋ kpaakpaate kamasɛ na se bia kpaakpaa, te daŋ kum mɔ na see bia kumɛɛ.

eng: In the same way, let your light shine before people, so they can see the good things you do and praise your Father who is in heaven.
local: Mena mɔ te ɛ yela balaŋ kena ɛ keŋmaŋee keŋ. Kei ne gyae la ke yela balaŋ kena ɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan ɛ ne yɛɛ nɛ, na baa nyiŋ lese ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ yele.

eng: Stop collecting treasures for your own benefit on earth, where moth and rust eat them and where thieves break in and steal them.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ na gyae abɔɔ see tɛɛle kei dɔɔ fa ɛ wose ya. Nawolo nyi tɛɛle kei dɔɔ na, paapaase na kandorɔse gyae ke tale wɔlɛɛ ŋa, yaa ŋmɛɛlatena mɔ baa tale kaa ŋmɛɛle ŋa.

eng: They came and woke him, saying, Lord, rescue us! Were going to drown.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu kaseela baŋ gyuuwɔ ke gyuusu e yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo lɛɛ daa, dɛɛ gyae dɛɛ munu.

eng: He said, Bring them here to me.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ mo ŋa kɔna maŋ.

eng: We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt mourn.
local: De kpee alowɔle nɔɔneŋse fa ŋon mɔna ɛ te sɔe ya! De kpee lia nɔɔneŋse mɔ fa ŋon, mɔna ɛ te wii ya.

eng: But he ordered them not to spread the word about him.
local: mɔna ɔ faree wɔ nyi be na yako ɔ wose man nombia fa ŋolo na ŋolo ya.

eng: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt.
local: Gyoo Herode yeŋ wɔle nɛ, Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo kaa lese ɔ wose wola Gyosɛfo dosɛɛ man Igyipite tɛɛle man te ɔ yako e nyiaa.

eng: A good tree cant produce bad fruit. And a rotten tree cant produce good fruit.
local: Daŋ kpaakpaate be gyae ke tale see bia kumɛɛ ya, te daŋ kum mɔ be gyae ke tale see bia kpaakpaa ya.

eng: Dont even begin to think that I have come to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I havent come to do away with them but to fulfill them.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ na gyueŋ nyi me kɔŋae me kaa bɛɛ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase, yaa nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra be yako see nɛ ya. Ma te kɔŋ nyi me kaa bɛɛ ŋa ya, mɔna me kɔŋae na maa yela nombia ŋenaŋ pou ke kɔŋ ŋgba ba ke yako see nɛ.

eng: Instead, collect treasures for yourselves in heaven, where moth and rust dont eat them and where thieves dont break in and steal them.
local: Mɔna ɛ gyae abɔɔ see ɛ wose Wurubuarɛ man, botɔɔ keŋ paapaase na kandorɔse be wɔlɛɛ ŋa ya, te ŋmɛɛlatena mɔ be gyae baa tale gyu ke ŋmɛɛle ŋa ya nɛ.

eng: He said to them, Why are you afraid, you people of weak faith? Then he got up and gave orders to the winds and the lake, and there was a great calm.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ ɛ kelɛɛdi ta boo ya. Weera dɔɔ te ɛ dana gyakoloŋ mena? Botɔɔ te ɔ korowɔ te ɔ fae feliŋ keŋ na loŋ keŋ te ŋe yɛɛwɔ yididi.

eng: He ordered the crowds to sit down on the grass. He took the five loaves of bread and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them and broke the loaves apart and gave them to his disciples. Then the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: Te ɔ yela balaŋ baŋ tisiwɔ kyakee tɛɛle man. Keŋte Yesu moo bodobodose anoŋ na kpebia bala baŋ, te ɔ gyinaa ɔ nyee adido te ɔ fanewɔ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ, te ɔ kɛrɛɛ ŋa te ɔ moo ŋa fa ɔ kpilala baŋ, te baŋ mɔ moowɔ kpɛlɛɛ balaŋ baŋ.

eng: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He has a demon.
local: Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon kɔŋawɔ na ɔ ne kyaa bake ɔ nɔɔ. Ɔ te nyɔɔ soloŋ kamasɛ ya te ɛ kpa nyi Feliŋkumɛɛ doola ɔ man.

eng: so that what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled.
local: Kei yele la Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ɔ be tɛɛ ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya dɔɔ yako nɛ kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Yesaya yakowɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa.

eng: Get up, the angel said, and take the child and his mother and go to the land of Israel. Those who were trying to kill the child are dead.
local: Koro na n ka mo bu ŋon na ɔ naa, na ɛ ka bese gyu Iserae tɛɛle man. Nawolo nyi balaŋ baŋ nafɔ bɛɛ gyae bu ŋon baa ko nɛ ta yekee.

eng: Every tree that doesnt produce good fruit is chopped down and thrown into the fire.
local: Daŋ kamasɛ keŋ bɛɛ se bia kpaakpaa ya nɛ, bɛɛ kara ke na be ta mo do boalaŋ.

eng: I say to you very seriously that as long as heaven and earth exist, neither the smallest letter nor even the smallest stroke of a pen will be erased from the Law until everything there becomes a reality.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, adido na tɛɛle kei pou nyiŋa te wɔlɛɛ ta ya nɛ, mmaraase ŋan man dokoloŋ, yaa keŋmarasebii dokoloŋ kooŋ gba be gyae ka feefo lee ŋe man ya, kelii debaŋ keŋ nombia ŋenaŋ pou gyae ke kɔŋ, ŋgba mena keŋ bɔɔ yako see nɛ.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
local: Nawolo nyi botɔɔ keŋ nya abɔɔ kpaakpaa doo nɛ, botɔɔ mɔ te n loo gyakaa.

eng: The people were amazed and said, What kind of person is this? Even the winds and the lake obey him.
local: Balaŋ baŋ wose yeŋa wɔ te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Walaŋ woŋti yɛna kei, te feliŋ na loŋ koraŋ ne nyii e nɛ.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and they filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Balaŋ baŋ pou diiwɔ baa, te be tɔɔsee weenɛɛ buruburu akaŋ ŋan na, be nyiŋa doŋase kufu ala .

eng: Yet the Human One came eating and drinking, and they say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners. But wisdom is proved to be right by her works.
local: Maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon kɔŋawɔ mee dii mɛɛ nyɔɔ, te ɛ kpa nyi, Ɛ kɛɛ balee kei. Ɔ yɛɛ kedikumte na soloŋnyɔɔre e. Ɔ gyoona yɛna lempoolɛɛra na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra. Mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ dana Wurubuarɛ nyansa nɛ, ɔ tom na wola la.

eng: Look, my Servant whom I chose, the one I love, in whom I find great pleasure . Ill put my Spirit upon him, and hell announce judgment to the Gentiles.
local: Kei yɛna me tobaale ŋon mɔɔ lese e, ŋon te mɛɛ gyae te me sia na gye ɔ dɔɔ. Maa mo me feliŋ do ɔ man, na waa yako debiise pou nyi debaŋ keŋ maa lɛɛ ba nyee nɛ te lii.

eng: Joseph got up, took the child and his mother, and went to the land of Israel.
local: Mena dɔɔ Gyosɛfo desina ɔ wose, te ɔ moo bu ŋon na ɔ naa te be laŋa Iserae tɛɛle man.

eng: Therefore, you will know them by their fruit.
local: Mena dɔɔ nombia ŋan balaŋ benaŋ ne yɛɛ nɛ, te ɛ ka mo gyeŋ wɔ.

eng: Therefore, whoever ignores one of the least of these commands and teaches others to do the same will be called the lowest in the kingdom of heaven. But whoever keeps these commands and teaches people to keep them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Mena dɔɔ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ bɛɛ nyi ɔ be di Wurubuarɛ mmaraase kɛŋa man kamasɛ dɔɔ ya, te ɔ wola balaŋ mɔ nyi be na di ŋe dɔɔ ya nɛ, na mena walaŋ ŋonaŋ waa yɛɛ walaŋ kemaasabii Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man. Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ di Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan kamasɛ dɔɔ, te ɔ wola balaŋ mɔ nyi baa di ŋe dɔɔ nɛ, baa baake e nyi walaŋ kegyia Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.

eng: The eye is the lamp of the body. Therefore, if your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Sia yɛɛ ŋgba kanea nɛ fa deniwalaŋ wose. N sia dei na n wose pou doo keŋmaŋee man.

eng: When Jesus arrived on the other side of the lake in the country of the Gadarenes, two men who were demon-possessed came from among the tombs to meet him. They were so violent that nobody could travel on that road.
local: Yesu ka toŋ gyu boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ Gadaratena tɛɛle man nɛ, baala bala baale leewɔ amuseese man kɔŋ kaa gyaŋee na e. Na feliŋkumɛɛ doo balaŋ kɛwɔ man, te ba wose dana gyakoloŋ. Mena dɔɔ ŋolo bɛɛ tale mo gbɛɛ kenaŋ man ya.

eng: About five thousand men plus women and children had eaten.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ di weenɛɛ ŋan nɛ, be yaanɔɔle gyae ke yɛɛ ŋgba baala kakpoŋse banoŋ nɛ. Ba te kala ala na bia kpu ya.

eng: Then he began to scold the cities where he had done his greatest miracles because they didnt change their hearts and lives.
local: Donɔɔse ŋan man Yesu be yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia nɛ, ŋe man balaŋ burum te lɛɛ e di na baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man, na baa kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya. Keŋte Yesu kolosiwɔ tia wɔ nyiaa.

eng: He wont argue or shout, and nobody will hear his voice in the streets.
local: Ɔ be gyae waa ta gyae kale yaa waa ta fae yeesa na abɔnterese na balaŋ ke nyii ya.

eng: But when he heard that Archelaus ruled over Judea in place of his father Herod, Joseph was afraid to go there. Having been warned in a dream, he went to the area of Galilee.
local: Mɔna Gyosɛfo ke nyii nyi gyoo Herode bu Akelɔse kyaa la ɔ kya nawɔɔ man ɔ ne di gyoori Gyudia tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ yee botɔɔ ke gyu. Te keŋ Wurubuarɛ be faree e dosɛɛ man dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ kpasewɔ gyu Galelia tɛɛle man.

eng: Not everybody who says to me, Lord, Lord, will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only those who do the will of my Father who is in heaven will enter.
local: Na walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne baake maŋ nyi Me Gbeŋgyoo, me Gbeŋgyoo nɛ, ne gyae la waa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man ya. Mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ kpene keŋ me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido ne gyae nɛ, ŋon waageŋ ne gyae la waa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.

eng: I say to you that unless your righteousness is greater than the righteousness of the legal experts and the Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, akpaa ɛ gbɛɛneŋ ta tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman kela Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ, na Farasiitena dikpii keŋ balaŋ ya nɛ, ɛ be gyae ɛ ka gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man ya.

eng: But if your eye is bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light in you is darkness, how terrible that darkness will be!.
local: Mɔna n sia de wɔlɛɛ na, n wose pou doo ditiŋtɛɛ tuum man. Nyi akpaa kanea keŋ doo n man nɛ da gyoo ditiŋtɛɛ na, kenaŋ na ditiŋtɛɛ dinaa ta gyoo neŋ.

eng: They cried out, What are you going to do with us, Son of God? Have you come to torture us before the time of judgment.
local: Ba kena Yesu nɛ be fae yeesa nedoŋ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ Bu, woŋ te n ne gyae de gyaŋ? N kɔŋae n kaa naase daa diyem pɛna debaŋ keŋ kelii.

eng: Right then, Jesus made the disciples get into the boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake while he dismissed the crowds.
local: Debokenaŋ man te Yesu yela ɔ kaseela baŋ gyoowɔ degbele man kyaŋ gbɛɛ gyu boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ na waa nyiŋ lɔ balaŋ baŋ gbɛɛ.
