eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin! How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives and put on funeral clothes and ashes a long time ago.
local: Ɛmɛɛ Korasintena na Bɛtesaidatena, ɛ laako! Nawolo nyi gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ ɛ gyaŋ nɛ, nyi akpaa beŋ yɛɛ ŋa Tae na Sidɔn donɔɔse man na nafɔ beŋ te wulaa do titere abɔɔ kyaa mo tɔŋ buraa ba wose, na kawola nyi ba te kyɛɛkee ba agyueŋ lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

eng: He wont break a bent stalk, and he wont snuff out a smoldering wick, until he makes justice win.
local: Waa nyiŋ konɔɔ fa baŋ be nombia dana waraŋase nɛ, te ɔ be gyae waa yɛɛ baŋ bɔɔ taŋ doŋ mɔ nɛ akpanyaŋnyaŋ ya. Debaŋ kamasɛ na waa nyiŋ konɔɔ fa mena balaŋ kɛwɔ dinɔɔ, keŋ nyi ɔ be gyae waa yɛɛ kolo na ba wose keyɔkɔse wɔ ya mo kelii debaŋ keŋ baa nyiŋ nyeekelɛɛ.

eng: He settled in a city called Nazareth so that what was spoken through the prophets might be fulfilled: He will be called a Nazarene.
local: Te ɔ gyuuwɔ ke kyaa donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Nasarɛte nɛ man. Kei yela kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ wa akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra dɔɔ yako see nɛ, kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Be yakowɔ nyiaa, Baa baake e nyi Nasarɛte baale.

eng: On the Judgment Day, many people will say to me, Lord, Lord, didnt we prophesy in your name and expel demons in your name and do lots of miracles in your name.
local: Nombia kedi wee dinaa keŋ man nɛ, balaŋ burum baa kaa yako nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, na n yele man te de kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia yee? Yaa na n yele te da moowɔ gegi feliŋkumɛɛ lee balaŋ man yee? Na n yele te da moowɔ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana yee.

eng: You have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago, Dont commit murder, and all who commit murder will be in danger of judgment.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyi, Ɛ nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia yakowɔ nyiaa, beneŋ beneŋ keŋ na, Wurubuarɛ yako de naanaɔ nyi, Ɛ na ko walaŋ ya, te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ko walaŋ na, maa bu e fɔɔ.

eng: No one can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: Walaŋ be kyaa keŋ waa tale som gbeŋgyoona bala ya. Dekɔŋ mena na, waa kɔla ŋolo na waa gyae ŋolo nombia, yaa waa som ŋolo nideli kela ŋolo. Ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale som Wurubuarɛ na kɔba bom ya.

eng: Far off in the distance a large herd of pigs was feeding.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na, kekuiriise dikpii dinaa daale seŋɛɛ botɔɔ yenaŋ bee dii.

eng: When he sent them away, he went up onto a mountain by himself to pray. Evening came and he was alone.
local: Yesu ke lɔ balaŋ baŋ gbɛɛ taŋ nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ bula dɔɔ waageŋ ɔ kaa fane. Kelii balinɔɔ nɛ, na Yesu waageŋ kaa la botɔɔ.

eng: But I say to you that Tyre and Sidon will be better off on Judgment Day than you.
local: Mɔna mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa kaa di balaŋ nombia nɛ, waa wii Taetena na Sidɔntena waraŋase kela ŋon.

eng: And the Gentiles will put their hope in his name.
local: Te debiise balaŋ pou baa mo be loo gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ bo.

eng: Then Ill tell them, Ive never known you. Get away from me, you people who do wrong.
local: Botɔɔ te maa toro yako wɔ nyiaa, Mɔɔ gyeŋ ŋon ya, ɛ lee me siaman. Ɛmɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra! .

eng: But I say to you that everyone who is angry with their brother or sister will be in danger of judgment. If they say to their brother or sister, You idiot, they will be in danger of being condemned by the governing council. And if they say, You fool, they will be in danger of fiery hell.
local: Mɔna maŋ ne yako ŋon baa nyiaa, Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ gyeŋ baŋ do ɔ dɔɔ na baa bu e fɔɔ, te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ tee ɔ dɔɔ nyi ɔ yɛɛ ohuhoo e na, baa mo e gyu kegyiise siaman na be kaa di ɔ nombia. Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ baake ɔ dɔɔ nyi Diyiŋte na, ɔtenate benaa na boalaŋ man kagyoo.

eng: Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what youll eat or what youll drink, or about your body, what youll wear. Isnt life more than food and the body more than clothes?.
local: Mena dɔɔ te mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, ɛ na yeyɛɛ na ɛ kekyaa man kedi na kenyɔɔ, na abɔɔ ŋan ɛ ka do ya. Nyeedoŋ ne hia kela weenɛɛ, te deniwalaŋ wose mɔ ne hia kela abɔɔ ŋan waa do.

eng: Meanwhile, the boat, fighting a strong headwind, was being battered by the waves and was already far away from land.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na degbele keŋ te lii lem, na feliŋ dinaa daale mɔ ne gyɔɔ degbele keŋ wosi loŋ keŋ dɔɔ.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be thrown down to the place of the dead. After all, if the miracles that were done among you had been done in Sodom, it would still be here today.
local: Te nɛnɛɛ ɛmɛɛ Kapanumtena, ɛ gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ waa kɛlɛɛ ŋon gyina ŋon adido? Koa, waa yɔkɔse ŋon, na waa mo ŋon do boalaŋ keŋ bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man, na ɛ kena diyem kekpaakekpaa. Nafɔ gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan mɔɔ yɛɛ ɛ man yaa meŋ yɛɛwɔ Sodom donɔɔ man nɛ, nafɔ gyɛŋ ke deŋ sɛɛ ke kyaa.

eng: The demons pleaded with him, If you throw us out, send us into the herd of pigs.
local: Keŋte feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan waase Yesu nyiaa, Akpaa n ke gegi daa na, yela de kaa gyoo kekuiriise dikpii kei man.

eng: They brought to Jesus a demon-possessed man who was blind and unable to speak. Jesus healed him so that he could both speak and see.
local: Wee daale ba moo walaŋ ŋon feliŋkum doo ɔ man, te ɔ sia be yɛlɛɛ te ɔ bɛɛ tale kolosi mɔ ya nɛ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ. Keŋte Yesu kyɔɔ e te ɔ kolosiwɔ te ɔ sia mɔ gyuusuwɔ.

eng: Everybody who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise builder who built a house on bedrock.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Mena dɔɔ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne nyii me nɔɔwoya kɛŋa te ɔ ne di ŋe dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ yɛɛ ŋgba siakaralate ŋon ɔ be baŋ ɔ deni gyakaa boe dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: Therefore, if you bring your gift to the altar and there remember that your brother or sister has something against you.
local: Mena dɔɔ nyi akpaa nyaŋ ŋon n mo kolo kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ kedeesa boe keŋ tɛɛ n kaa deesi, te n tɔɔse nyi ɛmɛɛ na ŋolo dana nombia na.

eng: Look at the birds in the sky. They dont sow seed or harvest grain or gather crops into barns. Yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Arent you worth much more than they are?.
local: Ɛ kɛɛ gyebuse, bɔɔ ne duu abɔɔ ya te bɔɔ ne kɔɔ ya te bɔɔ dana kpeese yaa denɛɛ ŋan man baa see weenɛɛ ya. Mɔna ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ ne fa wɔ weenɛɛ. Kaboena ya gyeŋ nyi, ɛ wose ne hia kela gyebuse.

eng: Very early in the morning he came to his disciples, walking on the lake.
local: Tɛɛ kɛɛ bee kena nɛ, Yesu tɛɛwɔ loŋ keŋ dɔɔ ɔ ne gyu ɔ kaseela baŋ gyaŋ.

eng: But I say to you that it will be better for the land of Sodom on the Judgment Day than it will be for you.
local: Mɔna mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyi wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa kaa di balaŋ pou nombia nɛ, waa wii Sodomtena waraŋase kela ŋon.

eng: Then he said to the demons, Go away, and they came out and went into the pigs. The whole herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan nyiaa, Ɛ lee ta. Mena dɔɔ feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan leewɔ balaŋ baŋ man gyu ka gyoo kekuiriise baŋ man, te be dikpii keŋ pou yeuwɔ tisi bula kyoloŋkyoloŋ keŋ gyu ka gyoo loŋ keŋ man te be munuwɔ pou.

eng: All the crowds were amazed and said, This man couldnt be the Son of David, could he.
local: Nombia kɛŋa doo balaŋ baŋ pou nɔɔ te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Na kei yɛna gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii ŋon baa yaa.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It didnt fall because it was firmly set on bedrock.
local: Ɔga ŋɛɛwɔ te booneŋ wuuwɔ te feliŋ dinaa gyɔɔ deni keŋ, mɔna ka te yale ya. Nawolo nyi ɔ see ka mokɔɔ gyakaa boe dɔɔ.

eng: leave your gift at the altar and go. First make things right with your brother or sister and then come back and offer your gift.
local: tina nya abɔɔ ŋan yela kedeesa boe keŋ tɛɛ botɔɔ, na n ke gyu n dɔɔ ŋon gyaŋ na ɛ kaa desina ya nsana, pɛna n kaa mo nya abɔɔ ŋan deesi fa Wurubuarɛ.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Ɛ man woŋti te lee ɔ kayeyɛɛ man nɛ, waa tale mo debaŋ dokoloŋ kooŋ pɛ kpu na ɔ weeya ŋan Wurubuarɛ be fa e nɛ?.

eng: When the disciples saw him walking on the lake, they were terrified and said, Its a ghost! They were so frightened they screamed.
local: Ɔ kaseela baŋ kena e ɔ dɔŋɛɛ loŋ keŋ dɔɔ ɔ ne kɔŋ nɛ, be wɔe lɔɔ wɔ gbaa. Keŋte be kpa Ɔ yɛɛ woo e, te be fae yeesa nawolo nyi gyakoloŋ te kyaŋ wɔ.

eng: At that time Jesus said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and have shown them to babies.
local: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ man te Yesu fanewɔ nyiaa, Me kya, adido na ateta pou Gbeŋgyoo, mɛɛ fa neŋ karaŋ nawolo nyi n ta mo abɔɔ kɛŋa weese nyansatena na baŋ ba gyeŋ nombia nɛ, te n lese ŋa wola balaŋ baŋ be dinɔɔ yɛɛ ŋgba bia nɛ.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran into the city and told everything that had happened to the demon-possessed men.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɛɛ kekuiriise baŋ dɔɔ nɛ, yeuwɔ gyu donɔɔ man te be yako nombia ŋan be kɔŋ nɛ pou na kpene keŋ be kɔŋ balaŋ baŋ feliŋkumɛɛ doo be man dɔɔ nɛ fa balaŋ.

eng: When the Pharisees heard, they said, This man throws out demons only by the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of the demons.
local: Mɔna Farasiitena baŋ kenyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ be yakowɔ nyiaa, Feliŋkumɛɛ pou gyoo Bɛlisɛbu faa na balee kei doŋ te ɔ ne tale gegi feliŋkumɛɛ nɛ.

eng: But everybody who hears these words of mine and doesnt put them into practice will be like a fool who built a house on sand.
local: Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne nyii me nɔɔwoya kɛŋa te ɔ bee di ŋe dɔɔ ya nɛ, ɔ yɛɛ ŋgba diyiŋte ŋon ɔ be ma ɔ deni gyakaa aŋasa dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: Be sure to make friends quickly with your opponents while you are with them on the way to court. Otherwise, they will haul you before the judge, the judge will turn you over to the officer of the court, and you will be thrown into prison.
local: Nyi akpaa ŋolo de samenaa neŋ na, yɛɛ kakyeŋ na ɛ ke desina ya nsana pɛna nombia kedi wee keŋ kelii. Na mena ya wee kenaŋ de lii na, waa mo neŋ ka do nombiadiiri ŋon nyiŋmaa man, na ŋon mɔ ka mo neŋ ka do kotɔtɔɔtena nyiŋmaa man, na baa mo neŋ ketɔ deni.

eng: And why do you worry about clothes? Notice how the lilies in the field grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth.
local: Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ na yeyɛɛ na abɔɔ ŋan ɛ ka do. Ɛ kɛɛ mena keŋ kyeŋkyeŋkɔɔse na bee, ŋe bɛɛ yɛɛ tom ya te ŋe bɛɛ loo kanyaŋse mɔ ya.

eng: Just then Jesus spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: Debokenaŋ man te Yesu kolosiwɔ fa wɔ nyi, Ɛ nyiŋ konɔɔ, ɛ na yee ya, maŋ e.

eng: Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: Taata, ma gyeŋ nyi kei yɛna nyaŋ gbagba n kegyaebii.

eng: Then the whole city came out and met Jesus. When they saw him, they pleaded with him to leave their region.
local: Mena dɔɔ donɔɔ keŋ man balaŋ pou leewɔ kɔŋ kaa gyaŋee na Yesu. Ba kena e nɛ, be waase e nyi waa lee be tɛɛle dɔɔ.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he replied, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland. Every city or house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Mɔna Yesu gyeŋ kpene keŋ bɛɛ gyueŋ nɛ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Tɛɛle kamasɛ keŋ ke man balaŋ de kpase ba wose kpookpoo bee yoo na dɔŋa na, tɛɛle kenaŋ be gyae ke de ya. Mena mɔ te donɔɔ yaa dekpaŋalaŋ daale man balaŋ de kpase ba wose kpookpoo bee yoo na dɔŋa na, dekpaŋalaŋ kenaŋ ne yɛlɛɛ bo.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It fell and was completely destroyed.
local: Ɔga ŋɛɛwɔ te booneŋ wuuwɔ te feliŋ dinaa gyɔɔ deni keŋ te ka leewɔ yala worom tekerii pou.

eng: I say to you in all seriousness that you wont get out of there until youve paid the very last penny.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako neŋ n ke nyii nyiaa, n be gyae n ka lee deni kenaŋ man ya, kaboena n ke tɔ kom keŋ pou baa do neŋ nɛ taŋ.

eng: But I say to you that even Solomon in all of his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: Mɔna mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, Gyoo Solomɔn gba na ɔ wosekenyiŋ koraŋ nɛ, ɔ te nyiŋ kyeŋkyeŋkɔɔse kɛŋa man dokoloŋ kyeo ya.

eng: Peter replied, Lord, if its you, order me to come to you on the water.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita yako e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, akpaa nyaŋ e na, yela maa ta loŋ keŋ dɔɔ kɔŋ n gyaŋ.

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows the Son except the Father. And nobody knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, Me kya ta mo kpene kamasɛ do maŋ me nyiŋmaa man. Ŋolo ba gyeŋ maŋ ɔ bu ŋon ya, me kya waageŋ kooŋ gyeŋa na maŋ. Ŋolo mɔ ba gyeŋ kya ŋon ya, maŋ ɔ bu ŋon na baŋ maa lese me kya ŋon wola wɔ nɛ, baageŋ ne gyae la baa gyeŋ e.

eng: If Satan throws out Satan, he is at war with himself. How then can his kingdom endure?.
local: Akpaa nyi ba mo ɔbɔnsam doŋ gegi feliŋkumɛɛ nɛ, na keŋ nawolo baa nyi ɔ gyoori keŋ man balaŋ te kpase ba wose kpookpoo bee yoo na dɔŋa. Dekɔŋ mena na, sena te ɔ gyoori keŋ gyae ke tale seŋ?.

eng: When Jesus finished these words, the crowds were amazed at his teaching.
local: Yesu ke yako nombia kɛŋa taŋ nɛ, ɔ kawola keŋ doo balaŋ dikpii keŋ nɔɔ.

eng: And Jesus said, Come. Then Peter got out of the boat and was walking on the water toward Jesus.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, Kɔŋ te Pita leewɔ degbele keŋ man ta loŋ keŋ dɔɔ ɔ ne gyu Yesu gyaŋ.

eng: Come to me, all you who are struggling hard and carrying heavy loads, and I will give you rest.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ seelena kasolɔse yuŋnyiŋan te ne takaa ŋon nɛ, ɛ kɔŋ me gyaŋ na maa yela ɛ ke fɛɛfo.

eng: You have heard that it was said, Dont commit adultery.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia yakowɔ nyi alo na baale de yala dɔŋa na, ŋolo na lee ɔ dɔɔ wɔle ya.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, wont God do much more for you, you people of weak faith?.
local: Weera dɔɔ te ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di nideli ya. Nyi akpaa mena te Wurubuarɛ ne mia ŋalese ŋan ɛ kena ŋa gyɛŋ kyoo na be ta too ŋa na, kenaŋ na ɛ tɔɔse nyi Wurubuarɛ waa mia ŋon kela ŋalese ŋenaŋ.

eng: And if I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Akpaa nyi Bɛlisɛbu doŋ te mɛɛ mo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ na, amɔte doŋ yaa ɛ balaŋ baŋ mɔ na mo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ? Mena na ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ balaŋ ne gyae la baa bu ŋon fɔɔ.

eng: because he was teaching them like someone with authority and not like their legal experts.
local: Nawolo nyi ɔ wolawɔ ŋgba walaŋ ŋon ɔ dana doŋ nombia ŋan dɔɔ nɛ, be yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ ba mmaraa wolala baŋ na wolo nɛ ya.

eng: But when Peter saw the strong wind, he became frightened. As he began to sink, he shouted, Lord, rescue me.
local: Mɔna Pita kena mena keŋ feliŋ keŋ ne gyɔɔ loŋ keŋ dɔɔ nɛ, gyakoloŋ gyoo e te ɔ fiasɛɛ ɔ na gyoo loŋ keŋ man. Keŋte ɔ fae yeesa nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, lɛɛ maŋ.

eng: Put on my yoke, and learn from me. Im gentle and humble. And you will find rest for yourselves.
local: Ɛ kɔŋ na daa na ŋon ke yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ na ɛ ke kasee maŋ, na ɛ ke nyiŋ wosefɛɛreŋ. Nawolo nyi ma woɛ, te ma te yɔkɔse ma wose mɔ lee me konɔɔ man.

eng: But I say to you that every man who looks at a woman lustfully has already committed adultery in his heart.
local: Mɔna maŋ ne yako ŋon baa nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ kɛɛ alo te ɔ gyakaa e loo na, ɔtenate te yɛɛ dukum Wurubuarɛ siaman. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ siaman na ɔ gyae e baa.

eng: Therefore, dont worry and say, What are we going to eat? or What are we going to drink? or What are we going to wear.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na yeyɛɛ na kpene keŋ ɛ ke di, yaa kpene keŋ ɛ ke nyɔɔ yaa kpene keŋ ɛ ka do nɛ dɔɔ ya.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the power of Gods Spirit, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Te akpaa Wurubuarɛ doŋ te mɛɛ mo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ na, kenaŋ na ɛ wulaa gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ taŋ kekɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ.

eng: Jesus immediately reached out and grabbed him, saying, You man of weak faith! Why did you begin to have doubts.
local: Debokenaŋ man te Yesu tenee ɔ nyiŋmaa kyaŋ e te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Baale, n kelɛɛdi ta boo ya. Weera dɔɔ te n lɛɛ maŋ aŋmaareŋ n konɔɔ man.

eng: My yoke is easy to bear, and my burden is light.
local: Nawolo nyi gbɛɛ keŋ dɔɔ maa mo ŋon ta nɛ, be taŋ baŋ ya te kasolɔ keŋ maa bake seele ŋon nɛ, be gyae ke yɛɛ ŋon yuŋ ya.

eng: And if your right eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body be thrown into hell.
local: Akpaa n dunoluŋ sibii ne yele la te n ne yɛɛ dukum na kulu ke leki. Dei nyi nya lee kadeŋ na n ke kyaa Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ kela nyi baa mo n dibiliŋ pou ka do boalaŋ man.

eng: Can people go into a house that belongs to a strong man and steal his possessions, unless they first tie up the strong man? Then they can rob his house.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Sena te ŋolo waa tale gyu doŋte ŋolo dɛɛ na ɔ kaa mo ɔ kolo, keŋ nyi ɔ te di doŋte ŋon dɔɔ ya? Nyi akpaa ɔ di doŋte ŋon dɔɔ te ɔ bake e see nɛ, kenaŋ na waa tale kuu wa abɔɔ pou.

eng: Gentiles long for all these things. Your heavenly Father knows that you need them.
local: Nawolo nyi mena abɔɔ kɛŋa te tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ ya nɛ sia ne yɛɛ ŋe dɔɔ, mɔna ɛ kya Wurubuarɛ gyeŋ nyi mena abɔɔ kɛŋa ne hia ɛmɛɛ mɔ.

eng: When they got into the boat, the wind settled down.
local: Yesu na Pita ka gyeŋ gyoo degbele keŋ man nɛ, te feliŋ keŋ tinaa kegyɔ yela.

eng: And if your right hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body go into hell.
local: Te akpaa nyi n dunoluŋ baam ne yele la te n ne yɛɛ dukum na kara ke leki. Dei nyi nya lee kadeŋ na n ke kyaa Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ kela nyi baa mo n dibiliŋ pou ka do boalaŋ man.

eng: Instead, desire first and foremost Gods kingdom and Gods righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ taŋgbɛɛ mo ɛ loo pou gyakaa Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ na ɔ gbɛɛneŋ ŋan tenɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ. Nyi akpaa ɛ yɛɛ mena na, Wurubuarɛ waa mo abɔɔ akaŋ ŋan pou kpu ŋon.

eng: It was said, Whoever divorces his wife must give her a divorce certificate.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa Ɛ nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia yakowɔ nyiaa, Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa bɛɛ ɔ wɛɛle na, kaboena nyi waa fa e tɔne na kawola nyi ɔ te bɛɛ e.

eng: Then those in the boat worshipped Jesus and said, You must be Gods Son.
local: Botɔɔ te baŋ be doo degbele keŋ man nɛ, korowɔ buŋ Yesu nawɔɔtɛɛ te be yako e nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon ampaŋ.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me scatters.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ be kpuɛ na maŋ ya nɛ na ɔ ne kɔla maŋ, te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon mɔ ɔ bɛɛ kyɔ maŋ na dee yilaa balaŋ fa Wurubuarɛ ya nɛ, na ɔ ne yaasee wɔ bo.

eng: Therefore, stop worrying about tomorrow, because tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na yeyɛɛ na kyoo wui nombia ya, te ɛ na mo gyɛŋ wui nombia kpu kyoo wui man ya. Nawolo nyi wee kamasɛ na ke wui nombia.

eng: But I say to you that whoever divorces his wife except for sexual unfaithfulness forces her to commit adultery. And whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
local: Mɔna maŋ ne yako ŋon baa nyi akpaa alo te gyae baala ya te ɔ baale de bɛɛ e na, ɔ te yeli alo ŋon te wɔlɛɛ yale mmaraase Wurubuarɛ siaman. Te nyaŋ ŋon mɔ n yala alo ŋon ɔ baale be bɛɛ e na, Wurubuarɛ siaman na n gyae ŋolo wɛɛle baa.

eng: When they had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret.
local: Be ka toŋ boo keŋ nɛ, be gyuuwɔ kelii tɛɛle keŋ bɛɛ baake ke Genesareti nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that people will be forgiven for every sin and insult to God. But insulting the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ waa mo balaŋ nombiakumɛɛ na mmusuo nombia ŋan bɛɛ yako nɛ pou kyɛɛ wɔ, mɔna walaŋ ŋon waa mo mmusuo nombia gyakaa Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon dɔɔ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ be gyae waa mo kyɛɛ e korakora ya.

eng: Again you have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago: Dont make a false solemn pledge, but you should follow through on what you have pledged to the Lord.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ nyiiwɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia besewɔ yako gyaŋgba keŋ nyiaa, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ kaŋ ntam fa de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ na, waa yɛɛ kakyeŋ na waa di ke dɔɔ.

eng: When the people who lived in that place recognized him, they sent word throughout that whole region, and they brought to him everyone who was sick.
local: Balaŋ baŋ be kyaa tɛɛle kenaŋ dɔɔ ka gyeŋ nyi Yesu ŋonaŋ nɛ, be kpiliiwɔ gyu ke yako donɔɔse ŋan pou kyaa botɔɔ yenaŋ nɛ. Mena dɔɔ balaŋ moo be kaweesetena pou kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ.

eng: And whoever speaks a word against the Human One will be forgiven. But whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven, not in this age or in the age that is coming.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ kolosi nombiikum tia maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa mo kyɛɛ e. Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa kolosi nombiikum tia Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon nɛ, Wurubuarɛ be gyae waa mo kyɛɛ e gyɛŋ yaa kyoo ya.

eng: But I say to you that you must not pledge at all. You must not pledge by heaven, because its Gods throne.
local: Mɔna maŋ ne yako ŋon baa nyiaa, ɛ na kaŋ ntam korakora gba ya. Ɛ na kaŋ adido ntam ya, nawolo nyi botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ kyaa ɔ ne di gyoori balaŋ pou dɔɔ.

eng: Then they begged him that they might just touch the edge of his clothes. Everyone who touched him was cured.
local: Keŋte be waase e nyi waa yela kaweesetena baŋ ka mo be nyiŋmaase tina ɔ kegba nɔɔbii paŋpaŋ bo gba. Te baŋ pou bɔɔ tina ɔ kegba keŋ nɛ, wose yɛɛ wɔ doŋ.

eng: Either consider the tree good and its fruit good, or consider the tree rotten and its fruit rotten. A tree is known by its fruit.
local: N kɛɛ daŋ dɔɔ nideli te ke de nyiŋ doŋ nɛ, kɛɛ se bia kpaakpaa. Te akpaa n te kɛɛ ke dɔɔ nideli ya te kawee de kyaŋ ke na, kɛɛ se bia kumɛɛ bo. Nawolo nyi daŋ kamasɛ nɛ, ke bia te bɛɛ mo gyeŋ ke dinɔɔ.

eng: You must not pledge by the earth, because its Gods footstool. You must not pledge by Jerusalem, because its the city of the great king.
local: Ɛ na kaŋ tɛɛle mɔ ntam ya, nawolo nyi botɔɔ yɛna Wurubuarɛ nae dɛɛ see. Ɛ na kaŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ mɔ ntam ya, nawolo nyi keŋ yɛna gyoo dinaa ŋon donɔɔ.

eng: Children of snakes! How can you speak good things while you are evil? What fills the heart comes out of the mouth.
local: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ dana nombiakumɛɛ ŋgba doma nɛ, sena te ɛmɛɛ balaŋ kumɛɛ kɛwɔ ɛ ke tale kolosi nombia kpaakpaa? Nawolo nyi kpene kamasɛ keŋ be wulu walaŋ konɔɔ man nɛ, keŋ na lee la ɔ nɔɔman.

eng: And you must not pledge by your head, because you cant turn one hair white or black.
local: Ɛ na kaŋ ɛ nyee ntam ya, nawolo nyi ŋolo be gyae waa tale yela ɔ nyeenyɔɔse dokoloŋ ke biri yaa ka wolose ya.

eng: Good people bring out good things from their good treasure. But evil people bring out evil things from their evil treasure.
local: Mena ke te denibalaŋ mɔ doo. Walaŋ kpaakpaate kamasɛ na lese abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan doo ɔ konɔɔ man nɛ wola, te walaŋkum kamasɛ mɔ na lese nombiakumɛɛ ŋan doo ɔ konɔɔ man nɛ wola.

eng: Let your yes mean yes, and your no mean no. Anything more than this comes from the evil one.
local: Nombii keŋ kaboena nyi nya sɛɛ Ayi na sɛɛ nyi Ayi te keŋ kaboena nyi nya bɛɛ nyi Koa na, bɛɛ nyi Koa. Nombii da lee kɛŋa wɔle na, lee walaŋkum ŋon gyaŋ.

eng: I tell you that people will have to answer on Judgment Day for every useless word they speak.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, nombiikum kamasɛ keŋ gyae ka lee walaŋ kamasɛ nɔɔman nɛ, wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa di balaŋ pou nombia tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, ɔtenate waa seŋ Wurubuarɛ siaman na waa tira ke nɔɔ fa e.

eng: You have heard that it was said, An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth .
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako nyiaa, Ɛ nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia yakowɔ nyiaa, akpaa n dɔɔ de yɛlɛɛ n sibii na nyaŋ mɔ yɛlɛɛ ɔ wui tɔ kom. Te akpaa n dɔɔ de kulu n keli na, nyaŋ mɔ kulu ɔ wui tɔ kom.

eng: By your words you will be either judged innocent or condemned as guilty.
local: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ nɔɔwoya te baa mo di ɔ nombia, na baa naa nyi ɔ dii deŋele, yaa ɔ dii fɔɔ.

eng: But I say to you that you must not oppose those who want to hurt you. If people slap you on your right cheek, you must turn the left cheek to them as well.
local: Mɔna maŋ ne yako ŋon baa nyiaa, akpaa ŋolo de yɛɛ neŋ nombiikum na, nyaŋ na mo nombiikum tɔ e kom ya. Te akpaa ŋolo da ŋmera neŋ n dunoluŋ lakaa man na, besena n demena mɔ wui kpu e.

eng: At that time some of the legal experts and the Pharisees requested of Jesus, Teacher, we would like to see a sign from you.
local: Botɔɔ te Farasiitena na mmaraa wolala baŋ baale yako Yesu nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, dɛɛ gyae n ke yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia wola daa.

eng: When they wish to haul you to court and take your shirt, let them have your coat too.
local: Nyi ŋolo de samenaa neŋ nyi waa lɛo n kegba wurikyim na, kpee n kegba atetate keŋ mɔ kpu e.

eng: But he replied, An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign, but it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ, ɛ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ te ɛ be yee Wurubuarɛ ya. Ɛ ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na ɛ kena, mɔna mɔɔ gyae maa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombii kamasɛ kpu na keŋ be kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Gyona dɔɔ nɛ ya.

eng: When they force you to go one mile, go with them two.
local: Akpaa ŋolo da ŋere neŋ nyi ɛmɛɛ na e ke ta nawɔɔtɔɔse kufu na, sɛɛ na ɛmɛɛ na e ke ta nawɔɔtɔɔse sao.

eng: Just as Jonah was in the whales belly for three days and three nights , so the Human One will be in the heart of the earth for three days and three nights.
local: Mena keŋ Gyona be doo kpebii dinaa ŋon tɔɔman weeya atooro nelim na ka weese nɛ, mena te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon mɔ maa doo tɛɛle tɔɔman weeya atooro nelim na ka weese.

eng: Give to those who ask, and dont refuse those who wish to borrow from you.
local: Akpaa ŋolo da sola neŋ kolo na fa e, te akpaa ŋolo de kɔŋ ɔ kaa paa n kolo na, na bɛɛ ya.

eng: The citizens of Nineveh will stand up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it as guilty, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching. And look, someone greater than Jonah is here.
local: Wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa di balaŋ nombia nɛ, Niniwetena baa koro seŋ na baa bu gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ fɔɔ, nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ Gyona be kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan fa wɔ nɛ, be kyɛɛkeewɔ lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man te be kɔŋawɔ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ. Mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyiaa, walaŋ ŋon ɔ kela Gyona nɛ kyaa kɛbo.

eng: You have heard that it was said, You must love your neighbor and hate your enemy.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako nyiaa, Ɛ nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia yakowɔ nyiaa, gyae n dɔɔ na n ke kɔla ŋon ɔ ne kɔla neŋ nɛ.

eng: The queen of the South will be raised up by God at the judgment with this generation and condemn it because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: Mena mɔ te wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa di balaŋ nombia nɛ, Seba gyoo ŋon ɔ yɛɛ alo e te ɔ ba lee ata yenaŋ nɛ, waa koro seŋ na waa bu gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ fɔɔ. Nawolo nyi ɔ ke nyii Solomɔn nyansa nombia ŋan nɛ, ɔ tɛɛwɔ leenaŋ tɛɛle ɔto kɔŋ kaa nyii ŋa. Mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyiaa, walaŋ ŋon ɔ kela gyoo Solomɔn ŋonaŋ nɛ kyaa kɛbo.

eng: But I say to you, love your enemies and pray for those who harass you.
local: Mɔna maŋ ne yako ŋon baa nyiaa, ɛ gyae balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɔla ŋon nɛ, na ɛ ke fane fa baŋ bɛɛ naase ŋon diyem nɛ.

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Feliŋkum da lee walaŋ man nɛ ɔ ne tɛɛ kila kɛo tɛɛle awolɛɛ ɔ ne gyae dekyae, mɔna ɔ bee nyim ya.

eng: so that you will be acting as children of your Father who is in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both the evil and the good and sends rain on both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: na kawola nyi ɛ yɛɛ ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ bia gbagbawɔ. Nawolo nyi ɔ ne yele weese ne gyɔɔ fa balaŋ kumɛɛ na balaŋ kpaakpaa pou, te ɔ ne yele ɔga ne ŋɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ɔ siaman, na baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ ba tenɛɛ ɔ siaman ya nɛ pou.

eng: Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left. When it arrives, it finds the place vacant, cleaned up, and decorated.
local: Akpaa ɔ te nyiŋ dekyae ya na ɔ ne yako ɔ wose nyiaa, maa bese gyu ma dekyae bene keŋ mɔɔ lee nɛ man. Akpaa ɔ kɔŋ kaa naa nyi ba te gbee botɔɔ desina ke nideli keŋ nyi ayimɛɛ kamasɛ be doo ya nɛ.

eng: If you love only those who love you, what reward do you have? Dont even the tax collectors do the same?.
local: Nyi akpaa ɛ ne gyae balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ gyae ŋon nɛ baageŋ bo na, ɛ na gyueŋ nyi ɛ ke nyiŋ kolo kpaakpaa lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya, nawolo nyi nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra mɔ koraŋ ne gyae baŋ bɛɛ gyae wɔ nɛ.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning. This is the way it will be also for this evil generation.
local: ɔ na bese gyu ka mo feliŋkumɛɛ nyetooro, ŋan ŋe dana nombiakumɛɛ kela e nɛ kpu na ɔ wose na be kaa kyaa walaŋ ŋonaŋ man. Dekɔŋ mena na, walaŋ ŋonaŋ dinɔɔ na bese wɔlɛɛ kela ɔ kekyaa gyaŋgba keŋ. Mena ne gyae la ke kɔŋ gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ kumɛɛ dɔɔ.

eng: And if you greet only your brothers and sisters, what more are you doing? Dont even the Gentiles do the same?.
local: Te akpaa ɛ do ɛ gyoona baageŋ nɔɔ na, woŋ te ɛ ne yɛɛ kela balaŋ akaŋ baŋ? Na mena te balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ ya nɛ mɔ ne yɛɛ yee?.

eng: While Jesus was speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers stood outside trying to speak with him.
local: Yesu ke gyakaa ɔ ne kolosi fa balaŋ baŋ nɛ, ɔ naa na ɔ naabia kɔŋawɔ kaa seŋ debɔɔ man keŋ bɛɛ gyae gbɛɛ baa kolosi na e.

eng: Therefore, just as your heavenly Father is complete in showing love to everyone, so also you must be complete.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ yɛɛ balaŋ kpaakpaa keŋ nombiikum kamasɛ na doo ɛ man ya kelii ɔto, ŋgba ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ.

eng: Someone said to him, Look, your mother and brothers are outside wanting to speak with you.
local: Botɔɔ te ŋolo yako Yesu nyiaa, N naa na n naabia seŋɛɛ debɔɔ man bɛɛ gyae baa naa neŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Amɔte yɛna me naa, te bomɔ yɛna me naabia.

eng: He stretched out his hand toward his disciples and said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: Keŋte ɔ tenee ɔ nyiŋmaa wola ɔ kaseela baŋ te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Kɛwɔ yɛna me naa na me naabia.

eng: Whoever does the will of my Father who is in heaven is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ kegyaebii na, ŋon yɛna me naabu na me deesi na me naa.

eng: In those days John the Baptist appeared in the desert of Judea announcing.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ man nɛ, Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre kɔŋawɔ ɔ ne kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia Gyudia depampaa dinaa keŋ man. Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: Ɛ kyɛɛkee lee ɛ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na ɛ ke kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ ta benaa.

eng: He was the one of whom Isaiah the prophet spoke when he said: The voice of one shouting in the wilderness, Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight. .
local: Gyɔn kei yɛna ŋon Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya be yako ɔ wose man nombia nyiaa, Ŋolo ne fae yeesa depampaa dinaa keŋ man nyiaa, ɛ desina de Gbeŋgyoo gbɛɛneŋ. Ɛ yela ɔ gbɛɛneŋ pou ka tenee.

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Kurumɔɔ tɔne te ba moowɔ ŋara Gyɔn kegba, te wonembu tɔne te ɔ moowɔ yɛɛ abɔsɔɔ kpaŋ ɔ teŋa man. Akpataana na teebɔɔ yɛna ɔ weenɛɛ.

eng: People from Jerusalem, throughout Judea, and all around the Jordan River came to him.
local: Balaŋ lee Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man na Gyudia tɛɛlese man, na donɔɔse ŋan ŋe kyaa Gyɔɔdan boo keŋ yenaase nɛ kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ.

eng: As they confessed their sins, he baptized them in the Jordan River.
local: Bɛɛ kaa tɔlɛɛ be nombiakumɛɛ ŋan pou bɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ fa e, na ɔ te sɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ Gyɔɔdan boo keŋ man.

eng: Many Pharisees and Sadducees came to be baptized by John. He said to them, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Gyɔn kena balaŋ burum baŋ bɔɔ lee Farasiitena na Sadukiitena akpii ŋan man kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ nyi waa sɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ nɛ ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Kyoa, ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ dana nombiakumɛɛ ŋgba doma nɛ. Amɔte faree na ŋon nyi yaa yeu lee Wurubuarɛ deŋele kewɔŋ keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ balaŋ dɔɔ nɛ man.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives.
local: Ɛ yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan nawolo nyi ɛ te kyɛɛkee lee ɛ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

eng: And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Ɛ na yɛɛ ya agyueŋ nyi keŋ ɛ yɛɛ Aberaham ɔ naanasewɔ dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ lɛɛ ŋon ya. Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ waa tale yela boya adoŋ kɛŋa ka bese naanase fa Aberaham.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Ɛ kɛɛ, Wurubuarɛ taŋ desina ɔ debeŋa see waa tuŋii daase lɔ. Mena dɔɔ daŋ kamasɛ keŋ ke bɛɛ se bia kpaakpaa ya na, waa tuŋ ke lɔ na baa keretii ke do boalaŋ man.

eng: I baptize with water those of you who have changed your hearts and lives. The one who is coming after me is stronger than I am. Im not worthy to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
local: Keŋte Gyɔn besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, maŋ dɔɔ loŋ te me dana mɛɛ sɔ ŋon Wurubuarɛ loŋ na kawola nyi ɛ te kyɛɛkee lee ɛ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ. Mɔna maŋ wɔle na ŋon ɔ kela maŋ nɛ waa kɔŋ, keŋ maŋ tekaboe gba nyi maa tela ɔtenate nkyokotase gba ya. Ŋon dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ feliŋ na boalaŋ te waa mo sɔ ŋon Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: Ɔ dana ɔ pampaa ɔ nyiŋmaa man waa mo feefu wa amɔla na waa lese amɔla bia ŋan do ɔ kpee man, na waa kuu wura ŋan ka do boalaŋ keŋ ke bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man.

eng: At that time Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan River so that John would baptize him.
local: Gyɔn kɛɛ sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ keŋ nɛ, te Yesu leewɔ Galelia tɛɛle man kɔŋ Gyɔɔdan boo nɔɔman nyi Gyɔn waa sɔ e Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

eng: John tried to stop him and said, I need to be baptized by you, yet you come to me.
local: Mɔna Gyɔn gyaewɔ waa yɛɛ na Yesu ke kyɛɛkee wa agyueŋ. Keŋte ɔ bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, Nyaŋ te kaboena nyi nya sɔ maŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ. Weera te nyaŋ besewɔ kɔŋ me gyaŋ nyi maŋ maa sɔ neŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

eng: Jesus answered, Allow me to be baptized now. This is necessary to fulfill all righteousness. So John agreed to baptize Jesus.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa Gyɔn nyiaa, Debaŋ kei man nɛ, nyaŋ yɛɛ ke mena. De yɛɛ nnɛ na, Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii yaa dɛɛ yɛɛ baa. Keŋte Gyɔn mɔ sɛɛwɔ.

eng: When Jesus was baptized, he immediately came up out of the water. Heaven was opened to him, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down like a dove and resting on him.
local: Gyɔn ke sɔ Yesu Wurubuarɛ loŋ taŋ, te Yesu ka mo waa lee loŋ keŋ man pɛ, te adido torowɔ. Keŋte ɔ naa Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon moo duwuluŋ dinɔɔ tisi kaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ.

eng: A voice from heaven said, This is my Son whom I dearly love; I find happiness in him.
local: Botɔɔ te be nyii Wurubuarɛ kolosiwɔ lee adido yako nyiaa, Kei yɛna me bu ŋon mɛɛ gyae lee me konɔɔ man, te ɔ na gyoo me sia nideli.

eng: Boarding a boat, Jesus crossed to the other side of the lake and went to his own city.
local: Yesu gyoowɔ degbele man te ɔ toŋa boo keŋ gyu ŋon gbagba ɔ donɔɔ man.

eng: People brought to him a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the man who was paralyzed, Be encouraged, my child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baale seele yenaŋ dayeŋasate ŋolo ɔ dofoŋoŋ dɔɔ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ. Yesu kena mena keŋ balaŋ baŋ be lɛɛ e di nɛ, ɔ yako kaweete ŋon nyiaa, Me bu, nyiŋ konɔɔ. Me ta mo n nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ neŋ.

eng: Some legal experts said among themselves, This man is insulting God.
local: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ mmaraa wolala baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ ŋorokoteewɔ nyiaa, Balee kei ne kolosi mmusuo nombia.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking and said, Why do you fill your minds with evil things?.
local: Na Yesu gyeŋ kpene keŋ bɛɛ gyueŋ ba nyeeya man nɛ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te ɛ na gyueŋ nombiakumɛɛ ɛ nyeeya man?.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: Nombia kɛŋa man woŋti woɛla na keyako? Me ta mo n dukum kyɛɛ neŋ, yaa koro seŋ na n ke ta.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed—Get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: Mɔna mɛɛ gyae ɛ ka gyeŋ nyi, maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu dana doŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyi maa mo dukum kyɛɛ. Mena dɔɔ ɔ yako kaweete ŋon nyiaa, Koro seŋ, gate n dofoŋoŋ na n ke ta gyu n dɛɛ.

eng: The man got up and went home.
local: Keŋte balee ŋon korowɔ seŋ te ɔ laŋa ɔ dɛɛ.

eng: When the crowds saw what had happened, they were afraid and praised God, who had given such authority to human beings.
local: Balaŋ dikpii keŋ kena nombia kɛŋa nɛ, doo be nɔɔ te ba lese Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ba mo mena doŋ kei fa balaŋ nɛ yele.

eng: As Jesus continued on from there, he saw a man named Matthew sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. He said to him, Follow me, and he got up and followed him.
local: Yesu ka lee botɔɔ ɔ ne lam nɛ, ɔ naa balee ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Mateo nɛ, na ɔ kyaa ɔ lempoo dɛɛlɛe man. Te Yesu yako e nyiaa, Koro sila maŋ Keŋte Mateo korowɔ sila e.

eng: As Jesus sat down to eat in Matthews house, many tax collectors and sinners joined Jesus and his disciples at the table.
local: Wee daale Yesu ke kyaa Mateo dɛɛ ɔ ne dii nɛ, lempoolɛɛra na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra burum kɔŋawɔ kaa di bom na Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ.

eng: But when the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Farasiitena baŋ kena mena nɛ be bɔɔse Yesu kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te ɛ Gbeŋgyoo ŋon ne dii bom na lempoolɛɛra na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra.

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Yesu ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Kaweesetena te kaboena nyi baa kyɔ wɔ kawee. Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ na wee ya nɛ dɔɔ, bɔɔ ne hia kawee kekyɔ ya.

eng: Go and learn what this means: I want mercy and not sacrifice . I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Mɔna ɛ gyu na ɛ kaa gyueŋ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombii aŋmaraseŋ see kei na wolo nɛ man. Be ŋmarasewɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa, Na kedeesa te maŋ Wurubuarɛ ne gyae lee ɛ gyaŋ ya. Keŋ mɛɛ gyae yɛna nyi yaa wii dɔŋa waraŋase. Ma te kɔŋ nyi me kaa baake balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ wulaa tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ ya, mɔna me kɔŋae me kaa baake nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra.

eng: At that time Johns disciples came and asked Jesus, Why do we and the Pharisees frequently fast, but your disciples never fast.
local: Wee daale nɛ, Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon kaseela kɔŋawɔ kaa bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te daa na Farasiitena ne bake de nɔɔ, te n kaseela baŋ dɔɔ baŋ bɛɛ bake be nɔɔ ya.

eng: Jesus responded, The wedding guests cant mourn while the groom is still with them, can they? But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then theyll fast.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Sena te balaŋ baŋ be kyaa alowɔletɛɛ nɛ baa wii walaŋsa keŋ alowɔle baale kyaa be gyaŋ? Mɔna debaŋ daale gyae kekɔŋ keŋ baa mo alowɔle baale ŋon lee be gyaŋ. Mena debaŋ kenaŋ man na baa bake be nɔɔ.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes because the patch tears away the cloth and makes a worse tear.
local: Ŋolo bɛɛ kara kanyaŋ wɔle mo taa kakyeŋgbelaa keŋ ba yem nɛ ya. Akpaa ɔ yɛɛ mena na, kanyaŋ wɔle keŋ gyae ke wɔŋee bene keŋ tirii na bɔɔ keŋ ke wolose kpu.

eng: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the wineskins would burst, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined. Instead, people pour new wine into new wineskins so that both are kept safe.
local: Mena mɔ te ŋolo bɛɛ mo soloŋ wɔle do wonembu tɔne ogele bene man ya. Ɔ yɛɛ mena na, soloŋ wɔle keŋ gyae ke yela wonembu tɔne ogele keŋ ke bɛɛ, na soloŋ keŋ ke leki na wonembu tɔne ogele keŋ ke wɔlɛɛ. Mena dɔɔ kaboena bo nyi baa mo soloŋ wɔle do wonembu tɔne ogele wɔle man, na ŋe popou be nyiŋ wɔlɛɛ ya.

eng: While Jesus was speaking to them, a ruler came and knelt in front of him, saying, My daughter has just died. But come and place your hand on her, and shell live.
local: Yesu kɛɛ kolosi nombia kɛŋa fa wɔ nɛ, Gyudatena ɔsom kegyia ŋolo kaa buŋawɔ ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ sola e nyiaa, Me bu alebu ta yem debokeiman. Mɔna kɔŋ na n kaa mo n nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ, na waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.

eng: So Jesus and his disciples got up and went with him.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu korowɔ sila e, te ɔ kaseela baŋ mɔ sila e gyu.

eng: Then a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and touched the hem of his clothes.
local: Botɔɔ na te alo ŋolo ŋon fatabo ne gyɔ e kulutooneŋ kufu ala nɛ, moowɔ Yesu wɔle te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaa tina Yesu kegba nɔɔbii.

eng: She thought, If I only touch his robe Ill be healed.
local: Nawolo nyi alo ŋon yakowɔ ɔ nyeeman nyiaa, Akpaa me nyiŋ tina ɔ kegba koraŋ na, me kawee gyae ke taŋ.

eng: When Jesus turned and saw her, he said, Be encouraged, daughter. Your faith has healed you. And the woman was healed from that time on.
local: Keŋte Yesu gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ te ɔ naa alo ŋon te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Me bu yela n wɔe ke fɛɛ neŋ. N kelɛɛ maŋ di dɔɔ te n nyiŋa kawee kekyɔ. Debokenaŋ man te alo ŋon kawee taŋawɔ.

eng: When Jesus went into the rulers house, he saw the flute players and the distressed crowd.
local: Yesu ke gyu ka gyoo Gyudatena ɔsom kegyia ŋon dɛɛ, te ɔ kena baŋ bɛɛ wola kefereese na baŋ bee wii lia ŋan tɛɛ nɛ.

eng: He said, Go away, because the little girl isnt dead but is asleep; but they laughed at him.
local: ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ popou ɛ lee. Nawolo nyi alebu ŋon ta yeŋa ta ya, ɔ doo dosoroŋ bo. Keŋte balaŋ baŋ pou boŋo Yesu.

eng: After he had sent the crowd away, Jesus went in and touched her hand, and the little girl rose up.
local: Balaŋ baŋ ka lee debɔɔ man nɛ, Yesu gyuuwɔ deni keŋ man woo ŋon doo nɛ, te ɔ kyaŋa e ɔ nyiŋmaa man te alebu ŋon foŋawɔ.

eng: News about this spread throughout that whole region.
local: Mena nombia kɛŋa yaaseewɔ tɛɛle kenaŋ dɔɔ pou.

eng: As Jesus departed, two blind men followed him, crying out, Show us mercy, Son of David.
local: Yesu ka lee botɔɔ ɔ ne gyu nɛ, siayɛlɛɛsatena bala baale sila e na bɛɛ fae yeesa baake nyiaa. Gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii, wii de waraŋase.

eng: When he came into the house, the blind men approached him. Jesus said to them, Do you believe I can do this? Yes, Lord, they replied.
local: Yesu ke gyu ka gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ man nɛ, siayɛlɛɛsatena baŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ ne lɛɛ maŋ di nyi maa tale gyuusu ɛ sia? Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa e nyi, Ayi de Gbeŋgyoo.

eng: Then Jesus touched their eyes and said, It will happen for you just as you have believed.
local: Keŋte Yesu moo ɔ nyiŋmaa tina be sia te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Mena keŋ ɛ be lɛɛ di nɛ, dɛɛkɔŋ mena fa ŋon. Keŋte be sia gyuusuwɔ.

eng: Their eyes were opened. Then Jesus sternly warned them, Make sure nobody knows about this.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu faree wɔ nideli nyi, Ɛ na yele ŋolo na ŋolo ke nyii nombia kɛŋa ya.

eng: But they went out and spread the word about him throughout that whole region.
local: Mɔna baŋ moo ɔ wose man nombia kpeŋkpeleŋ fa balaŋ tɛɛle kenaŋ pou dɔɔ.

eng: As they were leaving, people brought to him a man who was demon-possessed and unable to speak.
local: Be ka lee botɔɔ bee gyu nɛ, ba moo walaŋ ŋon feliŋkum doo ɔ man te ɔ bɛɛ tale kolosi ya nɛ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ.

eng: When Jesus had thrown out the demon, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed and said, Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel.
local: Yesu ke gegi feliŋkum keŋ lee balee ŋon man nɛ, te ɔ kolosiwɔ. Balaŋ dikpii keŋ kena mena nɛ, nombia ŋan doo be nɔɔ te be yakowɔ nyiaa, Da te naa ta mena nombia kɛŋa see Iserae tɛɛle kei dɔɔ ya.

eng: But the Pharisees said, He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Mɔna Farasiitena baŋ yakowɔ nyiaa, Feliŋkumɛɛ pou gyoo ŋon doŋ yaa ɔ na mo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ nɛ.

eng: Jesus traveled among all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, announcing the good news of the kingdom, and healing every disease and every sickness.
local: Keŋte Yesu tɛɛwɔ kila donɔɔse na akuraase ŋan pou man, na ɔ na wola wɔ Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man. Ɔ ne kolosi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ nombia kpaakpaa fa wɔ, te ɔ kyɔɔ kaweese kpookpoo kamasɛ mɔ.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he had compassion for them because they were troubled and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
local: Ɔ kena balaŋ dikpii keŋ nɛ, be nombia yɛɛ e waraŋase. Nawolo nyi bɛɛ yeyɛɛ te bɔɔ dana walaŋ ŋon waa lɛɛ wɔ ya. Be nombia yɛɛ ŋgba namense baŋ bɔɔ dana gbeŋgyoo ya nɛ.

eng: Then he said to his disciples, The size of the harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Wɔɔman abɔɔ ŋan boe na kekɛrɛɛ, mɔna tomyɛɛra baŋ ta boo ya.

eng: Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ fane fa wɔɔte ŋon, na waa lese balaŋ ke kɔŋ ɔ wɔɔ keŋ man kaa yɛɛ tom.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John his brother, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain.
local: Weeya looro wɔle nɛ, Yesu moo Pita na Gyemisi na ŋon Gyemisi ɔ naabu Gyɔn kpu na ɔ wose te be gyuuwɔ bula dinaa daale dɔɔ baageŋ.

eng: He was transformed in front of them. His face shone like the sun, and his clothes became as white as light.
local: Be ka seŋ botɔɔ nɛ, karatetee dokoloŋ te Yesu wose kyɛɛkeewɔ be siaman. Na ɔ sia gbɛɛ ne ŋalakee ŋgba weese nɛ, te wa abɔɔ ŋan ɔ doona nɛ bese abɔɔ wolomɛɛ futututu na ŋɛɛ ŋalakee polɛpolɛpolɛ.

eng: Moses and Elijah appeared to them, talking with Jesus.
local: Debokenaŋ man te Mosesi na Elia kaa bɛɛwɔ lee be dɔɔ, te be kolosiwɔ na Yesu.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Lord, its good that were here. If you want, Ill make three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Keŋte Pita yako Yesu nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, dei ŋgba daa mɔ be kɔŋ kɛbo nɛ. Nyi akpaa n ne gyae na, yela dɛɛ baŋ kefɛɛse atooro kɛbo. N wui daale, Mosesi wui daale te Elia mɔ wui daale.

eng: While he was still speaking, look, a bright cloud overshadowed them. A voice from the cloud said, This is my Son whom I dearly love. I am very pleased with him. Listen to him.
local: Pita ke gyakaa ɔ ne kolosi nɛ, te ŋɔma ŋɛlɛɛŋɛlɛɛ ŋaale kaa wuuwɔ be dɔɔ. Keŋte be nyii nɔɔwoya ŋaale lee ŋɔma ŋan man nyiaa, Kei yɛna me bu ŋon mɛɛ gyae e lee me konɔɔ man te ɔ na gyoo me sia, ɛ nyii e.

eng: Hearing this, the disciples fell on their faces, filled with awe.
local: Ɔ kaseela baŋ ke nyii nɔɔwoya kɛŋa nɛ, gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ te be tisiwɔ buŋii tɛɛle man.

eng: But Jesus came and touched them. Get up, he said. Dont be afraid.
local: Keŋte Yesu kaa mo ɔ nyiŋmaa tina wɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ koro, ɛ na yee ya.

eng: When they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus.
local: Be ka koro gyina be sia nɛ, ba te naa ŋolo kpu na Yesu ya.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus commanded them, Dont tell anybody about the vision until the Human One is raised from the dead.
local: Be ke tisi lee bula keŋ dɔɔ bɛɛ kɔŋ nɛ Yesu tɔɔ wɔ kiiŋ nyiaa, Ɛ na yako walaŋ na walaŋ kpene keŋ ɛ be naa nɛ ya, kelii debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon lee yeŋ man.

eng: The disciples asked, Then why do the legal experts say that Elijah must first come.
local: Keŋte ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse e nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ ne yako nyi, kaboena nyi Elia waa taŋgbɛɛ kɔŋ pɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ ke kɔŋ.

eng: Jesus responded, Elijah does come first and will restore all things.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ke doo mena nyi Elia waa taŋgbɛɛ nyeelɛɛre ŋon kɔŋ na ɔ kaa desina kpene kamasɛ see ŋgba ŋa kedoobii nɛ.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has already come, and they didnt know him. But they did to him whatever they wanted. In the same way the Human One is also going to suffer at their hands.
local: Mɔna mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyi Elia taŋ wulaa kɔŋ laŋ mɔna balaŋ ta gyeŋ e ya, te be yɛɛ e kpene kamasɛ keŋ bɛɛ gyae. Mena mɔ te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa naa diyem be nyiŋmaa man.

eng: Then the disciples realized he was telling them about John the Baptist.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ gyeŋawɔ nyiaa, Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon wose man nombia yaa ɔ ne yako nɛ.

eng: When they came to the crowd, a man met Jesus. He knelt before him.
local: Ba ke kɔŋ kaa gyoo balaŋ burum baŋ man nɛ, balee ŋolo kaa tisiwɔ buŋ Yesu nawɔɔtɛɛ.

eng: saying, Lord, show mercy to my son. He is epileptic and suffers terribly, for he often falls into the fire or the water.
local: te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, wii me bu kei waraŋase fa maŋ. Ɔ na wee keleuleuse, te ɔ ne naa diyem nideli mɔ. Ne kyaa kuu e lɔ boalaŋ yaa loŋ man.

eng: I brought him to your disciples, but they couldnt heal him.
local: Ma moo e kɔŋ n kaseela baŋ gyaŋ, mɔna ba te tale kyɔ e ya.

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring the boy here to me.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ao! Ɛmɛɛ gyɛŋ balaŋ baŋ ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ di ya, te ɛ yɛɛ balaŋ kumɛɛwɔ. Weeya aŋmoŋ te kaboena nyi maa kyaa ɛ gyaŋ, na maa nyiŋ konɔɔ fa ŋon? Keŋte ɔ yako balee ŋon nyiaa, Mo n bu ŋon kɔna maŋ.

eng: Then Jesus spoke harshly to the demon. And it came out of the child, who was healed from that time on.
local: Keŋte Yesu fae feliŋkum keŋ te ka leewɔ bu ŋon man, te ɔ kawee taŋawɔ debokenaŋ man.

eng: Then the disciples came to Jesus in private and said, Why couldnt we throw the demon out.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu kaseela baŋ gyuuwɔ ɔ gyaŋ, te be baake e lee yenaŋ bɔɔse e nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te daa te tale gegi feliŋkum keŋ lee bu ŋon man ya.

eng: Because you have little faith, he said. I assure you that if you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mountain, Go from here to there, and it will go. There will be nothing that you cant do.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Nawolo nyi ɛ kelɛɛdi ta boo ya. Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, nyi akpaa ɛ kelɛɛdi yɛɛ peperee ŋgba kefaabii koraŋ nɛ, nafɔ ɛ ke tale yako bula kei nyiaa, Kulu lee kɛbo ke yala nnɛ te ke gyae ke kɔŋ mena. Te kolo be kyaa keŋ nyi ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale yɛɛ ya.

eng: When the disciples came together in Galilee, Jesus said to them, The Human One is about to be delivered over into human hands.
local: Debaŋ keŋ be popou be kɔŋ kaa gyaŋee Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ, Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Baa lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon fa balaŋ.

eng: They will kill him. But he will be raised on the third day. And they were heartbroken.
local: na baa ko maŋ, mɔna weeya atoorote keŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu maŋ lee yeŋ man. Botɔɔ te dekɔkɔrɔkɔɔ kyaŋa ɔ kaseela baŋ.

eng: When they came to Capernaum, the people who collected the half-shekel temple tax came to Peter and said, Doesnt your teacher pay the temple tax.
local: Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ ke kɔŋ kaa gyoo Kapanum donɔɔ man nɛ, balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ lɛɛ lempoo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ, kɔŋawɔ kaa bɔɔse Pita nyiaa, Ɛ gbeŋgyoo ŋon bɛɛ tɔ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa lempoo keŋ yee.

eng: Yes, he said. But when they came into the house, Jesus spoke to Peter first.What do you think, Simon? From whom do earthly kings collect taxes, from their children or from strangers.
local: Keŋte Pita tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, ɔ ne tɔɔ. Pita ke kɔŋ kaa gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ man pɛ nɛ, Yesu taŋgbɛɛ la kolosi na e te ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, Simɔn, sena yɛna nya agyueŋ? Balaŋ bomɔ gyaŋ te tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyooneŋ ne lɛɛ lempoo lee? Donɔɔsetena gyaŋ yaa ŋɔɔla gyaŋ.

eng: From strangers, he said. Jesus said to him, Then the children dont have to pay.
local: Te Pita tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Ŋɔɔla gyaŋ te bɛɛ lɛɛ. Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, Akpaa mena na donɔɔsetena te nyiŋ ba wose.

eng: But just so we dont offend them, go to the lake, throw out a fishing line and hook, and take the first fish you catch. When you open its mouth, you will find a shekel coin. Take it and pay the tax for both of us.
local: Mɔna keŋ dɔɔ ne gyae nyi daa na wɔ ke yala dɔŋa dɔɔ ya nɛ, mo n darawaa na n ke gyu boo man ke lɔ. Kpebii gyaŋgbate ŋon n ke kyaŋ nɛ, n wola ɔ nɔɔ na n kena kɔba keŋ gyae ka yee daa na neŋ wui lempoo keŋ ketɔ. Lese ke na n ka mo gyina wɔ.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he left Galilee and came to the area of Judea on the east side of the Jordan.
local: Yesu kekolosi nombia kɛŋa pou taŋ nɛ, ɔ leewɔ Galelia tɛɛle man gyu Gyudia tɛɛle man, Gyɔɔdan boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ.

eng: Large crowds followed him, and he healed them.
local: Balaŋ burum sila e gyu botɔɔ te ɔ kyɔɔ wɔ be kaweese pou.

eng: Some Pharisees came to him. In order to test him, they said, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife for just any reason.
local: Keŋte Farasiitena baale kɔŋawɔ be kaa teese ɔ nɔɔman te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, Da mmaraa ne fa daa gbɛɛ nyi ŋolo waa tale bɛɛ ɔ wɛɛle lee nombii kamasɛ dɔɔ.

eng: Jesus answered, Havent you read that at the beginning the creator made them male and female?.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ te kala ta Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan nyi, lee kewalaŋ keŋ na, Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ alo na baale bo.

eng: And God said, Because of this a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife, and the two will be one flesh .
local: te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Lee kei dɔɔ nɛ baale waa tina ɔ kya na ɔ naa yela, na waa mo ɔ wose mataa na ɔ wɛɛle, na be balaŋ bala ka bese walaŋ dokoloŋ yee.

eng: So they are no longer two but one flesh. Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Mena dɔɔ be ta tekaa yɛɛ balaŋ bala bela ya, mɔna be ta bese walaŋ dokoloŋ. Mena dɔɔ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo bom yɛɛ ke dokoloŋ nɛ, ŋolo na ŋolo na kpase ke man ya.

eng: The Pharisees said to him, Then why did Moses command us to give a divorce certificate and divorce her? .
local: Keŋte Farasiitena baŋ bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, Nyi akpaa mena te doo na, weera dɔɔ te de naana Mosesi yɛɛ mmaraa see nyi, baale waa tale ŋmarase yale kebɛɛ tɔne fa ɔ wɛɛle na waa bɛɛ e.

eng: Jesus replied, Moses allowed you to divorce your wives because your hearts are unyielding. But it wasnt that way from the beginning.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ konɔɔyuŋ dɔɔ te de naana Mosesi fa ŋon mena gbɛɛ kenaŋ. Mɔna lee kewalaŋ keŋ na, be doo mena ya.

eng: I say to you that whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual unfaithfulness, and marries another woman commits adultery.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyiaa, nyi akpaa na alo lee ɔ baale wɔle bo ya, te ɔ baale de bɛɛ e gyu ke yala alo wɔle nɛ, Wurubuarɛ siaman na ɔtenate gyae ŋolo wɛɛle baa.

eng: His disciples said to him, If thats the way things are between a man and his wife, then its better not to marry.
local: Keŋte Yesu kaseela baŋ yako e nyiaa, Nyi akpaa nnɛ te yale wose man nombia doo na, kenaŋ na walaŋ te yale koraŋ ya na dei.

eng: He replied, Not everybody can accept this teaching, but only those who have received the ability to accept it.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Nombia ŋan mɛɛ yako ŋon nɛ, na walaŋ kamasɛ ne gyae la waa tale lɛɛ ŋa do ya, gyisɛ balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo mena bɛrɛɛ kenaŋ boele wɔ nɛ baageŋ.

eng: For there are eunuchs who have been eunuchs from birth. And there are eunuchs who have been made eunuchs by other people. And there are eunuchs who have made themselves eunuchs because of the kingdom of heaven. Those who can accept it should accept it.
local: Balaŋ baale kyaa keŋ nyi bɔɔ gyae baa tale yala ala ya, nawolo nyi mena te ba lola wɔ. Balaŋ baale mɔ dɔɔ, balaŋ yɛɛ na wɔ keŋ nyi bɔɔ gyae baa tale yala ala ya. Balaŋ baale mɔ dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ dɔɔ te baŋ gbagba bɛɛwɔ nyi bɔɔ yale ya. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa tale lɛɛ nombia kɛŋa do na, waa lɛɛ ŋa do.

eng: Some people brought children to Jesus so that he would place his hands on them and pray. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baale moo bia kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ nyi waa mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa be dɔɔ na waa fane fa wɔ. Mɔna ɔ kaseela baŋ kena mena nɛ, be ŋmanyaŋ balaŋ baŋ.

eng: Allow the children to come to me, Jesus said. Dont forbid them, because the kingdom of heaven belongs to people like these children.
local: Yesu kena mena nɛ ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ yela bia baŋ ke kɔŋ me gyaŋ. Ɛ na tɔ wɔ gbɛɛ ya, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɔkɔse ba wose ŋgba bia kɛwɔ dinɔɔ nɛ wui.

eng: Then he blessed the children and went away from there.
local: Yesu ka mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa bia baŋ dɔɔ taŋ nɛ, te ɔ leewɔ botɔɔ laŋ.

eng: A man approached him and said, Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life.
local: Wee daale nɛ tobaale ŋolo kaa bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, woŋ deeli yaa maa yɛɛ na maa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ.

eng: Jesus said, Why do you ask me about what is good? Theres only one who is good. If you want to enter eternal life, keep the commandments.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, Weera te n ne bɔɔse maŋ lee deeli keyɛɛ wose man. Wurubuarɛ waageŋ dei la. Mena dɔɔ nyi akpaa n ne gyae n ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ nɛ, kenaŋ na kaboena nyi nya di Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ.

eng: The man said, Which ones? Then Jesus said, Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony .
local: Botɔɔ te tobaale ŋon bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, Mmaraase ŋemɔ? Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Na ko walaŋ ya, na lee n wɛɛle yaa n baale wɔle ya, na ŋmɛɛle ya, na kara n dɔɔ nɔɔ ya.

eng: Honor your father and mother, and love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: Bu n kya na n naa, na n ke gyae n tebia ŋgba mena keŋ n ne gyae nyaŋ gbagba n wose nɛ.

eng: The young man replied, Ive kept all these. What am I still missing.
local: Keŋte tobaale ŋon yakowɔ nyiaa, Me wulaa mee di mmaraase kɛŋa pou dɔɔ. Woŋ kaa la nyi maa yɛɛ bela.

eng: Jesus said, If you want to be complete, go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come follow me.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, Nyi akpaa n ne gyae n ke de kelii ɔto na, gyu ke yɔɔ nya abɔɔ ŋan pou n dana nɛ, na n ka mo kɔba keŋ kpɛlɛɛ ayematena na n ke kɔŋ kaa sila maŋ. N yɛɛ mena na, n ke nyiŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa see n wose Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ adido.

eng: But when the young man heard this, he went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Tobaale ŋon ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ɔ moo waraŋase lee botɔɔ nawolo nyi ɔ dana kɔba na nsendee ya.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, I assure you that it will be very hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, gyae ke yɛɛ doŋ fa baŋ be dana kɔba nɛ nyi baa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.

eng: In fact, its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Mɛɛ yako ŋon bela nyiaa, gyae ke yɛɛ doŋ fa baŋ be dana kɔba nɛ nyi baa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man, kela nyi kurumɔɔ ka gyoo ŋarale bɔɔ man lee.

eng: When his disciples heard this, they were stunned. Then who can be saved? they asked.
local: Yesu kaseela baŋ ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ doo be nɔɔ te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, Nyi nnɛ te doo na, amɔte ne gyae la waa nyiŋ nyeekelɛɛ baa.

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible for human beings. But all things are possible for God.
local: Keŋte Yesu kɛɛ wɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Denibalaŋ gyaŋ na ŋolo be tale yɛɛ nombii kei ya, mɔna kpene kamasɛ be kyaa keŋ Wurubuarɛ be tale yɛɛ ya.

eng: Then Peter replied, Look, weve left everything and followed you. What will we have.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo! Daa dɔɔ de te tina kpene kamasɛ keŋ de dana nɛ yela, te de dɔŋɛɛ de silana neŋ. Woŋ yaa dee nyiŋ baa.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you who have followed me that, when everything is made new, when the Human One sits on his magnificent throne, you also will sit on twelve thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, debaŋ keŋ kpene kamasɛ de kaa bese wɔle te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon de kyaa me gyooro gbeli keŋ dɔɔ me gyoorobiiri man nɛ, ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ be sila maŋ nɛ ɛ ke kyaa gyooro gbelɛɛ kufu ala dɔɔ, na ɛ ke di Iseraetena akpaŋalaŋse kufu ala ŋan balaŋ nombia.

eng: And all who have left houses, brothers, sisters, father, mother, children, or farms because of my name will receive one hundred times more and will inherit eternal life.
local: Te ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ be tina ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ yaa ɔ naabia yaa ɔ deesina, yaa ɔ kya yaa ɔ naa yaa ɔ bia yaa ɔ tɛɛlese yela lee maŋ dɔɔ nɛ, waa bese nyiŋ ŋa akpeŋ sɔŋonoŋ kpu, na waa bese nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ mɔ.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Mɔna balaŋ burum baŋ be yɛɛ siamantenawɔ nɛ, baa kaa bese wɔle nyibaŋ, na balaŋ burum baŋ be yɛɛ wɔle nyibaŋwɔ nɛ kaa bese siamantena.

eng: At that time the disciples came to Jesus and asked, Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ man nɛ, Yesu kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa, Amɔte yɛna walaŋ kegyia Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.

eng: Then he called a little child over to sit among the disciples.
local: Keŋte Yesu baake biikyaabii ŋolo kaa seŋ be siaman.

eng: and said, I assure you that if you dont turn your lives around and become like this little child, you will definitely not enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, akpaa ɛ te kyɛɛkee ɛ dinɔɔ na ɛ ke yɔkɔse ɛ wose yɛɛ ŋgba bia ya nɛ, ɛ be gyae ɛ ka gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man ya.

eng: Those who humble themselves like this little child will be the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Mena dɔɔ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ yɔkɔse ɔ wose ŋgba biikyaabii kei nɛ, ŋon yɛna walaŋ kegyia Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.

eng: Whoever welcomes one such child in my name welcomes me.
local: Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ biikyaabii kei dinɔɔ ma yele man na, maŋ yaa ɔ lɛɛwɔ.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and be drowned in the bottom of the lake.
local: Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ yeli bia buruburu kɛwɔ baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ maŋ di nɛ man ŋolo de yɛɛ dukum nɛ, kenaŋ na nyi akpaa ba mo boe dinaa dinaa kpaŋ ɔ wɔŋe man, te be fuŋ e do ɛpo man na dei kela.

eng: How terrible it is for the world because of the things that cause people to trip and fall into sin! Such things have to happen, but how terrible it is for the person who causes those things to happen!.
local: Ɛmɛɛ balaŋ baŋ ɛ kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ ɛ laako lee abɔɔ ŋan ne yeli balaŋ ne yɛɛ dukum nɛ dɔɔ. Kaboena nyi mena abɔɔ kɛŋa dɛɛ kɔŋ, mɔna nyaŋ walaŋ ŋon ŋe gyae ke ta n dɔɔ kɔŋ nɛ nyaa laako.

eng: If your hand or your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better to enter into life crippled or lame than to be thrown into the eternal fire with two hands or two feet.
local: Nyi akpaa n nyiŋmaa yaa n nae ne yele la te n ne yɛɛ dukum na kara ke leki. Nawolo nyi, dei nyi nya lee kadeŋ na n ka gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man, kela nyi baa mo neŋ nyiŋmaase ala yaa nawɔɔ ala ka do boalaŋ keŋ ke bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man.

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better to enter into life with one eye than to be cast into a burning hell with two eyes.
local: Te akpaa nyi n sibii ne yele la te n ne yɛɛ dukum na kulu ke leki. Dei nyi nya mo sibii dokoloŋ gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man, kela nyi baa mo neŋ na n sia ala ka do boalaŋ keŋ ke bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man.

eng: Be careful that you dont look down on one of these little ones. I say to you that their angels in heaven are always looking into the face of my Father who is in heaven.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, ɛ kɛɛ nideli nyi ɛ be kɛɛ balaŋ kɛwɔ baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ maŋ di te bɔɔ yɔkɔse ba wose ŋgba bia nɛ nyi be man ŋolo yɛɛ walaŋ yakaate e ya. Mɛɛ yako ŋon baa ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, mena balaŋ kɛwɔ dana Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ ba seŋɛɛ me kya Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ siaman, bɛɛ fane fa wɔ debaŋ kamasɛ.

eng: What do you think? If someone had one hundred sheep and one of them wandered off, wouldnt he leave the ninety-nine on the hillsides and go in search for the one that wandered off?.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, sena te ɛ na gyueŋ lee nombia kɛŋa man? Akpaa ŋolo dana namense sɔŋonoŋ te be man dokoloŋ da yoo na woŋ te ɔ ne yɛɛ? Ɔ be gyae waa tina sɔŋonaara na kufu kadaale baŋ yela bula dɔɔ botɔɔ, na waa ta kɛo ŋon ɔ ba yoo nɛ kaboena waa naa e yee?.

eng: If he finds it, I assure you that he is happier about having that one sheep than about the ninety-nine who didnt wander off.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi akpaa ɔ naa e na, ɔ sia na gye ɔ dɔɔ nideli kela sɔŋonaara na kufu kadaale baŋ be ta yoo ya nɛ.

eng: In the same way, my Father who is in heaven doesnt want to lose one of these little ones.
local: Mena mɔ te ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ, bɛɛ gyae nyi balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ maŋ di nɛ, be man ŋolo dɛɛ yo ya.

eng: If your brother or sister sins against you, go and correct them when you are alone together. If they listen to you, then youve won over your brother or sister.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, akpaa n dɔɔ Kristo baale de yɛɛ neŋ nombiikum na, gyu ɔ gyaŋ na n kaa bɔɔse e. Mɔna yela ka ke yɛɛ ɛmɛɛ na e ɛ bala nombia. Akpaa ɔ nyii neŋ na, n te tale baa besena e kɔŋ gbɛɛ kpaakpaate dɔɔ.

eng: But if they wont listen, take with you one or two others so that every word may be established by the mouth of two or three witnesses .
local: Mɔna nyi akpaa ɔ te nyii neŋ ya na, mo Kristo baale dokoloŋ yaa balaŋ bala kpu na n wose na ɛ ke gyu ɔ gyaŋ. Kei gyae ke yela n ke nyiŋ adansɛɛtena bala yaa batooro nombia ŋenaŋ man, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia ne yako nɛ.

eng: But if they still wont pay attention, report it to the church. If they wont pay attention even to the church, treat them as you would a Gentile and tax collector.
local: Akpaa ɔ bɛɛ ɔ te nyii ŋon ya nɛ, kenaŋ na baake e n tebia Kristotena dikpii keŋ gyaŋ. Te akpaa ɔ bɛɛ ɔ te nyii baŋ mɔ ya nɛ, kenaŋ na kɛɛ e nyi ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ ya, yaa ɔ yɛɛ dukumyɛɛre e.

eng: I assure you that whatever you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. And whatever you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ gyɔ ke dowoe tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, na ba te gyɔ ke dowoe adido, te kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ booli ke tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, na ba te booli ke adido mɔ.

eng: Again I assure you that if two of you agree on earth about anything you ask, then my Father who is in heaven will do it for you.
local: Te mɛɛ yako ŋon baa bela nyiaa, kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ man balaŋ bala de yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ seŋ ke dɔɔ fane tɛɛle kei dɔɔ na, me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ waa yɛɛ ke fa ŋon.

eng: For where two or three are gathered in my name, Im there with them.
local: Nawolo nyi botɔɔ keŋ balaŋ bala yaa balaŋ batooro de gyaŋee ma yele man na, me kpuɛ na wɔ.

eng: Then Peter said to Jesus, Lord, how many times should I forgive my brother or sister who sins against me? Should I forgive as many as seven times.
local: Keŋte Pita kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, betaboŋmon te me dɔɔ de yɛɛ nombiikum me dɔɔ na te maa mo kyɛɛ e? Maa mo kyɛɛ e betabonyetooro.

eng: Jesus said, Not just seven times, but rather as many as seventy-seven times.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Koa, na betabonyetooro ya, mɔna betabosɔŋotooro na kufu akpeŋ nyetooro .

eng: Therefore, the kingdom of heaven is like a king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.
local: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Nnɛ te Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ doo. Ke yɛɛ ŋgba gyoo ŋon ɔ ne gyae nyi ŋon na ɔ tewulɛɛ ke kyaa, na baa kɛɛ koma ŋan be dana e nɛ.

eng: When he began to settle accounts, they brought to him a servant who owed him ten thousand bags of gold.
local: Be ka beta fiasɛɛ bɛɛ gyoo ŋe man nɛ, te ba moo tobaale ŋon ɔ dana e kom kɔba na nsendee ya nɛ kɔna e.

eng: Because the servant didnt have enough to pay it back, the master ordered that he should be sold, along with his wife and children and everything he had, and that the proceeds should be used as payment.
local: Keŋ tobaale ŋon te nyiŋ kɔba keŋ waa tɔ ya dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon yako wɔ nyi baa mo ŋon na ɔ wɛɛle na ɔ bia na wa abɔɔ ŋan pou ɔ dana nɛ ke yɔɔ, na baa mo kaa tɔ kom keŋ.

eng: But the servant fell down, kneeled before him, and said, Please, be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Keŋte ɔ tobaale ŋon tisiwɔ buŋ ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ waase e nyiaa, Nyiŋ konɔɔ fa maŋ, maa tɔ neŋ kom keŋ pou.

eng: The master had compassion on that servant, released him, and forgave the loan.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ nombia yɛɛ ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon waraŋase, te ɔ moo kom keŋ pou kyɛɛ e te ɔ tina e yela nyi waa ta.

eng: When that servant went out, he found one of his fellow servants who owed him one hundred coins. He grabbed him around the throat and said, Pay me back what you owe me.
local: Mɔna mena tobaale ŋonaŋ ka lee botɔɔ nɛ, ɔ ke gyaŋee na ɔ dɔɔ ŋolo ŋon ɔ dana e kom bii daale nɛ. Botɔɔ te tobaale ŋon lɔɔwɔ kyaŋ ɔ dɔɔ ŋon wɔŋe man te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Tɔ maŋ ma kom keŋ pou n dana maŋ nɛ.

eng: Then his fellow servant fell down and begged him, Be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Keŋte ɔ dɔɔ ŋon mɔ tisiwɔ buŋ ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ waase e nyiaa, Me dɔɔ nyiŋ konɔɔ fa maŋ maa tɔ neŋ n kom keŋ pou.

eng: But he refused. Instead, he threw him into prison until he paid back his debt.
local: Mɔna tobaale ŋon bɛe, te ɔ yela ba moo ɔ dɔɔ ŋon ketɔ deni nyi waa doo botɔɔ, kelii debaŋ keŋ waa tɔ e ɔ kom keŋ pou.

eng: When his fellow servants saw what happened, they were deeply offended. They came and told their master all that happened.
local: Tewulɛɛ akaŋ baŋ kena mena nɛ takaa wɔ nideli, keŋte be gyuuwɔ ke tɔlɛɛ nombia ŋan pou fa be gbeŋgyoo ŋon.

eng: His master called the first servant and said, You wicked servant! I forgave you all that debt because you appealed to me.
local: Botɔɔ te be gbeŋgyoo ŋon baake tobaale ŋon te ɔ yako e nyiaa, N yɛɛ walaŋkum ŋolo e. Nyaŋ waase maŋ te ma moo n kom keŋ n dana maŋ nɛ pou kyɛɛ neŋ.

eng: Shouldnt you also have mercy on your fellow servant, just as I had mercy on you.
local: Woŋ dɔɔ te nyaŋ mɔ n te wii n dɔɔ ŋon waraŋase, ŋgba mena keŋ maŋ be wii neŋ nɛ ya.

eng: His master was furious and handed him over to the guard responsible for punishing prisoners, until he had paid the whole debt.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon gyeŋa baŋ, te ɔ yela ba moo tobaale ŋon ketɔ deni na waa naa diyem kelii debaŋ keŋ, ŋon mɔ waa tɔ ɔ koma ŋan ɔ dana e nɛ pou.

eng: My heavenly Father will also do the same to you if you dont forgive your brother or sister from your heart.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ta mo ɛ tebia nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ lee ɛ konɔɔse man ya nɛ, mena te me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ waa yɛɛ ŋon.

eng: At that time the kingdom of heaven will be like ten young bridesmaids who took their lamps and went out to meet the groom.
local: Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu fa wɔ nyiaa, Weeya ŋenaŋ man na Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ gyae ke yɛɛ ŋgba, alebia kufu baŋ bɔɔ koro mo be kanease be kaa gyaŋee na alowɔle baale nɛ.

eng: Now five of them were wise, and the other five were foolish.
local: Be man banoŋ yɛɛ ayiŋtenawɔ, te banoŋ mɔ yɛɛ siakaralatenawɔ.

eng: The foolish ones took their lamps but didnt bring oil for them.
local: Ayiŋtena baŋ ka mo be kanease na, be ta gyem na baa mo nuŋse tela kpu na ŋan doo be kanease man nɛ ya.

eng: But the wise ones took their lamps and also brought containers of oil.
local: Mɔna siakaralatena baŋ dɔɔ, baŋ moo nuŋ do porontiase man tela kpu na ŋan doo be kanease man nɛ.

eng: When the groom was late in coming, they all became drowsy and went to sleep.
local: Alowɔle baale ŋon te kɔŋ bileŋ ya, mena dɔɔ alebia baŋ pou moowɔ bee deŋeli te be doo dosoroŋ.

eng: But at midnight there was a cry, Look, the groom! Come out to meet him.
local: Ke lii tɛɛ nsana nɛ te akelelee yalawɔ nyiaa, Alowɔle baale ŋon te kɔŋ! Ɛ lee kaa gyaŋee na e.

eng: Then all those bridesmaids got up and prepared their lamps.
local: Keŋte alebia kufu baŋ pou korowɔ te be gyinaa be kanea ŋmeeseŋ ŋan.

eng: But the foolish bridesmaids said to the wise ones, Give us some of your oil, because our lamps have gone out.
local: Botɔɔ te ayiŋtena baŋ yako siakaralatena baŋ nyiaa, De kanease na yekee, mena dɔɔ ɛ fa daa ɛ nuŋse ŋan kyomii.

eng: But the wise bridesmaids replied, No, because if we share with you, there wont be enough for our lamps and yours. We have a better idea. You go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.
local: Keŋte siakaralatena baŋ yako wɔ nyiaa, Koa de nuŋse ŋan be gyae ka yee dɛɛ fa ŋon ya. Mena dɔɔ ɛ gyu donɔɔ man na ɛ kaa gyae ŋaale lɛɛ.

eng: But while they were gone to buy oil, the groom came. Those who were ready went with him into the wedding. Then the door was shut.
local: Mɔna debaŋ keŋ ayiŋtena baŋ be lam be kaa lɛɛ nuŋse ŋan nɛ te alowɔle baale ŋon kaa gyoowɔ. Alebia banoŋ baŋ bɔɔ desina ba wose see nɛ, gyoowɔ alowɔle nɔɔkedi deni keŋ man, te be kyaŋa disim keŋ tɔ.

eng: Later the other bridesmaids came and said, Lord, lord, open the door for us.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle te alebia akaŋ baŋ mɔ kɔŋawɔ kaa gyoo. Keŋte be fae yeesa baake nyi, De Gbeŋgyoo toro daa.

eng: But he replied, I tell you the truth, I dont know you.
local: Keŋte alowɔle baale ŋon yako wɔ nyiaa, Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa mɔɔ gyeŋ ŋon ya.

eng: Therefore, keep alert, because you dont know the day or the hour.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ doo ɛ wose dɔɔ nideli, nawolo nyi ɛ ba gyeŋ wee yaa debaŋ keŋ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa bese kɔŋ ya. .

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a man who was leaving on a trip. He called his servants and handed his possessions over to them.
local: Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu fa wɔ nyiaa, Debaŋ kenaŋ man na Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ gyae ke yɛɛ ŋgba balee ŋon ɔ ne gyae waa gyu gbɛɛ, te ɔ be baake ɔ tewulɛɛ te ɔ be kpɛlɛɛ wa abɔɔ do wɔ be nyiŋmaa man nɛ.

eng: To one he gave five valuable coins, and to another he gave two, and to another he gave one. He gave to each servant according to that servants ability. Then he left on his journey.
local: Ɔ faa walaŋ kamasɛ ŋgba mena keŋ waa tale tela nɛ. Ɔ faa ŋolo kɔba kakpoŋse anoŋ te ɔ faa ŋolo kakpoŋse ala te ɔ faa ŋolo mɔ kakpoŋ dokoloŋ . Keŋte ɔ korowɔ laŋ ɔ gbɛɛ keŋ.

eng: After the man left, the servant who had five valuable coins took them and went to work doing business with them. He gained five more.
local: Tobaale ŋon ɔ be fa e kakpoŋse anoŋ nɛ, korowɔ debokenaŋ man te ɔ moo kɔba keŋ ke yɛɛ tom te ɔ nyiŋa kakpoŋse anoŋ kpu bela.

eng: In the same way, the one who had two valuable coins gained two more.
local: Mena mɔ te ŋon ɔ be fa e kakpoŋse ala nɛ, nyiŋa kakpoŋse ala bela kpu.

eng: But the servant who had received the one valuable coin dug a hole in the ground and buried his masters money.
local: Mɔna tobaale ŋon bɔɔ fa e kakpoŋ dokoloŋ nɛ, korowɔ ke wula bɔɔ te ɔ moo ɔ gbeŋgyoo kɔba keŋ kewuu.

eng: Now after a long time the master of those servants returned and settled accounts with them.
local: Be gbeŋgyoo ŋon kyaawɔ botɔɔ kyare pɛte ɔ kɔŋawɔ. Ɔ ke kɔŋ nɛ te ɔ baake ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ gyaŋee nyi baa gyoo be kɔbase ŋan man.

eng: The one who had received five valuable coins came forward with five additional coins. He said, Master, you gave me five valuable coins. Look, Ive gained five more.
local: Tobaale ŋon ɔ be fa e kɔba kakpoŋse anoŋ nɛ, moo kakpoŋse kufu kɔŋ te ɔ yako ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon nyiaa, kɛɛ kakpoŋse anoŋ te n faa maŋ mɔna me ta bese nyiŋ kakpoŋse anoŋ kpu bela.

eng: His master replied, Excellent! You are a good and faithful servant! Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Keŋte ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon yako e nyiaa, Ao baale kpaakpaa anokoarete, karaŋ na keyɛɛ. N te di anokoare lee kyomii maa moo fa neŋ kei man, mena dɔɔ maa mo abɔɔ burum do n nyiŋmaa man. Kɔŋ na dee di gyoŋ keŋ mee dii nɛ.

eng: The second servant also came forward and said, Master, you gave me two valuable coins. Look, Ive gained two more.
local: Tobaale ŋon ɔ be fa e kɔba kakpoŋse ala nɛ mɔ kɔŋawɔ kaa yako e nyiaa, Me gbeŋgyoo, n faa maŋ kakpoŋse ala, mɔna me te nyiŋ kakpoŋse ala kpu bela.

eng: His master replied, Well done! You are a good and faithful servant. Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Keŋte ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon yako e nyiaa, Tobaale kpaakpaa anokoarete, karaŋ na ke yɛɛ. N te di anokoare lee kyomii maa moo fa neŋ kei man, mena dɔɔ maa mo abɔɔ burum do n nyiŋmaa man. Kɔŋ na dee di gyoŋ keŋ mee dii nɛ.

eng: Now the one who had received one valuable coin came and said, Master, I knew that you are a hard man. You harvest grain where you havent sown. You gather crops where you havent spread seed.
local: Tobaale ŋon ɔ be fa e kakpoŋ dokoloŋ nɛ mɔ kɔŋawɔ te ɔ yako ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon nyiaa, Me gbeŋgyoo, ma gyeŋ nyi n nombia dana doŋ te botɔɔ keŋ n te duu kolo mɔ ya nɛ, botɔɔ te n ne kɔɔ.

eng: So I was afraid. And I hid my valuable coin in the ground. Here, you have whats yours.
local: Me yee neŋ mena dɔɔ ma moo n kɔba keŋ ka weese bɔɔ man. Kɛɛ, n kɔba yaa, lɛɛ n woni.

eng: His master replied, You evil and lazy servant! You knew that I harvest grain where I havent sown and that I gather crops where I havent spread seed?.
local: Keŋte ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon yako e nyiaa, N yɛɛ tobaale kum na kebɔŋalete e. N gyeŋ nyi mɛɛ kɔ abɔɔ botɔɔ keŋ me te duu ya, te mee yilaa abɔɔ botɔɔ keŋ ma te ŋmatee ya na.

eng: In that case, you should have turned my money over to the bankers so that when I returned, you could give me what belonged to me with interest.
local: weera n ta moo me kɔba ka see maŋ kɔba dɛɛsee man ya. Nafɔ ma ke kɔŋ kei na, ŋgba maa gyu ke lɛɛ keŋ na ka loŋ. Keŋte ɔ yako ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ nyiaa.

eng: Therefore, take from him the valuable coin and give it to the one who has ten coins.
local: ɛ lɛo kɔba keŋ lee ɔ gyaŋ na ɛ ka mo kpu ŋon ɔ be nyiŋ kakpoŋse kufu nɛ.

eng: Those who have much will receive more, and they will have more than they need. But as for those who dont have much, even the little bit they have will be taken away from them.
local: Nawolo nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ dana kolo na baa kpu e na waa nyiŋ burum, te ŋon ɔ be dana ya na, kyomii keŋ koraŋ ɔ dana nɛ baa lɛo ke lee ɔ gyaŋ.

eng: Now take the worthless servant and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ fuŋ tobaale ohuhoo kei ŋon ɔ be dana tɔnɔɔ ya nɛ leki ditiŋtɛɛ keŋ man. Botɔɔ te waa kyaŋ ɔ nyee wii na waa tao ɔ kela. .

eng: Now when the Human One comes in his majesty and all his angels are with him, he will sit on his majestic throne.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Debaŋ keŋ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa bese kɔŋ na, daa na Wurubuarɛ kpilala ne gyae la dɛɛ kɔŋ. Maa kɔŋ me gyoorobiiri man kaa kyaa me gyooro gbeli keŋ dɔɔ.
