eng: All the nations will be gathered in front of him. He will separate them from each other, just as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
local: Tɛɛle kei balaŋ pou baa kaa yilaa me gyaŋ, na maa kpase be man akpeŋ ala ŋgba mena keŋ wonembia kɛɛlere ne kpase asoose lee namense man nɛ.

eng: He will put the sheep on his right side. But the goats he will put on his left.
local: Maa mo namense baŋ see me dunoluŋ dɔɔ, na maa mo asoose baŋ see ma demena dɔɔ.

eng: Then the king will say to those on his right, Come, you who will receive good things from my Father. Inherit the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world began.
local: Botɔɔ te maŋ gyoo ŋon maa yako baŋ be kyaa ma dunoluŋ dɔɔ nɛ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ baŋ me kya be hiraa ŋon. Ɛ kɔŋ kaa kyaa Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ ɔ be wulaa desina see ŋon leenaŋ debaŋ keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ tɛɛle kei nɛ man.

eng: I was hungry and you gave me food to eat. I was thirsty and you gave me a drink. I was a stranger and you welcomed me.
local: Nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ tanam na ko maŋ na, ɛ faa maŋ weenɛɛ me diiwɔ. Debaŋ keŋ loŋboaneŋ na ko maŋ na ɛ faa maŋ loŋ me nyɔɔwɔ. Debaŋ keŋ me yɛɛ ŋɔɔle e na, ɛ lɛɛ maŋ gyu ya akpaŋalaŋse man.

eng: I was naked and you gave me clothes to wear. I was sick and you took care of me. I was in prison and you visited me.
local: debaŋ keŋ me dɔŋɛɛ diteŋteli na, ɛ faa maŋ abɔɔ ma doowɔ. Debaŋ keŋ mɛɛ wee na ɛ kaa kɛɛ me dɔɔ, debaŋ keŋ bɔɔ kyaŋ maŋ ketɔ deni na, ɛ ka doo maŋ nɔɔ.

eng: Then those who are righteous will reply to him, Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give you a drink?.
local: Keŋte mena balaŋ kɛwɔ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ, baa bɔɔse gyoo ŋon nyiaa, De gbeŋgyoo, woromɔ te de naa neŋ nyi tanam na ko neŋ te de faa neŋ weenɛɛ, yaa loŋboaneŋ na ko neŋ te de faa neŋ loŋ n nyɔɔwɔ.

eng: When did we see you as a stranger and welcome you, or naked and give you clothes to wear?.
local: Woromɔ te de naa neŋ nyi n yɛɛ ŋɔɔle te de lɛɛ neŋ gyu da akpaŋalaŋse man, yaa n dɔŋɛɛ diteŋteli te de faa neŋ abɔɔ n doowɔ?.

eng: When did we see you sick or in prison and visit you.
local: Woromɔ te de naa neŋ nyi n na wee, yaa ba te kyaŋ neŋ ketɔ deni te de kaa doo neŋ nɔɔ.

eng: Then the king will reply to them, I assure you that when you have done it for one of the least of these brothers and sisters of mine, you have done it for me.
local: Botɔɔ te maŋ gyoo ŋon maa tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ be yɛɛ fa me balaŋ kɛwɔ man kemaasabii koraŋ na, maŋ yaa ɛ yɛɛwɔ fa.

eng: Then he will say to those on his left, Get away from me, you who will receive terrible things. Go into the unending fire that has been prepared for the devil and his angels.
local: Botɔɔ te maa yako baŋ be kyaa ma demena dɔɔ nɛ nyiaa, Ɛ seŋ lee me gyaŋ, ɛmɛɛ baŋ Wurubuarɛ be falaa ŋon nyi ya gyu ka gyoo boalaŋ degyapaa keŋ ɔ be desina see fa ɔbɔnsam na ɔ kpilala baŋ nɛ man.

eng: I was hungry and you didnt give me food to eat. I was thirsty and you didnt give me anything to drink.
local: Nawolo nyi tanam koo maŋ na, ɛ te fa maŋ weenɛɛ ya, loŋboaneŋ koo maŋ na ɛ te fa maŋ loŋ ya.

eng: I was a stranger and you didnt welcome me. I was naked and you didnt give me clothes to wear. I was sick and in prison, and you didnt visit me.
local: Debaŋ keŋ me yɛɛ ŋɔɔle e na ɛ ta mo maŋ gyu ya akpaŋalaŋse man ya. Me tɛɛwɔ diteŋteli na, ɛ te fa maŋ kolo maa do ya. Ma weewɔ mɔna ɛ te kaa do maŋ nɔɔ ya, te be kyaŋa maŋ ketɔ deni, mɔna ɛ te kaa do maŋ nɔɔ ya.

eng: Then they will reply, Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison and didnt do anything to help you.
local: Botɔɔ te balaŋ baŋ baa bɔɔse maŋ gyoo ŋon nyiaa, De gbeŋgyoo woromɔ te de naa neŋ nyi tanam na ko neŋ, yaa loŋboaneŋ na ko neŋ, yaa n yɛɛ ŋɔɔle e yaa n dɔŋ diteŋteli yaa n weewɔ yaa be kyaŋa neŋ ketɔ deni te da te kyɔ neŋ ya.

eng: Then he will answer, I assure you that when you havent done it for one of the least of these, you havent done it for me.
local: Keŋte maa tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, Kpene keŋ ɛ be bɛɛ ɛ te yɛɛ fa me balaŋ kɛwɔ man kemaasabii koraŋ ya nɛ, na maŋ yaa ɛ bɛɛwɔ ɛ te yɛɛ fa ya.

eng: And they will go away into eternal punishment. But the righteous ones will go into eternal life.
local: Botɔɔ te baa mo balaŋ benaŋ gyu botɔɔ keŋ baa naa diyem kekpaakekpaa nɛ, mɔna balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ dɔɔ, baa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaakekpaa.

eng: After the Sabbath, at dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to look at the tomb.
local: Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee wɔle tɛɛ kena nebo nebo fuu nɛ, Mɛɛre ŋon ɔ ba lee Magedala na Mɛɛre balate ŋon korowɔ gyu botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ wuu Yesu nɛ.

eng: Look, there was a great earthquake, for an angel from the Lord came down from heaven. Coming to the stone, he rolled it away and sat on it.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ nɛ tɛɛle wosiwɔ nawolo nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpilale te tisi lee adido kɔŋ kaa biliŋi boe keŋ see yenaŋ, te ɔ tisiwɔ kyaa ke dɔɔ.

eng: Now his face was like lightning and his clothes as white as snow.
local: Na Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon ne ŋalakee ŋgba ɔga keŋmaale nɛ, te ɔ kegba wolosɛɛ futututu ŋgba akaralɛɛ nɛ.

eng: The guards were so terrified of him that they shook with fear and became like dead men.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ deke yebɔɔ keŋ kena Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon nɛ, gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ nideli te be dɔɔwɔ ba seŋɛɛ botɔɔ ŋgba woya nɛ.

eng: But the angel said to the women, Dont be afraid. I know that you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.
local: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon yako ala baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ na yee ya, ma gyeŋ nyi Yesu ŋon bɔɔ gyɔɔ sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ yaa ɛ ne kɛo.

eng: He isnt here, because hes been raised from the dead, just as he said. Come, see the place where they laid him.
local: Ɔ be kyaa kɛbo ya. Ɔ te fom lee woya man ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yako see nɛ. Ɛ kɔŋ kaa kɛɛ ɔ dedoi man.

eng: Now hurry, go and tell his disciples, Hes been raised from the dead. Hes going on ahead of you to Galilee. You will see him there. Ive given the message to you.
local: na ɛ ke ta bileŋbileŋ ke yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa Ɔ te fom lee woya man te ɔ te kyaŋ ŋon gbɛɛ ɔ ne gyu Galelia tɛɛle man. Botɔɔ te ɛ kena e. Kei yɛna nombii keŋ me dana maa yako ŋon. .

eng: With great fear and excitement, they hurried away from the tomb and ran to tell his disciples.
local: Keŋte ala baŋ moo gyakoloŋ yeu lee yebɔɔ keŋ gyaŋ. Mɔna yɛɛ wɔ gyoŋ te be yeuwɔ gyu be kaa yako ɔ kaseela baŋ.

eng: But Jesus met them and greeted them. They came and grabbed his feet and worshipped him.
local: Karatetee dokoloŋ te Yesu gyaŋee na ala baŋ te ɔ doo wɔ nɔɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ waa fa ŋon wosefɛɛreŋ. Keŋte ala baŋ tuuwɔ benaa e te be tisiwɔ buŋ kyaŋ ɔ nawɔɔ som e.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
local: Keŋte Yesu gyɔɔ dudu lese nombia ŋan ɔ be yako nɛ man wola wɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba wɔɔfarale ŋon, ɔ ba koro nebo nebo fuu gyu ɔ ne gyae paasetena na be kaa yɛɛ tom ɔ wɔɔman nɛ.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Dont be afraid. Go and tell my brothers that I am going into Galilee. They will see me there.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ na yee ya, ɛ gyu ke yako me tebia baŋ nyiaa, baa gyu Galelia tɛɛle man. Botɔɔ te baa naa maŋ.

eng: After he agreed with the workers to pay them a denarion, he sent them into his vineyard.
local: Balee ŋon na paasetena baŋ pou ke sɛɛ wee dokoloŋ koya dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ moo wɔ gyu ɔ wɔɔ keŋ man nyi ba ke yɛɛ tom.

eng: Now as the women were on their way, some of the guards came into the city and told the chief priests everything that had happened.
local: Debaŋ keŋ ala baŋ ne gyuu nɛ, yoonɔɔtena baŋ bee gyee yebɔɔ keŋ nɛ man baale gyuuwɔ donɔɔ man ke yako Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise kpene kamasɛ keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: Then he went out around nine in the morning and saw others standing around the marketplace doing nothing.
local: Kegyɔ debaŋ kadaale nɛ, ɔ leewɔ gyu abɔntere man te ɔ kenaa tewulɛɛ baale mɔ seŋɛɛ yakaa bɔɔ ne yɛɛ kolo ya.

eng: They met with the elders and decided to give a large sum of money to the soldiers.
local: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise baŋ gyaŋeewɔ, te be bakewɔ te be faa yoonɔɔtena baŋ bɛɛ deke yebɔɔ keŋ nɛ kɔba burum daale.

eng: He said to them, You also go into the vineyard, and Ill pay you whatever is right.
local: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa Ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ gyu me wɔɔ keŋ man ke yɛɛ tom. Maa tɔ ŋon kom keŋ kaboena nyi maa tɔ ŋon nɛ. .

eng: They told them, Say that Jesus disciples came at night and stole his body while you were sleeping.
local: na baa yako nyi nelim ba ke doo na, te Yesu kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ kaa ŋmɛɛle ɔ woo ŋon.

eng: And they went. Again around noon and then at three in the afternoon, he did the same thing.
local: Mena dɔɔ baŋ mɔ gyuuwɔ ɔ wɔɔ keŋ man. Weese ka seŋ dɛɛ, na ɔ ke gɛɛse nɛ, ɔ besewɔ ka mo balaŋ gyu ɔ wɔɔ keŋ man bela.

eng: And if the governor hears about this, we will take care of it with him so you will have nothing to worry about.
local: Te be yako wɔ nyi akpaa Pilato de nyii nombia kɛŋa na, daa dee gyu kekolosi na e na dɛɛ lese ŋon lee nombia man.

eng: Around five in the afternoon he went and found others standing around, and he said to them, Why are you just standing around here doing nothing all day long.
local: Ke lii balinɔɔ weese ŋgba debaŋ anoŋ nɛ, ɔ besewɔ gyu kena balaŋ baale mɔ seŋɛɛ yakaa. Keŋte ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Weera te gyɛŋ pou ɛ seŋɛɛ kɛbo yakaa ɛ bɛɛ yɛɛ kolo ya.

eng: So the soldiers took the money and did as they were told. And this report has spread throughout all Judea to this very day.
local: Mena dɔɔ yoonɔɔtena baŋ lɛɛ kɔba keŋ te be gyuuwɔ ke yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ kegyiise baŋ be yako wɔ nɛ. Mena nombia kɛŋa te yaasee Gyudatena tɛɛle man kaa lii na gyɛŋ.

eng: Because nobody has hired us, they replied. He responded, You also go into the vineyard.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Nawolo nyi ŋolo te fa daa tom dɛɛ yɛɛ ya. Botɔɔ te balee ŋon yako wɔ nyiaa, Kenaŋ na ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ gyu ke yɛɛ tom me wɔɔman.

eng: Now the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus told them to go.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu kaseela kufu daale akaŋ baŋ gyuuwɔ Galelia tɛɛle man, te be gyuu bula keŋ dɔɔ Yesu ba wola wɔ nyi baa gyu nɛ.

eng: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his manager, Call the workers and give them their wages, beginning with the last ones hired and moving on finally to the first.
local: Ke lii balinɔɔ nɛ, wɔɔte ŋon yako ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ man kegyia ŋon nyiaa, Baake paasetena baŋ na n ke tɔ wɔ kom. Taŋgbɛɛ tɔ baŋ bɔɔ dɔɔ wɔle gyu wɔɔ keŋ man nɛ kom, pɛna n kaa tɔ baŋ bɔɔ taŋgbɛɛ gyu nɛ.

eng: When they saw him, they worshipped him, but some doubted.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu ka lese ɔ wose wola wɔ te be buŋawɔ som e, mɔna be man baale te lɛɛ di ya.

eng: When those who were hired at five in the afternoon came, each one received a denarion.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ dɔɔ wɔle gyu wɔɔ keŋ man balinɔɔ weese nɛ kɔŋawɔ, te be tɔɔ wɔ kom ŋgba mena keŋ wɔɔte ŋon be yako nyi waa tɔ gyaŋgba nyibaŋ baŋ nɛ.

eng: Jesus came near and spoke to them, Ive received all authority in heaven and on earth.
local: Keŋte Yesu tuuwɔ benaa wɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ te fa maŋ doŋ adido na tɛɛle kei pou dɔɔ.

eng: Now when those hired first came, they thought they would receive more. But each of them also received a denarion.
local: Mena dɔɔ baŋ bɔɔ taŋgbɛɛ gyu wɔɔ keŋ man lee nebo nebo nɛ kɔŋawɔ na, ba agyueŋ yɛna nyi baŋ baa nyiŋ kela be tebia baŋ. Mɔna be tɔɔ baŋ mɔ man walaŋ kamasɛ kom ŋgba wɔle nyibaŋ baŋ nɛ.

eng: Therefore, go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ gyu tɛɛle kei pou dɔɔ na ɛ kaa wola balaŋ na baa bese me kaseela. Ɛ sɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ lee Wurubuarɛ kya ŋon na ɔ Bu ŋon na ɔ Feliŋ ŋon yele man.

eng: When they received it, they grumbled against the landowner.
local: Ba ke lɛɛ be kɔbase taŋ nɛ, be fiasɛɛ bɛɛ kolosi tia wɔɔte ŋon nyiaa.

eng: teaching them to obey everything that Ive commanded you. Look, I myself will be with you every day until the end of this present age.
local: Na ɛ ka wola wɔ na baa di kpene kamasɛ keŋ mɔɔ faree ŋon nɛ dɔɔ. Te mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, me kpuɛ na ŋon debaŋ kamasɛ kelii tɛɛle kei ɔto.

eng: These who were hired last worked one hour, and they received the same pay as we did even though we had to work the whole day in the hot sun.
local: Balaŋ kɛwɔ baŋ bɔɔ kɔŋ wɔle wɔle kei nɛ, yɛɛ tom bo debaŋ dokoloŋ kooŋ. Mɔna daa dɔɔ da seŋawɔ weese kpalala kei man leenaŋ nebo kaa lii balinɔɔ kei. Kenaŋ wɔle koraŋ na, n besewɔ tɔ de popou kom kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

eng: But he replied to one of them, Friend, I did you no wrong. Didnt I agree to pay you a denarion?.
local: Botɔɔ te wɔɔte ŋon tiranɔɔ fa be man ŋolo nyiaa, Me gyoo, me ta ŋere neŋ do ya na. Ŋgba daa na neŋ kolosiwɔ see nyi n yɛɛ tom wee dibiliŋ na, nnɛ te maa tɔ neŋ kom.

eng: Take what belongs to you and go. I want to give to this one who was hired last the same as I give to you.
local: Mo n kɔba na n ke ta gyu n dɛɛ. Maŋ gbagba gyae la nyi maa tɔ baŋ bɔɔ dɔɔ wɔle kɔŋ nɛ kom ŋgba mena keŋ mɔɔ tɔ neŋ nɛ.

eng: Dont I have the right to do what I want with what belongs to me? Or are you resentful because Im generous.
local: Me dana gbɛɛ keŋ nyi maa mo maŋ gbagba me kɔba yɛɛ kpene keŋ mɛɛ gyae. Keŋ me dana wɔe dɔɔ te na wee neŋ nɛ.

eng: So those who are last will be first. And those who are first will be last.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Mena mɔ te wɔle nyibaŋ baa kaa bese gyaŋgba nyibaŋ, na gyaŋgba nyibaŋ mɔ kaa bese wɔle nyibaŋ.

eng: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the Twelve aside by themselves on the road. He told them.
local: Wee daale Yesu kee gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ baake ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ lee yenaŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa.

eng: Look, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and legal experts. They will condemn him to death.
local: Dee gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man baa. Baa lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon fa Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wola baŋ, na baa di me nombia bu maŋ fɔɔ nyi baa ko maŋ.

eng: They will hand him over to the Gentiles to be ridiculed, tortured, and crucified. But he will be raised on the third day.
local: Baa mo maŋ do balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ nyiŋmaa man na baa kulu maŋ, na baa tuku maŋ na baa gyɔ maŋ sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ na maa yeŋ. Mɔna weeya atoorote na, Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu maŋ lee yeŋ man.

eng: Then the mother of Zebedees sons came to Jesus along with her sons. Bowing before him, she asked a favor of him.
local: Keŋte Sebedeo bia, Gyemisi na Gyɔn be naa moo wɔ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ te ɔ buŋawɔ ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ sola e nyi waa kyɔ e na nombii daale.

eng: What do you want? he asked. She responded, Say that these two sons of mine will sit, one on your right hand and one on your left, in your kingdom.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, Woŋ te n ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ fa neŋ? Te alo ŋon tiranɔɔ fa Yesu nyiaa, Mɛɛ gyae nyi n ke fa me bia bala kɛwɔ gbɛɛ, na ŋolo ke kyaa n dunoluŋ dɔɔ, na ŋolo mɔ ke kyaa n demena dɔɔ n gyoori keŋ man.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink from the cup that Im about to drink from? They said to him, We can.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ ba gyeŋ kpene ɛ na solo nɛ ya. Ɛ ke tale naa diyem keŋ maa naa nɛ? Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa e nyi, Ayi, dɛɛ tale.

eng: He said to them, You will drink from my cup, but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom my Father prepared it.
local: Te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ ke tale naa diyem keŋ maa naa nɛ, mɔna mɔɔ taŋ doŋ keŋ maa lese walaŋ ŋon waa kyaa ma dunoluŋ yaa ma demena dɔɔ ya. Mena akyaese kɛŋa doe fa balaŋ baŋ me kya gbagba be desina see wɔ nɛ.

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with the two brothers.
local: Yesu kaseela kufu akaŋ baŋ ke nyii mena nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ba gyeŋa baŋ Gyemisi na Gyɔn dɔɔ.

eng: But Jesus called them over and said, You know that those who rule the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu baake ɔ kaseela baŋ pou yilaa te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ gyeŋ nyi tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyooneŋ ne di gyoori bo be balaŋ dɔɔ, te be kegyiise baŋ mɔ dana doŋ be dɔɔ.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: Mɔna ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ, na mena te kaboena nyi dɛɛ doo ɛ man ya. Ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae nyi waa bese ɛ kegyia nɛ, kaboena nyi waa bese ɛ kegbetibii.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be your slave.
local: Te ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae nyi waa bese ɛ siamante nɛ, kaboena nyi waa bese ɛ popou ɛ kegbetibii.

eng: just as the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: Mena mɔ te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon te kɔŋ nyi balaŋ baa som maŋ ya, mɔna me kɔŋae me kaa som balaŋ, na maa yeŋ lɛɛ balaŋ burum nyee.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples were going out of Jericho a large crowd followed him.
local: Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ kɛɛ lee Gyeriko donɔɔ man nɛ, balaŋ burum sila wɔ.

eng: When two blind men sitting along the road heard that Jesus was passing by, they shouted, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Na siayɛlɛɛsatena bala baale kyaa gbɛɛnaa nɔɔman botɔɔ. Be ke nyii nyi Yesu ne laŋa laa nɛ, be gyinaa be nɔɔ baake nyiaa De Gbeŋgyoo, gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii, wii de waraŋase.

eng: Now the crowd scolded them and told them to be quiet. But they shouted even louder, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ ŋmanyaŋ wɔ nyi baa wu be nɔɔ. Mɔna nɛnɛɛ gba te be desina fae yeesa nedoŋ nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii, wii de waraŋase.

eng: Jesus stopped in his tracks and called to them. What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu dɔɔwɔ seŋ te ɔ baake wɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Woŋ te ɛ ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ fa ŋon.

eng: Lord, we want to see, they replied.
local: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, dɛɛ gyae de sia ke gyuusu.

eng: Jesus had compassion on them and touched their eyes. Immediately they were able to see, and they followed him.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu wii be waraŋase, te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaa tinaa be sia. Debokenaŋ man te be sia gyuusuwɔ, te be sila Yesu laŋ.

eng: Early in the morning all the chief priests and the elders of the people reached the decision to have Jesus put to death.
local: Tɛɛ kena nebo nebo fuu nɛ, saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise baŋ gyaŋeewɔ te be yakowɔ sɛɛ dɔŋa nyi baa ko Yesu.

eng: They bound him, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate the governor.
local: Mena dɔɔ be bake e te ba moo e gyu ka do Pilato ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ Romantena gyoo dinaa nawɔɔ man nɛ nyiŋmaa man.

eng: When Judas, who betrayed Jesus, saw that Jesus was condemned to die, he felt deep regret. He returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders, and.
local: Gyudase ŋon ɔ ba lese Yesu fa kena nyi be te bu Yesu fɔɔ nyi baa ko e nɛ, takaa e nideli te ɔ kyɛɛkee wa agyueŋ. Mena dɔɔ ɔ besena kɔba keŋ ɔ be lɛɛ Yesu nyeeman nɛ gyina kegyiise baŋ.

eng: said, I did wrong because I betrayed an innocent man. But they said, What is that to us? Thats your problem.
local: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ma te yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ ŋgba mɔɔ lese walaŋ ŋon dukum kamasɛ be doo ɔ man ya fa ŋon nɛ. Te kegyiise baŋ mɔ tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, daa be famenɛ na ŋenaŋ ya. Ŋe yɛɛ nyaŋ gbagba n nombia ŋa.

eng: Judas threw the silver pieces into the temple and left. Then he went and hanged himself.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyudase fuŋa kɔba keŋ lɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man, te ɔ gyuuwɔ kado ɔ wose ŋmeleŋ.

eng: The chief priests picked up the silver pieces and said, According to the Law its not right to put this money in the treasury. Since it was used to pay for someones life, its unclean.
local: Keŋte saese kegyiise baŋ tɔɔsee kɔba ŋan te be yakowɔ nyiaa, Da mmaraa bɛɛ fa daa gbɛɛ nyi dɛɛ mo kɔba kei kyakale na dɔ ɔsom deni kɔba man ya. Nawolo nyi kɔba keŋ dana ayimɛɛ.

eng: So they decided to use it to buy the potters field where strangers could be buried.
local: Keŋte kegyiise baŋ moo ba nyeeya baŋ na dɔŋa te be sɛɛwɔ nyi, baa mo kɔba kenaŋ ke lɛɛ tɛɛle saabuse maare ŋolo gyaŋ, na baa kyaa wuu ŋɔɔla botɔɔ.

eng: Thats why that field is called Field of Blood to this very day.
local: Mena dɔɔ kaa lii naŋ gyɛŋ nɛ, bɛɛ baake tɛɛle kenaŋ bo nyi fatabo tɛɛle.

eng: This fulfilled the words of Jeremiah the prophet: And I took the thirty pieces of silver, the price for the one whose price had been set by some of the Israelites.
local: Kɛŋa pou yela Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ɔ be tɛɛ ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Gyeremia dɔɔ yako see nɛ kɔŋawɔ mena. Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Ba moo kɔba wolom bia sao na kufu ŋan ŋe yɛɛ koya keŋ Iseraetena be dii ɔ nyeeman nɛ.

eng: and I gave them for the potters field, as the Lord commanded me .
local: ke lɛɛ tɛɛle saabuse maare gyaŋ ŋgba mena keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ba wola maŋ nɛ.

eng: Jesus was brought before the governor. The governor said, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Keŋte ba moo Yesu kaa seŋ Pilato ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ Romantena gyoo dinaa nawɔɔ man nɛ siaman. Te Pilato bɔɔse e nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna Gyudatena gyoo ŋon? Te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Keŋ yaa n yakowɔ nɛ.

eng: But he didnt answer when the chief priests and elders accused him.
local: Mɔna debaŋ keŋ saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise ba mo nombia gyakaa Yesu dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ te gyuusu ɔ nɔɔ ya.

eng: Then Pilate said, Dont you hear the testimony they bring against you.
local: Botɔɔ te Pilato bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, Nombia burum kɛŋa bɛɛ mo gyakaa n dɔɔ nɛ, n bee nyii yee.

eng: But he didnt answer, not even a single word. So the governor was greatly amazed.
local: Mɔna Yesu bɛe ɔ te gyuusu ɔ nɔɔ yako nombii dokoloŋ kooŋ gba ya. Nombii kei doo Pilato nɔɔ nideli.

eng: It was customary during the festival for the governor to release to the crowd one prisoner, whomever they might choose.
local: Gyudatena nyeekelɛɛwee kedi wee kamasɛ nɛ, kaboena nyi Pilato ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ Romantena gyoo dinaa nawɔɔ man nɛ, waa tina balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ kyam tɔ deni nɛ man dokoloŋ, ŋon balaŋ benaŋ gbagba ne gyae nɛ yela fa wɔ.

eng: At that time there was a well-known prisoner named Jesus Barabbas.
local: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ na Barabase ŋon ɔ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛre e, te balaŋ pou be nyii ɔ nombia nɛ doo deni man.

eng: When the crowd had come together, Pilate asked them, Whom would you like me to release to you, Jesus Barabbas or Jesus who is called Christ.
local: Balaŋ dikpii keŋ ke kɔŋ kaa yilaa nɛ Pilato bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Balaŋ bala kɛwɔ man na be man woŋti te ɛ ne gyae nyi maa tina yela fa ŋon. Barabase yaa Yesu ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Kristo nɛ.

eng: He knew that the leaders of the people had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: Nawolo nyi na Pilato gyeŋ nyi, keŋ balaŋ baŋ ne kɔla Yesu dɔɔ te ba lese e fa.

eng: While he was serving as judge, his wife sent this message to him, Leave that righteous man alone. Ive suffered much today in a dream because of him.
local: Pilato kekyaa ɔ ne di nombia ŋan nɛ, ɔ wɛɛle kpilawɔ kaa yako e nyiaa, Lese n nyiŋmaase lee mena walaŋ ŋon nombiikum kamasɛ be doo ɔ man ya nɛ nombia man. Nawolo nyi nelim me doosi dosɛɛ ŋaale lee ɔ wose man na, yɛɛ maŋ gyakoloŋ te me naa diyem nideli.

eng: But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas and kill Jesus.
local: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na kegyiise akaŋ baŋ feefuwɔ do balaŋ dikpii keŋ nyi, baa sola Pilato na waa tina Barabase yela na baa ko Yesu.

eng: The governor said, Which of the two do you want me to release to you? Barabbas, they replied.
local: Keŋte Pilato besewɔ bɔɔse balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, Balaŋ bala kɛwɔ man na, be man woŋti te ɛ ne gyae nyi maa tina e yela? Te be tiranɔɔ fa nyiaa, Tina Barabase yela fa daa.

eng: Pilate said, Then what should I do with Jesus who is called Christ? They all said, Crucify him.
local: Keŋte Pilato besewɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Kenaŋ na sena te maa yɛɛ Yesu ŋon bɛɛ baake nyi Kristo nɛ? Botɔɔ te be popou yakowɔ nyi, Yela baa gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ.

eng: But he said, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Keŋte Pilato bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Woŋ dukum te ɔ yɛɛwɔ? Mɔna be sɛɛ bɛɛ fae yeesa nedoŋ nyiaa, Yela baa gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ.

eng: Pilate saw that he was getting nowhere and that a riot was starting. So he took water and washed his hands in front of the crowd. Im innocent of this mans blood, he said. Its your problem.
local: Pilato kena nyi balaŋ baŋ nɔɔwoya na kela e te ɔ be gyae waa tale yɛɛ kolo lee Yesu wose man ya nɛ, ɔ kyee loŋ faafo ɔ nyiŋmaase balaŋ dikpii keŋ siaman te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Maŋ nyiŋmaa be doo balee kei yeŋ man korakora ya. Ke yɛɛ ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ nombia ŋa.

eng: All the people replied, Let his blood be on us and on our children.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ pou sɛɛwɔ yako nyiaa, Deŋele kewɔŋ keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ lee ɔ kako wose man nɛ, dɛɛkɔŋ daa na de bia dɔɔ.

eng: Then he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Mena dɔɔ Pilato tinaa Barabase yela fa wɔ, te ɔ yela ba ŋmeree Yesu dambiise te ɔ moo e fa yoonɔɔtena baŋ nyi baa gyu ke gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ.

eng: The governors soldiers took Jesus into the governors house, and they gathered the whole company of soldiers around him.
local: Keŋte Pilato yoonɔɔtena baŋ moo Yesu gyu ŋon Pilato dekpaŋalaŋ man, te be baake yoonɔɔtena akaŋ baŋ pou kaa gyaŋee kilisi Yesu.

eng: They stripped him and put a red military coat on him.
local: Be kyaŋa ɔ kegbase kpee te ba moo kegba yayam wurikyim daale ŋgba ŋan Romantena gyooneŋ na doo nɛ do e.

eng: They twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head. They put a stick in his right hand. Then they bowed down in front of him and mocked him, saying, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: Keŋte ba moo sɔɔse loo gyooro kotɔtɔɔ wuu e, te ba moo gbutu do e ɔ dunoluŋ nyiŋmaa man na bɛɛ buŋ ɔ siaman kulu e nyiaa, Gyudatena gyoo nyaŋ na ke yɛɛ. N nyee dɛɛ wolose.

eng: After they spit on him, they took the stick and struck his head again and again.
local: Be tɔɔ nɔɔloŋse woli e, te be lɛo ɔ gbutu keŋ mo ŋmeree e ɔ nyee man.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the military coat and put his own clothes back on him. They led him away to crucify him.
local: Be ke kulu e taŋ nɛ, te ba kpee kegba yayam keŋ bɔɔ mo do nɛ te ba moo ŋon gbagba wui abɔɔ do e. Te ba moo lee be kaa gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ.

eng: As they were going out, they found Simon, a man from Cyrene. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Be ka lee debɔɔ man bee gyu nɛ, te be gyaŋee na balee ŋolo ŋon ɔ ba lee Kirene te bɛɛ baake e nyi Simɔn nɛ. Keŋte ba ŋere e nyi waa lɛɛ Yesu ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ seele.

eng: When they came to a place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Keŋte be gyuuwɔ ka gyoo botɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake nyi Golgata, keŋ nawolo nyi Nyeepookoo dɛɛ nɛ.

eng: they gave Jesus wine mixed with vinegar to drink. But after tasting it, he didnt want to drink it.
local: Botɔɔ te ba moo tee ŋaŋte daale mo kyakale na soloŋ fa Yesu nyi waa nyɔɔ. Mɔna ɔ ke daa ke kɛɛ nɛ te ɔ bɛɛwɔ nyi ɔ be nyɔɔ ya.

eng: After they crucified him, they divided up his clothes among them by drawing lots.
local: Ba ke gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ, te be kpɛlɛɛ ɔ kegbase buŋii te be lɔɔ boe ŋe dɔɔ na baa naa keŋ walaŋ kamasɛ waa nyiŋ.

eng: They sat there, guarding him.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, te be kyaawɔ botɔɔ bɛɛ deke e.

eng: When they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage on the Mount of Olives, Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Yesu na ɔ kaseela ka benaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ, te be kelii Bɛtifagi donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa oliifi bula keŋ dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ kpila ɔ kaseela bala.

eng: They placed above his head the charge against him. It read, This is Jesus, the king of the Jews.
local: Keŋte be ŋmarase nombii keŋ dɔɔ bɔɔ gyɔ e ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ mataa ɔ nyee gbɛɛ nyiaa, kei yɛna yesu, gyudatena gyoo ŋon.

eng: He said to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter, you will find a donkey tied up and a colt with it. Untie them and bring them to me.
local: te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ gyu donɔɔ keŋ kyaa ɛ siaman nɛ man. Ɛ gyoo ke man pɛ nɛ, ɛ kena afurum kpaŋɛɛ na ɔ bu seŋɛɛ ɔ gyaŋ. Ɛ booli wɔ na ɛ ka mo wɔ kɔna maŋ.

eng: They crucified with him two outlaws, one on his right side and one on his left.
local: Botɔɔ te be gyɔɔ ŋmɛɛlatena bala baale sakaa ɔpaŋdaase dɔɔ kpu na Yesu. Ŋolo sakaa ɔ dunoluŋ dɔɔ te ŋolo mɔ sakaa ɔ demena dɔɔ.

eng: If anyone says anything to you, say that their master needs them. He sent them off right away.
local: Akpaa ŋolo de bɔɔse ŋon nombii daale nɛ, ɛ yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ne gyae na wɔ. Ɛ yako mena nɛ, waa tina wɔ yela fa ŋon debokenaŋ man.

eng: Those who were walking by insulted Jesus, shaking their heads.
local: Na balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ lam botɔɔ nɛ na tee e na bee kpiisi ba nyeeya.

eng: Now this happened to fulfill what the prophet said.
local: Mena nombia kɛŋa pou kɔŋawɔ mena, te yela Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ɔ be tɛɛ ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre dɔɔ yako nɛ, kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ.

eng: and saying, So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you? Save yourself! If you are Gods Son, come down from the cross.
local: na bɛɛ yako e nyiaa, Nyaŋ ŋon n kpa n ke tekerii Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ na n ka mo weeya atooro ma ke nɛ, lɛɛ n wose na dɛɛ kɛɛ. Akpaa nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon na, lɛɛ n wose na n ke tisi lee ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ dɔɔ.

eng: Say to Daughter Zion, Look, your king is coming to you, humble and riding on a donkey, and on a colt the donkeys offspring. .
local: Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Ɛ yako Gyerusalɛmtena nyiaa, Ɛ kɛɛ! Ɛ gyoo ne kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ. Ɔ te yɔkɔse ɔ wose, te ɔ kyaa afurum dɔɔ. Ɔ kyae afurum bu dɔɔ ɔ ne kɔŋ. .

eng: In the same way, the chief priests, along with the legal experts and the elders, were making fun of him, saying.
local: Mena mɔ te saese kegyiise na mmaraa wolala na donɔɔ kegyiise baŋ mɔ kulu e nyiaa.

eng: The disciples went and did just as Jesus had ordered them.
local: Keŋte Yesu kaseela baŋ gyuuwɔ ke yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yako wɔ nɛ.

eng: He saved others, but he cant save himself. Hes the king of Israel, so let him come down from the cross now. Then well believe in him.
local: Ɔ ne lɛɛ balaŋ nyee, mɔna ɔ be tale lɛɛ ŋon gbagba wose ya. Ŋgba n kpa nyaŋ yɛna Iseraetena gyoo ŋon, tisi lee daŋ keŋ dɔɔ na dɛɛ lɛɛ neŋ di.

eng: They brought the donkey and the colt and laid their clothes on them. Then he sat on them.
local: Ba moo afurum ŋon na ɔ bu kɔŋ, te ba moo be kanyaŋse gyakaa be dɔɔ, te Yesu gyeŋawɔ kyaa.

eng: He trusts in God, so let God deliver him now if he wants to. He said, Im Gods Son.
local: Ɔ na mo ɔ wose lɔ Wurubuarɛ dɔɔ te ɔ ne yako nyi, Ŋon yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu. Akpaa Wurubuarɛ ne gyae e na, ɛ yela dɛɛ kɛɛ nyi waa lɛɛ e. .

eng: Now a large crowd spread their clothes on the road. Others cut palm branches off the trees and spread them on the road.
local: Balaŋ burum fɛlɛɛ be kanyaŋse lɔ gbɛɛnaa keŋ man, te baale mɔ kɛrɛɛ daase tombia na ŋe faareŋse lɔ gbɛɛnaa man fa e.

eng: The outlaws who were crucified with him insulted him in the same way.
local: Mena mɔ te nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra baŋ bɔɔ gyɔɔ wɔ sakaa ɔpaŋdaase dɔɔ kpu na e nɛ mɔ kulu e.

eng: The crowds in front of him and behind him shouted, Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Balaŋ burum baŋ be dɔŋɛɛ Yesu siaman, na baŋ be dɔŋɛɛ ɔ wɔle nɛ pou fiasɛɛ bɛɛ fae yeesa nyiaa, Gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii, lɛɛ daa! Ɛ fa dɛɛ lese walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kɔŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ yele man nɛ yele. Wurubuarɛ ŋon n kyaa adido, lɛɛ daa.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Lee weese kaseŋ dɛɛ kelii ɔ ke gɛɛse nɛ, ditiŋtɛɛ kaa lɛɛ tɛɛle keŋ dɔɔ pou.

eng: And when Jesus entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred up. Who is this? they asked.
local: Yesu ke kɔŋ kaa gyoo Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, donɔɔ keŋ pou siŋiiwɔ te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Walaŋ woŋti yɛna kei.

eng: At about three Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani , which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ nɛ, te Yesu fae yeesa keŋkeŋ nyiaa, Eli, Eli lama sabatani? Keŋ nawolo nyi, Me Wurubuarɛ me Wurubuarɛ, weera te n lese n sia yela maŋ.

eng: The crowds answered, Its the prophet Jesus from Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Kei yɛna Yesu, Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋon ɔ ba lee Nasarɛte donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ.

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Hes calling Elijah.
local: Balaŋ baŋ ba seŋɛɛ botɔɔ ke nyii mena nɛ be yakowɔ nyiaa, Ɛ nyii, ɔ ne baake Elaegya.

eng: Then Jesus went into the temple and threw out all those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Yesu gyuu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ dekpaŋalaŋ man, te ɔ kenaawɔ nyi balaŋ ne di kebu botɔɔ. Keŋte ɔ gegi balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɔɔ abɔɔ na baŋ bɛɛ lɛɛ abɔɔ botɔɔ nɛ pou. Ɔ besenaa balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɛlɛɛ kɔba nɛ tebuluse buŋii, te ɔ tɛɛse balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɔɔ awuluŋna nɛ mɔ gbelɛɛ lɔ.

eng: One of them ran over, took a sponge full of vinegar, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink.
local: Debokenaŋ man te be man ŋolo yeuwɔ ka mo kolo ŋgba kokyaa keŋ gyae ke tale lɛɛ loŋ nɛ do soloŋ denyaŋesa daale man, te ɔ moo ke tooso na daŋ te ɔ gyinaa ke adido fa Yesu nyi waa nyɔɔ.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be called a house of prayer. But youve made it a hideout for crooks.
local: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako daa nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa, Me deni gyae ke yɛɛ botɔɔ keŋ baa fane, mɔna ɛmɛɛ ta besena ke yɛɛ ŋmɛɛlatena dɛɛgyaŋee.

eng: But the rest of them said, Lets see if Elijah will come and save him.
local: Keŋte balaŋ akaŋ baŋ yakowɔ nyiaa, Ɛ yela dɛɛ kɛɛ nyi Elaegya waa kɔŋ kaa lɛɛ e.

eng: People who were blind and lame came to Jesus in the temple, and he healed them.
local: Siayɛlɛɛsatena na ɔtakasese kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man botɔɔ, te ɔ kyɔɔ wɔ be kaweese pou.

eng: Again Jesus cried out with a loud shout. Then he died.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ fae yeesa keŋkeŋ te ɔ kara weesi.

eng: But when the chief priests and legal experts saw the amazing things he was doing and the children shouting in the temple, Hosanna to the Son of David! they were angry.
local: Mɔna saese kegyiise na mmaraa wola baŋ kena gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan Yesu be yɛɛ, na mena keŋ bia ne fae yeesa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nyiaa, Gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii lɛɛ daa nɛ, ba gyeŋa baŋ Yesu dɔɔ.

eng: Look, the curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, the rocks split.
local: Kekɔŋ mena nɛ, kanyaŋ keŋ bɔɔ mo kpase Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ bɛɛsewɔ kyɛɛkyɛɛ ala lee adido kaa lii ka mokɔɔ man. Tɛɛle wosiwɔ, te boya dinaana mɔ yɛlɛɛwɔ.

eng: They said to Jesus, Do you hear what these children are saying? Yes, he answered. Havent you ever read, From the mouths of babies and infants youve arranged praise for yourself? .
local: Keŋte be bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, N ne nyii nombia ŋan bia baŋ ne yako? Te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ayi, mee nyii. Keŋte ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ te kala ta na ɛ kena kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nɛ yee? Ba te ŋmarase see nyiaa, Bia buruburu na kyokyoose te n lesewɔ see nyi baa lese n yele .

eng: and the bodies of many holy people who had died were raised.
local: Yebɔɔse gyuusuwɔ te Wurubuarɛ gyuusu ɔ balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ɔ siaman te bɔɔ yekee nɛ, man balaŋ burum lee ba yebɔɔse man.

eng: Then he left them and went out of the city to Bethany and spent the night there.
local: Keŋte Yesu leewɔ be gyaŋ donɔɔ keŋ man, te ɔ gyuuwɔ Bɛtani donɔɔ man, te ɔ doowɔ botɔɔ.

eng: After Jesus resurrection they came out of their graves and went into the holy city where they appeared to many people.
local: Yesu kefoŋ lee yeŋ man nɛ, Wurubuarɛ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ foŋii lee ba yebɔɔse man nɛ, gyuu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man ka lese ba wose wola balaŋ burum.

eng: Early in the morning as Jesus was returning to the city, he was hungry.
local: Tɛɛ kena nebo nebo fuu keŋ Yesu ba bese ɔ wɔle ɔ ne kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, na tanam na ko e.

eng: When the centurion and those with him who were guarding Jesus saw the earthquake and what had just happened, they were filled with awe and said, This was certainly Gods Son.
local: Romantena yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon na baŋ ba seŋɛɛ bɛɛ gyee Yesu nɛ kena tɛɛle kewosi na gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan be yɛɛ pou nɛ, ba wose yeŋa wɔ te be yakowɔ nyiaa, Balee kei yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon ampaŋ.

eng: He saw a fig tree along the road, but when he came to it, he found nothing except leaves. Then he said to it, Youll never again bear fruit! The fig tree dried up at once.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ naa fiigi daŋ daale seŋɛɛ gbɛɛnaa nɔɔman, te ɔ kpasewɔ gyu ke tɛɛ nyi mberem waa nyiŋ ke bia ŋaale di. Mɔna ɔ te nyiŋ bii dokoloŋ na dokoloŋ ya. Ke yɛɛ faareŋse wolowolo bo. Botɔɔ te Yesu falaa daŋ kenaŋ nyiaa, N be gyae n ka tekaa se bii na bii bela ya. Debokenaŋ man te fiigi daŋ keŋ botowɔ.

eng: Many women were watching from a distance. They had followed Jesus from Galilee to serve him.
local: Na ala burum baale mɔ seŋɛɛ yenaŋ botɔɔ lemlem bɛɛ kɛɛ. Be wulaa be silana Yesu leenaŋ Galelia donɔɔ man bɛɛ kɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ.

eng: When the disciples saw it, they were amazed. How did the fig tree dry up so fast? they asked.
local: Ɔ kaseela baŋ kena mena nɛ, doo be nɔɔ te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, Kɔŋaa sena te fiigi daŋ keŋ botowɔ karatetee dokoloŋ mena.

eng: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of Zebedees sons.
local: Ala benaŋ man baale yɛna Mɛɛre ŋon ɔ ba lee Magedala nɛ, na Gyemisi na Gyosɛfo be naa Mɛɛre, na Sebedeo bia baŋ be naa.

eng: Jesus responded, I assure you that if you have faith and dont doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree. You will even say to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the lake. And it will happen.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, akpaa ɛ lɛɛ maŋ di nideli te ɛ te lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ ya nɛ, na kpene keŋ be kɔŋ fiigi daŋ kei dɔɔ nɛ kaageŋ te ɛ ke tale yɛɛ ya. Mɔna ɛ ke tale yako bula kei koraŋ nyiaa, Koro ke yala ɛpo man te ke gyae ke kɔŋ mena.

eng: That evening a man named Joseph came. He was a rich man from Arimathea who had become a disciple of Jesus.
local: Kelii balinɔɔ nɛ kɔbate ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Gyosɛfo, ŋon ɔ ba lee Arimatia donɔɔ man nɛ kɔŋawɔ botɔɔ. Na ŋon mɔ yɛɛ Yesu silala baŋ man ŋolo e.

eng: If you have faith, you will receive whatever you pray for.
local: Nyi akpaa ɛ lɛɛ maŋ di nɛ, kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ ke fane bɔɔse Wurubuarɛ nɛ, ɛ nyiŋmaa gyae ke kaŋ ke.

eng: He came to Pilate and asked for Jesus body. Pilate gave him permission to take it.
local: Ɔ gyuu Pilato gyaŋ ka sola e nyi waa fa e gbɛɛ na waa mo Yesu woo ŋon ke wuu. Keŋte Pilato sɛɛwɔ nyi baa moo Yesu woo ŋon fa e.

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, the chief priests and elders of the people came to him as he was teaching. They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man, te ɔ kɛɛ wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan nɛ, saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa, Doŋ woŋti yaa n dana n ne yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou? Amɔte faa na neŋ mena doŋ kenaŋ.

eng: Joseph took the body, wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
local: Keŋte Gyosɛfo moo woo ŋon, te ɔ moo akaralɛɛ wɔle wɔle daale milii e.

eng: Jesus replied, I have a question for you. If you tell me the answer, Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Maŋ mɔ ne gyae maa bɔɔse ŋon nombii dokoloŋ. Nyi akpaa ɛ tiranɔɔ fa maŋ nɛ kenaŋ na maŋ mɔ maa wola ŋon doŋ keŋ me dana mɛɛ yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou.

eng: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had carved out of the rock. After he rolled a large stone at the door of the tomb, he went away.
local: Te ɔ moo e ke wuu boe bɔɔ wɔle keŋ ŋon Gyosɛfo gbagba be kare see ɔ wose nɛ man. Keŋte ɔ biliŋi boe dinaa daale tɔ yebɔɔ keŋ nɔɔ te ɔ laŋawɔ.

eng: Where did John get his authority to baptize? Did he get it from heaven or from humans? They argued among themselves, If we say from heaven, hell say to us, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋkesɔ keŋ ɔ be sɔ balaŋ nɛ, ka lee aleŋ? Ka lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ yaa balaŋ gyaŋ? Botɔɔ te ba gyoo nombia ŋan man te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Woŋ te dɛɛ yako? Akpaa de yako nyi ka lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ na, waa bɔɔse daa nyi woŋ dɔɔ te da te lɛɛ Gyɔn nombia ŋan di ya.

eng: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there, sitting in front of the tomb.
local: Na Mɛɛre ŋon ɔ ba lee Magedala nɛ, na Mɛɛre balate ŋon baŋɛɛ botɔɔ be sia tee na yebɔɔ keŋ.

eng: But we cant say from humans because were afraid of the crowd, since everyone thinks John was a prophet.
local: Te akpaa de yako nyi ka lee balaŋ gyaŋ na, dee yee balaŋ burum kɛwɔ mɔ. Nawolo nyi be popou ne lɛɛ di nideli nyi Gyɔn yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e.

eng: The next day, which was the day after Preparation Day, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered before Pilate.
local: Tɛɛ kena keŋ ke yɛɛ Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee ke nɛ, Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Farasiitena baŋ gyuuwɔ ke gyaŋee Pilato dekpaŋalaŋ man.

eng: Then they replied, We dont know. Jesus also said to them, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Mena dɔɔ be tiranɔɔ fa Yesu nyiaa, Dɔɔ gyeŋ ya. Keŋte Yesu mɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Akpaa mena na, maŋ mɔ ba wola ŋon me doŋ keŋ da lee ya.

eng: They said, Sir, we remember that while that deceiver was still alive he said, After three days I will arise.
local: Keŋte be yako Pilato nyiaa, Kegyia dɛɛ tɔɔse nyi Yesu dekarate ŋon ke kyaa keŋ nɛ ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, weeya atoorote wɔle na waa foŋ lee yeŋ man.

eng: What do you think? A man had two sons. Now he came to the first and said, Son, go and work in the vineyard today.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu fa wɔ nyiaa, Balee ŋolo kyaawɔ keŋ ɔ dana bia baala bala. Wee daale nɛ, ɔ gyuu ɔ bu kegyia ŋon gyaŋ ke yako e nyiaa, Me bu, mɛɛ gyae nyi gyɛŋ n ke gyu me wɔɔman ke yɛɛ tom fa maŋ.

eng: Therefore, order the grave to be sealed until the third day. Otherwise, his disciples may come and steal the body and tell the people, Hes been raised from the dead. This last deception will be worse than the first.
local: Mena dɔɔ yela be kaa deke yebɔɔ keŋ na baa gyee ke nideli kelii weeya atoorote keŋ. Na mena ya na ɔ kaseela baŋ baa yo gyu ke ŋmɛɛle e lese na baa yako nyi, ɔ foŋae lee yeŋ man. Na mena ya na, dekara alate kei gyae ka kela gyaŋgba te keŋ.

eng: No, I dont want to, he replied. But later he changed his mind and went.
local: Keŋte ɔ bu ŋon yako e nyiaa, mɔɔ gyae maa gyu ya, mɔna wɔle wɔle nɛ, ɔ kaa kyɛɛkee wa agyueŋ te ɔ gyuuwɔ.

eng: Pilate replied, You have soldiers for guard duty. Go and make it as secure as you know how.
local: Keŋte Pilato yako wɔ nyiaa, ɛ lese ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ balaŋ na baa gyu ka deke yebɔɔ keŋ nideli fa ŋon ŋgba mena keŋ baa tale nɛ.

eng: Then Pharisees and legal experts came to Jesus from Jerusalem and said.
local: Keŋte Farasiitena na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ baale korowɔ lee Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man gyu ke bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa.

eng: The father said the same thing to the other son, who replied, Yes, sir. But he didnt go.
local: Keŋte balee ŋon gyuuwɔ ɔ bu kemaasa ŋon gyaŋ ke yako ŋon mɔ mena ke. Ɔ bu kemaasa ŋon sɛɛwɔ nyi, Yoo taata me nyiiwɔ, maa gyu, mɔna ɔ te gyu ya.

eng: Then they went and secured the tomb by sealing the stone and posting the guard.
local: Mena dɔɔ be gyuuwɔ ke desina yebɔɔ keŋ tɔ nideli keŋ nyi ŋolo na ŋolo be tale tɛɛse boe keŋ lee ke nɔɔ man ya. Keŋte ba lese balaŋ nyi baa gyee yebɔɔ keŋ nideli.

eng: Why are your disciples breaking the elders rules handed down to us? They dont ritually purify their hands by washing before they eat.
local: Weera dɔɔ te n kaseela baŋ bee di da beneŋ amanberɛse dɔɔ ya? Nawolo nyi bɔɔ ne faafo be nyiŋmaase ŋgba mena keŋ da beneŋ amanberɛse nawolo nɛ pɛte baa di ya.

eng: Which one of these two did his fathers will? They said, The first one. Jesus said to them, I assure you that tax collectors and prostitutes are entering Gods kingdom ahead of you.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, ɛ wola maŋ ya agyueŋ. Bia bala kɛwɔ man na, woŋti yɛɛna ɔ kya ŋon kegyaebii? Te balaŋ baŋ tiranɔɔ fae e nyiaa, Bu kegyia ŋon. Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, lempoolɛɛra na kafoŋtena te taŋgbɛɛ ŋon bɛɛ gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you break the command of God by keeping the rules handed down to you?.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te ɛmɛɛ mɔ, lee ɛ beneŋ amanberɛse dɔɔ te ɛ bee di Wurubuarɛ mmaraase dɔɔ ya.

eng: For John came to you on the righteous road, and you didnt believe him. But tax collectors and prostitutes believed him. Yet even after you saw this, you didnt change your hearts and lives and you didnt believe him.
local: Nawolo nyi Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon kɔŋawɔ kaa wola ŋon gbɛɛ kpaakpaate keŋ ɛ ka mo, mɔna ɛ te lɛɛ e di ya. Lempoolɛɛra na kafoŋtena baŋ dɔɔ, be lɛɛ e di. Kenaŋ wɔle ɛ kena mena koraŋ nɛ, ɛ te kyɛɛkee ya agyueŋ na ɛ ke lɛɛ e di ya.

eng: For God said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ yakowɔ nyiaa, Bu n kya na n naa, te nyaŋ ŋon n falaa n kya yaa n naa na, kaboena nyi baa ko neŋ.

eng: Listen to another parable. There was a landowner who planted a vineyard. He put a fence around it, dug a winepress in it, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ nyii dudu daale bela. Wɔɔfarale ŋolo fara wɔɔ te ɔ duu daasebia ŋaale ŋan ŋe yɛɛ ŋgba akutuse te bɛɛ baake ŋa nyi gerepo nɛ. Ɔ dii abaŋ kilisi wɔɔ keŋ, te ɔ wula bɔɔ keŋ baa sara daasebia ŋan ke man na baa lese ŋa loŋ lee ŋe man nɛ. Te ɔ baŋa deni dɔɔloŋ dɔɔloŋ daale, keŋ walaŋ ke kyaa ke man na waa gyee wɔɔ kenaŋ. Te ɔ moo wɔɔ keŋ do paasetena baale nyiŋmaa man nyi baa kɛɛ ke dɔɔ, te ɔ korowɔ laŋ gbɛɛ.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you Im giving to God as a gift,then you dont have to honor your father.
local: Mɔna ɛmɛɛ ne yako nyiaa, Akpaa ŋolo de yako ɔ kya yaa ɔ naa nyi, Kpene keŋ nafɔ me dana maa fa neŋ nɛ, yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ wui.

eng: When it was time for harvest, he sent his servants to the tenant farmers to collect his fruit.
local: Debaŋ kelii nyi baa kɔ daasebia ŋan nɛ, ɔ kpila ɔ tewulɛɛ gyu paasetena baŋ gyaŋ nyi baa fa e ɔ wui nɔɔ.

eng: So you do away with Gods Law for the sake of the rules that have been handed down to you.
local: Mena dɔɔ be kaboena nyi ɔtenate waa mo kpene kenaŋ fa ɔ lolala ya. Nnɛ na ɛ ne yako baa nyi ɔtenate na bu ɔ kya na ɔ naa na waa yɛɛ kpene keŋ kaboena nyi waa yɛɛ fa wɔ nɛ ya. Kei nawolo baa nyi lee ɛ beneŋ amanberɛse dɔɔ, ɛ te bɛɛ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ kpa yaa yɛɛ nɛ.

eng: But the tenant farmers grabbed his servants. They beat some of them, and some of them they killed. Some of them they stoned to death.
local: Botɔɔ te paasetena baŋ kyaŋa ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ, te be tuku ŋolo te ba koo ŋolo te be fuŋii ŋolo mɔ boya.

eng: Hypocrites! Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you.
local: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ɔ be tɛɛ ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya dɔɔ yako see lee ɛ wose man nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ. Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa.

eng: Again he sent other servants, more than the first group. They treated them in the same way.
local: Keŋte wɔɔte ŋon besewɔ kpila ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ ba boe kela gyaŋgba nyibaŋ baŋ gyu paasetena baŋ gyaŋ, mɔna be yɛɛ baŋ mɔ mena ke.

eng: This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: Balaŋ kɛwɔ na mo be nɔɔ bo yako nyi bee bu maŋ, mɔna be konɔɔse man na, bɔɔ na mo maŋ yɛɛ kolo ya.

eng: Finally he sent his son to them. They will respect my son, he said.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, wɔɔte ŋon kpila ŋon gbagba ɔ bu te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Mberem ŋon na baa bu e.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human rules .
local: Bɛɛ som maŋ bo yakaa, nawolo nyi mmaraase ŋan denibalaŋ gbagba be kyaa yɛɛ nɛ, ŋan te bɛɛ mo wola balaŋ beo wɔ nyi ŋe yɛɛ maŋ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋa. .

eng: Jesus responded by speaking again in parables.
local: Yesu besewɔ kolosi fa balaŋ baŋ aduya man nyiaa.

eng: But when the tenant farmers saw the son, they said to each other, This is the heir. Come on, lets kill him and well have his inheritance.
local: Mɔna paasetena baŋ kena ɔ bu ŋon nɛ be yako dɔŋa nyiaa, Kei yɛna bu ŋon waa kaa di ɔ kya abɔɔ ŋan. Ɛ yela dɛɛ ko e na abɔɔ ŋan nafɔ waa di nɛ ka bese de wui.

eng: Jesus called the crowd near and said to them, Listen and understand.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ baake balaŋ baŋ pou kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ popou ɛ tei me gyaŋ, na ɛ ke nyii nombia kɛŋa man nideli.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a king who prepared a wedding party for his son.
local: Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba gyoo ŋon ɔ ba do kesaa fa ɔ bu ŋon ɔ ba kpeo alo nɛ.

eng: They grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
local: Mena dɔɔ be wɔŋee e lee wɔɔ keŋ man ka ko.

eng: Its not what goes into the mouth that contaminates a person in Gods sight. Its what comes out of the mouth that contaminates the person.
local: Na kpene keŋ na gyoo walaŋ nɔɔman nɛ ne yɛɛ na e ayimɛɛ ya, mɔna kpene keŋ na lee ɔ nɔɔman nɛ, keŋ ne yɛɛ na e ayimɛɛ.

eng: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and his disciples.
local: Keŋte Yesu kolosiwɔ fa balaŋ dikpii keŋ na ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa.

eng: He sent his servants to call those invited to the wedding party. But they didnt want to come.
local: Keŋte ɔ kpila ɔ tewulɛɛ nyiaa, baa gyu ke baake balaŋ baŋ ɔ be lɔ yele baake wɔ nɛ nyi baa kɔŋ na be kaa di, mɔna be bɛe ba te kɔŋ ya.

eng: When the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenant farmers.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Mena dɔɔ wee keŋ wɔɔte ŋon de kɔŋ na, woŋ te waa yɛɛ paasetena baŋ.

eng: Then the disciples came and said to him, Do you know that the Pharisees were offended by what you just said.
local: Keŋte Yesu kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse nyiaa, Ŋgba n gyeŋ nyi debaŋ keŋ Farasiitena baŋ be nyii nombia ŋan na, te yɛɛ wɔ gyoŋ ya.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees sit on Moses seat.
local: Gyudatena mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ yɛna balaŋ baŋ be dana gbɛɛ nyii baa tale lese Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan man.

eng: Again he sent other servants and said to them, Tell those who have been invited,Look, the meal is all prepared. Ive butchered the oxen and the fattened cattle. Now everythings ready. Come to the wedding party.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔ besewɔ kpila ɔ tewulɛɛ wɔlɛɛ baale bela nyiaa, ɛ gyu ke yako balaŋ baŋ mɔɔ lɔ yele baake wɔ nɛ nyiaa, Ɛ kɔŋ, ma te taŋ kpene kamasɛ. Be ta koe me nunkyue baala na me wonembia baŋ be dana nuŋ nɛ, te ba te yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ mɔ taŋ. Ɛ kɔŋ alowɔle keŋ tɛɛ na ɛ kaa di.

eng: They said, He will totally destroy those wicked farmers and rent the vineyard to other tenant farmers who will give him the fruit when its ready.
local: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Waa koe mena balaŋ kumɛɛ benaŋ pou, na waa mo wɔɔ keŋ do paasetena wɔlɛɛ baŋ debaŋ de lii na, baa fa e ɔ wui nɔɔ nɛ nyiŋmaa man.

eng: Jesus replied, Every plant that my heavenly Father didnt plant will be pulled up.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Daŋ kamasɛ keŋ ka te yɛɛ nyi me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ duu na ke ya na, waa kulu ke na ke lila pou ke leki.

eng: Therefore, you must take care to do everything they say. But dont do what they do.
local: Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi yaa tei wɔ, na ɛ ke sila kpene kamasɛ keŋ bɛɛ wola ŋon nɛ. Mɔna ɛ na yɛɛ kpene keŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ ya, nawolo nyi bɔɔ ne di be kawola keŋ dɔɔ ya.

eng: But they paid no attention and went away—some to their fields, others to their businesses.
local: Mɔna balaŋ baŋ ɔ be lɔ yele baake nɛ ta mo ɔ nombia ŋan yɛɛ kolo ya. Kei da koro na ɔ te laŋ ɔ wɔɔ, kei mɔ da koro na ɔ te laŋ ɔ tom man.

eng: Jesus said to them, Havent you ever read in the scriptures, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes?.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ te kala ta Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan na ɛ kena kpene keŋ ne yako nɛ yee? Be ŋmarasewɔ see nyiaa, Boe keŋ deni mmaraa baŋ be bɛɛ nɛ, keŋ kaa bese na mokɔɔ kasee boe keŋ ne yeli deni keŋ ne yɛɛ doŋ. De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ la te kɔŋawɔ mena, te ke yɛɛ kpene keŋ ke dana gyakoloŋ de siaman .

eng: Leave the Pharisees alone. They are blind people who are guides to blind people. But if a blind person leads another blind person, they will both fall into a ditch.
local: Ɛ tina Farasiitena benaŋ yela botɔɔ, be yɛɛ ŋgba siayɛlɛɛsatena baŋ bɛɛ wola siayɛlɛɛsatena gbɛɛ nɛ. Akpaa siayɛlɛɛsate na wola siayɛlɛɛsate gbɛɛ nɛ, be bala pou baa kaa yala bɔɔ man bo.

eng: For they tie together heavy packs that are impossible to carry. They put them on the shoulders of others, but are unwilling to lift a finger to move them.
local: Bɛɛ bake kasolɔse ŋan kela balaŋ nɛ yere wɔ, mɔna baŋ gbagba bɛɛ sɛɛ yɛɛ kolo kyomii kyɔ wɔ na baa tale seele be kasolɔse ŋenaŋ ya.

eng: The rest of them grabbed his servants, abused them, and killed them.
local: Balaŋ akaŋ baŋ kyaŋa gyoo ŋon tewulɛɛ baŋ, te be tuku wɔ te ba koe wɔ.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that Gods kingdom will be taken away from you and will be given to a people who produce its fruit.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ waa lɛo ɔ gyoori keŋ lee ɛ gyaŋ, na waa mo fa balaŋ baŋ baa yɛɛ ɔ kegyaebii nɛ.

eng: Then Peter spoke up, Explain this riddle to us.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, lese dudu kei asɛɛ fa daa.

eng: Everything they do, they do to be noticed by others. They make extra-wide prayer bands for their arms and long tassels for their clothes.
local: Kpene kamasɛ keŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ, bɛɛ yɛɛ bo na balaŋ ke nyiŋ naa wɔ. Wonembu tɔne adakaasebia ŋan bɛɛ yɛɛ te bɛɛ ŋmarase Wurubuarɛ nombia do ŋe man te bɛɛ mo mataa be tirinɔɔse na be baamense dɔɔ nɛ, bee yeli be wui ŋan na keeree. Te ŋmeeseŋ ŋan bɛɛ mo gbili ba kegba wurikyimse ŋan nɔɔsebia te ŋee wuluŋu nɛ, bee yeli be wui ne yɛɛ dɔɔloŋ dɔɔloŋ.

eng: The king was angry. He sent his soldiers to destroy those murderers and set their city on fire.
local: Mena dɔɔ gyoo ŋon gyeŋa baŋ nideli te ɔ kpila ɔ yoonɔɔtena te be ka koe balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ koe ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ nɛ, te ba too be donɔɔ keŋ mɔ.

eng: Whoever falls on this stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ yale mena boe kei dɔɔ na, gyae ke keretii e kpetekpetekpete. Te nyaŋ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon mɔ boe keŋ de yale n dɔɔ na, nyaŋ ke pɛtɛɛ.

eng: Jesus said, Dont you understand yet?.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Keŋ dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ mɔ nyeeya ta yem?.

eng: They love to sit in places of honor at banquets and in the synagogues.
local: Bɛɛ gyae akyaese kpaakpaa be dɛɛgyaŋee di gyoŋ, na Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man.

eng: Then he said to his servants, The wedding party is prepared, but those who were invited werent worthy.
local: Keŋte gyoo ŋon baake ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ bɔɔ kaa nɛ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ba te saa ma alowɔle weenɛɛ ŋan taŋ, mɔna balaŋ baŋ mɔɔ lɔ yele baake wɔ nɛ, ba tekaboena nyi baa kɔŋ kaa di ya.

eng: Now when the chief priests and the Pharisees heard the parable, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
local: Saese kegyiise na Farasiitena baŋ ke nyii Yesu aduya kɛŋa nɛ, ba gyeŋawɔ nyi ba wose man nombia yaa ɔ ne yako.

eng: Dont you know that everything that goes into the mouth enters the stomach and goes out into the sewer?.
local: Keŋ dɔɔ ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi kpene kamasɛ keŋ da gyoo walaŋ nɔɔman na, ne gyu ka gyoo ɔ dubulu man na ke ta bese lee ɔ wose man yee?.

eng: They love to be greeted with honor in the markets and to be addressed as Rabbi.
local: Bɛɛ gyae nyi balaŋ ke buŋii do wɔ nɔɔ balaŋ dɛɛgyaŋee man, te bɛɛ gyae mɔ nyi balaŋ ke baake wɔ nyi Wolala.

eng: Therefore, go to the roads on the edge of town and invite everyone you find to the wedding party.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ ŋere gyoo donɔɔ keŋ abɔnterese man, na ɛ ke baake walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɛ kena nɛ, nyi waa kɔŋ alowɔle keŋ tɛɛ.

eng: They were trying to arrest him, but they feared the crowds, who thought he was a prophet.
local: Mena dɔɔ be gyae gbɛɛ keŋ baa mo kyaŋ e, mɔna be yee balaŋ dikpii keŋ. Nawolo nyi bɛɛ lɛɛ di nideli nyi Yesu yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e.

eng: But what goes out of the mouth comes from the heart. And thats what contaminates a person in Gods sight.
local: Mɔna nombii kamasɛ keŋ na lee walaŋ nɔɔman nɛ, na lee bo ɔ konɔɔ man, te keŋ ne yɛɛ na e ayimɛɛ.

eng: But you shouldnt be called Rabbi , because you have one teacher, and all of you are brothers and sisters.
local: Mɔna ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ, ɛ na yele baa baake ŋon nyi Wolala ya, nawolo nyi ɛ popou dana wolale dokoloŋ kooŋ bo, te ɛ yɛɛ walaŋ dokoloŋ mɔ biawɔ.

eng: Then those servants went to the roads and gathered everyone they found, both evil and good. The wedding party was full of guests.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ gyoowɔ donɔɔ keŋ man te be baake balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ naa nɛ. Be baake balaŋ kpaakpaa nyibaŋ na balaŋ kumɛɛ pou, te balaŋ kaa wulu alowɔle dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man.

eng: Out of the heart come evil thoughts, murders, adultery, sexual sins, thefts, false testimonies, and insults.
local: Nawolo nyi walaŋ konɔɔ man te nombiakumɛɛ ŋgba, balaŋ kako, na yale kewɔlɛɛ na kafoŋ na ŋmɛɛleŋ na balaŋ nɔɔ kekɛrɛɛ na balaŋ yela kewɔlɛɛ na lee.

eng: Now when the king came in and saw the guests, he spotted a man who wasnt wearing wedding clothes.
local: Mɔna gyoo ŋon ke kɔŋ kaa gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man ɔ kaa kɛɛ balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ baake nɛ, ɔ naa baale ŋolo kyaa be man keŋ ɔ ba doona alowɔletɛɛ kegba keŋ kaboena nyi walaŋ kamasɛ waa do nɛ ya.

eng: Dont call anybody on earth your father, because you have one Father, who is heavenly.
local: Te ɛ na baake ŋolo mɔ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyi ɛ Kya ya, nawolo nyi ɛ dana kya dokoloŋ kooŋ bo. Ŋon yɛna ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ.

eng: These contaminate a person in Gods sight. But eating without washing hands doesnt contaminate in Gods sight.
local: Mena abɔɔ kɛŋa ne yɛɛ na walaŋ ayimɛɛ, mɔna akpaa ŋolo te faafo ɔ nyiŋmaase pɛte waa di ya nɛ, ke bɛɛ yɛɛ e ayimɛɛ ya.

eng: He said to him, Friend, how did you get in here without wedding clothes? But he was speechless.
local: Keŋte gyoo ŋon bɔɔse e nyiaa, Me gyoo, sena te n yɛɛwɔ kɔŋ kɛbo keŋ n ba doona alowɔletɛɛ kegba ya? Mɔna baale ŋon te tale tiranɔɔ fa e ya.

eng: Dont be called teacher , because Christ is your one teacher.
local: Mena ke te ɛ na yele baa baake ɛ man ŋolo nyi Siamante ya, nawolo nyi ɛ dana siamante dokoloŋ bo. Ŋon yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: From there, Jesus went to the regions of Tyre and Sidon.
local: Yesu ka lee botɔɔ nɛ, ɔ wɔŋa ɔ wose lee botɔɔ gyu Tae na Sidɔn donɔɔse ŋan yenaŋ.

eng: Now Jesus left the temple and was going away. His disciples came to point out to him the temple buildings.
local: Yesu ka lee Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ɔ ne gyu nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ wɔŋa wa agyueŋ kɔŋ denɛɛ ŋan seŋɛɛ botɔɔ nɛ kyeo dɔɔ.

eng: Then the king said to his servants, Tie his hands and feet and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Botɔɔ te gyoo ŋon yako ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ nyi, Ɛ bake e ɔ nyiŋmaase na ɔ nawɔɔ, na ɛ ke fuŋ e leki ditiŋtɛɛ man. Botɔɔ te waa kyaŋ ɔ nyee wii na waa naa diyem nideli.

eng: But the one who is greatest among you will be your servant.
local: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ kegyia e ɛ man nɛ, waa bese ɛ kegbetibii.

eng: A Canaanite woman from those territories came out and shouted, Show me mercy, Son of David. My daughter is suffering terribly from demon possession.
local: Keŋte Kaanan alo ŋolo leewɔ botɔɔ yenaŋ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ kaa wii fa e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii, wii me waraŋase! Feliŋkum doo me bu alebubii man, te kɛɛ naase e diyem nideli.

eng: He responded, Do you see all these things? I assure that no stone will be left on another. Everything will be demolished.
local: Keŋte ɔ tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ŋgba ɛ ne naa abɔɔ kɛŋa pou. Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, baa tekerii ŋe popou lɔ keŋ nyi ɛ be gyae ɛ kena boe na boe gyakaa ke dɔɔ dɔɔ ya.

eng: Many people are invited, but few people are chosen.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ baake balaŋ burum, mɔna balaŋ kyomii te ɔ lesewɔ lee be man.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low. But all who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ gyina ɔ wose adido na, Wurubuarɛ waa yɔkɔse e, te ŋon ɔ yɔkɔse ɔ wose na, Wurubuarɛ waa gyina adido.

eng: But he didnt respond to her at all. His disciples came and urged him, Send her away; she keeps shouting out after us.
local: Mɔna Yesu te yako daale ya. Mena dɔɔ ɔ kaseela baŋ waase e nyiaa, Lɔ alo ŋon gbɛɛ na waa ta, nawolo nyi ɔ ne fae yeesa tɔ daa deŋela.

eng: Now while Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately and said, Tell us, when will these things happen? What will be the sign of your coming and the end of the age.
local: Keŋte Yesu gyuuwɔ ke kyaa oliifi bula keŋ dɔɔ. Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ baageŋ korowɔ gyu ɔ gyaŋ te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo yako daa, debaŋ woŋti te kɛŋa pou gyae ke kɔŋ. Woŋ te dɛɛ mo gyeŋ nyi n kabese kɔŋ, na debaŋ ɔtomante keŋ ta benaa.

eng: Then the Pharisees met together to find a way to trap Jesus in his words.
local: Keŋte Farasiitena baŋ korowɔ lee yenaŋ gyu ke bake Yesu nyeeman, mena keŋ baa kaa teese ɔ nɔɔman na baa mo ŋon gbagba ɔ nɔɔwoya kyaŋ e.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You shut people out of the kingdom of heaven. You dont enter yourselves, and you wont allow those who want to enter to do so.
local: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, ɛ laako. Nawolo nyi ɛ ne tɔ balaŋ gbɛɛ nyi be na gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man ya. Ɛmɛɛ gbagba bɛɛ gyoo ke man ya, mɔna baŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ baa gyoo ke man mɔ nɛ, ɛ bɛɛ fa wɔ gbɛɛ ya.

eng: Jesus replied, Ive been sent only to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako alo ŋon nyiaa, Iseraetena baŋ bɔɔ yoo Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛ nɛ baageŋ gyaŋ te Wurubuarɛ kpila maŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose dɔɔ nideli keŋ nyi ŋolo ba beo ŋon ya.

eng: They sent their disciples, along with the supporters of Herod, to him. Teacher, they said, we know that you are genuine and that you teach Gods way as it really is. We know that you are not swayed by peoples opinions, because you dont show favoritism.
local: Mena dɔɔ be kpila be kaseela baŋ man baale kpu na gyoo Herode balaŋ gyu Yesu gyaŋ. Keŋte be yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, da gyeŋ nyi n nombia doo na ŋe gbɛɛ, te n na wola balaŋ Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛneŋ kpaakpaa ŋan bo na anokoare. Da gyeŋ nyi ŋolo be tale beo neŋ ya, nawolo nyi n bɛɛ kɛɛ balaŋ siaman ya.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You travel over sea and land to make one convert. But when theyve been converted, they become twice the child of hell you are.
local: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, ɛ laako. Nawolo nyi ɛ na mo ɛpo dɔɔ, kila tɛɛle dɔɔ pou nyi ɛ ke nyiŋ walaŋ dokoloŋ na ɔ kaa sila Wurubuarɛ. Mɔna akpaa ɛ nyiŋ walaŋ ŋonaŋ na, ɛ ne yeli ɔ dinɔɔ na bese wɔlɛɛ koraŋ keŋ nyi diyem keŋ waa naa boalaŋ man nɛ, kela ɛmɛɛ gbagba wui betabola.

eng: But she knelt before him and said, Lord, help me.
local: Mɔna alo ŋon kɔŋawɔ kaa buŋ Yesu nawɔɔ tɛɛ waase e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, lɛɛ maŋ.

eng: Many will come in my name, saying, Im the Christ. They will deceive many people.
local: Nawolo nyi balaŋ burum baa kɔŋ ma yele man, na be kaa mo ba wose nyi baŋ yɛna maŋ nyeelɛɛre ŋon, na baa beo balaŋ burum na baa yo gbɛɛ.

eng: So tell us what you think: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Mena dɔɔ yako daa nya agyueŋ. Dei nyi dɛɛ tɔ lempoo fa Romantena gyoo dinaa ŋon, yaa de na tɔɔ ya.

eng: How terrible it will be for you blind guides who say, If people swear by the temple, its nothing. But if people swear by the gold in the temple, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Ɛmɛɛ siayɛlɛɛsatena baŋ ɛ na wola balaŋ gbɛɛ nɛ, ɛ laako nawolo nyi ɛ kpa akpaa ŋolo de kaŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ ntam na ka te yɛɛ kolo ya, mɔna akpaa ŋolo de kaŋ kɔba yayam abɔɔ ŋan doo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ntam na, kaboena nyi waa di ntam kenaŋ dɔɔ.

eng: He replied, It is not good to take the childrens bread and toss it to dogs.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako alo ŋon nyiaa, Tekaboena nyi baa mo bia weenɛɛ fa babaase ya.

eng: You will hear about wars and reports of wars. Dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Ɛ nyii yoo nombia na, ɛ na yele ɛ wɔe ke lɔ ŋon ya. Nawolo nyi kaboena nyi nombia kɛŋa pou dɛɛkɔŋ, mɔna kɛŋa bɛɛ wolo nyi ɔto keŋ te lii ya.

eng: Knowing their evil motives, Jesus replied, Why do you test me, you hypocrites?.
local: Mɔna Yesu kena ba agyueŋ kumɛɛ ŋan nɛ ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ ne gyae ɛ ka teese me nɔɔman.

eng: You foolish and blind people! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold holy?.
local: Ɛmɛɛ siayɛlɛɛsatena ayiŋtena, woŋti kela na ke dɔɔ? Kɔba yayam abɔɔ ŋan yaa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ ne yele kɔba abɔɔ ŋan na bese Wurubuarɛ wui nɛ?.

eng: She said, Yes, Lord. But even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall off their masters table.
local: Botɔɔ te alo ŋon mɔ sɛɛwɔ nyiaa, Ayi, me Gbeŋgyoo, doo mena, mɔna babaase baa tale fiu weenɛɛ ŋan be gbeŋgyoo ne dii te ŋɛɛ yale tɛɛle man nɛ di.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be famines and earthquakes in all sorts of places.
local: Tɛɛlese baa koro seŋ tia dɔŋa, na gyooneŋ mɔ ka koro seŋ tia dɔŋa. Tanam dinaa gyae ke yala, te tɛɛle mɔ gyae kewosi yenaase burum.

eng: Show me the coin used to pay the tax. And they brought him a denarion.
local: Ɛ mo kɔbabii keŋ ɛ na mo tɔ lempoo nɛ kɔŋ maa kɛɛ. Keŋte ba moo kɔbabii keŋ daale kɔna e.

eng: You say, If people swear by the altar, its nothing. But if they swear by the gift on the altar, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Ɛ na bese yako nyi, akpaa ŋolo de kaŋ Wurubuarɛ kedeesa boe keŋ ntam na te yɛɛ kolo ya, mɔna akpaa ɔ kaŋ abɔɔ ŋan gyakaa kedeesa boe keŋ dɔɔ nɛ ntam na, kaboena nyi waa di ntam keŋ dɔɔ.

eng: Jesus answered, Woman, you have great faith. It will be just as you wish. And right then her daughter was healed.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako alo ŋon nyiaa, Alo n kelɛɛdi boe. Kpene keŋ n ne gyae nɛ, dɛɛkɔŋ mena fa neŋ. Debokenaŋ man te ɔ bu ŋon kawee taŋawɔ.

eng: But all these things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Kɛŋa pou yɛɛ yaa ŋgba fulute kadoŋee kewalaŋ nɛ.

eng: Whose image and inscription is this? he asked.
local: Keŋte ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Amɔte nyee na ɔ yele gyakaa la ke dɔɔ.

eng: You blind people! Which is greater, the gift or the altar that makes the gift holy?.
local: Ɛmɛɛ siayɛlɛɛsatena, woŋti kela na ke dɔɔ? Abɔɔ ŋan gyakaa kedeesa boe keŋ dɔɔ nɛ, yaa kedeesa boe keŋ ne yele kedeesa abɔɔ ŋan na bese Wurubuarɛ wui nɛ?.

eng: Jesus moved on from there along the shore of the Galilee Sea. He went up a mountain and sat down.
local: Yesu ka lee botɔɔ nɛ, te ɔ tɛɛwɔ na Galelia boo keŋ nɔɔ nɔɔ. Keŋte ɔ gyuuwɔ ka gyeŋ bulabii daale gyu ke kyaa ke dɔɔ.

eng: They will arrest you, abuse you, and they will kill you. All nations will hate you on account of my name.
local: Lee maŋ dɔɔ balaŋ pou baa kɔla ŋon, na baa kyaŋee ŋon na baa naase ŋon diyem na baa koe ŋon.

eng: Caesars, they replied. Then he said, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Botɔɔ te be tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Romantena gyoo dinaa ŋon. Mena dɔɔ Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Mena na kpene keŋ yɛɛ Gyoo dinaa ŋon wui na ɛ mo fa e, te kpene keŋ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ mɔ wui na ɛ mo fa e.

eng: Therefore, those who swear by the altar swear by it and by everything thats on it.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ kaŋ Wurubuarɛ kedeesa boe keŋ ntam na, nawolo baa nyi ɔ te kaŋ abɔɔ ŋan pou gyakaa ke dɔɔ nɛ ntam.

eng: Large crowds came to him, including those who were paralyzed, blind, injured, and unable to speak, and many others. They laid them at his feet, and he healed them.
local: Balaŋ burum moo be kaweesetena kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ. Kaweese ŋgba baŋ be nawɔɔ ba yekee, na baŋ be sia be yɛlɛɛ na ɔtakasese na omumuuse na kaweese kpookpoo nɛ. Keŋte Yesu kyɔɔ be popou.

eng: At that time many will fall away. They will betray each other and hate each other.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ man te balaŋ burum baa tina maŋ kelɛɛdi yela. Baa kɔla dɔŋa, na baa lese dɔŋa fa.

eng: When they heard this they were astonished, and they departed.
local: Be ke nyii Yesu nɔɔwoya kɛŋa nɛ, doo be nɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ be laŋawɔ tina e yela.

eng: Those who swear by the temple swear by it and by everything thats part of it.
local: Te walaŋ ŋon ɔ kaŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ ntam na, nawolo baa nyi ɔ te kaŋ keŋ na Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa ke man nɛ ntam.

eng: So the crowd was amazed when they saw those who had been unable to speak talking, and the paralyzed cured, and the injured walking, and the blind seeing. And they praised the God of Israel.
local: Balaŋ baŋ kena nyi baŋ be nawɔɔ ba yekee ne tɛɛ, te omumuuse ne kolosi te ɔtakasese wose ne yɛɛ wɔ doŋ, te siayɛlɛɛsatena sia ne naa nɛ, doo be nɔɔ. Keŋte ba lese Iseraetena Wurubuarɛ ŋon yele.

eng: Many false prophets will appear and deceive many people.
local: Akɛrɛɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baa kɔŋ, na be kaa beo balaŋ burum.

eng: That same day Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus.
local: Mena wee kenaŋ ke nɛ, Sadukiitena baŋ bɛɛ yako nyi yeŋ wɔle kefoŋ be kyaa ya nɛ kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa.

eng: Those who swear by heaven swear by Gods throne and by the one who sits on it.
local: Mena mɔ te walaŋ ŋon ɔ kaŋ adido ntam na, nawolo baa nyi ɔ te kaŋ Wurubuarɛ gyoorogbeli keŋ, na Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa ke dɔɔ nɛ ntam.

eng: Now Jesus called his disciples and said, I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat. I dont want to send them away hungry for fear they wont have enough strength to travel.
local: Keŋte Yesu baake ɔ kaseela baŋ kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Balaŋ kɛwɔ nombia dana maŋ waraŋase, nawolo nyi gyɛŋ weeya kpaakpaa atooro yaa ŋgba be kyaa me gyaŋ te be te dii ta kolo ya nɛ. Mɔɔ ne gyae maa lɔ wɔ gbɛɛ na tanam ya, nawolo nyi be sia de kaa biri wɔ gbɛɛnaa man.

eng: Because disobedience will expand, the love of many will grow cold.
local: Keŋ nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ gyae ka bo dɔɔ, balaŋ burum balaŋ kegyae nɔɔ gyae ke yɔkɔse.

eng: They asked, Teacher, Moses said, If a man who doesnt have children dies, his brother must marry his wife and produce children for his brother.
local: De Gbeŋgyoo, de naana Mosesi ŋmarase mmaraa kei fa daa nyi, akpaa ŋolo da yem tina ɔ wɛɛle yela te be ta lolo bia ya na, kaboena nyi ɔ naabu waa yala lialo ŋon na baa lola bia ɔ kegyia ŋon yele man.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You give to God a tenth of mint, dill, and cumin, but you forget about the more important matters of the Law: justice, peace, and faith. You ought to give a tenth but without forgetting about those more important matters.
local: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, ɛ laako. Nawolo nyi ɛ na mo ɛ wɔɔman abɔɔ buruburu gba kufu man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ fa Wurubuarɛ, mɔna ɛ te tina kpene keŋ ne hia nyi yaa yɛɛ mmaraa keŋ man nɛ yela. Ɛ bee di nombia na ŋe gbɛɛ ya, te ɛ bee wii balaŋ waraŋase ya, te ɛ bee di anokoare mɔ ya. Nafɔ kaboena nyi yaa yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou, na ɛ ka bese fa ɛ wɔɔman abɔɔ buruburu ŋan kufu man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ bela kpu.

eng: His disciples replied, Where are we going to get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy such a big crowd.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse e nyiaa, Aleŋ yaa dee nyiŋ weenɛɛ fa balaŋ burum kɛwɔ ŋale nsana kɛbo.

eng: But the one who endures to the end will be delivered.
local: Mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ kakyeŋ seŋ keŋkeŋ kelii ɔto na, Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ ɔ nyee.

eng: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first one married, then died. Because he had no children he left his widow to his brother.
local: Kɔŋawɔ nyi naa bia nyetooro baale kyaawɔ te kegyia ŋon yala alo te ɔ yeŋawɔ. Mɔna keŋ be ta lola bia dɔɔ ya nɛ, te balate ŋon mɔ yala alo ŋon.

eng: You blind guides! You filter out an ant but swallow a camel.
local: Ɛmɛɛ siayɛlɛɛsatena baŋ ɛ dɔŋ balaŋ siaman. Ɛ ne tale kyɔsee lese wonembia buruburu lee ɛ soloŋse man, mɔna ɛ ne lilii wonembia dinaana.

eng: Jesus said, How much bread do you have? They responded, Seven loaves and a few fish.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Bodobodose aŋmoŋ te ɛ dana? Te be tiranɔɔ fa e nyi, Bodobodose nyetooro na kpebia buruburu akalansɛɛ baale te de dana.

eng: This gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the world as a testimony to all the nations. Then the end will come.
local: Baa yako nombia kpaakpaa lee Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man fa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou, na debiise pou balaŋ ke nyii, pɛte ɔto keŋ ke kɔŋ.

eng: The same thing happened with the second brother and the third, and in fact with all seven brothers.
local: Ɔ naabu balate na batoorote mɔ yeŋawɔ yela alo ŋon mena keŋ be ta lola bia ya. Balaŋ nyetooro kɛwɔ pou yala alo kei te ba yekeewɔ, mɔna be man ŋolo na alo ŋon ta lolo ya.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and plate, but inside they are full of violence and pleasure seeking.
local: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, ɛ laako. Nawolo nyi ɛ ne faafo ɛ leenɔɔse na ɛ nyefalɛɛ wɔle bo, mɔna ŋe man dana ayimɛɛ. Nawolo nyi abɔɔ ŋan doo ŋe man nɛ, anibiri na doŋ te ɛ na mo ŋere balaŋ lɛo ŋa.

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground.
local: Keŋte Yesu yela balaŋ baŋ tisiwɔ kyakee tɛɛle man.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing that Daniel talked about standing in the holy place.
local: Akpaa ɛ naa mmusuo nombii Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Daniɛle dɔɔ kolosi see lee ka wose man nɛ, na kaboena nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa kala mena nɔɔwoya ŋenaŋ na, waa yɛɛ kakyeŋ na waa nyii ŋe man nideli.

eng: Finally, the woman died.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, te alo ŋon mɔ yeŋawɔ.

eng: Blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup so that the outside of the cup will be clean too.
local: Ɛmɛɛ Farasiitena siayɛlɛɛsatena, ɛ taŋgbɛɛ faafo ɛ leenɔɔse na ɛ nyefalɛɛ ŋan tɔɔman na, ŋe wɔle mɔ gyae ka lee.

eng: He took the seven loaves of bread and the fish. After he gave thanks, he broke them into pieces and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: Ɔ moo bodobodose nyetooro ŋan, na kpebia baŋ te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ, te ɔ kɛrɛɛ ŋa mo fa ɔ kaseela baŋ. Keŋte ɔ kaseela baŋ mɔ moo ŋa kpɛlɛɛ balaŋ baŋ.

eng: then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: De kɔŋ mena na, balaŋ baŋ be kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ, baa yeu gyu bulase dɔɔ.

eng: At the resurrection, which of the seven brothers will be her husband? They were all married to her.
local: Mena dɔɔ wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ yem nɛ lee yeŋ man na, baala nyetooro kɛwɔ baŋ bɔɔ yala alo kei gyaŋee nɛ, be man woŋti te alo ŋon waa yɛɛ ɔ wɛɛle.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs. They look beautiful on the outside. But inside they are full of dead bones and all kinds of filth.
local: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, ɛ laako! Nawolo nyi ɛ yɛɛ ŋgba yebɔɔse ŋan bɔɔ mo kolo wolom gyilaa ŋe wɔle na ke yɛɛ kyeo nɛ. Mɔna ŋe tɔɔman na, denibalaŋ ŋɔɔ na ba wosenaane adalɛɛ te wulu ŋe man.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full. The disciples collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Balaŋ baŋ pou diiwɔ baa. Te ɔ kaseela baŋ tɔɔsee buruburu akaŋ ŋan na, be nyiŋa doŋase nyetooro.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down to grab things from their houses.
local: Nyi akpaa ŋolo kyaa ɔ deni nyee dɔɔ na, ɔ na tisi kɔŋ tɛɛle man nyi ɔ kaa mo ɔ kolo ya.

eng: Jesus responded, You are wrong because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ ta yoo, nawolo nyi ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ya, te ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ doŋ mɔ ya.

eng: In the same way you look righteous to people. But inside you are full of pretense and rebellion.
local: Mena ke te ɛmɛɛ mɔ doo. Balaŋ siaman na ɛ ne yɛɛ ɛ wose ŋgba ɛ yɛɛ balaŋ kpaakpaawɔ nɛ, mɔna balaŋ kabeo na nombiakumɛɛ te wulu ɛ konɔɔse man.

eng: Four thousand men ate, plus women and children.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ di weenɛɛ ŋan nɛ, be yaanɔɔle yɛɛ baala kakpoŋse banaara . Ba te kala ala na bia kpu ya.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: Te akpaa ŋolo mɔ kyaa wɔɔman na, ɔ na bese gyu dɛɛ nyi ɔ kaa mo ɔ kegba ya.

eng: At the resurrection people wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like angels from God.
local: Nawolo nyi wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu balaŋ lee yeŋ man nɛ, baala be gyae baa yala ya, te bɔɔ gyae baa mo ala mɔ fa yale ya. Be popou baa yɛɛ ŋgba Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ be kyaa adido nɛ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You build tombs for the prophets and decorate the graves of the righteous.
local: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, ɛ laako. Nawolo nyi ɛ ne ma Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra na balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ yebɔɔse nideli, na ŋa ke yɛɛ kyeo.

eng: After dismissing the crowds, Jesus got into the boat and came to the region of Magadan.
local: Yesu ke lɔ balaŋ baŋ gbɛɛ taŋ nɛ, ɔ gyoowɔ degbele man te ɔ gyuu tɛɛle keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Magadan nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Weeya ŋenaŋ man na, fulɛɛtena na talabose baa laako.

eng: As for the resurrection of the dead, havent you read what God told you.
local: Ɛ te kala ta Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan, na ɛ kena kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yako lee kefoŋ lee yeŋ man wose man nombia yee? Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa.

eng: You say, If we had lived in our ancestors days, we wouldnt have joined them in killing the prophets.
local: Te ɛ ne yako nyi, Daa deŋ kyaa la de naanaɔ debaŋ keŋ nɛ, nafɔ deŋ te kpuu na wɔ na baa koe Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ ya.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter or on the Sabbath day.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ fane fa Wurubuarɛ nyi, ɛ keyeu wee kenaŋ na kɔŋ waaretɛɛ yaa kefɛɛfowee ya.

eng: Im the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? He isnt the God of the dead but of the living.
local: Maŋ yɛna Aberaham na Asiki na Gyekɔpo Wurubuarɛ. Wurubuarɛ te yɛɛ woya Wurubuarɛ e ya, mɔna ɔ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ be kyaa na be sia nɛ Wurubuarɛ e.

eng: You testify against yourselves that you are children of those who murdered the prophets.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ nɔɔwoya kɛŋa nawola la nyi, ɛ te sɛɛ nyi balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ koe Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ nɛ, ɛ yɛɛ be duuluŋ man balaŋwɔ.

eng: There will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Nawolo nyi debaŋ kenaŋ wui kayeyɛɛ gyae ka bo kela ŋan pou be kɔŋ kaalaŋ, leenaŋ debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ tɛɛle kei kaa lii gyɛŋ wee kei nɛ. Te kaale kenaŋ dinɔɔ be gyae ka tekaa kɔŋ wee daale wee daale bela ya.
