eng: Now when the crowd heard this, they were astonished at his teaching.
local: Balaŋ dikpii keŋ ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ɔ kawola keŋ doo be nɔɔ.

eng: Go ahead, complete what your ancestors did.
local: Ɛ betaa kyaŋ nombiakumɛɛ ŋan ɛ naanaɔ be yɛɛ nɛ man, na ɛ ke yɛɛ ŋa kelii ɔto.

eng: If that time werent shortened, nobody would be rescued. But for the sake of the ones whom God chose, that time will be cut short.
local: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ taŋ wula kɔkee kayeyɛɛ weeya kɛŋa. Na waa yɛɛ mena ya nɛ, nafɔ ŋolo na ŋolo be gyae waa ka ya. Mɔna lee ɔ balaŋ baŋ ɔ ba lese see dɔɔ nɛ, te waa kɔkee weeya ŋenaŋ.

eng: When the Pharisees heard that Jesus had left the Sadducees speechless, they met together.
local: Farasiitena baŋ ke nyii nyi Yesu ta mo ɔ nyansa di Sadukiitena baŋ dɔɔ nɛ, be popou kaa gyaŋeewɔ debodokoloŋ.

eng: You snakes! You children of snakes! How will you be able to escape the judgment of hell?.
local: Ɛ dana nombiakumɛɛ ŋgba doma nɛ. Ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale yeu lee Wurubuarɛ nyiŋmaa man, keŋ nyi ɔ be bu ŋon fɔɔ na waa yela ɛ ka gyoo boalaŋ man ya.

eng: Then if somebody says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or Hes over here, dont believe it.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ de lii te ŋolo de yako ŋon nyi Ɛ kɛɛ Kristo ŋon kyaa kɛbo yaa ɔ kyaa yenaŋ daale na, ɛ na lɛɛ ke di ya.

eng: One of them, a legal expert, tested him.
local: Keŋte be maŋ ŋolo ŋon ɔ yɛɛ mmaraa wolale e nɛ, kɔŋawɔ ɔ kaa teese Yesu nɔɔman te ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa.

eng: Therefore, look, Im sending you prophets, wise people, and legal experts. Some of them you will kill and crucify. And some you will beat in your synagogues and chase from city to city.
local: Mɛɛ yako ŋon baa ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, maa kpila Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra na siakaralatena na wolala ɛ gyaŋ. Ɛ ka ko be man baale na ɛ ke gyɔ be man baale ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ, na ɛ ke tuku be man baale yɔ ɔsom denɛɛ man na ɛ ke gegi wɔ na donɔɔse.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer great signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, even those whom God has chosen.
local: Nawolo nyi balaŋ baa dii akɛrɛɛ nyi baŋ yɛna maŋ Kristo ŋon, te baale mɔ baa dii akɛrɛɛ nyi be yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔrawɔ. Baa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana, na akpaa be tale nyiŋ gbɛɛ koraŋ na, baa mo beo Wurubuarɛ balaŋ baŋ ɔ ba lese see nɛ.

eng: Teacher, what is the greatest commandment in the Law.
local: De Gbeŋgyoo, Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan pou na, woŋti kela la.

eng: When Jesus finished speaking all these words, he said to his disciples.
local: Yesu kawola abɔɔ kɛŋa pou taŋ nɛ, ɔ yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa.

eng: Therefore, upon you will come all the righteous blood that has been poured out on the earth, from the blood of that righteous man Abel to the blood of Zechariah the son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the temple and the altar.
local: Keŋ ɛ ba koe balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman, lee Abɛle dɔɔ kelii Berekia bu Sakaria ŋon ɛ ba koo kedeesa boe keŋ na Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ nsana nsana dɔɔ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa wɔŋ ɛ deŋela nideli.

eng: Look, Ive told you ahead of time.
local: Mɔna ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ, me te wulaa yako ŋon nombia kɛŋa pɛna debaŋ keŋ ke kɔŋ.

eng: He replied, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, and with all your mind.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Mo n konɔɔ pou na n wose pou na nya agyueŋ pou gyae n Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ.

eng: You know that the Passover is two days from now. And the Human One will be handed over to be crucified.
local: Ŋgba ɛ gyeŋ nyi teka weeya ala na nyeekelɛɛwee kedi keŋ te lii. Debaŋ kenaŋ man te baa lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon fa na baa gyɔ maŋ sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ.

eng: I assure you that all these things will come upon this generation.
local: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, kɛŋa pou deŋele kewɔŋ gyae ke kɔŋ ɛmɛɛ gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ dɔɔ.

eng: So if they say to you, Look, hes in the desert, dont go out. And if they say, Look, hes in the rooms deep inside the house, dont believe it.
local: Mena dɔɔ akpaa ŋolo de yako ŋon nyi, ɛ kɛɛ ɔ kyaa depampaa dinaa keŋ man na, ɛ na gyu botɔɔ ya. Te akpaa be yako ŋon nyi, ɔ te kɔŋ ɔ yoɛ yenaŋ daale na, ɛ na lɛɛ di.

eng: This is the first and greatest commandment.
local: Kei yɛna mmaraa siamante na keŋ ka kela ŋe popou.

eng: Then the chief priests and elders of the people gathered in the courtyard of Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ man te Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena kegyiise gyuuwɔ ke gyaŋee saese pou kegyia Kaifase dekpaŋalaŋ man.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! You who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you. How often I wanted to gather your people together, just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: O Gyerusalɛmtena! O Gyerusalɛmtena! Ɛmɛɛ koona Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra, te ɛ fuŋii balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ be kpilii ɛ gyaŋ nɛ boya. Ma te yɛɛ kakyeŋ nyi maa yilaa ŋon do me tɛɛ, ŋgba mena keŋ kegyim talabo ne fɛlɛɛ ɔ beŋkelɛɛ wuu ɔ bia dɔɔ nɛ, mɔna ɛ te fa maŋ gbɛɛ ya.

eng: Just as the lightning flashes from the east to the west, so it will be with the coming of the Human One.
local: Nawolo nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon kekɔŋ gyae ke yɛɛ bo ŋgba mena keŋ ɔga ne ŋmaale lee weese dɛɛlee man, te balaŋ pou ne naa ke ɔ dɛɛyalae man nɛ.

eng: And the second is like it: You must love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: Alate keŋ mɔ yɛɛ ŋgba gyaŋgbate keŋ nɛ. Keŋ yɛna nyi, Gyae n dɔɔ ŋgba nyaŋ gbagba n wose nɛ.

eng: They were plotting to arrest Jesus by cunning tricks and to kill him.
local: Keŋte be bakewɔ nyi baa yo kyaŋ Yesu na baa ko e.

eng: Look, your house is left to you deserted.
local: Ɛ kɛɛ, Wurubuarɛ ta lese ɔ sia lee ɛ donɔɔ keŋ dɔɔ. Ka seŋɛɛ bo yakaa.

eng: The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Botɔɔ keŋ kolo punta doo na, botɔɔ te bapɛtɛɛna mɔ ne yilaa.

eng: All the Law and the Prophets depend on these two commands.
local: Mosesi mmaraase ŋan pou na Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ pou nombia ŋan bɔɔ wolo nɛ, seŋae mmaraase ala kɛŋa dɔɔ.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the feast so there wouldnt be an uproar among the people.
local: Mɔna be yakowɔ nyi, Tekaboena nyi dɛɛ ko e nyeekelɛɛwee kedi kei man ya, na mena ya na balaŋ baŋ baa yela donɔɔ keŋ ke siŋii.

eng: I tell you, you wont see me until you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyi nyiaa, ɛ be gyae ɛ ka tekaa naa maŋ bela ya, kelii debaŋ keŋ ɛ ke yako nyiaa Walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kɔŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ yele man nɛ, ŋon na nyeebam. .

eng: Now immediately after the suffering of that time the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light. The stars will fall from the sky and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: Weeya ŋenaŋ man kaale kɛŋa pou wɔle nɛ, be gyae ke di weeya ala ya weese waa gyoo ditiŋtɛɛ, te gyaalaŋ mɔ be gyae waa wolose ya. Kyolobiise baa lee adido kaa yala. Te abɔɔ ŋan pou ŋe kyaa adido nɛ, gyae ke wosi.

eng: Now as the Pharisees were gathering, Jesus asked them.
local: Farasiitena baŋ ke yilaa debodokoloŋ nɛ, Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa.

eng: When Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle na te Yesu gyuuwɔ Simɔn ŋon nafɔ ɔ na wee kawee yayam nɛ dɛɛ Bɛtani donɔɔ man.

eng: Then the sign of the Human One will appear in the sky. At that time all the tribes of the earth will be full of sadness, and they will see the Human One coming in the heavenly clouds with power and great splendor.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ nɛ, balaŋ baa naa kolo adido keŋ gyae kawola nyi, maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon kekɔŋ te lii. Te tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou baa wii walaŋsa. Baa naa maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon ta mo doŋ na gyoorobiiri lee ŋɔma man adido mɛɛ kɔŋ.

eng: a woman came to him with a vase made of alabaster containing very expensive perfume. She poured it on Jesus head while he was sitting at dinner.
local: Ɔ kekyaa botɔɔ nɛ, te alo ŋolo kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ na ɔ dana nuŋ ofoŋofoŋte keŋ ke dana koyadoŋ nɛ porontia tɔɔ. Keŋte ɔ toosi nuŋ keŋ woli Yesu nyeeman.

eng: What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is he? Davids son, they replied.
local: Sena te ɛ na gyueŋ lee nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ wose man? Ɔ yɛɛ amɔte ɔ naanabii e? Keŋte be tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Ɔ yɛɛ gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii e.

eng: He will send his angels with the sound of a great trumpet, and they will gather his chosen ones from the four corners of the earth, from one end of the sky to the other.
local: Ba wola kaberɛ dinaa keŋ na, maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa kpila me kpilala lee adido kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, na be kaa yilaa me balaŋ baŋ mɔɔ lese see nɛ lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ yenaase kamasɛ.

eng: Now when the disciples saw it they were angry and said, Why this waste?.
local: Ɔ kaseela baŋ kena kpene keŋ alo ŋon be yɛɛ nɛ, yɛɛ wɔ baŋ te be yakowɔ nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te ɔ ne wɔlɛɛ nuŋ kei mena.

eng: He said, Then how is it that David, inspired by the Holy Spirit, called him Lord when he said.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Akpaa mena na, weera dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon yela gyoo Defidi baake nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ nyi me Gbeŋgyoo? Gyoo Defidi yakowɔ nyiaa.

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, ɛ kɛɛ fiigi daŋ na ɛ ke kasee kolo lee ka wose man. Akpaa ɛ naa ŋgba ke tombia da potee faareŋse wɔlɛɛ nɛ, na ɛ gyeŋ nyi awuluŋtɛɛ ta benaa.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for a lot of money and given to the poor.
local: Nafɔ beŋ yɔɔ nuŋ kei na, baa nyiŋ kɔba burum na baa mo fa ayematena.

eng: The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool?.
local: De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ŋon yako me Gbeŋgyoo nyi, tisi kyaa me dunoluŋ dɔɔ, kelii debaŋ keŋ maa mo n kɔlala baŋ do n tɛɛ.

eng: In the same way, when you see all these things, you know that the Human One is near, at the door.
local: Mena mɔ te akpaa ɛ naa mena nombia kɛŋa pou ne kɔŋ na, ɛ gyeŋ nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon kekɔŋ ta benaa taŋ.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking. He said, Why do you make trouble for the woman? Shes done a good thing for me.
local: Na Yesu gyeŋ nombia ŋan bɛɛ gyueŋ nɛ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Weera te ɛ ne kale na alo ŋon mena? Nombii kpaakpaa yaa ɔ yɛɛwɔ fa maŋ nɛ.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi balaŋ baŋ be kyaa gyɛŋ kei nɛ, be duuluŋ be gyae ke munu taŋ ya, pɛte nombia kɛŋa kekɔŋ.

eng: If David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: Akpaa nyi gyoo Defidi baake nyeelɛɛre ŋon nyi ɔ Gbeŋgyoo na, kenaŋ na sena te nyeelɛɛre ŋon waa bese gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii.

eng: You always have the poor with you, but you wont always have me.
local: Ayematena dɔɔ be kyaa ɛ gyaŋ debaŋ kamasɛ, mɔna maŋ dɔɔ mɔɔ gyae maa kyaa ɛ gyaŋ debaŋ kamasɛ ya.

eng: Nobody was able to answer him. And from that day forward nobody dared to ask him anything.
local: Be man ŋolo te tale tiranɔɔ fa Yesu ya. Te lee mena wee kenaŋ ne gyu mɔ na, ŋolo te tale gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ bɔɔse e nombii daale bela ya.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Adido na tɛɛle kei pou gyae ke gyu ɔto, mɔna me nɔɔwoya dɔɔ ŋan gyae ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa.

eng: By pouring this perfume over my body shes prepared me to be buried.
local: Mena keŋ ɔ be kyɛɛkee nuŋ kei woli maŋ nɛ, kei nawolo baa nyi ɔ te desina maŋ fa me kewuu.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the heavenly angels and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, ŋolo ba gyeŋ wee na debaŋ keŋ kɛŋa pou gyae ke kɔŋ ya. Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ be kyaa adido, na maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon koraŋ ba gyeŋ ya. Gyisɛ me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ waageŋ gyeŋa la.

eng: I tell you the truth that wherever in the whole world this good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, tɛɛle kei dɔɔ yenaŋ kamasɛ keŋ baa kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan nɛ, baa yako alo kei nombii waa ayɛɛŋ kei na baa mo tɔɔse e.

eng: As it was in the time of Noah, so it will be at the coming of the Human One.
local: Mena keŋ be kɔŋ Nowa debaŋ keŋ nɛ, mena te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon mɔ kabese kɔŋ gyae ke yɛɛ.

eng: Then one of the Twelve, who was called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ man ŋolo, ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Gyudase Isekarɔte nɛ, korowɔ gyu saese kegyiise baŋ gyaŋ.

eng: In those days before the flood, people were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark.
local: Weeya ŋenaŋ man pɛte loŋ keŋ ke kɔŋ kaa wɔlɛɛ tɛɛle nɛ, na balaŋ ne dii na bɛɛ nyɔɔ. Baala ne yala ala na bɛɛ mo ala mɔ fa yale, kelii wee keŋ Nowa ba gyoo adakaa dinaa keŋ man nɛ.

eng: and said, What will you give me if I turn Jesus over to you? They paid him thirty pieces of silver.
local: Keŋte ɔ ke bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Akpaa ma lese Yesu fa ŋon na, woŋ te ɛ ke fa maŋ? Botɔɔ te be tiranɔɔ fa e nyi dɛɛ fa neŋ kɔba wolom bia sao na kufu Te be kala kɔba keŋ do e ɔ nyiŋmaa man.

eng: They didnt know what was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. The coming of the Human One will be like that.
local: Be ta gyeŋ kpene keŋ ne kɔŋ ya, kelii debaŋ keŋ loŋ be kaa waale kuu wɔ pou laŋ nɛ. Mena te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon ke kɔŋ gyae ke yɛɛ.

eng: From that time on he was looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Lee debaŋ kenaŋ te Gyudase fiasɛɛ ɔ ne gyae gbɛɛ keŋ waa mo lese Yesu fa wɔ.

eng: At that time there will be two men in the field. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ man na, baala bala baa kyaa wɔɔman bɛɛ yɛɛ tom. Wurubuarɛ waa mo ŋolo tina ŋolo yela.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, the disciples came to Jesus and said, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Nyeekelɛɛwee kedi wee gyaŋgbate keŋ bee di bodobodo keŋ tee be doo ke man ya nɛ, Yesu kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa, Aleŋ te n ne gyae nyi de kaa desina see fa nyeekelɛɛwee weenɛɛ kedi keŋ.

eng: Two women will be grinding at the mill. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Ala bala baa seŋ bɛɛ naŋ abɔɔ. Wurubuarɛ waa mo ŋolo tina ŋolo yela.

eng: He replied, Go into the city, to a certain man, and say, The teacher says,My time is near. Im going to celebrate the Passover with my disciples at your house.
local: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ gyu balee ŋolo gyaŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ keŋ man na ɛ kaa yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo ŋon kpa ma debaŋ keŋ te lii, te n dekpaŋalaŋ man te ɔ ne gyae nyi ŋon na daa ɔ kaseela baŋ kaa di nyeekelɛɛ weenɛɛ ŋan. .

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know what day the Lord is coming.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ doo ɛ wose dɔɔ, nawolo nyi ɛ ba gyeŋ wee keŋ ɛ gbeŋgyoo ŋon waa kɔŋ ya.

eng: The disciples did just as Jesus instructed them. They prepared the Passover.
local: Keŋte ɔ kaseela baŋ gyuuwɔ ke yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yako wɔ nɛ te be desina nyeekelɛɛ weenɛɛ ŋan see.

eng: But you understand that if the head of the house knew at what time the thief would come, he would keep alert and wouldnt allow the thief to break into his house.
local: Ɛ tɔɔse nyiaa, nyi dekpaŋalaŋte deŋ gyeŋ debaŋ keŋ ŋmɛɛlate waa kɔŋ kaa ŋmɛɛle e nɛ, nafɔ waa deke ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ nyi ŋmɛɛlate be nyiŋ gbɛɛ kaa gyoo kuu wa abɔɔ ya.

eng: That evening he took his place at the table with the twelve disciples.
local: Kelii balinɔɔ nɛ, Yesu na ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ gyuuwɔ ke kyaa weenɛɛ ŋan dedii man.

eng: Therefore, you also should be prepared, because the Human One will come at a time you dont know.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ doo ɛ wose, nawolo nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon mɔ maa kɔŋ debaŋ keŋ ɛ loo be gyakaa ya.

eng: As they were eating he said, I assure you that one of you will betray me.
local: Ba ke kyaa bee dii nɛ Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyi, ɛ man ŋolo waa lese maŋ fa.

eng: Who then are the faithful and wise servants whom their master puts in charge of giving food at the right time to those who live in his house?.
local: Keŋte ɔ besewɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, amɔte yɛna tobaale ŋon ɔ sia be kare te ɔ ne di ɔ gbeŋgyoo mmaraase dɔɔ? Ŋon yɛna tobaale ŋon ɔ gbeŋgyoo waa mo ɔ tebia akaŋ baŋ do ɔ nyiŋmaa man nyi waa kɛɛ be dɔɔ, na waa fa wɔ weenɛɛ debaŋ keŋ kaboena nɛ.

eng: Deeply saddened, each one said to him, Im not the one, am I, Lord.
local: Lee nombia ŋan ɔ be yako wɔ nɛ dɔɔ, dekɔkɔrɔkɔɔ kyaŋa ɔ kaseela baŋ pou te be fiasɛɛ bɛɛ bɔɔse e dokoloŋ dokoloŋ nyiaa, De gbeŋgyoo, maŋ yaa amɔte.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: Akpaa tobaale ŋonaŋ seŋɛɛ ɔ tom man ɔ ne yɛɛ ke nideli mena, te ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon de kɔŋ kaa naa e na, ŋon na nyeebam.

eng: He replied, The one who will betray me is the one who dips his hand with me into this bowl.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Walaŋ ŋon daa na e kyaa dɛɛ do nyiŋmaa nyefale dokoloŋ kei man nɛ, ŋon ne gyae la waa lese maŋ fa.

eng: I assure you that he will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, mena tobaale ŋonaŋ ɔ gbeŋgyoo waa mo wa abɔɔ pou do e ɔ nyiŋmaa man nyi waa kɛɛ ŋe dɔɔ.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: Maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa yeŋ ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako lee ma wose man nɛ, mɔna ŋon waa lese maŋ fa nɛ waa laako. Nafɔ beŋ ta lola walaŋ ŋonaŋ ya na, dei.

eng: But suppose those bad servants should say to themselves, My master wont come until later.
local: Mɔna akpaa nyi tobaale ŋonaŋ yɛɛ tobaale kum e, te ɔ yɛɛ wa agyueŋ nyi ɔ gbeŋgyoo be gyae waa kɔŋ bileŋ ya.

eng: Now Judas, who would betray him, replied, Its not me, is it, Rabbi? Jesus answered, You said it.
local: Keŋte Gyudase ŋon waa kaa lese Yesu fa nɛ mɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, maŋ yaa amɔte? Te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Mena yaa nyaŋ gbagba yakowɔ nɛ.

eng: And suppose they began to beat their fellow servants and to eat and drink with the drunks?.
local: te ɔ gyoo ɔ tebia akaŋ baŋ ketuku, te ŋon na soloŋ nyɔɔra ne dii bɛɛ nyɔɔ na.

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to the disciples and said, Take and eat. This is my body.
local: Be kee dii nɛ, Yesu moo bodobodo te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ, te ɔ kara ke mo fa ɔ kaseela baŋ. Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ lɛɛ di, kei yɛna ma wosenaane keŋ.

eng: The master of those servants will come on a day when they are not expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict.
local: ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon waa kaa lee ɔ dɔɔ wee keŋ ɔ loo be gyakaa ya nɛ.

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, Drink from this, all of you.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle te ɔ moo leenɔɔ keŋ te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ, te ɔ moo ke fa ɔ kaseela baŋ. Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa Ɛ popou ɛ lɛɛ nyɔɔ.

eng: He will cut them in pieces and put them in a place with the hypocrites. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon de kɔŋ kaa naa e mena na, waa wɔŋ ɔ deŋele nideli na waa gegi e lee ɔ gyaŋ, na waa yela ɔ kaa kyaa balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ beo ba wose nɛ gyaŋ. Botɔɔ te waa kyaŋ ɔ nyee wii na waa tao ɔ kela.

eng: This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many so that their sins may be forgiven.
local: Kei yɛna me fatabo keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo tɔ ŋon na ɔ balaŋ nɔɔkebakesee keŋ nɔɔ. Kei yɛna fatabo keŋ gyae ke kyɛɛkee leki, na Wurubuarɛ ka mo balaŋ burum nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ.

eng: I tell you, I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way with you in my Fathers kingdom.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi mɔɔ gyae maa tekaa nyɔɔ soloŋ kei bela ya, kelii wee keŋ daa na ŋon dɛɛ nyɔɔ soloŋ wɔle me kya Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.

eng: Then, after singing songs of praise, they went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, be kpee nɔɔneŋ lese Wurubuarɛ yele te ba leewɔ laŋ oliifi bula keŋ dɔɔ.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Tonight you will all fall away because of me. This is because it is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will go off in all directions .
local: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Gyɛŋ nelim kei nɛ ɛ popou ɛ ke yeu yela maŋ. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ yakowɔ ɔ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man lee ma wose man nyiaa, Maa gyɔ namensekɛɛlere ŋon lɔ, na ɔ namense baŋ pou ke yaasee.

eng: But after Im raised up, Ill go before you to Galilee.
local: Mɔna me fom lee yeŋ man na, maa kyaŋ ŋon gbɛɛ gyu Galelia tɛɛle man.

eng: Peter replied, If everyone else stumbles because of you, Ill never stumble.
local: Keŋte Pita yako e nyiaa, Akpaa be popou de yeu tina neŋ yela koraŋ na, maŋ dɔɔ mɔɔ gyae maa tina neŋ yela korakora ya.

eng: Jesus said to him, I assure you that, before the rooster crows tonight, you will deny me three times.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako Pita nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako neŋ nyi gyɛŋ nelim kei pɛte kegyim kelɔ woya nɛ, nyaŋ ke bɛɛ betabotooro nyi n ba gyeŋ maŋ korakora ya.

eng: Peter said, Even if I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. All the disciples said the same thing.
local: Keŋte Pita tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Akpaa nyi ke yɛɛ yeŋ ke koraŋ na, mɔɔ gyae maa bɛɛ neŋ korakora ya. Te ɔ kaseela akaŋ baŋ pou mɔ yakowɔ mena.

eng: Then Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. He said to the disciples, Stay here while I go and pray over there.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyuu yenaŋ daale keŋ bɛɛ baake botɔɔ nyi Gɛkyɛmane nɛ. Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kyaa kɛbo, mee gyu siaman me kaa fane maa kɔŋ.

eng: When he took Peter and Zebedees two sons, he began to feel sad and anxious.
local: Keŋte ɔ moo Pita na Sebedeo bia bala baŋ kpu na ɔ wose. Botɔɔ te dekɔkɔrɔkɔɔ kyaŋa e keŋ nyi ɔ bɛɛ gyeŋ mena keŋ waa yɛɛ ɔ wose ya.

eng: Then he said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert with me.
local: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Dekɔkɔrɔkɔɔ te kyaŋ maŋ gbaa keŋ nyi ne gyae ka ko maŋ. Ɛ kyaa kɛbo na ɛ ka deke.

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell on his face and prayed, My Father, if its possible, take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tuuwɔ gyu siaman kyomii te ɔ tisiwɔ buŋ wuluŋu ɔ nyee te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ nyiaa, Taata, akpaa nyi gyae ke de na, yela mena kayeyɛɛ kei ke laŋ me dɔɔ. Mɔna na maŋ kegyaebii ya. Yɛɛ kpene keŋ nyaŋ gbagba ne gyae.

eng: He came back to the disciples and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Couldnt you stay alert one hour with me?.
local: Yesu ka bese kɔŋ ɔ kaseela baŋ gyaŋ nɛ, ɔ kaa naawɔ nyi ba te doo dosoroŋ. Keŋte ɔ bɔɔse Pita nyiaa, Mena dɔɔ ɛ te tale deke debaŋ dokoloŋ kooŋ keŋ nyi ɛ be doo yee.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Keŋte ɔ besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ deke na ɛ ke fane, na ɛ be nyiŋ ka gyoo kekɛɛsekɛɛ man ya. Nawolo nyi ma gyeŋ nyi ɛ konɔɔse man na ɛ ne gyae ɛ ke yɛɛ ma kegyaebii, mɔna ɛ wosenaane betaŋ mena doŋ kenaŋ ya.

eng: A second time he went away and prayed, My Father, if its not possible that this cup be taken away unless I drink it, then let it be what you want.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ lee ɔ kaseela baŋ gyaŋ bolate gyu ke fane nyiaa, Taata, nyi be gyae ke de nyi mena kayeyɛɛ kei ka lee me dɔɔ ya nɛ, kenaŋ na yɛɛ nyaŋ gbagba n kegyaebii.

eng: Again he came and found them sleeping. Their eyes were heavy with sleep.
local: Yesu ka bese kɔŋ nɛ, ɔ kaa naawɔ nyi ɔ kaseela baŋ sɛɛ be dokɛɛ, nawolo nyi dosoroŋ doo be siaman.

eng: But he left them and again went and prayed the same words for the third time.
local: Keŋte ɔ besewɔ lee be gyaŋ gyu ɔ kaa fane botoorote. Te ɔ fanewɔ yako nombii dokoloŋ kenaŋ ke bela.

eng: Then he came to his disciples and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Look, the time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: Keŋte ɔ besewɔ kɔŋ ɔ kaseela baŋ gyaŋ te ɔ kaa bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ sɛɛ ɛ doo ɛ ne fɛɛfo? Ɛ kɛɛ, debaŋ keŋ te lii. Be ta lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon do balaŋ kumɛɛ nyiŋmaa man.

eng: Get up. Lets go. Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Ɛ koro dee gyu, ɛ kɛɛ walaŋ ŋon waa lese maŋ fa nɛ te tuŋ daa taŋ.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came. With him was a large crowd carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests and elders of the people.
local: Yesu ke gyakaa ɔ ne kolosi nɛ, ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ maŋ ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Gyudase nɛ kaa leewɔ botɔɔ. Ŋon na balaŋ dikpii keŋ bɔɔ lee saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise gyaŋ nɛ kɔŋala. Na be man baale dana yoosilase na akpokpose.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss.
local: Gyudase ŋon waa kaa lese Yesu fa nɛ taŋ wulaa yako balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, Walaŋ ŋon maa kaa pukaa e nɛ, ŋon yɛna walaŋ ŋon ɛ ne gyae nɛ. Ɛ kyaŋ e.

eng: Just then he came to Jesus and said, Hello, Rabbi. Then he kissed him.
local: Mena dɔɔ Gyudase tɛɛwɔ teeŋ gyu Yesu gyaŋ te ɔ baake e nyi De Gbeŋgyoo, te ɔ pukaa e doo e nɔɔ.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Friend, do what you came to do. Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, Me gyoo, yɛɛ kpene keŋ dɔɔ n be kɔŋ kɛbo nɛ. Botɔɔ te balaŋ baŋ kaa tɔɔ Yesu do te be kyaŋa e.

eng: One of those with Jesus reached for his sword. Striking the high priests slave, he cut off his ear.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu kaseela baŋ maŋ ŋolo lese ɔ yoosila, te ɔ moowɔ kara saese pou kegyia ŋon tobaale deŋele lese ke lɔ.

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Put the sword back into its place. All those who use the sword will die by the sword.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseele ŋon nyiaa, Mo n yoosila keŋ do ke bɔɔlee man. Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne wɔŋ yoosila na, waa yeŋ bo yoosila nɔɔman.

eng: Or do you think that Im not able to ask my Father and he will send to me more than twelve battle groups of angels right away?.
local: N ba gyeŋ nyi maa tale sola me kya na waa yela ɔ kpilala kakpoŋkakpoŋse ka lee adido kɔŋ debokeiman kaa yoo lɛɛ maŋ yee?.

eng: But if I did that, how would the scriptures be fulfilled that say this must happen.
local: Mɔna akpaa me yɛɛ mena na, sena te Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyi, kaboena nyi mena nombia kɛŋa dɛɛkɔŋ nɛ, gyae ke yɛɛ ampaŋ.

eng: Then Jesus said to the crowds, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like a thief? Day after day, I sat in the temple teaching, but you didnt arrest me.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse balaŋ dikpii keŋ bɔɔ kɔŋ be kaa kyaŋ e nɛ nyiaa, Me yɛɛ walaŋkum e te ɛ moo yoosilase na akpokpose ɛ ne kɔŋ ɛ kaa kyaŋ maŋ? Wee kamasɛ na mɛɛ kyaa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa man wola ŋon Wurubuarɛ nombia, mɔna ɛ te kyaŋ maŋ ya.

eng: But all this has happened so that what the prophets said in the scriptures might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples left Jesus and ran away.
local: Mɔna kɛŋa pou kɔŋae mena na nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra be ŋmarase see nɛ ke kɔŋ mena. Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ pou korowɔ yeu tina e yela.

eng: Those who arrested Jesus led him to Caiaphas the high priest. The legal experts and the elders had gathered there.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ kyaŋ Yesu nɛ moo e gyu saese pou kegyia Kaifase dekpaŋalaŋ man, botɔɔ keŋ mmaraa wolala na donɔɔ kegyiise pou be gyaŋee nɛ.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance until he came to the high priests courtyard. He entered that area and sat outside with the officers to see how it would turn out.
local: Pita sila Yesu wɔle wɔle menɛɛ kelii saese kegyia ŋon dekpaŋalaŋ man. Ɔ gyoowɔ ke man te ɔ gyuuwɔ ke kyaa tewulɛɛ baŋ bee gyee dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ nɛ gyaŋ, na waa naa kpene gyae ke kɔŋ Yesu dɔɔ.

eng: The chief priests and the whole council were looking for false testimony against Jesus so that they could put him to death.
local: Saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise baŋ pou bɔɔ gyaŋee botɔɔ nɛ, ne gyae nombii keŋ baa mo gyakaa Yesu dɔɔ na baa nyiŋ gbɛɛ ko e.

eng: They didnt find anything they could use from the many false witnesses who were willing to come forward. But finally they found two.
local: Balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ kaa kɛrɛɛ Yesu nɔɔse, mɔna be te nyiŋ nombiikum keŋ baa mo gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ ya. Wɔle wɔle na te baala bala baale kɔŋawɔ kaa yako nyi.

eng: who said, This man said, I can destroy Gods temple and rebuild it in three days.
local: De nyiiwɔ balee kei yakowɔ nyi Waa tale tekerii Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa kei lɔ, na waa mo weeya atooro ma ke bela. .

eng: Then the high priest stood and said to Jesus, Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Botɔɔ te saese kegyia ŋon korowɔ seŋ te ɔ bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, Nombia ŋan bɛɛ mo gyakaa n dɔɔ nɛ, n be tale tira ŋe nɔɔ yee.

eng: But Jesus was silent. The high priest said, By the living God, I demand that you tell us whether you are the Christ, Gods Son.
local: Mɔna Yesu te gyuusu ɔ nɔɔ ya. Keŋte saese kegyia ŋon yako Yesu nyiaa, Ma te kɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ nae fa neŋ, akpaa nyaŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ, ŋon ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bu dokoloŋ kooŋ ŋon e na, yako daa.

eng: You said it, Jesus replied. But I say to you that from now on youll see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds .
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Keŋ yaa n yakowɔ nɛ. Mɔna mɛɛ yako ɛ popou ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, wee daale nɛ ɛ kena maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon kyaa Wurubuarɛ doŋ pou gbeŋgyoo ŋon dunoluŋ dɔɔ, na ɛ ka bese naa maŋ na me ta lee ŋɔma man adido mɛɛ kɔŋ.

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Hes insulting God! Why do we need any more witnesses? Look, youve heard his insult against God.
local: Saese kegyia ŋon ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ɔ kyaŋa ɔ kegba tirii te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Ɔ te kolosi mmusuo nombia! Woŋ nombii yaa dɛɛ gyae dɛɛ tekaa nyii kpu bela? Nɛnɛɛ ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ nyii mmusuo nombia ŋan ɔ be kolosi nɛ.

eng: What do you think? And they answered, He deserves to die.
local: Sena yɛna ya agyueŋ lee nombia kɛŋa man? Keŋte be tiranɔɔ nyi, Ɔ kaboena yeŋ.

eng: Then they spit in his face and beat him. They hit him.
local: Keŋte be tɔɔ nɔɔloŋse woli ɔ siagbɛɛ te be takee e akɔtase. Baale mɔ ŋmeree e ɔ lakaase man.

eng: and said, Prophesy for us, Christ! Who hit you.
local: te be yako e nyiaa Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Kristo, wola daa ŋon ɔ be gyɔ neŋ.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard. A servant woman came and said to him, You were also with Jesus the Galilean.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na Pita kyaa dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man. Keŋte saese kegyia ŋon alebu ŋolo kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa, Nyaŋ mɔ n kpuɛ na Yesu ŋon ɔ ba lee Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ.

eng: But he denied it in front of all of them, saying, I dont know what you are talking about.
local: Keŋte Pita lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ balaŋ baŋ pou siaman botɔɔ nyiaa, Mɔɔ gyeŋ nombia ŋan n ne yako nɛ man korakora ya.

eng: When he went over to the gate, another woman saw him and said to those who were there, This man was with Jesus, the man from Nazareth.
local: Te ɔ leewɔ gyu dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ denanɔɔ man. Botɔɔ te alebubii ŋolo mɔ besewɔ naa e te ɔ yako ɔ tebia baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ nyiaa, Balee kei mɔ kpuɛ na Yesu ŋon ɔ ba lee Nasarɛte donɔɔ man nɛ.

eng: With a solemn pledge, he denied it again, saying, I dont know the man.
local: Keŋte Pita besewɔ lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ te ɔ kaŋa ntam nyiaa, Mɔɔ gyeŋ balee kei korakora ya.

eng: A short time later those standing there came and said to Peter, You must be one of them. The way you talk gives you away.
local: Te kyare ya nɛ, balaŋ baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ kɔŋawɔ Pita gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa, Kei dɔɔ ke yɛɛ ampaŋ ke kaeŋ nyi nyaŋ mɔ n kpuɛ na balee kei. N kekolosi koraŋ nawolo nyi n yɛɛ be man ŋolo e.

eng: Then he cursed and swore, I dont know the man! At that very moment the rooster crowed.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita fiasɛɛ ɔ ne falaa ɔ wose te ɔ kaŋa ntam nyiaa, Mɔɔ gyeŋ balee kei korakora ya. Debokenaŋ man te kegyim lɔɔ woya.

eng: Peter remembered Jesus words, Before the rooster crows you will deny me three times.And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Keŋte Pita tɔɔse nombia ŋan Yesu be yako e nyiaa, Pɛte kegyim ke lɔ woya na, nyaŋ ke bɛɛ betabotooro nyi n ba gyeŋ maŋ ya nɛ. Te Pita leewɔ debɔɔman ke kyaŋ ɔ nyee wii.

eng: The beginning of the good news about Jesus Christ, Gods Son.
local: Wurubuarɛ bu Yesu Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan kewalaŋ yaa.

eng: happened just as it was written about in the prophecy of Isaiah: Look, I am sending my messenger before you . He will prepare your way.
local: Wurubuarɛ kpila tobaale ŋon bɛɛ baake nyi Gyɔn nɛ nyi waa kyaŋ Yesu gbɛɛ kɔŋ, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Yesaya be yako Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man nɛ. Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Kɛɛ! Mee kpila me kpilale na waa ta n siaman. Ŋon ne gyae la waa desina n gbɛɛneŋ see neŋ.

eng: a voice shouting in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight .
local: Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya besewɔ yako Gyɔn wose man nombia Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man bela nyiaa, Ŋolo ne fae yeesa depampaa dinaa keŋ man nyiaa, Ɛ desina de Gbeŋgyoo gbɛɛnaa, na ɛ ke yela ɔ gbɛɛneŋ pou ka tenee. .

eng: John the Baptist was in the wilderness calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Mena dɔɔ kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ nyiaa, Gyɔn ŋon ɔ ne sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ nɛ kyaawɔ depampaa dinaa keŋ man. Na ɔ ne kolosi fa balaŋ nyi baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na baa kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ, na baa sɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ na Wurubuarɛ ka mo be nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ.

eng: Everyone in Judea and all the people of Jerusalem went out to the Jordan River and were being baptized by John as they confessed their sins.
local: Mena dɔɔ balaŋ burum leewɔ Gyudia tɛɛlese pou man, na Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man gyu ketɔlɛɛ be nombiakumɛɛ ŋan bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ fa Gyɔn, te ɔ sɔɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ Gyɔɔdan boo man.

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Na kurumɔɔ tɔne te ba moowɔ ŋara Gyɔn kegba, te wonembu tɔne te ɔ moowɔ yɛɛ abɔsɔɔ kpaŋ ɔ teŋa man. Akpataana na teebɔɔ yɛna ɔ weenɛɛ ŋan ɔ ne dii.

eng: He announced, One stronger than I am is coming after me. Im not even worthy to bend over and loosen the strap of his sandals.
local: Keŋte Gyɔn yako balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, Walaŋ ŋolo dɔŋ me wɔle ɔ ne kɔŋ keŋ ɔ kela maŋ. Maŋ tekaboena nyi maa buŋ booli ɔtenate nkyokota ŋmeeseŋ gba ya.

eng: I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.
local: Maŋ dɔɔ loŋ te maŋ dana mɛɛ sɔ ŋon Wurubuarɛ loŋ, mɔna akpaa ŋon de kɔŋ na Wurubuarɛ feliŋ te waa mo sɔ ŋon Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

eng: About that time, Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
local: Gyɔn kɛɛ sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ nɛ, te Yesu leewɔ Nasarɛte donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ kɔŋ, te Gyɔn sɔɔ e Wurubuarɛ loŋ Gyɔɔdan boo man.

eng: While he was coming up out of the water, Jesus saw heaven splitting open and the Spirit, like a dove, coming down on him.
local: Yesu ka mo waa lee loŋ keŋ man nɛ, karatetee dokoloŋ te ɔ naawɔ na adido ta toro, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon moo duwuluŋ dinɔɔ tisi lee adido kaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ.

eng: And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: Botɔɔ te be nyii woya ŋaale kolosiwɔ lee adido yako nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna me bu ŋon mɛɛ gyae lee me konɔɔ man, te n na gyoo me sia nideli.

eng: At once the Spirit forced Jesus out into the wilderness.
local: Debokenaŋ man te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon yela Yesu gyuuwɔ depampaa dinaa daale man.

eng: He was in the wilderness for forty days, tempted by Satan. He was among the wild animals, and the angels took care of him.
local: Yesu kyaawɔ depampaa kenaŋ man weeya sɔŋola . Ɔ kekyaa botɔɔ nɛ, te ɔbɔnsam kɔŋawɔ kaa kɛɛse e kɛɛse e kina. Na Yesu kyae ŋale man wonembia man, te Wurubuarɛ kpilala leewɔ adido kɔŋ kaa kɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ.

eng: After John was arrested, Jesus came into Galilee announcing Gods good news.
local: Te di weeya ala ya te be kyaŋa Gyɔn tɔ deni. Ba ke kyaŋ e tɔ deni wɔle nɛ, Yesu kɔŋawɔ Galelia tɛɛle dɔɔ ɔ ne kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa balaŋ nyiaa.

eng: saying, Now is the time! Here comes Gods kingdom! Change your hearts and lives, and trust this good news.
local: Debaŋ keŋ te lii, te Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ mɔ ta benaa. Ɛ kyɛɛkee lee ɛ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ, na ɛ ke lɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan di.

eng: As Jesus passed alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, for they were fishermen.
local: Keŋte Yesu gyuuwɔ ka mo Galelia boo nɔɔman. Botɔɔ te ɔ naa Simɔn na ɔ naabu Anderiase na bɛɛ lɔ asawuse boo keŋ man, nawolo nyi be yɛɛ kpɛɛ kyaŋarawɔ.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kaa sila maŋ, na maa yela ɛ ke yilaa balaŋ fa maŋ ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ ne kyaŋ kpɛɛ nɛ.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: Debokenaŋ man te be tinaa ba asawuse ŋan yela, te be kaa sila Yesu.

eng: After going a little farther, he saw James and John, Zebedees sons, in their boat repairing the fishing nets.
local: Ɔ ke gyu ɔ siaman kyomii nɛ, ɔ naa Sebedeo bia Gyemisi na ɔ naabu Gyɔn na be doo ba degbele man bee desina ba asawuse.

eng: At that very moment he called them. They followed him, leaving their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired workers.
local: Debokenaŋ man te Yesu baake wɔ, te be tinaa be kya Sebedeo na be paasetena baŋ pou yela degbele keŋ man te be kaa sila Yesu.

eng: Jesus and his followers went into Capernaum. Immediately on the Sabbath Jesus entered the synagogue and started teaching.
local: Keŋte be gyuuwɔ Kapanum donɔɔ man. Te Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee kelii nɛ, Yesu gyuuwɔ bɔ ɔsom deni man ɔ kaa wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia.

eng: The people were amazed by his teaching, for he was teaching them with authority, not like the legal experts.
local: Yesu kawola keŋ doo balaŋ baŋ pou nɔɔ. Nawolo nyi ɔ ne gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ wola ŋgba walaŋ ŋon ɔ dana doŋ nombia ŋan ɔ na wolo nɛ dɔɔ nɛ. Ɔ kawola keŋ te yɛɛ ŋgba Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ nɛ ya.

eng: Suddenly, there in the synagogue, a person with an evil spirit screamed.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na balee ŋolo kyaa bɔ ɔsom deni man botɔɔ keŋ feliŋkum doo ɔ man. Karatetee dokoloŋ te ɔ fae yeesa keŋkeŋ nyiaa.

eng: What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: Nyaŋ Yesu Nasarɛte baale, woŋ yaa n ne gyae de gyaŋ? N kɔŋae n kaa wɔlɛɛ daa? Ma gyeŋ neŋ nyi nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ walaŋ ŋon ayimɛɛ be doo n man ya nɛ.

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu faree feliŋkum keŋ nyiaa, Wu n nɔɔ, na n ka lee ɔ man.

eng: The unclean spirit shook him and screamed, then it came out.
local: Keŋte feliŋkum keŋ wosi balee ŋon nideli, te ke fae yeesa keŋkeŋ te ka leewɔ ɔ man laŋ.

eng: Everyone was shaken and questioned among themselves, Whats this? A new teaching with authority! He even commands unclean spirits and they obey him.
local: Nombii kei doo balaŋ baŋ pou nɔɔ te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Woŋ nombii yaa? Kawola wɔle? Ɔ dana doŋ feliŋkumɛɛ dɔɔ, keŋ nyi ɔ ne kolosi fa ŋa na ŋee nyii ɔ dei.

eng: Right away the news about him spread throughout the entire region of Galilee.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu wose man nombia yaaseewɔ gyu Galelia tɛɛle man na ka yenaase pou.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus, James, and John went home with Simon and Andrew.
local: Be ka lee bɔ ɔsom deni keŋ man pɛ, te baŋ na Gyemisi na Gyɔn gyuuwɔ Simɔn na Anderiase dɛɛ.

eng: Simons mother-in-law was in bed, sick with a fever, and they told Jesus about her at once.
local: Be ke gyu na Simɔn wa awu doo ɔ na wee kpaŋalaŋ, keŋte be yako Yesu.

eng: He went to her, took her by the hand, and raised her up. The fever left her, and she served them.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu gyuuwɔ alo ŋon gyaŋ, te ɔ kyaŋa e ɔ nyiŋmaa man gate e kyaase. Debokenaŋ man te alo ŋon kawee keŋ taŋawɔ, te ɔ kɛɛ be dɔɔ.

eng: That evening, at sunset, people brought to Jesus those who were sick or demon-possessed.
local: Mena wee kenaŋ balinɔɔ weese kagyoo nɛ, balaŋ moo be kaweesetena na baŋ pou feliŋkumɛɛ doo be man nɛ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ.

eng: The whole town gathered near the door.
local: Keŋte donɔɔ keŋ man balaŋ pou kɔŋawɔ kaa wuu dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ denanɔɔ man.

eng: He healed many who were sick with all kinds of diseases, and he threw out many demons. But he didnt let the demons speak, because they recognized him.
local: Te Yesu kyɔɔ balaŋ burum be kaweese kpookpoo pou. Ɔ gegi feliŋkumɛɛ burum mɔ lee balaŋ man keŋ ɔ te fa ŋa gbɛɛ nyi ŋe dee kolosi ya, nawolo nyi ŋa gyeŋ walaŋ ŋon dinɔɔ Yesu yɛɛ.

eng: Early in the morning, well before sunrise, Jesus rose and went to a deserted place where he could be alone in prayer.
local: Ke tɛɛ kena nebo nebo fuu nɛ, Yesu karatɛɛwɔ lee dekpaŋalaŋ man gyu yenaŋ daale keŋ botɔɔ yɛɛ yididi nɛ, te ɔ ke fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ.

eng: Simon and those with him tracked him down.
local: Keŋte Simɔn na ɔ tebia baŋ gyuuwɔ bɛɛ kɛo e.

eng: When they found him, they told him, Everyones looking for you.
local: Ba kena e nɛ te be yako e nyiaa, Balaŋ baŋ pou ne kɛo neŋ.

eng: He replied, Lets head in the other direction, to the nearby villages, so that I can preach there too. Thats why Ive come.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ yela dee gyu donɔɔse ŋan ŋe kyaa siaman nɛ man, na me kaa kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan botɔɔ mɔ. Nawolo nyi keŋ dɔɔ te me kɔŋawɔ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ.

eng: He traveled throughout Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and throwing out demons.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔ tɛɛwɔ kila Galelia tɛɛle pou dɔɔ, te ɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan fa wɔ bɔ ɔsom denɛɛ man pou, te ɔ gegi feliŋkumɛɛ lee balaŋ man.

eng: A man with a skin disease approached Jesus, fell to his knees, and begged, If you want, you can make me clean.
local: Te akuŋtuŋte ŋolo kaa buŋawɔ ɔ doona dɔɔ Yesu nawɔɔtɛɛ waase e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, akpaa n ne gyae na n ke tale kyɔ maŋ me kawee kei.

eng: Incensed, Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean.
local: Botɔɔ nɛ, ɔ nombia yɛɛ Yesu waraŋase te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ yako e nyiaa, Mɛɛ gyae, n kawee dɛɛ taŋ.

eng: Instantly, the skin disease left him, and he was clean.
local: Debokenaŋ man te ɔ kawee keŋ taŋawɔ, te ɔ nyiŋa ɔ wose.

eng: Sternly, Jesus sent him away.
local: Pɛ Yesu ke lɔ balee ŋon gbɛɛ nɛ, ɔ faree e nideli nyiaa.

eng: saying, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the sacrifice for your cleansing that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Kɛɛ nyi walaŋ na walaŋ na nyii kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ ya. Mɔna mo n wose gyu kawola Wurubuarɛ sae ŋon, na n ketɔ kedeesa abɔɔ ŋan de naana Mosesi ba wolo nyi yaa tɔ nɛ. Kei gyae ke yela walaŋ kamasɛ kena nyi n kawee keŋ te taŋ.

eng: Instead, he went out and started talking freely and spreading the news so that Jesus wasnt able to enter a town openly. He remained outside in deserted places, but people came to him from everywhere.
local: Mɔna balee ŋon ka lee debɔɔ man pɛ te ɔ gyoo nombia ŋan dekpeŋkpeŋ kegyɔ, te walaŋ kamasɛ nyiiwɔ. Lee mena dɔɔ Yesu te tale gyoo donɔɔ kamasɛ man na balaŋ kena e ya. Ɔ ne gyu bo ke kyaa donɔɔ keŋ nɔɔman, botɔɔ keŋ balaŋ be gyae baa naa e ya nɛ. Mɔ keŋ koraŋ na, balaŋ na lee yenaŋ kamasɛ kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ.

eng: After a few days, Jesus went back to Capernaum, and people heard that he was at home.
local: Weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale wɔle nɛ, Yesu besewɔ gyu Kapanum donɔɔ man te balaŋ nyiiwɔ nyi ɔ te kɔŋ dɛɛ.

eng: So many gathered that there was no longer space, not even near the door. Jesus was speaking the word to them.
local: Keŋte balaŋ burum kaa wuuwɔ botɔɔ, keŋ nyi nae daseŋae be doo ya. Mena dɔɔ gbɛɛ be doo keŋ ŋolo waa mo gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man gba ya. Te Yesu kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia fa wɔ.

eng: Some people arrived, and four of them were bringing to him a man who was paralyzed.
local: Keŋte balaŋ banaara baale seele ɔtakase ŋolo bɛɛ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ.

eng: They couldnt carry him through the crowd, so they tore off part of the roof above where Jesus was. When they had made an opening, they lowered the mat on which the paralyzed man was lying.
local: Keŋ balaŋ boe dɔɔ be te nyiŋ gbɛɛ mo e ta denanɔɔ man ya. Mena dɔɔ ba gyeŋa deni keŋ man Yesu kyaa nɛ nyee dɔɔ te be wolee yenaŋ daale, te be tisinaa kaweete ŋon na ɔ dofoŋoŋ keŋ pou dɛɛdɛɛ see Yesu siaman.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, Child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Yesu kena mena keŋ balaŋ baŋ be lɛɛ e di nɛ, ɔ yako kaweete ŋon nyiaa, Me bu, me ta mo n nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ neŋ.

eng: Some legal experts were sitting there, muttering among themselves.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ yakowɔ ba nyeeya man nyiaa.

eng: Why does he speak this way? Hes insulting God. Only the one God can forgive sins.
local: Weera dɔɔ te balee kei ne kolosi mena? Ɔ te kolosi mmusuo nombia. Lee Wurubuarɛ wɔle na, amɔte kyaa la keŋ waa tale mo dukum kyɛɛ.

eng: Jesus immediately recognized what they were discussing, and he said to them, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Debokenaŋ man te Yesu gyeŋa kpene keŋ bɛɛ gyueŋ nɛ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ na gyueŋ kɛŋa ɛ nyeeya man?.

eng: Which is easier—to say to a paralyzed person, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up, take up your bed, and walk?.
local: Nombia kɛŋa man woŋti woɛla na keyako fa ɔtakase ŋon? Me ta mo n dukum kyɛɛ neŋ, yaa Koro seŋ na n ka mo n dofoŋoŋ na n ke ta.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed.
local: Mɔna mɛɛ gyae ɛ ka gyeŋ nyi, Maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu dana doŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyi maa mo dukum kyɛɛ. Mena dɔɔ ɔ yako kaweete ŋon nyiaa.

eng: Get up, take your mat, and go home.
local: Koro seŋ. Gate n dofoŋoŋ, na n ke ta gyu n dɛɛ.

eng: Jesus raised him up, and right away he picked up his mat and walked out in front of everybody. They were all amazed and praised God, saying, Weve never seen anything like this.
local: Debokenaŋ man te kaweete ŋon korowɔ seŋ ɔ nawɔɔ dɔɔ, te ɔ gate ɔ dofoŋoŋ keŋ dɔɔ ɔ doo nɛ, te ɔ leewɔ laŋ ɔ dɛɛ balaŋ baŋ pou siaman. Nombia kɛŋa doo balaŋ baŋ pou nɔɔ te ba lese Wurubuarɛ yele yako nyiaa, Da te naa ta mena nombia kɛŋa dinɔɔ see ya.

eng: Jesus went out beside the lake again. The whole crowd came to him, and he began to teach them.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu besewɔ gyu Galelia boo nɔɔman, te balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia.

eng: As he continued along, he saw Levi, Alphaeus son, sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me. Levi got up and followed him.
local: Ɔ ke ta ɔ ne lam nɛ, ɔ naa Alfeose bu Lewi ŋon ɔ yɛɛ lempoolɛɛre e nɛ, na ɔ kyaa ɔ lempoo delɛe man. Te Yesu yako e nyiaa, Sila maŋ. Keŋte Lewi korowɔ sila e.

eng: Jesus sat down to eat at Levis house. Many tax collectors and sinners were eating with Jesus and his disciples. Indeed, many of them had become his followers.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, Lewi baake Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyu ɔ dɛɛ be kaa di. Lempoolɛɛra na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra burum sila Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyu botɔɔ, te baŋ na wɔ pou ke diiwɔ bom.

eng: When some of the legal experts from among the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they asked his disciples, Why is he eating with sinners and tax collectors.
local: Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ bɔɔ lee Farasiitena dikpii keŋ man kena ŋgba Yesu ne dii bom na lempoolɛɛra na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra nɛ be bɔɔse ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te Yesu ne dii bom na lempoolɛɛra na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra.

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do. I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Yesu kenyii mena nɛ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Kaweesetena te kaboena nyi baa kyɔ wɔ kawee. Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ na wee ya nɛ, dɔɔ bɔɔ ne hia kawee kekyɔ ya. Ma te kɔŋ nyi me kaa baake balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ wulaa tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ ya. Mɔna me kɔŋae na me kaa baake balaŋ baŋ be yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ nɛ.

eng: Johns disciples and the Pharisees had a habit of fasting. Some people asked Jesus, Why do Johns disciples and the Pharisees disciples fast, but yours dont.
local: Wee daale balaŋ baale naawɔ na Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre silala na Farasiitena ne bake be nɔɔ. Keŋte be bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre silala na Farasiitena ne bake be nɔɔ, te nyaŋ wui kɛwɔ dɔɔ bɔɔ ne bake be nɔɔ ya.

eng: Jesus said, The wedding guests cant fast while the groom is with them, can they? As long as they have the groom with them, they cant fast.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Sena te balaŋ baŋ be kyaa alowɔletɛɛ nɛ baa tale bake be nɔɔ keŋ alowɔle baale kyaa be gyaŋ? Alowɔle baale nyiŋa ɔ kyaa be gyaŋ nɛ, bɔɔ gyae baa tale bake be nɔɔ ya.

eng: But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast.
local: Mɔna debaŋ daale gyae kekɔŋ keŋ baa mo alowɔle baale ŋon lee be gyaŋ. Mena wee kenaŋ man te baa bake be nɔɔ.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes; otherwise, the patch tears away from it, the new from the old, and makes a worse tear.
local: Ŋolo bɛɛ kara kanyaŋ wɔle mo taa kakyeŋgbelaa keŋ ba yem nɛ bɔɔ ya. Akpaa ɔ yɛɛ mena nɛ, kanyaŋ wɔle keŋ gyae ke tao kakyeŋgbelaa keŋ wolose ke bɔɔ keŋ yɛɛ ke dinaa.

eng: No one pours new wine into old leather wineskins; otherwise, the wine would burst the wineskins and the wine would be lost and the wineskins destroyed. But new wine is for new wineskins.
local: Mena mɔ te ŋolo bɛɛ mo soloŋ wɔle do wonembu tɔne ogele bene man ya. Akpaa ɔ yɛɛ mena na soloŋ keŋ gyae ke tao tɔne keŋ lii, na soloŋ keŋ na wonembu tɔne ogele keŋ pou ke wɔlɛɛ. Bɛɛ mo soloŋ wɔle bo do wonembu tɔne ogele wɔle man.

eng: Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. As the disciples made their way, they were picking the heads of wheat.
local: Kelii Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee nɛ, Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyuuwɔ ka mo wɔɔ daale man. Na weenɛɛ ŋan seŋɛɛ wɔɔ kenaŋ man nɛ yɛɛ ŋgba kefaa nɛ. Keŋte ba kpera ŋaale posaa bɛɛ tao.

eng: The Pharisees said to Jesus, Look! Why are they breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Keŋte Farasiitena bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, Weera te n kaseela baŋ ne yɛɛ kpene keŋ dɛɛ kɔle nɛ da kefɛɛfowee.

eng: He said to them, Havent you ever read what David did when he was in need, when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ te kala ta lee Wurubuarɛ tɔne keŋ man kpene keŋ de naana Defidi be yɛɛ debaŋ keŋ tanam be kyaŋ ŋon na ɔ tebia, te ba wose be kyaŋ wɔ nɛ yee?.

eng: During the time when Abiathar was high priest, David went into Gods house and ate the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat. He also gave bread to those who were with him.
local: Debaŋ keŋ Abiata yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia e nɛ, de naana Defidi gyoowɔ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man, te ɔ dii bodobodo keŋ bɔɔ mo deesi fa Wurubuarɛ keŋ ŋolo be dana gbɛɛ waa di ke kpu na Wurubuarɛ saese ya nɛ. Keŋte ɔ moo ŋaale mɔ fa ɔ tebia baŋ be kpuɛ na e nɛ, te baŋ mɔ diiwɔ.

eng: Then he said, The Sabbath was created for humans; humans werent created for the Sabbath.
local: Te Yesu torowɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ kefɛɛfowee bo fa walaŋ. Ɔ te yɛɛ walaŋ fa kefɛɛfowee ya.

eng: This is why the Human One is Lord even over the Sabbath.
local: Mena dɔɔ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon yɛna kefɛɛfowee keŋ gba Gbeŋgyoo.

eng: Jesus returned to the synagogue. A man with a withered hand was there.
local: Wee daale Yesu besewɔ gyu Gyudatena ɔsom deni man. Ɔ ke gyu nɛ, na baam dayeŋasate ŋolo kyaa botɔɔ.

eng: Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they were watching Jesus closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath.
local: Na balaŋ baŋ baale na deke Yesu nyi waa kyɔ balee ŋon kawee ba kefɛɛfowee keŋ, na baa nyiŋ mo nombia gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ.

eng: He said to the man with the withered hand, Step up where people can see you.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako baam dayeŋasate ŋon nyiaa, Lee kaa seŋ siaman kɛbo.

eng: Then he said to them, Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill? But they said nothing.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Woŋ te da mmaraa ne fa gbɛɛ nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ kefɛɛfowee? Da mmaraa ne fa daa gbɛɛ nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ kolo kpaakpaa yaa dɛɛ yɛɛ kolo kum? Dɛɛ lɛɛ walaŋ nyee yaa, dee yela waa yeŋ? Botɔɔ na, ŋolo te tale gyuusu ɔ nɔɔ ya.

eng: Looking around at them with anger, deeply grieved at their unyielding hearts, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did, and his hand was made healthy.
local: Te Yesu kɛɛ wɔ pou gyaŋee baŋ man, te takaa e nideli lee ba deŋele doŋ keŋ dɔɔ. Keŋte ɔ yako kaweete ŋon nyiaa, Tenee n baam. Botɔɔ te balee ŋon tenee ɔ baam keŋ, te kawee keŋ taŋawɔ te ɔ baam keŋ yɛɛ doŋ.

eng: At that, the Pharisees got together with the supporters of Herod to plan how to destroy Jesus.
local: Mena dɔɔ Farasiitena na Herode silala leewɔ be kaa bake mena keŋ baa mo ko Yesu.

eng: Jesus left with his disciples and went to the lake. A large crowd followed him because they had heard what he was doing. They were from Galilee.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ wɔŋa ba wose lee botɔɔ gyu Galelia boo nɔɔman, te balaŋ burum baŋ bɔɔ lee Galelia nɛ sila e gyu botɔɔ.

eng: Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan, and the area surrounding Tyre and Sidon.
local: Be ke nyii kpene keŋ ɔ ne yɛɛ nɛ, balaŋ burum leewɔ Gyerusalɛm na Idumia donɔɔse na tɛɛle keŋ ke kyaa Gyɔɔdan boo diŋgyiŋ nɛ, na donɔɔse ŋan be kilisi Tae na Sidɔn nɛ man kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ.

eng: Jesus told his disciples to get a small boat ready for him so the crowd wouldnt crush him.
local: Balaŋ baŋ booroŋ dɔɔ ɔ yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyi baa gyae degbele fa e, na balaŋ baŋ be nyiŋ tɔ e do ya.

eng: He had healed so many people that everyone who was sick pushed forward so that they could touch him.
local: Nawolo nyi ɔ te kyɔ balaŋ burum kaweese, mena dɔɔ baŋ be sɛɛ be dana kaweese nɛ te kaa wuu ɔ dɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ gyae baa mo be nyiŋmaase tina e.

eng: Whenever the evil spirits saw him, they fell down at his feet and shouted, You are Gods Son.
local: Te akpaa balaŋ baŋ feliŋkum doo be man nɛ de naa e na, bɛɛ buŋii ɔ nawɔɔtɛɛ na bɛɛ fae yeesa nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon.

eng: But he strictly ordered them not to reveal who he was.
local: Keŋte Yesu faree wɔ nyi, be na yela balaŋ ka gyeŋ walaŋ ŋon dinɔɔ ɔ yɛɛ ya.

eng: Jesus went up on a mountain and called those he wanted, and they came to him.
local: Keŋte Yesu gyeŋa bula daale, te ɔ baake balaŋ baŋ ɔ ne gyae nɛ te be kaa yilaawɔ ɔ gyaŋ.

eng: He appointed twelve and called them apostles. He appointed them to be with him, to be sent out to preach.
local: Te ɔ lese balaŋ baŋ man baala kufu bala, te ɔ baake wɔ ɔ kpilala nyi baa kpu na e na waa kpilii wɔ na baa ta yako Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan.

eng: and to have authority to throw out demons.
local: Na waa fa wɔ doŋ na baa gberaa feliŋkumɛɛ lee balaŋ man.

eng: He appointed twelve: Peter, a name he gave Simon.
local: Baala kufu bala baŋ Yesu ba lese nɛ yela yaa, Simɔn te ɔ doo e yele nyi Pita, na.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, whom he nicknamed Boanerges, which means sons of Thunder.
local: Sebedeo bu Gyemisi na ɔ naabu Gyɔn, te ɔ besewɔ do wɔ yela nyi Boanegesi. Yele kei nawolo nyi bɛɛ yɛɛ nombia bo yakaa yakaa na doŋ ŋgba ɔga kefae nɛ.

eng: and Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James, Alphaeus son; Thaddaeus; Simon the Cananaean.
local: na Anderiase na Filipo na Batolomeo na Mateo na Tomase na Alfeose bu Gyemisi na Tadeose na Kaanan baale Simɔn ŋon ɔ ne gyae ɔ balaŋ nideli nɛ.

eng: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed Jesus.
local: na Gyudase Isekarɔte ŋon ɔ ba lese Yesu fa nɛ.

eng: Jesus entered a house. A crowd gathered again so that it was impossible for him and his followers even to eat.
local: Yesu ka lese ɔ kpilala baŋ taŋ nɛ te ɔ gyuuwɔ ɔ dɛɛ. Keŋte balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ kaa wuu ɔ dɔɔ, keŋ ŋon na ɔ kaseela bee nyiŋ gbɛɛ baa di weenɛɛ paŋpaŋ gba ya.

eng: When his family heard what was happening, they came to take control of him. They were saying, Hes out of his mind.
local: Ɔ loŋtona kenyii mena nɛ be gyuuwɔ be kaa kyaŋ e gyu dɛɛ, nawolo nyi na balaŋ baŋ kpa ɔ nyee ne wɔlɛɛ.

eng: The legal experts came down from Jerusalem. Over and over they charged, Hes possessed by Beelzebul. He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ bɔɔ lee Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man kɔŋ botɔɔ nɛ yakowɔ nyiaa, Feliŋkumɛɛ pou gyoo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Bɛlisɛbu nɛ doola Yesu man. Ŋon faa na e doŋ te ɔ na mo gberaa feliŋkumɛɛ lee balaŋ man nɛ.

eng: When Jesus called them together he spoke to them in a parable: How can Satan throw Satan out?.
local: Keŋte Yesu baake balaŋ baŋ kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ yako wɔ aduya man nyiaa, Sena te ɔbɔnsam waa tale gegi ɔ wose?.

eng: A kingdom involved in civil war will collapse.
local: Akpaa tɛɛle daale man balaŋ de kpase ba wose bee yoo na dɔŋa nɛ, tɛɛle kenaŋ be gyae ke seŋ ya.

eng: And a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Mena mɔ te akpaa dekpaŋalaŋ daale man balaŋ de kpase ba wose bee yoo na dɔŋa nɛ, dekpaŋalaŋ kenaŋ mɔ be gyae ke tale seŋ ya.

eng: If Satan rebels against himself and is divided, then he cant endure. Hes done for.
local: Te akpaa ɔbɔnsam na ɔ tewulɛɛ de kpase ba wose kpookpoo te ba koro seŋ tia dɔŋa na, ɔ be gyae waa tale seŋ ya. Na ɔ gyoori keŋ ɔto kenaŋ.

eng: No one gets into the house of a strong person and steals anything without first tying up the strong person. Only then can the house be burglarized.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, ŋolo be tale gyu doŋte ŋolo dɛɛ na ɔ kaa mo ɔ kolo, keŋ nyi ɔ te di doŋte ŋon dɔɔ ya. Nyi akpaa ɔ di doŋte ŋon dɔɔ te ɔ bake e see nɛ, kenaŋ na waa tale kuu wa abɔɔ pou.

eng: I assure you that human beings will be forgiven for everything, for all sins and insults of every kind.
local: Mɛɛ yako ŋon anokoare baa ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ waa tale mo balaŋ pou nombiakumɛɛ na mmusuo nombia ŋan bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ kyɛɛ wɔ.

eng: But whoever insults the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven. That person is guilty of a sin with consequences that last forever.
local: Mɔna walaŋ ŋon waa kolosi mmusuo nombia tia Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon nɛ, ɔ be gyae waa mo kyɛɛ ɔtenate korakora ya. Nawolo nyi walaŋ ŋonaŋ te yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ ŋan ŋe doo kekpaakekpaa.

eng: He said this because the legal experts were saying, Hes possessed by an evil spirit.
local: Keŋ dɔɔ te Yesu be yako nombia kɛŋa yɛna nyiaa, na balaŋ ne yako nyi feliŋkum doo la ɔ man.

eng: His mother and brothers arrived. They stood outside and sent word to him, calling for him.
local: Keŋte Yesu ɔ naa na ɔ naabia kɔŋawɔ kaa seŋ debɔɔman kpila nyi baa baake e fa wɔ.

eng: A crowd was seated around him, and those sent to him said, Look, your mother, brothers, and sisters are outside looking for you.
local: Na balaŋ burum kyaa kilisi e te be yako e nyiaa, Kɛɛ, n naa na n naabia seŋɛɛ debɔɔman bɛɛ gyae neŋ.

eng: He replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Bomɔ yɛna me naa na me naabia.

eng: Looking around at those seated around him in a circle, he said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: Keŋte ɔ kɛɛ baŋ be kyaa kilisi e nɛ gyaŋee te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Kɛɛ, me naa na me naabia yɛna kɛwɔ.

eng: Whoever does Gods will is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii nɛ, ŋon yɛna me naabu na me deesi na me naa.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to the other side of the lake, to the region of the Gerasenes.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ toŋa boo keŋ gyu Gerasenetena tɛɛle man.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a man possessed by an evil spirit came out of the tombs.
local: Yesu ka lee degbele keŋ man pɛ te balee ŋolo ŋon feliŋkum doo ɔ man nɛ, leewɔ amusee man kɔŋ kaa gyaŋee na e.

eng: This man lived among the tombs, and no one was ever strong enough to restrain him, even with a chain.
local: Na mena balee ŋonaŋ kyae amusee man botɔɔ, te ŋolo na ŋolo bɛɛ tekaa tale kyaŋ e do agbaraagbaraa bela ya.

eng: He had been secured many times with leg irons and chains, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one was tough enough to control him.
local: Nawolo nyi nafɔ bɛɛ mo agbaraagbaraa do e ɔ nyiŋmaase na ɔ nawɔɔ man, mɔna ɔ ne tirii ŋa pou leki. Te ŋolo be dana doŋ keŋ waa tale yɔkɔse e ya.

eng: Night and day in the tombs and the hills, he would howl and cut himself with stones.
local: Nelim na ka weese na ɔ dɔŋ amusee man na bulase dɔɔ botɔɔ ɔ ne fae yeesa, na ɔ na mo boya kɛrɛɛ ɔ wose.

eng: When he saw Jesus from far away, he ran and knelt before him.
local: Balee ŋonaŋ kena Yesu ne kɔŋ lemlem nɛ, te ɔ yeuwɔ ke buŋ ɔ siaman.

eng: shouting, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? Swear to God that you wont torture me.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu fae feliŋkum keŋ nyi waa lee balee ŋon man. Keŋte balee ŋon fae yeesa keŋkeŋ nyiaa, Yesu, Wurubuarɛ Adidote Bu, woŋ te n ne gyae me gyaŋ? Mɛɛ mo Wurubuarɛ mɛɛ doo neŋ teree nyi, na naase maŋ diyem ya.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? He responded, Legion is my name, because we are many.
local: Te Yesu bɔɔse balee ŋon nyi, N yele yɛna sena? Te ɔ yako e nyi, Ma yele yɛna dikpii, nawolo nyi da boe.

eng: They pleaded with Jesus not to send them out of that region.
local: Keŋte balee ŋon waase Yesu agbaatee agbaatee nyi, ɔ na gegi feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan lee depuŋ kenaŋ man ya.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside.
local: Kekɔŋ mena na kekuiriise dikpii daale seŋɛɛ bula keŋ teŋa man bee dii.

eng: Send us into the pigs! they begged. Let us go into the pigs.
local: Keŋte feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan doo Yesu teree nyi, Waaseda, fa daa gbɛɛ na de kaa gyoo kekuiriise kɛwɔ man.

eng: Jesus gave them permission, so the unclean spirits left the man and went into the pigs. Then the herd of about two thousand pigs rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu sɛɛwɔ te ba leewɔ ka gyoo kekuiriise baŋ man. Kekuiriise baŋ baa yɛɛ ŋgba kakpoŋse ala nɛ, te be yeuwɔ tisi dekeŋkeŋ kyoloŋkyoloŋ keŋ gyu ka gyoo loŋ man, te be popou munuwɔ.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside. People came to see what had happened.
local: Mena dɔɔ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɛɛ kekuiriise baŋ dɔɔ nɛ yeuwɔ, te be gyuuwɔ ke yako kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ fa balaŋ baŋ be kyaa donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ na akuraase ŋan man pou. Keŋte balaŋ kɔŋawɔ kaa kɛɛ kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: They came to Jesus and saw the man who used to be demon-possessed. They saw the very man who had been filled with many demons sitting there fully dressed and completely sane, and they were filled with awe.
local: Mena dɔɔ te kyare ya te balaŋ kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ te be naa walaŋ ŋon nafɔ feliŋkumɛɛ doo ɔ man nɛ baŋɛɛ, na ɔ doona wa abɔɔ na ɔ diteŋ keŋ te taŋ. Ba kena nyi balee ŋon diteŋ te taŋ nɛ, gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ nideli.

eng: Those who had actually seen what had happened to the demon-possessed man told the others about the pigs.
local: Botɔɔ te balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ naa kpene keŋ be kɔŋ balee ŋon feliŋkumɛɛ doo ɔ man na kekuiriise baŋ dɔɔ nɛ, yako nombia ŋan pou be kɔŋ nɛ fa balaŋ akaŋ baŋ.

eng: Then they pleaded with Jesus to leave their region.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ waase Yesu nyi waa lee be tɛɛle dɔɔ.

eng: While he was climbing into the boat, the one who had been demon-possessed pleaded with Jesus to let him come along as one of his disciples.
local: Yesu ka mo waa gyoo degbele man nɛ, balee ŋon ɔ be kyɔ e nɛ waase e nyi waa sila e.

eng: But Jesus wouldnt allow it. Go home to your own people, Jesus said, and tell them what the Lord has done for you and how he has shown you mercy.
local: Mɔna Yesu te sɛɛ ya te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Ta gyu n dɛɛ, na n kaa yako n loŋtona kpene keŋ n Gbeŋgyoo be yɛɛ fa neŋ, na mena keŋ ɔ be wii n waraŋase nɛ.

eng: The man went away and began to proclaim in the Ten Cities all that Jesus had done for him, and everyone was amazed.
local: Mena dɔɔ balee ŋon laŋawɔ, te ɔ gyuuwɔ donɔɔse kufu ŋan ŋe kyaa dupuŋ kenaŋ man nɛ ɔ ne yako kpene keŋ Yesu be yɛɛ fa e nɛ. Te gyakoloŋ gyoo balaŋ baŋ pou.

eng: Jesus crossed the lake again, and on the other side a large crowd gathered around him on the shore.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu besewɔ gyoo degbele man toŋ boo keŋ gyu diŋgyiŋ. Keŋte balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ kaa wuu ɔ dɔɔ boo nɔɔman botɔɔ.

eng: Jairus, one of the synagogue leaders, came forward. When he saw Jesus, he fell at his feet.
local: Keŋte Gyudatena ɔsom deni man kegyia ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Gyariose nɛ kɔŋawɔ botɔɔ. Ɔ kena Yesu nɛ, ɔ ke buŋawɔ ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ.

eng: and pleaded with him, My daughter is about to die. Please, come and place your hands on her so that she can be healed and live.
local: te ɔ waase e nideli nyiaa, Me bu alebubii ne gyae waa yeŋ. Kɔŋ na n kaa mo n nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ na waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.

eng: So Jesus went with him. A swarm of people were following Jesus, crowding in on him.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu sila e laŋ. Be kee gyu na balaŋ burum silana wɔ, na bɛɛ soko e.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years.
local: Na alo ŋolo kyaa keŋ fatabo ne gyɔ e kulutooneŋ kufu ala yaa.

eng: She had suffered a lot under the care of many doctors, and had spent everything she had without getting any better. In fact, she had gotten worse.
local: Ɔ te naa diyem nideli teeneŋtena nyiŋmaa man, te ɔ wɔlɛɛ ɔ kɔba keŋ pou ɔ dana nɛ nyi waa nyiŋ kawee kekyɔ. Mɔna ɔ kawee keŋ na bese bo yɛɛ doŋ kpu.

eng: Because she had heard about Jesus, she came up behind him in the crowd and touched his clothes.
local: Alo ŋon ke nyii Yesu wose man nombia nɛ, ɔ gyoowɔ balaŋ baŋ man kaa sila Yesu te ɔ yakowɔ ɔ nyeeman nyiaa, Akpaa me nyiŋ mo me nyiŋmaa tina ɔ kegba koraŋ na, me kawee gyae ke taŋ. Keŋte ɔ ŋereketee ɔ wose do balaŋ baŋ man ka seŋ Yesu wɔle, te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaa tina ɔ kegba.

eng: Her bleeding stopped immediately, and she sensed in her body that her illness had been healed.
local: Karatetee dokoloŋ te fatabo keŋ ne gyɔ e nɛ karawɔ, te ɔ naawɔ ɔ wose man nyi ɔ kawee te taŋ.

eng: At that very moment, Jesus recognized that power had gone out from him. He turned around in the crowd and said, Who touched my clothes.
local: Debokenaŋ man te Yesu naawɔ nyi doŋ daale ta lee ɔ man. Keŋte ɔ besenawɔ kɛɛ balaŋ baŋ gyaŋee te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Amɔte tinaa na ma kegba.

eng: His disciples said to him, Dont you see the crowd pressing against you? Yet you ask, Who touched me.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ yako e nyiaa, Nyaŋ gbagba n naa mena keŋ balaŋ be wuu n dɔɔ, na n na bese bɔɔse nyi Amɔte tinaa na maŋ? .

eng: But Jesus looked around carefully to see who had done it.
local: Mɔna Yesu gyakaa ɔ ne kɛo nyi waa naa walaŋ ŋon ɔ be tina ɔ kegba nɛ.

eng: The woman, full of fear and trembling, came forward. Knowing what had happened to her, she fell down in front of Jesus and told him the whole truth.
local: Alo ŋon kena mena keŋ ɔ wose be yɛɛ e nɛ, ɔ moo gyakoloŋ na wose keseree kaa buŋ Yesu nawɔɔtɛɛ te ɔ yako e kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ pou.

eng: He responded, Daughter, your faith has healed you; go in peace, healed from your disease.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, Me bu, n kelɛɛ maŋ di dɔɔ te n nyiŋa kawee kekyɔ. Ta wosefɛɛreŋ man, nawolo nyi n kaale keŋ pou te taŋ.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking with her, messengers came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Why bother the teacher any longer.
local: Yesu ka seŋ botɔɔ ɔ ne kolosi nɛ, baale leewɔ Gyudatena ɔsom deni man kegyia Gyariose ŋon dɛɛ kaa yako e nyiaa, N bu alebu ŋon ta yem. Weera te n ne naase de Gbeŋgyoo ŋon diyem bela.

eng: But Jesus overheard their report and said to the synagogue leader, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting.
local: Mɔ Yesu ta mo balaŋ baŋ nombia ŋan bɔɔ kaa yako ŋon nɛ yɛɛ kolo ya. Mena dɔɔ ɔ yako ɔsom deni man kegyia ŋon nyiaa, Na yee ya, nyaŋ lɛɛ maŋ di.

eng: He didnt allow anyone to follow him except Peter, James, and John, James brother.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu te yela ŋolo ke sila e kpu na Pita na Gyemisi, na Gyemisi ɔ naabu Gyɔn baageŋ ya.

eng: They came to the synagogue leaders house, and he saw a commotion, with people crying and wailing loudly.
local: Be ka benaa Gyudatena ɔsom deni man kegyia Gyariose ŋon dɛɛ nɛ, Yesu nyiiwɔ na balaŋ ne wii na bɛɛ fae yeesa keŋkeŋ.

eng: He went in and said to them, Whats all this commotion and crying about? The child isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: Yesu ka gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man nɛ te ɔ bɔɔse balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, Weera te ɛ ne wii te ɛ ne fae yeesa? Bu ŋon ta yeŋa ta ya, ɔ doo dosoroŋ bo.

eng: They laughed at him, but he threw them all out. Then, taking the childs parents and his disciples with him, he went to the room where the child was.
local: Botɔɔ te balaŋ baŋ boŋo Yesu. Keŋte ɔ gegi balaŋ baŋ pou lee te ɔ moo bu ŋon ɔ kya na ɔ naa, na ɔ kaseela batooro baŋ be silana e nɛ, te be gyuuwɔ deni keŋ man woo ŋon doo nɛ.

eng: Taking her hand, he said to her, Talitha koum , which means, Young woman, get up.
local: Keŋte ɔ kyaŋa bu ŋon nyiŋmaa man te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Talita kumi! Kei nawolo nyi, Alebubii, me kpa koro.

eng: Suddenly the young woman got up and began to walk around. She was years old. They were shocked!.
local: Karatetee dokoloŋ te alebu ŋon korowɔ seŋ ɔ ne tɛɛ. Keŋte gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ nideli. Na bu ŋon te di kulutooneŋ kufu ala.

eng: He gave them strict orders that no one should know what had happened. Then he told them to give her something to eat.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu faree wɔ nideli nyi be na yeli walaŋ na walaŋ ke nyii nombii kei ya. Te ɔ yako wɔ nyi baa gyae kolo fa bu ŋon ke di.

eng: That day Jesus went out of the house and sat down beside the lake.
local: Mena wee kenaŋ nɛ, Yesu leewɔ dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man ɔ kyaa nɛ gyu ke kyaa boo nɔɔman.

eng: Such large crowds gathered around him that he climbed into a boat and sat down. The whole crowd was standing on the shore.
local: Keŋte balaŋ burum kaa yilaawɔ kilisi e. Mena dɔɔ ɔ gyoowɔ ke kyaa degbele man, na balaŋ baŋ mɔ pou seŋɛɛ dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ.

eng: He said many things to them in parables: A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nombia burum aduya man nyiaa, Wɔɔfarale ŋolo gyuuwɔ ke ŋmatee abɔɔ bia ŋgba amɔla nɛ ɔ wɔɔman.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path, and birds came and ate it.
local: Ɔ kɛɛ ŋmatee abɔɔ bia ŋan nɛ, ŋaale yalawɔ wɔɔ keŋ gbɛɛneŋ man te gyebuse kaa tɔɔsee ŋa di.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Ŋaale mɔ yalawɔ kpanaŋse dɔɔ te ŋe tuuwɔ, mɔna tɛɛle ta boo botɔɔ ya.

eng: But when the sun came up, it scorched the plants, and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔga kaa seŋawɔ na, te ŋa botowɔ. Te keŋ ŋe te nyiŋ lila burum dɔɔ ya nɛ, ŋe popou yekeewɔ.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked them.
local: Bia ŋan ŋaale mɔ yalawɔ kpalamse man te ŋe tuuwɔ, te kpalamse ŋan mɔ tuuwɔ milii ŋa mena dɔɔ ŋe te nyiŋ ŋa wose ya.

eng: Other seed fell on good soil and bore fruit, in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Abɔɔ bia ŋan ŋaale mɔ yalawɔ tɛɛle kpaakpaa dɔɔ te ŋe tuuwɔ bee, te ŋa see bia burum. Ŋaale see sɔŋonoŋ , te ŋaale mɔ see sɔŋotooro , te ŋaale mɔ see saonakufu .

eng: Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ŋon ɔ dana deŋela waa nyii na, waa tei nideli.

eng: Jesus disciples came and said to him, Why do you use parables when you speak to the crowds.
local: Keŋte Yesu kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa, Weera n ne kolosi fa balaŋ baŋ aduya man.

eng: Jesus replied, Because they havent received the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, but you have.
local: Te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ ta toro ya agyueŋse na ɛ ka gyeŋ nombia ŋan weesɛɛ lee ɔ gyoori keŋ wose man, mɔna na balaŋ pou ya.

eng: For those who have will receive more and they will have more than enough. But as for those who dont have, even the little they have will be taken away from them.
local: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ nombia aweeseŋ ŋan man na, ŋon te Wurubuarɛ waa toro wa agyueŋ na waa gyeŋ kpu. Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ba gyeŋ kolo lee Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ nombia aweeseŋ ŋan man ya na, kyomii keŋ ɔ na gyueŋ nyi ɔ gyeŋ koraŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa lɛo ke lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man.

eng: This is why I speak to the crowds in parables: although they see, they dont really see; and although they hear, they dont really hear or understand.
local: Keŋ dɔɔ te mee kolosi fa wɔ aduya man yɛna nyiaa, Bɛɛ kɛo kɛo mɔna bɔɔ ne naa kolo ya. Bɛɛ tei tei, mɔna bɔɔ ne nyii nombia ŋenaŋ man ya.

eng: What Isaiah prophesied has become completely true for them: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing .
local: Nombia akɔŋaŋ balaŋ kɛwɔ dɔɔ yela Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya nombia ŋan ɔ be yako see nɛ kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Yesaya yakowɔ nyiaa, Balaŋ kɛwɔ baa tei tei, mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa nyii nombia ŋenaŋ man ya. Te baa kɛo kɛo, mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa naa kolo ya.

eng: For this peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them .
local: Nawolo nyi mena balaŋ kɛwɔ dana konɔɔyuŋ, te be dana deŋela tae. Ba te kyaŋ be sia wuu na bɔɔ nyiŋ naa ya. Nawolo nyi akpaa be sia ne naa te ba deŋela mɔ ne nyii na, keŋte baa nyii nombia ŋan man na baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na baa kɔŋ me gyaŋ, na maa mo be nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ.

eng: Happy are your eyes because they see. Happy are your ears because they hear.
local: Mɔna ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ ɛ te yɛɛ nyeebam, nawolo nyi ɛ sia ne naa te ɛ deŋela mɔ ne nyii.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and righteous people wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra na Wurubuarɛ balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ɔ siaman baŋ bɔɔ kyaa kaalaŋ nɛ, gyaewɔ nyi baa naa kpene keŋ ɛ ne naa nɛ, mɔna ba te naa ya. Te be gyaewɔ nyi baa nyii kpene keŋ ɛ ne nyii nɛ, mɔna be te nyii ya.

eng: Consider then the parable of the farmer.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Nɛnɛɛ ɛ tei na ɛ ke nyii wɔɔfarale ŋon dudu keŋ asɛɛ.

eng: Whenever people hear the word about the kingdom and dont understand it, the evil one comes and carries off what was planted in their hearts. This is the seed that was sown on the path.
local: Abɔɔ bia ŋan be yale wɔɔ keŋ gbɛɛneŋ man nɛ, seŋɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ bee nyii Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man nombia, te ɔbɔnsam ne kɔŋ kaa ŋmɛɛle ŋa lee be konɔɔse man, na bɔɔ nyiŋ nyii asɛɛ ya nɛ.

eng: As for the seed that was spread on rocky ground, this refers to people who hear the word and immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Bia ŋan be yale kpanaŋse dɔɔ nɛ, seŋɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ bee nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan, te bɛɛ lɛɛ ŋa na gyoŋ debokenaŋ man nɛ.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: Mɔna keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan te nyiŋ lila be man dɔɔ ya nɛ, bɔɔ ne tale seŋ keŋkeŋ kelii ɔto ya. Mena dɔɔ akpaa de kɔŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan dɔɔ te bɛɛ naase wɔ diyem na, be ta bese be wɔle bileŋ.

eng: As for the seed that was spread among thorny plants, this refers to those who hear the word, but the worries of this life and the false appeal of wealth choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: Bia ŋan be yale kpalamse man nɛ, seŋɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ bee nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan, te tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nombia, na kɔba kegyae beiŋ, na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ akaŋ ŋan kegyae da gyoo be man nɛ, na bee yeli baa bee Kristo man ya.

eng: As for what was planted on good soil, this refers to those who hear and understand, and bear fruit and produce—in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Abɔɔ bia ŋan be yale tɛɛle kpaakpaa dɔɔ nɛ, seŋɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ bee nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan te bɛɛ lɛɛ ŋa do konɔɔ kpaakpaa man, te bɛɛ tela ŋa nideli te bɛɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ di ŋe dɔɔ nɛ. Mena dɔɔ nombia ŋan ne yeli bɛɛ yɛɛ abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋgba ŋan ŋɔɔ se bia sɔŋonoŋ , na sɔŋotooro , na saonakufu nɛ.

eng: Jesus told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like someone who planted good seed in his field.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu wɔle fa wɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ balee ŋolo be gyu ke ŋmatee abɔɔ bia kpaakpaa nyiŋan ɔ wɔɔman nɛ.

eng: While people were sleeping, an enemy came and planted weeds among the wheat and went away.
local: Mɔna nelim balaŋ pou kedokii nɛ, ɔ kɔlare kɔŋawɔ kaa ŋmatee ŋalese kumɛɛ bom na abɔɔ bia ŋan te ɔ laŋawɔ.

eng: When the stalks sprouted and bore grain, then the weeds also appeared.
local: Abɔɔ bia ŋan ketu se bia nɛ, te ŋalese kumɛɛ ŋan mɔ lese ŋa wose debɔɔ.

eng: The servants of the landowner came and said to him, Master, didnt you plant good seed in your field? Then how is it that it has weeds.
local: Botɔɔ te wɔɔte ŋon tewulɛɛ kɔŋawɔ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa, De gbeŋgyoo, na abɔɔ bia kpaakpaa nyiŋan te n ŋmateewɔ n wɔɔ keŋ man yee? Te aleŋ yaa ŋalese kumɛɛ kɛŋa leewɔ.

eng: An enemy has done this, he answered. The servants said to him, Do you want us to go and gather them.
local: Keŋte balee ŋon yakowɔ nyiaa, Me kɔlare ŋolo kaa yɛɛ laa mena nɛ. Botɔɔ te ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ bɔɔse e nyiaa, N ne gyae nyi dee gyu ke kulii ŋalese kumɛɛ ŋan.

eng: But the landowner said, No, because if you gather the weeds, youll pull up the wheat along with them.
local: Keŋte balee ŋon tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Koa ɛ yela ŋe ka seŋ, na mena ya nɛ, akpaa ɛ gyu ɛ ne kulii ŋalese kumɛɛ ŋan nɛ, ɛ te kɛɛ nideli ya na ɛ ka kulii abɔɔ bia kpaakpaa ŋan mɔ kpu na ŋa.

eng: Let both grow side by side until the harvest. And at harvesttime Ill say to the harvesters,First gather the weeds and tie them together in bundles to be burned. But bring the wheat into my barn.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ yela ŋe popou ka seŋ kelii kekɛrɛɛ tɛɛ. Debaŋ kenaŋ nɛ, maa yako baŋ baa kɛrɛɛ ŋa nɛ nyiaa, ɛ taŋgbɛɛ kulii ŋalese kumɛɛ ŋan pɛ, na ɛ ke bake ŋa kaboese man na ɛ ka too ŋa. Kenaŋ wɔle na ɛ ke yilaa abɔɔ bia kpaakpaa ŋan pou ka do me kpeese man.

eng: He told another parable to them: The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in his field.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu wɔle fa wɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba daŋ daale bii peperee keŋ ke yɛɛ ŋgba kpeli bii, keŋ balee ŋolo ba mo ke duu ɔ wɔɔman nɛ.

eng: Its the smallest of all seeds. But when its grown, its the largest of all vegetable plants. It becomes a tree so that the birds in the sky come and nest in its branches.
local: Daŋbii kei yɛna bii peperee daasebia pou man. Mɔna nyi akpaa ke da bee na, kɛɛ bee bese daŋ dinaa keŋ nyi gyebuse ne kɔŋ kaa loo ba asaase do ke tombia man.

eng: He told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through all the dough.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu fa wɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba tee keŋ alo ŋolo ba mo kyakale bodobodo biliŋ tasa dinaa man, kaboena biliŋ keŋ pou korowɔ wuku nɛ.

eng: Jesus said all these things to the crowds in parables, and he spoke to them only in parables.
local: Aduya man te Yesu kolosiwɔ fa balaŋ dikpii keŋ pou. Ɔ te kolosi nombii daale fa ŋolo keŋ ɔ te gyɔ dudu ya.
