eng: This was to fulfill what the prophet spoke: Ill speak in parables; Ill declare what has been hidden since the beginning of the world.
local: Kei yele la Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ɔ be tɛɛ ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya dɔɔ yako see nɛ kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Aduya man te maa kolosi fa wɔ na maa yako wɔ nombia aweeseŋ ŋan bɔɔ gyeŋ ya, leenaŋ tɛɛle kewalaŋ tɛɛ nɛ.

eng: Jesus left the crowds and went into the house. His disciples came to him and said, Explain to us the parable of the weeds in the field.
local: Keŋte Yesu lɔɔ balaŋ dikpii keŋ gbɛɛ te ɔ gyuuwɔ dekpaŋalaŋ man. Ɔ kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ te be yako e nyiaa, Wola daa dudu keŋ n be gyɔɔ lee ŋalese kumɛɛ ŋan ŋɔɔ tuu wɔɔ keŋ man nɛ asɛɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, The one who plants the good seed is the Human One.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Walaŋ ŋon ɔ be duu abɔɔ bia kpaakpaa ŋan nɛ yɛna maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon.

eng: The field is the world. And the good seeds are the followers of the kingdom. But the weeds are the followers of the evil one.
local: wɔɔ keŋ yɛna tɛɛle kei pou. Abɔɔ bia kpaakpaa ŋan yɛna Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man bia baŋ. Ŋalese kumɛɛ ŋan yɛna ɔbɔnsam ŋon bia.

eng: The enemy who planted them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the present age. The harvesters are the angels.
local: Te walaŋkum ŋon ɔ be duu ŋalese kumɛɛ ŋan yɛna ɔbɔnsam ŋon. Kekɛrɛɛ tɛɛ keŋ yɛna debaŋ ɔtomante keŋ. Baŋ baa kɛrɛɛ nɛ yɛna Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ.

eng: Just as people gather weeds and burn them in the fire, so it will be at the end of the present age.
local: Mena keŋ bɔɔ yilaa ŋalese kumɛɛ ŋan te bɔɔ too ŋa nɛ, mena ne gyae la ke kɔŋ debaŋ ɔtomante keŋ man.

eng: The Human One will send his angels, and they will gather out of his kingdom all things that cause people to fall away and all people who sin.
local: Maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa kpila me kpilala lee adido na be kaa yilaa kpene kamasɛ keŋ ne yeli walaŋ ne yɛɛ dukum nɛ, na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra pou lee me gyoori keŋ man.

eng: He will throw them into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ baa fuŋ balaŋ kumɛɛ baŋ do boalaŋ degyapaa keŋ man. Botɔɔ te baa fae yeesa na baa tao be kela.

eng: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in their Fathers kingdom. Those who have ears should hear.
local: Mɔna baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ, baa ŋalakee ŋgba weese nɛ be kya ŋon gyoori keŋ man. Baŋ be dana deŋela anenyiiŋ dei na, baa tei nideli.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a treasure that somebody hid in a field, which someone else found and covered up. Full of joy, the finder sold everything and bought that field.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba kolo kpaakpaate keŋ ke dana koya doŋ te bɔɔ mo weese tɛɛle daale man nɛ. Balee ŋolo ke gyu kena ke nɛ, te ɔ desina ke weese nideli. Ke yɛɛ e gyoŋ gbaa mena dɔɔ ɔ gyuuwɔ ka mo kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɔ dana nɛ yɔɔ, te ɔ moowɔ ke lɛɛ tɛɛle kenaŋ.

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant in search of fine pearls.
local: Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba kebudiiri ŋon ɔ dɔŋ ɔ ne gyae kegyaŋase kpaakpaa nyiŋan waa lɛɛ nɛ.

eng: When he found one very precious pearl, he went and sold all that he owned and bought it.
local: Ɔ ke gyu ke nyiŋ kegyaŋa kpaakpaate nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ ke yɔɔ kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɔ dana nɛ, te ɔ moowɔ ke lɛɛ ke.

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net that people threw into the lake and gathered all kinds of fish.
local: Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba asawu lɔɔra baŋ bɔɔ fuŋ ba asawu do loŋ man te bɔɔ kyaŋ kpebia kpookpoo nɛ.

eng: When it was full, they pulled it to the shore, where they sat down and put the good fish together into containers. But the bad fish they threw away.
local: Ba kena nyi asawu keŋ te wulu nɛ, be wɔŋee ke kɔŋ dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ. Be tisiwɔ kyaa te be tɔɔsee kpebia kpaakpaa nyibaŋ do be doŋase man, te be fuŋii akumɛɛ baŋ pou leki.

eng: Thats the way it will be at the end of the present age. The angels will go out and separate the evil people from the righteous people.
local: Mena ne gyae la ke kɔŋ wee ɔtomante keŋ man. Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ baa kɔŋ kaa lese balaŋ kumɛɛ baŋ lee balaŋ kpaakpaa baŋ man.

eng: and will throw the evil ones into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Na baa fuŋ balaŋ kumɛɛ baŋ do boalaŋ keŋ ke bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man. Botɔɔ te baa fae yeesa na baa tao be kela.

eng: Have you understood all these things?Jesus asked. They said to him, Yes.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ nyii nombia kɛŋa pou asɛɛ? Te be sɛɛwɔ nyi Ayi.

eng: Then he said to them, Therefore, every legal expert who has been trained as a disciple for the kingdom of heaven is like the head of a household who brings old and new things out of their treasure chest.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Nombii kei nawolo baa nyiaa, mmaraa wolale kamasɛ ŋon ɔ be kasee Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ nombia gyeŋ ŋa nɛ, ɔ yɛɛ ŋgba dekpaŋalaŋ kegyia ŋon ɔ ne kyaa lese wa abɔɔ wɔlɛɛ na benɛɛ lee wa abɔɔ dɛɛsee man nɛ.

eng: When Jesus finished these parables, he departed.
local: Yesu ke gyɔ aduya kɛŋa taŋ nɛ, ɔ leewɔ botɔɔ laŋ.

eng: When he came to his hometown, he taught the people in their synagogue. They were surprised and said, Where did he get this wisdom? Where did he get the power to work miracles?.
local: Ɔ ke gyu ka gyoo ɔ dɛɛ nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ ka wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia bɔ ɔsom deni man, te ɔ kawola keŋ doo be popou nɔɔ, keŋte be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Aleŋ yaa ɔ nyiŋa nombia kagyeŋ kei, na doŋ keŋ ɔ na mo yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana kɛŋa lee.

eng: Isnt he the carpenters son? Isnt his mother named Mary? Arent James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?.
local: Na kapenta ŋon bu yaa yee? Na ɔ naa yɛna Mɛɛre yee, na ɔ naabia yɛna Gyemisi na Gyosɛfo na Simɔn na Gyuda yee?.

eng: And his sisters, arent they here with us? Where did this man get all this.
local: Na daa na ɔ desina kyaa la kɛbo yee? Aleŋ kitii yaa ɔ nyiŋa mena doŋ kei lee?.

eng: They were repulsed by him and fell into sin. But Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns and in their own households.
local: Mena dɔɔ ba te lɛɛ e ya. Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Balaŋ ne bu Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre yenaŋ kamasɛ, mɔna ŋon gbagba ɔ donɔɔ man balaŋ, na ɔ dekpaŋalaŋmantena bee buu na e ya.

eng: He was unable to do many miracles there because of their disbelief.
local: Te keŋ ba te lɛɛ Yesu di dɔɔ ya nɛ, ɔ te yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia burum ɔ dɛɛ botɔɔ ya.

eng: Jesus began to teach beside the lake again. Such a large crowd gathered that he climbed into a boat there on the lake. He sat in the boat while the whole crowd was nearby on the shore.
local: Wee daale Yesu besewɔ ɔ na wola Wurubuarɛ nombia Galelia boo nɔɔman. Te mena keŋ balaŋ burum be kaa wuu ɔ dɔɔ dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ gyoowɔ ke kyaa degbele man gyu loŋ keŋ dɔɔ na balaŋ akaŋ baŋ pou seŋɛɛ dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ.

eng: He said many things to them in parables. While teaching them, he said.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ wola wɔ nombia aduya man.

eng: Listen to this! A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: Te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ tei kɛbo, wɔɔfarale ŋolo gyuuwɔ ke ŋmatee abɔɔbia ŋgba amɔla nɛ ɔ wɔɔman.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path; and the birds came and ate it.
local: Ɔ kɛɛ ŋmatee ŋa nɛ, ŋaale yalawɔ gbɛɛneŋbia ŋan doo wɔɔ keŋ man nɛ man, te gyebuse kaa tɔɔsee ŋa di.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Ŋaale mɔ yalawɔ kpanaŋse dɔɔ te ŋe tuuwɔ, mɔna tɛɛle ta boo botɔɔ ya.

eng: When the sun came up, it scorched the plants; and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔga kaa seŋawɔ na te ŋa botowɔ. Te keŋ ŋe te nyiŋ lila burum dɔɔ ya nɛ, ŋe popou yekeewɔ.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked the seeds, and they produced nothing.
local: Bia ŋan ŋaale mɔ yalawɔ kpalamse man te ŋe tuuwɔ, te kpalamse ŋan mɔ tuuwɔ milii ŋa. Mena dɔɔ ŋe te nyiŋ ŋa wose se bia ya.

eng: Other seed fell into good soil and bore fruit. Upon growing and increasing, the seed produced in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of one hundred to one.
local: Abɔɔbia ŋan ŋaale mɔ yalawɔ tɛɛle kpaakpaa dɔɔ te ŋe tuuwɔ bee, te ŋa see bia burum. Ŋaale see bia sao na kufu , te ŋaale mɔ see bia sɔŋotooro , te ŋaale mɔ see bia sɔŋonoŋ .

eng: He said, Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ŋon ɔ dana deŋela waa nyii na, waa tei nideli.

eng: When they were alone, the people around Jesus, along with the Twelve, asked him about the parables.
local: Keka Yesu waageŋ nɛ, ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ na balaŋ baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ kpu na wɔ nɛ yako e nyi waa wola wɔ dudu keŋ asɛɛ.

eng: He said to them, The secret of Gods kingdom has been given to you, but to those who are outside everything comes in parables.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ ta toro ya agyueŋse na ɛ ka gyeŋ nombia ŋan weesɛɛ lee ɔ gyoori keŋ wose man. Mɔna balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ kpuɛ na ŋon ya nɛ dɔɔ, aduya man te baa kolosi fa wɔ.

eng: This is so that they can look and see but have no insight, and they can hear but not understand. Otherwise, they might turn their lives around and be forgiven.
local: na ke yela, Baa kɛo kɛo mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa naa kolo ya, te baa tei tei mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa nyii nombia ŋenaŋ man ya. Na mena ya nɛ, baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man, na Wurubuarɛ ka mo be nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ. .

eng: Dont you understand this parable? Then how will you understand all the parables?.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Akpaa ɛ bee nyii dudu kei asɛɛ ya na, sena te ɛ ke yɛɛ na ɛ ke nyii aduya akaŋ man?.

eng: The farmer scatters the word.
local: Abɔɔbia ŋan wɔɔfarale ŋon be ŋmatee nɛ seŋɛɛ fa Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan.

eng: This is the meaning of the seed that fell on the path: When the word is scattered and people hear it, right away Satan comes and steals the word that was planted in them.
local: Balaŋ baale yɛɛ ŋgba bia ŋan be yale wɔɔ keŋ gbɛɛneŋbia man nɛ. Be nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan pɛ, na ɔbɔnsam te kɔŋ kaa lese ŋa lee be konɔɔse man.

eng: Heres the meaning of the seed that fell on rocky ground: When people hear the word, they immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Balaŋ baale mɔ yɛɛ ŋgba bia ŋan be yale kpanaŋse dɔɔ nɛ. Be nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan na, bɛɛ lɛɛ ŋa na gyoŋ debokenaŋ man.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: Mɔna keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan te nyiŋ lila be man dɔɔ ya nɛ, bɔɔ ne tale seŋ keŋkeŋ kelii ɔto ya. Mena dɔɔ akpaa de kɔŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan dɔɔ te bɛɛ naase wɔ diyem na, be ta bese be wɔle bileŋ.

eng: Others are like the seed scattered among the thorny plants. These are the ones who have heard the word.
local: Balaŋ baale mɔ yɛɛ ŋgba bia ŋan be yale kpalamse man nɛ. Bee nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan.

eng: but the worries of this life, the false appeal of wealth, and the desire for more things break in and choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: mɔna akpaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nombia na kɔba kegyae beiŋ, na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ akaŋ ŋan kegyae da gyoo be man nɛ, na bee yele baa bee Kristo man ya.

eng: The seed scattered on good soil are those who hear the word and embrace it. They bear fruit, in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case sixty to one, and in another case one hundred to one.
local: Balaŋ baale mɔ yɛɛ ŋgba bia ŋan be yale tɛɛle kpaakpaa dɔɔ nɛ. Bee nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan te bɛɛ lɛɛ ŋa do konɔɔ kpaakpaa man, te bɛɛ tela ŋa nideli te bɛɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ di ŋe dɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ nombia ŋan ne yele bɛɛ yɛɛ abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋgba ŋan ŋɔɔ se bia sao na kufu na sɔŋotooro , na sɔŋonoŋ , nɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Does anyone bring in a lamp in order to put it under a basket or a bed? Shouldnt it be placed on a lampstand?.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ŋolo bɛɛ kyaŋee kanea na waa mo kolo wuu ke dɔɔ yaa waa mo ke do mpa ateta ya. Mɔna ɔ na mo ke bo gyakaa kolo dɔɔ adido.

eng: Everything hidden will be revealed, and everything secret will come out into the open.
local: Nawolo nyi kolo ba weesɛɛ keŋ be gyae ka lee debɔɔ ya, te kolo mɔ be wuɛ keŋ nyi be gyae ka lee debɔɔ ya.

eng: Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ dana deŋele anenyiiŋ dei nɛ, waa see deŋele na waa tei.

eng: He said to them, Listen carefully! God will evaluate you with the same standard you use to evaluate others. Indeed, you will receive even more.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ bela nyiaa, Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose nideli lee nombia ŋan ɛ ne nyii nɛ man. Nawolo nyi kpene keŋ n mo yɛɛ n dɔɔ nɛ, keŋ ke te Wurubuarɛ mɔ waa mo yɛɛ neŋ, te waa yɛɛ neŋ gba kela mena keŋ n ba mo yɛɛ n dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they dont have will be taken away from them.
local: Nyaŋ ŋon n dana kolo na Wurubuarɛ waa kpu neŋ na n ke nyiŋ kpu. Te nyaŋ ŋon n be dana kolo ya na, kyomii keŋ n na gyueŋ nyi n dana koraŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa lɛo ke lee n nyiŋmaa man.

eng: Then Jesus said, This is what Gods kingdom is like. Its as though someone scatters seed on the ground.
local: Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu fa wɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ walaŋ ne ŋmatee abɔɔbia ŋaale ŋgba amɔla ɔ wɔɔman nɛ.

eng: then sleeps and wakes night and day. The seed sprouts and grows, but the farmer doesnt know how.
local: Nelim na ka weese ɔ doo te ɔ fom na abɔɔbia ŋan ne tuu na ŋɛɛ bee, mɔna ŋon gbagba ba gyeŋ mena keŋ ne yɛɛ pɛte ŋɛɛ bee ya.

eng: The earth produces crops all by itself, first the stalk, then the head, then the full head of grain.
local: Tɛɛle keŋ gbagba ne yele la te bia ŋan ne tuu. Ne taŋgbɛɛ yɛɛ faareŋse na te fulu, pɛte kaa yɛɛ bia.

eng: Whenever the crop is ready, the farmer goes out to cut the grain because its harvesttime.
local: Amɔla bia ŋan de yɛɛ pɛ, na wɔɔte ŋon ta mo ɔ sakawa gyu ke kara ŋa, nawolo nyi amɔla ŋan kekara debaŋ te lii.

eng: He continued, Whats a good image for Gods kingdom? What parable can I use to explain it?.
local: Yesu besewɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Woŋ te dɛɛ mo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ maa na? Yaa dudu woŋti te dɛɛ gyɔ na dɛɛ mo lese ke man?.

eng: Consider a mustard seed. When scattered on the ground, its the smallest of all the seeds on the earth.
local: Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba daŋbii peperee daale keŋ bɛɛ baake nyi Mastade nɛ. Wɔɔ man abɔɔ ŋan bee duu pou nɛ, daŋbii kei yɛna ŋe man kemaasa.

eng: but when its planted, it grows and becomes the largest of all vegetable plants. It produces such large branches that the birds in the sky are able to nest in its shade.
local: Mɔna nyi akpaa be duu ke nɛ, kɛɛ bee bese kolo dinaa kela daasebia akaŋ ŋan pou, keŋ nyi gyebuse ne kɔŋ kaa loo ba asaase do ke tombia man.

eng: With many such parables he continued to give them the word, as much as they were able to hear.
local: Aduya burum ŋgba kei dinɔɔ nɛ te Yesu moowɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia fa balaŋ baŋ, ŋgba mena keŋ baa tale nyii ŋe man nideli nɛ.

eng: He spoke to them only in parables, then explained everything to his disciples when he was alone with them.
local: Ɔ te kolosi nombii yakaa fa wɔ keŋ ɔ te gyɔ dudu ya. Mɔ nyi akpaa de ka ŋon na ɔ kaseela baŋ baageŋ nɛ, ɔ na lese aduya ŋan pou man wola wɔ.

eng: Later that day, when evening came, Jesus said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake.
local: Mena wee kenaŋ balinɔɔ nɛ, Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ yela dɛɛ toŋ gyu boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ.

eng: They left the crowd and took him in the boat just as he was. Other boats followed along.
local: Keŋte be tinaa balaŋ dikpii keŋ yela, te ŋon na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyoowɔ degbele man te be laŋawɔ. Na agbelese ŋaale mɔ silana wɔ.

eng: Gale-force winds arose, and waves crashed against the boat so that the boat was swamped.
local: Be kee gyu na te feliŋ dinaa daale korowɔ ne gyɔɔ, te gyɔɔ loŋ do degbele keŋ man te ke yela ke munu.

eng: But Jesus was in the rear of the boat, sleeping on a pillow. They woke him up and said, Teacher, dont you care that were drowning.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na Yesu te doo dosoroŋ degbele keŋ wɔle mo ɔ nyee gyakaa keputu dɔɔ. Keŋte ɔ kaseela baŋ ke gyuusu e bɔɔse e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, n be famenɛ ŋgba dɛɛ gyae dee munu yee.

eng: He got up and gave orders to the wind, and he said to the lake, Silence! Be still! The wind settled down and there was a great calm.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu korowɔ te ɔ fae feliŋ keŋ te ɔ yako loŋ keŋ nyiaa, Yɛɛ yididi! Keŋte feliŋ keŋ tinaa kegyɔ yela, te loŋ keŋ mɔ yɛɛwɔ yididi.

eng: Jesus asked them, Why are you frightened? Dont you have faith yet.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ dana gyakoloŋ mena? Ɛ sɛɛ ɛ te lɛɛ ta maŋ di yee.

eng: Overcome with awe, they said to each other, Who then is this? Even the wind and the sea obey him.
local: Gyakoloŋ kyaŋa ɔ kaseela baŋ te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Walaŋ woŋti yaa kei, te feliŋ na loŋ gba ne nyii ɔ dei nɛ.

eng: Jesus left that place and came to his hometown. His disciples followed him.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ lee botɔɔ gyu ŋon gbagba ɔ dɛɛ.

eng: On the Sabbath, he began to teach in the synagogue. Many who heard him were surprised. Where did this man get all this? Whats this wisdom hes been given? What about the powerful acts accomplished through him?.
local: Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee kelii nɛ, ɔ korowɔ gyu bɔ ɔsom deni man ɔ na wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia. Ɔ nombia ŋan ɔ na wolo nɛ doo balaŋ baŋ pou nɔɔ te be bɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, Aleŋ yaa balee kei nyiŋa abɔɔ kɛŋa pou lee? Aleŋ yaa ɔ nyiŋa mena nyansa kei lee? Kɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan ɔ ne yɛɛ?.

eng: Isnt this the carpenter? Isnt he Marys son and the brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? Arent his sisters here with us? They were repulsed by him and fell into sin.
local: Na kapenta ŋon yaa yee? Na ɔ naa yɛna Mɛɛre yee? Na ɔ naabia yɛna Gyemisi na Gyosɛfo na Simɔn na Gyuda yee? Na daa na ɔ desina kyaa la kɛbo yee? Mena dɔɔ ba te lɛɛ e do ya.

eng: Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns, among their relatives, and in their own households.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Balaŋ ne bu Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre yenaŋ kamasɛ, mɔna ŋon gbagba ɔ donɔɔ man balaŋ, na ɔ loŋtona na ɔ dekpaŋalaŋmantena bee buu na e ya.

eng: He was unable to do any miracles there, except that he placed his hands on a few sick people and healed them.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu te tale yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia burum ɔ dɛɛ botɔɔ ya. Ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaa bo gyakaa balaŋ kyomii baale bo dɔɔ, te ɔ kyɔɔ wɔ be kaweese.

eng: He was appalled by their disbelief. Then Jesus traveled through the surrounding villages teaching.
local: Yesu wose yeŋa e ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ balaŋ gbagba te lɛɛ e di ya nɛ dɔɔ. Keŋte ɔ leewɔ donɔɔ keŋ man, ta kila akuraase ŋan man ɔ na wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia.

eng: He called for the Twelve and sent them out in pairs. He gave them authority over unclean spirits.
local: Te Yesu baake ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ te ɔ kpilii wɔ bala bala. Te ɔ fa wɔ doŋ keŋ baa mo di feliŋkumɛɛ dɔɔ.

eng: He instructed them to take nothing for the journey except a walking stick—no bread, no bags, and no money in their belts.
local: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ na mo kolo tela kpu na ɛ gbutuse ya. Ɛ na mo weenɛɛ, yaa akpo yaa kɔba tela ya.

eng: He told them to wear sandals but not to put on two shirts.
local: Ɛ do nkyokotase, mɔna ɛ na mo kegbase tela kpu ya.

eng: He said, Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: Ɛ gyu donɔɔ daale man te dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man be lɛɛ ŋon nɛ, ɛ kyaa botɔɔ kaboena ɛ ka mo lee donɔɔ kenaŋ man.

eng: If a place doesnt welcome you or listen to you, as you leave, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Te akpaa ɛ gyu yenaŋ daale te ba te lɛɛ ŋon yaa be te nyii ŋon ya nɛ, ɛ lee botɔɔ na ɛ kpiisi ya nkyokotase aŋasa lɔ wɔ na ke di adansɛɛ tia wɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ be gyae waa tina wɔ yela ya.

eng: So they went out and proclaimed that people should change their hearts and lives.
local: Mena dɔɔ be gyuuwɔ kekolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan fa balaŋ, nyi baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

eng: They cast out many demons, and they anointed many sick people with olive oil and healed them.
local: Be gegi feliŋkumɛɛ burum lee balaŋ man, te ba moo nuŋ fee kaweesetena burum te ba wose yɛɛ wɔ doŋ.

eng: Herod the king heard about these things, because the name of Jesus had become well-known. Some were saying, John the Baptist has been raised from the dead, and this is why miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: Abɔɔ ŋan pou Yesu be yɛɛ nɛ, yela ɔ yele yaaseewɔ gyu yenaŋ kamasɛ te gyoo Herode mɔ nyiiwɔ. Be ke nyii Yesu yele nɛ, na baale kpa Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon ɔ ba yem nɛ yaa Wurubuarɛ gyuusuwɔ lee yeŋ man, mena dɔɔ te ɔ nyiŋa doŋ ɔ ne yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana kɛŋa nɛ.

eng: Others were saying, He is Elijah. Still others were saying, He is a prophet like one of the ancient prophets.
local: Baale mɔ kpa, Ŋon yɛna Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Elia, te baale mɔ kpa Ɔ yɛɛ gyaŋgba Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ bɔɔ kyaa kaalaŋ nɛ man ŋolo e.

eng: But when Herod heard these rumors, he said, John, whom I beheaded, has been raised to life.
local: Gyoo Herode kenyii mena nɛ ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Gyɔn ŋon mɔɔ kara ɔ nyee nɛ bese la foŋ nɛ.

eng: He said this because Herod himself had arranged to have John arrested and put in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip. Herod had married her.
local: Nawolo nyi na Gyɔn gyakaa ɔ ne yako gyoo Herode nyi, Gbɛɛ be doo nyi waa mo ɔ kegyia Filipo wɛɛle yala ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ ya. Mena dɔɔ gyoo Herode gbagba yela be kyaŋa Gyɔn te ba doo e agbaraagbaraase mo e ketɔ deni.

eng: So Herodias had it in for John. She wanted to kill him, but she couldnt.
local: Nombii kei dɔɔ Herodia moo Gyɔn do ɔ tɔɔ man, te ɔ gyae gbɛɛ keŋ waa mo ko e mɔna ɔ bɛɛ tale ya.

eng: This was because Herod respected John. He regarded him as a righteous and holy person, so he protected him. Johns words greatly confused Herod, yet he enjoyed listening to him.
local: Nawolo nyi gyoo Herode gyeŋ nyi Gyɔn yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman, te ɔ ta mo ɔ wose mɔ fa Wurubuarɛ. Mena dɔɔ gyoo Herode na weese Gyɔn keŋ nyi nombiikum daale be yɛɛ e ya. Debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ gyoo Herode de nyii Gyɔn nɔɔwoya na bɛɛ seŋ e nideli ya, mɔna ɔ ne gyae nyi waa tei e.

eng: Finally, the time was right. It was on one of Herods birthdays, when he had prepared a feast for his high-ranking officials and military officers and Galilees leading residents.
local: Kɛŋa pou wɔle nɛ, gbɛɛ kaa torowɔ fa Herodia. Nawolo nyi Gyoo Herode kee di ɔ kalolawee nɛ, ɔ baake ɔ kegyiise na ɔ yoonɔɔtena kegyiise na Galeliatena baŋ ba wose dana obuo nɛ, nyi be kaa di wee keŋ kyɔ e.

eng: Herods daughter Herodias came in and danced, thrilling Herod and his dinner guests. The king said to the young woman, Ask me whatever you wish, and I will give it to you.
local: Ba ke gyaŋee keŋ nɛ, Herodia bu alebu kɔŋawɔ kaa sɔe te yɛɛ gyoo Herode na balaŋ baŋ pou kyeo. Botɔɔ te gyoo Herode yako alebu ŋon nyiaa, Yako maŋ kpene kamasɛ keŋ n ne gyae, na maa fa neŋ.

eng: Then he swore to her, Whatever you ask I will give to you, even as much as half of my kingdom.
local: Keŋte gyoo Herode kaŋa ntam kpu fa bu ŋon nyiaa, Kpene kamasɛ keŋ n bɔɔse maŋ na maa fa neŋ. Akpaa me gyoori koraŋ te n ne gyae na, maa kpase ke man fa neŋ.

eng: She left the banquet hall and said to her mother, What should I ask for? John the Baptists head, Herodias replied.
local: Botɔɔ te alebu ŋon leewɔ yenaŋ gyu ke bɔɔse ɔ naa nyiaa, Nna, woŋ te maa bɔɔse? Botɔɔ te ɔ naa yako e nyiaa, Yako nyi n ne gyae Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon nyee.

eng: Hurrying back to the ruler, she made her request: I want you to give me John the Baptists head on a plate, right this minute.
local: Debokenaŋ man te alebu ŋon barasewɔ gyu gyoo Herode gyaŋ ke yako e nyiaa, Mɛɛ gyae n ke kara Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre nyee do tasa taŋtaŋalaŋ man fa maŋ.

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests, he didnt want to refuse her.
local: Nombii kei takaa gyoo Herode nideli, mɔna ntam keŋ ɔ be kam ŋɔɔla baŋ siaman dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ te tale bɛɛ ya.

eng: So he ordered a guard to bring Johns head. The guard went to the prison, cut off Johns head.
local: Mena dɔɔ debokenaŋ man te ɔ kpila ɔ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɛrɛɛ balaŋ nyeeya nɛ man ŋolo nyi, waa gyu ke kara Gyɔn nyee kɔna e. Keŋte balee ŋon gyuu deni keŋ man Gyɔn doo nɛ ka lese e kara ɔ nyee.

eng: brought his head on a plate, and gave it to the young woman, and she gave it to her mother.
local: Keŋte ɔ moo ke do tasa taŋtaŋalaŋ man kɔna gyoo Herode te ɔ moowɔ fa alebu ŋon, te ŋon mɔ moo ke fa ɔ naa.

eng: When Johns disciples heard what had happened, they came and took his dead body and laid it in a tomb.
local: Gyɔn kaseela baŋ kenyii ɔ yeŋ nɛ, be gyuuwɔ ka mo e ke wuu.

eng: The apostles returned to Jesus and told him everything they had done and taught.
local: Yesu kpilala kufu bala baŋ ɔ be kpilii nɛ besewɔ kɔŋ kaa yako e nombia ŋan pou bɔɔ yɛɛ, na ŋan pou bɔɔ wola balaŋ nɛ.

eng: Many people were coming and going, so there was no time to eat. He said to the apostles, Come by yourselves to a secluded place and rest for a while.
local: Na mena keŋ balaŋ na gyem tisi be dɔɔ beiŋ dɔɔ nɛ, be te nyiŋ gbɛɛ baa di weenɛɛ gba ya. Mena dɔɔ Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ yela dɛɛ wɔŋ da wose lee yenaŋ gyu botɔɔ keŋ balaŋ be kyaa ya nɛ, na ɛ kaa fɛɛfo.

eng: They departed in a boat by themselves for a deserted place.
local: Keŋte ba gyoowɔ degbele man te be laŋa botɔɔ keŋ balaŋ be kyaa ya nɛ.

eng: Many people saw them leaving and recognized them, so they ran ahead from all the cities and arrived before them.
local: Mɔ be kee gyu nɛ, balaŋ burum naa wɔ te ba gyeŋawɔ nyi baŋ benaŋ. Mena dɔɔ balaŋ leewɔ donɔɔse ŋan man pou yeu gyu ka gyoo botɔɔ keŋ bee gyu nɛ, pɛte Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ kaa gyoowɔ botɔɔ.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion on them because they were like sheep without a shepherd. Then he began to teach them many things.
local: Yesu ke tisi lee degbele keŋ man na ɔ kena balaŋ burum baŋ nɛ, be nombia yɛɛ e waraŋase. Nawolo nyi na be yɛɛ ŋgba namense baŋ bɔɔ dana gbeŋgyoo ya nɛ. Mena dɔɔ ɔ fiasɛɛ ɔ na wola wɔ abɔɔ burum.

eng: Late in the day, his disciples came to him and said, This is an isolated place, and its already late in the day.
local: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ na te lii balinɔɔ, mena dɔɔ ɔ kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa, Kɛbo yɛɛ ŋale nsana ke, te tɛɛ mɔ te yuu.

eng: Send them away so that they can go to the surrounding countryside and villages and buy something to eat for themselves.
local: Lɔ balaŋ baŋ gbɛɛ na baa gyu donɔɔse na akuraase ŋan ŋa benaa nɛ man, na be kaa gyae kolo lɛɛ di.

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said to him, Should we go off and buy bread worth almost eight months pay and give it to them to eat.
local: Mɔ Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ gyae kolo fa wɔ na baa di. Keŋte be bɔɔse e nyiaa, Da mo tomyɛɛre kulutoo dokoloŋ kɔba lɛɛ weenɛɛ koraŋ na, gyae ka yee balaŋ burum kɛwɔ pou.

eng: He said to them, How much bread do you have? Take a look. After checking, they said, Five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu mɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Bodobodose aŋmoŋ te ɛ dana? Ɛ gyu ke kɛɛ. Be ke gyu ke kɛɛ nɛ te be kaa yako e nyiaa, Bodobodose abiliŋ anoŋ na kpebia bala pɛ te de dana.

eng: He directed the disciples to seat all the people in groups as though they were having a banquet on the green grass.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyi baa yela balaŋ baŋ ke kyakee akpii akpii ŋalese nalaŋ ŋan dɔɔ.

eng: They sat down in groups of hundreds and fifties.
local: Mena dɔɔ balaŋ baŋ kyakeewɔ akpii akpii. Akpii ŋaale yɛɛ balaŋ sɔŋonoŋ te ŋaale mɔ yɛɛ balaŋ sɔŋola na kufu .

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, broke the loaves into pieces, and gave them to his disciples to set before the people. He also divided the two fish among them all.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu moo bodobodose anoŋ na kpebia bala baŋ, te ɔ gyinaa ɔ nyee kɛɛ adido te ɔ fanewɔ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ. Te ɔ kɛrɛɛ ŋa fa ɔ kaseela baŋ nyi baa kpɛlɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full.
local: Balaŋ baŋ pou diiwɔ baa ba kegyaebii.

eng: They filled twelve baskets with the leftover pieces of bread and fish.
local: Be ke di taŋ nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ tɔɔsee bodobodose na kpebia buruburu ŋan bɔɔ dii kaase na, be nyiŋa doŋase kufu ala .

eng: About five thousand had eaten.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ di weenɛɛ ŋan nɛ, be man baala baageŋ yɛɛ balaŋ kakpoŋse banoŋ .

eng: Right then, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake, toward Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.
local: Debokenaŋ man te Yesu yela ɔ kaseela baŋ gyoowɔ degbele man kyaŋ gbɛɛ bee gyu Bɛtesaida donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ nɛ, na ŋon ke lɔ balaŋ baŋ gbɛɛ.

eng: After saying good-bye to them, Jesus went up onto a mountain to pray.
local: Ɔ ke lɔ balaŋ baŋ gbɛɛ taŋ nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ bula dɔɔ ɔ kaa fane.

eng: Evening came and the boat was in the middle of the lake, but he was alone on the land.
local: Kelii balinɔɔ nɛ, na degbele keŋ te lii boo keŋ nsana nsana, te kaa Yesu waageŋ boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ.

eng: He saw his disciples struggling. They were trying to row forward, but the wind was blowing against them. Very early in the morning, he came to them, walking on the lake. He intended to pass by them.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu kɛɛwɔ na feliŋ ne gyɔɔ gyaŋee na ɔ kaseela baŋ, mena dɔɔ bɛɛ kaa na degbele keŋ kesaa. Keŋte ɔ karatɛɛwɔ ta loŋ keŋ dɔɔ gyu be gyaŋ, te ɔ yɛɛwɔ ŋgba ɔ ne gbaa wɔ nɛ.

eng: When they saw him walking on the lake, they thought he was a ghost and they screamed.
local: Ɔ kaseela baŋ kena e ɔ dɔŋ loŋ keŋ dɔɔ ɔ ne kɔŋ nɛ, na ba gyeŋ nyi ɔ yɛɛ woo e te be fae yeesa.

eng: Seeing him was terrifying to all of them. Just then he spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: Nawolo nyi be popou naa e te gyakoloŋ kyaŋa wɔ. Debokenaŋ man te Yesu kolosiwɔ na wɔ nyi, Ɛ nyiŋ konɔɔ! Ɛ na yee ya! Maŋ e.

eng: He got into the boat, and the wind settled down. His disciples were so baffled they were beside themselves.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ gyoowɔ kyaa degbele keŋ man kpu na wɔ, te feliŋ ŋon nɔɔ yɔkɔsewɔ. Nombia kɛŋa doo ɔ kaseela baŋ nɔɔ nideli.

eng: Thats because they hadnt understood about the loaves. Their minds had been closed so that they resisted Gods ways.
local: Yesu ka mo bodobodose anoŋ kpala balaŋ kakpoŋse banoŋ baŋ nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ te nyii ka asɛɛ ya, nawolo nyi na be sɛɛ be ta gyeŋa walaŋ ŋon dinɔɔ Yesu yɛɛ ya.

eng: When Jesus and his disciples had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret, anchored the boat.
local: Keŋte ba toŋa boo keŋ gyu Genesareti tɛɛle man, te be kpaŋa ba degbele keŋ botɔɔ.

eng: and came ashore. People immediately recognized Jesus.
local: Be ka betaa lee degbele keŋ man pɛ, te balaŋ naa Yesu te ba gyeŋawɔ nyi ŋon ŋonaŋ.

eng: and ran around that whole region bringing sick people on their mats to wherever they heard he was.
local: Mena dɔɔ balaŋ yekiiwɔ lee tɛɛle kenaŋ yenaŋ kamasɛ man kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ. Yenaŋ kamasɛ keŋ be nyii nyi Yesu kyaa na, bɛɛ kɔŋ na ba seele be kaweesetena ba afoŋoŋse dɔɔ bɛɛ kɔŋ botɔɔ.

eng: Wherever he went—villages, cities, or farming communities—they would place the sick in the marketplaces and beg him to allow them to touch even the hem of his clothing. Everyone who touched him was healed.
local: Te akuraase yaa donɔɔse kamasɛ ŋan man Yesu de gyu nɛ, balaŋ na mo be kaweesetena kaa see abɔntere man, na ba te waase e nyi waa yela kaweesetena baŋ ka mo be nyiŋmaase tina ɔ kegba nɔɔbii paŋpaŋ bo gba. Te baŋ pou bɔɔ tina ɔ kegba keŋ nɛ, wose yɛɛ wɔ doŋ.

eng: Jesus continued, I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom arrive in power.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi ɛ man baale kyaa kɛbo kei keŋ bɔɔ gyae baa yeŋ ya, kelii debaŋ keŋ baa naa Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ nyi ka seŋɛɛ na ke doŋ.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain where they were alone. He was transformed in front of them.
local: Weeya looro wɔle nɛ, Yesu moo Pita na Gyemisi na Gyɔn kpu na ɔ wose te be gyuuwɔ bula dinaa daale dɔɔ baŋ baageŋ. Botɔɔ te Yesu wose kyɛɛkeewɔ be siaman.

eng: and his clothes were amazingly bright, brighter than if they had been bleached white.
local: Wa abɔɔ ŋan ɔ doona nɛ bese abɔɔ wolomɛɛ pareparepare, na ŋɛɛ ŋalakee polɛpolɛpolɛ. Ŋa wolosewɔ keŋ nyi, ŋolo be kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ keŋ waa tale faafo kolo na ke ka lee mena ya.

eng: Elijah and Moses appeared and were talking with Jesus.
local: Keŋte Mosesi na Elia kaa bɛɛwɔ lee be dɔɔ, te be kolosiwɔ na Yesu.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Rabbi, its good that were here. Lets make three shrines—one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita yako Yesu nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, dei ŋgba daa mɔ be kɔŋ kɛbo nɛ. Yela dɛɛ baŋ kefɛɛse atooro kɛbo. N wui daale, Mosesi wui daale te Elia mɔ wui daale.

eng: He said this because he didnt know how to respond, for the three of them were terrified.
local: Na Pita ta gyeŋ kpene waa yako ya, nawolo nyi gyakoloŋ te kyaŋ wɔ gbaa.

eng: Then a cloud overshadowed them, and a voice spoke from the cloud, This is my Son, whom I dearly love. Listen to him.
local: Botɔɔ te ŋɔma kaa wuu be dɔɔ, te be nyii nɔɔwoya ŋaale ŋɔma keŋ man nyiaa, Kei yɛna me bu ŋon mɛɛ gyae e. Ɛ nyii e.

eng: Suddenly, looking around, they no longer saw anyone with them except Jesus.
local: Debokenaŋ man ɔ kaseela batooro baŋ besewɔ kɛɛ na, ba te naa walaŋ kamasɛ kpu na Yesu ya.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, he ordered them not to tell anyone what they had seen until after the Human One had risen from the dead.
local: Be ke tisi bula keŋ bɛɛ kɔŋ nɛ Yesu faree wɔ nyi, Mɔɔ ne gyae nyi ɛ ke yako ŋolo na ŋolo kpene keŋ ɛ be naa nɛ ya, kelii debaŋ keŋ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa foŋ lee yeŋ man.

eng: So they kept it to themselves, wondering, Whats this rising from the dead.
local: Mena dɔɔ nombia ŋan dɔɔwɔ ba nyeeya man na bɛɛ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, kefoŋ lee yeŋ man kei na wolo baa nyi sena?.

eng: They asked Jesus, Why do the legal experts say that Elijah must come first.
local: Keŋte be bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ ne yako nyi, kaboena nyi Elia waa kyaŋ gbɛɛ kɔŋ pɛna Kristo ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ ke kɔŋ.

eng: He answered, Elijah does come first to restore all things. Why was it written that the Human One would suffer many things and be rejected?.
local: Te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ke yɛɛ ampaŋ nyi Elia waa taŋgbɛɛ nyeelɛɛre ŋon kɔŋ, na ɔ kaa desina kpene kamasɛ see nideli. Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan besewɔ yako yenaŋ daale lee Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon wose man nyiaa, kaboena nyi waa naa diyem nideli na baa bɛɛ e nyi na ŋon yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon ya. Weera dɔɔ te be ŋmarase mena nombia kɛŋa?.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has come, but they did to him whatever they wanted, just as it was written about him.
local: Mɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyiaa, Elia taŋ kekɔŋ te balaŋ yɛɛ e kpene kamasɛ keŋ be gyae ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see be yako lee ɔ wose man nɛ.

eng: When Jesus, Peter, James, and John approached the other disciples, they saw a large crowd surrounding them and legal experts arguing with them.
local: Ba ke kɔŋ ɔ kaseela akaŋ baŋ gyaŋ nɛ, be naa balaŋ burum te yilaa kilisi wɔ. Na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ, na ɔ kaseela baŋ ne wɔŋee nombia ŋaale.

eng: Suddenly the whole crowd caught sight of Jesus. They ran to greet him, overcome with excitement.
local: Balaŋ baŋ pou ke gyɔ sibii naa Yesu nɛ, doo be nɔɔ te be yeuwɔ ke gyaŋee na e te ba doo e nɔɔ.

eng: Jesus asked them, What are you arguing about.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Woŋ nombia yaa ɛmɛɛ na wɔ ne wɔŋee.

eng: Someone from the crowd responded, Teacher, I brought my son to you, since he has a spirit that doesnt allow him to speak.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ man ŋolo tiranɔɔ nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, ma moo me bu tobaale ŋon feliŋkum doo ɔ man, te keŋ dɔɔ ɔ bɛɛ tale kolosi ya nɛ baa kɔna neŋ nyi nya kyɔ.

eng: Wherever it overpowers him, it throws him into a fit. He foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and stiffens up. So I spoke to your disciples to see if they could throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: Debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ ka de kɔŋ ɔ dɔɔ nɛ, ne fuŋ e lɔ tɛɛle man. Afofose na lee ɔ nɔɔman, na ɔ ne tao ɔ kela na ɔ te kem. Me yako n kaseela baŋ nyi baa gegi feliŋkum keŋ lee ɔ man, mɔna ba te tale ya.

eng: Jesus answered them, You faithless generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring him to me.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ao! Ɛmɛɛ gyɛŋ balaŋ baŋ ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di ya. Weeya aŋmoŋ yaa ɛ ne gyae nyi maa nyiŋ konɔɔ kyaa ɛ gyaŋ, pɛna ɛ ke lɛɛ maŋ di? Ɛ mo bu ŋon kɔna maŋ.

eng: They brought him. When the spirit saw Jesus, it immediately threw the boy into a fit. He fell on the ground and rolled around, foaming at the mouth.
local: Mena dɔɔ ba moo tobaale ŋon kɔna Yesu. Feliŋkum keŋ kena Yesu nɛ, debokenaŋ man te ke yela bu ŋon keŋawɔ ŋgba keleuleuse kyaŋa na e nɛ. Bu ŋon leewɔ yala tɛɛle man, na ɔ ne biliŋi gyu kɔŋ, na afofose na lee ɔ nɔɔman.

eng: Jesus asked his father, How long has this been going on? He said, Since he was a child.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse bu ŋon ɔ kya nyiaa, Weeya aŋmoŋ yaa ŋgba kawee kei ba gyoo e nɛ? Keŋte balee ŋon tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Lee naŋ ɔ biatɛɛ.

eng: It has often thrown him into a fire or into water trying to kill him. If you can do anything, help us! Show us compassion.
local: Kawee keŋ ne tale fuŋ e do boalaŋ yaa loŋ man yeŋ yeŋ wui. Nyi akpaa n ke tale yɛɛ kolo na, wii de waraŋase na n ke kyɔ daa.

eng: Jesus said to him, If you can do anything? All things are possible for the one who has faith.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse e nyi, N ne bɔɔse maŋ nyi maa tale nɛ, nafɔ nyaŋ gbagba nyaŋ ke tale, nyi akpaa n lɛɛ maŋ di na. Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di nɛ, kpene kamasɛ ne dee fa e.

eng: At that the boys father cried out, I have faith; help my lack of faith.
local: Debokenaŋ man te bu ŋon ɔ kya fae yeesa yako nyiaa, Mɛɛ lɛɛ di. Kyɔ maŋ na maa lɛɛ neŋ di kpu nideli.

eng: Noticing that the crowd had surged together, Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, Mute and deaf spirit, I command you to come out of him and never enter him again.
local: Yesu kena nyi balaŋ te yeu bɛɛ kɔŋ botɔɔ nɛ, ɔ faree feliŋkum keŋ nyiaa, Nyaŋ deŋela atɔɔsa na omumuu feliŋkum, mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi ɛ lee ɔ man. Lee gyɛŋ ne gyu, ɛ na tekaa kaa gyoo ɔ man bela ya.

eng: After screaming and shaking the boy horribly, the spirit came out. The boy seemed to be dead; in fact, several people said that he had died.
local: Botɔɔ te feliŋkum keŋ fae yeesa te ke wosi bu ŋon nideli ŋgba keleuleu weere nɛ, te ka leewɔ ɔ man laŋ. Na bu ŋon doo tɛɛle man ŋgba ɔ yeŋae nɛ, mena dɔɔ balaŋ burum kpa ɔ ta yem.

eng: But Jesus took his hand, lifted him up, and he arose.
local: Keŋte Yesu kyaŋa bu nyiŋmaa man gate e seŋ ɔ nawɔɔ dɔɔ, te ɔ seŋawɔ.

eng: After Jesus went into a house, his disciples asked him privately, Why couldnt we throw this spirit out.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle Yesu ka gyoo deni man nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse e deni man nyiaa, Woŋ dɔɔ te daa te tale gegi feliŋkum keŋ lee bu ŋon man ya.

eng: Jesus answered, Throwing this kind of spirit out requires prayer.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Nyi akpaa ɛ te fane fa Wurubuarɛ nideli ya nɛ, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale gegi mena feliŋkum kei dinɔɔ ya.

eng: From there Jesus and his followers went through Galilee, but he didnt want anyone to know it.
local: Be ka lee botɔɔ nɛ, be tɛɛwɔ na Galelia tɛɛle man. Na Yesu te gyae nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ka gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ be kyaa ya.

eng: This was because he was teaching his disciples, The Human One will be delivered into human hands. They will kill him. Three days after he is killed he will rise up.
local: Nawolo nyi na ɔ na wola ɔ kaseela baŋ nombia. Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Baa lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon fa balaŋ na baa ko, mɔ weeya atoorote keŋ nɛ maa foŋ lee yeŋ man.

eng: But they didnt understand this kind of talk, and they were afraid to ask him.
local: Mɔna ɔ kaseela baŋ te nyii keŋ ɔ be yako nɛ man ya, te be yee mena keŋ baa bɔɔse e nombia ŋenaŋ man.

eng: They entered Capernaum. When they had come into a house, he asked them, What were you arguing about during the journey.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle te be kɔŋawɔ Kapanum donɔɔ man. Be ke gyu dɛɛ nɛ te ɔ bɔɔse ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Woŋ aŋmaareŋ yaa ɛ ne lɛɛ gbɛɛnaa man nɛ.

eng: They didnt respond, since on the way they had been debating with each other about who was the greatest.
local: Mɔna be man ŋolo te tale kolosi ya. Nawolo nyi gbɛɛnaa man nɛ na bɛɛ lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ nyi be man woŋti kela na ɔ dɔɔ.

eng: He sat down, called the Twelve, and said to them, Whoever wants to be first must be least of all and the servant of all.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tisiwɔ kyaa te ɔ baake ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ yako wɔ nyiaa, Akpaa ŋolo ne gyae nyi waa yɛɛ siamante nɛ, kaboena nyi waa yɔkɔse ɔ wose ŋgba biikyaa nɛ, na waa som ɛ popou.

eng: Jesus reached for a little child, placed him among the Twelve, and embraced him. Then he said.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ moo biikyaabii ŋolo kaa seŋ ba nsana, te ɔ pukaa e te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa.

eng: Whoever welcomes one of these children in my name welcomes me; and whoever welcomes me isnt actually welcoming me but rather the one who sent me.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ biikyaabii kei dinɔɔ ma yele man na ɔ te lɛɛ maŋ, te ŋon ɔ lɛɛ maŋ na ɔ te lɛɛ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ.

eng: John said to Jesus, Teacher, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he wasnt following us.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyɔn yako Yesu nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, de naa balee ŋolo na ɔ ne gegi feliŋkumɛɛ lee n yele man te de tɔɔ e, nawolo nyi ɔ be kpuɛ de man ya.

eng: The Pharisees and some legal experts from Jerusalem gathered around Jesus.
local: Wee daale nɛ, Farasiitena na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baale baŋ bɔɔ lee Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, kaa yilaawɔ Yesu gyaŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Dont stop him. No one who does powerful acts in my name can quickly turn around and curse me.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ na tɔ e gbɛɛ ya. Nawolo nyi ŋolo be kyaa keŋ waa mo ma yele yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia, na waa bese kolosi ma wose man nombiikum debokenaŋ man ya.

eng: They saw some of his disciples eating food with unclean hands. They were eating without first ritually purifying their hands through washing.
local: Botɔɔ te be naawɔ nyi, ɔ kaseela baŋ baale te faafo be nyiŋmaase ŋgba mena keŋ ba beneŋ amanberɛse na wola wɔ nɛ ya pɛte bee dii.

eng: Whoever isnt against us is for us.
local: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ bee yoo na daa ya nɛ, na ɔ kpuɛ na daa.

eng: The Pharisees and all the Jews dont eat without first washing their hands carefully. This is a way of observing the rules handed down by the elders.
local: Nawolo nyi Farasiitena na Gyudatena baŋ pou bee tina ba beneŋ amanberɛse ŋan yela ya. Mena dɔɔ nyi akpaa ba te faafo be nyiŋmaase ŋgba mena keŋ be naanaɔ be yako see ya na bɔɔ ne dii ya.

eng: I assure you that whoever gives you a cup of water to drink because you belong to Christ will certainly be rewarded.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ fa ŋon loŋ leenɔɔ tɔɔ nyi keŋ ɛ yɛɛ maŋ Kristo balaŋwɔ dɔɔ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa tɔ ɔtenate kom.

eng: Upon returning from the marketplace, they dont eat without first immersing themselves. They observe many other rules that have been handed down, such as the washing of cups, jugs, pans, and sleeping mats.
local: Mena ke mɔ te akpaa ba lee kebu man kɔŋ na, bɛɛ saase ba wose pɛte baa di weenɛɛ. Ba beneŋ amanberɛse boe nideli. Be dana gbɛɛneŋ ŋan bɛɛ mo saase ba leenɔɔse na tasase na osuŋse mɔ.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and to be thrown into the lake.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ yeli bia buruburu kɛwɔ baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ maŋ di nɛ man ŋolo de yɛɛ dukum nɛ, kenaŋ na nyi akpaa ba mo boe dinaa dinaa kpaŋ ɔ wɔŋe man, te be fuŋ e do ɛpo man na dei kela.

eng: So the Pharisees and legal experts asked Jesus, Why are your disciples not living according to the rules handed down by the elders but instead eat food with ritually unclean hands.
local: Mena dɔɔ Farasiitena na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te n kaseela baŋ bee di da beneŋ amanberɛse dɔɔ ya? Nawolo nyi bɔɔ ne faafo be nyiŋmaase ŋgba mena keŋ da beneŋ amanberɛ nawolo nɛ pɛ baa di ya.

eng: If your hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter into life crippled than to go away with two hands into the fire of hell, which cant be put out.
local: Nyi akpaa n nyiŋmaa ne yele la te n ne yɛɛ dukum na, kara ke leki. Nawolo nyi dei nyi nya lee kadeŋ na n gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man, kela keŋ n ka mo nyiŋmaase ala ka gyoo boalaŋ keŋ ke bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man.

eng: He replied, Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you hypocrites. He wrote, This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ ne yɛɛ ɛ wose ŋgba ɛ yɛɛ balaŋ kpaakpaawɔ nɛ, mɔna ɛ na beo ɛ wose bo. Ɛ nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya dɔɔ yako ɔ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ. Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa, balaŋ kɛwɔ dana be nɔɔ bo bɛɛ yako nyi bee bu maŋ, mɔna be konɔɔse man na bɔɔ na mo maŋ yɛɛ kolo ya.

eng: If your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter life lame than to be thrown into hell with two feet.
local: Te akpaa n nae ne yele la te n ne yɛɛ dukum na kara ke leki. Nawolo nyi dei nyi nya mo nae dokoloŋ gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man, kela nyi baa mo neŋ na n nawɔɔ ala ka do boalaŋ keŋ ke bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human words.
local: Bɛɛ som maŋ bo yakaa, nawolo nyi mmaraase ŋan denibalaŋ be yɛɛ nɛ te be dana bɛɛ wola balaŋ nombia.

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out. Its better for you to enter Gods kingdom with one eye than to be thrown into hell with two.
local: Te akpaa n sibii ne yele la te n ne yɛɛ dukum na kulu ke leki. Nawolo nyi dei nyi nya mo sibii dokoloŋ gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man, kela nyi baa mo neŋ na n sia ala ka do boalaŋ keŋ ke bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man.

eng: You ignore Gods commandment while holding on to rules created by humans and handed down to you.
local: Ɛ te tina Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan yela, te ɛ dana ɛ beneŋ amanberɛse ŋan balaŋ na wolo nɛ man.

eng: Thats a place where worms dont die and the fire never goes out .
local: Nawolo nyi feelɛɛ baŋ bɛɛ gyoo balaŋ botɔɔ nɛ bɔɔ na yem ya, te boalaŋ keŋ mɔ bɛɛ yem korakora ya.

eng: Jesus continued, Clearly, you are experts at rejecting Gods commandment in order to establish these rules.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ gyeŋ adao gbɛɛneŋ ŋan ɛ na mo bɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan, na ɛ ke nyiŋ di ɛ beneŋ amanberɛse ŋan dɔɔ.

eng: Everyone will be salted with fire.
local: Wurubuarɛ waa mo boalaŋ desina walaŋ kamasɛ, ŋgba mena keŋ bɛɛ mo yabia do kolo man te ke bɛɛ wɔlɛɛ ya nɛ.

eng: Moses said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Nawolo nyi gyaŋgba keŋ de naana Mosesi yakowɔ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan man nyiaa, Bu n kya na naa, te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ kolosi nombiakumɛɛ tia ɔ kya yaa ɔ naa na, kaboena nyi baa ko e.

eng: Salt is good; but if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Maintain salt among yourselves and keep peace with each other.
local: Yabia dei, mɔna akpaa ka gyoŋ da lee ke man na, sena te ɛ ke yɛɛ na ka gyoŋ keŋ ka bese kaa gyoo ke man bela? Ɛ yela ɛ nombia ayɛɛsa ke yɛɛ gyoŋ ŋgba yabia nɛ, na ɛ ke kyaa na dɔŋa wosefɛɛreŋ man.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you is corban .
local: Mɔna ɛmɛɛ ne yako nyiaa, Akpaa ŋolo de yako ɔ kya yaa ɔ naa nyi, Kpene keŋ nafɔ me dana maa mo kyɔ neŋ nɛ yɛɛ Koriban ke. .

eng: then you are no longer required to care for your father or mother.
local: Dekɔŋ mena na, ɛ bɛɛ tekaa fa ɔtenate gbɛɛ waa yɛɛ kolo fa ɔ kya yaa ɔ naa ya.

eng: In this way you do away with Gods word in favor of the rules handed down to you, which you pass on to others. And you do a lot of other things just like that.
local: Mena yaa ɛ na mo ɛ beneŋ amanberɛse ŋan wɔlɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan, te ɛ yɛɛ ŋa see ɛ bia mɔ. Te ɛ ne yɛɛ nombia burum ŋan ŋe yɛɛ ŋgba kɛŋa dinɔɔ nɛ kpu.

eng: Then Jesus called the crowd again and said, Listen to me, all of you, and understand.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, Yesu besewɔ baake balaŋ baŋ pou kaa yilaa ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ popou ɛ tei maŋ, na ɛ ke nyii nombia kɛŋa man nideli.

eng: Nothing outside of a person can enter and contaminate a person in Gods sight; rather, the things that come out of a person contaminate the person.
local: Kolo be kyaa keŋ ke da lee yenaŋ gyoo walaŋ wose man na, ke gyae ke tale yɛɛ e ayimɛɛ ya. Mɔna kpene keŋ na lee walaŋ man kɔŋ nɛ, keŋ ne yɛɛ na e ayimɛɛ.

eng: After leaving the crowd, he entered a house where his disciples asked him about that riddle.
local: Yesu ka lee balaŋ baŋ man gyu dekpaŋalaŋ man nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse e dudu keŋ asɛɛ.

eng: Jesus left that place and went beyond the Jordan and into the region of Judea. Crowds gathered around him again and, as usual, he taught them.
local: Yesu ka lee botɔɔ nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ Gyudia tɛɛle keŋ ke kyaa Gyɔɔdan boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ nɛ. Balaŋ burum besewɔ kaa wuu ɔ dɔɔ botɔɔ, te ɔ wola wɔ nombia ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ ne kyaa wola wɔ nɛ.

eng: He said to them, Dont you understand either? Dont you know that nothing from the outside that enters a person has the power to contaminate?.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ mɔ bee nyii nombia asɛɛ yee? Ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi kpene kamasɛ keŋ na lee yenaŋ gyoo walaŋ wose man na, ke bɛɛ yɛɛ e ayimɛɛ yee?.

eng: Some Pharisees came and, trying to test him, they asked, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife.
local: Keŋte Farasiitena baale kɔŋawɔ be kaa teese ɔ nɔɔman te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, Da mmaraa ne fa daa gbɛɛ nyi ŋolo waa tale bɛɛ ɔ wɛɛle.

eng: Thats because it doesnt enter into the heart but into the stomach, and it goes out into the sewer. By saying this, Jesus declared that no food could contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: Ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi na ɔ konɔɔ man te ŋee gyu ka gyoo yee? Ŋee gyu bo ka gyoo ɔ dubulu man, na ta lee ɔ wose man. Yesu ke yako kei nɛ, na ɔ nawolo baa nyi weenɛɛ kamasɛ dei na kedi.

eng: Jesus answered, What did Moses command you.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Sena te de naana Mosesi yako ŋon nyi yaa yɛɛ.

eng: Its what comes out of a person that contaminates someone in Gods sight, he said.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Kpene kamasɛ keŋ na lee walaŋ konɔɔ man nɛ, keŋ ne yɛɛ na e ayimɛɛ.

eng: They said, Moses allowed a man to write a divorce certificate and to divorce his wife.
local: Keŋte be yako e nyi, De naana Mosesi faa gbɛɛ nyi baale waa tale ŋmarase yale kebɛɛ tɔne keŋ nawolo nyi ɔ te bɛɛ ɔ wɛɛle nɛ, pɛna waa bɛɛ e.

eng: Its from the inside, from the human heart, that evil thoughts come: sexual sins, thefts, murders.
local: Nawolo nyi walaŋ konɔɔ man te nombiakumɛɛ pou na lee. Mena abɔɔ kɛŋa ŋaale yɛna, kafoŋ na ŋmɛɛleŋ na balaŋ kako na yale kewɔlɛɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, He wrote this commandment for you because of your unyielding hearts.
local: Te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ konɔɔyuŋ dɔɔ te Mosesi yɛɛ mena mmaraa kenaŋ fa ŋon.

eng: adultery, greed, evil actions, deceit, unrestrained immorality, envy, insults, arrogance, and foolishness.
local: na sibii na nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ, na kabeo na kewɔlɛɛ nombia na balaŋ kekɔla, na nombiakumɛɛ ka mo gyakaa balaŋ dɔɔ, na wose kegate adido na diyiŋ nombia.

eng: At the beginning of creation, God made them male and female .
local: Nawolo nyi lee kewalaŋ keŋ nɛ, Alo na baale te Wurubuarɛ yɛɛwɔ.

eng: All these evil things come from the inside and contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: Nombiakumɛɛ kɛŋa pou na lee bo walaŋ konɔɔ man, te ŋan ne yɛɛ na e ayimɛɛ.

eng: Because of this, a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife.
local: Lee kei dɔɔ baale waa tina ɔ kya na ɔ naa yela na ŋon na ɔ wɛɛle kaa bom.

eng: Jesus left that place and went into the region of Tyre. He didnt want anyone to know that he had entered a house, but he couldnt hide.
local: Lee botɔɔ te Yesu korowɔ gyu Tae na Sidɔn donɔɔse ŋan ŋe yɛɛ Iseraetena donɔɔse ŋa nɛ yenaŋ. Te ɔ ka gyoowɔ dekpaŋalaŋ daale man keŋ ɔ te gyae nyi ŋolo ka gyeŋ nyi ɔ kyaa botɔɔ ya, mɔna balaŋ naa e.

eng: and the two will be one flesh. So they are no longer two but one flesh.
local: na ŋon na ɔ wɛɛle ka bese walaŋ dokoloŋ. Mena dɔɔ be ta tekaa yɛɛ kpookpoo ya, mɔna be ta bese walaŋ dokoloŋ.

eng: In fact, a woman whose young daughter was possessed by an unclean spirit heard about him right away. She came and fell at his feet.
local: Te alo ŋolo ŋon feliŋkum doo ɔ bu alebubii man ka betaa nyii nyi Yesu kyaa botɔɔ nɛ, te ɔ kaa buŋawɔ ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ.

eng: Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo bom yɛɛ ke dokoloŋ nɛ, ŋolo na ŋolo na kpase ke man ya.

eng: The woman was Greek, Syrophoenician by birth. She begged Jesus to throw the demon out of her daughter.
local: Na alo ŋonaŋ yɛɛ Giriki alo e, te ba lola e bo Fonisia keŋ ke kyaa Siria tɛɛle man nɛ. Keŋte ɔ sola Yesu nyi waa gegi feliŋkum keŋ lee ɔ bu ŋon man.

eng: Inside the house, the disciples asked him again about this.
local: Be ke gyu kagyoo deni man bela nɛ, Yesu kaseela baŋ bɔɔse e nombia kɛŋa man.

eng: He responded, The children have to be fed first. It isnt right to take the childrens bread and toss it to the dogs.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, Nyi n dana bia na, n ke sɛɛ mo be weenɛɛ fa babaase? Koa, n ke taŋgbɛɛ mo weenɛɛ ŋan fa bia kedi pɛ. Te akpaa be dii kaase na, pɛna na n ka mo fa babaase.

eng: He said to them, Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her.
local: Keŋte ɔ tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Nyi akpaa baale de bɛɛ ɔ wɛɛle te ɔ ke yala alo wɔle nɛ, na yɛɛ yaa ŋgba ɔ lee ɔ wɛɛle ŋon wɔle baa nɛ.

eng: But she answered, Lord, even the dogs under the table eat the childrens crumbs.
local: Keŋte alo ŋon mɔ yako e nyiaa, Ayi, me Gbeŋgyoo, doo mena. Mɔ babaase baa tale fiu weenɛɛ ŋan bia ne dii te ŋɛɛ yale tɛɛle man nɛ di.

eng: and if a wife divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.
local: Te akpaa alo mɔ de bɛɛ ɔ baale te ɔ ke yala baale wɔle nɛ, na yɛɛ yaa ŋgba ŋon mɔ lee ɔ baale wɔle baa nɛ.

eng: Good answer! he said. Go on home. The demon has already left your daughter.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako alo ŋon nyiaa, Nɔɔ keŋ n be tiri fa maŋ nɛ dɔɔ ta, feliŋkum keŋ ta lee n bu alebubii ŋon man.

eng: People were bringing children to Jesus so that he would bless them. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Botɔɔ te balaŋ moo bia kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ nyi waa mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa be dɔɔ. Mɔna ɔ kaseela baŋ ŋmanyaŋ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ mo bia baŋ kɔŋ nɛ bo.

eng: When she returned to her house, she found the child lying on the bed and the demon gone.
local: Alo ŋon ka lee botɔɔ gyu ɔ dɛɛ nɛ, te ɔ kenaawɔ nyi ɔ bu ŋon doo mpa dɔɔ na feliŋkum keŋ ta lee ɔ man.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he grew angry and said to them, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Yesu kena mena nɛ, te yɛɛ e gyoŋ ya te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ yela bia baŋ kekɔŋ me gyaŋ, ɛ na tɔ wɔ gbɛɛ ya. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɔkɔse ba wose ŋgba bia kɛwɔ dinɔɔ nɛ wui.

eng: After leaving the region of Tyre, Jesus went through Sidon toward the Galilee Sea through the region of the Ten Cities.
local: Yesu ka lee Tae donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ tɛɛwɔ Sidɔn donɔɔ man ka lee Galelia boo nɔɔ nɔɔ kɔŋ donɔɔse kufu ŋaale tɛɛle man.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ te yɔkɔse ɔ wose do Wurubuarɛ tɛɛ ŋgba biikyaa e ya nɛ, ɔ be gyae waa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man korakora ya.

eng: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly speak, and they begged him to place his hand on the man for healing.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baale moo balee ŋolo ŋon ɔ deŋela be tɔɔ te ɔ bɛɛ tale kolosi nideli ya nɛ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ. Te ba sola e nyi waa mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ, na waa kyɔ e ɔ kawee.

eng: Then he hugged the children and blessed them.
local: Keŋte Yesu kyaŋa bia baŋ nyiŋmaa man te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa be dɔɔ, te ɔ hiraa wɔ.

eng: Jesus took him away from the crowd by himself and put his fingers in the mans ears. Then he spit and touched the mans tongue.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu moo kaweete ŋon lee balaŋ baŋ man gyu yenaŋ. Te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaasebia do kaweete ŋon deŋela man, te ɔ tɔɔ nɔɔloŋ mo tina ɔ laakem.

eng: As Jesus continued down the road, a man ran up, knelt before him, and asked, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Yesu ka mo gbɛɛ ɔ ne gyu nɛ, tobaale ŋolo yeuwɔ kaa buŋ ɔ nawɔɔtɛɛ te ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo kpaakpaa, woŋ te kaboena nyi maa yɛɛ na maa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa.

eng: Looking into heaven, Jesus sighed deeply and said, Ephphatha, which means, Open up.
local: Te Yesu kɛɛ adido te ɔ lee weesɛɛ te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Efata! Keŋ nawolo nyi toro!.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, Woŋ dɔɔ te n ne baake maŋ walaŋ kpaakpaa? Lee Wurubuarɛ wɔle nɛ, walaŋ kamasɛ be kyaa keŋ ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ kpaakpaate e ya.

eng: At once, his ears opened, his twisted tongue was released, and he began to speak clearly.
local: Debokenaŋ man te ɔ deŋela torowɔ te ɔ laakem booliwɔ, te ɔ talewɔ kolosi nideli.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony . Dont cheat. Honor your father and mother .
local: Ŋgba n gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ne yako nyi, Na ko walaŋ ya, na lee n baale yaa n wɛɛle wɔle ya, na ŋmɛɛle ya, na kara n dɔɔ nɔɔ ya, na ŋere ŋolo do ya, bu n kya na n naa nɛ.

eng: Jesus gave the people strict orders not to tell anyone. But the more he tried to silence them, the more eagerly they shared the news.
local: Keŋte Yesu faree balaŋ baŋ nideli nyi be na yako walaŋ kamasɛ ya. Mɔna keŋ na ɔ ke faree wɔ koraŋ nɛ, keŋ gba te be yela balaŋ nyiiwɔ beiŋ.

eng: Teacher, he responded, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Keŋte balee ŋon mɔ yakowɔ nyiaa Me Gbeŋgyoo, me wulaa mee di mmaraase kɛŋa pou dɔɔ leenaŋ me biatɛɛ.

eng: People were overcome with wonder, saying, He does everything well! He even makes the deaf to hear and gives speech to those who cant speak.
local: Balaŋ baŋ wose yeŋawɔ nideli te be yakowɔ nyiaa, Yesu te yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ nideli. Ɔ te yela deŋela atɔɔsatena ne nyii dei, te omumuuse mɔ ne kolosi.

eng: Jesus looked at him carefully and loved him. He said, You are lacking one thing. Go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu kɛɛ ɔ siaman yididi, na ɔ ne gyae ɔ nombia, mena dɔɔ ɔ yako e nyiaa, Ta bese ka neŋ kolo dokoloŋ n ke yɛɛ. Gyu ke yɔɔ nya abɔɔ ŋan pou n dana nɛ, na n ka mo kɔba keŋ kpɛlɛɛ ayematena, na n ke kɔŋ kaa sila maŋ. N yɛɛ mena na, n ke nyiŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa see n wose Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ adido.

eng: But the man was dismayed at this statement and went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Tobaale ŋon ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ɔ moo waraŋase lee botɔɔ nawolo nyi na ɔ dana ɔ wose nideli.

eng: Looking around, Jesus said to his disciples, It will be very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Keŋte Yesu kɛɛ balaŋ baŋ gyaŋee te ɔ yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Dana doŋ fa balaŋ baŋ be dana ba wose nideli nɛ, nyi baa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.

eng: His words startled the disciples, so Jesus told them again, Children, its difficult to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Ɔ kaseela baŋ ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, doo be nɔɔ. Te Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Me bia, ŋgba ɛ naa mena keŋ gyae ke yɛɛ doŋ fa balaŋ baŋ be dana ba wose nideli nɛ, nyi baa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Gyae ke yɛɛ doŋ fa balaŋ baŋ be dana ba wose nideli nɛ nyi baa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man, kela nyi kurumɔɔ ka gyoo ŋarale bɔɔ man lee.

eng: They were shocked even more and said to each other, Then who can be saved.
local: Ɔ kaseela baŋ ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, besewɔ doo be nɔɔ nideli te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Nyi nnɛ te doo na, amɔte ne gyae la waa nyiŋ nyeekelɛɛ baa.

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible with human beings, but not with God. All things are possible for God.
local: Te Yesu kɛɛ wɔ yididi yako wɔ nyiaa, Balaŋ gyaŋ na dana doŋ, mɔna Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ na be dana doŋ ya. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ na, kpene kamasɛ gyae ke tale kɔŋ mena.

eng: Peter said to him, Look, weve left everything and followed you.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita yako Yesu nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, daa mɔ te tina kpene kamasɛ keŋ de dana nɛ yela, te de silana neŋ.

eng: Jesus said, I assure you that anyone who has left house, brothers, sisters, mother, father, children, or farms because of me and because of the good news.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, ɛ man ŋolo be kyaa keŋ ɔ be tina ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ yaa ɔ naabia yaa ɔ desina, yaa ɔ kya yaa ɔ naa yaa ɔ bia yaa ɔ tɛɛlese yela lee maŋ na nyeekelɛɛ nombia ŋan dɔɔ.

eng: will receive one hundred times as much now in this life—houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and farms —and in the coming age, eternal life.
local: te ɔ be gyae waa bese nyiŋ akpaŋalaŋse na naabia na desina na naa na kya na bia na tɛɛlese akpeŋ sɔŋonoŋ kpu gyɛŋ weeya kɛŋa man ya. Mɔ kayeyɛɛ gyae ke kɔŋ ɔ dɔɔ, na balaŋ kekɔla e kpu. Te kenaŋ wɔle na, waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaakekpaa.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Mɔ balaŋ burum baŋ be yɛɛ siamantenawɔ nɛ baa kaa bese wɔle nyibaŋ, na wɔle nyibaŋ kaa bese siamantena.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, with Jesus in the lead. The disciples were amazed while the others following behind were afraid. Taking the Twelve aside again, he told them what was about to happen to him.
local: Lee botɔɔ te Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ moo gbɛɛ bee gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man, na Yesu dɔŋɛɛ la be siaman. Be kee gyu nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ wose yeŋa wɔ, te gyakoloŋ mɔ kyaŋa balaŋ baŋ be silana wɔ nɛ. Keŋte ɔ baake ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ lee yenaŋ te ɔ yako wɔ kpene keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ ɔ dɔɔ nɛ nyiaa.

eng: Look! he said. Were going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and the legal experts. They will condemn him to death and hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: Ɛ kɛɛ! Dee gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man baa. Baa lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon fa Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ. Baa di me nombia na baa bu maŋ fɔɔ nyi baa ko maŋ, na baa mo maŋ do balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ nyiŋmaa man.

eng: They will ridicule him, spit on him, torture him, and kill him. After three days, he will rise up.
local: Baa kulu maŋ na baa tɔ nɔɔloŋ woli maŋ, na baa ŋmeree maŋ dambiise na baa ko maŋ. Mɔna weeya atooro wɔle na, maa foŋ lee yeŋ man.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, came to Jesus and said, Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, Sebedeo bia Gyemisi na Gyɔn kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, dɛɛ gyae nyi kpene kamasɛ keŋ de bɔɔse neŋ na, n ke yɛɛ ke fa daa.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyi, Woŋ te ɛ ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ fa ŋon.

eng: They said, Allow one of us to sit on your right and the other on your left when you enter your glory.
local: Keŋte be yako e nyiaa, Dɛɛ gyae nyi de man ŋolo ke kyaa n dunoluŋ dɔɔ, na ŋolo mɔ ke kyaa n demena dɔɔ n gyoori keŋ man.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink the cup I drink or receive the baptism I receive.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ ba gyeŋ kpene keŋ ɛ ne bɔɔse nɛ ya. Ɛ ke tale nyiŋ konɔɔ naa diyem keŋ maa naa nɛ, yaa ɛ ke tale gyoo kayeyɛɛ keŋ man maa gyoo nɛ.

eng: We can, they answered. Jesus said, You will drink the cup I drink and receive the baptism I receive.
local: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa e nyi, Ayi, dɛɛ tale. Te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ ke tale naa diyem keŋ maa naa, na ɛ ka gyoo kayeyɛɛ keŋ maa gyoo nɛ man.

eng: but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom it has been prepared.
local: Mɔna mɔɔ taŋ doŋ keŋ maa lese walaŋ ŋon waa kyaa ma dunoluŋ yaa ma demena dɔɔ ya. Mena akyaese kɛŋa doe fa balaŋ baŋ me kya Wurubuarɛ gbagba be desina see wɔ nɛ.

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with James and John.
local: Yesu kaseela kufu akaŋ baŋ kenyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ba gyeŋa baŋ Gyemisi na Gyɔn dɔɔ.

eng: Jesus called them over and said, You know that the ones who are considered the rulers by the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu baake ɔ kaseela baŋ pou yilaa te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ gyeŋ nyi tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyooneŋ ne di gyoori bo be balaŋ dɔɔ, te be kegyiise baŋ mɔ dana doŋ be dɔɔ.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: Mɔ ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ, na mena te kaboena nyi dɛɛ doo ɛ man ya. Ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae nyi waa bese ɛ kegyia nɛ, kaboena nyi waa bese ɛ kegbetibii.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be the slave of all.
local: Te ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae nyi waa bese ɛ siamante nɛ, kaboena nyi waa bese ɛ popou ɛ kegbetibii.

eng: for the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: Nawolo nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon te kɔŋ nyi balaŋ baa som maŋ ya, mɔna me kɔŋae me kaa som balaŋ, na maa yeŋ mo ma nyeedoŋ lɛɛ balaŋ burum nyee.

eng: Jesus and his followers came into Jericho. As Jesus was leaving Jericho, together with his disciples and a sizable crowd, a blind beggar named Bartimaeus, Timaeus son, was sitting beside the road.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyuuwɔ Gyeriko donɔɔ man. Ŋon na ɔ kaseela na balaŋ burum baŋ ka lee botɔɔ bee gyu nɛ, be naa Timeo bu Batimeo ŋon ɔ sia be yɛlɛɛ nɛ na ɔ kyaa gbɛɛnaa nɔɔman ɔ na solee abɔɔ.

eng: When he heard that Jesus of Nazareth was there, he began to shout, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Balee ŋon kenyii nyi Yesu Nasarɛte baale ŋon ne kɔŋ nɛ, ɔ fae yeesa yako nyiaa, Yesu, gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii, wii me waraŋase.

eng: Many scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ man burum too be nɔɔ gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ nyi waa wu ɔ nɔɔ. Mɔna nɛnɛɛ gba te ɔ ne fae yeesa keŋkeŋ nyiaa, Gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii, wii me waraŋase.

eng: Jesus stopped and said, Call him forward. They called the blind man, Be encouraged! Get up! Hes calling you.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu seŋawɔ yako wɔ nyi, Ɛ baake e fa maŋ. Te be baake siayɛlɛɛsate ŋon yako e nyiaa, Yela ke yɛɛ neŋ gyoŋ na n ka koro seŋ. Ɔ ne baake neŋ.

eng: Throwing his coat to the side, he jumped up and came to Jesus.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ fuŋa ɔ kegba wurikyim lɔ yenaŋ, te ɔ toŋawɔ seŋ ta kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ.

eng: Jesus asked him, What do you want me to do for you? The blind man said, Teacher, I want to see.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, Woŋ te n ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ fa neŋ? Botɔɔ te siayɛlɛɛsate ŋon yako e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, mɛɛ gyae me sia ke gyuusu.

eng: Jesus said, Go, your faith has healed you. At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus on the way.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, Ta, n ke lɛɛ maŋ di dɔɔ n kawee te taŋ. Debokenaŋ man te ɔ sia gyuusuwɔ, te ɔ sila Yesu laŋ.

eng: As Jesus left the temple, one of his disciples said to him, Teacher, look! What awesome stones and buildings.
local: Yesu kɛɛ lee Gyudatena ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ man ŋolo yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, kɛɛ boya ŋan bɔɔ mo ma deni kei, na deni keŋ mɔ kyeo.

eng: Jesus responded, Do you see these enormous buildings? Not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu mɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ŋgba ɛ ne naa denɛɛ dinaana kɛŋa? Baa tekerii ŋe popou lɔ keŋ nyi ɛ be gyae ɛ kena boe na boe gyakaa ke dɔɔ dɔɔ ya.

eng: Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives across from the temple. Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately.
local: Keŋte Yesu gyuuwɔ ke kyaa oliifi bula keŋ dɔɔ, te ɔ moo ɔ sia tee Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ. Ɔ kekyaa botɔɔ te Pita na Gyemisi na Gyɔn na Anderiase baageŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa.

eng: Tell us, when will these things happen? What sign will show that all these things are about to come to an end.
local: Wola daa debaŋ keŋ nombia kɛŋa pou gyae ke kɔŋ. Woŋ te dɛɛ mo naa nyi kɛŋa pou gyae ke kɔŋ.

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose man nideli, na ŋolo ba beo ŋon ya.

eng: Many people will come in my name, saying, Im the one! They will deceive many people.
local: Balaŋ burum baa kɔŋ ma yele man na be kaa yako nyi, Baŋ yɛna maŋ Nyeelɛɛre ŋon, na baa beo balaŋ burum na baa yo gbɛɛ.

eng: When you hear of wars and reports of wars, dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Akpaa ɛ nyii yoo na yoose nombia nɛ, ɛ na yeli ɛ wɔe ke lɔ ŋon ya. Kaboena nyi mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou dɛɛkɔŋ mena, mɔna kei bɛɛ wolo nyi debaŋ ɔtomante keŋ te lii ya.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be earthquakes and famines in all sorts of places. These things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Tɛɛlese baa koro seŋ tia dɔŋa, na gyooneŋ mɔ ka koro seŋ tia dɔŋa. Tɛɛle gyae ke wosi yenaase burum, te tanam mɔ gyae ke yala tɛɛle dɔɔ. Mɔna kɛŋa pou yɛɛ yaa ŋgba fulute kadoŋee kewalaŋ nɛ.

eng: Watch out for yourselves. People will hand you over to the councils. You will be beaten in the synagogues. You will stand before governors and kings because of me so that you can testify before them.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose dɔɔ nideli, nawolo nyi baa mo ŋon gyina donɔɔ kegyiise na baa tuku ŋon Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man. Lee maŋ dɔɔ, baa mo ŋon gyu tɛɛle kegyiise na gyooneŋ siaman. Kei gyae ke yela ɛ ke nyiŋ gbɛɛ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan fa wɔ.

eng: First, the good news must be proclaimed to all the nations.
local: Mɔna kaboena nyi me silala baa taŋgbɛɛ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou pɛ.

eng: When they haul you in and hand you over, dont worry ahead of time about what to answer or say. Instead, say whatever is given to you at that moment, for you arent doing the speaking but the Holy Spirit is.
local: Nyi akpaa be kyaŋ ŋon gyu nombia dedii man nɛ, ɛ na wɔlɛɛ na kpene keŋ ɛ keyako ya. Kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa mo do ɛ nɔɔse man debaŋ kenaŋ nɛ, keŋ te kaboena nyi yaa yako. Nawolo nyi na ɛmɛɛ ne gyae la ɛ kekolosi ya, mɔna Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne gyae la waa kolosi fa ŋon.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to death. A father will turn in his children. Children will rise up against their parents and have them executed.
local: Naa lolo bia baa lese dɔŋa fa yeŋ, na kya mɔ ka lese ɔ bu fa yeŋ. Bia baa koro seŋ tia ba lolala, na baa lese wɔ fa yeŋ.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Maŋ dɔɔ balaŋ pou baa kɔla ŋon. Mɔ walaŋ ŋon ɔ kyaŋ ɔ wose seŋ keŋkeŋ kelii ɔto nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ ɔ nyee.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing standing where it shouldnt be , then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Nyi akpaa ɛ naa kpene keŋ bɛɛ kɔle te ke dana duwaare nɛ de kaa seŋ botɔɔ keŋ tekaboena nyi ke dɛɛ seŋ ya nɛ, . Dekɔŋ mena na, balaŋ baŋ be kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ, baa yeu gyu bulase dɔɔ.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down or enter their houses to grab anything.
local: Nyi akpaa ŋolo kyaa ɔ deni nyee dɔɔ na, ɔ na tisi kɔŋ tɛɛle man nyi ɔ kaa mo ɔ kolo ya.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: Te akpaa ŋolo mɔ kyaa wɔɔman na, ɔ na bese gyu dɛɛ nyi ɔ ka mo ɔ kegba ya.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Mena weeya ŋenaŋ man nɛ, fulɛɛtena na talabose baa laako.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ fane fa Wurubuarɛ nyi, mena nombia kɛŋa naa kɔŋ waaretɛɛ ya.

eng: In those days there will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Nawolo nyi weeya ŋenaŋ man nɛ, balaŋ baa naa diyem keŋ leenaŋ debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ tɛɛle kei kaa lii na gyɛŋ nɛ, mena diyem kenaŋ te kɔŋa ta see ya, te ke dinɔɔ mɔ be gyae kekɔŋ mɔ see ya.

eng: If the Lord hadnt shortened that time, no one would be rescued. But for the sake of the chosen ones, the ones whom God chose, he has cut short the time.
local: Na Wurubuarɛ ke kɔkee kayeyɛɛ weeya kɛŋa ya nɛ, nafɔ ŋolo na ŋolo be gyae waa ka ya. Mɔna lee ɔ balaŋ baŋ ɔ ba lese see nɛ dɔɔ, te ɔ kɔkee weeya ŋenaŋ.

eng: Then if someone says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or There he is, dont believe it.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ de lii te ŋolo de yako ŋon nyi Ɛ kɛɛ Kristo ŋon kyaa kɛbo, yaa ɔ kyaa yenaŋ daale na ɛ na lɛɛ ke di ya.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, those whom God has chosen.
local: Nawolo nyi balaŋ baa dii akɛrɛɛ nyi baŋ yɛna maŋ Kristo ŋon, te baale mɔ baa dii akɛrɛɛ nyi be yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔrawɔ. Baa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana, te akpaa be tale nyiŋ gbɛɛ koraŋ na, baa mo beo Wurubuarɛ balaŋ baŋ ɔ ba lese see nɛ.

eng: But you, watch out! Ive told you everything ahead of time.
local: Mɔna ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ ɛ doo ɛ wose dɔɔ, nawolo nyi me taŋ wulaa yako ŋon nombia kɛŋa.

eng: In those days, after the suffering of that time, the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light.
local: Weeya ŋenaŋ man kayeyɛɛ kɛŋa pou wɔle na weese waa gyoo ditiŋtɛɛ, te gyaalaŋ mɔ be gyae waa wolose ya.

eng: The stars will fall from the sky, and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: Kyolobiise baa lee adido kaa yala. Te abɔɔ ŋan pou ŋe kyaa adido nɛ, gyae ke wosi.

eng: Then they will see the Human One coming in the clouds with great power and splendor.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ nɛ, balaŋ baa naa maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon ta mo doŋ na gyoorobiiri lee ŋɔma man adido mɛɛ kɔŋ.

eng: Then he will send the angels and gather together his chosen people from the four corners of the earth, from the end of the earth to the end of heaven.
local: Te maa kpila me kpilala lee adido na be kaa yilaa me balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ba lese wɔ see tɛɛle kei dɔɔ yenaŋ kamasɛ nɛ.

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kɛɛ fiigi daŋ na ɛ ke kasee kolo lee ka wose man. Akpaa ɛ naa ŋgba ke tombia da potee faareŋse wɔlɛɛ nɛ, na ɛ gyeŋ nyi awuluŋtɛɛ ta benaa baa.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that hes near, at the door.
local: Mena mɔ te akpaa ɛ naa mena nombia kɛŋa ne kɔŋ na, ɛ gyeŋ nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon kekɔŋ ta benaa taŋ.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi balaŋ baŋ be kyaa gyɛŋ kei nɛ, be duuluŋ be gyae ke munu taŋ ya, pɛte nombia kɛŋa kekɔŋ.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Adido na tɛɛle kei pou gyae ke gyu ɔto, mɔna me nɔɔwoya dɔɔ ŋan gyae ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the angels in heaven and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ŋolo ba gyeŋ wee na debaŋ keŋ kɛŋa pou gyae ke kɔŋ ya. Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ be kyaa adido, na maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon koraŋ ba gyeŋ ya. Gyisɛ me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ waageŋ gyeŋa la.

eng: Watch out! Stay alert! You dont know when the time is coming.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ kɛɛ nideli na ɛ ke desina ɛ wose see, nawolo nyi ɛ ba gyeŋ wee keŋ debaŋ kenaŋ gyae ke kɔŋ ya.

eng: It is as if someone took a trip, left the household behind, and put the servants in charge, giving each one a job to do, and told the doorkeeper to stay alert.
local: Ke yɛɛ ŋgba walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba koro ɔ ne gyu gbɛɛ, te ɔ ba mo ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ do ɔ tewulɛɛ nyiŋmaa man nɛ. Ɔ faa be man walaŋ kamasɛ tom keŋ waa yɛɛ, te ɔ yako ŋon ɔ na deke denanɔɔ keŋ nɛ nyi waa deke ke nideli.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know when the head of the household will come, whether in the evening or at midnight, or when the rooster crows in the early morning or at daybreak.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ doo ɛ wose dɔɔ, nawolo nyi ɛ ba gyeŋ wee keŋ dekpaŋalaŋte ŋon waa kɔŋ ya. Waa tale kɔŋ balinɔɔ yaa tɛɛnsana, yaa kegyim kɛɛ lɔ woya, yaa nebo nebo.

eng: Dont let him show up when you werent expecting and find you sleeping.
local: Ɛ doo ɛ wose dɔɔ na ɔ be kɔŋ kaa futaa ŋon nyi ɛ doo dosoroŋ man ya.

eng: What I say to you, I say to all: Stay alert.
local: Nombii kei mɛɛ yako ŋon nɛ, mɛɛ yako baa fa balaŋ pou. Ɛ doo ɛ wose dɔɔ.

eng: In those days there was another large crowd with nothing to eat. Jesus called his disciples and told them.
local: Debaŋ daale bela nɛ, balaŋ burum baale besewɔ kɔŋ kaa yilaa Yesu gyaŋ. Na bɔɔ dana kolo baa di ya, te Yesu baake ɔ kaseela baŋ kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ yako wɔ nyiaa.

eng: I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat.
local: Balaŋ kɛwɔ nombia dana maŋ waraŋase. Nawolo nyi gyɛŋ weeya kpaakpaa atooro yaa ŋgba be kyaa me gyaŋ, te kolo be doo baa di ya nɛ.

eng: If I send them away hungry to their homes, they wont have enough strength to travel, for some have come a long distance.
local: Nyi akpaa me lɔ wɔ gbɛɛ na tanam na, be sia gyae kaa biri wɔ gbɛɛnaa man. Nawolo nyi be man baale lee lemlem.

eng: His disciples responded, How can anyone get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy these people.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse e nyiaa, Aleŋ te walaŋ waa nyiŋ weenɛɛ depampaa kei dɔɔ na ka yee mena balaŋ burum kɛwɔ.

eng: Jesus asked, How much bread do you have? They said, Seven loaves.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Bodobodose aŋmoŋ te ɛ dana? Te be yako e nyi be dana bodobodose nyetooro.

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground. He took the seven loaves, gave thanks, broke them apart, and gave them to his disciples to distribute; and they gave the bread to the crowd.
local: Te Yesu yela balaŋ baŋ kyakeewɔ tɛɛle man, te ɔ moo bodobodose nyetooro ŋan te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ, te ɔ kɛrɛɛ ŋa mo fa ɔ kaseela baŋ nyi baa mo kpɛlɛɛ balaŋ baŋ. Keŋte baŋ mɔ moo ŋa kpɛlɛɛ balaŋ baŋ.

eng: They also had a few fish. He said a blessing over them, then gave them to the disciples to hand out also.
local: Na ɔ kaseela baŋ dana kpebia kyomii baale mɔ. Te Yesu moo kpebia baŋ mɔ te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ te ɔ moo ŋa fa wɔ nyi baa mo kpɛlɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ.

eng: They ate until they were full. They collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Balaŋ baŋ pou diiwɔ baa nideli. Kenaŋ wɔle te ɔ kaseela baŋ tɔɔsee buruburu akaŋ ŋan na, be nyiŋa doŋatɔɔse nyetooro.
