eng: This was a crowd of about four thousand people! Jesus sent them away.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ di weenɛɛ ŋan nɛ, be man baala yɛɛ balaŋ kakpoŋse banaara . Yesu ke lɔ balaŋ baŋ gbɛɛ nɛ.

eng: then got into a boat with his disciples and went over to the region of Dalmanutha.
local: te ŋon na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyoowɔ degbele man gyu Dalimanuta tɛɛle man.

eng: The Pharisees showed up and began to argue with Jesus. To test him, they asked for a sign from heaven.
local: Keŋte Farasiitena baale kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ bɛɛ bɔɔse e nombia na baa mo teese ɔ nɔɔ man. Be yako e nyi waa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na kawola nyi ɔ lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ampaŋ.

eng: With an impatient sigh, Jesus said, Why does this generation look for a sign? I assure you that no sign will be given to it.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu lee weesɛɛ ɔ man te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛmɛɛ gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia wola ŋon? Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, mɔɔ gyae maa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia wola ŋon ya.

eng: Leaving them, he got back in the boat and crossed to the other side of the lake.
local: Keŋte Yesu leewɔ balaŋ baŋ gyaŋ, te ɔ moo degbele toŋ gyu boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ.

eng: Jesus disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, so they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
local: Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ kee gyu nɛ, na ɔ kaseela baŋ te wolee be ta mo bodobodo ŋaale tela kpu na dokoloŋ keŋ doo ba degbele keŋ man nɛ ya.

eng: He gave them strict orders: Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees as well as the yeast of Herod.
local: Te Yesu faree ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose man nideli lee Farasiitena na Herodetena wose man. Nawolo nyi be dinɔɔ yɛɛ ŋgba tee keŋ bɛɛ mo do bodobodo man te kɛɛ koro nɛ.

eng: The disciples discussed this among themselves, He said this because we have no bread.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ yako dɔŋa nyiaa, Keŋ de ta mo bodobodo tela ya dɔɔ te ɔ ne yako mena nɛ.

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, Why are you talking about the fact that you dont have any bread? Dont you grasp what has happened? Dont you understand? Are your hearts so resistant to what God is doing?.
local: Yesu ka gyeŋ kpene keŋ bɛɛ yako nɛ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne kolosi nyi ɛ ta mo bodobodo tela ya? Mena dɔɔ ɛ ta gyeŋa ta nombia man yee? Ya agyueŋse man ta toro ta yee?.

eng: Dont you have eyes? Why cant you see? Dont you have ears? Why cant you hear? Dont you remember?.
local: Ɛ dana sia nɛ, ɛ bɛɛ naa yee? Te ɛ dana deŋela mɔ nɛ, ɛ bee nyii nombia yee? Ɛ bɛɛ tɔɔse nombia yee?.

eng: When I broke five loaves of bread for those five thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Twelve.
local: Debaŋ keŋ mɔɔ kɛrɛɛ bodobodose anoŋ ŋan kpɛlɛɛ balaŋ kakpoŋse banoŋ baŋ te bɔɔ dii baa nɛ, doŋatɔɔse aŋmoŋ te ɛ tɔɔsee buruburu akaŋ ŋan wulu? Keŋte be tiranɔɔ nyi, Doŋatɔɔse kufu ala.

eng: And when I broke seven loaves of bread for those four thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Seven.
local: Te Yesu besewɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Debaŋ keŋ mɔ kɛrɛɛ bodobodose nyetooro ŋan kpɛlɛɛ balaŋ kakpoŋse banaara baŋ te bɔɔ dii baa nɛ, doŋatɔɔse aŋmoŋ te ɛ tɔɔsee buruburu akaŋ ŋan wulu? Keŋte be tiranɔɔ nyi, Doŋatɔɔse nyetooro.

eng: Jesus said to them, And you still dont understand.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyi, Ɛ sɛɛ ɛ te nyii ta nombia ŋan man yee.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to Bethsaida. Some people brought a blind man to Jesus and begged him to touch and heal him.
local: Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ ke kɔŋ Bɛtesaida donɔɔ man nɛ, te balaŋ baale moo siayɛlɛɛsate ŋolo kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ te ba waase e nyi waa mo ɔ nyiŋmaa tina e na ɔ sia ke gyuusu.

eng: Taking the blind mans hand, Jesus led him out of the village. After spitting on his eyes and laying his hands on the man, he asked him, Do you see anything.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu kyaŋa siayɛlɛɛsate ŋon nyiŋmaa man te ɔ moo e lee donɔɔ keŋ man, te ɔ tɔɔ nɔɔloŋ woli ɔ sia te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ te ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, N ne naa kolo.

eng: The man looked up and said, I see people. They look like trees, only they are walking around.
local: Keŋte balee ŋon gyinaa ɔ sia kɛɛ adido te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Mɛɛ naa balaŋ, mɔna be yɛɛ ŋgba daase ne tɛɛ la nɛ.

eng: Then Jesus placed his hands on the mans eyes again. He looked with his eyes wide open, his sight was restored, and he could see everything clearly.
local: Te Yesu besewɔ mo ɔ nyiŋmaa tina balee ŋon sia bela. Keŋte ɔ sia gyuusuwɔ, te ɔ naa kpene kamasɛ nideli.

eng: Then Jesus sent him home, saying, Dont go into the village.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu lɔɔ balee ŋon gbɛɛ te ɔ faree e nyiaa, Na bese gyu donɔɔ keŋ man bela ya. Ta gyu n dɛɛ.

eng: When Jesus and his followers approached Jerusalem, they came to Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives. Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Keŋte Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ korowɔ mo gbɛɛ bee gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man. Be kelii Bɛtifagi na Bɛtani donɔɔse ŋan ŋe kyaa oliifi bula keŋ teŋa man nɛ, ɔ kpila ɔ kaseela baŋ man bala nyiaa.

eng: Jesus and his disciples went into the villages near Caesarea Philippi. On the way he asked his disciples, Who do people say that I am.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyuu donɔɔse ŋan ŋe kyaa benaa na Kaesaria Filipi donɔɔ keŋ nɛ. Be kee gyu keŋ nɛ te ɔ bɔɔse ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Balaŋ ne kolosi ma wose man nombia na, be kpa maŋ yɛna amɔ.

eng: saying to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: Ɛ gyu donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa ɛ siaman nɛ man. Ɛ betaa ɛ na gyoo ke man pɛ nɛ, ɛ kena afurum bu ŋolo kpaŋɛɛ keŋ ŋolo te kyaa ta ɔ dɔɔ see ya. Ɛ booli e na ɛ ka mo e kɔna maŋ.

eng: They told him, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others one of the prophets.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ tiranɔɔ nyi, Baale kpa nyaŋ yɛna Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon, baale mɔ kpa nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Elia, te baale mɔ kpa n yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ man ŋolo e.

eng: If anyone says to you, Why are you doing this? say, Its master needs it, and he will send it back right away.
local: Akpaa ŋolo de bɔɔse ŋon nyi, Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne booli e na? Ɛ yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo ne gyae na e, mɔna be gyae ke kyare ya waa besena e kɔŋ.

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, You are the Christ.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ mɔ de? Ɛ kpa maŋ yɛna amɔ? Botɔɔ te Pita tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna Kristo, nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: They went and found a colt tied to a gate outside on the street, and they untied it.
local: Keŋte be gyuuwɔ kena afurum bu ŋon kpaŋɛɛ denanɔɔ man benaa na gbɛɛ te be booli e.

eng: Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone about him.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu faree wɔ nyi be na yako ɔ wose man nombia fa ŋolo na ŋolo ya.

eng: Some people standing around said to them, What are you doing, untying the colt.
local: Be kɛɛ booli e nɛ, balaŋ baŋ ba seŋɛɛ botɔɔ nɛ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Woŋ yaa ɛ ne booli e ɛ ke yɛɛ.

eng: Then Jesus began to teach his disciples: The Human One must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts, and be killed, and then, after three days, rise from the dead.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu fiasɛɛ ɔ na wola ɔ kaseela baŋ nombia nyiaa, Kaboena nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa naa diyem nideli, na Gyudatena kegyiise na Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ ke bɛɛ maŋ nyi na maŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon ya. Baa ko maŋ, mɔ weeya atoorote keŋ na maa foŋ lee yeŋ man.

eng: They told them just what Jesus said, and they left them alone.
local: Botɔɔ te be tiranɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ Yesu be yako wɔ nɛ, te balaŋ baŋ yela ba moo e laŋ.

eng: It was two days before Passover and the Festival of Unleavened Bread. The chief priests and legal experts through cunning tricks were searching for a way to arrest Jesus and kill him.
local: Keka weeya ala na Gyudatena nyeekelɛɛwee dinaa keŋ kedi kelii nɛ, Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise baŋ na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala gyae gbɛɛ keŋ baa mo adao gbɛɛneŋ kyaŋ Yesu na baa ko e.

eng: He said this plainly. But Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him.
local: Yesu ka toro nombia kɛŋa yako fa ɔ kaseela baŋ nɛ, te Pita moo e lee yenaŋ te ɔ faree e nyi ɔ na kolosi mena ya.

eng: They brought the colt to Jesus and threw their clothes upon it, and he sat on it.
local: Keŋte ba moo e kɔna Yesu te ba moo be kanyaŋse gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ, te Yesu gyeŋawɔ kyaa ɔ dɔɔ.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the festival; otherwise, there would be an uproar among the people.
local: Keŋte be yakowɔ nyi, Ɛ na fa dɛɛ kyaŋ e nyeekelɛɛwee dinaa kei kedi man ya, na mena ya na balaŋ baŋ baa yela donɔɔ keŋ ke siŋii.

eng: Jesus turned and looked at his disciples, then sternly corrected Peter: Get behind me, Satan. You are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ ɔ kaseela baŋ te ɔ fae Pita nyiaa, Ta lee me gyaŋ, ɔbɔnsam! Na Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii te n na gyueŋ ya, mɔna denibalaŋ kegyaebii te n na gyueŋ.

eng: Many people spread out their clothes on the road while others spread branches cut from the fields.
local: Botɔɔ te balaŋ burum moo be kanyaŋse na ba kegbase ŋaale lɔ gbɛɛnaa man, te baale mɔ moo faareŋse ŋan bɔɔ kɛrɛɛ lee ŋale man nɛ lɔ gbɛɛnaa man.

eng: Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease. During dinner, a woman came in with a vase made of alabaster and containing very expensive perfume of pure nard. She broke open the vase and poured the perfume on his head.
local: Yesu ke kyaa Bɛtani donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ Simɔn ŋon nafɔ ɔ na wee kawee yayam nɛ dɛɛ ɔ kaa di. Te alo ŋolo kɔŋawɔ botɔɔ na ɔ dana nuŋ ofoŋofoŋte keŋ ke dana koyadoŋ nɛ porontia tɔɔ. Botɔɔ te ɔ toro ke te ɔ toosi nuŋ keŋ woli Yesu nyeeman.

eng: After calling the crowd together with his disciples, Jesus said to them, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Keŋte Yesu baake balaŋ dikpii keŋ na ɔ kaseela baŋ kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Akpaa ŋolo ne gyae waa sila maŋ na ɔ na yɛɛ kpene keŋ ŋon gbagba ne gyae ya. Mɔna waa mo ɔ ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ seele, na waa sila maŋ.

eng: Those in front of him and those following were shouting, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord!.
local: Na balaŋ baŋ be dɔŋɛɛ Yesu siaman na baŋ be dɔŋ ɔ wɔle nɛ ne fae yeesa kɛlɛɛ e akelegyi yako nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ waa hiraa walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kɔŋ De Gbeŋgyoo yele man nɛ.

eng: Some grew angry. They said to each other, Why waste the perfume?.
local: Te balaŋ baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ baale gyeŋa baŋ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te ɔ wɔlɛɛ nuŋ kei mena.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me and because of the good news will save them.
local: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae waa lɛɛ ŋon gbagba nyeedoŋ na, ke gyae ka lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man. Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ nyeedoŋ da lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man lee maŋ Yesu na me nombia dɔɔ nɛ, waa bese nyiŋ ke.

eng: Blessings on the coming kingdom of our ancestor David! Hosanna in the highest.
local: De naana Defidi gyoori keŋ bese la kɛɛ kɔŋ. Wurubuarɛ dee yela ka kekɔŋ. Mena dɔɔ ɛ yela dɛɛ lese Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ yele.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for almost a years pay and the money given to the poor. And they scolded her.
local: Nafɔ baa tale yɔɔ nuŋ kei kela tomyɛɛre kulutoo dokoloŋ kɔba, na waa mo ke fa ayematena. Botɔɔ te ba too be nɔɔ gyakaa alo dɔɔ.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives?.
local: Akpaa ŋolo de nyiŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ pou te ɔ nyeedoŋ da lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man na, woŋ tɔnɔɔ te waa nyiŋ?.

eng: Jesus entered Jerusalem and went into the temple. After he looked around at everything, because it was already late in the evening, he returned to Bethany with the Twelve.
local: Yesu kelii Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ gyoo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man te ɔ kɛɛ abɔɔ ŋan pou gyaŋee. Na tɛɛ te lam dɔɔ, ŋon na ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ gyuu Bɛtani donɔɔ man.

eng: Jesus said, Leave her alone. Why do you make trouble for her? She has done a good thing for me.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ yela e, weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne kale na alo ŋon mena e? Ɔ te yɛɛ kolo kpaakpaa fa maŋ.

eng: What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Yaa woŋ te walaŋ waa tale mo kyɛɛkee na ɔ nyeedoŋ?.

eng: The next day, after leaving Bethany, Jesus was hungry.
local: Tɛɛ kena keŋ Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ ba lee Bɛtani bɛɛ kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ tanam kyaŋa e.

eng: You always have the poor with you; and whenever you want, you can do something good for them. But you wont always have me.
local: Ayematena dɔɔ, baa kyaa ɛ gyaŋ debaŋ kamasɛ. Te debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ ɛ gyae na, ɛ ke tale yɛɛ deeli fa wɔ. Mɔna maŋ dɔɔ, mɔɔ gyae maa kyaa ɛ gyaŋ kelii ɔto ya.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words in this unfaithful and sinful generation, the Human One will be ashamed of that person when he comes in the Fathers glory with the holy angels.
local: Akpaa maŋ Yesu na me nombia de yɛɛ ŋolo desɛŋ gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ besena be wɔle tee Wurubuarɛ te be yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ nɛ siaman na, kenaŋ na wee keŋ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa bese kɔŋ daa na me kya Wurubuarɛ na Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ gyoorobiiri man nɛ, walaŋ ŋonaŋ mɔ nombia gyae ke yɛɛ maŋ desɛŋ.

eng: At daybreak, the chief priests—with the elders, legal experts, and the whole Sanhedrin—formed a plan. They bound Jesus, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate.
local: Tɛɛ kena nebo nebo fuu nɛ, saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala gyaŋeewɔ te be bake Yesu nyeeman. Mena dɔɔ be bake e te ba moo e gyu ka do Pilato ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ Romantena gyoo dinaa nawɔɔ man nɛ, nyiŋmaa man.

eng: From far away, he noticed a fig tree in leaf, so he went to see if he could find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing except leaves, since it wasnt the season for figs.
local: Keŋte ɔ naa fiigi daŋ daale lemlem keŋ faareŋse doo ke man, te ɔ tuuwɔ benaa ke nyi waa nyiŋ ke bia di. Ɔ ke gyu ke gyaŋ na ke yɛɛ faareŋse bo keŋ bia be doo ke man ya. Nawolo nyi na te lii ta debaŋ keŋ fiigi daase na see ya.

eng: She has done what she could. She has anointed my body ahead of time for burial.
local: Ɔ te yɛɛ kpene keŋ waa tale. Ɔ te taŋgbɛɛ kyɛɛkee nuŋ woli maŋ, na kawola nyi ɔ te desina maŋ fa me ke wuu pɛna maa yeŋ.

eng: Pilate questioned him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Keŋte Pilato bɔɔse e nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna Gyudatena gyoo ŋon? Te Yesu tiranɔɔ nyi, Mena yaa n yakowɔ nɛ.

eng: I tell you the truth that, wherever in the whole world the good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, tɛɛle kei dɔɔ yenaŋ kamasɛ keŋ baa kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan nɛ, baa yako kpene keŋ alo kei be yɛɛ nɛ na baa mo tɔɔse e.

eng: So he said to it, No one will ever again eat your fruit! His disciples heard this.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ falaa daŋ keŋ nyiaa, Lee gyɛŋ ne gyu n be gyae n ka tekaa se bia na ŋolo ke di ya. Te ɔ kaseela baŋ nyii kpene keŋ ɔ be yako nɛ.

eng: The chief priests were accusing him of many things.
local: Te Gyudatena saese kegyiise baŋ kɛrɛɛ ɔ nɔɔse mo nombia burum gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of the Twelve, went to the chief priests to give Jesus up to them.
local: Keŋte Gyudase Isekarɔte ŋon ɔ yɛɛ Yesu kaseela kufu bala baŋ man ŋolo e nɛ, gyuuwɔ Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise baŋ gyaŋ nyi waa lese Yesu fa wɔ.

eng: They came into Jerusalem. After entering the temple, he threw out those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Be kelii Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ dekpaŋalaŋ man. Ɔ ke gyu botɔɔ nɛ, na balaŋ baŋ ta mo botɔɔ besena kebu dedii. Keŋte ɔ gegi balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɔɔ abɔɔ na baŋ bɛɛ lɛɛ abɔɔ botɔɔ nɛ. Ɔ besena baŋ bɛɛ yɛlɛɛ kɔbase nɛ tebuluse buŋii, te ɔ tɛɛse baŋ bɛɛ yɔɔ awuluŋna nɛ gbelɛɛ mɔ lɔ.

eng: Pilate asked him again, Arent you going to answer? What about all these accusations.
local: Keŋte Pilato bɔɔse e nyi, Be ta mo nombia kɛŋa bɛɛ gyakaa n dɔɔ na, n ba lese n nɔɔse yee.

eng: When they heard it, they were delighted and promised to give him money. So he started looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Be ke nyii mena nɛ, yɛɛ wɔ gyoŋ te be yakowɔ see nyi baa fa e kɔba. Lee botɔɔ te Gyudase Isekarɔte kɛo gbɛɛ keŋ waa mo lese e fa wɔ.

eng: He didnt allow anyone to carry anything through the temple.
local: Te ɔ te fa ŋolo gbɛɛ waa seele kolo aneyɔɔŋ kaalaŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ dekpaŋalaŋ man ya.

eng: But Jesus gave no more answers, so that Pilate marveled.
local: Botɔɔ te nombia ŋan doo gyoo Pilato nɔɔ, nawolo nyi Yesu te yako daale ya.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb was sacrificed, the disciples said to Jesus, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Nyeekelɛɛwee kedi wee gyaŋgbate keŋ bee di bodobodo keŋ tee be doo ke man ya, te bɛɛ ko Nyeekelɛɛwee kedi nambu ŋon kelii nɛ, Yesu kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa, Aleŋ te n ne gyae nyi de kaa desina see fa nyeekelɛɛwee dinaa weenɛɛ ŋan.

eng: He taught them, Hasnt it been written, My house will be called a house of prayer for all nations? But youve turned it into a hideout for crooks .
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ bɔɔse balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ te kala ta Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyi, Wurubuarɛ kpa, Me deni gyae ke yɛɛ botɔɔ keŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou baa kɔŋ kaa fane, mɔna ɛmɛɛ ta besena ke yɛɛ ŋmɛɛlatena dɛɛgyaŋee. .

eng: During the festival, Pilate released one prisoner to them, whomever they requested.
local: Gyudatena nyeekelɛɛwee kedi kamasɛ nɛ, kaboena nyi Pilato waa tina balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ kyam tɔ deni nɛ man dokoloŋ, ŋon balaŋ benaŋ gbagba ne gyae nɛ yela fa wɔ.

eng: He sent two of his disciples and said to them, Go into the city. A man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kpila ɔ kaseela baŋ bala te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man. Ɛ ne gyu na ɛ ke gyaŋee na balee ŋolo seele na loŋ na ogele. Ɛ sila e.

eng: A man named Barabbas was locked up with the rebels who had committed murder during an uprising.
local: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ na ba te kyaŋ Barabase tɔ deni. Barabase kei kpuɛ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ koro seŋ tia gyoo dinaa, te bɔɔ ko balaŋ nɛ man.

eng: The chief priests and legal experts heard this and tried to find a way to destroy him. They regarded him as dangerous because the whole crowd was enthralled at his teaching.
local: Saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ ke nyii kpene keŋ ɔ be yako nɛ, be gyae gbɛɛ keŋ baa mo koe e. Mɔna gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ, nawolo nyi ɔ kawola keŋ te yeli balaŋ baŋ pou wose ta yeŋ wɔ.

eng: Wherever he enters, say to the owner of the house, The teacher asks,Where is my guest room where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: Dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man waa gyoo nɛ, ɛ yako dekpaŋalaŋte ŋon nyi, De Gbeŋgyoo ŋon kpa dɛɛ bɔɔse neŋ nyiaa, Aleŋ te ŋɔɔla deni keŋ daa na me kaseela baŋ dɛɛ kaa di nyeekelɛɛwee dinaa weenɛɛ ŋan nɛ kyaa.

eng: The crowd pushed forward and asked Pilate to release someone, as he regularly did.
local: Botɔɔ te balaŋ dikpii keŋ kɔŋa Pilato gyaŋ nyi waa yɛɛ kpene keŋ ɔ ne taŋ yɛɛ debaŋ kamasɛ nɛ fa wɔ.

eng: When it was evening, Jesus and his disciples went outside the city.
local: Kelii balinɔɔ nɛ, te ba leewɔ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man laŋ.

eng: He will show you a large room upstairs already furnished. Prepare for us there.
local: Balee ŋonaŋ waa wola ŋon abansoro deni dinaa daale keŋ ke kyaa adido te bɔɔ desina ke man nideli nɛ. Ɛ desina botɔɔ na de kaa di weenɛɛ ŋan.

eng: Pilate answered them, Do you want me to release to you the king of the Jews.
local: Te Pilato bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ ne gyae nyi maa lese Gyudatena gyoo ŋon fa ŋon.

eng: Early in the morning, as Jesus and his disciples were walking along, they saw the fig tree withered from the root up.
local: Tɛɛ kena nebo keŋ be dɔŋ bee gyu nɛ, be naawɔ nyi fiigi daŋ keŋ Yesu be falaa ke nɛ ta yem leenaŋ ke lila man.

eng: The disciples left, came into the city, found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Ɔ kaseela baŋ ke gyu kelii donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ, be naa kpene kamasɛ keŋ Yesu be yako wɔ nɛ pɛpɛɛpɛ, te be desina nyeekelɛɛ weenɛɛ ŋan see botɔɔ.

eng: He knew that the chief priests had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: Keŋ dɔɔ Pilato be bɔɔse mena yɛna nyi, ɔ gyeŋ nyi saese kegyiise baŋ ne kɔla Yesu dɔɔ te ba lese e fa yeŋ.

eng: Peter remembered and said to Jesus, Rabbi, look how the fig tree you cursed has dried up.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita tɔɔse kpene keŋ Yesu be yako nɛ te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, kɛɛ! Fiigi daŋ keŋ n be falaa ke nɛ ta yem.

eng: That evening, Jesus arrived with the Twelve.
local: Kelii balinɔɔ nɛ, Yesu na ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ kɔŋawɔ botɔɔ.

eng: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have him release Barabbas to them instead.
local: Mɔ Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise baŋ feefuwɔ do balaŋ dikpii keŋ nyi, baa yako Pilato nyi waa lese Barabase fa wɔ.

eng: Jesus responded to them, Have faith in God!.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di.

eng: During the meal, Jesus said, I assure you that one of you will betray me—someone eating with me.
local: Ba ke kyaa bee dii nɛ, te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi ɛmɛɛ baŋ daa na ŋon kyaa dee dii nɛ, ɛ man ŋolo waa lese maŋ fa.

eng: Pilate replied, Then what do you want me to do with the one you call king of the Jews.
local: Botɔɔ te Pilato bɔɔse wɔ nyi, Sena te ɛ ne gyae maa yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɛ ne baake e nyi Gyudatena gyoo nɛ.

eng: I assure you that whoever says to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the sea—and doesnt waver but believes that what is said will really happen—it will happen.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa yako bula kei nyi, Kulu lee kɛbo ka gyoo ɛpo man te ɔ te lɛɛ ɔ wose aŋmaareŋ ya, te ɔ lɛɛ di nyi kpene keŋ ɔ be yako nɛ gyae ke kɔŋ mena fa e nɛ, ke gyae ke kɔŋ mena fa ɔtenate.

eng: Deeply saddened, they asked him, one by one, Its not me, is it.
local: Botɔɔ te dekɔkɔrɔkɔɔ kyaŋa ɔ kaseela baŋ, te be bɔɔse Yesu dokoloŋ dokoloŋ nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, maŋ yaa amɔte.

eng: They shouted back, Crucify him.
local: Te balaŋ dikpii keŋ fae yeesa nyi, Yela baa gyɔ e nyeelabia sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ na waa yeŋ.

eng: Therefore I say to you, whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you will receive it, and it will be so for you.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ fane nyi Wurubuarɛ waa yɛɛ fa ŋon nɛ, ɛ lɛɛ di nyi ɛ te wulaa nyiŋ ke, te ɛ ke nyiŋ ke mɔ.

eng: Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Keŋte Pilato bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Woŋ dukum te ɔ yɛɛwɔ? Mɔna be gyakaa bo bɛɛ fae yeesa keŋkeŋ nyi, Gyɔ e nyeelabia sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ.

eng: Jesus answered, Its one of the Twelve, one who is dipping bread with me into this bowl.
local: Te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ kufubala kɛwɔ man ŋolo ŋon daa na e na do de nyiŋmaa nyefale dokoloŋ kei man nɛ.

eng: And whenever you stand up to pray, if you have something against anyone, forgive so that your Father in heaven may forgive you your wrongdoings.
local: Te akpaa n seŋɛɛ n ne fane fa Wurubuarɛ, te n tɔɔse nyi ŋolo nombia doo n konɔɔ man nɛ, kaboena nyi nya mo ɔtenate nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ e, na n kya Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa adido mɔ ke nyiŋ mo n wui nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ neŋ.

eng: Pilate wanted to satisfy the crowd, so he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Keŋ Pilato ne gyae mena keŋ be wɔe ke fɛɛ wɔ dɔɔ ɔ lese Barabase fa wɔ, te ɔ yela ɔ yoonɔɔtena tuku Yesu te ɔ moo e fa nyi baa gyu ke gyɔ e nyeelabia sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: Maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa yeŋ ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako lee ma wose man nɛ, mɔna walaŋ ŋon waa lese maŋ fa nɛ waa laako. Nyi beŋ ta lola walaŋ ŋonaŋ ya na, nafɔ dei.

eng: The soldiers led Jesus away into the courtyard of the palace known as the governors headquarters, and they called together the whole company of soldiers.
local: Keŋte yoonɔɔtena baŋ moo Yesu gyu Pilato dekpaŋalaŋ man botɔɔ keŋ bee di nombia nɛ, te be baake be tebia yoonɔɔtena akaŋ baŋ pou gyaŋee.

eng: Jesus and his disciples entered Jerusalem again. As Jesus was walking around the temple, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders came to him.
local: Keŋte Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ besewɔ kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man. Te Yesu ke ta Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ dekpaŋalaŋ man ɔ ne lam nɛ, Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala na Gyudatena kegyiise kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ.

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to them, and said, Take; this is my body.
local: Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ kee dii nɛ, ɔ moo bodobodo te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ, te ɔ kara ke mo fa ɔ kaseela baŋ. Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ lɛɛ di, kei yɛna ma wosenaane keŋ.

eng: They dressed him up in a purple robe and twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on him.
local: Te ba moo kegba yayam do e, te ba moo sɔɔse loo gyooro kotɔtɔɔ wuu e.

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle te ɔ moo leenɔɔ keŋ soloŋ doo ke man nɛ te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ, te ɔ moo ke fa ɔ kaseela baŋ. Keŋte be popou nyɔɔwɔ lee ke man.

eng: They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority to do them.
local: Te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, Doŋ woŋti yaa n dana n ne yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou? Amɔte faa na neŋ mena doŋ kenaŋ.

eng: They saluted him, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: Na bee kulu e nyiaa, Gyudatena gyoo, n nyee dɛɛ wolose.

eng: He said to them, This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa Kei yɛna me fatabo keŋ Wurubuarɛ be kyɛɛkee leki na waa mo tɔ nɔɔkebakesee wɔle keŋ nɔɔ. Kei ne gyae la ke yela na Wurubuarɛ ka mo balaŋ burum nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ.

eng: Jesus said to them, I have a question for you. Give me an answer, then Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Maŋ mɔ ne gyae maa bɔɔse ŋon nombii dokoloŋ. Nyi akpaa ɛ tiranɔɔ fa maŋ nɛ, kenaŋ na maa wola ŋon doŋ keŋ me dana mɛɛ yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou.

eng: Again and again, they struck his head with a stick. They spit on him and knelt before him to honor him.
local: Botɔɔ na be gyakaa bɛɛ mo daŋ ŋmeree e ɔ nyeeman, na bɛɛ tɔ nɔɔloŋ woli e na bɛɛ buŋ ɔ nawɔɔtɛɛ kulu e.

eng: I assure you that I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way in Gods kingdom.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, mɔɔ gyae maa tekaa nyɔɔ soloŋ kei bela ya, kelii wee keŋ maa bese nyɔɔ ke wɔle Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin? Answer me.
local: Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋkesɔ keŋ ɔ be sɔ balaŋ nɛ, ka lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ yaa ka lee balaŋ gyaŋ? Ɛ tiranɔɔ fa maŋ.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the purple robe and put his own clothes back on him. Then they led him out to crucify him.
local: Be ke kulu e taŋ nɛ, ba kpee kegba yayam keŋ te ba moo ŋon gbagba wui keŋ do e. Te ba moo e lee be kaa gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ.

eng: After singing songs of praise, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
local: Ba ke kpee nɔɔneŋ lese Wurubuarɛ yele nɛ, te ba leewɔ donɔɔ keŋ man gyu oliifi bula keŋ dɔɔ.

eng: They argued among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Botɔɔ te ba gyoo nombia ŋan man te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Akpaa de yako nyi ka lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ na waa bɔɔse daa nyi, Kenaŋ na woŋ dɔɔ te da te lɛɛ Gyɔn nombia ŋan di ya.

eng: Simon, a man from Cyrene, Alexander and Rufus father, was coming in from the countryside. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Be ka lee donɔɔ keŋ man bee gyu nɛ, te be gyaŋee na Simɔn ŋon ɔ ba lee Kirene donɔɔ man nɛ na ɔ ne kɔŋ. Simɔn kei bia yɛna Alɛkesanda na Rufuse. Keŋte ba ŋere e nyi waa lɛɛ Yesu ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ seele.

eng: Jesus said to them, You will all falter in your faithfulness to me. It is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep will go off in all directions .
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ popou ɛ ke yeu tina maŋ yela. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ yakowɔ ɔ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man lee ma wose man nyiaa, Maa gyɔ namensekɛɛlere ŋon lɔ, na ɔ namense baŋ pou ke yaasee.

eng: But we cant say, Its of earthly origin. They said this because they were afraid of the crowd, because they all thought John was a prophet.
local: Te akpaa de yako nyi Ka lee balaŋ gyaŋ na, dee yee balaŋ burum kɛwɔ mɔ. Nawolo nyi be popou ne lɛɛ di nideli nyi Gyɔn yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e.

eng: But after Im raised up, I will go before you to Galilee.
local: Mɔna me fom lee yeŋ man na, maa kyaŋ ŋon gbɛɛ gyu Galelia tɛɛle man.

eng: They brought Jesus to the place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Te ba moo Yesu gyu botɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake nyi Golgata keŋ nawolo nyi Nyeepookoo dɛɛ nɛ.

eng: They answered Jesus, We dont know. Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Mena dɔɔ be tiranɔɔ fa Yesu nyiaa, Dɔɔ gyeŋ ya. Keŋte Yesu mɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Akpaa mena na maŋ mɔ ba wola ŋon me doŋ keŋ me dana mɛɛ yɛɛ tom nɛ dalee ya.

eng: They tried to give him wine mixed with myrrh, but he didnt take it.
local: Keŋte ba moo tee keŋ bɛɛ baake nyi mɛɛrɛ nɛ kyakale soloŋ man fa Yesu nyi waa nyɔɔ, mɔna ɔ te nyɔɔ ke ya.

eng: Peter said to him, Even if everyone else stumbles, I wont.
local: Keŋte Pita yako e nyiaa, Akpaa be popou de yeu tina neŋ yela koraŋ na, maŋ dɔɔ mɔɔ gyae maa tina neŋ yela korakora ya.

eng: They crucified him. They divided up his clothes, drawing lots for them to determine who would take what.
local: Ba ke gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ, te be kpɛlɛɛ ɔ kegbase buŋii te be lɔɔ boe ŋe dɔɔ, na baa naa keŋ walaŋ kamasɛ waa nyiŋ.

eng: But Jesus said to him, I assure you that on this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako Pita nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako neŋ nyi gyɛŋ nelim kei pɛte kegyim kelɔ woya betabola nɛ, nyaŋ ke bɛɛ betabotooro nyi n ba gyeŋ maŋ korakora ya.

eng: It was nine in the morning when they crucified him.
local: Gyae ke yɛɛ ŋgba nebo weese debaŋ atoorote man nɛ, te be gyɔɔ Yesu sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ.

eng: But Peter insisted, If I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. And they all said the same thing.
local: Keŋte Pita yakowɔ seŋ ke dɔɔ keŋkeŋ nyiaa, Akpaa nyi ke yɛɛ yeŋ ke koraŋ na, mɔɔ gyae maa bɛɛ nyi mɔɔ gyeŋ neŋ korakora ya. Te ɔ kaseela akaŋ baŋ pou mɔ yakowɔ mena.

eng: The notice of the formal charge against him was written, The king of the Jews.
local: Keŋte be ŋmarase dukum keŋ be kpa ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ mataa ɔ nyee gbɛɛ nyiaa, gyudatena gyoo ŋon yaa.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to a place called Gethsemane. Jesus said to them, Sit here while I pray.
local: Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ kelii botɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake nyi Gɛkyɛmane nɛ ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kyaa kɛbo na me kaa fane.

eng: They crucified two outlaws with him, one on his right and one on his left.
local: Botɔɔ te be gyɔɔ ŋmɛɛlatena bala baale mɔ sakaa ɔpaŋdaase dɔɔ kpu na Yesu. Ŋolo sakaa ɔ dunoluŋ dɔɔ, te ŋolo mɔ sakaa ɔ demena dɔɔ.

eng: He took Peter, James, and John along with him. He began to feel despair and was anxious.
local: Te ɔ moo Pita na Gyemisi na Gyɔn sila ɔ wose. Botɔɔ te nombia takaa e nideli, keŋ nyi ɔ bɛɛ gyeŋ mena keŋ waa yɛɛ ɔ wose ya.

eng: People walking by insulted him, shaking their heads and saying, Ha! So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you?.
local: Na balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ lam botɔɔ nɛ, na tee e na bee kpiisi ba nyeeya yako nyiaa, Nyaŋ ŋon n kpa n ke tekerii Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ na n ka mo weeya atooro maa ke nɛ.

eng: He said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert.
local: Te ɔ yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Nombia ne takaa maŋ nideli keŋ mɛɛ gyae maa yeŋ. Ɛ kyaa kɛbo, ɛ na doo ya.

eng: Save yourself and come down from that cross.
local: lɛɛ n wose na n ke tisi lee ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ dɔɔ na dɛɛ kɛɛ.

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell to the ground. He prayed that, if possible, he might be spared the time of suffering.
local: Keŋte ɔ gyuuwɔ ɔ siaman kyomii te ɔ tisiwɔ buŋ te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ nyi, akpaa ɔ ne gyae na waa yela kaale kei ke laŋ ɔ nyee dɔɔ.

eng: In the same way, the chief priests were making fun of him among themselves, together with the legal experts. He saved others, they said, but he cant save himself.
local: Mena mɔ te Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ mɔ kulu e. Te be yako dɔŋa nyiaa, Ɔ ne tale lɛɛ baale nyee, mɔna ɔ be tale lɛɛ ŋon gbagba nyee ya.

eng: He said, Abba, Father, for you all things are possible. Take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: Te ɔ yakowɔ nyi, Me kya, kpene kamasɛ be taŋ neŋ doŋ ya. Lese kaale kei me dɔɔ. Mɔ na yɛɛ kpene keŋ maŋ ne gyae ya. Yɛɛ kpene keŋ nyaŋ gbagba ne gyae.

eng: Let the Christ, the king of Israel, come down from the cross. Then well see and believe. Even those who had been crucified with Jesus insulted him.
local: Nyi akpaa ŋon yɛna nyeedoŋ Gbeŋgyoo na Iseraetena gyoo ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ, kenaŋ na waa lɛɛ ɔ wose lee ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ dɔɔ na dɛɛ naa na dɛɛ lɛɛ e di. Te balaŋ bala baŋ bɔɔ gyɔ wɔ sakaa ɔpaŋdaase dɔɔ kpu na e nɛ mɔ tee e mena.

eng: He came and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Simon, are you asleep? Couldnt you stay alert for one hour?.
local: Ɔ ka bese kɔŋ nɛ, ɔ kaa naawɔ na ba te doo te ɔ gyuusu wɔ bɔɔse Simɔn Pita nyiaa, Simɔn, ɛ te doo? Ɛ te tale kyaa na ɛ ka deke debaŋ dokoloŋ kooŋ keŋ nyi ɛ be doo yee?.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Weese ka seŋ dɛɛ nɛ, ditiŋtɛɛ kaa lɛɛ tɛɛle keŋ dɔɔ pou kelii ɔ ke gɛɛse.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Ɛ na doo ya, ɛ fane na ɛ be nyiŋ gyoo kekɛɛsekɛɛ man ya. Ma gyeŋ nyi ɛ konɔɔse man na ɛ ne gyae ɛ ke yɛɛ ma kegyaebii, mɔna ɛ wosenaane man na ɛ be taŋ mena doŋ kenaŋ ya.

eng: At three, Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eloi, eloi, lama sabachthani, which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Te weese ke gɛɛse kenaŋ nɛ Yesu fae yeesa keŋkeŋ nyiaa, Eli, Eli, lama sabatani? Keŋ nawolo nyi, Me Wurubuarɛ, Me Wurubuarɛ, weera te n lese n sia yela maŋ.

eng: Again, he left them and prayed, repeating the same words.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ gyu ke fane yako nombii dokoloŋ kenaŋ bela.

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Look! Hes calling Elijah.
local: Balaŋ baŋ ba seŋɛɛ botɔɔ ke nyii mena nɛ be yakowɔ nyiaa, Ɛ nyii, ɔ ne baake Elaegya.

eng: And, again, when he came back, he found them sleeping, for they couldnt keep their eyes open, and they didnt know how to respond to him.
local: Ɔ ka bese kɔŋ be gyaŋ na be sɛɛ be doo, nawolo nyi dosoroŋ doo be siaman te be ta gyeŋ kpene keŋ baa yako ya.

eng: Someone ran, filled a sponge with sour wine, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink, saying, Lets see if Elijah will come to take him down.
local: Debokenaŋ man te be man ŋolo yeuwɔ ka mo kolo ŋgba kokyaa keŋ gyae ke tale lɛɛ loŋ nɛ do soloŋ denyaŋesa daale man, te ɔ moo ke tooso na daŋ te ɔ gyinaa ke adido fa Yesu nyi waa nyɔɔ. Keŋte ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Ɛ seŋ dɛɛ kɛɛ nyi Elaegya waa kɔŋ kaa lese e lee ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ dɔɔ.

eng: He came a third time and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Thats enough! The time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ kɔŋ be gyaŋ betabotoorote te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyi, Ɛ sɛɛ ɛ gyakaa dosoroŋ kei dɔɔ ɛ ne fɛɛfo? Tekaboe mena, debaŋ keŋ te lii. Be ta lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon fa nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra.

eng: But Jesus let out a loud cry and died.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu besewɔ fae yeesa keŋkeŋ, te ɔ kara weesi.

eng: Get up! Lets go! Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Ɛ koro dee gyu de kaa gyaŋee na wɔ. Ŋon ɔ na lese maŋ waa fa nɛ te tuŋ daa taŋ.

eng: The curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom.
local: Kekɔŋ mena nɛ, kanyaŋ keŋ bɔɔ mo kpase Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ bɛɛsewɔ kyɛɛkyɛɛ ala lee adido kaa lii ka mokɔɔ man.

eng: Suddenly, while Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came with a mob carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests, legal experts, and elders.
local: Yesu ke gyakaa ɔ ne kolosi nɛ, Gyudase ŋon ɔ yɛɛ kaseela kufu bala baŋ man ŋolo e nɛ, kaa bɛɛwɔ lee be dɔɔ. Na balaŋ dikpii keŋ be dana yoosilase na akpokpose, na Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ Gyudatena kegyiise ba lese do na e nɛ silana e.

eng: When the centurion, who stood facing Jesus, saw how he died, he said, This man was certainly Gods Son.
local: Yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ botɔɔ kena Yesu ke fae yeesa kara weesi nɛ, ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Balee kei yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon ampaŋ.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss, and take him away under guard.
local: Na Gyudase ŋon ɔ ba lese e fa nɛ taŋ taŋgbɛɛ yako balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, Walaŋ ŋon maa pukaa e nɛ ŋon ŋonaŋ, ɛ kyaŋ e keŋkeŋ na ɛ ka mo e ta.

eng: Some women were watching from a distance, including Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and Salome.
local: Na ala baale seŋɛɛ lemlem bɛɛ kɛɛ. Ala benaŋ man baale yɛna Mɛɛre ŋon ɔ ba lee Magedala nɛ, na Gyemisi kemaasa na Gyose be naa Mɛɛre na Salome.

eng: As soon as he got there, Judas said to Jesus, Rabbi! Then he kissed him.
local: Gyudase ka betaa lii Yesu gyaŋ nɛ, ɔ baake e nyi, Me Gbeŋgyoo, te ɔ pukaa e.

eng: When Jesus was in Galilee, these women had followed and supported him, along with many other women who had come to Jerusalem with him.
local: Ala kɛwɔ silana Yesu kɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ debaŋ keŋ ɔ kya Galelia nɛ. Na ala burum baŋ bɔɔ sila e kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ mɔ seŋɛɛ botɔɔ.

eng: Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Botɔɔ te balaŋ baŋ kyaŋa e.

eng: Since it was late in the afternoon on Preparation Day, just before the Sabbath.
local: Na wee kenaŋ yɛɛ wee keŋ Gyudatena na mo desina ba wose fa ba kefɛɛfowee keŋ kedi. Ke lii balinɔɔ nɛ.

eng: One of the bystanders drew a sword and struck the high priests slave and cut off his ear.
local: Keŋte Yesu kaseela baŋ man ŋolo ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ botɔɔ nɛ lese ɔ yoosila kara Wurubuarɛ saese kegyia ŋon tobaale deŋele lese ke lɔ.

eng: Joseph from Arimathea dared to approach Pilate and ask for Jesus body.
local: balee ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Gyosɛfo ŋon ɔ ba lee Arimatia donɔɔ man nɛ gyɔɔ ɔ konɔɔ gyu Pilato gyaŋ ka sola e nyi waa mo Yesu woo ŋon fa e ɔ kaa wuu. Gyosɛfo kei yɛɛ donɔɔ kegyia ŋolo e, te ŋon mɔ ne gyeki Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ.

eng: Jesus responded, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like an outlaw?.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Me yɛɛ walaŋkum e te ɛ moo yoosilase na akpokpose ɛ ne kɔŋ ɛ kaa kyaŋ maŋ?.

eng: Pilate wondered if Jesus was already dead. He called the centurion and asked him whether Jesus had already died.
local: Pilato ke nyii nyi Yesu ta yem nɛ, yɛɛ e gyakoloŋ. Mena dɔɔ ɔ baake ɔ yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon bɔɔse e nyiaa, Yesu ŋon wulaa yeŋawɔ.

eng: Day after day, I was with you, teaching in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But let the scriptures be fulfilled.
local: Wee kamasɛ keŋ me kyaa ɛ gyaŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man mɛɛ wola ŋon nɛ, ɛ te kyaŋ maŋ ya. Mɔna nombia kɛŋa pou kɔŋae, na ke yela Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ke kɔŋ mena.

eng: When he learned from the centurion that Jesus was dead, Pilate gave the dead body to Joseph.
local: Ɔ ke nyii lee yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon nɔɔman nyi ke yɛɛ ampaŋ nɛ, ɔ faa Gyosɛfo ŋon gbɛɛ nyi waa gyu ka mo woo ŋon.

eng: And all his disciples left him and ran away.
local: Keŋte Yesu kaseela baŋ pou yeuwɔ tina e yela.

eng: He bought a linen cloth, took Jesus down from the cross, wrapped him in the cloth, and laid him in a tomb that had been carved out of rock. He rolled a stone against the entrance to the tomb.
local: Mena dɔɔ Gyosɛfo gyuuwɔ ke lɛɛ akaralɛɛ te ɔ lese woo ŋon lee ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ dɔɔ te ɔ moo akaralɛɛ keŋ milii e. Keŋte ɔ moo e ke wuu yebɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ kare boe daale man nɛ man. Te ɔ biliŋi boe dinaa daale tɔ yebɔɔ keŋ nɔɔ.

eng: One young man, a disciple, was wearing nothing but a linen cloth. They grabbed him.
local: Na tobaale ŋolo ŋon ɔ wuuna kanyaŋbii nɛ silana Yesu. Be ka mo baa kyaŋ e nɛ.

eng: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses saw where he was buried.
local: Na Mɛɛre ŋon ɔ ba lee Magedala nɛ, na Gyose ɔ naa Mɛɛre seŋɛɛ botɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ be naa botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ wuu e nɛ.

eng: but he left the linen cloth behind and ran away naked.
local: te ɔ leewɔ be nyiŋmaa man yeu diteŋteli tina ɔ kanyaŋbii keŋ yela.

eng: They led Jesus away to the high priest, and all the chief priests, elders, and legal experts gathered.
local: Keŋte ba moo Yesu gyu Wurubuarɛ saese kegyia ŋon gyaŋ, te Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise baŋ pou na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala, na Gyudatena kegyiise kaa gyaŋeewɔ botɔɔ.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance, right into the high priests courtyard. He was sitting with the guards, warming himself by the fire.
local: Na Pita silana Yesu wɔle wɔle lemlem gyu Wurubuarɛ saese kegyia ŋon dekpaŋalaŋ man. Botɔɔ te Pita gyuuwɔ ke kyaa kotɔtɔɔtena baŋ bee gyee dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ nɛ gyaŋ bee woli boalaŋ.

eng: The chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were looking for testimony against Jesus in order to put him to death, but they couldnt find any.
local: Na Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise, na Gyudatena kegyiise akaŋ baŋ ne kɛo nombii keŋ baa mo gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ na baa nyiŋ gbɛɛ ko e, mɔ be te nyim ya.

eng: Many brought false testimony against him, but they contradicted each other.
local: Balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ kaa kɛrɛɛ ɔ nɔɔse, mɔna be nɔɔwoya ŋan te kyaa na dɔŋa ya.

eng: Some stood to offer false witness against him, saying.
local: Te baale korowɔ kɛrɛɛ ɔ nɔɔse nyiaa.

eng: We heard him saying, I will destroy this temple, constructed by humans, and within three days I will build another, one not made by humans.
local: De te nyii nyi ɔ kpa waa tekerii Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ balaŋ be maa nɛ, te weeya atooro wɔle na waa ma wɔle keŋ te yɛɛ walaŋ maa na ke ya.

eng: But their testimonies didnt agree even on this point.
local: Baŋ mɔ nombia ŋan bɔɔ kaa yako nɛ, te kyaa na dɔŋa ya.

eng: Then the high priest stood up in the middle of the gathering and examined Jesus. Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ saese kegyia ŋon korowɔ seŋ kebu keŋ nsana nsana te ɔ bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, N be tiranɔɔ yee? Woŋ dɔɔ te balaŋ kɛwɔ ne kɛrɛɛ n nɔɔse, te n be tiranɔɔ ya.

eng: But Jesus was silent and didnt answer. Again, the high priest asked, Are you the Christ, the Son of the blessed one.
local: Mɔ Yesu te gyuusu ɔ nɔɔ ya. Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ saese kegyia ŋon bɔɔse e nyi, Nyaŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi nyaŋ ke kɔŋ nɛ? Nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ ŋon kaboena nyi baa lese ɔ yele nɛ bu dokoloŋ ŋon.

eng: Jesus said, I am. And you will see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ nyi, Ayi, maŋ ŋonaŋ. Ɛ kena maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon kyaa Wurubuarɛ doŋte ŋon dunoluŋ dɔɔ. Na ɛ ka bese naa maŋ na me dɔŋ ŋɔma man adido mɛɛ kɔŋ.

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Why do we need any more witnesses?.
local: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ saese kegyia ŋon tirii ŋon gbagba kegba te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Woŋ adansɛɛ yaa dɛɛ gyae dee nyii bela.

eng: Youve heard his insult against God. What do you think? They all condemned him. He deserves to die.
local: Ɛ te nyii mmusuo nombia ŋan ɔ be yako yee? Sena yaa ɛ na gyueŋ lee ke man? Te kegyiise baŋ pou too be nɔɔ gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ bu e fɔɔ nyi, Kaboena nyi baa ko e.

eng: Some began to spit on him. Some covered his face and hit him, saying, Prophesy! Then the guards took him and beat him.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ baale fiasɛɛ bɛɛ tɔ nɔɔloŋ woli e, te be wuu ɔ sia na bɛɛ takee e akɔtase. Te be bɔɔse e nyi, Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre, wola daa ŋon ɔ be gyɔ neŋ. Keŋte ba moo e fa kotɔtɔɔtena baŋ te baŋ mɔ ke tuku e.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was below in the courtyard. A woman, one of the high priests servants, approached.
local: Pita ke kyaa dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man nɛ, Wurubuarɛ saese kegyia ŋon alebu ŋolo kɔŋawɔ botɔɔ.

eng: and saw Peter warming himself by the fire. She stared at him and said, You were also with the Nazarene, Jesus.
local: Ɔ kena Pita kyaa ɔ ne woli boalaŋ nɛ, ɔ kɛɛ e yididi te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Nyaŋ mɔ n kpuɛ na Yesu Nasarɛte baale ŋon.

eng: But he denied it, saying, I dont know what youre talking about. I dont understand what youre saying. And he went outside into the outer courtyard. A rooster crowed.
local: Mɔ Pita lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ te ɔ yako e nyi, Mɔɔ gyeŋ kpene keŋ n ne yako nɛ man korakora ya. Botɔɔ te Pita leewɔ gyu denanɔɔ keŋ man.

eng: The female servant saw him and began a second time to say to those standing around, This man is one of them.
local: Alebu ŋon ka bese naa Pita botɔɔ nɛ ɔ yako balaŋ baŋ ba seŋɛɛ botɔɔ nɛ nyiaa, Balee kei mɔ kpuɛ na wɔ.

eng: But he denied it again. A short time later, those standing around again said to Peter, You must be one of them, because you are also a Galilean.
local: Mɔ Pita besewɔ bɛɛ ke bela. Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, balaŋ baŋ ba seŋɛɛ botɔɔ nɛ besewɔ yako Pita nyiaa, Ke yɛɛ ampaŋ nyi n kpuɛ na wɔ, nawolo nyi n yɛɛ Galelia baale e.

eng: But he cursed and swore, I dont know this man youre talking about.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita moo nɔɔdoŋ kolosi te ɔ kaŋa ntam kpu nyiaa, Mɔɔ gyeŋ balee kei ŋon ɛ ne kolosi ɔ wose man nombia nɛ korakora ya.

eng: At that very moment, a rooster crowed a second time. Peter remembered what Jesus told him, Before a rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times. And he broke down, sobbing.
local: Debokenaŋ man te kegyim lɔɔ woya betabolate. Keŋte Pita tɔɔse nombia ŋan Yesu be yako e nɛ nyiaa, Pɛ kegyim kelɔ woya betabola na, nyaŋ ke bɛɛ betabotooro nyi n ba gyeŋ maŋ ya. Keŋte ɔ yɛlɛɛwɔ do ɔ ne wii.

eng: Jesus spoke to them in parables. A man planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a pit for the winepress, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ kolosi nombia burum fa wɔ aduya man, te ɔ gyɔɔ dudu fa wɔ nyiaa, Wɔɔfarale ŋolo fara wɔɔ te ɔ duu daasebia ŋaale ŋan ŋe yɛɛ ŋgba akutuse te bɛɛ baake ŋa nyi gerepo nɛ ke man. Ɔ dii abaŋ kilisi wɔɔ keŋ, te ɔ wula bɔɔ keŋ baa sara daasebia ŋan ke man na baa lese ŋa loŋ lee ŋe man nɛ. Te ɔ baŋa deni dɔɔloŋ dɔɔloŋ daale keŋ walaŋ ke kyaa ke man na waa gyee wɔɔ kenaŋ. Te ɔ moo wɔɔ keŋ do paasetena baale nyiŋmaa man nyi baa kɛɛ ke dɔɔ, te ɔ korowɔ laŋ gbɛɛ.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard.
local: Debaŋ kelii nyi baa kɔ daasebia ŋan nɛ, balee ŋon kpila ɔ tobaale ŋolo gyu paasetena baŋ gyaŋ nyi baa fa e ɔ wui nɔɔ.

eng: But they grabbed the servant, beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
local: Mɔna paasetena baŋ kyaŋa ɔ tobaale ŋon, te be tuku e te be yela ɔ laŋawɔ nyiŋmaase kufu nawɔɔ kufu.

eng: Again the landowner sent another servant to them, but they struck him on the head and treated him disgracefully.
local: Keŋte wɔɔte ŋon besewɔ kpila ɔ tobaale ŋolo bela gyu paasetena baŋ gyaŋ. Mɔna be tuku ŋon mɔ do e gyamɛɛ ɔ nyee man, te ba doo e desɛŋ.

eng: He sent another one; that one they killed. The landlord sent many other servants, but the tenants beat some and killed others.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle wɔɔte ŋon besewɔ kpila ɔ tobaale ŋolo bela, mɔna paasetena baŋ koo e bo. Ɔ kpila tewulɛɛ burum, mɔna be tuku baale te ba koe baale.

eng: Now the landowner had one son whom he loved dearly. He sent him last, thinking, They will respect my son.
local: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kaa keŋ wɔɔte ŋon waa kpila yɛna, ŋon gbagba bu dokoloŋ kooŋ ŋon ɔ bɛɛ sɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ ya nɛ. Wɔle wɔle nɛ, ɔ kaa kpila ŋon mɔ gyu paasetena baŋ gyaŋ te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Mberem me bu na, baa bu e.

eng: But those tenant farmers said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.
local: Mɔ paasetena baŋ kena ɔ bu ŋon nɛ be yako dɔŋa nyiaa, Kei yɛna bu ŋon waa kaa di ɔ kya abɔɔ ŋan. Ɛ yela dɛɛ ko e, na abɔɔ ŋan nafɔ waa di nɛ ka bese de wui.

eng: They grabbed him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
local: Mena dɔɔ be kyaŋa bu ŋon ko, te be wɔŋee e lee wɔɔ keŋ man ke leki.

eng: So what will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Woŋ te wɔɔte ŋon waa yɛɛ? Waa kɔŋ kaa koe mena paasetena benaŋ, na waa mo wɔɔ keŋ do paasetena wɔlɛɛ nyiŋmaa man.

eng: Havent you read this scripture, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
local: Te ɔ besewɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ te kala ta Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan na ɛ kena kpene keŋ ne yako nɛ yee? Be ŋmarasewɔ nyiaa, Boe keŋ deni maara baŋ be bɛɛ nɛ, keŋ kaa bese na mokɔɔ kasee boe keŋ ne yeli deni keŋ ne yɛɛ doŋ.

eng: The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes? .
local: De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ la te kɔŋawɔ mena, te ke yɛɛ kpene keŋ ke dana gyakoloŋ de siaman Saese kegyiise na Farasiitena baŋ ke nyii Yesu aduya kɛŋa nɛ, ba gyeŋawɔ nyi ba wose man nombia yaa ɔ ne yako nɛ.

eng: They wanted to arrest Jesus because they knew that he had told the parable against them. But they were afraid of the crowd, so they left him and went away.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ gyae gbɛɛ baa kyaŋ Yesu, nawolo nyi be naawɔ nyi baŋ dɔɔ te ɔ gyɔɔ dudu kei. Mɔna bee yee balaŋ baŋ, mena dɔɔ be tinaa e yela te be laŋawɔ.

eng: They sent some of the Pharisees and supporters of Herod to trap him in his words.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle te Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ gyuuwɔ ke kpila gyoo Herode balaŋ baale, na Farasiitena baŋ baale nyi baa kɔŋ kaa teese Yesu nɔɔman na baa nyiŋ gbɛɛ kyaŋ e.

eng: They came to him and said, Teacher, we know that youre genuine and you dont worry about what people think. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is. Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not? Should we pay taxes or not.
local: Baŋ mɔ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, da gyeŋ nyi n bee di dekara ya, te n be famenɛ na kpene keŋ balaŋ ne yako nɛ ya. N bɛɛ kɛɛ walaŋ siaman ya, te n na wola Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛneŋ kpaakpaa ŋan ŋe gbɛɛ dɔɔ. Dei nyi dɛɛ tɔ lempoo fa Romantena gyoo dinaa Kaesa, yaa de na tɔɔ ya.

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deceit, he said to them, Why are you testing me? Bring me a coin. Show it to me.
local: Mɔ Yesu kena ba agyueŋ kumɛɛ ŋan nɛ ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ ne gyae ɛ ka teese me nɔɔman. Ɛ mo kɔbabii keŋ kɔŋ maa kɛɛ.

eng: And they brought one. He said to them, Whose image and inscription is this? Caesars, they replied.
local: Keŋte ba moo kɔbabii dokoloŋ kɔna e. Botɔɔ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Amɔte nyee na ɔ yele gyakaa la ke dɔɔ? Te be yako e nyi gyoo dinaa Kaesa wui.

eng: Jesus said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God. His reply left them overcome with wonder.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Kpene keŋ yɛɛ gyoo Kaesa wui na ɛ mo fa e, te kpene keŋ mɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ wui nɛ, ɛ mo ke fa e. Botɔɔ te gyakoloŋ gyoo balaŋ baŋ lee nɔɔ keŋ Yesu be tiri fa wɔ nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle te Sadukiitena baŋ bɔɔ ne lɛɛ di nyi walaŋ da yem na waa bese foŋ ya nɛ, man baale kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies , leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
local: De Gbeŋgyoo, de naana Mosesi ŋmarase mmaraa fa daa nyi, akpaa walaŋ da yem tina ɔ wɛɛle yela te be ta lola bia ya na, ɔ naabu waa yala lialo ŋon na baa lola bia ɔ kegyia ŋon yele man.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married a woman; when he died, he left no children.
local: Kɔŋawɔ nyi balee ŋolo bia baala nyetooro baale kyaawɔ, te be kegyia ŋon yala alo keŋ be ta lola bia ya te ɔ yeŋawɔ.

eng: The second married her and died without leaving any children. The third did the same.
local: Balate ŋon mɔ yala alo ŋonaŋ te ŋon mɔ yeŋawɔ keŋ be ta lola bia ya. Batoorote ŋon mɔ mena.

eng: None of the seven left any children. Finally, the woman died.
local: Mena mɔ te akaŋ baŋ mɔ pou yala alo ŋonaŋ, mɔna be ta lolo ya. Te kyare ya, te alo ŋon mɔ yeŋawɔ.

eng: At the resurrection, when they all rise up, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Mena dɔɔ wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ yem nɛ lee yeŋ man na, baala nyetooro kɛwɔ baŋ bɔɔ yala alo kei gyaŋee nɛ, be man woŋti te alo ŋon waa yɛɛ ɔ wɛɛle.

eng: Jesus said to them, Isnt this the reason you are wrong, because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power?.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ ta yoo, nawolo nyi ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ya, te ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ doŋ keŋ mɔ ya.

eng: When people rise from the dead, they wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like Gods angels.
local: Wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu balaŋ lee yeŋ man nɛ, baala be gyae baa yala ya, te bɔɔ gyae baa mo ala mɔ fa yale ya. Be popou baa yɛɛ bo ŋgba Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ be kyaa adido nɛ.

eng: As for the resurrection from the dead, havent you read in the scroll from Moses, in the passage about the burning bush, how God said to Moses, I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?.
local: Lee kefoŋ lee yeŋ man wose man nombia na, ɛ te kala ta de naana Mosesi tɔne man mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ be kolosi na ŋon Mosesi depoŋfa keŋ ne kpaa nɛ man yee? Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Maŋ yɛna Aberaham na Asiki na Gyekɔpo Wurubuarɛ. Wurubuarɛ keyako kei nɛ, na nawolo baa nyi ɔ siaman na balaŋ kɛwɔ sɛɛ be kyaa.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. You are seriously mistaken.
local: Wurubuarɛ te yɛɛ woya Wurubuarɛ e ya, ɔ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ be kyaa na be sia nɛ Wurubuarɛ e. Mena dɔɔ ɛ ta yoo nideli ŋgba ɛ ne yako nyi yeŋ wɔle kefoŋ be kyaa ya nɛ.

eng: One of the legal experts heard their dispute and saw how well Jesus answered them. He came over and asked him, Which commandment is the most important of all.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ man ŋolo kɔŋawɔ botɔɔ. Ɔ ke kɔŋ kaa nyii mena keŋ bɛɛ wɔŋee nombia ŋan, na ɔ kena nyi Yesu ke tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nideli nɛ ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan pou na, woŋti yɛna keŋ ne hia kela.

eng: Jesus replied, The most important one is Israel, listen! Our God is the one Lord.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Iseraetena pou ɛ nyii, De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ŋon yɛɛ dokoloŋ.

eng: and you must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your mind, and with all your strength.
local: Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi nya mo n konɔɔ pou na nya agyueŋ pou na n doŋ pou gyae n Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ.

eng: The second is this, You will love your neighbor as yourself . No other commandment is greater than these.
local: Alate keŋ mɔ yɛna nyi, Gyae n dɔɔ ŋgba nyaŋ gbagba n wose nɛ. Mmaraa daale be kyaa keŋ kela kɛŋa ya.

eng: The legal expert said to him, Well said, Teacher. You have truthfully said that God is one and there is no other besides him.
local: Keŋte Gyudatena mmaraa wolale ŋon yako Yesu nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, ampaŋ yaa n yakowɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ dokoloŋ, te ŋolo be kyaa kpu na e ya.

eng: And to love God with all of the heart, a full understanding, and all of ones strength, and to love ones neighbor as oneself is much more important than all kinds of entirely burned offerings and sacrifices.
local: Te n besewɔ yako nyi kaboena nyi walaŋ waa mo ɔ konɔɔ pou na ɔ doŋ pou na wa agyueŋ pou gyae Wurubuarɛ, na waa bese gyae ɔ dɔɔ ŋgba ŋon gbagba nɛ. Mmaraase kɛŋa dɔɔ kedi dei kela keŋ walaŋ ka mo abɔɔ ke deesi fa Wurubuarɛ.

eng: When Jesus saw that he had answered with wisdom, he said to him, You arent far from Gods kingdom. After that, no one dared to ask him any more questions.
local: Yesu kena nyi Gyudatena mmaraa wolale ŋon te tiranɔɔ nideli nɛ ɔ yako e nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ na Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ nsana be dana lem ya. Lee kei wɔle nɛ, ŋolo te tale gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ bɔɔse e nombii daale bela ya.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, Why do the legal experts say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Yesu ke gyakaa ɔ na wolo Gyudatena ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ dekpaŋalaŋ man nɛ ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Sena dɔɔ te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ ne tale yako nyi, Kristo ŋon Wurubuarɛ ba lese nɛ yɛɛ gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii e?.

eng: David himself, inspired by the Holy Spirit, said, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon yela gyoo Defidi gbagba yakowɔ nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ yako me Gbeŋgyoo nyi, Tisi kyaa me dunoluŋ dɔɔ, kelii debaŋ keŋ maa mo n kɔlala pou do n tɛɛ.

eng: David himself calls him Lord, so how can he be Davids son? The large crowd listened to him with delight.
local: Te akpaa nyi gyoo Defidi baake nyeelɛɛre ŋon nyi ɔ Gbeŋgyoo na, kenaŋ na sena te nyeelɛɛre ŋon waa bese gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii? Keŋte yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ gyaŋee bee tei nɛ gyoŋ.

eng: As he was teaching, he said, Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They want to be greeted with honor in the markets.
local: Yesu kɛɛ wolo nɛ ɔ faree wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose nideli na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ. Bɛɛ gyae kegbase dɔɔloŋ nyiŋan kado na be dɔŋ na kebu agantaŋ man, na balaŋ kenyiŋ do wɔ nɔɔ.

eng: They long for places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: Akpaa be gyaŋee Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man, na botɔɔse ŋan bɛɛ gyaŋee di be nɔɔ nɛ, na bɛɛ gyae akyaese kpaakpaa baa kyaa.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Bɛɛ beo liala lɛo ba abɔɔ kamasɛ ŋan be dana nɛ. Te bɛɛ fane balaŋ man kyare mo wola ba wose. Wurubuarɛ waa wɔŋ mena balaŋ benaŋ dinɔɔ deŋela nideli.

eng: Jesus sat across from the collection box for the temple treasury and observed how the crowd gave their money. Many rich people were throwing in lots of money.
local: Yesu ke gyu ka gyoo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ, ɔ ke kyaawɔ yenaŋ mo ɔ sia tee adakaa keŋ bɛɛ do kɔba ke man nɛ. Te ɔ gyinaa ɔ sia ɔ ne kɛɛ mena keŋ balaŋ na mo kɔba kaa do adakaa keŋ man. Balaŋ baŋ be dana kɔba nɛ, moo kɔba burum kaa do adakaa keŋ man.

eng: One poor widow came forward and put in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: Keŋte lialo diyemte ŋolo moo kɔbabia ala, ŋan ŋe gyae ke yɛɛ ŋgba kotoku dokoloŋ nɛ kaa do adakaa keŋ man.

eng: Jesus called his disciples to him and said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than everyone whos been putting money in the treasury.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu baake ɔ kaseela baŋ gyaŋee te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi lialo diyemte kei ta do kɔba kela balaŋ akaŋ baŋ pou.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had, even what she needed to live on.
local: Nawolo nyi balaŋ baŋ pou lese kɔba kyomii bo lee burum ŋan be dana nɛ man kaa do. Mɔ lialo diyemte kei dɔɔ, keŋ na ɔ diyem koraŋ nɛ kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɔ dana nɛ yaa ɔ moowɔ kaa do nɛ.

eng: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so that they could go and anoint Jesus dead body.
local: Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee keŋ ke taŋ nɛ, Mɛɛre ŋon ɔ ba lee Magedala na Gyemisi ɔ naa Mɛɛre na Salome, lɛɛ abɔɔ ofoŋ nyiŋan be kaa buraa Yesu woo ŋon.

eng: Very early on the first day of the week, just after sunrise, they came to the tomb.
local: Kosida nebo nebo weese ke bɛɛ nɛ, ala baŋ korowɔ bee gyu yebɔɔ keŋ tɛɛ.

eng: They were saying to each other, Whos going to roll the stone away from the entrance for us.
local: Be kee gyu na bɛɛ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Amɔte ne gyae la waa biliŋi boe keŋ lee yebɔɔ keŋ nɔɔman fa daa.

eng: When they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away.
local: Mɔ be ke gyina be sia kɛɛ na, be naawɔ nyi be te biliŋi boe dinaa dinaa keŋ lee yebɔɔ keŋ nɔɔman.

eng: Going into the tomb, they saw a young man in a white robe seated on the right side; and they were startled.
local: Be ka mo baa gyoo yebɔɔ keŋ man nɛ, te be naa tobaale ŋolo na ɔ doona kegba wolom wurikyim na ɔ baŋɛɛ dunoluŋ dɔɔ te be ŋeŋawɔ.

eng: But he said to them, Dont be alarmed! You are looking for Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has been raised. He isnt here. Look, heres the place where they laid him.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ yako ala baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ na ŋeŋii ya. Ma gyeŋ nyi Yesu ŋon ɔ ba lee Nasarɛte, ŋon bɔɔ gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ yaa ɛ ne kɛo. Ɔ te fom. Ɔ be kyaa kɛbo ya. Ɛ kɛɛ botɔɔ keŋ ɔ be doo nɛ.

eng: Go, tell his disciples, especially Peter, that he is going ahead of you into Galilee. You will see him there, just as he told you.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ gyu ke yako ɔ kaseela baŋ na Pita nyiaa, Ɔ te kyaŋ ŋon gbɛɛ ɔ ne gyu Galelia donɔɔ man. Botɔɔ te ɛ kena e ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yako ŋon nɛ. .

eng: Overcome with terror and dread, they fled from the tomb. They said nothing to anyone, because they were afraid. .
local: Keŋte ala baŋ leewɔ yebɔɔ keŋ man bileŋ, nawolo nyi gyakoloŋ ta gyoo wɔ te ba wose mɔ ta yeŋ wɔ. Te ba te yako ŋolo mɔ kolo ya, nawolo nyi bee yee.

eng: They promptly reported all of the young mans instructions to those who were with Peter. Afterward, through the work of his disciples, Jesus sent out, from the east to the west, the sacred and undying message of eternal salvation. Amen. .
local: Yesu kefoŋ lee yeŋ man Kosida nebo nebo fuu keŋ nɛ, ɔ taŋgbɛɛwɔ lese ɔ wose wola Mɛɛre ŋon ɔ ba lee Magedala nɛ. Mɛɛre kei te Yesu gegi feliŋkumɛɛ nyetooro lee ɔ man nɛ.

eng: She went and reported to the ones who had been with him, who were mourning and weeping.
local: Mena dɔɔ ŋon mɔ gyuuwɔ ke yako balaŋ baŋ be silana Yesu nɛ. Ɔ ke gyu na be kyaa bee wii na bee di walaŋsa.

eng: But even after they heard the news, they didnt believe that Jesus was alive and that Mary had seen him.
local: Keŋ na be ke nyii nyi Yesu te fom te Mɛɛre naa e koraŋ nɛ, ba te lɛɛ di ya.

eng: After that he appeared in a different form to two of them who were walking along in the countryside.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, Yesu lese ɔ wose dinɔɔ wɔle daale man wola ɔ kaseela bala baale debaŋ keŋ bee gyu donɔɔ daale man nɛ.

eng: When they returned, they reported it to the others, but they didnt believe them.
local: Keŋte baŋ mɔ besewɔ kɔŋ kaa yako be tebia akaŋ baŋ. Mɔ baŋ mɔ te lɛɛ wɔ di ya.

eng: Finally he appeared to the eleven while they were eating. Jesus criticized their unbelief and stubbornness because they didnt believe those who saw him after he was raised up.
local: Kɛŋa pou wɔle nɛ, ɔ kaseela kufu daale baŋ yilaawɔ bee dii te Yesu besewɔ kaa lese ɔ wose wola wɔ. Keŋte ɔ gyeŋa baŋ do wɔ lee mena keŋ balaŋ be kɔŋ kaa yako wɔ nyi ɔ te fom, te ba lɛɛ wɔ di ya nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: He said to them, Go into the whole world and proclaim the good news to every creature.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ gyu tɛɛle kei pou dɔɔ na ɛ kaa kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan fa balaŋ pou.

eng: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever doesnt believe will be condemned.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ nombia ŋan di te be sɔ e Wurubuarɛ loŋ na, waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ. Mɔ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ te lɛɛ nombia ŋan di ya nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa bu e fɔɔ.

eng: These signs will be associated with those who believe: they will throw out demons in my name. They will speak in new languages.
local: Balaŋ baŋ baa lɛɛ maŋ di nɛ, baa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia kɛŋa. Baa gberaa feliŋkumɛɛ lee balaŋ man lee ma yele man, na baa kolosi deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo ŋan bɔɔ gyeŋ ya.

eng: They will pick up snakes with their hands. If they drink anything poisonous, it will not hurt them. They will place their hands on the sick, and they will get well.
local: Nyi akpaa be gate doma na be nyiŋmaase yaa be nyɔɔ teekum daale koraŋ na, be gyae ke yɛɛ wɔ kolo ya. Baa mo be nyiŋmaase gyakaa kaweesetena dɔɔ, na ba wose ke yɛɛ wɔ doŋ.

eng: After the Lord Jesus spoke to them, he was lifted up into heaven and sat down on the right side of God.
local: De Gbeŋgyoo Yesu kekolosi fa wɔ taŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ moo e gyu adido te ɔ ke kyaawɔ Wurubuarɛ dunoluŋ dɔɔ.

eng: But they went out and proclaimed the message everywhere. The Lord worked with them, confirming the word by the signs associated with them.
local: Yesu kaseela baŋ ka lee botɔɔ nɛ, be gyuuwɔ tɛɛle kei pou dɔɔ kekolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan. Te de Gbeŋgyoo seŋawɔ be wɔle te be yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia, na kawola nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan yɛɛ ampaŋ.

eng: Many people have already applied themselves to the task of compiling an account of the events that have been fulfilled among us.
local: Kegyia kpaakpaa Teyofilose, balaŋ burum te yɛɛ kakyeŋ ŋmarase nombia ŋan be kɔŋ de gyaŋ kɛbo lee Yesu Kristo wose man nɛ see.

eng: They used what the original eyewitnesses and servants of the word handed down to us.
local: Nombia ŋan bɔɔ ŋmarase nɛ, mena nombia ŋenaŋ ke te baŋ bɔɔ mo be sia naa ŋa leenaŋ kewalaŋ, te bɔɔ kolosi de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo wose man nombia fa daa nɛ yakowɔ.

eng: Now, after having investigated everything carefully from the beginning, I have also decided to write a carefully ordered account for you, most honorable Theophilus.
local: Kegyia Teyofilose, lee mena dɔɔ maŋ gbagba gyoo nombia kɛŋa man nideli leenaŋ ŋa kewalaŋ keŋ na, me naawɔ nyi kaboena nyi maa tɔlɛɛ ŋa kekɔŋ bii dokoloŋ dokoloŋ fa neŋ.

eng: I want you to have confidence in the soundness of the instruction you have received.
local: na n ke nyiŋ gyeŋ nyi nombia ŋan pou bɔɔ wola neŋ nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ.

eng: During the rule of King Herod of Judea there was a priest named Zechariah who belonged to the priestly division of Abijah. His wife Elizabeth was a descendant of Aaron.
local: Debaŋ keŋ gyoo Herode ne di gyoori Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ, na Wurubuarɛ sae ŋolo kyaa keŋ bɛɛ baake e nyi Sakaria. Na ɔ kpuɛ Abigya saere dikpii man, te ɔ wɛɛle Elisabɛte mɔ lee Wurubuarɛ sae Aarɔn duuluŋ man.

eng: They were both righteous before God, blameless in their observance of all the Lords commandments and regulations.
local: Sakaria na ɔ wɛɛle gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman. Te bee di de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ mmaraase na ɔ nombia ŋan pou dɔɔ nideli keŋ ba wose man be dana nombiikum daale ya.

eng: They had no children because Elizabeth was unable to become pregnant and they both were very old.
local: Mɔna bɔɔ taŋ bu ya nawolo nyi Elisabɛte yɛɛ ɔlamanbo e, te ŋon na ɔ baale Sakaria mɔ pou te ŋmɛɛ.

eng: One day Zechariah was serving as a priest before God because his priestly division was on duty.
local: Wee daale nɛ, kaa liiwɔ Sakaria saese dikpii keŋ dɔɔ nyi baa gyu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ke fa Wurubuarɛ kedi.

eng: Following the customs of priestly service, he was chosen by lottery to go into the Lords sanctuary and burn incense.
local: Be lɔɔ boe ŋgba mena keŋ ba amanberɛ na wolo nɛ, te yalawɔ Sakaria dɔɔ nyi waa gyu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ɔ kaa too oliifi.

eng: All the people who gathered to worship were praying outside during this hour of incense offering.
local: Debaŋ keŋ ɔ ba gyoo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ɔ kaa too oliifi nɛ, na balaŋ dikpii keŋ pou kyaa debɔɔ man bɛɛ fane.

eng: An angel from the Lord appeared to him, standing to the right of the altar of incense.
local: Sakaria kɛɛ too oliifi keŋ nɛ, botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ kpilale kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa seŋ kedeesa boe keŋ ɔ na too oliifi ke dɔɔ nɛ dunoluŋ dɔɔ.

eng: When Zechariah saw the angel, he was startled and overcome with fear.
local: Sakaria kena e nɛ, ɔ wɔe lɔɔ e te gyakoloŋ gyoo e.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Zechariah. Your prayers have been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will give birth to your son and you must name him John.
local: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon yako e nyiaa, Sakaria na yee ya, Wurubuarɛ te nyii n kefane mena dɔɔ n wɛɛle Elisabɛte waa lola bu baale fa neŋ, na n ka do e yele nyi Gyɔn.

eng: He will be a joy and delight to you, and many people will rejoice at his birth.
local: Ba lola bu ŋon na, gyae ke yɛɛ neŋ gyoŋ nideli te balaŋ burum mɔ sia gyae ka gye ɔ dɔɔ.

eng: for he will be great in the Lords eyes. He must not drink wine and liquor. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit even before his birth.
local: nawolo nyi bu ŋon waa yɛɛ walaŋ dinaa Wurubuarɛ siaman, mena dɔɔ ɔ na mo soloŋ na soloŋ daa ɔ nɔɔ ya. Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ feliŋ do ɔ man leenaŋ ɔ naa tɔɔman pɛna baa lola e.

eng: He will bring many Israelites back to the Lord their God.
local: Te waa kyɛɛkee Iseraetena balaŋ burum agyueŋ kɔŋ be Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

eng: He will go forth before the Lord, equipped with the spirit and power of Elijah. He will turn the hearts of fathers back to their children, and he will turn the disobedient to righteous patterns of thinking. He will make ready a people prepared for the Lord.
local: Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ feliŋ do ɔ man, na waa fa e doŋ ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be fa ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Elia nɛ. Waa ta de Gbeŋgyoo ŋon siaman na waa desina kyaɔ na be bia nsana, te balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ ne bu Wurubuarɛ ya nɛ, waa besena wɔ kɔŋ gbɛɛ kpaakpaate keŋ dɔɔ na waa desina de Gbeŋgyoo balaŋ see e.

eng: Zechariah said to the angel, How can I be sure of this? My wife and I are very old.
local: Botɔɔ te Sakaria bɔɔse Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon nyiaa, Sena te maa yɛɛ na maa naa nyi nombia kɛŋa yɛɛ ampaŋ? Nawolo nyi daa na me wɛɛle pou te ŋmɛɛ.

eng: The angel replied, I am Gabriel. I stand in Gods presence. I was sent to speak to you and to bring this good news to you.
local: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Maŋ yɛna Gaberɛle, Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon ma seŋɛɛ ɔ siaman debaŋ kamasɛ. Ŋon kpila na maŋ nyi me kaa yako neŋ mena nombia kpaakpaa kɛŋa.

eng: Know this: What I have spoken will come true at the proper time. But because you didnt believe, you will remain silent, unable to speak until the day when these things happen.
local: Nombia ŋan mɔɔ yako neŋ nɛ, gyae kekɔŋ ŋgba mena keŋ mɔɔ yako see nɛ. Mɔna keŋ n te lɛɛ me nombia kɛŋa di ya dɔɔ nɛ, n be gyae n ke tale kolosi ya kaboena nombia ŋan mɛɛ yako neŋ nɛ kekɔŋ ampaŋ debaŋ keŋ mɔɔ see neŋ nɛ.

eng: Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah, and they wondered why he was in the sanctuary for such a long time.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ pou man na, balaŋ baŋ kyaa debɔɔ man bɛɛ daa Sakaria, na bɛɛ gyueŋ kpene keŋ dɔɔ ɔ be dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man mena.

eng: When he came out, he was unable to speak to them. They realized he had seen a vision in the temple, for he gestured to them and couldnt speak.
local: Debaŋ keŋ ɔ ba lee kɔŋ balaŋ baŋ gyaŋ nɛ, ɔ te tale kolosi na wɔ ya, mena dɔɔ ba gyeŋawɔ nyi ɔ te naa kalesewola daale Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man. Ɔ nyiŋmaase te ɔ moowɔ kolosi na wɔ, nawolo nyi ɔ ta bese omumuu.

eng: When he completed the days of his priestly service, he returned home.
local: Debaŋ keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ ɔ saere tom keŋ taŋ nɛ, te ɔ besewɔ gyu ɔ dɛɛ.

eng: Afterward, his wife Elizabeth became pregnant. She kept to herself for five months, saying.
local: Nombia kɛŋa kekɔŋ wɔle nɛ, te kyare ya te ɔ wɛɛle Elisabɛte kyaŋa fulu, te ɔ weese ɔ wose gyaalaŋna banoŋ keŋ ɔ bɛɛ lee debɔɔ ya.

eng: This is the Lords doing. He has shown his favor to me by removing my disgrace among other people.
local: Keŋte ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ te wii me waraŋase ŋgba ɔ ba lese desɛŋ dinaa kei lee me dɔɔ balaŋ siaman nɛ.

eng: When Elizabeth was six months pregnant, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a city in Galilee.
local: Elisabɛte fulu keŋ ke di gyaalaŋna looro nɛ, Wurubuarɛ kpila ɔ kpilale Gaberɛle gyu Nasarɛte keŋ kyaa Galelia tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: to a virgin who was engaged to a man named Joseph, a descendant of Davids house. The virgins name was Mary.
local: Ɔ kpila e gyu alebu ŋolo ŋon ɔ te doo ta na baale ya nɛ gyaŋ. Ɔ yele yɛna Mɛɛre. Mɛɛre kei na Gyosɛfo ŋon ɔ ba lee gyoo Defidi duuluŋ man nɛ bake la see nyi baa yala dɔŋa nɛ.

eng: When the angel came to her, he said, Rejoice, favored one! The Lord is with you.
local: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon gyuuwɔ ke yako e nyiaa, Mɛɛre nyaŋ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ kpuɛ na neŋ, te ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa n dɔɔ nideli.

eng: She was confused by these words and wondered what kind of greeting this might be.
local: Mɛɛre kenyii nɔɔwoya kɛŋa nɛ, ɔ wose gbirisi e nawolo nyi ɔ te nyii nombia ŋan man nideli ya.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Mary. God is honoring you.
local: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon yako e nyiaa, Mɛɛre na yee ya, Wurubuarɛ ta mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele neŋ.

eng: Look! You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you will name him Jesus.
local: Nyaŋ ke kyaŋ fulu na n ka lola bu baale na n ka do e yele nyi Yesu.

eng: He will be great and he will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of David his father.
local: Waa yɛɛ walaŋ dinaa Wurubuarɛ siaman, na baa baake e nyi Wurubuarɛ Kpatakpere ŋon bu. De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ waa yela waa di gyoori ŋgba ɔ naana Defidi nɛ.

eng: He will rule over Jacobs house forever, and there will be no end to his kingdom.
local: Waa di gyoori Gyekɔpo duuluŋ man balaŋ dɔɔ kekpaakekpaa, te ɔ gyoori kenaŋ betaŋ ɔto ya.

eng: Then Mary said to the angel, How will this happen since I havent had sexual relations with a man.
local: Botɔɔ te Mɛɛre bɔɔse Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon nyiaa, Sena te nombia kɛŋa gyae ke tale kɔŋ mena, debaŋ keŋ mɔɔ gyeŋ baale na baale ya.

eng: The angel replied, The Holy Spirit will come over you and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore, the one who is to be born will be holy. He will be called Gods Son.
local: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon gyae kekɔŋ kaa gyoo n man, na Wurubuarɛ Kpatakpere ŋon ka mo ɔ doŋ keŋ do n man na n ke kyaŋ fulu. Mena dɔɔ bu ŋon nyaŋ ka lola nɛ, waa yɛɛ bu ŋon ayimɛɛ be doo ɔ man ya, te baa baake e nyi Wurubuarɛ bu.

eng: Look, even in her old age, your relative Elizabeth has conceived a son. This woman who was labeled unable to conceive is now six months pregnant.
local: Kɛɛ! N loŋto Elisabɛte ŋon be kpa ɔ yɛɛ ɔlamanbo e te ɔ be ŋmɛɛ koraŋ mɔ nɛ, te kyaŋ fulu gyaalaŋna looro yaa.

eng: Nothing is impossible for God.
local: Nawolo nyi kpene kamasɛ be kyaa keŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ be gyae waa tale yɛɛ ya.

eng: Then Mary said, I am the Lords servant. Let it be with me just as you have said. Then the angel left her.
local: Keŋte Mɛɛre yakowɔ nyiaa, Ma nyee tesɛɛ bo na Wurubuarɛ. Ŋan pou n be yako maŋ nɛ dɛɛkɔŋ mena. Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon laŋawɔ yela e.

eng: Mary got up and hurried to a city in the Judean highlands.
local: Weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale wɔle nɛ, Mɛɛre desina ɔ wose te ɔ gyuu Sakaria na ɔ wɛɛle Elisabɛte gyaŋ Gyudia tɛɛle man. Na donɔɔ keŋ man be kyaa nɛ, gyakae bulase dɔɔ.

eng: She entered Zechariahs home and greeted Elizabeth.
local: Ɔ ka gyoo donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ Sakaria dɛɛ te ɔ doo Elisabɛte nɔɔ.

eng: When Elizabeth heard Marys greeting, the child leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Debaŋ keŋ Elisabɛte be nyii Mɛɛre nɔɔ kado keŋ nɛ, ɔ bu ŋon wosi ɔ wose ɔ tɔɔman, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon kaa gyoowɔ Elisabɛte man nideli.

eng: With a loud voice she blurted out, God has blessed you above all women, and he has blessed the child you carry.
local: Keŋte Elisabɛte nyaŋeewɔ fae yeesa nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna alo ŋon Wurubuarɛ ba mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa n dɔɔ nideli ala pou man, te ɔ ta mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa n bu ŋon nyaŋ ka lola nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: Why do I have this honor, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?.
local: Woŋ nombia dinaana yaa Wurubuarɛ yɛɛwɔ fa maŋ nɛ, nyi me Gbeŋgyoo ɔ naa waa kɔŋ kaa do maŋ nɔɔ?.

eng: As soon as I heard your greeting, the baby in my womb jumped for joy.
local: Nawolo nyi me ka betaa nyii n nɔɔ kado pɛ, te me bu ŋon ɔ doo me tɔɔman nɛ nyaŋeewɔ wosi ɔ wose.

eng: Happy is she who believed that the Lord would fulfill the promises he made to her.
local: Wurubuarɛ te hiraa neŋ, ŋgba n be lɛɛdi nyi de Gbeŋgyoo nombia ŋan ɔ be yako nɛ, gyae kekɔŋ mena fa neŋ nɛ.

eng: Mary said, With all my heart I glorify the Lord!.
local: Keŋte Mɛɛre yakowɔ nyiaa, Mɛɛ lese de Gbeŋgyoo yele lee me konɔɔ man.

eng: In the depths of who I am I rejoice in God my savior.
local: Wurubuarɛ yɛna ma nyeelɛɛre, mena dɔɔ te mɛɛ nyaŋee.

eng: He has looked with favor on the low status of his servant. Look! From now on, everyone will consider me highly favored.
local: nawolo nyi ɔ te tɔɔse maŋ wa alebu kei dinɔɔ, ŋon nafɔ bɔɔ ne kala maŋ do balaŋ man ya nɛ. Lee nɛnɛɛ ne gyuu nɛ, walaŋ kamasɛ waa baake maŋ nyi, alo ŋon Wurubuarɛ be hiraa e.

eng: because the mighty one has done great things for me. Holy is his name.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ Kpatakpere ŋon te yɛɛ nombia dinaana fa maŋ. Ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ŋon ayimɛɛ be doo ɔ man ya.

eng: He shows mercy to everyone, from one generation to the next, who honors him as God.
local: Deniwalaŋ duuluŋ kamasɛ man nɛ, ɔ ne wii baŋ pou bee yee e nɛ waraŋase.

eng: He has shown strength with his arm. He has scattered those with arrogant thoughts and proud inclinations.
local: Ɔ ta mo ɔ doŋ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia, te ɔ yaasee balaŋ baŋ bee gyina ba wose nɛ na be nombia ŋan pou bɛɛ gyueŋ ba nyeeya man nɛ lee ɔ siaman.

eng: He has pulled the powerful down from their thrones and lifted up the lowly.
local: Ɔ te yɔkɔse gyooneŋ dinaana tɛɛle man, te ɔ gyinaa balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɔkɔse ba wose nɛ adido.

eng: He has filled the hungry with good things and sent the rich away empty-handed.
local: Ɔ te yeli baŋ tanam na ko wɔ nɛ te nyiŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa, te ɔ yela baŋ be dana kɔba nɛ laŋawɔ nyiŋmaase kufu nawɔɔ kufu.

eng: He has come to the aid of his servant Israel, remembering his mercy.
local: Ɔ te di ntam keŋ ɔ be kaŋ fa de naanaɔ nɛ dɔɔ, te ɔ kɔŋawɔ kaa kyɔ ɔ tewulɛɛ Iseraetena.

eng: just as he promised to our ancestors, to Abraham and to Abrahams descendants forever.
local: ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yako see nyi, waa wii Aberaham na ɔ duuluŋ man balaŋ pou waraŋase kekpaakekpaa nɛ.

eng: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months, and then returned to her home.
local: Mɛɛre kyaawɔ Elisabɛte gyaŋ ŋgba gyaalaŋna batooro nɛ, te ɔ besewɔ gyu ɔ dɛɛ.

eng: When the time came for Elizabeth to have her child, she gave birth to a boy.
local: Debaŋ keŋ Elisabɛte kalola be lii nɛ, ɔ lola bu baale.

eng: Her neighbors and relatives celebrated with her because they had heard that the Lord had shown her great mercy.
local: Balaŋ baŋ be kyaa benaa na e, na ɔ loŋtona nyii mena keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo be wii ɔ waraŋase nideli nɛ, be popou nyaŋeewɔ kpu na e.

eng: On the eighth day, it came time to circumcise the child. They wanted to name him Zechariah because that was his fathers name.
local: Bu ŋon kedi weeya gyanaara nɛ, be kɔŋawɔ be kaa kara ɔ baala wose na baa mo ɔ kya Sakaria yele do e.

eng: But his mother replied, No, his name will be John.
local: mɔna ɔ naa bɛɛwɔ te ɔ kpa Baa baake e nyi Gyɔn.

eng: They said to her, None of your relatives have that name.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ yako e nyiaa, Yele kei te yɛɛ n dekpaŋalaŋ man yele ya.

eng: Then they began gesturing to his father to see what he wanted to call him.
local: Botɔɔ te ba moo be nyiŋmaase kolosi na ɔ kya bɔɔse e yele keŋ ɔ ne gyae nyi baa mo do ɔ bu ŋon.

eng: After asking for a tablet, he surprised everyone by writing, His name is John.
