local: Keŋte Sakaria yako wɔ nyiaa baa fa e kolo waa ŋmarase ke dɔɔ, te ɔ ŋmarasewɔ nyi, Ɔ yele yɛna Gyɔn. Nombii kei yɛɛ be popou gyakoloŋ.

eng: At that moment, Zechariah was able to speak again, and he began praising God.
local: Debokenaŋ man te Sakaria nɔɔ gyuusuwɔ te ɔ fiasɛɛ kolosi lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

eng: All their neighbors were filled with awe, and everyone throughout the Judean highlands talked about what had happened.
local: Keŋte gyakoloŋ gyoo be tebia baŋ pou, te nombia ŋenaŋ yaaseewɔ gyu Gyudia tɛɛle man donɔɔse ŋan ŋe kyaa bulase dɔɔ nɛ pou man.

eng: All who heard about this considered it carefully. They said, What then will this child be? Indeed, the Lords power was with him.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ be nyii nombii kei nɛ bɔɔse ɔ wose nyiaa, Walaŋ woŋti dinɔɔ yaa bu kei waa kaa yɛɛ baa? Nawolo nyi be naawɔ nyi, de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ nideli.

eng: Johns father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied.
local: Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do Gyɔn ɔ kya Sakaria man, te ɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia see nyiaa.

eng: Bless the Lord God of Israel because he has come to help and has delivered his people.
local: De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ, dɛɛ lese n yele nawolo nyi n te kɔŋ kaa lɛɛ n balaŋ Iseraetena nyee.

eng: He has raised up a mighty savior for us in his servant Davids house.
local: N te fa daa nyeelɛɛre dinaa ŋolo, ŋon ɔ ba lee n tobaale Defidi duuluŋ man.

eng: just as he said through the mouths of his holy prophets long ago.
local: Ŋgba mena keŋ n be tɛɛ nya akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ ayimɛɛ be doo be man ya nɛ dɔɔ kolosi see nɛ nyiaa.

eng: He has brought salvation from our enemies and from the power of all those who hate us.
local: waa lɛɛ daa lee de kɔlala, na baŋ bɔɔ ne naa de sia laŋ ya nɛ nyiŋmaa man.

eng: He has shown the mercy promised to our ancestors, and remembered his holy covenant.
local: N yakowɔ nyi nyaŋ ke wii de naanaɔ waraŋase, na n ke tɔɔse nɔɔkebakesee keŋ ɛmɛɛ na wɔ be bake see nɛ.

eng: the solemn pledge he made to our ancestor Abraham. He has granted.
local: N kaŋa ntam fa de naana Aberaham, te ɛmɛɛ na e bakewɔ see nyi.

eng: that we would be rescued from the power of our enemies so that we could serve him without fear.
local: n ka lese daa lee de kɔlala nyiŋmaase man, na ke yela dɛɛ tale gyɔ de konɔɔ som neŋ keŋ dɔɔ yee gyakoloŋ ya.

eng: in holiness and righteousness in Gods eyes, for as long as we live.
local: na dɛɛ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ n siaman, na baŋ ba wose betaŋ ayimɛɛ ya kelii debaŋ keŋ dɛɛ yeŋ nɛ.

eng: You, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High, for you will go before the Lord to prepare his way.
local: Sakaria ke yako kɛŋa pou taŋ nɛ te ɔ kolosiwɔ fa bu ŋon nyiaa, nɛnɛɛ nyaŋ me bu Gyɔn, baa baake neŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ Kpatakpere ŋon dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre, nawolo nyi nyaŋ keta de Gbeŋgyoo ŋon siaman na n ke desina ɔ gbɛɛneŋ fa e.

eng: You will tell his people how to be saved through the forgiveness of their sins.
local: Na n ke yela ɔ balaŋ ka gyeŋ nyi ba sola Wurubuarɛ te ɔ mo be nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ na, baa nyiŋ nyeekelɛɛ.

eng: Because of our Gods deep compassion, the dawn from heaven will break upon us.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ waraŋasete e, te ɔ te kpila nyeelɛɛre ŋon nyi waa kɔŋ kaa lɛɛ da nyee. Ɔ kekɔŋ gyae ke yɛɛ ŋgba keŋmaŋee nɛ.

eng: to give light to those who are sitting in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide us on the path of peace.
local: keŋmaŋee kei gyae ka lee adido kɔŋ baŋ pou be kyaa ditiŋtɛɛ man na yeŋ gbɛɛneŋ man nɛ dɔɔ, na kawola daa gbɛɛ kpaakpaa keŋ dɛɛ ta wosefɛɛreŋ man.

eng: The child grew up, becoming strong in character. He was in the wilderness until he began his public ministry to Israel.
local: Keŋte bu ŋon beewɔ te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do ɔ man te ɔ nyiŋa doŋ. Ɔ kyaawɔ depampaa dinaa man kaboena wee keŋ ɔ ba lese ɔ wose debɔɔ wola Iseraetena nɛ liiwɔ.

eng: In those days Caesar Augustus declared that everyone throughout the empire should be enrolled in the tax lists.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ man te Roman gyoo dinaa ŋon bɛɛ baake e Agustuse nɛ, faa mmaraa nyi baa kala balaŋ baŋ pou be kyaa ɔ tɛɛle man nɛ.

eng: This first enrollment occurred when Quirinius governed Syria.
local: Kei yɛna balaŋ kekala gyaŋgbate, debaŋ keŋ Kireniuse kyaa Romantena gyoo dinaa nawɔɔ man Siria tɛɛle man nɛ.

eng: Everyone went to their own cities to be enrolled.
local: Lee mmaraa keŋ gyoo ŋon be faa dɔɔ, walaŋ kamasɛ gyuu ɔ dɛɛ ɔ kaa ŋmarase ɔ yele.

eng: Since Joseph belonged to Davids house and family line, he went up from the city of Nazareth in Galilee to Davids city, called Bethlehem, in Judea.
local: Keŋte Gyosɛfo mɔ lee Nasarɛte keŋ kyaa Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ, gyu Bɛtelɛhɛm keŋ ke kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ. Bɛtelɛhɛm donɔɔ kei man te ba lola gyoo Defidi. Gyosɛfo gyuu botɔɔ nawolo nyi ɔ lee gyoo Defidi duuluŋ man.

eng: He went to be enrolled together with Mary, who was promised to him in marriage and who was pregnant.
local: Ŋon na Mɛɛre ŋon, ŋon na e be yako see nyi baa yala dɔŋa nɛ, gyuu la be kaa ŋmarase ba yela. Debaŋ kenaŋ man na Mɛɛre dana fulu.

eng: While they were there, the time came for Mary to have her baby.
local: Ba kekyaa Bɛtelɛhɛm botɔɔ nɛ te kadoŋee kyaŋa Mɛɛre.

eng: She gave birth to her firstborn child, a son, wrapped him snugly, and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the guestroom.
local: Keŋ be te nyiŋ deni ŋɔɔla dasoe keŋ man ya dɔɔ, be gyue ka gyoo wonembia dedoi man. Botɔɔ te ɔ lola ɔ bu baale gyaŋgbate te ɔ mo kakyeŋgbelaase milii e mo e doosi adakaa keŋ wonembia ne dii ke man nɛ man.

eng: Nearby shepherds were living in the fields, guarding their sheep at night.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ man na, namensekɛɛlera baale kyaa ŋale man benaa na botɔɔ yenaŋ bee gyee be namense nelim kenaŋ.

eng: The Lords angel stood before them, the Lords glory shone around them, and they were terrified.
local: Botɔɔ te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo kaa bɛɛwɔ lee be dɔɔ te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ ŋalakeewɔ kilisi wɔ, keŋte gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid! Look! I bring good news to you—wonderful, joyous news for all people.
local: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ naa yee ya! Ɛ kɛɛ! Me ta mo nombia kpaakpaa ŋaale mɛɛ kɔna ŋon. Nombia kɛŋa gyae ke yɛɛ balaŋ pou gyoŋ.

eng: Your savior is born today in Davids city. He is Christ the Lord.
local: Nawolo nyi gyɛŋ wee kei nɛ, be ta lola ɛ nyeelɛɛre fa ŋon gyoo Defidi donɔɔ Bɛtelɛhɛm man. Ŋon yɛna de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo, .

eng: This is a sign for you: you will find a newborn baby wrapped snugly and lying in a manger.
local: Kpene keŋ gyae kawola nyi nombia ŋan mɛɛ yako nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ yɛna nyi, ɛ kena kyokyoo ŋon doo adakaa keŋ wonembia ne dii ke man nɛ man, na be ta mo kakyeŋgbelaase milii e.

eng: Suddenly a great assembly of the heavenly forces was with the angel praising God. They said.
local: Debokenaŋ man te Wurubuarɛ kpilala burum baale kɔŋawɔ kaa kpu na kpilale gyaŋgbate ŋon, te be kpee nɔɔneŋ lese Wurubuarɛ yele nyiaa.

eng: Glory to God in heaven, and on earth peace among those whom he favors.
local: Wurubuarɛ ŋon n kyaa adido, n gyoorobiiri kaboena neŋ. N wosefɛɛreŋ dɛɛkɔŋ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ gyoo n sia tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ man.

eng: When the angels returned to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, Lets go right now to Bethlehem and see whats happened. Lets confirm what the Lord has revealed to us.
local: Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ ka bese lee be gyaŋ gyu adido nɛ, namensekɛɛlera baŋ yako dɔŋa nyiaa, Ɛ fa dee gyu Bɛtelɛhɛm, na de kaa kɛɛ kpene keŋ be kɔŋ te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ba lese wola daa nɛ.

eng: They went quickly and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby lying in the manger.
local: Mena dɔɔ be yɛɛwɔ bileŋ te be gyuuwɔ te ba ke naa Mɛɛre na Gyosɛfo na kyokyoo ŋon, na ɔ doo adakaa keŋ wonembia ne dii ke man nɛ man.

eng: When they saw this, they reported what they had been told about this child.
local: Namensekɛɛlera baŋ kena kyokyoo ŋon nɛ, be yako wɔ mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon be kolosi kyokyoo ŋon wose man nombia fa wɔ nɛ.

eng: Everyone who heard it was amazed at what the shepherds told them.
local: Nombia ŋan namensekɛɛlera baŋ be yako nɛ, yela balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ nyii ŋa nɛ wose gbirisi wɔ.

eng: Mary committed these things to memory and considered them carefully.
local: Mɔna Mɛɛre kyaŋa nombia kɛŋa pou do ɔ konɔɔ man, na ɔ na gyueŋ ŋe man nideli.

eng: The shepherds returned home, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen. Everything happened just as they had been told.
local: Namensekɛɛlera baŋ besewɔ laŋ, na bee kpee nɔɔneŋ lese Wurubuarɛ yele lee ŋan pou bɔɔ nyii te bɔɔ naa nɛ dɔɔ, nawolo nyi nombia ŋan pou bɔɔ nyii te bɔɔ naa nɛ, kyaawɔ na ŋan Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon be yako wɔ nɛ.

eng: When eight days had passed, Jesus parents circumcised him and gave him the name Jesus. This was the name given to him by the angel before he was conceived.
local: Weeya gyanaarate keŋ kelii nyi baa kara bu ŋon baala wose nɛ, ba doo e yele nyi Yesu. Mena yele kei te Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon yakowɔ nyi baa baake e pɛte Mɛɛre kyaŋa ɔ fulu.

eng: When the time came for their ritual cleansing, in accordance with the Law from Moses, they brought Jesus up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord. .
local: Debaŋ keŋ ba ayimɛɛ kegberaa weeya ŋan be gyaŋee ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ɔ be yeli Mosesi be ŋmarase see nawolo nɛ, Gyosɛfo na Mɛɛre moo Yesu gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man be kaa do de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ nyiŋmaa man.

eng: Its written in the Law of the Lord, Every firstborn male will be dedicated to the Lord.
local: nawolo nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ɔ be yeli Mosesi be ŋmarase see nɛ ne yako nyiaa, Bu baale kamasɛ ŋon ɔ naa waa taŋgbɛɛ lola na, baa mo e ka do de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ nyiŋmaa man.

eng: They offered a sacrifice in keeping with whats stated in the Law of the Lord, A pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons .
local: Keŋte Gyosɛfo na Mɛɛre gyuuwɔ ke deesi fa Wurubuarɛ ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ɔ be yeli Mosesi be ŋmarase see ne yako nɛ nyiaa, Baa mo donɔɔ man yaa ŋale man awuluŋna bala deesi nɛ.

eng: A man named Simeon was in Jerusalem. He was righteous and devout. He eagerly anticipated the restoration of Israel, and the Holy Spirit rested on him.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na balee ŋolo kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man keŋ ɔ yele yɛna Simeɔn. Ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman te ɔ ne yee Wurubuarɛ. Wurubuarɛ feliŋ doo ɔ man te ɔ ne daa wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa kaa lɛɛ Iseraetena nyee nɛ.

eng: The Holy Spirit revealed to him that he wouldnt die before he had seen the Lords Christ.
local: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon lese la wola e nyi, waa naa nyeelɛɛre ŋon de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ pɛna ŋon Simeɔn ka yeŋ.

eng: Led by the Spirit, he went into the temple area. Meanwhile, Jesus parents brought the child to the temple so that they could do what was customary under the Law.
local: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon yela Simeɔn gyuu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man. Gyosɛfo na Mɛɛre ka mo be bu Yesu ŋon kɔŋ be kaa do de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ nyiŋmaa man, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ne yako nɛ.

eng: Simeon took Jesus in his arms and praised God. He said.
local: te Simeɔn tee ɔ nyiŋmaase lɛɛ bu ŋon te ɔ lese Wurubuarɛ yele nyiaa.

eng: Now, master, let your servant go in peace according to your word.
local: De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ, nombii keŋ n be yako see nɛ n te di ke dɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ nyaŋ ke tale lɔ n tobaale gbɛɛ wosefɛɛreŋ man.

eng: because my eyes have seen your salvation.
local: Nawolo nyi maŋ gbagba ta mo me sibii naa nyeelɛɛre ŋon n be kpili nɛ.

eng: You prepared this salvation in the presence of all peoples.
local: n kpila e nyi waa kɔŋ na balaŋ pou kena e.

eng: Its a light for revelation to the Gentiles and a glory for your people Israel.
local: Ŋon yɛna keŋmaŋee keŋ gyae ke yela baŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ, kena n gbɛɛneŋ na baa lese n balaŋ Iseraetena yele.

eng: His father and mother were amazed by what was said about him.
local: Simeɔn nɔɔwoya ŋan ɔ be yako lee be bu ŋon wose man nɛ, doo bu ŋon ɔ kya na ɔ naa nɔɔ.

eng: Simeon blessed them and said to Mary his mother, This boy is assigned to be the cause of the falling and rising of many in Israel and to be a sign that generates opposition.
local: Keŋte Simeɔn fanewɔ fa wɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ waa hiraa wɔ te ɔ yako bu ŋon ɔ naa Mɛɛre nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ lese na bu kei. Iseraetena burum baŋ baa bɛɛ e na baa naa diyem, te baŋ mɔ baa lɛɛ e di na baa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ. Bu kei waa yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ gbagba tobaale, mɔna balaŋ baa koro seŋ tia e.

eng: so that the inner thoughts of many will be revealed. And a sword will pierce your innermost being too.
local: na ka lese ŋan balaŋ burum na gyueŋ nɛ pou debɔɔ. Te nyaŋ Mɛɛre n kena diyem ŋgba sila te ba moowɔ sɔɔ neŋ n konɔɔ man nɛ.

eng: There was also a prophet, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, who belonged to the tribe of Asher. She was very old. After she married, she lived with her husband for seven years.
local: Alo kegyia ŋolo kyaawɔ ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Ana. Ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e te ɔ kya yele yɛna Fanuwɛli. Fanuwɛli kei lee Asa dekpaŋalaŋ man. Ana kei yalawɔ te ŋon na ɔ baale kyaawɔ kulutooneŋ nyetooro, te ɔ baale yeŋawɔ yela e.

eng: She was now an -year-old widow. She never left the temple area but worshipped God with fasting and prayer night and day.
local: Te ɔ kyaawɔ lialo man kulutooneŋ sɔŋonaara na anaara . Nelim na ka weese pou na ɔ kyae Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ɔ ne bake ɔ nɔɔ fane.

eng: She approached at that very moment and began to praise God and to speak about Jesus to everyone who was looking forward to the redemption of Jerusalem.
local: Simeɔn kɛɛ kolosi nɛ, te ŋon Ana mɔ kɔŋawɔ. Ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ, te ɔ kolosiwɔ lee bu ŋon wose man fa balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ daa nyeelɛɛre ŋon waa kɔŋ kaa lese Gyerusalɛmtena lee ba diyem man nɛ.

eng: When Mary and Joseph had completed everything required by the Law of the Lord, they returned to their hometown, Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Gyosɛfo na Mɛɛre keyɛɛ kpene kamasɛ taŋ ŋgba mena keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan nawolo nɛ, ba besewɔ gyu be dɛɛ Nasarɛte keŋ kyaa Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ.

eng: The child grew up and became strong. He was filled with wisdom, and Gods favor was on him.
local: Bu ŋon beewɔ yɛɛ doŋ te ɔ sia karawɔ nideli, te Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele e.

eng: Each year his parents went to Jerusalem for the Passover Festival.
local: Kulutoo kamasɛ nɛ, Yesu ɔ kya na ɔ naa ne gyu Gyerusalɛm ke di ba nyeekelɛɛwee dinaa keŋ.

eng: When he was years old, they went up to Jerusalem according to their custom.
local: Yesu ke di kulutooneŋ kufu ala nɛ, be gyuu Gyerusalɛm be kaa di ba nyeekelɛɛwee dinaa kei ŋgba mena keŋ bɛɛ taŋ gyu nɛ.

eng: After the festival was over, they were returning home, but the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. His parents didnt know it.
local: Be ke di wee keŋ taŋ bese bee gyu nɛ, Yesu dɔɔwɔ Gyerusalɛm botɔɔ mɔna ɔ kya na ɔ naa ta gyem ya.

eng: Supposing that he was among their band of travelers, they journeyed on for a full day while looking for him among their family and friends.
local: Na baŋ gyeŋ nyi ɔ kpuɛ na be balaŋ dikpii keŋ mena dɔɔ be tɛɛwɔ wee debiliŋ pou, pɛte ba gyoo e kekɛo be tebia na ba loŋtona man.

eng: When they didnt find Jesus, they returned to Jerusalem to look for him.
local: Keŋ ba te naa e ya dɔɔ, ba besewɔ gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man be kaa kɛo e.

eng: After three days they found him in the temple. He was sitting among the teachers, listening to them and putting questions to them.
local: Ka weeya atoorote te ba ke naa e Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man na ŋon na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ kyaa la. Ɔ ne tei be nombia, na ŋon mɔ ne bɔɔse wɔ nombia.

eng: Everyone who heard him was amazed by his understanding and his answers.
local: Mena keŋ ɔ ne nyii nombia ŋan asɛɛ na mena keŋ ɔ ne tira ŋe nɔɔ fa wɔ nɛ, doo baŋ pou be tei nɛ nɔɔ.

eng: When his parents saw him, they were shocked. His mother said, Child, why have you treated us like this? Listen! Your father and I have been worried. Weve been looking for you.
local: Ɔ kya na ɔ naa ke gyu kena e botɔɔ nɛ, ba wose gbirisi wɔ. Keŋte ɔ naa bɔɔse e nyiaa, Me bu, weera te n yɛɛ daa mena? Kɛɛ, daa na n kya sia te yɛɛ, de dɔŋɛɛ dɛɛ kɛo neŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Why were you looking for me? Didnt you know that it was necessary for me to be in my Fathers house.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne kɛo maŋ? Ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi kaboena nyi maa kyaa me kya dɛɛ yee.

eng: But they didnt understand what he said to them.
local: Mɔna ɔ kya na ɔ naa te nyii kpene keŋ ɔ be yako wɔ nɛ asɛɛ ya.

eng: Jesus went down to Nazareth with them and was obedient to them. His mother cherished every word in her heart.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu korowɔ sila wɔ gyu Nasarɛte donɔɔ man, te ɔ nyii ɔ kya na ɔ naa dei. Ɔ naa moo nombia kɛŋa pou do ɔ konɔɔ man, na ɔ na gyueŋ ŋe man nideli.

eng: Jesus matured in wisdom and years, and in favor with God and with people.
local: Yesu beewɔ nideli te ɔ sia karawɔ kpu, te ɔ gyoo Wurubuarɛ na balaŋ pou sia.

eng: Jesus returned from the Jordan River full of the Holy Spirit, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness.
local: Yesu ka lee Gyɔɔdan boo keŋ man nɛ, na Wurubuarɛ feliŋ doo ɔ man nideli. Te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon yela Yesu gyuu depampaa dinaa daale man.

eng: There he was tempted for forty days by the devil. He ate nothing during those days and afterward Jesus was starving.
local: botɔɔ te ɔbɔnsam kaa kɛɛse e weeya sɔŋola. Weeya ŋenaŋ man pou na ɔ ta mo kolo daa ɔ nɔɔ ya. Te weeya ŋenaŋ kesi nɛ, tanam kyaŋa e nideli.

eng: The devil said to him, Since you are Gods Son, command this stone to become a loaf of bread.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔbɔnsam yako e nyiaa, Akpaa nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon na, yela boe kei ka bese weenɛɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread .
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako daa nyiaa, na weenɛɛ kaageŋ ne gyae la ke fa walaŋ nyeedoŋ ya.

eng: Next the devil led him to a high place and showed him in a single instant all the kingdoms of the world.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔbɔnsam moo Yesu gyu bula dinaa daale nyee dɔɔ, te ɔ lese tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyoorobiiri pou wola e karatetee dokoloŋ.

eng: The devil said, I will give you this whole domain and the glory of all these kingdoms. Its been entrusted to me and I can give it to anyone I want.
local: Keŋte ɔ yako Yesu nyiaa, Maa mo gyoorobiiri na abɔɔ nya anaŋ kɛŋa pou doŋ do n nyiŋmaa man na n ke di ŋe dɔɔ. Nawolo nyi be ta mo ŋe popou fa maŋ, mena dɔɔ maa tale mo ŋa fa walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon mɛɛ gyae.

eng: Therefore, if you will worship me, it will all be yours.
local: Mena dɔɔ nyi akpaa n buŋ me nawɔɔtɛɛ som maŋ na, abɔɔ kɛŋa pou gyae ka bese n wui.

eng: Jesus answered, Its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him. .
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako daa nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ waageŋ te kaboena nyi nya buŋ na n ka som e.

eng: The devil brought him into Jerusalem and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him, Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down from here.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔbɔnsam moo Yesu gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man te ɔ mo e gyu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ nyee man adido adido te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Akpaa nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon na, fuŋ n wose lee kɛbo ke lɔ tɛɛle man.

eng: for its written: He will command his angels concerning you, to protect you.
local: nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako daa nyi, Nyaŋ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ waa kpila ɔ kpilala na be kaa gyee neŋ nideli.

eng: and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: na baa kɔlee neŋ keŋ nyi, n be gyae n ka mo n nae gyɔ na boe koraŋ gba ya. .

eng: Jesus answered, Its been said, Dont test the Lord your God. .
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see na bese yako daa nyiaa, Na kɛɛse n Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ŋon kɛɛ ya. .

eng: After finishing every temptation, the devil departed from him until the next opportunity.
local: Debaŋ keŋ ɔbɔnsam be kɛɛse e kina taŋ nɛ, ɔ tinaa Yesu yela pɛ kelii debaŋ keŋ waa bese nyiŋ gbɛɛ wɔle bela.

eng: Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit to Galilee, and news about him spread throughout the whole countryside.
local: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon yela Yesu besewɔ gyu Galelia tɛɛle man, te ɔ wose man nombia yaaseewɔ tɛɛle kenaŋ pou dɔɔ.

eng: He taught in their synagogues and was praised by everyone.
local: Yesu tɛɛwɔ wola Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man, te walaŋ kamasɛ kɛlɛɛ e.

eng: Jesus went to Nazareth, where he had been raised. On the Sabbath he went to the synagogue as he normally did and stood up to read.
local: Yesu gyuuwɔ Nasarɛte botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ kɛɛle e nɛ, te Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee kelii nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ Gyudatena ɔsom deni man ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ ne taŋ yɛɛ nɛ. Keŋte ɔ korowɔ seŋ nyi waa kala Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan.

eng: The synagogue assistant gave him the scroll from the prophet Isaiah. He unrolled the scroll and found the place where it was written.
local: botɔɔ te ba moo Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya tɔne fa e. Ɔ gyuusu tɔne keŋ te ɔ kala botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ ŋmarase nyiaa.

eng: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because the Lord has anointed me . He has sent me to preach good news to the poor, to proclaim release to the prisoners and recovery of sight to the blind, to liberate the oppressed.
local: De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ feliŋ doo me man, nawolo nyi ɔ ta lese maŋ see nyi maa kolosi ɔ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa ayematena. Ɔ te kpila maŋ nyi maa yako yoo gbeŋa nyi Wurubuarɛ waa lese wɔ lee be gbeŋiri man, na maa yako siayɛlɛɛsatena nyi be sia gyae ke gyuusu, na baŋ mɔ bee wuritii wɔ nɛ ke nyiŋ ba wose.

eng: and to proclaim the year of the Lords favor.
local: na maa yela balaŋ pou ka gyeŋ nyi, debaŋ keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ waa kaa lɛɛ ɔ balaŋ nyee nɛ ta benaa.

eng: He rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the synagogue assistant, and sat down. Every eye in the synagogue was fixed on him.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔ tɔne keŋ te ɔ besena ke fa tobaale ŋon ɔ ba mo ke fa e nɛ, te ɔ tisiwɔ kyaa. Balaŋ baŋ pou be kyaa bɔ ɔsom deni keŋ man nɛ, fɛɛ be sia bɛɛ kɛɛ e nɛ fee.

eng: He began to explain to them, Today, this scripture has been fulfilled just as you heard it.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Gyɛŋ wee kei nɛ, Wurubuarɛ nombia ma akalaŋ kɛŋa te kɔŋ mena ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ siaman ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ be nyii mɔɔ kale nɛ.

eng: Everyone was raving about Jesus, so impressed were they by the gracious words flowing from his lips. They said, This is Josephs son, isnt it.
local: Nombia kpaakpaa ŋan Yesu be yako nɛ, doo balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ gyaŋee botɔɔ nɛ nɔɔ te be kɛlɛɛ e. Keŋte be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Na Gyosɛfo bu ŋon yaa yee.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Undoubtedly, you will quote this saying to me: Doctor, heal yourself. Do here in your hometown what weve heard you did in Capernaum.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ma gyeŋ nyi ɛ ke gyɔ maŋ dudu kei nyi Nyaŋ ŋon n ne kyɔ balaŋ nɛ, kyɔ n wose na dɛɛ kɛɛ. Te ɛ ka bese yako maŋ nyi, Gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan dɔɔ nyii n be yɛɛ Kapanum donɔɔ man nɛ, yɛɛ ŋa nyaŋ gbagba n donɔɔ man kɛbo mɔ na dɛɛ kɛɛ. .

eng: He said, I assure you that no prophet is welcome in the prophets hometown.
local: Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ kpu nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋolo be kyaa keŋ bɛɛ lɛɛ e ŋon gbagba ɔ dɛɛ ya.

eng: And I can assure you that there were many widows in Israel during Elijahs time, when it didnt rain for three and a half years and there was a great food shortage in the land.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi, debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Elia be kyaa nɛ, na liala burum kyaa Iserae tɛɛle dɔɔ, mɔna debaŋ keŋ ɔga ba sem kulutooneŋ atooro na yaale, te tanam dinaa be yale tɛɛle keŋ pou dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: Yet Elijah was sent to none of them but only to a widow in the city of Zarephath in the region of Sidon.
local: Wurubuarɛ te kpila Elia nyi waa gyu ke kyɔ be man ŋolo ya. Ɔ kpila e bo gyu lialo ŋon ɔ te yɛɛ Iserae alo e ya, te ɔ kyaa Sarefate donɔɔ keŋ kyaa Sidɔn tɛɛle man nɛ gyaŋ.

eng: There were also many persons with skin diseases in Israel during the time of the prophet Elisha, but none of them were cleansed. Instead, Naaman the Syrian was cleansed.
local: Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Elisa mɔ ke kyaa nɛ, na akuŋtuŋtena burum kyaa Iserae tɛɛle dɔɔ mɔna Wurubuarɛ te kyɔ be man ŋolo ya. Siria baale Naaman ŋon waageŋ te ɔ kyɔɔwɔ.

eng: When they heard this, everyone in the synagogue was filled with anger.
local: Balaŋ baŋ pou be kyaa Gyudatena ɔsom deni keŋ man kenyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ba gyeŋa baŋ nideli.

eng: They rose up and ran him out of town. They led him to the crest of the hill on which their town had been built so that they could throw him off the cliff.
local: Botɔɔ te balaŋ baŋ korowɔ gyu ke kyaŋ Yesu wɔŋee e lee donɔɔ keŋ man, te ba moo e gyu bula keŋ dɔɔ donɔɔ keŋ gyakaa nɛ nyee man, nyi baa fuŋ e ke lɔ delɔkɔtɔɔ keŋ man.

eng: But he passed through the crowd and went on his way.
local: Mɔna Yesu moowɔ balaŋ baŋ pou nsana nsana te ɔ laŋawɔ.

eng: Jesus went down to the city of Capernaum in Galilee and taught the people each Sabbath.
local: Yesu gyuuwɔ Kapanum donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ man. Te Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee kelii na, ɔ gyuuwɔ ka wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia.

eng: They were amazed by his teaching because he delivered his message with authority.
local: Yesu kawola keŋ doo balaŋ baŋ pou nɔɔ, nawolo nyi ɔ ne gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ wola ŋgba walaŋ ŋon ɔ dana doŋ nombia ŋan ɔ na wolo nɛ dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: A man in the synagogue had the spirit of an unclean demon. He screamed.
local: Na balee ŋolo kyaa Gyudatena ɔsom deni keŋ man keŋ feliŋkum doo ɔ man. Ɔ fae yeesa keŋkeŋ nyiaa.

eng: Hey! What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: Nyaŋ Yesu Nasarɛte baale, woŋ yaa n ne gyae de gyaŋ? N kɔŋae n kaa wɔlɛɛ daa? Ma gyeŋ neŋ nyi nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ walaŋ ŋon ayimɛɛ be doo n man ya nɛ.

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him! The demon threw the man down before them, then came out of him without harming him.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yase ɔ sia yako feliŋkum keŋ nyiaa, Wu n nɔɔ, na n ka lee ɔ man. Keŋte feliŋkum keŋ fuŋa balee ŋon lɔ balaŋ baŋ pou siaman te ka leewɔ ɔ man laŋ, mɔna balee ŋon te gooli ya.

eng: They were all shaken and said to each other, What kind of word is this, that he can command unclean spirits with authority and power, and they leave.
local: Nombii kei doo balaŋ baŋ pou nɔɔ te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Woŋ kawola yɛna kei? Ɔ na mo doŋ kolosi fa feliŋkumɛɛ na ŋɛɛ lee balaŋ man.

eng: Reports about him spread everywhere in the surrounding region.
local: Keŋte Yesu wose man nombia yaaseewɔ gyu debiise ŋan ŋa benaa na botɔɔ nɛ pou man.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus went home with Simon. Simons mother-in-law was sick with a high fever, and the family asked Jesus to help her.
local: Yesu ka lee Gyudatena ɔsom deni keŋ man na, te ɔ gyuuwɔ Simɔn dɛɛ. Ɔ ke gyu botɔɔ na Simɔn wa awu doo ɔ na wee kpaŋalaŋ. Keŋte be waase Yesu nyi waa kyɔ e kawee fa wɔ.

eng: He bent over her and spoke harshly to the fever, and it left her. She got up at once and served them.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ ka seŋawɔ alo ŋon yenaŋ te ɔ fae kpaŋalaŋ keŋ, te ɔ kawee keŋ taŋawɔ. Debokenaŋ man te alo ŋon korowɔ kyaa te ɔ kɛɛ be dɔɔ.

eng: When the sun was setting, everyone brought to Jesus relatives and acquaintances with all kinds of diseases. Placing his hands on each of them, he healed them.
local: Kelii balinɔɔ nɛ, balaŋ burum moo kaweesetena baŋ bɛɛ wee kaweese kpookpoo nɛ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ, te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa kaweete kamasɛ dɔɔ te ɔ kyɔɔ wɔ.

eng: Demons also came out of many people. They screamed, You are Gods Son. But he spoke harshly to them and wouldnt allow them to speak because they recognized that he was the Christ.
local: Feliŋkumɛɛ mɔ leewɔ balaŋ burum man na ŋɛɛ fae yeesa nyi, Nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon. Mɔna ɔ fae ŋa keŋ ɔ te fa ŋa gbɛɛ ŋa ke kolosi ya, nawolo nyi ŋa gyeŋ nyi ŋon yɛna Kristo, nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: When daybreak arrived, Jesus went to a deserted place. The crowds were looking for him. When they found him, they tried to keep him from leaving them.
local: Tɛɛ kena nɛ, Yesu wɔŋa ɔ wose lee yenaŋ. Botɔɔ na balaŋ baŋ te fiasɛɛ bɛɛ kɛo e. Ba kena e nɛ, ba te fa e gbɛɛ waa lee be gyaŋ ya.

eng: But he said to them, I must preach the good news of Gods kingdom in other cities too, for this is why I was sent.
local: Mɔna ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Kaboena nyi maa kolosi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa donɔɔse akaŋ ŋan mɔ, nawolo nyi keŋ dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ kpila maŋ.

eng: So he continued preaching in the Judean synagogues.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu tɛɛwɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man Galelia tɛɛle dɔɔ pou.

eng: In the fifteenth year of the rule of the emperor Tiberius—when Pontius Pilate was governor over Judea and Herod was ruler over Galilee, his brother Philip was ruler over Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias was ruler over Abilene.
local: Debaŋ keŋ Sakaria bu Gyɔn sɛɛ ɔ kyaa depampaa dinaa keŋ man nɛ, Wurubuarɛ kolosiwɔ fa e nyi waa gyu kekolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia fa balaŋ. Debaŋ kenaŋ na gyoo dinaa Kaesa Tiberiuse ke di gyoori Roman tɛɛle dɔɔ kulutooneŋ kufu anoŋte man kenaŋ, na Pɔntiɔse Pilato ne diina gyoori Gyudia tɛɛle dɔɔ. Herode mɔ ne diina gyoori Galelia tɛɛle dɔɔ, te ɔ naabu Filipo mɔ ne diina gyoori Iturea na Tirakonitise tɛɛlese dɔɔ, te Lisaniase mɔ ne di gyoori Abilene tɛɛle dɔɔ. Na Ana na Kaifase mɔ yɛna Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man.

eng: John went throughout the region of the Jordan River, calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Keŋte Gyɔn tɛɛwɔ donɔɔse ŋan ŋe kyaa Gyɔɔdan boo nɔɔman nɛ pou dɔɔ ɔ ne kolosi nyiaa, Ɛ kyɛɛkee lee ɛ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na ɛ ke kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ, na waa mo ɛ nombiakumɛɛ pou kyɛɛ ŋon.

eng: This is just as it was written in the scroll of the words of Isaiah the prophet, A voice crying out in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight.
local: Ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋon bɛɛ baake nyi Yesaya be ŋmarase ɔ tɔne man lee Gyɔn wose man nɛ nyiaa, Ŋolo ne fae yeesa depampaa dinaa man nyiaa, ɛ desina de Gbeŋgyoo gbɛɛneŋ, na ɛ ke yela ɔ gbɛɛneŋ pou ka tenee.

eng: Every valley will be filled, and every mountain and hill will be leveled. The crooked will be made straight and the rough places made smooth.
local: Ɛ tɔ boŋobii kamasɛ na ɛ ke yɛlɛɛ bulase na bulasebia pou, na ŋa ke doo kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Ɛ tenee gbɛɛneŋ ŋan be gokii nɛ, na ɛ ke kyalase gbɛɛneŋ ŋan ŋa be kyalasɛɛ ya nɛ.

eng: All humanity will see Gods salvation. .
local: Kɛŋa pou wɔle na, balaŋ pou baa naa nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ ba lese nyi ɔ kaa lɛɛ daa nɛ.

eng: Then John said to the crowds who came to be baptized by him, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ Gyɔn gyaŋ nyi waa sɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ dana nombiakumɛɛ ŋgba doma nɛ, amɔte faree na ŋon nyi yaa yeu lee Wurubuarɛ baŋ dinaa keŋ gyae kekɔŋ balaŋ dɔɔ nɛ man?.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives. And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Ɛ yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan nawolo nyi ɛ te kyɛɛkee lee ɛ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man nɛ. Ɛ na yɛɛ ya agyueŋ nyi keŋ ɛ yɛɛ Aberaham ɔ naanasewɔ dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ lɛɛ ŋon ya. Mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi Wurubuarɛ waa tale yela boya adoŋ kɛŋa ka bese naanase fa Aberaham.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Wurubuarɛ te desina ɔ debeŋa see waa tuŋii daase lɔ, mena dɔɔ daŋ kamasɛ keŋ bɛɛ se bia kpaakpaa ya na, waa tuŋ ke lɔ na baa keretii ke do boalaŋ man.

eng: The crowds asked him, What then should we do.
local: Botɔɔ te balaŋ dikpii keŋ bɔɔse Gyɔn nyiaa, Akpaa mena na, woŋ te kaboena nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ.

eng: He answered, Whoever has two shirts must share with the one who has none, and whoever has food must do the same.
local: Keŋte Gyɔn tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Nyaŋ ŋon n dana kegbase ala na, mo dokoloŋ fa ŋon ɔ be taŋ ya nɛ, te nyaŋ ŋon mɔ n dana weenɛɛ na, fa ŋon ɔ be taŋ ya nɛ.

eng: Even tax collectors came to be baptized. They said to him, Teacher, what should we do.
local: Lempoolɛɛra baale mɔ kɔŋawɔ nyi Gyɔn dɛɛ sɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, woŋ te kaboena nyi daa mɔ dɛɛ yɛɛ.

eng: He replied, Collect no more than you are authorized to collect.
local: Keŋte Gyɔn tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ na lɛɛ kɔba kela keŋ kaboena nyi yaa lɛɛ nɛ ya.

eng: Soldiers asked, What about us? What should we do? He answered, Dont cheat or harass anyone, and be satisfied with your pay.
local: Yoonɔɔtena baale mɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, Daa mɔ de? Woŋ te kaboena nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ? Keŋte Gyɔn yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ na mo siadoŋ lɛo ŋolo kɔba ya, yaa ɛ na mo nombiakumɛɛ gyakaa ŋolo dɔɔ ya. Ɛ yela kɔba keŋ bɛɛ tɔ ŋon nɛ ka gyoo ɛ sia.

eng: The people were filled with expectation, and everyone wondered whether John might be the Christ.
local: Lee mena keŋ balaŋ baŋ ne daa nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ dɔɔ nɛ, walaŋ kamasɛ yɛɛ wa agyueŋ nyi Gyɔn yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon.

eng: John replied to them all, I baptize you with water, but the one who is more powerful than me is coming. Im not worthy to loosen the strap of his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
local: Keŋte Gyɔn tiranɔɔ fa be popou nyiaa, Maŋ dɔɔ loŋ te mɛɛ mo sɔ ŋon Wurubuarɛ loŋ, mɔna ŋon ɔ kela maŋ nɛ waa kɔŋ keŋ maŋ tekaboe gba nyi maa buŋ booli ɔ nkyokota ŋmeeseŋ koraŋ gba ya. Ŋon dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ na boalaŋ, te waa mo sɔ ŋon Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: Ɔ dana ɔ pampaa ɔ nyiŋmaa man waa mo feefu wa amɔla, na waa lese amɔla bia ŋan do ɔ kpee man, na waa kuu wura ŋan ka do boalaŋ keŋ bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man.

eng: With many other words John appealed to them, proclaiming good news to the people.
local: Keŋte Gyɔn tɛɛwɔ gbɛɛneŋ burum dɔɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia fa wɔ, te ɔ doo wɔ kakyeŋ nyi baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man.

eng: But Herod the ruler had been criticized harshly by John because of Herodias, Herods brothers wife, and because of all the evil he had done.
local: Gyɔn lese nombiakumɛɛ burum ŋan Herode ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ Romantena gyoo dinaa nawɔɔ man be yɛɛ nɛ si e, na mena keŋ ɔ be lɛo ŋon gbagba ɔ kegyia wɛɛle Herodia yala nɛ.

eng: He added this to the list of his evil deeds: he locked John up in prison.
local: Te nombiikum keŋ Herode be yɛɛ tɔ nɔɔ koraŋ yɛna nyi, ɔ yela be kyaŋa Gyɔn ke tɔ deni.

eng: When everyone was being baptized, Jesus also was baptized. While he was praying, heaven was opened.
local: Gyɔn ke sɔ balaŋ dikpii keŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ taŋ nɛ, ɔ sɔɔ Yesu mɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ. Ɔ ke sɔ e taŋ na te Yesu moowɔ ɔ ne fane. Botɔɔ te adido torowɔ.

eng: and the Holy Spirit came down on him in bodily form like a dove. And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: keŋte Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon moo duwuluŋ dinɔɔ te ɔ kaa gyakaawɔ Yesu dɔɔ. Botɔɔ te be nyii nɔɔwoya ŋaale lee adido nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna me bu ŋon mɛɛ gyae neŋ lee me konɔɔ man, te n na gyoo me sia nideli.

eng: Jesus was about years old when he began his ministry. People supposed that he was the son of Joseph son of Heli.
local: Debaŋ keŋ Yesu be fi ɔ tom keyɛɛ asɛɛ nɛ, na ɔ te di ŋgba kulutooneŋ sao na kufu nɛ, na balaŋ na gyueŋ nyi Yesu yɛɛ Gyosɛfo bu dalolasa e. Gyosɛfo ɔ kya yɛna Heli.

eng: son of Matthat son of Levi son of Melchi son of Jannai son of Joseph.
local: Heli mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Matati, te Matati mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Lewi. Lewi ɔ kya yɛna Melki te Melki mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Yanai te Yanai ɔ kya yɛna Gyosɛfo.

eng: son of Mattathias son of Amos son of Nahum son of Esli son of Naggai.
local: Gyosɛfo ɔ kya yɛna Matatia, te Matatia ɔ kya yɛna Amose te Amose ɔ kya yɛna Nahum te Nahum mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Esili, te Esili ɔ kya yɛna Nagai.

eng: son of Maath son of Mattathias son of Semein son of Josech son of Joda.
local: Nagai ɔ kya yɛna Maati te Maati mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Matatia, te Matatia ɔ kya yɛna Semeni te Semeni ɔ kya yɛna Gyoseki te Gyoseki ɔ kya yɛna Gyoda.

eng: son of Joanan son of Rhesa son of Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel son of Neri.
local: Gyoda ɔ kya yɛna Gyoana te Gyoana ɔ kya yɛna Resa te Resa ɔ kya yɛna Sorobabeli, te Sorobabeli ɔ kya yɛna Selateli te Selateli ɔ kya yɛna Neri.

eng: son of Melchi son of Addi son of Cosam son of Elmadam son of Er.
local: Neri mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Mɛliki te Mɛliki mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Adi. Adi ɔ kya yɛna Kosam te Kosam mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Elemadam te Elemadam mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Eri.

eng: son of Joshua son of Eliezer son of Jorim son of Matthat son of Levi.
local: Eri ɔ kya yɛna Gyosua te Gyosua mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Eliasa te Eliasa ɔ kya yɛna Gyorim te Gyorim ɔ kya yɛna Matati te Matati mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Lewi.

eng: son of Simeon son of Judah son of Joseph son of Jonam son of Eliakim.
local: Lewi ɔ kya yɛna Simeɔn te Simeɔn ɔ kya yɛna Gyuda, te Gyuda ɔ kya yɛna Gyosɛfo te Gyosɛfo ɔ kya yɛna Gyonam, te Gyonam ɔ kya yɛna Eliakim.

eng: son of Melea son of Menna son of Mattatha son of Nathan son of David.
local: Eliakim ɔ kya yɛna Melea te Melea ɔ kya yɛna Mena. Mena ɔ kya yɛna Matata te Matata mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Natan, te Natan mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Defidi.

eng: son of Jesse son of Obed son of Boaz son of Sala son of Nahshon.
local: Defidi ɔ kya yɛna Gyese te Gyese mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Obedi. Obedi ɔ kya yɛna Boase te Boase ɔ kya yɛna Salimɔn, te Salimɔn mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Nasɔn.

eng: son of Amminadab son of Admin son of Arni son of Hezron son of Perez son of Judah.
local: Nasɔn ɔ kya yɛna Aminadabo te Aminadabo ɔ kya yɛna Adimi, te Adimi mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Arini te Arini ɔ kya yɛna Heserɔn te Heserɔn mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Peresi, te Peresi ɔ kya yɛna Gyuda.

eng: son of Jacob son of Isaac son of Abraham son of Terah son of Nahor.
local: Gyuda ɔ kya yɛna Gyekɔpo te Gyekɔpo ɔ kya yɛna Asiki te Asiki ɔ kya yɛna Aberaham, te Aberaham mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Tera te Tera ɔ kya yɛna Nahɔ.

eng: son of Serug son of Reu son of Peleg son of Eber son of Shelah.
local: Nahɔ mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Serugi te Serugi ɔ kya yɛna Rewu, te Rewu ɔ kya yɛna Pelegi te Pelegi mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Eberi, te Eberi mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Sɛla.

eng: son of Cainan son of Arphaxad son of Shem son of Noah son of Lamech.
local: Sɛla ɔ kya yɛna Kainan te Kainan mɔ kya yɛna Afahade te Afahade mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Sem, te Sem mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Nowa, te Nowa mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Lamɛke.

eng: son of Methuselah son of Enoch son of Jared son of Mahalalel son of Cainan.
local: Lamɛke ɔ kya yɛna Metusala te Metusala mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Enɔke, te Enɔke mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Gyarɛdi te Gyarɛdi mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Mahalali, te Mahalali mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Kainan.

eng: son of Enos son of Seth son of Adam son of God.
local: Kainan mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Enose te Enose mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Sɛɛte, te Sɛɛte ɔ kya yɛna Adam, te Wurubuarɛ mɔ yɛɛna Adam.

eng: One Sabbath, as Jesus was going through the wheat fields, his disciples were picking the heads of wheat, rubbing them in their hands, and eating them.
local: Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee daale nɛ, Yesu na ɔ kaseela gyuuwɔ ka mo wɔɔ daale man. Na weenɛɛ ŋan seŋɛɛ wɔɔ keŋ man nɛ yɛɛ ŋgba kefaa nɛ. Keŋte ɔ kaseela baŋ kpera ŋaale posaa bɛɛ tao.

eng: Some Pharisees said, Why are you breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Keŋte Farasiitena baale naa wɔ te be bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne yɛɛ kpene keŋ da mmaraa bɛɛ fa gbɛɛ nyi baa yɛɛ da kefɛɛfowee ya nɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Havent you read what David and his companions did when they were hungry?.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ te kala ta lee Wurubuarɛ tɔne man kpene keŋ gyoo Defidi be yɛɛ debaŋ keŋ tanam be kyaŋ ŋon na ɔ balaŋ baŋ yee?.

eng: He broke the Law by going into Gods house and eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests can eat. He also gave some of the bread to his companions.
local: Ɔ gyuuwɔ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man te ɔ ka moo weenɛɛ ŋan bɔɔ mo deesi fa Wurubuarɛ, keŋ nafɔ tekaboena nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ te yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ sae e ya waa di ya nɛ, te ɔ diiwɔ te ɔ moo ŋaale mɔ fa ɔ balaŋ baŋ be silana e nɛ.

eng: Then he said to them, The Human Oneis Lord of the Sabbath.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon yɛna kefɛɛfowee keŋ Gbeŋgyoo.

eng: On another Sabbath, Jesus entered a synagogue to teach. A man was there whose right hand was withered.
local: Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee daale bela nɛ, Yesu gyuu Gyudatena ɔsom deni man ɔ na wola wɔ. Na baale ŋolo kyaa botɔɔ keŋ ɔ dunoluŋ baam ba yem.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees were watching him closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath. They were looking for a reason to bring charges against him.
local: Gyudatena mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baale gyae gbɛɛ keŋ baa mo nombiikum daale seele Yesu na baa nyiŋ kyaŋ e, mena dɔɔ ba deke e na baa kɛɛ nyi waa kyɔ ŋolo kawee Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee keŋ.

eng: Jesus knew their thoughts, so he said to the man with the withered hand, Get up and stand in front of everyone. He got up and stood there.
local: Mɔna na Yesu gyeŋ kpene keŋ bɛɛ gyueŋ te ɔ yako baam dayeŋasate ŋon nyiaa, Koro seŋ na n ke kɔŋ kaa seŋ balaŋ kɛwɔ pou siaman. Keŋte balee ŋon korowɔ kaa seŋ be siaman.

eng: Jesus said to the legal experts and Pharisees, Heres a question for you: Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to destroy it.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Woŋ te da mmaraa ne fa gbɛɛ nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ kefɛɛfowee keŋ? Da mmaraa ne fa daa gbɛɛ nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ kolo kpaakpaa yaa dɛɛ yɛɛ kolo kum? Dɛɛ lɛɛ walaŋ nyee yaa dee yela waa yeŋ.

eng: Looking around at them all, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and his hand was made healthy.
local: Keŋte Yesu kɛɛ wɔ pou gyaŋee te ɔ yako balee ŋon nyiaa, Tenee n baam. Balee ŋon tenee ɔ baam keŋ te kawee keŋ taŋawɔ te ɔ baam keŋ yɛɛ doŋ.

eng: They were furious and began talking with each other about what to do to Jesus.
local: Botɔɔ te kegyiise baŋ gyeŋa baŋ nideli, te be fiasɛɛ bɛɛ gyueŋ kpene keŋ baa mo yɛɛ Yesu.

eng: During that time, Jesus went out to the mountain to pray, and he prayed to God all night long.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ man wee daale nɛ Yesu gyuuwɔ bula dɔɔ ɔ kaa fane, te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ nelim kenaŋ pou.

eng: At daybreak, he called together his disciples. He chose twelve of them whom he called apostles.
local: Tɛɛ kena nɛ ɔ baake ɔ kaseela kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ lese baala kufu bala lee be man nyi baa yɛɛ ɔ kpilala.

eng: Simon, whom he named Peter; his brother Andrew; James; John; Philip; Bartholomew.
local: Baala kufu bala baŋ Yesu ba lese nɛ yela yɛna Simɔn, ŋon Yesu ba do e yele nyi Pita nɛ, na ɔ naabu Anderiase na Gyemisi na Gyɔn na Filipo na Batolomeo na.

eng: Matthew; Thomas; James the son of Alphaeus; Simon, who was called a zealot.
local: Mateo na Tomase na Alfeose bu Gyemisi na Simɔn ŋon ɔ ne gyae ɔ balaŋ nideli nɛ.

eng: Judas the son of James; and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.
local: na Gyemisi bu Gyudase na Gyudase Isekarɔte ŋon waa kaa lese Yesu fa nɛ.

eng: Jesus came down from the mountain with them and stood on a large area of level ground. A great company of his disciples and a huge crowd of people from all around Judea and Jerusalem and the area around Tyre and Sidon joined him there.
local: Yesu na ɔ kpilala baŋ ka lee bulabii keŋ dɔɔ kɔŋ detaŋgya man nɛ, ɔ kaseela burum na balaŋ dikpii burum baŋ bɔɔ lee Gyudia tɛɛle dɔɔ na Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man na Tae na Sidɔn donɔɔse ŋan kyaa ɛpo nɔɔman nɛ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ.

eng: They came to hear him and to be healed from their diseases, and those bothered by unclean spirits were healed.
local: Be kɔŋawɔ be kaa nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia na waa kyɔ wɔ be kaweese. Baŋ feliŋkumɛɛ ne haa wɔ nɛ, ɔ kyɔɔ baŋ mɔ.

eng: The whole crowd wanted to touch him, because power was going out from him and he was healing everyone.
local: Na balaŋ baŋ pou ne gyae nyi baa tina e, nawolo nyi doŋ na lee ɔ man na ne kyɔ wɔ be kaweese pou.

eng: Jesus raised his eyes to his disciples and said: Happy are you who are poor, because Gods kingdom is yours.
local: Yesu gyinaa ɔ sia kɛɛ ɔ kaseela baŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ yɛɛ ayematenawɔ nɛ ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi ɛmɛɛ te Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ɛ wui.

eng: Happy are you who hunger now, because you will be satisfied. Happy are you who weep now, because you will laugh.
local: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ tanam dana ŋon debokeiman nɛ ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi ɛmɛɛ ne gyaela ɛ kaa baa. Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ne wii debokeiman nɛ ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi ɛmɛɛ ne gyaela ɛ kaa boŋo.

eng: Happy are you when people hate you, reject you, insult you, and condemn your name as evil because of the Human One.
local: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ balaŋ ne kɔla ŋon te bɔɔ ne lɛɛ ŋon do ba wose man ya te bɛɛ tee ŋon, te bɛɛ baake ŋon nyi balaŋ kumɛɛ lee maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon dɔɔ nɛ ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam. Mena ke te be naanaɔ mɔ naase Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ diyem. Dekɔŋ mena na ɛ yela ɛ sia ka gye na ɛ ke nyaŋee nideli, nawolo nyi kom keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa tɔ ŋon adido nɛ te yɛɛ nsendee ya.

eng: But how terrible for you who are rich, because you have already received your comfort.
local: Mɔna ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ dana kɔba nɛ ɛ laako, nawolo nyi ɛ taŋ ke di ɛ gyoŋ pou taŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ.

eng: How terrible for you who have plenty now, because you will be hungry. How terrible for you who laugh now, because you will mourn and weep.
local: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ be dii baa debaŋ kei nɛ ɛ laako, nawolo nyi tanam gyae kaa ko ŋon. Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ na boŋo debaŋ kei nɛ ɛ laako, nawolo nyi yaa kaa wii na ɛ ke di walaŋsa.

eng: How terrible for you when all speak well of you. Their ancestors did the same things to the false prophets.
local: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ balaŋ pou ne kolosi ɛ wose man nombia kpaakpaa nɛ ɛ laako, nawolo nyi mena mɔ te be kyaɔ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ dii akɛrɛɛ nyi be yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔrawɔ nɛ.

eng: But I say to you who are willing to hear: Love your enemies. Do good to those who hate you.
local: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ne tei maŋ nɛ mɛɛ yako ŋon na ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, ɛ gyae balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɔla ŋon nɛ na ɛ ke yɛɛ deeli fa wɔ.

eng: Bless those who curse you. Pray for those who mistreat you.
local: Ɛ sola Wurubuarɛ na waa hiraa baŋ bɛɛ falaa ŋon nɛ, na ɛ kefane fa baŋ bɛɛ naase ŋon diyem nɛ.

eng: If someone slaps you on the cheek, offer the other one as well. If someone takes your coat, dont withhold your shirt either.
local: Akpaa ŋolo de gyɔ n lakaa man na besena alate keŋ mɔ fa e. Mena mɔ te akpaa ŋolo da mo n kegba wurikyim na mo atetate keŋ mɔ kpu e.

eng: Give to everyone who asks and dont demand your things back from those who take them.
local: Akpaa ŋolo da sola neŋ kolo na fa e te akpaa ŋolo da mo n kolo na, na gyu ke bɔɔse ke dɔɔ bela ya.

eng: Treat people in the same way that you want them to treat you.
local: Kpene keŋ n ne gyae nyi walaŋ waa yɛɛ fa neŋ nɛ nyaŋ mɔ yɛɛ ke fa e.

eng: If you love those who love you, why should you be commended? Even sinners love those who love them.
local: Akpaa ɛ ne gyae balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ gyae ŋon nɛ baageŋ bo na, ɛ na gyueŋ nyi ɛ ke nyiŋ kolo kpaakpaa lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya, nawolo nyi nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra mɔ koraŋ ne gyae baŋ bɛɛ gyae wɔ nɛ.

eng: If you do good to those who do good to you, why should you be commended? Even sinners do that.
local: Akpaa ɛ yɛɛ deeli fa baŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ deeli fa ŋon nɛ baageŋ na, ɛ na gyueŋ nyi ɛ ke nyiŋ kolo kpaakpaa lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya. Nawolo nyi nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra mɔ koraŋ ne yɛɛ mena.

eng: If you lend to those from whom you expect repayment, why should you be commended? Even sinners lend to sinners expecting to be paid back in full.
local: Te akpaa ɛ pakee abɔɔ fa balaŋ baŋ ɛ gyeŋ nyi baa nyiŋ tɔ ŋon na, ɛ na gyueŋ nyi ɛ ke nyiŋ kolo kpaakpaa lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya. Nawolo nyi nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra koraŋ ne pakee abɔɔ fa be tebia baŋ ba gyeŋ nyi baa nyiŋ pou tɔ wɔ nɛ.

eng: Instead, love your enemies, do good, and lend expecting nothing in return. If you do, you will have a great reward. You will be acting the way children of the Most High act, for he is kind to ungrateful and wicked people.
local: Mɔna ɛ gyae baŋ bɛɛ kɔla ŋon nɛ na ɛ ke yɛɛ deeli fa wɔ. Ɛ paa wɔ kolo na ɛ lese ɛ loo lee ke dɔɔ. Ɛ yɛɛ mena na, Wurubuarɛ waa tɔ ŋon kom nideli te ɛ ka bese Wurubuarɛ ŋon kpene kamasɛ doo ɔ tɛɛ nɛ bia. Nawolo nyi ɔ dana wɔe fa baŋ kolo bɛɛ gyoo be sia ya, na baŋ be dana nyeemandoŋ nɛ pou.

eng: Be compassionate just as your Father is compassionate.
local: Ɛ wii balaŋ waraŋase ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ kya Wurubuarɛ ne wii balaŋ waraŋase nɛ.

eng: Dont judge, and you wont be judged. Dont condemn, and you wont be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven.
local: Ɛ na mo ɛ wose yɛɛ balaŋ nombiadiira na ɛ ke bu wɔ fɔɔ, na Wurubuarɛ mɔ be nyiŋ di ɛ nombia na waa bu ŋon fɔɔ ya. Ɛ mo balaŋ nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ, na Wurubuarɛ mɔ ka mo ɛ nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ ŋon.

eng: Give, and it will be given to you. A good portion—packed down, firmly shaken, and overflowing—will fall into your lap. The portion you give will determine the portion you receive in return.
local: Ɛ yɛɛ deeli fa balaŋ na Wurubuarɛ mɔ ke yɛɛ deeli fa ŋon. Ɛ yɛɛ mena na, kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa fa ŋon nɛ te yɛɛ nsendee ya. Waa nyiŋ konɔɔ maa ke nideli na ke wulu gba leki, na waa mo do ɛ nyiŋmaa man. Nawolo nyi mena keŋ ɛ ke maa fa ɛ tebia nɛ, mena ke te Wurubuarɛ mɔ waa maa fa ŋon.

eng: Jesus also told them a riddle. A blind person cant lead another blind person, right? Wont they both fall into a ditch?.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu gyɔɔ wɔ dudu kei nyiaa, Siayɛlɛɛsate waa tale wola ɔ dɔɔ siayɛlɛɛsate gbɛɛ? Akpaa ɔ yɛɛ mena na, be balaŋ bala pou be gyae be kaa gyoo bɔɔ man yee?.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, but whoever is fully prepared will be like their teacher.
local: Kaseele bɛɛ kela ɔ gbeŋgyoo ya, mɔna kaseele kamasɛ ŋon ɔ kasee tom nideli nɛ waa yɛɛ ŋgba ɔ gbeŋgyoo nɛ.

eng: Why do you see the splinter in your brothers or sisters eye but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: Weera dɔɔ te n ne naa woŋsilibii keŋ doo n dɔɔ sibii man, te n be famenɛ na daakpee keŋ doo nyaŋ gbagba n sibii man ya?.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Brother, Sister, let me take the splinter out of your eye, when you dont see the log in your own eye? You deceive yourselves! First take the log out of your eye, and then you will see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Sena te n ke tale yako n dɔɔ nyi, Me dɔɔ, yela maa lese woŋsilibii lee n sibii man, keŋ nyaŋ gbagba be famenɛ na daakpee keŋ doo n sibii man ya? Nyaŋ walaŋ ŋon n na beo n wose, kaboena nyi nya taŋgbɛɛ lese daakpee keŋ doo n sibii man nɛ, pɛna n kena nideli lese woŋsilibii keŋ doo n dɔɔ sibii man nɛ.

eng: A good tree doesnt produce bad fruit, nor does a bad tree produce good fruit.
local: Daŋ kpaakpaate kamasɛ bɛɛ se bia kumɛɛ ya, mena mɔ te daŋ kum bɛɛ se bia kpaakpaa ya.

eng: Each tree is known by its own fruit. People dont gather figs from thorny plants, nor do they pick grapes from prickly bushes.
local: Daŋ kamasɛ nɛ, ke bia te bɛɛ mo gyeŋ ke. Bɔɔ ne kɔ akutu bia lee mango daŋ man ya, mena mɔ te bɔɔ ne kɔ apaŋ lee abotoo woŋse man ya.

eng: A good person produces good from the good treasury of the inner self, while an evil person produces evil from the evil treasury of the inner self. The inner self overflows with words that are spoken.
local: Mena ke te denibalaŋ mɔ doo. Walaŋ kpaakpaate kamasɛ na lese abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan doo ɔ konɔɔ man nɛ wola, te walaŋkum kamasɛ mɔ na lese nombiakumɛɛ ŋan doo ɔ konɔɔ man nɛ wola. Kpene kamasɛ keŋ be wulu walaŋ konɔɔ man nɛ, keŋ na lee la ɔ nɔɔman.

eng: Why do you call me Lord, Lord and dont do what I say?.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ ne baake maŋ nyi, Ɛ Gbeŋgyoo, te ɛ bɛɛ yɛɛ kpene keŋ mɔɔ yako ŋon nyi yaa yɛɛ nɛ ya?.

eng: Ill show what its like when someone comes to me, hears my words, and puts them into practice.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne kɔŋ me gyaŋ te ɔ ne nyii me nɔɔwoya te ɔ ne di ŋe dɔɔ nɛ, maa wola ŋon mena keŋ ɔ dinɔɔ doo.

eng: Its like a person building a house by digging deep and laying the foundation on bedrock. When the flood came, the rising water smashed against that house, but the water couldnt shake the house because it was well built.
local: Ɔ yɛɛ ŋgba walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne ma deni te ɔ be wula bɔɔ te ɔ ba see ka mokɔɔ nideli boe dɔɔ nɛ. Keŋte boo wuuwɔ kaa mo ke dɔɔ mɔna ka te yale ya, nawolo nyi ɔ see ka mokɔɔ nideli.

eng: But those who dont put into practice what they hear are like a person who built a house without a foundation. The floodwater smashed against it and it collapsed instantly. It was completely destroyed.
local: Mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne nyii me nɔɔwoya te ɔ bee di ŋe dɔɔ ya nɛ, ɔ yɛɛ ŋgba walaŋ ŋon ɔ be ma deni te ɔ ta see ka mokɔɔ nideli ya nɛ. Boo wuuwɔ kaa mo ke dɔɔ, te ke yalawɔ tekerii pou keŋ n be naa ka mokɔɔ gba ya.

eng: One day Jesus was standing beside Lake Gennesaret when the crowd pressed in around him to hear Gods word.
local: Wee daale Yesu ka seŋ Genesareti boo nɔɔman nɛ, balaŋ burum kaa yilaawɔ kilisi e bee nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia.

eng: Jesus saw two boats sitting by the lake. The fishermen had gone ashore and were washing their nets.
local: Keŋte ɔ naa agbelese ala boo keŋ nɔɔman, na kpɛɛ kyaŋara baŋ ta lee bɛɛ faafo ba asawuse.

eng: Jesus boarded one of the boats, the one that belonged to Simon, then asked him to row out a little distance from the shore. Jesus sat down and taught the crowds from the boat.
local: Yesu gyoowɔ degbele keŋ daale man, keŋ ke yɛɛ Simɔn wui nɛ. Te ɔ yako e nyi waa tɛɛse e gyu loŋ keŋ man kyomii. Yesu kyaawɔ degbele keŋ man botɔɔ te ɔ wola balaŋ dikpii keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia.

eng: When he finished speaking to the crowds, he said to Simon, Row out farther, into the deep water, and drop your nets for a catch.
local: Ɔ kekolosi taŋ nɛ, ɔ yako Simɔn nyiaa, Ɛ saa gyu loŋ keŋ nsana nsana botɔɔ keŋ dana siiliŋ nɛ, na ɛ ke lɔ ya asawuse ŋan na ɛ ke kyaŋ kpɛɛ.

eng: Simon replied, Master, weve worked hard all night and caught nothing. But because you say so, Ill drop the nets.
local: Botɔɔ te Simɔn tiranɔɔ nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, da te yɛɛ tom nelim pou da te kyaŋ kolo ya! Mɔna keŋ nyaŋ be yako dɔɔ, dɛɛ lɔ da asawuse.

eng: So they dropped the nets and their catch was so huge that their nets were splitting.
local: Ba ke lɔ ba asawuse ŋan nɛ, be kyaŋa kpɛɛ burum keŋ nyi ba asawuse ŋan ne lɛɛ gbɛɛ ŋe ke tirii.

eng: They signaled for their partners in the other boat to come and help them. They filled both boats so full that they were about to sink.
local: Mena dɔɔ be baake be tebia baŋ be doo degbele daale man nɛ nyi be kaa kyɔ wɔ. Ba kekɔŋ nɛ, be kaa lese kpɛɛ wulu agbelese ala ŋan pou keŋ nyi, ŋe lɛɛ gbɛɛ nafɔ ŋe ke munu.

eng: When Simon Peter saw the catch, he fell at Jesus knees and said, Leave me, Lord, for Im a sinner.
local: Simɔn Pita kena kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ, ɔ buŋawɔ ɔ doona dɔɔ Yesu siaman yako e nyiaa, Lee me gyaŋ, me Gbeŋgyoo! Me yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛre e.

eng: Peter and those with him were overcome with amazement because of the number of fish they caught.
local: Nawolo nyi lee kpɛɛ burum baŋ bɔɔ kyam nɛ dɔɔ, doo ŋon na ɔ tebia baŋ pou nɔɔ.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, were Simons partners and they were amazed too. Jesus said to Simon, Dont be afraid. From now on, you will be fishing for people.
local: Mena mɔ te doo ɔ tebia Sebedeo bia Gyemisi na Gyɔn baŋ baŋ na wɔ ne yɛɛ tom nɛ nɔɔ. Botɔɔ te Yesu yako Simɔn nyiaa, Na yee ya, lee gyɛŋ ne gyu nɛ, balaŋ te maa yela n ke kyaŋ.

eng: As soon as they brought the boats to the shore, they left everything and followed Jesus.
local: Mena dɔɔ ba ke saa ba agbelese ŋan kɔŋ dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ nɛ, be tinaa kpene kamasɛ yela botɔɔ te be sila Yesu laŋ.

eng: Jesus was in one of the towns where there was also a man covered with a skin disease. When he saw Jesus, he fell on his face and begged, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: Yesu kekyaa donɔɔse ŋan daale man nɛ, balee ŋolo kɔŋawɔ keŋ kaweeyayam te di ɔ wose pou. Ɔ kena Yesu nɛ, ɔ kaa buŋawɔ ɔ nawɔɔtɛɛ waase e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, akpaa n ne gyae na nyaŋ ke tale kyɔ maŋ kawee.

eng: Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean. Instantly, the skin disease left him.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tenee ɔ nyiŋmaa tina e te ɔ yako nyiaa, Mɛɛ gyae, n kawee dɛɛ taŋ. Debokenaŋ man te balee ŋon kawee taŋawɔ.

eng: Jesus ordered him not to tell anyone. Instead, Jesus said, go and show yourself to the priest and make an offering for your cleansing, as Moses instructed. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Keŋte Yesu tɔɔ e kiiŋ nyi, Na yeli walaŋ na walaŋ ke nyii ya, mɔna mo n wose gyu kawola Wurubuarɛ sae ŋon, na n ke deesi ŋgba mena keŋ Mosesi ba wola ŋon nɛ, na kedi adansɛɛ fa walaŋ kamasɛ nyi n kawee te taŋ.

eng: News of him spread even more and huge crowds gathered to listen and to be healed from their illnesses.
local: Mɔna nɛnɛɛ gba te Yesu wose man nombia yaaseewɔ gyu yenaŋ kamasɛ, te balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ be kaa nyii e na waa kyɔ wɔ be kaweese.

eng: But Jesus would withdraw to deserted places for prayer.
local: Mɔna Yesu ne kyaa wɔŋ ɔ wose lee yenaŋ gyu kefane.

eng: One day when Jesus was teaching, Pharisees and legal experts were sitting nearby. They had come from every village in Galilee and Judea, and from Jerusalem. Now the power of the Lord was with Jesus to heal.
local: Wee daale Yesu kɛɛ wolo nɛ, na Farasiitena na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ bɔɔ lee Galelia na Gyudia tɛɛlese man, na Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, baale kyaa botɔɔ. Debaŋ kenaŋ na, de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ doŋ doo Yesu man keŋ waa tale mo kyɔ kaweese.

eng: Some men were bringing a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. They wanted to carry him in and place him before Jesus.
local: Te balaŋ baale seele walaŋ ŋon ɔ yenaŋ ba yem nɛ dofoŋoŋ dɔɔ kɔŋ, na bɛɛ gyae gbɛɛ na baa mo e gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man ka see Yesu siaman.

eng: but they couldnt reach him because of the crowd. So they took him up on the roof and lowered him—cot and all—through the roof tiles into the crowded room in front of Jesus.
local: mɔna be te nyiŋ gbɛɛ mo e ta denanɔɔ man ya, nawolo nyi balaŋ boe. Mena dɔɔ ba gyeŋawɔ gyu deni keŋ nyee dɔɔ te be wolee yenaŋ daale, te be tisinaa e na ɔ dofoŋoŋ keŋ pou dɛɛdɛɛ see Yesu siaman balaŋ baŋ nsana nsana.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said, Friend, your sins are forgiven.
local: Yesu kena mena keŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ e di nɛ, ɔ yako kaweete ŋon nyiaa, Me gyoo, me ta mo n nombiakumɛɛ pou kyɛɛ neŋ.

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees began to mutter among themselves, Who is this who insults God? Only God can forgive sins.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ fiasɛɛ bɛɛ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Walaŋ woŋti yaa kei te ɔ na mo ɔ wose kɛɛse na Wurubuarɛ mena? Lee Wurubuarɛ wɔle na amɔ kyaa la keŋ waa tale mo dukum kyɛɛ.

eng: Jesus recognized what they were discussing and responded, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Na Yesu gyeŋ kpene keŋ bɛɛ gyueŋ nɛ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ na gyueŋ kɛŋa ɛ nyeeya man?.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: Nombia kɛŋa man woŋti woɛla na keyako? Me ta mo n dukum kyɛɛ neŋ, yaa Koro seŋ na n ke ta.

eng: But so that you will know that the Human Onehas authority on the earth to forgive sins—Jesus now spoke to the man who was paralyzed,I say to you, get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: Mɔna mɛɛ gyae ɛ ka gyeŋ nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu dana doŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyi maa mo dukum kyɛɛ. Mena dɔɔ ɔ yako kaweete ŋon nyiaa, Koro seŋ, gate n dofoŋoŋ na n ke ta gyu n dɛɛ.

eng: Right away, the man stood before them, picked up his cot, and went home, praising God.
local: Debokenaŋ man te kaweete ŋon korowɔ seŋ ɔ nawɔɔ dɔɔ be siaman te ɔ gate ɔ dofoŋoŋ keŋ dɔɔ ɔ doo nɛ, te ɔ laŋa ɔ dɛɛ. Ɔ ne gyu na ɔ na lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

eng: All the people were beside themselves with wonder. Filled with awe, they glorified God, saying, Weve seen unimaginable things today.
local: Nombia kɛŋa doo balaŋ baŋ pou nɔɔ nideli te ba lese Wurubuarɛ yele. Gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ te be yakowɔ nyiaa, gyɛŋ dɔɔ da te naa nombia ŋan te kɔŋa ta see ya.

eng: Afterward, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: Nombia kɛŋa wɔle nɛ Yesu leewɔ te ɔ ke naa lempoolɛɛre ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Lewi nɛ, na ɔ kyaa ɔ lempoo delɛe man. Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, Koro sila maŋ.

eng: Levi got up, left everything behind, and followed him.
local: Debokenaŋ man te Lewi tinaa kpene kamasɛ yela te ɔ korowɔ sila Yesu.

eng: Then Levi threw a great banquet for Jesus in his home. A large number of tax collectors and others sat down to eat with them.
local: Keŋte Lewi moo Yesu gyu ŋon Lewi dɛɛ te ɔ ke saa e weenɛɛ ampaŋ ampaŋ. Lempoolɛɛra burum na balaŋ yakaa nyibaŋ kɔŋawɔ kaa di bom na wɔ.

eng: The Pharisees and their legal experts grumbled against his disciples. They said, Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Botɔɔ te Farasiitena na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ bɔɔ lee Farasiitena dikpii keŋ man nɛ ŋorokoteewɔ bɔɔse Yesu kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te ɛmɛɛ na lempoolɛɛra na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra ne dii te ɛ ne nyɔɔ bom.

eng: Jesus answered, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Kaweesetena te kaboena nyi baa kyɔ wɔ kawee. Baŋ bɔɔ na wee ya nɛ dɔɔ, bɔɔ ne hia kawee kekyɔ ya.

eng: I didnt come to call righteous people but sinners to change their hearts and lives.
local: Ma te kɔŋ nyi me kaa baake balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ wulaa tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ ya, mɔna me kɔŋae me kaa baake nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra na baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na baa kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

eng: Some people said to Jesus, The disciples of John fast often and pray frequently. The disciples of the Pharisees do the same, but your disciples are always eating and drinking.
local: Keŋte be yako Yesu nyiaa, Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon silala ne kyaa bake be nɔɔ fane, te mena mɔ yaa Farasiitena silala mɔ ne yɛɛ. Mɔna nyaŋ wui kɛwɔ dɔɔ bee dii bɛɛ nyɔɔ debaŋ kamasɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, You cant make the wedding guests fast while the groom is with them, can you?.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ ke tale yela balaŋ baŋ be kyaa alowɔletɛɛ nɛ ke bake be nɔɔ keŋ alowɔle baale kyaa be gyaŋ?.

eng: The days will come when the groom will be taken from them, and then they will fast.
local: Mɔna debaŋ daale gyae kekɔŋ keŋ baa mo alowɔle baale ŋon lee be gyaŋ. Mena weeya ŋenaŋ man te baa bake be nɔɔ.

eng: Then he told them a parable. No one tears a patch from a new garment to patch an old garment. Otherwise, the new garment would be ruined, and the new patch wouldnt match the old garment.
local: Keŋte Yesu gyɔɔ wɔ dudu kei nyiaa, Ŋolo bɛɛ kara kanyaŋ wɔle mo taa kakyeŋgbelaa keŋ ba yem nɛ ya. Akpaa ɔ yɛɛ mena na ɔ te wɔlɛɛ kanyaŋ wɔle keŋ, nawolo nyi kanyaŋ wɔle keŋ be gyae ke kyaa na bene keŋ ya.

eng: Nobody pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the new wine would burst the wineskins, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined.
local: Mena mɔ te ŋolo bɛɛ mo soloŋ wɔle do wonembu tɔne ogele bene man ya. Ɔ yɛɛ mena na soloŋ wɔle keŋ gyae ke tao wonembu tɔne ogele keŋ lii na soloŋ keŋ ke leki, na wonembu tɔne ogele keŋ mɔ ke wɔlɛɛ.

eng: Instead, new wine must be put into new wineskins.
local: Mena dɔɔ kaboena bo nyi baa mo soloŋ wɔle do wonembu tɔne ogele wɔle man.

eng: No one who drinks a well-aged wine wants new wine, but says, The well-aged wine is better.
local: Te ŋolo be kyaa keŋ nyi ɔ nyɔɔ soloŋ apaesa na, waa yako nyi ɔ ne gyae ntoŋkum waa nyɔɔ bela ya. Nawolo nyi ɔ ne yako nyi apaesate keŋ yɛna kpaakpaate.

eng: After Jesus finished presenting all his words among the people, he entered Capernaum.
local: Yesu kekolosi nombia kɛŋa fa balaŋ baŋ taŋ nɛ, ɔ gyuu Kapanum donɔɔ man.

eng: A centurion had a servant who was very important to him, but the servant was ill and about to die.
local: Donɔɔ kenaŋ man na, Romantena yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋolo kyaa na ɔ dana tobaale ŋolo ŋon ɔ bɛɛ sɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ ya. Te kawee kaa gyɔɔ mena tobaale kei yeŋ yeŋ.

eng: When the centurion heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders to Jesus to ask him to come and heal his servant.
local: Yoonɔɔtena kegyia kei ke nyii Yesu nkaŋ nɛ, ɔ kpila Gyudatena kegyiise baale gyu ɔ gyaŋ nyi waa kɔŋ kaa kyɔ ɔ tobaale ŋon fa e.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they earnestly pleaded with Jesus. He deserves to have you do this for him, they said.
local: Keŋte Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ kaa waase e nideli nyiaa, Yɛɛ kakyeŋ na n ke kyɔ balee kei fa daa.

eng: He loves our people and he built our synagogue for us.
local: nawolo nyi ɔ ne gyae de tɛɛle kei deeli, te ŋon gba maana dɔ ɔsom deni fa daa.

eng: Jesus went with them. He had almost reached the house when the centurion sent friends to say to Jesus, Lord, dont be bothered. I dont deserve to have you come under my roof.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu sila wɔ gyu. Ɔ ka benaa yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon dɛɛ nɛ, ɔ kpila ɔ gyoona gyu keyako Yesu nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, na haa n wose ya nawolo nyi ma tekaboena nyi nya kɔŋ me dɛɛ ya.

eng: Soon afterward, Jesus traveled through the cities and villages, preaching and proclaiming the good news of Gods kingdom. The Twelve were with him.
local: Kedi weeya ala nɛ Yesu gyuuwɔ ke kila donɔɔse na akuraase pou man, na ɔ ne yako Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa balaŋ, na ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ mɔ silana e.

eng: In fact, I didnt even consider myself worthy to come to you. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: mena ke te ma gyeŋ nyi maŋ gbagba mɔ tekaboena nyi maa kɔŋ n gyaŋ ya. Mena dɔɔ nyaŋ yako nombii dokoloŋ bo, me tobaale ŋon waa koro.

eng: along with some women who had been healed of evil spirits and sicknesses. Among them were Mary Magdalene.
local: Na ala baale baŋ ɔ be kyɔ wɔ be kaweese te ɔ be gegi feliŋkumɛɛ lee be baale man nɛ mɔ kpuɛ na e. Be man baale yɛna Mɛɛre ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Magedala, ŋon Yesu be gegi feliŋkumɛɛ nyetooro lee ɔ man nɛ.

eng: Im also a man appointed under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Nawolo nyi maŋ gbagba mɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon me doo baale tɛɛ, te yoonɔɔtena mɔ doo me tɛɛ. Me yako ŋolo nyi waa gyu yenaŋ daale na ɔ ne gyu, te me yako ŋolo nyi waa kɔŋ na ɔ ne kɔŋ. Kpene kamasɛ keŋ me yako be man ŋolo nyi waa yɛɛ na, keŋ te ɔ ne yɛɛ.

eng: Joanna , Susanna, and many others who provided for them out of their resources.
local: na Gyoana ŋon ɔ baale yɛna Kusa nɛ. Kusa kei yɛɛ kegyia ŋolo e gyoo Herode dekpaŋalaŋ man, na Susana na ala burum baŋ bɔɔ mo ba abɔɔ kɛɛ Yesu na ɔ kaseela dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: When Jesus heard these words, he was impressed with the centurion. He turned to the crowd following him and said, I tell you, even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Yesu ke nyii nɔɔwoya kɛŋa nɛ, doo ɔ nɔɔ te ɔ gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ balaŋ dikpii keŋ be silana e nɛ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, Iserae tɛɛle kei dɔɔ pou nɛ ma te naa ta walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di ŋgba balee kei nɛ ya.

eng: When a great crowd was gathering and people were coming to Jesus from one city after another, he spoke to them in a parable.
local: Debaŋ keŋ balaŋ burum kpu na baŋ bɔɔ lee debiise man kɔŋ be kaa yilaa Yesu gyaŋ nɛ, ɔ gyɔɔ dudu fa wɔ nyiaa.

eng: When the centurions friends returned to his house, they found the servant restored to health.
local: Keŋte yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon kpilala baŋ besewɔ gyu be dɛɛ. Be gyuuwɔ na ɔ tobaale ŋon ta koro.

eng: A farmer went out to scatter his seed. As he was scattering it, some fell on the path where it was crushed, and the birds in the sky came and ate it.
local: Wɔɔfarale ŋolo gyuuwɔ ke ŋmatee abɔɔ bia ŋgba amɔla nɛ ɔ wɔɔman. Ɔ kɛɛ ŋmatee ŋa nɛ ŋaale yalawɔ wɔɔ keŋ gbɛɛneŋ man te balaŋ tɛɛwɔ ŋe dɔɔ sakatee ŋa laŋ, te gyebuse kaa tɔɔsee ŋa di.

eng: A little later Jesus went to a city called Nain. His disciples and a great crowd traveled with him.
local: Kei wɔle nɛ Yesu gyuu donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Nai nɛ man. Ɔ kaseela baŋ na balaŋ burum sila e gyu botɔɔ.

eng: Other seed fell on rock. As it grew, it dried up because it had no moisture.
local: Ŋaale mɔ yalawɔ kpanaŋse dɔɔ te ŋe tuuwɔ, mɔna te kyare ya te ŋa yekeewɔ. Nawolo nyi tɛɛle ta boo kpanaŋse ŋan dɔɔ na ke lɛɛ loŋ see ya.

eng: As he approached the city gate, a dead man was being carried out. He was his mothers only son, and she was a widow. A large crowd from the city was with her.
local: Yesu ka betaa benaa donɔɔ keŋ nɔɔman nɛ, te ɔ naa ba seelena woo ŋolo bɛɛ kɔŋ be kaa wuu. Na woo ŋonaŋ waageŋ yɛna bu baale ŋon ɔ naa ba lolo te ɔ naa ŋon mɔ yɛɛ lialo e. Na donɔɔtena baŋ burum silana alo ŋon.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorns grew with the plants and choked them.
local: Bia ŋan ŋaale mɔ yalawɔ kpalamse man te ŋe tuuwɔ, te kpalamse ŋan mɔ tuuwɔ milii ŋa.

eng: When he saw her, the Lord had compassion for her and said, Dont cry.
local: De Gbeŋgyoo Yesu kena alo ŋon nɛ waraŋase kyaŋa e te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Alo na wii ya.

eng: Still other seed landed on good soil. When it grew, it produced one hundred times more grain than was scattered. As he said this, he called out, Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Ŋaale mɔ yalawɔ tɛɛle kpaakpaa dɔɔ te ŋe tuuwɔ bee, te ŋa see bia burum. Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ŋon ɔ dana deŋele waa nyii na, waa tei nideli.

eng: He stepped forward and touched the stretcher on which the dead man was being carried. Those carrying him stood still. Jesus said, Young man, I say to you, get up.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu gyuuwɔ ke tina woo ŋon, te baŋ ba seelena e nɛ seŋawɔ. Keŋte Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, Tobaale, koro.

eng: His disciples asked him what this parable meant.
local: Keŋte Yesu kaseela baŋ yako e nyi waa wola wɔ dudu keŋ asɛɛ.

eng: The dead man sat up and began to speak, and Jesus gave him to his mother.
local: Keŋte woo ŋon korowɔ kyaa te ɔ fiasɛɛ ɔ ne kolosi, te Yesu besena e fa ɔ naa.

eng: He said, You have been given the mysteries of Gods kingdom, but these mysteries come to everyone else in parables so that when they see, they cant see, and when they hear, they cant understand .
local: Te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ ta toro ya agyueŋse na ɛ ka gyeŋ nombia ŋan weesɛɛ lee ɔ gyoori keŋ wose man. Mɔna balaŋ akaŋ baŋ dɔɔ aduya kaageŋ man te maa kolosi fa wɔ. Baa kɛo na baa kɛo mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa naa ya, te baa tei na baa tei mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa nyii asɛɛ ya. .

eng: Awestruck, everyone praised God. A great prophet has appeared among us, they said. God has come to help his people.
local: Nombia kɛŋa yɛɛ walaŋ kamasɛ gyakoloŋ te ba lese Wurubuarɛ yele yako nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre dinaa ŋolo te kaa bɛɛ lee de gyaŋ, te ba besewɔ yako nyiaa Wurubuarɛ te kɔŋ kaa kyɔ ɔ balaŋ.

eng: The parable means this: The seed is Gods word.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, dudu keŋ mɔɔ gyɔɔ nɛ ka asɛɛ yaa. Abɔɔ bia ŋan seŋɛɛ fa Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan.

eng: This news about Jesus spread throughout Judea and the surrounding region.
local: Yesu wose man nombia kɛŋa yaaseewɔ Gyudia tɛɛle dɔɔ pou na tɛɛlese ŋan pou benaa na botɔɔ nɛ.

eng: The seed on the path are those who hear, but then the devil comes and steals the word from their hearts so that they wont believe and be saved.
local: Bia ŋan be yale gbɛɛnaa man nɛ, seŋɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ bee nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan te ɔbɔnsam ne kɔŋ kaa ŋmɛɛle ŋa lee be konɔɔse man, na bɔɔ nyiŋ lɛɛ ŋa di na Wurubuarɛ ke lɛɛ ba nyee ya nɛ.

eng: Johns disciples informed him about all these things. John called two of his disciples.
local: Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre kaseela gyuuwɔ keyako e abɔɔ ŋan pou Yesu be yɛɛ nɛ.

eng: The seed on the rock are those who receive the word joyfully when they hear it, but they have no root. They believe for a while but fall away when they are tempted.
local: Bia ŋan be yale kpanaŋse dɔɔ nɛ seŋɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ bee nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan te bɛɛ lɛɛ ŋa na gyoŋ, mɔna ŋe bee nyiŋ lila be man ya. Bɛɛ lɛɛ ŋa di debaŋ kyomii bo, te akpaa ɔbɔnsam de kɛɛse wɔ kɛɛ na ba te yale.

eng: and sent them to the Lord. They were to ask him, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: Keŋte Gyɔn kpila ɔ kaseela bala baale nyi baa gyu ke bɔɔse de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi nyaŋ kekɔŋ nɛ yaa dɛɛ kɛɛ walaŋ wɔle gbɛɛ.

eng: As for the seed that fell among thorny plants, these are the ones who, as they go about their lives, are choked by the concerns, riches, and pleasures of life, and their fruit never matures.
local: Bia ŋan be yale kpalamse man nɛ seŋɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ bee nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan, te tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nombia, na kɔba kegyae beiŋ, na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ akaŋ ŋan kegyae da gyoo be man nɛ, na bee yeli baa bee Kristo man ya.

eng: When they reached Jesus, they said, John the Baptist sent us to you. He asks, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: Gyɔn kaseela baŋ ke gyu Yesu gyaŋ nɛ be yako e nyiaa, Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon kpila na daa n gyaŋ nyi de kaa bɔɔse neŋ nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi nyaŋ kekɔŋ nɛ yaa dɛɛ kɛɛ walaŋ wɔle siaman? .

eng: The seed that fell on good soil are those who hear the word and commit themselves to it with a good and upright heart. Through their resolve, they bear fruit.
local: Abɔɔ bia ŋan be yale tɛɛle kpaakpaa dɔɔ nɛ seŋɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ bee nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan, te bɛɛ lɛɛ ŋa do konɔɔ kpaakpaa man, te bɛɛ tela ŋa nideli te bɛɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ di ŋe dɔɔ kelii debaŋ keŋ nombia ŋan ne yeli bɛɛ yɛɛ abɔɔ kpaakpaa nɛ.

eng: Right then, Jesus healed many of their diseases, illnesses, and evil spirits, and he gave sight to a number of blind people.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ man na Yesu te kyɔ balaŋ burum kaweese lese wɔ lee be kayeyɛɛ man. Ɔ gegi feliŋkumɛɛ lee baale mɔ man, te ɔ kyɔɔ siayɛlɛɛsatena burum mɔ.

eng: No one lights a lamp and then covers it with a bowl or puts it under a bed. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand so that those who enter can see the light.
local: Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu fa wɔ nyiaa, Ŋolo bɛɛ kyaŋee kanea na waa mo kolo wu ke dɔɔ yaa waa mo ke do mpa ateta ya. Mɔna ɔ na mo ke bo gyakaa kolo dɔɔ adido na kena dekpaŋalaŋ pou, na baŋ bɛɛ kɔŋ na baa nyiŋ naa.

eng: Then he replied to Johns disciples, Go, report to John what you have seen and heard. Those who were blind are able to see. Those who were crippled now walk. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. And good news is preached to the poor.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu tiranɔɔ fa Gyɔn kpilala baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ bese gyu keyako Gyɔn kpene keŋ ɛ be naa na ɛ sia, na ŋan ɛ be nyii na ɛ deŋela. Siayɛlɛɛsatena ne naa, ɔtakasese ne tɛɛ, baŋ bɛɛ wee kawee yayam nɛ kaweese te taŋ, te deŋele atɔɔsatena ne nyii. Woya ne foŋii, te bɛɛ yako Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan mɔ fa ayematena.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be exposed. Nor is anything concealed that wont be made known and brought to the light.
local: Kolo ba weesɛɛ keŋ be gyae ka lee debɔɔ ya, te kolo mɔ be wuɛ keŋ nyi be gyae ka lee debɔɔ ya.

eng: Happy is anyone who doesnt stumble along the way because of me.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ aŋmaareŋ nyi maŋ yɛna ŋon Wurubuarɛ be kpili ya nɛ, ŋon na nyeebam.

eng: Therefore, listen carefully. Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they seem to have will be taken away from them.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose nideli lee mena keŋ ɛ na mo tei nombia ŋan. Nawolo nyi nyaŋ ŋon n dana kolo na Wurubuarɛ waa kpu neŋ burum, te nyaŋ ŋon n be dana kolo ya na kyomii keŋ n na gyueŋ nyi n dana koraŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa lɛo ke lee n nyiŋmaa man.

eng: After Johns messengers were gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John. What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Gyɔn kpilala baŋ ka bese laŋ nɛ, Yesu kolosi Gyɔn wose man nombia fa balaŋ dikpii keŋ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ ke gyu Gyɔn gyaŋ depampaa dinaa keŋ man nɛ, woŋ te ɛ gyaewɔ nyi ɛ kena? Ŋale keŋ feliŋ ne gyɔ ke wosi nɛ?.

eng: Jesus mother and brothers came to him but were unable to reach him because of the crowd.
local: Yesu ɔ naa na ɔ naabia kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ, mɔna balaŋ dikpii keŋ dɔɔ be te nyiŋ baa benaa e ya.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who dress in fashionable clothes and live in luxury are in royal palaces.
local: Akpaa na keŋ ya na, woŋ te ɛ gyuuwɔ ɛ kaa kɛɛ? Walaŋ ŋon ɔ doona abɔɔ kpaakpaa nyiŋan yaa? Baŋ bee mia mena te bee di gyoŋ nɛ ne kyaa bo gyooneŋ akpaŋalaŋ man.

eng: Someone told him, Your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to see you.
local: Botɔɔ te ŋolo yako Yesu nyiaa, N naa na n naabia seŋɛɛ debɔɔ man bɛɛ gyae baa naa neŋ.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Ɛ yako maŋ maa nyii, woŋ te nafɔ ɛ gyuuwɔ nyi ɛ kaa kɛɛ? Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre? Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi, ŋon ɛ be naa nɛ gba kela Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre.

eng: He replied, My mother and brothers are those who listen to Gods word and do it.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Me naa na me naabia yɛna baŋ bee nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan te bee di ŋe dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: He is the one of whom its written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: Nawolo nyi Gyɔn yɛna Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa, Mee kpila me kpilale keta n siaman, ŋon ne gyae la waa desina n gbɛɛ fa neŋ. .

eng: One day Jesus and his disciples boarded a boat. He said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake. So they set sail.
local: Wee daale nɛ Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ yela dɛɛ toŋ gyu boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ. Mena dɔɔ ba gyoowɔ degbele man te be laŋawɔ.

eng: I tell you that no greater human being has ever been born than John. Yet whoever is least in Gods kingdom is greater than he.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ lolo tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, ŋolo be kyaa keŋ nyi ɔ kela Gyɔn ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man nɛ, walaŋ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ kamasɛ e koraŋ nɛ ɔ kela Gyɔn ŋonaŋ.

eng: While they were sailing, he fell asleep. Gale-force winds swept down on the lake. The boat was filling up with water and they were in danger.
local: Debaŋ keŋ be kyaa degbele keŋ man bee gyu nɛ te dosoroŋ moo Yesu. Keŋte feliŋ dinaa daale korowɔ ne gyɔɔ loŋ keŋ dɔɔ, mena dɔɔ ke gyɔɔ loŋ do degbele keŋ man te Yesu kaseela baŋ sia yɛɛ wɔ nideli.

eng: Everyone who heard this, including the tax collectors, acknowledged Gods justice because they had been baptized by John.
local: Debaŋ keŋ balaŋ baŋ pou na lempoolɛɛra be nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, be lɛɛwɔ do nyi Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ, nawolo nyi be sɛɛwɔ Gyɔn sɔɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

eng: So they went and woke Jesus, shouting, Master, Master, were going to drown! But he got up and gave orders to the wind and the violent waves. The storm died down and it was calm.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu kaseela baŋ gyuuwɔ ke gyuusu e yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, de Gbeŋgyoo dɛɛ gyae dee munu. Botɔɔ te Yesu korowɔ te ɔ fae feliŋ keŋ na loŋ keŋ pou te ŋe yɛɛwɔ yididi.

eng: But the Pharisees and legal experts rejected Gods will for themselves because they hadnt been baptized by John.
local: Mɔna Farasiitena na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ bɛɛ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne gyae nyi waa yɛɛ fa wɔ nɛ, nawolo nyi be bɛɛwɔ nyi Gyɔn na sɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ ya.

eng: He said to his disciples, Where is your faith? Filled with awe and wonder, they said to each other, Who is this? He commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him.
