local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ di ya? Gyakoloŋ kyaŋa wɔ te ba wose yeŋa wɔ te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Walaŋ woŋti yɛna kei? Ɔ ne kolosi fa feliŋ na loŋ koraŋ na ŋee nyii e.

eng: To what will I compare the people of this generation? Jesus asked. What are they like?.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, Woŋ te maa mo gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ maa na? Sena te be doo?.

eng: Jesus and his disciples sailed to the Gerasenes land, which is across the lake from Galilee.
local: Keŋte Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ toŋawɔ gyu Gerasenetena tɛɛle man keŋ kyaa Galelia boo diŋgyiŋ nɛ.

eng: They are like children sitting in the marketplace calling out to each other, We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt cry.
local: Be yɛɛ ŋgba bia kyaa la kebu man bee yoi nɛ, na bɛɛ fae yeesa yako dɔŋa nyiaa, De kpee alowɔle nɔɔneŋse fa ŋon, mɔna ɛ te sɔe ya! De kpee lia nɔɔneŋse mɔ, mɔna ɛ te wii ya.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a certain man met him. The man was from the city and was possessed by demons. For a long time, he had lived among the tombs, naked and homeless.
local: Yesu ka betaa lee degbele keŋ man pɛ, te baale ŋolo ŋon feliŋkumɛɛ doo ɔ man nɛ leewɔ donɔɔ keŋ man kɔŋ kaa gyaŋee na e. Ɔ te tɛɛ diteŋteli kyare te ɔ bɛɛ kyaa dɛɛ ya. Ɔ kyae amuseese man.

eng: John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, He has a demon.
local: Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon kɔŋawɔ na ɔ ne kyaa bake ɔ nɔɔ keŋ ɔ te nyɔɔ soloŋ kamasɛ ya. Te ɛ kpa nyi, Feliŋkumɛɛ doola ɔ man.

eng: When he saw Jesus, he shrieked and fell down before him. Then he shouted, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, dont torture me.
local: Debaŋ keŋ ɔ be naa Yesu nɛ ɔ fae yeesa te ɔ leewɔ yala ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ, na ɔ ne fae yeesa keŋkeŋ nyiaa, Yesu Wurubuarɛ Adidote Bu, woŋ te n ne gyae me gyaŋ? Mɛɛ waase neŋ bo, na naase maŋ diyem ya.

eng: Yet the Human Onecame eating and drinking, and you say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners.
local: Maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon mɔ kɔŋawɔ mee dii mɛɛ nyɔɔ, te ɛ kpa nyi, Ɛ kɛɛ balee kei! Ɔ yɛɛ kedikumte na soloŋnyɔɔre e. Ɔ gyoona yɛna lempoolɛɛra na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra.

eng: He said this because Jesus had already commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. Many times it had taken possession of him, so he would be bound with leg irons and chains and placed under guard. But he would break his restraints, and the demon would force him into the wilderness.
local: Nawolo nyi na Yesu taŋ ke fae feliŋkum keŋ nyi ke dɛɛ lee ɔ man. Debaŋ kamasɛ na feliŋkum keŋ ne naase e diyem, na ba te kyaŋ e bake e agbaraagbaraase ɔ nyiŋmaase na ɔ nawɔɔ man na balaŋ ne gyee e. Keŋ koraŋ na ɔ ne tirii ŋa, na feliŋkum keŋ ta mo e gyu kefula man.

eng: But wisdom is proved to be right by all her descendants.
local: Mɔna baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di nɛ, ta lese wola nyi Wurubuarɛ dana nyansa nideli.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? Legion, he replied, because many demons had entered him.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, N yele yɛna sena? Te balee ŋon tiranɔɔ nyi ma yele yɛna Dikpii Ɔ tiranɔɔ mena nawolo nyi feliŋkumɛɛ burum doo ɔ man.

eng: One of the Pharisees invited Jesus to eat with him. After he entered the Pharisees home, he took his place at the table.
local: Wee daale nɛ Farasii baale ŋolo baake Yesu nyi waa kɔŋ ɔ dɛɛ na ŋon na e ke di bom. Yesu sɛɛwɔ te ɔ gyuuwɔ ke kyaa bee dii.

eng: They pleaded with him not to order them to go back into the abyss.
local: Botɔɔ te feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan waase Yesu nyi, ɔ na gegi wɔ ka do bɔɔ keŋ betaŋ ɔto ya nɛ man ya.

eng: Meanwhile, a woman from the city, a sinner, discovered that Jesus was dining in the Pharisees house. She brought perfumed oil in a vase made of alabaster.
local: Na alo ŋolo kyaa donɔɔ kenaŋ man keŋ ɔ yɛɛ futooli alo e. Ɔ kenyii nyi Yesu kyaa Farasii baale ŋon dɛɛ ɔ ne dii nɛ, ɔ moo nuŋ ofoŋofoŋte daale porontia tɔɔ kɔŋ botɔɔ.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside. The demons begged Jesus to let them go into the pigs. Jesus gave them permission.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na kekuiriise dikpii daale seŋɛɛ bula daale dɔɔ bee dii. Keŋte feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan waase Yesu nyi waa yela be kaa gyoo kekuiriise baŋ man, te Yesu faa wɔ gbɛɛ.

eng: Standing behind him at his feet and crying, she began to wet his feet with her tears. She wiped them with her hair, kissed them, and poured the oil on them.
local: keŋte ɔ kaa seŋawɔ Yesu wɔle benaa na ɔ nawɔɔ. Keŋte ɔ wii siasaraŋ woli Yesu nawɔɔ. Te ɔ moo ɔ nyeenyɔɔse feefo Yesu nawɔɔ ŋenaŋ te ɔ kyaŋa ŋa obuo man, te ɔ toosi nuŋ ofoŋofoŋte keŋ woli ŋa.

eng: and the demons left the man and entered the pigs. The herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Mena dɔɔ feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan leewɔ balee ŋon man ka gyoo kekuiriise baŋ man, te ba dikpii keŋ pou yeuwɔ tisi bula kyoloŋkyoloŋ keŋ gyu ka gyoo loŋ keŋ man te be munuwɔ pou.

eng: When the Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw what was happening, he said to himself, If this man were a prophet, he would know what kind of woman is touching him. He would know that she is a sinner.
local: Farasii baale ŋon ɔ be baake Yesu gyu ɔ dɛɛ kena mena nɛ, ɔ yakowɔ ɔ nyeeman nyiaa, Akpaa balee kei deŋ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre gbagba e na, nafɔ waa gyeŋ alo ŋon ɔ ne tina e nɛ dinɔɔ. Nawolo nyi ɔ yɛɛ dukumyɛɛre e.

eng: When those who tended the pigs saw what happened, they ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɛɛ kekuiriise baŋ dɔɔ kena kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ, ba korowɔ yeu gyu ke yako kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ fa balaŋ baŋ be kyaa donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ na akuraase ŋan man pou.

eng: Jesus replied, Simon, I have something to say to you. Teacher, speak, he said.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Simɔn, me dana kolo maa yako neŋ. Keŋte ɔ yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, yako maŋ.

eng: People came to see what had happened. They came to Jesus and found the man from whom the demons had gone. He was sitting at Jesus feet, fully dressed and completely sane. They were filled with awe.
local: Keŋte balaŋ yeuwɔ gyu be kaa kɛɛ kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ. Ba ke kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ nɛ, be naa walaŋ ŋon nafɔ feliŋkumɛɛ doo ɔ man nɛ baŋɛɛ Yesu nawɔɔ tɛɛ, na ɔ wose te fɛɛ e keŋ ɔ doona abɔɔ. Te gyakoloŋ gyoo balaŋ baŋ pou.

eng: A certain lender had two debtors. One owed enough money to pay five hundred people for a days work.The other owed enough money for fifty.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu gyɔɔ e dudu kei nyiaa, Balaŋ bala baale kyaawɔ te be gyuuwɔ ke paa kɔba lee kɔbate ŋolo gyaŋ. Ŋolo wui yɛɛ kɔbabia kakpoŋ yaale, te ŋolo mɔ wui yɛɛ sɔŋola na kufu.

eng: Those people who had actually seen what had happened told them how the demon-possessed man had been delivered.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ naa kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ yako baŋ bɔɔ kɔŋ nɛ mena keŋ Yesu be kyɔ balee ŋon kawee.

eng: When they couldnt pay, the lender forgave the debts of them both. Which of them will love him more.
local: Be man kamasɛ bɛɛ tale waa tɔ ɔ kom ya, mena dɔɔ balee ŋon moo ba koma ŋan pou kyɛɛ wɔ. Yako maŋ! Balaŋ bala kɛwɔ na, be man woŋti ne gyae la waa gyae kɔbate ŋon nombia nideli.

eng: Then everyone gathered from the region of the Gerasenes asked Jesus to leave their area because they were overcome with fear. So he got into the boat and returned across the lake.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ pou be kyaa Gerasene tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ waase Yesu nyi waa lee be gyaŋ nawolo nyi gyakoloŋ dinaa ta gyoo wɔ. Mena dɔɔ Yesu gyoowɔ degbele keŋ man te ɔ laŋawɔ.

eng: Simon replied, I suppose the one who had the largest debt canceled. Jesus said, You have judged correctly.
local: Keŋte Simɔn tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Mɛɛ lɛɛdi nyi ŋon ɔ kom boe nɛ. Te Yesu yako e nyiaa, N tiranɔɔ nideli.

eng: The man from whom the demons had gone begged to come along with Jesus as one of his disciples. Jesus sent him away, saying.
local: Walaŋ ŋon Yesu be gegi feliŋkumɛɛ lee ɔ man nɛ waase Yesu nyi waa sila e. Mɔna Yesu bɛɛwɔ te ɔ yako e nyiaa.

eng: Jesus turned to the woman and said to Simon, Do you see this woman? When I entered your home, you didnt give me water for my feet, but she wet my feet with tears and wiped them with her hair.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu besena ɔ sia tee alo ŋon te ɔ yako Simɔn nyiaa, N naa alo kei? Me kɔŋa n dɛɛ na n te fa maŋ loŋ maa faafo me nawɔɔ ya. Mɔna alo kei dɔɔ ŋon ta mo ɔ siasaraŋ faafo me nawɔɔ te ɔ moo ɔ nyeenyɔɔse feefo ŋa.

eng: Return home and tell the story of what God has done for you. So he went throughout the city proclaiming what Jesus had done for him.
local: Bese gyu n dɛɛ, na n kaa yako kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ fa neŋ nɛ fa balaŋ. Mena dɔɔ balee ŋon gyuuwɔ donɔɔ keŋ man ke yako balaŋ pou kpene keŋ Yesu be yɛɛ fa e nɛ.

eng: You didnt greet me with a kiss, but she hasnt stopped kissing my feet since I came in.
local: N te pukaa maŋ ya, mɔna lee debaŋ keŋ mɔɔ kɔŋ kɛbo nɛ, alo kei te tinaa ta me nawɔɔ ke pukaa yela ya.

eng: When Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed him, for they had been waiting for him.
local: Yesu ka bese lee boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ kɔŋ nɛ, balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ kaa gyaŋee na e. Nawolo nyi na be popou ne kɛɛ ɔ gbɛɛ.

eng: You didnt anoint my head with oil, but she has poured perfumed oil on my feet.
local: N ta mo nuŋ paŋpaŋ gba tiu ma nyee ya, mɔna alo kei dɔɔ ŋon kyɛɛkee nuŋ woli me nawɔɔ.

eng: A man named Jairus, who was a synagogue leader, came and fell at Jesus feet. He pleaded with Jesus to come to his house.
local: Botɔɔ te balee ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake nyi Gyariose, ŋon ɔ yɛɛ Gyudatena ɔsom deni man kegyia e nɛ kɔŋawɔ kaa buŋ Yesu nawɔɔ tɛɛ sola e nyi waa kɔŋ ɔ dɛɛ.

eng: This is why I tell you that her many sins have been forgiven; so she has shown great love. The one who is forgiven little loves little.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako neŋ n ke nyii nyi ɔ nombiakumɛɛ ŋan pou ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ ta mo ŋa kyɛɛ e, mena dɔɔ te ɔ ne gyae me nombia nideli. Nyaŋ ŋon ŋolo da mo n dukum kyomii kyɛɛ neŋ na, n ne gyae ɔtenate nombia bo kyomii.

eng: because his only daughter, a twelve-year-old, was dying. As Jesus moved forward, he faced smothering crowds.
local: Nawolo nyi ɔ bu alebu dokoloŋ kooŋ ŋon ɔ be di kulutooneŋ kufu ala nɛ ne gyae waa yeŋ. Yesu kee gyuu keŋ nɛ na balaŋ baŋ be silana e te yɛɛ nsendee ya, mena dɔɔ na ɔ bee nyiŋ gbɛɛ gba waa besena ɔ wose ya.

eng: Then Jesus said to her, Your sins are forgiven.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako alo ŋon nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ ta mo n nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ neŋ.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years. She had spent her entire livelihood on doctors, but no one could heal her.
local: Na alo ŋolo kpuɛ balaŋ burum baŋ be silana Yesu nɛ man keŋ fatabo ne gyɔ e kulutooneŋ kufu ala yaa, mɔna ŋolo te tale kyɔ e ya.

eng: The other table guests began to say among themselves, Who is this person that even forgives sins.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ bee dii nɛ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Amɔte yɛna kei, ŋon ɔ na mo nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ.

eng: She came up behind him and touched the hem of his clothes, and at once her bleeding stopped.
local: Alo ŋon moowɔ balaŋ baŋ man ka lee Yesu wɔle te ɔ tinaa ɔ kegba nɔɔbii. Debokenaŋ man te fatabo keŋ ne gyɔ e nɛ karawɔ.

eng: Jesus said to the woman, Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako alo ŋon nyiaa, N kelɛɛdi te yeli n te nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa, ta wosefɛɛreŋ man.

eng: Who touched me? Jesus asked. When everyone denied it, Peter said, Master, the crowds are surrounding you and pressing in on you.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, Amɔte tinaa na maŋ? Balaŋ baŋ pou kebɛɛ nyi be te tina e ya nɛ te Pita yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, balaŋ burum ke kilisi neŋ gyaŋee te bɛɛ tɛɛse dɔŋa kei na, n ne bɔɔse nyi amɔte tinaa na neŋ.

eng: Jesus called the Twelve together and he gave them power and authority over all demons and to heal sicknesses.
local: Wee daale Yesu baake ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ gyaŋee, te ɔ faa wɔ doŋ nyi baa mo di feliŋkumɛɛ dɔɔ na baa kyɔ kaweese.

eng: But Jesus said, Someone touched me. I know that power has gone out from me.
local: Mɔna Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, Ŋolo te tina maŋ. Nawolo nyi ma te naa nyi doŋ daale ta lee me man.

eng: He sent them out to proclaim Gods kingdom and to heal the sick.
local: Keŋte ɔ kpila wɔ nyi baa gyu ke yako Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man nombia na baa kyɔ kaweesetena.

eng: When the woman saw that she couldnt escape notice, she came trembling and fell before Jesus. In front of everyone, she explained why she had touched him and how she had been immediately healed.
local: Alo ŋon kena nyi ɔ be gyae waa tale weese ɔ wose ya nɛ, ɔ moo gyakoloŋ na wose keseree kaa buŋ Yesu nawɔɔ tɛɛ. Botɔɔ te ɔ yako kpene keŋ dɔɔ ɔ be tina Yesu kegba, na mena keŋ ɔ kawee keŋ be taŋ debokenaŋ man nɛ balaŋ baŋ pou siaman.

eng: He told them, Take nothing for the journey—no walking stick, no bag, no bread, no money, not even an extra shirt.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ ne gyu na ɛ na mo kolo na kolo tela ya. Ɛ na mo gbutu yaa akpo yaa weenɛɛ, yaa kɔba, yaa kegba alate gba tela ya.

eng: Daughter, your faith has healed you, Jesus said. Go in peace.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako alo ŋon nyiaa, Me bu, n kelɛɛ maŋ di dɔɔ te n nyiŋa kawee kekyɔ. Ta wosefɛɛreŋ man.

eng: Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: Dekpaŋalaŋ kamasɛ keŋ man ɛ soo te be lɛɛ ŋon atuu na, ɛ kyaa botɔɔ kaboena ɛ ka mo lee donɔɔ kenaŋ man.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, someone came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Dont bother the teacher any longer.
local: Yesu ke gyakaa ɔ ne kolosi nɛ, walaŋ ŋolo lee Gyudatena ɔsom deni man kegyia Gyariose ŋon dɛɛ kaa yako e nyiaa, N bu alebu ŋon ta yem. Na tekaa naase de Gbeŋgyoo ŋon diyem bela ya.

eng: Wherever they dont welcome you, as you leave that city, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Donɔɔ keŋ man ɛ gyu te ba te lɛɛ ŋon ya na, ɛ na lee ke man na ɛ kpiisi ɛ nawɔɔ aŋasa lɔ wɔ na ke di adansɛɛ tia wɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ be gyae waa tina wɔ yela ya.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he responded, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting, and she will be healed.
local: Yesu ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ ɔ yako Gyariose nyiaa, Na yee ya, nyaŋ lɛɛ maŋ di. N bu ŋon waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.

eng: They departed and went through the villages proclaiming the good news and healing people everywhere.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu kaseela baŋ korowɔ gyu akuraa kamasɛ man, na be dɔŋ bɛɛ yako Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan te be kyɔɔ balaŋ kaweese yenaŋ kamasɛ.

eng: When he came to the house, he didnt allow anyone to enter with him except Peter, John, and James, and the childs father and mother.
local: Yesu ke gyu ka gyoo Gyariose dɛɛ nɛ, ɔ te yeli ŋolo ke sila e gyoo deni keŋ man ya. Pita na Gyɔn na Gyemisi na bu ŋon ɔ kya na ɔ naa baageŋ te ɔ moowɔ sila ɔ wose.

eng: Herod the ruler heard about everything that was happening. He was confused because some people were saying that John had been raised from the dead.
local: Galeliatena Gyoo dinaa Herode ke nyii abɔɔ ŋan pou Yesu be yɛɛ nɛ ŋe haa e ɔ konɔɔ man, nawolo nyi balaŋ baale ne yako nyi, Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon ɔ ba yem nɛ foŋala lee yeŋ man.

eng: They were all crying and mourning for her, but Jesus said, Dont cry. She isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: Na balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ yilaa botɔɔ nɛ pou ne wii na bee di walaŋsa. Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ na wii ya, alebu kei ta yeŋa ta ya, ɔ doo dosoroŋ bo.

eng: others that Elijah had appeared, and still others that one of the ancient prophets had come back to life.
local: Baale mɔ kpa Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Elia bɛɛ la lee, te baale mɔ kpa Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra benɛɛ baŋ maŋ ŋolo foŋala lee yeŋ man.

eng: They laughed at him because they knew she was dead.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ pou boŋo Yesu, nawolo nyi ba gyeŋ nyi bu ŋon ta yem.

eng: Herod said, I beheaded John, so now who am I hearing about? Herod wanted to see him.
local: Botɔɔ te gyoo Herode yakowɔ nyiaa, Ma te yela ba te kara Gyɔn nyee, te walaŋ woŋti yɛna ŋon mee nyii ɔ wose man gyakoloŋ nombia kɛŋa nɛ? Mena dɔɔ ɔ yase ɔ sia gyae gbɛɛ keŋ waa mo naa Yesu.

eng: Taking her hand, Jesus called out, Child, get up.
local: Keŋte Yesu kyaŋa alebu ŋon nyiŋmaa man te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Me bu! Koro seŋ.

eng: When the apostles returned, they described for Jesus what they had done. Taking them with him, Jesus withdrew privately to a city called Bethsaida.
local: Debaŋ keŋ kpilala kufu bala baŋ ba bese kɔŋ nɛ, be yako Yesu kpene kamasɛ keŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ. Keŋte Yesu baake wɔ te ŋon na wɔ gyuu donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Bɛtesaida nɛ man.

eng: Her life returned and she got up at once. He directed them to give her something to eat.
local: Botɔɔ te bu ŋon woo besewɔ kɔŋ kaa gyoo ɔ man te ɔ korowɔ seŋ debokenaŋ man. Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyi baa gyae kolo fa bu ŋon na waa di.

eng: When the crowds figured it out, they followed him. He welcomed them, spoke to them about Gods kingdom, and healed those who were sick.
local: Balaŋ dikpii keŋ kenyii nyi ɔ te lam botɔɔ nɛ be sila ɔ gbɛɛ. Be ke gyu kena nɛ ɔ lɛɛ wɔ atuu te ɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man nombia fa wɔ, te ɔ kyɔɔ baŋ be hia kawee kekyɔ nɛ.

eng: Her parents were beside themselves with joy, but he ordered them to tell no one what had happened.
local: Gyakoloŋ gyoo bu ŋon ɔ kya na ɔ naa, te Yesu tɔɔ wɔ nideli nyi be na yako walaŋ na walaŋ kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ ya.

eng: When the day was almost over, the Twelve came to him and said, Send the crowd away so that they can go to the nearby villages and countryside and find lodging and food, because we are in a deserted place.
local: Ke lii balinɔɔ weese nɛ, Yesu kpilala kufu bala baŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa, Yela balaŋ baŋ keta na baa gyu akuraase na donɔɔse ŋan benaa nɛ man, na be kaa gyae weenɛɛ na adoise, nawolo nyi kɛbo kei yɛɛ ŋale nsana keŋ kolo be kyaa ya.

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said, We have no more than five loaves of bread and two fish—unless we go and buy food for all these people.
local: Mɔna Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ fa wɔ kolo baa di. Botɔɔ te be tiranɔɔ nyi, Ŋan pou de dana yɛna bodobodose abiliŋ anoŋ na kpebia bala. Yaa n ne gyae nyi dee gyu ke lɛɛ weenɛɛ kɔna balaŋ dikpii kei pou.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Seat them in groups of about fifty.
local: Na baala baŋ baageŋ baa yɛɛ ŋgba balaŋ kakpoŋse banoŋ nɛ. Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kpilala baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ yela balaŋ baŋ ke kyaa dikpii, dikpii. Dikpii kamasɛ dɛɛ yɛɛ balaŋ sɔŋola na kufu .

eng: They did so, and everyone was seated.
local: Keŋte kpilala baŋ yela balaŋ baŋ pou kyakeewɔ ŋgba mena keŋ Yesu be yako nɛ.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, and broke them and gave them to the disciples to set before the crowd.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu moo bodobodose abiliŋ anoŋ ŋan na kpebia bala baŋ te ɔ gyinaa ɔ nyee adido, te ɔ fanewɔ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ te ɔ kɛrɛɛ ŋa te ɔ moo ŋa fa ɔ kpilala baŋ nyi baa mo kpɛlɛɛ balaŋ baŋ.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and the disciples filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Balaŋ baŋ pou diiwɔ baa, te be tɔɔsee weenɛɛ buruburu akaŋ ŋan na be nyiŋa doŋase kufu ala .

eng: Once when Jesus was praying by himself, the disciples joined him, and he asked them, Who do the crowds say that I am.
local: Wee daale Yesu kɛɛ fane waageŋ nɛ ɔ kaseela baŋ gyuuwɔ ke kpu na e te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Balaŋ ne kolosi ma wose man nombia na be kpa maŋ yɛna amɔ.

eng: They answered, John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others that one of the ancient prophets has come back to life.
local: Keŋte ɔ kaseela baŋ tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Balaŋ baale kpa nyaŋ yɛna Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon. Baale mɔ kpa nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Elia, te baale mɔ kpa Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra benɛɛ baŋ man ŋolo foŋala.

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, The Christ sent from God.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ mɔ de? Ɛ kpa maŋ yɛna amɔ? Te Pita tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: Jesus gave them strict orders not to tell this to anyone.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tɔɔ wɔ nideli nyi be na yako nombii kei fa walaŋ na walaŋ ya.

eng: He said, The Human Onemust suffer many things and be rejected—by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts—and be killed and be raised on the third day.
local: Keŋte ɔ besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Kaboena nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu maa naa diyem nideli. Gyudatena kegyiise na Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ baa bɛɛ maŋ. Baa ko maŋ, te weeya atoorote keŋ man na, Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu maŋ lee yeŋ man.

eng: Jesus said to everyone, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross daily, and follow me.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako be popou nyiaa, Akpaa ŋolo ne gyae waa sila maŋ na ɔ na yɛɛ kpene keŋ ŋon gbagba ne gyae ya, mɔna waa mo ɔ ɔpaŋdaŋ seele na waa sila maŋ wee kamasɛ.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will save them.
local: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae waa lɛɛ ŋon gbagba nyeedoŋ na, ke gyae ka lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man. Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ nyeedoŋ da lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man lee maŋ Yesu dɔɔ nɛ waa bese nyiŋ ke.

eng: What advantage do people have if they gain the whole world for themselves yet perish or lose their lives?.
local: Akpaa ŋolo de nyiŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ pou te ɔ nyeedoŋ da lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man na, woŋ tɔnɔɔ te waa nyiŋ?.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words, the Human Onewill be ashamed of that person when he comes in his glory and in the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.
local: Akpaa maŋ Yesu na me nombia de yɛɛ ŋolo desɛŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, kenaŋ na wee keŋ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa bese kɔŋ daa na me kya Wurubuarɛ na Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ gyoorobiiri man nɛ, walaŋ ŋonaŋ mɔ nombia gyae ke yɛɛ maŋ desɛŋ.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi ɛ man baale kyaa kɛbo kei keŋ bɔɔ gyae baa yeŋ ya, kelii debaŋ keŋ baa naa Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ.

eng: About eight days after Jesus said these things, he took Peter, John, and James, and went up on a mountain to pray.
local: Yesu keyako nombia kɛŋa weeya gyanaara wɔle nɛ, ɔ moo Pita na Gyemisi na Gyɔn sila ɔ wose gyu bula dɔɔ ɔ kaa fane.

eng: As he was praying, the appearance of his face changed and his clothes flashed white like lightning.
local: Ɔ kɛɛ fane nɛ, karatetee dokoloŋ te ɔ sia gbɛɛ kyɛɛkeewɔ te wa abɔɔ ŋan ɔ doona nɛ bese abɔɔ wolomɛɛ futututu na ŋɛɛ ŋalakee nɛ polɛpolɛpolɛ.

eng: Two men, Moses and Elijah, were talking with him.
local: Debokenaŋ man te baala bala baale kaa bɛɛwɔ lee Yesu dɔɔ te be kolosiwɔ na e. Balaŋ bala kɛwɔ yɛna Mosesi na Elia.

eng: They were clothed with heavenly splendor and spoke about Jesus departure, which he would achieve in Jerusalem.
local: Be kɔŋawɔ gyoorobiiri man kaa yako Yesu mena keŋ waa yeŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ be bake see nɛ.

eng: Peter and those with him were almost overcome by sleep, but they managed to stay awake and saw his glory as well as the two men with him.
local: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ na Pita na ɔ tebia baŋ bɔɔ sila Yesu gyu nɛ te doo dosoroŋ. Ba ke foŋii nɛ, be naa Yesu gyoorobiiri man na baala bala seŋɛɛ ɔ gyaŋ.

eng: As the two men were about to leave Jesus, Peter said to him, Master, its good that were here. We should construct three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah—but he didnt know what he was saying.
local: Baala bala baŋ ka lee Yesu gyaŋ bee gyu nɛ te Pita yako Yesu nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, dei ŋgba daa mɔ be kɔŋ kɛbo nɛ. Yela dɛɛ baŋ kefɛɛse atooro kɛbo. N wui daale, Mosesi wui daale, te Elia mɔ wui daale. Mɔna ɔ ba gyeŋ kpene keŋ ɔ ne yako nɛ ya.

eng: Peter was still speaking when a cloud overshadowed them. As they entered the cloud, they were overcome with awe.
local: Pita kɛɛ kolosi keŋ nɛ te ŋɔma kaa wuuwɔ be dɔɔ, keŋte gyakoloŋ kyaŋa kaseela baŋ ŋgba ŋɔma ŋan be kaa wuu be dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: Then a voice from the cloud said, This is my Son, my chosen one. Listen to him.
local: Botɔɔ te be nyii nɔɔwoya ŋaale lee ŋɔma ŋan man nyiaa, Kei yɛna me bu ŋon mɔɔ lese e, ɛ nyii e.

eng: Even as the voice spoke, Jesus was found alone. They were speechless and at the time told no one what they had seen.
local: Nɔɔwoya ŋan kekolosi taŋ nɛ, be kɛɛ na Yesu waageŋ seŋɛɛ la te kaseela baŋ wuu be nɔɔ. Ba te yako walaŋ kamasɛ kpene keŋ bɔɔ naa weeya ŋenaŋ man nɛ ya.

eng: The next day, when Jesus, Peter, John, and James had come down from the mountain, a large crowd met Jesus.
local: Tɛɛ kena keŋ bɔɔ lee bula keŋ dɔɔ bɛɛ kɔŋ nɛ, balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ kaa gyaŋee na Yesu.

eng: A man from the crowd shouted, Teacher, I beg you to take a look at my son, my only child.
local: botɔɔ te balee ŋolo fae yeesa lee balaŋ burum baŋ man nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, mɛɛ waase neŋ kɛɛ me bu fa maŋ, nawolo nyi ŋon waageŋ yɛna me bu.

eng: Look, a spirit seizes him and, without any warning, he screams. It shakes him and causes him to foam at the mouth. It tortures him and rarely leaves him alone.
local: Feliŋkum ne kyaa ɔ dɔɔ na ɔ te peti fae yeesa na te fuŋ e ŋmera na ɔ nɔɔ ne tɔ afofose. Ne yɛɛ doŋ pɛte kɛɛ koro ɔ dɔɔ, mɔna ke gyakaa kɛɛ wɔlɛɛ e.

eng: I begged your disciples to throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: Me waase n kaseela baŋ nyi baa gegi feliŋkum keŋ, mɔna ba te tale ya.

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you and put up with you? Bring your son here.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Ao! Ɛ mɛɛ gyɛŋ balaŋ baŋ ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ di ya te ɛ gbɛɛneŋ ba tenɛɛ ya. Weeya aŋmoŋ te kaboena nyi maa kyaa ɛ gyaŋ na maa nyiŋ konɔɔ fa ŋon? Keŋte ɔ yako balee ŋon nyi, Mo n bu ŋon kɔna maŋ.

eng: While he was coming, the demon threw him down and shook him violently. Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, healed the child, and gave him back to his father.
local: Bu ŋon kɛɛ kɔŋ nɛ, feliŋkum keŋ doo ɔ man nɛ kara e lɔ na ne wɔŋee e tokii. Mɔna Yesu fae feliŋkum keŋ te ɔ kyɔɔ bu ŋon kawee te ɔ besena e fa ɔ kya.

eng: Everyone was overwhelmed by Gods greatness. While everyone was marveling at everything he was doing, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: Balaŋ baŋ wose yeŋa wɔ lee Wurubuarɛ doŋ dinaa keŋ bɔɔ naa nɛ dɔɔ. Nombia ŋan Yesu be yɛɛ nɛ doo balaŋ baŋ nɔɔ te be ka seŋ bɛɛ gyueŋ ŋe man nɛ ɔ yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa.

eng: Take these words to heart: the Human Oneis about to be delivered into human hands.
local: Ɛ nyii nombia ŋan mɛɛ gyae maa yako ŋon nɛ nideli. Baa lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon do balaŋ nyiŋmaa man.

eng: They didnt understand this statement. Its meaning was hidden from them so they couldnt grasp it. And they were afraid to ask him about it.
local: Mɔna ɔ kaseela baŋ te nyii kpene keŋ ɔ ne yako wɔ nɛ man ya. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ ta lese wola wɔ na baa nyii asɛɛ ya, mɔna bee yee nyi baa bɔɔse e nombii kenaŋ man.

eng: An argument arose among the disciples about which of them was the greatest.
local: Keŋte aŋmaareŋ gyoo Yesu kaseela baŋ man nyi be man woŋti kela na ɔ dɔɔ.

eng: Aware of their deepest thoughts, Jesus took a little child and had the child stand beside him.
local: Na Yesu gyeŋ kpene keŋ bɛɛ gyueŋ nɛ te ɔ kyaŋa biikyaabii ŋolo kaa seŋ ɔ yenaŋ.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Whoever welcomes this child in my name welcomes me. Whoever welcomes me, welcomes the one who sent me. Whoever is least among you all is the greatest.
local: keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Walaŋ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ biikyaabii kei ma yele man na ɔ te lɛɛ maŋ, te ŋon ɔ lɛɛ maŋ na ɔ te lɛɛ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ. Nawolo nyi walaŋ ŋon ɛ bɛɛ mo e yɛɛ kolo ɛ man ya nɛ, ŋon kela na ɛ popou.

eng: John replied, Master, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he isnt in our group of followers.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyɔn yako Yesu nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, de naa balee ŋolo na ɔ ne gegi feliŋkumɛɛ lee n yele man te de tɔɔ e, nawolo nyi ɔ be kpuɛ de man ya.

eng: But Jesus replied, Dont stop him, because whoever isnt against you is for you.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Ɛ na tɔ e gbɛɛ ya, nawolo nyi ŋon ɔ bee yoo na ŋon ya nɛ, na ɔ seŋɛɛ ɛ wɔle.

eng: As the time approached when Jesus was to be taken up into heaven, he determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Weeya ŋan Wurubuarɛ waa mo Yesu gyu adido kɛɛ benaa nɛ, ɔ yɛɛ wa gyueŋ nyi waa gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man.

eng: He sent messengers on ahead of him. Along the way, they entered a Samaritan village to prepare for his arrival.
local: Keŋte ɔ kpila ɔ kaseela baale nyi baa kyaŋ gbɛɛ gyu Samaria donɔɔ daale man na be kaa desina botɔɔ see e.

eng: but the Samaritan villagers refused to welcome him because he was determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Mɔna balaŋ baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ bɛe nyi bɔɔ lɛɛ e ya, nawolo nyi na ɔ sia gyakae nyi ɔ ne gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man.

eng: When the disciples James and John saw this, they said, Lord, do you want us to call fire down from heaven to consume them.
local: Ɔ kaseela Gyemisi na Gyɔn kena mena nɛ be bɔɔse e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, n ne gyae dɛɛ baake boalaŋ lee adido na kaa kpa wɔ wɔlɛɛ.

eng: But Jesus turned and spoke sternly to them.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu besenawɔ kɛɛ wɔ te ɔ faree wɔ nyi be naa yɛɛ mena ya.

eng: and they went on to another village.
local: Keŋte be popou gbaawɔ gyu akuraa wɔle man.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples traveled along the road, someone said to him, I will follow you wherever you go.
local: Ba ke ta bee gyu keŋ nɛ, walaŋ ŋolo kɔŋawɔ kaa yako Yesu nyiaa, Yenaŋ kamasɛ keŋ n ke gyu nɛ maa sila neŋ gyu.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human Onehas no place to lay his head.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, Nyanyase dana bɔɔneŋ te gyebuse mɔ dana asaase, mɔna Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon betaŋ botɔɔ keŋ waa mo ɔ nyee teeli ya.

eng: Then Jesus said to someone else, Follow me. He replied, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Walaŋ wɔle ŋolo mɔ kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ te Yesu yako e nyi, Sila maŋ. Mɔna walaŋ ŋon yako e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, yela maa taŋgbɛɛ gyu kewuu me kya pɛna me kaa sila neŋ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Let the dead bury their own dead. But you go and spread the news of Gods kingdom.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Yela woya kewuu ba woya, mɔna nyaŋ dɔɔ gyu na n kaa kolosi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man nombia.

eng: Someone else said to Jesus, I will follow you, Lord, but first let me say good-bye to those in my house.
local: Ŋolo mɔ yako Yesu nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo maa sila neŋ, mɔna yela maa gyu kekalaa ma loŋtona pɛ.

eng: Jesus said to him, No one who puts a hand on the plow and looks back is fit for Gods kingdom.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, Nyaŋ walaŋ ŋon n buŋɛɛ fare dɔɔ te n ne gɛɛse kɛɛ n wɔle nɛ, n tekaboena Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man ka gyoo ya.

eng: Jesus was praying in a certain place. When he finished, one of his disciples said, Lord, teach us to pray, just as John taught his disciples.
local: Wee daale Yesu gyuu yenaŋ daale ɔ ne fane. Ɔ kefane taŋ nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ man ŋolo yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, wola daa mena keŋ dɛɛ fane ŋgba mena keŋ Gyɔn ba wola ɔ kaseela nɛ.

eng: Jesus told them, When you pray, say: Father, uphold the holiness of your name. Bring in your kingdom.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ ne gyae ɛ ke fane na ɛ yako nyiaa, De kya, dɛɛ fane nyi balaŋ pou baa lese n yele Lese n gyoori debɔɔ.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Fa daa wee kamasɛ weenɛɛ ŋan ne hia daa nɛ.

eng: Forgive us our sins, for we also forgive everyone who has wronged us. And dont lead us into temptation.
local: Mo de nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ daa, nawolo nyi daa mɔ na mo kyɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ tia daa nɛ. Na yeli dɛɛ yala kekɛɛsekɛɛ man ya. .

eng: He also said to them, Imagine that one of you has a friend and you go to that friend in the middle of the night. Imagine saying, Friend, loan me three loaves of bread.
local: Yesu besewɔ yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ yela dɛɛ mo ke nyi n dana gyoo, te n gyu ɔ dɛɛ tɛɛ nsana ke gyɔ ɔ disim yako e nyi Me gyoo, waaseda paa maŋ weenɛɛ kyomii.

eng: because a friend of mine on a journey has arrived and I have nothing to set before him.
local: Nawolo nyi me gyoo ŋolo ta lee gbɛɛ kɔŋ me gyaŋ, te mɔɔ dana kolo na kolo maa fa e ya.

eng: Imagine further that he answers from within the house, Dont bother me. The door is already locked, and my children and I are in bed. I cant get up to give you anything.
local: Na n gyoo ŋon te tiranɔɔ deni tɔɔman nyi, Na haa maŋ ya, me taŋ ketɔ me disim te daa na me bia mɔ taŋ kedoo. Mɔɔ gyae maa tale koro kaa fa neŋ kolo ya. .

eng: I assure you, even if he wouldnt get up and help because of his friendship, he will get up and give his friend whatever he needs because of his friends brashness.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Akpaa nyi ŋon na e gyoori kaageŋ wui dɔɔ nɛ nafɔ ɔ be gyae waa koro ya. Mɔna mena keŋ ɔ ne gyɔ disim keŋ kakyekyemaare man dɔɔ nɛ, waa koro toro na waa fa e kpene keŋ ɔ ne gyae nɛ.

eng: And I tell you: Ask and you will receive. Seek and you will find. Knock and the door will be opened to you.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyi ɛ sola Wurubuarɛ kolo na, ɛ nyiŋmaa gyae ke kaŋ ke. Ɛ kɛo kolo lee ɔ gyaŋ na ɛ ke nyiŋ ke, te ɛ gyɔ e disim mɔ na waa toro fa ŋon.

eng: Everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. To everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ bɔɔse e kolo na ɔ nyiŋmaa ne kaŋ ke, te ŋon ɔ gyae kolo lee ɔ gyaŋ na ɔ ne nyiŋ ke, te ŋon mɔ ɔ gyɔ e disim na waa toro fa e.

eng: Which father among you would give a snake to your child if the child asked for a fish?.
local: Lolala, ɛ man woŋti kyaa la keŋ nyi ɔ bu da sola e kpebii na, waa mo dom fa e?.

eng: If a child asked for an egg, what father would give the child a scorpion?.
local: Yaa ɔ sola e wɔe na, waa mo kana fa e?.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will the heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him.
local: Akpaa ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ koraŋ gyeŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa ka mo fa ɛ bia na, de yaa sena te ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ, ba mo ɔ feliŋ do baŋ bɛɛ sola e nɛ man nideli ya.

eng: Jesus was throwing out a demon that causes muteness. When the demon was gone, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed.
local: Wee daale Yesu gberaa feliŋkum keŋ ne yeli walaŋ bɛɛ tale kolosi ya nɛ lee balee ŋolo man. Feliŋkum keŋ ka lee nɛ, balee ŋon kolosiwɔ te doo balaŋ dikpii keŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ pou nɔɔ.

eng: But some of them said, He throws out demons with the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ baale yakowɔ nyiaa, Feliŋkumɛɛ pou gyoo Bɛlisɛbu faa na balee kei doŋ te ɔ ne tale gegi feliŋkumɛɛ nɛ.

eng: Others were testing him, seeking a sign from heaven.
local: Keŋ baale mɔ ne gyae baa teese Yesu nɔɔman dɔɔ be yako e nyi waa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia, na kawola nyi ɔ lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he said to them, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland, and a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Mɔna Yesu gyeŋ kpene keŋ bɛɛ gyueŋ nɛ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Tɛɛle kamasɛ keŋ ke man balaŋ de kpase ba wose kpookpoo bee yoo na dɔŋa na, tɛɛle kenaŋ be gyae ke de ya. Mena mɔ te dekpaŋalaŋ daale man balaŋ de kpase ba wose kpookpoo bee yoo na dɔŋa na, dekpaŋalaŋ kenaŋ ne yɛlɛɛ bo.

eng: If Satan is at war with himself, how will his kingdom endure? I ask this because you say that I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul.
local: Akpaa nyi ɔbɔnsam gyoori keŋ man balaŋ de kpase ba wose kpookpoo bee yoo na dɔŋa nɛ, sena te ɔ gyoori keŋ gyae ke tale seŋ? Keŋ dɔɔ mɛɛ yako kei yɛna nyi, ɛmɛɛ kpa feliŋkumɛɛ pou gyoo Bɛlisɛbu faa na maŋ doŋ te mɛɛ mo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ.

eng: If I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Akpaa nyi Bɛlisɛbu doŋ te mɛɛ mo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ na, amɔte doŋ yaa ɛ silala baŋ na mo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ? Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ silala ne gyae la baa bu ŋon fɔɔ.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the powerof God, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Te akpaa Wurubuarɛ doŋ te mɛɛ mo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ na, kenaŋ na ɛ wulaa gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ taŋ kekɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ.

eng: When a strong man, fully armed, guards his own palace, his possessions are secure.
local: Akpaa doŋte ŋolo de desina ɔ wose mo yoo abɔɔ ɔ na deke ɔ dɛɛ nɛ, ŋolo be tale kaa mo ɔ kolo ya.

eng: But as soon as a stronger one attacks and overpowers him, the stronger one takes away the armor he had trusted and divides the stolen goods.
local: Mɔna akpaa walaŋ ŋon ɔ dana doŋ kela e de kɔŋ kaa yokii na e te ɔ di ɔ dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ ne lɛo ɔ yoo abɔɔ ŋan ɔ na mo ɔ wose lɔ ŋe dɔɔ nɛ pou, na ɔ te kuu wa abɔɔ pou ke kpɛlɛɛ balaŋ.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me, scatters.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ be kpuɛ na maŋ ya nɛ na ɔ ne kɔla maŋ, te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon mɔ ɔ bɛɛ kyɔ maŋ na dee yilaa balaŋ fa Wurubuarɛ ya nɛ, ɔ ne yaasee wɔ bo.

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any. Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Feliŋkum da lee walaŋ man nɛ, ɔ ne tɛɛ kila kɛo tɛɛle awolɛɛ ɔ ne gyae dekyae. Akpaa ɔ te nyiŋ dekyae ya na ɔ ne yako ɔ wose nyiaa, Maa bese gyu ma dekyae bene keŋ mɔɔ lee nɛ man.

eng: When it arrives, it finds the house cleaned up and decorated.
local: Akpaa ɔ kɔŋ kaa naa nyi ba te gbee botɔɔ desina ke nideli keŋ nyi ayimɛɛ kamasɛ be doo ya nɛ.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning.
local: Ɔ na bese gyu ka mo feliŋkumɛɛ nyetooro ŋan ŋe dana nombiakumɛɛ kela e nɛ kpu na ɔ wose na be kaa kyaa walaŋ ŋonaŋ man. Dekɔŋ mena na, walaŋ ŋonaŋ dinɔɔ na bese wɔlɛɛ kela ɔ kekyaa gyaŋgba keŋ.

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, a certain woman in the crowd spoke up: Happy is the mother who gave birth to you and who nursed you.
local: Yesu kɛɛ kolosi nombia kɛŋa nɛ, alo ŋolo gyinaa ɔ nɔɔ balaŋ baŋ man yako nyiaa, Alo ŋon ɔ ba lola neŋ te ɔ be kɛɛle neŋ nɛ, ŋon na nyeebam.

eng: But he said, Happy rather are those who hear Gods word and put it into practice.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Balaŋ baŋ bee nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan te bee di ŋe dɔɔ nɛ, baŋ gba te kaboena nyi be sia dɛɛ gye.

eng: When the crowds grew, Jesus said, This generation is an evil generation. It looks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except Jonahs sign.
local: Balaŋ baŋ ke kɔŋ kaa wuku nɛ Yesu besewɔ yako nyiaa, Gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ. Bɛɛ gyae bo nyi baa naa gyakoloŋ nombia, mɔna mɔɔ gyae maa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia kpu na ŋan be kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Gyona dɔɔ nɛ ya.

eng: Just as Jonah became a sign to the people of Nineveh, so the Human Onewill be a sign to this generation.
local: Mena keŋ Gyona nombia ŋan ba bese kolo kekasee fa Niniwetena nɛ, mena mɔ te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon mɔ me nombia gyae ka bese kolo kekasee fa gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ.

eng: The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with the people of this generation and condemn them, because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: Wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa kɔŋ ɔ kaa di balaŋ nombia nɛ, Seba gyoo ŋon ɔ yɛɛ alo e te ɔ ba lee ata yenaŋ nɛ, waa koro seŋ na waa bu gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ fɔɔ. Nawolo nyi ɔ ke nyii Solomɔn nyansa nombia ŋan nɛ, ɔ tɛɛwɔ leenaŋ tɛɛle ɔto kɔŋ kaa nyii ŋa. Mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ kela gyoo Solomɔn ŋonaŋ kyaa kɛbo mɔna ɛ bee nyii e ya.

eng: The people of Nineveh will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching—and one greater than Jonah is here.
local: Mena mɔ te wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa kɔŋ ɔ kaa di balaŋ nombia nɛ, Niniwetena baa koro seŋ na baa bu gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ fɔɔ. Nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ Gyona be kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan fa wɔ nɛ, be kyɛɛkeewɔ lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man te be kɔŋawɔ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ. Mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ kela Gyona ŋonaŋ nɛ kyaa kɛbo.

eng: People dont light a lamp and then put it in a closet or under a basket. Rather, they place the lamp on a lampstand so that those who enter the house can see the light.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa Ŋolo bɛɛ kyaŋee kanea na waa mo ke weese yaa waa mo kolo wu ke ya. Mɔna ɔ na mo ke bo gyakaa kolo dɔɔ adido na balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɔŋ dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man nɛ ke nyiŋ naa.

eng: Your eye is the lamp of your body. When your eye is healthy, your whole body is full of light. But when your eye is bad, your whole body is full of darkness.
local: Deniwalaŋ kanea yɛna ɔ sia. N sia ne naa na n wose pou yɛɛ keŋmaŋee ke, mɔna n sia de wɔlɛɛ na n wose pou doo ditiŋtɛɛ tuum man.

eng: Therefore, see to it that the light in you isnt darkness.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ kɛɛ nideli nyi keŋmaŋee keŋ doo ɛ man nɛ ba bese ditiŋtɛɛ ya.

eng: If your whole body is full of light—with no part darkened—then it will be as full of light as when a lamp shines brightly on you.
local: Nyi akpaa n wose pou doo keŋmaŋee man te ka yenaŋ daale be doo ditiŋtɛɛ man ya na, kenaŋ na n wose pou gyae ke ŋalakee ŋgba mena keŋ kanea ne ŋalakee nɛ.

eng: While Jesus was speaking, a Pharisee invited him to share a meal with him, so Jesus went and took his place at the table.
local: Yesu kekolosi taŋ nɛ Farasii baale ŋolo baake e gyu ɔ dɛɛ ɔ kaa di, mena dɔɔ ɔ gyuuwɔ kekyaa weenɛɛ ŋan tɛɛ.

eng: When the Pharisee saw that Jesus didnt ritually purify his hands by washing before the meal, he was astonished.
local: Farasii baale ŋon kena nyi Yesu te faafo ɔ nyiŋmaase ŋgba mena keŋ ba beneŋ mmaraa nawolo ya nɛ, doo ɔ nɔɔ.

eng: The Lord said to him, Now, you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and platter, but your insides are stuffed with greed and wickedness.
local: Keŋte de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu yako e nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ Farasiitena ne faafo ɛ leenɔɔse na ɛ nyefalɛɛ wɔle bo, mɔna ŋe man dana ayimɛɛ. Mena te ɛmɛɛ mɔ konɔɔse man doo, ɛ dana sibii te nombiakumɛɛ mɔ te wulu ɛ man.

eng: Foolish people! Didnt the one who made the outside also make the inside?.
local: Ɛmɛɛ ayiŋtena; Ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be yɛɛ kolo wɔle nɛ, ŋon yɛna ke tɔɔman mɔ yee?.

eng: Therefore, give to those in need from the core of who you are and you will be clean all over.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ yɛɛ deeli fa ayematena, na kawola nyi ɛ wose betaŋ ayimɛɛ ya.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You give a tenth of your mint, rue, and garden herbs of all kinds, while neglecting justice and love for God. These you ought to have done without neglecting the others.
local: Ɛmɛɛ Farasiitena ɛ laako! Nawolo nyi ɛ na mo ɛ wɔɔman abɔɔ buruburu gba kufu man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ fa Wurubuarɛ, mɔna ɛ bee di nombia na ŋe gbɛɛ ya te ɛ bɛɛ mo Wurubuarɛ mɔ yɛɛ kolo ya. Kaboena nyi nafɔ yaa yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou, na ɛ ka bese fa ɛ wɔɔman abɔɔ buruburu ŋan kufu man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ bela kpu.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You love the most prominent seats in the synagogues and respectful greetings in the marketplaces.
local: Ɛmɛɛ Farasiitena ɛ laako! Nawolo nyi ɛ ne gyae akyaese kpaakpaa Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man, te ɛ ne gyae mɔ nyi ɛ lii balaŋ man na balaŋ dee buŋ do ŋon nɔɔ.

eng: How terrible for you! You are like unmarked graves, and people walk on them without recognizing it.
local: Ɛ laako! Nawolo nyi ɛ yɛɛ ŋgba yebɔɔse ŋan ba yoo te balaŋ ne tɛɛ ŋe dɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ nyi ŋe yɛɛ yebɔɔse ŋa ya nɛ.

eng: One of the legal experts responded, Teacher, when you say these things, you are insulting us too.
local: Botɔɔ te mmaraa wolala baŋ man ŋolo bɔɔse e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, nya ayakoŋ kɛŋa nɛ ŋgba n tee daa mɔ baa.

eng: Jesus said, How terrible for you legal experts too! You load people down with impossible burdens and you refuse to lift a single finger to help them.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala mɔ ɛ laako! Nawolo nyi ɛ ne bake kasolɔse ŋan kela balaŋ nɛ yere wɔ, mɔna ɛmɛɛ gbagba bɛɛ yɛɛ kolo kyomii kyɔ wɔ na baa tale seele be kasolɔse ŋenaŋ ya.

eng: How terrible for you! You built memorials to the prophets, whom your ancestors killed.
local: Ɛ laako! Nawolo nyi ɛ ne desina Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ yebɔɔse, mɔna ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ naanaɔ koona wɔ.

eng: In this way, you testify that you approve of your ancestors deeds. They killed the prophets, and you build memorials!.
local: Mena dɔɔ kei nawolo baa nyiaa, ɛmɛɛ gbagba te lɛɛ nombia ŋan ɛ naanaɔ be yɛɛ nɛ do. Nawolo nyi ɛ naanaɔ koona Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ, te ɛmɛɛ mɔ kɔŋawɔ ɛ ne desina ba yebɔɔse.

eng: Therefore, Gods wisdom has said, I will send prophets and apostles to them and they will harass and kill some of them.
local: Kei dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ nyansate ŋon kolosiwɔ nyiaa, Maa kpila ma akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra na me kpilala be gyaŋ. Baa ko be man baale, na baa naase be man baale mɔ diyem.

eng: As a result, this generation will be charged with the murder of all the prophets since the beginning of time.
local: Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ bɔɔ koo lee debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ tɛɛle kei nɛ, ka deŋele kewɔŋ gyae ke kɔŋ ɛmɛɛ gyɛŋ balaŋ kɛwɔ dɔɔ.

eng: This includes the murder of every prophet—from Abel to Zechariah—who was killed between the altar and the holy place. Yes, Im telling you, this generation will be charged with it.
local: lee Abɛle kako man kelii Sakaria dɔɔ. Sakaria kei te ba koowɔ Wurubuarɛ kedeesa boe keŋ, na Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ nsana nsana nɛ. Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, kɛŋa pou deŋele kewɔŋ gyae ke kɔŋ ɛmɛɛ gyɛŋ balaŋ kɛwɔ dɔɔ.

eng: How terrible for you legal experts! You snatched away the key of knowledge. You didnt enter yourselves, and you stood in the way of those who were entering.
local: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala ɛ laako! Ɛ ta mo Wurubuarɛ kagyeŋ gbɛɛ weese balaŋ te ɛmɛɛ gbagba mɔ bɛɛ tɛɛ gbɛɛ kenaŋ dɔɔ ya, te ɛ ne tɔ baŋ bɛɛ gyae baa ta gbɛɛ kenaŋ dɔɔ mɔ nɛ gbɛɛ.

eng: As he left there, the legal experts and Pharisees began to resent him deeply and to ask him pointed questions about many things.
local: Yesu ka lee botɔɔ nɛ, mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ moo nombia gyu ke tuŋ e baŋ man te be bɔɔse e nombia burum.

eng: They plotted against him, trying to trap him in his words.
local: mo teese ɔ nɔɔman na baa nyiŋ kyaŋ e.

eng: When a crowd of thousands upon thousands had gathered so that they were crushing each other, Jesus began to speak first to his disciples: Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees—I mean, the mismatch between their hearts and lives.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ man nɛ, balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ kaa yilaa keŋ nae daseŋae be doo ya. Keŋte Yesu fiasɛɛ ɔ ne yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose man nideli na Farasiitena dinɔɔ keŋ ke yɛɛ ŋgba tee keŋ bɛɛ mo do kolo man te ne wuku nɛ. Nawolo nyi bɛɛ yɛɛ ba wose ŋgba be yɛɛ balaŋ kpaakpaawɔ nɛ mɔna bɛɛ beo bo.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing is secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Te kolo be kyaa keŋ bɔɔ wu ke dɔɔ keŋ nyi bɔɔ gyae baa gyuusu ke ya, te nombia aweeseŋ ŋaale mɔ be kyaa keŋ be gyae ka lee debɔɔ ya.

eng: Therefore, whatever you have said in the darkness will be heard in the light, and whatever you have whispered in the rooms deep inside the house will be announced from the rooftops.
local: Mena dɔɔ nombii kamasɛ keŋ ɛ be yako ke ditiŋtɛɛ man nɛ gyae ka lee debɔɔ, te nombii kamasɛ keŋ ɛ be kolosi adadawoya man do ŋolo deŋele man deni tɔɔman nɛ, baa mo ke gyɔ dekpeŋkpeŋ.

eng: I tell you, my friends, dont be terrified by those who can kill the body but after that can do nothing more.
local: Me tebia mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyi ɛ na yee balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ ko wosenaane kaageŋ bo, mɔna bɔɔ tale yɛɛ kolo kpu bela nɛ ya.

eng: Ill show you whom you should fear: fear the one who, after you have been killed, has the authority to throw you into hell. Indeed, I tell you, thats the one you should fear.
local: Mɛɛ gyae maa wola ŋon walaŋ ŋon kaboena nyi yaa yee. Ɛ yee Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ko walaŋ na, ɔ dana doŋ nyi waa fuŋ ɔ woo ka do boalaŋ keŋ bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man. Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, ŋon yɛna walaŋ ŋon kaboena nyi yaa yee.

eng: Arent five sparrows sold for two small coins?Yet not one of them is overlooked by God.
local: Akpelee koya yɛɛ aŋmoŋ? Na kaperɛ ala te bɛɛ yɔɔ akpeleese banoŋ yee? Mɔna Wurubuarɛ bee wolee be man dokoloŋ koraŋ gba ya.

eng: Even the hairs on your head are all counted. Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: Ɛ nyeenyɔɔse koraŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ gyeŋ ŋe yaanɔɔle. Mena dɔɔ ɛ na yee ya, nawolo nyi ɛ dana tɔnɔɔ kela akpeleese burum.

eng: I tell you, everyone who acknowledges me before humans, the Human Onewill acknowledge before Gods angels.
local: Keŋte Yesu kpa, Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ maŋ do denibalaŋ siaman nyi me yɛɛ ɔ Gbeŋgyoo e na, maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon mɔ maa lɛɛ e do Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ siaman adido.

eng: But the one who rejects me before others will be rejected before Gods angels.
local: Mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ te lɛɛ maŋ do denibalaŋ siaman nyi me yɛɛ ɔ Gbeŋgyoo e ya nɛ, maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon mɔ maa bɛɛ e Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ siaman adido.

eng: Anyone who speaks a word against the Human Onewill be forgiven, but whoever insults the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: Nyi akpaa ŋolo de kolosi nombiakumɛɛ tia maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon nɛ Wurubuarɛ waa mo kyɛɛ e, mɔna nyi akpaa ŋolo de kolosi mmusuo nombia tia Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon na, Wurubuarɛ be gyae waa mo kyɛɛ e korakora ya.

eng: When they bring you before the synagogues, rulers, and authorities, dont worry about how to defend yourself or what you should say.
local: Akpaa be wɔŋee ŋon gyu Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man na gyooneŋ na donɔɔ kegyiise siaman nyi be kaa di ɛ nombia na, ɛ na wɔlɛɛ na mena keŋ ɛ ke tira ɛ nɔɔse yaa kpene keŋ ɛ ke yako ya.

eng: The Holy Spirit will tell you at that very moment what you must say.
local: Nawolo nyi debaŋ kenaŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne gyae la waa wola ŋon kpene keŋ ɛ ke yako.

eng: Someone from the crowd said to him, Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.
local: Yesu kee kolosi nɛ, walaŋ ŋolo korowɔ balaŋ dikpii keŋ man yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, yako me kegyia na dɛɛ kpala de kya abɔɔ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Man, who appointed me as judge or referee between you and your brother.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyi Baale, amɔte lese na maŋ nyi maa yɛɛ ɛ nombiadiiri yaa ya abɔɔ kpalare.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Watch out! Guard yourself against all kinds of greed. After all, ones life isnt determined by ones possessions, even when someone is very wealthy.
local: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kɛɛ nideli na ɛ ka deke ɛ wose lee kedikum kamasɛ man, nawolo nyi deniwalaŋ nyeedoŋ be doo abɔɔ burum kenyiŋ man ya.

eng: Then he told them a parable: A certain rich mans land produced a bountiful crop.
local: Keŋte Yesu gyɔɔ dudu kei fa wɔ nyiaa, Kɔbate ŋolo kyaawɔ keŋ ɔ wɔɔman weenɛɛ deewɔ fa e nideli.

eng: He said to himself, What will I do? I have no place to store my harvest!.
local: Keŋte balee ŋonaŋ yako ɔ wose nyiaa, Woŋ te maa yɛɛ, nawolo nyi mɔɔ taŋ botɔɔ keŋ maa see me weenɛɛ bela ya.

eng: Then he thought, Heres what Ill do. Ill tear down my barns and build bigger ones. Thats where Ill store all my grain and goods.
local: Keŋte balee ŋon yako ɔ wose nyi Ma gyeŋ kpene keŋ maa yɛɛ, maa tekerii me kpeese benɛɛ ŋan pou na maa baŋ dinaana na maa see me weenɛɛ na ma abɔɔ pou ŋe man.

eng: Ill say to myself, You have stored up plenty of goods, enough for several years. Take it easy! Eat, drink, and enjoy yourself.
local: Na maa yako ma wose nyiaa, Me dana abɔɔ kpaakpaa burum ŋan gyae ke doo fa maŋ kulutooneŋ burum. Mena dɔɔ maa kyaa fɛɛfo na maa di na maa nyɔɔ na maa di gyoŋ! .

eng: But God said to him, Fool, tonight you will die. Now who will get the things you have prepared for yourself.
local: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ yako e nyiaa, Nyaŋ diyiŋte! Gyɛŋ nelim kei maa lɛo n nyeedoŋ lee n nyiŋmaa man. Na nya abɔɔ ŋan pou n be gyae see nɛ, gyae ka bese amɔte wui.

eng: This is the way it will be for those who hoard things for themselves and arent rich toward God.
local: Keŋte Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, Nnɛ kei te gyae ke kɔŋ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae abɔɔ burum fa ɔ wose tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, mɔna Wurubuarɛ siaman na ɔ be doo kolo man ya nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what you will eat, or about your body, what you will wear.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Mena dɔɔ te mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi Ɛ na naase ɛ wose diyem lee ɛ kekyaa man kedi na kenyɔɔ na abɔɔ ŋan ɛ ka do ya.

eng: There is more to life than food and more to the body than clothing.
local: Nyeedoŋ ne hia kela weenɛɛ, te deniwalaŋ wose mɔ ne hia kela abɔɔ ŋan waa do.

eng: Consider the ravens: they neither plant nor harvest, they have no silo or barn, yet God feeds them. You are worth so much more than birds!.
local: Ɛ kɛɛ gyebuse, bɔɔ ne duu abɔɔ ya te bɔɔ ne kɔɔ ya, te bɔɔ dana kpeese yaa denɛɛ ŋan man baa see weenɛɛ ya, mɔna Wurubuarɛ ne fa wɔ weenɛɛ. Kaboena yaa gyeŋ nyi ɛ wose ne hia kela gyebuse.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Ɛ man woŋti te lee ɔ kayeyɛɛ man nɛ, waa tale mo debaŋ dokoloŋ kooŋ pɛ kpu na ɔ weeya ŋan Wurubuarɛ be fa e nɛ?.

eng: If you cant do such a small thing, why worry about the rest?.
local: Akpaa nyi ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale yɛɛ kolo peperee kei ya na, woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ ne naase ɛ wose diyem abɔɔ akaŋ ŋan dɔɔ?.

eng: Notice how the lilies grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth. But I say to you that even Solomon in all his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: Ɛ kɛɛ mena keŋ kyeŋkyeŋkɔɔse na bee, ŋe bɛɛ yɛɛ tom ya te ŋe bɛɛ loo kanyaŋse mɔ ya. Mɔna mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyi Gyoo Solomɔn na ɔ wosekenyiŋ koraŋ nɛ, ɔ te nyiŋ kyeŋkyeŋkɔɔse kɛŋa man dokoloŋ kyeo ya.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, how much more will God do for you, you people of weak faith!.
local: Weera dɔɔ te ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di nideli ya? Nyi akpaa mena te Wurubuarɛ ne desina ŋalese ŋan ɛ kena ŋa gyɛŋ kyoo na be ta too ŋa na, kenaŋ na ɛ tɔɔse nyi Wurubuarɛ waa tale desina ŋon kela ŋalese ŋenaŋ.

eng: Dont chase after what you will eat and what you will drink. Stop worrying.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na mo ɛ konɔɔ gyakaa kpene keŋ ɛ ke di yaa kpene keŋ ɛ ke nyɔɔ dɔɔ ya. Ɛ na yeyɛɛ na mena abɔɔ ŋenaŋ ya.

eng: All the nations of the world long for these things. Your Father knows that you need them.
local: Nawolo nyi mena abɔɔ kɛŋa te tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ ya nɛ sia ne yɛɛ ŋe dɔɔ, mɔna ɛ kya Wurubuarɛ gyeŋ nyi mena abɔɔ kɛŋa ne hia ɛmɛɛ mɔ.

eng: Instead, desire his kingdom and these things will be given to you as well.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ taŋgbɛɛ mo ɛ loo pou gyakaa Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ dɔɔ. Nyi akpaa ɛ yɛɛ mena na, Wurubuarɛ waa mo abɔɔ akaŋ ŋan pou kpu ŋon. .

eng: Dont be afraid, little flock, because your Father delights in giving you the kingdom.
local: Ɛmɛɛ me balaŋ dikpiibii, ɛ ta boo ya mɔna ɛ na yee ya nawolo nyi dana ɛ kya Wurubuarɛ gyoŋ nyi waa yela ɛ ke di gyoori kpu na e.

eng: Sell your possessions and give to those in need. Make for yourselves wallets that dont wear out—a treasure in heaven that never runs out. No thief comes near there, and no moth destroys.
local: Ɛ yɔɔ ya abɔɔ na ɛ ka mo ŋe kɔba pou fa ayematena. Kei gyae ke yela ɛ ke nyiŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa see ɛ wose Wurubuarɛ man. Ŋmɛɛlate be gyae waa tale gyu botɔɔ ke ŋmɛɛle ya, te kolo be gyae ke tale wɔlɛɛ ŋa mɔ ya.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be too.
local: Ɛ tɔɔse nyi botɔɔ keŋ ya abɔɔ kpaakpaa doo nɛ, botɔɔ te ɛ loo mɔ gyakaa.

eng: Be dressed for service and keep your lamps lit.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ doo ɛ wose dɔɔ nideli na ɛ ke kyaŋee ɛ kanease see.

eng: Be like people waiting for their master to come home from a wedding celebration, who can immediately open the door for him when he arrives and knocks on the door.
local: ŋgba tewulɛɛ baŋ bɛɛ daa be gbeŋgyoo ka lee alowɔle tɛɛ kɔŋ nɛ. Na nyi akpaa ɔ betaa kɔŋ kaa gyɔ disim na baa koro toro e bileŋ.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds waiting up when he arrives. I assure you that, when he arrives, he will dress himself to serve, seat them at the table as honored guests, and wait on them.
local: Tewulɛɛ baŋ be gbeŋgyoo waa kaa naa nyi ba delena nɛ, baŋ na nyeebam. Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, be gbeŋgyoo ŋon waa kpee wa abɔɔ desina ɔ wose na waa yela baa kyakee na waa fa wɔ weenɛɛ baa di.

eng: Happy are those whom he finds alert, even if he comes at midnight or just before dawn.
local: Akpaa nyi be gbeŋgyoo ŋon da bese kɔŋ tɛɛ nsana yaa nebo fuu koraŋ te ɔ naa nyi ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ delena na, baŋ na nyeebam.

eng: But know this, if the homeowner had known what time the thief was coming, he wouldnt have allowed his home to be broken into.
local: Mɔna ɛ tɔɔse nyiaa, akpaa dekpaŋalaŋte deŋ gyeŋ debaŋ keŋ ŋmɛɛlatena baa kɔŋ kaa ŋmɛɛle e nɛ, nafɔ waa doo ɔ wose dɔɔ na bɔɔ nyiŋ kɔŋ kaa kuu wa abɔɔ ya.

eng: You also must be ready, because the Human Oneis coming at a time when you dont expect him.
local: Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi ɛmɛɛ mɔ yaa doo ɛ wose dɔɔ, nawolo nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon mɔ maa kɔŋ bo debaŋ keŋ ɛ loo be gyakaa ya nɛ.

eng: Peter said, Lord, are you telling this parable for us or for everyone.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, daa yaa n gyɔɔ dudu kei fa yaa walaŋ kamasɛ.

eng: The Lord replied, Who are the faithful and wise managers whom the master will put in charge of his household servants, to give them their food at the proper time?.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyi, Amɔte yɛna tobaale ŋon bɛɛ lɛɛ e di te ɔ sia be kare? Ŋon yɛna walaŋ ŋon ɔ gbeŋgyoo ba mo e nyi waa kɛɛ ɔ tewulɛɛ akaŋ baŋ dɔɔ, na waa fa wɔ weenɛɛ debaŋ keŋ kaboena nɛ man.

eng: Happy are the servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: Nyi ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon de kɔŋ kaa naa e nyi ɔ ne yɛɛ ɔ tom ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ kpa waa yɛɛ nɛ, na ŋon na nyeebam.

eng: I assure you that the master will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon waa mo wa abɔɔ pou do tobaale ŋonaŋ nyiŋmaa man nyi waa kɛɛ ŋe dɔɔ.

eng: But suppose that these servants should say to themselves, My master is taking his time about coming. And suppose they began to beat the servants, both men and women, and to eat, drink, and get drunk.
local: Mɔna akpaa tobaale kei de yako ɔ wose nyiaa, Me gbeŋgyoo te kyare ɔ bɛɛ kɔŋ ya, te ɔ gyoo tewulɛɛ na alebia akaŋ baŋ akpanyaŋnyaŋ keyɛɛ te ɔ ne dii te ɔ ne nyɔɔ dɛɛse nɛ.

eng: The master of those servants would come on a day when they werent expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict. The master will cut them into pieces and assign them a place with the unfaithful.
local: ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon da bese kɔŋ wee yaa debaŋ keŋ tobaale ŋon loo be gyakaa ya nɛ, waa wɔŋ tobaale ŋonaŋ deŋele na waa gegi e na waa yela waa naa diyem ŋgba baŋ bɔɔ ne lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di ya nɛ.

eng: That servant who knew his masters will but didnt prepare for it or act on it will be beaten severely.
local: Tobaale ŋon ɔ gyeŋ kpene keŋ ɔ gbeŋgyoo ne gyae te ɔ te desina ɔ wose see na waa yɛɛ ɔ gbeŋgyoo kegyaebii ya nɛ, baa kebii e dambiise nideli.

eng: The one who didnt know the masters will but who did things deserving punishment will be beaten only a little. Much will be demanded from everyone who has been given much, and from the one who has been entrusted with much, even more will be asked.
local: Mɔna tobaale ŋon ɔ ba gyeŋ kpene keŋ ɔ gbeŋgyoo ne gyae ya te ɔ yɛɛ kpene keŋ kaboena ketuku na, baa tuku e bo kyomii. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ba mo abɔɔ burum do ɔ nyiŋmaa man na, baa bɔɔse abɔɔ burum lee ɔ gyaŋ. Te ŋon mɔ ba mo abɔɔ ŋan ŋa boe nideli do e ɔ nyiŋmaa man na, mena ke mɔ te baa bɔɔse lee ɔ gyaŋ.

eng: I came to cast fire upon the earth. How I wish that it was already ablaze!.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Me kɔŋae me kaa do boalaŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, te ma kegyaebii yɛna nyi nafɔ ke deŋ wulaa kɛɛ kpaa.

eng: I have a baptism I must experience. How I am distressed until its completed!.
local: Kaale daale doo daa na maŋ te mɛɛ yeyɛɛ kelii debaŋ keŋ kaale kenaŋ gyae ke taŋ.

eng: Do you think that I have come to bring peace to the earth? No, I tell you, I have come instead to bring division.
local: Ɛ gyeŋ nyi wosefɛɛreŋ yaa ma moowɔ kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ? Koa! Na wosefɛɛreŋ ya, mɔna me kɔŋae na balaŋ kekpase kpookpoo.

eng: From now on, a household of five will be divided—three against two and two against three.
local: Lee gyɛŋ ne gyu nɛ dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ ke man balaŋ yɛɛ balaŋ banoŋ na baa kpase ba wose. Balaŋ batooro baa koro seŋ tia balaŋ bala, na balaŋ bala mɔ ka koro seŋ tia balaŋ batooro.

eng: Father will square off against son and son against father; mother against daughter and daughter against mother; and mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.
local: Kyaɔ baa koro seŋ tia be bia baala te bia baala mɔ baa koro seŋ tia be kyaɔ. Naɔ baa koro seŋ tia be bia alebia te bia alebia mɔ baa koro seŋ tia be naɔ. Awuna baa koro seŋ tia be bia ala te bia ala mɔ baa koro seŋ tia ba awuna.

eng: Jesus also said to the crowds, When you see a cloud forming in the west, you immediately say, Its going to rain. And indeed it does.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako balaŋ dikpii keŋ nyiaa, Ɛ naa ŋɔma da lee weese dɛɛyalae man nɛ, debokenaŋ man na ɛ kpa Ɔga ne kɔŋ na ampaŋ ɔga te ŋɛɛ.

eng: And when a south wind blows, you say, A heat wave is coming. And it does.
local: Te ɛ naa ŋgba feliŋ ne gyɔɔ lee ata yenaŋ na ɛ kpa Weese waa seŋ na ampaŋ te kɔŋ mena.

eng: Hypocrites! You know how to interpret conditions on earth and in the sky. How is it that you dont know how to interpret the present time?.
local: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose! Ɛ ne tale kɛɛ adido na ateta na ɛ ta wola kpene keŋ gyae ke yɛɛ. Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ be tale kɛɛ abɔɔ ŋan ne kɔŋ gyɛŋ nɛ, na ɛ ka wola ŋe man ya?.

eng: And why dont you judge for yourselves what is right?.
local: Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛmɛɛ gbagba be tale gyueŋ naa kpene keŋ dei fa ŋon na ɛ ke yɛɛ ya?.

eng: As you are going to court with your accuser, make your best effort to reach a settlement along the way. Otherwise, your accuser may bring you before the judge, and the judge hand you over to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.
local: Nyi akpaa ŋolo de samenaa neŋ te ɛ ne gyu ɛ kaa di nombia ŋan na, yɛɛ kakyeŋ na ɛ ke desina ya nsana gbɛɛnaa man. Na mena ya na waa mo neŋ gyu nombiadiiri ŋon gyaŋ na ŋon mɔ ka mo neŋ kefa kotɔtɔɔtena na baŋ mɔ kewɔŋee neŋ ketɔ deni.

eng: I tell you, you wont get out of there until you have paid the very last cent.
local: Mɛɛ yako neŋ n ke nyi nyiaa, n be gyae n ka lee deni man botɔɔ ya kaboena n ketɔ kom keŋ baa kara fa neŋ nɛ pou taŋ.

eng: One Sabbath, when Jesus went to share a meal in the home of one of the leaders of the Pharisees, they were watching him closely.
local: Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee daale nɛ, Yesu gyuu Farasiitena siamante ŋolo dɛɛ ɔ kaa di. Ɔ ke kyaa botɔɔ keŋ na, Farasiitena baŋ na deke e baa kɛɛ nyi baa nyiŋ kolo yako lee ɔ wose man.

eng: A man suffering from an abnormal swelling of the body was there.
local: Keŋte balee ŋolo ŋon ɔ ba bokee nɛ kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ.

eng: Jesus asked the lawyers and Pharisees, Does the Law allow healing on the Sabbath or not.
local: te Yesu bɔɔse Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ na Farasiitena baŋ nyiaa, Da mmaraa ne fa gbɛɛ nyi dɛɛ kyɔ walaŋ kawee kefɛɛfowee yaa bɛɛ fa daa gbɛɛ ya.

eng: But they said nothing. Jesus took hold of the sick man, cured him, and then let him go.
local: Mɔna be man ŋolo te gyuusu ɔ nɔɔ ya. Keŋte Yesu kyaŋa kaweete ŋon nyiŋmaa man te ɔ kyɔɔ e kawee te ɔ lɔɔ e gbɛɛ.

eng: He said to them, Suppose your child or ox fell into a ditch on the Sabbath day. Wouldnt you immediately pull it out.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Akpaa ɛ man ŋolo dana bu baale bii yaa wonembu te ɔ yale ditiribuŋ man kefɛɛfowee na, ɔ be gyae waa lese e debokenaŋ man yee.

eng: But they had no response.
local: Mɔna ba te tale tiranɔɔ fa e ya.

eng: When Jesus noticed how the guests sought out the best seats at the table, he told them a parable.
local: Yesu kena mena keŋ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ baake wɔ kɔŋ weenɛɛ ŋan tɛɛ kɛɛ tɔɔsee akyaese kpaakpaa nyiŋan nɛ, ɔ gyɔɔ dudu kei fa wɔ nyiaa.

eng: When someone invites you to a wedding celebration, dont take your seat in the place of honor. Someone more highly regarded than you could have been invited by your host.
local: Akpaa ŋolo de baake neŋ alowɔlere weenɛɛ tɛɛ nɛ na peree dekyae kpaakpaate ya. Nawolo nyi gyae ke tale kɔŋ nyi ɔ te baake walaŋ ŋolo ŋon ɔ kela neŋ nɛ mɔ kɔŋ botɔɔ.

eng: The host who invited both of you will come and say to you, Give your seat to this other person. Embarrassed, you will take your seat in the least important place.
local: Akpaa de kɔŋ mena na walaŋ ŋon ɔ be baake ɛ bala nɛ, waa kɔŋ kaa yako neŋ nyiaa Koro mo n dekyae fa balee kei. Botɔɔ na desɛŋ gyae ke kyaŋ neŋ, na n ke gyu ke kyaa botɔɔ keŋ be taŋ obuo ya nɛ.

eng: Instead, when you receive an invitation, go and sit in the least important place. When your host approaches you, he will say, Friend, move up here to a better seat. Then you will be honored in the presence of all your fellow guests.
local: Mɔna nyi akpaa ŋolo de baake neŋ gyu ɔ dɛɛ nɛ, gyu ke kyaa botɔɔ keŋ be taŋ obuo ya nɛ te akpaa walaŋ ŋon ɔ be baake neŋ de kɔŋ nɛ waa yako neŋ nyiaa, Me gyoo koro ke kyaa dekyae kpaakpaate man. Kei gyae ke fa neŋ obuo balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ baake wɔ nɛ pou siaman.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ gyina ɔ wose na Wurubuarɛ waa yɔkɔse e, te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ yɔkɔse ɔ wose na Wurubuarɛ waa gyina e adido.

eng: Then Jesus said to the person who had invited him, When you host a lunch or dinner, dont invite your friends, your brothers and sisters, your relatives, or rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return and that will be your reward.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako walaŋ ŋon ɔ be baake e gyu ɔ dɛɛ nɛ nyiaa, Akpaa n do kesaa te n ne gyae n ke baake balaŋ kaa di be nɔɔ nɛ, na baake n gyoona yaa n naabia yaa n loŋtona yaa n tebia baŋ be dana kɔba nɛ ya. Nawolo nyi n yɛɛ mena na baŋ mɔ baa kaa baake neŋ wee daale tɔ kom.

eng: Instead, when you give a banquet, invite the poor, crippled, lame, and blind.
local: Mena dɔɔ nyi akpaa n do kesaa na, baake ayematena na ɔtakasese na abafaŋse na siayɛlɛɛsatena.

eng: And you will be blessed because they cant repay you. Instead, you will be repaid when the just are resurrected.
local: N yɛɛ mena na Wurubuarɛ waa hiraa neŋ. Nawolo nyi baŋ be gyae baa tale yɛɛ mena tɔ neŋ kom ya, mɔna Wurubuarɛ ne gyae la waa tɔ neŋ kom wee keŋ waa gyuusu balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ɔ siaman lee yeŋ man nɛ.

eng: When one of the dinner guests heard Jesus remarks, he said to Jesus, Happy are those who will feast in Gods kingdom.
local: Balaŋ baŋ baŋ na wɔ kyaa bee di weenɛɛ ŋan man ŋolo kenyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ ɔ yako Yesu nyiaa, Walaŋ ŋon waa di weenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man nɛ, ŋon na nyeebam.

eng: Jesus replied, A certain man hosted a large dinner and invited many people.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Balee ŋolo kyaawɔ te wee daale ɔ doo kesaa, te ɔ baake balaŋ burum nyi be kaa di.

eng: When it was time for the dinner to begin, he sent his servant to tell the invited guests, Come! The dinner is now ready.
local: Debaŋ keŋ weenɛɛ ŋan kedi be lii nɛ, ɔ kpila ɔ tobaale nyi waa ta baake balaŋ baŋ ɔ be lɔ yele baake wɔ nɛ nyiaa, Ɛ kɔŋ, ba te taŋ kpene kamasɛ.

eng: One by one, they all began to make excuses. The first one told him, I bought a farm and must go and see it. Please excuse me.
local: Mɔna be popou wɔŋee ba wose lee wɔle dokoloŋ dokoloŋ. Walaŋ gyaŋgbate ŋon tobaale ŋon be gyu ɔ gyaŋ nɛ yakowɔ nyiaa, Ma ke lɛɛ tɛɛle daale yaa te mee gyu me kaa kɛɛ ke mena dɔɔ mɛɛ waase neŋ bo mɔɔ gyae maa tale kɔŋ ya.

eng: Another said, I bought five teams of oxen, and Im going to check on them. Please excuse me.
local: Ŋolo mɔ yako e nyiaa, Ma te lɛɛ nunkyuese baala kufu baŋ baa yɛɛ tom fa maŋ te mee gyu me kaa mo wɔ fara kɛɛ be doŋ man, mena dɔɔ mɛɛ waase neŋ bo mɔɔ gyae maa tale kɔŋ ya.

eng: After these things, the Lord commissioned seventy-two others and sent them on ahead in pairs to every city and place he was about to go.
local: Kei wɔle nɛ de Gbeŋgyoo lese baala sɔŋotooro na kufu bala te ɔ kpila wɔ bala bala nyi, baa kyaŋ gbɛɛ gyu donɔɔse na yenaase ŋan pou man ŋon gbagba ne gyae waa gyu nɛ.

eng: Another said, I just got married, so I cant come.
local: Keŋte ŋolo mɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Ma ke yala alo yaa, mena dɔɔ mɔɔ gyae maa tale kɔŋ ya.

eng: He said to them, The harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers. Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Wɔɔman weenɛɛ ŋan boe na ke tekerii, mɔna balaŋ baŋ baa tekerii nɛ ta boo ya. Mena dɔɔ ɛ waase wɔɔte ŋon na waa kpila balaŋ burum kɔŋ wɔɔ keŋ man na be kaa tekerii.

eng: When he returned, the servant reported these excuses to his master. The master of the house became angry and said to his servant, Go quickly to the citys streets, the busy ones and the side streets, and bring the poor, crippled, blind, and lame.
local: Mena dɔɔ tobaale ŋon besewɔ gyu ke yako ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon nombia ŋan bɔɔ yako e nɛ. Ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ ɔ gyeŋa baŋ nideli te ɔ yako ɔ tobaale ŋon nyiaa, Bese gyu donɔɔ keŋ abɔnterese na ŋe gbɛɛneŋ nɔɔman, na n kaa baake ayematena na ɔtakasese na siayɛlɛɛsatena na abafaŋse nyi be popou baa kɔŋ.

eng: Go! Be warned, though, that Im sending you out as lambs among wolves.
local: Ɛ ta! Mee kpila ŋon baa ŋgba namensebia ne gyuula gbougbouse man nɛ.

eng: The servant said, Master, your instructions have been followed and there is still room.
local: Te kyare ya nɛ tobaale ŋon besewɔ kɔŋ kaa yako ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon nyiaa, Me gbeŋgyoo, kpene keŋ n be kpila maŋ nyi maa yɛɛ nɛ, ma te yɛɛ mɔna akyaese sɛɛ doo burum.

eng: Carry no wallet, no bag, and no sandals. Dont even greet anyone along the way.
local: Ɛ ne gyu na ɛ na mo kɔba yaa akpo yaa nkyokota ya. Te ɛ na wɔlɛɛ debaŋ na balaŋ nɔɔ kado gbɛɛnaa man ya.

eng: The master said to the servant, Go to the highways and back alleys and urge people to come in so that my house will be filled.
local: Keŋte balee ŋon yako ɔ tobaale ŋon nyiaa, Gyu donɔɔ keŋ denɛɛ wɔla na ke gbɛɛneŋ pou man, na n kaa mo balaŋ kɔŋ na ma dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ kenyiŋ wulu.

eng: Whenever you enter a house, first say, May peace be on this house.
local: Dekpaŋalaŋ kamasɛ keŋ man ɛ gyoo na, ɛ taŋgbɛɛ yako dekpaŋalaŋmantena baŋ nyi, Wosefɛɛreŋ dɛɛkɔŋ dekpaŋalaŋ kei man.

eng: I tell you, not one of those who were invited will taste my dinner.
local: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, Balaŋ baŋ mɔɔ lɔ yele baake wɔ nɛ, be man ŋolo na ŋolo nɔɔ be gyae ke daa me weenɛɛ kɛŋa ya. .

eng: If anyone there shares Gods peace, then your peace will rest on that person. If not, your blessing will return to you.
local: Akpaa walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne gyae wosefɛɛreŋ kyaa dekpaŋalaŋ kenaŋ man na, ɛ wosefɛɛreŋ gyae ke kyaa na e. Te akpaa walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne gyae wosefɛɛreŋ be kyaa botɔɔ ya na, ɛ wosefɛɛreŋ keŋ gyae ka bese kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ.

eng: Large crowds were traveling with Jesus. Turning to them, he said.
local: Wee daale balaŋ burum baale kesila Yesu bee gyuu nɛ, ɔ besena ɔ sia tee wɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa.

eng: Remain in this house, eating and drinking whatever they set before you, for workers deserve their pay. Dont move from house to house.
local: Nyi be lɛɛ ŋon na ɛ kyaa dekpaŋalaŋ kenaŋ man na ɛ ke di na ɛ ke nyɔɔ kpene kamasɛ keŋ baa fa ŋon nɛ, nawolo nyi kaboena nyi baa tɔ tomyɛɛre kamasɛ kom. Ɛ na tɛɛ na akpaŋalaŋ yakaa yakaa ya.

eng: Whoever comes to me and doesnt hate father and mother, spouse and children, and brothers and sisters—yes, even ones own life—cannot be my disciple.
local: Akpaa ŋolo ne kɔŋ me gyaŋ ɔ kaa sila maŋ te ɔ te gyae maŋ kela ɔ kya na ɔ naa na ɔ wɛɛle na ɔ bia na ɔ kegyiise na ɔ naabia na ɔ deesina na ŋon gbagba ɔ wose koraŋ ya nɛ, ɔ be gyae waa tale bese me kaseele ya.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and its people welcome you, eat what they set before you.
local: Akpaa ɛ gyu donɔɔ daale man te be lɛɛ ŋon atuu na, ɛ di kpene kamasɛ keŋ balaŋ baŋ baa fa ŋon nɛ.

eng: Whoever doesnt carry their own cross and follow me cannot be my disciple.
local: Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ta mo ɔ ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ ka seŋɛɛ fa kaale na yeŋ na waa sila maŋ ya nɛ, ɔ be gyae waa tale bese me kaseele ya.

eng: Heal the sick who are there, and say to them, Gods kingdom has come upon you.
local: Ɛ kyɔ kaweesetena kawee donɔɔ kenaŋ man, na ɛ keyako donɔɔsetena benaŋ nyi, Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ ta benaa ŋon taŋ.

eng: If one of you wanted to build a tower, wouldnt you first sit down and calculate the cost, to determine whether you have enough money to complete it?.
local: Akpaa ɛ man ŋolo ne gyae waa baŋ deni dinaa na, ɔ bɛɛ kyaa na waa bu deni keŋ abɔɔ koya kɛɛ nyi kɔba keŋ ɔ dana nɛ gyae ka yee deni kenaŋ kebaŋ yee?.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and the people dont welcome you, go out into the streets and say.
local: Mɔna donɔɔ keŋ man ɛ gyu te ba te lɛɛ ŋon ya nɛ, ɛ gyu ka abɔnterese ŋan man na ɛ ke yako nyiaa.

eng: Otherwise, when you have laid the foundation but couldnt finish the tower, all who see it will begin to belittle you.
local: Nawolo nyi akpaa ɔ see deni keŋ mokɔɔ te ɔ te tale taŋ ke ya na, baŋ pou baa naa ke nɛ baa boŋo e.

eng: As a complaint against you, we brush off the dust of your city that has collected on our feet. But know this: Gods kingdom has come to you.
local: Ɛ donɔɔ aŋasa ŋan be mataa de nawɔɔ koraŋ nɛ, de te kpiisi ŋa woli ɛ nyeeya nsana. Mɔna ɛ tɔɔse nyi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ ta benaa taŋ.

eng: They will say, Heres the person who began construction and couldnt complete it.
local: nyiaa Balee kei korowɔ nyi ɔ ne baŋ deni mɔna ɔ nyiŋmaa te gyaŋee ya.

eng: I assure you that Sodom will be better off on Judgment Day than that city.
local: Mɛɛ yako ŋon na ɛ ke nyii nyi wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa kɔŋ kaa di balaŋ nombia nɛ, waa wii Sodomtena waraŋase koraŋ kela mena donɔɔ kenaŋ man balaŋ.

eng: Or what king would go to war against another king without first sitting down to consider whether his ten thousand soldiers could go up against the twenty thousand coming against him?.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako nyiaa, akpaa gyoo ŋolo dana yoonɔɔtena kakpoŋse kufu te ɔ ne gyu ɔ kaa yoo na ɔ dɔɔ gyoo ŋon ɔ dana yoonɔɔtena kakpoŋse sao na, ɔ be gyae waa kyaa na waa gyueŋ kɛɛ nyi waa tale mo ɔ wui yoonɔɔtena kakpoŋse kufu baŋ ke yoo na ɔ dɔɔ ŋon wui yoonɔɔtena kakpoŋse sao baŋ yee?.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin. How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida. If the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives long ago. They would have sat around in funeral clothes and ashes.
local: Korasin na Bɛtesaida donɔɔsetena! Mɛɛ fa ŋon laako. Nawolo nyi gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ ɛ gyaŋ nɛ, nyi akpaa beŋ yɛɛ ŋa Tae na Sidɔn donɔɔse man nɛ nafɔ beŋ te wulaa do titere abɔɔ kyaa mo tɔŋ buraa ba wose, na kawola nyi ba te kyɛɛkee ba agyueŋ lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

eng: And if he didnt think he could win, he would send a representative to discuss terms of peace while his enemy was still a long way off.
local: Nyi akpaa ɔ kɛɛ nyi ɔ be tale yoo na e ya nɛ, debaŋ keŋ ɔ ta benaa ta e ya na waa kpila teree gyu ke doo e nyi waa yela baa desina ba nsana na baa kyaa wosefɛɛreŋ man.

eng: But Tyre and Sidon will be better off at the judgment than you.
local: Mɔna wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa kaa di balaŋ nombia nɛ, waa wii Taetena na Sidɔntena waraŋase kela ŋon.

eng: In the same way, none of you who are unwilling to give up all of your possessions can be my disciple.
local: Mena mɔ te ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ be gyae waa tale tina kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɔ dana yela ya nɛ, ɔ be gyae waa tale bese me kaseele ya.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be cast down to the place of the dead.
local: Te nɛnɛɛ ɛmɛɛ Kapanumtena! Ɛ gyeŋ nyi baa gyina ŋon adido gyu Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ? Koa, baa tisina ŋon ateta ateta kɔŋ botɔɔ keŋ ɛ kena diyem kekpaakekpaa.

eng: Salt is good. But if salt loses its flavor, how will it become salty again?.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako nyiaa, Yabia dei mɔna akpaa ka gyoŋ keŋ da lee ke man nɛ, n be gyae n ke tale yela ka ke yɛɛ gyoŋ bela ya.

eng: Whoever listens to you listens to me. Whoever rejects you rejects me. Whoever rejects me rejects the one who sent me.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Walaŋ ŋon ɔ nyii ŋon na maŋ yaa ɔ nyiiwɔ, te ŋon ɔ bɛɛ ŋon na maŋ yaa ɔ bɛɛwɔ. Te ŋon ɔ bɛɛ maŋ na ɔ te bɛɛ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ.

eng: It has no value, neither for the soil nor for the manure pile. People throw it away. Whoever has ears to hear should pay attention.
local: De kɔŋ mena na, yabia kenaŋ be dei fa kolo na kolo keyɛɛ bela ya, bee fuŋ ke bo leki. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ dana deŋela anenyiiŋ dei na, waa tei nombia ŋan mɛɛ yako nɛ.

eng: The seventy-two returned joyously, saying, Lord, even the demons submit themselves to us in your name.
local: Te kyare ya te balaŋ sɔŋotooro na kufu bala baŋ Yesu be kpila wɔ nɛ besewɔ kɔŋ. Na te yɛɛ wɔ gyoŋ nideli te be yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, lee n yele man dɔɔ feliŋkumɛɛ gba ne nyii daa.

eng: Jesus replied, I saw Satan fall from heaven like lightning.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Me naa ɔbɔnsam lee adido kaa yala ŋgba ɔga ŋmaalela nɛ.

eng: Look, I have given you authority to crush snakes and scorpions underfoot. I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will harm you.
local: Ɛ nyii, ma te fa ŋon doŋ nyi yaa mo sakatee doma na kanase dɔɔ na ɛ ke di ɛ kɔlare ŋon doŋ dɔɔ, te kolo be gyae ke tale yɛɛ ŋon ya.

eng: Nevertheless, dont rejoice because the spirits submit to you. Rejoice instead that your names are written in heaven.
local: Ɛ na yela keyɛɛ ŋon gyoŋ nyi feliŋkumɛɛ ne nyii ŋon ya, mɔna ɛ yela keyɛɛ ŋon gyoŋ nyi ba te ŋmarase ɛ yela adido.

eng: At that very moment, Jesus overflowed with joy from the Holy Spirit and said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and shown them to babies. Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ man te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon yela gyoŋ kaa gyoowɔ Yesu man nideli te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Me kya adido na ateta pou Gbeŋgyoo! Mɛɛ fa neŋ karaŋ nawolo nyi n ta mo abɔɔ kɛŋa weese nyansatena na baŋ ba gyeŋ nombia nɛ, te n lese ŋa wola baŋ be dinɔɔ yɛɛ ŋgba bia nɛ. Taata, ma gyeŋ nyi kei yɛna nyaŋ gbagba n kegyaebii.

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, or who the Father is except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, Me kya ta mo kpene kamasɛ do maŋ me nyiŋmaa man. Ŋolo ba gyeŋ maŋ ɔ Bu ŋon ya, me kya waageŋ kooŋ gyeŋa na maŋ. Ŋolo mɔ ba gyeŋ kya ŋon ya, maŋ ɔ Bu ŋon na baŋ maa lese me kya ŋon wola wɔ nɛ daageŋ ne gyae la dɛɛ gyeŋ e.

eng: Turning to the disciples, he said privately, Happy are the eyes that see what you see.
local: Keŋte Yesu besenawɔ kɛɛ ɔ kaseela baŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ te yɛɛ nyeebam ŋgba ɛ ne naa mena abɔɔ ŋan ɛ ne naa nɛ!.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and kings wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra na gyooneŋ gyaewɔ baa naa kpene keŋ ɛ ne naa nɛ mɔna ba te naa ya, te be gyaewɔ baa nyii kpene keŋ ɛ ne nyii nɛ mɔna be te nyii ya.

eng: A legal expert stood up to test Jesus. Teacher, he said, what must I do to gain eternal life.
