local: Wee daale nɛ Gyudatena mmaraa wolale ŋolo kɔŋawɔ ɔ kaa teese Yesu nɔɔman te ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, woŋ te maa yɛɛ na maa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, What is written in the Law? How do you interpret it.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse e nyi, Woŋ te be ŋmarasewɔ do Wurubuarɛ mmaraa tɔne keŋ man? Sena te ɛ ne nyii mmaraase ŋan man.

eng: He responded, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your strength, and with all your mind, and love your neighbor as yourself .
local: Keŋte balee ŋon tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Be ŋmarasewɔ nyi mo n konɔɔ pou na n wose pou na n doŋ pou na nya agyueŋ pou gyae n Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ, na n ke gyae n dɔɔ ŋgba nyaŋ gbagba n wose nɛ.

eng: Jesus said to him, You have answered correctly. Do this and you will live.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, N tiranɔɔ nideli, yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ n be yako nɛ na n ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ.

eng: But the legal expert wanted to prove that he was right, so he said to Jesus, And who is my neighbor.
local: Mɔna keŋ mmaraa wolale ŋon ne gyae waa wola nyi ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ bɔɔse Yesu nyi, Amɔte yɛna me dɔɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, A man went down from Jerusalem to Jericho. He encountered thieves, who stripped him naked, beat him up, and left him near death.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu gyɔɔ dudu daale tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Wee daale balee ŋolo lee Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man tisi ɔ ne gyu Gyeriko donɔɔ man. Keŋte ɔ gyuuwɔ kegyaŋee na ŋmɛɛlatena, te be lɛo ɔ kpene kamasɛ te be tuku e yeŋ yeŋ, te be tinaa e yela diteŋteli.

eng: Now it just so happened that a priest was also going down the same road. When he saw the injured man, he crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: Te kɔŋawɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ Sae ŋolo tisiwɔ mo gbɛɛ kenaŋ ɔ ne gyu. Ɔ kena balee ŋon doo nɛ ɔ kpasewɔ mo yenaŋ laŋ.

eng: Likewise, a Levite came by that spot, saw the injured man, and crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: Mena mɔ te Lewi baale ŋolo mɔ kɔŋawɔ kaa mo botɔɔ te ɔ kena balee ŋon doo nɛ, ŋon mɔ kpasewɔ mo yenaŋ laŋ.

eng: A Samaritan, who was on a journey, came to where the man was. But when he saw him, he was moved with compassion.
local: Mɔna Samaria baale ŋolo ŋon ɔ ne gyu ɔ gbɛɛ nɛ, kaa leewɔ balee ŋon dɔɔ. Ɔ kena e keŋ nɛ ɔ tɔɔ nyakee e, te waraŋase kyaŋa e fa balee ŋon.

eng: The Samaritan went to him and bandaged his wounds, tending them with oil and wine. Then he placed the wounded man on his own donkey, took him to an inn, and took care of him.
local: Ɔ tuuwɔ benaa e te ɔ doo e tee ɔ gyamɛɛ ŋan man te ɔ bake ŋa. Botɔɔ te ɔ moo balee ŋon gyakaa ŋon gbagba ɔ wonembu ŋon ɔ kyaa nɛ dɔɔ, te ɔ moo e gyu ŋɔɔla dɛɛsoe keŋ man nyi baa kɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ fa e.

eng: The next day, he took two full days worth of wages and gave them to the innkeeper. He said, Take care of him, and when I return, I will pay you back for any additional costs.
local: Tɛɛ kena nɛ, ɔ lese kɔba fa kegyia ŋon ɔ ne kɛɛ ŋɔɔla dɛɛsoe keŋ dɔɔ nɛ te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Kɛɛ balee kei dɔɔ fa maŋ. Ma bese kɔŋ na me kaa tɔ neŋ kom kamasɛ keŋ n be wɔlɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ nɛ kpu. .

eng: What do you think? Which one of these three was a neighbor to the man who encountered thieves.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse balee ŋon nyiaa, Nya agyueŋ man na, balaŋ batooro kɛwɔ man woŋti yɛna balee ŋon ŋmɛɛlatena be tuku e nɛ ɔ dɔɔ.

eng: Then the legal expert said, The one who demonstrated mercy toward him. Jesus told him, Go and do likewise.
local: Keŋte mmaraa wolale ŋon tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Walaŋ ŋon ɔ be wii ɔ waraŋase nɛ. Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, Nyaŋ mɔ gyu na n kaa yɛɛ wɔe mena.

eng: While Jesus and his disciples were traveling, Jesus entered a village where a woman named Martha welcomed him as a guest.
local: Yesu na ɔ kaseela kegyakaa be gbɛɛ dɔɔ bee gyu nɛ, ɔ keliiwɔ akuraa daale man te alo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Maata nɛ moo e gyu ɔ dɛɛ.

eng: She had a sister named Mary, who sat at the Lords feet and listened to his message.
local: Na Maata dana naabu ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Mɛɛre. Keŋte Mɛɛre kɔŋawɔ kaa kyaa de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu gyaŋ ɔ ne nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia.

eng: By contrast, Martha was preoccupied with getting everything ready for their meal. So Martha came to him and said, Lord, dont you care that my sister has left me to prepare the table all by myself? Tell her to help me.
local: Maata kɛɛ ka ɔ wose na ŋɔɔla baŋ kedi man nɛ, ɔ kɔŋawɔ kaa bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, n be famenɛ ŋgba me naabu be tina maŋ yela maageŋ yee? Yako e nyi waa kɔŋ kaa kyɔ maŋ.

eng: The Lord answered, Martha, Martha, you are worried and distracted by many things.
local: Keŋte de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Maata, Maata, n ne naase n wose diyem abɔɔ burum dɔɔ.

eng: One thing is necessary. Mary has chosen the better part. It wont be taken away from her.
local: mɔna kolo dokoloŋ ne hia la keŋ kaboena nyi nya yɛɛ. Mena kolo kpaakpaa kenaŋ yaa Mɛɛre moowɔ, te bɔɔ gyae baa lɛo ke lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man ya.

eng: Some who were present on that occasion told Jesus about the Galileans whom Pilate had killed while they were offering sacrifices.
local: Yesu ke gyakaa ɔ ne kolosi fa balaŋ baŋ nɛ balaŋ baale kɔŋawɔ kaa yako e nyiaa, Galeliatena baale gyuuwɔ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man be kaa deesi fa Wurubuarɛ. Keŋte gyoo dinaa Pilato yela be ka koo wɔ, te be fatabo kyakalewɔ na ba kedeesa fatabo keŋ.

eng: He replied, Do you think the suffering of these Galileans proves that they were more sinful than all the other Galileans?.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ na gyueŋ nyi Galeliatena baŋ bɔɔ naa mena diyem kei gyu ka yeŋ nɛ, be nombiakumɛɛ kela Galeliatena akaŋ baŋ pou?.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: Koa be doo mena ya, mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyiaa akpaa ɛ te kyɛɛkee lee ɛ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na ɛ ke kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya nɛ, ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ ka yeŋ.

eng: What about those eighteen people who were killed when the tower of Siloam fell on them? Do you think that they were more guilty of wrongdoing than everyone else who lives in Jerusalem?.
local: Yaa ɛ na gyueŋ nyi balaŋ kufu gyanaara baŋ deni dɔɔloŋte keŋ be yale ŋere wɔ Siloam nɛ, be nombiakumɛɛ kela balaŋ akaŋ baŋ be kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ pou?.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: Koa be doo mena ya, mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyiaa akpaa ɛ te kyɛɛkee lee ɛ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na ɛ ke kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya nɛ, ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ ka yeŋ.

eng: Jesus told this parable: A man owned a fig tree planted in his vineyard. He came looking for fruit on it and found none.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu gyɔɔ dudu kei fa wɔ nyiaa, Balee ŋolo duu daŋ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi fiigi nɛ ɔ wɔɔman, te ɔ gyuuwɔ ɔ kaa kɛɛ nyi waa nyiŋ ke bia di mɔna ke ta see ya.

eng: He said to his gardener, Look, Ive come looking for fruit on this fig tree for the past three years, and Ive never found any. Cut it down! Why should it continue depleting the soils nutrients.
local: Keŋte ɔ yako ɔ paate ŋon nyiaa, Kɛɛ kulutooneŋ atooro sɔɔŋ yaa ŋgba mɛɛ kɔŋ kɛbo kaa kɛɛ nyi maa nyiŋ daŋ kei bia, mɔna me bee nyim ya. Kara ke leki! Woŋ dɔɔ te ka seŋɛɛ kɛɛ wɔlɛɛ me tɛɛle.

eng: The gardener responded, Lord, give it one more year, and I will dig around it and give it fertilizer.
local: Keŋte ɔ paate ŋon tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Me gbeŋgyoo, yela ke ka seŋ kulutoo dokoloŋ kpu na maa wula tɛɛle keŋ ke dei nɛ kaa do ka ateta na dɛɛ kɛɛ.

eng: Maybe it will produce fruit next year; if not, then you can cut it down.
local: Akpaa ke da see bia kulutoo anekɔŋaŋ na dei, te akpaa ke ta see ya na dɛɛ kara ke leki. .

eng: Jesus was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath.
local: Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee daale nɛ, na Yesu na wolo Gyudatena ɔsom deni daale man.

eng: A woman was there who had been disabled by a spirit for eighteen years. She was bent over and couldnt stand up straight.
local: Na alo ŋolo kyaa botɔɔ keŋ feliŋkum ba do e kawee kulutooneŋ kufu gyanaara, te ke yela ɔ goowɔ keŋ ɔ bɛɛ tale waa tenee ɔ wose koraŋ ya.

eng: When he saw her, Jesus called her to him and said, Woman, you are set free from your sickness.
local: Yesu kena alo ŋon nɛ, ɔ baake e kaa seŋ balaŋ siaman te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Alo, n te nyiŋ kawee kekyɔ.

eng: He placed his hands on her and she straightened up at once and praised God.
local: Keŋte Yesu moo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ, debokenaŋ man te alo ŋon teneewɔ te ɔ lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

eng: The synagogue leader, incensed that Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, responded, There are six days during which work is permitted. Come and be healed on those days, not on the Sabbath day.
local: Botɔɔ te bɔ ɔsom deni man kegyia ŋon gyeŋa baŋ nyi Yesu te kyɔ walaŋ kawee ba kefɛɛfowee, mena dɔɔ ɔ kolosiwɔ fa balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyaŋee botɔɔ nɛ nyiaa, Ɛ dana weeya looro keŋ ɛ na mo yɛɛ tom. Ɛ kɔŋ weeya ŋenaŋ man na baa kyɔ ŋon ɛ kaweese, te yɛɛ nyi kefɛɛfowee te ɛ ke kɔŋ ya.

eng: The Lord replied, Hypocrites! Dont each of you on the Sabbath untie your ox or donkey from its stall and lead it out to get a drink?.
local: Keŋte de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose! Ɛ man woŋti bɛɛ booli na ɔ wonembu lee ɔ dekpaŋae man mo e gyu kefa e loŋ kefɛɛfowee ya?.

eng: Then isnt it necessary that this woman, a daughter of Abraham, bound by Satan for eighteen long years, be set free from her bondage on the Sabbath day.
local: Te nɛnɛɛ alo kei ŋon ɔ ba lee Aberaham duuluŋ man te ɔbɔnsam ba do e kawee kulutooneŋ kufu gyanaara sɔɔŋ yaa nɛ, tekaboena nyi maa kyɔ e kawee kefɛɛfowee yee.

eng: When he said these things, all his opponents were put to shame, but all those in the crowd rejoiced at all the extraordinary things he was doing.
local: Yesu nɔɔ ketira keŋ yela ɔ kɔlala baŋ pou sia yeŋawɔ, te gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ pou gyoŋ.

eng: Jesus asked, What is Gods kingdom like? To what can I compare it?.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Sena te Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ doo? Yaa woŋ te dɛɛ mo ke maa na?.

eng: Its like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in a garden. It grew and developed into a tree and the birds in the sky nested in its branches.
local: Ke yɛɛ ŋgba daŋ daale bii peperee ŋgba kpeli bii keŋ balee ŋolo ba mo ke duu ɔ wɔɔman nɛ. Te ke tuuwɔ bese daŋ dinaa dinaa te gyebuse kɔŋawɔ kaa loo ba asaase do ke tombia man.

eng: Again he said, To what can I compare Gods kingdom?.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Woŋ te maa mo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ maa na?.

eng: Its like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through the whole.
local: Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba tee keŋ alo ŋolo ba mo kyakale bodobodo biliŋ tasa dinaa man, kaboena biliŋ keŋ pou korowɔ wuku nɛ.

eng: Jesus traveled through cities and villages, teaching and making his way to Jerusalem.
local: Yesu ka mo ɔ nyee baŋ ɔ ne gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ tɛɛwɔ donɔɔse na akuraase man ɔ na wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia.

eng: Someone said to him, Lord, will only a few be saved? Jesus said to them.
local: Keŋte walaŋ ŋolo bɔɔse e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, balaŋ akalansɛɛ te Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ ba nyee yaa? Te Yesu tiranɔɔ nyiaa.

eng: Make every effort to enter through the narrow gate. Many, I tell you, will try to enter and wont be able to.
local: Nyi akpaa ɛ ne gyae ɛ ka gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man nɛ, ɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ na ɛ ka mo denanɔɔ bɔbɔɔkyee keŋ man gyoo. Nawolo nyi balaŋ burum baa peree nyi baa mo botɔɔ gyoo, mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa tale ya.

eng: Once the owner of the house gets up and shuts the door, then you will stand outside and knock on the door, saying, Lord, open the door for us. He will reply, I dont know you or where you are from.
local: Debaŋ keŋ dekpaŋalaŋte ŋon waa koro na waa tɔ ɔ disim nɛ, debaŋ kenaŋ te ɛ ka seŋ debɔɔ man na ɛ ke gyɔ disim keŋ na ɛ ke waase e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo toro denanɔɔ keŋ fa daa. Keŋte waa tiranɔɔ fa ŋon nyi, Mɔɔ gyeŋ ŋon ya te mɔɔ gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ ɛ ba lee mɔ ya.

eng: Then you will begin to say, We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets.
local: Botɔɔ te ɛ ke tiranɔɔ nyiaa, daa na neŋ doo nyiŋmaa di te de nyɔɔwɔ, te n wola nombia de debiise mɔ man.

eng: He will respond, I dont know you or where you are from. Go away from me, all you evildoers.
local: Mɔna dekpaŋalaŋte ŋon waa bese yako nyiaa, Mɔɔ gyeŋ ŋon ya te mɔɔ gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ ɛ ba lee mɔ ya. Ɛ ta lee me siaman, ɛmɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra.

eng: There will be weeping and grinding of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in Gods kingdom, but you yourselves will be thrown out.
local: Debaŋ keŋ ɛ kena de naanaɔ Aberaham na Asiki na Gyekɔpo na Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ pou Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man te ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale ke kpu na wɔ ya nɛ, botɔɔ te ɛ ke kyaŋ ɛ nyeeya wii na ɛ ka doŋee ɛ nyimbia.

eng: People will come from east and west, north and south, and sit down to eat in Gods kingdom.
local: Balaŋ baa lee weese dɛɛlee na weese dɛɛyalae man, na ado na ata yenaŋ kɔŋ kaa kyaa Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man di gyoŋ.

eng: Look! Those who are last will be first and those who are first will be last.
local: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ dɔɔ wɔle nɛ baa kaa laŋ siaman, te baŋ be kyaa siaman nɛ baa dɔɔ wɔle.

eng: At that time, some Pharisees approached Jesus and said, Go! Get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ man te Farasiitena baale kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ kaa yako e nyi Lee kɛbo kei gyu yenaŋ daale, nawolo nyi gyoo Herode ne gyae neŋ waa ko.

eng: Jesus said to them, Go, tell that fox, Look, Im throwing out demons and healing people today and tomorrow, and on the third day I will complete my work.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ gyu ke yako mena adaote ŋonaŋ nyiaa, maa gberaa feliŋkumɛɛ na maa kyɔ balaŋ gyɛŋ na kyoo, te weeya atoorote man na maa taŋ ma tom.

eng: However, its necessary for me to travel today, tomorrow, and the next day because its impossible for a prophet to be killed outside of Jerusalem.
local: Mɔna kaboena nyi maa mo me sia tee me dee gyue dɛɛdɛɛ gyɛŋ na kyoo na ke tɛɛ kena. Nawolo nyi yɛɛ senaa nɛ, Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man te kaboena nyi Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre waa yeŋ.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you! How often I have wanted to gather your people just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: Keŋte Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, O Gyerusalɛmtena, O Gyerusalɛmtena, ɛmɛɛ koona Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ te ɛ fuŋii Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ ɔ be kpili ɛ gyaŋ nɛ boya. Ma te yɛɛ mena keŋ maa yilaa ŋon kɔŋ me gyaŋ ŋgba mena keŋ kegyim talabo ne yilaa ɔ bia do ɔ beŋkelɛɛ tɛɛ nɛ, mɔna ɛ te fa maŋ gbɛɛ ya.

eng: Look, your house is abandoned. I tell you, you wont see me until the time comes when you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: Ɛ kɛɛ, Wurubuarɛ ta lese ɔ sia lee ɛ donɔɔ keŋ dɔɔ. Te mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, ɛ be gyae ɛ kena maŋ bela ya kelii debaŋ keŋ ɛ keyako nyiaa, Walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kɔŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ yele man nɛ, ŋon na nyeebam. .

eng: All the tax collectors and sinners were gathering around Jesus to listen to him.
local: Debaŋ daale nɛ, lempoolɛɛra burum na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ be kaa nyii nombia ŋan ɔ na wolo nɛ.

eng: The Pharisees and legal experts were grumbling, saying, This man welcomes sinners and eats with them.
local: Keŋte Farasiitena na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala ŋorokosowɔ yako nyiaa, Balee kei dɔɔ ɔ ne lɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra do ɔ wose man te ɔ ne dii bom na wɔ gba koraŋ.

eng: Jesus told them this parable.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu gyɔɔ dudu kei fa wɔ nyiaa.

eng: Suppose someone among you had one hundred sheep and lost one of them. Wouldnt he leave the other ninety-nine in the pasture and search for the lost one until he finds it?.
local: Akpaa ɛ man ŋolo dana namense sɔŋonoŋ te be man ŋolo da yoo na woŋ te ɔ ne yɛɛ? Ɔ be gyae waa tina sɔŋonaara na kufu kadaale baŋ yela ŋale nsana botɔɔ na waa ta kɛo ŋon ɔ ba yoo nɛ kaboena waa naa e yee?.

eng: And when he finds it, he is thrilled and places it on his shoulders.
local: Te akpaa ɔ naa e na, ɔ be gyae waa nyaŋee na waa gate e gyakaa ɔ degbaŋgbaŋ dɔɔ mo e gyu dɛɛ yee?.

eng: When he arrives home, he calls together his friends and neighbors, saying to them, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost sheep.
local: Ɔ gyu dɛɛ nɛ waa baake ɔ gyoona na ɔ tebia gyaŋee na waa yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ yela dɛɛ nyaŋee nawolo nyi ma te naa me nam ŋon ɔ ba yoo nɛ.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who changes both heart and life than over ninety-nine righteous people who have no need to change their hearts and lives.
local: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, mena ke te akpaa nombiakumɛɛyɛɛre dokoloŋ de kyɛɛkee lee ɔ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man te ɔ kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ nɛ, baŋ be kyaa Wurubuarɛ man adido nɛ baa nyaŋee ɔ dɔɔ kela balaŋ sɔŋonaara na kufu kadaale baŋ bɛɛ gyueŋ nyi be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman, mena dɔɔ tekaboena nyi baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man ya nɛ.

eng: Or what woman, if she owns ten silver coins and loses one of them, wont light a lamp and sweep the house, searching her home carefully until she finds it?.
local: Yesu besewɔ yako nyiaa, akpaa alo ŋolo dana kɔba wolom bia kufu te ŋe man dokoloŋ da yoo na woŋ te ɔ ne yɛɛ? Ɔ be gyae waa kyaŋee kanea na waa gbee ɔ deni man na waa kɛo yenaŋ kamasɛ nideli kaboena waa naa ke yee?.

eng: When she finds it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost coin.
local: Te akpaa ɔ naa ke na, ɔ ne baake ɔ gyoona na ɔ tebia gyaŋee na waa yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ yela dɛɛ nyaŋee nawolo nyi ma te naa me kɔba wolom bii keŋ kɔɔ yoo nɛ.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, joy breaks out in the presence of Gods angels over one sinner who changes both heart and life.
local: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, mena ke te akpaa nombiakumɛɛyɛɛre dokoloŋ de kyɛɛkee lee ɔ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man te ɔ kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ be kyaa adido nɛ baa nyaŋee ɔ dɔɔ.

eng: Jesus said, A certain man had two sons.
local: Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu kei fa wɔ nyiaa, Balee ŋolo kyaawɔ na ɔ dana bia baala bala.

eng: The younger son said to his father, Father, give me my share of the inheritance. Then the father divided his estate between them.
local: Wee daale balee kei bu kemaasa ŋon yako ɔ kya nyiaa, Taata, kpala nya abɔɔ ŋan n dana nɛ na n ka mo me wui fa maŋ. Mena dɔɔ ɔ kya ŋon kpala wa abɔɔ ŋan fa ɔ bia baŋ.

eng: Soon afterward, the younger son gathered everything together and took a trip to a land far away. There, he wasted his wealth through extravagant living.
local: Te di weeya ala ya te ɔ bu kemaasa ŋon yɔɔ abɔɔ ŋan ɔ be nyim nɛ pou te ɔ moo kɔba keŋ do ɔ wose man te ɔ moo ɔ nyee baŋ tɛɛle wɔle daale man. Ɔ ke gyu botɔɔ nɛ, ɔ moo kɔba keŋ ɔ dana nɛ ke yɛɛ kehuhoori wɔlɛɛ ke pou.

eng: When he had used up his resources, a severe food shortage arose in that country and he began to be in need.
local: Ɔ ke wɔlɛɛ kɔba keŋ pou taŋ nɛ, tanam dinaa daale yalawɔ tɛɛle kenaŋ pou dɔɔ te ɔ wose kyaŋa e nideli.

eng: He hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed pigs.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔ gyuuwɔ ɔ kaa di paa fa donɔɔte ŋolo, te donɔɔte ŋon moo e gyu nyi waa gyu ke kɛɛ ɔ kekuiriise dɔɔ.

eng: He longed to eat his fill from what the pigs ate, but no one gave him anything.
local: Na nafɔ ɔ loo gyakaa nyi waa nyiŋ kekuiriise baŋ weenɛɛ ŋan ŋaale di, mɔna ŋolo te fa e ŋa ya.

eng: When he came to his senses, he said, How many of my fathers hired hands have more than enough food, but Im starving to death!.
local: Ɔ sia ke ŋmaŋee e nɛ ɔ yako ɔ wose nyiaa, Me kya tewulɛɛ pou ne dii baa kaase, te maŋ kyaa kɛbo keŋ tanam ne gyae ke kara ma nyee.

eng: I will get up and go to my father, and say to him,Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.
local: Maa koro na maa bese gyu me kya gyaŋ na me kaa yako e nyiaa, Taata, ma te yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ tia Wurubuarɛ te ma te yɛɛ tia nyaŋ mɔ.

eng: I no longer deserve to be called your son. Take me on as one of your hired hands.
local: Mena dɔɔ ma tekaboena nyi nya baake maŋ n bu bela ya, mo maŋ yɛɛ ŋgba n tewulɛɛ baŋ man ŋolo nɛ. .

eng: So he got up and went to his father. While he was still a long way off, his father saw him and was moved with compassion. His father ran to him, hugged him, and kissed him.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔ korowɔ mo gbɛɛ ɔ ne gyu ɔ kya gyaŋ. Debaŋ keŋ ɔ dɔŋɛɛ lemlem ɔ ne kɔŋ dɛɛ nɛ, ɔ kya naa e te ɔ bu ŋon nombia yɛɛ e waraŋase te ɔ yeuwɔ kegyaŋee na e te ɔ pukaa e nyaŋee na e.

eng: Then his son said, Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you. I no longer deserve to be called your son.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ bu ŋon yako e nyiaa, Taata, ma te yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ tia Wurubuarɛ te ma te yɛɛ tia nyaŋ mɔ. Mena dɔɔ ma tekaboena nyi nya baake maŋ n bu bela ya. Mo maŋ yɛɛ ŋgba n tewulɛɛ baŋ man ŋolo nɛ.

eng: But the father said to his servants, Quickly, bring out the best robe and put it on him! Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet!.
local: Keŋte ɔ kya ŋon baake ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kaŋ ɛ wose na ɛ ka lese kegba dɔɔloŋ kpaakpaate keŋ kɔŋ kaa do e. Ɛ do e kɔlabia ɔ nyiŋmaa man na ɛ ka do e nkyokotase.

eng: Fetch the fattened calf and slaughter it. We must celebrate with feasting.
local: Na ɛ ke gyu ka mo nunkyue baale dinaa ŋon kɔŋ kaa ko na dee di de nɔɔ.

eng: because this son of mine was dead and has come back to life! He was lost and is found! And they began to celebrate.
local: Nawolo nyi yɛɛ yaa ŋgba me bu kei yeŋae te ɔ besewɔ foŋ nɛ. Nafɔ na ɔ ta yoo te nɛnɛɛ ma te naa e bela. Keŋte be fiasɛɛ bɛɛ nyaŋee na bee di be nɔɔ.

eng: Now his older son was in the field. Coming in from the field, he approached the house and heard music and dancing.
local: Debaŋ keŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ kɛŋa pou nɛ, na ɔ bu kegyia ŋon kyaa wɔɔman. Ɔ ka lee wɔɔ ɔ ne kɔŋ te ɔ ba benaa dɛɛ nɛ, ɔ nyii nɔɔneŋ na suya ne tao kela.

eng: He called one of the servants and asked what was going on.
local: Keŋte ɔ baake ɔ kya tewulɛɛ baŋ man ŋolo bɔɔse e kpene keŋ be kɔŋ dekpaŋalaŋ man.

eng: The servant replied, Your brother has arrived, and your father has slaughtered the fattened calf because he received his son back safe and sound.
local: Te ɔ tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, N naabu ŋon ta bese kɔŋ donɔɔ te n kya koo nunkyue baale dinaa ŋon, nawolo nyi ɔ ta bese kɔŋ dɛɛ na nyeedoŋ.

eng: Then the older son was furious and didnt want to enter in, but his father came out and begged him.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ bu kegyia ŋon gyeŋa baŋ bɛɛ nyi ɔ ba gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man bela ya. Ɔ kya ke nyii mena na te ɔ leewɔ kɔŋ kaa waase e nyi waa gyoo dɛɛ.

eng: He answered his father, Look, Ive served you all these years, and I never disobeyed your instruction. Yet youve never given me as much as a young goat so I could celebrate with my friends.
local: Keŋte ɔ yako ɔ kya nyiaa, Taata nyaŋ kɛɛ, kulutooneŋ kɛŋa pou me kyaa mɛɛ som neŋ ŋgba gbeŋi nɛ, te ma te yɛɛ deŋele doŋ n dɔɔ ya. Woŋ te n faa maŋ? N te faa ta maŋ asoobii paŋpaŋ gba na daa na me gyoona mɔ kedi de nɔɔ ya.

eng: But when this son of yours returned, after gobbling up your estate on prostitutes, you slaughtered the fattened calf for him.
local: Mɔna n bu kei ŋon ɔ ba mo nya abɔɔ pou ke wɔlɛɛ futooli ala dɔɔ te ɔ ba bese kɔŋ donɔɔ nyiŋmaase wolo nɛ te n koo nunkyue baale dinaa ŋon fa.

eng: Then his father said, Son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
local: Keŋte ɔ kya ŋon yako e nyiaa, Me bu, debaŋ kamasɛ n kyae me gyaŋ te kpene kamasɛ keŋ me dana nɛ yɛɛ n wui.

eng: But we had to celebrate and be glad because this brother of yours was dead and is alive. He was lost and is found.
local: Mɔna kaboena nyi dee di de nɔɔ na dɛɛ nyaŋee. Nawolo nyi yɛɛ yaa ŋgba n naabu kei yeŋae te ɔ besewɔ foŋ nɛ, nafɔ na ɔ ta yoo te nɛnɛɛ da te naa e.

eng: Jesus also said to the disciples, A certain rich man heard that his household manager was wasting his estate.
local: Keŋte Yesu gyɔɔ dudu fa ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Kɔbate ŋolo kyaawɔ keŋ ɔ dana tobaale ŋon ɔ ne kɛɛ wa abɔɔ pou dɔɔ, te be yako balee ŋon nyi ɔ tobaale ŋon ne wɔlɛɛ ɔ kɔba.

eng: He called the manager in and said to him, What is this I hear about you? Give me a report of your administration because you can no longer serve as my manager.
local: Mena dɔɔ balee ŋon baake ɔ tobaale ŋon te ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, woŋ nombia yaa mee nyii lee n wose man nɛ? Kɔŋ kaa bu abɔɔ ŋan pou doo n nyiŋmaa man nɛ akɔntaa fa maŋ, nawolo nyi n ba tekaa kɛɛ ma abɔɔ dɔɔ bela ya.

eng: The household manager said to himself, What will I do now that my master is firing me as his manager? Im not strong enough to dig and too proud to beg.
local: Botɔɔ te tobaale ŋon yakowɔ ɔ nyee man nyiaa, Me gbeŋgyoo ne gyae waa gegi maŋ lee ma tom man. Sena te maa yɛɛ? Mɔɔ taŋ doŋ me kaa fara ya, te abɔɔ kasolee mɔ dana maŋ desɛŋ.

eng: I know what Ill do so that, when I am removed from my management position, people will welcome me into their houses.
local: Keŋte ɔ yako ɔ wose nyi me ta gyeŋ kpene keŋ maa yɛɛ keŋ nyi akpaa ma tom de wɔlɛɛ na, maa nyiŋ balaŋ baŋ baa lɛɛ maŋ be dɛɛ.

eng: One by one, the manager sent for each person who owed his master money. He said to the first, How much do you owe my master.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔ baake balaŋ baŋ pou be dana ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon koma nɛ dokoloŋ dokoloŋ. Keŋte ɔ bɔɔse gyaŋgbate ŋon nyiaa, Kom aŋmoŋ te n dana me gbeŋgyoo.

eng: He said, Nine hundred gallons of olive oil.The manager said to him, Take your contract, sit down quickly, and write four hundred fifty gallons.
local: Keŋte ɔ tiranɔɔ fa nyiaa, Nuŋ garawaase sɔŋonaara na kufu . Te tobaale ŋon lese ɔ kom tɔne keŋ fa e te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Tisi kyaa bileŋ na n ke kyɛɛkee ke yɛɛ garawaase sɔŋola na anoŋ .

eng: Then the manager said to another, How much do you owe? He said, One thousand bushels of wheat.He said, Take your contract and write eight hundred.
local: Keŋte ɔ besewɔ bɔɔse balate ŋon mɔ nyi, Kom aŋmoŋ te nyaŋ dana me gbeŋgyoo? Te ŋon mɔ tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Maŋ dana e kom kameese tasase kakpoŋ . Keŋte tobaale ŋon lese ɔ kom tɔne fa e te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Kyɛɛkee ke yɛɛ sɔŋonoŋse akpeŋ gyanaara .

eng: The master commended the dishonest manager because he acted cleverly. People who belong to this world are more clever in dealing with their peers than are people who belong to the light.
local: Ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon ke nyii wa ayɛɛŋ nɛ, ɔ kɛlɛɛ ɔ tobaale nkontopoote ŋon nyi ɔ gyeŋ adao nideli. Nawolo nyi tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ gyeŋ adaosebia ŋan baa mo kyaa na be tebia kela balaŋ baŋ bee yee Wurubuarɛ nɛ.

eng: I tell you, use worldly wealth to make friends for yourselves so that when its gone, you will be welcomed into the eternal homes.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyi ɛ mo tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ yɛɛ deeli lese gyoori, na akpaa abɔɔ ŋan de taŋ nɛ, na ke yela Wurubuarɛ ke lɛɛ ŋon na ɛ ke kyaa ɔ gyaŋ kekpaakekpaa.

eng: Whoever is faithful with little is also faithful with much, and the one who is dishonest with little is also dishonest with much.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne di anokoare kolo peperee dɔɔ kekɛɛ man nɛ, waa di anokoare kolo dinaa mɔ dɔɔ kekɛɛ man. Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ bee di anokoare kolo peperee dɔɔ kekɛɛ man ya nɛ, ɔ be gyae waa di anokoare kolo dinaa mɔ dɔɔ kekɛɛ man ya.

eng: If you havent been faithful with worldly wealth, who will trust you with true riches?.
local: Akpaa nyi n bee di anokoare tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ kekɛɛ dɔɔ ya na, amɔte ne gyae la waa mo abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan ŋe dana tɔnɔɔ Wurubuarɛ man nɛ do neŋ n nyiŋmaa man?.

eng: If you havent been faithful with someone elses property, who will give you your own?.
local: Te akpaa n te di anokoare kɛɛ ŋolo abɔɔ dɔɔ nideli ya na, amɔte ne gyae la waa sɛɛ mo nyaŋ gbagba wui do neŋ n nyiŋmaa man?.

eng: No household servant can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: Tobaale kamasɛ be gyae waa tale som gbeŋgyoona bala ya. De kɔŋ mena na waa kɔla ŋolo na waa gyae ŋolo nombia, yaa waa som ŋolo kela ŋolo. Ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale som Wurubuarɛ na kɔba bom ya.

eng: The Pharisees, who were money-lovers, heard all this and sneered at Jesus.
local: Farasiitena baŋ be sia ne yɛɛ kɔba dɔɔ nideli ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ba boŋo Yesu kulu e.

eng: He said to them, You are the ones who justify yourselves before other people, but God knows your hearts. What is highly valued by people is deeply offensive to God.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ yɛna balaŋ baŋ ɛ ne desina ɛ wose balaŋ siaman, mɔna Wurubuarɛ gyeŋ ɛ konɔɔse man. Nawolo nyi kpene kamasɛ keŋ balaŋ ne bu ke nideli nɛ, ke yɛɛ kolo anekɔlaŋ ke Wurubuarɛ siaman.

eng: Until John, there was only the Law and the Prophets. Since then, the good news of Gods kingdom is preached, and everyone is urged to enter it.
local: Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan, na Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra nombia ŋan dɔɔ te balaŋ ne dii pɛte Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon kɔŋawɔ. Lee mena debaŋ keŋ Gyɔn be kɔŋ pou na, bee kolosi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man nombia kpaakpaa ŋan, te walaŋ kamasɛ na peree nyi waa gyoo ke man.

eng: Its easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for the smallest stroke of a pen in the Law to drop out.
local: Dana doŋ nyi kolo bii peperee ka yo lee Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan man, kela nyi adido na ateta ke ŋaalaa.

eng: Any man who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery, and a man who marries a woman divorced from her husband commits adultery.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ bɛɛ ɔ wɛɛle te ɔ yala alo wɔle nɛ, Wurubuarɛ siaman na ɔtenate gyae ŋolo wɛɛle baa Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon mɔ ɔ yala alo ŋon ɔ baale be bɛɛ e na, Wurubuarɛ siaman na ɔtenate mɔ gyae ŋolo wɛɛle baa.

eng: There was a certain rich man who clothed himself in purple and fine linen, and who feasted luxuriously every day.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako nyiaa, Kɔbate ŋolo kyaawɔ keŋ ɔ na do abɔɔ kpaakpaa nyiŋan ŋan ŋe dana koyadoŋ te ɔ ne di gyoŋ wee kamasɛ.

eng: At his gate lay a certain poor man named Lazarus who was covered with sores.
local: Wee na wee mɔ na, bɛɛ mo balee diyemte ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Lasarose nɛ, kaa see kɔbate ŋon denanɔɔ man. Na Lasarose kei wose pou te kulii gyamɛɛ.

eng: Lazarus longed to eat the crumbs that fell from the rich mans table. Instead, dogs would come and lick his sores.
local: Ɔ ne kɔŋ kaa kyaa botɔɔ gyakaa loo nyi waa nyiŋ kɔbate ŋon weenɛɛ ŋan ne yale tɛɛle man nɛ ŋaale di. Na babaase gba ne kɔŋ kaa deŋee ɔ gyamɛɛ ŋan.

eng: The poor man died and was carried by angels to Abrahams side. The rich man also died and was buried.
local: Wee daale nɛ, balee diyemte ŋon kaa yeŋawɔ te be wuu e te Wurubuarɛ kpilala kaa seele ɔ woo gyu Aberaham gyaŋ Wurubuarɛ man. Kenaŋ wɔle te kɔbate ŋon mɔ kaa yeŋawɔ te be wuu e te ɔ gyuuwɔ ka gyoo boalaŋ keŋ ke bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man.

eng: While being tormented in the place of the dead, he looked up and saw Abraham at a distance with Lazarus at his side.
local: Ɔ ke doo botɔɔ ɔ ne naa diyem nɛ, ɔ gyinaa ɔ sia kɛɛ adido na te ɔ naa Aberaham lemlem na Lasarose ŋon kyaa ɔ yenaŋ.

eng: He shouted, Father Abraham, have mercy on me. Send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, because Im suffering in this flame.
local: Botɔɔ te kɔbate ŋon gyinaa ɔ nɔɔ baake Aberaham nyiaa, Me kya Aberaham! Wii me waraŋase na n ke yela Lasarose ka mo ɔ nyimbii ke sɔɔ loŋ man, na waa mo kaa tina me laakem na ma wose ke fɛɛ maŋ kyomii, nawolo nyi mɛɛ naa diyem nideli boalaŋ kei man.

eng: But Abraham said, Child, remember that during your lifetime you received good things, whereas Lazarus received terrible things. Now Lazarus is being comforted and you are in great pain.
local: Keŋte Aberaham tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Me bu, tɔɔse nyi debaŋ keŋ n kyaa tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ n nyiŋa abɔɔ kpaakpaa di gyoŋ, na Lasarose ŋon doo diyem man ɔ na yeyɛɛ, te nɛnɛɛ ŋon mɔ ne di gyoŋ kɛbo te nyaŋ mɔ n doo diyem man n na yeyɛɛ.

eng: Moreover, a great crevasse has been fixed between us and you. Those who wish to cross over from here to you cannot. Neither can anyone cross from there to us.
local: Te agyakaŋ yɛna nyi, bɔɔ dinaa dinaa daale doo daa na ŋon nsana keŋ nyi baŋ be kyaa kɛbo nɛ be gyae baa tale gbaa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ ya, mena mɔ te baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ be gyae baa tale gbaa kɔŋ de gyaŋ kɛbo ya.

eng: The rich man said, Then I beg you, Father, send Lazarus to my fathers house.
local: Keŋte kɔbate ŋon yako e nyiaa, Me kya, kenaŋ na mɛɛ sola neŋ bo, kpila Lasarose ka bese gyu me kya dɛɛ.

eng: I have five brothers. He needs to warn them so that they dont come to this place of agony.
local: nawolo nyi me dana naabia baala banoŋ na ɔ kaa faree wɔ, na mena ya na baŋ mɔ baa kɔŋ kaa naa diyem boalaŋ kei man.

eng: Abraham replied, They have Moses and the Prophets. They must listen to them.
local: Keŋte Aberaham yako kɔbate ŋon nyiaa, Ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ɔ be yeli Mosesi be ŋmarase see, na Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra ba wolo nɛ. Mena dɔɔ baa tei nombia ŋenaŋ nideli.

eng: The rich man said, No, Father Abraham! But if someone from the dead goes to them, they will change their hearts and lives.
local: Botɔɔ te kɔbate ŋon tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Koa me kya Aberaham! Akpaa ŋolo da lee woya man kɛbo gyu keyako wɔ nɛ, baa kyɛɛkee lee be nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ man na baa kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

eng: Abraham said, If they dont listen to Moses and the Prophets, then neither will they be persuaded if someone rises from the dead.
local: Keŋte Aberaham yako e nyiaa, Nyi akpaa be te nyii Mosesi na Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ nombia ya nɛ, mena mɔ te ŋolo de fom lee yeŋ man kɛbo gyu be gyaŋ koraŋ na bɔɔ gyae baa taŋ bua e ya. .

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Things that cause people to trip and fall into sin must happen, but how terrible it is for the person through whom they happen.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Abɔɔ ŋan ne yeli balaŋ ne yɛɛ dukum nɛ gyae ke kɔŋ, mɔna nyaŋ ŋon ŋe gyae ke ta n dɔɔ kɔŋ nɛ nya laako!.

eng: It would be better for them to be thrown into a lake with a large stone hung around their neck than to cause one of these little ones to trip and fall into sin.
local: Akpaa nyi ba mo boe dinaa dinaa kpaŋ n wɔŋe man te be fuŋ neŋ do ɛpo man nɛ, dei fa neŋ kela nyi n ke yela bia buruburu kɛwɔ man ŋolo keyɛɛ dukum.

eng: Watch yourselves! If your brother or sister sins, warn them to stop. If they change their hearts and lives, forgive them.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose nideli. Akpaa n dɔɔ de yɛɛ neŋ dukum na lese si e, te akpaa ɔ nyii ɔ wose asɛɛ te ɔ kyɛɛkee na mo kyɛɛ e.

eng: Even if someone sins against you seven times in one day and returns to you seven times and says, I am changing my ways, you must forgive that person.
local: Akpaa ɔ yɛɛ nombiikum tia neŋ betabonyetooro wee dokoloŋ, te ɔ bese kɔŋ n gyaŋ betabonyetooro wee dokoloŋ kenaŋ kaa doo neŋ teree na mo kyɛɛ e.

eng: The apostles said to the Lord, Increase our faith.
local: Keŋte Yesu kpilala kufu bala baŋ yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, yela da kelɛɛdi ka bo kpu.

eng: The Lord replied, If you had faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, Be uprooted and planted in the sea, and it would obey you.
local: Keŋte de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Akpaa ɛ kelɛɛdi yɛɛ peperee ŋgba kefaabii koraŋ nɛ, ɛ ke tale yako daŋ nyi Kulu na n lila pou na n kaa seŋ ɛpo man, te gyae ke kɔŋ mena.

eng: Would any of you say to your servant, who had just come in from the field after plowing or tending sheep, Come! Sit down for dinner?.
local: Dɛɛ mo ke nyi ɛ man ŋolo dana tobaale ŋon ɔ ne fare fa e yaa ɔ ne kɛɛ ɔ namense dɔɔ. Akpaa tobaale ŋon da lee ŋale man kɔŋ nɛ, ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon bɛɛ yako e nyi tisi kyaa bileŋ na n kedi ya.

eng: Wouldnt you say instead, Fix my dinner. Put on the clothes of a table servant and wait on me while I eat and drink. After that, you can eat and drink?.
local: Mɔna ɔ ne yako e bo nyi, Desina n wose na n ke saa me balinɔɔ weenɛɛ fa maŋ na n ke kyaa daa maŋ maa di na maa nyɔɔ, me taŋ na pɛna nyaŋ mɔ n kaa di na n ke nyɔɔ.

eng: You wont thank the servant because the servant did what you asked, will you?.
local: Kaboena nyi balee ŋon waa do ɔ tobaale ŋon nɔɔ nyi ɔ te yɛɛ kpene keŋ ɔ be yako e nyi waa yɛɛ dɔɔ? Koa.

eng: In the same way, when you have done everything required of you, you should say, We servants deserve no special praise. We have only done our duty.
local: Mena te doo fa ɛmɛɛ mɔ, nyi akpaa ɛ yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ keŋ mɔɔ yako ŋon nyi yaa yɛɛ nɛ, kaboena yaa yako nyi de taŋ yɛɛ n tewulɛɛwɔ, tom keŋ kaboena nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ te de yɛɛwɔ.

eng: On the way to Jerusalem, Jesus traveled along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
local: Yesu ka mo ɔ nyee baŋ ɔ ne gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ tɛɛwɔ Samaria na Galelia tɛɛlese wulu man.

eng: As he entered a village, ten men with skin diseases approached him. Keeping their distance from him.
local: Debaŋ keŋ ɔ na gyoo akuraa daale man nɛ, balaŋ kufu baale baŋ bɛɛ wee kawee yayam nɛ kɔŋawɔ be kaa gyaŋee na e te be dɔɔwɔ seŋ lemlem.

eng: they raised their voices and said, Jesus, Master, show us mercy.
local: keŋte be gyinaa be nɔɔ nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo Yesu! Waaseda wii de waraŋase.

eng: When Jesus saw them, he said, Go, show yourselves to the priests. As they left, they were cleansed.
local: Yesu kena wɔ keŋ nɛ, ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa Ɛ mo ɛ wose kawola Wurubuarɛ saese baŋ. Be kee gyuu nɛ te be kaweese ŋan taŋawɔ.

eng: One of them, when he saw that he had been healed, returned and praised God with a loud voice.
local: Be man ŋolo kena nyi ɔ kawee te taŋ nɛ, ɔ besewɔ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ na ɔ ne fae yeesa lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

eng: He fell on his face at Jesus feet and thanked him. He was a Samaritan.
local: Keŋte ɔ kaa buŋawɔ Yesu nawɔɔtɛɛ do e nɔɔ. Na mena balee kei yɛɛ Samaria baale e.

eng: Jesus replied, Werent ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, Na balaŋ kufu te me kyɔɔwɔ yee? Te nde balaŋ kadaale akaŋ baŋ?.

eng: No one returned to praise God except this foreigner.
local: Weera dɔɔ te ŋɔɔle kei waageŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba bese kɔŋ ɔ kaa fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ.

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Get up and go. Your faith has healed you.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, Koro na n ke ta, n kelɛɛdi dɔɔ te n nyiŋa kawee kekyɔ.

eng: Pharisees asked Jesus when Gods kingdom was coming. He replied, Gods kingdom isnt coming with signs that are easily noticed.
local: Wee daale nɛ Farasiitena baale bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, debaŋ woŋti te Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ. Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyi, Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ be gyae ke kɔŋ na ɛ kena ke na ɛ sia fonɛɛ nyi kɛɛ kɔŋ ya.

eng: Nor will people say, Look, here it is! or There it is! Dont you see? Gods kingdom is already among you.
local: Ŋolo be gyae waa yako ŋon nyiaa Ɛ kɛɛ, keŋ yaa kɛbo, yaa ke kyaa yenaŋ daale ya! Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ kyaa ɛ man.

eng: Then Jesus said to the disciples, The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Human One,and you wont see it.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Debaŋ daale gyae kekɔŋ keŋ ɛ ke gyae nyi ɛ ka bese naa maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon kekyaa man wee dokoloŋ gba, mɔna ɛ be gyae ɛ kena ya.

eng: People will say to you, Look there! or Look here! Dont leave or go chasing after them.
local: Balaŋ baa yako ŋon nyiaa, Ɔ kyaa kɛbo, yaa ɔ kyaa yenaŋ daale! Mɔna ɛ na yekii sila wɔ ya.

eng: The Human Onewill appear on his day in the same way that a flash of lightning lights up the sky from one end to the other.
local: Nawolo nyi wee keŋ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa bese kɔŋ nɛ, walaŋ kamasɛ waa naa maŋ ŋgba mena keŋ ɔga ne ŋmaale adido te walaŋ kamasɛ ne naa ka keŋmaŋee keŋ nɛ.

eng: However, first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
local: Mɔna kaboena nyi maa taŋgbɛɛ naa diyem nideli, na gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ ke bɛɛ maŋ pɛ.

eng: As it was in the days of Noah, so it will be during the days of the Human One.
local: Mena keŋ be kɔŋ Nowa kekyaa man nɛ, mena mɔ te gyae ke kɔŋ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon ka bese kɔŋ man.

eng: People were eating, drinking, marrying, and being given in marriage until the day Noah entered the ark and the flood came and destroyed them all.
local: Mena Nowa weeya ŋenaŋ man nɛ, na balaŋ gyakaa bee dii na bɛɛ nyɔɔ, na ala na baala mɔ ne yala dɔŋa kelii debaŋ keŋ Nowa ba gyoo adakaa keŋ man te loŋ keŋ waalewɔ kaa koe balaŋ pou.

eng: Likewise in the days of Lot, people were eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, and building.
local: Mena mɔ te yɛɛwɔ Lɔɔte kekyaa man. Balaŋ gyakaa bee dii na bɛɛ nyɔɔ, na baale ne yɔɔ abɔɔ na baale mɔ ne lɛɛ, na baale ne duu abɔɔ na baale mɔ ne ma denɛɛ.

eng: But on the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
local: Mɔna wee keŋ Lɔɔte ba lee Sodom donɔɔ man nɛ, Wurubuarɛ yela boalaŋ na kpatakpela leewɔ adido kɔŋ kaa kpa Sodom donɔɔ pou.

eng: Thats the way it will be on the day the Human Oneis revealed.
local: Mena mɔ te wee keŋ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa bɛɛ lee nɛ gyae ke futaa balaŋ.

eng: On that day, those on the roof, whose possessions are in the house, shouldnt come down to grab them. Likewise, those in the field shouldnt turn back.
local: Mena wee kenaŋ nɛ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ be kyaa ɔ deni man ya te wa abɔɔ doo ɔ deni man nɛ, ɔ na bese gyu ka lese ŋa ya. Mena mɔ te walaŋ ŋon ɔ kyaa wɔɔman nɛ ɔ na bese gyu dɛɛ nyi ɔ kaa mo ɔ kolo ya.

eng: Remember Lots wife!.
local: Ɛ na wolee kpene keŋ be kɔŋ Lɔɔte wɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ ya.

eng: Whoever tries to preserve their life will lose it, but whoever loses their life will preserve it.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae nyi waa lɛɛ ŋon gbagba nyeedoŋ na ke gyae ka lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man, mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ nyeedoŋ da lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man lee maŋ dɔɔ nɛ waa bese nyiŋ ke.

eng: I tell you, on that night two people will be in the same bed: one will be taken and the other left.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyi mena wee kenaŋ nelim nɛ balaŋ bala baa doo dofoŋoŋ dokoloŋ dɔɔ, mɔna Wurubuarɛ waa mo ŋolo tina ŋolo yela.

eng: Two women will be grinding grain together: one will be taken and the other left.
local: Ala bala baa seŋ debodokoloŋ bɛɛ naŋ abɔɔ, mɔna Wurubuarɛ waa mo ŋolo tina ŋolo yela.

eng: The disciples asked, Where, Lord? Jesus said, The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, aleŋ gbɛɛ te mena nombia kɛŋa pou gyae kekɔŋ? Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Botɔɔ keŋ kolo punta doo nɛ, botɔɔ te bapɛtɛɛna ne yilaa.

eng: Jesus was telling them a parable about their need to pray continuously and not to be discouraged.
local: Keŋte Yesu gyɔɔ dudu fa ɔ kaseela baŋ wola wɔ nyiaa, kaboena nyi baa fane wee kamasɛ keŋ ba wose na yɔkɔse wɔ ya.

eng: He said, In a certain city there was a judge who neither feared God nor respected people.
local: Ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa Donɔɔ daale man nɛ nombiadiiri ŋolo kyaawɔ keŋ ɔ bee yee Wurubuarɛ ya, te ɔ be famenɛ na walaŋ mɔ ya.

eng: In that city there was a widow who kept coming to him, asking, Give me justice in this case against my adversary.
local: Na lialo ŋolo mɔ kyaa donɔɔ kenaŋ man. Ɔ ne kyaa kɔŋ nombiadiiri ŋon gyaŋ kaa waase e wee kamasɛ nyiaa, Kɛɛ na n ke di daa na me walaŋ ŋon daa na e be nyim nɛ nombia ŋan nideli fa maŋ.

eng: For a while he refused but finally said to himself, I dont fear God or respect people.
local: Nafɔ nombiadiiri ŋon bɛɛwɔ kelii debaŋ daale, mɔna wɔle wɔle nɛ ɔ yako ɔ wose nyiaa, Yɛɛ ampaŋ nyi mɔɔ ne yee Wurubuarɛ ya te mɔɔ famenɛ na walaŋ mɔ ya.

eng: but I will give this widow justice because she keeps bothering me. Otherwise, there will be no end to her coming here and embarrassing me.
local: Mɔna alo kei nombia te poro maŋ mena dɔɔ maa kɛɛ na maa di ɔ nombia ŋan nideli fa e, na ɔ be nyiŋ gyem tisi me dɔɔ naase maŋ diyem bela ya. .

eng: The Lord said, Listen to what the unjust judge says.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, Ɛ nyii kpene keŋ nombiadiiri kum kei be yako.

eng: Wont God provide justice to his chosen people who cry out to him day and night? Will he be slow to help them?.
local: Akpaa mena na Wurubuarɛ be gyae waa di nombia anokoare man fa ɔ balaŋ baŋ bee wii baake e nelim na ka weese nɛ yee? Ɛ ne lɛɛdi nyi waa wɔŋee ɔ wose nyi ɔ be kyɔ wɔ yee?.

eng: I tell you, he will give them justice quickly. But when the Human Onecomes, will he find faithfulness on earth.
local: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ waa di nombia ŋan anokoare man fa wɔ bileŋ na waa yela baa di deŋele. Te nyi akpaa maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon ma bese kɔŋ na, balaŋ baa kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ keŋ baa lɛɛ maŋ di.

eng: Jesus told this parable to certain people who had convinced themselves that they were righteous and who looked on everyone else with disgust.
local: Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu kei fa balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɛɛ ba wose gyɔ konɔɔ nyi be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman, te bɔɔ na mo walaŋ kamasɛ yɛɛ kolo ya nɛ nyiaa!.

eng: Two people went up to the temple to pray. One was a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
local: Baala bala baale gyuu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man be kaa fane. Ŋolo yɛɛ Farasii baale e te ŋolo mɔ yɛɛ lempoolɛɛre e.

eng: The Pharisee stood and prayed about himself with these words, God, I thank you that Im not like everyone else—crooks, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.
local: Farasii baale ŋon korowɔ seŋ te ɔ fanewɔ kɛlɛɛ ɔ wose nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ mɛɛ fa neŋ karaŋ nyi ma te yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛre e ŋgba balaŋ akaŋ baŋ nɛ ya. Mɔɔ yɛɛ ŋgba ŋmɛɛlatena na balaŋ kumɛɛ na kafoŋtena yaa ŋgba lempoolɛɛre kei nɛ ya.

eng: I fast twice a week. I give a tenth of everything I receive.
local: Mɛɛ bake me nɔɔ betabola weeya nyetooro kamasɛ man, te mɛɛ kpase ma kpene kamasɛ keŋ mee nyim nɛ akpeŋ kufu na me ta mo dekpeŋ dokoloŋ fa nyaŋ Wurubuarɛ.

eng: But the tax collector stood at a distance. He wouldnt even lift his eyes to look toward heaven. Rather, he struck his chest and said, God, show mercy to me, a sinner.
local: Mɔna lempoolɛɛre ŋon dɔɔ, ŋon gyuuwɔ ka seŋ yenaŋ keŋ nyi ɔ te gyina ɔ sia kɛɛ adido gba ya te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaase gyakaa ɔ konɔɔ dɔɔ waraŋase man yako nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ, wii maŋ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛre kei waraŋase. .

eng: I tell you, this person went down to his home justified rather than the Pharisee. All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Keŋte Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyi ba ke fane taŋ nɛ, lempoolɛɛre ŋon te Wurubuarɛ lɛɛwɔ do nyi ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ŋon Wurubuarɛ siaman. Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ gyina ɔ wose adido na Wurubuarɛ waa yɔkɔse e, te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ yɔkɔse ɔ wose na Wurubuarɛ waa gyina e adido.

eng: People were bringing babies to Jesus so that he would bless them. When the disciples saw this, they scolded them.
local: Wee daale balaŋ baale moo be bia kɔŋ nyi Yesu waa mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa be dɔɔ na waa hiraa wɔ. Ɔ kaseela baŋ kena mena nɛ, be ŋmanyaŋ balaŋ baŋ.

eng: Then Jesus called them to him and said, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Keŋte Yesu baake bia baŋ kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Ɛ yela bia baŋ kekɔŋ me gyaŋ, ɛ na tɔ wɔ gbɛɛ ya. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɔkɔse ba wose ŋgba bia kɛwɔ dinɔɔ nɛ wui.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ te yɔkɔse ɔ wose do Wurubuarɛ tɛɛ ŋgba biikyaa e ya nɛ, ɔ be gyae waa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man korakora ya.

eng: A certain ruler asked Jesus, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Wee daale nɛ Gyudatena siamante ŋolo kaa bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa De Gbeŋgyoo kpaakpaa, woŋ te maa yɛɛ na maa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, Woŋ dɔɔ te n ne baake maŋ walaŋ kpaakpaa? Lee Wurubuarɛ wɔle nɛ walaŋ kamasɛ be kyaa keŋ ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ kpaakpaate e ya.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit adultery. Dont murder. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony. Honor your father and mother. .
local: Ŋgba n gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ne yako nyi, Na lee n baale yaa n wɛɛle wɔle ya, na ko walaŋ ya na ŋmɛɛle ya, na kara n dɔɔ nɔɔ ya. Bu n kya na n naa nɛ.

eng: Then the ruler said, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Keŋte balee ŋon mɔ tiranɔɔ nyiaa Me wulaa mee di mmaraase kɛŋa pou dɔɔ leenaŋ me biatɛɛ.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he said, Theres one more thing. Sell everything you own and distribute the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Yesu ke nyii ɔ nɔɔwoya ŋan nɛ ɔ yako e nyiaa, Ta bese ka neŋ kolo dokoloŋ kooŋ n ke yɛɛ. Gyu ke yɔɔ nya abɔɔ ŋan pou n dana nɛ na n ka mo kɔba keŋ kpɛlɛɛ ayematena na n ke kɔŋ kaa sila maŋ. N yɛɛ mena na n ke nyiŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa see n wose Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ adido.

eng: When he heard these words, the man became sad because he was extremely rich.
local: Balee ŋon ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, takaa e nideli nawolo nyi ɔ dana abɔɔ burum.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he said, Its very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Keŋte Yesu gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ e te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Gyae ke yɛɛ doŋ fa baŋ be dana kɔba nɛ nyi baa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, gyae ke yɛɛ doŋ fa baŋ be dana kɔba nɛ nyi baa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man, kela nyi kurumɔɔ ka gyoo ŋarale bɔɔ man lee.

eng: Those who heard this said, Then who can be saved.
local: Balaŋ baŋ ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ be bɔɔse e nyiaa, Nyi nnɛ te doo na, amɔte ne gyae la waa nyiŋ nyeekelɛɛ baa.

eng: Jesus replied, What is impossible for humans is possible for God.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Kpene keŋ deniwalaŋ be gyae waa tale yɛɛ ya nɛ, Wurubuarɛ dɔɔ waa tale yɛɛ.

eng: Peter said, Look, we left everything we own and followed you.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita yakowɔ nyiaa, De gbeŋgyoo, daa dɔɔ de te tina kpene kamasɛ keŋ de dana nɛ yela, te de dɔŋɛɛ de silana neŋ.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you that anyone who has left house, husband, wife, brothers, sisters, parents, or children because of Gods kingdom.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ be tina ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ yaa ɔ wɛɛle yaa ɔ naabia yaa ɔ kya na ɔ naa yaa ɔ bia yela lee Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: will receive many times more in this age and eternal life in the coming age.
local: waa bese nyiŋ ŋa burum kpu gyɛŋ weeya kɛŋa man, na waa bese nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa wee daale.

eng: Jesus took the Twelve aside and said, Look, were going up to Jerusalem, and everything written about the Human Oneby the prophets will be accomplished.
local: Wee daale nɛ Yesu baake ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ lee yenaŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Dee gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man baa, te kpene kamasɛ keŋ Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra be ŋmarase lee maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon wose man nɛ, gyae ke kɔŋ mena.

eng: He will be handed over to the Gentiles. He will be ridiculed, mistreated, and spit on.
local: Baa mo maŋ do balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ nyiŋmaa man, na baa kulu maŋ na baa tee maŋ na baa tɔ nɔɔloŋ woli maŋ na baa tuku maŋ na baa ko maŋ.

eng: After torturing him, they will kill him. On the third day, he will rise up.
local: Mɔna weeya atoorote keŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu maŋ lee yeŋ man.

eng: But the Twelve understood none of these words. The meaning of this message was hidden from them and they didnt grasp what he was saying.
local: Yesu kaseela baŋ te nyii nombia kɛŋa man ya, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ ta lese ŋe man wola wɔ ya mena dɔɔ be te nyii ŋe man dokoloŋ man ya.

eng: As Jesus came to Jericho, a certain blind man was sitting beside the road begging.
local: Yesu kɛɛ benaa Gyeriko donɔɔ man nɛ, na siayɛlɛɛsate ŋolo kyaa gbɛɛnaa man ɔ na solee abɔɔ.

eng: When the man heard the crowd passing by, he asked what was happening.
local: Ɔ ke nyii balaŋ burum baŋ kɛɛ lam nɛ, ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyi woŋ kɔŋala?.

eng: They told him, Jesus the Nazarene is passing by.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ yako e nyiaa Yesu Nasarɛte baale ŋon ne laŋa laa.

eng: The blind man shouted, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ gyinaa ɔ nɔɔ baake nyiaa, Yesu, Defidi ɔ naanabii! Wii me waraŋase.

eng: Those leading the procession scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Balaŋ baŋ be dɔŋɛɛ siaman nɛ ŋmanyaŋ e nyi waa wu ɔ nɔɔ. Mɔna nɛnɛɛ gba te ɔ desinaa fae yeesa nedoŋ nyiaa, Defidi ɔ naanabii! Wii me waraŋase.

eng: Jesus stopped and called for the man to be brought to him. When he was present Jesus asked.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu seŋawɔ te ɔ yakowɔ nyi baa mo siayɛlɛɛsate ŋon kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ. Ɔ ka benaa e nɛ, Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? He said, Lord, I want to see.
local: Woŋ te n ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ fa neŋ? Keŋte siayɛlɛɛsate ŋon tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, mɛɛ gyae me sia ke gyuusu.

eng: Jesus said to him, Receive your sight! Your faith has healed you.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, N sia dee gyuusu na n kena. N ke lɛɛ maŋ di dɔɔ te n nyiŋa kawee kekyɔ.

eng: At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus, praising God. When all the people saw it, they praised God too.
local: Debokenaŋ man te ɔ sia gyuusuwɔ, te ɔ naawɔ te ɔ sila Yesu na ɔ na lese Wurubuarɛ yele. Balaŋ dikpii keŋ pou kena nɛ, baŋ mɔ lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

eng: Jesus entered Jericho and was passing through town.
local: Yesu ke gyu ka gyoo Gyeriko donɔɔ man ɔ ne lam nɛ.

eng: A man there named Zacchaeus, a ruler among tax collectors, was rich.
local: na balee ŋolo kyaa botɔɔ keŋ ɔ yele yɛna Sakio. Ɔ yɛɛ lempoolɛɛra kegyia e te ɔ dana kɔba mɔ nideli.

eng: He was trying to see who Jesus was, but, being a short man, he couldnt because of the crowd.
local: Sakio kei gyaewɔ nyi waa naa Yesu mɔna ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ duguŋte e dɔɔ ɔ te tale naa e ya, nawolo nyi balaŋ baŋ boe.

eng: So he ran ahead and climbed up a sycamore tree so he could see Jesus, who was about to pass that way.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔ kyaŋa gbɛɛ gyu siaman ka gyeŋ daŋ daale, na waa nyiŋ naa e nawolo nyi botɔɔ te Yesu waa kaa mo.

eng: When Jesus came to that spot, he looked up and said, Zacchaeus, come down at once. I must stay in your home today.
local: Yesu kelii daŋ keŋ tɛɛ nɛ ɔ gyinaa ɔ sia kɛɛ adido te ɔ naa Sakio te ɔ baake e nyiaa, Sakio tisi kɔŋ bileŋ, nawolo nyi gyɛŋ n dɛɛ te maa soo.

eng: So Zacchaeus came down at once, happy to welcome Jesus.
local: Mena dɔɔ Sakio mɔ tisiwɔ bileŋ kɔŋ te ɔ lɛɛ Yesu gyoŋ man.

eng: Everyone who saw this grumbled, saying, He has gone to be the guest of a sinner.
local: Balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ naa nɛ fiasɛɛ bɛɛ ŋorokotee nyi, Balee kei ne gyu ɔ kaa soo nombiakumɛɛyɛɛre kei.

eng: Zacchaeus stopped and said to the Lord, Look, Lord, I give half of my possessions to the poor. And if I have cheated anyone, I repay them four times as much.
local: Be ke gyu ka gyoo Sakio dɛɛ nɛ, ɔ korowɔ kaa seŋ balaŋ baŋ siaman te ɔ yako Yesu nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo kɛɛ, maa kpase ma abɔɔ ŋan pou me dana nɛ yaale fa ayematena. Te akpaa me te sisii ŋolo na maa tɔ e betabonaara kpu.

eng: Jesus said to him, Today, salvation has come to this household because he too is a son of Abraham.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako Sakio nyiaa, Gyɛŋ Wurubuarɛ te lɛɛ ɛmɛɛ na n dekpaŋalaŋmantena pou nyee, nawolo nyi nyaŋ mɔ n yɛɛ Aberaham ɔ naanabii e.

eng: The Human Onecame to seek and save the lost.
local: Nawolo nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu kɔŋae me kaa kɛo na maa lɛɛ baŋ bɔɔ yoo nɛ nyee.

eng: As they listened to this, Jesus told them another parable because he was near Jerusalem and they thought Gods kingdom would appear right away.
local: Balaŋ baŋ kee tei mena nombia kɛŋa nɛ, Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu fa wɔ nawolo nyi bɛɛ benaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man, na balaŋ gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ gyae ke bɛɛ lee debokenaŋ man.

eng: He said, A certain man who was born into royalty went to a distant land to receive his kingdom and then return.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, Balee ŋolo kyaawɔ keŋ ɔ yɛɛ gyooro bu e, te ɔ korowɔ gyu tɛɛle daale dɔɔ lemlem ɔ kaa di gyoori na waa bese kɔŋ.

eng: He called together ten servants and gave each of them money worth four months wages.He said, Do business with this until I return.
local: Debaŋ keŋ ɔ ne gyae waa gyu nɛ ɔ baake ɔ tewulɛɛ kufu baale te ɔ faa wɔ kɔba kyɛɛkyɛɛ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ mo kɔba kei besena ɛ wose kelii debaŋ keŋ maa bese kɔŋ.

eng: On one of the days when Jesus was teaching the people in the temple and proclaiming the good news, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders approached him.
local: Wee daale Yesu ke kyaa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ɔ na wola balaŋ nombia te ɔ ne kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa wɔ nɛ, saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala na donɔɔ kegyiise kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ.

eng: His citizens hated him, so they sent a representative after him who said, We dont want this man to be our king.
local: Mɔna ɔ dɛɛtena baŋ bɛɛ gyae ɔ nombia ya, mena dɔɔ be kpila balaŋ gyu keyako donɔɔ keŋ man ɔ ne gyu nɛ kegyiise nyiaa, De bɛɛ gyae balee kei kaa bese de gyoo ya.

eng: They said, Tell us: What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa, Yako daa, doŋ woŋti yaa n dana n ne yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou? Amɔte faa na neŋ mena doŋ kenaŋ.

eng: After receiving his kingdom, he returned and called the servants to whom he had given the money to find out how much they had earned.
local: Mɔna kegyiise baŋ see balee ŋon gyoo te ɔ besewɔ kɔŋ ɔ dɛɛ. Ɔ ka bese kɔŋ nɛ ɔ kpilawɔ baake ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ ɔ be fa wɔ kɔba nɛ nyi baa kɔŋ, na waa kɛɛ kpene keŋ bɔɔ nyim gyakaa be kɔbase ŋan dɔɔ.

eng: He replied, I have a question for you. Tell me.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Maŋ mɔ ne gyae maa bɔɔse ŋon nombii dokoloŋ.

eng: The first servant came forward and said, Your money has earned a return of one thousand percent.
local: Ɔ tobaale gyaŋgbate ŋon kɔŋawɔ kaa yako e nyiaa, Me gbeŋgyoo, kɔba keŋ n ba mo fa maŋ nɛ ta lola kufu kpu na bene keŋ.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin.
local: Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋkesɔ keŋ ɔ be sɔ balaŋ nɛ, lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ yaa balaŋ gyaŋ.

eng: The king replied, Excellent! You are a good servant. Because you have been faithful in a small matter, you will have authority over ten cities.
local: Keŋte ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon yako e nyiaa, Nyaŋ na keyɛɛ, n yɛɛ tobaale kpaakpaate e! Mena keŋ n be di anokoare abɔɔ peperee kɛŋa man dɔɔ nɛ, maa yela n ke kɛɛ donɔɔse kufu dɔɔ fa maŋ.

eng: They discussed among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Why didnt you believe him.
local: Botɔɔ te be lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ na dɔŋa nyiaa, Woŋ te dɛɛ yako? Akpaa de yako nyi ka lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ na, waa bɔɔse daa nyi woŋ dɔɔ te da te lɛɛ Gyɔn nombia ŋan di ya?.

eng: The second servant came and said, Master, your money has made a return of five hundred percent.
local: Ɔ tobaale balate ŋon mɔ kɔŋawɔ kaa yako e nyiaa, Me gbeŋgyoo, n kɔba keŋ n ba mo fa maŋ nɛ ta lola anoŋ kpu na bene keŋ. .

eng: But if we say, Its of human origin, all the people will stone us to death because they are convinced that John was a prophet.
local: Te akpaa de yako nyi ka lee balaŋ gyaŋ na, balaŋ dikpii kɛwɔ pou baa fuŋii daa boya. Nawolo nyi bɛɛ lɛɛ di nideli nyi Gyɔn yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e.

eng: To this one, the king said, You will have authority over five cities.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Nyaŋ mɔ n ke kɛɛ donɔɔse anoŋ dɔɔ fa maŋ.

eng: They answered that they didnt know where it came from.
local: Mena dɔɔ be tiranɔɔ fa Yesu nyiaa Dɔɔ gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ kɔɔ lee ya.

eng: Another servant came and said, Master, here is your money. I wrapped it up in a scarf for safekeeping.
local: Keŋte ɔ tobaale ŋolo mɔ kɔŋawɔ kaa yako e nyiaa, Me gbeŋgyoo, n kɔba keŋ n ba mo fa maŋ nɛ yaa, ma moo ke kpaŋ na kakyeŋgbelaa ka weese.

eng: Then Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Keŋte Yesu mɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Akpaa mena na, maŋ mɔ ba wola ŋon me doŋ keŋ da lee ya.

eng: I was afraid of you because you are a stern man. You withdraw what you havent deposited and you harvest what you havent planted.
local: Nawolo nyi na mee yee neŋ nyi n nombia dana doŋ. N na mo kpene keŋ te yɛɛ n wui ya, te n ne kɔ abɔɔ ŋan n te duu ya nɛ mɔ.

eng: Jesus told the people this parable: A certain man planted a vineyard, rented it to tenant farmers, and went on a trip for a long time.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu gyɔɔ dudu kei fa balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, Wɔɔfarale ŋolo fara wɔɔ te ɔ duu daasebia ŋaale ŋan ŋe yɛɛ ŋgba akutuse te bɛɛ baake ŋa nyi gerepo nɛ, te ɔ moo ŋa do paasetena baale nyiŋmaa man nyi baa kɛɛ ŋe dɔɔ, te ɔ korowɔ gyu tɛɛle wɔle daale man lemlem ke kyaa botɔɔ kyare.

eng: The king replied, I will judge you by the words of your own mouth, you worthless servant! You knew, did you, that Im a stern man, withdrawing what I didnt deposit, and harvesting what I didnt plant?.
local: Keŋte ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon yako e nyiaa, N yɛɛ tobaale kum e, nyaŋ gbagba n nɔɔwoya te maa mo bu neŋ fɔɔ. N gyeŋ nyi me nombia dana doŋ te mɛɛ mo kpene keŋ te yɛɛ me wui ya, te mɛɛ kɔ abɔɔ ŋan me te duu ya na.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants sent him away, beaten and empty-handed.
local: Debaŋ kelii nyi bɛɛ kɔ daasebia ŋan nɛ, ɔ kpila ɔ tobaale ŋolo gyu paasetena baŋ gyaŋ nyi baa fa e ɔ wui nɔɔ. Botɔɔ te paasetena baŋ tuku tobaale ŋon, te be tina e yela ɔ laŋawɔ nawɔɔ kufu nyiŋmaase kufu.

eng: Why then didnt you put my money in the bank? Then when I arrived, at least I could have gotten it back with interest.
local: woŋ dɔɔ te n ta mo me kɔba keŋ ka do kɔba dɛɛsee man na me kɔŋ na, me kaa lɛɛ keŋ na ka loŋ keŋ ya.

eng: The man sent another servant. But they beat him, treated him disgracefully, and sent him away empty-handed as well.
local: Wɔɔte ŋon besewɔ kpila tobaale wɔle bela, mɔna be tuku ŋon mɔ do e desɛŋ te be yela e ɔ laŋawɔ nawɔɔ kufu nyiŋmaase kufu.

eng: He said to his attendants, Take his money and give it to the one who has ten times as much.
local: Botɔɔ te be gbeŋgyoo ŋon yako baŋ ba seŋɛɛ botɔɔ nɛ nyiaa, Ɛ lɛo kɔba keŋ lee ɔ gyaŋ na ɛ ka mo fa walaŋ ŋon ɔ kɔba ba lola kufu kpu nɛ.

eng: He sent a third servant. They wounded this servant and threw him out.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ besewɔ kpila tobaale batoorote, mɔna be tuku ŋon mɔ do e gyamɛɛ te be gegi e lee wɔɔ keŋ man.

eng: But Master, they said, he already has ten times as much.
local: Keŋte ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ yako e nyiaa, De gbeŋgyoo, ŋgba ŋon wulaa ɔ dana kɔba burum.

eng: The owner of the vineyard said, What should I do? Ill send my son, whom I love dearly. Perhaps they will respect him.
local: Keŋte wɔɔte ŋon yakowɔ nyiaa, Woŋ te maa yɛɛ? Maa kpila maŋ gbagba me tɔɔman lolo bu, mberem ŋon na baa bu e.

eng: He replied, I say to you that everyone who has will be given more, but from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.
local: Botɔɔ te be gbeŋgyoo ŋon tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ wulaa ɔ dana abɔɔ burum nɛ baa mo ŋaale kpu e bela. Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ betaŋ ya nɛ, kyomii keŋ gba ɔ dana koraŋ nɛ baa lɛo ke lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man.

eng: But when they saw him, they said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him so the inheritance will be ours.
local: Mɔna paasetena baŋ kena bu ŋon nɛ be yako dɔŋa nyiaa, Kei yɛna bu ŋon waa di ɔ kya abɔɔ ŋan. Ɛ yela dɛɛ ko e na wa abɔɔ ŋan ka bese de wui.

eng: As for my enemies who dont want me as their king, bring them here and slaughter them before me.
local: Te me kɔlala baŋ ba te gyae nyi maa di gyoori be dɔɔ ya nɛ, ɛ mo wɔ kɔŋ na ɛ kaa koe wɔ me siaman kɛbo.

eng: They threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What will the owner of the vineyard do to them?.
local: Mena dɔɔ be wɔŋee e lee wɔɔ keŋ man ka ko. Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Woŋ te ɛ ne lɛɛdi nyi wɔɔte ŋon waa yɛɛ paasetena baŋ.

eng: After Jesus said this, he continued on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
local: Yesu keyako nombia kɛŋa taŋ nɛ, ɔ tɛɛwɔ balaŋ baŋ siaman te ba moo ba nyee baŋ bee gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man.

eng: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others. When the people heard this, they said, May this never happen.
local: Waa kɔŋ na ɔ kaa ko paasetena benaŋ, na waa mo wɔɔ keŋ do balaŋ wɔlɛɛ nyiŋmaa man. Debaŋ keŋ balaŋ baŋ be nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ be yakowɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ na yeli kekɔŋ mena ya.

eng: As Jesus came to Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he gave two disciples a task.
local: Ɔ kɛɛ benaa Bɛtifagi na Bɛtani donɔɔse ŋan kyaa oliifi bula keŋ teŋa man nɛ, ɔ kpila ɔ kaseela baŋ man balaŋ bala.

eng: Staring at them, Jesus said, Then what is the meaning of this text of scripture: The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone?.
local: Keŋte Yesu kɛɛ wɔ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Nyi akpaa mena na, sena te ɛ ne nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia kɛŋa asɛɛ? Boe keŋ deni maara baŋ be bɛɛ nɛ, ta bese mokɔɔ kasee boe keŋ ne yeli deni keŋ ne yɛɛ doŋ.

eng: He said, Go into the village over there. When you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ever ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: nyiaa, Ɛ gyu akuraa keŋ kyaa ɛ siaman nɛ man. Ɛ betaa ɛ na gyoo ke man pɛ nɛ, ɛ kena afurum bu ŋolo kpaŋɛɛ keŋ walaŋ na walaŋ te kyaa ta ɔ dɔɔ see ya. Ɛ booli e na ɛ ka mo e kɔna maŋ kɛbo.

eng: Everyone who falls on that stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ yale boe kei dɔɔ na, ke gyae ke keretii e kpetekpetekpete. Te nyaŋ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon mɔ boe keŋ de yale n dɔɔ na, nyaŋ ke pɛtɛɛ.

eng: If anyone asks, Why are you untying it? just say, Its master needs it.
local: Akpaa ŋolo de bɔɔse ŋon nyi, Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne booli e? na Ɛ yako e nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ne gyae na e. .

eng: The legal experts and chief priests wanted to arrest him right then because they knew he had told this parable against them. But they feared the people.
local: Botɔɔ na saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ gyae gbɛɛ baa kyaŋ Yesu debokenaŋ man. Nawolo nyi ba gyeŋ nyi baŋ yaa ɔ gyɔɔ dudu keŋ, mɔna na bee yee balaŋ baŋ.

eng: Those who had been sent found it exactly as he had said.
local: Keŋte baŋ Yesu be kpili nɛ gyuuwɔ kena afurum bu ŋon kpaŋɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yako wɔ nɛ.

eng: The legal experts and chief priests were watching Jesus closely and sent spies who pretended to be sincere. They wanted to trap him in his words so they could hand him over to the jurisdiction and authority of the governor.
local: Keŋ dɔɔ ba moo be sia gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ. Botɔɔ te kegyiise baŋ gyae kɔba fa balaŋ te be kpila wɔ nyi baa yɛɛ ba wose ŋgba be yɛɛ balaŋ kpaakpaawɔ nɛ na baa deke Yesu, na be kaa teese ɔ nɔɔman na baa nyiŋ lese e fa gyoo dinaa ŋon ɔ ne di be dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: As they were untying the colt, its owners said to them, Why are you untying the colt.
local: Be kee booli afurum bu ŋon nɛ ɔ gbeŋgyoo bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne booli ma afurum bu ŋon.

eng: They asked him, Teacher, we know that you are correct in what you say and teach. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ gyuuwɔ ke bɔɔse e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, da gyeŋ nyi kpene keŋ n ne yako te n na wolo mɔ nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ. Da gyeŋ nyi n bɛɛ kɛɛ walaŋ siaman ya, mɔna n na wola balaŋ Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛneŋ kpaakpaa ŋan bo na anokoare.

eng: They replied, Its master needs it.
local: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ne gyae na e.

eng: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Dei nyi dɛɛ tɔ lempoo fa Romantena gyoo dinaa ŋon yaa de na tɔɔ ya.

eng: They brought it to Jesus, threw their clothes on the colt, and lifted Jesus onto it.
local: Te ba moo afurum bu ŋon kɔna Yesu. Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ moo be kanyaŋse gyakaa afurum bu ŋon dɔɔ te be gate Yesu gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ.

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deception, he said to them.
local: Yesu kena ba adao ŋan bee dii nɛ ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa.

eng: As Jesus rode along, they spread their clothes on the road.
local: Yesu kekyaa ɔ dɔɔ ɔ ne gyuu nɛ, baale fɛlɛɛ be kanyaŋse lɔ gbɛɛnaa man fa e.

eng: Show me a coin.Whose image and inscription does it have on it? Caesars, they replied.
local: Ɛ mo kɔbabii keŋ daale kɔŋ maa kɛɛ. Amɔte nyee na ɔ yele gyakaa la ke dɔɔ? Botɔɔ te be tiranɔɔ nyi Romantena gyoo dinaa ŋon.

eng: As Jesus approached the road leading down from the Mount of Olives, the whole throng of his disciples began rejoicing. They praised God with a loud voice because of all the mighty things they had seen.
local: Yesu kɛɛ gyoo Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man, te ɔ ka benaa na botɔɔ keŋ gbɛɛ keŋ be tisi lee oliifi bula keŋ nyee man ne kɔŋ ka teŋa man nɛ, ɔ silala burum baŋ pou sia gyeewɔ te be fiasɛɛ bɛɛ fae yeesa lese Wurubuarɛ yele gyoŋ man lee nombia dinaana na gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan bɔɔ naa nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: He said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Mena na kpene keŋ yɛɛ Gyoo dinaa ŋon wui na ɛ mo fa e, te kpene keŋ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ mɔ wui na ɛ mo fa e.

eng: They said, Blessings on the king who comes in the name of the Lord. Peace in heaven and glory in the highest heavens.
local: Be fae yeesa yako nyiaa, Ɛ fa dɛɛ lese gyoo ŋon ɔ ne kɔŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ yele man nɛ yele. Wosefɛɛreŋ dɛɛ kyaa adido, te Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa adido mɔ nɛ gyoorobiiri dɛɛ kaboena e.

eng: They couldnt trap him in his words in front of the people. Astonished by his answer, they were speechless.
local: Kpene keŋ ɔ be yako nɛ dɔɔ ba te tale mo ɔ nɔɔwoya ŋan kyaŋ e balaŋ baŋ siaman ya. Mena dɔɔ ɔ nɔɔwoya yɛɛ wɔ gyakoloŋ te be wuu be nɔɔse.

eng: Some of the Pharisees from the crowd said to Jesus, Teacher, scold your disciples! Tell them to stop.
local: Na Farasiitena baale kpuɛ na balaŋ baŋ be silana e nɛ te be yako Yesu nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, tɔ n silala baŋ nyi baa wu be nɔɔ.

eng: Some Sadducees, who deny that theres a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Keŋte Sadukiitena baŋ bɛɛ yako nyi yeŋ wɔle kefoŋ be kyaa ya nɛ man baale kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa.

eng: He answered, I tell you, if they were silent, the stones would shout.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Akpaa balaŋ kɛwɔ de wu be nɔɔ koraŋ nɛ, boya gyae ke fae yeesa lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother .
local: De Gbeŋgyoo, de naana Mosesi ŋmarase mmaraa kei fa daa nyi, Akpaa walaŋ ŋolo ɔ kegyia yaa ɔ naabu da yem tina ɔ wɛɛle yela te be ta lola bia ya na, kaboena nyi ŋon ɔ kyaa nɛ waa mo lialo ŋon yala na baa lola bia yere ŋon yele man.

eng: As Jesus came to the city and observed it, he wept over it.
local: Yesu ka benaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ te ɔ kena ke nɛ, ɔ wiiwɔ fa ke.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first man married a woman and then died childless.
local: Kɔŋawɔ nyi baala nyetooro baale kyaawɔ te be kegyia ŋon yala alo te ɔ yeŋawɔ keŋ ɔ ta lola bia ya.

eng: He said, If only you knew on this of all days the things that lead to peace. But now they are hidden from your eyes.
local: keŋte ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Nafɔ akpaa ɛmɛɛ Gyerusalɛmtena deŋ gyeŋ kpene keŋ gyae ke fa ŋon wosefɛɛreŋ gyɛŋ wee kei nɛ, nafɔ na dei. Mɔna ɛ ba gyeŋ ke ya!.

eng: The second.
local: Botɔɔ te balate ŋon mɔ yala alo ŋon.

eng: The time will come when your enemies will build fortifications around you, encircle you, and attack you from all sides.
local: Te weeya ŋaale gyae kekɔŋ keŋ ɛ kɔlala baa kilisi ŋon, na baa tɔ ŋon do lee yenaŋ kamasɛ.

eng: and then the third brother married her. Eventually all seven married her, and they all died without leaving any children.
local: te batoorote ŋon mɔ mena. Balaŋ nyetooro kɛwɔ pou yala alo kei te ba yekeewɔ, mɔna be man ŋolo na alo ŋon ta lolo ya.

eng: They will crush you completely, you and the people within you. They wont leave one stone on top of another within you, because you didnt recognize the time of your gracious visit from God.
local: Na baa wɔlɛɛ ɛmɛɛ na ɛ donɔɔ keŋ pou keŋ nyi boe na boe be gyae ke gyakaa ke dɔɔ dɔɔ ya, nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be kɔŋ nyi ɔ kaa lɛɛ ŋon nɛ ɛ bɛɛ e bo.

eng: Finally, the woman died too.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle na te alo ŋon mɔ yeŋawɔ.

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, he threw out those who were selling things there.
local: Keŋte Yesu gyuuwɔ ka gyoo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ dekpaŋalaŋ man. Na balaŋ ne yɔɔ abɔɔ botɔɔ te ɔ gegi wɔ.

eng: In the resurrection, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Mena dɔɔ wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ yem nɛ lee yeŋ man na, baala nyetooro kɛwɔ baŋ bɔɔ yala alo kei gyaŋee nɛ, be man woŋti te alo ŋon waa yɛɛ ɔ wɛɛle.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be a house of prayer, but you have made it a hideout for crooks. .
local: te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nyi Wurubuarɛ kpa, Me deni dinaa keŋ gyae ke yɛɛ botɔɔ keŋ baa fane. Mɔna ɛmɛɛ ta besena ke yɛɛ ŋmɛɛlatena dɛɛgyaŋee! .

eng: Jesus said to them, People who belong to this age marry and are given in marriage.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Bɛɛ mo ala baŋ be kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ fa yale te baala baŋ mɔ be kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ ne yala ala.

eng: Jesus was teaching daily in the temple. The chief priests, the legal experts, and the foremost leaders among the people were seeking to kill him.
local: Wee na wee na Yesu ne kyaa wola balaŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man. Te saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala na donɔɔ kegyiise baŋ gyae gbɛɛ nyi baa ko e.

eng: But those who are considered worthy to participate in that age, that is, in the age of the resurrection from the dead, wont marry nor will they be given in marriage.
local: mɔna balaŋ baŋ be kaboena nyi Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu wɔ lee yeŋ man na baa kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ, baala be gyae baa yala ya te bɔɔ gyae baa mo ala mɔ fa yale debaŋ wɔle kenaŋ man ya.

eng: However, they couldnt find a way to do it because all the people were enthralled with what they heard.
local: Mɔna be ta gyeŋ gbɛɛ keŋ baa mo kyaŋ e ya, nawolo nyi na balaŋ baŋ pou ne tei ɔ nombia ŋan ɔ na wolo nɛ nideli keŋ bɔɔ ne gyae ŋa ke laŋ wɔ ya.

eng: They can no longer die, because they are like angels and are Gods children since they share in the resurrection.
local: Yeŋ be tale ko wɔ bela ya nawolo nyi be yɛɛ ŋgba Wurubuarɛ kpilala nɛ. Be yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ biawɔ, nawolo nyi ba te fom lee yeŋ man.

eng: Even Moses demonstrated that the dead are raised—in the passage about the burning bush, when he speaks of the Lord as the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob .
local: Mosesi mɔ torowɔ yako nyiaa, balaŋ ayeŋaŋ baa foŋ lee yeŋ man. Nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ ɔ be naa depoŋfa keŋ ne kpaa nɛ ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ yɛna Aberaham na Asiki na Gyekɔpo Wurubuarɛ.

eng: Looking up, Jesus saw rich people throwing their gifts into the collection box for the temple treasury.
local: Yesu kekyaa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ, ɔ gyinaa ɔ nyee na ɔ naa kɔbatena kɛɛ mo be kɔba kaa do kɔba adakaa keŋ man.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. To him they are all alive.
local: Kei nawolo baa nyiaa, ɔ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ be kyaa na be sia nɛ Wurubuarɛ e. Ɔ te yɛɛ woya Wurubuarɛ e ya, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ siaman na ɔ balaŋ pou delena.

eng: He also saw a poor widow throw in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: te ɔ naa lialo diyemte ŋolo mɔ moo kɔbasebia ala kaa do kɔba adakaa keŋ man.

eng: Some of the legal experts responded, Teacher, you have answered well.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ baale yakowɔ nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, n tiranɔɔ nideli.

eng: He said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than them all.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi lialo diyemte kei ta do kɔba kela balaŋ akaŋ baŋ pou.

eng: No one dared to ask him anything else.
local: Mena dɔɔ ŋolo te tale gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ bɔɔse e nombii bela ya.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had to live on.
local: Nawolo nyi baŋ pou bɔɔ do kɔba nɛ, ba lese kyomii bo lee ŋan be dana nɛ man. Mɔna lialo kei na ɔ diyem koraŋ na, ɔ moo kɔba keŋ pou ɔ dana nɛ kaa do.

eng: Jesus said to them, Why do they say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Sena te baa tale yako nyi nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ yɛɛ gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii e?.

eng: Some people were talking about the temple, how it was decorated with beautiful stones and ornaments dedicated to God. Jesus said.
local: Na Yesu kaseela baŋ man baale ne kolosi Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ kyeo na mena keŋ bɔɔ mo boya kpaakpaa nyiŋan ma ke, na abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan balaŋ ba mo kaa fa Wurubuarɛ te bɔɔ mo desina deni kenaŋ nɛ wose man nombia. Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa.

eng: David himself says in the scroll of Psalms, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side.
local: Nawolo nyi gyoo Defidi gbagba yakowɔ nɔɔneŋ tɔne keŋ man nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ŋon yako me Gbeŋgyoo nyi Tisi kyaa me dunoluŋ dɔɔ.

eng: As for the things you are admiring, the time is coming when not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: Abɔɔ ŋan pou ɛ ne naa kɛbo nɛ, debaŋ daale gyae kekɔŋ keŋ baa tekerii ŋe popou lɔ keŋ nyi ɛ be gyae ɛ kena boe na boe gyakaa ke dɔɔ dɔɔ ya.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.
local: kelii debaŋ keŋ maa mo n kɔlala baŋ do n tɛɛ. .

eng: They asked him, Teacher, when will these things happen? What sign will show that these things are about to happen.
local: Keŋte ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, debaŋ woŋti te mena nombia kɛŋa gyae kekɔŋ? Woŋ ne gyae la ke yɛɛ na kawola nyi debaŋ keŋ nombia kɛŋa gyae ke kɔŋ nɛ te lii.

eng: Since David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: Gyoo Defidi baake nyeelɛɛre ŋonaŋ nyi ɔ Gbeŋgyoo. Akpaa mena na, sena te nyeelɛɛre ŋonaŋ waa bese gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii.

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that you arent deceived. Many will come in my name, saying, Im the one! and Its time! Dont follow them.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose nideli nyi ŋolo na beo ŋon ya. Nawolo nyi balaŋ burum baa kɔŋ ma yele man nyi, Baŋ yɛna maŋ nyeelɛɛre ŋon, na baa yako ŋon nyi Debaŋ keŋ te lii. Akpaa ɛ nyii mena na ɛ na sila wɔ ya.

eng: In the presence of all the people, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: Balaŋ baŋ pou kee tei Yesu nɛ ɔ yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa.

eng: When you hear of wars and rebellions, dont be alarmed. These things must happen first, but the end wont happen immediately.
local: Te akpaa ɛ nyii yoo na yoo nombia na ɛ na yeli gyakoloŋ kekyaŋ ŋon ya, nawolo nyi kaboena nyi mena nombia kɛŋa pou dɛɛkɔŋ. Mɔna kɛŋa bɛɛ wolo nyi debaŋ ɔtomante keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ debokenaŋ man ya.

eng: Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They love being greeted with honor in the markets. They long for the places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose nideli na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ. Bɛɛ gyae kegbase dɔɔloŋ nyiŋan keta do kila, te bɛɛ gyae balaŋ ke buŋii do wɔ nɔɔ balaŋ dɛɛgyaŋee man, te bɛɛ gyae akyaese kpaakpaa Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ na be dɛɛgyaŋee di gyoŋ man.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Tɛɛlese gyae ka koro yokii na dɔŋa, te gyooneŋ mɔ baa yokii na dɔŋa.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Bɛɛ beo liala lɛo ba abɔɔ ŋan be dana nɛ. Bɛɛ fane kyare na baa mo beo balaŋ nyi be yɛɛ balaŋ kpaakpaawɔ. Wurubuarɛ waa wɔŋ mena balaŋ kɛwɔ dinɔɔ deŋela nideli kela walaŋ kamasɛ.

eng: There will be great earthquakes and wide-scale food shortages and epidemics. There will also be terrifying sights and great signs in the sky.
