local: Tɛɛle gyae ke wosi, tanam dinaa gyae ke yala tɛɛlese dɔɔ te kaweese mɔ gyae ke gbiki tɛɛlese dɔɔ. Gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana gyae ka lee adido kɔŋ.

eng: But before all this occurs, they will take you into custody and harass you because of your faith. They will hand you over to synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors because of my name.
local: Mɔna pɛte kɛŋa pou kekɔŋ nɛ, lee maŋ dɔɔ baa kyaŋ ŋon na baa naase ŋon diyem. Baa mo ŋon fa Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man kegyiise na gyooneŋ na gyooneŋ kegyiise na baa di ɛ nombia na baa do ŋon denɛɛ man.

eng: This will provide you with an opportunity to testify.
local: Kɛŋa gyae ke yela ɛ kenyiŋ gbɛɛ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa wɔ.

eng: Make up your minds not to prepare your defense in advance.
local: Mɔna ɛ yela ke doo ya agyueŋ man nyiaa, te yɛɛ keŋ ɛ ka yeyɛɛ na nombii keŋ ɛ ka mo kolosi lese ɛ nɔɔse ya.

eng: Ill give you words and wisdom that none of your opponents will be able to counter or contradict.
local: nawolo nyi maŋ ne gyae la maa wola ŋon nombia ŋan ɛ ke yako. Na maa fa ŋon nyansa keŋ ɛ kɔlala be gyae baa tale seŋ ɛ siaman nyi ɛmɛɛ na wɔ kedi nombia ya.

eng: You will be betrayed by your parents, brothers and sisters, relatives, and friends. They will execute some of you.
local: Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ kyaɔ na ɛ naɔ na ɛ naabia na ɛ loŋtona na ɛ gyoona ne gyae la baa lese ŋon fa, na baa koe ɛ man baale.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name.
local: Lee maŋ dɔɔ balaŋ pou baa kɔla ŋon.

eng: Still, not a hair on your heads will be lost.
local: Mɔna ɛ nyeenyɔɔse dokoloŋ koraŋ gba be gyae ka yo ya.

eng: By holding fast, you will gain your lives.
local: Ɛ seŋ keŋkeŋ ɛ kelɛɛdi man kelii ɔto na, ɛ ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.

eng: When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then you will know that its destruction is close at hand.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyi, Debaŋ keŋ ɛ naa yoonɔɔtena de kaa kilisi Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ nɛ, botɔɔ te ɛ ka gyeŋ nyi ka kewɔlɛɛ ta benaa.

eng: At that time, those in Judea must flee to the mountains, those in the city must escape, and those in the countryside must not enter the city.
local: Akpaa de kɔŋ mena na balaŋ baŋ be kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ, baa yeu gyu bulase dɔɔ te baŋ mɔ be kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ baa lee ke man, te baŋ mɔ be kyaa ka yenaase nɛ be na bese kɔŋ donɔɔ keŋ man ya.

eng: These are the days of punishment, when everything written will find its fulfillment.
local: Nawolo nyi kei yɛna deŋele kewɔŋ keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan be yako nɛ.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant or for women who are nursing their children. There will be great agony on the earth and angry judgment on this people.
local: Weeya ŋenaŋ man nɛ, fulɛɛtena na talabose baa laako. Nawolo nyi kayeyɛɛ dinaa gyae ke kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, na Wurubuarɛ deŋele kewɔŋ kekɔŋ balaŋ kɛwɔ dɔɔ.

eng: They will fall by the edge of the sword and be taken away as captives among all nations. Jerusalem will be plundered by Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are concluded.
local: Baa mo sila ko balaŋ baŋ baale, na baa kyaŋ baale ketɔ deni tɛɛlese wɔlɛɛ dɔɔ. Balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ baa di Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ dɔɔ kelii debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba see nɛ.

eng: There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars. On the earth, there will be dismay among nations in their confusion over the roaring of the sea and surging waves.
local: Keŋte Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kena gyakoloŋ nombia weese na gyaalaŋ na kyolobiise man, te gyakoloŋ gyae ka gyoo tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou te bɔɔ gyae baa gyeŋ kpene keŋ baa yɛɛ ya, lee mena keŋ ɛpo ne muusu te kɛɛ gyɔɔ nedoŋ nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: The planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken, causing people to faint from fear and foreboding of what is coming upon the world.
local: Debaŋ keŋ bɛɛ wɔlɛɛ na nombia ŋan gyae ke yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, gyakoloŋ gyae ke yela balaŋ woya ke yekii lee be man, nawolo nyi abɔɔ ŋan ŋe kyaa adido te ŋe dana doŋ nɛ pou gyae ke wosi.

eng: Then they will see the Human Onecoming on a cloud with power and great splendor.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ nɛ, ɛ kena maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon ta mo doŋ na gyoorobiiri lee ŋɔma man adido mɛɛ kɔŋ.

eng: Now when these things begin to happen, stand up straight and raise your heads, because your redemption is near.
local: Akpaa ɛ naa abɔɔ kɛŋa ne kɔŋ na ɛ koro seŋ na ɛ ke kpaŋ ɛ gbegyase, nawolo nyi ɛ nyeekelɛɛ ta benaa.

eng: Jesus told them a parable: Look at the fig tree and all the trees.
local: Keŋte Yesu gyɔɔ dudu fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kɛɛ fiigi daŋ keŋ na daase akaŋ ŋan pou.

eng: When they sprout leaves, you can see for yourselves and know that summer is near.
local: Akpaa ɛ naa ŋgba ŋe tombia ne pɔrɔkee na, ɛ gyeŋ nyi dekpao ta benaa.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that Gods kingdom is near.
local: Mena mɔ te akpaa ɛ naa mena abɔɔ kɛŋa ne kɔŋ na, ɛ gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ ta benaa.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until everything has happened.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, mena duuluŋ kei be gyae ke munu pɛte abɔɔ kɛŋa pou ke kɔŋ ya.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Adido na tɛɛle kei pou gyae ke gyu ɔto, mɔna me nɔɔwoya ŋan dɔɔ ŋan gyae ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa.

eng: Take care that your hearts arent dulled by drinking parties, drunkenness, and the anxieties of day-to-day life. Dont let that day fall upon you unexpectedly.
local: Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose dɔɔ nideli! Ɛ na yeli tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ kegyae ka bere ɛ sia ya. Ɛ na mo soloŋ kenyɔɔ do ɛ wose ya. Ɛ na gyueŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nombia beiŋ, na wee keŋ be nyiŋ kɔŋ kaa futaa ŋon ya.

eng: like a trap. It will come upon everyone who lives on the face of the whole earth.
local: Nawolo nyi mena wee kenaŋ gyae ke kɔŋ tɛɛle kei balaŋ pou dɔɔ.

eng: Stay alert at all times, praying that you are strong enough to escape everything that is about to happen and to stand before the Human One.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose dɔɔ nideli debaŋ kamasɛ na ɛ ke fane na ɛ kenyiŋ tale lese ɛ wose lee nombia ŋan pou gyae ke kɔŋ nɛ man, na ɛ ke nyiŋ tale seŋ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon siaman.

eng: Every day Jesus was teaching in the temple, but he spent each night on the Mount of Olives.
local: Wee kamasɛ na, Yesu na wolo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man te balinɔɔ de lii na ɔ ne gyu ke doo oliifi bula keŋ dɔɔ.

eng: All the people rose early in the morning to hear him in the temple area.
local: Tɛɛ de naa nebo nebo na, balaŋ baŋ pou ne gyu ke yilaa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man na baa nyii ɔ nombia ŋan.

eng: The whole assembly got up and led Jesus to Pilate and.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyaŋee botɔɔ nɛ pou korowɔ mo Yesu gyu Pilato ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ Romantena gyoo dinaa nawɔɔ man nɛ gyaŋ.

eng: began to accuse him. They said, We have found this man misleading our people, opposing the payment of taxes to Caesar, and claiming that he is the Christ, a king.
local: te be fiasɛɛ bɛɛ mo nombia gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ nyiaa, De naa balee kei dɔŋ de balaŋ siaman ɔ na wola wɔ nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ. Ɔ ne bɛɛ nyi be na tɔ lempoo fa gyoo dinaa Kaesa ya, te ɔ ne gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ yako nyi ŋon yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ, te ɔ yɛɛ gyoo mɔ e.

eng: Pilate asked him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Botɔɔ te Pilato bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna Gyudatena gyoo ŋon? Te Yesu tiranɔɔ nyi, Mena yaa n yakowɔ nɛ.

eng: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, I find no legal basis for action against this man.
local: Keŋte Pilato yako saese kegyiise baŋ na balaŋ dikpii keŋ nyiaa, Ma te naa nombiikum keŋ balee kei be yɛɛ keŋ kaboena nyi maa bu e fɔɔ ya.

eng: But they objected strenuously, saying, He agitates the people with his teaching throughout Judea—starting from Galilee all the way here.
local: Mɔna nɛnɛɛ gba te ba seŋɛɛ be nɔɔwoya dɔɔ keŋkeŋ nyi, Ɔ ta mo ɔ kawola keŋ wɔlɛɛ balaŋ agyueŋ Gyudia tɛɛle kei dɔɔ pou. Ɔ fiasɛɛ lee Galelia tɛɛle dɔɔ te nɛnɛɛ ɔ te kaa lii de gyaŋ kɛbo.

eng: Hearing this, Pilate asked if the man was a Galilean.
local: Pilato ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ ɔ bɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, Balee kei yɛɛ Galelia baale e.

eng: When he learned that Jesus was from Herods district, Pilate sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem at that time.
local: Pilato ka gyeŋ nyi Yesu lee gyoo Herode tɛɛle man nɛ, ɔ yela ba mo e gyu gyoo Herode gyaŋ. Debaŋ kenaŋ na gyoo Herode mɔ kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man botɔɔ.

eng: Herod was very glad to see Jesus, for he had heard about Jesus and had wanted to see him for quite some time. He was hoping to see Jesus perform some sign.
local: Gyoo Herode kena Yesu nɛ yɛɛ e gyoŋ nideli, nawolo nyi ɔ wulaa nyii Yesu wose man nombia te ɔ ne gyae e mɔ waa naa. Lee mena keŋ ɔ be nyii Yesu wose man nombia dɔɔ nɛ, wa agyueŋ yɛna nyi waa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia wola e.

eng: Herod questioned Jesus at length, but Jesus didnt respond to him.
local: Mena dɔɔ gyoo Herode bɔɔse Yesu nombia burum, mɔna ɔ te tira ŋe man dokoloŋ nɔɔ ya.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were there, fiercely accusing Jesus.
local: Botɔɔ na saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ seŋɛɛ keŋkeŋ bɛɛ kɛrɛɛ nombia dinaana gyakaa Yesu dɔɔ.

eng: Herod and his soldiers treated Jesus with contempt. Herod mocked him by dressing Jesus in elegant clothes and sent him back to Pilate.
local: Keŋte gyoo Herode na ɔ yoonɔɔtena baŋ kulu Yesu te ba do e desɛŋ. Ba doo e gyooro kegba te ɔ yela ba besena e gyina Pilato.

eng: Pilate and Herod became friends with each other that day. Before this, they had been enemies.
local: Lee wee kenaŋ te gyoo Herode na Pilato nsana besewɔ de, nafɔ na bɔɔ na gyoo ya.

eng: Then Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people.
local: Botɔɔ te Pilato baake saese kegyiise baŋ na donɔɔ kegyiise na balaŋ baŋ pou gyaŋee.

eng: He said to them, You brought this man before me as one who was misleading the people. I have questioned him in your presence and found nothing in this mans conduct that provides a legal basis for the charges you have brought against him.
local: te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ moona balee kei kɔŋ me gyaŋ te ɛ kpa ɔ na beo balaŋ baŋ. Maŋ gyoo ke man ɛ siaman kɛbo mɔna ma te naa nombiakumɛɛ ŋan ɔ be yɛɛ te ɛ ne kara ɔ nɔɔ ya.

eng: Neither did Herod, because Herod returned him to us. Hes done nothing that deserves death.
local: Gyoo Herode mɔ te naa nombiikum lee ɔ wose man ya, te ɔ yela ba besena e kɔna daa. N kɛɛ paa na balee kei te yɛɛ kolo kum keŋ kaboena nyi baa ko e ya.

eng: Therefore, Ill have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Mena dɔɔ maa yela baa tuku e na baa tina e yela.

eng: But with one voice they shouted, Away with this man! Release Barabbas to us. .
local: Botɔɔ te balaŋ dikpii keŋ pou fae yeesa nedoŋ nyiaa, Ko balee kei na n ke tina Barabase yela fa daa.

eng: Barabbas had been thrown into prison because of a riot that had occurred in the city, and for murder.
local: Keŋ dɔɔ bɔɔ kyaŋ Barabase tɔ deni yɛna nyi, ŋon tɛɛ la balaŋ siaman te ba korowɔ seŋ tia kegyiise te ɔ koo walaŋ nombia ŋenaŋ man.

eng: Pilate addressed them again because he wanted to release Jesus.
local: Keŋ Pilato ne gyae waa tina Yesu yela dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ besewɔ kolosi fa balaŋ baŋ.

eng: They kept shouting out, Crucify him! Crucify him.
local: Mɔna balaŋ baŋ gyakaa bɛɛ fae yeesa nyiaa, Yela baa gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ.

eng: For the third time, Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? Ive found no legal basis for the death penalty in his case. Therefore, I will have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Keŋte Pilato besewɔ kolosi fa wɔ betabotoorote nyiaa, Woŋ nombiikum yaa balee kei yɛɛwɔ? Maŋ te naa kpene keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ keŋ kaboena nyi baa ko e ya. Mena dɔɔ maa yela baa tuku e na maa tina e yela.

eng: But they were adamant, shouting their demand that Jesus be crucified. Their voices won out.
local: Mɔna balaŋ baŋ seŋɛɛ keŋkeŋ bɛɛ fae yeesa nedoŋ nyi baa gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ, te be yeesa ŋan kela Pilato.

eng: Pilate issued his decision to grant their request.
local: Mena dɔɔ Pilato yɛɛ wa agyueŋ nyi waa yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ kegyaebii fa wɔ.

eng: He released the one they asked for, who had been thrown into prison because of a riot and murder. But he handed Jesus over to their will.
local: Keŋte Pilato tinaa Barabase ŋon bɔɔ kyam tɔ deni nɛ yela fa wɔ. Barabase kei tɛɛ la balaŋ baale siaman te ba korowɔ seŋ tia kegyiise te ɔ koo walaŋ mɔ. Ŋon yɛna walaŋ ŋon balaŋ baŋ be yako nyi waa tina e yela fa wɔ nɛ. Te ɔ moo Yesu fa wɔ nyi baa gyu ke yɛɛ e ba kegyaebii.

eng: As they led Jesus away, they grabbed Simon, a man from Cyrene, who was coming in from the countryside. They put the cross on his back and made him carry it behind Jesus.
local: Be ka mo Yesu lee Pilato gyaŋ bee gyuu nɛ, be gyaŋee na balee ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Simɔn ŋon ɔ ba lee Kirene donɔɔ man nɛ, na ɔ ta lee akuraa ɔ ne kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man. Keŋte be kyaŋa e te ba ŋere e mo ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ seele e na ɔ silana Yesu wɔle wɔle.

eng: A huge crowd of people followed Jesus, including women, who were mourning and wailing for him.
local: Na balaŋ burum silana Yesu, mo kpu na ala baŋ bee wii te be tɔɔse ne kyoŋii wɔ fa e nɛ.

eng: Jesus turned to the women and said, Daughters of Jerusalem, dont cry for me. Rather, cry for yourselves and your children.
local: Keŋte Yesu besenawɔ kɛɛ wɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Gyerusalɛm ala, ɛ na wii fa maŋ ya. Mɔna ɛ wii fa ɛ wose na ɛ bia.

eng: The time will come when they will say, Happy are those who are unable to become pregnant, the wombs that never gave birth, and the breasts that never nursed a child.
local: Nawolo nyi debaŋ daale ne kɔŋ keŋ ɛ ke yako nyiaa, Ɔlamanbose, ɛmɛɛ baŋ bia te doo ɛ tɔɔman see ya te bia te ŋmase ya ade ya nɛ, ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam.

eng: Then they will say to the mountains, Fall on us, and to the hills, Cover us.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ te balaŋ baa yako bulase nyiaa, Ɛ kaa ŋere daa, na baa yako bulasebia mɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kaa wuu de dɔɔ.

eng: If they do these things when the tree is green, what will happen when it is dry.
local: Akpaa daŋ laŋte ne kpaa la nnɛ na, sena te daŋ dowolo gyae ke yɛɛ.

eng: They also led two other criminals to be executed with Jesus.
local: Keŋte ba moo nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra bala baale mɔ kaa kpu na Yesu na be kaa ko wɔ.

eng: When they arrived at the place called The Skull, they crucified him, along with the criminals, one on his right and the other on his left.
local: Be kelii botɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Nyeepookoo dɛɛ nɛ, be gyɔɔ Yesu sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ. Keŋte be gyɔɔ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra bala baŋ mɔ sakaa ɔpaŋdaase kpu na e. Ŋolo sakaa ɔ dunoluŋ dɔɔ te ŋolo mɔ sakaa ɔ demena dɔɔ.

eng: Jesus said, Father, forgive them, for they dont know what theyre doing. They drew lots as a way of dividing up his clothing.
local: Keŋte Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, Taata! Mo balaŋ kɛwɔ nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ, nawolo nyi bɔɔ gyeŋ kpene keŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ ya. Keŋte be lɔɔ boe mo kpala Yesu kegbase.

eng: The people were standing around watching, but the leaders sneered at him, saying, He saved others. Let him save himself if he really is the Christ sent from God, the chosen one.
local: Na balaŋ burum seŋɛɛ botɔɔ bɛɛ kɛɛ te Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ mɔ kulu e nyiaa, Ɔ ne lɛɛ balaŋ nyee, ɛ yela waa lɛɛ ɔ wose na dɛɛ kɛɛ. Ŋgba ŋon kpa ŋon yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: The soldiers also mocked him. They came up to him, offering him sour wine.
local: Keŋte yoonɔɔtena baŋ mɔ kulu e te be faa e soloŋ keŋ kɔɔ nyaŋe nɛ.

eng: and saying, If you really are the king of the Jews, save yourself.
local: te be yakowɔ nyiaa, Akpaa nyaŋ yɛna Gyudatena gyoo ŋon na, kenaŋ na lɛɛ n wose.

eng: Very early in the morning on the first day of the week, the women went to the tomb, bringing the fragrant spices they had prepared.
local: Ba kefɛɛfowee keŋ wɔle tɛɛ kena nebo nebo fuu nɛ, ala baŋ moo ba teeneŋ ofoŋ nyiŋan ŋan bɔɔ gyae see nɛ te be gyuuwɔ yebɔɔ keŋ tɛɛ.

eng: Above his head was a notice of the formal charge against him. It read This is the king of the Jews.
local: Te be ŋmarase nombia kɛŋa see ɔ nyee adido nyiaa, kei yɛna gyudatena gyoo ŋon.

eng: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb.
local: Be kelii botɔɔ nɛ be naawɔ ŋgba boe keŋ bɔɔ mo tɔ yebɔɔ keŋ nɔɔman nɛ te biliŋi laŋ yenaŋ.

eng: One of the criminals hanging next to Jesus insulted him: Arent you the Christ? Save yourself and us.
local: Botɔɔ te nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra bala baŋ be sakaa ɔpaŋdaase dɔɔ nɛ man ŋolo tee Yesu nyiaa, Na nyaŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon yee? Lɛɛ n wose na n ke lɛɛ daa mɔ.

eng: but when they went in, they didnt find the body of the Lord Jesus.
local: mena dɔɔ ba gyoowɔ yebɔɔ keŋ man mɔna ba te naa de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu woo ŋon ya.

eng: Responding, the other criminal spoke harshly to him, Dont you fear God, seeing that youve also been sentenced to die?.
local: Keŋte ɔ balate ŋon gyɔɔ ɔ nɔɔ dɔɔ nyiaa, N bee yee Wurubuarɛ ŋgba de popou doo deŋele kewɔŋ dokoloŋ man yee?.

eng: They didnt know what to make of this. Suddenly, two men were standing beside them in gleaming bright clothing.
local: Be ka seŋ botɔɔ bɛɛ gyueŋ nombia kɛŋa man nɛ, karatetee dokoloŋ te baala bala baŋ ba doona kegbase wolomɛɛ ŋan ŋɛɛ ŋalakee ŋgba akaralɛɛ nɛ kaa bɛɛwɔ lee be dɔɔ.

eng: We are rightly condemned, for we are receiving the appropriate sentence for what we did. But this man has done nothing wrong.
local: De nombiakumɛɛ ŋan dɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ, ŋa deŋele kewɔŋ yaa de nyiŋawɔ nɛ. Mɔna balee kei dɔɔ ɔ te yɛɛ nombiikum daale ya.

eng: The women were frightened and bowed their faces toward the ground, but the men said to them, Why do you look for the living among the dead?.
local: Gyakoloŋ kyaŋa ala baŋ te be gɔŋase ba nyeeya. Keŋte baala baŋ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne kɛo walaŋ ŋon ɔ delena nɛ woya dɛɛ?.

eng: Then he said, Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ yako Yesu nyi Yesu, n kɔŋ n gyoori keŋ man na, na wolee maŋ ya.

eng: He isnt here, but has been raised. Remember what he told you while he was still in Galilee.
local: Ɔ be kyaa kɛbo ya ɔ te fom. Ɛ te wolee nombia ŋan ɔ be yako ŋon debaŋ keŋ ɔ kyaa ɛ gyaŋ Galelia nɛ nyiaa.

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that today you will be with me in paradise.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako neŋ nyiaa, gyɛŋ nyaŋ ke kyaa me gyaŋ Wurubuarɛ man.

eng: that the Human One must be handed over to sinners, be crucified, and on the third day rise again.
local: Kaboena nyi baa lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon fa nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra, na baa gyɔ maŋ nyeelabia sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ na maa yeŋ. Mɔna weeya atoorote na Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu maŋ lee yeŋ man. .

eng: It was now about noon, and darkness covered the whole earth until about three oclock.
local: Weese ka seŋ dɛɛ nɛ, weese tinaa kegyɔ yela te ditiŋtɛɛ lɛɛ tɛɛle keŋ dɔɔ pou kelii debaŋ keŋ weese be gɛɛse nɛ.

eng: Then they remembered his words.
local: Botɔɔ te ala baŋ tɔɔse ɔ nɔɔwoya kɛŋa.

eng: while the sun stopped shining. Then the curtain in the sanctuary tore down the middle.
local: Botɔɔ te kanyaŋ keŋ kpaŋɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ bɛɛsewɔ kyɛɛkyɛɛ ala.

eng: When they returned from the tomb, they reported all these things to the eleven and all the others.
local: Be ka lee yebɔɔ keŋ tɛɛ nɛ, ba ke yako nombia kɛŋa pou fa ɔ kpilala kufu daale baŋ na ɔ kaseela akaŋ baŋ pou.

eng: Crying out in a loud voice, Jesus said, Father, into your hands I entrust my life . After he said this, he breathed for the last time.
local: Keŋte Yesu fae yeesa keŋkeŋ nyiaa, Taata, me ta mo ma woo do n nyiŋmaa man. Ɔ ka betaa yako mena pɛ te ɔ kara weesi.

eng: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them who told these things to the apostles.
local: Mɛɛre Magedala na Gyoana na Gyemisi ɔ naa Mɛɛre, na ala akaŋ baŋ be kpuɛ na wɔ nɛ gyuu la ke yako nombia kɛŋa fa kpilala baŋ.

eng: When the centurion saw what happened, he praised God, saying, Its really true: this man was righteous.
local: Yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon kena kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ, ɔ lese Wurubuarɛ yele yako nyiaa Ampaŋ, balee kei gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman.

eng: Their words struck the apostles as nonsense, and they didnt believe the women.
local: Mɔna kpilala baŋ gyueŋwɔ nyi ŋan ala baŋ be yako nɛ pou yɛɛ diyiŋ nombia ŋa, mena dɔɔ ba te lɛɛ wɔ di ya.

eng: All the crowds who had come together to see this event returned to their homes beating their chests after seeing what had happened.
local: Balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ gyaŋee botɔɔ bɛɛ kɛɛ nɛ naa kpene keŋ be kɔŋ, te ba besewɔ gyu donɔɔ man na ba nyiŋmaase gyakaa ba nyeeya dɔɔ waraŋase man.

eng: But Peter ran to the tomb. When he bent over to look inside, he saw only the linen cloth. Then he returned home, wondering what had happened.
local: Keŋte Pita ŋon korowɔ yeu gyu yebɔɔ keŋ tɛɛ. Ɔ ke buŋawɔ do ɔ sibii kɛɛ yebɔɔ keŋ man na, ɔ naa akaralɛɛ keŋ bɔɔ mo milii e nɛ kaageŋ doo la. Botɔɔ te ɔ besewɔ gyu dɛɛ na ɔ na gyueŋ nombia ŋan be kɔŋ nɛ man.

eng: And everyone who knew him, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance observing these things.
local: Mɔna baŋ pou ba gyeŋ Yesu na ala baŋ bɔɔ sila e lee Galelia donɔɔ man nɛ, seŋawɔ lemlem bɛɛ kɛɛ nombia kɛŋa.

eng: On that same day, two disciples were traveling to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.
local: Mena wee kenaŋ nɛ, Yesu kaseela bala baale leewɔ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man bee gyu donɔɔbii daale keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Emause nɛ man. Lee botɔɔ gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man gyae ke yɛɛ ŋgba gbɛɛkemaase nyetooro nɛ.

eng: Now there was a man named Joseph who was a member of the council. He was a good and righteous man.
local: Balee ŋolo kyaawɔ na bɛɛ baake e nyi Gyosɛfo. Ɔ lee Gyudatena donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Arimatia nɛ man. Na ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman te bee bu e mɔ nideli. Ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kɛɛ Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ siaman te ŋon mɔ kpuɛ Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ man, mɔna ɔ te lɛɛ kpene keŋ bɔɔ bake na kpene keŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ do ya.

eng: They were talking to each other about everything that had happened.
local: Na be dɔŋɛɛ bɛɛ yɔɔ nombia ŋan be kɔŋ nɛ afa.

eng: This man went to Pilate and asked for Jesus body.
local: Mena balee kei gyuuwɔ Pilato gyaŋ ka sola gbɛɛ nyi waa mo Yesu woo ŋon ke wuu.

eng: While they were discussing these things, Jesus himself arrived and joined them on their journey.
local: Ba ke ta bɛɛ yɔɔ afa kɛŋa nɛ, Yesu gbagba kaa bɛɛwɔ lee be dɔɔ.

eng: Taking it down, he wrapped it in a linen cloth and laid it in a tomb carved out of the rock, in which no one had ever been buried.
local: Keŋte ɔ ka lese woo ŋon lee ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ dɔɔ te ɔ moo akaralɛɛ milii e te ɔ moo e ke wuu yebɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ kare boe man te be te wuu ta walaŋ na walaŋ ke man see ya nɛ man.

eng: They were prevented from recognizing him.
local: Mɔna be ta gyem nyi Yesu ŋonaŋ ya.

eng: It was the Preparation Day for the Sabbath, and the Sabbath was quickly approaching.
local: Wee kenaŋ yɛɛ wee keŋ Gyudatena na mo desina ba wose fa ba kefɛɛfowee keŋ kedi.

eng: He said to them, What are you talking about as you walk along? They stopped, their faces downcast.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Woŋ afa yaa ɛ dɔŋɛɛ ɛ ne yɔɔ mena? Keŋte be dɔɔwɔ seŋ waraŋase man.

eng: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph. They saw the tomb and how Jesus body was laid in it.
local: Ala baŋ bɔɔ sila Yesu lee Galelia kɔŋ nɛ, sila Gyosɛfo gyu te ba ke naa yebɔɔ keŋ na mena keŋ ɔ be wuu Yesu.

eng: The one named Cleopas replied, Are you the only visitor to Jerusalem who is unaware of the things that have taken place there over the last few days.
local: Te be man ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Kilopase nɛ bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, N yɛɛ ŋɔɔle e Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man kɛbo, keŋ n ba gyeŋ kpene keŋ be kɔŋ weeya ala kɛŋa yee.

eng: then they went away and prepared fragrant spices and perfumed oils. They rested on the Sabbath, in keeping with the commandment.
local: Keŋte ba besewɔ gyu ba akpaŋalaŋse man be kaa gyae teeneŋ ofoŋofoŋ ŋan bɛɛ mo desina woo nɛ, na baa mo kaa buraa woo ŋon na ɔ be nyiŋ wɔlɛɛ bileŋ ya. Mɔna be fɛɛfowɔ Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee keŋ ŋgba mena keŋ Mosesi mmaraase ŋan na wolo nɛ.

eng: He said to them, What things? They said to him, The things about Jesus of Nazareth. Because of his powerful deeds and words, he was recognized by God and all the people as a prophet.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa Woŋ nombia kɔŋala? Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Yesu Nasarɛte baale ŋon wose man nombia. Balee kei yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e te ɔ kekolosi na ɔ nombia ŋan ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ, lesewɔ wola nyi Wurubuarɛ kpuɛ na e te balaŋ mɔ lɛɛ e di.

eng: But our chief priests and our leaders handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him.
local: Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na de donɔɔ kegyiise lese na e fa nyi baa ko, te be gyɔɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ.

eng: We had hoped he was the one who would redeem Israel. All these things happened three days ago.
local: Mɔna nafɔ ŋon te de loo gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ nyi waa kaa lɛɛ Iseraetena nyee. Gyɛŋ yɛna weeya atoorote ŋgba kɛŋa pou be kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: But theres more: Some women from our group have left us stunned. They went to the tomb early this morning.
local: Kɛŋa wɔle nɛ, ala baale baŋ be kpuɛ na daa nɛ kɔŋawɔ kaa yako daa gyakoloŋ nombia ŋaale. Be kpa be karatɛɛwɔ gyu yebɔɔ keŋ tɛɛ.

eng: and didnt find his body. They came to us saying that they had even seen a vision of angels who told them he is alive.
local: mɔna ba te naa Yesu woo ŋon ya te ba besewɔ kɔŋ kaa yako nyi, Wurubuarɛ kpilala bala baale kaa lese ba wose wola wɔ, te be yako wɔ nyi Yesu delena.

eng: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found things just as the women said. They didnt see him.
local: De man baale mɔ gyuu yebɔɔ keŋ tɛɛ na, ba ke naa kpene kamasɛ ŋgba mena keŋ ala baŋ be yako nɛ mɔna baŋ mɔ te naa Yesu ŋon ya.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, You foolish people! Your dull minds keep you from believing all that the prophets talked about.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, O ɛmɛɛ ayiŋtena, weera dɔɔ te ɛ yɛɛ nyoɛnyoɛ na nombia ŋan pou Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ be yako see nɛ ke lɛɛ di mena.

eng: Wasnt it necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and then enter into his glory.
local: Tekaboena nyi nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ, waa naa diyem kei pɛna waa gyoo ɔ gyoori keŋ man yee.

eng: Then he interpreted for them the things written about himself in all the scriptures, starting with Moses and going through all the Prophets.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu fiasɛɛ ɔ na wola wɔ kpene keŋ bɔɔ ŋmarase Mosesi mmaraase ŋan man, na ŋan Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra be yako see lee ɔ wose man nɛ man.

eng: When they came to Emmaus, he acted as if he was going on ahead.
local: Be ka benaa donɔɔbii keŋ man bee gyuu nɛ, Yesu yɛɛwɔ ŋgba ɔ ne gbaa waa laŋ nɛ.

eng: But they urged him, saying, Stay with us. Its nearly evening, and the day is almost over. So he went in to stay with them.
local: Mɔna be waase e nideli nyi, Tɛɛ te yuu dɔɔ waa kɔŋ kaa doo be gyaŋ. Mena dɔɔ ɔ gyuuwɔ kekyaa be gyaŋ.

eng: After he took his seat at the table with them, he took the bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.
local: Ŋon na wɔ ke kyaa baa di nɛ, ɔ moo bodobodo te ɔ fanewɔ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ te ɔ kɛrɛɛ ke kpala wɔ.

eng: Their eyes were opened and they recognized him, but he disappeared from their sight.
local: Botɔɔ te be sia gyuusuwɔ te ba gyeŋawɔ nyi Yesu ŋonaŋ, keŋte ɔ ŋaalaawɔ be siaman.

eng: They said to each other, Werent our hearts on fire when he spoke to us along the road and when he explained the scriptures for us.
local: Keŋte be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Debaŋ keŋ ɔ ne kolosi na daa gbɛɛnaa man te ɔ ba lese Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man wola daa nɛ, de wɔe te lɔ daa yee.

eng: They got up right then and returned to Jerusalem. They found the eleven and their companions gathered together.
local: Debokenaŋ man te ba korowɔ bileŋ bese gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man. Botɔɔ te ba ke naa kpilala kufu daale baŋ na balaŋ akaŋ baŋ te yilaa botɔɔ.

eng: They were saying to each other, The Lord really has risen! He appeared to Simon.
local: na bɛɛ yako nyiaa, Ampaŋ de Gbeŋgyoo ŋon te fom! Ɔ ta lese ɔ wose wola Simɔn.

eng: Then the two disciples described what had happened along the road and how Jesus was made known to them as he broke the bread.
local: Botɔɔ te kaseela bala baŋ mɔ yako wɔ kpene keŋ be kɔŋ Emause gbɛɛnaa man nɛ, na mena keŋ Yesu be kɛrɛɛ bodobodo kpala wɔ te bɔɔ gyem nyi ŋon ŋonaŋ nɛ.

eng: While they were saying these things, Jesus himself stood among them and said, Peace be with you.
local: Ba ke gyakaa bee kolosi ɔ wose nombia kɛŋa nɛ, Yesu gbagba kaa seŋawɔ be gyaŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Wosefɛɛreŋ dɛɛ kyaa ɛ man.

eng: They were terrified and afraid. They thought they were seeing a ghost.
local: Be ŋeŋawɔ te gyakoloŋ kyaŋa wɔ, na baŋ gyeŋ nyi woo yaa be naawɔ.

eng: He said to them, Why are you startled? Why are doubts arising in your hearts?.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ ne yee yaa woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ ne lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ ɛ nyeeya man?.

eng: Look at my hands and my feet. Its really me! Touch me and see, for a ghost doesnt have flesh and bones like you see I have.
local: Ɛ kɛɛ me nyiŋmaase na me nawɔɔ! Maŋ gbagba ŋonaŋ. Ɛ tina maŋ kɛɛ! Nawolo nyi woo be dana wosenaane na ŋɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ ne naa maŋ dana nɛ ya.

eng: As he said this, he showed them his hands and feet.
local: Ɔ kekolosi taŋ nɛ, ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaase na ɔ nawɔɔ wola wɔ.

eng: Because they were wondering and questioning in the midst of their happiness, he said to them, Do you have anything to eat.
local: Nombia ŋan doo be nɔɔ te yɛɛ wɔ gyoŋ gbaa keŋ nyi ba te lɛɛ di ya. Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ dana weenɛɛ ŋaale kɛbo.

eng: They gave him a piece of baked fish.
local: Keŋte ba moo kpebii keŋ bɔɔ ŋasee ke nɛ duguŋ fa e.

eng: Taking it, he ate it in front of them.
local: Te ɔ lɛɛ ke tao be siaman botɔɔ.

eng: Jesus said to them, These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you—that everything written about me in the Law from Moses, the Prophets, and the Psalms must be fulfilled.
local: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Debaŋ keŋ me kyaa ɛ gyaŋ nɛ me yako ŋon nyiaa, nombii kamasɛ keŋ bɔɔ ŋmarase Mosesi mmaraase ŋan man, na ŋan Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra be ŋmarase see na ŋan bɔɔ ŋmarase see nɔɔneŋ tɔne man lee ma wose man nɛ, kaboena nyi ŋe dɛɛ kɔŋ mena.

eng: Then he opened their minds to understand the scriptures.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ toro ba agyueŋ na baa nyiŋ nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man.

eng: He said to them, This is what is written: the Christ will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Be yakowɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man nyiaa, nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ, waa naa diyem na waa yeŋ mɔna ka weeya atoorote keŋ na waa foŋ lee yeŋ man.

eng: and a change of heart and life for the forgiveness of sins must be preached in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
local: te baa kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ɔ yele man fa balaŋ pou tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, na baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na baa kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ, na Wurubuarɛ ka mo be nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ. Baa fiasɛɛ lee Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man, pɛna baa gyu donɔɔse akaŋ ŋan man.

eng: You are witnesses of these things.
local: Ɛmɛɛ yɛna balaŋ baŋ ɛ ke di mena nombia kɛŋa wose man adansɛɛ.

eng: Look, Im sending to you what my Father promised, but you are to stay in the city until you have been furnished with heavenly power.
local: Maa mo me kya Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ɔ be yako see nyi waa fa ŋon nɛ kaa do ɛ man. Mɔna ɛ kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man kɛbo kelii debaŋ keŋ waa mo mena doŋ kei lee adido kaa do ɛ man.

eng: He led them out as far as Bethany, where he lifted his hands and blessed them.
local: Keŋte Yesu moo ɔ kaseela baŋ lee donɔɔ kenaŋ man gyu Bɛtani donɔɔ nɔɔman, botɔɔ te ɔ gyinaa ɔ nyiŋmaase adido te ɔ hiraa ɔ kaseela baŋ.

eng: As he blessed them, he left them and was taken up to heaven.
local: Ɔ kee hiraa wɔ nɛ, ɔ tinaa wɔ yela te Wurubuarɛ moo e laŋ adido.

eng: They worshipped him and returned to Jerusalem overwhelmed with joy.
local: Keŋte ba som e te ba moo kenyaŋee dinaa bese kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man.

eng: And they were continuously in the temple praising God.
local: te debaŋ kamasɛ na bee gyu ke kyaa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

eng: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee. Jesus mother was there, and.
local: Ka weeya atoorote keŋ nɛ, ba kpeo alo ŋolo Kaanan donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ man. Na Yesu ɔ naa mɔ kyaa botɔɔ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were also invited to the celebration.
local: Be baake Yesu na ɔ kaseela mɔ kɔŋ alowɔle keŋ tɛɛ botɔɔ.

eng: When the wine ran out, Jesus mother said to him, They dont have any wine.
local: Balaŋ baŋ soloŋ ke taŋ nɛ, Yesu ɔ naa yako e nyi Balaŋ baŋ soloŋ te taŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Woman, what does that have to do with me? My time hasnt come yet.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Nna kei te yɛɛ daa na neŋ de nombia ya. Debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba see maŋ nɛ te lii ta ya.

eng: His mother told the servants, Do whatever he tells you.
local: Keŋte Yesu ɔ naa yako dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ tewulɛɛ baŋ nyiaa, Kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɔ yako ŋon nyi yaa yɛɛ na, ɛ yɛɛ ke.

eng: Nearby were six stone water jars used for the Jewish cleansing ritual, each able to hold about twenty or thirty gallons.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na, osuŋse dinaana looro ŋaale baŋɛɛ botɔɔ. Gyudatena ɔsom man na, osuŋse kɛŋa man te bee kyekii loŋ do mo faafo ba wose. Dokoloŋ gyae ke lɛɛ loŋ ŋgba garawaase anoŋ yaa looro nɛ.

eng: Jesus said to the servants, Fill the jars with water, and they filled them to the brim.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako tewulɛɛ baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ kyekii loŋ do osuŋse ŋan man wulu. Keŋte tewulɛɛ baŋ kyekii loŋ wulu ŋa belebelebele.

eng: Then he told them, Now draw some from them and take it to the headwaiter, and they did.
local: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Nɛnɛɛ ɛ kyee loŋ keŋ ŋaale gyina kegyia ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ alowɔle keŋ siaman nɛ. Te be kyeewɔ gyina e.

eng: The headwaiter tasted the water that had become wine. He didnt know where it came from, though the servants who had drawn the water knew. The headwaiter called the groom.
local: Kegyia ŋon daa loŋ keŋ ba bese soloŋ nɛ kɛɛ. Ɔ naawɔ nyi soloŋ keŋ dei, mɔna ɔ ba gyeŋ ke dalee ya, mɔna tewulɛɛ baŋ bɔɔ kyee loŋ keŋ kɔna e nɛ baŋ gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ kɔɔ lee. Keŋte kegyia ŋon baake alowɔle baale ŋon lee yenaŋ.

eng: and said, Everyone serves the good wine first. They bring out the second-rate wine only when the guests are drinking freely. You kept the good wine until now.
local: te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Balaŋ degyaŋee na, bɛɛ taŋgbɛɛ bo mo soloŋ kpaakpaate fa wɔ, te akpaa be dɛɛse na te bɛɛ mo soloŋ keŋ kɔɔ dei ya nɛ kɔŋ. Mɔna nyaŋ moo soloŋ kpaakpaate keŋ bo weese kaa lii debaŋ kei pɛte n lese ke debɔɔ.

eng: This was the first miraculous sign that Jesus did in Cana of Galilee. He revealed his glory, and his disciples believed in him.
local: Kei yɛna gyakoloŋ nombia gyaŋgbate keŋ Yesu be yɛɛ Kaanan donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa Galelia tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ man. Ɔ moo ke lese ɔ gyoorobiiri wola, te ɔ kaseela baŋ mɔ lɛɛ e di.

eng: After this, Jesus and his mother, his brothers, and his disciples went down to Capernaum and stayed there for a few days.
local: Kei wɔle nɛ, ŋon na ɔ naa na ɔ naabia na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyuu donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke Kapanum nɛ man ke di weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale botɔɔ.

eng: It was nearly time for the Jewish Passover, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Debaŋ keŋ Gyudatena nyeekelɛɛwee keŋ na benaa nɛ, Yesu gyuu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man.

eng: He found in the temple those who were selling cattle, sheep, and doves, as well as those involved in exchanging currency sitting there.
local: Yesu ke gyu ka gyoo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ dekpaŋalaŋ man nɛ, ɔ naawɔ nyi baale ne yɔɔ nunkyuese na namense na awuluŋna, na balaŋ baale mɔ kyakɛɛ bɛɛ yɛlɛɛ kɔba.

eng: He made a whip from ropes and chased them all out of the temple, including the cattle and the sheep. He scattered the coins and overturned the tables of those who exchanged currency.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔ moo ŋmeeseŋ ŋmɛ yɛɛ dambila te ɔ moowɔ gegi balaŋ baŋ na be nunkyuese na be namense pou lee dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man. Ɔ yaasee be kɔbasebia ŋan te ɔ besena be tebuluse ŋan pou buŋii.

eng: He said to the dove sellers, Get these things out of here! Dont make my Fathers house a place of business.
local: Keŋte ɔ yako balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɔɔ awuluŋna nɛ nyiaa, Ɛ mo wɔ lee kɛbo! Ɛ na mo me kya dɛɛ besena ɛ kebu dedii ya.

eng: His disciples remembered that it is written, Passion for your house consumes me.
local: Nombia kɛŋa yela ɔ kaseela baŋ tɔɔse Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyi, O Wurubuarɛ, n deni dinaa keŋ man nombia ne hia maŋ nideli, te ŋe te di ma nyee mɔ.

eng: Then the Jewish leaders asked him, By what authority are you doing these things? What miraculous sign will you show us.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena kegyiise kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa, Woŋ gyakoloŋ nombia te nyaŋ ke tale yɛɛ na kawola daa nyi Wurubuarɛ faa na neŋ doŋ nyi nya yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa.

eng: Jesus answered, Destroy this temple and in three days Ill raise it up.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ yɛlɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa kei, weeya atoorote na maa ma ke bela.

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, It took forty-six years to build this temple, and you will raise it up in three days.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ yako Yesu nyiaa, Ba moo kulutooneŋ sɔŋola na looro bo ma Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa kei, te nyaŋ n ke tale mo weeya atooro ma ke.

eng: But the temple Jesus was talking about was his body.
local: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ Yesu ne kolosi ka wose man nombia nɛ, yɛna ŋon gbagba ɔ wose.

eng: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered what he had said, and they believed the scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
local: Mena dɔɔ debaŋ keŋ Yesu be fom lee yeŋ man nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ tɔɔsewɔ nyi ɔ yako mena nombia kɛŋa. Te be lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see, na Yesu nombia ŋan ɔ be yako nɛ di nideli.

eng: While Jesus was in Jerusalem for the Passover Festival, many believed in his name because they saw the miraculous signs that he did.
local: Yesu ke kyaa Gyerusalɛm Gyudatena nyeekelɛɛweeya kedi ŋan man nɛ, balaŋ burum naa gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ te be lɛɛ e di.

eng: But Jesus didnt trust himself to them because he knew all people.
local: Mɔna Yesu ta mo ɔ wose lɔ be dɔɔ ya, nawolo nyi ɔ gyeŋ walaŋ kamasɛ dinɔɔ.

eng: He didnt need anyone to tell him about human nature, for he knew what human nature was.
local: Te yɛɛ keŋ nyi ŋolo keyako deniwalaŋ nombii fa e ya, nawolo nyi ɔ gyeŋ walaŋ kamasɛ nyeeman nombia.

eng: The Festival of Unleavened Bread, which is called Passover, was approaching.
local: Gyudatena debaŋ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi nyeekelɛɛ debaŋ keŋ bee di bodobodo keŋ bodobodo tee be doo ke man ya nɛ kɛɛ benaa nɛ.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were looking for a way to kill Jesus, because they were afraid of the people.
local: Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala gyae gbɛɛ keŋ baa mo ko Yesu, mɔna bee yee balaŋ baŋ.

eng: Then Satan entered Judas, called Iscariot, who was one of the Twelve.
local: Keŋte ɔbɔnsam gyoo Yesu kaseela kufu bala baŋ man ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake nyi Gyudase Isekarɔte nɛ man.

eng: He went out and discussed with the chief priests and the officers of the temple guard how he could hand Jesus over to them.
local: Keŋte Gyudase gyuuwɔ te ŋon na Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ kotɔtɔɔtena kegyiise bakewɔ see mena keŋ waa lese Yesu fa wɔ.

eng: They were delighted and arranged payment for him.
local: Yɛɛ kegyiise baŋ gyoŋ te be sɛɛwɔ nyi baa fa e kɔba.

eng: He agreed and began looking for an opportunity to hand Jesus over to them—a time when the crowds would be absent.
local: Keŋte Gyudase mɔ sɛɛwɔ te ɔ gyae gbɛɛ keŋ waa mo lese Yesu fa wɔ keŋ balaŋ ba gyem ya.

eng: The Day of Unleavened Bread arrived, when the Passover had to be sacrificed.
local: Gyudatena wee dinaa keŋ bee di bodobodo keŋ bodobodo tee be doo ke man ya, te bɛɛ mo nyeekelɛɛ nambu ŋon mɔ deesi kelii nɛ.

eng: Jesus sent Peter and John with this task: Go and prepare for us to eat the Passover meal.
local: Yesu kpila Pita na Gyɔn nyiaa, Ɛ gyu ke desina abɔɔ ŋan dɛɛ mo di nyeekelɛɛ weenɛɛ ŋan nɛ see.

eng: They said to him, Where do you want us to prepare it.
local: Botɔɔ te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, Aleŋ te n ne gyae de kaa desina see.

eng: Jesus replied, When you go into the city, a man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him to the house he enters.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ betaa ɛ na gyoo donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ, ɛ kena balee ŋolo seelena loŋ na ogele. Ɛ sila e gyu dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man waa gyoo nɛ.

eng: Say to the owner of the house, The teacher says to you,Where is the guestroom where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: na ɛ ke yako dekpaŋalaŋte ŋon nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo ŋon kpa dɛɛ bɔɔse neŋ nyi aleŋ te ŋɔɔla deni keŋ daa na me kaseela dɛɛ kaa di nyeekelɛɛwee weenɛɛ ŋan nɛ kyaa.

eng: He will show you a large upstairs room, already furnished. Make preparations there.
local: Balee ŋonaŋ waa wola ŋon abansoro deni dinaa daale keŋ ke kyaa adido te bɔɔ desina ke man nideli nɛ. Ɛ desina botɔɔ na de kaa di weenɛɛ ŋan.

eng: They went and found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Yesu kaseela baŋ ke gyu keŋ nɛ, be naa kpene kamasɛ doo pɛpɛɛpɛ ŋgba mena keŋ Yesu be yako wɔ nɛ, te be desina nyeekelɛɛ weenɛɛ ŋan see botɔɔ.

eng: When the time came, Jesus took his place at the table, and the apostles joined him.
local: Debaŋ keŋ kelii nɛ, Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ kyaawɔ bom baa di.

eng: He said to them, I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Me gyaewɔ nideli me konɔɔ man nyi daa na ŋon ke di mena nyeekelɛɛ weenɛɛ kɛŋa pɛna me kaa naa diyem!.

eng: I tell you, I wont eat it until it is fulfilled in Gods kingdom.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, mɔɔ gyae maa tekaa di weenɛɛ kɛŋa bela ya kelii wee keŋ kpene keŋ mena kedi kei seŋɛɛ fa nɛ, gyae ke kɔŋ mena Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.

eng: After taking a cup and giving thanks, he said, Take this and share it among yourselves.
local: Keŋte Yesu moo leenɔɔ keŋ te ɔ fanewɔ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Ɛ lɛɛ kei na ɛ ke nyɔɔ fa dɔŋa.

eng: I tell you that from now on I wont drink from the fruit of the vine until Gods kingdom has come.
local: Nawolo nyi mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyiaa, mɔɔ gyae maa nyɔɔ soloŋ kei bela ya kelii wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ.

eng: After taking the bread and giving thanks, he broke it and gave it to them, saying, This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.
local: Keŋte ɔ moo bodobodo te ɔ fanewɔ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ te ɔ kara ke mo fa wɔ te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Kei yɛna ma wosenaane keŋ mɔɔ mo deesi fa ŋon. Ɛ kyaa yɛɛ kei na ɛ ka mo tɔɔse maŋ.

eng: In the same way, he took the cup after the meal and said, This cup is the new covenant by my blood, which is poured out for you.
local: Mena mɔ te be ke di taŋ nɛ, ɔ moo leenɔɔ te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Kei yɛna me fatabo keŋ Wurubuarɛ be kyɛɛkee leki mo tɔ nɔɔkebakesee wɔle keŋ nɔɔ fa ŋon.

eng: But look! My betrayer is with me; his hand is on this table.
local: Mɔna mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, walaŋ ŋon waa lese maŋ fa nɛ kpuɛ na maŋ weenɛɛ kɛŋa dedii man kɛbo.

eng: The Human Onegoes just as it has been determined. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays him.
local: Maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa yeŋ ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yako see nɛ, mɔna walaŋ ŋon waa lese maŋ fa nɛ waa laako.

eng: They began to argue among themselves about which of them it could possibly be who would do this.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ gyoo dɔŋa ke bɔɔse nyiaa, De man walaŋ woŋti kitii ne gyae la waa yɛɛ mena nombii kei.

eng: An argument broke out among the disciples over which one of them should be regarded as the greatest.
local: Keŋte aŋmaareŋ gyoo Yesu kaseela baŋ man nyi be man woŋti ne gyae la waa yɛɛ be kegyia?.

eng: But Jesus said to them, The kings of the Gentiles rule over their subjects, and those in authority over them are called friends of the people.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyooneŋ ne di doŋ be balaŋ dɔɔ, mɔna balaŋ ne baake mena gyooneŋ kɛwɔ nyi be yɛɛ balaŋ kpaakpaawɔ.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Instead, the greatest among you must become like a person of lower status and the leader like a servant.
local: Mɔna tekaboena nyi ɛmɛɛ ke yɛɛ ŋgba mena balaŋ kɛwɔ nɛ ya. Walaŋ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ kegyia e ɛ man nɛ waa yɛɛ ɔ wose ŋgba ɛ man kemaasabii nɛ, te ŋon ɔ yɛɛ ɛ siamante e nɛ waa som ŋon.

eng: So which one is greater, the one who is seated at the table or the one who serves at the table? Isnt it the one who is seated at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
local: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ kyaa ɔ ne dii na ŋon ɔ na som e nɛ, be man woŋti yɛna kegyia? Na walaŋ ŋon ɔ kyaa ɔ ne dii nɛ yee? Mɔna maŋ kyaa ɛ man ŋgba walaŋ ŋon ɔ na som nɛ.

eng: You are the ones who have continued with me in my trials.
local: Ɛmɛɛ yɛna baŋ ɛ ba sem me wɔle lee me kayeyɛɛ man pou.

eng: And I confer royal power on you just as my Father granted royal power to me.
local: mena dɔɔ mɛɛ see ŋon gyoori ŋgba mena keŋ me kya ba see maŋ gyoo nɛ.

eng: Thus you will eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and you will sit on thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Na daa na ŋon kedi na dɛɛ nyɔɔ me gyoori keŋ man, na ɛ ke kyaa me gyooro gbelɛɛ ŋan dɔɔ ɛ ke di Iseraetena akpaŋalaŋ kufu ala ŋan balaŋ nombia.

eng: Simon, Simon, look! Satan has asserted the right to sift you all like wheat.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako Simɔn Pita nyi Simɔn, Simɔn nyii! Ɔbɔnsam ta sola gbɛɛ lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ waa kɛɛse ɛ popou kɛɛ na waa kpase ɛ man ŋgba mena keŋ bee feefu amɔla lese bia nɛ.

eng: However, I have prayed for you that your faith wont fail. When you have returned, strengthen your brothers and sisters.
local: Mɔna ma te fane fa nyaŋ Simɔn na n kelɛɛdi be yɔkɔse ya. Te akpaa n kyɛɛkee lee n gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man bese kɔŋ me gyaŋ nɛ, do n tebia me kaseela akaŋ baŋ kakyeŋ.

eng: Peter responded, Lord, Im ready to go with you, both to prison and to death.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, nyi akpaa ke yɛɛ deni kedoo ke yaa yeŋ ke koraŋ na, ma te desina ma wose nyi maa sila neŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, I tell you, Peter, the rooster wont crow today before you have denied three times that you know me.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Pita, mɛɛ yako neŋ nyi pɛte kegyim kelɔ woya gyɛŋ na, nyaŋ ke bɛɛ betabotooro nyi n ba gyeŋ maŋ korakora ya.

eng: Jesus said to them, When I sent you out without a wallet, bag, or sandals, you didnt lack anything, did you? They said, Nothing.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Debaŋ keŋ mɔɔ kpila ŋon keŋ ɛ ta mo kɔba yaa akpo, yaa ɛ ta mo nkyokotase tela ya nɛ kolo hia ŋon? Keŋte be tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Koa, kolo te hia daa ya.

eng: Then he said to them, But now, whoever has a wallet must take it, and likewise a bag. And those who dont own a sword must sell their clothes and buy one.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ nyaŋ ŋon n dana kɔba yaa akpo na mo tela, te nyaŋ ŋon n be dana sila ya na yɔɔ n kegba na n ka mo ke lɛɛ daale.

eng: I tell you that this scripture must be fulfilled in relation to me: And he was counted among criminals .Indeed, whats written about me is nearing completion.
local: Nawolo nyi ba te ŋmarase ma wose man nombia see nyiaa, Ba te kala e do nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra man, te mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan bɔɔ ŋmarase see lee ma wose man nɛ, kaboena nyi ŋe dɛɛ kɔŋ mena. Ampaŋ kpene keŋ bɔɔ ŋmarase see lee ma wose man nɛ ta benaa ka ke kɔŋ.

eng: They said to him, Lord, look, here are two swords. He replied, Enough of that.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo kɛɛ silase ala yaa. Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyi, Tekaboe mena.

eng: Jesus left and made his way to the Mount of Olives, as was his custom, and the disciples followed him.
local: Keŋte Yesu leewɔ donɔɔ keŋ man gyu oliifi bula keŋ dɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ ne kyaa gyu nɛ, te ɔ kaseela baŋ sila e gyu.

eng: When he arrived, he said to them, Pray that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Be kelii botɔɔ nɛ Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ fane na ɛ be nyiŋ gyoo kekɛɛsekɛɛ man ya.

eng: He withdrew from them about a stones throw, knelt down, and prayed.
local: Keŋte ɔ wɔŋa ɔ wose lee yenaŋ gyu siaman kyomii te ɔ ke buŋawɔ fane nyiaa.

eng: He said, Father, if its your will, take this cup of suffering away from me. However, not my will but your will must be done.
local: Taata, akpaa ke yɛɛ n kegyaebii ke na, lese maŋ lee kaale kei man. Mɔna na yɛɛ maŋ kegyaebii ya, yɛɛ nyaŋ gbagba n kegyaebii.

eng: Then a heavenly angel appeared to him and strengthened him.
local: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ kpilale lee adido kɔŋ kaa do e kakyeŋ.

eng: He was in anguish and prayed even more earnestly. His sweat became like drops of blood falling on the ground.
local: Yesu kɛɛ yeyɛɛ keŋ nɛ, ɔ kyaŋa ɔ wose fane nideli te dufuŋ keŋ ba lee e nɛ kyɔseewɔ yala tɛɛle man ŋgba fatabo nɛ.

eng: When he got up from praying, he went to the disciples. He found them asleep, overcome by grief.
local: Ɔ ke fane taŋ keŋ nɛ te ɔ besewɔ gyu ɔ kaseela baŋ gyaŋ. Ɔ ke gyu keŋ na ɔ ke naawɔ ŋgba ɔ kaseela baŋ te dokii, nawolo nyi dekɔkɔrɔkɔɔ te kyaŋ wɔ.

eng: He said to them, Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray so that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Weera ɛ ne doo? Ɛ koro na ɛ ke fane na ɛ be nyiŋ gyoo kekɛɛsekɛɛ man ya.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, a crowd appeared, and the one called Judas, one of the Twelve, was leading them. He approached Jesus to kiss him.
local: Yesu ka seŋ ɔ ne kolosi nɛ balaŋ dikpii baale kaa bɛɛwɔ lee ɔ dɔɔ, na Gyudase ŋon ɔ yɛɛ Yesu kaseela kufu bala baŋ man ŋolo e nɛ dɔŋɛɛ la be siaman. Ɔ kena Yesu nɛ te ɔ kɔŋawɔ ɔ kaa pukaa e.

eng: Jesus said to him, Judas, would you betray the Human Onewith a kiss.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, Gyudase, kepukaa te nyaŋ ka mo lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon fa.

eng: When those around him recognized what was about to happen, they said, Lord, should we fight with our swords.
local: Yesu kaseela baŋ kena kpene keŋ ne gyae ke kɔŋ nɛ te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, dɛɛ mo de silase na dee yoo.

eng: One of them struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu kaseela baŋ man ŋolo moo ɔ yoosila kara saese kegyia tobaale ŋolo dunoluŋ deŋele lese ke lɔ.

eng: Jesus responded, Stop! No more of this! He touched the slaves ear and healed him.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ tina yela, ɛ na yɛɛ mena ya. Te ɔ tinaa balee ŋon deŋele keŋ te ka towaawɔ bela.

eng: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple guard, and the elders who had come to get him, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, as though I were a thief?.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse saese kegyiise na Wurubuarɛ dɛɛ kotɔtɔɔtena kegyiise na kegyiise baŋ bɔɔ kɔŋ botɔɔ be kaa kyaŋ e nɛ nyiaa, Weera te ɛ moo yoosilase na akpokpose ɛ ne kɔŋ me dɔɔ ŋgba me yɛɛ walaŋkum e nɛ?.

eng: Day after day I was with you in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But this is your time, when darkness rules.
local: Wee kamasɛ daa na ŋon ne kyaa la Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man, mɔna ɛ te kyaŋ maŋ ya. Nɛnɛɛ te gbɛɛ torowɔ fa ŋon nyi yaa kyaŋ maŋ, na ɛ ke yɛɛ ɔbɔnsam ditiŋtɛɛ gyoo ŋon kegyaebii.

eng: After they arrested Jesus, they led him away and brought him to the high priests house. Peter followed from a distance.
local: Botɔɔ te be kyaŋa Yesu mo e gyu Wurubuarɛ saese kegyia ŋon dekpaŋalaŋ man, na Pita mɔ silana wɔ wɔle wɔle.

eng: When they lit a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat among them.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ doo boalaŋ dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ nsana nsana te be yilaawɔ ke gyaŋ bee woli, te Pita mɔ gyuuwɔ ke kyaa be gyaŋ.

eng: Then a servant woman saw him sitting in the firelight. She stared at him and said, This man was with him too.
local: Alebia baŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ tom dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man nɛ ŋolo naa Pita kyaa boalaŋ keŋ gyaŋ, te ɔ gyene e nideli te ɔ yakowɔ nyi Na balee kei mɔ kpuɛ na Yesu.

eng: But Peter denied it, saying, Woman, I dont know him.
local: Keŋte Pita lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ nyiaa, Alebu, mɔɔ gyeŋ e ya.

eng: A little while later, someone else saw him and said, You are one of them too. But Peter said, Man, Im not.
local: Te kyare ya te ŋolo mɔ naa e te ɔ yako e bela nyiaa, Nyaŋ mɔ n yɛɛ be man ŋolo e! Keŋte Pita tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Baale, mɔɔ kpuɛ na wɔ ya.

eng: An hour or so later, someone else insisted, This man must have been with him, because he is a Galilean too.
local: Debaŋ dokoloŋ daale wɔle nɛ, ŋolo mɔ kaa yase ɔ sia do Pita ŋonaŋ nyiaa, Ke yɛɛ ampaŋ kaeŋ nyi balee kei kpuɛ na Yesu, nawolo nyi ɔ yɛɛ Galelia baale e.

eng: Peter responded, Man, I dont know what you are talking about! At that very moment, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Baale, mɔɔ gyeŋ nombia ŋan n ne yako nɛ man ya. Pita ke gyakaa ɔ ne kolosi nɛ te kegyim lɔɔ woya.

eng: The Lord turned and looked straight at Peter, and Peter remembered the Lords words: Before a rooster crows today, you will deny me three times.
local: Botɔɔ te de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ Pita, te Pita tɔɔse de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nombia ŋan ɔ be yako e nɛ nyiaa, Pɛ kegyim ke lɔ woya gyɛŋ na, nyaŋ ke bɛɛ betabotooro nyi n ba gyeŋ maŋ ya nɛ.

eng: And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Keŋte Pita leewɔ debɔɔ man gyu ke kyaŋ ɔ nyee wii nideli.

eng: The men who were holding Jesus in custody taunted him while they beat him.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɛɛ Yesu dɔɔ nɛ fiasɛɛ bee kulu e na bee tuku e.

eng: They blindfolded him and asked him repeatedly, Prophesy! Who hit you.
local: Ba moo kanyaŋ bake ɔ sia na bɛɛ takee e akɔtase na bɛɛ bɔɔse e nyi Yako daa, amɔte gyɔɔ na neŋ.

eng: Insulting him, they said many other horrible things against him.
local: Te ba tee e tuubia kpaŋ kpaŋ kpu.

eng: As morning came, the elders of the people, both chief priests and legal experts, came together, and Jesus was brought before their council.
local: Tɛɛ kena keŋ nɛ, donɔɔ kegyiise na Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ gyaŋeewɔ te ba moo Yesu kɔŋ kaa seŋ be siaman.

eng: They said, If you are the Christ, tell us! He answered, If I tell you, you wont believe.
local: Keŋte be yako Yesu nyiaa, Akpaa nyaŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ na yako daa dee nyii. Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Akpaa me yako ŋon na ɛ be gyae ɛ ke lɛɛ maŋ di ya.

eng: And if I ask you a question, you wont answer.
local: te akpaa me bɔɔse ŋon nombia mɔ nɛ ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tiranɔɔ fa maŋ ya.

eng: But from now on, the Human One will be seated on the right side of the power of God. .
local: Mɔna lee debaŋ kei ne gyu nɛ, maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa kyaa Wurubuarɛ Kpatakpere ŋon dunoluŋ dɔɔ.

eng: They all said, Are you Gods Son, then? He replied, You say that I am.
local: Keŋte be popou bɔɔse e nyiaa, Akpaa mena na nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon? Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ nyi, Mena yaa ɛ ne yako nɛ.

eng: Then they said, Why do we need further testimony? Weve heard it from his own lips.
local: Keŋte be yakowɔ nyiaa, Woŋ nombia yaa dɛɛ gyae dɛɛ bese nyii kpu bela. Daa gbagba te nyii lee ŋon gbagba nɔɔman.

eng: In the beginning was the Word and the Word was with God and the Word was God.
local: Lee kewalaŋ keŋ pɛna Wurubuarɛ ke yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ na, ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Wurubuarɛ nombii ŋon nɛ wulaa ɔ kyaa. Na ŋon na Wurubuarɛ kyaa la, te ŋon yɛna Wurubuarɛ.

eng: The Word was with God in the beginning.
local: Ŋon na Wurubuarɛ kyaa la leenaŋ kewalaŋ kenaŋ man.

eng: Everything came into being through the Word, and without the Word nothing came into being. What came into being.
local: Ɔ dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ tɛɛwɔ yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ, te Wurubuarɛ ta lee ɔ wɔle yɛɛ kolo na kolo ya.

eng: through the Word was life, and the life was the light for all people.
local: Wurubuarɛ nombii kei ne faa na nyeedoŋ, te nyeedoŋ kenaŋ yɛna keŋmaŋee keŋ ne gyuusu balaŋ sia na baa naa Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛ.

eng: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness doesnt extinguish the light.
local: Keŋmaŋee kenaŋ ne ŋmaŋee ditiŋtɛɛ man, mɔna ditiŋtɛɛ keŋ bɛɛ tale di ke dɔɔ ya.

eng: A man named John was sent from God.
local: Debaŋ daale liiwɔ na Wurubuarɛ kpila ɔ kpilale ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Gyɔn.

eng: He came as a witness to testify concerning the light, so that through him everyone would believe in the light.
local: Ŋon kɔŋala ɔ kaa di adansɛɛ lee keŋmaŋee keŋ wose man fa balaŋ, na balaŋ pou kenyiŋ ta ɔ dɔɔ lɛɛ keŋmaŋee keŋ di.

eng: He himself wasnt the light, but his mission was to testify concerning the light.
local: Na ŋon Gyɔn gbagba yɛna keŋmaŋee keŋ ya, mɔna ɔ kɔŋae kaa di adansɛɛ lee keŋmaŋee keŋ wose man.

eng: The true light that shines on all people was coming into the world.
local: Ŋon bɛɛ baake e keŋmaŋee gbagba keŋ nɛ waa kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, na ɔ kaa ŋmaŋee fa walaŋ kamasɛ.

eng: The light was in the world, and the world came into being through the light, but the world didnt recognize the light.
local: Keŋmaŋee kenaŋ kaa kyaawɔ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ. Ɔ dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ tɛɛwɔ yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, mɔna tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ ta gyeŋ e ya.

eng: The light came to his own people, and his own people didnt welcome him.
local: Ɔ kɔŋawɔ ŋon gbagba ɔ balaŋ gyaŋ, mɔna ɔ balaŋ te lɛɛ e ya.

eng: But those who did welcome him, those who believed in his name, he authorized to become Gods children.
local: Mɔna balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ lɛɛ e te bɔɔ lɛɛ di nɛ, ɔ faa wɔ gbɛɛ te ba bese Wurubuarɛ bia.

eng: born not from blood nor from human desire or passion, but born from God.
local: Ba te yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bia ŋgba mena keŋ alo na baale na lola bia ba kegyae man nɛ ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ gbagba kegyaebii man te ɔ lese wɔ nyi baa yɛɛ ɔ bia.

eng: The Word became flesh and made his home among us. We have seen his glory, glory like that of a fathers only son, full of grace and truth.
local: Ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Wurubuarɛ nombii nɛ, kaa bese deniwalaŋ te ɔ kaa kyaawɔ de man weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale. De naa ɔ gyoorobiiri keŋ nawolo nyi ŋon waageŋ yɛna ɔ kya Wurubuarɛ bu dokoloŋ ŋon nɛ. Ɔ man te Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ na anokoare keŋ doo.

eng: John testified about him, crying out, This is the one of whom I said, He who comes after me is greater than me because he existed before me.
local: Gyɔn dii adansɛɛ lee ɔ wose man. Ɔ fae yeesa yako nyi, Walaŋ kei wose man nombia yaa me wulaa yako ŋon nyiaa, Walaŋ ŋon ɔ dɔŋ me wɔle ɔ ne kɔŋ nɛ ɔ kela maŋ, nawolo nyi ɔ wulaa ɔ kyaa pɛte ba lola maŋ. .

eng: From his fullness we have all received grace upon grace.
local: Lee ɔ bɛrɛɛ keŋ be wulu ɔ man nɛ dɔɔ, te ɔ ne hiraa daa agbaatee agbaatee.

eng: as the Law was given through Moses, so grace and truth came into being through Jesus Christ.
local: Mosesi dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ tɛɛwɔ mo mmaraase ŋan fa daa, mɔna Yesu Kristo dɔɔ te ɔ tɛɛwɔ wola daa ɔ bɛrɛɛ na wa anokoare keŋ.

eng: No one has ever seen God. God the only Son, who is at the Fathers side, has made God known.
local: Ŋolo na ŋolo te naa ta Wurubuarɛ na ɔ sia ya, mɔna Wurubuarɛ bu dokoloŋ kooŋ ŋon loŋ bɛɛ lam ŋon na ɔ kya nsana ya nɛ, kaa lesena Wurubuarɛ dinɔɔ wola daa.

eng: This is Johns testimony when the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him, Who are you.
local: Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ be kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, kpila Wurubuarɛ saese na Lewitena baale nyi be kaa bɔɔse Gyɔn nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna amɔte? Gyɔn ta beo wɔ ya.

eng: John confessed , Im not the Christ.
local: Mɔna ɔ torowɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Na maŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ ya.

eng: They asked him, Then who are you? Are you Elijah? John said, Im not. Are you the prophet? John answered, No.
local: Keŋte be bɔɔse e nyiaa, Kenaŋ na nyaŋ yɛna amɔte? Nyaŋ yɛna Elaegya? Keŋte ɔ tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyi, Koa, na maŋ yɛna Elaegya ya. Ba besewɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋon dɛɛ daa nɛ? Keŋte Gyɔn tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyi Koa, na maŋ ya.

eng: They asked, Who are you? We need to give an answer to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself.
local: Keŋte be bɔɔse e nyi Te kenaŋ na nyaŋ yɛna amɔte? Yako daa nombii dokoloŋ na dee nyiŋ gyu ke yako baŋ bɔɔ kpila daa nɛ. Sena te n ne yako lee nyaŋ gbagba n wose man.

eng: John replied, I am a voice crying out in the wilderness, Make the Lords path straight, just as the prophet Isaiah said.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyɔn moo nɔɔwoya ŋan Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya be yako see nɛ tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Maŋ yɛna woya ŋan ne fae yeesa depampaa dinaa keŋ man nɛ nyiaa, Ɛ tenee de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛ see e na ɔ kaa ta ke dɔɔ.

eng: Those sent by the Pharisees.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ kpili gyu Gyɔn gyaŋ nɛ, man baale yɛɛ Farasiitenawɔ.

eng: asked, Why do you baptize if you arent the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the prophet.
local: keŋte be bɔɔse Gyɔn nyiaa, Akpaa na nyaŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ ya, yaa Elaegya yaa Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋon ya na, woŋ dɔɔ te n ne sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

eng: John answered, I baptize with water. Someone greater stands among you, whom you dont recognize.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyɔn tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Maŋ dɔɔ loŋ yakaa te me dana mɛɛ sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ, mɔna ŋolo kyaa ɛ man keŋ ɛ ba gyeŋ e ya.

eng: He comes after me, but Im not worthy to untie his sandal straps.
local: Ŋon yɛna walaŋ ŋon ɔ dɔŋ me wɔle ɔ ne kɔŋ, keŋ ma te kaboe gba nyi maa buŋ booli ɔ nkyokota ŋmeeseŋ koraŋ gba ya.

eng: This encounter took place across the Jordan in Bethany where John was baptizing.
local: Nombia kɛŋa pou kɔŋae Gyɔɔdan boo diŋgyiŋ donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke Bɛtani nɛ man, botɔɔ keŋ Gyɔn ne sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ nɛ.

eng: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, Look! The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!.
local: Tɛɛ kena nɛ, Gyɔn naa Yesu ne kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ yakowɔ lee Yesu wose man nyiaa, Ɛ kɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nambu ŋon. Ŋon na lesena balaŋ lee be dukum man.

eng: This is the one about whom I said, He who comes after me is really greater than me because he existed before me.
local: Kei yɛna walaŋ ŋon mɔɔ wulaa yako ŋon ɔ wose man nombia nyiaa, Walaŋ ŋon ɔ dɔŋ me wɔle ɔ ne kɔŋ nɛ ɔ kela maŋ. Nawolo nyi ɔ wulaa ɔ kyaa pɛte ba lola maŋ.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but I came baptizing with water so that he might be made known to Israel.
local: Nafɔ na maŋ gbagba ba gyeŋ e ya, mɔna ŋon dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ kpila maŋ nyi me kaa sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ, na maa lese e debɔɔ wola Iseraetena na baa nyiŋ gyeŋ e.

eng: John testified, I saw the Spirit coming down from heaven like a dove, and it rested on him.
local: Keŋte Gyɔn dii adansɛɛ nyiaa, Me naa Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon moo duwuluŋ dinɔɔ tisi lee adido kaa kyaa ɔ dɔɔ.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but the one who sent me to baptize with water said to me, The one on whom you see the Spirit coming down and resting is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.
local: Nafɔ maŋ gbagba ba gyeŋ e ya, mɔna Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nyi me kaa mo loŋ sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ nɛ yako na maŋ nyiaa, Walaŋ ŋon n naa Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon de tisi kaa kyaa ɔ dɔɔ nɛ, ŋon ne gyaela waa mo Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

eng: I have seen and testified that this one is Gods Son.
local: Ma te naa te mee di adansɛɛ nyiaa, ŋon yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon.

eng: The next day John was standing again with two of his disciples.
local: Tɛɛ kena nɛ, Gyɔn na ɔ kaseela bala besewɔ gyu boo keŋ nɔɔman.

eng: When he saw Jesus walking along he said, Look! The Lamb of God.
local: Keŋte Gyɔn naa Yesu ne lam. Ɔ kena e nɛ te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nambu ŋon.

eng: After this there was a Jewish festival, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Kɛŋa wɔle nɛ, Gyudatena ne di bɔ ɔsom wee dinaa daale Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man, te Yesu mɔ gyuu botɔɔ.

eng: The two disciples heard what he said, and they followed Jesus.
local: Gyɔn kaseela bala baŋ kenyii Gyɔn nɔɔwoya kɛŋa nɛ, be tinaa Gyɔn yela gyu ke sila Yesu.

eng: In Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate in the north city wall is a pool with the Aramaic name Bethsaida. It had five covered porches.
local: Na depaa daale kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man keŋ bɛɛ baake ke be dɛɛ dei man nyi Bɛtesaida. Mena depaa kei benaa na denanɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Namense Denanɔɔ nɛ, te be baŋee kefɛɛse anoŋ kilisi ke.

eng: When Jesus turned and saw them following, he asked, What are you looking for? They said, Rabbi , where are you staying.
local: Yesu gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ na te ɔ naa wɔ keŋte ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Woŋ yaa ɛ ne gyae? Botɔɔ te baŋ mɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, aleŋ te n kyaa.

eng: and a crowd of people who were sick, blind, lame, and paralyzed sat there.
local: Kaweesetena kpookpoo burum ŋgba siayɛlɛɛsatena na ɔtakasese na baŋ ba yenaase ba yekee nɛ na lee donɔɔ keŋ man kaa doo botɔɔ daa loŋ keŋ kewosi.

eng: He replied, Come and see. So they went and saw where he was staying, and they remained with him that day. It was about four oclock in the afternoon.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kɔŋ na ɛ kaa kɛɛ. Mena dɔɔ be gyuuwɔ kena botɔɔ keŋ ɔ kyaa. Debaŋ kenaŋ na te gyɔ balinɔɔ debaŋ anaara te be kyaawɔ ɔ gyaŋ wee kenaŋ pou.

eng: A certain man was there who had been sick for thirty-eight years.
local: Na balee ŋolo ŋon ɔ be tekerii kulutooneŋ sao na kufu gyanaara nɛ doo botɔɔ.

eng: One of the two disciples who heard what John said and followed Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
local: Balaŋ bala baŋ bɔɔ nyii Gyɔn nɔɔwoya te bɔɔ sila Yesu nɛ, be man ŋolo yɛna Simɔn Pita ɔ naabu Anderiase.

eng: When Jesus saw him lying there, knowing that he had already been there a long time, he asked him, Do you want to get well.
local: Yesu kena balee ŋonaŋ te ɔ ke nyii nyi ɔ ta wee kyare nɛ ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, N ne gyae n kawee ke taŋ.

eng: He first found his own brother Simon and said to him, We have found the Messiah .
local: Kpene keŋ Anderiase be taŋgbɛɛ yɛɛ yɛna nyi, ɔ gyuuwɔ ke kɛo ɔ kegyia Simɔn te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Da te naa Mesaya ŋon, nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: The sick man answered him, Sir, I dont have anyone who can put me in the water when it is stirred up. When Im trying to get to it, someone else has gotten in ahead of me.
local: Botɔɔ te kaweete ŋon tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, akpaa be wosi loŋ keŋ na, mɔɔ ne nyiŋ ŋon waa gate maŋ ka do ke man ya. Akpaa ma mo maa yɛɛ kakyeŋ koro ka gyoo na ŋolo te taŋgbɛɛ maŋ.

eng: He led him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, You are Simon, son of John. You will be called Cephas .
local: Keŋte Anderiase moo Simɔn kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ. Yesu kena Simɔn nɛ ɔ kɛɛ e yididi te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Me gyeŋ nyi nyaŋ yɛna Gyɔn bu Simɔn ŋon. Baa baake neŋ nyi Kefase yaa Pita. Ka asɛɛ yɛna nyi boe.

eng: Jesus said to him, Get up! Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, Koro! Gate n dofoŋoŋ na n ke ta.

eng: The next day Jesus wanted to go into Galilee, and he found Philip. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: Tɛɛ kena nɛ, Yesu yɛɛ wa agyueŋ nyi waa gyu Galelia tɛɛle man. Keŋte ɔ naa Filipo te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Kaa sila maŋ.

eng: Immediately the man was well, and he picked up his mat and walked. Now that day was the Sabbath.
local: Debokenaŋ man te balee ŋon kawee taŋawɔ te ɔ korowɔ gate ɔ dofoŋoŋ te ɔ tɛɛwɔ laŋ. Mena wee kenaŋ yɛɛ Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee ke.

eng: Philip was from Bethsaida, the hometown of Andrew and Peter.
local: Filipo lee donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke Bɛtesaida nɛ man. Donɔɔ kenaŋ man mɔ te Anderiase na Pita mɔ leewɔ.

eng: The Jewish leaders said to the man who had been healed, Its the Sabbath; you arent allowed to carry your mat.
local: Mena dɔɔ Gyudatena kegyiise yako balee ŋon bɔɔ kyɔ e nɛ nyiaa, Gyɛŋ yɛɛ da kefɛɛfowee ke, te da mmaraa bɛɛ fa gbɛɛ nyi nya seele n dofoŋoŋ ya.

eng: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, We have found the one Moses wrote about in the Law and the Prophets: Jesus, Josephs son, from Nazareth.
local: Filipo mɔ naa Natanɛle te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Da te naa walaŋ ŋon Mosesi be ŋmarase ɔ wose man nombia do mmaraa tɔne keŋ man, te Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ mɔ be ŋmarase ɔ wose man nombia nɛ. Bɛɛ baake e nyi Yesu. Ŋon yɛna Yesu, Gyosɛfo bu ŋon ɔ ba lee Nasarɛte nɛ.

eng: He answered, The man who made me well said to me, Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Keŋte balee ŋon tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kyɔ maŋ me kawee nɛ, yako na maŋ nyi maa gate ma dofoŋoŋ na maa ta.

eng: Nathanael responded, Can anything from Nazareth be good? Philip said, Come and see.
local: Keŋte Natanɛle bɔɔse e nyiaa, Nasarɛte ya? Kolo kpaakpaa daale gyae ke tale lee botɔɔ? Keŋte Filipo yako e nyiaa, Nyaŋ kɔŋ na n kaa kɛɛ.

eng: They inquired, Who is this man who said to you, Pick it up and walk.
local: Botɔɔ te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, Walaŋ woŋti yako na neŋ nyi nya gate n dofoŋoŋ na n keta.

eng: Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said about him, Here is a genuine Israelite in whom there is no deceit.
local: Yesu kena Natanɛle ne kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ nɛ ɔ kolosiwɔ lee ɔ wose man nyiaa, Ɛ kɛɛ, Iserae baale gbagba yaa kei keŋ dekara be doo ɔ man ya.

eng: The man who had been cured didnt know who it was, because Jesus had slipped away from the crowd gathered there.
local: Mɔna balee ŋon Yesu be kyɔ e ɔ kawee nɛ, ta gyem nyi Yesu kyɔɔna e ɔ kawee keŋ ya. Nawolo nyi na balaŋ boe botɔɔ te Yesu mɔ ta ŋere gyoo be man.

eng: Nathanael asked him, How do you know me? Jesus answered, Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.
local: Botɔɔ te Natanɛle bɔɔse e nyiaa, Sena te n yɛɛwɔ gyeŋ maŋ? Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyi, Me wulaa naa neŋ fiigi daŋ keŋ tɛɛ pɛte Filipo baake neŋ.

eng: Later Jesus found him in the temple and said, See! You have been made well. Dont sin anymore in case something worse happens to you.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, Yesu kena balee ŋonaŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Kɛɛ, nɛnɛɛ n wose te yɛɛ neŋ doŋ, na tekaa yɛɛ dukum bela ya. Na mena ya na kpene keŋ gyae kekɔŋ n dɔɔ nɛ, gyae ka kela kei.

eng: Nathanael replied, Rabbi, you are Gods Son. You are the king of Israel.
local: Keŋte Natanɛle torowɔ yako nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon! Nyaŋ yɛna Iseraetena gyoo ŋon.

eng: The man went and proclaimed to the Jewish leaders that Jesus was the man who had made him well.
local: Botɔɔ te balee ŋon gyuuwɔ keyako Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ nyiaa, Yesu kyɔɔ na e ɔ kawee keŋ.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than these!.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, Keŋ mɔ yako neŋ nyi me naa neŋ fiigi daŋ keŋ tɛɛ dɔɔ te n ne lɛɛ maŋ di? N kena nombia dinaana ŋan kela kɛŋa.

eng: As a result, the Jewish leaders were harassing Jesus, since he had done these things on the Sabbath.
local: Mena dɔɔ Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ naase Yesu diyem, nawolo nyi ɔ te kyɔ kawee ba kefɛɛfowee.

eng: I assure you that you will see heaven open and Gods angels going up to heaven and down to earth on the Human One.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, ɛ kena ŋgba adido ta toro na Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ na gyem tisi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon dɔɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, My Father is still working, and I am working too.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Me kya Wurubuarɛ ne yɛɛ tom debaŋ kamasɛ kaa lii na gyɛŋ, mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi maŋ mɔ maa yɛɛ tom mena.

eng: For this reason the Jewish leaders wanted even more to kill him—not only because he was doing away with the Sabbath but also because he called God his own Father, thereby making himself equal with God.
local: Lee mena nombia kɛŋa dɔɔ nɛ Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ yase sia gyae gbɛɛ keŋ baa mo ko Yesu. Nawolo nyi na keŋ ɔ be wɔlɛɛ ba kefɛɛfowee keŋ kaageŋ dɔɔ ya, mɔna ɔ kpa ɔ kya yɛna Wurubuarɛ keŋ nawolo nyi ɔ dokoso yɛna Wurubuarɛ.

eng: Jesus responded to the Jewish leaders, I assure you that the Son cant do anything by himself except what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son does likewise.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, kolo be kyaa keŋ maŋ Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon ne yɛɛ ma wose dɔɔ ya. Kpene keŋ me naa me kya ne yɛɛ na keŋ te mɛɛ yɛɛ. Kpene keŋ kya ŋon ne yɛɛ na, keŋ te maŋ ɔ bu ŋon mɔ ne yɛɛ.

eng: The Father loves the Son and shows him everything that he does. He will show him greater works than these so that you will marvel.
local: Nawolo nyi me kya ne gyae maŋ te ɔ na wola maŋ kpene kamasɛ keŋ ŋon gbagba ne yɛɛ nɛ. Waa wola maŋ abɔɔ dinaana ŋan kela kɛŋa na maa yɛɛ, na ɛ wose ka yeŋ ŋon.

eng: As the Father raises the dead and gives life, so too does the Son give life to whomever he wishes.
local: Mena keŋ kya ŋon ne gyuusu woya te ɔ ne fa wɔ nyeedoŋ nɛ, mena kee te maŋ ɔ bu ŋon mɔ maa fa balaŋ baŋ maŋ gbagba ne gyae nɛ nyeedoŋ.

eng: The Father doesnt judge anyone, but he has given all judgment to the Son.
local: Kya ŋon gbagba bee di balaŋ nombia ya. Ɔ ta mo balaŋ nombia kedi pou do maŋ ɔ bu ŋon nyiŋmaa man.

eng: so that everyone will honor the Son just as they honor the Father. Whoever doesnt honor the Son doesnt honor the Father who sent him.
local: na balaŋ pou ke nyiŋ lese maŋ ɔ bu ŋon yele ŋgba mena keŋ bɛɛ lese me kya ŋon yele nɛ. Walaŋ ŋon ɔ bɛɛ lese maŋ ɔ bu ŋon yele ya na, ɔ bɛɛ lese me kya ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ ɔ bu ŋon nɛ yele ya.

eng: I assure you that whoever hears my word and believes in the one who sent me has eternal life and wont come under judgment but has passed from death into life.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ nyii me nɔɔwoya te ɔ lɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ di na, waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa te bɔɔ gyae baa bu e fɔɔ ya. Kei nawolo baa nyi ɔ ta lee yeŋ man kɔŋ nyeedoŋ man.

eng: I assure you that the time is coming—and is here!—when the dead will hear the voice of Gods Son, and those who hear it will live.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, debaŋ daale ne kɔŋ te ke taŋ ke kɔŋ keŋ balaŋ baŋ be yɛɛ ŋgba woya nɛ, nawolo nyi ba te lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan di dɔɔ ya nɛ, baa nyii maŋ Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon dei. Balaŋ baŋ baa nyii me dei te baa yɛɛ kpene keŋ mɔɔ yako wɔ nɛ na baa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.

eng: Just as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son to have life in himself.
local: Mena keŋ me kya ŋon ne fa balaŋ nyeedoŋ nɛ, mena mɔ te ɔ faa maŋ ɔ bu doŋ nyi maa fa balaŋ nyeedoŋ.

eng: He gives the Son authority to judge, because he is the Human One.
local: Ɔ te fa maŋ ɔ bu ŋon doŋ nyi maa di balaŋ nombia, nawolo nyi maŋ yɛna Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon.

eng: Dont be surprised by this, because the time is coming when all who are in their graves will hear his voice.
local: Ɛ na yeli nombia kɛŋa ke yɛɛ ŋon gyakoloŋ ya, nawolo nyi debaŋ daale gyae kekɔŋ keŋ woya pou baa nyii ma woya.

eng: Those who did good things will come out into the resurrection of life, and those who did wicked things into the resurrection of judgment.
local: na baa lee ba yebɔɔse man kɔŋ. Baŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa nɛ baa koro na baa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ, te baŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ nɛ baa koro na baa bu wɔ fɔɔ.

eng: I cant do anything by myself. Whatever I hear, I judge, and my judgment is just. I dont seek my own will but the will of the one who sent me.
local: Maŋ gbagba bɛɛ yɛɛ kolo me baam dɔɔ ya. Mena keŋ me kya Wurubuarɛ de yako maŋ nɛ, mena te mee di balaŋ nombia, te me nombia kedi mɔ doo na ke gbɛɛ. Nawolo nyi na maŋ gbagba kegyaebii te mɛɛ yɛɛ ya, mɔna me kya ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ kegyaebii te mɛɛ yɛɛ.

eng: If I testify about myself, my testimony isnt true.
local: Nyi akpaa maŋ gbagba de di ma wose man adansɛɛ na, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke lɛɛ maŋ di ya.

eng: There is someone else who testifies about me, and I know his testimony about me is true.
local: Mɔna ŋolo kyaa keŋ ɔ ne di ma wose man adansɛɛ, te ma gyeŋ nyi wa adansɛɛ keŋ ɔ ne dii lee ma wose man nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ.

eng: You sent a delegation to John, and he testified to the truth.
local: Ɛ kpila balaŋ gyu Gyɔn gyaŋ, te ɔ dii anokoare keŋ wose man adansɛɛ fa ŋon.

eng: Although I dont accept human testimony, I say these things so that you can be saved.
local: Na mɛɛ gyae bo nyi denibalaŋ baa di ma wose man adansɛɛ ya, mɔna ɛ nyeedoŋ kenyiŋ dɔɔ te mɛɛ yako kɛŋa.

eng: John was a burning and shining lamp, and, at least for a while, you were willing to celebrate in his light.
local: Gyɔn yɛɛ ŋgba kanea keŋ kɛɛ naa te kɛɛ ŋalakee nɛ, te ɛ gyaewɔ nyi ɛ ke di kanea kenaŋ gyoŋ kyomii.

eng: I have a witness greater than Johns testimony. The Father has given me works to do so that I might complete them. These works I do testify about me that the Father sent me.
local: Me dana ŋolo ŋon ɔ ne di ma wose man adansɛɛ, te wa adansɛɛ kenaŋ kela Gyɔn wui keŋ ɔ be dii lee ma wose man nɛ. Nawolo nyi tom keŋ me kya Wurubuarɛ ba mo do maŋ me nyiŋmaa man nyi maa yɛɛ ke kelii ɔto te mɛɛ yɛɛ ke nɛ, ne diina adansɛɛ lee ma wose man nyi me kya ŋonaŋ kpila na maŋ.

eng: And the Father who sent me testifies about me. You have never even heard his voice or seen his form.
local: Me kya ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ, ŋon gbagba mɔ ne di ma wose man adansɛɛ. Ɛ te nyii ta ɔ nɔɔwoya ya, te ɛ te naa ta ɔ dinɔɔ mɔ ya.

eng: and you dont have his word dwelling with you because you dont believe the one whom he has sent.
local: Ɔ nombia be doo ɛ konɔɔse man ya, nawolo nyi ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kpili nɛ di ya.

eng: Examine the scriptures, since you think that in them you have eternal life. They also testify about me.
local: Ɛ ne yase ɛ sia kasee Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan nawolo nyi na ya agyueŋ yɛna nyi ŋe man te ɛ ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ. Te mena Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see kɛŋa ne di ma wose man adansɛɛ.

eng: yet you dont want to come to me so that you can have life.
local: Mɔna ɛ bɛɛ gyae ɛ ke kɔŋ me gyaŋ na ɛ kaa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ ya.

eng: I dont accept praise from people.
local: Na ma kegyaebii yɛna nyi balaŋ baa kɛlɛɛ maŋ ya.

eng: but I know you, that you dont have Gods love in you.
local: Mɔna ma gyeŋ ɛ dinɔɔ te ma gyeŋ nyi ɛ bɛɛ gyae Wurubuarɛ lee ɛ konɔɔse man ya.

eng: I have come in my Fathers name, and you dont receive me. If others come in their own name, you receive them.
local: Me kɔŋae me kya ŋon yele man mɔna ɛ te lɛɛ maŋ ya. Mɔna akpaa ŋolo de kɔŋ ŋon gbagba yele man na ɛ ke lɛɛ e.

eng: How can you believe when you receive praise from each other but dont seek the praise that comes from the only God?.
local: Ɛ ne gyae nyi ɛ ke kɛlɛɛ dɔŋa, mɔna ɛ bɛɛ gyae nyi Wurubuarɛ dokoloŋ ŋon ke kɛlɛɛ ŋon ya. Kenaŋ na sena te ɛ ke tale lɛɛ maŋ di?.

eng: Dont think that I will accuse you before the Father. Your accuser is Moses, the one in whom your hope rests.
local: Ɛ na gyueŋ nyi maŋ ne gyaela me kaa yako ɛ nombiakumɛɛ fa me kya Wurubuarɛ ya. Mosesi ŋon ɛ ba mo ɛ loo gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ nɛ, ŋon ne gyaela waa mo ɛ nombiakumɛɛ gyu me kya siaman.

eng: If you believed Moses, you would believe me, because Moses wrote about me.
local: Nyi yeŋ lɛɛ Mosesi di na nafɔ ɛ kelɛɛ maŋ mɔ di, nawolo nyi ma wose man nombia te Mosesi ŋmarasewɔ.

eng: If you dont believe the writings of Moses, how will you believe my words.
local: Mɔna nyi akpaa ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ kpene keŋ ɔ be ŋmarase nɛ di ya na, sena te ɛ kelɛɛ kpene keŋ mɛɛ yako nɛ di.

eng: There was a Pharisee named Nicodemus, a Jewish leader.
local: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ man nɛ, na Gyudatena kegyia ŋolo kyaawɔ keŋ ɔ yele yɛna Nikodemose. Ɔ kpuɛ Farasiitena dikpii keŋ man.

eng: He came to Jesus at night and said to him, Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God, for no one could do these miraculous signs that you do unless God is with him.
local: Wee daale nelim nɛ ɔ korowɔ gyu Yesu gyaŋ te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Kegyia, da gyeŋ nyi n yɛɛ wolale e ŋon Wurubuarɛ be kpila neŋ. Nawolo nyi ŋolo be kyaa keŋ waa tale yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana ŋan n ne yɛɛ nɛ, keŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ be kpuɛ na e ya.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born anew, its not possible to see Gods kingdom.
