local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, Mɛɛ yako neŋ anokoare n ke nyii nyiaa, akpaa be ta bese lola walaŋ ya nɛ, ɔ be gyae waa naa Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ ya.

eng: Nicodemus asked, How is it possible for an adult to be born? Its impossible to enter the mothers womb for a second time and be born, isnt it.
local: Keŋte Nikodemose bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, Walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba bee na, sena te baa bese tale lola e? Waa bese tale gyoo ɔ naa tɔɔman na baa lola e bela.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born of water and the Spirit, its not possible to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Mɛɛ yako neŋ anokoare n ke nyii nyiaa, akpaa ba te sɔ neŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ na Wurubuarɛ ka mo ɔ feliŋ do n man ya na, n be gyae n ke tale gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man ya.

eng: Whatever is born of the flesh is flesh, and whatever is born of the Spirit is spirit.
local: Deniwalaŋ na lola ɔ dinɔɔ bo, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon mɔ na lola walaŋ bo ŋgba Wurubuarɛ dinɔɔ nɛ.

eng: Dont be surprised that I said to you, You must be born anew.
local: Na yela keyɛɛ neŋ gyakoloŋ nyi me kpa, kaboena nyi ba bese lola neŋ bela ya.

eng: Gods Spirit blows wherever it wishes. You hear its sound, but you dont know where it comes from or where it is going. Its the same with everyone who is born of the Spirit.
local: Feliŋ ne gyɔɔ bo gyu botɔɔ keŋ kɛɛ gyae. N ne nyii mena keŋ kɛɛ gyɔɔ mɔna n ba gyeŋ ke dalee yaa ke dee gyue ya. Mena mɔ te doo fa walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ba lola e nɛ.

eng: Nicodemus said, How are these things possible.
local: Keŋte Nikodemose bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, Sena te kɛŋa gyae ke tale kɔŋ mena.

eng: Jesus answered, You are a teacher of Israel and you dont know these things?.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, Ŋgba n yɛɛ Gyudatena wolale kegyia e. N bee nyii mena nombia kɛŋa man yee?.

eng: I assure you that we speak about what we know and testify about what we have seen, but you dont receive our testimony.
local: Mɛɛ yako neŋ anokoare n ke nyii nyiaa, nombia ŋan da gyeŋ nɛ te dɛɛ yako te ŋan dɛɛ naa mɔ nɛ te dee di ŋa wose man adansɛɛ, mɔna ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ nombia ŋan dɛɛ yako ŋon nɛ di ya.

eng: If I have told you about earthly things and you dont believe, how will you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?.
local: Mɛɛ yako ŋon tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nombia te ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ ŋa di ya na, sena te me yako ŋon Wurubuarɛ man nombia na ɛ kelɛɛ ŋa di?.

eng: No one has gone up to heaven except the one who came down from heaven, the Human One.
local: Ŋolo na ŋolo te gyuu ta Wurubuarɛ man ya, Gyisɛ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon mɔɔ lee botɔɔ kɔŋ nɛ maageŋ.

eng: Just as Moses lifted up the snake in the wilderness, so must the Human One be lifted up.
local: Ɛ gyeŋ nyi gyaŋgba keŋ na Wurubuarɛ yela Mosesi yɛɛ dom te ɔ moowɔ mataa na daŋ adido depampaa dinaa keŋ man. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ be gyina ɔ sia kɛɛ mena dom kei na ɔ nyiŋa nyeedoŋ. Mena ke te baa gyɔ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon mɔ mataa na ɔpaŋdaŋ adido.

eng: so that everyone who believes in him will have eternal life.
local: Na walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ maŋ di na, waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ.

eng: God so loved the world that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who believes in him wont perish but will have eternal life.
local: Lee mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne gyae balaŋ dɔɔ te ɔ moo ɔ bu dokoloŋ kooŋ ŋon fa, nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ e di na ɔ be gyae waa yeŋ leki ya, mɔna waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa.

eng: God didnt send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world might be saved through him.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ te kpila ɔ bu tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyi ɔ kaa bu balaŋ fɔɔ ya, mɔna ɔ kpila e bo na balaŋ ke ta ɔ dɔɔ nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.

eng: Whoever believes in him isnt judged; whoever doesnt believe in him is already judged, because they dont believe in the name of Gods only Son.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ e di na bɔɔ gyae baa bu e fɔɔ ya, te ŋon ɔ te lɛɛ e di ya na be wulaa bu e fɔɔ, nawolo nyi ɔ te lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bu dokoloŋ kooŋ ŋon di ya.

eng: This is the basis for judgment: The light came into the world, and people loved darkness more than the light, for their actions are evil.
local: Nombia ŋan bɔɔ mo bu tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ fɔɔ yɛna nyi, keŋmaŋee te kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, mɔna balaŋ ne gyae ditiŋtɛɛ bo kela keŋmaŋee keŋ. Nawolo nyi be nombia ŋan bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ yɛɛ dukum ke.

eng: All who do wicked things hate the light and dont come to the light for fear that their actions will be exposed to the light.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ dukum na, ɔ ne kɔla keŋmaŋee keŋ te ɔ bɛɛ benaa ke ya. Nawolo nyi ɔ bɛɛ gyae nyi ɔ nombiakumɛɛ ŋan ka lee debɔɔ ya.

eng: Whoever does the truth comes to the light so that it can be seen that their actions were done in God.
local: Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa nɛ, ɔ na benaa keŋmaŋee keŋ. Nawolo nyi keŋmaŋee keŋ gyae ka lese ɔ nombia ŋan ɔ ne yɛɛ nɛ wola nyi ɔ ne yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii.

eng: After this Jesus and his disciples went into Judea, where he spent some time with them and was baptizing.
local: Kɛŋa wɔle nɛ Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyuu Gyudia tɛɛle man. Yesu kyaawɔ botɔɔ weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale te ɔ sɔɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

eng: John was baptizing at Aenon near Salem because there was a lot of water there, and people were coming to him and being baptized. .
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na Gyɔn mɔ ne sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Aenɔn keŋ ka benaa na Salim donɔɔ nɛ man. Nawolo nyi na loŋ boe botɔɔ, te balaŋ mɔ na gyem tisi ɔ dɔɔ nyi waa sɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

eng: John hadnt yet been thrown into prison.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na ba te kyaŋa ta Gyɔn tɔ deni ya.

eng: A debate started between Johns disciples and a certain Jew about cleansing rituals.
local: Gyuda baale ŋolo na Gyɔn kaseela baŋ man baale lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ lee mena keŋ bɛɛ mo loŋ faafo ba wose lee ba amanberɛ gbɛɛ nɛ wose man.

eng: They came to John and said, Rabbi, look! The man who was with you across the Jordan, the one about whom you testified, is baptizing and everyone is flocking to him.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyɔn kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ Gyɔn gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, n ne tɔɔse balee ŋon nafɔ ɔ kpuɛ na daa Gyɔɔdan boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ, te n be di ɔ wose man adansɛɛ nideli nɛ? Kɛɛ, ɔ ne sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ, te balaŋ pou ne gyu ɔ gyaŋ.

eng: John replied, No one can receive anything unless it is given from heaven.
local: Keŋte Gyɔn tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Nyi Wurubuarɛ te fa walaŋ gbɛɛ nyi waa yɛɛ kolo ya nɛ, ɔtenate be gyae waa tale yɛɛ ya.

eng: You yourselves can testify that I said that Im not the Christ but that Im the one sent before him.
local: Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ ke tale di maŋ adansɛɛ nyi me yakowɔ nyiaa, Na maŋ yɛna Nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ ya, mɔna Wurubuarɛ kpila maŋ bo nyi maa kyaŋ gbɛɛ ta ɔ siaman kɔŋ.

eng: The groom is the one who is getting married. The friend of the groom stands close by and, when he hears him, is overjoyed at the grooms voice. Therefore, my joy is now complete.
local: Keŋte Gyɔn gyɔɔ dudu yako wɔ nyiaa, Walaŋ ŋon ɔ dana alowɔle na, ŋon yɛna alowɔle ŋon baale. Mɔna alowɔle baale ŋon ɔ gyoona dɔɔ bɛɛ sem bo yenaŋ tei, te akpaa be nyii alowɔle baale ŋon woya na ne yɛɛ wɔ gyoŋ nideli. Mena mɔ yaa yɛɛ maŋ gyoŋ nideli debokeiman ŋgba mɔɔ nyii Yesu nkaŋ nɛ.

eng: He must increase and I must decrease.
local: Kaboena nyi Yesu yele dɛɛ lee kela maŋ.

eng: The one who comes from above is above all things. The one who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks as one from the earth. The one who comes from heaven is above all things.
local: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba lee adido kɔŋ nɛ ɔ kela walaŋ kamasɛ, te walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ ɔ yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ walaŋ e te ɔ ne kolosi tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nombia bo. Walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba lee adido nɛ, ɔ kela kpene kamasɛ.

eng: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts his testimony.
local: Nombia ŋan ɔ be naa na ŋan ɔ be nyii nɛ wose man adansɛɛ te ɔ ne dii, mɔna ŋolo te lɛɛ wa adansɛɛ keŋ di ya.

eng: Whoever accepts his testimony confirms that God is true.
local: Te walaŋ ŋon ɔ be lɛɛ wa adansɛɛ keŋ di na, na ɔtenate te sɛɛ baa nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan yɛɛ ampaŋ.

eng: The one whom God sent speaks Gods words because God gives the Spirit generously.
local: Walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ be kpila e na ɔ ne kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia bo, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ ta mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do walaŋ ŋonaŋ man nideli.

eng: The Father loves the Son and gives everything into his hands.
local: Kya ŋon ne gyae ɔ bu ŋon nombia, te ɔ moo kpene kamasɛ do e ɔ nyiŋmaa man.

eng: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life. Whoever doesnt believe in the Son wont see life, but the angry judgment of God remains on them.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ ɔ bu ŋon di na waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa, te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ bɛɛ ɔ bu ŋon na ɔ be gyae waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kei ya, mɔna Wurubuarɛ waa gyeŋ baŋ do e kekpaakekpaa.

eng: After this Jesus traveled throughout Galilee. He didnt want to travel in Judea, because the Jewish authorities wanted to kill him.
local: Nombia kɛŋa wɔle nɛ Yesu tɛɛwɔ kila Galelia donɔɔse man. Ɔ te gyae nyi waa gyu Gyudia tɛɛle man ya, nawolo nyi Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ ne gyae gbɛɛ baa ko e.

eng: When it was almost time for the Jewish Festival of Booths.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na Gyudatena kefɛɛse man kekyaa wee dinaa kedi ta benaa.

eng: Jesus brothers said to him, Leave Galilee. Go to Judea so that your disciples can see the amazing works that you do.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu ɔ naabia yako e nyiaa, Kaboena nyi nya lee kɛbo na n ke gyu Gyuda tɛɛle man na n kaseela baŋ ke nyiŋ naa gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan n ne yɛɛ nɛ.

eng: Those who want to be known publicly dont do things secretly. Since you can do these things, show yourself to the world.
local: Nawolo nyi walaŋ ne gyae ɔ yele ka lee na, ɔ bɛɛ yɛɛ kolo weese ya. Mena dɔɔ n keta n ne yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia kɛŋa nɛ, kaboena nyi nya lese n wose debɔɔ fa balaŋ pou.

eng: His brothers said this because even they didnt believe in him.
local: Ɔ naabia baŋ kolosi nombia kɛŋa nawolo nyi baŋ gbagba koraŋ te lɛɛ e di ya.

eng: Jesus replied, For you, anytime is fine. But my time hasnt come yet.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba see fa maŋ nɛ te lii ta ya, mɔna ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ debaŋ kamasɛ dei fa ŋon.

eng: The world cant hate you. It hates me, though, because I testify that its works are evil.
local: Tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ bɛɛ kɔla ŋon ya mɔna maŋ dɔɔ bɛɛ kɔla maŋ, nawolo nyi me gyakaa mɛɛ yako wɔ nyi be nombia ŋan bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ be dei ya.

eng: You go up to the festival. Im not going to this one because my time hasnt yet come.
local: Ɛmɛɛ ɛ ta gyu kedi wee dinaa keŋ, maŋ be gyae maa gyu ya nawolo nyi ma debaŋ keŋ te lii ta ya.

eng: Having said this, he stayed in Galilee.
local: Ɔ ke yako nombia kɛŋa fa wɔ taŋ nɛ, te ŋon dɔɔwɔ Galelia tɛɛle man.

eng: However, after his brothers left for the festival, he went too—not openly but in secret.
local: Yesu ɔ naabia ke laŋ ba wee dinaa keŋ dedii man nɛ, te ŋon mɔ korowɔ gyu botɔɔ. Mɔna ɔ weese ɔ wose bo.

eng: The Jewish leaders were looking for Jesus at the festival. They kept asking, Where is he.
local: Na Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ dɔŋɛɛ bɛɛ kɛo e na bɛɛ bɔɔse nyiaa Ɔ kyaa aleŋ.

eng: The crowds were murmuring about him. Hes a good man, some said, but others were saying, No, he tricks the people.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ man balaŋ burum gyoo kekolosi na dɔŋa lee Yesu wose man. Baale kpa ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ kpaakpaa e, te baale mɔ kpa koa, ɔ na beo balaŋ bo na baa yo lee Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛneŋ ŋan man.

eng: No one spoke about him publicly, though, for fear of the Jewish authorities.
local: Mɔna ŋolo te tale kolosi ɔ wose man nombia balaŋ man ya, nawolo nyi be yee Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ.

eng: Halfway through the festival, Jesus went up to the temple and started to teach.
local: Be kedi ba wee dinaa keŋ kelii nsana nsana nɛ, Yesu gyuu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ɔ na wola balaŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia.

eng: Astonished, the Jewish leaders asked, Hes never been taught! How has he mastered the Law.
local: Ɔ kawola keŋ doo Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ nɔɔ te be bɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, Sena te balee kei yɛɛwɔ gyeŋ nombia kɛŋa keŋ nyi ɔ te kasee lee ŋolo gyaŋ ya.

eng: Jesus responded, My teaching isnt mine but comes from the one who sent me.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Kpene keŋ mɛɛ wolo nɛ ta lee maŋ gbagba gyaŋ ya, mɔna ka lee me kya Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ gyaŋ.

eng: Whoever wants to do Gods will can tell whether my teaching is from God or whether I speak on my own.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae nyi waa di Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan dɔɔ nɛ, ɔtenate waa gyeŋ nyi kpene keŋ mɛɛ wolo nɛ, lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ yaa lee maŋ gbagba gyaŋ.

eng: Those who speak on their own seek glory for themselves. Those who seek the glory of him who sent me are people of truth; theres no falsehood in them.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne kolosi ŋon gbagba ɔ baam dɔɔ nɛ, na ɔ ne gyae yele bo wa fa ɔ wose. Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae bo nyi waa lese ŋon ɔ be kpila e nɛ yele na, ɔ nombia doo na ke gbɛɛ keŋ nyi dekara be doo ke man ya.

eng: Didnt Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you keep the Law. Why do you want to kill me.
local: Na Mosesi faa na ŋon mmaraase ŋan yee? Mɔna ɛ man ŋolo bee di ŋe dɔɔ ya. Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ ne gyae ɛ ka ko maŋ.

eng: The crowd answered, You have a demon. Who wants to kill you.
local: Botɔɔ te balaŋ dikpii keŋ tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Feliŋkumɛɛ doo n man. Amɔte ne gyae la waa ko neŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, I did one work, and you were all astonished.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Gyakoloŋ nombii dokoloŋ te me yɛɛwɔ ɛ kefɛɛfowee, keŋ na doo na ŋon gyakoloŋ nɛ.

eng: Because Moses gave you the commandment about circumcision , you circumcise a man on the Sabbath.
local: Mosesi yako ŋon nyi ya kara ɛ bia baalawose. , mena dɔɔ ɛ ne kara ɛ bia baalawose ɛ kefɛɛfowee.

eng: If a man can be circumcised on the Sabbath without breaking Moses Law, why are you angry with me because I made an entire man well on the Sabbath?.
local: Nyi akpaa ɛ ne kara ɛ bia baalawose ɛ kefɛɛfo te keŋ bɛɛ wɔlɛɛ ya mmaraa ya na, weera dɔɔ te ɛ na gyeŋ baŋ do maŋ nyi ma te kyɔ walaŋ dibiliŋ kawee kefɛɛfowee?.

eng: Dont judge according to appearances. Judge with right judgment.
local: Ɛ na barase bu balaŋ fɔɔ ya, ɛ di nombia na ŋe gbɛɛ.

eng: Some people from Jerusalem said, Isnt he the one they want to kill?.
local: Keŋte Gyerusalɛmtena baale bɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, Na balee kei te bɛɛ kɛo nyi baa ko nɛ yee?.

eng: Here he is, speaking in public, yet they arent saying anything to him. Could it be that our leaders actually think he is the Christ?.
local: Ɛ kɛɛ ɔ ka seŋ balaŋ man ɔ ne kolosi keŋ ŋolo bɛɛ bɔɔse e daale ya. Yaa nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ kegyiise baŋ te lɛɛ e do nyi ŋon yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon?.

eng: We know where he is from, but when the Christ comes, no one will know where he is from.
local: Balee kei dɔɔ de popou gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ ɔ ba lee. Mɔna akpaa nyi nyeelɛɛre ŋon waa kɔŋ na, ŋolo be gyae waa gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ waa lee ya.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he exclaimed, You know me and where I am from. I havent come on my own. The one who sent me is true, and you dont know him.
local: Yesu kɛɛ wola abɔɔ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ, ɔ gyinaa ɔ woya bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Mena dɔɔ ɛ gyeŋ maŋ nideli na botɔɔ keŋ mɔɔ lee? Na maŋ gbagba me baam dɔɔ te me kɔŋawɔ ya. Walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ ɔ yɛɛ anokoarete e, mɔna ŋon dɔɔ ɛ ba gyeŋ e ya.

eng: I know him because I am from him and he sent me.
local: Mɔna maŋ dɔɔ ma gyeŋ e nawolo ɔ gyaŋ te ma leewɔ te ŋon kpila na maŋ.

eng: So they wanted to seize Jesus, but they couldnt because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Mena dɔɔ be yela baa kyaŋ Yesu, mɔna ŋolo te tale mo ɔ nyiŋmaa tina e ya, nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba see nyi waa yeŋ nɛ te lii ta ya.

eng: Many from that crowd believed in Jesus. They said, When the Christ comes, will he do more miraculous signs than this man does.
local: Mɔna balaŋ burum lɛɛ e di te be bɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, Akpaa nyeelɛɛre ŋon de kɔŋ na, waa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia kela balee kei aneyɛɛŋ kɛŋa.

eng: The Pharisees heard the crowd whispering such things about Jesus, and the chief priests and Pharisees sent guards to arrest him.
local: Farasiitena ke nyii mena keŋ balaŋ ne kolosi nombia kɛŋa adadawoya man lee Yesu wose man nɛ, baŋ na Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise kpila kotɔtɔɔtena baŋ bɛɛ deke Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ nɛ nyi baa gyu kekyaŋ e kɔŋ.

eng: Therefore, Jesus said, Im still with you for a little while before I go to the one who sent me.
local: Keŋte Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, Maa kyaa ɛ gyaŋ bo weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale na maa bese gyu walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ gyaŋ.

eng: You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: Ɛ ke kɛo maŋ mɔna ɛ be gyae ɛ kena maŋ ya, te botɔɔ keŋ me kyaa mɔ nɛ, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale kɔŋ ya.

eng: The Jewish opposition asked each other, Where does he intend to go that we cant find him? Surely he doesnt intend to go where our people have been scattered and are living among the Greeks! He isnt going to teach the Greeks, is he?.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Aleŋ yaa balee kei ne gyae waa yeu gyu te dɔɔ gyae dɛɛ naa e ya? Yaa ɔ ne gyae waa yeu ba gyu Girikitena gyaŋ botɔɔ keŋ de balaŋ be yaasee gyu nɛ ɔ kaa wola wɔ abɔɔ.

eng: What does he mean when he says, You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: Ɔ ke yako nyi, Ɛ ke kɛo maŋ mɔna ɛ be gyae ɛ kena maŋ ya, Te botɔɔ keŋ me gyu nɛ ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale gyu ya nɛ, na sena yaa ɔ ne yako baa? Na woŋ nombia yɛna kɛŋa baa.

eng: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted, All who are thirsty should come to me!.
local: Gyudatena kefɛɛse man kekyaa weekedi ɔtomante keŋ nɛ, Yesu korowɔ seŋ te ɔ gyinaa ɔ nɔɔ kolosi te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Akpaa nyaŋ ŋon loŋboaneŋ dana neŋ na kɔŋ me gyaŋ na n kaa nyɔɔ loŋ.

eng: All who believe in me should drink! As the scriptures said concerning me, Rivers of living water will flow out from within him. .
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ maŋ di na, nyeedoŋ nombia gyae ka lee ɔ man kɔŋ ŋgba mena keŋ loŋ na lee ditiribuŋ man kɔŋ nɛ. Nnɛ te Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see yakowɔ. .

eng: Jesus said this concerning the Spirit. Those who believed in him would soon receive the Spirit, but they hadnt experienced the Spirit yet since Jesus hadnt yet been glorified.
local: Yesu kekolosi kei nɛ, na Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon waa kaa mo do baŋ baa lɛɛ e di nɛ man nombia yaa ɔ ne kolosi nɛ. Debaŋ kenaŋ na ɔ ta moo ta Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon do ŋolo man ya, nawolo nyi Yesu gyoorobiiri keŋ ta lee ta debɔɔ ya.

eng: When some in the crowd heard these words, they said, This man is truly the prophet.
local: Balaŋ baŋ ke nyii Yesu nɔɔwoya kɛŋa nɛ be man baale yakowɔ nyiaa, Ampaŋ, balee kei yɛna Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: Others said, Hes the Christ. But others said, The Christ cant come from Galilee, can he?.
local: Baale mɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Balee kei yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kaa kɔŋ nɛ ampaŋ. Mɔna baale mɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, na Galelia tɛɛle man te nyeelɛɛre ŋon waa lee ya.

eng: Didnt the scripture say that the Christ comes from Davids family and from Bethlehem, Davids village.
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyiaa, Nyeelɛɛre ŋon waa lee bo Bɛtelɛhɛm donɔɔ man botɔɔ keŋ Gyoo Defidi be kyaa nɛ, te waa lee bo gyoo Defidi loŋtoro man.

eng: So the crowd was divided over Jesus.
local: Lee Yesu dɔɔ, balaŋ baŋ man kpasewɔ.

eng: Some wanted to arrest him, but no one grabbed him.
local: Balaŋ baŋ baale gyaewɔ nyi baa kyaŋ Yesu, mɔna ŋolo te tale mo ɔ nyiŋmaa tina e ya.

eng: The guards returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked, Why didnt you bring him.
local: Wɔle wɔle keŋ nɛ, kotɔtɔɔtena baŋ bɛɛ deke Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ te bɔɔ kpila wɔ nɛ besewɔ kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Farasiitena baŋ gyaŋ. Keŋte kegyiise baŋ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Weera dɔɔ te ɛ te kyaŋ e kɔŋ ya.

eng: The guards answered, No one has ever spoken the way he does.
local: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa kegyiise baŋ nyiaa, Ŋolo na ŋolo te kolosi ta see ŋgba balee kei nɛ ya.

eng: Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard that he was making more disciples and baptizing more than John .
local: Farasiitena baŋ nyiiwɔ nyi Yesu ne nyiŋ balaŋ burum sɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ kela Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre.

eng: The Pharisees replied, Have you too been deceived?.
local: Te Farasiitena baŋ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Doona ɔ taŋ beo ɛmɛɛ mɔ?.

eng: although Jesus disciples were baptizing, not Jesus himself.
local: Mɔna Yesu gbagba dɔɔ ɔ te sɔ ŋolo na ŋolo Wurubuarɛ loŋ ya, ɔ kaseela baŋ sɔɔ na wɔ.

eng: Have any of the leaders believed in him? Has any Pharisee?.
local: Ɛ te nyii nyi donɔɔ kegyiise kɛwɔ man ŋolo yaa Farasiitena kɛwɔ man ŋolo te lɛɛ Yesu di?.

eng: Therefore, he left Judea and went back to Galilee.
local: Yesu ke nyii mena keŋ balaŋ ne kolosi ɔ wose man nombia nɛ, ɔ korowɔ lee Gyudia tɛɛle man bese gyu Galelia tɛɛle man.

eng: No, only this crowd, which doesnt know the Law. And they are under Gods curse.
local: Mɔna mena balaŋ burum kɛwɔ baŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ Mosesi mmaraa keŋ ya nɛ, baŋ doola kefaala tɛɛ.

eng: Jesus had to go through Samaria.
local: Na kaboena nyi waa mo na Samaria tɛɛle man.

eng: Nicodemus, who was one of them and had come to Jesus earlier, said.
local: Nikodemose ŋon ɔ be kyaa gyu Yesu gyaŋ nɛ yɛɛ kegyiise baŋ man ŋolo e. Te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa.

eng: He came to a Samaritan city called Sychar, which was near the land Jacob had given to his son Joseph.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔ moowɔ botɔɔ kelii Samariatena donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Sikya nɛ man. Sikya donɔɔ kei benaa na tɛɛle keŋ Gyekɔpo ba mo fa ɔ bu Gyosɛfo nɛ.

eng: Our Law doesnt judge someone without first hearing him and learning what he is doing, does it.
local: Da mmaraa ne fa daa gbɛɛ nyi dɛɛ bu ŋolo fɔɔ keŋ de te nyii ta ɔ nɔɔman nombia yee.

eng: Jacobs well was there. Jesus was tired from his journey, so he sat down at the well. It was about noon.
local: Na Gyekɔpo ditiribuŋ doo botɔɔ. Yesu kelii botɔɔ nɛ, na ɔ te kaa mena dɔɔ ɔ tisiwɔ kyaa ditiribuŋ keŋ gyaŋ. Debaŋ kenaŋ na weese ta seŋ dɛɛ.

eng: They answered him, You are not from Galilee too, are you? Look it up and you will see that the prophet doesnt come from Galilee.
local: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Weera nyaŋ mɔ ne kolosi ŋgba n yɛɛ Galelia baale e nɛ? Nyaŋ kɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man nideli, n kena nyi Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋolo ta lee ta Galelia ya.

eng: A Samaritan woman came to the well to draw water. Jesus said to her, Give me some water to drink.
local: Ɔ ke kyaa botɔɔ nɛ Samaria alo ŋolo kɔŋawɔ botɔɔ ɔ kaa kyee loŋ, keŋte Yesu sola e nyi Fa maŋ loŋ maa nyɔɔ.

eng: They each went to their own homes.
local: Keŋte be popou yaaseewɔ dokoloŋ dokoloŋ gyu be dɛɛse.

eng: His disciples had gone into the city to buy him some food.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na, ɔ kaseela baŋ te lam donɔɔ keŋ man be kaa lɛɛ weenɛɛ.

eng: The Samaritan woman asked, Why do you, a Jewish man, ask for something to drink from me, a Samaritan woman.
local: Botɔɔ te Samaria alo ŋon yako Yesu nyiaa, N yɛɛ Gyuda baale e te maŋ mɔ yɛɛ Samaria alo e. Woŋ dɔɔ te n na sola maŋ loŋ? Keŋ dɔɔ ɔ be bɔɔse mena yɛna nyi, Gyudatena na Samariatena bɛɛ gyoo ya.

eng: Jesus responded, If you recognized Gods gift and who is saying to you, Give me some water to drink, you would be asking him and he would give you living water.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Akpaa nyeŋ gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ kolo anefaŋ na walaŋ ŋon ɔ na sola neŋ loŋ kei nɛ, nafɔ nyaŋ ka sola e na waa fa neŋ loŋ keŋ kɛɛ fa nyeedoŋ nɛ.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, you dont have a bucket and the well is deep. Where would you get this living water?.
local: Botɔɔ te alo ŋon yako e nyiaa, Kegyia, ditiribuŋ keŋ dana siiliŋ te n be dana kpene keŋ n ka mo kyee loŋ keŋ ya. Aleŋ te nyaŋ kenyiŋ mena nyeedoŋ loŋ kei.

eng: You arent greater than our father Jacob, are you? He gave this well to us, and he drank from it himself, as did his sons and his livestock.
local: Yaa nyaŋ kela na de naana Gyekɔpo ŋon ɔ be wula ditiribuŋ kei fa daa nɛ? Ŋon gbagba na ɔ bia na ɔ wonembia pou nyɔɔ loŋ lee ditiribuŋ kei man.

eng: Jesus answered, Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa alo ŋon nyiaa, Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa nyɔɔ loŋ kei nɛ, loŋboaneŋ gyae ka ko e bela.

eng: but whoever drinks from the water that I will give will never be thirsty again. The water that I give will become in those who drink it a spring of water that bubbles up into eternal life.
local: Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa nyɔɔ loŋ keŋ maa fa e nɛ, loŋboaneŋ be gyae ka ko e bela ya. Loŋ keŋ maa fa e nɛ gyae ke doo ɔ man ŋgba ditiribuŋ nɛ, na ka ke fa e nyeedoŋ loŋ na waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaakekpaa.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, give me this water, so that I will never be thirsty and will never need to come here to draw water.
local: Botɔɔ te alo ŋon yako e nyiaa, Kegyia, akpaa mena na fa maŋ mena loŋ kei na loŋboaneŋ ba tekaa ko maŋ bela ya, na maa tina ditiribuŋ kei man kekɔŋ yela.

eng: Jesus said to her, Go, get your husband, and come back here.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, Gyu ke baake n baale na ɛ ke kɔŋ.

eng: The woman replied, I dont have a husband. You are right to say, I dont have a husband, Jesus answered.
local: Te alo ŋon yako e nyiaa, Mɔɔ dana baale ya. Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, N te yako anokoare nyi n be dana baale ya.

eng: Youve had five husbands, and the man you are with now isnt your husband. Youve spoken the truth.
local: N taŋ ke yala baala banoŋ, te ŋon gyaŋ n kyaa debokeiman nɛ ɔ te yɛɛ n baale e ya. Mena dɔɔ nombia ŋan n be yako maŋ nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ.

eng: The woman said, Sir, I see that you are a prophet.
local: Botɔɔ te alo ŋon yako e nyiaa, Kegyia ma te naa nyi n yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e ampaŋ.

eng: Our ancestors worshipped on this mountain, but you and your people say that it is necessary to worship in Jerusalem.
local: De naanaɔ som Wurubuarɛ bo bula kei dɔɔ, mɔna ɛmɛɛ Gyudatena kpa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man te kaboena nyi dɛɛ som Wurubuarɛ.

eng: Jesus said to her, Believe me, woman, the time is coming when you and your people will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, Alo lɛɛ maŋ di, debaŋ daale gyae kekɔŋ keŋ bɔɔ gyae baa tekaa som Wurubuarɛ bula kei dɔɔ yaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man ya.

eng: You and your people worship what you dont know; we worship what we know because salvation is from the Jews.
local: Ɛmɛɛ Samariatena na som Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɛ ba gyeŋ e ya, te daa Gyudatena dɔɔ dɛɛ som Wurubuarɛ ŋon da gyeŋ e, nawolo nyi nyeekelɛɛ gyae ke ta bo Gyudatena dɔɔ kɔŋ.

eng: But the time is coming—and is here!—when true worshippers will worship in spirit and truth. The Father looks for those who worship him this way.
local: Debaŋ daale ne kɔŋ te ke taŋ kekɔŋ, keŋ nyi baŋ bɛɛ som Wurubuarɛ anokoare man nɛ, baa mo Wurubuarɛ Feliŋ na anokoare bo som Wurubuarɛ nawolo nyi mena somra kɛwɔ dinɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ ne gyae.

eng: God is spirit, and it is necessary to worship God in spirit and truth.
local: Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ feliŋ e, mena dɔɔ baŋ baa som e nɛ kaboena nyi baa mo ɔ feliŋ na anokoare som e.

eng: The woman said, I know that the Messiah is coming, the one who is called the Christ. When he comes, he will teach everything to us.
local: Botɔɔ te Samaria alo ŋon yako e nyiaa, Ma gyeŋ nyi Nyeelɛɛre ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Kristo nɛ waa kɔŋ. Ɔ kɔŋ na waa kaa lese kpene kamasɛ man fa daa.

eng: Jesus said to her, I Am—the one who speaks with you.
local: Keŋte Yesu torowɔ yako e nyiaa, Maŋ ŋon mɛɛ kolosi na neŋ nɛ, maŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon.

eng: Just then, Jesus disciples arrived and were shocked that he was talking with a woman. But no one asked, What do you want? or Why are you talking with her.
local: Be kee kolosi kenaŋ nɛ, te Yesu kaseela baŋ leewɔ donɔɔ man kɔŋ. Ba kena ŋgba Yesu na alo ŋon seŋɛɛ bee kolosi nɛ, yɛɛ wɔ gyakoloŋ. Mɔna be man ŋolo te tale bɔɔse alo ŋon nyi woŋ te ɔ ne gyae ya, te ba te tale bɔɔse Yesu mɔ nyi weera ɔ ne kolosi na alo ŋon ya.

eng: The woman put down her water jar and went into the city. She said to the people.
local: Botɔɔ te alo ŋon tina osuŋ keŋ yela, te ɔ yeuwɔ gyu donɔɔ keŋ man ke yako balaŋ nyiaa.

eng: Come and see a man who has told me everything Ive done! Could this man be the Christ.
local: Ɛ kɔŋ kaa kɛɛ balee ŋolo ŋon ɔ be yako maŋ ma wose man nombia ŋan pou mɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ. Na ŋon yɛna Kristo, nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ baa yaa.

eng: They left the city and were on their way to see Jesus.
local: Mena dɔɔ balaŋ leewɔ donɔɔ keŋ man kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ.

eng: In the meantime the disciples spoke to Jesus, saying, Rabbi, eat.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na te ɔ kaseela baŋ waase e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo mo kolo tina n nɔɔ.

eng: Jesus said to them, I have food to eat that you dont know about.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Me dana weenɛɛ ŋaale maa di keŋ ɛ ba gyeŋ ya.

eng: The disciples asked each other, Has someone brought him food.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Doona ŋolo saa weenɛɛ baa kɔna e.

eng: Jesus said to them, I am fed by doing the will of the one who sent me and by completing his work.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Me weenɛɛ yɛna nyi maa yɛɛ me kya ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ kegyaebii, na maa yɛɛ tom keŋ ɔ ba mo fa maŋ nɛ kelii ɔto.

eng: Dont you have a saying, Four more months and then its time for harvest? Look, I tell you: open your eyes and notice that the fields are already ripe for the harvest.
local: Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ne tale yako nyi Te ka gyaalaŋna banaara na weenɛɛ kekɛrɛɛ te lii. Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, ɛ gyina ɛ sia na ɛ ke kɛɛ wɔɔse ŋan. Ɛ kena nyi abɔɔ ŋan te yɛɛ na kekɛrɛɛ.

eng: Those who harvest are receiving their pay and gathering fruit for eternal life so that those who sow and those who harvest can celebrate together.
local: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kɛrɛɛ weenɛɛ ŋan nɛ baa tɔ e kom, te ɔ ne yilaa abɔɔ ŋenaŋ bo see fa nyeedoŋ kekpaakekpaa keŋ. Mena dɔɔ walaŋ ŋon ɔ be fare na walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kɛrɛɛ nɛ pou baa bom di gyoŋ.

eng: This is a true saying, that one sows and another harvests.
local: Dudu keŋ bɔɔ gyɔɔ nyi, Ŋolo ne duula te ŋolo mɔ ne karala nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ.

eng: I have sent you to harvest what you didnt work hard for; others worked hard, and you will share in their hard work.
local: Me kpila ŋon baa nyi ya gyu ke kɛrɛɛ weenɛɛ wɔɔ keŋ ɛ te yɛɛ tom ya nɛ man. Baale yɛɛna tom keŋ, te ɛmɛɛ kɔŋawɔ ɛ ne di ba tom keŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ tɔnɔɔ.

eng: Many Samaritans in that city believed in Jesus because of the womans word when she testified, He told me everything Ive ever done.
local: Balaŋ baŋ be kyaa Samaria donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ, be man burum lɛɛ Yesu di nawolo nyi alo ŋon yakowɔ nyiaa, Ɔ te yako maŋ ma wose man nombia ŋan pou weesɛɛ nɛ.

eng: So when the Samaritans came to Jesus, they asked him to stay with them, and he stayed there two days.
local: Mena dɔɔ Samariatena baŋ kekɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ nɛ, be waase e nyi waa kyaa be gyaŋ, te Yesu dii weeya ala be gyaŋ.

eng: Many more believed because of his word.
local: Yesu nombia ŋan ɔ ba wolo nɛ yela balaŋ burum lɛɛ e di.

eng: and they said to the woman, We no longer believe because of what you said, for we have heard for ourselves and know that this one is truly the savior of the world.
local: Keŋte be yako alo ŋon nyiaa, Nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ nɛ na n nɔɔwoya ŋan dɔɔ te de lɛɛ e di ya, mɔna daa gbagba te nyii e te da te naa nyi ŋon yɛna balaŋ pou nyeelɛɛre gbagba ŋon.

eng: After two days Jesus left for Galilee. .
local: Yesu kedi weeya ala botɔɔ wɔle nɛ, ɔ leewɔ botɔɔ bese gyu Galelia tɛɛle man.

eng: Jesus himself had testified that prophets have no honor in their own country.
local: Nawolo nyi Yesu gbagba yakowɔ nyiaa, Bɔɔ ne lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre kamasɛ do ŋon gbagba ɔ donɔɔ man ya.

eng: When he came to Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him because they had seen all the things he had done in Jerusalem during the festival, for they also had been at the festival.
local: Yesu kekɔŋ kaa gyoo Galelia nɛ, botɔɔtena lɛɛ e nideli, nawolo nyi be naa nombia ŋan pou ɔ be yɛɛ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man debaŋ keŋ bɔɔ gyu be kaa di ba nyeekelɛɛwee keŋ nɛ.

eng: He returned to Cana in Galilee where he had turned the water into wine. In Capernaum there was a certain royal official whose son was sick.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ gyu Kaanan donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ man. Mena donɔɔ kenaŋ man te ɔ besena loŋ yɛɛ soloŋ nɛ. Debaŋ kenaŋ na tomyɛɛre kegyia ŋolo kyaa botɔɔ keŋ ɔ bu na wee Kapanum donɔɔ man.

eng: When he heard that Jesus was coming from Judea to Galilee, he went out to meet him and asked Jesus if he would come and heal his son, for his son was about to die.
local: Tomyɛɛre kegyia ŋon kenyii nyi Yesu ta lee Gyudia kɔŋ Galelia botɔɔ nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ ɔ gyaŋ ke waase e nyi ɔ bu na wee ɔ doo Kapanum donɔɔ man ɔ ne gyae waa yeŋ. Mena dɔɔ waa kɔŋ na ɔ kaa kyɔ e kawee fa e.

eng: Jesus said to him, Unless you see miraculous signs and wonders, you wont believe.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, Na ɛ kena gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana ya nɛ, nafɔ ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ be gyae waa lɛɛ maŋ di ya.

eng: The royal official said to him, Lord, come before my son dies.
local: Keŋte kegyia ŋon yako Yesu nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, waase kɔŋ dee gyu bileŋ na mena ya na me bu ŋon waa yeŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Go home. Your son lives. The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him and set out for his home.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, Ta, n bu ŋon waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ. Balee ŋon lɛɛ Yesu nɔɔwoya ŋan di te ɔ laŋawɔ.

eng: While he was on his way, his servants were already coming to meet him. They said, Your son lives.
local: Balee ŋon keta ɔ ne gyu ɔ dɛɛ nɛ, ɔ gyaŋee na ɔ tewulɛɛ gbɛɛnaa man te be yako e nyiaa, N bu ŋon wose te yɛɛ e doŋ.

eng: So he asked them at what time his son had started to get better. And they said, The fever left him yesterday at about one oclock in the afternoon.
local: Botɔɔ te balee ŋon bɔɔse ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ nyiaa, debaŋ woŋti man te me bu ŋon wose yɛɛ e doŋ? Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa nyiaa, Dana weeseteŋteŋ kegyɔ ŋgba debaŋ dokoloŋ nɛ, te bu ŋon wose yɛɛ e doŋ.

eng: Then the father realized that this was the hour when Jesus had said to him, Your son lives. And he and his entire household believed in Jesus.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kya ŋon tɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, debaŋ kenaŋ man pɛpɛɛpɛ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, N bu ŋon wose gyae ke yɛɛ e doŋ. Mena dɔɔ ŋon na ɔ dekpaŋalaŋmantena pou lɛɛ Yesu di.

eng: This was the second miraculous sign Jesus did while going from Judea to Galilee.
local: Yesu ka lee Gyudia kɔŋ Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ, kei yɛna gyakoloŋ nombia bolate keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ.

eng: And Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Balaŋ baŋ ke yaasee nɛ, Yesu gyuuwɔ oliifi bula keŋ dɔɔ.

eng: Early in the morning he returned to the temple. All the people gathered around him, and he sat down and taught them.
local: Tɛɛ kena nɛ ɔ besewɔ kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man. Balaŋ baŋ pou be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ kaa wuuwɔ ɔ dɔɔ te ɔ tisiwɔ kyaa ɔ na wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia.

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees brought a woman caught in adultery. Placing her in the center of the group.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ moo alo ŋolo ŋon ɔ ba lee ɔ baale wɔle te bɔɔ kyaŋ e nyiŋmaa nyiŋmaa nɛ kɔŋ kaa seŋ Yesu na balaŋ baŋ pou siaman.

eng: they said to Jesus, Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of committing adultery.
local: Keŋte be yako Yesu nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, da te kyaŋ alo kei ɔ ne gyae baala.

eng: In the Law, Moses commanded us to stone women like this. What do you say.
local: Mosesi mmaraa ŋan na wola daa nyi dee fuŋii mena alo kei dinɔɔ boya ko. Te nyaŋ mɔ n kpoo.

eng: They said this to test him, because they wanted a reason to bring an accusation against him. Jesus bent down and wrote on the ground with his finger.
local: Be yako nombia kɛŋa nyi baa mo teese Yesu nɔɔman, na baa nyiŋ mo nombia gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ. Mɔna Yesu gɔŋasewɔ te ɔ moo ɔ nyimbii ŋmarase kolo tɛɛle man.

eng: They continued to question him, so he stood up and replied, Whoever hasnt sinned should throw the first stone.
local: Be kaseŋ bɛɛ daa e nyi waa tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nɛ, ɔ korowɔ seŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Nyaŋ ŋon n gyeŋ nyi n te yɛɛ ta dukum see ya nɛ taŋgbɛɛ mo boe fuŋ e.

eng: Bending down again, he wrote on the ground.
local: Te ɔ besewɔ gɔŋase ŋmarase kolo tɛɛle man.

eng: Those who heard him went away, one by one, beginning with the elders. Finally, only Jesus and the woman were left in the middle of the crowd.
local: Be ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, be yaaseewɔ dokoloŋ dokoloŋ tina e yela. Be man kegyiise baŋ taŋgbɛɛ la lee botɔɔ, te kaa Yesu na alo ŋon na balaŋ dikpii keŋ baageŋ.

eng: Jesus stood up and said to her, Woman, where are they? Is there no one to condemn you.
local: Keŋte Yesu korowɔ seŋ te ɔ bɔɔse alo ŋon nyiaa, Alo nde wɔ? Ŋolo te tale bu neŋ fɔɔ yee.

eng: She said, No one, sir. Jesus said, Neither do I condemn you. Go, and from now on, dont sin anymore.
local: Te alo ŋon tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, ŋolo te bu maŋ fɔɔ ya. Te Yesu yako e nyiaa, Maŋ mɔ be gyae maa bu neŋ fɔɔ ya. Ta, na tekaa yɛɛ dukum bela ya.

eng: Jesus spoke to the people again, saying, I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me wont walk in darkness but will have the light of life.
local: Nombia kɛŋa wɔle nɛ Yesu besewɔ kolosi fa balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, Maŋ yɛna keŋmaŋee fa balaŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ sila maŋ na ɔ sia gyae ke gyuusu na waa naa nyeedoŋ gbɛɛ keŋ. Ɔ be gyae waa ta ditiŋtɛɛ man ya.

eng: Then the Pharisees said to him, Because you are testifying about yourself, your testimony isnt valid.
local: Keŋte Farasiitena baŋ yako e nyiaa, Nyaŋ gbagba ne diina n wose man adansɛɛ, mɔna nya adansɛɛ keŋ betaŋ tɔnɔɔ fa daa ya.

eng: Jesus replied, Even if I testify about myself, my testimony is true, since I know where I came from and where Im going. You dont know where I come from or where Im going.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Nyi akpaa maŋ gbagba de di ma wose man adansɛɛ koraŋ nɛ, ke doo na ke gbɛɛ. Nawolo nyi ma gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ mɔɔ lee na botɔɔ keŋ mee gyu. Ɛmɛɛ ba gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ mɔɔ lee ya, te ɛ ba gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ mee gyu ya.

eng: You judge according to human standards, but I judge no one.
local: Ɛmɛɛ ne bu balaŋ fɔɔ bo lee denibalaŋ nombia kedi gbɛɛ man, mɔna maŋ dɔɔ mɔɔ ne bu ŋolo fɔɔ ya.

eng: Even if I do judge, my judgment is truthful, because Im not alone. My judgments come from me and from the Father who sent me.
local: Mɔna nyi akpaa me yako nyi maa bu ŋolo fɔɔ koraŋ nɛ, ke doo na ke gbɛɛ nawolo nyi na maŋ maageŋ ne buu na fɔɔ keŋ ya. Daa na me kya ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ ne buu na fɔɔ kenaŋ.

eng: In your Law it is written that the witness of two people is true.
local: Ba te ŋmarase do ya mmaraa tɔne man nyiaa, akpaa adansɛɛtena bala nɔɔ de yɛɛ dokoloŋ lee nombii man na, na ke yɛɛ ampaŋ.

eng: I am one witness concerning myself, and the Father who sent me is the other.
local: Maŋ gbagba ne di ma wose man adansɛɛ, te me kya ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ mɔ ne di ma wose man adansɛɛ.

eng: They asked him, Where is your Father? Jesus answered, You dont know me and you dont know my Father. If you knew me, you would also know my Father.
local: Keŋte be bɔɔse e nyiaa, N kya ŋonaŋ kyaa aleŋ? Te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ ba gyeŋ maŋ ya te ɛ ba gyeŋ me kya ŋon mɔ ya. Nyi akpaa yeŋ gyeŋ maŋ nɛ, nafɔ ɛ ka gyeŋ me kya ŋon mɔ.

eng: He spoke these words while he was teaching in the temple area known as the treasury. No one arrested him, because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Debaŋ keŋ Yesu na wola nombia kɛŋa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ, na ɔ seŋɛɛ benaa na adakaase ŋan bɛɛ mo abɔɔ do ŋe man fa Wurubuarɛ nɛ. Mɔna ŋolo te tale kyaŋ e ya, nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba see e nɛ te lii ta ya.

eng: Jesus continued, Im going away. You will look for me, and you will die in your sin. Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Mee gyu baa. Ɛ ke kɛo maŋ, mɔna ɛ ka yeŋ ɛ nombiakumɛɛ man. Ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale gyu botɔɔ keŋ mee gyu nɛ ya.

eng: The Jewish leaders said, He isnt going to kill himself, is he? Is that why he said, Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Mena dɔɔ Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Na ɔ ne gyae waa ko ɔ wose baa yaa sena te ɔ ne yako nyi, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale gyu botɔɔ keŋ mee gyu ya nɛ.

eng: He said to them, You are from below; Im from above. You are from this world; Im not from this world.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ kɛbo, mɔna maŋ dɔɔ ma lee adido. Ɛ yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋwɔ, mɔna maŋ te yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ walaŋ e ya.

eng: This is why I told you that you would die in your sins. If you dont believe that I Am, you will die in your sins.
local: Mena dɔɔ te mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, ɛ ka yeŋ ɛ nombiakumɛɛ man nɛ. Nyi akpaa ɛ bɛɛ ɛ te lɛɛ maŋ di nyi maŋ yɛna ŋon ɔ kyaa pɛte be yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ ya nɛ, ɛ ka yeŋ ɛ nombiakumɛɛ man.

eng: Who are you? they asked. Jesus replied, Im exactly who I have claimed to be from the beginning.
local: Keŋte be bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna amɔte? Te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Maŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋon mɔɔ taŋ wulaa yako ŋon lee kewalaŋ keŋ nɛ.

eng: I have many things to say in judgment concerning you. The one who sent me is true, and what I have heard from him I tell the world.
local: Me dana nombia burum ŋan kaboena nyi maa yako lee ɛ wose man na maa bu ŋon fɔɔ. Mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ ɔ nombia doo na ke gbɛɛ, mena dɔɔ kpene keŋ me nyi lee ɔ gyaŋ na, keŋte mɛɛ yako fa balaŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ.

eng: They didnt know he was speaking about his Father.
local: Mɔna balaŋ baŋ te nyii nombia ŋan man nyi Yesu ne kolosi baa lee ɔ kya Wurubuarɛ wose man ya.

eng: So Jesus said to them, When the Human One is lifted up, then you will know that I Am. Then you will know that I do nothing on my own, but I say just what the Father has taught me.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Nyi akpaa ɛ gyɔ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ mataa adido nɛ, botɔɔ te ɛ kena nyi maŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋon me kpa me yɛɛ nɛ. Botɔɔ te ɛ kena nyiaa mɔɔ ne yɛɛ kolo me baam dɔɔ ya. Mɔna mɛɛ kolosi kpene keŋ me kya ŋon ne yako maŋ nɛ bo.

eng: He who sent me is with me. He doesnt leave me by myself, because I always do what makes him happy.
local: Te ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ ne kpuɛ na maŋ. Ɔ te tinaa ta maŋ yela maageŋ ya, nawolo nyi debaŋ kamasɛ na mɛɛ yɛɛ ɔ kegyaebii bo.

eng: After this Jesus went across the Galilee Sea .
local: Weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale wɔle nɛ, Yesu toŋa Galelia depaa keŋ bɛɛ baake ke bela nyi Tiberiase nɛ gyu ke diŋgyiŋ.

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, many people came to believe in him.
local: Keŋte balaŋ burum baŋ bɔɔ nyii nombia ŋan Yesu ne yako nɛ lɛɛ e di.

eng: A large crowd followed him, because they had seen the miraculous signs he had done among the sick.
local: Balaŋ burum sila ɔ gbɛɛ gyu botɔɔ, nawolo nyi be naa gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana ŋan ɔ be yɛɛ lee mena keŋ ɔ be kyɔ balaŋ kaweese nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: Jesus said to the Jews who believed in him, You are truly my disciples if you remain faithful to my teaching.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu yako Gyudatena baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ e di nɛ nyiaa Akpaa ɛ di kpene keŋ mɛɛ wola ŋon nɛ dɔɔ na, kenaŋ na ɛ yɛɛ me kaseela gbagbawɔ.

eng: Jesus went up a mountain and sat there with his disciples.
local: Keŋte Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyeŋa bula daale kekyaa botɔɔ.

eng: Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.
local: Kei gyae ke yela ɛ ka gyeŋ anokoare keŋ, te anokoare keŋ gyae ke yela ɛ ke nyiŋ ɛ wose.

eng: It was nearly time for Passover, the Jewish festival.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na Gyudatena nyeekelɛɛwee kedi keŋ ta benaa.

eng: They responded, We are Abrahams children; weve never been anyones slaves. How can you say that we will be set free.
local: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ nyiaa, De yɛɛ Aberaham ɔ naanasewɔ, te de ta bese ta ŋolo gbeŋi ya. Sena yaa n ne yako nyi dɛɛ lee gbeŋiri man na dee nyiŋ da wose.

eng: Jesus looked up and saw the large crowd coming toward him. He asked Philip, Where will we buy food to feed these people.
local: Yesu gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ te ɔ naawɔ nyi balaŋ burum ne kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ keŋte ɔ bɔɔse Filipo nyiaa, Aleŋ te dee nyiŋ weenɛɛ lɛɛ fa balaŋ burum kɛwɔ dinɔɔ kedi.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you that everyone who sins is a slave to sin.
local: Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Anokoare yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi nyaŋ ŋon n yɛɛ dukum nɛ, n ta bese gbeŋi fa dukum keŋ.

eng: Jesus said this to test him, for he already knew what he was going to do.
local: Yesu bɔɔse Filipo mena nombia kɛŋa na waa mo kɛɛ wa agyueŋ nawolo nyi ŋon gbagba wulaa ɔ gyeŋ kpene keŋ waa yɛɛ.

eng: A slave isnt a permanent member of the household, but a son is.
local: Gbeŋi bɛɛ bese dekpaŋalaŋ duuluŋ walaŋ ya, mɔna dekpaŋalaŋte bu dɔɔ ɔ yɛɛ dekpaŋalaŋ duuluŋ walaŋ e kekpaa.

eng: Philip replied, More than a half years salary worth of food wouldnt be enough for each person to have even a little bit.
local: Botɔɔ te Filipo tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Pɛ walaŋ kamasɛ kenyiŋ kyomii di koraŋ na, kaboena nyi dɛɛ mo tomyɛɛre kegyia ŋolo gyaalaŋna gyanaara kɔba bo lɛɛ weenɛɛ.

eng: Therefore, if the Son makes you free, you really will be free.
local: Mena dɔɔ nyi maŋ Wurubuarɛ bu de yela n nyiŋ n wose na, n ke nyiŋ n wose kekpaa.

eng: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peters brother, said.
local: Keŋte ɔ kaseela baŋ ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Anderiase, ŋon ɔ kegyia yɛna Simɔn Pita nɛ yakowɔ nyiaa.

eng: I know that you are Abrahams children, yet you want to kill me because you dont welcome my teaching.
local: Ma gyeŋ nyi ɛ yɛɛ Aberaham ɔ naanasewɔ mɔna ɛ ne gyae ɛ ka ko maŋ, nawolo nyi me nombia be doo ɛ konɔɔse man ya.

eng: A youth here has five barley loaves and two fish. But what good is that for a crowd like this.
local: Bu ŋolo kyaa kɛbo keŋ ɔ dana bodobodose anoŋ na kpebia bala. Mɔna woŋ te kɛŋa gyae keyɛɛ balaŋ burum kɛwɔ.

eng: Im telling you what Ive seen when I am with the Father, but you are doing what youve heard from your father.
local: Nombia ŋan me kya ba wola maŋ nɛ ŋan te mee kolosi fa ŋon, te ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ ŋan ɛ be nyii lee ɛ kya gyaŋ nɛ ŋan te ɛ ne yɛɛ.

eng: Jesus said, Have the people sit down. There was plenty of grass there. They sat down, about five thousand of them.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ yela balaŋ baŋ pou ke tisi kyakee. Na ŋalese murumuru ŋaale kyaa botɔɔ, mena dɔɔ balaŋ baŋ pou tisiwɔ kyakee botɔɔ. Baala baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ baa yɛɛ ŋgba balaŋ kakpoŋse banoŋ nɛ.

eng: They replied, Our father is Abraham. Jesus responded, If you were Abrahams children, you would do Abrahams works.
local: Botɔɔ te be tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Aberaham yɛna de kya. Te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Akpaa ɛ yɛɛ Aberaham biawɔ nɛ, nafɔ ɛ keyɛɛ abɔɔ ŋan Aberaham be yɛɛ nɛ.

eng: Then Jesus took the bread. When he had given thanks, he distributed it to those who were sitting there. He did the same with the fish, each getting as much as they wanted.
local: Keŋte Yesu moo bodobodose ŋan te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ, te ɔ moo ŋa kpɛlɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ. Mena ke te ɔ moo kpebia ŋan mɔ te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ te ɔ kpɛlɛɛ ŋa fa balaŋ baŋ pou. Balaŋ baŋ diiwɔ baa ba kegyaebii.

eng: Instead, you want to kill me, though I am the one who has spoken the truth I heard from God. Abraham didnt do this.
local: Mɔna ɛ te yase sia nyi ɛ ka ko maŋ nawolo nyi ma te yako ŋon anokoare keŋ mɔɔ nyii lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ nɛ. Aberaham te yɛɛ mena nombia kɛŋa dinɔɔ ya.

eng: When they had plenty to eat, he said to his disciples, Gather up the leftover pieces, so that nothing will be wasted.
local: Balaŋ baŋ pou kedi baa nɛ Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ tɔɔsee weenɛɛ akaŋ ŋan pou, ɛ na yeli ŋa kewɔlɛɛ ya.

eng: You are doing your fathers works. They said, Our ancestry isnt in question! The only Father we have is God.
local: Ɛ ne yɛɛ kpene keŋ ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ kya ne yɛɛ nɛ bo. Te be lɛɛ ka aŋmaareŋ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ waageŋ yɛna de kya, da te yɛɛ diidɔɔre bia ya.

eng: So they gathered them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces of the five barley loaves that had been left over by those who had eaten.
local: Weenɛɛ ŋan balaŋ baŋ be dii kaase nɛ, be tɔɔsee ŋa buruburu akaŋ ŋan na be nyiŋa doŋase kufu ala.

eng: Jesus replied, If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God. Here I am. I havent come on my own. God sent me.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Nyi akpaa ɛ kya yɛna Wurubuarɛ nɛ nafɔ ɛ ke gyae maŋ, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ ŋonaŋ gyaŋ te ma leewɔ kɔŋ kɛbo. Na maŋ gbagba me baam dɔɔ te me kɔŋawɔ ya. Wurubuarɛ ŋonaŋ gbagba kpila na maŋ.

eng: When the people saw that he had done a miraculous sign, they said, This is truly the prophet who is coming into the world.
local: Balaŋ baŋ kena gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan Yesu be yɛɛ nɛ be yakowɔ nyiaa, Kei yɛna Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋon ɔ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ ampaŋ.

eng: Why dont you understand what Im saying? Its because you cant really hear my words.
local: Weera dɔɔ te ɛ be nyii me nombia ŋan man ya? Nawolo nyi ɛ bɛɛ gyae ɛ ke nyii me nombia ya.

eng: Jesus understood that they were about to come and force him to be their king, so he took refuge again, alone on a mountain.
local: Na Yesu taŋ kena nyi balaŋ baŋ ne gyae be kaa kyaŋ e, na baa ŋere e mo e see be gyoo mena dɔɔ ŋon waageŋ wɔŋa ɔ wose lee be man gyu ke kyaa bula keŋ dɔɔ bela.

eng: Your father is the devil. You are his children, and you want to do what your father wants. He was a murderer from the beginning. He has never stood for the truth, because theres no truth in him. Whenever that liar speaks, he speaks according to his own nature, because hes a liar and the father of liars.
local: Ɛ kya yɛna ɔbɔnsam te ɛ kya ŋonaŋ kegyaebii te ɛ ne gyae ɛ ke yɛɛ. Ɔ yɛɛ awudiini e leenaŋ gyaŋgba gyaŋgba keŋ, te ɔ bɛɛ gyae anokoare ya nawolo nyi anokoare be doo ɔ man ya. Akpaa ɔ ne di dekara nɛ ke yɛɛ ŋon gbagba dinɔɔ ke, nawolo nyi akɛrɛɛ te wulu ɔ man te ŋon yɛna akɛrɛɛ pou nyee.

eng: When evening came, Jesus disciples went down to the lake.
local: Kelii balinɔɔ nɛ Yesu kaseela baŋ besewɔ gyu depaa keŋ nɔɔman.

eng: Because I speak the truth, you dont believe me.
local: Mɔna maŋ dɔɔ nombia ŋan ŋe doo na ŋe gbɛɛ nɛ te mɛɛ yako ŋon, mena dɔɔ te ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ di ya.

eng: They got into a boat and were crossing the lake to Capernaum. It was already getting dark and Jesus hadnt come to them yet.
local: keŋte ba gyoowɔ degbele daale man te ba moo ba nyee baŋ bee gyu Kapanum donɔɔ man. Debaŋ kenaŋ na tɛɛ te biri mɔna Yesu sɛɛ ɔ te kɔŋa ta be gyaŋ ya.

eng: Who among you can show Im guilty of sin? Since I speak the truth, why dont you believe me?.
local: Ɛ man woŋti ne gyae la waa tale wola nyi nombiikum kei te me yɛɛwɔ? Te akpaa mɛɛ yako ŋon anokoare na, woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ di ya?.

eng: The water was getting rough because a strong wind was blowing.
local: Te feliŋ dinaa daale korowɔ ne gyɔɔ loŋ keŋ dɔɔ keŋ te yɛɛ nsendee ya.

eng: Gods children listen to Gods words. You dont listen to me because you arent Gods children.
local: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ walaŋ e nɛ, ɔ ne nyii Wurubuarɛ dei. Kpene keŋ dɔɔ te ɛ bee nyii Wurubuarɛ dei yɛna nyi, ɛ te yɛɛ ɔ balaŋwɔ ya.

eng: When the wind had driven them out for about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He was approaching the boat and they were afraid.
local: Ɔ kaseela baŋ kesaa degbele keŋ gyu siaman keŋ gyae keyɛɛ ŋgba gbɛɛkemaa atooro na yale nɛ, be naa Yesu dɔŋɛɛ loŋ keŋ dɔɔ ɔ ne kɔŋ ba degbele keŋ gyaŋ, keŋte gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ.

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and have a demon, werent we.
local: Keŋte Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, De yako nyi n yɛɛ Samaria baale e te feliŋkum doo n man na, de karae.

eng: He said to them, I Am. Dont be afraid.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ na yee ya, maŋ ŋonaŋ.

eng: I dont have a demon, Jesus replied. But I honor my Father and you dishonor me.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Feliŋkum be doo me man ya. Mɛɛ lese me kya ŋon yele bo, mɔna ɛmɛɛ bee bu maŋ ya.

eng: Then they wanted to take him into the boat, and just then the boat reached the land where they had been heading.
local: Botɔɔ te be lɛɛ Yesu gyoŋ man do ba degbele keŋ man, debokenaŋ man te be kelii botɔɔ keŋ bee gyu nɛ.

eng: Im not trying to bring glory to myself. Theres one who is seeking to glorify me, and hes the judge.
local: Mɔɔ ne gyae maa lese maŋ gbagba yele ya, mɔna ŋolo kyaa keŋ waa lese ma yele. Ŋon yɛna nombiadiiri ŋon.

eng: The next day the crowd that remained on the other side of the lake realized that only one boat had been there. They knew Jesus hadnt gone with his disciples, but that the disciples had gone alone.
local: Tɛɛ kena nɛ, balaŋ burum baŋ bɔɔ kaa depaa keŋ diŋgyiŋ nɛ naawɔ nyi degbele dokoloŋ doo la botɔɔ. Te ba gyeŋ mɔ nyi Yesu ta gyoo degbele kenaŋ man kpu na ɔ kaseela baŋ ya. Ɔ kaseela baŋ baageŋ gyoola ke man laŋ.

eng: I assure you that whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ di me nombia dɔɔ nɛ ɔ be gyae waa yeŋ ya.

eng: Some boats came from Tiberias, near the place where they had eaten the bread over which the Lord had given thanks.
local: Agbelese ŋaale mɔ lee Tiberiase donɔɔ man kɔŋ diŋgyiŋ botɔɔ, benaa na botɔɔ keŋ Yesu ba mo bodobodo fane fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ te ɔ ba mo fa balaŋ burum baŋ be dii nɛ.

eng: The Jewish opposition said to Jesus, Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham and the prophets died, yet you say, Whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: Keŋte Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ yako e nyiaa, Nɛnɛɛ da te naa gyeŋ nyi feliŋkum doo n man. Aberaham ta yem mena mɔ te Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ mɔ yekeewɔ. Mɔna nyaŋ ne yako nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ di n nombia dɔɔ na ɔ be gyae waa yeŋ ya.

eng: When the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and came to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
local: Balaŋ burum baŋ kena nyi Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ ya nɛ, ba gyoowɔ agbelese ŋenaŋ man gyu Kapanum donɔɔ man bɛɛ kɛo e.

eng: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died and the prophets died, so who do you make yourself out to be.
local: Nyaŋ kela na de naana Aberaham ŋon? Aberaham ta yem mena mɔ te Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ mɔ yekeewɔ. N ne yako baa nyi nyaŋ yɛna amɔte.

eng: When they found him on the other side of the lake, they asked him, Rabbi, when did you get here.
local: Balaŋ baŋ ke gyu kena Yesu boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ nɛ be bɔɔse e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, debaŋ woŋti yaa n taŋ kaa lii kɛbo.

eng: Jesus answered, If I glorify myself, my glory is meaningless. My Father, who you say is your God, is the one who glorifies me.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Akpaa ma lese maŋ gbagba yele nɛ, ma yele kalese keŋ yɛɛ yakaa. Me kya ŋon ɛmɛɛ kpa ŋon yɛna ɛ Wurubuarɛ nɛ na lesena ma yele.

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that you are looking for me not because you saw miraculous signs but because you ate all the food you wanted.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, na gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan ɛ be naa mɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ te ɛ silana me gbɛɛ ya, mɔna weenɛɛ ŋan ɛ be nyim di baa ɛ kegyaebii nɛ dɔɔ te ɛ silana me gbɛɛ.

eng: You dont know him, but I do. If I said I didnt know him, I would be like you, a liar. But I do know him, and I keep his word.
local: Ɛmɛɛ ba gyeŋ e ya, mɔna maŋ dɔɔ ma gyeŋ e. Nyi akpaa me yako ŋon nyi mɔɔ gyeŋ e ya nɛ na me ta bese dekarate ŋgba ɛmɛɛ nɛ, mɔna ma gyeŋ e te mee dii ɔ nombia dɔɔ.

eng: Dont work for the food that doesnt last but for the food that endures for eternal life, which the Human One will give you. God the Father has confirmed him as his agent to give life.
local: Ɛ na naase ɛ wose diyem gyae weenɛɛ ŋan gyae kewɔlɛɛ nɛ ya, mɔna ɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ gyae weenɛɛ ŋan gyae kefa ŋon nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ nɛ. Weenɛɛ kɛŋa te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa fa ŋon, nawolo nyi me kya Wurubuarɛ ta mo ɔ doŋ do me man keŋ nawolo nyi ɔ te lɛɛ maŋ do.

eng: Your father Abraham was overjoyed that he would see my day. He saw it and was happy.
local: Yɛɛ ɛ kya Aberaham gyoŋ ŋgba ɔ be naa ma kekɔŋ nɛ. Ɔ naa ke te yɛɛ e gyoŋ.

eng: They asked, What must we do in order to accomplish what God requires.
local: Keŋte be bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, Woŋ te kaboena nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ, na dɛɛ tale yɛɛ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne gyae nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ.

eng: You arent even years old! the Jewish opposition replied. How can you say that you have seen Abraham.
local: Keŋte Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, N te dii ta kulutooneŋ sɔŋola na kufu gba ya, sena te n yɛɛwɔ naa Aberaham.

eng: Jesus replied, This is what God requires, that you believe in him whom God sent.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Tom keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne gyae nyi yaa yɛɛ yɛna nyiaa, ɛ lɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kpila e nɛ di.

eng: I assure you, Jesus replied, before Abraham was, I Am.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, pɛ baa lola Aberaham nɛ na maŋ kyaa.

eng: They asked, What miraculous sign will you do, that we can see and believe you? What will you do?.
local: Keŋte be bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, Woŋ gyakoloŋ nombia te nyaŋ ke yɛɛ wola daa na dɛɛ lɛɛ neŋ di nyi Wurubuarɛ kpila na neŋ? Woŋ te nyaŋ ke yɛɛ?.

eng: So they picked up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and left the temple.
local: Botɔɔ te be gate boya baa fuŋii e, mɔna Yesu sarakeewɔ lee Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man.

eng: Our ancestors ate manna in the wilderness, just as it is written, He gave them bread from heaven to eat .
local: De naanaɔ dii weenɛɛ ŋan bɛɛ baake ŋa nyi maana nɛ depampaa keŋ man, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako daa nɛ nyiaa, Ɔ faa wɔ weenɛɛ lee adido te be diiwɔ. .

eng: Jesus told them, I assure you, it wasnt Moses who gave the bread from heaven to you, but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, na Mosesi faa na ŋon weenɛɛ lee adido ya, mɔna me kya yɛna walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne fa weenɛɛ gbagba ŋan ba lee adido nɛ.

eng: The bread of God is the one who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.
local: Nawolo nyi weenɛɛ ŋan me kya Wurubuarɛ ne faa nɛ yɛna ŋon ɔ ba lee adido kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, te ɔ ne fa balaŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa nɛ.

eng: They said, Sir, give us this bread all the time.
local: Keŋte be yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, akpaa mena na lee gyɛŋ ne gyu na fa daa weenɛɛ kɛŋa wee kamasɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never go hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.
local: Te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Maŋ yɛna nyeedoŋ weenɛɛ ŋan. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ kɔŋ me gyaŋ nɛ, tanam be gyae ka ko e ya. Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ maŋ di na, loŋboaneŋ be gyae ka ko e ya.

eng: But I told you that you have seen me and still dont believe.
local: Ŋgba mena keŋ mɔ taŋ yako ŋon kaalaŋ nɛ, ɛ te naa maŋ mɔna ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ di ya.

eng: Everyone whom the Father gives to me will come to me, and I wont send away anyone who comes to me.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon me kya ba mo e fa maŋ nɛ, waa kɔŋ me gyaŋ. Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ kɔŋ me gyaŋ nɛ, mɔɔ gyae maa gegi e ya.

eng: I have come down from heaven not to do my will, but the will of him who sent me.
local: Nawolo nyi me ta lee adido kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyi me kaa yɛɛ maŋ gbagba kegyaebii ya, mɔna me kɔŋae me kaa yɛɛ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ kegyaebii.

eng: This is the will of the one who sent me, that I wont lose anything he has given me, but I will raise it up at the last day.
local: Ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ kegyaebii yɛna nyiaa, balaŋ baŋ pou ɔ ba mo do maŋ me nyiŋmaa man nɛ, me na yela be man dokoloŋ ka yo ya. Mɔna wee ɔtomante keŋ man nɛ, maa gyuusu wɔ pou lee yeŋ man kɔŋ nyeedoŋ man.

eng: This is my Fathers will: that all who see the Son and believe in him will have eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Nawolo nyi me kya ŋon kegyaebii yɛna nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ mo ɔ loo gyakaa me dɔɔ te ɔ lɛɛ maŋ di na, waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ. Te wee ɔtomante keŋ man nɛ, maa gyuusu mena walaŋ ŋonaŋ lee yeŋ man kɔŋ nyeedoŋ man.

eng: The Jewish opposition grumbled about him because he said, I am the bread that came down from heaven.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena baŋ fiasɛɛ bɛɛ ŋorokoso lee Yesu wose man nawolo nyi ɔ kpa, ŋon yɛna weenɛɛ ŋan ba lee adido kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: They asked, Isnt this Jesus, Josephs son, whose mother and father we know? How can he now say, I have come down from heaven.
local: Keŋte be yakowɔ nyiaa, Na kei yɛna Gyosɛfo bu ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Yesu nɛ yee? Da gyeŋ ɔ kya na ɔ naa. Woŋ dɔɔ te nɛnɛɛ ɔ ne yako daa nyi ɔ lee adido bo kɔŋ.

eng: Jesus responded, Dont grumble among yourselves.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ na ŋorokoso na dɔŋa ya.

eng: No one can come to me unless they are drawn to me by the Father who sent me, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Nyi akpaa me kya ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ te wɔŋ ŋolo benaa na maŋ ya nɛ, mena walaŋ ŋonaŋ be gyae waa tale kɔŋ me gyaŋ ya. Te wee ɔtomante keŋ nɛ, maa gyuusu mena walaŋ ŋonaŋ lee yeŋ man na waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.

eng: It is written in the Prophets, And they will all be taught by God. Everyone who has listened to the Father and learned from him comes to me.
local: Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ man ŋolo ŋmarasewɔ see nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ ne gyaela waa wola walaŋ kamasɛ nombia. Mena dɔɔ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ tei me kya Wurubuarɛ nombia te ɔ kasee ŋa nɛ, waa kɔŋ me gyaŋ.

eng: No one has seen the Father except the one who is from God. He has seen the Father.
local: Kei bɛɛ wolo nyi ŋolo te naa me kya ŋon ya. Walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ kɔŋ nɛ, ŋon waageŋ naa na e.

eng: I assure you, whoever believes has eternal life.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, walaŋ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ maŋ di na waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaakekpaa.

eng: I am the bread of life.
local: Maŋ yɛna nyeedoŋ weenɛɛ ŋan.

eng: Your ancestors ate manna in the wilderness and they died.
local: Ɛ naanaɔ dii weenɛɛ ŋan bɛɛ baake ŋa nyi maana nɛ depampaa keŋ man, mɔna ba yekeewɔ.

eng: This is the bread that comes down from heaven so that whoever eats from it will never die.
local: Mɔna nyeedoŋ weenɛɛ ŋan ba lee Wurubuarɛ man kɔŋ te mɛɛ yako ŋon ŋa wose man nombia nɛ dɔɔ, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ di ke na ɔ be gyae waa yeŋ ya.

eng: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. Whoever eats this bread will live forever, and the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.
local: Maŋ yɛna nyeedoŋ weenɛɛ ŋan ba lee adido kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ di mena weenɛɛ kɛŋa na, waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaakekpaa. Weenɛɛ ŋan maa mo fa balaŋ na baa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ nɛ yɛna ma wosenaane.

eng: Then the Jews debated among themselves, asking, How can this man give us his flesh to eat.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena baŋ gyoo aŋmaareŋ na dɔŋa nyiaa, Sena te balee kei waa tale mo ɔ wosenaane fa daa na dee di.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you, unless you eat the flesh of the Human One and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, nyi akpaa ɛ te di maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu wosenaane na ɛ kenyɔɔ me fatabo ya nɛ, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ ya.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa di ma wosenaane na waa nyɔɔ me fatabo nɛ, waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ. Wee ɔtomante keŋ man nɛ, maa gyuusu mena walaŋ ŋonaŋ lee yeŋ man.

eng: My flesh is true food and my blood is true drink.
local: Nawolo nyi ma wosenaane yɛna weenɛɛ anediŋ gbagba, te me fatabo mɔ yɛna kolo anenyɔɔŋ gbagba.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me and I in them.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ di ma wosenaane te ɔ nyɔɔ me fatabo na, daa na e ta bese walaŋ dokoloŋ.

eng: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever eats me lives because of me.
local: Me kya ŋon ɔ ne fa balaŋ nyeedoŋ nɛ kpila na maŋ, te wa adaworoma dɔɔ te me dana nyeedoŋ. Mena ke mɔ te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ di ma wosenaane na, maa fa e nyeedoŋ.

eng: This is the bread that came down from heaven. It isnt like the bread your ancestors ate, and then they died. Whoever eats this bread will live forever.
local: Maŋ yɛna weenɛɛ ŋan ba lee adido kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ. Ma te yɛɛ ŋgba weenɛɛ ŋan ɛ naanaɔ be dii te bɔɔ yekee nɛ ya. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon wa di mena weenɛɛ kɛŋa nɛ, waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaakekpaa.

eng: Jesus said these things while he was teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.
local: Debaŋ keŋ Yesu na wolo Gyudatena ɔsom deni man Kapanum donɔɔ man nɛ, te ɔ yako mena nombia kɛŋa.

eng: Many of his disciples who heard this said, This message is harsh. Who can hear it.
local: Yesu kaseela baŋ kenyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ be yakowɔ nyiaa, Mena kawola kɛŋa man dana doŋ. Amɔte ne gyaela waa tale lɛɛ ŋa do.

eng: Jesus knew that the disciples were grumbling about this and he said to them, Does this offend you?.
local: Yesu ka gyeŋ nyi ɔ kaseela baŋ na ŋorokoso lee nombia kɛŋa wose man nɛ ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Nombia kɛŋa ne takaa ŋon?.

eng: What if you were to see the Human One going up where he was before?.
local: Kenaŋ na akpaa ɛ naa maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon na gyem mee gyu adido, botɔɔ keŋ mɔɔ lee na sena te ɛ ke yɛɛ?.

eng: The Spirit is the one who gives life and the flesh doesnt help at all. The words I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
local: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ne faa na balaŋ nyeedoŋ. Kpene keŋ denibalaŋ na mo baŋ gbagba be doŋ yɛɛ nɛ be taŋ tɔnɔɔ ya. Nombia ŋan mɔɔ yako ŋon nɛ lee Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon man te ŋɛɛ fa nyeedoŋ.

eng: Yet some of you dont believe.Jesus knew from the beginning who wouldnt believe and the one who would betray him.
local: Mɔna ɛ man baale bɛɛ lɛɛ di ya. Yesu yako nombia kɛŋa nawolo nyi lee kewalaŋ keŋ na, ɔ wulaa ɔ gyeŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyae baa lɛɛ e di ya, na ŋon waa kaa lese e fa.

eng: He said,For this reason I said to you that none can come to me unless the Father enables them to do so.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Kei dɔɔ te me yako ŋon nyiaa, akpaa me kya te fa ŋolo gbɛɛ nyi waa kɔŋ me gyaŋ ya na, ɔ be gyae waa tale kɔŋ ya nɛ.

eng: At this, many of his disciples turned away and no longer accompanied him.
local: Kei wɔle nɛ, ɔ silala baŋ man balaŋ burum wɔŋa ba wose lee wɔle keŋ be te sila e bela ya.

eng: Jesus asked the Twelve, Do you also want to leave.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ mɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ mɔ ne gyae ɛ ka bese ɛ wɔle yaa.

eng: Simon Peter answered, Lord, where would we go? You have the words of eternal life.
local: Botɔɔ te Simɔn Pita tiranɔɔ nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, amɔte gyaŋ te dee gyu? Nyaŋ nyaageŋ danaana nombia ŋan ŋɛɛ fa nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ.

eng: We believe and know that you are Gods holy one.
local: Nɛnɛɛ da te lɛɛ neŋ di, te de ta gyem nyi nyaŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ be kpili te ayimɛɛ be doo ɔ man ya nɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I choose you twelve? Yet one of you is a devil.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Na ɛmɛɛ balaŋ kufu bala te me baakewɔ yee? Mɔna ɛ man ŋolo yɛɛ walaŋkum e.

eng: He was speaking of Judas, Simon Iscariots son, for he, one of the Twelve, was going to betray him.
local: Na Simɔn Isekarɔte bu Gyudase wose man nombia yaa Yesu ne yako nɛ. Nawolo nyi Gyudase yɛɛ ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ man ŋolo e, mɔna ŋon ne gyae la ɔ kaa lese Yesu fa yeŋ.

eng: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who was blind from birth.
local: Yesu keta ɔ ne lam nɛ ɔ naa balee ŋolo ŋon ɔ sia be yɛlɛɛ leenaŋ ɔ biatɛɛ nɛ.

eng: Jesus disciples asked, Rabbi, who sinned so that he was born blind, this man or his parents.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, amɔte dukum faa la te ba lola e siayɛlɛɛsate? Yɛɛ ŋon gbagba wui yaa ɔ lolala wui.

eng: Jesus answered, Neither he nor his parents. This happened so that Gods mighty works might be displayed in him.
local: Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɔ sia keyɛlɛɛ te yɛɛ ɔ dukum yaa ɔ lolala wui ya. Ba lola e bo mena na balaŋ kena gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ waa yɛɛ ɔ man.

eng: While its daytime, we must do the works of him who sent me. Night is coming when no one can work.
local: Weese nyiŋa ɔ kyaa nɛ, kaboena nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ tom, Tɛɛ gyae ke yuu keŋ ŋolo be gyae waa tale yɛɛ tom bela ya.

eng: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
local: Me nyiŋa me kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, maŋ yɛna keŋmaŋee fa tɛɛle kei balaŋ.

eng: After he said this, he spit on the ground, made mud with the saliva, and smeared the mud on the mans eyes.
local: Ɔ keyako nombia kɛŋa taŋ nɛ, Yesu tɔɔ nɔɔloŋ lɔ tɛɛle man te ɔ buu tɛɛle te ɔ moowɔ taa balee ŋon sia ŋan dɔɔ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam . So the man went away and washed. When he returned, he could see.
local: keŋte ɔ yako e nyiaa, Gyu kefaafo n sia depaa keŋ bɛɛ baake ke Siloam nɛ man. Mena dɔɔ balee ŋon gyuuwɔ kefaafo ɔ sia, ɔ ka bese kɔŋ dɛɛ na ɔ sia te gyuusu.

eng: The mans neighbors and those who used to see him when he was a beggar said, Isnt this the man who used to sit and beg.
local: Ɔ tebia na balaŋ baŋ ba gyeŋ e nyi nafɔ ɔ kyaa na ɔ na solee abɔɔ nɛ bɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, Na balee kei ne kyaa la kyaa solee abɔɔ nɛ yee.

eng: Some said, It is, and others said, No, its someone who looks like him. But the man said, Yes, its me.
local: Botɔɔ te baale kpa Ŋon ŋonaŋ mɔna baale mɔ kpa Koa, na ŋon ŋonaŋ ya, ɔ lee e bo. Mena dɔɔ balee ŋon gbagba yakowɔ nyiaa, Maŋ ŋonaŋ.

eng: So they asked him, How are you now able to see.
local: Te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, Sena yaa n yɛɛwɔ te n sia gyuusuwɔ.

eng: He answered, The man they call Jesus made mud, smeared it on my eyes, and said, Go to the pool of Siloam and wash. So I went and washed, and then I could see.
local: Keŋte ɔ tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Balee ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Yesu nɛ, buuna tɛɛle te ɔ moowɔ mataa me sia te ɔ yako maŋ nyi maa gyu Siloam depaa keŋ man kefaafo me sia. Mena dɔɔ me gyuuwɔ, te me kabetaa faafo me sia na te ŋe gyuusuwɔ.

eng: They asked, Where is this man? He replied, I dont know.
local: Keŋte be bɔɔse e nyiaa, Ɔ kyaa aleŋ? Te ɔ tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyi Mɔɔ gyeŋ ya.

eng: Then they led the man who had been born blind to the Pharisees.
local: Te ba moo balee ŋon nafɔ ɔ sia be yɛlɛɛ nɛ kɔŋ Farasiitena gyaŋ.

eng: Now Jesus made the mud and smeared it on the mans eyes on a Sabbath day.
local: Wee kenaŋ keŋ Yesu be buu tɛɛle mo kyɔ siayɛlɛɛsate ŋon nɛ yɛɛ Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee ke.

eng: So Pharisees also asked him how he was able to see. The man told them, He put mud on my eyes, I washed, and now I see.
local: Mena dɔɔ Farasiitena baŋ besewɔ bɔɔse balee ŋon bela mena keŋ be yɛɛ te ɔ sia ŋan be gyuusu. Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyi, Ɔ buu tɛɛle bo taa me sia, te me gyuuwɔ kefaafo te ŋe gyuusuwɔ.

eng: Some Pharisees said, This man isnt from God, because he breaks the Sabbath law. Others said, How can a sinner do miraculous signs like these? So they were divided.
local: Botɔɔ te Farasiitena baŋ baale yakowɔ nyi Balee ŋon ɔ be yɛɛ nombii kei nɛ ta lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya, nawolo nyi ɔ bee di da kefɛɛfowee keŋ dɔɔ ya. Mɔna baale mɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Sena te walaŋ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛre e nɛ, waa tale yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia kɛŋa? Botɔɔ na te be man kpasewɔ.

eng: Some of the Pharisees questioned the man who had been born blind again: What do you have to say about him, since he healed your eyes? He replied, Hes a prophet.
local: Mena dɔɔ Farasiitena baŋ besewɔ bɔɔse balee ŋon bela nyiaa, N sia te ɔ gyuusuwɔ. Mena dɔɔ sena te n ne yako lee ɔ wose man? Keŋte balee ŋon tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e.

eng: The Jewish leaders didnt believe the man had been blind and received his sight until they called for his parents.
local: Kɛŋa pou wɔle nɛ Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ te sɛɛ lɛɛ di nyi ɔ sia yɛlɛɛwɔ te nɛnɛɛ ba te kyɔ e kawee ya, te ba ke baake ɔ kya na ɔ naa kɔŋ.

eng: The Jewish leaders asked them, Is this your son? Are you saying he was born blind? How can he now see.
local: te be bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ bu yɛna kei? Ɛ ne yako baa nyi ba lola e na ɔ sia te yɛlɛɛ? Sena kɔŋala te nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ ɔ ne naa.

eng: His parents answered, We know he is our son. We know he was born blind.
local: Keŋte ɔ kya na ɔ naa tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Da gyeŋ nyi de bu ŋonaŋ, te da gyeŋ nyi da lola e na ɔ sia te yɛlɛɛ.

eng: But we dont know how he now sees, and we dont know who healed his eyes. Ask him. Hes old enough to speak for himself.
local: Mɔna dɔɔ gyeŋ mena keŋ be kɔŋ te ɔ ne naa nɛ ya, te dɔɔ gyeŋ ŋon ɔ be kyɔ e ya. Ɛ bɔɔse e, ɔ ta bee keŋ waa tale tiranɔɔ fa ɔ wose.

eng: His parents said this because they feared the Jewish authorities. This is because the Jewish authorities had already decided that whoever confessed Jesus to be the Christ would be expelled from the synagogue.
local: Ɔ kya na ɔ naa yako nombia kɛŋa nawolo nyi bee yee Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ. Na Gyudatena baŋ taŋ kaŋ ntam nyi nyaŋ ŋon n ka koro yako nyi Yesu yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon na baa gegi neŋ lee bɔ ɔsom deni man.

eng: Thats why his parents said, Hes old enough. Ask him.
local: Mena dɔɔ te balee ŋon ɔ kya na ɔ naa yakowɔ nyi, Ɔ ta bee, ɛ bɔɔse e nɛ.

eng: Therefore, they called a second time for the man who had been born blind and said to him, Give glory to God. We know this man is a sinner.
local: Te ba besewɔ baake balee ŋon nafɔ ɔ sia be yɛlɛɛ nɛ kɔŋ te be yako e nyiaa, Kaŋ Wurubuarɛ nyi nyaŋ keyako anokoare. Da gyeŋ nyi balee ŋon ɔ be kyɔ neŋ nɛ yɛɛ dukumyɛɛre e. Yako mena na n ka mo lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

eng: The man answered, I dont know whether hes a sinner. Heres what I do know: I was blind and now I see.
local: Keŋte balee ŋon tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɔ yɛɛ dukumyɛɛre o, ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ kpaakpaa e o maŋ ba gyeŋ ya. Keŋ maŋ gyeŋ yɛna nyi nafɔ na me sia te yɛlɛɛ te nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ mɛɛ naa.

eng: They questioned him: What did he do to you? How did he heal your eyes.
local: Keŋte ba besewɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, Woŋ te ɔ yɛɛ neŋ te n sia gyuusuwɔ.

eng: He replied, I already told you, and you didnt listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you want to become his disciples too.
local: Te ɔ tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Me taŋ keyako ŋon, te ɛ bɛɛ gyae ɛ ke tei ya. Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ ne gyae ɛ kenyii ke bela? Ɛmɛɛ mɔ ne gyae ɛ ka bese ɔ kaseela.

eng: They insulted him: You are his disciple, but we are Moses disciples.
local: Keŋte ba tee e te be yakowɔ nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋonaŋ kaseele, mɔna daa yɛɛ Mosesi wui kaseelawɔ.

eng: We know that God spoke to Moses, but we dont know where this man is from.
local: Da gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ kolosiwɔ fa Mosesi, te balee kei dɔɔ dɔɔ gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ ɔ ba lee gba ya.

eng: The man answered, This is incredible! You dont know where he is from, yet he healed my eyes!.
local: Botɔɔ te balee ŋon yako wɔ nyiaa, Te doo me nɔɔ ŋgba ɛ ba gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ ɔ ba lee ya, mɔna ŋon gyuusu na me sia.

eng: We know that God doesnt listen to sinners. God listens to anyone who is devout and does Gods will.
local: Da gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ bee nyii nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra ya. Ɔ ne nyii balaŋ baŋ bee nyii e te bɛɛ yɛɛ kpene keŋ ɔ ne gyae nɛ bo.

eng: No one has ever heard of a healing of the eyes of someone born blind.
local: Lee tɛɛle kei kewalaŋ nɛ, ŋolo te nyii ta nyi ŋolo te gyuusu walaŋ ŋon bɔɔ lola e na ɔ sia te yɛlɛɛ nɛ sia ya.

eng: If this man wasnt from God, he couldnt do this.
local: Akpaa balee kei ta lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya nɛ, ɔ be gyae waa tale yɛɛ nombii kei ya.

eng: They responded, You were born completely in sin! How is it that you dare to teach us? Then they expelled him.
local: Te be tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Nyaŋ ŋon bɔɔ lola neŋ dukum man te bɔɔ kɛɛle neŋ dukum man na, nyaŋ ne gyae n kawola daa nombia? Keŋte be gegi e lee te be tɔɔ e nyi ɔ na kɔŋ bɔ ɔsom deni keŋ man bela ya.

eng: Jesus heard they had expelled the man born blind. Finding him, Jesus said, Do you believe in the Human One.
local: Yesu kenyii nyi ba te gegi e lee bɔ ɔsom deni keŋ man nɛ, ɔ ke kɛo e te ɔ naa e te bɔɔse e nyiaa, N ne lɛɛ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon di.

eng: He answered, Who is he, sir? I want to believe in him.
local: Te balee ŋon tiranɔɔ fa e nyi, Me Gbeŋgyoo, amɔte ŋonaŋ? Yako maŋ na maa lɛɛ e di.

eng: Jesus said, You have seen him. In fact, he is the one speaking with you.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, N taŋ kena e. Ŋon yɛna ŋon ɔ ne kolosi fa neŋ debokeiman nɛ.

eng: The man said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped Jesus.
local: Te balee ŋon yakowɔ nyiaa, Mɛɛ lɛɛ e di me Gbeŋgyoo. Te ɔ buŋawɔ Yesu siaman som e.

eng: Jesus said, I have come into the world to exercise judgment so that those who dont see can see and those who see will become blind.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, Me kɔŋae tɛɛle kei dɔɔ na balaŋ nombiakumɛɛ ka lee debɔɔ. Na baŋ bɔɔ ne naa ya nɛ kena, na baŋ bɛɛ naa nɛ sia keyɛlɛɛ.

eng: Some Pharisees who were with him heard what he said and asked, Surely we arent blind, are we.
local: Farasiitena baŋ be kyaa ɔ gyaŋ botɔɔ nɛ, baale nyii kpene keŋ ɔ be yako mena dɔɔ be bɔɔse e nyiaa, Mena dɔɔ n ne yako baa nyi daa mɔ sia te yɛlɛɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, If you were blind, you wouldnt have any sin, but now that you say, We see, your sin remains.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ sia deŋ te yɛlɛɛ nɛ nafɔ ŋolo be gyae waa bu ŋon fɔɔ ya. Te keŋ ɛ kpa ɛ ne naa dɔɔ te ɛ sɛɛ ɛ doo ɛ dukum keyɛɛ man.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt enter into the sheep pen through the gate but climbs over the wall is a thief and an outlaw.
local: Keŋte Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, walaŋ ŋon ɔ ta mo denanɔɔ keŋ man gyoo namense debɔɔlɔɔ keŋ man ya, te ɔ gyem mo yenaŋ kale gyoo na ɔ yɛɛ ŋmɛɛlate e, te ɔ na mo doŋ bo lɛo balaŋ.

eng: The one who enters through the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
local: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ na mo denanɔɔ keŋ man gyoo na, ŋon yɛna namensekɛɛlere ŋon.

eng: The guard at the gate opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
local: Denanɔɔ dekere ŋon na toro fa e te namense baŋ ne nyii ɔ woya. Ɔ ne baake ŋon gbagba namense baŋ yele na ɔ ta mo wɔ lee.

eng: Whenever he has gathered all of his sheep, he goes before them and they follow him, because they know his voice.
local: Te ɔ mo wɔ pou lee na ɔ ne tɛɛ be siaman, te namense baŋ ne sila e nawolo nyi ba gyeŋ ɔ woya.

eng: They wont follow a stranger but will run away because they dont know the strangers voice.
local: Bɔɔ gyae baa sila walaŋ yakaate ya. Baa yeu lee ɔ gyaŋ nawolo nyi bɔɔ gyeŋ ɔ woya ya.

eng: Those who heard Jesus use this analogy didnt understand what he was saying.
local: Yesu gyɔɔ dudu kei fa wɔ, mɔna be te nyii kpene keŋ ɔ ne yako nɛ asɛɛ ya.

eng: So Jesus spoke again, I assure you that I am the gate of the sheep.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, maŋ yɛna namense debɔɔlɔɔ keŋ denanɔɔ.

eng: All who came before me were thieves and outlaws, but the sheep didnt listen to them.
local: Baŋ pou bɔɔ taŋgbɛɛ maŋ kɔŋ kaa beo nyi Wurubuarɛ kpila na wɔ nɛ, be yɛɛ ŋmɛɛlatenawɔ na baŋ bɛɛ mo doŋ lɛo balaŋ abɔɔ nɛ, mɔna namense baŋ te nyii wɔ ya.

eng: I am the gate. Whoever enters through me will be saved. They will come in and go out and find pasture.
local: Maŋ yɛna denanɔɔ keŋ, akpaa ŋolo de tɛɛ me man gyoo na, maa lɛɛ ɔ nyee. Waa gyoo na waa lee na waa nyiŋ weenɛɛ kpaakpaa di.

eng: The thief enters only to steal, kill, and destroy. I came so that they could have life—indeed, so that they could live life to the fullest.
local: Ŋmɛɛlate dɔɔ ɔ ne kɔŋ bo kaa ŋmɛɛle na ɔ ta koo na ɔ te wɔlɛɛ abɔɔ. Maŋ kɔŋae na ɛ kenyiŋ nyeedoŋ, na ɛ kenyiŋ ke kekpaa.

eng: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
local: Maŋ yɛna namensekɛɛlere kpaakpaate ŋon. Namensekɛɛlere kpaakpaate ŋon na mo ɔ wose fa yeŋ ɔ namense baŋ dɔɔ.

eng: When the hired hand sees the wolf coming, he leaves the sheep and runs away. Thats because he isnt the shepherd; the sheep arent really his. So the wolf attacks the sheep and scatters them.
local: Mɔna paate ŋon bɔɔ moo nyi waa kɛɛ namense baŋ dɔɔ na baa tɔ e kom nɛ, na ŋon yɛna namensekɛɛlere ŋon ɔ tee namense baŋ ya. Mena dɔɔ ɔ naa nyi gbougbouse ne kɔŋ na, ɔ ne yeu tina namense baŋ yela, na gbougbouse baŋ te kyaŋ baale na akaŋ baŋ te yaasee.

eng: Hes only a hired hand and the sheep dont matter to him.
local: Ɔ ne yeu nawolo nyi ba mo e bo na bɛɛ tɔ e kom, mena dɔɔ ɔ be famenɛ na namense baŋ ya.

eng: I am the good shepherd. I know my own sheep and they know me.
