local: Maŋ yɛna namensekɛɛlere kpaakpaate ŋon. Mena keŋ me kya gyeŋ maŋ te maŋ mɔ gyeŋ e nɛ mena ke te ma gyeŋ me namense te baŋ mɔ gyeŋ maŋ, te ma te sɛɛ nyi maa mo ma wose fa yeŋ me namense baŋ dɔɔ.

eng: I have other sheep that dont belong to this sheep pen. I must lead them too. They will listen to my voice and there will be one flock, with one shepherd.
local: Me dana namense baale baŋ bɔɔ kpuɛ kɛwɔ man ya. Kaboena nyi maa mo baŋ mɔ kaa kpu na kɛwɔ na be popou keyɛɛ walaŋ dokoloŋ namense, debɔɔlɔɔ dokoloŋ man, na be kɛɛlere ke yɛɛ dokoloŋ na baa nyii ma dei.

eng: This is why the Father loves me: I give up my life so that I can take it up again.
local: Kpene keŋ dɔɔ me kya ne gyae me nombia yɛna nyi, ma te desina ma wose nyi maa mo ma wose fa yeŋ ma kegyaebii man na maa bese foŋ lee yeŋ man.

eng: No one takes it from me, but I give it up because I want to. I have the right to give it up, and I have the right to take it up again. I received this commandment from my Father.
local: Ŋolo be gyae waa tale lɛo ma nyeedoŋ lee me gyaŋ ya. Mɔna maŋ gbagba ma kegyaebii man te maa mo ma wose fa yeŋ. Me dana doŋ maa mo ma wose fa yeŋ, te me dana doŋ maa bese nyiŋ nyeedoŋ bela. Kei yɛna kpene keŋ me kya ŋon be yako maŋ nyi maa yɛɛ. .

eng: There was another division among the Jews because of Jesus words.
local: Lee nɔɔwoya kɛŋa dɔɔ Gyudatena baŋ besewɔ kpase ba wose man.

eng: Many of them said, He has a demon and has lost his mind. Why listen to him.
local: Be man balaŋ burum yakowɔ nyiaa, Ɔ dana feliŋkum mena dɔɔ ɔ nyee ne wɔlɛɛ. Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ ne nyii e.

eng: Others said, These arent the words of someone who has a demon. Can a demon heal the eyes of people who are blind.
local: Baale mɔ kpa, Walaŋ ŋon feliŋkum doo ɔ man be gyae waa tale kolosi nnɛ ya! Sena te feliŋkum gyae ke tale gyuusu siayɛlɛɛsate sia.

eng: The time came for the Festival of Dedication in Jerusalem. It was winter.
local: Keŋte Gyudatena wee dinaa daale kaa liiwɔ. Mena wee kei te bee dii mo tɔɔse debaŋ keŋ bɔɔ mo bɔ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ do Wurubuarɛ nyiŋmaa man nɛ. Mena debaŋ kenaŋ yɛɛ waare tɛɛ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man.

eng: and Jesus was in the temple, walking in the covered porch named for Solomon.
local: Na Yesu dɔŋɛɛ ɔ ne kila poto keŋ bɛɛ baake ke Solomɔn poto Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ dekpaŋalaŋ man nɛ.

eng: The Jewish opposition circled around him and asked, How long will you test our patience? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.
local: Keŋte Gyudatena baale kaa yilaawɔ ɔ gyaŋ bɔɔse e nyiaa, Sena yaa n tinaa daa yela te de bɛɛ gyeŋ da nyee na de nawɔɔ ya nɛ? Yako daa anokoare keŋ. Nyaŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, I have told you, but you dont believe. The works I do in my Fathers name testify about me.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Me taŋ keyako ŋon mɔna ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ di ya. Gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan mɔɔ yɛɛ me kya yele man nɛ, ne diina ma wose man adansɛɛ.

eng: but you dont believe because you dont belong to my sheep.
local: mɔna ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ di ya, nawolo nyi ɛ te yɛɛ me namensewɔ ya.

eng: My sheep listen to my voice. I know them and they follow me.
local: Me namense ne nyii me dei, te ma gyeŋ wɔ te bee sila maŋ.

eng: I give them eternal life. They will never die, and no one will snatch them from my hand.
local: Mɛɛ fa wɔ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ, te bɔɔ gyae baa yo lee Wurubuarɛ siaman ya. Ŋolo be gyae waa tale lɛo wɔ lee me gyaŋ ya.

eng: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all, and no one is able to snatch them from my Fathers hand.
local: Me kya ŋon ɔ ba mo wɔ do me nyiŋmaa man nɛ, dana doŋ kela kolo kamasɛ te ŋolo be gyae waa tale lɛo wɔ lee me kya nyiŋmaa man ya.

eng: I and the Father are one.
local: Daa na me kya yɛɛ walaŋ dokoloŋ.

eng: Again the Jewish opposition picked up stones in order to stone him.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena baŋ gate boya baa fuŋ e bela.

eng: Jesus responded, I have shown you many good works from the Father. For which of those works do you stone me.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Me kya te yeli ma te yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa burum ɛ siaman, ŋe man woŋti dɔɔ te ɛ ne gyae ɛ ke fuŋii maŋ boya.

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We dont stone you for a good work but for insulting God. You are human, yet you make yourself out to be God.
local: Te Gyudatena baŋ tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Na abɔɔ nya ayɛɛŋ kɛŋa dɔɔ te dɛɛ gyae dee fuŋii neŋ boya ya, mɔna mena keŋ n ne yako mmusuo nombia dɔɔ. Nawolo nyi n yɛɛ deniwalaŋ e, mɔna n na mo n wose maa na Wurubuarɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Isnt it written in your Law, I have said, you are gods?.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ba te ŋmarase ɛmɛɛ gbagba mmaraa tɔne keŋ man nyi, Wurubuarɛ kpa, ɛ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ kemaasasewɔ yee?.

eng: Scripture calls those to whom Gods word came gods , and scripture cant be abolished.
local: Da gyeŋ nyi kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ne yako nɛ yɛɛ anokoare kekpaa. Te Wurubuarɛ de baake balaŋ baŋ ɔ ba mo ɔ nombia ŋan fa nɛ nyi be yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ kemaasasewɔ na.

eng: So how can you say that the one whom the Father has made holy and sent into the world insults God because he said, I am Gods Son?.
local: weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne yako maŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ ba lese maŋ te ɔ be kpila maŋ kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ nyi mɛɛ mo ma wose maa na Wurubuarɛ keŋ mɛɛ yako nyi me yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bu e dɔɔ?.

eng: If I dont do the works of my Father, dont believe me.
local: Akpaa me bɛɛ yɛɛ kpene keŋ me kya ŋon kpa ma yɛɛ ya na, ɛ na lɛɛ maŋ di ya.

eng: But if I do them, and you dont believe me, believe the works so that you can know and recognize that the Father is in me and I am in the Father.
local: te akpaa mɛɛ yɛɛ ŋa na, nyi ɛ be lɛɛ maŋ di koraŋ ya na ɛ lɛɛ abɔɔ ŋan mɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ di, na ɛ ka gyeŋ na ɛ kenyii asɛɛ nyiaa daa na me kya ŋon yɛɛ dokoloŋ.

eng: Again, they wanted to arrest him, but he escaped from them.
local: Mena dɔɔ be gyaewɔ baa kyaŋ e bela, te ɔ sarakeewɔ lee be man.

eng: Jesus went back across the Jordan to the place where John had baptized at first, and he stayed there.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ toŋ Gyɔɔdan boo keŋ gyu botɔɔ keŋ Gyɔn be kyaa sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ kaalaŋ nɛ, te ɔ ke kyaawɔ botɔɔ.

eng: Many people came to him. John didnt do any miraculous signs, they said, but everything John said about this man was true.
local: Balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ te be yako dɔŋa nyiaa, Gyɔn te yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia ya, mɔna kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɔ be yako lee balee kei wose man nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ.

eng: Many believed in Jesus there.
local: Keŋte balaŋ burum baŋ ba seŋɛɛ botɔɔ nɛ lɛɛ Yesu di.

eng: A certain man, Lazarus, was ill. He was from Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha. .
local: Balee ŋolo kyaawɔ Bɛtani donɔɔ man keŋ bɛɛ baake e nyi Lasarose. Bɛtani donɔɔ kenaŋ man te Mɛɛre na ɔ kɔɔ Maata leewɔ.

eng: This was the Mary who anointed the Lord with fragrant oil and wiped his feet with her hair. Her brother Lazarus was ill.
local: Mɛɛre kei yɛna walaŋ ŋon ɔ be toosi nuŋ ofoŋofoŋ woli Yesu nawɔɔ, te ɔ ba mo ɔ nyeenyɔɔse feefo ŋa nɛ. Ɔ ŋmɛɛtee yɛna Lasarose ŋon ɔ na wee nɛ.

eng: So the sisters sent word to Jesus, saying, Lord, the one whom you love is ill.
local: Mena dɔɔ Mɛɛre na Maata doo Yesu tiila nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, n gyoo ŋon loŋ bɛɛ lam ɛmɛɛ na e nsana ya nɛ na wee.

eng: When he heard this, Jesus said, This illness isnt fatal. Its for the glory of God so that Gods Son can be glorified through it.
local: Yesu kenyii tiila keŋ nɛ ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Kawee kei te yɛɛ kawee keŋ gyae ka ko Lasarose ya. Mɔna ke gyae ke yela balaŋ kena Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri na ka ke yela baa lese maŋ Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon mɔ yele.

eng: Jesus loved Martha, her sister, and Lazarus.
local: Na Yesu ne gyae Maata na ɔ naabu Mɛɛre na Lasarose nombia.

eng: When he heard that Lazarus was ill, he stayed where he was. After two days.
local: Mɔna ɔ kenyii nyi Lasarose na wee nɛ, ɔ besewɔ di weeya ala botɔɔ keŋ ɔ kyaa nɛ.

eng: he said to his disciples, Lets return to Judea again.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ ɔ yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ fa dɛɛ bese gyu Gyudia tɛɛle man bela.

eng: The disciples replied, Rabbi, the Jewish opposition wants to stone you, but you want to go back.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale yaa ŋgba Gyudatena be yele baa fuŋii neŋ boya nɛ. Woŋ dɔɔ te n ne gyae n ka bese gyu botɔɔ bela.

eng: Jesus answered, Arent there twelve hours in the day? Whoever walks in the day doesnt stumble because they see the light of the world.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Wee kamasɛ dana ke nelim na ka weese. Akpaa ŋolo dɔŋɛɛ weeseteŋteŋ nɛ, ɔ bɛɛ gyɔ ɔ nae ya nawolo nyi weese kyaa.

eng: But whoever walks in the night does stumble because the light isnt in them.
local: Mɔna nyi akpaa ɔ dɔŋɛɛ ditiŋtɛɛ man na ɔ ne gyɔ ɔ nae, nawolo nyi keŋmaŋee be kyaa na waa naa ya.

eng: He continued, Our friend Lazarus is sleeping, but I am going in order to wake him up.
local: Kei wɔle na Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, De gyoo Lasarose te doo dosoroŋ mɔna maa gyu na me kaa gyuusu e.

eng: The disciples said, Lord, if hes sleeping, he will get well.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ tiranɔɔ nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, akpaa ɔ doe na ɔ kawee keŋ gyae ke taŋ.

eng: They thought Jesus meant that Lazarus was in a deep sleep, but Jesus had spoken about Lazarus death.
local: Na Lasarose yeŋ nombia yaa Yesu ne yako, mɔna ɔ kaseela baŋ na gyueŋ bo nyi dosoroŋ gbagba yaa Lasarose doowɔ.

eng: Jesus told them plainly, Lazarus has died.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu torowɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Lasarose ta yem.

eng: For your sakes, Im glad I wasnt there so that you can believe. Lets go to him.
local: mɔna lee ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ dana maŋ gyoŋ nyi mɔɔ kyaa botɔɔ pɛte ɔ yeŋawɔ ya, na ɛ kenyiŋ lɛɛ maŋ di. Ɛ fa dee gyu ɔ gyaŋ.

eng: Then Thomas said to the other disciples, Let us go too so that we may die with Jesus.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔ kaseela baŋ man ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e Tomase yaa Ata nɛ yako ɔ tebia kaseela akaŋ baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ fa de popou ke sila da wolale ŋon gyu. Nyi yɛɛ yeŋ ke na, de popou ka yeŋ kpu na e.

eng: When Jesus arrived, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days.
local: Yesu ke gyu ka gyoo Bɛtani donɔɔ man nɛ, be yako e nyiaa be kewuu Lasarose weeya anaarate kenaŋ.

eng: Bethany was a little less than two miles from Jerusalem.
local: Bɛtani yɛɛ donɔɔ keŋ ka benaa na Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ. Ke lem gyae ke yɛɛ ŋgba gbɛɛkemaase atooro nɛ.

eng: Many Jews had come to comfort Martha and Mary after their brothers death.
local: Na Gyudatena burum te kɔŋ Maata na Mɛɛre gyaŋ be kaa gyam wɔ lee be ŋmɛɛtee ŋon yeŋ man.

eng: When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went to meet him, while Mary remained in the house.
local: Maata ke nyii nyi Yesu ne kɔŋ nɛ, ɔ leewɔ gyu ke gyaŋee na e. Na Mɛɛre dɔɔ ŋon kyaa dekpaŋalaŋ man.

eng: Martha said to Jesus, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Maata ke gyu ke tuŋ Yesu nɛ ɔ yako e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, nyi akpaa nyeŋ kyaa kɛbo na nafɔ me ŋmɛɛtee deŋ ta yem ya!.

eng: Even now I know that whatever you ask God, God will give you.
local: Mɔna ma gyeŋ nyi debokeiman koraŋ nɛ, kpene kamasɛ keŋ n bɔɔse Wurubuarɛ na waa yɛɛ fa neŋ.

eng: Jesus told her, Your brother will rise again.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, N ŋmɛɛtee ŋon waa bese foŋ na waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ bela.

eng: Martha replied, I know that he will rise in the resurrection on the last day.
local: Te Maata yako e nyiaa, Ayi ma gyeŋ nyi wee ɔtomante keŋ woya pou baa foŋ lee yeŋ man nɛ, ŋon mɔ waa foŋ.

eng: Jesus said to her, I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in me will live, even though they die.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako e bela nyiaa, Maŋ yɛna walaŋ ɔ ne gyuusu balaŋ lee yeŋ man te be nyiŋ nyeedoŋ. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa lɛɛ maŋ di na, nyi ɔ yem koraŋ na waa bese nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.

eng: Everyone who lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this.
local: te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa te ɔ lɛɛ maŋ di na, ɔ be gyae wa yeŋ leki ya. Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse Maata nyiaa, N ne lɛɛ nombia kɛŋa di.

eng: She replied, Yes, Lord, I believe that you are the Christ, Gods Son, the one who is coming into the world.
local: Te Maata sɛɛ e nyiaa, Ayi me Gbeŋgyoo, mɛɛ lɛɛ di nyi nyaŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre, Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon dɛɛ daa nyi waa kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: After she said this, she went and spoke privately to her sister Mary, The teacher is here and hes calling for you.
local: Maata kekolosi nombia kɛŋa taŋ nɛ, ɔ besewɔ gyu dekpaŋalaŋ man ke baake ɔ naabu Mɛɛre lee yenaŋ te ɔ yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo ŋon te kɔŋ te ɔ kpa nya kɔŋ.

eng: When Mary heard this, she got up quickly and went to Jesus.
local: Mɛɛre kenyii mena nɛ, ɔ korowɔ bileŋ gyu ke gyaŋee na e.

eng: He hadnt entered the village but was still in the place where Martha had met him.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na Yesu ta gyoo ta donɔɔ keŋ man ya. Na ɔ sɛɛ ɔ kyaa botɔɔ keŋ Maata be gyu ke tuŋ e nɛ.

eng: When the Jews who were comforting Mary in the house saw her get up quickly and leave, they followed her. They assumed she was going to mourn at the tomb.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ mɔ na, Gyudatena te kɔŋ dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man bee kolosi do Mɛɛre. Ba kena ŋgba Mɛɛre ba koro karatetee dokoloŋ lee ɔ ne gyu nɛ, na baŋ gyeŋ nyi ɔ ne gyu baa ɔ ŋmɛɛtee Lasarose yebɔɔ keŋ tɛɛ ɔ kaa wii. Mena dɔɔ baŋ mɔ korowɔ sila e.

eng: When Mary arrived where Jesus was and saw him, she fell at his feet and said, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Mɛɛre ke gyu kelii botɔɔ keŋ Yesu kyaa nɛ te ɔ tisiwɔ buŋ Yesu nawɔɔ tɛɛ te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, nyeŋ kyaa kɛbo na nafɔ me ŋmɛɛtee deŋ ta yem ya.

eng: When Jesus saw her crying and the Jews who had come with her crying also, he was deeply disturbed and troubled.
local: Yesu kena mena keŋ Mɛɛre na Gyudatena baŋ be silana e ne wii nɛ, takaa e te dekɔkɔrɔkɔɔ kyaŋa e.

eng: He asked, Where have you laid him? They replied, Lord, come and see.
local: Keŋte ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Aleŋ te ɛ wuu e? Te be yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, kɔŋ na n kaa kɛɛ.

eng: Jesus began to cry.
local: Keŋte Yesu wiiwɔ.

eng: The Jews said, See how much he loved him.
local: Keŋte Gyudatena baŋ yakowɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kɛɛ mena keŋ ɔ ne gyae Lasarose nombia.

eng: But some of them said, He healed the eyes of the man born blind. Couldnt he have kept Lazarus from dying.
local: Mɔna balaŋ baale yakowɔ nyiaa, Na Yesu kei gyuusuna siayɛlɛɛsate ŋon sia yee? Nafɔ ŋgba ŋon talewɔ lɛɛ Lasarose na ɔ ba yem ya.

eng: Jesus was deeply disturbed again when he came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone covered the entrance.
local: Dekɔkɔrɔkɔɔ kyaŋa Yesu bela te ɔ gyuu botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ wuu Lasarose nɛ. Na be wuu e bo boe bɔɔ daale maŋ, te be biliŋi boe dinaa daale tɔ ke nɔɔ.

eng: Jesus said, Remove the stone. Martha, the sister of the dead man, said, Lord, the smell will be awful! Hes been dead four days.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ biliŋi boe keŋ lee yebɔɔ keŋ nɔɔman. Te yere ŋon ɔ deesi Maata yako Yesu nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, debaŋ kei dɔɔ ɔ ne wɔlɛɛ. Nawolo nyi gyɛŋ yɛna weeya anaara yaa ŋgba bɔɔ wuu e nɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I tell you that if you believe, you will see Gods glory.
local: Te Yesu yako e nyiaa, Ma te yako neŋ nyi akpaa n lɛɛ maŋ di na nyaŋ kena Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ yee.

eng: So they removed the stone. Jesus looked up and said, Father, thank you for hearing me.
local: Mena dɔɔ be biliŋi boe keŋ lee yebɔɔ keŋ nɔɔman. Botɔɔ te Yesu gyinaa ɔ nyee kɛɛ adido te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ nyiaa, Taata, mɛɛ fa neŋ karaŋ ŋgba n ne nyii maŋ nɛ.

eng: I know you always hear me. I say this for the benefit of the crowd standing here so that they will believe that you sent me.
local: Maŋ dɔɔ ma gyeŋ nyi n ne nyii maŋ debaŋ kamasɛ, mɔna balaŋ baŋ ba seŋɛɛ kɛbo dɔɔ te mɛɛ yako mena na baa nyiŋ gyeŋ nyi nyaŋ kpila na maŋ.

eng: Having said this, Jesus shouted with a loud voice, Lazarus, come out.
local: Yesu ke yako mena taŋ nɛ, te ɔ fae yeesa keŋkeŋ baake Lasarose nyiaa, Lasarose, lee kɔŋ.

eng: The dead man came out, his feet bound and his hands tied, and his face covered with a cloth. Jesus said to them, Untie him and let him go.
local: Keŋte Lasarose ŋon ɔ ba yem nɛ leewɔ kɔŋ, na kewuu abɔɔ ŋan bɔɔ mo bake ɔ nyiŋmaase na ɔ nawɔɔ, na kanyaŋ keŋ bɔɔ mo bake ɔ sia gbɛɛ nɛ doo mena. Te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa Ɛ booli e, na waa ta.

eng: Therefore, many of the Jews who came with Mary and saw what Jesus did believed in him.
local: Gyudatena burum baŋ bɔɔ kɔŋ Mɛɛre gyaŋ te bɔɔ naa kpene keŋ Yesu be yɛɛ nɛ lɛɛ e di.

eng: But some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
local: Mɔna be man baale besewɔ gyu Farasiitena baŋ gyaŋ gyu ke yako wɔ kpene keŋ Yesu be yɛɛ nɛ.

eng: Then the chief priests and Pharisees called together the council and said, What are we going to do? This man is doing many miraculous signs!.
local: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Farasiitena baake donɔɔ kegyiise gyaŋee. Te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa Sena yaa dɛɛ yɛɛ? Nawolo nyi balee kei ne yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia burum.

eng: If we let him go on like this, everyone will believe in him. Then the Romans will come and take away both our temple and our people.
local: Akpaa de tina e yela te ɔ gyakaa ɔ ne yɛɛ mena tom kei na, balaŋ baŋ pou baa lɛɛ e di na ke yela Romantena kaa yɛlɛɛ de Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ na baa lɛo de tɛɛle pou.

eng: One of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, told them, You dont know anything!.
local: Te balaŋ baŋ man ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Kaifase ŋon ɔ be yɛɛ saese kegyia kulutoo kenaŋ man nɛ yakowɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ ɛ ba gyeŋ daale ya.

eng: You dont see that it is better for you that one man die for the people rather than the whole nation be destroyed.
local: Ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi dei nyi walaŋ dokoloŋ waa yeŋ fa balaŋ baŋ pou kela keŋ nyi tɛɛle pou balaŋ kewɔlɛɛ yee.

eng: He didnt say this on his own. As high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus would soon die for the nation.
local: Te yɛɛ Kaifase gbagba agyueŋ yaa ɔ moowɔ yako nombia kɛŋa ya, mɔna keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia kulutoo kenaŋ man dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ yela ɔ yako mena nombia ŋenaŋ see nyi, kaboena nyi Yesu waa yeŋ fa Gyudatena pou.

eng: and not only for the nation. Jesus would also die so that Gods children scattered everywhere would be gathered together as one.
local: Te yɛɛ nyi Gyudatena baageŋ te Yesu waa yeŋ fa ya, mɔna waa yeŋ bo fa Wurubuarɛ bia baŋ pou bɔɔ yaasee nɛ na waa nyiŋ mo wɔ kaa yilaa.

eng: From that day on they plotted to kill him.
local: Leenaŋ wee kenaŋ keŋ kegyiise baŋ be gyaŋee nɛ, te be fiasɛɛ bɛɛ gyae gbɛɛ keŋ baa mo ko Yesu.

eng: Therefore, Jesus was no longer active in public ministry among the Jewish leaders. Instead, he left Jerusalem and went to a place near the wilderness, to a city called Ephraim, where he stayed with his disciples.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ na Yesu bɛɛ tekaa lese ɔ wose debɔɔ Gyudia tɛɛle man bela ya. Mɔna ɔ leewɔ botɔɔ gyu donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Eferam te ka benaa na depampaa dinaa daale nɛ. Botɔɔ te ŋon na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyuuwɔ ke kyaa.

eng: It was almost time for the Jewish Passover, and many people went from the countryside up to Jerusalem to purify themselves through ritual washing before the Passover.
local: Na Gyudatena nyeekelɛɛwee kedi keŋ ta benaa, mena dɔɔ balaŋ burum lee donɔɔse ŋan man gyu Gyerusalɛm be kaa gyoraa ba wose pɛna nyeekelɛɛwee kedi keŋ kelii.

eng: They were looking for Jesus. As they spoke to each other in the temple, they said, What do you think? He wont come to the festival, will he.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ man pou na bɛɛ kɛo Yesu. Be ka seŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Sena yɛna ya gyueŋ? Doona ɔ be gyae waa kɔŋ nyeekelɛɛwee kedi kei man ya.

eng: The chief priests and Pharisees had given orders that anyone who knew where he was should report it, so they could arrest him.
local: Na Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Farasiitena baŋ te yako wɔ nyiaa, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ gyeŋ Yesu dekyae na ɔ kaa yako wɔ na be kaa kyaŋ e.

eng: Before the Festival of Passover, Jesus knew that his time had come to leave this world and go to the Father. Having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them fully.
local: Pɛ tɛɛ kena na Gyudatena kedi ba nyeekelɛɛwee keŋ nɛ, na Yesu wulaa gyeŋ nyi ɔ debaŋ te lii keŋ waa lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyu ɔ kya gyaŋ. Mɔna mena keŋ ɔ ne gyae ɔ balaŋ baŋ be kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, mena te waa gyae be nombia kelii ɔto.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were sharing the evening meal. The devil had already provoked Judas, Simon Iscariots son, to betray Jesus.
local: Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ ke kyaa baa di be balinɔɔ weenɛɛ nɛ, na ɔbɔnsam taŋ ka mo nombiakumɛɛ do Simɔn bu Gyudase Isekarɔte nyeeman nyi waa lese Yesu fa na baa ko.

eng: Jesus knew the Father had given everything into his hands and that he had come from God and was returning to God.
local: Yesu mɔ gyeŋ nyi ɔ kya ta mo kpene kamasɛ do ɔ nyiŋmaa man, te ɔ gyeŋ mɔ nyi ɔ lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ bo kɔŋ te ɔ gyaŋ te waa bese gyu.

eng: So he got up from the table and took off his robes. Picking up a linen towel, he tied it around his waist.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu korowɔ lee weenɛɛ ŋan tɛɛ te ɔ kpee ɔ kegba wurikyim keŋ ɔ doona nɛ, te ɔ moo bɔroboa kpaŋ ɔ teŋa man.

eng: Then he poured water into a washbasin and began to wash the disciples feet, drying them with the towel he was wearing.
local: Keŋte ɔ toosi loŋ do tasa man te ɔ fiasɛɛ ɔ ne faafo ɔ kaseela baŋ nawɔɔ, te ɔ moo bɔroboa keŋ kpaŋɛɛ ɔ teŋa man nɛ feefo wɔ.

eng: When Jesus came to Simon Peter, Peter said to him, Lord, are you going to wash my feet.
local: Ɔ kelii Simɔn Pita gyaŋ nɛ Pita bɔɔse e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, nyaŋ ne gyaela n kefaafo me nawɔɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont understand what Im doing now, but you will understand later.
local: Te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Kpene keŋ mɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ n be gyae n ke nyii ke man debokeiman ya, mɔna nyaŋ kaa nyii ke man wɔle wɔle.

eng: No! Peter said. You will never wash my feet! Jesus replied, Unless I wash you, you wont have a place with me.
local: Keŋte Pita yako e nyiaa, Koa, mɔɔ sɛɛ na n ke faafo me nawɔɔ korakora ya. Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Akpaa ma te faafo n nawɔɔ ya na, daa na neŋ be doo daale man ya.

eng: Simon Peter said, Lord, not only my feet but also my hands and my head.
local: Te Simɔn Pita yako e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, akpaa mena na na faafo me nawɔɔ kaageŋ ya, mɔna faafo me nyiŋmaase na ma nyee mɔ.

eng: Jesus responded, Those who have bathed need only to have their feet washed, because they are completely clean. You disciples are clean, but not every one of you.
local: Te Yesu yako e nyiaa, Walaŋ ŋon ɔ be sɔɔ nɛ ɔ wose pou ta lee. Ɔ be kaboena nyi waa sɔ bela ya. Kaboena bo nyi waa faafo ɔ nawɔɔ kaageŋ. Ɛ popou wose ta lee, mɔna walaŋ dokoloŋ wose danaana ayimɛɛ.

eng: He knew who would betray him. Thats why he said, Not every one of you is clean.
local: Nawolo nyi na Yesu gyeŋ walaŋ ŋon waa lese e fa, mena dɔɔ te ɔ yakowɔ nyi Walaŋ dokoloŋ wose danaana ayimɛɛ.

eng: After he washed the disciples feet, he put on his robes and returned to his place at the table. He said to them, Do you know what Ive done for you?.
local: Yesu kefaafo be nawɔɔ taŋ nɛ, ɔ moo ɔ kegba wurikyim keŋ do bela te ɔ gyuuwɔ kekyaa ɔ dekyae man. Te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ ne nyii kpene keŋ mɔɔ yɛɛ fa ŋon nɛ man?.

eng: You call me Teacher and Lord, and you speak correctly, because I am.
local: Ɛ ne baake maŋ nyi ɛ wolale na ɛ Gbeŋgyoo. Dei ŋgba ɛ ne baake maŋ mena nɛ, nawolo nyi ŋe kaboena maŋ.

eng: If I, your Lord and teacher, have washed your feet, you too must wash each others feet.
local: Te akpaa maŋ ɛ Gbeŋgyoo na ɛ wolale te faafo ɛ nawɔɔ nɛ, mena te kaboena nyi ɛmɛɛ mɔ yaa faafo dɔŋa nawɔɔ.

eng: I have given you an example: Just as I have done, you also must do.
local: Me yɛɛ kei baa wola ŋon na ɛmɛɛ mɔ kenyiŋ yɛɛ fa dɔŋa ŋgba mena keŋ maŋ be yɛɛ nɛ.

eng: I assure you, servants arent greater than their master, nor are those who are sent greater than the one who sent them.
local: Anokoare yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, gbeŋi bɛɛ kela ɔ gbeŋgyoo ya, te kpilale mɔ bɛɛ kela walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kpila e nɛ ya.

eng: Since you know these things, you will be happy if you do them.
local: Nɛnɛɛ keŋ ɛ ba gyeŋ nombia kɛŋa nɛ, akpaa ɛ di ŋe dɔɔ na gyoŋ gyae ke doo ɛ man.

eng: Im not speaking about all of you. I know those whom Ive chosen. But this is to fulfill the scripture, The one who eats my bread has turned against me.
local: Na ɛ popou yaa me dana ya, nawolo nyi ma gyeŋ balaŋ baŋ mɔɔ lese. Mɔna kaboena nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan bɔɔ ŋmarase see nɛ dɛɛ kɔŋ mena. Be ŋmarasewɔ see nyiaa, walaŋ ŋon daa na e na do nyiŋmaa di nɛ, ta koro seŋ me dɔɔ.

eng: Im telling you this now, before it happens, so that when it does happen you will believe that I Am.
local: Mena dɔɔ me wulaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nombii kei baa pɛna ka ke kɔŋ, na ka de kɔŋ na ɛ ke lɛɛ di nyiaa, maŋ yɛna ŋon ɔ kyaa pɛte be yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ.

eng: I assure you that whoever receives someone I send receives me, and whoever receives me receives the one who sent me.
local: Anokoare yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, walaŋ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon maa kpila nɛ na maŋ yaa ɔ lɛɛwɔ, te walaŋ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ maŋ na ɔ te lɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ.

eng: After he said these things, Jesus was deeply disturbed and testified, I assure you, one of you will betray me.
local: Yesu keyako nombia kɛŋa taŋ nɛ, nombia takaa e ɔ konɔɔ man nideli te ɔ kolosiwɔ balaŋ man keŋ ɔ ta mo kolo weese ya nyi, Ɛ man ŋolo waa lese maŋ fa na baa ko.

eng: His disciples looked at each other, confused about which of them he was talking about.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ wose yeŋawɔ te be kɛɛ dɔŋa dokoloŋ dokoloŋ, nawolo nyi be ta gyeŋ ŋon ɔ dana ya.

eng: One of the disciples, the one whom Jesus loved, was at Jesus side.
local: Ɔ kaseela baŋ man ŋolo ŋon Yesu ne gyae nideli nɛ kyaa la benaa e.

eng: Simon Peter nodded at him to get him to ask Jesus who he was talking about.
local: mena dɔɔ Simɔn Pita fatee e te ɔ yakowɔ nyi, Bɔɔse e walaŋ ŋon ɔ dana.

eng: Leaning back toward Jesus, this disciple asked, Lord, who is it.
local: Mena dɔɔ kaseele ŋon tuuwɔ benaa na Yesu te ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, amɔte yaa n dana.

eng: Jesus answered, Its the one to whom I will give this piece of bread once I have dipped into the bowl.Then he dipped the piece of bread and gave it to Judas, Simon Iscariots son.
local: Te Yesu tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Walaŋ ŋon maa kara bodobodo mo nyoko tasa kei man fa e nɛ, na ŋon yaa me dana. Keŋte ɔ kara bodobodo keŋ te ɔ moowɔ nyoko tasa keŋ man te ɔ moowɔ fa Simɔn bu Gyudase Isekarɔte ŋon.

eng: After Judas took the bread, Satan entered into him. Jesus told him, What you are about to do, do quickly.
local: Gyudase kedi bodobodo keŋ pɛ te ɔbɔnsam gyoo ɔ man. Te Yesu yako e nyi, Kpene keŋ n ne gyae n keyɛɛ nɛ, yɛɛ ke bileŋ.

eng: No one sitting at the table understood why Jesus said this to him.
local: Mɔna baŋ be kyaa weenɛɛ ŋan tɛɛ nɛ, be ta gyeŋ kpene keŋ dɔɔ te Yesu be yako e mena ya.

eng: Some thought that, since Judas kept the money bag, Jesus told him, Go, buy what we need for the feast, or that he should give something to the poor.
local: Na Gyudase ne kɛɛ na be kɔba dɔɔ, mena dɔɔ kaseela baŋ baale gyueŋwɔ nyi Yesu yako e baa nyi waa gyu ke lɛɛ weenɛɛ ŋan baa mo gyu kedi ba nyeekelɛɛwee kedi keŋ man nɛ, yaa waa lese kɔba fa ayematena.

eng: So when Judas took the bread, he left immediately. And it was night.
local: Mena dɔɔ Gyudase ka betaa lɛɛ bodobodo keŋ di pɛ, te ɔ leewɔ bileŋ. Na tɛɛ te yuu.

eng: When Judas was gone, Jesus said, Now the Human One has been glorified, and God has been glorified in him.
local: Gyudase kalee laŋ nɛ Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, Nɛnɛɛ te Wurubuarɛ waa lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon gyoorobiiri debɔɔ. Waa ta me dɔɔ yela balaŋ kena ɔ gyoorobiiri keŋ.

eng: If God has been glorified in him, God will also glorify the Human One in himself and will glorify him immediately.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako nyi, Akpaa Wurubuarɛ waa ta me dɔɔ lese ɔ gyoorobiiri wola na, mena mɔ te waa ta ŋon gbagba dɔɔ lese me gyoorobiiri wola. Be gyae ke kyare pɛte waa yɛɛ nombii kei ya.

eng: Little children, Im with you for a little while longer. You will look for me—but, just as I told the Jewish leaders, I also tell you now—Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Me bia mɔɔ gyae maa kyaa ɛ gyaŋ kyare ya. Ɛ ke kɛo maŋ, mɔna ŋgba mena keŋ mɔɔ yako Gyudatena baŋ nɛ, mena yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon debokeiman nyiaa Botɔɔ keŋ mee gyu nɛ ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale kɔŋ botɔɔ ya.

eng: I give you a new commandment: Love each other. Just as I have loved you, so you also must love each other.
local: Mɛɛ fa ŋon mmaraa wɔle nyiaa, ɛ gyae dɔŋa. Ŋgba mena keŋ maŋ be gyae ŋon nɛ, ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ gyae dɔŋa mena.

eng: This is how everyone will know that you are my disciples, when you love each other.
local: Akpaa ɛ ne gyae dɔŋa na, walaŋ kamasɛ waa naa nyi ɛ yɛɛ me kaseelawɔ.

eng: Simon Peter said to Jesus, Lord, where are you going? Jesus answered, Where I am going, you cant follow me now, but you will follow later.
local: Botɔɔ te Simɔn Pita bɔɔse e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, aleŋ yaa n ne gyuu? Te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Botɔɔ keŋ mee gyu nɛ, n be gyae n ke tale sila maŋ debokeiman ya, mɔna nyaŋ kaa sila maŋ wɔle wɔle.

eng: Peter asked, Lord, why cant I follow you now? Ill give up my life for you.
local: Te Pita bɔɔse e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, weera dɔɔ te mɔɔ tale sila neŋ debaŋ kei ya? Ma te sɛɛ nyi maa yeŋ fa neŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Will you give up your life for me? I assure you that you will deny me three times before the rooster crows.
local: Te Yesu tiranɔɔ nyiaa, N ne lɛɛdi nyi nyaŋ ke tale yeŋ fa maŋ? Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako neŋ nyi pɛ kegyim kelɔ woya nɛ, nyaŋ ke bɛɛ betabotooro nyi n ba gyeŋ maŋ korakora ya.

eng: Six days before Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, home of Lazarus, whom Jesus had raised from the dead.
local: Keka weeya looro na Gyudatena kedi ba nyeekelɛɛwee dinaa keŋ nɛ, Yesu gyuu Bɛtani donɔɔ man. Mena donɔɔ kenaŋ man te Lasarose ŋon Yesu be gyuusu e lee yeŋ man nɛ leewɔ.

eng: Lazarus and his sisters hosted a dinner for him. Martha served and Lazarus was among those who joined him at the table.
local: Be lɔɔ yele saa weenɛɛ fa Yesu. Maata mɔ kpuɛ baŋ bɛɛ kpɛlɛɛ weenɛɛ ŋan nɛ man. Na Lasarose mɔ kpuɛ na baŋ be kyaa bee dii nɛ.

eng: Then Mary took an extraordinary amount, almost three-quarters of a pound, of very expensive perfume made of pure nard. She anointed Jesus feet with it, then wiped his feet dry with her hair. The house was filled with the aroma of the perfume.
local: Keŋte Mɛɛre moo nuŋ ofoŋofoŋte keŋ ke dana koyadoŋ nɛ porontia tɔɔ, te ɔ kaa toosi ke woli Yesu nawɔɔ te ɔ moo ɔ nyeenyɔɔse feefo ɔ nawɔɔ ŋan. Te nuŋ keŋ ofoŋ lɛɛ dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ pou. Daŋ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Naade nɛ te ba moowɔ yɛɛ nuŋ kenaŋ.

eng: Dont be troubled. Trust in God. Trust also in me.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ na yeli ɛ wɔe ke lɔ ŋon ya. Ɛ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di na ɛ ke lɛɛ maŋ mɔ di.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples , complained.
local: Te Yesu kaseela baŋ man ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Gyudase Isekarɔte, ŋon waa kaa lese Yesu fa na baa ko nɛ yakowɔ nyiaa.

eng: My Fathers house has room to spare. If that werent the case, would I have told you that Im going to prepare a place for you?.
local: Denɛɛ burum doo me kya ŋon dekpaŋalaŋ man. Akpaa te yɛɛ mena ya na nafɔ mɔɔ yako nyi mee gyu me kaa desina akyaese see ŋon ya.

eng: This perfume was worth a years wages! Why wasnt it sold and the money given to the poor? .
local: Woŋ dɔɔ te ba te yɔɔ mena nuŋ ofoŋofoŋ kei na baa mo ke kɔba keŋ fa ayematena ya. Nyi beŋ yɔɔ ke na nafɔ baa nyiŋ tomyɛɛre kulutoo dokoloŋ kɔba lee ke man.

eng: When I go to prepare a place for you, I will return and take you to be with me so that where I am you will be too.
local: Akpaa me gyu ke desina botɔɔ taŋ na maa bese kɔŋ kaa mo ŋon gyu me gyaŋ, na botɔɔ keŋ me kyaa na ɛmɛɛ mɔ ke kyaa botɔɔ.

eng: He said this not because he cared about the poor but because he was a thief. He carried the money bag and would take what was in it.
local: Gyudase keyako nombia kɛŋa nɛ, na ayematena wose man yaa ɔ na gyueŋ ya, mɔna ɔ yɛɛ ŋmɛɛlate e. Ŋon na see na be kɔba, te ɔ ne kyaa mo ŋaale desina ɔ wose.

eng: You know the way to the place Im going.
local: Botɔɔ keŋ mee gyu nɛ ɛ gyeŋ ke gbɛɛ.

eng: Then Jesus said, Leave her alone. This perfume was to be used in preparation for my burial, and this is how she has used it.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, Ɛ tina alo ŋon yela! Ɔ yɛɛ kei baa mo daa me kewuu.

eng: Thomas asked, Lord, we dont know where you are going. How can we know the way.
local: Botɔɔ te Tomase bɔɔse e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, dɔɔ gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ ne gyu ya, sena te dɛɛ yɛɛ na dɛɛ gyeŋ ke gbɛɛ.

eng: You will always have the poor among you, but you wont always have me.
local: Ayematena dɔɔ be kyaa ɛ gyaŋ debaŋ kamasɛ, mɔna maŋ dɔɔ mɔɔ gyae maa kyaa ɛ gyaŋ kekpaa ya.

eng: Jesus answered, I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Maŋ yɛna gbɛɛ keŋ na anokoare keŋ na nyeedoŋ keŋ. Ŋolo be kyaa nyi waa tale gyu me kya ŋon gyaŋ keŋ ɔ te tɛɛ me dɔɔ ya.

eng: Many Jews learned that he was there. They came not only because of Jesus but also to see Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
local: Gyudatena burum kenyii nyi Yesu kyaa Bɛtani donɔɔ man nɛ, be gyuuwɔ botɔɔ. Na Yesu waageŋ dɔɔ te be gyuuwɔ botɔɔ ya, mɔna bɛɛ gyae baa naa Lasarose ŋon Yesu be gyuusu e lee yeŋ man nɛ mɔ.

eng: If you have really known me, you will also know the Father. From now on you know him and have seen him.
local: Nyi akpaa yeŋ gyeŋ maŋ na nafɔ ɛ ka gyeŋ me kya ŋon mɔ. Lee gyɛŋ ne gyu nɛ, ɛ ta gyeŋ e te ɛ ne naa e mɔ.

eng: The chief priests decided that they would kill Lazarus too.
local: Mena dɔɔ saese kegyiise baŋ mɔ gyae gbɛɛ baa ko Lasarose mɔ.

eng: Philip said, Lord, show us the Father; that will be enough for us.
local: Keŋte Filipo mɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, wola daa n kya ŋon. Akpaa n wola daa n kya ŋon na, keŋ kaageŋ koraŋ dei fa daa.

eng: It was because of Lazarus that many of the Jews had deserted them and come to believe in Jesus.
local: nawolo nyi Yesu ke gyuusu e lee yeŋ man dɔɔ Gyudatena burum te bɛɛ wɔ te ba ke lɛɛ Yesu di.

eng: Jesus replied, Dont you know me, Philip, even after I have been with you all this time? Whoever has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, Show us the Father?.
local: Te Yesu mɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, Filipo, mena dɔɔ leenaŋ ma ke kyaa ɛ gyaŋ kei nɛ, na ɛ ba gyeŋ maŋ yee? Walaŋ ŋon ɔ naa maŋ na, ɔ te naa me kya ŋon mɔ. Sena yaa n ke tale yako maŋ nyi, Maa wola ŋon me kya ŋon bela.

eng: The next day the great crowd that had come for the festival heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem.
local: Tɛɛ kena nɛ, balaŋ burum baŋ bɔɔ kɔŋ nyeekelɛɛwee kedi keŋ man nyiiwɔ nyi Yesu dɔŋ gbɛɛ ɔ ne kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man.

eng: Dont you believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words I have spoken to you I dont speak on my own. The Father who dwells in me does his works.
local: Filipo n bɛɛ lɛɛ di nyi daa na me kya ŋon yɛɛ dokoloŋ yee? Na maŋ gbagba me baam dɔɔ te mee kolosi nombia kɛŋa ya. Mɔna me kya ŋon ɔ doo me man nɛ, ŋon ne tɛɛ la me dɔɔ yɛɛ ɔ tom.

eng: They took palm branches and went out to meet him. They shouted, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Blessings on the king of Israel.
local: Mena dɔɔ be gyae bakɛɛna faareŋse gyu be kaa gyaŋee na e na bɛɛ fae yeesa nyiaa, Lɛɛ daa. De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ waa hiraa walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kɔŋ ɔ yele man nɛ, na waa hiraa Iseraetena gyoo ŋon mɔ.

eng: Trust me when I say that I am in the Father and the Father is in me, or at least believe on account of the works themselves.
local: Me yako nyi daa na me kya ŋon yɛɛ dokoloŋ na, ɛ lɛɛ maŋ di. Akpaa nyi ɛ be lɛɛ maŋ di koraŋ ya nɛ, ɛ kɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan mɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ na ɛ ke lɛɛ maŋ di.

eng: Jesus found a young donkey and sat on it, just as it is written.
local: Yesu gyae afurum te ɔ kyaawɔ ɔ dɔɔ, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see be yako nɛ. Nombia ŋenaŋ yɛna nyiaa.

eng: I assure you that whoever believes in me will do the works that I do. They will do even greater works than these because I am going to the Father.
local: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa lɛɛ maŋ di nɛ, waa yɛɛ abɔɔ ŋan mɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ, te waa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia kela ŋan mɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ. Nawolo nyi mee gyu me kya ŋon gyaŋ.

eng: Dont be afraid, Daughter Zion. Look! Your king is coming , sitting on a donkeys colt .
local: Ɛmɛɛ Gyerusalɛmtena, ɛ na yela ɛ wɔe ke lɔ ŋon ya. Ɛ kɛɛ, ɛ gyoo ne kɔŋ, ɔ kyaa afurum bu dɔɔ ɔ ne kɔŋ.

eng: I will do whatever you ask for in my name, so that the Father can be glorified in the Son.
local: Te kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ ke bɔɔse lee ma yele man na, me kya waa yɛɛ fa ŋon na maŋ ɔ bu ŋon ke yela baa lese ɔ yele.

eng: His disciples didnt understand these things at first. After he was glorified, they remembered that these things had been written about him and that they had done these things to him.
local: Kewalaŋ keŋ nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ te nyii nombia kɛŋa man ya. Mɔna kenaŋ wɔle Wurubuarɛ ka lese Yesu gyoorobiiri wola nɛ te be tɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see be yako lee ɔ wose man nɛ kɔŋala mena nɛ.

eng: When you ask me for anything in my name, I will do it.
local: Akpaa ɛ bɔɔse kpene kamasɛ ma yele man na, maa yɛɛ fa ŋon.

eng: The crowd who had been with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead were testifying about him.
local: Keŋte balaŋ burum baŋ be kpuɛ na Yesu, debaŋ keŋ ɔ be baake Lasarose lee yebɔɔ man te ɔ be gyuusu e lee yeŋ man nɛ, yaasee nombia kɛŋa.

eng: If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
local: Akpaa ɛ ne gyae maŋ na, nafɔ ɛ ke di maŋ mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ.

eng: Thats why the crowd came to meet him, because they had heard about this miraculous sign that he had done.
local: Keŋ dɔɔ te balaŋ burum leewɔ donɔɔ man kɔŋ kaa gyaŋee na e nɛ, nawolo nyi be nyiiwɔ nyi ɔ yɛɛ mena gyakoloŋ nombii kei.

eng: I will ask the Father, and he will send another Companion, who will be with you forever.
local: Te maa waase me kya ŋon na waa fa ŋon walaŋ ŋon waa yako do ŋon, na ɔ kaa kyaa me nawɔɔ man kekpaakekpaa. Ŋon yɛna ɔ feliŋ ŋon waa lese Wurubuarɛ dinɔɔ wola nɛ.

eng: Therefore, the Pharisees said to each other, See! Youve accomplished nothing! Look! The whole world is following him.
local: Botɔɔ te Farasiitena baŋ yako dɔŋa nyiaa, Ŋgba ɛ ne naa nyi da te tale yɛɛ kolo ya na? Ɛ kɛɛ! Balaŋ pou silana e.

eng: This Companion is the Spirit of Truth, whom the world cant receive because it neither sees him nor recognizes him. You know him, because he lives with you and will be with you.
local: Balaŋ baŋ be loo gyakaa tɛɛle kei abɔɔ dɔɔ nɛ, bɔɔ gyae baa tale lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ya. Nawolo nyi bɔɔ ne naa e ya te bɔɔ gyeŋ e mɔ ya. Mɔna ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ ɛ gyeŋ e, nawolo nyi ɔ kyaa na ŋon te ɔ doo ɛ man.

eng: Some Greeks were among those who had come up to worship at the festival.
local: Na Girikitena baale mɔ kpuɛ na balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyu Gyerusalɛm be kaa som Wurubuarɛ Gyudatena nyeekelɛɛwee kedi keŋ man nɛ.

eng: I wont leave you as orphans. I will come to you.
local: Debaŋ keŋ me lam nɛ, mɔɔ gyae maa tina ŋon yela ŋgba yoyolobiise nɛ ya, mɔna maa bese kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ.

eng: They came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and made a request: Sir, we want to see Jesus.
local: Be gyuuwɔ Filipo ŋon ɔ ba lee Bɛtesaida keŋ ke kyaa Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ gyaŋ ke yako e nyiaa, Kegyia, dɛɛ gyae dɛɛ naa Yesu.

eng: Soon the world will no longer see me, but you will see me. Because I live, you will live too.
local: Teka kyomii balaŋ be gyae baa tekaa naa maŋ bela ya, mɔna ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ ɛ kena maŋ. Keŋ me dana nyeedoŋ dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.

eng: Philip told Andrew, and Andrew and Philip told Jesus.
local: Keŋte Filipo mɔ gyuuwɔ ke yako Anderiase. Te be balaŋ bala gyuuwɔ keyako Yesu.

eng: On that day you will know that I am in my Father, you are in me, and I am in you.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ de lii na ɛ kena nyi daa na me kya ŋon kyaa la, te ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ kyaa na maŋ te maŋ mɔ me kyaa na ŋon.

eng: Jesus replied, The time has come for the Human One to be glorified.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tira nombia kɛŋa nɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Debaŋ keŋ te lii nyi baa lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon gyoorobiiri keŋ wola.

eng: Whoever has my commandments and keeps them loves me. Whoever loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love them and reveal myself to them.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ maŋ mmaraase ŋan do te ɔ di ŋe dɔɔ na, ŋon ne gyae na me nombia ta me kya ŋon mɔ waa gyae ŋon ɔ ne gyae me nombia nɛ nombia. Maŋ mɔ maa gyae ɔ nombia na maa lese ma wose wola e.

eng: I assure you that unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it can only be a single seed. But if it dies, it bears much fruit.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, nyi akpaa be ta mo wambelebii do bɔɔ man na ke ka yeŋ ya nɛ, na ke sɛɛ ke yɛɛ bii dokoloŋ. Mɔna nyi akpaa ba mo ke do bɔɔ man te ke da yem te ke de tuu na te kɛɛ see bia burum.

eng: Judas asked, Lord, why are you about to reveal yourself to us and not to the world.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyudase bɔɔse e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, sena te gyae ke tale kɔŋ nyi nyaŋ ka lese n wose wola daa daageŋ, te n be gyae n ka lese wola balaŋ pou ya.

eng: Those who love their lives will lose them, and those who hate their lives in this world will keep them forever.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae ŋon gbagba nyeedoŋ kela Wurubuarɛ nɛ, waa yeŋ leki. Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon lee Wurubuarɛ nombia dɔɔ ɔ be famenɛ na ŋon gbagba nyeedoŋ ya nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa fa e nyeedoŋ kekpaa.

eng: Jesus answered, Whoever loves me will keep my word. My Father will love them, and we will come to them and make our home with them.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Akpaa ŋolo ne gyae maŋ na, waa lɛɛ me nombia ŋan mɛɛ wolo nɛ do, na daa na me kya ke gyae ɔ nombia na de kaa kyaa ɔ gyaŋ.

eng: Whoever serves me must follow me. Wherever I am, there my servant will also be. My Father will honor whoever serves me.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako nyiaa, Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae nyi waa som maŋ nɛ, kaboena nyi waa sila maŋ. Na botɔɔ keŋ me kyaa na, ŋon mɔ ke nyiŋ kpu na maŋ. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ na som maŋ na, me kya mɔ waa lese ɔ yele.

eng: Whoever doesnt love me doesnt keep my words. The word that you hear isnt mine. It is the word of the Father who sent me.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ bɛɛ gyae me nombia ya na, ɔ bee di me nombia ŋan mɛɛ wolo nɛ dɔɔ ya. Nombia ŋan mɛɛ yako ŋon nɛ ta lee me gyaŋ ya mɔna ŋa lee me kya ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ gyaŋ.

eng: Now I am deeply troubled. What should I say? Father, save me from this time? No, for this is the reason I have come to this time.
local: Te Yesu besewɔ yako nyiaa, Nɛnɛɛ dekɔkɔrɔkɔɔ te kyaŋ maŋ nideli, te sena yaa maa yako? Maa yako nyi me kya, lese maŋ lee mena kaale dinaa kei man? Mɔna mena kaale kei dɔɔ te me kɔŋawɔ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ.

eng: I have spoken these things to you while I am with you.
local: Debaŋ keŋ me sɛɛ me kyaa ɛ gyaŋ nɛ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nombia kɛŋa.

eng: Father, glorify your name! Then a voice came from heaven, I have glorified it, and I will glorify it again.
local: Keŋte Yesu fanewɔ nyiaa, Me kya, lese n gyoorobiiri wola. Te be nyii woya ŋaale kolosiwɔ lee adido nyiaa, Me te wulaa lese me gyoorobiiri wola, te maa bese lese ke wola.

eng: The Companion, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you everything and will remind you of everything I told you.
local: Walaŋ ŋon waa yako do ŋon nɛ, ŋon yɛna feliŋ ŋon me kya waa kpila ma yele man nɛ. Waa kaa wola ŋon abɔɔ pou, na waa tɔɔse ŋon abɔɔ ŋan pou mɔɔ yako ŋon nɛ.

eng: The crowd standing there heard and said, Its thunder. Others said, An angel spoke to him.
local: Balaŋ burum baŋ ba seŋɛɛ botɔɔ te bɔɔ nyii woya ŋan nɛ, be man baale yakowɔ nyiaa, Ɔga faela te baale mɔ yakowɔ nyi, Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo kolosi la na e lee adido.

eng: Peace I leave with you. My peace I give you. I give to you not as the world gives. Dont be troubled or afraid.
local: Ma wosefɛɛreŋ dɛɛ kyaa na ŋon, mɛɛ fa ŋon wosefɛɛreŋ. Na wosefɛɛreŋ keŋ balaŋ ne nyim tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ yaa mɛɛ fa ŋon ya, mɔna ma wosefɛɛreŋ gbagba yaa mɛɛ fa ŋon. Ɛ na yele ɛ wɔe ke lɔ ŋon ya, te ɛ na yele gyakoloŋ mɔ ke kyaŋ ŋon ya.

eng: Jesus replied, This voice wasnt for my benefit but for yours.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ te mena nɔɔwoya kɛŋa kɔŋawɔ, na maŋ dɔɔ ya.

eng: You have heard me tell you, Im going away and returning to you. If you loved me, you would be happy that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than me.
local: Me yako ŋon nyi, mee gyu mɔna maa bese kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ. Akpaa nyi yeŋ ne gyae me nombia na nafɔ gyae ke yɛɛ ŋon gyoŋ nyi mee gyu me kya ŋon gyaŋ, nawolo nyi ɔ kela maŋ.

eng: Now is the time for judgment of this world. Now this worlds ruler will be thrown out.
local: Nɛnɛɛ yɛna debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa di balaŋ nombia tɛɛle kei dɔɔ. Nɛnɛɛ te Wurubuarɛ waa gegi ɔbɔnsam ŋon ɔ ne di gyoori tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ lee ɔ gbeli keŋ dɔɔ.

eng: I have told you before it happens so that when it happens you will believe.
local: Me wulaa mɛɛ yako nombia kɛŋa pou pɛna ŋa ke kɔŋ, na akpaa nyi ŋa de kɔŋ na, ɛ ke nyiŋ lɛɛ maŋ di.

eng: When I am lifted up from the earth, I will draw everyone to me. .
local: Mɔna maŋ dɔɔ nyi akpaa be gyina maŋ adido lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, maa wɔŋ walaŋ kamasɛ benaa ma wose.

eng: I wont say much more to you because this worlds ruler is coming. He has nothing on me.
local: Mɔɔ gyae maa tekaa kolosi na ŋon burum bela ya, nawolo nyi ɔbɔnsam ŋon ɔ ne di doŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ ne kɔŋ. Ɔ be taŋ doŋ me dɔɔ berɛ ya.

eng: He said this to show how he was going to die.
local: Ɔ kekolosi kei na wola yeŋ keŋ waa kaa yeŋ nɛ baa.

eng: Rather, he comes so that the world will know that I love the Father and do just as the Father has commanded me. Get up. Were leaving this place.
local: mɔna mɛɛ gyae balaŋ pou ka gyeŋ nyi mɛɛ gyae me kya ŋon nombia, te kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɔ yako maŋ nyi maa yɛɛ na keŋ te mɛɛ yɛɛ. Ɛ koro dɛɛ lee kɛbo.

eng: The crowd responded, We have heard from the Law that the Christ remains forever. How can you say that the Human One must be lifted up? Who is this Human One.
local: Botɔɔ te balaŋ burum baŋ tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, De kalawɔ lee da mmaraa tɔne man nyiaa, Nyeelɛɛre ŋon waa kyaa keŋ ɔ be gyae waa yeŋ ya. Te sena yaa n ne yako nyi kaboena nyi baa gyina Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon adido? Amɔte yɛna Deniwalaŋ Bu kei.

eng: Jesus replied, The light is with you for only a little while. Walk while you have the light so that darkness doesnt overtake you. Those who walk in the darkness dont know where they are going.
local: Te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Keŋmaŋee keŋ nyiŋa ke kyaa ɛ gyaŋ nɛ ɛ ta ke man na ditiŋtɛɛ be nyiŋ futaa ŋon ya. Nawolo nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ dɔŋɛɛ ditiŋtɛɛ man nɛ, ɔ ba gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ ɔ ne gyu ya.

eng: As long as you have the light, believe in the light so that you might become people whose lives are determined by the light. After Jesus said these things, he went away and hid from them.
local: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne fa keŋmaŋee nyiŋa ɔ kyaa ɛ gyaŋ nɛ, ɛ lɛɛ e di na ɛ kenyiŋ bese balaŋ baŋ be doo keŋmaŋee man. Yesu kekolosi nombia kɛŋa taŋ nɛ, ɔ wɔŋa ɔ wose kekyaa yenaŋ daale keŋ bɔɔ ne naa e ya.

eng: Jesus had done many miraculous signs before the people, but they didnt believe in him.
local: Yesu yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia burum be siaman, mɔna kɛŋa pou wɔle na be bɛe ba te lɛɛ e di ya.

eng: This was to fulfill the word of the prophet Isaiah: Lord, who has believed through our message? To whom is the arm of the Lord fully revealed?.
local: Kei yela nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya be yako nɛ kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Yesaya yakowɔ nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, amɔte lɛɛ na nombia ŋan dɔɔ yako nɛ di? Amɔte te de Gbeŋgyoo lese ɔ wɔɔle wola.

eng: Isaiah explains why they couldnt believe.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ bɔɔ ne tale baa lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia di ya ŋgba mena keŋ Yesaya be yako nɛ.

eng: He made their eyes blind and closed their minds so that they might not see with their eyes, understand with their minds, and turn their lives around— and I would heal them.
local: Ɔ besewɔ yako nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ te yɛlɛɛ be sia na bɔɔ nyiŋ naa ya, te ɔ tɔɔ ba agyueŋse na bɔɔ tale nyii kolo man ya. Na mena ya na nafɔ baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na baa kɔŋ me gyaŋ na maa kyɔ wɔ.

eng: Isaiah said these things because he saw Jesus glory; he spoke about Jesus.
local: Yesaya te kolosi nombia kɛŋa yakaa ya, mɔna ɔ wulaa naa Yesu gyoorobiiri keŋ dinɔɔ keŋ dɔɔ te ɔ kolosiwɔ lee ɔ wose man.

eng: Even so, many leaders believed in him, but they wouldnt acknowledge their faith because they feared that the Pharisees would expel them from the synagogue.
local: Nombia kɛŋa wɔle nɛ Gyudatena kegyiise burum lɛɛ Yesu di, mɔna Farasiitena baŋ dɔɔ ba te tale lese ba kelɛɛdi keŋ debɔɔ ya, nawolo nyi be yeewɔ nyi Farasiitena baŋ baa tɔɔ wɔ gbɛɛ nyi be na kɔŋ bɔ ɔsom deni man bela ya.

eng: They believed, but they loved human praise more than Gods glory.
local: Be ta lese ba kelɛɛdi keŋ debɔɔ ya nawolo nyi bɛɛ gyae bo nyi balaŋ ka lese ba yele kela Wurubuarɛ ka lese ba yele.

eng: Jesus shouted, Whoever believes in me doesnt believe in me but in the one who sent me.
local: Keŋte Yesu gyinaa ɔ nɔɔ kolosi nyiaa, Nyaŋ ŋon n lɛɛ maŋ di nɛ, na maageŋ te n lɛɛwɔ di ya mɔna ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ mɔ yaa n lɛɛwɔ di.

eng: Whoever sees me sees the one who sent me.
local: Nyaŋ walaŋ ŋon n naa maŋ na, n te naa walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ.

eng: I have come as a light into the world so that everyone who believes in me wont live in darkness.
local: Me kɔŋae tɛɛle kei dɔɔ ŋgba keŋmaŋee nɛ na maa ŋmaŋee fa balaŋ. Nyaŋ ŋon n lɛɛ maŋ di na n be gyae n ke dɔɔ ditiŋtɛɛ man ya.

eng: If people hear my words and dont keep them, I dont judge them. I didnt come to judge the world but to save it.
local: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ nyii me nombia te ɔ te lɛɛ ŋa do ya na, mɔɔ gyae maa di ɔ nombia ya. Nawolo nyi ma te kɔŋ me kaa di balaŋ nombia tɛɛle kei dɔɔ na maa bu wɔ fɔɔ ya, mɔna me kɔŋae me kaa lɛɛ ba nyee.

eng: Whoever rejects me and doesnt receive my words will be judged at the last day by the word I have spoken.
local: Nyaŋ ŋon n bɛɛ maŋ te n te lɛɛ me nombia ya na, nombiadiiri ŋolo kyaa keŋ waa di n nombia. Nɔɔwoya ŋan mɔɔ kolosi nɛ, ŋan te baa mo di n nombia wee ɔtomante keŋ man na baa bu neŋ fɔɔ.

eng: I dont speak on my own, but the Father who sent me commanded me regarding what I should speak and say.
local: Nawolo nyi ma te kolosi maŋ gbagba me doŋ man ya, mɔna me kya ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ, ŋon yako na maŋ kpene keŋ maa yako na mena keŋ maa yako ke.

eng: I know that his commandment is eternal life. Therefore, whatever I say is just as the Father has said to me.
local: Te maŋ gyeŋ mɔ nyi kpene keŋ ɔ be yako daa nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ, keŋ ne faa na nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ. Mena dɔɔ kpene keŋ mɛɛ yako nɛ, yɛɛ keŋ me kya be yako maŋ nyi maa yako.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he looked up to heaven and said, Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son, so that the Son can glorify you.
local: Yesu ke yako nombia kɛŋa taŋ nɛ, ɔ gyinaa ɔ sia kɛɛ adido te ɔ fanewɔ nyiaa, Taata, debaŋ keŋ te lii. Lese maŋ n bu ŋon yele na maŋ mɔ ka lese n yele.

eng: You gave him authority over everyone so that he could give eternal life to everyone you gave him.
local: Nawolo nyi n te fa maŋ doŋ balaŋ pou dɔɔ, nyi maa fa baŋ pou n ba mo do maŋ me nyiŋmaa man nɛ nyeedoŋ kekpaakekpaa.

eng: This is eternal life: to know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you sent.
local: Nyeedoŋ kekpaa kenaŋ yɛna nyiaa, balaŋ pou baa gyeŋ neŋ na baa gyeŋ nyi nyaŋ nyaageŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ ŋon n ne di anokoare. Na baa bese gyeŋ maŋ Yesu Kristo ŋon n be kpili nɛ.

eng: I have glorified you on earth by finishing the work you gave me to do.
local: Me ta lese n yele tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, nawolo nyi ma te yɛɛ tom keŋ n ba fa maŋ nɛ kelii kɔ ɔto.

eng: Now, Father, glorify me in your presence with the glory I shared with you before the world was created.
local: Taata, nɛnɛɛ lese me gyoorobiiri keŋ me dana n siaman pɛte n be yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ wola.

eng: I have revealed your name to the people you gave me from this world. They were yours and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
local: Me ta lese n dinɔɔ wola balaŋ baŋ n ba lese wɔ lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ fa maŋ nɛ. Be yɛɛ n balaŋwɔ te n moo wɔ fa maŋ. Be te di n nombia ŋan dɔɔ.

eng: Now they know that everything you have given me comes from you.
local: te nɛnɛɛ ba te naa nyi kpene kamasɛ keŋ me dana nɛ lee n gyaŋ.

eng: This is because I gave them the words that you gave me, and they received them. They truly understood that I came from you, and they believed that you sent me.
local: Nawolo nyi ma te yako wɔ nombii kamasɛ keŋ n be yako maŋ nɛ, te be lɛɛ ŋa do. Ba te naa nyi ke yɛɛ ampaŋ nyi ma lee n gyaŋ, te ba te lɛɛ di nyi nyaŋ kpila na maŋ.

eng: Im praying for them. Im not praying for the world but for those you gave me, because they are yours.
local: Mɛɛ fane baa fa wɔ. Na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou yaa mɛɛ fane maa fa ya. Mɛɛ fane maa fa balaŋ baŋ n ba mo wɔ fa maŋ nɛ, nawolo nyi be yɛɛ n wui.

eng: Everything that is mine is yours and everything that is yours is mine; I have been glorified in them.
local: Kpene kamasɛ keŋ n dana nɛ yɛɛ me wui, te keŋ maŋ mɔ dana nɛ yɛɛ n wui. Te n te tɛɛ be dɔɔ lese me gyoorobiiri wola.

eng: Im no longer in the world, but they are in the world, even as Im coming to you. Holy Father, watch over them in your name, the name you gave me, that they will be one just as we are one.
local: Nɛnɛɛ mɛɛ kɔŋ baa n gyaŋ. Mɔɔ gyae maa tekaa kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ bela ya, mɔna baŋ sɛɛ be kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ. Me kya ŋon ayimɛɛ be doo n man ya, doŋ keŋ n ba mo gyee maŋ nɛ, mo mena doŋ kenaŋ gyee wɔ nideli, na baa yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ ŋgba mena keŋ daa na neŋ yɛɛ dokoloŋ nɛ.

eng: When I was with them, I watched over them in your name, the name you gave to me, and I kept them safe. None of them were lost, except the one who was destined for destruction, so that scripture would be fulfilled.
local: Debaŋ keŋ me kyaa be gyaŋ nɛ, me gyee wɔ nideli lee n doŋ keŋ n ba do me man nɛ dɔɔ. Me gyee wɔ nideli keŋ be man ŋolo ta yoo ya. Ŋon kaboena nyi waa yo nɛ waageŋ yoo la, na ke yela Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ke nyiŋ kɔŋ mena.

eng: Now Im coming to you and I say these things while Im in the world so that they can share completely in my joy.
local: Nɛnɛɛ mɛɛ kɔŋ n gyaŋ. Te debaŋ kei keŋ me sɛɛ me kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, mɛɛ yako nombia kɛŋa baa na ke yela gyoŋ keŋ doo me maŋ nɛ ke nyiŋ doo be man nideli.

eng: I gave your word to them and the world hated them, because they dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: Ma te kolosi n nombia fa wɔ mɔna tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ kɔla wɔ, nawolo nyi baŋ na wɔ ta tekaa yɛɛ dokoloŋ ya, ŋgba mena keŋ daa na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ ta tekaa yɛɛ dokoloŋ ya nɛ.

eng: Im not asking that you take them out of this world but that you keep them safe from the evil one.
local: Mɔɔ ne fane nyi nya lese wɔ lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ ya, mɔna mɛɛ fane bo nyi nya gyee wɔ nideli na bɔɔ yale walaŋkum ŋon kɔta man ya.

eng: They dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: Ŋgba mena keŋ daa na tɛɛle kei balaŋ te yɛɛ dokoloŋ ya nɛ, mena kee yaa baŋ mɔ na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ te yɛɛ dokoloŋ ya.

eng: Make them holy in the truth; your word is truth.
local: Mo nya anokoare nombia ŋan desina wɔ see. Nawolo nyi n nombia ŋan doo na ŋe gbɛɛ.

eng: As you sent me into the world, so I have sent them into the world.
local: Mena keŋ n be kpila maŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, mena ke yaa maŋ mɔ ne kpila wɔ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ.

eng: I made myself holy on their behalf so that they also would be made holy in the truth.
local: Baŋ dɔɔ te ma moo ma wose fa neŋ, na baŋ mɔ ke nyiŋ mo ba wose fa neŋ kekpaakekpaa.

eng: Im not praying only for them but also for those who believe in me because of their word.
local: Na me kaseela kɛwɔ baageŋ yaa mɛɛ fane fa ya. Mɔna mɛɛ fane mɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ pou baa nyii me kaseela kɛwɔ nombia te baa lɛɛ maŋ di nɛ.

eng: I pray they will be one, Father, just as you are in me and I am in you. I pray that they also will be in us, so that the world will believe that you sent me.
local: Mɛɛ fane bela nyiaa, be popou baa yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ ŋgba mena keŋ me doo nyaŋ me kya man te nyaŋ mɔ n doo me man nɛ. Mena ke yaa yela baa doo de man na tɛɛle kei balaŋ pou ke nyiŋ lɛɛ di nyi nyaŋ kpila na maŋ.

eng: Ive given them the glory that you gave me so that they can be one just as we are one.
local: Ma te fa wɔ gyoorobiiri keŋ n ba mo fa maŋ nɛ, na baa yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ ŋgba mena keŋ daa na neŋ yɛɛ dokoloŋ te loŋ bɛɛ lam da nsana ya nɛ.

eng: Im in them and you are in me so that they will be made perfectly one. Then the world will know that you sent me and that you have loved them just as you loved me.
local: Me doo be man ŋgba mena keŋ nyaŋ mɔ n doo me man nɛ, na baa nyiŋ yɛɛ dokoloŋ keŋ nyi loŋ gba be lam ba nsana ya. Na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ ke nyiŋ gyeŋ nyi nyaŋ kpila na maŋ, te n ne gyae be nombia ŋgba mena keŋ n ne gyae me nombia nɛ.

eng: Father, I want those you gave me to be with me where I am. Then they can see my glory, which you gave me because you loved me before the creation of the world.
local: Taata! Mɛɛ gyae nyi me kaseela baŋ n ba mo wɔ fa maŋ nɛ, baa kyaa me gyaŋ botɔɔ keŋ me kyaa nɛ, na baa nyiŋ naa me gyoorobiiri keŋ n ba mo fa maŋ nɛ. N moo gyoorobiiri kei fa maŋ nawolo nyi, n wulaa n ne gyae maŋ pɛte n yɛɛ tɛɛle kei.

eng: Righteous Father, even the world didnt know you, but Ive known you, and these believers know that you sent me.
local: Me kya, nyaŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋon n gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ. Balaŋ baŋ be loo gyakaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, be ta gyeŋa ta neŋ ya mɔna maŋ dɔɔ ma gyeŋ neŋ. Te me kaseela baŋ mɔ gyeŋ nyi nyaŋ kpila na maŋ.

eng: Ive made your name known to them and will continue to make it known so that your love for me will be in them, and I myself will be in them.
local: Me ta lese n dinɔɔ wola wɔ te maa bese lese ke wola wɔ bela, na kegyae keŋ n ne gyae maŋ nɛ ke nyiŋ doo be man na maŋ mɔ ke doo be man.

eng: I am the true vine, and my Father is the vineyard keeper.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, Maŋ yɛna daŋ keŋ gbagba, te me kya yɛna wɔɔfarale ŋon.

eng: He removes any of my branches that dont produce fruit, and he trims any branch that produces fruit so that it will produce even more fruit.
local: Tombii kamasɛ keŋ doo me man te ke bɛɛ see bia ya nɛ waa kara ke leki. Mɔna tombii kamasɛ keŋ doo me man te kɛɛ se bia na, waa kpaase ke na ke kenyiŋ se bia burum.

eng: You are already trimmed because of the word I have spoken to you.
local: Lee nombia ma ayakoŋ ŋon kɛŋa dɔɔ nɛ, ŋe taŋ ke yela ɛ wose ta lee keŋ ayimɛɛ be doo ɛ man ya.

eng: Remain in me, and I will remain in you. A branch cant produce fruit by itself, but must remain in the vine. Likewise, you cant produce fruit unless you remain in me.
local: Ɛ doo me maŋ, na maŋ mɔ ke doo ɛ man. Tombii kamasɛ be kyaa nyi ke gyae ke tale se bia ka wose dɔɔ, keŋ kɔɔ towaa na ke daŋ ya. Mena mɔ yaa ɛmɛɛ mɔ be gyae ɛ ke tale yɛɛ kolo ɛ wose dɔɔ keŋ nyi ɛ be doo me man ya.

eng: I am the vine; you are the branches. If you remain in me and I in you, then you will produce much fruit. Without me, you cant do anything.
local: Maŋ yɛna daŋ keŋ te ɛmɛɛ yɛna ke tombia ŋan. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ doo me man, te maŋ mɔ doo ɔ man nɛ, waa se bia burum. Nawolo nyi ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale yɛɛ kolo ɛ wose dɔɔ keŋ mɔɔ kpuɛ na ŋon ya.

eng: If you dont remain in me, you will be like a branch that is thrown out and dries up. Those branches are gathered up, thrown into a fire, and burned.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ be doo me man ya nɛ, ɔ yɛɛ ŋgba tombii keŋ bɔɔ kare leki te kɔɔ wolo nɛ. Baa yilaa mena tombia ŋenaŋ bo na baa mo ŋa do boalaŋ man na ŋa ke kpa.

eng: If you remain in me and my words remain in you, ask for whatever you want and it will be done for you.
local: Nyi akpaa ɛ sɛɛ doo me man te me nombia ŋan mɔ de doo ɛ man nɛ, kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ ke bɔɔse na maa yɛɛ fa ŋon.

eng: My Father is glorified when you produce much fruit and in this way prove that you are my disciples.
local: Akpaa ɛ se bia burum nɛ, ke gyae ke yela baa lese me kya ŋon yele. Kei gyae ke yela balaŋ ka gyeŋ nyi ɛ yɛɛ me kaseelawɔ.

eng: As the Father loved me, I too have loved you. Remain in my love.
local: Ŋgba mena keŋ me kya ŋon ne gyae me nombia nɛ, mena ke yaa maŋ mɔ ne gyae ɛ nombia. Mena dɔɔ ɛ yela maa gyae ŋon kelii ɔto.

eng: If you keep my commandments, you will remain in my love, just as I kept my Fathers commandments and remain in his love.
local: Nyi akpaa ɛ di ma mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ nɛ, maa gyae ɛ nombia kelii ɔto ŋgba mena keŋ mɔɔ di me kya ŋon mmaraase dɔɔ te ɔ ne gyae me nombia nɛ.

eng: I have said these things to you so that my joy will be in you and your joy will be complete.
local: Mɛɛ yako mena nombia kɛŋa baa na ke yela ma gyoŋ keŋ ke doo ɛ man, na ɛ ke nyiŋ di gyoŋ nideli.

eng: This is my commandment: love each other just as I have loved you.
local: Mmaraa keŋ mɛɛ fa ŋon yɛna nyiaa, ɛ gyae dɔŋa ŋgba mena keŋ mɔɔ gyae ŋon nɛ.

eng: No one has greater love than to give up ones life for ones friends.
local: Walaŋ kegyae daale be kyaa keŋ ka kela kegyae keŋ ŋolo waa gyae ɔ gyoona na waa yeŋ fa wɔ nɛ ya.

eng: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
local: Nyi akpaa ɛ yɛɛ kpene keŋ mɔɔ yako ŋon nyi yaa yɛɛ nɛ, mena na ɛ yɛɛ me gyoona gbagbawɔ.

eng: I dont call you servants any longer, because servants dont know what their master is doing. Instead, I call you friends, because everything I heard from my Father I have made known to you.
local: Mɔɔ tekaa baake ŋon me tewulɛɛ bela ya, nawolo nyi tobaale ba gyeŋ ɔ gbeŋgyoo nyeeman nombia ya. Mɔna mɛɛ baake ŋon me gyoona, nawolo nyi ma te yako ŋon kpene kamasɛ keŋ me kya be yako maŋ.

eng: You didnt choose me, but I chose you and appointed you so that you could go and produce fruit and so that your fruit could last. As a result, whatever you ask the Father in my name, he will give you.
local: Na ɛmɛɛ lese na maŋ ya, mɔna maŋ lese na ŋon. Ma lese ŋon bo nyi yaa yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan ŋe dana tɔnɔɔ debaŋ kamasɛ. Ɛ yɛɛ mena na me kya waa fa ŋon kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ ka sola e lee ma yele man.

eng: I give you these commandments so that you can love each other.
local: Mmaraa keŋ mɛɛ fa ŋon yɛna nyi, Ɛ gyae dɔŋa. .

eng: If the world hates you, know that it hated me first.
local: Akpaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ ne kɔla ŋon nɛ, ɛ tɔɔse nyi mena te be taŋgbɛɛwɔ kɔla maŋ.

eng: If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. However, I have chosen you out of the world, and you dont belong to the world. This is why the world hates you.
local: Nyi yeŋ yɛɛ ŋgba balaŋ baŋ be kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, nafɔ baa gyae ŋon ŋgba baŋ gbagba be balaŋ nɛ. Mɔna kpene keŋ dɔɔ te bɛɛ kɔla ŋon yɛna nyi me ta lese ŋon lee be man, te ɛ ba tekaa kpuɛ na wɔ ya.

eng: Remember what I told you, Servants arent greater than their master. If the world harassed me, it will harass you too. If it kept my word, it will also keep yours.
local: Ɛ tɔɔse nɔɔwoya ŋan mɔɔ yako ŋon nɛ nyi, Tobaale kamasɛ ba kela ɔ gbeŋgyoo ya. Nyi be naase maŋ diyem nɛ, mena ke te baa naase ɛmɛɛ mɔ diyem. Nyi akpaa be lɛɛ me kawola keŋ di na, mena mɔ te baa lɛɛ ɛmɛɛ mɔ wui di.

eng: The world will do all these things to you on account of my name, because it doesnt know the one who sent me.
local: Mɔna lee maŋ dɔɔ baa mo mena nombia kɛŋa pou yɛɛ ŋon, nawolo nyi bɔɔ gyeŋ walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ ya.

eng: If I hadnt come and spoken to the people of this world, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
local: Akpaa meŋ te kɔŋ kaa kolosi fa wɔ ya nɛ, nafɔ bɔɔ gyae baa di fɔɔ be dukum keyɛɛ man ya. Mɔna nɛnɛɛ nyi be yɛɛ dukum na, bɔɔ gyae baa tale yako nyi be ta gyem bo te be yɛɛwɔ ya.

eng: Whoever hates me also hates the Father.
local: Nyaŋ ŋon n ne kɔla maŋ na, n ne kɔla me kya ŋon mɔ.

eng: If I hadnt done works among them that no one else had done, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
local: Akpaa meŋ te yɛɛ tomse ŋan ŋolo te yɛɛ ta see be man ya nɛ, nafɔ beŋ te di fɔɔ be dukum keyɛɛ man ya. Mɔna nɛnɛɛ ba te naa gyakoloŋ nombia kɛŋa pou mɔna bɛɛ kɔla daa na me kya ŋon.

eng: This fulfills the word written in their Law, They hated me without a reason.
local: Kei yela kpene keŋ bɔɔ ŋmarase see ba mmaraa tɔne man nɛ kɔŋawɔ mena. Be ŋmarasewɔ nyiaa, Be kɔla maŋ bo yakaa.

eng: When the Companion comes, whom I will send from the Father—the Spirit of Truth who proceeds from the Father—he will testify about me.
local: Mɔna maa yela Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ka lee me kya ŋon gyaŋ kɔŋ na ɔ kaa kyaa na ŋon. Ŋon ne gyae la waa yako do ŋon na waa lese anokoare keŋ doo Wurubuarɛ man nɛ wola ŋon, na waa di adansɛɛ lee ma wose man.

eng: You will testify too, because you have been with me from the beginning.
local: Te ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ di adansɛɛ lee ma wose man, nawolo nyi lee kewalaŋ keŋ pou na ɛ kyaa me gyaŋ.

eng: After he said these things, Jesus went out with his disciples and crossed over to the other side of the Kidron Valley. He and his disciples entered a garden there.
local: Yesu ke fane taŋ nɛ, ŋon na ɔ kaseela baŋ korowɔ gyu ka toŋ boŋobii keŋ bɛɛ baake ke Kidrɔn nɛ, te be ka gyoowɔ oliifi wɔɔbii daale man.

eng: Judas, his betrayer, also knew the place because Jesus often gathered there with his disciples.
local: Na Gyudase ŋon ɔ ba lese Yesu fa nɛ mɔ gyeŋ botɔɔ, nawolo nyi Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ ne kyaa gyu ke gyaŋee botɔɔ.

eng: Judas brought a company of soldiers and some guards from the chief priests and Pharisees. They came there carrying lanterns, torches, and weapons.
local: Mena dɔɔ Gyudase moo balaŋ burum baŋ be dana kanease na kefuluse te ba doona yoo abɔɔ nɛ gyu botɔɔ. Mena balaŋ kɛwɔ baale yɛɛ yoonɔɔtenawɔ, te baale mɔ yɛɛ kotɔtɔɔtena baŋ bɛɛ deke Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ nɛ. Gyudatena saese kegyiise na Farasiitena kpila na wɔ.

eng: Jesus knew everything that was to happen to him, so he went out and asked, Who are you looking for.
local: Keŋ Yesu gyeŋ kpene keŋ ne kɔŋ ɔ dɔɔ dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ leewɔ ɔ kaseela baŋ man kɔŋ siaman te ɔ bɔɔse balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, Amɔte yaa ɛ ne gyae.

eng: They answered, Jesus the Nazarene. He said to them, I Am.
local: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Yesu Nasarɛte baale ŋon yaa dɛɛ gyae. Te Yesu yako wɔ nyi, Maŋ yaa. Debaŋ kenaŋ na Gyudase ŋon waa lese Yesu fa nɛ mɔ kpuɛ balaŋ baŋ man.

eng: When he said, I Am, they shrank back and fell to the ground.
local: Yesu ke yako wɔ nyi Maŋ yaa nɛ, be tuuwɔ lee wɔle wɔle te ba ke yalawɔ tɛɛle man.

eng: He asked them again, Who are you looking for? They said, Jesus the Nazarene.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu besewɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Amɔte yaa ɛ ne gyae? Te be yakowɔ nyi, Yesu Nasarɛte baale ŋon yaa dɛɛ gyae.

eng: Jesus answered, I told you, I Am. If you are looking for me, then let these people go.
local: Keŋte Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa Me wulaa yako ŋon nyi maŋ yaa. Akpaa maŋ te ɛ ne gyae berɛ na, ɛ yela me balaŋ kɛwɔ ke ta.

eng: This was so that the word he had spoken might be fulfilled: I didnt lose anyone of those whom you gave me.
local: Kei yela nombia ŋan ɔ be wulaa yako nɛ kɔŋawɔ mena. Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Balaŋ baŋ nyaŋ me kya ba mo fa maŋ nɛ, ma te yeli be man dokoloŋ ka yo ya.

eng: Then Simon Peter, who had a sword, drew it and struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Botɔɔ na Simɔn Pita dana yoosila, te ɔ wɔŋa ke lese kara saese kegyia ŋon tobaale ŋolo dunoluŋ deŋele lese ke lɔ. Tobaale ŋonaŋ yele yɛna Maakuse.

eng: Jesus told Peter, Put your sword away! Am I not to drink the cup the Father has given me.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu fae Pita nyiaa, Besena n sila keŋ do ke bɔɔlee man. N bɛɛ gyae nyi maa naa diyem keŋ me kya be yako nyi me kaa naa nɛ yee.

eng: Then the company of soldiers, the commander, and the guards from the Jewish leaders took Jesus into custody. They bound him.
local: Keŋte yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon na ɔ yoonɔɔtena dikpii keŋ, na Gyudatena kotɔtɔɔtena baŋ kyaŋa Yesu te be bake e.

eng: and led him first to Annas. He was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year. .
local: te be taŋgbɛɛwɔ mo e gyu Anase gyaŋ. Kaifase ŋon ɔ yɛɛ saese kegyia e kulutoo kenaŋ man nɛ, ɔ neŋgya yɛna Anase ŋonaŋ.

eng: Caiaphas was the one who had advised the Jewish leaders that it was better for one person to die for the people.
local: Kaifase kei faa na Gyudatena agyueŋ nyi, dei nyi walaŋ dokoloŋ waa yeŋ fa balaŋ baŋ pou.

eng: Simon Peter and another disciple followed Jesus. Because this other disciple was known to the high priest, he went with Jesus into the high priests courtyard.
local: Debaŋ keŋ bɔɔ mo Yesu bee gyu saese kegyia ŋon dɛɛ nɛ, na Simɔn Pita na ɔ dɔɔ kaseela baŋ man ŋolo silana e wɔle wɔle. Keŋ kaseele ɔ dɔɔ ŋon gyeŋ saese kegyia ŋon dɔɔ nɛ, ŋon sila Yesu ka gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man.

eng: However, Peter stood outside near the gate. Then the other disciple came out and spoke to the woman stationed at the gate, and she brought Peter in.
local: Mɔna Pita ŋon dɔe denanɔɔ man. Botɔɔ te kaseele ŋon saese kegyia ŋon gyeŋ nɛ, besewɔ kɔŋ kaa kolosi na alebu ŋon ɔ na deke denanɔɔ keŋ nɛ nyi waa yela Pita mɔ ka gyoo. Keŋte ɔ moo e gyu dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man.

eng: The servant woman stationed at the gate asked Peter, Arent you one of this mans disciples? Im not, he replied.
local: Keŋte alebu ŋon bɔɔse Pita nyiaa, Ŋgba nyaŋ mɔ n yɛɛ Yesu kaseela baŋ man ŋolo e? Te Pita tiranɔɔ nyi, Koa, ma te yɛɛ be man ŋolo e ya.

eng: The servants and the guards had made a fire because it was cold. They were standing around it, warming themselves. Peter joined them there, standing by the fire and warming himself.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na waare kyaa, mena dɔɔ kotɔtɔɔtena baŋ na dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man tomyɛɛra baale doo boalaŋ bee woli. Te Pita mɔ gyuuwɔ ka seŋ be gyaŋ ɔ ne woli boalaŋ keŋ.

eng: Meanwhile, the chief priest questioned Jesus about his disciples and his teaching.
local: Be ka mo Yesu gyu saese kegyia ŋon dekpaŋalaŋ man nɛ, ɔ bɔɔse Yesu nombia lee ɔ kaseela na ɔ kawola keŋ wose man.

eng: Jesus answered, Ive spoken openly to the world. Ive always taught in synagogues and in the temple, where all the Jews gather. Ive said nothing in private.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Ma seŋawɔ balaŋ man kolosi. Debaŋ kamasɛ mɔ na mɛɛ wola Wurubuarɛ nombia Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man, na Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ Gyudatena pou ne kyaa gyaŋee ke man nɛ man. Mɔna ma te yako kolo weese ya.

eng: Why ask me? Ask those who heard what I told them. They know what I said.
local: Woŋ dɔɔ te n ne bɔɔse maŋ mena nombia kɛŋa? Bɔɔse balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ nyii me kawola keŋ nɛ. Baŋ gyeŋa na kpene keŋ mɔɔ yako nɛ.

eng: After Jesus spoke, one of the guards standing there slapped Jesus in the face. Is that how you would answer the high priest? he asked.
local: Yesu kekolosi nombia kɛŋa nɛ te kegyiise baŋ man ŋolo gyɔɔ e ɔ lakaa man te ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, Mena te kaboena nyi nya tiranɔɔ fa saese kegyia ŋon? .

eng: Jesus replied, If I speak wrongly, testify about what was wrong. But if I speak correctly, why do you strike me.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, Akpaa me yako nombiikum daale na wola maŋ ke. Te akpaa me nombia doo na ke gbɛɛ na weera te ne gyɔ maŋ.

eng: Then Annas sent him, bound, to Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Keŋte Anase yela ba moo Yesu gyu saese kegyia Kaifase gyaŋ, na ɔ sɛɛ ɔ doo ŋmeeseŋ ŋan bɔɔ mo bake e nɛ man.

eng: Meanwhile, Simon Peter was still standing with the guards, warming himself. They asked, Arent you one of his disciples? Peter denied it, saying, Im not.
local: Simɔn Pita ka seŋ botɔɔ ɔ ne woli boalaŋ keŋ nɛ te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, Ŋgba nyaŋ mɔ n yɛɛ Yesu kaseela baŋ man ŋolo e? Keŋte Pita tiranɔɔ nyi Koa, ma te yɛɛ be man ŋolo e ya.

eng: A servant of the high priest, a relative of the one whose ear Peter had cut off, said to him, Didnt I see you in the garden with him.
local: Botɔɔ te saese kegyia ŋon tobaale ŋolo ŋon ɔ loŋto yɛna tobaale ŋon Pita be kara ɔ deŋele nɛ bɔɔse Pita nyiaa, Ŋgba me naa nyaŋ mɔ n kpuɛ na wɔ oliifi wɔɔbii keŋ man.

eng: Peter denied it again, and immediately a rooster crowed.
local: Keŋte Pita besewɔ bɛɛ nyi koa. Debokenaŋ man te kegyim lɔɔ woya.

eng: The Jewish leaders led Jesus from Caiaphas to the Roman governors palace. It was early in the morning. So that they could eat the Passover, the Jewish leaders wouldnt enter the palace; entering the palace would have made them ritually impure.
local: Tɛɛ kena nebo nebo fuu nɛ, ba moo Yesu lee Kaifase dɛɛ gyu Pilato ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ Romantena gyoo dinaa nawɔɔ man ɔ ne kɛɛ Gyuda tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ dɛɛ. Debaŋ kenaŋ na Gyudatena nyeekelɛɛwee kedi keŋ ta benaa. Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ ta gyoo Pilato dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man ya. Nawolo nyi ba te gyae baa walaa ba wose pɛ baa di ba nyeekelɛɛwee dinaa keŋ ya.

eng: So Pilate went out to them and asked, What charge do you bring against this man.
local: Mena dɔɔ kegyia Pilato leewɔ kɔŋ be gyaŋ debɔɔ man kaa bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Woŋ dukum te balee kei yɛɛwɔ te ɛ ne gyae ɛ ke bu e fɔɔ.

eng: They answered, If he had done nothing wrong, we wouldnt have handed him over to you.
local: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Nyi ŋon te yɛɛ bu kum e ya nɛ, nafɔ dɔɔ mo e kɔŋ kɛbo ya.

eng: Pilate responded, Take him yourselves and judge him according to your Law. The Jewish leaders replied, The Law doesnt allow us to kill anyone. .
local: Botɔɔ te Pilato yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ mo e ta na ɛmɛɛ gbagba kaa di ɔ nombia ŋgba mena keŋ ya mmaraa na wola ŋon nɛ. Keŋte Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ Romantena mmaraa bɛɛ fa daa gbɛɛ nyi dɛɛ ko walaŋ ya.

eng: This was so that Jesus word might be fulfilled when he indicated how he was going to die.
local: Kei yela Yesu nombia ŋan ɔ be wulaa yako see lee ɔ yeŋ keŋ waa yeŋ wose man nɛ kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ.

eng: Pilate went back into the palace. He summoned Jesus and asked, Are you the king of the Jews.
local: Keŋte kegyia Pilato besewɔ gyoo ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ man te ɔ baake Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna Gyudatena gyoo ŋon.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you say this on your own or have others spoken to you about me.
local: Te Yesu mɔ besenawɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, Nyaŋ gbagba nyeeman nombii yaa n ne bɔɔse maŋ nɛ yaa ŋolo yako na ma wose man nombia fa neŋ.

eng: Pilate responded, Im not a Jew, am I? Your nation and its chief priests handed you over to me. What have you done.
local: Botɔɔ te Pilato bɔɔse e nyiaa, Me yɛɛ Gyuda baale e? Nyaŋ gbagba n balaŋ na ɛ saese kegyiise moona neŋ kɔna maŋ. Woŋ dukum te n yɛɛwɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, My kingdom doesnt originate from this world. If it did, my guards would fight so that I wouldnt have been arrested by the Jewish leaders. My kingdom isnt from here.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Me gyoori ta lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ ya, nyi ke deŋ lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, nafɔ me tewulɛɛ baa yoo lɛɛ maŋ na bɔɔ nyiŋ mo maŋ ka do Gyudatena nyiŋmaa man ya. Mɔna me gyoori ta lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ ya.

eng: So you are a king? Pilate said. Jesus answered, You say that I am a king. I was born and came into the world for this reason: to testify to the truth. Whoever accepts the truth listens to my voice.
local: Keŋte Pilato yako e nyiaa, Kenaŋ na ŋgba n yɛɛ gyoo e baa? Te Yesu tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Keŋ yaa n yakowɔ nɛ. Mena nombia kɛŋa dɔɔ te ba lola maŋ, te ŋan mɔ dɔɔ te me kɔŋawɔ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ na me kaa yako anokoare keŋ. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae anokoare keŋ nɛ ɔ ne tei maŋ.

eng: What is truth? Pilate asked. After Pilate said this, he returned to the Jewish leaders and said, I find no grounds for any charge against him.
local: Botɔɔ te Pilato bɔɔse e nyiaa, Woŋ yɛna anokoare kei? Ɔ ke bɔɔse e nombia kɛŋa taŋ nɛ te ɔ besewɔ lee kɔŋ Gyudatena baŋ gyaŋ kaa yako wɔ nyiaa, Ma ta naa nombiikum daale lee ɔ wose man ya.

eng: You have a custom that I release one prisoner for you at Passover. Do you want me to release for you the king of the Jews.
local: Mɔna ke yɛɛ ya mmaraa ke nyi ɛ nyeekelɛɛwee kedi kamasɛ man na, maa tina balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ kyam tɔ deni nɛ man dokoloŋ yela fa ŋon. Mena dɔɔ ɛ ne gyae nyi maa tina Gyudatena gyoo ŋon yela yaa sena.

eng: They shouted, Not this man! Give us Barabbas.
local: Keŋte be fae yeesa yako nyiaa, Koa, na Yesu te dɛɛ gyae nyi nya tina e yela ya. Barabase te dɛɛ gyae nyi nya tina e yela. Barabase kei yɛna walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kyaa koro seŋ tia gyooneŋ te ɔ ba ko walaŋ nɛ.

eng: I have said these things to you so that you wont fall away.
local: Mɛɛ yako mena nombia kɛŋa pou baa na ɛ be nyiŋ tina ɛ kelɛɛdi keŋ yela ya.

eng: They will expel you from the synagogue. The time is coming when those who kill you will think that they are doing a service to God.
local: Gyudatena kegyiise baa tɔ ŋon nyi ɛ na kɔŋ bɔ ɔsom denɛɛ man donɔɔ kamasɛ man ya. Debaŋ daale gyae kelii nɛ, akpaa walaŋ da ko ɛ man ŋolo na waa yako nyi Wurubuarɛ ɔsom nombia dɔɔ te ɔ koo ŋon. Na waa yako nyi ɔ yɛɛ deeli baa fa Wurubuarɛ.

eng: They will do these things because they dont know the Father or me.
local: Baa mo mena nombia kɛŋa yɛɛ ŋon nawolo nyi be ta gyeŋa ta daa na me kya ŋon ya.

eng: But I have said these things to you so that when their time comes, you will remember that I told you about them. I didnt say these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
local: Mɛɛ wulaa yako ŋon nombia kɛŋa baa na akpaa debaŋ kenaŋ de lii na, ɛ ke tɔɔse nyi me wulaa yako ŋon nombia kɛŋa na ɛ ke nyiŋ do ogo.

eng: But now I go away to the one who sent me. None of you ask me, Where are you going.
local: Ma te yako ŋon nombia kɛŋa lee kewalaŋ keŋ ya, nawolo nyi na maŋ gbagba kyaa ɛ gyaŋ dɔɔ. Mɔna nɛnɛɛ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nawolo nyi mee gyu ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ gyaŋ, mɔna ɛ man ŋolo te tale bɔɔse maŋ nyi Aleŋ yaa n ne gyu ya.

eng: Yet because I have said these things to you, you are filled with sorrow.
local: Te nɛnɛɛ keŋ mɔɔ yako ŋon na, te ɛ gyoo kayeyɛɛ?.

eng: I assure you that it is better for you that I go away. If I dont go away, the Companion wont come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.
local: Mɔna mɛɛ yako ŋon nombii anokoare baa nyiaa, ɛ deeli dɔɔ te mee gyu nɛ. Nawolo nyi akpaa me te gyu ya na, Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon waa yako do ŋon nɛ be gyae waa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ ya. Mɔna akpaa me gyu na, maa kpila e na waa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ.

eng: When he comes, he will show the world it was wrong about sin, righteousness, and judgment.
local: Tɛɛle kei balaŋ kpa ba gyeŋ kpene keŋ yɛɛ dukum ke, te ba gyeŋ kpene keŋ doo na ke gbɛɛ, te ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia kedi keŋ wose man nombia mɔ. Mɔna akpaa ɔ kɔŋ na waa yela baa gyeŋ nyi be nombia be dei ya.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about sin because they dont believe in me.
local: Bɔɔ gyeŋ kpene keŋ yɛɛ dukum ke ya, nawolo nyi ba te lɛɛ maŋ di ya.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about righteousness because Im going to the Father and you wont see me anymore.
local: Te bɔɔ gyeŋ kpene keŋ mɔ doo na ke gbɛɛ ya, nawolo nyi mɛɛ bese mee gyu me kya ŋon gyaŋ te ɛ be gyae ɛ ka tekaa naa maŋ bela ya.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about judgment because this worlds ruler stands condemned.
local: Bɔɔ gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia kedi keŋ wose man daale ya, nawolo nyi ɔbɔnsam ŋon ɔ ne di tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyoori nɛ, Wurubuarɛ te wulaa di ɔ nombia bu e fɔɔ.

eng: I have much more to say to you, but you cant handle it now.
local: Me dana nombia burum maa yako ŋon, mɔna debokeiman man nɛ ŋe dana yuŋ kela ŋon.

eng: However, when the Spirit of Truth comes, he will guide you in all truth. He wont speak on his own, but will say whatever he hears and will proclaim to you what is to come.
local: Mɔna akpaa Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ɔ bee di dekara ya nɛ de kɔŋ na, waa lese nombia ŋan ŋe doo na ŋe gbɛɛ pou nɛ wola ŋon. Nawolo nyi na ŋon gbagba ɔ baam dɔɔ te ɔ ne kolosi ya, mɔna nombia ŋan pou ɔ be nyii nɛ yaa ɔ ne kolosi na waa lese nombia ŋan pou gyae ke kɔŋ wee daale nɛ wola ŋon.

eng: Then Pilate had Jesus taken and whipped.
local: Keŋte Kegyia Pilato yela ba ŋmeree Yesu mpiree.

eng: He will glorify me, because he will take what is mine and proclaim it to you.
local: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon waa yela balaŋ kena me gyoorobiiri, nawolo nyi waa lese kpene kamasɛ keŋ me dana maa yako ŋon nɛ wola ŋon.
