eng: The soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head, and dressed him in a purple robe.
local: Kei wɔle nɛ, Romantena yoonɔɔtena baŋ moo sɔɔse loo gyooro kotɔtɔɔ te ba moowɔ wuu Yesu. Te ba doo e kegba yayam wurikyim daale ŋgba gyoo nɛ.

eng: Everything that the Father has is mine. Thats why I said that the Spirit takes what is mine and will proclaim it to you.
local: Kpene kamasɛ keŋ me kya dana na ke yɛɛ me wui, mena dɔɔ te mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon waa lese kpene kamasɛ keŋ me dana maa yako ŋon nɛ wola ŋon.

eng: Over and over they went up to him and said, Greetings, king of the Jews! And they slapped him in the face.
local: Na bee gyu bɛɛ kɔŋ kaa ŋmiŋ e kulu e nyiaa, Gyudatena gyoo! N nyee dɛɛ wolose! Na bɛɛ felee e ɔ lakaase man.

eng: Soon you wont be able to see me; soon after that, you will see me.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako nyiaa, Teka kyomii ɛ be gyae ɛ kena maŋ bela ya, mɔna kenaŋ wɔle nɛ be gyae ke kyare ya, ɛ ka bese naa maŋ.

eng: Pilate came out of the palace again and said to the Jewish leaders, Look! Im bringing him out to you to let you know that I find no grounds for a charge against him.
local: Botɔɔ te kegyia Pilato besewɔ kɔŋ debɔɔ man te ɔ yako Gyudatena baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ nyii, mɛɛ mo e maa lee kɔna ŋon debɔɔ man kɛbo na ɛ kena nyi ma te naa ɔ dukum keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ ya.

eng: Some of Jesus disciples said to each other, What does he mean: Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me and Because Im going to the Father?.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ baale bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Sena yaa balee kei ne yako baa, ɔ kpa Teka kyomii ɛ be gyae ɛ kena maŋ bela ya, te kenaŋ wɔle na be gyae ke kyare ya na, ɛ ka bese naa maŋ. Nawolo nyi mee gyu me kya gyaŋ dɔɔ.

eng: When Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe, Pilate said to them, Heres the man.
local: Mena dɔɔ te kyare ya te Yesu leewɔ debɔɔ man, na ɔ wuuna gyooro kotɔtɔɔ keŋ te ɔ doona ɔ kegba yayam keŋ mɔ! Keŋte kegyia Pilato yako wɔ nyiaa, ɛ walaŋ ŋon yaa.

eng: What does he mean by soon? We dont understand what hes talking about.
local: Keŋte ba besewɔ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, Teka kyomii kei mɔ na wolo baa sena? Dɔɔ ne nyii nombia kɛŋa man ya.

eng: When the chief priests and their deputies saw him, they shouted out, Crucify, crucify! Pilate told them, You take him and crucify him. I dont find any grounds for a charge against him.
local: Saese kegyiise na Wurubuarɛ dɛɛ kotɔtɔɔtena baŋ ke gyɔ ba sibii na Yesu nɛ, ba fae yeesa nedoŋ nyiaa, Gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ, gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ. Botɔɔ te kegyia Pilato yako wɔ nyiaa, Ma te naa dukum keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ ya. Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ mo e kegyɔ sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ.

eng: Jesus knew they wanted to ask him, so he said, Are you trying to find out from each other what I meant when I said, Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me?.
local: Na Yesu gyeŋ nyi bɛɛ gyae baa bɔɔse e nombia kɛŋa man, mena dɔɔ ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Keŋ mɔɔ yako ŋon nyi, Teka kyomii ɛ be gyae ɛ kena maŋ bela ya, mɔna kenaŋ wɔle na be gyae ke kyare ya ɛ ka bese naa maŋ nɛ yaa ɛ ne bɔɔse dɔŋa nɛ?.

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, We have a Law, and according to this Law he ought to die because he made himself out to be Gods Son.
local: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena baŋ tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Daa dana mmaraa daale te kɛɛ wolo nyi kaboena nyi waa yeŋ, nawolo nyi ɔ ta mo ɔ wose nyi ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bu e.

eng: I assure you that you will cry and lament, and the world will be happy. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will turn into joy.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, ɛ ke wii na ɛ ka yeyɛɛ mɔna balaŋ baŋ be loo gyakaa tɛɛle kei abɔɔ dɔɔ nɛ baa di gyoŋ. Ɛmɛɛ ɛ ka yeyɛɛ, mɔna ɛ kayeyɛɛ keŋ gyae kaa bese gyoŋ kedi.

eng: When Pilate heard this word, he was even more afraid.
local: Kegyia Pilato ka betaa nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, gyakoloŋ gyoo e gbaa.

eng: When a woman gives birth, she has pain because her time has come. But when the child is born, she no longer remembers her distress because of her joy that a child has been born into the world.
local: Akpaa alo na doŋee na ɔ na yeyɛɛ, nawolo nyi ɔ debaŋ te lii. Mɔna akpaa ɔ lola bu ŋon na, ne yɛɛ e gyoŋ nyi ɔ ta lola bu kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ. Mena gyoŋ kei ne yele ɔ ne wolee ɔ kaale keŋ ɔ be naa nɛ.

eng: He went back into the residence and spoke to Jesus, Where are you from? Jesus didnt answer.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ besewɔ gyu dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man ke bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, N lee aleŋ? Mɔna Yesu diŋi e bo.

eng: In the same way, you have sorrow now; but I will see you again, and you will be overjoyed. No one takes away your joy.
local: Mena ke mɔ yaa doo fa ŋon. Debaŋ kei na ɛ na yeyɛɛ, mɔna maa bese kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ na ɛ wose ke fɛɛ ŋon, na ɛ ke di gyoŋ. Te ŋolo be gyae waa tale lese gyoŋ kenaŋ lee ɛ man ya.

eng: So Pilate said, You wont speak to me? Dont you know that I have authority to release you and also to crucify you.
local: Mena dɔɔ kegyia Pilato bɔɔse e nyiaa, N be kolosi yee? N ba gyeŋ nyi me dana doŋ keŋ maa tale tina neŋ yela, yaa maa yela baa gyɔ neŋ sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ yee.

eng: In that day, you wont ask me anything. I assure you that the Father will give you whatever you ask in my name.
local: Mena wee kenaŋ dɔɔ nɛ, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke bɔɔse maŋ daale ya. Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ ka sola me kya ŋon ma yele man na, waa mo fa ŋon.

eng: Jesus replied, You would have no authority over me if it had not been given to you from above. Thats why the one who handed me over to you has the greater sin.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Me kya faa na neŋ gbɛɛ, mena dɔɔ te n nyiŋa doŋ me dɔɔ. Nafɔ nyaŋ gbagba n baam dɔɔ na, n be dana doŋ me dɔɔ ya. Mena dɔɔ walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba lese maŋ fa nɛ, ŋon wui dukum kela la.

eng: Up to now, you have asked nothing in my name. Ask and you will receive so that your joy will be complete.
local: Kaalii na gyeŋ kei nɛ, ɛ ta sola ta kolo ma yele man ya. Ɛ sola na ɛ ke nyiŋ, na ke yɛɛ ŋon gyoŋ nideli.

eng: From that moment on, Pilate wanted to release Jesus. However, the Jewish leaders cried out, saying, If you release this man, you arent a friend of the emperor! Anyone who makes himself out to be a king opposes the emperor.
local: Debaŋ keŋ kegyia Pilato be nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ɔ gyae gbɛɛ keŋ waa mo tina Yesu yela, mɔna na Gyudatena baŋ ne fae yeesa yako nyiaa, Akpaa n tina balee kei yela na, n te yɛɛ Romantena gyoo dinaa Kaesa ɔ dɔɔ e ya. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ see ɔ wose gyoo nɛ, na ɔ na koro baa seŋ tia gyoo dinaa Kaesa.

eng: Ive been using figures of speech with you. The time is coming when I will no longer speak to you in such analogies. Instead, I will tell you plainly about the Father.
local: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, debaŋ daale gyae kelii na mɔɔ gyae maa tekaa kolosi na ŋon aduya man bela ya. Mɔna maa toro me kya ŋon nombia yako ŋon.

eng: When Pilate heard these words, he led Jesus out and seated him on the judges bench at the place called Stone Pavement .
local: Kegyia Pilato ka betaa nyii nɔɔwoya kɛŋa nɛ, ɔ moo Yesu lee kɔŋ debɔɔ man te ɔ kaa gyeŋawɔ kyaa ɔ gbeli keŋ dɔɔ ɔ ne kyaa di nombia nɛ. Boya te ba moowɔ ma ɔ dekyae kenaŋ, te Gyudatena dei man na bɛɛ baake ke nyi Gabata.

eng: In that day you will ask in my name. Im not saying that I will ask the Father on your behalf.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ de lii nɛ, ɛ ka sola e ma yele man. Mɔna te yɛɛ nyi maa seŋ ɛ nawɔɔ man na maa sola me kya ŋon fa ŋon ya.

eng: It was about noon on the Preparation Day for the Passover. Pilate said to the Jewish leaders, Heres your king.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na weese ta seŋ dɛɛ. Wee kenaŋ te Gyudatena ne desina ba wose na ke tɛɛ naa na, baa di ba nyeekelɛɛwee keŋ. Keŋte kegyia Pilato yako Gyudatena baŋ nyiaa, Ɛ kɛɛ, ɛ gyoo ŋon yaa.

eng: The Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and believed that I came from God.
local: Nawolo nyi me kya ŋon gbagba ne gyae ŋon, keŋ ɛ ne gyae maŋ te ɛ be lɛɛ di nyi ma lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: The Jewish leaders cried out, Take him away! Take him away! Crucify him! Pilate responded, What? Do you want me to crucify your king? We have no king except the emperor, the chief priests answered.
local: Botɔɔ te be fae yeesa nyiaa, Ɛ mo e lee de siaman, ɛ mo e lee de siaman. Ɛ gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ. Te kegyia Pilato bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa Ɛ gyoo yaa te ɛ ne gyae nyi maa ko e? Keŋte saese kegyiise baŋ tiranɔɔ nyi, Da be dana gyoo kamasɛ kpu na Kaesa ya.

eng: I left the Father and came into the world. I tell you again: I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
local: Daa na me kya kyaa la pɛte ma lee ɔ gyaŋ kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, te nɛnɛɛ mɛɛ bese mɛɛ lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ mee gyu me kya ŋonaŋ gyaŋ.

eng: Then Pilate handed Jesus over to be crucified. The soldiers took Jesus prisoner.
local: Kɛŋa pou wɔle nɛ, kegyia Pilato moo Yesu do balaŋ baŋ nyiŋmaa man nyi baa gyu ke gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ.

eng: His disciples said, See! Now you speak plainly; you arent using figures of speech.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ yako e nyiaa, Nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ n bɛɛ kolosi aduya man fa daa bela ya. N ta lese nombia ŋan man wola daa.

eng: Carrying his cross by himself, he went out to a place called Skull Place .
local: Mena dɔɔ yoonɔɔtena baŋ moo Yesu lee donɔɔ keŋ man na ɔ seelena ɔ ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ te ba moo e laŋ gyu botɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake Nyeepookoo dɛɛ nɛ. Gyudatena dei man na bɛɛ baake ke bo nyi Golgata.

eng: Now we know that you know everything and you dont need anyone to ask you. Because of this we believe you have come from God.
local: Nɛnɛɛ da te naa nyi n gyeŋ kpene kamasɛ, te tekaboena nyi ŋolo dɛɛ bɔɔse neŋ nombia daale bela ya. Kei te yeli da te naa nyi n lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

eng: Thats where they crucified him—and two others with him, one on each side and Jesus in the middle.
local: Botɔɔ te be gyɔɔ e nyeelabia sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ. Keŋte be gyɔɔ balaŋ bala baale mɔ sakaa ɔpaŋdaase dɔɔ kpu na e. Ŋolo sakaa ɔ dunoluŋ dɔɔ te ŋolo mɔ sakaa ɔ demena dɔɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, Now you believe?.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Nɛnɛɛ ɛ ne lɛɛ maŋ di?.

eng: Pilate had a public notice written and posted on the cross. It read Jesus the Nazarene, the king of the Jews.
local: Keŋte kegyia Pilato yela be ŋmarase nɔɔwoya kɛŋa mataa na ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ. Nɔɔwoya ŋenaŋ yɛna nyiaa, yesu nasarɛte baale, gyudatena gyoo ŋon.

eng: Look! A time is coming—and is here!—when each of you will be scattered to your own homes and you will leave me alone. Im not really alone, for the Father is with me.
local: Debaŋ keŋ ne kɔŋ te ke taŋ kekɔŋ, keŋ ɛ popou ɛ ke yaasee. Walaŋ kamasɛ waa gyu ɔ dɛɛ tina maŋ yela na ke ka maageŋ. Mɔna na maageŋ kyaa la ya, nawolo nyi me kya ŋon kpuɛ na maŋ.

eng: Many of the Jews read this sign, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city and it was written in Aramaic, Latin, and Greek.
local: Gyudatena burum kala keŋmarase kei nawolo nyi botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ gyɔ Yesu ɔpaŋ dɔɔ nɛ betaŋ lem na donɔɔ keŋ ya. Be ŋmarase ke Gyudatena dei man na Romantena dei man na Giriki dei man.

eng: Ive said these things to you so that you will have peace in me. In the world you have distress. But be encouraged! I have conquered the world.
local: Mɛɛ yako nombia kɛŋa baa, na ɛ ke ta me dɔɔ nyiŋ wosefɛɛreŋ. Balaŋ baa yela ɛ kena diyem nideli tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, mɔna ɛ nyiŋ konɔɔ na ɛ ka seŋ keŋkeŋ nawolo nyi me te di tɛɛle kei dɔɔ.

eng: Therefore, the Jewish chief priests complained to Pilate, Dont write, The king of the Jews but This man said, I am the king of the Jews.
local: Gyudatena saese kegyiise kena keŋmarase keŋ nɛ be yako kegyia Pilato nyiaa, Nyeŋ te ŋmarase ke nyi Gyudatena gyoo ya. Nyeŋ ŋmarase ke bo nyi, balee kei kpa ŋon yɛna Gyudatena gyoo.

eng: Pilate answered, What Ive written, Ive written.
local: Botɔɔ te kegyia Pilato tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Kpene keŋ mɔɔ ŋmarase nɛ, me taŋ keŋmarase.

eng: When the soldiers crucified Jesus, they took his clothes and his sandals, and divided them into four shares, one for each soldier. His shirt was seamless, woven as one piece from the top to the bottom.
local: Debaŋ keŋ yoonɔɔtena baŋ be gyɔ Yesu ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ taŋ nɛ, ba moo ɔ wose man abɔɔ ŋan te be kpala ŋa akpeŋ anaara, na be man kamasɛ ka mo dekpeŋ dokoloŋ. Ba ke kpala taŋ na te kaa ɔ kegba atetate keŋ. Mena kegba kei dɔɔ kanyaŋ dokoloŋ te ba moowɔ loo ke karatetee lee ka nyeeman tisi. Be ta loo ke towaa towaa ŋgba mena keŋ bɛɛ loo kegba yakaa nyiŋan nɛ ya.

eng: They said to each other, Lets not tear it. Lets cast lots to see who will get it. This was to fulfill the scripture, They divided my clothes among themselves, and they cast lots for my clothing. Thats what the soldiers did.
local: Keŋte yoonɔɔtena baŋ yako dɔŋa nyiaa, Ɛ na fa dee tirii kegba kei ya. Ɛ fa dɛɛ lɔ boe na dɛɛ kɛɛ ŋon waa nyiŋ ke. Kei yela Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Be ŋmarasewɔ see nyiaa, Be kpala ma abɔɔ, te ma kegba atetate dɔɔ be lɔɔ boe bo ke dɔɔ Keŋ dɔɔ mena mɔ te yoonɔɔtena baŋ yɛɛwɔ.

eng: Jesus mother and his mothers sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene stood near the cross.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na Yesu ɔ naa na ɔ niinaa na Kilopase wɛɛle Mɛɛre, na Mɛɛre ŋon ɔ ba lee Magedala nɛ seŋɛɛ benaa na Yesu ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ.

eng: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing nearby, he said to his mother, Woman, here is your son.
local: Yesu kena ɔ naa na ɔ kaseele ŋon ɔ ne gyae ɔ nombia seŋɛɛ benaa na dɔŋa nɛ, ɔ yako ɔ naa nyiaa, Nna, n bu ŋonaŋ.

eng: Then he said to the disciple, Here is your mother. And from that time on, this disciple took her into his home.
local: Keŋte ɔ kɛɛ ɔ kaseele ŋon mɔ te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Kɛɛ, n naa ŋonaŋ. Lee debaŋ kenaŋ na, te kaseele ŋon moo Yesu ɔ naa gyu ɔ dɛɛ te ɔ ke kyaawɔ ɔ gyaŋ.

eng: After this, knowing that everything was already completed, in order to fulfill the scripture, Jesus said, I am thirsty.
local: Kɛŋa wɔle Yesu kena nyi nɛnɛɛ kpene kamasɛ te taŋ nɛ ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Loŋboaneŋ dana maŋ. Kei yela Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan kɔŋawɔ mena.

eng: A jar full of sour wine was nearby, so the soldiers soaked a sponge in it, placed it on a hyssop branch, and held it up to his lips.
local: Na osuŋ na soloŋ denyaŋesa daale baŋɛɛ botɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ ba moo kolo ŋgba kokyaa keŋ gyae ke tale lɛɛ loŋ nɛ do soloŋ keŋ man, te ba moo ke tooso na daŋ daale te ba moo ke sɔɔ ɔ nɔɔ nyi waa nyɔɔ.

eng: When he had received the sour wine, Jesus said, It is completed. Bowing his head, he gave up his life.
local: Soloŋ keŋ ke tina Yesu nɔɔ nɛ te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Te taŋ. Botɔɔ te ɔ gɔŋase ɔ nyee te ɔ kara weesi.

eng: It was the Preparation Day and the Jewish leaders didnt want the bodies to remain on the cross on the Sabbath, especially since that Sabbath was an important day. So they asked Pilate to have the legs of those crucified broken and the bodies taken down.
local: Wee kenaŋ yɛna wee keŋ Gyudatena na mo desina ba wose fa ba kefɛɛfowee kedi. Mena kefɛɛfowee kei yɛɛ wee dinaa ke. Te ke yɛɛ kpene keŋ bɛɛ kɔle nyi baa tina woo yela ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ na ba kefɛɛfowee keŋ kaa tuŋ e. Lee mena dɔɔ Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ gyuu kegyia Pilato gyaŋ ka sola e nyi, waa fa wɔ gbɛɛ na be kaa tekerii balaŋ baŋ bee fiti na yeŋ nɛ nawɔɔ na baa nyiŋ yeŋ bileŋ, na baa nyiŋ lese wɔ ɔpaŋ daase ŋan dɔɔ.

eng: Therefore, the soldiers came and broke the legs of the two men who were crucified with Jesus.
local: Mena dɔɔ yoonɔɔtena baŋ gyuuwɔ ke tekerii balaŋ bala baŋ bɔɔ gyɔ wɔ sakaa kpu na Yesu nɛ nawɔɔ.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they saw that he was already dead so they didnt break his legs.
local: Mɔna ba kekɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ te ba kena nyi ɔ taŋ ke wulaa yeŋ nɛ, ba te tekerii ɔ nawɔɔ ya.

eng: However, one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out.
local: Keŋte yoonɔɔtena baŋ ŋolo moo wa apia sɔɔ Yesu yenaabia man. Debokenaŋ man te fatabo na loŋ leewɔ.

eng: The one who saw this has testified, and his testimony is true. He knows that he speaks the truth, and he has testified so that you also can believe.
local: Maŋ walaŋ ŋon mɔɔ naa nombia kɛŋa na me sia nɛ ne diina ka wose man adansɛɛ, te ma adansɛɛ keŋ yɛɛ anokoare. Ma gyeŋ nyi anokoare yaa mɛɛ yako, te mee di adansɛɛ baa na ɛmɛɛ mɔ ke nyiŋ lɛɛ di.

eng: These things happened to fulfill the scripture, They wont break any of his bones .
local: Kei yela Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Nombia ŋenaŋ yɛna nyiaa, Bɔɔ gyae baa tekeri ɔ ŋɔe dokoloŋ pɛ gba ya.

eng: And another scripture says, They will look at him whom they have pierced.
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ daale mɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Balaŋ baa kɛɛ walaŋ ŋon bɔɔ sɔɔ e apia nɛ.

eng: After this Joseph of Arimathea asked Pilate if he could take away the body of Jesus. Joseph was a disciple of Jesus, but a secret one because he feared the Jewish authorities. Pilate gave him permission, so he came and took the body away.
local: Kɛŋa pou wɔle nɛ, Gyosɛfo ŋon ɔ ba lee Arimatia donɔɔ man nɛ kaa sola gbɛɛ lee kegyia Pilato gyaŋ na ɔ kaa lese Yesu woo ŋon kewuu. Na Gyosɛfo kei mɔ yɛɛ Yesu kaseele e, mɔna ɔ weese ɔ wose bo nawolo nyi ɔ ne yee Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ. Kegyia Pilato ke fa e gbɛɛ nɛ te ɔ gyuuwɔ ka lese woo ŋon.

eng: Nicodemus, the one who at first had come to Jesus at night, was there too. He brought a mixture of myrrh and aloe, nearly seventy-five pounds in all.
local: Nikodemose ŋon ɔ be kyaa kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ nelim nɛ, mɔ kaa kpuuwɔ na Gyosɛfo ŋon. Nikodemose kei moo pɛperɛ na oliifi na abɔɔ ofoŋ nyiŋan ŋaale tasatɔɔ kɔŋ te ba moowɔ desina woo ŋon.

eng: Following Jewish burial customs, they took Jesus body and wrapped it, with the spices, in linen cloths.
local: Be balaŋ bala moo akaralɛɛ na abɔɔ ofoŋ nyiŋan mo milii Yesu ŋgba mena keŋ Gyudatena ne desina woo pɛte bee wuu e nɛ.

eng: There was a garden in the place where Jesus was crucified, and in the garden was a new tomb in which no one had ever been laid.
local: Na wɔɔbii daale kyaa botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ gyɔ Yesu sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ. Yebɔɔ wɔle wɔle daale doo botɔɔ keŋ be te wuu ta walaŋ ke man see ya.

eng: Because it was the Jewish Preparation Day and the tomb was nearby, they laid Jesus in it.
local: Keŋ Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee keŋ na benaa te bee desina ba wose mɔ te yebɔɔ keŋ mɔ benaa na botɔɔ dɔɔ nɛ, ba moo woo ŋon kewuu ke man. Keŋte be biliŋi boe dinaa daale tɔ ke nɔɔ.

eng: Early in the morning of the first day of the week, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene came to the tomb and saw that the stone had been taken away from the tomb.
local: Kosida nebo nebo fuu keŋ tɛɛ te ŋmaŋee ta nideli ya nɛ, Mɛɛre ŋon ɔ ba lee Magedala donɔɔ man nɛ, karatɛɛwɔ gyu botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ wuu Yesu nɛ. Ɔ ke gyu botɔɔ nɛ, ɔ naawɔ nyi be te biliŋi boe keŋ lee yebɔɔ keŋ nɔɔman.

eng: She ran to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved, and said, They have taken the Lord from the tomb, and we dont know where theyve put him.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔ yeuwɔ kɔŋ Simɔn Pita na kaseele ŋon Yesu ne gyae ɔ nombia nɛ gyaŋ kaa yako wɔ nyiaa, Be ta lese de Gbeŋgyoo lee yebɔɔ keŋ man, te dɔɔ gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ moo e ka see ya.

eng: Peter and the other disciple left to go to the tomb.
local: Keŋte Pita na kaseele ŋon yeuwɔ gyu yebɔɔ keŋ tɛɛ.

eng: They were running together, but the other disciple ran faster than Peter and was the first to arrive at the tomb.
local: Be kee yeu keŋ nɛ, kaseele ŋon Yesu ne gyae ɔ nombia nɛ yeuwɔ gbaa Pita kyaŋ gbɛɛ ka gyoo yebɔɔ keŋ tɛɛ.

eng: Bending down to take a look, he saw the linen cloths lying there, but he didnt go in.
local: Ɔ buŋawɔ kɛɛ yebɔɔ keŋ man na, ɔ naa akaralɛɛ keŋ kaageŋ doo la mɔna ɔ ta gyoo ke man ya.

eng: Following him, Simon Peter entered the tomb and saw the linen cloths lying there.
local: Simɔn Pita ka lee wɔle kɔŋ na te ŋon kaa gyoowɔ yebɔɔ keŋ man. Ɔ gyoowɔ na te ɔ naa akaralɛɛ keŋ.

eng: He also saw the face cloth that had been on Jesus head. It wasnt with the other clothes but was folded up in its own place.
local: na kanyaŋ keŋ bɔɔ mo bake ɔ nyee nɛ. Kanyaŋ keŋ be doo akaralɛɛ keŋ gyaŋ ya, mɔna akaralɛɛ keŋ te bɔɔ ke doo yenaŋ.

eng: Then the other disciple, the one who arrived at the tomb first, also went inside. He saw and believed.
local: Wɔle wɔle na te kaseele ɔ dɔɔ ŋon mɔ gyoowɔ kena abɔɔ ŋan, keŋte ŋon mɔ lɛɛwɔ di.

eng: They didnt yet understand the scripture that Jesus must rise from the dead.
local: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyi Yesu waa foŋ lee yeŋ man nɛ, be sɛɛ be te nyii ta ŋe man nideli ya.

eng: Then the disciples returned to the place where they were staying.
local: Keŋte kaseela baŋ besewɔ gyu be dɛɛ.

eng: Mary stood outside near the tomb, crying. As she cried, she bent down to look into the tomb.
local: Mɔna Mɛɛre ŋon ɔ be sila wɔ gyu nɛ seŋɛɛ yebɔɔ keŋ tɛɛ ɔ ne wii. Ɔ ka seŋ ɔ ne wii nɛ te ɔ buŋawɔ kɛɛ yebɔɔ keŋ man.

eng: She saw two angels dressed in white, seated where the body of Jesus had been, one at the head and one at the foot.
local: keŋte ɔ naa Wurubuarɛ kpilala bala na ba doona kegbase wolomɛɛ be kyaa botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ mo Yesu woo ŋon doosi nɛ. Ŋolo kyaa yebɔɔ keŋ nyee gbɛɛ te ŋolo mɔ kyaa ke nawɔɔ gbɛɛ.

eng: The angels asked her, Woman, why are you crying? She replied, They have taken away my Lord, and I dont know where theyve put him.
local: Keŋte be bɔɔse e nyiaa, Alo, weera n ne wii? Te ɔ tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, Nawolo nyi be ta lese me Gbeŋgyoo lee yebɔɔ keŋ man, te mɔɔ gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ mo e ka see ya.

eng: As soon as she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she didnt know it was Jesus.
local: Ɔ ke yako mena nɛ, ɔ gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ na te ɔ naa Yesu seŋɛɛ, mɔna ɔ ta gyem nyi Yesu ŋonaŋ ya.

eng: Jesus said to her, Woman, why are you crying? Who are you looking for? Thinking he was the gardener, she replied, Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have put him and I will get him.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, Alo, weera n ne wii? Amɔte yaa n ne kɛo? Na Mɛɛre gyeŋ nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kɛɛ wɔɔbii keŋ dɔɔ nɛ ŋonaŋ, mena dɔɔ ɔ yako e nyiaa, Tobaale, akpaa nyaŋ moona woo ŋon ka weese na, wola maŋ botɔɔ keŋ n ba mo e ka see na me kaa mo e ta.

eng: Jesus said to her, Mary. She turned and said to him in Aramaic, Rabbouni .
local: Keŋte Yesu baake e nyiaa, Mɛɛre! Mɛɛre gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ te ɔ fae yeesa baake e be dɛɛ dei man nyiaa, Raboni keŋ nawolo nyi De Gbeŋgyoo.

eng: Jesus said to her, Dont hold on to me, for I havent yet gone up to my Father. Go to my brothers and sisters and tell them, Im going up to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, Na tina maŋ ya, nawolo nyi me te gyuu ta me kya ŋon gyaŋ ya. Mɔna bese gyu me kaseela baŋ gyaŋ na n kaa yako wɔ nyi Mee gyu me kya ŋon ɔ yɛɛ ɛ kya e nɛ, na me Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ ɛ Wurubuarɛ e nɛ gyaŋ. .

eng: Mary Magdalene left and announced to the disciples, Ive seen the Lord. Then she told them what he said to her.
local: Mena dɔɔ Mɛɛre Magedala ŋon gyuuwɔ ke yako kaseela baŋ nyiaa Ma te naa de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ŋon. Te ɔ besewɔ yako wɔ nombia ŋan Yesu be yako e nyi waa yako wɔ nɛ.

eng: It was still the first day of the week. That evening, while the disciples were behind closed doors because they were afraid of the Jewish authorities, Jesus came and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: Kosida kenaŋ balinɔɔ nɛ, kaseela baŋ gyuuwɔ ke gyaŋee deni man te be tɔɔ disim do ba wose, nawolo nyi bee yee Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ. Debokenaŋ man te Yesu kaa bɛɛwɔ lee ba nsana te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Wosefɛɛreŋ dɛɛ kyaa na ŋon.

eng: After he said this, he showed them his hands and his side. When the disciples saw the Lord, they were filled with joy.
local: Ɔ ke yako wɔ mena nɛ, ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaa na ɔ yenaabia gyam keŋ wola wɔ. Keŋte yɛɛ ɔ kaseela baŋ gyoŋ nideli nyi ba te naa be Gbeŋgyoo ŋon bela.

eng: Jesus said to them again, Peace be with you. As the Father sent me, so I am sending you.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Wosefɛɛreŋ dɛɛ kyaa na ŋon, mena keŋ me kya ŋon be kpila maŋ nɛ, mena ke yaa mɛɛ kpila ɛmɛɛ mɔ.

eng: Then he breathed on them and said, Receive the Holy Spirit.
local: Ɔ kekolosi nombia kɛŋa taŋ nɛ, ɔ lee weesɛɛ woli wɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon na waa kyaa ɛ man.

eng: If you forgive anyones sins, they are forgiven; if you dont forgive them, they arent forgiven.
local: Akpaa ɛ mo ŋolo nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ e na, Wurubuarɛ mɔ waa mo kyɛɛ e. Te akpaa ɛ bɛɛ ɛ ta mo ŋolo nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ e ya na, Wurubuarɛ mɔ be gyae waa mo kyɛɛ e ya.

eng: Thomas, the one called Didymus, one of the Twelve, wasnt with the disciples when Jesus came.
local: Wee kenaŋ keŋ Yesu be kaa lese ɔ wose wola ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ nɛ, na be man ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Tomase yaa Ata nɛ be kpuɛ na ɔ tebia ya.

eng: The other disciples told him, Weve seen the Lord! But he replied, Unless I see the nail marks in his hands, put my finger in the wounds left by the nails, and put my hand into his side, I wont believe.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu kaseela akaŋ baŋ yako Tomase nyiaa, Da te naa de Gbeŋgyoo ŋon. Keŋte Tomase yako wɔ nyiaa, Akpaa me ta mo me sia naa nyeelabia gyaŋtarɛɛ ŋan, na maa mo me nyimbii do ɔ yenaabia gyam keŋ man ya nɛ, maŋ be gyae maa lɛɛ di korakora ya.

eng: After eight days his disciples were again in a house and Thomas was with them. Even though the doors were locked, Jesus entered and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: Weeya gyanaara wɔle nɛ, na ɔ kaseela baŋ te gyaŋee deni man bela. Wee kenaŋ na Tomase mɔ kpuɛ na wɔ. Ba te tɔ asima ŋan pou, karatetee dokoloŋ te Yesu kaa bɛɛwɔ lee be dɔɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Wosefɛɛreŋ dɛɛ kyaa na ŋon.

eng: Then he said to Thomas, Put your finger here. Look at my hands. Put your hand into my side. No more disbelief. Believe.
local: Keŋte ɔ yako Tomase nyiaa, Mo n nyiŋmaa do me nyiŋmaa bɔɔ man na n ke kɛɛ me nyiŋmaase, na n ka mo n nyimbii do ma yenaŋ kɛɛ. Tina aŋmaareŋ kelɛɛ yela na n ke lɛɛ di.

eng: Thomas responded to Jesus, My Lord and my God.
local: Botɔɔ te Tomase tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Nyaŋ yɛna me Gbeŋgyoo na me Wurubuarɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Do you believe because you see me? Happy are those who dont see and yet believe.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, Keŋ n be naa maŋ dɔɔ te n lɛɛwɔ di? Balaŋ baŋ ba te naa maŋ ya te bɔɔ lɛɛ di nɛ, baŋ na nyeebam.

eng: Then Jesus did many other miraculous signs in his disciples presence, signs that arent recorded in this scroll.
local: Yesu besewɔ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia burum ŋan ɔ kaseela baŋ be naa na be sia, mɔna na ŋe popou te be ŋmarasewɔ do tɔne kei man ya.

eng: But these things are written so that you will believe that Jesus is the Christ, Gods Son, and that believing, you will have life in his name.
local: Mɔna be ŋmarase kɛŋa bo na ɛ ke lɛɛ di nyi Yesu yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu na nyeelɛɛre ŋon. Na nyaŋ ŋon n lɛɛ e di na, n ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ ɔ yele man.

eng: Later, Jesus himself appeared again to his disciples at the Sea of Tiberias. This is how it happened.
local: Kɛŋa pou wɔle nɛ, Yesu besewɔ lese ɔ wose wola ɔ kaseela baale Tiberiase boo nɔɔman. Mena keŋ ɔ ba lese ɔ wose wola wɔ yaa.

eng: Simon Peter, Thomas , Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, Zebedees sons, and two other disciples were together.
local: Ɔ kaseela baŋ bɛɛ baake wɔ Simɔn Pita na Tomase ŋon bɛɛ baake e Ata nɛ na Natanɛle ŋon ɔ ba lee Kaanan donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ, na Sebedeo bia na ɔ kaseela bala baale gyuuwɔ ke kyaa bom.

eng: Simon Peter told them, Im going fishing. They said, Well go with you. They set out in a boat, but throughout the night they caught nothing.
local: Keŋte Simɔn Pita yako wɔ nyiaa, Mee gyu boo man me kaa kyaŋ kpebia. Te baŋ mɔ yako e nyiaa, Daa mɔ dee sila neŋ gyu. Mena dɔɔ be gyuuwɔ ka gyoo degbele man te be laŋawɔ. Be lɔɔ asawu nelim kenaŋ pou mɔna ba te kyaŋ kolo ya.

eng: Early in the morning, Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples didnt realize it was Jesus.
local: Tɛɛ kena nebo nebo fuu nɛ, be kɛɛ na ŋolo seŋɛɛ la boo nɔɔman, na n kɛɛ nɛ Yesu ŋonaŋ. Mɔna ɔ kaseela baŋ ta gyeŋ e ya.

eng: Jesus called to them, Children, have you caught anything to eat? They answered him, No.
local: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Tewulɛɛ, doona ɛ te kyaŋ kolo ya? Te be tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Koa de te nyiŋ kpebii dokoloŋ paŋpaŋ gba ya.

eng: He said, Cast your net on the right side of the boat and you will find some. So they did, and there were so many fish that they couldnt haul in the net.
local: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ fuŋ ya asawu keŋ lɔ degbele keŋ dunoluŋ dɔɔ na ɛ ke kyaŋ kpebia. Mena dɔɔ be fuŋa asawu keŋ lɔ botɔɔ yenaŋ te be kyaŋa kpebia burum keŋ nyi bɔɔ ne tale baa wɔŋee asawu keŋ gba ya.

eng: Then the disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, Its the Lord! When Simon Peter heard it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around himself and jumped into the water.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu kaseele ŋon ɔ ne gyae ɔ nombia nɛ yako Pita nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ŋonaŋ. Simɔn Pita kenyii nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ŋonaŋ nɛ te ɔ moo ɔ kegba wurikyim milii ɔ wose, nawolo nyi ɔ wulaa kpee ke. Keŋte ɔ toŋawɔ gyoo loŋ keŋ man yɛlɛɛ ɔ ne gyu Yesu gyaŋ.

eng: The other disciples followed in the boat, dragging the net full of fish, for they werent far from shore, only about one hundred yards.
local: Mɔna kaseela akaŋ baŋ saa degbele keŋ bɛɛ kɔŋ dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ na bɛɛ wɔŋee asawu keŋ kpebia be wulu ke man nɛ, nawolo nyi na ba dekyae keŋ taŋ lem na dekeŋkeŋ ya. Ke lem gyae ke yɛɛ ŋgba baam tɔɔse sɔŋola na kufu nɛ .

eng: When they landed, they saw a fire there, with fish on it, and some bread.
local: Be kelii dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ nɛ be naa boalaŋ na nyewalaŋ, na kpebia gyakaa ke dɔɔ na bodobodo mɔ doo yenaŋ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Bring some of the fish that youve just caught.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ mo kpebia baŋ ɛ be kyam nɛ baale kɔŋ.

eng: Simon Peter got up and pulled the net to shore. It was full of large fish, one hundred fifty-three of them. Yet the net hadnt torn, even with so many fish.
local: Mena dɔɔ Simɔn Pita besewɔ gyu degbele keŋ man, te ɔ ke wɔŋee asawu keŋ kɔŋ dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ. Na kpebia dinaana baŋ bɔɔ wulu asawu keŋ nɛ, be yaanɔɔle yɛɛ sɔŋonoŋ na sɔŋola na kufu batooro . Keŋ na kpebia baŋ booroŋ kei koraŋ nɛ, asawu keŋ te tirii ya.

eng: Jesus said to them, Come and have breakfast. None of the disciples could bring themselves to ask him, Who are you? They knew it was the Lord.
local: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kɔŋ kaa di ɛ nebo weenɛɛ. Mɔna ɔ kaseela baŋ ŋolo te tale gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ bɔɔse e nyi Nyaŋ yɛna amɔte ya? Nawolo nyi ba gyeŋ nyi be Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ŋonaŋ.

eng: Jesus came, took the bread, and gave it to them. He did the same with the fish.
local: Te Yesu kɔŋawɔ kaa mo bodobodo keŋ fa wɔ, kenaŋ wɔle te ɔ moo kpebia baŋ mɔ fa wɔ.

eng: This was now the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from the dead.
local: Yesu kefoŋ lee yeŋ man nɛ, kei yɛna betabotoorote keŋ ɔ ba lese ɔ wose wola ɔ kaseela baŋ.

eng: When they finished eating, Jesus asked Simon Peter, Simon son of John, do you love me more than these? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my lambs.
local: Be kedi weenɛɛ ŋan taŋ nɛ te Yesu bɔɔse Simɔn Pita nyiaa, Gyɔn bu Simɔn, n ne gyae maŋ kela n tebia kɛwɔ kɛɛ gyae maŋ? Keŋte Pita tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Ayi, me Gbeŋgyoo, n gyeŋ nyi mɛɛ gyae n nombia. Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, Kɛɛ me silala baŋ dɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ namensekɛɛlere ne kɛɛ namense dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: Jesus asked a second time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Take care of my sheep.
local: Te Yesu besewɔ bɔɔse e bolate nyiaa, Gyɔn bu Simɔn, n ne gyae me nombia? Te Pita tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Ayi me Gbeŋgyoo, nyaŋ gbagba gyeŋ nyi mɛɛ gyae n nombia. Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa Kɛɛ me silala baŋ dɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ namensekɛɛlere ne kɛɛ ɔ namense dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: He asked a third time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Peter was sad that Jesus asked him a third time, Do you love me? He replied, Lord, you know everything; you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my sheep.
local: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ bɔɔse e betabotoorote nyiaa, Gyɔn bu Simɔn, n ne gyae me nombia nideli? Nombia ŋenaŋ takaa Pita nideli nawolo nyi ɔ te bɔɔse e betabotooro nyi, N ne gyae maŋ nideli? Keŋte Pita yako e nyi, Me Gbeŋgyoo, nyaŋ gbagba n gyeŋ kpene kamasɛ. N gyeŋ nyi mɛɛ gyae neŋ nideli. Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, Mena na kɛɛ me silala baŋ dɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ namensekɛɛlere ne kɛɛ ɔ namense dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: I assure you that when you were younger you tied your own belt and walked around wherever you wanted. When you grow old, you will stretch out your hands and another will tie your belt and lead you where you dont want to go.
local: Mɛɛ yako neŋ anokoare nyiaa, Debaŋ keŋ n yɛɛ tobaale e nɛ nyaŋ gbagba ne tale mo n kegba do n wose, na n ka koro gyu botɔɔ keŋ n ne gyae. Mɔna n kaa ŋmɛɛ na ŋolo ne gyae la waa do neŋ kegba na waa kyaŋ neŋ n nyiŋmaa man mo neŋ gyu botɔɔ keŋ n bɛɛ gyae nyi n ke gyu ya.

eng: He said this to show the kind of death by which Peter would glorify God. After saying this, Jesus said to Peter, Follow me.
local: Yesu yako nombia kɛŋa baa na kawola yeŋ keŋ Pita waa yeŋ na ka lese Wurubuarɛ yele. Yesu kekolosi nombia kɛŋa pou taŋ nɛ te ɔ yako Pita nyiaa, Sila maŋ.

eng: Peter turned around and saw the disciple whom Jesus loved following them. This was the one who had leaned against Jesus at the meal and asked him, Lord, who is going to betray you.
local: Ba ke ta bee gyu nɛ, Pita gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ te ɔ naawɔ ŋgba kaseele ŋon Yesu ne gyae ɔ nombia nideli nɛ silana wɔ wɔle wɔle. Mena kaseele kei yɛna ŋon ɔ be kyaa teelina Yesu debaŋ keŋ bee di be balinɔɔ weenɛɛ te ɔ be bɔɔse e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, amɔte ne gyae la waa lese neŋ fa yeŋ nɛ.

eng: When Peter saw this disciple, he said to Jesus, Lord, what about him.
local: Pita kena kaseele ŋon nɛ ɔ bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, sena te baa yɛɛ balee kei mɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you? You must follow me.
local: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, Akpaa mɛɛ gyae nyi waa kyaa kelii debaŋ keŋ maa bese kɔŋ na, n woŋ faa la? Nyaŋ sila maŋ.

eng: Therefore, the word spread among the brothers and sisters that this disciple wouldnt die. However, Jesus didnt say he wouldnt die, but only, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you.
local: Mena dɔɔ nombia kɛŋa yaaseewɔ Yesu kaseela baŋ man nyiaa, kaseele ŋon Yesu ne gyae ɔ nombia nɛ be gyae waa yeŋ ya. Mɔna Yesu te yako nyi ɔ be gyae waa yeŋ ya. Keŋ ɔ be yako yɛna nyi, Akpaa mɛɛ gyae nyi waa kyaa kelii debaŋ keŋ maa bese kɔŋ na, n woŋ faa la.

eng: This is the disciple who testifies concerning these things and who wrote them down. We know that his testimony is true.
local: Mena kaseele kei diina nombia kɛŋa wose man adansɛɛ te ɔ ŋmarase ŋa see. Te da gyeŋ mɔ nyi wa adansɛɛ keŋ yɛɛ ampaŋ.

eng: Jesus did many other things as well. If all of them were recorded, I imagine the world itself wouldnt have enough room for the scrolls that would be written.
local: Yesu yɛɛ abɔɔ burum ŋaale mɔ. Akpaa beŋ ŋmarase ŋa dokoloŋ dokoloŋ nɛ, tɛɛle kei dɔɔ pou bɔɔ gyae baa nyiŋ botɔɔ keŋ baa mo ŋa see ya.

eng: Theophilus, the first scroll I wrote concerned everything Jesus did and taught from the beginning.
local: Kegyia Teyofilose, maŋ Luka ne ŋmarase na tɔne kei mee yelee neŋ. Me tɔne gyaŋgbate keŋ man nɛ, ma wola abɔɔ ŋan pou Yesu be yɛɛ na ŋan ɔ ba wolo debaŋ keŋ ɔ be fi ɔ tom asɛɛ.

eng: right up to the day when he was taken up into heaven. Before he was taken up, working in the power of the Holy Spirit, Jesus instructed the apostles he had chosen.
local: mo kelii wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo e laŋ adido nɛ. Pɛ Wurubuarɛ ka mo Yesu gyu adido nɛ, Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon tɛɛwɔ ŋon Yesu dɔɔ wola ɔ kpilala baŋ ɔ ba lese nɛ tom keŋ baa yɛɛ.

eng: After his suffering, he showed them that he was alive with many convincing proofs. He appeared to them over a period of forty days, speaking to them about Gods kingdom.
local: Yesu Kristo ka yeŋ wɔle weeya sɔŋola man nɛ, ɔ lese ɔ wose wola ɔ kaseela baŋ agbaatee agbaatee lee gbɛɛneŋ burum man wola nyi ɔ delena, keŋ nyi ŋolo be tale lɛɛ ka aŋmaareŋ ya. Be naa e na be sia, te ɔ kolosiwɔ fa wɔ lee Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ wose man.

eng: While they were eating together, he ordered them not to leave Jerusalem but to wait for what the Father had promised. He said, This is what you heard from me.
local: Ba kekyaa man nɛ, wee daale ɔ faree ɔ kaseela baŋ nyi Ɛ na lee Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man ya, mɔna ɛ kyaa botɔɔ daa kpene keŋ me kya be yako see nyi waa fa ŋon nɛ. Me wulaa yako ŋon ka wose man nombia nyi.

eng: John baptized with water, but in only a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: loŋ te Gyɔn moowɔ sɔ ŋon Wurubuarɛ loŋ, mɔna weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale wɔle na Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon bo sɔ ŋon Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

eng: As a result, those who had gathered together asked Jesus, Lord, are you going to restore the kingdom to Israel now.
local: Debaŋ keŋ Yesu na ɔ kpilala baŋ be yilaa nɛ te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, mena dɔɔ nɛnɛɛ n ka besena gyoori keŋ fa Iseraetena.

eng: Jesus replied, It isnt for you to know the times or seasons that the Father has set by his own authority.
local: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyi, Ka te yɛɛ kpene keŋ nyi ɛ ka gyeŋ debaŋ keŋ ya, nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ na wee keŋ mena nombii kei gyae ke kɔŋ dɔɔ, kyae me kya gyaŋ. Ɔ doŋ dɔɔ te ɔ see ŋa.

eng: Rather, you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you, and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the end of the earth.
local: Mɔna debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do ɛ man nɛ, ɛ ke nyiŋ doŋ. Na ɛ ka bese ma adansɛɛtena Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man, na Gyudia na Samaria tɛɛlese pou dɔɔ mo kelii tɛɛle pou ɔto.

eng: After Jesus said these things, as they were watching, he was lifted up and a cloud took him out of their sight.
local: Ɔ kekolosi kɛŋa pou taŋ na, te Wurubuarɛ mo e laŋ adido be popou siaman ka gyoo ŋɔma man. Mena dɔɔ ba te naa e bela ya.

eng: While he was going away and as they were staring toward heaven, suddenly two men in white robes stood next to them.
local: Be ka seŋ botɔɔ be sɛɛ bɛɛ kɛɛ adido fee nɛ, karatetee na tewulɛɛ bala baale seŋɛɛ laa ba yenaŋ na ba doona abɔɔ wolomɛɛ.

eng: They said, Galileans, why are you standing here, looking toward heaven? This Jesus, who was taken up from you into heaven, will come in the same way that you saw him go into heaven.
local: Te be bɔɔse Yesu kpilala baŋ nyi, Galeliatena, weera te ɛ seŋɛɛ ɛ ne kɛɛ adido fee mena? Yesu kei, ŋon Wurubuarɛ ba mo e lee ɛ gyaŋ laŋ adido nɛ, mena ke te waa bese kɔŋ ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ be naa e ɔ ne gyu adido nɛ.

eng: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the Mount of Olives, which is near Jerusalem—a sabbath days journey away.
local: Te ɔ kpilala baŋ besewɔ lee oliifi bula keŋ dɔɔ kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm. Lee botɔɔ kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ keŋ man benaa na dɔŋa. Gyae ke yɛɛ ŋgba wɔɔ ŋmeeseŋ sao na kufu nɛ.

eng: When they entered the city, they went to the upstairs room where they were staying. Peter, John, James, and Andrew; Philip and Thomas; Bartholomew and Matthew; James, Alphaeus son; Simon the zealot; and Judas, James son.
local: Ba ke kɔŋ kaa gyoo Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ, te ba gyeŋawɔ gyu abansoro deni be dɛɛkyae keŋ man. Balaŋ baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ yɛna, Pita na Gyɔn na Anderiase na Filipo na Tomase na Batolomeo na Mateo na Alfeose bu Gyemisi na Simɔn ŋon ɔ ne gyae ɔ balaŋ nideli nɛ, na Gyemisi bu Gyudase.

eng: all were united in their devotion to prayer, along with some women, including Mary the mother of Jesus, and his brothers.
local: Be popou na ala baale na Yesu ɔ naa Mɛɛre, na Yesu ɔ naabia ne yɛɛ na nɔɔdokoloŋ yilaa fane debaŋ kamasɛ.

eng: During this time, the family of believers was a company of about one hundred twenty persons. Peter stood among them and said.
local: Wee daale balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di nɛ kaa yilaawɔ, na baa yɛɛ ŋgba balaŋ sɔŋonoŋ na balaŋ sao nɛ . Botɔɔ te Pita korowɔ seŋ yako wɔ nyiaa.

eng: Brothers and sisters, the scripture that the Holy Spirit announced beforehand through David had to be fulfilled. This was the scripture concerning Judas, who became a guide for those who arrested Jesus.
local: Me tebia, nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ nombia be yako see lee Gyudase wose man nɛ, kaboena nyi ŋe dɛɛ kɔŋ mena. Gyudase kei wose man te gyoo Defidi yakowɔ see nyiaa, ŋon ne gyae la waa ta balaŋ baŋ baa gyu ke kyaŋ Yesu nɛ siaman.

eng: This happened even though he was one of us and received a share of this ministry. .
local: Nafɔ na Gyudase kei kpuɛ de man, nawolo nyi Yesu lese na ŋon mɔ nyi waa kpu na daa na dɛɛ yɛɛ tom kei.

eng: In fact, he bought a field with the payment he received for his injustice. Falling headfirst, he burst open in the middle and all his intestines spilled out.
local: Kɔba keŋ ŋon Gyudase be nyim lee ɔ nombiakumɛɛ kɛŋa keyɛɛ man nɛ, te ɔ moowɔ lɛɛ tɛɛle. Tɛɛle kenaŋ man te ɔ yalawɔ te ɔ tɔɔ bɛɛwɔ te ɔ loobia pou leewɔ.

eng: This became known to everyone living in Jerusalem, so they called that field in their own language Hakeldama, or Field of Blood.
local: Balaŋ baŋ pou be kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ nyii mena nombii kei, mena dɔɔ ba doo botɔɔ yele be dɛɛ dei man nyi Akɛledama, keŋ nawolo nyi Fatabo tɛɛle.

eng: It is written in the Psalms scroll, Let his home become deserted and let there be no one living in it; and Give his position of leadership to another. .
local: Gyoo Defidi yako Gyudase wose man nombia Wurubuarɛ Nɔɔneŋ tɔne keŋ man nyi, Ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ dɛɛ doo yakaa keŋ ŋolo na kyaa ke man ya. Ɔ besewɔ yako nyi, Walaŋ wɔle waa kyaa ɔ nawɔɔ man na waa yɛɛ ɔ tom.

eng: Therefore, we must select one of those who have accompanied us during the whole time the Lord Jesus lived among us.
local: Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi ŋolo waa kyaa ɔ nawɔɔ man kpu na daa, na dee di de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu kefoŋ lee yeŋ man keŋ adansɛɛ. Kaboena nyi walaŋ ŋonaŋ waa yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon na ɔ kpuɛ na daa debaŋ keŋ pou daa na de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ne gyu dɛɛ kɔŋ, lee debaŋ keŋ Gyɔn be sɔ e Wurubuarɛ loŋ kelii debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo e lee de gyaŋ gyu adido nɛ.

eng: So they nominated two: Joseph called Barsabbas, who was also known as Justus, and Matthias.
local: Mena dɔɔ ba lese Gyosɛfo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Basabase yaa Gyustuse nɛ na Matiase.

eng: They prayed, Lord, you know everyones deepest thoughts and desires. Show us clearly which one you have chosen from among these two.
local: Te be fanewɔ nyi, De Gbeŋgyoo, nyaŋ gyeŋa na walaŋ kamasɛ konɔɔ man. Mena dɔɔ wola daa tewulɛɛ bala kɛwɔ man ŋon n ba lese nyi waa yɛɛ Yesu kpilale.

eng: to take the place of this ministry and apostleship, from which Judas turned away to go to his own place.
local: na waa kyaa Gyudase ŋon ɔ be tina ɔ tom yela te ɔ be lam gyu botɔɔ keŋ kaboena e nɛ nawɔɔ man.

eng: When they cast lots, the lot fell on Matthias. He was added to the eleven apostles.
local: Mena dɔɔ be lɔɔ boe, te boe keŋ yalawɔ Matiase yele dɔɔ. Kei nawolo nyi ŋon te Wurubuarɛ lesewɔ, te ba moo e kpu Yesu kpilala kufu daale baŋ man.

eng: When Pentecost Day arrived, they were all together in one place.
local: Gyudatena wee dinaa keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Pentekosite keŋ kelii nɛ, baŋ pou bɔɔ lɛɛ Kristo Yesu di nɛ ke yilaawɔ debodokoloŋ.

eng: Suddenly a sound from heaven like the howling of a fierce wind filled the entire house where they were sitting.
local: Karatetee dokoloŋ te be nyii nkaŋ daale lee adido ŋgba feliŋ dinaa daale ne gyɔɔ la nɛ, te ke kaa lɛɛ dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man be kyaa nɛ pou.

eng: They saw what seemed to be individual flames of fire alighting on each one of them.
local: Botɔɔ te be naa kolo ŋgba boalaŋ laakemse nɛ, te ŋe yaaseewɔ kaa gyakaa walaŋ kamasɛ nyee dɔɔ.

eng: They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other languages as the Spirit enabled them to speak.
local: Te Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do be popou man, te be fiasɛɛ bɛɛ kolosi deise kpookpoo ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon be fa wɔ nɛ.

eng: There were pious Jews from every nation under heaven living in Jerusalem.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na Gyudatena baŋ bee yee Wurubuarɛ te bɔɔ lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ pou nɛ, kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ keŋ man.

eng: When they heard this sound, a crowd gathered. They were mystified because everyone heard them speaking in their native languages.
local: Be kenyii mena nkaŋ kei nɛ, te balaŋ burum kaa yilaawɔ. Balaŋ baŋ pou wose yeŋa wɔ nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ nyiiwɔ ŋgba bee kolosi ɔ dɛɛ dei nɛ.

eng: They were surprised and amazed, saying, Look, arent all the people who are speaking Galileans, every one of them?.
local: Yɛɛ wɔ gyakoloŋ te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, balaŋ anekoloseŋ kɛwɔ pou te yɛɛ Galeliatenawɔ yee?.

eng: How then can each of us hear them speaking in our native language?.
local: Te weera de man walaŋ kamasɛ ne nyii bee kolosi ŋon gbagba ɔ dɛɛ dei?.

eng: Parthians, Medes, and Elamites; as well as residents of Mesopotamia, Judea, and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia.
local: De popou lee tɛɛlese kpookpoo dɔɔ. De man baale lee Patia na Media na Elam te baale mɔ lee Mesopotamia na Gyudia na Kapadosia, te baale mɔ lee Pɔntuse na Esia.

eng: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the regions of Libya bordering Cyrene; and visitors from Rome.
local: Baale lee Firigia na Pamfilia na Igyipite te baale mɔ lee Libia tɛɛlese ŋaale ŋan benaa na donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke Kirene nɛ man.

eng: Cretans and Arabs—we hear them declaring the mighty works of God in our own languages.
local: Gyudatena na baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya te bɔɔ kyɛɛkee gyoo Gyudatena ɔsom man nɛ, man baale mɔ lee Roman donɔɔ man kɔŋ. De baale mɔ lee Kreti na Arebia, mɔna dee nyii bee kolosi nombia dinaana ŋan Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ nɛ daa gbagba de deise man.

eng: They were all surprised and bewildered. Some asked each other, What does this mean.
local: Gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ te ba nyeeya man mɔ yɛɛ wɔ basaa te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, na nombia kɛŋa asɛɛ yɛna sena baa?.

eng: Others jeered at them, saying, Theyre full of new wine.
local: Mɔna be man baale mɔ kulu wɔ nyi kei dɔɔ be dɛɛsee.

eng: Peter stood with the other eleven apostles. He raised his voice and declared, Judeans and everyone living in Jerusalem! Know this! Listen carefully to my words!.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita na ɔ tebia Yesu kpilala kufu daale baŋ korowɔ seŋ, te ɔ gyinaa ɔ woya kolosi fa balaŋ dikpii keŋ nyiaa, Me tebia Gyudatena na ɛmɛɛ baŋ pou ɛ kyaa Gyerusalɛm nɛ, ɛ tei me gyaŋ nideli na maa wola ŋon kpene keŋ nombii kei nawolo.

eng: These people arent drunk, as you suspect; after all, its only nine oclock in the morning!.
local: Ne yɛɛ ŋon nyi balaŋ kɛwɔ dɛɛsee, mɔna na be dɛɛsee ya. Nawolo nyi nebo weese gba yaa, nɛnɛɛ te gyɔɔ debaŋ kadaale.

eng: Rather, this is what was spoken through the prophet Joel.
local: Mɔna nombii kei yɛna kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Gyowɛle be kolosi see nyi ke gyae ke kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: In the last days, God says, I will pour out my Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young will see visions. Your elders will dream dreams. .
local: Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Gyowɛle yakowɔ see nyi, Wurubuarɛ kpa, Tɛɛle kei na benaa kɔ ɔto na, maa mo me feliŋ do walaŋ kamasɛ man. Ɛ bia ala na baala baa kolosi nombia see ma yele man. Ɛ tewulɛɛ baa naa me nombia ŋan ŋe doo na ŋe gbɛɛ nɛ na be sia ŋgba dosɛɛ man nɛ. Akpegyiina mɔ baa doosi naa me nombia ŋan ŋe doo na ŋe gbɛɛ nɛ.

eng: Even upon my servants, men and women, I will pour out my Spirit in those days, and they will prophesy. .
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ man nɛ maa mo me feliŋ do ma alebia na me tewulɛɛ man na baa kolosi nombia see ma yele man.

eng: I will cause wonders to occur in the heavens above and signs on the earth below, blood and fire and a cloud of smoke. .
local: Maa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia adido, na maa lese me wɔɔle wola tɛɛle kei dɔɔ. Ɛ gyae ɛ kena fatabo na boalaŋ na nyɔɔse tuum.

eng: The sun will be changed into darkness, and the moon will be changed into blood, before the great and spectacular day of the Lord comes. .
local: Weeseteŋteŋ gyae ka bese ditiŋtɛɛ tuum na gyaalaŋ ke yase ŋgba fatabo nɛ. Nombia kɛŋa pou gyae ke kɔŋ, pɛte wee dinaa keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo waa lese ɔ gyoorobiiri wola nɛ kelii.

eng: And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved. .
local: Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa baake de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ yele na, waa lɛɛ ɔ nyee. .

eng: Fellow Israelites, listen to these words! Jesus the Nazarene was a man whose credentials God proved to you through miracles, wonders, and signs, which God performed through him among you. You yourselves know this.
local: Pita gyina ɔ kekolosi keŋ dɔɔ nyiaa, Iseraetena, ɛ tei nombia kɛŋa nideli. Wurubuarɛ tɛɛwɔ Yesu Nasarɛte baale ŋon dɔɔ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia lese ɔ wɔɔle wola ɛ man ŋgba mena keŋ ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ gyeŋ nɛ. Kɛŋa diina adansɛɛ nyi Wurubuarɛ kpila na e.

eng: In accordance with Gods established plan and foreknowledge, he was betrayed. You, with the help of wicked men, had Jesus killed by nailing him to a cross.
local: Lee Wurubuarɛ gbagba kegyaebii na ɔ ka gyeŋ man te ɔ wulaa bakewɔ see nyi baa lese Yesu fa ŋon, te ɛ yela balaŋ kumɛɛ gyɔɔ e ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ ko.

eng: God raised him up! God freed him from deaths dreadful grip, since it was impossible for death to hang on to him.
local: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ gyuusu e lee yeŋ man, te ɔ lese e lee yeŋ weeleŋ man, nawolo nyi yeŋ be tale kyaŋ e see ɔ gyaŋ kekpaakekpaa ya.

eng: David says about him, I foresaw that the Lord was always with me; because he is at my right hand I wont be shaken. .
local: Gyoo Defidi yakowɔ lee Yesu wose man nyi, Me naa de Gbeŋgyoo ŋon me siaman debaŋ kamasɛ. Ɔ seŋɛɛ ma yenaŋ mena dɔɔ kolo be tale tɛɛse maŋ ya.

eng: Therefore, my heart was glad and my tongue rejoiced. Moreover, my body will live in hope, .
local: mena dɔɔ me wɔe te fɛɛ maŋ, me sia mɔ ta gye. Me yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon maa yeŋ wee daale, mɔna me sia gyakaa abɔɔ ŋan Wurubuarɛ waa kaa fa man nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: because you wont abandon me to the grave, nor permit your holy one to experience decay. .
local: Nawolo nyi n be gyae n ke yela maa dɔɔ yebɔɔ man ya. N be gyae n ke yela n walaŋ ŋon ayimɛɛ be doo ɔ man ya nɛ ke doo yebɔɔ man dala ya.

eng: You have shown me the paths of life; your presence will fill me with happiness. .
local: N ta wola maŋ nyeedoŋ gbɛɛ. Nawolo nyi n kpuɛ na maŋ dɔɔ te yɛɛ maŋ gyoŋ nideli.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I can speak confidently about the patriarch David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this very day.
local: Me tebia, mɛɛ gyae maa yako ŋon kolo lee de naana Defidi wose man, ɔ yeŋawɔ te be wuu e, ɔ yebɔɔ sɛɛ doo de gyaŋ kɛbo kaa lii na gyɛŋ.

eng: Because he was a prophet, he knew that God promised him with a solemn pledge to seat one of his descendants on his throne.
local: Na ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e, te ɔ gyeŋ nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ be kaŋ ntam yako see lee ɔ wose man nyi, waa mo Defidi duuluŋ man ŋolo see gyoo na waa kyaa ɔ gyoorogbeli keŋ dɔɔ di gyoori ŋgba ŋon Defidi nɛ.

eng: Having seen this beforehand, David spoke about the resurrection of Christ, that he wasnt abandoned to the grave, nor did his body experience decay .
local: Gyoo Defidi naa kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa kaa yɛɛ, mena dɔɔ ɔ kolosiwɔ lee Kristo kefoŋ lee yeŋ man wose man nombia nyi, Wurubuarɛ te tina e yela yebɔɔ man na waa dala ya.

eng: This Jesus God raised up. We are all witnesses to that fact.
local: Wurubuarɛ te gyuusu Yesu kei lee yeŋ man, te de popou ne di adansɛɛ nyi nombii kei yɛɛ ampaŋ.

eng: He was exalted to Gods right side and received from the Father the promised Holy Spirit. He poured out this Spirit, and you are seeing and hearing the results of his having done so.
local: Ɔ kya Wurubuarɛ gyinaa e adido te ɔ moo e see ɔ dunoluŋ dɔɔ te ɔ moo ɔ feliŋ ŋon fa e ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be kaŋ ntam see nɛ. Mena feliŋ kei yaa Yesu moowɔ do de man gyɛŋ. Wurubuarɛ feliŋ kei tom yaa ɛ ne naa te ɛ ne nyii nɛ.

eng: David didnt ascend into heaven. Yet he says, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right side, .
local: Gyoo Defidi gbagba te gyu adido ŋgba Yesu nɛ ya, mɔna ɔ talewɔ yako Yesu wose man nombia nyi, De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ŋon yako me Gbeŋgyoo nyi, Kyaa me dunoluŋ dɔɔ.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet. .
local: kelii debaŋ keŋ maa mo baŋ bɛɛ kɔla neŋ nɛ do n nawɔɔ tɛɛ. .

eng: Therefore, let all Israel know beyond question that God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, both Lord and Christ.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ Iseraetena pou ɛ gyeŋ nideli nyi, Yesu kei ŋon ɛ be gyɔ e nyeelabia ko ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ, ŋon ke te Wurubuarɛ moowɔ yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo na da nyeelɛɛre ŋon ɔ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: When the crowd heard this, they were deeply troubled. They said to Peter and the other apostles, Brothers, what should we do.
local: Balaŋ baŋ kenyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, takaa wɔ nideli te be bɔɔse Pita na Yesu kpilala akaŋ baŋ nyiaa, De tebia, sena yaa dɛɛ yɛɛ.

eng: Peter replied, Change your hearts and lives. Each of you must be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. Then you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
local: Te Pita tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyi, Walaŋ kamasɛ waa kyɛɛkee lee ɔ nombiakumɛɛ man, na baa sɔ e Wurubuarɛ loŋ lee Yesu Kristo yele man, na Wurubuarɛ ka mo ɔ nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ e, na waa mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do ɔ man.

eng: This promise is for you, your children, and for all who are far away—as many as the Lord our God invites.
local: Wurubuarɛ nombii keŋ ɔ be yako see nyi waa mo ɔ feliŋ do ɛ man nɛ, yɛɛ ɛmɛɛ na ɛ bia na baŋ pou be kyaa lemlem, na baŋ pou de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ waa baake wɔ kɔŋ ŋon gbagba gyaŋ nɛ wui.

eng: With many other words he testified to them and encouraged them, saying, Be saved from this perverse generation.
local: Pita kolosiwɔ fa wɔ nideli te ɔ faree wɔ te ɔ sola wɔ nyi, Ɛ lese ɛ wose lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ kumɛɛ kɛwɔ man, na Wurubuarɛ ke lɛɛ ɛ nyee.

eng: Those who accepted Peters message were baptized. God brought about three thousand people into the community on that day.
local: Be man balaŋ burum lɛɛ ɔ nombia ŋan di, te be sɔɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ. Balaŋ kakpoŋse batooro kaa kpu la na Kristotena dikpii keŋ mena wee kenaŋ.

eng: The believers devoted themselves to the apostles teaching, to the community, to their shared meals, and to their prayers.
local: Be kyaŋa Yesu kpilala baŋ kawola man nideli, te be popou ne yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ gyaŋee debaŋ kamasɛ. Bɛɛ yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ di weenɛɛ ŋan Yesu kpa baa di na baa mo tɔɔse e nɛ, te bɛɛ bese yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ gyaŋee fane.

eng: A sense of awe came over everyone. God performed many wonders and signs through the apostles.
local: Yesu Kpilala baŋ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia burum, te gyakoloŋ gyoo balaŋ baŋ pou.

eng: All the believers were united and shared everything.
local: Baŋ pou bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di nɛ, yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ ba kekyaa man, na bɛɛ kɛɛ ba abɔɔ ŋan be dana nɛ nyi yɛɛ be popou wui.

eng: They would sell pieces of property and possessions and distribute the proceeds to everyone who needed them.
local: Te be yɔɔ ba abɔɔ ŋaale mɔ, na bɛɛ kpala kɔba keŋ fa walaŋ kamasɛ ŋgba mena keŋ ne hia e nɛ.

eng: Every day, they met together in the temple and ate in their homes. They shared food with gladness and simplicity.
local: Wee kamasɛ na, be dikpii keŋ ne gyaŋee be Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man. Bɛɛ yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ te bɛɛ mo konɔɔ kpaakpaa, na sia kagye di weenɛɛ bom ba akpaŋalaŋse man.

eng: They praised God and demonstrated Gods goodness to everyone. The Lord added daily to the community those who were being saved.
local: Bɛɛ lese Wurubuarɛ yele, te balaŋ pou mɔ ne kɛlɛɛ wɔ. Te wee kamasɛ mɔ nɛ, de Gbeŋgyoo na mo balaŋ wɔlɛɛ baŋ ɔ ne lɛɛ ba nyeeya nɛ kaa kpu na wɔ.

eng: Peter and John were going up to the temple at three oclock in the afternoon, the established prayer time.
local: Wee daale balinɔɔ weese ŋgba debaŋ atoorote man nɛ, Pita na Gyɔn korowɔ gyu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nyi be kaa fane. Debaŋ kenaŋ yɛna kefane debaŋ.

eng: Meanwhile, a man crippled since birth was being carried in. Every day, people would place him at the temple gate known as the Beautiful Gate so he could ask for money from those entering the temple.
local: Na balee ŋolo kyaa keŋ ɔ yɛɛ ɔtakase e leenaŋ ɔ biatɛɛ. Wee kamasɛ nɛ bɛɛ mo e kaa see Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa denanɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Denanɔɔ Kyeote nɛ man, na waa sola kɔba lee balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ gyoo deni kenaŋ man nɛ gyaŋ.

eng: When he saw Peter and John about to enter, he began to ask them for a gift.
local: Debaŋ keŋ ɔ be naa nyi Pita na Gyɔn ne kɔŋ be kaa gyoo deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ, te ɔ sola wɔ nyi baa fa e kɔba.

eng: Peter and John stared at him. Peter said, Look at us.
local: Keŋte be kɛɛ e yididi te Pita yako e nyiaa, Kɛɛ daa.

eng: So the man gazed at them, expecting to receive something from them.
local: Te balee ŋon mɔ kɛɛ wɔ gyakaa loo nyi, waa nyiŋ kolo lee be gyaŋ.

eng: Peter said, I dont have any money, but I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene, rise up and walk.
local: Te Pita yako e nyi, Nyi kɔba na, mɔɔ dana ŋaale maa fa neŋ ya, mɔna kpene keŋ me dana nɛ te maa fa neŋ. Lee Yesu Kristo Nasarɛte baale ŋon yele man nɛ, koro seŋ na n ke ta.

eng: Then he grasped the mans right hand and raised him up. At once his feet and ankles became strong.
local: Te Pita kyaŋa ɔ dunoluŋ nyiŋmaa man kyɔ e te ɔ korowɔ seŋ. Debokenaŋ man te ɔ doona na ɔ nawɔɔ pou yɛɛ e doŋ.

eng: Jumping up, he began to walk around. He entered the temple with them, walking, leaping, and praising God.
local: Karatetee dokoloŋ te ɔ toŋawɔ seŋ ɔ nawɔɔ dɔɔ, te ɔ fiasɛɛ ɔ ne tɛɛ. Keŋte ɔ sila wɔ gyoo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ɔ dɔŋɛɛ na ɔ na toŋee na ɔ na lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

eng: All the people saw him walking and praising God.
local: Balaŋ baŋ pou be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ, naa e na ɔ dɔŋɛɛ ɔ na lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

eng: They recognized him as the same one who used to sit at the temples Beautiful Gate asking for money. They were filled with amazement and surprise at what had happened to him.
local: Te be ka gyeŋ e nyi ŋon yɛna walaŋ ŋon nafɔ ɔ ne kyaa Denanɔɔ Kyeote keŋ man solee abɔɔ nɛ, ba wose yeŋa wɔ gbaa, te gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ lee kpene keŋ bɔɔ naa ɔ man nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: While the healed man clung to Peter and John, all the people rushed toward them at Solomons Porch, completely amazed.
local: Balaŋ baŋ kena balee ŋon mataa na Pita na Gyɔn botɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake nyi Solomɔn poto man nɛ, doo be popou nɔɔ te be yeuwɔ gyu be gyaŋ botɔɔ.

eng: Seeing this, Peter addressed the people: You Israelites, why are you amazed at this? Why are you staring at us as if we made him walk by our own power or piety?.
local: Pita kena balaŋ baŋ nɛ ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Iseraetena, weera dɔɔ te kei ne doo ɛ nɔɔ? Yaa weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne kɛɛ daa fee mena? Ɛ fɛɛ ɛ sia baa ɛ ne kɛɛ daa nyi mberem daa gbagba de doŋ na de Wurubuarɛ keyee dɔɔ te de yela balee kei tɛɛwɔ?.

eng: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—the God of our ancestors—has glorified his servant Jesus. This is the one you handed over and denied in Pilates presence, even though he had already decided to release him.
local: De naanaɔ Aberaham na Asiki na Gyekɔpo Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ de naanaɔ pou Wurubuarɛ e nɛ, lesena ɔ tobaale Yesu gyoorobiiri wola. Ɛmɛɛ moona e do Romantena kegyiise nyiŋmaa man, te ɛ bɛɛ e Pilato siaman nyi baa ko e, mɔna nafɔ ɔ yɛɛ wa agyueŋ koraŋ nyi waa tina e yela, nyi ɔ te naa dukum ɔ man ya.

eng: You rejected the holy and righteous one, and asked that a murderer be released to you instead.
local: Ɛ bɛɛ Yesu ŋon ayimɛɛ be doo ɔ man ya te ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman man nɛ, te ɛ sola Pilato nyi waa tina awudiini Barabase ŋon bɔɔ kyaŋ tɔ deni nɛ yela fa ŋon.

eng: You killed the author of life, the very one whom God raised from the dead. We are witnesses of this.
local: Te ɛ koo Yesu ŋon ɔ ne fa balaŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa nɛ. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ gyuusu e lee yeŋ man keŋ de popou ne di ka wose man adansɛɛ.

eng: His name itself has made this man strong. That is, because of faith in Jesus name, God has strengthened this man whom you see and know. The faith that comes through Jesus gave him complete health right before your eyes.
local: Mena keŋ dɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di dɔɔ, te Wurubuarɛ yela balee aseŋaŋ ɛ siaman kei ŋon ɛ gyeŋ e nɛ, nyiŋa kawee kekyɔ. Ke yɛɛ ampaŋ nyi mena keŋ dɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di dɔɔ, te balee kei nyiŋa wosedoŋ, ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ ne naa e nɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I know you acted in ignorance. So did your rulers.
local: Me tebia, ma gyeŋ nyi kpene keŋ ɛmɛɛ na ɛ siamantena be yɛɛ Yesu nɛ, ɛ ta gyem bo ya.

eng: But this is how God fulfilled what he foretold through all the prophets: that his Christ would suffer.
local: Lee gyaŋgba gyaŋgba keŋ, te Wurubuarɛ tɛɛwɔ wa akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra pou dɔɔ yako see nyi, nyeelɛɛre ŋon ɔ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ, waa naa diyem. Wurubuarɛ yele la nombia kɛŋa pou kɔŋawɔ mena.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Turn back to God so that your sins may be wiped away.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ kyɛɛkee lee ɛ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man, na ɛ kekɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ, na waa gberaa ya ayimɛɛ pou lee ɛ wose man.

eng: Then the Lord will provide a season of relief from the distress of this age and he will send Jesus, whom he handpicked to be your Christ.
local: Na de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kefa ŋon wosefɛɛreŋ na waa yela ŋon ɔ yɛɛ nyeelɛɛre ŋon ɔ be wula lese e see fa ŋon nɛ kekɔŋ. Ŋon yɛna Yesu.

eng: Jesus must remain in heaven until the restoration of all things, about which God spoke long ago through his holy prophets.
local: Kaboena nyi waa kyaa adido kelii debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ wɔle, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ wa akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra dɔɔ yako see gyaŋgba keŋ nɛ.

eng: Moses said, The Lord your God will raise up from your own people a prophet like me. Listen to whatever he tells you. .
local: Na Mosesi mɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e, ɔ yakowɔ see nyi, Ɛ Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ waa lese dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre lee ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ balaŋ man fa ŋon, ŋgba maŋ Mosesi nɛ. Ɛ tei kpene kamasɛ keŋ waa yako ŋon nɛ.

eng: Whoever doesnt listen to that prophet will be totally cut off from the people. .
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ te tei kpene keŋ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre kei waa yako ya nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa lese e lee ɔ balaŋ man na waa yo.

eng: All the prophets who spoke—from Samuel forward—announced these days.
local: Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ be dɔɔ kolosi nɛ pou, lee Samuɛle dɔɔ kelii baŋ bɔɔ kɔŋ ɔ wɔle pou nɛ, be wula yako kolo see lee nombia ŋan ne kɔŋ weeya kɛŋa man nɛ man.

eng: You are the heirs of the prophets and the covenant that God made with your ancestors when he told Abraham, Through your descendants, all the families on earth will be blessed. .
local: Nombia ŋan pou Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ wa akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra dɔɔ yako see nɛ yɛɛ ɛ wui, te ɛmɛɛ mɔ kpuɛ kebakesee keŋ Wurubuarɛ be bake na ɛ naanaɔ nɛ man. Nawolo nyi ɔ yako Aberaham nyi, N duuluŋ man te maa ta hiraa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou.

eng: After God raised his servant, he sent him to you first—to bless you by enabling each of you to turn from your evil ways.
local: Mena dɔɔ debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba lese ɔ tobaale Yesu nɛ, ɔ yela ɔ taŋgbɛɛwɔ kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ nyi ɔ kaa hiraa ŋon, na waa yela ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ kekyɛɛkee lee ɔ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man.

eng: While Peter and John were speaking to the people, the priests, the captain of the temple guard, and the Sadducees confronted them.
local: Debaŋ keŋ Pita na Gyɔn sɛɛ bɛɛ kolosi fa balaŋ baŋ nɛ, te Wurubuarɛ saese na kegyia ŋon ɔ ne kɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ gyee Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ nɛ, na balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ baake wɔ nyi Sadukiitena nɛ baale kɔŋawɔ be gyaŋ.

eng: They were incensed that the apostles were teaching the people and announcing that the resurrection of the dead was happening because of Jesus.
local: Ba gyeŋa baŋ nideli nawolo nyi, Pita na Gyɔn na wola balaŋ baŋ nyi, Yesu te fom lee yeŋ man, keŋ nawolo nyi balaŋ pou baa foŋ lee yeŋ man bela.

eng: They seized Peter and John and put them in prison until the next day.
local: Mena dɔɔ be kyaŋa Pita na Gyɔn ka do deni man, na tɛɛ te lam dɔɔ, be yela be doowɔ kelii tɛɛ kena.

eng: Many who heard the word became believers, and their number grew to about five thousand.
local: Mɔna balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ nyii nombia ŋan Pita na Gyɔn be yako nɛ, be man burum lɛɛ ŋa di, mena dɔɔ balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di nɛ, be man baala wukuwɔ kelii balaŋ kakpoŋse banoŋ .

eng: The next day the leaders, elders, and legal experts gathered in Jerusalem.
local: Tɛɛ kena keŋ nɛ, Gyudatena kegyiise na be siamantena na ba mmaraa wolala baŋ gyaŋeewɔ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man.

eng: along with Annas the high priest, Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and others from the high priests family.
local: Be gyaŋeewɔ na saese pou kegyia Anase na Kaifase na Gyɔn na Alɛkesanda na baala baale baŋ bɔɔ lee saese pou kegyia ŋon dekpaŋalaŋ man nɛ.

eng: They had Peter and John brought before them and asked, By what power or in what name did you do this.
local: Be yela ba moo Pita na Gyɔn kaa seŋ be siaman te be bɔɔse wɔ nyi, Woŋ wɔɔle yaa amɔte yele man yaa ɛ kyɔɔ balee ŋon.

eng: Then Peter, inspired by the Holy Spirit, answered, Leaders of the people and elders.
local: Te Pita ŋon Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon doo ɔ man nideli nɛ, tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyi, Kegyiise na siamantena.

eng: are we being examined today because something good was done for a sick person, a good deed that healed him?.
local: akpaa gyɛŋ ɛ te baake daa kaa seŋ ɛ siaman ɛ ne bɔɔse daa lee kolo kpaakpaa keŋ dɔɔ yɛɛ, te ɔtakase ŋon be nyiŋ doŋ koro seŋ nɛ dɔɔ na.

eng: If so, then you and all the people of Israel need to know that this man stands healthy before you because of the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene—whom you crucified but whom God raised from the dead.
local: kenaŋ na mɛɛ gyae ɛmɛɛ na Iseraetena pou ka gyeŋ nyi, Yesu Kristo ŋon ɔ ba lee Nasarɛte nɛ yele man yaa balee aseŋaŋ ɛ siaman kei nyiŋa doŋ nɛ. Ŋon te ɛ gyɔɔwɔ sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ ko, te Wurubuarɛ gyuusu e lee yeŋ man.

eng: This Jesus is the stone you builders rejected; he has become the cornerstone!.
local: Yesu yɛna walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ nombia tɔne be yako nyi, Boe keŋ ɛmɛɛ deni maara baŋ be bɛɛ ke nɛ, keŋ kaa bese na deni mokɔɔ kasee boe keŋ ne yeli deni keŋ ne yɛɛ doŋ nideli.

eng: Salvation can be found in no one else. Throughout the whole world, no other name has been given among humans through which we must be saved.
local: Yesu waageŋ kooŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre, ŋon waageŋ kooŋ te Wurubuarɛ lesewɔ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyi de baake ɔ yele na waa lɛɛ da nyee.

eng: The council was caught by surprise by the confidence with which Peter and John spoke. After all, they understood that these apostles were uneducated and inexperienced. They also recognized that they had been followers of Jesus.
local: Mena keŋ Pita na Gyɔn be kolosi nideli te ba te yee gyakoloŋ ya, te kegyiise baŋ kenyii nyi be yɛɛ balaŋ yakaa nyibaŋwɔ keŋ be te gyu sukuu ya nɛ, ba wose yeŋa wɔ. Botɔɔ te be naawɔ nyi baŋ na Yesu tɛɛ la.

eng: However, since the healed man was standing with Peter and John before their own eyes, they had no rebuttal.
local: Mɔna ba kena balee ŋon bɔɔ kyɔ e seŋɛɛ Pita na Gyɔn gyaŋ botɔɔ nɛ, ba te tale nyiŋ nombiikum daale yako lee ba wose man ya.

eng: After ordering them to wait outside, the council members began to confer with each other.
local: Mena dɔɔ be yako wɔ nyi baa lee nombia dedii deni keŋ man, botɔɔ te kegyiise baŋ moo ba nyeeya baŋ na dɔŋa.

eng: What should we do with these men? Everyone living in Jerusalem is aware of the sign performed through them. Its obvious to everyone and we cant deny it.
local: Bɛɛ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyi, Woŋ yaa dɛɛ yɛɛ balaŋ kɛwɔ? Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, gyeŋ nyi baŋ yɛɛna gyakoloŋ nombii kei, te dɔɔ gyae dɛɛ tale lɛɛ ka aŋmaareŋ ya.

eng: To keep it from spreading further among the people, we need to warn them not to speak to anyone in this name.
local: Mena keŋ dɔɔ ne gyae nyi baa mo nombia kɛŋa kpeŋkpeleŋ dɔɔ ya nɛ, ɛ yela dɛɛ tɔ wɔ nideli nyi be na tekaa kolosi Yesu kei nombia fa walaŋ na walaŋ ya.

eng: When they called Peter and John back, they demanded that they stop all speaking and teaching in the name of Jesus.
local: Mena dɔɔ be baake wɔ gyoo be gyaŋ bela, te be tɔɔ wɔ nyi be na tekaa kolosi Yesu nombia, yaa baa wola balaŋ kolo lee ɔ yele man korakora bela ya.

eng: Peter and John responded, Its up to you to determine whether its right before God to obey you rather than God.
local: Keŋte Pita na Gyɔn tiranɔɔ nyi, Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ kɛɛ nombia kɛŋa man nideli nyi woŋti dei la Wurubuarɛ siaman? Dee nyii ɛ wui yaa dee nyii Wurubuarɛ wui?.

eng: As for us, we cant stop speaking about what we have seen and heard.
local: Nawolo nyi daa dɔɔ, dɔɔ tale tina kpene keŋ dɔɔ naa, na keŋ dɔɔ nyii nɛ kekolosi yela ya.

eng: They threatened them further, then released them. Because of public support for Peter and John, they couldnt find a way to punish them. Everyone was praising God for what had happened.
local: Mena dɔɔ kegyiise baŋ tɔɔ wɔ nideli bela, te be tina wɔ yela. Be te nyiŋ gbɛɛ keŋ baa mo wɔŋ ba deŋela ya, nawolo nyi balaŋ baŋ pou na lese Wurubuarɛ yele lee kpene keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: because the man who had experienced this sign of healing was over years old.
local: Balee ŋon bɔɔ kyɔ e kawee kei nɛ, kela kulutooneŋ sɔŋola .

eng: After their release, Peter and John returned to the brothers and sisters and reported everything the chief priests and elders had said.
local: Be ke tina Pita na Gyɔn yela nɛ, te ba besewɔ gyu be tebia gyaŋ keyako wɔ kpene kamasɛ keŋ saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise baŋ be yako wɔ nɛ.

eng: They listened, then lifted their voices in unison to God, Master, you are the one who created the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
local: Kristotena baŋ kenyii nombia ŋenaŋ nɛ, be popou moo nɔɔdokoloŋ fane fa Wurubuarɛ nyi, De Gbeŋgyoo, nyaŋ ŋon n be yɛɛ adido na ateta, na ɛpo na kpene kamasɛ keŋ doo ŋe man.

eng: You are the one who spoke by the Holy Spirit through our ancestor David, your servant: Why did the Gentiles rage , and the peoples plot in vain? .
local: Nyaŋ tɛɛ la n feliŋ ŋon dɔɔ te n yela de naana Defidi ŋon ɔ yɛɛ n tobaale e nɛ yakowɔ nyi, Weera dɔɔ te tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ na gyeŋee baŋse, te bɛɛ bake nombiakumɛɛ yakaa yakaa, mɔna ŋe bee dee ya nɛ?.

eng: The kings of the earth took their stand and the rulers gathered together as one against the Lord and against his Christ. .
local: Tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyooneŋ ne desina ba wose fa yoo, te siamantena baŋ mɔ na koro seŋ tia de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ na nyeelɛɛre ŋon ɔ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: Indeed, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with Gentiles and Israelites, did gather in this city against your holy servant Jesus, whom you anointed.
local: Ampaŋ te gyoo Herode na Pɔntiɔse Pilato na Iseraetena na baŋ mɔ ba te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ, gyaŋeewɔ donɔɔ kei man te be bakewɔ tia n tobaale Yesu ŋon ayimɛɛ be doo ɔ wose man ya, te nyaŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo e yɛɛ nyeelɛɛre na gyoo nɛ.

eng: They did what your power and plan had already determined would happen.
local: Be gyaŋeewɔ te be yɛɛ kpene keŋ nyaŋ Wurubuarɛ n be wula yako see nyi gyae kekɔŋ nɛ, lee n doŋ na n kegyaebii man.

eng: Now, Lord, take note of their threats and enable your servants to speak your word with complete confidence.
local: Nɛnɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ, kɛɛ mena keŋ bɛɛ ŋmaase daa na n ka do daa n tewulɛɛ kakyeŋ na dɛɛ tale gyɔ de konɔɔ kolosi n nombia ŋan.

eng: Stretch out your hand to bring healing and enable signs and wonders to be performed through the name of Jesus, your holy servant.
local: Wurubuarɛ koro seŋ na n kekyɔ kaweese, na n ke ta de dɔɔ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia lese n wɔɔle wola lee n tobaale Yesu ŋon ayimɛɛ be doo ɔ wose man ya nɛ yele man.

eng: After they prayed, the place where they were gathered was shaken. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began speaking Gods word with confidence.
local: Ba ke fane taŋ keŋ nɛ, te botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ gyaŋee nɛ wosiwɔ te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon kaa gyoowɔ be popou man nideli te be gyɔɔ be konɔɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan.

eng: The community of believers was one in heart and mind. None of them would say, This is mine! about any of their possessions, but held everything in common.
local: Balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ lɛɛ Kristo Yesu di nɛ, kyaawɔ nɔɔdokoloŋ man te ba agyueŋ mɔ yɛɛ dokoloŋ. Ŋolo bɛɛ yako nyi ɔ kolo yɛɛ waageŋ wui ya, mɔna be kpɛlɛɛ ba abɔɔ pou fa dɔŋa.

eng: The apostles continued to bear powerful witness to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and an abundance of grace was at work among them all.
local: Yesu kpilala baŋ moo Wurubuarɛ doŋ dinaa keŋ di adansɛɛ nyi, de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu te fom lee yeŋ man, te Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele baŋ pou bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di nɛ.

eng: There were no needy persons among them. Those who owned properties or houses would sell them, bring the proceeds from the sales.
local: Kolo te hia be man ŋolo ya, nawolo nyi baŋ be dana denɛɛ na tɛɛlese nɛ yɔɔ ŋa na bɛɛ mo kɔba keŋ bɔɔ nyim nɛ.

eng: and place them in the care and under the authority of the apostles. Then it was distributed to anyone who was in need.
local: kɔna Yesu kpilala baŋ na be ta mo kpala fa wɔ. Bɛɛ fa walaŋ kamasɛ ŋgba mena keŋ ne hia e nɛ.

eng: Joseph, whom the apostles nicknamed Barnabas , was a Levite from Cyprus.
local: Mena ke te Lewi baale ŋolo ŋon ɔ ba lee Saiporɔse tɛɛle man, te ɔ yele yɛna Gyosɛfo nɛ yɛɛwɔ. Yesu kpilala baŋ doo e yele nyi Banabase, keŋ nawolo nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ na do balaŋ kakyeŋ.

eng: He owned a field, sold it, brought the money, and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: Ŋon mɔ ka moo ɔ tɛɛle keŋ ɔ dana nɛ yɔɔ, te ɔ moo kɔba keŋ ɔ be nyim nɛ kaa fa Yesu kpilala baŋ.

eng: However, a man named Ananias, along with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property.
local: Balee ŋolo kyaawɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake e nyi Ananiase, na ɔ wɛɛle mɔ yele yɛna Safira. Baŋ mɔ gyuuwɔ ka mo ba agyapadɛɛ daale yɔɔ.

eng: With his wifes knowledge, he withheld some of the proceeds from the sale. He brought the rest and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: Ŋon na ɔ wɛɛle yɛɛ nɔɔ te ba moo kɔba keŋ bɔɔ nyim nɛ man ŋaale weese, te baale ŋon moo akaŋ keŋ gyu kefa Yesu kpilala baŋ.

eng: Peter asked, Ananias, how is it that Satan has influenced you to lie to the Holy Spirit by withholding some of the proceeds from the sale of your land?.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita bɔɔse e nyi, Ananiase, weera dɔɔ te n yela ɔbɔnsam dii n dɔɔ mena, te ɔ yela n dii dekara fa Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon, te n moo n kɔba keŋ n be nyim lee agyapadɛɛ keŋ n be yɔɔ nɛ man ŋaale weese?.

eng: Wasnt that property yours to keep? After you sold it, wasnt the money yours to do with whatever you wanted? What made you think of such a thing? You havent lied to other people but to God.
local: Agyapadɛɛ keŋ te yɛɛ n wui pɛte n yɔɔ ke yee? N yɔɔ ke taŋ mɔna kɔba keŋ n be nyim nɛ te yɛɛ n wui yee? Nɛnɛɛ weera n bakewɔ n konɔɔ man te n yɛɛ nombiikum kei mena? Na denibalaŋ te n dii dekara fa ya, Wurubuarɛ te n dii dekara fa.

eng: When Ananias heard these words, he dropped dead. Everyone who heard this conversation was terrified.
local: Ananiase ka betaa nyii nombia kɛŋa pɛ, te kara e lɔ te ɔ yeŋawɔ. Mena dɔɔ gyakoloŋ gyoo baŋ pou bɔɔ nyii nombia ŋenaŋ nɛ.

eng: Some young men stood up, wrapped up his body, carried him out, and buried him.
local: Keŋte tewulɛɛ kɔŋawɔ kaa mo kolo milii e, te be gate e lee gyu kewuu.

eng: About three hours later, his wife entered, but she didnt know what had happened to her husband.
local: Te kyare ya te ɔ wɛɛle ŋon mɔ kaa gyoowɔ botɔɔ mɔna ŋon ba gyeŋ kpene keŋ be kɔŋ ya.

eng: Peter asked her, Tell me, did you and your husband receive this price for the field? She responded, Yes, thats the amount.
local: Te Pita bɔɔse alo ŋon nyiaa, Yako maŋ, kɔba keŋ ɛmɛɛ na n baale Ananiase be nyim lee agyapadɛɛ keŋ keyɔɔ man pou yaa ɔ moowɔ kɔŋ nɛ? Te ɔ tiranɔɔ nyi ayi, mena te de yɔɔ ke.

eng: He replied, How could you scheme with each other to challenge the Lords Spirit? Look! The feet of those who buried your husband are at the door. They will carry you out too.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita bɔɔse e nyi, Weera te ɛmɛɛ na n baale yɛɛ nɔɔ nyi ɛ kekɛɛse de Gbeŋgyoo feliŋ ŋon kɛɛ! Kɛɛ, balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ moo n baale kewuu nɛ, baŋ seŋɛɛ la denanɔɔ man nɛ, baa seele nyaŋ mɔ lee.

eng: At that very moment, she dropped dead at his feet. When the young men entered and found her dead, they carried her out and buried her with her husband.
local: Karatetee dokoloŋ te kara ŋon mɔ lɔ Pita nawɔɔ tɛɛ te ɔ yeŋawɔ. Tewulɛɛ baŋ kekɔŋ kaa gyoo te ba kena nyi ŋon mɔ ta yem nɛ, te ba mo e lee gyu kewuu ɔ baale yenaŋ.

eng: Trepidation and dread seized the whole church and all who heard what had happened.
local: Mena dɔɔ gyakoloŋ dinaa daale gyoo Kristotena dikpii keŋ pou, na baŋ pou bɔɔ nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ.

eng: The apostles performed many signs and wonders among the people. They would come together regularly at Solomons Porch.
local: Yesu kpilala baŋ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana burum balaŋ baŋ man. Te baŋ pou bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu Kristo di nɛ, ne gyu ke yilaa Solomɔn poto keŋ man.

eng: No one from outside the church dared to join them, even though the people spoke highly of them.
local: Na balaŋ ne bu wɔ nideli, mɔna balaŋ yenaasetena baŋ yee mena keŋ baa kaa kpu na wɔ.

eng: Indeed, more and more believers in the Lord, large numbers of both men and women, were added to the church.
local: Mɔna ala na baala burum lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu di, te Wurubuarɛ moo wɔ kaa kpu na Kristotena baŋ.

eng: As a result, they would even bring the sick out into the main streets and lay them on cots and mats so that at least Peters shadow could fall on some of them as he passed by.
local: Lee gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan Yesu kpilala baŋ ne yɛɛ dɔɔ, balaŋ na mo be kaweesetena kaa doosi afoŋoŋse na mpase dɔɔ gbɛɛnaa nɔɔman, na akpaa Pita de kɔŋ ɔ ne lam na ɔ wɔŋtaa ke yala be man baale dɔɔ, na baa nyiŋ kawee kekyɔ.

eng: Even large numbers of persons from towns around Jerusalem would gather, bringing the sick and those harassed by unclean spirits. Everyone was healed.
local: Balaŋ lee donɔɔse ŋan benaa na Gyerusalɛm nɛ kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ keŋ man, te ba moo be kaweesetena na baŋ feliŋkumɛɛ ne naase wɔ diyem nɛ kɔŋ te be kyɔɔ wɔ pou.

eng: The high priest, together with his allies, the Sadducees, was overcome with jealousy.
local: Mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia ŋon, na ɔ kegyiise na baŋ bɔɔ lee Sadukiitena dikpii man nɛ sia yɛɛwɔ Yesu kpilala baŋ dɔɔ, te be bakewɔ see nyi baa gyae kolo yɛɛ wɔ.

eng: They seized the apostles and made a public show of putting them in prison.
local: Mena dɔɔ be kyaŋa Yesu kpilala baŋ te ba moo wɔ ketɔ deni.

eng: An angel from the Lord opened the prison doors during the night and led them out. The angel told them.
local: Mɔna ke nelim nɛ, de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpilale kɔŋawɔ kaa toro deni keŋ fa wɔ, te ɔ moo wɔ lee deni keŋ man, te ɔ yako wɔ nyi.

eng: Go, take your place in the temple, and tell the people everything about this new life.
local: Ɛ gyu ka seŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man na ɛ kekolosi nyeedoŋ wɔle kei wose man nombia fa balaŋ baŋ pou.

eng: Early in the morning, they went into the temple as they had been told and began to teach. When the high priest and his colleagues gathered, they convened the Jerusalem Council, that is, the full assembly of Israels elders. They sent word to the prison to have the apostles brought before them.
local: Yesu Kristo kpilala baŋ yɛɛwɔ ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon be yako nɛ, mena dɔɔ nebo nebo fuu te be gyuuwɔ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man, te be fiasɛɛ bɛɛ wola balaŋ baŋ. Wurubuarɛ Sae kegyia ŋon na ɔ kegyiise baake Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ pou, te be gyaŋeewɔ te be kpilawɔ nyi be ka mo Yesu kpilala baŋ lee deni keŋ man kɔŋ.

eng: However, the guards didnt find them in the prison. They returned and reported.
local: Mɔna balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ kpili nɛ gyuuwɔ kelii botɔɔ na, ba te naa Yesu kpilala baŋ deni keŋ man ya. Mena dɔɔ ba besewɔ kɔŋ kaa yako kegyiise baŋ nyi.

eng: We found the prison locked and well-secured, with guards standing at the doors, but when we opened the doors we found no one inside.
local: De gyuu botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ kyaŋ wɔ do nɛ na disim keŋ tɔɛ kiiŋ, na baŋ mɔ bee gyee ke nɛ seŋɛɛ be daseŋae man. Mɔna da toro disim keŋ na, da te naa Yesu kpilala baŋ deni keŋ man ya.

eng: When they received this news, the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests were baffled and wondered what might be happening.
