local: Debaŋ keŋ kegyia ŋon ɔ ne kɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ gyee Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ dɔɔ nɛ, na saese kegyiise baŋ be nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ta seŋ wɔ nideli ya. Te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyi, Na woŋ yɛna tewulɛɛ kɛwɔ baa.

eng: Just then, someone arrived and announced, Look! The people you put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people.
local: Botɔɔ te balee ŋolo kɔŋawɔ kaa yako wɔ nyi, Ɛ nyii, balaŋ baŋ ɛ be kyam do deni man nɛ, seŋɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man bɛɛ wola balaŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia.

eng: Then the captain left with his guards and brought the apostles back. They didnt use force because they were afraid the people would stone them.
local: Mena dɔɔ kegyia ŋon ɔ ne kɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bee gyee Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ dɔɔ nɛ, na ɔ tewulɛɛ gyuuwɔ ka moo wɔ kɔŋ kegyiise baŋ gyaŋ, mɔna be ta mo wɔ doŋ man ya, nawolo nyi bee yee nyi balaŋ baŋ baa fuŋii wɔ boya.

eng: The apostles were brought before the council where the high priest confronted them.
local: Be ka mo wɔ kɔŋ nombiadiira baŋ siaman nɛ, te sae kegyia ŋon yako wɔ.

eng: In no uncertain terms, we demanded that you not teach in this name. And look at you! You have filled Jerusalem with your teaching. And you are determined to hold us responsible for this mans death.
local: nyi, De faree ŋon nyi ɛ na wola kolo lee Yesu yele man ya, mɔna ɛ kɛɛ ya aneyɛɛŋ, ɛ ta mo ɔ nombia ŋan ɛ na wolo nɛ wulu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ pou, te ɛ ne gyae ɛ ka mo ɔ yeŋ seele daa.

eng: Peter and the apostles replied, We must obey God rather than humans!.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita na Yesu kpilala akaŋ baŋ tiranɔɔ nyi, Kaboena nyi dee nyii Wurubuarɛ dei kela balaŋ wui.

eng: The God of our ancestors raised Jesus from the dead—whom you killed by hanging him on a tree.
local: De naanaɔ Wurubuarɛ ŋon gyuusu na Yesu ŋon ɛ be gyɔɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ ko e nɛ lee yeŋ man.

eng: God has exalted Jesus to his right side as leader and savior so that he could enable Israel to change its heart and life and to find forgiveness for sins.
local: Wurubuarɛ gyina e adido te ɔ mo e see ɔ dunoluŋ dɔɔ, te ɔ moo e yɛɛ siamante na nyeelɛɛre, na waa yela Iseraetena ke nyiŋ gbɛɛ kyɛɛkee lee be nombiakumɛɛ man na baa kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ, na waa mo be nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ.

eng: We are witnesses of such things, as is the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to those who obey him.
local: Daa ne diina nombia kɛŋa adansɛɛ. Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ɔ ba mo do baŋ bee nyii ɔ dei man nɛ, mɔ ne di nombia kɛŋa adansɛɛ.

eng: When the council members heard this, they became furious and wanted to kill the apostles.
local: Kegyiise baŋ bɔɔ gyaŋee nɛ nyii nombia kɛŋa na ba gyeŋa baŋ nideli, mena dɔɔ be gyaewɔ nyi baa ko Yesu kpilala baŋ.

eng: One council member, a Pharisee and teacher of the Law named Gamaliel, well-respected by all the people, stood up and ordered that the men be taken outside for a few moments.
local: Mɔna be man ŋolo, ŋon ɔ doo Farasiitena dikpii keŋ man te bɛɛ baake e nyi Gamaleli, te ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ mmaraa keŋ wolale e, te ɔ wose man dana obuo donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ, korowɔ seŋ be man. Botɔɔ te ɔ yela ba lese Yesu kpilala baŋ yenaŋ.

eng: He said, Fellow Israelites, consider carefully what you intend to do to these people.
local: Te ɔ yako kegyiise baŋ nyi, Me tebia Iseraetena, ɛ kɛɛ kpene keŋ ɛ ne gyae ɛ ke yɛɛ balaŋ kɛwɔ nɛ nideli.

eng: Some time ago, Theudas appeared, claiming to be somebody, and some four hundred men joined him. After he was killed, all of his followers scattered, and nothing came of that.
local: Debaŋ duguŋ daale kaa laŋawɔ na, balee ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Teudase nɛ korowɔ seŋ te ɔ yɛɛ ɔ wose ŋgba walaŋ dinaa ŋolo nɛ, te balaŋ sɔŋonoŋ akpeŋ anaara korowɔ sila e. Mɔna be ka ko e na ɔ balaŋ baŋ pou mɔ yaaseewɔ, keŋte ɔ kawola keŋ pou mɔ kaa yɛɛwɔ yakaa.

eng: Afterward, at the time of the census, Judas the Galilean appeared and got some people to follow him in a revolt. He was killed too, and all his followers scattered far and wide.
local: Kei mɔ wɔle debaŋ keŋ bɛɛ kala balaŋ nɛ, te Galelia baale Gyuda korowɔ seŋ, te ŋon mɔ kaa beo balaŋ burum sila ɔ wose. Be ka ko ŋon mɔ nɛ, ɔ balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ sila e nɛ yaaseewɔ.

eng: Heres my recommendation in this case: Distance yourselves from these men. Let them go! If their plan or activity is of human origin, it will end in ruin.
local: Mena dɔɔ nombia kɛŋa man nɛ, mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi ɛ na yɛɛ balaŋ kɛwɔ kolo ya. Ɛ yela baa ta, nawolo nyi akpaa kpene keŋ bɛɛ wolo te bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ, lee deniwalaŋ gyaŋ na, keŋ mɔ gyae ke yɛɛ yakaa bo mena.

eng: If it originates with God, you wont be able to stop them. Instead, you would actually find yourselves fighting God! The council was convinced by his reasoning.
local: Mɔna akpaa ka lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ na, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale tɔ wɔ gbɛɛ ya. Ɛ kena bo nyi ɛ ne yoo bo na Wurubuarɛ. Mena dɔɔ kegyiise baŋ mɔ yɛɛwɔ ŋgba mena keŋ Gamaleli be yako wɔ nɛ.

eng: After calling the apostles back, they had them beaten. They ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, then let them go.
local: Mena dɔɔ be baake Yesu kpilala baŋ kɔŋ te be yela be tuku wɔ, te be tɔɔ wɔ nyi be na kolosi kolo lee Yesu yele man bela ya. Te be tina wɔ yela.

eng: The apostles left the council rejoicing because they had been regarded as worthy to suffer disgrace for the sake of the name.
local: Yesu kpilala baŋ ka lee kegyiise baŋ gyaŋ nɛ na be sia ta gye, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ te kɛɛ wɔ nyi baŋ mɔ kaboena nyi baa do wɔ desɛŋ lee Yesu dɔɔ.

eng: Every day they continued to teach and proclaim the good news that Jesus is the Christ, both in the temple and in houses.
local: Te wee kamasɛ na, bee gyu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa man na balaŋ akpaŋalaŋse man. Bɛɛ wola nombia kpaakpaa ŋan nyi Yesu yɛna Kristo, nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ. Te be te tina nombia kɛŋa kawola yela ya.

eng: About that time, while the number of disciples continued to increase, a complaint arose. Greek-speaking disciples accused the Aramaic-speaking disciples because their widows were being overlooked in the daily food service.
local: Weeya ala ŋenaŋ man na baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di nɛ korowɔ bee wuku te kekolosi kaa gyoo Gyudatena baŋ bɛɛ kolosi Giriki dei, na Gyudatena akaŋ baŋ man. Gyudatena baŋ bɛɛ kolosi Giriki dei nɛ, kpa Gyudatena akaŋ baŋ ne wuritii wɔ weenɛɛ kekpala man, nawolo nyi be kpa wee kamasɛ weenɛɛ ŋan bɛɛ kpɛlɛɛ nɛ, bee tuŋ baŋ wui liala baŋ ya.

eng: The high priest asked, Are these accusations true.
local: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia ŋon bɔɔse Sitifin nyi, Mena dɔɔ nombia ba ayakoŋ kɛŋa pou yɛɛ ampaŋ.

eng: The Twelve called a meeting of all the disciples and said, It isnt right for us to set aside proclamation of Gods word in order to serve tables.
local: Keŋte Yesu kpilala kufu bala baŋ baake baŋ pou bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di nɛ gyaŋee, te be yako wɔ nyiaa, Tekaboena nyi daa dɛɛ bese tina Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan keyako yela, na dɛɛ kɔŋ kaa kɛɛ abɔɔ kekpɛlɛɛ dɔɔ bela ya.

eng: Stephen responded, Brothers and fathers, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he settled in Haran.
local: Botɔɔ te Sitifin tiranɔɔ nyi, Me tebia na me kyaɔ, ɛ tei me gyaŋ. Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ne di gyoori nɛ lese na ɔ wose wola de naana Aberaham, debaŋ keŋ ɔ kyaa Mesopotamia tɛɛle man nɛ, kenaŋ wɔle pɛte ɔ korowɔ gyu kekyaa Haran donɔɔ man.

eng: Brothers and sisters, carefully choose seven well-respected men from among you. They must be well-respected and endowed by the Spirit with exceptional wisdom. We will put them in charge of this concern.
local: Mena dɔɔ de tebia, ɛ kɛɛ ɛ man na ɛ ka lese baala nyetooro baŋ ba wose man dana obuo, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon doo be man nideli te be sia be kare mɔ nɛ, na dɛɛ mo mena tom kei do be nyiŋmaa man.

eng: God told him, Leave your homeland and kin, and go to the land that I will show you.
local: botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ yako e nyi, Koro lee n tɛɛle man na n loŋtona gyaŋ, na n ke tu gyu ke kyaa tɛɛle keŋ maa wola neŋ nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: As for us, we will devote ourselves to prayer and the service of proclaiming the word.
local: Na daa ke nyiŋ mo de doŋ pou do kefane, na Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan keyako man.

eng: So Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After Abrahams father died, God had him resettle in this land where you now live.
local: Mena dɔɔ Aberaham korowɔ lee Kaldea tɛɛle man, te ɔ gyuuwɔ kekyaa Haran donɔɔ man. Ɔ kya yeŋ wɔle nɛ, Wurubuarɛ moo e kaa see tɛɛle ya akyaŋ ke dɔɔ kei dɔɔ.

eng: This proposal pleased the entire community. They selected Stephen, a man endowed by the Holy Spirit with exceptional faith, Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus from Antioch, a convert to Judaism.
local: Nombia naŋ Yesu kaseela baŋ be yako nɛ, seŋa balaŋ baŋ pou nideli. Te ba lese Sitifin ŋon ɔ ne lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di nideli te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ doo ɔ man nideli nɛ. Te ba besewɔ lese Filipo na Prokoro na Nikanɔ na Timɔn na Pamena na Nikolase ŋon ɔ ba lee Antiɔke te ɔ ba mo ɔ wose kpu Gyuda ɔsom keŋ man nɛ.

eng: God didnt give him an inheritance here, not even a square foot of land. However, God did promise to give the land as his possession to him and to his descendants, even though Abraham had no child.
local: Tɛɛle naetɔɔ dokoloŋ koraŋ ɔ te fa e botɔɔ nyi dɛɛ bese ŋon gbagba ɔ wui ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ yakowɔ see nyi, waa mo tɛɛle kenaŋ fa ŋon na ɔ duuluŋ man balaŋ. Debaŋ kenaŋ mɔ na Aberaham be dana bu koraŋ gba ya.

eng: The community presented these seven to the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.
local: Keŋte ba moo balaŋ nyetooro kɛwɔ kaa seŋ Yesu kpilala baŋ siaman. Te ba moo be nyiŋmaase gyakaa be dɔɔ te be fanewɔ fa wɔ.

eng: God put it this way: His descendants will be strangers in a land that belongs to others, who will enslave them and abuse them for four hundred years. .
local: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ yako e nyi, N duuluŋ man balaŋ baa kyaa dei wɔle daale tɛɛle dɔɔ bese ŋɔɔla. Baa moo wɔ yɛɛ gbeŋa, na baa yɛɛ wɔ nyeemandoŋ na akpanyaŋnyaŋ kulutooneŋ sɔŋonoŋ akpeŋ anaara .

eng: Gods word continued to grow. The number of disciples in Jerusalem increased significantly. Even a large group of priests embraced the faith.
local: Mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan yaaseewɔ bileŋbileŋ, te baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di nɛ wukuwɔ burum Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ keŋ man. Te Wurubuarɛ saese burum baale mɔ lɛɛ Yesu di.

eng: And I will condemn the nation they serve as slaves, God said, and afterward they will leave that land and serve me in this place.
local: Mɔna maa wɔŋ tɛɛle kenaŋ mantena baŋ baa mo n duuluŋ man balaŋ baŋ yɛɛ gbeŋa nɛ deŋele. Kenaŋ wɔle na, n duuluŋ man balaŋ baŋ baa lee tɛɛle kenaŋ man, na be kaa som maŋ kɛbo kei.

eng: Stephen, who stood out among the believers for the way Gods grace was at work in his life and for his exceptional endowment with divine power, was doing great wonders and signs among the people.
local: Te Sitifin ŋon Wurubuarɛ ba mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele e nideli, te ɔ doŋ doo ɔ man nɛ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana balaŋ baŋ man.

eng: God gave him the covenant confirmed through circumcision. Accordingly, eight days after Isaacs birth, Abraham circumcised him. Isaac did the same with Jacob, and Jacob with the twelve patriarchs.
local: Te Wurubuarɛ na Aberaham bakewɔ see nyi, ŋon na ɔ duuluŋ man balaŋ pou baa kara ba baala wose. Mena dɔɔ Aberaham ka lola ɔ bu Asiki weeya gyanaarate nɛ te ɔ kara ɔ baala wose. Kenaŋ wɔle Asiki mɔ kaa lola Gyekɔpo, te Gyekɔpo mɔ lola de naanaɔ kufu bala baŋ.

eng: Opposition arose from some who belonged to the so-called Synagogue of Former Slaves. Members from Cyrene, Alexandria, Cilicia, and Asia entered into debate with Stephen.
local: Mɔna Gyudatena baale baŋ bɛɛ gyaŋee Gyudatena ɔsom deni keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi gbeŋa baŋ bɔɔ tina wɔ yela nɛ ɔsom deni man nɛ, fiasɛɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ na Sitifin. Balaŋ benaŋ baale lee Kirene na Alɛkesanderia na Silisia na Esia tɛɛlese man.

eng: Because the patriarchs were jealous of Joseph, they sold him into slavery in Egypt. God was with him, however.
local: Gyosɛfo mɔ yɛɛ de naanaɔ kufu bala baŋ man ŋolo e. Keŋ ɔ kegyiise ne kɔla e dɔɔ nɛ te ba mo e yɔɔ te ɔ ka bese gbeŋi Igyipite tɛɛle man, mɔna Wurubuarɛ seŋawɔ ɔ wɔle.

eng: However, they couldnt resist the wisdom the Spirit gave him as he spoke.
local: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon faa Sitifin nyansa te ɔ kolosiwɔ nideli mena dɔɔ be te nyiŋ keŋ baa yako bela ya.

eng: and rescued him from all his troubles. The grace and wisdom he gave Joseph were recognized by Pharaoh, king of Egypt, who appointed him ruler over Egypt and over his whole palace.
local: te ɔ lese e lee ɔ diyem pou man. Te ɔ fa e nyansa, te ɔ yela Igyipitetena gyoo Faaro gyae ɔ nombia, mena dɔɔ gyoo ŋon moo ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ na Igyipite tɛɛle keŋ pou do ŋon Gyosɛfo nyiŋmaa man nyi waa kɛɛ ŋe dɔɔ.

eng: Then they secretly enticed some people to claim, We heard him insult Moses and God.
local: Keŋ dɔɔ be gyuuwɔ ke feefu do balaŋ baale nyi baa di dekara gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ nyi, De nyiiwɔ ɔ ne kolosi mmusuo nombia gyakaa de naana Mosesi na Wurubuarɛ dɔɔ.

eng: A famine came upon all Egypt and Canaan, and great hardship came with it. Our ancestors had nothing to eat.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ te tanam dinaa daale kaa yalawɔ Igyipite na Kaanan tɛɛlese pou dɔɔ, te ka moo kaale dinaa mɔ kaa lɔ be dɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ de naanaɔ baŋ te nyiŋ weenɛɛ baa di ya.

eng: They stirred up the people, the elders, and the legal experts. They caught Stephen, dragged him away, and brought him before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Nnɛ te be wɔlɛɛ e fa balaŋ baŋ na ɔsom kegyiise na mmaraa wolala baŋ pou, te ba korowɔ seŋ ɔ dɔɔ te ba kyaŋa e gyu donɔɔ kegyiise siaman.

eng: When Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
local: Debaŋ keŋ Gyekɔpo be nyii nyi weenɛɛ kyaa Igyipite nɛ, te ɔ kpila ɔ bia de naanaɔ baŋ botɔɔ.

eng: Before the council, they presented false witnesses who testified, This man never stops speaking against this holy place and the Law.
local: Mena dɔɔ be gyae balaŋ baale kɔŋ, te be kaa dii akɛrɛɛ gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ nyi, Debaŋ kamasɛ na balee kei ne kolosi bo tia de Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ, na Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan Mosesi ba wola daa nɛ.

eng: During their second visit, Joseph told his brothers who he was, and Pharaoh learned about Josephs family.
local: Be ka bese gyu Igyipite bolate nɛ, Gyosɛfo lese ɔ wose wola ɔ kegyiise baŋ, te gyoo Faaro mɔ kaa gyeŋa Gyosɛfo ɔ loŋtona baŋ.

eng: In fact, we heard him say that this man Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and alter the customary practices Moses gave us.
local: Nawolo nyi de nyiiwɔ ŋgba ɔ ne yako nyi, Yesu ŋon ɔ ba lee Nasarɛte nɛ, waa yɛlɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa kei lɔ, na waa kyɛɛkee abɔɔ ŋan pou Mosesi ba mo see daa nɛ.

eng: Joseph sent for his father Jacob and all his relatives—seventy-five in all—and invited them to live with him.
local: Mena dɔɔ Gyosɛfo doo ɔ kya Gyekɔpo tiila nyi ŋon na ɔ balaŋ pou baa tu kɔŋ Igyipite. Be yaanɔɔle pou yɛɛ balaŋ sɔŋotooro na kufu banoŋ .

eng: Everyone seated in the council stared at Stephen, and they saw that his face was radiant, just like an angels.
local: Te nombiadiira baŋ be kyaa deni keŋ man nɛ, kɛɛ Sitifin nɛ yididi te be naawɔ ŋgba ɔ sia gbɛɛ te kyɛɛkee yɛɛ ŋgba Wurubuarɛ kpilale sia gbɛɛ nɛ.

eng: So Jacob went down to Egypt, where he and our ancestors died.
local: Mena dɔɔ Gyekɔpo gyuuwɔ ke kyaa Igyipite, te ŋon na ɔ bia pou yekeewɔ botɔɔ.

eng: Their bodies were brought back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had purchased for a certain sum of money from Hamors children, who lived in Shechem.
local: Ba besena ba woya baŋ kɔŋ Sekyɛm donɔɔ man, te be kaa wuu wɔ boe bɔɔ keŋ Aberaham be kala kɔba lɛɛ lee Hamɔ bia gyaŋ nɛ man.

eng: When it was time for God to keep the promise he made to Abraham, the number of our people in Egypt had greatly expanded.
local: Debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombii ayakoŋ see fa Aberaham keŋ na benaa kekɔŋ ampaŋ nɛ, te de balaŋ baŋ be kyaa Igyipite nɛ wukuwɔ nideli.

eng: But then another king rose to power over Egypt who didnt know anything about Joseph. .
local: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, gyoo wɔle ŋolo ŋon ɔ ba gyeŋ kolo lee Gyosɛfo wose man ya nɛ, kaa dii gyoori Igyipite tɛɛle keŋ dɔɔ.

eng: He exploited our people and abused our ancestors. He even forced them to abandon their newly born babies so they would die.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔ yɛɛ de naanaɔ baŋ nyeemandoŋ na akpanyaŋnyaŋ, te ɔ ŋere wɔ dekpaŋkpasare man nyi baa tirii be bia wɔlɛɛ leki na baa nyiŋ yeŋ.

eng: Thats when Moses was born. He was highly favored by God, and for three months his parents cared for him in their home.
local: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ man mɔ te ba lola Mosesi, mɔna ɔ te yɛɛ bu yakaate e Wurubuarɛ siaman ya. Ba weese e ɔ kya deni man te be kɛɛle e botɔɔ gyaalaŋna batooro.

eng: After he was abandoned, Pharaohs daughter adopted and cared for him as though he were her own son.
local: Lii debaŋ daale na, be tira e leki debɔɔ man, te gyoo Faaro bu alebu naa e mo ke kɛɛle ŋgba ŋon gbagba ɔ bu nɛ.

eng: Moses learned everything Egyptian wisdom had to offer, and he was a man of powerful words and deeds.
local: Ba wola e Igyipitetena nyansa pou. Te ɔ kaa bese walaŋ dinaa ɔ kekolosi na ɔ tom keyɛɛ pou man.

eng: When Moses was years old, he decided to visit his family, the Israelites.
local: Mosesi kedi kulutooneŋ sɔŋola nɛ, te kɔŋawɔ ɔ nyeeman nyi waa koro gyu ke kɛɛ ɔ tebia Iseraetena kekyaa man.

eng: He saw one of them being wronged so he came to his rescue and evened the score by killing the Egyptian.
local: Ɔ ke gyu keŋ nɛ, ɔ ke naawɔ ŋgba Igyipite baale ŋolo ne naase Iserae baale ŋolo diyem na, te ɔ gyuuwɔ ke lɛɛ ɔ dɛɛ baale ŋon nɔɔ te ɔ koo Igyipite baale ŋon.

eng: He expected his own kin to understand that God was using him to rescue them, but they didnt.
local: Mosesi agyueŋ man na, ŋon gyeŋ nyi ɔ balaŋ baŋ baa nyii asɛɛ nyi Wurubuarɛ waa ta ɔ dɔɔ bo lese wɔ lee be kaale man, mɔna be te nyii asɛɛ mena ya.

eng: The next day he came upon some Israelites who were caught up in an argument. He tried to make peace between them by saying, You are brothers! Why are you harming each other.
local: Ke tɛɛ naa nɛ, ɔ ke naa Iseraetena wolɛɛwolɛɛ bala mɔ ne yoo, te ɔ gyuuwɔ ɔ kaa pataa wɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Me tebia ɛ kɛɛ, ɛ wolɛɛwolɛɛ. Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne yɛɛ dɔŋa akpanyaŋnyaŋ mena.

eng: The one who started the fight against his neighbor pushed Moses aside and said, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? .
local: Mɔna ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ akpanyaŋnyaŋ nɛ tɛɛse Mosesi lee yenaŋ te ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, Amɔte moona neŋ yɛɛ de siamante na de nombiadiiri.

eng: Are you planning to kill me like you killed that Egyptian yesterday? .
local: N ne gyae baa n ka ko maŋ ŋgba mena keŋ dana n ba ko Igyipite baale ŋon nɛ.

eng: When Moses heard this, he fled to Midian, where he lived as an immigrant and had two sons.
local: Mosesi kenyii nombii kei nɛ te ɔ korowɔ yeu gyu ke kyaa Midian tɛɛle man bese ŋɔɔle botɔɔ. Botɔɔ te ɔ yalawɔ te ɔ lola bia baala bala.

eng: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses in the flame of a burning bush in the wilderness near Mount Sinai.
local: Kulutooneŋ sɔŋola wɔle nɛ, Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo kaa lese ɔ wose wola Mosesi, boalaŋ daale keŋ ne kpaa depoŋfa daale man depampaa nsana Sinai bula keŋ gyaŋ nɛ.

eng: Enthralled by the sight, Moses approached to get a closer look and he heard the Lords voice.
local: Mosesi naa ke na, gyakoloŋ kyaŋa e. Te ɔ tuuwɔ benaa ke na waa nyiŋ naa ke nideli. Botɔɔ te ɔ nyii de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ woya nyiaa.

eng: I am the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Trembling with fear, Moses didnt dare to investigate any further.
local: Maŋ yɛna n naanaɔ Aberaham na Asiki na Gyekɔpo Wurubuarɛ ŋon. Te gyakoloŋ gyoo Mosesi te ɔ sereewɔ, mena dɔɔ ɔ te tale kɛɛ ke bela ya.

eng: The Lord continued, Remove the sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy ground. .
local: Te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ yako e nyiaa, Kpee nyaŋ nkyokotase lee n naawɔ man, nawolo nyi maŋ Wurubuarɛ gbagba kyaa botɔɔ keŋ n seŋɛɛ nɛ.

eng: I have clearly seen the oppression my people have experienced in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning. I have come down to rescue them. Come! I am sending you to Egypt .
local: Ampaŋ, ma te naa me balaŋ baŋ be kyaa Igyipite nɛ kaale te me te nyii mena keŋ mɔ bɛɛ toroŋ, mena dɔɔ me te tisi lee adido kɔŋ me kaa lɛɛ wɔ. Desina n wose na maa kpila neŋ Igyipite.

eng: This is the same Moses whom they rejected when they asked, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? This is the Moses whom God sent as leader and deliverer. God did this with the help of the angel who appeared before him in the bush.
local: Mosesi kei nombia te be bɛɛwɔ, te be bɔɔse e nyi, Amɔte yɛɛ na neŋ de siamante, na de nombiadiiri. Wurubuarɛ tɛɛwɔ ɔ kpilale ŋon ɔ ba lese ɔ wose wola Mosesi depoŋfa keŋ man nɛ dɔɔ, yɛɛ e siamante na nyeelɛɛre.

eng: This man led them out after he performed wonders and signs in Egypt at the Red Sea and for forty years in the wilderness.
local: Mosesi kei yɛna gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana debaŋ keŋ ɔ ba mo Iseraetena lee Igyipite ta be siaman mo wɔ kaa toŋ ɛpo yayam keŋ, gyu ke ta depampaa dinaa keŋ man kulutooneŋ sɔŋola nɛ.

eng: This is the Moses who told the Israelites, God will raise up for you a prophet like me from your own people. .
local: Mosesi kei yako na Iseraetena nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ waa kpila ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋolo ɛ gyaŋ ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ. Waa lee bo ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ balaŋ man.

eng: This is the one who was in the assembly in the wilderness with our ancestors and with the angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai. He is the one who received life-giving words to give to us.
local: Mosesi kei na Iseraetena baŋ ke kyaa depampaa keŋ man nɛ, Wurubuarɛ kpilale kaa kolosiwɔ na e Sinai bula keŋ dɔɔ te ɔ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nyeedoŋ nombia ŋan fa de naanaɔ, te ŋe kaa tuŋa daa mɔ.

eng: Hes also the one whom our ancestors refused to obey. Instead, they pushed him aside and, in their thoughts and desires, returned to Egypt.
local: Mɔna de naanaɔ yɛɛ deŋele doŋ te be bɛɛ ɔ nombia ŋan, te be tɛɛse e see yenaŋ. Te baŋ be konɔɔse man na, nafɔ be gyaewɔ nyi baa bese gyu Igyipite.

eng: They told Aaron, Make us gods that will lead us. As for this Moses who led us out of Egypt, we dont know whats happened to him! .
local: Mena dɔɔ be yako Aarɔn nyi, Yɛɛ bɛɛneŋ fa daa na baa ta de siaman, nawolo nyi dɔɔ gyeŋ kpene keŋ be yɛɛ Mosesi ŋon ɔ ba mo daa lee Igyipite nɛ ya.

eng: Thats when they made an idol in the shape of a calf, offered a sacrifice to it, and began to celebrate what they had made with their own hands.
local: Weeya ŋenaŋ man te be yɛɛ nunkyue bu mo yɛɛ be bane, te be faa ke kedi te be dii be nɔɔ mo lese kpene keŋ baŋ gbagba ba mo be nyiŋmaase yɛɛ nɛ yele.

eng: So God turned away from them and handed them over to worship the stars in the sky, just as it is written in the scroll of the Prophets: Did you bring sacrifices and offerings to me for forty years in the wilderness, house of Israel? .
local: Mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ lese ɔ sia yela wɔ, te ɔ moo wɔ fa adido kyolobiise kasom, ŋgba mena keŋ bɔɔ ŋmarase do Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra tɔne keŋ man nɛ. Ba ŋolo ŋmarasewɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ kpa, O Iseraetena, na maŋ te ɛ koo wonembia deesi fa kulutooneŋ sɔŋola depampaa dinaa keŋ man ya.

eng: No! Instead, you took the tent of Moloch with you, and the star of your god Rephan, the images that you made in order to worship them. Therefore, I will send you far away, farther than Babylon. .
local: Ɛ bane Molɛki te ɛ yɛɛ kanyaŋ deni do ke, na ɛ bane Refan wulu keŋ ke yɛɛ ŋgba kyolobii nɛ, te ɛ moowɔ sila ɛ wose. Baŋ yɛna banɛɛ baŋ ɛ be yɛɛ nyi ɛ ka som! Mena dɔɔ maa yela baa mo ŋon gyu lemlem, naŋ Babilɔn donɔɔ keŋ wɔle.

eng: The tent of testimony was with our ancestors in the wilderness. Moses built it just as he had been instructed by the one who spoke to him and according to the pattern he had seen.
local: Depampaa dinaa keŋ man nɛ, na de naanaɔ dana kanyaŋ deni dinaa daale keŋ ne di wɔ adansɛɛ nyi Wurubuarɛ kyaa be gyaŋ. Be yɛɛ kanyaŋ deni dinaa kei ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba lese wola Mosesi nyi waa yɛɛ ke nɛ.

eng: In time, when they had received the tent, our ancestors carried it with them when, under Joshuas leadership, they took possession of the land from the nations whom God expelled. This tent remained in the land until the time of David.
local: De naanaɔ ke lɛɛ kanyaŋ deni dinaa kei lee be kyaɔ gyaŋ nɛ, ba moo ke sila be siamante Gyosua kelii tɛɛle keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yeli bɔɔ yoo gegi ke balaŋ te ɔ be lɛo ke fa wɔ nɛ man. Kanyaŋ deni dinaa kei doowɔ botɔɔ kelii debaŋ keŋ Defidi be kaa di gyoori nɛ.

eng: God approved of David, who asked that he might provide a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
local: Defidi kei nombia gyoo Wurubuarɛ sia, te ɔ yako Wurubuarɛ nyi waa fa e gbɛɛ na waa baŋ ɔsom deni dinaa gbagba fa ɔ naana Gyekɔpo Wurubuarɛ ŋon.

eng: But it was Solomon who actually built a house for God.
local: Mɔna ɔ bu Solomɔn kaa baŋa na ɔsom deni dinaa kenaŋ.

eng: However, the Most High doesnt live in houses built by human hands. As the prophet says.
local: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kela kpene kamasɛ nɛ, bɛɛ kyaa deni keŋ bɔɔ mo nyiŋmaase ma ke nɛ man ya. Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋolo yako mena nombii kei nyiaa.

eng: Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me, says the Lord, or where is my resting place? .
local: De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpa, Adido te me gyooro gbeli kyaa, te tɛɛle dɔɔ yɛna me nae dɛɛsee. Woŋ deni dinɔɔ yaa ɛ ke ma fa maŋ, yaa aleŋ te maa kyaa fɛɛfo?.

eng: Didnt I make all these things with my own hand? .
local: Na maŋ gbagba yɛɛna abɔɔ kɛŋa pou yee? .

eng: You stubborn people! In your thoughts and hearing, you are like those who have had no part in Gods covenant! You continuously set yourself against the Holy Spirit, just like your ancestors did.
local: Te Sitifin yako wɔ bela nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ konɔɔyuŋtena, ɛ dana konɔɔyuŋ ŋgba balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ ne nyii Wurubuarɛ dei ya nɛ, te ɛ ne yɛɛ deŋele doŋ ŋgba ɛ kyaɔ nɛ. Nawolo nyi debaŋ kamasɛ na ɛ na koro seŋ tia Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon.

eng: Was there a single prophet your ancestors didnt harass? They even killed those who predicted the coming of the righteous one, and youve betrayed and murdered him!.
local: Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋolo kyaawɔ keŋ ɛ naanaɔ te naase e diyem yee? Ba koo Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ bɔɔ wula yako see nyi Yesu Kristo ŋon ɔ nombia tenɛɛ nɛ waa kɔŋ nɛ, te nɛnɛɛ ɛ lese Yesu ŋonaŋ fa te ba koo e.

eng: You received the Law given by angels, but you havent kept it.
local: Ɛmɛɛ yɛna baŋ ɛ be nyiŋ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan lee ɔ kpilala gyaŋ, mɔna ɛ te di ŋe dɔɔ ya.

eng: Once the council members heard these words, they were enraged and began to grind their teeth at Stephen.
local: Debaŋ keŋ donɔɔ kegyiise baŋ be nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, be sia yɛɛwɔ ɔ dɔɔ te ba gyeŋa baŋ nideli.

eng: But Stephen, enabled by the Holy Spirit, stared into heaven and saw Gods majesty and Jesus standing at Gods right side.
local: Mɔna Sitifin ŋon Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon doo ɔ man nideli nɛ kɛɛ adido na, te ɔ naa Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ, na Yesu mɔ seŋɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dunoluŋ dɔɔ.

eng: He exclaimed, Look! I can see heaven on display and the Human One standing at Gods right side.
local: Te Sitifin yakowɔ nyiaa, Ɛ kɛɛ, mɛɛ naa ŋgba adido ta toro, na Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon seŋɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dunoluŋ dɔɔ.

eng: At this, they shrieked and covered their ears. Together, they charged at him.
local: Ɔ keyako mena nɛ te kegyiise baŋ moo be nyiŋmaase tɔ ba deŋela, te be fae yeesa keŋkeŋ. Karatetee te be popou kaa wuuwɔ ɔ dɔɔ.

eng: threw him out of the city, and began to stone him. The witnesses placed their coats in the care of a young man named Saul.
local: te be wɔŋee e lee donɔɔ keŋ man ke fuŋii e boya. Baŋ bɔɔ gyu be kaa fuŋii e boya nɛ, kpee ba kegba wurikyimse ŋan fa tobaale ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Sɔɔl nɛ.

eng: As they battered him with stones, Stephen prayed, Lord Jesus, accept my life.
local: Be kee fuŋii Sitifin boya ŋan nɛ, na ɔ ne fane nyi, Me Gbeŋgyoo Yesu, lɛɛ ma woo.

eng: Falling to his knees, he shouted, Lord, dont hold this sin against them! Then he died.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ ŋmiŋawɔ ɔ doona dɔɔ te ɔ fae yeesa nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo Yesu, na mo nombiakumɛɛ kɛŋa tɔ wɔ kom ya. Ɔ kekolosi nombia kɛŋa taŋ pɛ, te ɔ kara weesi. Be kɛɛ ko Sitifin na Sɔɔl mɔ seŋɛɛ botɔɔ ɔ na do wɔ kakyeŋ.

eng: Saul was in full agreement with Stephens murder. At that time, the church in Jerusalem began to be subjected to vicious harassment. Everyone except the apostles was scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria.
local: Lee wee kenaŋ te be fiasɛɛ bɛɛ naase Kristotena dikpii keŋ diyem nideli Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man. Mena dɔɔ Kristotena baŋ pou yaaseewɔ laŋ Gyudia na Samaria tɛɛlese man, kaase kpilala baŋ baageŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man.

eng: Some pious men buried Stephen and deeply grieved over him.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ bee yee Wurubuarɛ nɛ, baale gyuuwɔ kewuu Sitifin te be wii ɔ lia nideli.

eng: Saul began to wreak havoc against the church. Entering one house after another, he would drag off both men and women and throw them into prison.
local: Mɔna Sɔɔl korowɔ seŋ ɔ ne naase Kristotena baŋ diyem nideli. Ɔ na gyoo akpaŋalaŋse man ke wɔŋee Kristotena ala na baala gyu ketɔ deni.

eng: Those who had been scattered moved on, preaching the good news along the way.
local: Yenaŋ kamasɛ keŋ Kristotena baŋ be yaasee gyu nɛ, be kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan fa balaŋ.

eng: Philip went down to a city in Samaria and began to preach Christ to them.
local: Te Filipo gyuu Samariatena donɔɔ dinaa daale man keyako Wurubuarɛ nombia nyi, Yesu Kristo yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: The crowds were united by what they heard Philip say and the signs they saw him perform, and they gave him their undivided attention.
local: Debaŋ keŋ balaŋ burum baŋ be nyii nombia ŋan Filipo be yako, te bɔɔ naa gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ, be tei ɔ nombia ŋan nideli.

eng: With loud shrieks, unclean spirits came out of many people, and many who were paralyzed or crippled were healed.
local: Filipo kekolosi keŋ nɛ, balaŋ baŋ feliŋkumɛɛ doo be man nɛ fae yeesa keŋkeŋ, te feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan leewɔ be man. Te baŋ mɔ be baamense ba yekee, na ɔtakasese burum mɔ wose yɛɛ wɔ doŋ.

eng: There was great rejoicing in that city.
local: Mena dɔɔ yɛɛ donɔɔ kenaŋ mantena baŋ pou gyoŋ nideli.

eng: Before Philips arrival, a certain man named Simon had practiced sorcery in that city and baffled the people of Samaria. He claimed to be a great person.
local: Donɔɔ kenaŋ man na balee ŋolo kyaa botɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake e nyi Simɔn. Ɔ ne yɛɛ baalabiiri nombia wola Samariatena, keŋ nyi ba wose na gbirisi wɔ. Ɔ ne gyina ɔ wose adido wola ɔ wose nyi ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ dinaa ŋolo e.

eng: Everyone, from the least to the greatest, gave him their undivided attention and referred to him as the power of God called Great.
local: Mena dɔɔ walaŋ kamasɛ, bia na kegyiise pou ne tei e te bɛɛ yako nyi, Ŋon yɛna Wurubuarɛ doŋ keŋ bɛɛ baake nyi doŋ dinaa nɛ.

eng: He had their attention because he had baffled them with sorcery for a long time.
local: Ɔ ta mo ɔ baalabiiri nombia kɛŋa wola wɔ yela ba wose te gbirisi wɔ kyare, mena dɔɔ bɛɛ lɛɛ e di nideli.

eng: After they came to believe Philip, who preached the good news about Gods kingdom and the name of Jesus Christ, both men and women were baptized.
local: Mɔna Filipo ke yako wɔ Wurubuarɛ gyoori na Yesu Kristo wose man nombia ŋan nɛ be lɛɛwɔ di, te be sɔɔ ala na baala pou Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

eng: Even Simon himself came to believe and was baptized. Afterward, he became one of Philips supporters. As he saw firsthand the signs and great miracles that were happening, he was astonished.
local: Simɔn gbagba mɔ lɛɛ nombia ŋan di te be sɔɔ e Wurubuarɛ loŋ, te ɔ moo ɔ wose do na Filipo nideli. Te gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana ŋan ɔ be naa Filipo ne yɛɛ dɔɔ, ɔ wose gbirisi e.

eng: When word reached the apostles in Jerusalem that Samaria had accepted Gods word, they commissioned Peter and John to go to Samaria.
local: Debaŋ keŋ Yesu kpilala baŋ be kyaa Gyerusalɛm be nyii nyi Samariatena te lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan di nɛ, be kpila Pita na Gyɔn be gyaŋ.

eng: Peter and John went down to Samaria where they prayed that the new believers would receive the Holy Spirit. .
local: Ba kekɔŋ kaa gyoo na, be fanewɔ fa wɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do be man.

eng: This was because the Holy Spirit had not yet fallen on any of them; they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Nawolo nyi na Wurubuarɛ ta moo ta ɔ feliŋ ŋon do baŋ Samariatena benaŋ man ŋolo man ya. Be sɔɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ bo lee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu waageŋ yele man.

eng: So Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
local: Te Pita na Gyɔn moo be nyiŋmaase gyakaa be dɔɔ te be fanewɔ fa wɔ, te Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do be man.

eng: When Simon perceived that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles hands, he offered them money.
local: Simɔn naawɔ nyi Yesu kpilala baŋ ka mo be nyiŋmaase gyakaa balaŋ baŋ dɔɔ fane fa wɔ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do be man keŋte ɔ lese kɔba waa fa wɔ.

eng: He said, Give me this authority too so that anyone on whom I lay my hands will receive the Holy Spirit.
local: te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, Ɛ mo mena doŋ kei fa maŋ mɔ, na walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon maa mo me nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ na, Wurubuarɛ ka mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do ɔ man.

eng: Peter responded, May your money be condemned to hell along with you because you believed you could buy Gods gift with money!.
local: Te Pita yako e nyi, Wurubuarɛ waa yela ɛmɛɛ na n kɔba pou ka gyoo boalaŋ man. Nawolo nyi n te yɛɛ nya agyueŋ nyi n ka mo kɔba lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ kolo anefaŋ.

eng: You can have no part or share in Gods word because your heart isnt right with God.
local: N be dana dedii yaa daseŋae da tom kei man ya, nawolo nyi n konɔɔ man nombia ba tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman ya.

eng: Therefore, change your heart and life! Turn from your wickedness! Plead with the Lord in the hope that your wicked intent can be forgiven.
local: Kyɛɛkee nya agyueŋ lee n nombiikum kei man, na n kefane fa de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ na waa mo ke kyɛɛ neŋ.

eng: for I see that your bitterness has poisoned you and evil has you in chains.
local: Nawolo nyi ma te naa nyi n dana sibiikum, te nombiakumɛɛ te di n dɔɔ.

eng: Simon replied, All of you, please, plead to the Lord for me so that nothing of what you have said will happen to me.
local: Te Simɔn yako wɔ nyi, Mɛɛ waase ŋon bo ɛ fane fa de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ fa maŋ, na ŋan pou ɛ be yako nɛ man dokoloŋ gba be kɔŋ me dɔɔ ya.

eng: After the apostles had testified and proclaimed the Lords word, they returned to Jerusalem, preaching the good news to many Samaritan villages along the way.
local: Pita na Gyɔn kekolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan di adansɛɛ lee Yesu Kristo wose man taŋ nɛ, te ba besewɔ laŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man. Be ka bese bee gyu keŋ nɛ, be kolosi Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan Samariatena akuraase burum man.

eng: An angel from the Lord spoke to Philip, At noon, take the road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.
local: De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo lee adido kaa yako Filipo nyiaa, Koro desina n wose na n ka mo ata yenaŋ gbɛɛ bene keŋ ba lee Gyerusalɛm ne gyu Gasa donɔɔ man nɛ.

eng: So he did. Meanwhile, an Ethiopian man was on his way home from Jerusalem, where he had come to worship. He was a eunuch and an official responsible for the entire treasury of Candace.
local: Keŋte Filipo korowɔ desina ɔ wose moo gbɛɛ. Ɔ kee gyu keŋ na te ɔ ke gyaŋee na Itiopia baale saayɛte ŋolo. Ɔ yɛɛ Itiopia ala gyoo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Kandase nɛ wose man walaŋ kegyia ŋolo e. Ŋon ne kɛɛ na ala gyoo ŋon kɔba pou dɔɔ. Balee kei gyuu Gyerusalɛm bo ka som Wurubuarɛ.

eng: He was reading the prophet Isaiah while sitting in his carriage.
local: te ɔ besewɔ kaa laŋ ɔ ne gyu ɔ dɛɛ. Na ɔ kyaa anebiliŋinbii daale man keŋ dɛɛkyɔŋse ne wɔŋee na ke. Ɔ kyaa ɔ ne kala Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya tɔne.

eng: The Spirit told Philip, Approach this carriage and stay with it.
local: Te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon yako Filipo nyi, Tuu benaa anebiliŋinbii keŋ.

eng: Running up to the carriage, Philip heard the man reading the prophet Isaiah. He asked, Do you really understand what you are reading.
local: Keŋte Filipo yeuwɔ gyu botɔɔ, te ɔ ke nyiiwɔ ŋgba balee ŋon ne kala kolo lee Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya tɔne keŋ man nɛ te Filipo bɔɔse e nyi, Mena dɔɔ n ne nyii kpene keŋ n ne kale nɛ man.

eng: The man replied, Without someone to guide me, how could I? Then he invited Philip to climb up and sit with him.
local: Te balee ŋon tiranɔɔ fa Filipo nyi, Sena te maa tale nyii ke man, keŋ ŋolo ta lese ke man fa maŋ ya? Te ɔ baake Filipo nyi waa gyeŋ kaa kyaa ɔ gyaŋ.

eng: This was the passage of scripture he was reading: Like a sheep he was led to the slaughter and like a lamb before its shearer is silent so he didnt open his mouth. .
local: Na Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan bɔɔ ŋmarase see te ɔ ne kale yɛna nyi, Ba te kparaa e laŋ ŋgba nam ŋon bee gyu be kaa ko e nɛ. Ɔ yɛɛ ŋgba nambu ŋon bɛɛ kɛrɛɛ ɔ wona te ɔ seŋɛɛ yididi nɛ. Ɔ te wii koraŋ gba ya.

eng: In his humiliation justice was taken away from him. Who can tell the story of his descendants because his life was taken from the earth? .
local: Be tisinaa e tɛɛle man, do e desɛŋ, te be sukutii ɔ nombia do. Amɔ ne gyae la waa tale yako kolo lee ɔ duuluŋ man balaŋ wose man, ŋgba bɔɔ ko e bileŋ nɛ.

eng: The eunuch asked Philip, Tell me, about whom does the prophet say this? Is he talking about himself or someone else.
local: Botɔɔ te Itiopia baale kegyia ŋon bɔɔse Filipo nyiaa, Kegyia waaseda, amɔte wose man nombia yaa Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre kei ne yako nɛ, ŋon gbagba yaa walaŋ wɔle ŋolo bo.

eng: Starting with that passage, Philip proclaimed the good news about Jesus to him.
local: Te Filipo seŋawɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan bɔɔ kale nɛ dɔɔ yako e Yesu wose man nombia ŋan.

eng: As they went down the road, they came to some water. The eunuch said, Look! Water! What would keep me from being baptized.
local: Be kee gyu keŋ nɛ, te be ka leewɔ loŋ daale dɔɔ. Botɔɔ te balee ŋon bɔɔse Filipo nyiaa, Kɛɛ loŋ daale yaa kei, woŋ ne tɔɔ na maŋ gbɛɛ na Wurubuarɛ loŋkesɔ.

eng: He ordered that the carriage halt. Both Philip and the eunuch went down to the water, where Philip baptized him.
local: Mena dɔɔ balee ŋon yela anebiliŋinbii keŋ seŋawɔ, te ŋon na Filipo pou tisiwɔ ka gyoo loŋ keŋ man te Filipo sɔɔ e Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

eng: When they came up out of the water, the Lords Spirit suddenly took Philip away. The eunuch never saw him again but went on his way rejoicing.
local: Be ka lee loŋ keŋ man nɛ, te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon moo Filipo laŋ debokenaŋ man, mena dɔɔ balee ŋon te naa e bela ya. Mɔna balee ŋon besewɔ mo ɔ gbɛɛ ɔ ne gyu gyoŋ man.

eng: Philip found himself in Azotus. He traveled through that area, preaching the good news in all the cities until he reached Caesarea.
local: Filipo kɛɛwɔ na, ɔ kyaa Asotuse donɔɔ man. Ɔ tɛɛwɔ donɔɔse ŋan man kolosi Kristo nombia ŋan, kaboena ɔ gyuuwɔ ka lee Kaesaria donɔɔ man.

eng: Meanwhile, Saul was still spewing out murderous threats against the Lords disciples. He went to the high priest.
local: Weeya ŋenaŋ pou man na Sɔɔl sɛɛ ɔ ne ŋmaase de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu silala baŋ nyi waa ko wɔ. Mena dɔɔ ɔ gyuu Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia ŋon gyaŋ.

eng: seeking letters to the synagogues in Damascus. If he found persons who belonged to the Way, whether men or women, these letters would authorize him to take them as prisoners to Jerusalem.
local: ka sola e lɛɛ ɔ nyiŋmaa man tɔne keŋ gyae ke fa e doŋ na waa mo gyu Damasekuse donɔɔ man kefa Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man kegyiise baŋ. Na baa fa e gbɛɛ waa kyaŋ walaŋ kamasɛ, ala na baala baŋ pou ɔ naa nyi be silana Yesu kawola wɔle kei nɛ. Na waa nyiŋ kyaŋ wɔ kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man kaa tɔ deni.

eng: During the journey, as he approached Damascus, suddenly a light from heaven encircled him.
local: Ɔ ke gyu ka benaa Damasekuse donɔɔ nɛ, debokenaŋ man te boalaŋ daale lee adido kaa ŋmaale polɛ kilisi e.

eng: He fell to the ground and heard a voice asking him, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔ leewɔ yala te ɔ nyii woya ŋaale baake e bɔɔse nyi, Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, weera dɔɔ te n ne naase maŋ diyem mena.

eng: Saul asked, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus, whom you are harassing, came the reply.
local: Botɔɔ te Sɔɔl tiranɔɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, Nyaŋ amɔte ŋonaŋ me Gbeŋgyoo? Te ŋon mɔ yako e nyiaa, Maŋ yɛna Yesu ŋon n ne naase diyem nɛ.

eng: Now get up and enter the city. You will be told what you must do.
local: Mɔna koro na n ke gyu donɔɔ keŋ man, na baa yako neŋ kpene keŋ kaboena nyi nya yɛɛ.

eng: Those traveling with him stood there speechless; they heard the voice but saw no one.
local: Balaŋ baŋ baŋ na Sɔɔl dɔŋɛɛ nɛ, ba woya yeuwɔ lee be gyaŋ keŋ ba te tale gyuusu be nɔɔ ya. Baŋ mɔ nyii nɔɔwoya ŋan, mɔna ba te naa ŋolo ya.

eng: After they picked Saul up from the ground, he opened his eyes but he couldnt see. So they led him by the hand into Damascus.
local: Sɔɔl korowɔ seŋ te ɔ gyuusu ɔ sia mɔna ɔ te tale naa kolo ya, mena dɔɔ be kyaŋa e ɔ nyiŋmaa man, te ba moo e gyu Damasekuse donɔɔ man.

eng: For three days he was blind and neither ate nor drank anything.
local: Ɔ kyaawɔ botɔɔ weeya kpaakpaa atooro keŋ nyi ɔ bɛɛ naa ya, te ɔ bee dii ya, te ɔ bɛɛ nyɔɔ mɔ ya.

eng: In Damascus there was a certain disciple named Ananias. The Lord spoke to him in a vision, Ananias! He answered, Yes, Lord.
local: Na Yesu silale ŋolo kyaa Damasekuse botɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake e nyi Ananiase. Ɔ nyiiwɔ de Gbeŋgyoo baake e ŋgba dosɛɛ man nɛ nyi, Ananiase. Te ɔ kpaawɔ nyi, Me Gbeŋgyoo maŋ yaa.

eng: The Lord instructed him, Go to Judas house on Straight Street and ask for a man from Tarsus named Saul. He is praying.
local: Te de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu yako e nyi, Koro gyu Gyuda dɛɛ, ke kyaa gbɛɛnaa keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi gbɛɛnaa keŋ ka Tenɛɛ nɛ nɔɔman, na n ke bɔɔse Sɔɔl ŋon ɔ ba lee Tasuse nɛ dɔɔ. Ɔ kyaa botɔɔ ɔ ne fane.

eng: In a vision he has seen a man named Ananias enter and put his hands on him to restore his sight.
local: Wurubuarɛ ta lese wola ŋon Sɔɔl mɔ ŋgba dosɛɛ man nɛ nyi, balee ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Ananiase nɛ, te kɔŋ ɔ kaa mo ɔ nyiŋmaase gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ, na waa bese naa bela.

eng: Ananias countered, Lord, I have heard many reports about this man. People say he has done horrible things to your holy people in Jerusalem.
local: Ananiase tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, balaŋ burum te yako maŋ balee kei wose man nombia, na mena keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ n balaŋ baŋ be kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ akpanyaŋnyaŋ.

eng: Hes here with authority from the chief priests to arrest everyone who calls on your name.
local: Ɔ lɛɛ tɔne keŋ ne fa e doŋ baa lee Gyudatena saese kegyiise gyaŋ kɔŋ Damasekuse kɛbo, na ɔ kaa kyaŋ baŋ pou bɛɛ som neŋ nɛ.

eng: The Lord replied, Go! This man is the agent I have chosen to carry my name before Gentiles, kings, and Israelites.
local: Keŋte de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu tiranɔɔ fa Ananiase nyiaa, Gyu ɔ gyaŋ, nawolo nyi maŋ lese na e nyi waa som maŋ, na waa yela baŋ pou ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya, na be gyooneŋ na Iseraetena pou ke nyii ma yele.

eng: I will show him how much he must suffer for the sake of my name.
local: Maŋ ne gyae la maa wola e mena keŋ kaboena nyi waa naa diyem nideli lee ma yele dɔɔ.

eng: Ananias went to the house. He placed his hands on Saul and said, Brother Saul, the Lord sent me—Jesus, who appeared to you on the way as you were coming here. He sent me so that you could see again and be filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Keŋte Ananiase gyuuwɔ ka gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man. Te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaase gyakaa Sɔɔl nyee dɔ fane nyiaa, Me dɔɔ Sɔɔl, de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ŋon ɔ ba lese ɔ wose wola neŋ gbɛɛnaa man debaŋ keŋ ne kɔŋ kɛbo nɛ, ŋon kpila na maŋ n gyaŋ nyi me kaa fane fa neŋ na n kabese naa. Na Wurubuarɛ kenyiŋ mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do n man.

eng: Instantly, flakes fell from Sauls eyes and he could see again. He got up and was baptized.
local: Debokenaŋ man te kolo leewɔ ɔ sia ŋan man yala ŋgba kpebii bɔɔlɛɛ bia nɛ, te Sɔɔl sia ŋan gyuusuwɔ, te ɔ besewɔ ɔ ne naa bela. Ɔ korowɔ seŋ te ba ke sɔɔ e Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

eng: After eating, he regained his strength. He stayed with the disciples in Damascus for several days.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle te be faa e weenɛɛ. Ɔ ke di taŋ nɛ, ɔ wose besewɔ yɛɛ e doŋ. Te ɔ dii weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale Yesu silala baŋ gyaŋ Damasekuse donɔɔ man botɔɔ.

eng: Right away, he began to preach about Jesus in the synagogues. He is Gods Son, he declared.
local: Debokenaŋ man te Sɔɔl fiasɛɛ ɔ ne kolosi Yesu nombia Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man nyi, Yesu yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon.

eng: Everyone who heard him was baffled. They questioned each other, Isnt he the one who was wreaking havoc among those in Jerusalem who called on this name? Hadnt he come here to take those same people as prisoners to the chief priests.
local: Baŋ pou bɔɔ nyii e nɛ wose gbirisi wɔ, te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyi, Na balee kei kyaa la Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man ɔ ne naase baŋ bɛɛ som Yesu nɛ diyem yee? Na keŋ dɔɔ te ɔ kɔŋawɔ kɛbo ɔ kaa kyaŋ wɔ na waa mo wɔ gyina saese kegyiise baŋ yee.

eng: But Saul grew stronger and stronger. He confused the Jews who lived in Damascus by proving that Jesus is the Christ.
local: Te Sɔɔl besewɔ nyiŋ doŋ kpu te ɔ kolosiwɔ lese ŋe man nideli fa Gyudatena baŋ nyi, Yesu yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ. Ɔ kolosiwɔ nideli keŋ nyi Gyudatena baŋ be kyaa Damasekuse donɔɔ man nɛ, te tale gyuusu be nɔɔ bela ya.

eng: After this had gone on for some time, the Jews hatched a plot to kill Saul.
local: Weeya ala ŋaale wɔle nɛ, Gyudatena kegyiise gyaŋeewɔ yɛɛ nɔɔ nyi baa ko Sɔɔl.

eng: However, he found out about their scheme. They were keeping watch at the city gates around the clock so they could assassinate him.
local: Mɔna Sɔɔl nyii be nombiakumɛɛ ŋan bɔɔ bake ɔ nyeeman nɛ. Nelim na ka weese pou na, bɛɛ deke donɔɔ keŋ anaanɔɔse ŋan na baa nyiŋ ko e.

eng: But his disciples took him by night and lowered him in a basket through an opening in the city wall.
local: Mɔna nelim daale nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ moo e do dokyoŋoloŋ man, te be tisinaa e mo nfansere man gbaa dekyaŋ keŋ bɔɔ maa kilisi donɔɔ keŋ nɛ, te ɔ yeuwɔ laŋ Gyerusalɛm.

eng: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the disciples, but they were all afraid of him. They didnt believe he was really a disciple.
local: Sɔɔl ka bese gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ gyaewɔ nyi waa mo ɔ wose kpu na Yesu silala baŋ. Mɔna be popou ne yee nawolo nyi na ba te lɛɛ ta di nyi, ŋon mɔ ta bese Yesu silale gbagba ya.

eng: Then Barnabas brought Saul to the apostles and told them the story about how Saul saw the Lord on the way and that the Lord had spoken to Saul. He also told them about the confidence with which Saul had preached in the name of Jesus in Damascus.
local: Keŋte Banabase moo e gyu Kristo kpilala baŋ gyaŋ. Te ɔ yako wɔ mena keŋ Sɔɔl be naa de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Damasekuse gbɛɛnaa man, na mena keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu be kolosi na e, na mena keŋ ɔ be gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ mo Yesu yele keyako Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan Damasekuse donɔɔ man nɛ.

eng: After this, Saul moved freely among the disciples in Jerusalem and was speaking with confidence in the name of the Lord.
local: Mena dɔɔ Sɔɔl kpuuwɔ na Yesu silala baŋ. Ɔ kyaawɔ be gyaŋ te be gyɔɔ be konɔɔ ta na Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan lee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu yele man.

eng: He got into debates with the Greek-speaking Jews as well, but they tried to kill him.
local: Sɔɔl kolosiwɔ nideli te ŋon na Gyudatena baŋ bee kolosi Giriki dei nɛ, lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ nideli. Te be gyae gbɛɛ keŋ baa mo ko e.

eng: When the family of believers learned about this, they escorted him down to Caesarea and sent him off to Tarsus.
local: Mena dɔɔ Yesu silala baŋ ka gyeŋ mena nɛ, ba moo e gyu Kaesaria donɔɔ man te be yela ɔ laŋa Tasuse.

eng: Then the church throughout Judea, Galilee, and Samaria enjoyed a time of peace. God strengthened the church, and its life was marked by reverence for the Lord. Encouraged by the Holy Spirit, the church continued to grow in numbers.
local: Mena dɔɔ Kristotena baŋ be kyaa Gyudia na Galelia na Samaria tɛɛlese man nɛ, wose fɛɛ wɔ mena debaŋ kenaŋ man. Be lɛɛ Yesu nombia ŋan di nideli, te be tɛɛwɔ Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii man, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon doo wɔ kakyeŋ te be wukuwɔ kpu.

eng: As Peter toured the whole region, he went to visit Gods holy people in Lydda.
local: Kɔŋawɔ debaŋ daale nyi Pita keta ɔ ne kila tɛɛle keŋ nɛ, ɔ gyuu Lida donɔɔ man ɔ kaa kɛɛ Kristotena baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ.

eng: There he found a man named Aeneas who was paralyzed and had been confined to his bed for eight years.
local: Keŋte ɔ ke naa balee ŋolo na ɔ yele yɛna Anease ŋon ɔ wose pou ba yem nɛ. Ɔ doowɔ dofoŋoŋ dɔɔ kulutooneŋ gyanaara keŋ ɔ bɛɛ tale koro ya.

eng: Peter said to him, Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you! Get up and make your bed. At once he got up.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita baake e nyiaa, Anease kɛɛ, Yesu Kristo te kyɔ neŋ n kawee. Koro seŋ na n ke bɔ n dofoŋoŋ! Debokenaŋ man te Anease korowɔ seŋ.

eng: Everyone who lived in Lydda and Sharon saw him and turned to the Lord.
local: Balaŋ baŋ pou be kyaa Lida na Sarɔn kena Anease dɔŋɛɛ nɛ, be lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nombia ŋan di te ba bese ɔ silala.

eng: In Joppa there was a disciple named Tabitha . Her life overflowed with good works and compassionate acts on behalf of those in need.
local: Na alo ŋolo mɔ kyaa Gyopa keŋ ŋon mɔ yɛɛ Yesu silale e te ɔ yele yɛna Tabita. Giriki dei man na baa baake e nyi Dɔkase keŋ nawolo nyi ŋaa. Debaŋ kamasɛ na alo kei ne yɛɛ deeli fa balaŋ, te ɔ ne kyɔ ayematena mɔ nideli.

eng: About that time, though, she became so ill that she died. After they washed her body, they laid her in an upstairs room.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ man nɛ, ɔ gyoo kawee te ɔ yeŋawɔ. Te be sɔɔ e teeli abansoro deni daale man.

eng: Since Lydda was near Joppa, when the disciples heard that Peter was there, they sent two people to Peter. They urged, Please come right away.
local: Lida na Gyopa donɔɔse taŋ lem na dɔŋa ya. Te kaseela baŋ be kyaa Gyopa kenyii nyi Pita kyaa Lida nɛ, be kpila tewulɛɛ bala gyu ɔ gyaŋ nyi, Pita, waaseda, yɛɛ bileŋ na n kekɔŋ de gyaŋ.

eng: Peter went with them. Upon his arrival, he was taken to the upstairs room. All the widows stood beside him, crying as they showed the tunics and other clothing Dorcas made when she was alive.
local: Mena dɔɔ Pita korowɔ sila wɔ. Be ka gyoo botɔɔ nɛ te ba moo e gyu deni adidote keŋ man. Te liala baŋ pou kaa yilaawɔ kilisi Pita bee wii, na bɛɛ mo kegbase na abɔɔ ŋan Tabita be ŋare fa wɔ pɛte ɔ ba yem nɛ wola e.

eng: Peter sent everyone out of the room, then knelt and prayed. He turned to the body and said, Tabitha, get up! She opened her eyes, saw Peter, and sat up.
local: Keŋte Pita lese be popou lee deni keŋ man, te ɔ ŋmiŋawɔ ɔ doona dɔɔ te ɔ fanewɔ. Te ɔ besenawɔ kɛɛ woo ŋon te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Tabita, koro! Te woo ŋon gyuusu ɔ sia, te ɔ kena Pita nɛ, ɔ korowɔ kyaa.

eng: He gave her his hand and raised her up. Then he called Gods holy people, including the widows, and presented her alive to them.
local: Keŋte Pita kyaŋa e ɔ nyiŋmaa man kyɔ e te ɔ korowɔ seŋ. Te ɔ baake Yesu silala baŋ na liala baŋ, te ɔ moo Dɔkase do wɔ be nyiŋmaa man na ɔ delena.

eng: The news spread throughout Joppa, and many put their faith in the Lord.
local: Nombia kɛŋa yaaseewɔ kila Gyopa keŋ pou, te balaŋ burum kaa lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu di.

eng: Peter stayed for some time in Joppa with a certain tanner named Simon.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle na Pita dii weeya burum Gyopa donɔɔ man botɔɔ. Na ɔ kyae Simɔn ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ wonembia tɔna wose man tom nɛ gyaŋ.

eng: There was a man in Caesarea named Cornelius, a centurion in the Italian Company.
local: Balee ŋolo kyaawɔ Kaesaria donɔɔ man na ɔ yele yɛna Kɔnelio. Ɔ yɛɛ Itali tɛɛle yoonɔɔtena dikpii siamante e. Na yoonɔɔtena sɔŋonoŋ doo la ɔ tɛɛ.

eng: He and his whole household were pious, Gentile God-worshippers. He gave generously to those in need among the Jewish people and prayed to God constantly.
local: Ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba mo ɔ wose fa Wurubuarɛ, te ŋon na ɔ dekpaŋalaŋmantena pou ne yee Wurubuarɛ nideli. Ɔ dana wɔe fa Gyudatena ayematena te ɔ ne fane fa Wurubuarɛ debaŋ kamasɛ.

eng: One day at nearly three oclock in the afternoon, he clearly saw an angel from God in a vision. The angel came to him and said, Cornelius.
local: Wee daale balinɔɔ weese debaŋ atoorote nɛ, ɔ naa kolo na ɔ sia ŋgba dosɛɛ man nɛ. Ɔ naa Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo ta lee adido kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ baake e nyi, Kɔnelio.

eng: Startled, he stared at the angel and replied, What is it, Lord? The angel said, Your prayers and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to God.
local: Te Kɔnelio kɛɛ e yididi gyakoloŋ man te ɔ bɔɔse e nyi, Me Gbeŋgyoo weera? Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon yako e nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ te lɛɛ n kefane. Ɔ te naa mena keŋ n ne yɛɛ wɔe fa ayematena baŋ, te ŋe ta gyoo ɔ sia.

eng: Send messengers to Joppa at once and summon a certain Simon, the one known as Peter.
local: Mena dɔɔ kpila balaŋ gyu Gyopa donɔɔ keŋ man, be kaa baake walaŋ ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Simɔn yaa Pita nɛ.

eng: He is a guest of Simon the tanner, whose house is near the seacoast.
local: Ɔ kyaa Simɔn ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ wonembia tɔna wose man tom nɛ dɛɛ. Ɔ dɛɛ kyaa ɛpo nɔɔman.

eng: When the angel who was speaking to him had gone, Cornelius summoned two of his household servants along with a pious soldier from his personal staff.
local: Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon kekɔŋ kaa kolosi fa e taŋ te ɔ be lam nɛ, Kɔnelio baake ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ man tewulɛɛ bala, na yoonɔɔtena baŋ bee gyee e nɛ man ŋolo, ŋon ɔ benaa na e te ɔ ne yee Wurubuarɛ nɛ.

eng: He explained everything to them, then sent them to Joppa.
local: Te ɔ yako wɔ kpene keŋ pou be kɔŋ nɛ, te ɔ kpila wɔ Gyopa donɔɔ man.

eng: At noon on the following day, as their journey brought them close to the city, Peter went up on the roof to pray.
local: Tɛɛ kena weeseteŋteŋ keŋ be dɔŋ bee gyuu te be ka benaa Gyopa donɔɔ keŋ nɛ, debaŋ kenaŋ te Pita mɔ gyeŋawɔ laŋ abansoro dɔɔ ɔ kaa fane.

eng: He became hungry and wanted to eat. While others were preparing the meal, he had a visionary experience.
local: Tanam kyaŋa e te ɔ gyae kolo waa di, mɔna debaŋ keŋ bɛɛ saa weenɛɛ ŋan nɛ, ɔ naa kolo na ɔ sia ŋgba dosɛɛ man nɛ.

eng: He saw heaven opened up and something like a large linen sheet being lowered to the earth by its four corners.
local: Ɔ naawɔ ŋgba adido ba toro na, te ɔ naa kolo ŋgba kanyaŋ dinaa daale nɛ, na be dana ke nɔɔsebia anaara ŋan man lee adido, te be tisinaa ke kaa see tɛɛle man.

eng: Inside the sheet were all kinds of four-legged animals, reptiles, and wild birds.
local: Na wonembia kpookpoo doo ke man. Baŋ be dana nawɔɔ anaara, na baŋ bee tuu be tɔɔse dɔɔ, na baŋ bee furufu nɛ doo ke man.

eng: A voice told him, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Keŋte ɔ nyii nɔɔwoya ŋaale nyi, Pita, koro na n ka ko di.

eng: Peter exclaimed, Absolutely not, Lord! I have never eaten anything impure or unclean.
local: Te Pita yakowɔ nyi, Koa, dee gyu lem me Gbeŋgyoo, nawolo nyi me te dii ta kolo kum yaa kpene keŋ dɛɛ kɔle nɛ see ya.

eng: The voice spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Te nɔɔwoya ŋan besewɔ yako e bela nyi, Kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ be lɛɛ do nyi ke dei nɛ, na baake ke nyi kolo anekɔlaŋ bela ya.

eng: This happened three times, then the object was suddenly pulled back into heaven.
local: Kei yɛɛwɔ mena betabotooro. Te debokenaŋ man ba moo kanyaŋ keŋ laŋ adido bela.

eng: Peter was bewildered about the meaning of the vision. Just then, the messengers sent by Cornelius discovered the whereabouts of Simons house and arrived at the gate.
local: Pita kekyaa ɔ na gyueŋ nombia ŋan ɔ be naa nɛ wose man nɛ, na Kɔnelio kpilala baŋ mɔ te bɔɔse gyu kena dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man Simɔn kyaa, te be gyuuwɔ ka seŋ ka denanɔɔ man.

eng: Calling out, they inquired whether the Simon known as Peter was a guest there.
local: Keŋte be baake ŋolo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man bɔɔse e nyiaa, Kɛbo te balee ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Simɔn Pita nɛ kyaa.

eng: While Peter was brooding over the vision, the Spirit interrupted him, Look! Three people are looking for you.
local: Na Pita sɛɛ ɔ na gyueŋ nombia ŋan ɔ be naa nɛ wose man, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ yako e nyiaa, Simɔn, nyii kɛbo, tewulɛɛ batooro baale ne gyae neŋ.

eng: Go downstairs. Dont ask questions; just go with them because I have sent them.
local: Mena dɔɔ desina n wose na n ke tisi gyu be gyaŋ. Sila wɔ ta, na lɛɛ n wose aŋmaareŋ ya nawolo nyi maŋ kpila na wɔ.

eng: So Peter went downstairs and told them, Im the one you are looking for. Why have you come.
local: Mena dɔɔ Pita tisiwɔ gyu balaŋ baŋ gyaŋ te ɔ ke yako wɔ nyiaa, Maŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋon ɛ ne kɛo nɛ. Ɛ keta gyakoloŋ.

eng: They replied, Weve come on behalf of Cornelius, a centurion and righteous man, a God-worshipper who is well-respected by all Jewish people. A holy angel directed him to summon you to his house and to hear what you have to say.
local: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ tiranɔɔ nyiaa, De gbeŋgyoo yoonɔɔtena dikpii siamante ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Konelio nɛ kpila na daa n gyaŋ. Ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne yee Wurubuarɛ, te ŋon na e mɔ nsana dei, te Gyudatena pou ne bu e. Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo kɔŋala kaa yako e nyi, waa kpila kaa mo neŋ kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ, na waa nyii kpene keŋ n dana fa e.

eng: Peter invited them into the house as his guests. The next day he got up and went with them, together with some of the believers from Joppa.
local: Te Pita moo balaŋ baŋ gyu ɔ dɛɛ, te ɔ yela be doowɔ ɔ gyaŋ. Tɛɛ kena keŋ nɛ, Pita desina ɔ wose te ŋon na wɔ pou laŋawɔ. Ɔ tebia Kristotena baale mɔ leewɔ Gyopa sila wɔ gyu.

eng: They arrived in Caesarea the following day. Anticipating their arrival, Cornelius had gathered his relatives and close friends.
local: Tɛɛ kena te ba gyoo Kaesaria, na Kɔnelio ne kɛɛ be gbɛɛ mena dɔɔ ɔ yilaa ɔ loŋtona na ɔ gyoona kpaakpaa nyibaŋ.

eng: As Peter entered the house, Cornelius met him and fell at his feet in order to honor him.
local: Pita ka gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man nɛ, Kɔnelio kaa gyaŋeewɔ na e te ɔ tisiwɔ buŋ ɔ nawɔɔtɛɛ obuo man.

eng: But Peter lifted him up, saying, Get up! Like you, Im just a human.
local: Keŋte Pita yako e nyi Koro, na yɛɛ mena ya. Nawolo nyi maŋ mɔ yɛɛ deniwalaŋ e ŋgba nyaŋ nɛ.

eng: As they continued to talk, Peter went inside and found a large gathering of people.
local: Te ŋon na Pita moo afa keyɔɔ mo gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man, te Pita ke naa balaŋ burum baŋ pou bɔɔ yilaa botɔɔ nɛ.

eng: He said to them, You all realize that it is forbidden for a Jew to associate or visit with outsiders. However, God has shown me that I should never call a person impure or unclean.
local: Te ɔ yako wɔ nyi, Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ gyeŋ nideli nyi nafɔ daa Gyudatena ɔsom kɛɛ wolo man na, gbɛɛ be doo keŋ nyi Gyuda baale ka mo ɔ wose do na walaŋ ŋon ɔ te yɛɛ Gyuda baale e ya, yaa waa gyu ɔ gyaŋ ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ ta lese wola maŋ nyi, me na kɛɛ walaŋ ŋolo nyi ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ yakaate e ya.

eng: For this reason, when you sent for me, I came without objection. I want to know, then, why you sent for me.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ n ke kpila baake maŋ na, ma te bɛɛ nyi mɔɔ kɔŋ ya. Mɛɛ gyae maa nyii kpene keŋ dɔɔ n be kpili baake maŋ.

eng: Cornelius answered, Four days ago at this same time, three oclock in the afternoon, I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in radiant clothing stood before me.
local: Botɔɔ te Kɔnelio tiranɔɔ nyiaa, Weeya anaara yaa ŋgba me kyaa me dɛɛ mɛɛ fane balinɔɔ weese ŋgba debaŋ atoorote man nɛ, debokenaŋ man te baale ŋolo kaa bɛɛwɔ lee me dɔɔ. Na ɔ doona abɔɔ ŋan ne ŋalakee nɛ, te ɔ kaa seŋawɔ me siaman.

eng: He said, Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to him.
local: Te ɔ yako maŋ nyi, Kɔnelio, Wurubuarɛ te nyii n kefane te ɔ ne tɔɔse n wɔe keŋ n dana fa ayematena nɛ, mena dɔɔ.

eng: Therefore, send someone to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter. He is a guest in the home of Simon the tanner, located near the seacoast.
local: kpila gyu Gyopa donɔɔ man ke baake Simɔn Pita, ɔ kyaa Simɔn ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ wonembia tɔna wose man tom te ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ kyaa ɛpo nɔɔman nɛ gyaŋ.

eng: I sent for you right away, and you were kind enough to come. Now, here we are, gathered in the presence of God to listen to everything the Lord has directed you to say.
local: Mena dɔɔ te me kpila balaŋ n gyaŋ debokenaŋ man. N yɛɛ ke nideli ŋgba n be kɔŋ nɛ. Nɛnɛɛ de popou te kaa gyaŋee kɛbo Wurubuarɛ siaman dɛɛ daa neŋ, na dee nyii nɔɔwoya ŋan de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpa nya yako daa nɛ.

eng: Peter said, I really am learning that God doesnt show partiality to one group of people over another.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita gyinaa ɔ nɔɔwoya nyi, Nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ ma te naa ampaŋ nyi balaŋ pou yɛɛ kyɛɛkyɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman, te ɔ bɛɛ kɛɛ ŋolo siaman ya.

eng: Rather, in every nation, whoever worships him and does what is right is acceptable to him.
local: Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne yee e te ɔ ne yɛɛ abɔɔ ŋan doo na ŋe gbɛɛ na, ɔ ne lɛɛ e do. Ɔ be famenɛ na ɔtenate dalee ya.

eng: This is the message of peace he sent to the Israelites by proclaiming the good news through Jesus Christ: He is Lord of all!.
local: Ɛmɛɛ gbagba gyeŋ nɔɔwoya kpaakpaa ŋan Wurubuarɛ ba mo fa Iseraetena nɛ. Ɔ yako wɔ nyi baa sila Kristo ŋon ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ kamasɛ Gbeŋgyoo e nɛ, na baŋ na ŋon Wurubuarɛ nsana ke de na baa nyiŋ wosefɛɛreŋ.

eng: You know what happened throughout Judea, beginning in Galilee after the baptism John preached.
local: Ɛ gyeŋ nombii dinaa keŋ be kɔŋ Iserae tɛɛle dɔɔ, keŋ be fiasɛɛ lee Galelia tɛɛle man debaŋ keŋ Gyɔn be kolosi lee Wurubuarɛ loŋkesɔ keŋ wose man nombia ŋan wɔle nɛ.

eng: You know about Jesus of Nazareth, whom God anointed with the Holy Spirit and endowed with power. Jesus traveled around doing good and healing everyone oppressed by the devil because God was with him.
local: Ɛ gyeŋ mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo ɔ feliŋ na ɔ doŋ keŋ do Yesu Nasarɛte baale ŋon man, mena dɔɔ Yesu tɛɛwɔ yɛɛ deeli te ɔ kyɔɔ baŋ pou ɔbɔnsam be di be dɔɔ nɛ kaweese. Ɔ yɛɛ kɛŋa pou nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ kpuɛ na e.

eng: We are witnesses of everything he did, both in Judea and in Jerusalem. They killed him by hanging him on a tree.
local: Te dee di adansɛɛ lee kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ Gyudatena tɛɛle man na Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ. Be gyɔɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ ko.

eng: but God raised him up on the third day and allowed him to be seen.
local: Mɔna weeya atoorote keŋ man nɛ, Wurubuarɛ gyuusu e lee yeŋ man, te ɔ yela ɔ lese ɔ wose wola balaŋ.

eng: not by everyone but by us. We are witnesses whom God chose beforehand, who ate and drank with him after God raised him from the dead.
local: Na balaŋ pou naa na e ya, mɔna daa baŋ Wurubuarɛ be wulaa lese nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ wa adansɛɛtena nɛ, daa daageŋ naa na e. Wurubuarɛ ke gyuusu e lee yeŋ man mɔ nɛ, daa na e diiwɔ te de nyɔɔwɔ.

eng: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one whom God appointed as judge of the living and the dead.
local: Te ɔ yako daa nyi dee kolosi ɔ nombia fa balaŋ na dee di adansɛɛ lee ɔ wose man nyi, ŋon yɛna walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ ba lese nyi, waa di balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ yekee na baŋ be kyaa na be sia nɛ pou nombia.

eng: All the prophets testify about him that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.
local: Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ pou kolosiwɔ lee ɔ wose man nyi, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa lɛɛ e di na, Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ e lee Yesu yele man.

eng: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit fell on everyone who heard the word.
local: Pita kegyakaa ɔ ne kolosi nɛ, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon kaa gyoo baŋ pou bee tei e nɛ man.

eng: The circumcised believers who had come with Peter were astonished that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles.
local: Te Gyudatena baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di te bɔɔ sila Pita kɔŋ nɛ, wose gbirisi wɔ ŋgba Wurubuarɛ ba mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya mɔ man nɛ.

eng: They heard them speaking in other languages and praising God. Peter asked.
local: Nawolo nyi be nyiiwɔ bee kolosi deise kpookpoo, na bɛɛ lese Wurubuarɛ yele. Keŋte Pita yako wɔ nyiaa.

eng: These people have received the Holy Spirit just as we have. Surely no one can stop them from being baptized with water, can they.
local: Wurubuarɛ ta mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do balaŋ kɛwɔ mɔ man, ŋgba mena keŋ keŋ ɔ ba mo do de man nɛ. Ŋolo waa tale tɔ wɔ gbɛɛ nyi be na sɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ yee.

eng: He directed that they be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they invited Peter to stay for several days.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔ yela be sɔɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ lee Yesu Kristo yele man. Te ba sola e nyi, waa kyaa be gyaŋ weeya ala.

eng: The apostles and the brothers and sisters throughout Judea heard that even the Gentiles had welcomed Gods word.
local: Yesu kpilala na Kristotena baŋ be kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle keŋ dɔɔ nyiiwɔ nyi, balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ mɔ te lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan do.

eng: When Peter went up to Jerusalem, the circumcised believers criticized him.
local: Pita ke gyu ka gyoo Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, Gyudatena baŋ ba seŋɛɛ nyi Yesu silala pou baa kara be baala wose nɛ te sɛɛ e ya.

eng: They accused him, You went into the home of the uncircumcised and ate with them.
local: Be bɔɔse e nyi, Weera te n gyuuwɔ balaŋ baŋ ba te kara be baala wose ya nɛ gyaŋ, te n ke diiwɔ bom na wɔ.

eng: Step-by-step, Peter explained what had happened.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita lese kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ man dokoloŋ dokoloŋ fa wɔ nyiaa.

eng: I was in the city of Joppa praying when I had a visionary experience. In my vision, I saw something like a large linen sheet being lowered from heaven by its four corners. It came all the way down to me.
local: Na me kyaa Gyopa donɔɔ man mɛɛ fane. Keŋte me naa kolo ŋgba kanyaŋ dinaa daale nɛ na me sia ŋgba dosɛɛ man nɛ, keŋ be dana ke nɔɔbia anaara ŋan man te be tisinaa ke lee adido kaa see me siaman.

eng: As I stared at it, wondering what it was, I saw four-legged animals—including wild beasts—as well as reptiles and wild birds.
local: Me kɛɛ ke man nideli na te me naa wonembia kpookpoo, baŋ be dana nawɔɔ anaara na baŋ bee tuu be tɔɔse dɔɔ na baŋ bee furufu na wonembia kumɛɛ pou.

eng: I heard a voice say, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Te me nyii nɔɔwoya ŋaale nyi, Pita, koro na n ka ko di.

eng: I responded, Absolutely not, Lord! Nothing impure or unclean has ever entered my mouth.
local: Te me yakowɔ nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo koa, dee gyu lem, kolo kum yaa kpene keŋ dɛɛ kɔle nɛ te tinaa ta me nɔɔ see ya.

eng: The voice from heaven spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Te nɔɔwoya ŋan besewɔ yako man lee adido bolate nyiaa, Kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ be lɛɛ do nyi ke dei nɛ, na baake ke nyi kolo anekɔlaŋ ya.

eng: This happened three times, then everything was pulled back into heaven.
local: Ke yɛɛwɔ mena betabotooro te be wɔŋa woni keŋ pou laŋ adido bela.

eng: At that moment three men who had been sent to me from Caesarea arrived at the house where we were staying.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ na baala batooro baŋ Konelio be kpili lee Kaesaria kɔŋ me gyaŋ nɛ, te kaa sem dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man me kyaa nɛ.

eng: The Spirit told me to go with them even though they were Gentiles. These six brothers also went with me, and we entered that mans house.
local: Te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ yako maŋ nyi, me na lɛɛ ma wose aŋmaareŋ ya, maa sila wɔ gyu. Me tebia looro kɛwɔ sila maŋ lee Gyopa gyu Kaesaria te de popou gyuuwɔ ka gyoo Konelio dɛɛ.

eng: He reported to us how he had seen an angel standing in his house and saying, Send to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter.
local: Botɔɔ te ɔ yako daa mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ kpilale be kaa bɛɛ lee ɔ dɔɔ ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ man, te ɔ yako e nyi kpila gyu Gyopa ke baake balee ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Simɔn Pita nɛ.

eng: He will tell you how you and your entire household can be saved.
local: Waa kɔŋ kaa yako ŋon nombia kpaakpaa ŋan gyae ke yela ɛmɛɛ na n dekpaŋalaŋmantena pou ke nyiŋ nyeekelɛɛ.

eng: When I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell on them, just as the Spirit fell on us in the beginning.
local: Me ke gyina me nɔɔ mee kolosi pɛ, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon kɔŋawɔ kaa gyoo be man, ŋgba mena keŋ ba gyoo de man kewalaŋ keŋ nɛ.

eng: I remembered the Lords words: John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Botɔɔ te me tɔɔse de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nɔɔwoya ŋan ɔ be yako nyi, Loŋ te Gyɔn moowɔ sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ, mɔna Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon bo sɔ ŋon Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

eng: If God gave them the same gift he gave us who believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, then who am I? Could I stand in Gods way.
local: Mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo ɔ feliŋ do de man debaŋ keŋ man dɔɔ lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo di nɛ, mena ke yaa ɔ moowɔ do baŋ mɔ man. Kenaŋ na, maŋ yɛna amɔte ŋon maa tale tɔ Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛ.

eng: Once the apostles and other believers heard this, they calmed down. They praised God and concluded, So then God has enabled Gentiles to change their hearts and lives so that they might have new life.
local: Be ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ be tinaa nombia ŋan kewɔŋee yela te ba lese Wurubuarɛ yele nyi, Nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ te fa balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ mɔ gbɛɛ nyi, baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na baa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.

eng: Now those who were scattered as a result of the trouble that occurred because of Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch. They proclaimed the word only to Jews.
local: Be ka ko Sitifin wɔle nɛ, baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Kristo di nɛ yaaseewɔ lee mena keŋ bɛɛ naase wɔ diyem dɔɔ. Baale gyuuwɔ naŋ Tunisia na Saiporɔse tɛɛle man, na Antiɔke donɔɔ man bɛɛ yako Yesu nombia ŋan fa Gyudatena baageŋ.

eng: Among them were some people from Cyprus and Cyrene. They entered Antioch and began to proclaim the good news about the Lord Jesus also to Gentiles.
local: Mɔna baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Kristo di nɛ man baale baŋ bɔɔ lee Saiporɔse na Kirene nɛ, gyuu Antiɔke ke yako de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu wose man nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ.

eng: The Lords power was with them, and a large number came to believe and turned to the Lord.
local: De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ seŋawɔ be wɔle te balaŋ burum lɛɛ nombia ŋan di te be kyɛɛkeewɔ lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man, te be kɔŋawɔ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu gyaŋ.

eng: When the church in Jerusalem heard about this, they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
local: Debaŋ keŋ Kristotena baŋ be kyaa Gyerusalɛm be nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, be kpila Banabase gyu Antiɔke.

eng: When he arrived and saw evidence of Gods grace, he was overjoyed and encouraged everyone to remain fully committed to the Lord.
local: Ɔ ke gyu ka gyoo botɔɔ te ɔ kena mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele wɔ nɛ, yɛɛ e gyoŋ te ɔ doo be popou kakyeŋ nyi baa mo be konɔɔ pou sila de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu.

eng: Barnabas responded in this way because he was a good man, whom the Holy Spirit had endowed with exceptional faith. A considerable number of people were added to the Lord.
local: Banabase yɛɛ walaŋ kpaakpaate ŋon ɔ be lɛɛ Kristo nombia ŋan di, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ doo ɔ man nideli. De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ moo balaŋ burum kaa kpu na Kristotena dikpii keŋ.

eng: Barnabas went to Tarsus in search of Saul.
local: Te Banabase gyuu Tasuse ɔ kaa kɛo Sɔɔl.

eng: When he found him, he brought him to Antioch. They were there for a whole year, meeting with the church and teaching large numbers of people. It was in Antioch where the disciples were first labeled Christians.
local: Ɔ kena e nɛ ɔ moo e kɔŋ Antiɔke. Kulutoo dibiliŋ pou na Banabase na Sɔɔl ne kyaa gyaŋee Yesu kaseela dikpii dinaa keŋ na bɛɛ wola wɔ. Antiɔke botɔɔ te be taŋgbɛɛwɔ baake Yesu silala baŋ nyi Kristotena.

eng: About that time, some prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.
local: Weeya ŋenaŋ man te Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baale lee Gyerusalɛm gyu Antiɔke.

eng: One of them, Agabus, stood up and, inspired by the Spirit, predicted that a severe famine would overtake the entire Roman world.
local: Keŋte be man ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e Agabuse nɛ korowɔ seŋ te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ tɛɛwɔ ɔ dɔɔ yako nombia see nyiaa, tanam dinaa daale gyae kekɔŋ tɛɛle keŋ pou dɔɔ.

eng: The disciples decided they would send support to the brothers and sisters in Judea, with everyone contributing to this ministry according to each persons abundance.
local: Mena dɔɔ kaseela baŋ yɛɛ ba agyueŋ nyi be man walaŋ kamasɛ waa fa kolo ɔ ke talebii man, na baa mo yelee be tebia Kristotena baŋ be kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ.

eng: They sent Barnabas and Saul to take this gift to the elders.
local: Te ampaŋ be yɛɛwɔ mena. Ba moo abɔɔ ŋan bɔɔ faa nɛ do Banabase na Sɔɔl nyiŋmaa man, te ba moowɔ gyina kegyiise baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ.

eng: About that time King Herod began to harass some who belonged to the church.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ man te gyoo Herode fiasɛɛ ɔ ne naase Kristotena baŋ baale diyem.

eng: He had James, Johns brother, killed with a sword.
local: Ɔ yela ba moo sila ko Gyɔn ɔ kegyia Gyemisi.

eng: When he saw that this pleased the Jews, he arrested Peter as well. This happened during the Festival of Unleavened Bread.
local: Ɔ kena nyi kpene keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ dana Gyudatena baŋ gyoŋ nɛ, te ɔ yela be kyaŋa Pita mɔ. Na ke yɛɛ Gyudatena wee dinaa keŋ bee di bodobodo keŋ tee keŋ ne yeli bodobodo na koro nɛ be doo ke man ya nɛ.

eng: He put Peter in prison, handing him over to four squads of soldiers, sixteen in all, who guarded him. He planned to charge him publicly after the Passover.
local: Ɔ ke kyaŋ Pita do deni man wɔle nɛ, ɔ moo e do yoonɔɔtena akpeŋ anaara nyiŋmaa man nyi baa gyee e. Yoonɔɔtena banaara doo la dekpeŋ kamasɛ man. Herode yɛɛ ke nyi akpaa be di ba nyeekelɛɛwee dinaa keŋ taŋ nɛ, na waa di ɔ nombia balaŋ nsana.

eng: While Peter was held in prison, the church offered earnest prayer to God for him.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɔ yela Pita doowɔ deni man botɔɔ, mɔna Kristotena dikpii keŋ ne fane nideli fa Wurubuarɛ ɔ dɔɔ.

eng: The night before Herod was going to bring Peters case forward, Peter was asleep between two soldiers and bound with two chains, with soldiers guarding the prison entrance.
local: Nelim keŋ pɛ tɛɛ kena, na gyoo Herode ka lese Pita lee deni man na baa di ɔ nombia nɛ, na ɔ doo yoonɔɔtena bala nsana keŋ bɔɔ do e agbaraagbaraase ala. Debaŋ kenaŋ na yoonɔɔtena baŋ bee gyee denanɔɔ keŋ mɔ seŋɛɛ be daseŋae man.

eng: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared and a light shone in the prison cell. After nudging Peter on his side to awaken him, the angel raised him up and said, Quick! Get up! The chains fell from his wrists.
local: Karatetee dokoloŋ na te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo bɛɛwɔ lee botɔɔ te deni keŋ man ŋmaŋeewɔ. Te ɔ kyaŋa Pita ɔ degbaŋgbaŋ dɔɔ wosi e te ɔ gyuusu e yako e nyi, Koro bileŋ. Debokenaŋ man te agbaraagbaraase ŋan leewɔ ɔ nyiŋmaase man yala.

eng: The angel continued, Get dressed. Put on your sandals. Peter did as he was told. The angel said, Put on your coat and follow me.
local: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon yako e nyiaa, Mo nya abɔɔ do na n ka do nyaŋ nkyokotase. Te Pita mɔ yɛɛwɔ mena te kpilale ŋon yako e bela nyi, mo n kegba wurikyim keŋ do na n ke sila maŋ.

eng: Following the angel, Peter left the prison. However, he didnt realize the angel had actually done all this. He thought he was seeing a vision.
local: Te Pita sila e lee debɔɔ man. Mɔna ɔ ta gyem nyi kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon ne yɛɛ nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ ya. Nawolo nyi ŋon gyaŋ na, yɛɛ e bo ŋgba dosɛɛ nɛ.

eng: They passed the first and second guards and came to the iron gate leading to the city. It opened for them by itself. After leaving the prison, they proceeded the length of one street, when abruptly the angel was gone.
local: Be tɛɛwɔ gbaa balaŋ gyaŋgba nyibaŋ baŋ bee gyee botɔɔ nɛ, na baŋ be gyakaa be dɔɔ nɛ, kaboena be ke lii nyeele denanɔɔ keŋ na mo lee donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ gyaŋ. Be kelii botɔɔ na, te nyeele denanɔɔ keŋ torowɔ fa wɔ ka wose dɔɔ, te ba leewɔ laŋ. Ba ke ta bee gyuu na, te kpilale ŋon ŋaalaawɔ yela Pita karatetee dokoloŋ.

eng: At that, Peter came to his senses and remarked, Now Im certain that the Lord sent his angel and rescued me from Herod and from everything the Jewish people expected.
local: Botɔɔ te Pita sia ŋmaŋee e te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Nɛnɛɛ, me ta gyem nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpila na ɔ kpilale nyi ɔ kaa lese maŋ lee gyoo Herode nyiŋmaa man, na ŋan pou nafɔ Gyudatena be bake see nyi baa yɛɛ maŋ nɛ.

eng: Realizing this, he made his way to Marys house. Many believers had gathered there and were praying.
local: Pita kena mena nombia kɛŋa nɛ, te ɔ gyuu Gyɔn Maake ɔ naa Mɛɛre dɛɛ. Debaŋ kenaŋ na balaŋ burum te yilaa botɔɔ bɛɛ fane.

eng: When Peter knocked at the outer gate, a female servant named Rhoda went to answer.
local: Pita ke gyɔ be disim keŋ te alebu ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Roda nɛ, gyuuwɔ ke kɛɛ nyi amɔte ne gyɔɔ laa.

eng: She was so overcome with joy when she recognized Peters voice that she didnt open the gate. Instead, she ran back in and announced that Peter was standing at the gate.
local: Ɔ ke gyu kenyii woya ŋan te ɔ ba gyem nyi Pita woya ŋenaŋ nɛ, yɛɛ e gyoŋ nideli te ɔ woleewɔ nyi waa toro e denanɔɔ keŋ. Mena dɔɔ ɔ yeuwɔ keyako wɔ nyiaa, Pita te kɔŋ ɔ seŋɛɛ denanɔɔ man.

eng: Youve lost your mind! they responded. She stuck by her story with such determination that they began to say, It must be his guardian angel.
local: Te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, Yaa n na gyoo diteŋ bo? Mɔna ɔ yase ɔ sia yako wɔ nyi yɛɛ ampaŋ. Keŋte be yakowɔ nyi gyae ke yɛɛ ɔ woo bo.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter remained outside, knocking at the gate. They finally opened the gate and saw him there, and they were astounded.
local: Na Pita gyakaa bo ɔ ne gyɔ denanɔɔ keŋ. Nɛnɛɛ be ka toro denanɔɔ keŋ na Pita seŋɛɛ laa, mena dɔɔ ba wose gbirisi wɔ.

eng: He gestured with his hand to quiet them down, then recounted how the Lord led him out of prison. He said, Tell this to James and the brothers and sisters. Then he left for another place.
local: Te Pita gyina ɔ nyiŋmaa adido mo yako wɔ nyi baa wu be nɔɔ. Te ɔ yako wɔ mena keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ba lese e lee deni keŋ man. Te ɔ yako wɔ nyi, Ɛ yako Gyemisi na Kristotena akaŋ baŋ mena nombia kɛŋa. Te ɔ korowɔ laŋ yenaŋ wɔle.

eng: The next morning the soldiers were flustered about what had happened to Peter.
local: Tɛɛ kena keŋ nɛ, te aŋmaareŋ dinaa daale gyoowɔ yoonɔɔtena baŋ nsana. Be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyi, Aleŋ yaa Pita moowɔ baa.

eng: Herod called for a thorough search. When Peter didnt turn up, Herod interrogated the guards and had them executed. Afterward, Herod left Judea in order to spend some time in Caesarea.
local: Gyoo Herode yela be kɛo Pita nideli, mɔna ba te naa e ya. Mena dɔɔ ɔ baake yoonɔɔtena baŋ bɔɔ gyee e nɛ, nyii be nɔɔman nombia, te ɔ yela ba koo wɔ pou. Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, gyoo Herode leewɔ Gyudia tɛɛle man gyu kekyaa Kaesaria donɔɔ man kyomii.

eng: Herod had been furious with the people of Tyre and Sidon for some time. They made a pact to approach him together, since their region depended on the kings realm for its food supply. They persuaded Blastus, the kings personal attendant, to join their cause, then appealed for an end to hostilities.
local: Gyoo Herode gyeŋa baŋ Taetena na Sidɔntena dɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ balaŋ benaŋ yɛɛ nɔɔ te be gyuuwɔ kena e. Be taŋgbɛɛwɔ mo nombia ŋenaŋ do Bilasituse nɔɔman nyi waa doo e teree fa wɔ, nawolo nyi donɔɔse ala ŋenaŋ pou ne nyiŋ be weenɛɛ bo lee gyoo ŋonaŋ tɛɛle man. Bilasituse yɛna gyoo ŋon dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ pou kegyia.

eng: On the scheduled day Herod dressed himself in royal attire, seated himself on the throne, and gave a speech to the people.
local: Wee keŋ bɔɔ see kelii nɛ, te gyoo Herode moo ɔ gyooro abɔɔ do, te ɔ gyuuwɔ ka gyeŋ gyakaa ɔ gyooro gbeli keŋ dɔɔ te ɔ kolosiwɔ fa balaŋ baŋ pou.

eng: Those assembled kept shouting, over and over, This is a gods voice, not the voice of a mere human.
local: Te balaŋ baŋ pou fae yeesa yako nyi, Kei dɔɔ te yɛɛ deniwalaŋ ne kolosi la ya, yɛɛ bane ŋolo e.

eng: Immediately an angel from the Lord struck Herod down, because he didnt give the honor to God. He was eaten by worms and died.
local: Debokenaŋ man te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpilale kɔŋawɔ kaa gyɔ e do kawee, nawolo nyi ɔ ta lese Wurubuarɛ yele ya. Te gyoo ŋon wose pou gyoo feelɛɛ te ɔ yeŋawɔ.

eng: Gods word continued to grow and increase.
