local: Nyi akpaa Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be gyuusu Yesu lee yeŋ man nɛ feliŋ doo ɛ man na, kenaŋ na waa ta ɔ feliŋ ŋonaŋ dɔɔ yela ɛ wosenaane kei keŋ gyae ka yeŋ nɛ ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ wɔle bela.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, we have an obligation, but it isnt an obligation to ourselves to live our lives on the basis of selfishness.
local: Mena dɔɔ me tebia, kpene keŋ doo fa daa yɛna nyi kaboena nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne gyae. Ɛ na tekaa yela dɛɛ yɛɛ kpene keŋ de dinɔɔ kum keŋ ne gyae nɛ ya.

eng: If you live on the basis of selfishness, you are going to die. But if by the Spirit you put to death the actions of the body, you will live.
local: Nawolo nyi akpaa ɛ yɛɛ kpene keŋ ɛ dinɔɔ kum keŋ ne gyae tɛɛle kei dɔɔ na ɛ ka yeŋ. Mɔna akpaa ɛ tɛɛ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon doŋ dɔɔ bɛɛ abɔɔ ŋan ɛmɛɛ gbagba ne gyae tɛɛle kei dɔɔ na, kenaŋ na ɛ ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaakekpaa.

eng: All who are led by Gods Spirit are Gods sons and daughters.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bee yeli Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon na wola wɔ gbɛɛ nɛ, baŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bia gbagba.

eng: You didnt receive a spirit of slavery to lead you back again into fear, but you received a Spirit that shows you are adopted as his children. With this Spirit, we cry, Abba, Father.
local: Nawolo nyi na feliŋ ŋon waa yela dɛɛ bese gbeŋa na dee yee gyakoloŋ nɛ te Wurubuarɛ faa daa ya, mɔna ɔ feliŋ ŋon waa yela dɛɛ bese Wurubuarɛ bia gbagba nɛ te ɔ faa daa. Feliŋ ŋonaŋ doŋ man dɔɔ te dɛɛ tale baake Wurubuarɛ nyi De kya Wurubuarɛ.

eng: The same Spirit agrees with our spirit, that we are Gods children.
local: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋonaŋ gbagba ne diina adansɛɛ fa daa, yela dɛɛ gyem de konɔɔse man nyi de yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bia gbagbawɔ.

eng: But if we are children, we are also heirs. We are Gods heirs and fellow heirs with Christ, if we really suffer with him so that we can also be glorified with him.
local: Nɛnɛɛ akpaa de yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bia gbagbawɔ na, kenaŋ na dee kpu na Kristo nyiŋ abɔɔ ŋan Wurubuarɛ be desina see fa ɔ balaŋ nɛ. Te akpaa daa mɔ de naa diyem ŋgba mena keŋ Kristo be naa diyem nɛ na, kenaŋ na Wurubuarɛ waa yela dee kpu na Kristo di gyoori ɔ gyoorobiiri keŋ man.

eng: I believe that the present suffering is nothing compared to the coming glory that is going to be revealed to us.
local: Akpaa ma to me sia kɛɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ ke doo ke daa na daa nɛ, na kaale keŋ dɛɛ kaa debaŋ kei nɛ te yɛɛ kolo ke ya.

eng: The whole creation waits breathless with anticipation for the revelation of Gods sons and daughters.
local: Tɛɛle kei na ka abɔɔ ŋan pou Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ nɛ, sia te yɛɛ ŋɛɛ daa wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa lese ɔ bia gbagba baŋ wola nɛ.

eng: Creation was subjected to frustration, not by its own choice—it was the choice of the one who subjected it—but in the hope.
local: Nawolo nyi tɛɛle kei na abɔɔ ŋan pou Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ nɛ ta koro yuŋ, mena dɔɔ ŋɔɔ gyae ke tale yɛɛ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ ŋa nyi ŋe dɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ ya. Te yɛɛ abɔɔ ŋenaŋ gbagba kegyaebii man yaa ŋe yɛɛwɔ mena ya, mɔna Wurubuarɛ yele la te ŋe kɔŋawɔ mena.

eng: that the creation itself will be set free from slavery to decay and brought into the glorious freedom of Gods children.
local: Mɔna kɛŋa pou wɔle na, Wurubuarɛ te fa daa loo kegyakaa nyi waa lɛɛ abɔɔ ŋenaŋ pou nyee na ŋe ke nyiŋ ŋa wose keŋ ŋɔɔ wɔlɛɛ leki ya. Na ŋan mɔ ke kpu na Wurubuarɛ bia ɔ gyoorobiiri keŋ man.

eng: We know that the whole creation is groaning together and suffering labor pains up until now.
local: Da gyeŋ nyi kaa lii na gyeŋ wee kei nɛ, tɛɛle kei na abɔɔ ŋan pou doo ke man nɛ na keketee ŋgba alo ŋon ɔ na doŋee nɛ.

eng: And its not only the creation. We ourselves who have the Spirit as the first crop of the harvest also groan inside as we wait to be adopted and for our bodies to be set free.
local: Na ŋan ŋaageŋ na keketee la ya. Mɔna daa baŋ dɔɔ nyiŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ kpene gyaŋgbate keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo fa daa nɛ, daa mɔ na keketee de konɔɔse man dɛɛ daa Wurubuarɛ ke lɛɛ da nyee lee de dinɔɔ kum kei man na waa yela dɛɛ bese ɔ bia.

eng: We were saved in hope. If we see what we hope for, that isnt hope. Who hopes for what they already see?.
local: Lee mena loo kegyakaa kei dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ lɛɛ da nyee. Nyi n loo gyakaa kpene keŋ n wulaa n dana ke nɛ dɔɔ na, kenaŋ na ka te yɛɛ loo kegyakaa ya. Nawolo nyi ŋolo be tale mo ɔ loo gyakaa kpene keŋ ɔ wulaa ɔ dana ke nɛ dɔɔ ya.

eng: But if we hope for what we dont see, we wait for it with patience.
local: Mɔna akpaa da mo de loo gyakaa kpene keŋ dɔɔ dana ke ya nɛ dɔɔ na, kenaŋ na dee nyiŋ konɔɔ kyaa daa ke.

eng: In the same way, the Spirit comes to help our weakness. We dont know what we should pray, but the Spirit himself pleads our case with unexpressed groans.
local: Mena mɔ te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne kyɔ daa de kaale man wee na wee lee da kefane man. Nawolo nyi dɔɔ gyeŋ mena keŋ dɛɛ fane na ke kaboena Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii ya. Mɔna ŋon Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ɔ doo de man nɛ, na seŋala de nawɔɔ man mo kakeketee dinaa keŋ ke taŋ keyako ya nɛ kolosi seŋ de wɔle.

eng: The one who searches hearts knows how the Spirit thinks, because he pleads for the saints, consistent with Gods will.
local: Wurubuarɛ gyeŋ kpene keŋ doo walaŋ kamasɛ konɔɔ man, te ɔ gyeŋ kpene kamasɛ keŋ mɔ doo ɔ feliŋ ŋon agyueŋ man. Nawolo nyi ɔ feliŋ ŋonaŋ seŋɛɛ la de nawɔɔ man ɔ ne fane fa daa Kristotena lee Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii man.

eng: We know that God works all things together for good for the ones who love God, for those who are called according to his purpose.
local: Da gyeŋ nyi balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ gyae Wurubuarɛ nɛ, nyi diyem man o, yaa gyoŋ man o, Wurubuarɛ ne yeli kpene kamasɛ ne gyu nideli fa wɔ. Baŋ yɛna balaŋ baŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ be baake wɔ, te ɔ ba mo wɔ yɛɛ ɔ bia gbagba ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be wulaa yako see nɛ.

eng: We know this because God knew them in advance, and he decided in advance that they would be conformed to the image of his Son. That way his Son would be the first of many brothers and sisters.
local: Nawolo nyi balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ wulaa ɔ gyeŋ nɛ, ɔ wulaa lese wɔ see na waa yela be dinɔɔ ke yɛɛ ŋgba ɔ bu ŋon wui nɛ. Na ɔ bu ŋon ke yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bia man pou bu kegyia.

eng: Those who God decided in advance would be conformed to his Son, he also called. Those whom he called, he also made righteous. Those whom he made righteous, he also glorified.
local: Baŋ Wurubuarɛ be wulaa lese wɔ see nɛ, baŋ te ɔ baakewɔ. Te baŋ mɔ ɔ be baake wɔ nɛ, ɔ lɛɛ wɔ do nyi be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ɔ siaman. Te baŋ ɔ be lɛɛ wɔ do nyi be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ɔ siaman nɛ, baŋ te ɔ yela ba bese gyoorobia.

eng: So what are we going to say about these things? If God is for us, who is against us?.
local: Kɛŋa pou wɔle na, sena te dɛɛ yako bela? Nyi akpaa Wurubuarɛ seŋɛɛ de wɔle na, amɔte ne gyae la waa tale koro seŋ tia daa?.

eng: He didnt spare his own Son but gave him up for us all. Wont he also freely give us all things with him?.
local: Ŋolo be kyaa ya. Nawolo nyi akpaa Wurubuarɛ te bɛɛse daa ŋon gbagba ɔ bu ya, mɔna ɔ sɛɛwɔ mo e fa nyi ɔ kaa yeŋ lɛɛ da nyee na, woŋ dɔɔ te ɔ ba mo abɔɔ ŋan pou ɔ be yako see nyi waa fa daa nɛ mɔ kpu daa ya?.

eng: Who will bring a charge against Gods elect people? It is God who acquits them.
local: Akpaa mena te doo na, amɔte ne gyae la waa tale bu balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ba lese wɔ besena ɔ balaŋ nɛ fɔɔ, keŋ nyi ŋon lɛɛ na wɔ do nyi be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ɔ siaman?.

eng: Who is going to convict them? It is Christ Jesus who died, even more, who was raised, and who also is at Gods right side. It is Christ Jesus who also pleads our case for us.
local: Amɔte ne gyae la waa tale bu wɔ fɔɔ? Kristo Yesu gba be gyae waa bu wɔ fɔɔ ya, nawolo nyi ŋon yɛna walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba yem fa daa. Na yeŋ kaageŋ gba te ɔ yeŋawɔ fa daa ya, mɔna Wurubuarɛ gyuusu e lee yeŋ man te ɔ kyaa Wurubuarɛ dunoluŋ dɔɔ ɔ na sola e fa daa.

eng: Who will separate us from Christs love? Will we be separated by trouble, or distress, or harassment, or famine, or nakedness, or danger, or sword?.
local: Woŋ ne gyae la ke tale tira daa lee kegyae keŋ Kristo ne gyae daa nɛ man? Diyem kena yaa kayeyɛɛ yaa kekɔla yaa diyem kenaase yaa tanam yaa diyem yaa gyakoloŋ nombia yaa yeŋ? Kolo be kyaa keŋ gyae ke tale tira daa na Kristo nsana ya.

eng: As it is written, We are being put to death all day long for your sake. We are treated like sheep for slaughter. .
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see yakowɔ yenaŋ daale nyiaa, Lee nyaŋ dɔɔ wee kamasɛ na bɛɛ gyae daa baa ko. Bɛɛ yɛɛ daa ŋgba namense baŋ bɛɛ wɔŋee wɔ be kaa ko nɛ.

eng: But in all these things we win a sweeping victory through the one who loved us.
local: Abɔɔ kɛŋa pou man na dee yoo lee, lee Yesu Kristo ŋon ɔ ne gyae daa nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: Im convinced that nothing can separate us from Gods love in Christ Jesus our Lord: not death or life, not angels or rulers, not present things or future things, not powers.
local: Nawolo nyi, ma gyeŋ nideli nyi kolo na kolo be kyaa keŋ ke gyae ke tale tira daa lee kegyae keŋ Kristo ne gyae daa nɛ man ya. Yeŋ yaa nyeedoŋ yaa Wurubuarɛ kpilala yaa feliŋkumɛɛ yaa abɔɔ ŋan ŋe kyaa debaŋ kei yaa ŋan ŋe gyae ke kɔŋ wɔle wɔle nɛ yaa doŋ wɔle daale koraŋ be gyae ke tale tira daa lee Kristo kegyae keŋ man ya.

eng: or height or depth, or any other thing that is created.
local: Mena mɔ te doŋse ŋan pou ŋe kyaa adido adido, na ŋan pou ŋe doo tɛɛle ateta ateta yaa kpene kamasɛ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, be gyae ke tale tira daa lee kegyae keŋ Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo dɔɔ gyae daa nɛ man ya.

eng: Im speaking the truth in Christ—Im not lying, as my conscience assures me with the Holy Spirit.
local: Nombii na anokoare yaa mee kolosi fa ŋon nɛ. Ka te yɛɛ dekara ya nawolo nyi me yɛɛ Kristo kpilale e. Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ɔ doo me man nɛ ne di maŋ adansɛɛ nyi me konɔɔ man nombii kei yɛɛ anokoare.

eng: I have great sadness and constant pain in my heart.
local: Dana maŋ waraŋase te na wee maŋ nideli, lee me tebia Gyudatena baŋ daa na wɔ yɛɛ fatabo dokoloŋ te bɔɔ bɛɛ nyi bɔɔ lɛɛ Kristo di ya nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: I wish I could be cursed, cut off from Christ if it helped my brothers and sisters, who are my flesh-and-blood relatives.
local: Nyi ke yɛɛ kpene keŋ ke gyae ke de nyi Wurubuarɛ waa falaa maŋ na daa na Kristo nsana ke tira, na me tebia Iseraetena kenyiŋ nyiŋ nyeedoŋ na nafɔ me gyae.

eng: They are Israelites. The adoption as Gods children, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the Law, the worship, and the promises belong to them.
local: Iseraetena yɛna balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ba lese wɔ te ɔ ba mo wɔ yɛɛ ɔ bia. Wurubuarɛ lese ɔ gyoorobiiri wola wɔ te ɔ yakowɔ see nyi waa hiraa wɔ. Ɔ moo ɔ mmaraase ŋan fa wɔ, te ɔ wola wɔ gbɛɛ kpaakpaate keŋ baa mo som e. Kɛŋa wɔle na Wurubuarɛ besewɔ yako see nyi, waa mo abɔɔ kpaakpaa burum ŋaale mɔ kpu wɔ.

eng: The Jewish ancestors are theirs, and the Christ descended from those ancestors. He is the one who rules over all things, who is God, and who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Iseraetena kɛwɔ man te Wurubuarɛ balaŋ tuturu baŋ bɔɔ kyaa kaalaŋ nɛ leewɔ. Kalola man na, Yesu Kristo mɔ lee Iseraetena duuluŋ man te ŋon yɛna kpene kamasɛ nyee. Wurubuarɛ te kaboena nyi baa lese ɔ yele kekpaakekpaa Amɛn.

eng: But its not as though Gods word has failed. Not all who are descended from Israel are part of Israel.
local: Kei bɛɛ wolo nyi abɔɔ ŋan Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa yɛɛ fa Iseraetena nɛ, ɔ te yɛɛ kina ya. Nawolo nyi na balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ lee Iserae nɛ yɛna Wurubuarɛ balaŋ gbagba ya.

eng: Not all of Abrahams children are called Abrahams descendants, but instead your descendants will be named through Isaac. .
local: Mena mɔ te na balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ lee Aberaham duuluŋ man nɛ yɛna Aberaham bia gbagba Wurubuarɛ siaman ya. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ yako Aberaham nyiaa, Asiki man te n duuluŋ man balaŋ gbagba baŋ baa lee.

eng: That means it isnt the natural children who are Gods children, but it is the children from the promise who are counted as descendants.
local: Kei nawolo baa nyiaa, na bia baŋ pou Aberaham ba lolo nɛ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bia gbagba ya. Mɔna baŋ Wurubuarɛ be yako see pɛte Aberaham ba lola wɔ nɛ, baŋ yɛna Aberaham duuluŋ man balaŋ gbagba.

eng: The words in the promise were: A year from now I will return, and Sarah will have a son .
local: Nawolo nyi nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ be yako see fa Aberaham yɛna nyiaa, Ŋaale kala debaŋ kei me kaa bese kɔŋ na, n wɛɛle Saara ta lola bu baale.

eng: Not only that, but also Rebecca conceived children with one man, our ancestor Isaac.
local: Na nombii kei kaageŋ ya. Mɔna Rebeka lola bia baala bala, te be popou be kya yɛna de naana Asiki.

eng: When they hadnt been born yet and when they hadnt yet done anything good or bad, it was shown that Gods purpose would continue because it was based on his choice.
local: Pɛte Rebeka ka lola bia kɛwɔ na be kaa bee gyeŋ kolo kpaakpaa na kolo kum nɛ, na Wurubuarɛ wulaa yako ba wose man nombia see nyiaa, Kegyia ŋon waa kaa som kemaasa ŋon. Kpene keŋ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ mena yɛna nyi, ɔ ne gyae balaŋ ka gyeŋ nyi te yɛɛ nyi ŋolo tom keŋ ɔ ne yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ te ŋon Wurubuarɛ na lese ɔtenate mo e yɛɛ ɔ wui ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ na lese walaŋ bo lee ŋon gbagba kegyaebii man.

eng: As it is written, I loved Jacob, but I hated Esau .
local: Mena ke te be yakowɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man nyiaa, Gyekɔpo te ma lesewɔ, te me tinaa Esawu yela.

eng: So what are we going to say? Isnt this unfair on Gods part? Absolutely not!.
local: Nɛnɛɛ sena yaa dɛɛ yako lee nombia kɛŋa man? Kei nawolo baa nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia be doo na ke gbɛɛ yee? Korakora, be doo mena ya.

eng: He says to Moses, Ill have mercy on whomever I choose to have mercy, and Ill show compassion to whomever I choose to show compassion .
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ wulaa yako Mosesi leenaŋ gyaŋgba gyaŋgba keŋ nyiaa, Balaŋ baŋ mɛɛ gyae nyi maa wii be waraŋase na, baŋ te maa wii be waraŋase. Te balaŋ baŋ mɛɛ gyae nyi maa nyiŋ konɔɔ fa wɔ na, baŋ te maa nyiŋ konɔɔ fa.

eng: So then, it doesnt depend on a persons desire or effort. It depends entirely on God, who shows mercy.
local: Mena dɔɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ da lese ŋolo na, te yɛɛ nyi ɔtenate kegyaebii yaa ɔ tom keŋ ɔ ne yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ ya, mɔna keŋ Wurubuarɛ gbagba be wii ɔtenate waraŋase dɔɔ te ɔ lese e.

eng: Scripture says to Pharaoh, I have put you in this position for this very thing: so I can show my power in you and so that my name can be spread through the entire earth. .
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ yakowɔ ɔ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man lee Igyipite gyoo Faaro wose man nyiaa, Kpene keŋ dɔɔ te mɔɔ see neŋ gyoori yɛna nyi, mɛɛ gyae nyi maa ta n dɔɔ lese me doŋ wola na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou kenyiŋ nyii ma yele.

eng: So then, God has mercy on whomever he wants to, but he makes resistant whomever he wants to.
local: Mena dɔɔ da te naa nyi akpaa Wurubuarɛ ne gyae nyi waa wii balaŋ waraŋase na ɔ ne wii be waraŋase, te akpaa ɔ ne gyae nyi balaŋ ke yɛɛ konɔɔyuŋ na ɔ ne yeli bɛɛ yɛɛ konɔɔyuŋ.

eng: So you are going to say to me, Then why does he still blame people? Who has ever resisted his will.
local: Ɛ man ŋolo waa tale bɔɔse nyiaa, Nyi akpaa mena te doo na, weera dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ ne bu balaŋ fɔɔ lee be nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ dɔɔ? Te amɔte ne gyae la waa tale kyɛɛkee Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii.

eng: You are only a human being. Who do you think you are to talk back to God? Does the clay say to the potter, Why did you make me like this? .
local: Mɛɛ bɔɔse ŋon baa nyiaa, deniwalaŋ yɛna amɔte keŋ waa tale ŋon na Wurubuarɛ ke wɔŋee nombia? Osuŋ gyae ke tale bɔɔse walaŋ ŋon ɔ be ma ke nɛ nyi, Weera dɔɔ te n maa maŋ nnɛ.

eng: Doesnt the potter have the power over the clay to make one pot for special purposes and another for garbage from the same lump of clay?.
local: Kei be tale kɔŋ mena ya, nawolo nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne ma osuŋse nɛ waa tale mo tɛɛle degbaŋ dokoloŋ ma osuŋ kpaakpaate. Mena mɔ te waa tale mo tɛɛle degbaŋ dokoloŋ kenaŋ ma osuŋ yakaate.

eng: What if God very patiently puts up with pots made for wrath that were designed for destruction, because he wanted to show his wrath and to make his power known?.
local: Mena ke te Wurubuarɛ mɔ doo. Nafɔ ɔ ne gyae nyi waa lese ɔ baŋ wola na balaŋ kena nyi ɔ dana doŋ. Mɔna ɔ ne nyiŋ konɔɔ nideli fa baŋ ɔ ba gyeŋ baŋ be dɔɔ nɛ. Nafɔ kaboena nyi waa wɔlɛɛ mena balaŋ benaŋ kekpaakekpaa.

eng: What if he did this to make the wealth of his glory known toward pots made for mercy, which he prepared in advance for glory?.
local: Wurubuarɛ nyiŋa konɔɔ fa daa baŋ ɔ ne gyae nyi waa nyiŋ konɔɔ fa nɛ, nawolo nyi ɔ ne gyae nyi waa lese ɔ gyoorobiiri dinaa keŋ wola balaŋ baŋ ɔ be wulaa lese wɔ see nyi baa kaa di gyoori kpu na e nɛ.

eng: We are the ones God has called. We dont come only from the Jews but we also come from the Gentiles.
local: Mena balaŋ benaŋ yɛna daa baŋ ɔ be baake nɛ. Te na de popou mɔ yɛna Gyudatena ya, mɔna baale mɔ lee yenaase.

eng: As it says also in Hosea, I will call my people those who arent my people, and the one who isnt well loved, I will call loved one. .
local: Wurubuarɛ yako mena nombia kɛŋa ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Hosea tɔne man nyiaa, Balaŋ baŋ nafɔ ba te yɛɛ me balaŋwɔ ya nɛ, maa baake wɔ nyi me balaŋ. Te balaŋ baŋ nafɔ mɔɔ ne gyae wɔ ya nɛ, baa kaa yɛɛ me balaŋ baŋ mɛɛ gyae.

eng: And in the place where it was said to them, You arent my people, there they will be called the living Gods children .
local: Mena mɔ te, Botɔɔ keŋ nafɔ bɛɛ yako wɔ nyi ba te yɛɛ me balaŋwɔ ya nɛ, botɔɔtena te baa kaa baake nyi Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ bia.

eng: But Isaiah cries out for Israel, Though the number of Israels children will be like the sand of the sea, only a remaining part will be saved, .
local: Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya mɔ gyɔɔ dekpeŋkpeŋ yako lee Iseraetena wose man nyiaa, Doo nyi Iseraetena boe ŋgba ɛpo nɔɔman aŋasa nɛ, mɔna be man balaŋ kyomii baale te Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ ba nyee.

eng: because the Lord does what he says completely and quickly. .
local: Nawolo nyi be gyae ke kyare ya, de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ waa di tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou nombia karatetee dokoloŋ.

eng: As Isaiah prophesied, If the Lord of the heavenly forces had not left descendants for us, we would have been like Sodom, and we would have become like Gomorrah. .
local: Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya mɔ yako mena nombii kei kaalaŋ nyiaa, Nafɔ Wurubuarɛ doŋte ŋon deŋ te kaase de duuluŋ man balaŋ kyomii baale see ya nɛ, nafɔ de popou deŋ munuwɔ ŋgba mena keŋ Sodom na Gomora donɔɔse na ŋe man balaŋ pou be munu nɛ.

eng: So what are we going to say? Gentiles who werent striving for righteousness achieved righteousness, the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ kpene keŋ mɛɛ gyae maa yako yɛna nyi balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, nafɔ ba te yase be sia nyi baa yela be gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman ya. Mɔna lee ba kelɛɛdi dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ lɛɛ wɔ do yela be gbɛɛneŋ teneewɔ ɔ siaman.

eng: But though Israel was striving for a Law of righteousness, they didnt arrive.
local: Te Iseraetena dɔɔ baŋ yase be sia be silana Mosesi mmaraase ŋan, na ŋa ke yela be gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman. Mɔna be yɛe kina keŋ be gbɛɛneŋ ta tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman ya.

eng: Why? Its because they didnt go for it by faith but they went for it as if it could be reached by doing something. They have tripped over a stumbling block.
local: Weera dɔɔ te be yɛɛwɔ kina? Nawolo nyi ba te kɛɛ ke nyi Wurubuarɛ kelɛɛdi ne gyae la ke yela be gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman ya. Baŋ gyeŋ nyi be nombia ayɛɛsa ne gyae la ke yela be gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman. Lee kei dɔɔ ba te yɛɛ ŋgba balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyoo na boe dinaa nɛ.

eng: As it is written: Look! Im putting a stumbling block in Zion, which is a rock that offends people. And the one who has faith in him will not be put to shame. .
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see yako boe kei nombia nyiaa, Kɛɛ, me ta mo boe dinaa daale see Siyɔn donɔɔ man. Balaŋ baa gyoo na boe kei kpaŋte, na baa gyoo na ke yala. Mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ mo ɔ wose lɔ walaŋ ŋon boe kei seŋɛɛ fa e nɛ dɔɔ na, desɛŋ be gyae ke di e ya.

eng: Brothers and sisters, my hearts desire is for Israels salvation. Thats my prayer to God for them.
local: Me tebia, kpene keŋ mɛɛ gyae me konɔɔ man te mɛɛ fane ke dɔɔ yɛna nyi, Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ me tebia Iseraetena pou nyee.

eng: I can vouch for them: they are enthusiastic about God. However, it isnt informed by knowledge.
local: Maa tale di ba wose man adansɛɛ nyi bɛɛ yase be sia lee Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan dɔɔ nideli. Mɔna be sia keyase keŋ bɛɛ kyaa na Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛneŋ ya.

eng: They dont submit to Gods righteousness because they dont understand his righteousness, and they try to establish their own righteousness.
local: Nawolo nyi bɔɔ gyeŋ gbɛɛ keŋ Wurubuarɛ na mo yela balaŋ gbɛɛneŋ na tenee ɔ siaman ya, te baŋ gbagba yase be sia be silana gbɛɛneŋ ŋan baŋ gyeŋ nyi ŋe gyae ke yela be gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ. Lee mena dɔɔ ba te yɔkɔse ba wose na baa sila Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛ keŋ ne yeli balaŋ gbɛɛneŋ na tenɛɛ ɔ siaman nɛ ya.

eng: Christ is the goal of the Law, which leads to righteousness for all who have faith in God.
local: Kristo yɛna mmaraase ŋan pou nyee, te ɔ kekɔŋ dɔɔ mmaraase ŋan kesila be dana tɔnɔɔ bela ya. Lee kei dɔɔ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ e di na, ɔtenate gbɛɛneŋ gyae ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman.

eng: Moses writes about the righteousness that comes from the Law: The person who does these things will live by them. .
local: De naana Mosesi ŋmarasewɔ lee mena keŋ deniwalaŋ waa sila mmaraase ŋan na ɔ gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ nyiaa, Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne di mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ na ɔ gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ, akpaa ɔ kyaŋ ɔ wose di ŋe dɔɔ nideli keŋ ɔ te yale ŋe man dokoloŋ dɔɔ ya nɛ, kenaŋ na waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.

eng: But the righteousness that comes from faith talks like this: Dont say in your heart , Who will go up into heaven? .
local: Mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ Yesu di te lee ɔ kelɛɛdi dɔɔ ɔ gbɛɛneŋ da tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ, na ɔ na wolo baa nyi ta tekaa yɛɛ nyi ŋolo ke bɔɔse nyi, Amɔte ne gyae la waa gyeŋ gyu adido na ɔ kaa mo Kristo kɔŋ ya.

eng: or Who will go down into the region below? .
local: Yaa ŋolo mɔ ke bɔɔse nyi, Amɔte ne gyae la waa tisi gyu tɛɛle ateta na ɔ kaa gyuusu Kristo lee woya man kɔŋ ya.

eng: But what does it say? The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart .
local: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ nombia ne yako lee balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di, te lee ba kelɛɛdi dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ be lɛɛ wɔ do nyi be gbɛɛneŋ tenee ŋon Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ wose man nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan benaa na ŋon. Ŋe doo ɛ nɔɔse man, te ŋe doo ɛ konɔɔse man mɔ. Nombia ŋenaŋ yɛna kelɛɛdi nombia ŋan dɛɛ yako nɛ.

eng: Because if you confess with your mouth Jesus is Lord and in your heart you have faith that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
local: Mena dɔɔ nyi akpaa n gyuusu n nɔɔ yako balaŋ man nyi, Yesu yɛna de Gbeŋgyoo te n lɛɛ di n konɔɔ man nyi Wurubuarɛ gyuusu e lee yeŋ man na, Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ n nyee.

eng: Trusting with the heart leads to righteousness, and confessing with the mouth leads to salvation.
local: Nawolo nyi walaŋ konɔɔ man te ɔ ne lɛɛ di te Wurubuarɛ ne lɛɛ ɔtenate do nyi ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ŋon Wurubuarɛ siaman. Walaŋ nɔɔ mɔ te ɔ na mo yako nyi Yesu yɛna ɔ Gbeŋgyoo te Wurubuarɛ ne lɛɛ ɔtenate nyee.

eng: The scripture says, All who have faith in him wont be put to shame .
local: Mena mɔ te Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyi, Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ Kristo di na, desɛŋ be gyae ke kyaŋ e korakora ya.

eng: There is no distinction between Jew and Greek, because the same Lord is Lord of all, who gives richly to all who call on him.
local: N yɛɛ Gyuda baale e o, n te yɛɛ Gyuda baale e ya o, de popou yɛɛ dokoloŋ Wurubuarɛ siaman. Nawolo nyi Kristo ŋonaŋ yɛna de popou de Gbeŋgyoo. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ wii baake e na, waa mo wa abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan boele e.

eng: All who call on the Lords name will be saved. .
local: Nawolo nyi be yakowɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man nyiaa, Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ wii baake de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ na waa lɛɛ ɔ nyee.

eng: So how can they call on someone they dont have faith in? And how can they have faith in someone they havent heard of? And how can they hear without a preacher?.
local: Te sena yaa balaŋ baa tale wii baake de Gbeŋgyoo ŋon bɔɔ ne lɛɛ e di ya nɛ yele na waa lɛɛ ba nyee? Yaa akpaa be te nyii ta ɔ wose man nombia ya na, sena te baa tale lɛɛ e di? Te akpaa ŋolo mɔ te kolosi ɔ wose man nombia fa wɔ ya na, sena te baa yɛɛ na baa nyii ŋa?.

eng: And how can they preach unless they are sent? As it is written, How beautiful are the feet of those who announce the good news. .
local: Te akpaa Wurubuarɛ te kpila balaŋ nyi baa gyu ke yako ɔ wose man nombia ŋenaŋ ya na, sena te baa yɛɛ na baa tale gyu ke yako ŋa? Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyiaa, Akpaa balaŋ ne kɔŋ be kaa kolosi nyeedoŋ nombia ŋan na, ba keta na mo nombia kpaakpaa bo kɔŋ.

eng: But everyone hasnt obeyed the good news. As Isaiah says, Lord, who has had faith in our message? .
local: Mɔna na baŋ pou bɔɔ nyii nombia kpaakpaa ŋan nɛ lɛɛ na ŋa di ya. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya yakowɔ nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, amɔte lɛɛ na de nombia ŋan dɔɔ yako nɛ di.

eng: So, faith comes from listening, but its listening by means of Christs message.
local: Mena dɔɔ lee Kristo nyeedoŋ nombia ŋan keyako man te balaŋ ne nyii ŋa. Mena mɔ te lee Kristo nombia ŋenaŋ kenyii man te balaŋ ne lɛɛ ŋa di.

eng: But I ask you, didnt they hear it? Definitely! Their voice has gone out into the entire earth, and their message has gone out to the corners of the inhabited world. .
local: Mɔna ɛ yela maa bɔɔse ŋon nyiaa, mena balaŋ kɛwɔ te nyii Kristo nombia ŋan baa yee? Kenyii dɔɔ be te nyii, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyiaa, Nombia ŋan te kili gyu tɛɛle kei dɔɔ pou. Te deniwalaŋ kamasɛ mɔ te nyii nombia ŋenaŋ.

eng: But I ask you again, didnt Israel understand? First, Moses says, I will make you jealous of those who arent a people, of a people without understanding. .
local: Ɛ yela maa bɔɔse ŋon bela nyiaa, mena dɔɔ ɛ ne yako baa nyi Iseraetena baŋ bɔɔ nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, be te nyii ŋe man yee? Kenyii dɔɔ be nyiiwɔ. Mosesi yɛna walaŋ gyaŋgbate ŋon ɔ be tira mena nombia kɛŋa nɔɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa, Maa mo balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ do ɛmɛɛ Gyudatena sibiiyam. Maa mo balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ daale lee ma wose man ya nɛ do ŋon baŋ.

eng: And Isaiah even dares to say, I was found by those who didnt look for me; I revealed myself to those who didnt ask for me. .
local: Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya mɔ gyɔɔ ɔ konɔɔ yako nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa, Balaŋ baŋ ba te yase be sia kɛo maŋ ya nɛ, baŋ naa na maŋ. Te balaŋ baŋ mɔ bɔɔ ne bɔɔse me dɔɔ ya nɛ, baŋ te maa lese ma wose wola.

eng: But he says about Israel, All day long I stretched out my hands to a disobedient and contrary people. .
local: Mɔna Iseraetena baŋ dɔɔ Yesaya yakowɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ kpa, Wee na wee na mɛɛ kɛɛ gbɛɛ nyi baa kaa lɛɛ maŋ di na maa lɛɛ ba nyee, mɔna ba te yɛɛ deŋele doŋ koro seŋ tia maŋ.

eng: So I ask you, has God rejected his people? Absolutely not! Im an Israelite, a descendant of Abraham, from the tribe of Benjamin.
local: Nyi akpaa nnɛ te nombia kɛŋa doo na, ɛ yela maa bɔɔse ŋon bela nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ bɛɛ ŋon gbagba ɔ balaŋ baŋ baa yaa? Korakora, ɔ te bɛɛ wɔ ya. Nawolo nyi maŋ Pɔɔl gbagba yɛɛ Iserae baale e. Ma lee Aberaham duuluŋ man, te ma lee Bɛngyamɛn dekpaŋalaŋ man.

eng: God hasnt rejected his people, whom he knew in advance. Or dont you know what the scripture says in the case of Elijah, when he pleads with God against Israel?.
local: Te yɛɛ nyi Wurubuarɛ bɛɛ ɔ balaŋ baŋ ɔ wulaa ɔ gyeŋ wɔ, te ɔ be wulaa lese wɔ see lee tɛɛle kei kewalaŋ nɛ bo ya. Ɛ tɔɔse mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan be yako lee Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Elia wose man.

eng: Lord, they have killed your prophets, and they have torn down your altars. Im the only one left, and they are trying to take my life. .
local: Elia moo Iseraetena nombiakumɛɛ kaa see Wurubuarɛ siaman te ɔ yako e nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo, Iseraetena ta koe nya akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ pou te be yɛlɛɛ n kedeesa boya ŋan pou. Maŋ maageŋ kooŋ kaa la, te bɛɛ kɛo maŋ gba koraŋ baa ko.

eng: But what is Gods reply to him? I have kept for myself seven thousand people who havent bowed their knees to Baal. .
local: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ tiranɔɔ fa Elia nyiaa, Koa, na nyaŋ nyaageŋ kaa la ya. Nawolo nyi me ta lese balaŋ kakpoŋse nyetooro see fa ma wose, keŋ be te buŋa ta som bane ŋon bɛɛ baake nyi Baal nɛ korakora ya.

eng: So also in the present time there is a remaining group by the choice of Gods grace.
local: Mena mɔ te gyɛŋ wee kei nɛ, Wurubuarɛ te tɛɛ ɔ bɛrɛɛ keŋ dɔɔ lese balaŋ akalansɛɛ baale see fa ɔ wose.

eng: But if it is by grace, it isnt by whats done anymore. If it were, Gods grace wouldnt be grace.
local: Akpaa Wurubuarɛ tɛe ɔ bɛrɛɛ keŋ dɔɔ lese balaŋ na, kenaŋ na te yɛɛ nyi ɔ tɛe ba tom keŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ lese wɔ ya. Nyi ŋon tɛe ba tom keŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ lese wɔ nɛ, kenaŋ na ɔ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ta tekaa yɛɛ bɛrɛɛ gbagba ya.

eng: So what? Israel didnt find what it was looking for. Those who were chosen found it, but the others were resistant.
local: Nɛnɛɛ sena yaa dɛɛ yako bela? Kpene keŋ Iseraetena be yase be sia kɛo nɛ, be te nyiŋ ke ya. Mɔna balaŋ akalansɛɛ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ba lese wɔ see nɛ, baŋ nyiŋa na ke. Balaŋ akaŋ baŋ dɔɔ, baŋ yɛɛ deŋele doŋ bo.

eng: As it is written, God gave them a dull spirit, so that their eyes would not see and their ears not hear, right up until the present day. .
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see yako mena nombii kei nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ te yeli ba nyeeya ta yem, mena dɔɔ bɔɔ ne nyii nombia ŋan man ya. Te kaa lii na gyɛŋ wee kei nɛ, be dana sia mɔna bɔɔ ne naa ya. Be dana deŋela, mɔna bɔɔ ne nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan man ya.

eng: And David says, Their table should become a pitfall and a trap, a stumbling block and payback to them for what they have done. .
local: Gyoo Defidi mɔ sola Wurubuarɛ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ yela be dedii ka bese ba deyalae. Yela baa naa diyem tɔ ba dukum kom.

eng: Their eyes should be darkened so they cant see, and their backs always bent. .
local: Yela be sia ke yɛlɛɛ na baa ta kpaŋte, na baa kyaa kaale man debaŋ kamasɛ.

eng: So Im asking you: They havent stumbled so that theyve fallen permanently, have they? Absolutely not! But salvation has come to the Gentiles by their failure, in order to make Israel jealous.
local: Ɛ yela maa bɔɔse ŋon bela nyiaa, ke yɛɛ ampaŋ nyi Iseraetena baŋ kpaŋtewɔ yala keŋ be ta bese tale koro yee? Koa, na mena ya. Mɔna kelɛɛdi keŋ ba te lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di ya nɛ, keŋ toro na gbɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ, na Wurubuarɛ ke lɛɛ ba nyee na ke nyiŋ do Iseraetena benaŋ sibiiyam.

eng: But if their failure brings riches to the world, and their defeat brings riches to the Gentiles, how much more will come from the completion of their number!.
local: Nombiakumɛɛ ŋan Iseraetena be yɛɛ nɛ, keŋ torona gbɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ te Wurubuarɛ hiraa wɔ. Iseraetena benaŋ keyala be Wurubuarɛ ɔsom man nɛ, ka moo tɔnɔɔ burum kɔna balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ. Mena dɔɔ akpaa Iseraetena baŋ de kaa nyiŋ Wurubuarɛ nyeekelɛɛ na, kenaŋ na tɔnɔɔ keŋ balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ baa nyiŋ na, ka te yɛɛ nsendee ya.

eng: Im speaking to you Gentiles. Considering that Im an apostle to the Gentiles, I publicize my own ministry.
local: Nɛnɛɛ ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ ɛ tei nombii kei. Me yɛɛ Kristo kpilale e te ŋon kpila na maŋ ɛ gyaŋ nyi me kaa kolosi ɔ nombia ŋan fa ŋon. Mena dɔɔ mɔɔ na mo ma tom kei do nsendee ya.

eng: in the hope that somehow I might make my own people jealous and save some of them.
local: Nawolo nyi me loo gyakaa nyi me kyɔ ŋon na, gyae ka do me tebia Gyudatena baŋ sibiiyam na ke yela Wurubuarɛ ke lɛɛ ba nyee.

eng: If their rejection has brought about a close relationship between God and the world, how can their acceptance mean anything less than life from the dead?.
local: Debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba besena ɔ wɔle tee Iseraetena nɛ, ke yela balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ kaa bese Wurubuarɛ ɔ gyoona. Nyi akpaa mena te doo nɛ, kenaŋ na debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa kaa lɛɛ Iseraetena baŋ do na, gyae ke yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ gyoŋ ŋgba be foŋae lee yeŋ man nɛ.

eng: But if part of a batch of dough is offered to God as holy, the whole batch of dough is holy too. If a root is holy, the branches will be holy too.
local: Akpaa n dana kolo te n lese ke man ŋaale deesi fa Wurubuarɛ na, kenaŋ na nawolo baa nyi kpene kenaŋ pou yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ wui. Mena mɔ te akpaa daŋ lila yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ wui na, kenaŋ na nawolo baa nyi daŋ kenaŋ pou yɛɛ ɔ wui.

eng: If some of the branches were broken off, and you were a wild olive branch, and you were grafted in among the other branches and shared the root that produces the rich oil of the olive tree.
local: Gyudatena yɛɛ ŋgba oliifi daŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ gbagba be duu nɛ. Te balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, baŋ yɛɛ yaa ŋgba ŋale man oliifi daŋ nɛ. Mena dɔɔ yɛɛ yaa ŋgba Wurubuarɛ te kara oliifi daŋ keŋ ɔ be duu nɛ tombia ŋaale leki, te ɔ ke kara ŋale man oliifi daŋ keŋ tombia ka towaa na ke nɛ. Kei nawolo baa nyi ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ ke nyiŋ doŋ nawolo nyi ɛ ke nyiŋ loŋ lee oliifi daŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ gbagba be duu nɛ man.

eng: then dont brag like youre better than the other branches. If you do brag, be careful: its not you that sustains the root, but its the root that sustains you.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na gyɔ ɛ konɔɔ nyi ɛ kela tombia ŋan bɔɔ kare leki te bɔɔ mo ŋon towaa nɛ ya. Nawolo nyi na daŋ tombia danaana ke ka seŋɛɛ ya, mɔna ke lila danaana ke ka seŋɛɛ.

eng: You will say then, Branches were broken off so that I could be grafted in.
local: Mberem na ɛ ke yako nyi, Be keretii tombia ŋan bo leki na daa baŋ da te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, ke nyiŋ gbɛɛ kaa towaa botɔɔ ne dee nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.

eng: Fine. They were broken off because they werent faithful, but you stand only by your faithfulness. So dont think in a proud way; instead be afraid.
local: Kei yɛɛ ampaŋ. Keŋ ba te lɛɛ di dɔɔ ya te Wurubuarɛ keretii wɔ leki, te keŋ ɛmɛɛ be kaa lɛɛ di dɔɔ te ɛ kaa seŋawɔ be nawɔɔ man. Mena dɔɔ ɛ na wola ɛ wose ya, mɔna ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose dɔɔ nideli.

eng: If God didnt spare the natural branches, he wont spare you either.
local: Nawolo nyi Iseraetena baŋ be yɛɛ tombia gbagbawɔ koraŋ nɛ, be yɛɛ dukum na Wurubuarɛ te tina wɔ yela ya, mɔna ɔ wɔŋa ba deŋele. Mena dɔɔ akpaa ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ yɛɛ dukum na, ɔ be gyae waa tina ŋon yela ya.

eng: So look at Gods kindness and harshness. Its harshness toward those who fell, but its Gods kindness for you, provided you continue in his kindness; otherwise, you could be cut off too.
local: N kɛɛ nombii kei na, dɛɛ tale naa nyi Wurubuarɛ dana wɔe nideli te ɔ nombia mɔ dana doŋ nideli. Ɔ nombia dana doŋ fa baŋ bɔɔ ne yɛɛ ɔ kegyaebii ya nɛ, mɔna ɛmɛɛ balaŋ baŋ ɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ te ɛ dɔŋ ɔ gbɛɛneŋ man keŋ ɛ te tina yela ya na, waa lese ɔ wɔe wola ŋon. Na mena ya na waa keretii ɛmɛɛ mɔ leki.

eng: And even those who were cut off will be grafted back in if they dont continue to be unfaithful, because God is able to graft them in again.
local: Te akpaa Iseraetena baŋ bɔɔ tina ba kelɛɛdi yela nɛ da bese kɔŋ kaa lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di na, kenaŋ na waa besena wɔ kaa see be daseŋae bene keŋ man. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ waa tale mo wɔ kaa towaa na oliifi daŋ keŋ bela.

eng: If you were naturally part of a wild olive tree and you were cut off from it, and then, contrary to nature, you were grafted into the cultivated olive tree, wont these natural branches stand an even better chance of being grafted back onto their own olive tree?.
local: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ, ɛ yɛɛ ŋgba ŋale man oliifi daŋ tombia ŋan bɔɔ kare mo kaa towaa na oliifi daŋ gbagba keŋ nɛ. N kɛɛ na nafɔ bɔɔ ne kara daŋ daale tombii kaa towaa na daale ya. Mɔna Iseraetena baŋ dɔɔ, baŋ yɛna oliifi daŋ gbagba keŋ bɔɔ kara ke tombia leki nɛ. Mena dɔɔ be gyae ke yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ doŋ nyi waa bese mo tombia ŋenaŋ kaa towaa na ke naa keŋ ya.

eng: I dont want you to be unaware of this secret, brothers and sisters. That way you wont think too highly of yourselves. A part of Israel has become resistant until the full number of the Gentiles comes in.
local: Me tebia, nombii daale weesɛɛ keŋ mɛɛ gyae ɛ ke nyii ke man. Akpaa ɛ nyii ke man na, be gyae ke yela ɛ ke yɛɛ ya agyueŋ nyi ɛ gyeŋ nombia gbaa ya. Nombii kenaŋ yɛna nyi Iseraetena dana deŋele doŋ, mɔna te yɛɛ nyi baa yɛɛ deŋele doŋ kenaŋ kekpaakekpaa ya. Baa yɛɛ deŋele doŋ kenaŋ bo kelii debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa kaa lɛɛ balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ yaanɔɔle keŋ ɔ ne gyae nɛ nyee pɛ.

eng: In this way, all Israel will be saved, as it is written: The deliverer will come from Zion. He will remove ungodly behavior from Jacob. .
local: Dekɔŋ mena na Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ Iseraetena pou nyee ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nɛ nyiaa, Siyɔn donɔɔ man te nyeelɛɛre ŋon waa lee kɔŋ, na ɔ kaa lese nombia nkaraŋkaraŋ pou lee Iseraetena man.

eng: This is my covenant with them, when I take away their sins .
local: Akpaa nyi ma lese nombiakumɛɛ pou lee be dɔɔ taŋ na, keŋ ne gyae la ka wola nyi nɔɔkebakesee keŋ doo daa na wɔ nsana nɛ te kɔŋ mena.

eng: According to the gospel, they are enemies for your sake, but according to Gods choice, they are loved for the sake of their ancestors.
local: Keŋ Iseraetena te lɛɛ nyeedoŋ nombia ŋan di dɔɔ ya, te be kaa bese balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɔla Wurubuarɛ. Mɔna kei kaa yɛɛ tɔnɔɔ fa ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ. Te keŋ be yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ be wulaa lese wɔ see dɔɔ, mena dɔɔ ɔ ne gyae be nombia lee be naanaɔ dɔɔ.

eng: Gods gifts and calling cant be taken back.
local: Nawolo nyi akpaa Wurubuarɛ da lese ŋolo te ɔ mo kolo boele e na, ɔ bɛɛ kyɛɛkee wa agyueŋ bela ya.

eng: Once you were disobedient to God, but now you have mercy because they were disobedient.
local: Debaŋ daale kaa laŋawɔ na ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ ɛ yɛɛ deŋele doŋ Wurubuarɛ dɔɔ, mɔna nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ lee Iseraetena deŋele doŋ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ te kaa wii ɛ waraŋase.

eng: In the same way, they have also been disobedient because of the mercy that you received, so now they can receive mercy too.
local: Mena ke te nɛnɛɛ baŋ mɔ dana deŋele doŋ, na ke yela Wurubuarɛ ke wii be waraŋase ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be wii ɛ waraŋase nɛ.

eng: God has locked up all people in disobedience, in order to have mercy on all of them.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ te yeli balaŋ pou te yɛɛ deŋele doŋ, na waa nyiŋ wii be popou waraŋase.

eng: Gods riches, wisdom, and knowledge are so deep! They are as mysterious as his judgments, and they are as hard to track as his paths!.
local: Wurubuarɛ wɔe keŋ ɔ dana nɛ, be dana ke maa ya. Ɔ siakekara na ɔ nombia kagyeŋ dokoso be kyaa ya. Amɔte ne gyae la waa tale nyii Wurubuarɛ abɔɔ ŋan ɔ ne yɛɛ, na mena keŋ ɔ ne yɛɛ ŋa nɛ man?.

eng: Who has known the Lords mind? Or who has been his mentor? .
local: Amɔte gyeŋa na de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ nyeeman nombia, te amɔte ne gyae la waa tale kolosi fa e?.

eng: Or who has given him a gift and has been paid back by him? .
local: Amɔte faa na Wurubuarɛ kolo see, keŋ kaboena nyi ŋon mɔ waa tɔ ɔtenate kom?.

eng: All things are from him and through him and for him. May the glory be to him forever. Amen.
local: Nawolo nyi kpene kamasɛ yɛɛ ɔ wui. Lee ɔ doŋ man dɔɔ te kpene kamasɛ kyaa, te ŋe popou mɔ kyae fa e. Ŋon kaboela nyi dɛɛ lese ɔ yele kekpaakekpaa. Amɛn.

eng: So, brothers and sisters, because of Gods mercies, I encourage you to present your bodies as a living sacrifice that is holy and pleasing to God. This is your appropriate priestly service.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ me tebia, keŋ Wurubuarɛ be wii de waraŋase dɔɔ mɛɛ waase ŋon bo nyiaa, ɛ mo ɛ wose pou fa e ŋgba kedeesa kolo nɛ lee ɛ kekyaabii man. Na ɛ ke kyaa fa e ŋgba balaŋ baŋ ayimɛɛ be doo ba wose man ya, te be nombia na gyoo ɔ sia nɛ. Nawolo nyi kei yɛna ɔsom kpaakpaate keŋ ke kaboena.

eng: Dont be conformed to the patterns of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your minds so that you can figure out what Gods will is—what is good and pleasing and mature.
local: Ɛ na tekaa mo ɛ wose do tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ abɔɔ ayɛɛsa man bela ya. Mɔna ɛ yela Wurubuarɛ ke kyɛɛkee ɛ konɔɔ man nombia na ɛ dinɔɔ pou, na ɛ ka bese balaŋ wɔlɛɛ ɛ kekyaabii man. Kei gyae ke yela ɛ ka gyeŋ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne gyae, te ke dei te kɛɛ gyoo ɔ sia nɛ nideli nɛ.

eng: Because of the grace that God gave me, I can say to each one of you: dont think of yourself more highly than you ought to think. Instead, be reasonable since God has measured out a portion of faith to each one of you.
local: Lee Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba mo boele maŋ te ɔ be fa maŋ tom kei nyi maa yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ, mɛɛ sola ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ nyi ɔ na kɛɛ ɔ wose nyi ŋolo be kyaa ɔ wɔle ya. Mɔna ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ waa yɔkɔse ɔ wose na waa gyoo ɔ wose man kɛɛ nyi, mena keŋ ɔ ne kɛɛ ɔ wose na bɛrɛɛ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo boele e lee ɔ kelɛɛdi dɔɔ nɛ doo na ke gbɛɛ.

eng: We have many parts in one body, but the parts dont all have the same function.
local: Nawolo nyi de man walaŋ kamasɛ yɛɛ deniwalaŋ na ɔ nyee e te ɔ dana yenaase kpookpoo, mɔna yenaŋ kamasɛ dana ka tom keŋ kɛɛ yɛɛ.

eng: In the same way, though there are many of us, we are one body in Christ, and individually we belong to each other.
local: Mena ke mɔ te daa Kristotena mɔ boe, mɔna lee Kristo kelɛɛdi dɔɔ de ta bese walaŋ dokoloŋ. De yɛɛ kpookpoo, mɔna Kristo dɔɔ da towaa na dɔŋa ŋgba walaŋ dokoloŋ nɛ.

eng: We have different gifts that are consistent with Gods grace that has been given to us. If your gift is prophecy, you should prophesy in proportion to your faith.
local: Wurubuarɛ abɔɔ anefaŋ ŋan ɔ ba mo boele daa nɛ yɛɛ kpookpoo, mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi dɛɛ mo ŋa yɛɛ tom ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ ba mo ŋa boele daa nɛ. Nyi ŋolo wui kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo fa e yɛna nyi Wurubuarɛ waa ta ɔ dɔɔ kolosi na, kaboena nyi waa yɛɛ ke ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ kelɛɛdi doo nɛ.

eng: If your gift is service, devote yourself to serving. If your gift is teaching, devote yourself to teaching.
local: Akpaa ŋolo wui yɛna balaŋ kasom na waa yɛɛ kakyeŋ som. Te akpaa ŋolo mɔ wui yɛna Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan kawola na, kaboena nyi waa yɛɛ kakyeŋ wola.

eng: If your gift is encouragement, devote yourself to encouraging. The one giving should do it with no strings attached. The leader should lead with passion. The one showing mercy should be cheerful.
local: Akpaa ŋolo wui yɛna balaŋ kakyeŋ kado na, waa do balaŋ kakyeŋ. Te akpaa ŋolo mɔ wui yɛna wɔe keyɛɛ na, kaboena nyi waa lese ɔ tɔɔman yɛɛ wɔe. Akpaa ŋolo wui yɛna nyi waa yɛɛ siamante ɔ tebia man na, waa lese ɔ konɔɔ man yɛɛ ke nideli. Akpaa ŋolo wui yɛna balaŋ waraŋase kewii na, waa yɛɛ ke gyoŋ man keŋ ɔ na toroŋ ya.

eng: Love should be shown without pretending. Hate evil, and hold on to what is good.
local: Ɛ gyae dɔŋa lee ɛ konɔɔse man. Ɛ kɔla abɔɔ ŋan pou ŋe yɛɛ abɔɔ kumɛɛ ŋa nɛ, na ɛ ke kyaŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan man keŋkeŋ.

eng: Love each other like the members of your family. Be the best at showing honor to each other.
local: Ɛ yase ɛ sia gyae ɛ tebia Kristotena nideli ŋgba naalolobia nɛ, na ɛ ka mo obuo fa dɔŋa nideli. Ɛ na kɛɛ nyi ɛmɛɛ kela na ŋolo ya.

eng: Dont hesitate to be enthusiastic—be on fire in the Spirit as you serve the Lord!.
local: Ɛ na yeli kegyae keŋ doo ɛ man fa Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan nɛ nɔɔ ke yɔkɔse ya. Ɛ yela Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ka do ŋon doŋ, na ɛ ka som de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ nideli.

eng: Be happy in your hope, stand your ground when youre in trouble, and devote yourselves to prayer.
local: Ɛ yela ɛ lookegyakaa keŋ ɛ dana nɛ ke yela ɛ ke di gyoŋ, na ɛ ke nyiŋ konɔɔ lee ɛ kayeyɛɛ man, na ɛ ke fane debaŋ kamasɛ.

eng: Contribute to the needs of Gods people, and welcome strangers into your home.
local: Ɛ kyɔ ɛ tebia Kristotena baŋ be yɛɛ ayematenawɔ nɛ, na ɛ ke lɛɛ ŋɔɔla mɔ nideli.

eng: Bless people who harass you—bless and dont curse them.
local: Ɛ sola wosefɛɛreŋ fa balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ naase ŋon diyem nɛ. Ɛ sola Wurubuarɛ wosefɛɛreŋ fa wɔ. Ɛ na falaa wɔ ya.

eng: Be happy with those who are happy, and cry with those who are crying.
local: Kolo de kɔŋ ŋolo dɔɔ te ɔ ne di gyoŋ na, ɛ kpu na e di gyoŋ. Mena mɔ te akpaa kolo de kɔŋ ŋolo dɔɔ te ɔ ne di walaŋsa na, ɛ kpu na ɛ na ɛ ke di walaŋsa.

eng: Consider everyone as equal, and dont think that youre better than anyone else. Instead, associate with people who have no status. Dont think that youre so smart.
local: Ɛ kyaa na dɔŋa nideli. Ɛ na gyina ɛ wose adido ya, mɔna ɛ mo ɛ wose do na balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ doo daale man ya nɛ. Ɛ na kɛɛ ɛ wose nyi ɛ dei kela wɔ ya.

eng: Dont pay back anyone for their evil actions with evil actions, but show respect for what everyone else believes is good.
local: Nyi akpaa ŋolo de yɛɛ ŋon nombiikum na, ɛ na mo dukum tɔ e kom ya. Ɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ na ɛ ke yɛɛ kpene keŋ balaŋ pou ne kɛɛ ke nyi ke dei nɛ.

eng: If possible, to the best of your ability, live at peace with all people.
local: Gbɛɛ kamasɛ man na, ɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ na ɛ ke kyaa na walaŋ kamasɛ wosefɛɛreŋ man.

eng: Dont try to get revenge for yourselves, my dear friends, but leave room for Gods wrath. It is written, Revenge belongs to me; I will pay it back, says the Lord .
local: Me tebia, ɛ na mo nombiakumɛɛ tɔ dɔŋa kom ya. Ɛ mo kpene kamasɛ fa Wurubuarɛ. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa, maŋ ɛ Gbeŋgyoo ne gyae la maa tɔ balaŋ kom lee be nombiakumɛɛ ŋan bɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: Instead, If your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him a drink. By doing this, you will pile burning coals of fire upon his head .
local: Lee mena dɔɔ, Akpaa tanam na ko walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kɔla neŋ na, fa e weenɛɛ waa di. Akpaa loŋboaneŋ na ko e na, fa e loŋ waa nyɔɔ. N yɛɛ mena na, desɛŋ gyae ke kyaŋ e nideli.

eng: Dont be defeated by evil, but defeat evil with good.
local: Na yeli nombiakumɛɛ ke di n dɔɔ ya, mɔna mo nombia kpaakpaa keyɛɛ di nombiakumɛɛ dɔɔ.

eng: Every person should place themselves under the authority of the government. There isnt any authority unless it comes from God, and the authorities that are there have been put in place by God.
local: Kaboena nyi walaŋ kamasɛ waa yɔkɔse ɔ wose fa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ kegyiise baŋ bee di de dɔɔ nɛ, nawolo nyi kegyia ŋolo be kyaa keŋ na Wurubuarɛ see na e ya. Kegyiise baŋ be kyaa nɛ pou, Wurubuarɛ see na wɔ.

eng: So anyone who opposes the authority is standing against what God has established. People who take this kind of stand will get punished.
local: Mena dɔɔ akpaa ŋolo da koro seŋ tia kegyiise benaŋ na, ɔ ta seŋ tia Wurubuarɛ baa. Te walaŋ ŋon waa yɛɛ mena na, Wurubuarɛ waa wɔŋ ɔ deŋele.

eng: The authorities dont frighten people who are doing the right thing. Rather, they frighten people who are doing wrong. Would you rather not be afraid of authority? Do whats right, and you will receive its approval.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa nɛ, bɔɔ ne yee kegyiise ya. Nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra ne yee na kegyiise benaŋ. Akpaa n bɛɛ gyae nyi n ke yee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ kegyiise ya na, kenaŋ na yɛɛ kpene keŋ dei na baa nyiŋ kɛlɛɛ neŋ.

eng: It is Gods servant given for your benefit. But if you do whats wrong, be afraid because it doesnt have weapons to enforce the law for nothing. It is Gods servant put in place to carry out his punishment on those who do what is wrong.
local: Nawolo nyi kegyia ŋon bɔɔ lese e see kolo nɔɔman nɛ, ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ tobaale e keŋ ɔ ne yɛɛ ɔ tom lee nyaŋ gbagba n deeli dɔɔ. Mɔna akpaa n yɛɛ kolo kum na yela gyakoloŋ ke kyaŋ neŋ, nawolo nyi ɔ doŋ keŋ ɔ dana nɛ te yɛɛ yakaa ya. Ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ tobaale e ŋon Wurubuarɛ ne tɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ wɔŋ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra deŋele.

eng: That is why it is necessary to place yourself under the governments authority, not only to avoid Gods punishment but also for the sake of your conscience.
local: Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi walaŋ kamasɛ waa yɔkɔse ɔ wose fa kegyiise baŋ. Te yɛɛ keŋ nyi ɔ ne yee nyi keŋ baa wɔŋ ɔ deŋele kaageŋ dɔɔ ya, mɔna keŋ ɔ gyeŋ lee ɔ konɔɔ man nyi kaboena nyi waa yɛɛ mena dɔɔ.

eng: You should also pay taxes for the same reason, because the authorities are Gods assistants, concerned with this very thing.
local: Mena mɔ dɔɔ te kaboena nyi yaa tɔ lempoo nɛ. Nawolo nyi kegyiise baŋ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ tewulɛɛwɔ te bɛɛ mo ba debaŋ pou yɛɛ tom.

eng: So pay everyone what you owe them. Pay the taxes you owe, pay the duties you are charged, give respect to those you should respect, and honor those you should honor.
local: Ɛ tɔ walaŋ kamasɛ kom keŋ ɛ dana e nɛ. Akpaa ke yɛɛ lempoo ke na tɔ ke, te akpaa ke yɛɛ toto ke na tɔ ke. Akpaa ke yɛɛ obuo ke na bu wɔ, te akpaa ke yɛɛ yele kalese ke na lese ba yele.

eng: Dont be in debt to anyone, except for the obligation to love each other. Whoever loves another person has fulfilled the Law.
local: Ɛ na yeli kom kamasɛ ke doo ɛmɛɛ na ŋolo nsana ya. Kom keŋ kaboena nyi ke dɛɛ doo ya nsana yɛna balaŋ kegyae keŋ kaboena nyi yaa gyae dɔŋa nɛ. Nawolo nyi akpaa ŋolo ne gyae ɔ dɔɔ na, ɔ te yɛɛ kpene keŋ mmaraa keŋ ne gyae nɛ kelii ɔto.

eng: The commandments, Dont commit adultery, dont murder, dont steal, dont desire what others have, and any other commandments, are all summed up in one word: You must love your neighbor as yourself. .
local: Nnɛ te Mosesi mmaraase ŋan ŋaale ne yako nyiaa, Na lee n wɛɛle yaa n baale wɔle ya, na ko walaŋ ya, na ŋmɛɛle ya, na yeli n sia keyɛɛ n dɔɔ kolo ya. Kɛŋa na mmaraase akaŋ ŋan pou nyee yɛna mmaraa keŋ ne yako nyiaa, Gyae n dɔɔ ŋgba nyaŋ gbagba n wose nɛ.

eng: Love doesnt do anything wrong to a neighbor; therefore, love is what fulfills the Law.
local: Nyi akpaa ŋolo ne gyae ɔ dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ be gyae waa yɛɛ e nombiikum ya. Mena dɔɔ nyi n gyae n dɔɔ na, n dii mmaraase ŋan pou dɔɔ baa kelii ɔto. Nawolo nyi mmaraase ŋan pou nyee yɛna kegyae.

eng: As you do all this, you know what time it is. The hour has already come for you to wake up from your sleep. Now our salvation is nearer than when we first had faith.
local: Kɛŋa pou wɔle nɛ, ɛ kɛɛ debaŋ keŋ man de kyaa nɛ na ɛ kena nyi te lii debaŋ keŋ dee foŋ lee da dosoroŋ man. Nawolo nyi nɛnɛɛ da nyeekelɛɛweeya ŋan ta benaa kela debaŋ keŋ dɔɔ taŋgbɛɛ lɛɛ Kristo di nɛ.

eng: The night is almost over, and the day is near. So lets get rid of the actions that belong to the darkness and put on the weapons of light.
local: Teka kyomii na nelim te lam, na tɛɛ kena mɔ te lii. Mena dɔɔ ɛ yela dee tina ditiŋtɛɛ man abɔɔ keyɛɛ yela, na dɛɛ mo keŋmaŋee kegba keŋ dɛɛ mo yoo na ditiŋtɛɛ abɔɔ nɛ do.

eng: Lets behave appropriately as people who live in the day, not in partying and getting drunk, not in sleeping around and obscene behavior, not in fighting and obsession.
local: Ɛ yela da kekyaabii man ke yɛɛ ŋgba balaŋ baŋ be kyaa keŋmaŋee man nɛ. Ɛ na yeli dɛɛ wɔlɛɛ debaŋ gyu ke kyaa nɔɔdedii man di de nɔɔ na dɛɛ dɛɛse ya. Ɛ na yeli dɛɛ yɛɛ kafoŋ nombia na nombia nkaraŋkaraŋ ya. Ɛ na gyae kale ya, te ɛ na yɛɛ sibiikum mɔ ya.

eng: Instead, dress yourself with the Lord Jesus Christ, and dont plan to indulge your selfish desires.
local: Mɔna ɛ yela de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo kedi ɛ nyee, na ba tekaa yela ɛ ka gyueŋ nombiakumɛɛ ŋan ɛ dinɔɔ kum keŋ ne yeli ɛ ne yɛɛ nɛ ya.

eng: Welcome the person who is weak in faith—but not in order to argue about differences of opinion.
local: Ɛ lɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ be dana doŋ ɔ Kristo kelɛɛdi man ya nɛ do ɛ man. Mɔna ɛ na yeli ɛ konɔɔ man nombia ŋan dɔɔ walaŋ kamasɛ seŋɛɛ nɛ, ka mo kewɔŋee kɔŋ ya nsana ya.

eng: One person believes in eating everything, while the weak person eats only vegetables.
local: Ŋolo kyaa na lee ɔ Kristo kelɛɛdi dɔɔ ɔ ne lɛɛ di nyi waa tale di kpene kamasɛ. Ŋolo mɔ kelɛɛdi be dana doŋ ya mena dɔɔ ŋon ne di faareŋ weenɛɛ kaageŋ bo.

eng: Those who eat must not look down on the ones who dont, and the ones who dont eat must not judge the ones who do, because God has accepted them.
local: Gbɛɛ be doo nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne di kpene kamasɛ nɛ, waa kɛɛ ɔ wose nyi ɔ dei kela walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne di faareŋ weenɛɛ nɛ ya. Mena mɔ te walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne di faareŋ weenɛɛ nɛ, na bu walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne di kpene kamasɛ nɛ fɔɔ ya. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ ne lɛɛ be popou do.

eng: Who are you to judge someone elses servants? They stand or fall before their own Lord .
local: Nyaŋ yɛna amɔte keŋ n ke tale bu ŋolo tobaale yaa wa alebu fɔɔ? Walaŋ ŋonaŋ ɔ gbeŋgyoo waageŋ ne gyae la waa tale naa nyi ɔ seŋɛɛ nideli yaa ɔ ba seŋɛɛ nideli ya. Te waa tale seŋ nideli nawolo nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ dana doŋ keŋ waa tale yela waa seŋ nideli.

eng: One person considers some days to be more sacred than others, while another person considers all days to be the same. Each person must have their own convictions.
local: Ŋolo waa tale yako nyi wee daale kela weeya akaŋ ŋan, te ŋolo mɔ gyaŋ na weeya ŋan pou yɛɛ kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Nombia kɛŋa man na, kaboena nyi walaŋ kamasɛ waa lɛɛ ɔ wui keŋ dɔɔ ɔ seŋɛɛ nɛ do nideli.

eng: Someone who thinks that a day is sacred, thinks that way for the Lord. Those who eat, eat for the Lord, because they thank God. And those who dont eat, dont eat for the Lord, and they thank the Lord too.
local: Nyi akpaa ŋolo da lese wee daale see nyi keŋ kela na weeya akaŋ ŋan na, ɔ ne yɛɛ mena bo na waa mo lese Wurubuarɛ yele. Te akpaa ŋolo mɔ ne di kpene kamasɛ na, ɔ ne di ke bo na waa mo lese Wurubuarɛ yele. Nawolo nyi ɔ ne fane fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ lee weenɛɛ ŋenaŋ dɔɔ pɛte ɔ ne dii. Walaŋ ŋon mɔ ɔ ne di faareŋ kaageŋ bo nɛ, ŋon mɔ ne di ŋa bo na waa mo lese Wurubuarɛ yele. Nawolo nyi ŋon mɔ ne fane fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ pɛte ɔ ne dii.

eng: We dont live for ourselves and we dont die for ourselves.
local: Da ke kyaa kei, yeŋ man o nyeedoŋ man o, ŋolo be kyaa fa ɔ wose ya.

eng: If we live, we live for the Lord, and if we die, we die for the Lord. Therefore, whether we live or die, we belong to God.
local: Nyi akpaa de kyaa na, de kyae fa de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo. Mena mɔ te akpaa da yem na, da yeŋae fa de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo. Mena dɔɔ nyi de kyae na de sia o, da yeŋae o, de yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo wui.

eng: This is why Christ died and lived: so that he might be Lord of both the dead and the living.
local: Kei dɔɔ te Kristo yeŋawɔ te ɔ foŋawɔ lee yeŋ man. Na ke yela waa nyiŋ bese balaŋ baŋ be kyaa na be sia, na baŋ bɔɔ yekee nɛ pou Gbeŋgyoo.

eng: But why do you judge your brother or sister? Or why do you look down on your brother or sister? We all will stand in front of the judgment seat of God.
local: Mena dɔɔ tekaboena nyi ŋolo waa bu ɔ dɔɔ fɔɔ yaa waa kɛɛ ɔ wose nyi ɔ dei kela ɔ dɔɔ ya. Nawolo nyi de popou dɛɛ kaa seŋ Wurubuarɛ siaman na waa di de nombia.

eng: Because it is written, As I live, says the Lord, every knee will bow to me, and every tongue will give praise to God. .
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo kpa, Maŋ nyiŋa me kyaa nɛ, walaŋ kamasɛ waa kaa buŋ me siaman na waa toro yako nyi maŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ. .

eng: So then, each of us will give an account of ourselves to God.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ de man walaŋ kamasɛ waa kaa tɔlɛɛ ɔ nɔɔwoya fa Wurubuarɛ.

eng: So stop judging each other. Instead, this is what you should decide: never put a stumbling block or obstacle in the way of your brother or sister.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ yela dee tina dɔŋa fɔɔ kebu yela. Mɔna kaboena bo nyi dɛɛ kɛɛ nyi dɔɔ yɛɛ kolo na ke yela ŋolo ke yɛɛ dukum ya.

eng: I know and Im convinced in the Lord Jesus that nothing is wrong to eat in itself. But if someone thinks something is wrong to eat, it becomes wrong for that person.
local: Me ka bese Kristo baale kei dɔɔ nɛ, ma te naa nyi weenɛɛ kamasɛ be kyaa keŋ ke yɛɛ kolo kum ke ya. Mɔna akpaa Kristo baale ŋolo de lɛɛ weenɛɛ ŋaale do nyi ke yɛɛ kolo anekɔlaŋ ke na, na ɔtenate gyaŋ te ke yɛɛ kolo anekɔlaŋ.

eng: If your brother or sister is upset by your food, you are no longer walking in love. Dont let your food destroy someone for whom Christ died.
local: Nyi akpaa n weenɛɛ ŋaale kedi de yeli n dɔɔ ŋolo wose te fɛɛ e ya nɛ, kenaŋ na n nombia ayɛɛsa bɛɛ wola balaŋ kegyae ya. Na yeli n weenɛɛ ŋaale kedi ke yela n dɔɔ ŋolo ke yala ɔ kelɛɛdi man ya. Nawolo nyi ŋon mɔ dɔɔ te Kristo yeŋawɔ.

eng: And dont let something you consider to be good be criticized as wrong.
local: Mena dɔɔ na yeli kpene keŋ nyaŋ ne kɛɛ ke nyi ke dei nɛ, ka bese kolo kum balaŋ siaman ya.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about eating food and drinking but about righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ nɛ, ka te yɛɛ kedi na kenyɔɔ nombia ŋaageŋ ya. Mɔna ke yɛɛ keŋ nyi walaŋ waa yɛɛ kpene keŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman, na waa yɛɛ kpene keŋ gyae ka mo wosefɛɛreŋ kɔŋ ŋon na ɔ dɔɔ nsana, na dee di gyoŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne fa daa nɛ.

eng: Whoever serves Christ this way pleases God and gets human approval.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ som Kristo lee mena gbɛɛ kei man nɛ, ɔ nombia na gyoo Wurubuarɛ sia. Mena walaŋ ŋonaŋ mɔ te balaŋ na lese ɔ yele.

eng: So lets strive for the things that bring peace and the things that build each other up.
local: Mena dɔɔ debaŋ kamasɛ na ɛ yela dɛɛ mo de loo gyakaa abɔɔ ŋan gyae ka mo wosefɛɛreŋ kɔŋ, te ŋɛɛ do daa doŋ da kelɛɛdi man nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: Dont destroy what God has done because of food. All food is acceptable, but its a bad thing if it trips someone else.
local: Na yeli weenɛɛ ŋan nyaŋ ke di nɛ, ke wɔlɛɛ Wurubuarɛ tom ya. Gbɛɛ doo nyi dɛɛ tale di weenɛɛ kamasɛ, mɔna akpaa weenɛɛ ŋan nyaŋ kedi nɛ ne gyae la ke yela ŋolo ke yala ɔ kelɛɛdi man na, kenaŋ na na di ŋa korakora ya.

eng: Its a good thing not to eat meat or drink wine or to do anything that trips your brother or sister.
local: Nawolo nyi akpaa ŋolo de bɛɛ naane ketao, yaa ɔ bɛɛ soloŋ kenyɔɔ yaa ɔ bɛɛ kpene kamasɛ keŋ gyae ke yela ɔ dɔɔ ŋolo ke yala ɔ kelɛɛdi man na, keŋ gba dei la kela.

eng: Keep the belief that you have to yourself—its between you and God. People are blessed who dont convict themselves by the things they approve.
local: Mena dɔɔ kpene kamasɛ keŋ n ne lɛɛ di lee mena nombia kɛŋa man na, yela ke doo ɛmɛɛ na n Wurubuarɛ nsana. Walaŋ ŋon ɔ konɔɔ man nombia bee bu e fɔɔ lee abɔɔ ŋan ŋon gbagba be lɛɛ ŋa do nɛ man ya na, ŋon na nyeebam.

eng: But those who have doubts are convicted if they go ahead and eat, because they arent acting on the basis of faith. Everything that isnt based on faith is sin.
local: Mɔna akpaa ŋolo de di kolo keŋ ɔ konɔɔ man nombia ta tenee e ya nɛ, kenaŋ na Wurubuarɛ waa bu e fɔɔ. Nawolo nyi ɔtenate te lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di lee kpene kenaŋ keyɛɛ man ya. Te kpene kamasɛ keŋ ŋolo te lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di lee ka keyɛɛ man ya nɛ, na ke yɛɛ dukum ke.

eng: We who are powerful need to be patient with the weakness of those who dont have power, and not please ourselves.
local: Daa baŋ da seŋɛɛ da kelɛɛdi man keŋkeŋ nɛ, kaboena nyi dee nyiŋ konɔɔ fa baŋ bɔɔ seŋɛɛ ba kelɛɛdi man keŋkeŋ ya nɛ, na dee nyiŋ kyɔ wɔ. Ɛ na yeli dɛɛ kɛɛ daa daageŋ wui ya.

eng: Each of us should please our neighbors for their good in order to build them up.
local: Ɛ yela de man walaŋ kamasɛ ke yɛɛ kpene keŋ dei fa ɔ dɔɔ, na ke kyɔ e na ŋon mɔ ka seŋ keŋkeŋ ɔ kelɛɛdi man.

eng: Christ didnt please himself, but, as it is written, The insults of those who insulted you fell on me. .
local: Nawolo nyi Kristo gba koraŋ te kɛɛ ŋon waageŋ wui ya. Be ŋmarasewɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see man nyi ɔ yako Wurubuarɛ nyiaa, Tuubia ŋan balaŋ ba tee neŋ nɛ, maŋ te ŋe kaa wuraawɔ.

eng: Whatever was written in the past was written for our instruction so that we could have hope through endurance and through the encouragement of the scriptures.
local: Nombia ŋan pou bɔɔ ŋmarase see Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man nɛ, be ŋmarase ŋa bo see na ŋe ka wola daa nombia. Kei gyae ke yela dɛɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ na dee nyiŋ konɔɔ seŋ keŋkeŋ, lee lookegyakaa keŋ de dana nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: May the God of endurance and encouragement give you the same attitude toward each other, similar to Christ Jesus attitude.
local: Mɛɛ sola Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ne yeli balaŋ ne nyiŋ konɔɔ seŋ keŋkeŋ te ɔ na do wɔ kakyeŋ nɛ nyiaa, debaŋ kei keŋ ɛ silana Kristo Yesu nɛ, waa yela ɛ ke yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ.

eng: That way you can glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ together with one voice.
local: Dekɔŋ mena na gyae ke yela ɛ popou ka mo konɔɔ dokoloŋ, na nɔɔdokoloŋ lese Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo ɔ kya e nɛ yele.

eng: So welcome each other, in the same way that Christ also welcomed you, for Gods glory.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ ɛ lɛɛ dɔŋa do ŋgba mena keŋ Kristo be lɛɛ ŋon do nɛ, na balaŋ ke nyiŋ lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

eng: Im saying that Christ became a servant of those who are circumcised for the sake of Gods truth, in order to confirm the promises given to the ancestors.
local: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, Kristo kaa bese Gyudatena somre na ka wola nyi Wurubuarɛ ne di ɔ nombia dɔɔ. Te ɔ nombia ŋan ɔ be yako see fa Gyudatena be naanaɔ baŋ nɛ, gyae ke kɔŋ mena.

eng: and so that the Gentiles could glorify God for his mercy. As it is written, Because of this I will confess you among the Gentiles, and I will sing praises to your name .
local: Kei gyae ke yela balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, ka lese Wurubuarɛ yele lee mena keŋ ɔ be wii be waraŋase ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan be yako nɛ nyiaa, Lee kei dɔɔ maa lese n yele balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ siaman, na maa kpee nɔɔneŋ kɛlɛɛ neŋ.

eng: And again, it says, Rejoice, Gentiles, with his people .
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan besewɔ yako nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ balaŋ baŋ ɛ te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, ɛ kpu na balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ba lese nɛ na ɛ ke nyaŋee.

eng: And again, Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles, and all the people should sing his praises .
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan besewɔ yako nyiaa, Ɛmɛɛ baŋ pou ɛ te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, ɛ lese de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ yele. Ɛmɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou, ɛ lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

eng: And again, Isaiah says, There will be a root of Jesse, who will also rise to rule the Gentiles. The Gentiles will place their hope in him .
local: Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya mɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Walaŋ ŋolo waa kaa lee de naana Gyese duuluŋ man kɔŋ. Ŋon ne gyae la ɔ kaa di gyoori balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ dɔɔ, te ɔ dɔɔ te baa mo be loo gyakaa.

eng: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in faith so that you overflow with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
local: Mɛɛ sola Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ne fa balaŋ lookegyakaa nɛ nyi waa yela ɛ ke kyaa gyoŋ na wosefɛɛreŋ man lee kelɛɛdi keŋ ɛ be lɛɛ e di nɛ dɔɔ. Na ɔ feliŋ ŋon ke yela ɛ ka mo ɛ loo gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ nideli.

eng: My brothers and sisters, I myself am convinced that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, and are able to teach each other.
local: Me tebia Kristotena, maŋ gbagba gyeŋ nideli nyi ɛ yɛɛ balaŋ kpaakpaawɔ, te ɛ gyeŋ nombia mɔ nideli keŋ ɛ ke tale wola ɛ tebia.

eng: But Ive written to you in a sort of daring way, partly to remind you of what you already know. Im writing to you in this way because of the grace that was given to me by God.
local: Mɔna ma te gyɔ me konɔɔ yako ŋon nombia ŋaale me tɔne kei man, na ɛ be nyiŋ wolee ŋa ya. Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba mo boele maŋ nɛ dɔɔ, te me talewɔ yako ŋon nombia ŋenaŋ.

eng: It helps me to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles. Im working as a priest of Gods gospel so that the offering of the Gentiles can be acceptable and made holy by the Holy Spirit.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ lese na maŋ nyi maa yɛɛ Yesu Kristo kpilale balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ man. Mɛɛ yɛɛ tom ŋgba Wurubuarɛ sae nɛ lee ŋon Wurubuarɛ nyeedoŋ nombia ŋan keyako man. Kei gyae ke yela balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, kaa yɛɛ ŋgba kpene keŋ bɛɛ mo deesi fa Wurubuarɛ te na gyoo ɔ sia nɛ. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon te desina wɔ keŋ kolo kum kamasɛ be doo be man bela ya.

eng: So in Christ Jesus I brag about things that have to do with God.
local: Mena dɔɔ me dana gbɛɛ keŋ maa tale wola ma wose, lee tom keŋ Kristo doŋ keŋ ne yeli mɛɛ yɛɛ fa Wurubuarɛ nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: I dont dare speak about anything except what Christ has done through me to bring about the obedience of the Gentiles. He did it by what Ive said and what Ive done.
local: Mɔɔ gyae maa yako nombii daale kpu na keŋ Yesu Kristo be tɛɛ me dɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ ya. Maa gyɔ me konɔɔ bo yako mena keŋ ɔ be tɛɛ me dɔɔ yela balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ be kaa lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di nɛ dɔɔ. Me nɔɔwoya na me nombia ayɛɛsa na gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana ŋan Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon be yeli mɔɔ tale yɛɛ nɛ, yele la te balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ kaa lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di. Mena dɔɔ me tɛɛwɔ kolosi Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan pou lee Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man menɛɛ kelii Ilirikum tɛɛle dɔɔ.

eng: In this way, I have a goal to preach the gospel where they havent heard of Christ yet, so that I wont be building on someone elses foundation.
local: Kpene keŋ doo me konɔɔ man debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ mɛɛ gyae nyi maa yɛɛ yɛna nyiaa, mɛɛ gyae nyi maa ta kolosi Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan botɔɔse ŋan be te nyii ta ŋa see ya nɛ. Mɔɔ ne gyae nyi me kaa kolosi ŋa botɔɔse ŋan baale be wulaa kolosi ŋa nɛ ya.

eng: Instead, as its written, Those who hadnt been told about him will see, and those who hadnt heard will understand .
local: Mena te Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyiaa, Balaŋ baŋ ba te yako ta wɔ Kristo nombia ŋan ya nɛ baa naa e, te balaŋ baŋ be te nyii ta ɔ nombia ŋan ya nɛ baa nyii ŋe man.

eng: Thats why Ive been stopped so many times from coming to see you.
local: Kei dɔɔ te kaa lii naŋ gyɛŋ me te nyiŋa ta gbɛɛ maa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ ya nɛ.

eng: But now, since I dont have any place to work in these regions anymore, and since Ive wanted to come to see you for many years.
local: Mɔna nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ ma te taŋ ma tom keyɛɛ kɛbo, te leenaŋ bene bene me wulaa mɛɛ gyae gbɛɛ nyi maa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ.

eng: Ill visit you when I go to Spain. I hope to see you while Im passing through. And I hope you will send me on my way there, after I have first been reenergized by some time in your company.
local: mena dɔɔ ma te yɛɛ ma agyueŋ nyi akpaa mɛɛ lam mee gyu Sepeni na maa ta ɛ gyaŋ. Mɛɛ kɔŋ me kaa talaa me sia ɛ gyaŋ weeya ala, na ɛ ke nyiŋ lɔ maŋ gbɛɛ.

eng: But now Im going to Jerusalem, to serve Gods people.
local: Mɔna nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ ma seŋɛɛ Gyerusalɛm gbɛɛ dɔɔ baa. Me dana kolo mee gyu me kaa kyɔ Kristotena baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ.

eng: Macedonia and Achaia have been happy to make a contribution for the poor among Gods people in Jerusalem.
local: Nawolo nyi Kristotena baŋ be kyaa Masedonia na Akaya tɛɛlese man nɛ, ta mo gyoŋ yilaa abɔɔ na baa mo yelee Kristotena ayematena baŋ be kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ.

eng: They were happy to do this, and they are actually in debt to Gods people in Jerusalem. If the Gentiles got a share of the Jewish peoples spiritual resources, they ought to minister to them with material resources.
local: Baŋ gbagba kegyaebii man te ba moowɔ yilaa abɔɔ kɛŋa, te kaboena nyi baa taŋ yɛɛ mena. Nawolo nyi nyeedoŋ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan bɔɔ nyii lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ nɛ, Gyudatena yele la te be nyii ŋa. Mena dɔɔ nyi baŋ mɔ da mo ba wose man abɔɔ gyu ke kyɔ Gyudatena benaŋ na, ke doo na ke gbɛɛ.

eng: So then after I have finished this job and have safely delivered the final amount of the Gentiles offering to them, I will leave for Spain, visiting you on the way.
local: Akpaa me taŋ mo abɔɔ ŋan pou bɔɔ yilaa nɛ do wɔ be nyiŋmaa man na, maa kaa laŋ gyu Sepeni tɛɛle man. Akpaa mɛɛ lam mee gyu botɔɔ na, te maa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ.

eng: And I know that when I come to you I will come with the fullest blessing of Christ.
local: Te ma gyeŋ nyi me kɛɛ kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ kei nɛ, gyae ke yela Kristo ke hiraa daa nideli.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I urge you, through our Lord Jesus Christ and through the love of the Spirit, to join me in my struggles in your prayers to God for me.
local: Me tebia kpaakpaa, me ta mo de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo na balaŋ kegyae keŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon be yeli ɛ dana nɛ mɛɛ sola ŋon nyiaa, ɛ yase ɛ sia fane fa Wurubuarɛ kyɔ maŋ.

eng: Pray that I will be rescued from the people in Judea who dont believe. Also, pray that my service for Jerusalem will be acceptable to Gods people there.
local: Ɛ fane fa Wurubuarɛ fa maŋ nyi waa lese maŋ lee balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Kristotenawɔ ya te be kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ nyiŋmaa man. Ɛ fane nyi Wurubuarɛ waa yela Kristotena baŋ be kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, sia kagye tom keŋ mee gyu me kaa yɛɛ be gyaŋ nɛ man.

eng: so that I can come to you with joy by Gods will and be reenergized with your company.
local: Te akpaa Wurubuarɛ de sɛɛ na maa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ na gyoŋ, na daa na ŋon pou ke kyaa na dee di gyoŋ na dɔŋa.

eng: May the God of peace be with you all. Amen.
local: Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ wosefɛɛreŋ Gbeŋgyoo e nɛ, waa kyaa na ɛ popou. Amɛn.

eng: Im introducing our sister Phoebe to you, who is a servant of the church in Cenchreae.
local: Mɛɛ lese de deesi Kristo alo Foobe maa wola ŋon nyi ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne yee Wurubuarɛ nideli. Foobe kei yɛɛ kegyia e Kristotena baŋ be kyaa Kenkiria donɔɔ man nɛ man.

eng: Welcome her in the Lord in a way that is worthy of Gods people, and give her whatever she needs from you, because she herself has been a sponsor of many people, myself included.
local: Nyi ɔ kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ na ɛ lɛɛ e lee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu yele man na ɛ ke kyɔ e lee gbɛɛ kamasɛ man, ŋgba mena keŋ kaboena nyi Kristotena baa kyɔ dɔŋa nɛ. Nawolo nyi ŋon gbagba mɔ te kyɔ balaŋ burum, te ɔ te kyɔ maŋ gbagba mɔ.

eng: Say hello to Prisca and Aquila, my coworkers in Christ Jesus.
local: Ɛ do Pirisila na Akuila nɔɔ fa maŋ, nawolo nyi be yɛɛ me tebiawɔ te daa na wɔ pou ne yɛɛ na Kristo Yesu tom keŋ.

eng: who risked their own necks for my life. Im not the only one who thanks God for them, but all the churches of the Gentiles do the same.
local: Ba moo ba wose fa yeŋ lee me dɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ do wɔ nɔɔ nideli. Na maŋ maageŋ na do na wɔ nɔɔ ya, mɔna Kristotena baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ mɔ na do wɔ nɔɔ.

eng: Also say hello to the church that meets in their house. Say hello to Epaenetus, my dear friend, who was the first convert in Asia for Christ.
local: Ɛ do Kristotena baŋ bɛɛ gyaŋee ba dekpaŋalaŋ man nɛ nɔɔ fa maŋ. Ɛ do me gyoo gyaagyi Epanetuse nɔɔ fa maŋ. Ŋon yɛna walaŋ ŋon ɔ be taŋgbɛɛ lɛɛ Kristo di Esia tɛɛle man.

eng: Say hello to Mary, who has worked very hard for you.
local: Ɛ do Mɛɛre ŋon ɔ be naa diyem nideli ɛ dɔɔ nɛ nɔɔ fa maŋ.

eng: Say hello to Andronicus and Junia, my relatives and my fellow prisoners. They are prominent among the apostles, and they were in Christ before me.
local: Ɛ do Andoronikuse na Gyuniase nɔɔ fa maŋ. Be yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ŋgba maŋ nɛ, te daa na wɔ pou te be kyaŋawɔ tɔ deni see. Be yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ Kristo kpilala baŋ pou ne bu wɔ nideli, te be taŋgbɛɛ maŋ lɛɛ Kristo di gba.

eng: Say hello to Ampliatus, my dear friend in the Lord.
local: Ɛ do Ampeliatuse nɔɔ fa maŋ. Ɔ yɛɛ me gyoo gyaagyi e lee Kristo dɔɔ.

eng: Say hello to Urbanus, our coworker in Christ, and my dear friend Stachys.
local: Ɛ do de gyoo Ubanuse ŋon daa na e pou ne yɛɛ tom Kristo man nɛ, na de dɔɔ kpaakpaa Setakise nɔɔ fa maŋ.

eng: Say hello to Apelles, who is tried and true in Christ. Say hello to the members of the household of Aristobulus.
local: Ɛ do Apelese nɔɔ fa maŋ. Ɔ ta lese wola nideli nyi ɔ seŋɛɛ Kristo nombia ŋan man nideli. Ɛ do balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ lee Aristobuluse dekpaŋalaŋ man nɛ nɔɔ fa maŋ.

eng: Say hello to my relative Herodion. Say hello to the members of the household of Narcissus who are in the Lord.
local: Ɛ do Herodiɔn nɔɔ fa maŋ. Ŋon mɔ yɛɛ Gyuda baale e ŋgba maŋ nɛ. Ɛ do Kristotena baŋ be kyaa Nasisuse dekpaŋalaŋ man nɛ nɔɔ fa maŋ.

eng: Say hello to Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who are workers for the Lord. Say hello to my dear friend Persis, who has worked hard in the Lord.
local: Ɛ do Tirifena na Tirifosa nɔɔ fa maŋ. Be yɛɛ ala baŋ bɔɔ kyaŋ ba wose yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo tom keŋ. Ɛ do me gyoo gyaagyi Pɛɛsi nɔɔ fa maŋ. Ŋon mɔ yɛɛ alo ŋon ɔ be yɛɛ tom dinaa fa de Gbeŋgyoo.

eng: Say hello to Rufus, who is an outstanding believer, along with his mother and mine.
local: Ɛ do Rufuse na ɔ naa ŋon ɔ ba mo maŋ yɛɛ ɔ bu nɛ nɔɔ fa maŋ. Rufuse yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo tobaale ŋon ɔ yele ba lee nideli.

eng: Say hello to Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers and sisters who are with them.
local: Ɛ do Asinkirituse na Felegɔn na Hemise na Patorobase na Hɛmase na Kristotena baŋ pou be kyaa kpu na wɔ nɛ nɔɔ fa maŋ.

eng: Say hello to Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.
local: Ɛ do Filologuse na Gyulia na Neruse na ɔ deesi na Olimpase na Kristotena baŋ pou be kyaa kpu na wɔ nɛ nɔɔ fa maŋ.

eng: Say hello to each other with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ say hello to you.
local: Ɛ pukaa dɔŋa, na ɛ ka do dɔŋa nɔɔ na gyoŋ. Kristotena akpii akpii ŋan pou na do ŋon nɔɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I urge you to watch out for people who create divisions and problems against the teaching that you learned. Keep away from them.
local: Me tebia, mɛɛ waase ŋon nyi ɛ kɛɛ nideli na ɛ ka gyene balaŋ baŋ be kpuɛ na ŋon te bɛɛ wɔlɛɛ ya nsana nɛ. Mena balaŋ benaŋ ne yeli baale ne yale ba kelɛɛdi man, te be nombia ŋan bɛɛ wolo nɛ mɔ yɛɛ kpoo lee ŋan mɔɔ wola ŋon nɛ man. Ɛ lese ɛ wose lee mena balaŋ benaŋ man.

eng: People like that arent serving the Lord. They are serving their own feelings. They deceive the hearts of innocent people with smooth talk and flattery.
local: Nawolo nyi mena balaŋ kɛwɔ bɛɛ som de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo ya, mɔna bɛɛ gyae baŋ gbagba deeli bo. Be dɔŋɛɛ bɛɛ mo nɔɔman yeliŋ na nɔɔwoya gyoŋ nyiŋan mo beo balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ daale ya nɛ.

eng: The news of your obedience has reached everybody, so Im happy for you. But I want you to be wise about whats good, and innocent about whats evil.
local: Ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ balaŋ pou te nyii mena keŋ ɛ ba mo ɛ wose fa Kristo nɛ, mena dɔɔ dana maŋ gyoŋ nideli. Mɔna mɛɛ gyae ɛ ka gyeŋ nyansa na ɛ ka gyeŋ nombia ŋan ŋe dei, na ɛ ka lese ɛ wose lee dukum keyɛɛ man.

eng: The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
local: Be gyae ke kyare ya Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ wosefɛɛreŋ pou Gbeŋgyoo e nɛ, waa yela ɛ ke sakatee ɔbɔnsam ɛ nawɔɔ tɛɛ. De Gbeŋgyoo Yesu waa mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele ŋon.

eng: Timothy my coworker says hello to you, and Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my relatives.
local: Me dɔɔ Timoti ŋon daa na e ne yɛɛ tom dokoloŋ nɛ na do ŋon nɔɔ. Mena ke mɔ te Lusiuse na Gyasɔn na Sosipata baŋ be yɛɛ me tebia Gyudatenawɔ nɛ mɔ na do ŋon nɔɔ.

eng: Im Tertius, and Im writing this letter to you in the Lord—hello!.
local: Maŋ Tɛtiyuse ŋon mɔɔ ŋmarase tɔne kei fa Pɔɔl nɛ, mɔ na do ŋon nɔɔ lee de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo yele man.

eng: Gaius, who is host to me and to the whole church, says hello to you. Erastus the city treasurer says hello to you, along with our brother Quartus.
local: Me denite Gayose mɔ na do ŋon nɔɔ. Ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ man te daa Kristotena baŋ pou de kyaa kɛbo nɛ ne gyaŋee. Erasituse ŋon ɔ ne kɛɛ donɔɔ kei kɔba dɔɔ nɛ, na de dɔɔ Kuatuse mɔ na do ŋon nɔɔ.

eng: May the glory be to God who can strengthen you with my good news and the message that I preach about Jesus Christ. He can strengthen you with the announcement of the secret that was kept quiet for a long time.
local: Ɛ yela dɛɛ lese Wurubuarɛ yele. Ŋon ne tale la yela ɛ ne tale seŋ keŋkeŋ ɛ kelɛɛdi man, lee Yesu Kristo nyeedoŋ nombia ŋan mɔɔ kolosi fa ŋon nɛ dɔɔ. Nafɔ anokoare nombii kei yɛɛ nombii aweeseŋ ke, mɔna nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ te yeli de ta gyeŋ ke.

eng: Now that secret is revealed through what the prophets wrote. It is made known to the Gentiles in order to lead to their faithful obedience based on the command of the eternal God.
local: Nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ nombii kei ta tekaa yɛɛ nombii aweeseŋ ya. Wurubuarɛ te yeli wa akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra te ŋmarase anokoare nombii kei see. Te Wurubuarɛ ŋon mɔ ɔ kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ te yeli ba te kolosi nombii kei fa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou, na baa lɛɛ e di na baa ta ɔ gbɛɛneŋ dɔɔ.

eng: May the glory be to God, who alone is wise! May the glory be to him through Jesus Christ forever! Amen.
local: Ɛ yela dɛɛ ta de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo dɔɔ na dɛɛ lese Wurubuarɛ yele kekpaakekpaa. Nawolo nyi ŋon waageŋ kooŋ yɛna nyansa pou nyee. Amɛn.

eng: From Paul, called by Gods will to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Sosthenes our brother.
local: Tɔne kei lee maŋ Pɔɔl ŋon Wurubuarɛ be baake lee ɔ kegyaebii man nyi me kaa yɛɛ ɔ kpilale nɛ, na de dɔɔ Sositene gyaŋ.

eng: To Gods church that is in Corinth: To those who have been made holy to God in Christ Jesus, who are called to be Gods people. Together with all those who call upon the name of our Lord Jesus Christ in every place—hes their Lord and ours!.
local: Dɛɛ ŋmarase ke dee yelee ɛmɛɛ Kristotena dikpii keŋ ɛ kyaa Korinto donɔɔ man, na baŋ pou be kyaa yenaŋ kamasɛ te bɛɛ som de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo ŋon ɔ yɛɛ daa na wɔ pou Gbeŋgyoo e nɛ. Wurubuarɛ te tɛɛ Kristo Yesu dɔɔ baake ŋon te ɔ gberaa ayimɛɛ lee ɛ wose man te ɔ desina ŋon see fa ɔ wose.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: De kya Wurubuarɛ na de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo baa mo be bɛrɛɛ boele ŋon, na baa fa ŋon wosefɛɛreŋ.

eng: I thank my God always for you, because of Gods grace that was given to you in Christ Jesus.
local: Debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ mɛɛ fane na, mɛɛ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ lee mena keŋ ɔ be tɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo Yesu dɔɔ mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele ŋon nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: That is, you were made rich through him in everything: in all your communication and every kind of knowledge.
local: Lee mena keŋ ɛmɛɛ na Kristo be yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ te hiraa ŋon gbɛɛ kamasɛ man. Ɔ te yeli ɛ ta gyeŋ kekolosi te ɛ nyiŋa nyansa mɔ kpu.

eng: in the same way that the testimony about Christ was confirmed with you.
local: Nawolo nyi Kristo nombia ŋan dee kolosi te ɛ be nyii nɛ, ta gyoo ɛ man nideli.

eng: The result is that you arent missing any spiritual gift while you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to be revealed.
local: Mena dɔɔ debaŋ kei keŋ ɛ kyaa ɛ ne daa de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu kabese kɔŋ na sibiiyam nɛ, Wurubuarɛ feliŋ kolo anefaŋ daale be kyaa keŋ ɛ te nyiŋa ta ya.

eng: He will also confirm your testimony about Christ until the end so that you will be blameless on the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ŋon gbagba waa yela ɛ ka seŋ keŋkeŋ kelii ɔto, keŋ nyi nombiikum kamasɛ be gyae ke doo ɛ man ya kelii wee keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo waa bese kɔŋ.

eng: God is faithful, and you were called by him to partnership with his Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.
local: Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ bɛɛ lee ɔ nombia wɔle ya nɛ baake na ŋon nyi ɛ kaa yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ na ɔ bu Yesu Kristo ŋon ɔ yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo e nɛ.

eng: Now I encourage you, brothers and sisters, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ: Agree with each other and dont be divided into rival groups. Instead, be restored with the same mind and the same purpose.
local: Me tebia Kristotena, me ta mo de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo yele mee doo ŋon teree nyiaa, ɛ popou ɛ yako nombii sɛɛ dɔŋa na kekpase be kɔŋ ɛ man ya. Ɛ yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ lee ɛ nyeeman nombia na ɛ kekolosi pou man.

eng: My brothers and sisters, Chloes people gave me some information about you, that youre fighting with each other.
local: Me tebia, balaŋ baale ta lee Koloe dekpaŋalaŋ man kaa yako maŋ nyi kale doo ya nsana.

eng: What I mean is this: that each one of you says, I belong to Paul, I belong to Apollos, I belong to Cephas, I belong to Christ.
local: Nombii keŋ doo yɛna nyi ɛ be dana nɔɔdokoloŋ ya. Nawolo nyi ɛ man baale kpa, Be silana Pɔɔl bo, te baale mɔ kpa, Be silana Apolo bo, te baale mɔ kpa, Be silana Pita bo. Mena mɔ te baale mɔ kpa, Kristo te be silana.

eng: Has Christ been divided? Was Paul crucified for you, or were you baptized in Pauls name?.
local: Mɛɛ bɔɔse ŋon baa nyiaa, dɛɛ tale kpase Kristo? Pɔɔl te be gyɔɔwɔ sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ fa ŋon? Yaa maŋ Pɔɔl yele maŋ te be sɔɔ ŋon Wurubuarɛ loŋ?.

eng: Thank God that I didnt baptize any of you, except Crispus and Gaius.
local: Mɛɛ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ nyi lee Krispuse na Gayose wɔle na, ma te sɔ ŋolo Wurubuarɛ loŋ ya.

eng: so that nobody can say that you were baptized in my name!.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ ŋolo be tale yako nyi, be sɔɔ e Wurubuarɛ loŋ bo lee ma yele man ya.

eng: Oh, I baptized the house of Stephanas too. Otherwise, I dont know if I baptized anyone else.
