local: Mɛɛ tɔɔse mɔ nyi me sɔɔ Setefanase na ɔ dekpaŋalaŋmantena Wurubuarɛ loŋ. Lee kɛwɔ wɔle na, mɔɔ ne tɔɔse nyi me sɔɔ ŋolo Wurubuarɛ loŋ bela ya.

eng: Christ didnt send me to baptize but to preach the good news. And Christ didnt send me to preach the good news with clever words so that Christs cross wont be emptied of its meaning.
local: Nawolo nyi Kristo te kpila maŋ nyi me kaa sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ ya. Mɔna ɔ kpila maŋ bo nyi me kaa kolosi ɔ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan. Me ta mo denibalaŋ nyansa kolosi nombia kpaakpaa ŋenaŋ ya. Nyi meŋ moo denibalaŋ nyansa kolosi nombia kpaakpaa ŋenaŋ nɛ, nafɔ Kristo yeŋ keŋ ɔ ba yem ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ wose man nombia be gyae ke yɛɛ nɔɔdoŋ ya.

eng: The message of the cross is foolishness to those who are being destroyed. But it is the power of God for those of us who are being saved.
local: Kristo ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ yeŋ keŋ nombia yɛɛ diyiŋ nombia ŋa fa balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yoo Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛ man nɛ, mɔna daa baŋ Wurubuarɛ ne lɛɛ da nyee nɛ dɔɔ ŋe yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ doŋ ke fa daa.

eng: It is written in scripture: I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and I will reject the intelligence of the intelligent .
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyiaa, Maa yela nyansatena baŋ nyansa ke yɛɛ yakaa, na maa yela baŋ be sia be kare nɛ, mɔ sia kekara keŋ ke yɛɛ yakaa.

eng: Where are the wise? Where are the legal experts? Where are todays debaters? Hasnt God made the wisdom of the world foolish?.
local: Nyi akpaa nnɛ te doo na, kenaŋ na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyansatena na tɔne gyeŋara na nombia gyeŋara baŋ daseŋae yɛna aleŋ Wurubuarɛ siaman? Wurubuarɛ ta lese wola nyi tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyansa yɛɛ diyiŋ nombia ŋa ɔ siaman.

eng: In Gods wisdom, he determined that the world wouldnt come to know him through its wisdom. Instead, God was pleased to save those who believe through the foolishness of preaching.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nyansa man na, ɔ yɛɛ ke nyi balaŋ be gyae baa tale mo baŋ gbagba be nyansa gyeŋ e ya. Mena dɔɔ ke yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii nyi waa ta na nombia kpaakpaa ŋan dee kolosi te balaŋ kpa ŋe yɛɛ diyiŋ nombia ŋa nɛ dɔɔ lɛɛ balaŋ baŋ baa lɛɛ ŋa di nɛ nyee.

eng: Jews ask for signs, and Greeks look for wisdom.
local: Gyudatena ne gyae gyakoloŋ nombia bo baa naa, te Girikitena mɔ ne gyae nyansa nombia bo baa nyii.

eng: but we preach Christ crucified, which is a scandal to Jews and foolishness to Gentiles.
local: Mɔna daa ne kolosi Kristo ŋon bɔɔ gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ wose man nombia bo. Mena nombia ŋenaŋ yɛɛ baŋ nombia ŋa fa Gyudatena, te ŋe yɛɛ diyiŋ nombia ŋa fa balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ.

eng: But to those who are called—both Jews and Greeks—Christ is Gods power and Gods wisdom.
local: Te balaŋ baŋ pou Wurubuarɛ be baake nɛ dɔɔ, be yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ o, yaa ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, Kristo yɛna Wurubuarɛ doŋ na ɔ nyansa fa wɔ.

eng: This is because the foolishness of God is wiser than human wisdom, and the weakness of God is stronger than human strength.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nyansa keŋ deniwalaŋ bee bu ke ya nɛ, keŋ kela na deniwalaŋ nyansa, te ɔ doŋ keŋ deniwalaŋ bee bu ke ya nɛ, keŋ kela na deniwalaŋ doŋ.

eng: Look at your situation when you were called, brothers and sisters! By ordinary human standards not many were wise, not many were powerful, not many were from the upper class.
local: Me tebia, ɛ tɔɔse mena keŋ ɛ doo pɛte Wurubuarɛ be baake ŋon nɛ. Nawolo nyi debaŋ kenaŋ na, na ɛ popou yɛna siakaralatena balaŋ siaman ya, te na ɛ popou mɔ yɛna balaŋ dinaana balaŋ siaman ya, te na ɛ popou mɔ yɛna gyoorobia ya.

eng: But God chose what the world considers foolish to shame the wise. God chose what the world considers weak to shame the strong.
local: Mɔna balaŋ baŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ ne kɛɛ wɔ nyi be yɛɛ ayiŋtenawɔ nɛ, baŋ te Wurubuarɛ lesewɔ te ɔ moo wɔ do siakaralatena desɛŋ. Mena mɔ te balaŋ baŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ ne kɛɛ wɔ nyi bɔɔ dana doŋ ya nɛ, baŋ te Wurubuarɛ lesewɔ te ɔ moo wɔ do baŋ be dana doŋ nɛ desɛŋ.

eng: And God chose what the world considers low-class and low-life—what is considered to be nothing—to reduce what is considered to be something to nothing.
local: Balaŋ baŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ ne kɛɛ wɔ nyi ba tekaboena ya, yaa bɔɔ taŋ tɔnɔɔ ya, te bɔɔ ne kala wɔ mɔ do kolo man ya nɛ, baŋ te Wurubuarɛ lesewɔ. Ɔ te yɛɛ mena yela balaŋ baŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ ne bu wɔ nyi baŋ yɛna balaŋ nɛ, te yɛɛ yakaa.

eng: So no human being can brag in Gods presence.
local: Ɔ yɛɛwɔ mena na ka wola nyi, ŋolo be tale gyina ɔ wose adido Wurubuarɛ siaman ya.

eng: It is because of God that you are in Christ Jesus. He became wisdom from God for us. This means that he made us righteous and holy, and he delivered us.
local: Nawolo nyi ŋon Wurubuarɛ yele la te daa na Kristo Yesu yɛɛ dokoloŋ. Te de nyansa pou lee ŋon Kristo gyaŋ. Ŋon yele la te de gbɛɛneŋ teneewɔ Wurubuarɛ siaman, te da bese balaŋ baŋ ɔ be tɔ da nyeeman kom te ɔ gberaa da ayimɛɛ pou lee da wose man.

eng: This is consistent with what was written: The one who brags should brag in the Lord! .
local: Mena dɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nɛ nyiaa, Nyaŋ walaŋ ŋon nyaŋ ke gyɔ n konɔɔ lee kolo dɔɔ na, kaboena nyi nya gyɔ n konɔɔ lee kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ fa neŋ nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: When I came to you, brothers and sisters, I didnt come preaching Gods secrets to you like I was an expert in speech or wisdom.
local: Me tebia, debaŋ keŋ mɔɔ kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ kaa di Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan wose man adansɛɛ fa ŋon nɛ, ma te kɔŋ kaa wola nyi ma gyeŋ kekolosi yaa ma gyeŋ nyansa kela ɛ man ŋolo ya.

eng: I had made up my mind not to think about anything while I was with you except Jesus Christ, and to preach him as crucified.
local: Debaŋ keŋ me kyaa ɛ gyaŋ nɛ, ma te gyae nyi maa gyeŋ kolo kpu na Yesu Kristo na ɔ yeŋ keŋ ɔ ba yem ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ ya.

eng: I stood in front of you with weakness, fear, and a lot of shaking.
local: Mena dɔɔ ma moo wose keyɔkɔse na gyakoloŋ na wose keseree kyaa ɛ gyaŋ.

eng: My message and my preaching werent presented with convincing wise words but with a demonstration of the Spirit and of power.
local: Ma kekolosi na me kawola ta lee deniwalaŋ agyueŋ na adao gbɛɛneŋ man ya, mɔna kɛɛ lese doŋ keŋ doo Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon man nɛ debɔɔ bo.

eng: I did this so that your faith might not depend on the wisdom of people but on the power of God.
local: Na ɛ be nyiŋ mo ɛ kelɛɛdi gyakaa deniwalaŋ nyansa dɔɔ ya, mɔna ɛ ka mo gyakaa Wurubuarɛ doŋ keŋ dɔɔ.

eng: What we say is wisdom to people who are mature. It isnt a wisdom that comes from the present day or from todays leaders who are being reduced to nothing.
local: Dɛɛ mo nyansa bo kolosi fa Kristotena baŋ bɔɔ bee Kristo ɔsom keŋ man nɛ. Na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyansa na gyooneŋ baŋ be doŋ pou ne gyu kɔ ɔto nɛ nyansa yaa de dana dee kolosi ya.

eng: We talk about Gods wisdom, which has been hidden as a secret. God determined this wisdom in advance, before time began, for our glory.
local: Wurubuarɛ nyansa keŋ ka weesɛɛ, te ɔ ba mo ke weese nyi waa mo lese de gyoorobiiri wola pɛte ɔ be yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ nɛ, keŋ te dɛɛ mo kolosi.

eng: It is a wisdom that none of the present-day rulers have understood, because if they did understand it, they would never have crucified the Lord of glory!.
local: Tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyooneŋ baŋ man ŋolo na ŋolo te nyii nyansa kei man ya. Nyi beŋ nyii ke man na, nafɔ beŋ te gyɔ gyoorobiiri Gbeŋgyoo ŋon sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ ya.

eng: But this is precisely what is written: God has prepared things for those who love him that no eye has seen, or ear has heard, or that havent crossed the mind of any human being .
local: Mɔna ke yɛɛ kpene keŋ bɔɔ ŋmarase do Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man. Be ŋmarasewɔ nyiaa, Kpene keŋ sibii te naa ta see ya, na keŋ deŋele te nyii ta see ya, na kpene keŋ deniwalaŋ agyueŋ te gyakaa ta ke dɔɔ see ya nɛ, keŋ te Wurubuarɛ desinawɔ see fa baŋ bɛɛ gyae e nɛ.

eng: God has revealed these things to us through the Spirit. The Spirit searches everything, including the depths of God.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ te tɛɛ ɔ feliŋ ŋon dɔɔ lese ɔ nombia aweeseŋ ŋan wola daa. Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋonaŋ ne kɛola kpene kamasɛ man, te ɔ gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia dinaana ŋan mɔ man.

eng: Who knows a persons depths except their own spirit that lives in them? In the same way, no one has known the depths of God except Gods Spirit.
local: Amɔte gyeŋa na deniwalaŋ nyeeman nombia kpu na feliŋ ŋon doo ɔtenate man nɛ? Mena mɔ te walaŋ be kyaa keŋ ɔ gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nyeeman nombia kpu na Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ya.

eng: We havent received the worlds spirit but Gods Spirit so that we can know the things given to us by God.
local: Daa dɔɔ, na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyansa te Wurubuarɛ faa daa ya, mɔna ɔ feliŋ ŋon te ɔ faa daa na dee nyiŋ nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia dinaana ŋan ɔ ba mo fa daa nɛ man.

eng: These are the things we are talking about—not with words taught by human wisdom but with words taught by the Spirit—we are interpreting spiritual things to spiritual people.
local: Mena dɔɔ na mena keŋ deniwalaŋ nyansa na wolo nɛ te dɛɛ mo kolosi ya, mɔna nyansa keŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ na wolo nɛ, te dɛɛ mo kolosi nombia ŋan fa balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ doo be man nɛ.

eng: But people who are unspiritual dont accept the things from Gods Spirit. They are foolishness to them and cant be understood, because they can only be comprehended in a spiritual way.
local: Walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ feliŋ be doo ɔ man ya na, ɔ bɛɛ tale lɛɛ nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ne yako nɛ do ya. Nawolo nyi nombia ŋenaŋ yɛɛ diyiŋ nombia ŋa fa e, te ɔ be gyae waa tale nyii ŋe man ya. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon waageŋ kooŋ ne tale la yela balaŋ ne nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan man.

eng: Spiritual people comprehend everything, but they themselves arent understood by anyone.
local: Walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ feliŋ doo ɔ man nɛ, ɔ ne tale gyeŋ abɔɔ pou man, mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ bɛɛ tale gyeŋ ɔtenate nombia man ya.

eng: Who has known the mind of the Lord, who will advise him? But we have the mind of Christ.
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nyiaa, Amɔte gyeŋa na de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ nyeeman, na waa wola e nombia? Mɔna daa gyeŋ nombia kɛŋa pou man, nawolo nyi de dana Kristo agyueŋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I couldnt talk to you like spiritual people but like unspiritual people, like babies in Christ.
local: Me tebia, debaŋ keŋ me kyaa ɛ gyaŋ nɛ, ma te tale kolosi fa ŋon ŋgba balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ doo be man nideli nɛ ya, mɔna me kolosiwɔ fa ŋon bo ŋgba balaŋ baŋ be sia gyakaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ dɔɔ nɛ, yaa balaŋ baŋ be sɛɛ be yɛɛ kyokyoosewɔ Kristo man nɛ.

eng: I gave you milk to drink instead of solid food, because you werent up to it yet.
local: Yɛɛ yaa ŋgba dide loŋ te ma moowɔ fa ŋon nɛ. Ŋa te yɛɛ weenɛɛ doŋ nyiŋan ya, nawolo nyi na ɛ ta bee ta na weenɛɛ doŋ nyiŋan ke di ya. Kaa lii na gyɛŋ wee kei koraŋ nɛ, ɛ ta bee ta na ŋe ke di ya.

eng: Now you are still not up to it because you are still unspiritual. When jealousy and fighting exist between you, arent you unspiritual and living by human standards?.
local: Ɛ sɛɛ ɛ yɛɛ ŋgba balaŋ baŋ be loo gyakaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ dɔɔ nɛ. Nawolo nyi daaworo na aŋmaareŋ na kale nombia doo ɛ man. Kenaŋ na ŋgba ɛ sɛɛ ɛ yɛɛ ŋgba balaŋ baŋ be loo gyakaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ dɔɔ nɛ baa?.

eng: When someone says, I belong to Paul, and someone else says, I belong to Apollos, arent you acting like people without the Spirit?.
local: Akpaa ŋolo de yako nyi, Me silana Pɔɔl bo te ŋolo mɔ kpa Me silana Apolo bo na, ŋgba ɛ sɛɛ ɛ yɛɛ baa ŋgba tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ nɛ?.

eng: After all, what is Apollos? What is Paul? They are servants who helped you to believe. Each one had a role given to them by the Lord.
local: Ke gbagba na, amɔte yɛna Apolo te amɔte yɛna maŋ Pɔɔl mɔ. De popou yɛɛ de kya Wurubuarɛ tewulɛɛ baŋ ɛ be tɛɛ de dɔɔ lɛɛ nyeedoŋ nombia ŋan di nɛ wɔ. Te de man walaŋ kamasɛ dana tom keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu be fa e nyi waa yɛɛ.

eng: I planted, Apollos watered, but God made it grow.
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan dee kolosi nɛ, yɛɛ yaa ŋgba kolo keŋ bɔɔ duu nɛ. Maŋ Pɔɔl duu la, te Apolo mɔ doo ke loŋ, mɔna Wurubuarɛ yele la te ka beewɔ.

eng: Because of this, neither the one who plants nor the one who waters is anything, but the only one who is anything is God who makes it grow.
local: Mena dɔɔ na walaŋ ŋon ɔ be duu, yaa ŋon ɔ ba do ke loŋ nɛ danaana yele ya, mɔna Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be yeli kɔɔ bee nɛ danaana yele.

eng: The one who plants and the one who waters work together, but each one will receive their own reward for their own labor.
local: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ be duu na ŋon ɔ ba do ke loŋ nɛ, be popou loo gyakaa bo kolo dokoloŋ dɔɔ. Te Wurubuarɛ waa tɔ walaŋ kamasɛ kom lee ɔ tom keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: We are Gods coworkers, and you are Gods field, Gods building.
local: Nawolo nyi daa na Wurubuarɛ bom la dɛɛ yɛɛ ɔ tom keŋ. Ɛ yɛɛ ŋgba Wurubuarɛ wɔɔ keŋ ɔ ne fare yaa ɔ deni keŋ ɔ ne maa nɛ.

eng: I laid a foundation like a wise master builder according to Gods grace that was given to me, but someone else is building on top of it. Each person needs to pay attention to the way they build on it.
local: Lee Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba mo boele maŋ dɔɔ nɛ, me ta see deni keŋ mokɔɔ ŋgba deni maare kpaakpaate ŋon ɔ gyeŋ ɔ tom nɛ. Keŋte baale mɔ kaŋawɔ bɛɛ maa gyakaa ke dɔɔ. Mɔna kaboena nyi walaŋ kamasɛ waa kɛɛ mena keŋ ɔ ne maa gyakaa nɛ nideli.

eng: No one can lay any other foundation besides the one that is already laid, which is Jesus Christ.
local: Yesu Kristo te Wurubuarɛ moowɔ see deni kei mokɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ ŋolo be gyae waa tale see mokɔɔ wɔle kpu na keŋ bɔɔ wulaa see nɛ ya.

eng: So, whether someone builds on top of the foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, grass, or hay.
local: Nyi akpaa ŋolo da mo kɔba yayam yaa kɔba wolom yaa boya ŋan ŋe dana koya doŋ yaa daase yaa kaase ma gyakaa mokɔɔ kasee gyaŋgbate keŋ dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: each ones work will be clearly shown. The day will make it clear, because it will be revealed with fire—the fire will test the quality of each ones work.
local: wee dinaa keŋ Kristo waa bese kɔŋ na, baa lese kpene kamasɛ debɔɔ na walaŋ kamasɛ tom keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ ka lee debɔɔ. Nawolo nyi boalaŋ te baa mo lese kpene kamasɛ debɔɔ. Boalaŋ kenaŋ ne gyaela ka lese tom keŋ walaŋ kamasɛ be yɛɛ nɛ dinɔɔ wola.

eng: If anyones work survives, theyll get a reward.
local: Akpaa nyi ŋolo wui abɔɔ ŋan ɔ ba mo ma nɛ te kpaa boalaŋ kenaŋ man ya na, Wurubuarɛ waa tɔ e kom nideli.

eng: But if anyones work goes up in flames, theyll lose it. However, they themselves will be saved as if they had gone through a fire.
local: Mɔna akpaa ŋolo wui de kpaa na, ɔ te yɛɛ tom leki. Walaŋ ŋonaŋ gbagba dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ e, mɔna gyae ke yɛɛ bo ŋgba ɔ kɔlee e bo lee boalaŋ man nɛ.

eng: Dont you know that you are Gods temple and Gods Spirit lives in you?.
local: Ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi ɛmɛɛ gbagba yɛna Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ, te ɔ feliŋ ŋon doo ɛ man yee?.

eng: If someone destroys Gods temple, God will destroy that person, because Gods temple is holy, which is what you are.
local: Mena dɔɔ akpaa ŋolo de wɔlɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa wɔlɛɛ ɔtenate mɔ. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ desinaa ɔ deni dinaa keŋ bo see fa ɔ wose, te ɛmɛɛ yɛna ɔ deni dinaa kenaŋ.

eng: Dont fool yourself. If some of you think they are worldly-wise, then they should become foolish so that they can become wise.
local: Ɛ na beo ɛ wose ya. Akpaa ŋolo doo ɛ man te ɔ ne kɛɛ ɔ wose nyi ɔ yɛɛ siakaralate e tɛɛle kei dɔɔ na, kaboena nyi waa besena ɔ wose yɛɛ diyiŋte na waa nyiŋ bese siakaralate gbagba.

eng: This worlds wisdom is foolishness to God. As its written, He catches the wise in their cleverness. .
local: Nawolo nyi tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyansa yɛɛ diyiŋ nombia ŋa Wurubuarɛ siaman. Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nyiaa, Siakaralatena adao man te Wurubuarɛ na mo kyaŋ wɔ.

eng: So a person should think about us this way—as servants of Christ and managers of Gods secrets.
local: Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi walaŋ kamasɛ waa lɛɛ daa do nyi de yɛɛ Kristo kpilalawɔ, te de yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo ɔ nombia dinaana aweeseŋ ŋan do de nyiŋmaa man.

eng: And also, The Lord knows that the thoughts of the wise are silly .
local: Te ba besewɔ ŋmarase see nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ gyeŋ nyi siakaralatena konɔɔ man nombia be dana tɔnɔɔ ya.

eng: In this kind of situation, what is expected of a manager is that they prove to be faithful.
local: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kɛɛ ŋolo kolo dɔɔ nɛ, kpene keŋ kaboena nyi waa yɛɛ yɛna nyi, waa di anokoare fa ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon.

eng: So then, no one should brag about human beings. Everything belongs to you.
local: Mena dɔɔ ŋolo na mo ɔ sia gyakaa deniwalaŋ dɔɔ, na waa gyina ɔ wose adido ya, nawolo nyi kpene kamasɛ yɛɛ ɛ wui.

eng: I couldnt care less if Im judged by you or by any human court; I dont even judge myself.
local: Te maŋ dɔɔ akpaa nyi ɛ man baale yaa balaŋ wɔlɛɛ baale de bu maŋ fɔɔ na, bɛɛ takaa maŋ ya. Maŋ gbagba koraŋ bee bu ma wose fɔɔ ya.

eng: Paul, Apollos, Cephas, the world, life, death, things in the present, things in the future—everything belongs to you.
local: Pɔɔl o Apolo o Pita o tɛɛle kei o nyeedoŋ o yeŋ o debaŋ kei o wee daale o, ŋe popou yɛɛ ɛ wui.

eng: Im not aware of anything against me, but that doesnt make me innocent, because the Lord is the one who judges me.
local: Me konɔɔ man nombia bee bu maŋ fɔɔ ya, mɔna kei bɛɛ wolo nyi nombiikum daale be doo me man ya. De Gbeŋgyoo Yesu yɛna ŋon ɔ ne di me nombia.

eng: but you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
local: Ɛmɛɛ mɔ yɛɛ Kristo wui, te Kristo mɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ wui.

eng: So dont judge anything before the right time—wait until the Lord comes. He will bring things that are hidden in the dark to light, and he will make peoples motivations public. Then there will be recognition for each person from God.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na barase bu ŋolo fɔɔ pɛte de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu kekɔŋ keŋ kelii ya. Ŋon ne gyae la ɔ kaa lese kpene kamasɛ keŋ weesɛɛ nɛ debɔɔ, na waa lese walaŋ konɔɔ man nombia pou debɔɔ. Debaŋ kenaŋ te Wurubuarɛ waa kɛlɛɛ walaŋ kamasɛ lee ɔ tom wa ayɛɛŋ dɔɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I have applied these things to myself and Apollos for your benefit. Ive done this so that you can learn what it means not to go beyond what has been written and so none of you will become arrogant by supporting one of us against the other.
local: Me tebia, ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ te mɛɛ yako nombia kɛŋa te ma moo ma wose mɛɛ maa na Apolo wola ŋon, na ke yela ɛ ka gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man. Nombia ŋenaŋ yɛna nyiaa, ɛ na gbaa nombia ŋan bɔɔ ŋmarase see nɛ dɔɔ ke yɛɛ kolo wɔle ya. Nyi akpaa ɛ yɛɛ mena na, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke kɛɛ ŋolo nyi ɔ dei kela ɔ dɔɔ ya.

eng: Who says that you are better than anyone else? What do you have that you didnt receive? And if you received it, then why are you bragging as if you didnt receive it?.
local: Amɔte yako na ŋon nyi ɛ kela ɛ tebia? Woŋ te ɛ dana keŋ nyi na Wurubuarɛ faa na ŋon ya? Te akpaa Wurubuarɛ faa na ŋon na, weera te ɛ ne gyina ɛ wose adido ŋgba na ŋon faa na ŋon ya nɛ?.

eng: Youve been filled already! Youve become rich already! You rule like kings without us! I wish you did rule so that we could be kings with you!.
local: Ɛ gyeŋ nyi ɛ taŋ ke wulaa nyiŋ kpene kamasɛ keŋ ne hia ŋon? Te ɛ gyeŋ nyi ɛ taŋ wulaa nyiŋ ɛ wose? Ɛ gyeŋ nyi ɛ taŋ lee de wɔle di gyoori keŋ daa sɛɛ de yɛɛ balaŋ yakaa nyibaŋwɔ? Nafɔ me gyaewɔ nyi yaa bese gyooneŋ, na daa mɔ ke di gyoori kpu na ŋon.

eng: I suppose that God has shown that we apostles are at the end of the line. We are like prisoners sentenced to death, because we have become a spectacle in the world, both to angels and to humans.
local: Nawolo nyi ne yɛɛ maŋ ŋgba Wurubuarɛ ta mo daa Kristo kpilala baŋ see wɔle wɔle, ŋgba balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ doo daale man ya, te bɔɔ lese wɔ see nyi baa ko balaŋ man nɛ. De ta bese kpene keŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ na Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ be kyaa adido nɛ pou ne kɛɛ.

eng: We are fools for Christ, but you are wise through Christ! We are weak, but you are strong! You are honored, but we are dishonored!.
local: Lee Kristo dɔɔ daa yɛɛ ayiŋtenawɔ, mɔna ɛmɛɛ te tɛɛ Kristo dɔɔ bese siakaralatena. Daa be dana doŋ ya, mɔna ɛmɛɛ dana doŋ. Balaŋ ne bu ŋon, mɔna daa dɔɔ bɔɔ ne bu daa ya.

eng: Up to this very moment we are hungry, thirsty, wearing rags, abused, and homeless.
local: Mo kaa lii naŋ gyɛŋ kei nɛ, de dɔŋ na de tanam na da loŋboaneŋ! De doo abɔɔ akyekpɛɛ man, te bee tuku daa yeŋ yeŋ te daa gbagba mɔ be dana dekyae ya.

eng: We work hard with our own hands. When we are insulted, we respond with a blessing; when we are harassed, we put up with it.
local: Dɛɛ mo daa gbagba nyiŋmaase yɛɛ tom nideli. Akpaa balaŋ de falaa daa na, daa na sola Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ bo fa wɔ. Akpaa be naase daa diyem na, dɛɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ seŋ kelii ɔto.

eng: when our reputation is attacked, we are encouraging. We have become the scum of the earth, the waste that runs off everything, up to the present time.
local: Akpaa be wɔlɛɛ da yele na, dɛɛ mo nɔɔwoya kpaakpaa nyiŋan tiranɔɔ fa wɔ. Kaa lii naŋ gyɛŋ wee kei nɛ, de ta bese tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ ayimɛɛ nyiŋan, na walaŋ kamasɛ dufuŋ.

eng: Im not writing these things to make you ashamed but to warn you, since you are my loved children.
local: Mɔɔ ne ŋmarase nombia kɛŋa na maa mo do ŋon desɛŋ ya, mɔna mɛɛ faree ŋon bo ŋgba me bia kpaakpaa nɛ.

eng: You may have ten thousand mentors in Christ, but you dont have many fathers. I gave birth to you in Christ Jesus through the gospel.
local: Akpaa de kɔŋ nyi ɛ dana balaŋ baŋ baa kɛɛ ɛ dɔɔ Kristo man ŋgba balaŋ kakpoŋse kufu koraŋ na, ɛ be dana kyaɔ burum mena ya. Nawolo nyi Kristo man dɔɔ me ta bese ɛ kya lee debaŋ keŋ mɔɔ yako ŋon nombia kpaakpaa ŋan nɛ.

eng: so I encourage you to follow my example.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ sola ŋon nideli nyi yaa kasee me dinɔɔ.

eng: This is why Ive sent Timothy to you; hes my loved and trusted child in the Lord; hell remind you about my way of life in Christ Jesus. Hell teach the same way as I teach everywhere in every church.
local: Kei dɔɔ te mee kpila me bu Timoti ŋon mɛɛ gyae e, te ɔ ne di anokoare Kristo man nɛ ɛ gyaŋ. Ŋon ne gyae la ɔ kaa tɔɔse ŋon me gbɛɛneŋ ŋan mɛɛ mo sila Kristo Yesu te mɛɛ mo wola Kristotena akpiise ŋan man yenaŋ kamasɛ nɛ.

eng: Some have become arrogant as if Im not coming to see you.
local: Ɛ man baale ne yɛɛ konɔɔyuŋ ŋgba mɔɔ gyae maa tekaa bese kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ bo ya nɛ.

eng: But, if the Lord is willing, Ill come to you soon. Then I wont focus on what these arrogant people say, but Ill find out what power they possess.
local: Mɔna akpaa de Gbeŋgyoo de sɛɛ na, be gyae ke kyare ya maa bese kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ. Balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ konɔɔyuŋ nɛ, na be nombia ŋan bee kolosi nɛ ŋaageŋ te mɛɛ gyae me kaa nyii ya, mɔna mɛɛ gyae me kaa naa doŋ keŋ be dana bɛɛ yɛɛ mena nombia kɛŋa nɛ.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about words but about power.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ nombia ba seŋɛɛ kekolosi yaa nɔɔwoya ŋaageŋ dɔɔ ya, mɔna ka seŋae Wurubuarɛ doŋ keŋ dɔɔ.

eng: Which do you want? Should I come to you with a big stick to punish you, or with love and a gentle spirit?.
local: Woŋti te ɛ ne gyae? Ɛ ne gyae nyi maa mo dambila kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ yaa, maa mo balaŋ kegyae na konɔɔ kefɛɛ bo kɔŋ.

eng: Everyone has heard that there is sexual immorality among you. This is a type of immorality that isnt even heard of among the Gentiles—a man is having sex with his fathers wife!.
local: Balaŋ te yeli me te nyii nyi kafoŋ kum daale dinɔɔ ta gyoo ɛ man, keŋ ɛ man baale gba ne doo na be kyaɔ wɛɛla. Mena dinɔɔ kum kei be doo banɛɛ somra man gba koraŋ ya.

eng: And youre proud of yourselves instead of being so upset that the one who did this thing is expelled from your community.
local: Mɔna ke dana ŋon gyoŋ te ɛ ne gyina ɛ wose adido. Nafɔ kaboena bo nyi nombia ŋenaŋ dɛɛ takaa ŋon na ke yɛɛ ŋon desɛŋ, na ɛ ka lese walaŋ ŋon ɔ be yɛɛ mena nombia ŋenaŋ dinɔɔ nɛ lee ɛ dikpii keŋ man.

eng: Though Im absent physically, Im present in the spirit and Ive already judged the man who did this as if I were present.
local: Doo nyi maŋ gbagba be kyaa ɛ gyaŋ koraŋ ya nɛ, me konɔɔ man na me kyaa ɛ gyaŋ. Mena dɔɔ me taŋ ke wulaa bu walaŋ ŋon ɔ be yɛɛ mena nombia nkaraŋkaraŋ ŋenaŋ nɛ fɔɔ lee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu yele man, ŋgba maŋ gbagba kyaa ɛ gyaŋ bo nɛ.

eng: When you meet together in the name of our Lord Jesus, Ill be present in spirit with the power of our Lord Jesus.
local: Debaŋ keŋ ɛmɛɛ Kristotena ɛ gyaŋee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu yele man, te me konɔɔ man na me kyaa ɛ gyaŋ, te de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu doŋ keŋ doo ɛ man nɛ.

eng: At that time we need to hand this man over to Satan to destroy his human weakness so that his spirit might be saved on the day of the Lord.
local: ɛ mo mena walaŋ ŋonaŋ do ɔbɔnsam nyiŋmaa man, na waa naase ɔ wosenaane diyem, na Wurubuarɛ kenyiŋ lɛɛ ɔ nyee de Gbeŋgyoo wee ɔtomante keŋ man.

eng: Your bragging isnt good! Dont you know that a tiny grain of yeast makes a whole batch of dough rise?.
local: Mena keŋ ɛ na wola ɛ wose nɛ be dei ya. Ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi bodobodo tee kyomii ne yele la te bodobodo na koro yee?.

eng: Clean out the old yeast so you can be a new batch of dough, given that youre supposed to be unleavened bread. Christ our Passover lamb has been sacrificed.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ lese bodobodo tee keŋ seŋɛɛ fa dukum nɛ lee ɛ wose man, na ɛ ka bese bodobodo biliŋ keŋ tee be doo ke man ya nɛ. Dinɔɔ kei kaboela na ŋon. Nawolo nyi be ta mo Kristo ŋon ɔ yɛɛ da nyeekelɛɛ nambu e nɛ deesi.

eng: so lets celebrate the feast with the unleavened bread of honesty and truth, not with old yeast or with the yeast of evil and wickedness.
local: Mena dɔɔ tekaboena nyi dɛɛ mo bodobodo keŋ tee doo ke man nɛ, di da nyeekelɛɛwee dinaa keŋ ya. Tee keŋ ne yeli bodobodo na koro nɛ, seŋɛɛ fa nombiakumɛɛ na akpanyaŋnyaŋ keyɛɛ. Mɔna ɛ yela dɛɛ mo bodobodo keŋ tee be doo ke man ya nɛ, di da nyeekelɛɛwee dinaa keŋ. Bodobodo kei mɔ seŋɛɛ fa nombia kpaakpaa na anokoare keŋ.

eng: I wrote to you in my earlier letter not to associate with sexually immoral people.
local: Me te wulaa ŋmarase tɔne yelee ŋon nyi, ɛ na mo ɛ wose do na balaŋ kafoŋtena ya.

eng: But I wasnt talking about the sexually immoral people in the outside world by any means—or the greedy, or the swindlers, or people who worship false gods—otherwise, you would have to leave the world entirely!.
local: Mɔna na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ kafoŋ, na baŋ be dana sibii, na ŋmɛɛlatena na banɛɛ somra yaa me dana ya. Nyi akpaa mena balaŋ kɛwɔ yaa me dana na, kenaŋ na nafɔ kaboena nyi yaa lese ɛ wose lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ pou.

eng: But now Im writing to you not to associate with anyone who calls themselves brother or sister who is sexually immoral, greedy, someone who worships false gods, an abusive person, a drunk, or a swindler. Dont even eat with anyone like this.
local: Mɔna kpene keŋ mɔɔ ŋmarase yelee ŋon yɛna nyi, akpaa ŋolo ne baake ɔ wose nyi Kristo walaŋ, te ɔ sɛɛ ɔ ne yɛɛ kewɔlɛɛ nombia, yaa ɔ dana sibii yaa ɔ na som bane yaa ɔ ne wɔlɛɛ balaŋ yele, yaa ɔ yɛɛ soloŋnyɔɔre e, yaa ɔ yɛɛ ŋmɛɛlate e na, ɛ wɔŋ ɛ wose lee mena walaŋ ŋonaŋ man. Ɛ na dii bom na mena walaŋ ŋonaŋ koraŋ gba ya.

eng: What do I care about judging outsiders? Isnt it your job to judge insiders?.
local: Me woŋ faa la keŋ nyi maa di balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Kristotenawɔ ya nɛ nombia? Mɔna ɛmɛɛ Kristotena dɔɔ, kaboena nyi yaa di dɔŋa nombia.

eng: God will judge outsiders. Expel the evil one from among you! .
local: Baŋ ba te yɛɛ Kristotenawɔ ya nɛ dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ ne gyae la waa di be nombia. Mɔna kaboena nyi yaa yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ne yako nɛ. Nombia ŋenaŋ yɛna nyiaa, ɛ lese walaŋkum ŋon lee ɛ dikpii keŋ man.

eng: When someone in your assembly has a legal case against another member, do they dare to take it to court to be judged by people who arent just, instead of by Gods people?.
local: Nyi akpaa ɛmɛɛ Kristotena wolɛɛwolɛɛ de nyiŋ nombia na, weera dɔɔ te ɛ na mo gyu balaŋ yakaa nyibaŋ gyaŋ, te ɛ bɛɛ mo gyu ɛ tebia Kristotena gyaŋ ya?.

eng: Or dont you know that Gods people will judge the world? If the world is to be judged by you, are you incompetent to judge trivial cases?.
local: Ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi ɛmɛɛ Wurubuarɛ balaŋ ne gyae la ɛ ke di tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ nombia yee? Akpaa nyi ɛmɛɛ ne gyae la ɛ ke di tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ nombia na, weera dɔɔ te ɛ be tale di ɛmɛɛ gbagba nsana nombia buruburu kɛŋa ya?.

eng: Dont you know that we will judge angels? Why not ordinary things?.
local: Ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi daa Kristotena ne gyae la dee di Wurubuarɛ kpilala nombia yee? Akpaa nyi daa ne gyae la dee di Wurubuarɛ kpilala nombia na, kenaŋ na dɔɔ tale di da kekyaabii kei man nombia yee?.

eng: So then if you have ordinary lawsuits, do you appoint people as judges who arent respected by the church?.
local: Akpaa nyi mena nombia kɛŋa doo ya nsana na, woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ na lese balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ dana daseŋae Kristotena siaman ya nɛ nyi baa di ɛ nombia?.

eng: Im saying this because you should be ashamed of yourselves! Isnt there one person among you who is wise enough to pass judgment between believers?.
local: Nombii kei dana desɛŋ! Mena dɔɔ ɛ be dana siakaralate ŋolo ɛ man keŋ waa tale di ɛ nombia fa ŋon yee?.

eng: But instead, does a brother or sister have a lawsuit against another brother or sister, and do they do this in front of unbelievers?.
local: Mɔna ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ, ɛ ne tale ke samenaa dɔŋa fa walaŋ ŋon ɔ te yɛɛ Kristo baale e ya nɛ nyi waa di ɛ nombia.

eng: The fact that you have lawsuits against each other means that youve already lost your case. Why not be wronged instead? Why not be cheated?.
local: Ɛ kɛɛ samenaa dɔŋa kei nawolo baa nyi ɛ te yale ɛ kelɛɛdi keŋ man. Akpaa ŋolo de yɛɛ nombiikum tia neŋ na, woŋ dɔɔ te n bee nyiŋ konɔɔ fa e ya? Yaa akpaa ŋolo da ŋere neŋ do na, woŋ dɔɔ te n bee nyiŋ konɔɔ fa e ya?.

eng: But instead you are doing wrong and cheating—and youre doing it to your own brothers and sisters.
local: Ɛmɛɛ gbagba koraŋ ne yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ tia ɛ tebia Kristotena, te ɛ na ŋere dɔŋa do.

eng: Dont you know that people who are unjust wont inherit Gods kingdom? Dont be deceived. Those who are sexually immoral, those who worship false gods, adulterers, both participants in same-sex intercourse.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra be gyae baa nyiŋ gbɛɛ gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man yee? Ɛ na yeli baa beo ŋon ya, nawolo nyi balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ kafoŋ, na baŋ bɛɛ som banɛɛ na baŋ bɛɛ lee be baala yaa be wɛɛla wɔle, na baala baŋ bee doo na dɔŋa.

eng: thieves, the greedy, drunks, abusive people, and swindlers wont inherit Gods kingdom.
local: na ŋmɛɛlatena na baŋ be dana sibii na soloŋ nyɔɔra na balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ wɔlɛɛ balaŋ na balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ mo doŋ lɛo balaŋ abɔɔ nɛ, be gyae baa nyiŋ gbɛɛ gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man ya.

eng: That is what some of you used to be! But you were washed clean, you were made holy to God, and you were made right with God in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and in the Spirit of our God.
local: Nafɔ na mena te ɛ man baale mɔ doo. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ baake na ŋon, te ɔ faafo ŋon te ɔ desina ŋon see fa ɔ wose. Nɛnɛɛ Wurubuarɛ te lɛɛ ŋon do nyi ɛ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ɔ siaman lee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo na Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon yele man.

eng: I have the freedom to do anything, but not everything is helpful. I have the freedom to do anything, but I wont be controlled by anything.
local: Me dana gbɛɛ nyi maa tale yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ, mɔna na ŋe popou deila fa maŋ ya. Me dana gbɛɛ nyi maa tale yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ, mɔna mɔɔ gyae maa fa kpene kamasɛ gbɛɛ na ke kedi gyoori me dɔɔ ya.

eng: Food is for the stomach and the stomach is for food, and yet God will do away with both. The body isnt for sexual immorality but for the Lord, and the Lord is for the body.
local: Weenɛɛ doo bo fa dubulu, te dubulu mɔ doo bo fa weenɛɛ. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ waa kɔŋ kaa wɔlɛɛ kɛŋa pou. Ba te yɛɛ wosenaane bo fa kafoŋ nombia ya, mɔna be yɛɛ ke bo nyi dɛɛ mo som de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ. De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ tee wosenaane keŋ.

eng: God has raised the Lord and will raise us through his power.
local: Wurubuarɛ tɛɛwɔ ɔ doŋ keŋ dɔɔ gyuusu de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu lee yeŋ man, mena ke te waa ta ɔ doŋ kenaŋ dɔɔ gyuusu daa mɔ lee yeŋ man.

eng: Dont you know that your bodies are parts of Christ? So then, should I take parts of Christ and make them a part of someone who is sleeping around? No way!.
local: Ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi ɛ wosenaane yɛɛ Kristo gbagba wosenaane yenaŋ daale ke yee? Kaboena nyi maa mo Kristo wosenaane yenaŋ daale ke tina kafoŋte? Korakora, be kɔŋ mena ya.

eng: Dont you know that anyone who is joined to someone who is sleeping around is one body with that person? The scripture says, The two will become one flesh .
local: Ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ mo ɔ wose tina kafoŋte na, ŋon na e ta bese wosenaane dokoloŋ yee? Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nyiaa, Alo na ɔ baale baa bese walaŋ dokoloŋ.

eng: The one who is joined to the Lord is one spirit with him.
local: Mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ mo ɔ wose fa de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu na, ŋon na e ta bese walaŋ dokoloŋ.

eng: Avoid sexual immorality! Every sin that a person can do is committed outside the body, except those who engage in sexual immorality commit sin against their own bodies.
local: Ɛ lese ɛ wose lee kafoŋ nombia man. Nombii kamasɛ be kyaa keŋ ne yɛɛ walaŋ wosenaane ayimɛɛ ŋgba kafoŋ nombia nɛ ya. Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ kafoŋ nombia na, ɔ ne yɛɛ dukum bo tia ŋon gbagba wosenaane.

eng: Or dont you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you? Dont you know that you have the Holy Spirit from God, and you dont belong to yourselves?.
local: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ɔ ba mo fa ŋon te ɔ doo ɛ man nɛ, ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi ɛ wosenaane yɛna ɔ deni dinaa yee? Ɛ be kyaa fa ɛ wose ya.

eng: You have been bought and paid for, so honor God with your body.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ lɛɛ ŋon bo na koya doŋ, mena dɔɔ ɛ mo wosenaane lese ŋon Wurubuarɛ yele.

eng: Now, about what you wrote: Its good for a man not to have sex with a woman.
local: Nɛnɛɛ lee nombia ŋan ɛ be ŋmarase kaa bɔɔse maŋ nɛ, keŋ me dana maa yako ŋon yɛna nyi, nafɔ dei nyi ŋolo na yale ya.

eng: Each man should have his own wife, and each woman should have her own husband because of sexual immorality.
local: Mɔna lee kafoŋ kum dɔɔ nɛ, kaboena nyi baale kamasɛ waa nyiŋ ɔ wose dɔɔ alo, na alo kamasɛ mɔ ke nyiŋ ŋon gbagba ɔ wose dɔɔ baale.

eng: The husband should meet his wifes sexual needs, and the wife should do the same for her husband.
local: Kaboena nyi baale kamasɛ waa yɛɛ kpene keŋ kaboena nyi waa yɛɛ yale keŋ tɛɛ nɛ fa ɔ wɛɛle, na alo kamasɛ mɔ ke yɛɛ mena fa ɔ baale.

eng: The wife doesnt have authority over her own body, but the husband does. Likewise, the husband doesnt have authority over his own body, but the wife does.
local: Alo yalale be kyaa ɔ wose dɔɔ ya, mɔna ɔ kyaa bo fa ɔ baale. Mena mɔ te baale yalale kamasɛ be kyaa ŋon gbagba ɔ wose dɔɔ ya, mɔna ɔ kyaa bo fa ɔ wɛɛle.

eng: Dont refuse to meet each others needs unless you both agree for a short period of time to devote yourselves to prayer. Then come back together again so that Satan might not tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
local: Alo na ɔ baale na bɛɛse dɔŋa dedoi ya, gyisɛ be bala pou be sɛɛ yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ nyi baa kyaa weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale na baa nyiŋ debaŋ fane. Mɔna kenaŋ wɔle na kaboena nyi baa bese kɔŋ dɔŋa gyaŋ bileŋ, na ɔbɔnsam be nyiŋ gbɛɛ kaa kɛɛse wɔ, nyi keŋ bɔɔ tale kyaŋ ba wose dɔɔ ya.

eng: Im saying this to give you permission; its not a command.
local: Mɛɛ yako ŋon nombia kɛŋa baa ŋgba kpene keŋ doo na ke gbɛɛ nɛ, mɔna ka te yɛɛ mmaraa te mɛɛ fa ŋon ya.

eng: I wish all people were like me, but each has a particular gift from God: one has this gift, and another has that one.
local: Nyi balaŋ pou deŋ doo ŋgba maŋ nɛ nafɔ me gyae, mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ dana kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ be fa e. Ŋolo dana kei, te ŋolo mɔ wui yɛɛ kpoo.

eng: Im telling those who are single and widows that its good for them to stay single like me.
local: Dayarase na liala dɔɔ nɛ, nyi beŋ kyaa ŋgba maŋ nɛ keŋ nyi bɔɔ yale ya na nafɔ me gyae.

eng: But if they cant control themselves, they should get married, because its better to marry than to burn with passion.
local: Mɔna akpaa nyi bɔɔ gyae baa tale kyaŋ ba wose ya na, dei nyi baa yala. Nawolo nyi be yale na dei kela keŋ baa kyaa na ba wose ke kyaŋ wɔ.

eng: Im passing on the Lords command to those who are married: A wife shouldnt leave her husband.
local: Ala na baala yalala baŋ dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi, alo na bɛɛ ɔ baale ya. Na maŋ ne yako na nombii kei ya, mɔna ka lee de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

eng: but if she does leave him, then she should stay single or be reconciled to her husband. And a man shouldnt divorce his wife.
local: Akpaa alo de bɛɛ ɔ baale na, kaboena nyi waa kyaa mena. Te akpaa ɔ be gyae waa tale kyaa ya na, kaboena nyi waa bese gyu ɔ baale ŋon gyaŋ na ŋon na e kabese bom. Mena mɔ te tekaboena nyi baale waa bɛɛ ɔ wɛɛle ya.

eng: Im telling everyone else : If a believer has a wife who doesnt believe, and she agrees to live with him, then he shouldnt divorce her.
local: Te balaŋ akaŋ baŋ mɔ nɛ mɛɛ yako wɔ nyiaa, kei ta lee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu gyaŋ ya mɔna ka lee me gyaŋ, akpaa Kristo baale ŋolo yala na alo ŋon ɔ te yɛɛ Kristo alo e ya, te alo ŋon de sɛɛ nyi waa kyaa ɔ gyaŋ na ɔ na bɛɛ e ya.

eng: If a woman has a husband who doesnt believe and he agrees to live with her, then she shouldnt divorce him.
local: Mena mɔ te akpaa Kristo alo ŋolo yala na baale ŋon ɔ te yɛɛ Kristo baale e ya, te baale ŋon de sɛɛ nyi ŋon na alo ŋon baa kyaa na, alo ŋon na bɛɛ e ya.

eng: The husband who doesnt believe belongs to God because of his wife, and the wife who doesnt believe belongs to God because of her husband. Otherwise, your children would be contaminated by the world, but now they are spiritually set apart.
local: Nawolo nyi lee Kristo alo ŋon dɔɔ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ te lɛɛ baale ŋon do. Mena mɔ te lee Kristo baale ŋon dɔɔ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ te lɛɛ alo ŋon do. Na mena ya na, nafɔ ɛ bia baŋ baa yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ ba wose dana ayimɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman. Te nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ te lɛɛ wɔ do.

eng: But if a spouse who doesnt believe chooses to leave, then let them leave. The brother or sister isnt tied down in these circumstances. God has called you to peace.
local: Mɔna akpaa ŋon ɔ bɛɛ lɛɛ Kristo di ya nɛ de bɛɛ nyi ɔ be kyaa yale keŋ tɛɛ ya na, yela e waa ta. Kolo be kyaa keŋ ne tɔ Kristo alo yaa Kristo baale gbɛɛ lee mena nombia kɛŋa man ya. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ baake daa bo nyi de kaa kyaa wosefɛɛreŋ man.

eng: How do you know as a wife if you will save your husband? Or how do you know as a husband if you will save your wife?.
local: Alo yalale, sena te nyaŋ ke tale gyeŋ nyi mberem lee nyaŋ dɔɔ na, n baale ŋon waa kaa lɛɛ Kristo di na waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa? Yaa baale yalale sena te nyaŋ ke tale gyeŋ nyi mberem lee nyaŋ dɔɔ na, n wɛɛle waa kaa lɛɛ Kristo di na waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa?.

eng: Nevertheless, each person should live the kind of life that the Lord assigned when he called each one. This is what I teach in all the churches.
local: Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi walaŋ kamasɛ waa kyaŋ kpene keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ba mo boele e nɛ man, na waa kyaa ŋgba mena keŋ nafɔ ɔ kyaa pɛte Wurubuarɛ be baake e nɛ. Kei yɛna mmaraa keŋ mɛɛ mo fa Kristotena pou yenaŋ kamasɛ keŋ mee gyu nɛ.

eng: If someone was circumcised when called, he shouldnt try to reverse it. If someone wasnt circumcised when he was called, he shouldnt be circumcised.
local: Nyi akpaa ŋolo kara ɔ baala wose pɛte Wurubuarɛ baake e te ɔ kaa lɛɛ Kristo di na, kaboena nyi waa kyaa mena. Mena mɔ te akpaa ŋolo te kara ɔ baala wose ya te Wurubuarɛ baake te ɔ kaa lɛɛ Kristo di na, ŋon mɔ waa kyaa mena.

eng: Circumcision is nothing; not being circumcised is nothing. What matters is keeping Gods commandments.
local: Nawolo nyi n kara n baala wose o, n te kare ya o, keŋ bee hia ya. Kpene keŋ ne hia yɛna nyi walaŋ kamasɛ waa di Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ.

eng: Each person should stay in the situation they were in when they were called.
local: Kaboena nyi walaŋ kamasɛ waa kyaa ɔ kekyaabii keŋ man ɔ kyaa pɛte Wurubuarɛ be baake e nɛ.

eng: If you were a slave when you were called, dont let it bother you. But if you are actually able to be free, take advantage of the opportunity.
local: Nyi akpaa n yɛɛ gbeŋi e pɛte Wurubuarɛ baake neŋ na, na yeli ke takaa neŋ ya. Mɔna akpaa nyi n ke tale nyiŋ gbɛɛ, keŋ n ka mo lese n wose lee gbeŋiri keŋ man na, yela n sia keyɛɛ.

eng: Anyone who was a slave when they were called by the Lord has the status of being the Lords free person. In the same way, anyone who was a free person when they were called is Christs slave.
local: Nawolo nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ gbeŋi e pɛte Wurubuarɛ be baake e nɛ, ɔ ta bese gyooro bu Kristo man. Mena mɔ te walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba lee gbeŋiri man pɛte Kristo be baake e na, ɔ ta bese Kristo gbeŋi.

eng: You were bought and paid for. Dont become slaves of people.
local: Wurubuarɛ lɛɛ ŋon bo na koya doŋ, mena dɔɔ ɛ na mo ɛ wose bese balaŋ gbeŋa ya.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, each of you should stay with God in the situation you were in when you were called.
local: Me tebia, mena keŋ walaŋ kamasɛ doo pɛte Wurubuarɛ be baake e nɛ, kaboena nyi waa kyaa mena fa Wurubuarɛ.

eng: I dont have a command from the Lord about people who have never been married, but Ill give you my opinion as someone you can trust because of the Lords mercy.
local: Dayarase dɔɔ, ma te lɛɛ ta mmaraa daale lee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu gyaŋ fa wɔ ya. Mɔna mee kolosi bo fa wɔ lee maŋ gbagba me nyansa man, ŋgba walaŋ ŋon de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ be wii me waraŋase, te ɔ be yeli ɛ ne lɛɛ maŋ di nɛ.

eng: So I think this advice is good because of the present crisis: Stay as you are.
local: Mo lee gyɛŋ wee kei tɛɛle nombia kedoo dɔɔ nɛ, kpene keŋ mɛɛ gyueŋ yɛna nyi walaŋ kamasɛ waa kyaa ɔ kekyaabii.

eng: If you are married, dont get a divorce. If you are divorced, dont try to find a spouse.
local: Akpaa n te yale na, na bɛɛ yale keŋ ya. Te akpaa n te yala ta ya na, na gyae yale gbɛɛ ya.

eng: But if you do marry, you havent sinned; and if someone who hasnt been married gets married, they havent sinned. But married people will have a hard time, and Im trying to spare you that.
local: Mɔna akpaa nyi n yale na, ka te yɛɛ dukum ke ya. Te akpaa alebu mɔ de yale na, ka te yɛɛ dukum ke ya. Mɔna baŋ bɔɔ yale nɛ, baa naa diyem ba kekyaa man. Mena dɔɔ te mɛɛ gyae maa lese ŋon lee ke man.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: The time has drawn short. From now on, those who have wives should be like people who dont have them.
local: Me tebia, kpene keŋ mɛɛ gyae maa yako ŋon yɛna nyiaa, dɔɔ gyae dɛɛ kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ kyare ya, mena dɔɔ lee gyɛŋ ne gyu nɛ, ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ be yale na, ɛ kyaa ŋgba ɛ te yala ta ya nɛ.

eng: Those who are sad should be like people who arent crying. Those who are happy should be like people who arent happy. Those who buy something should be like people who dont have possessions.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bee di walaŋsa nɛ, baa kyaa ŋgba balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ ne di walaŋsa ya nɛ. Balaŋ baŋ bee di gyoŋ nɛ, baa kyaa ŋgba balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ ne di gyoŋ ya nɛ. Te balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ lɛɛ abɔɔ nɛ, baa kyaa ŋgba abɔɔ ŋan bɛɛ lɛɛ nɛ te yɛɛ be wui ya nɛ.

eng: Those who use the world should be like people who arent preoccupied with it, because this world in its present form is passing away.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ mo be sia gyakaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ keyɛɛ dɔɔ nɛ, baa kyaa ŋgba balaŋ baŋ ba agyueŋ be gyakaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ kɛŋa dɔɔ ya nɛ. Nawolo nyi tɛɛle kei, na ka abɔɔ pou ne gyu ɔto baa.

eng: I want you to be free from concerns. A man who isnt married is concerned about the Lords concerns—how he can please the Lord.
local: Mɛɛ gyae nyi nafɔ yaa lee ɛ kayeyɛɛ pou man. Baale ŋon ɔ be dana alo ya na, wa agyueŋ pou gyakaa bo de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo tom keyɛɛ dɔɔ, lee mena keŋ waa yɛɛ na ɔ nombia ka gyoo de Gbeŋgyoo sia.

eng: But a married man is concerned about the worlds concerns—how he can please his wife.
local: Mɔna baale ŋon ɔ be yale dɔɔ, wa agyueŋ gyakaa bo tɛɛle kei abɔɔ dɔɔ, lee mena keŋ waa yɛɛ na ɔ nombia ka gyoo ɔ wɛɛle sia.

eng: His attention is divided. A woman who isnt married or who is a virgin is concerned about the Lords concerns so that she can be dedicated to God in both body and spirit. But a married woman is concerned about the worlds concerns—how she can please her husband.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ wa agyueŋ te kpase ala. Mena mɔ te alo yaa alebu keteŋ ŋon ɔ te yala ta ya na, wa agyueŋ gyakaa bo de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu tom keyɛɛ dɔɔ. Lee mena keŋ waa mo ɔ wose pou fa de Gbeŋgyoo, na ayimɛɛ be doo ɔ man ya. Mɔna alo yalale dɔɔ, wa agyueŋ pou gyakaa bo tɛɛle kei abɔɔ dɔɔ, lee mena keŋ waa yɛɛ na ɔ nombia ka gyoo ɔ baale sia.

eng: Im saying this for your own advantage. Its not to restrict you but rather to promote effective and consistent service to the Lord without distraction.
local: Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ deeli dɔɔ te mɛɛ yako nombia kɛŋa, te yɛɛ nyi mɛɛ tɔ ŋon kolo gbɛɛ bo ya. Mɔna mɛɛ gyae ɛ ke kyaa na ɛ ke yɛɛ kpene keŋ kaboena, na ɛ ka mo ɛ konɔɔ pou fa de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu kekpaa.

eng: If someone thinks he is acting inappropriately toward an unmarried woman whom he knows, and if he has strong feelings and it seems like the right thing to do, he should do what he wants—hes not sinning—they should get married.
local: Nyi akpaa baale ŋolo de kɛɛ nyi, ɔ be gyae waa tale kyaŋ ɔ wose kyaa daa alebu ŋon ŋon na e be bake see nyi baa yala dɔŋa ya, te ɔ kɛɛ nyi alebu ŋon ne ŋmɛɛ na, ɛ yela waa kpeo alo ŋon bileŋ. Kei te yɛɛ nombiikum ya.

eng: But if a man stands firm in his decision, and doesnt feel the pressure, but has his own will under control, he does right if he decides in his own heart not to marry the woman.
local: Mɔna walaŋ ŋon ŋon gbagba de yako ɔ konɔɔ man nyi waa tale kyaŋ ɔ wose keŋ ŋolo ta ŋere e ya, te ŋon gbagba ne tale di ɔ wose dɔɔ, te ɔ yɛɛ wa agyueŋ nyi ɔ be gyae waa yala alebu keteŋ ŋon ya na, ŋon mɔ te yɛɛ ke nideli.

eng: Therefore, the one who marries the unmarried woman does right, and the one who doesnt get married will do even better.
local: Mena dɔɔ walaŋ ŋon ɔ yala alebu keteŋ ŋon na, ɔ te yɛɛ ke nideli. Te ŋon mɔ ɔ bɛɛ ɔ te yala alebu keteŋ ŋon ya na, ŋon mɔ ta bese yɛɛ gbaa.

eng: A woman is obligated to stay in her marriage as long as her husband is alive. But if her husband dies, she is free to marry whomever she wants, only it should be a believer in the Lord.
local: Akpaa alo yalale baale kyaa na ɔ sia keŋ ɔ ta yeŋa ta ya na, yale mmaraa keŋ ne bake alo ŋon. Mɔna akpaa ɔ baale ŋon da yem na, gbɛɛ doo nyi waa tale yala baale ŋon ŋon gbagba ne gyae. Mɔna waa kɛɛ nyi baale ŋonaŋ yɛɛ Kristo walaŋ e.

eng: But in my opinion, she will be happier if she stays the way she is. And I think that I have Gods Spirit too.
local: Maŋ ma agyueŋ man na, akpaa ɔ kyaa mena ɔ te yale bela ya na, waa di gyoŋ nideli kela, te ma gyeŋ mɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon doo maŋ mɔ man.

eng: Now concerning meat that has been sacrificed to a false god: We know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge makes people arrogant, but love builds people up.
local: Nɛnɛɛ mɛɛ gyae maa kolosi fa ŋon lee bane weenɛɛ wose man. Ke yɛɛ ampaŋ ke nyi walaŋ kamasɛ gyeŋ nombia, mɔna mena nombia kagyeŋ kei ne yeli walaŋ ne gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ wola ɔ wose bo, te balaŋ kegyae ne yele la Kristotena ne tale kyɔ dɔŋa na baa seŋ nideli ba kelɛɛdi man.

eng: If anyone thinks they know something, they dont yet know as much as they should know.
local: Akpaa walaŋ na gyueŋ nyi ɔ gyeŋ nombia nɛ, ɔtenate ba gyeŋ kolo ya. Nawolo nyi ɔ ba gyeŋ ŋgba mena keŋ kaboena nyi waa gyeŋ nɛ ya.

eng: But if someone loves God, then they are known by God.
local: Mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne gyae Wurubuarɛ na, ŋon mɔ te Wurubuarɛ gyeŋ.

eng: So concerning the actual food involved in these sacrifices to false gods, we know that a false god isnt anything in this world, and that there is no God except for the one God.
local: Mena dɔɔ lee bane weenɛɛ kedi wose man nɛ, da gyeŋ nyi bane te yɛɛ kolo ke tɛɛle kei dɔɔ ya, te da gyeŋ mɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ dokoloŋ kyaa la.

eng: Granted, there are so-called gods, in heaven and on the earth, as there are many gods and many lords.
local: Doo nyi bɛɛneŋ na gbeŋgyoona burum kyaa. Nyi akpaa bɛɛneŋ baŋ balaŋ ne baake wɔ nyi be gbeŋgyoona nɛ kyaa adido bo o, yaa be kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ bo o nɛ.

eng: However, for us believers, There is one God the Father. All things come from him, and we belong to him. And there is one Lord Jesus Christ. All things exist through him, and we live through him.
local: daa dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ dokoloŋ kooŋ te de dana. Ŋon yɛna de kya ŋon kpene kamasɛ ba lee ɔ gyaŋ, te ŋon mɔ dɔɔ te de dana nyeedoŋ. Gbeŋgyoo dokoloŋ kooŋ mɔ te de dana, ŋon yɛna Yesu Kristo. Ɔ dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ tɛɛwɔ yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ, te ŋon mɔ dɔɔ te de dana nyeedoŋ.

eng: But not everybody knows this. Some are eating this food as though it really is food sacrificed to a real idol, because they were used to idol worship until now. Their conscience is weak because it has been damaged.
local: Mɔna na balaŋ pou gyeŋa na mena nombii kei ya. Nawolo nyi bane nombia ta gyoo be man nideli keŋ nyi akpaa be di mena weenɛɛ kɛŋa na, ne yɛɛ wɔ nyi be te di bane weenɛɛ. Te keŋ ba kelɛɛdi taŋ doŋ dɔɔ ya nɛ, na ne yɛɛ wɔ nyi be ta mo ayimɛɛ tina ba wose.

eng: Food wont bring us close to God. Were not missing out if we dont eat, and we dont have any advantage if we do eat.
local: Mɔna weenɛɛ be gyae ke tale mo daa benaa na Wurubuarɛ ya. Akpaa de di ŋa o, yaa de te di ŋa ya nɛ, ŋe bɛɛ wɔlɛɛ daa na Wurubuarɛ nsana ya.

eng: But watch out or else this freedom of yours might be a problem for those who are weak.
local: Mɔna ɛ kɛɛ ke nideli nyi gbɛɛ keŋ ɛ dana nyi ɛ ke tale di mena weenɛɛ ŋenaŋ nɛ, na bese kateesa fa walaŋ ŋon ɔ kelɛɛdi taŋ doŋ nɛ ya.

eng: Suppose someone sees you eating in an idols temple. Wont the person with a weak conscience be encouraged to eat the meat sacrificed to false gods?.
local: Akpaa walaŋ ŋon ɔ kelɛɛdi taŋ doŋ ya nɛ de naa nyi nyaŋ ŋon n gyeŋ nombia kɛŋa kyaa bane deni man n ne dii na, ŋgba n na wola e gbɛɛ baa nyi waa ta di bane dɛɛ weenɛɛ?.

eng: The weak brother or sister for whom Christ died is destroyed by your knowledge.
local: Mena dɔɔ n nombia kagyeŋ beiŋ gyae ke yela n dɔɔ ŋon ɔ kelɛɛdi taŋ doŋ ya, te Kristo ba yem lɛɛ ɔ nyee nɛ, waa yo Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛ man.

eng: You sin against Christ if you sin against your brothers and sisters and hurt their weak consciences this way.
local: Nyi akpaa n yɛɛ dukum kei dinɔɔ tia n dɔɔ ŋon ɔ kelɛɛdi taŋ doŋ ya, te n yeli ɔ konɔɔ man nombia de yɔkɔse lee ɔ kelɛɛdi man nɛ, na n te yɛɛ dukum tia Kristo.

eng: This is why, if food causes the downfall of my brother or sister, I wont eat meat ever again, or else I may cause my brother or sister to fall.
local: Mena dɔɔ akpaa nyi bane weenɛɛ ŋan maa di, ne gyae la ke yela me dɔɔ ke yala gyoo dukum man yeyɛɛ na, kenaŋ na mɔɔ gyae maa di ŋa korakora bela ya. Nawolo nyi mɔɔ ne gyae nyi me dɔɔ ke yala gyoo dukum man yeyɛɛ ya.

eng: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Havent I seen Jesus our Lord? Arent you my work in the Lord?.
local: Ɛ ne kɛɛ maŋ nɛ ma te yɛɛ Kristo kpilale e yee? Me doo ŋolo tɛɛ? Ma te yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon mɔɔ naa de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo na me sia yee? Na ma tom keŋ mɔɔ yɛɛ ɛ man nɛ, yele la te ɛ lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu di yee?.

eng: If Im not an apostle to others, at least I am to you! You are the seal that shows Im an apostle.
local: Akpaa nyi baale bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ do nyi me yɛɛ Kristo kpilale e ya na, ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ ɛ te lɛɛ maŋ do. Nawolo nyi mena keŋ ɛ be lɛɛ Kristo di te ɛ ɛmɛɛ na e be yɛɛ dokoloŋ nɛ, na lese la wola nyi me yɛɛ Kristo kpilale e.

eng: This is my defense against those who criticize me.
local: Nombia kɛŋa te mɛɛ mo tiranɔɔ fa baŋ bɛɛ yako nyi ma te yɛɛ Kristo kpilale e ya nɛ.

eng: Dont we have the right to eat and drink?.
local: Mena dɔɔ tekaboena nyi baa fa daa weenɛɛ na loŋ yee?.

eng: Dont we have the right to travel with a wife who believes like the rest of the apostles, the Lords brothers, and Cephas?.
local: Mena dɔɔ tekaboena nyi daa mɔ dɛɛ yala ala baŋ be yɛɛ Kristotenawɔ, na baa ta sila daa ŋgba mena keŋ bee sila Pita na Kristo kpilala akaŋ baŋ, na de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ɔ naabia baŋ nɛ yee?.

eng: Or is it only I and Barnabas who dont have the right to not work for our living?.
local: Yaa daa na Banabase daageŋ te kaboena nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ tom, na dɛɛ mo kɛɛ da wose dɔɔ?.

eng: Who joins the army and pays their own way? Who plants a vineyard and doesnt eat its fruit? Who shepherds a flock and doesnt drink its milk?.
local: Yoonɔɔte woŋti ne gyuu na yoo, te ŋon gbagba ne tɔ ɔ wose kom? Yaa wɔɔfarale woŋti kyaa la keŋ ɔ ne fara wɔɔ, te ɔ bee dii lee wɔɔ kenaŋ man ya? Wonembia kɛɛlere woŋti kyaa la, keŋ ɔ ne kɛɛle wonembia te ɔ bee di be naane ya?.

eng: Im not saying these things just based on common sense, am I? Doesnt the Law itself say these things?.
local: Denibalaŋ agyueŋ yaa me dana mee kolosi nombia kɛŋa yaa? Koa, Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan mɔ na wolo mena.

eng: In Moses Law its written: You will not muzzle the ox when it is threshing . Is God worried about oxen.
local: Nawolo nyi be ŋmarasewɔ do mmaraase ŋan Wurubuarɛ ba mo fa Mosesi nɛ man nyiaa, Na bake nunkyue ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ tom wɔɔman nɛ nɔɔ, na ɔ be nyiŋ di weenɛɛ ŋan ŋaale ya. Mena dɔɔ ɛ gyeŋ nyi nunkyue waraŋase yaa Wurubuarɛ ne wii nɛ?.

eng: or did he say this entirely for our sake? It was written for our sake because the one who plows and the one who threshes should each do so with the hope of sharing the produce.
local: Na daa te Wurubuarɛ ne yako mena nombia kɛŋa fa yee? Ayi, daa te be ŋmarase ŋa fa. Nawolo nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ na boolaa wɔɔ na ŋon ɔ ne gbee weenɛɛ nɛ, be popou loo gyakaa nyi baa nyiŋ kolo lee wɔɔ keŋ man.

eng: If we sowed spiritual things in you, is it so much to ask to harvest some material things from you?.
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan dɔɔ kolosi fa ŋon nɛ, ŋe yɛɛ yaa ŋgba abɔɔ ŋan dɔɔ duu ɛ man nɛ. Mena dɔɔ akpaa ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ fa daa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ na, ta kela ŋon?.

eng: If others have these rights over you, dont we deserve them all the more? However, we havent made use of this right, but we put up with everything so we dont put any obstacle in the way of the gospel of Christ.
local: Akpaa nyi ɛ ne kyɔ baale lee gbɛɛ kei man na, tekaboena nyi yaa kyɔ daa kela wɔ yee? Mɔna daa ta mo mena gbɛɛ kei dɔɔ ŋere ŋon ya. De kyaŋa da wose te da seŋawɔ keŋkeŋ nombia kɛŋa pou man, na dɔɔ nyiŋ tɔ Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan gbɛɛ ya.

eng: Dont you know that those who serve in the temple get to eat food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar share part of what is sacrificed on the altar?.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ tom Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ, bee nyiŋ be weenɛɛ bo lee botɔɔ yee? Te na botɔɔ keŋ bee deesi fa Wurubuarɛ nɛ, te somra baŋ mɔ na nyiŋ be weenɛɛ lee yee?.

eng: In the same way, the Lord commanded that those who preach the gospel should get their living from the gospel.
local: Mena ke te de Gbeŋgyoo mɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, balaŋ baŋ bee kolosi nombia kpaakpaa ŋan nɛ, kaboena nyi baa nyiŋ be weenɛɛ lee nombia kpaakpaa ŋan kekolosi man.

eng: But I havent taken advantage of this. And Im not writing this so that it will be done for me. Its better for me to die than to lose my right to brag about this!.
local: Mɔna me ta mo mena gbɛɛ keŋ me dana nɛ, yɛɛ kolo ya. Te mɔɔ ne ŋmarase nombia kɛŋa fa ŋon, na ke yela ɛ ke yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa fa maŋ ya. Nyi ma yem gba na dei kela nyi ŋolo ke fa maŋ kolo, keŋ gyae ketɔ maŋ gbɛɛ na mɔɔ nyiŋ gyɔ me konɔɔ wola ma wose lee tom keŋ mɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ man ya.

eng: If I preach the gospel, I have no reason to brag, since Im obligated to do it. Im in trouble if I dont preach the gospel.
local: Mɔna gbɛɛ be doo nyi maa wola ma wose lee nombia kpaakpaa kɛŋa kekolosi dɔɔ ya, nawolo nyi me gyae o, ma te gyae ya o, ke yɛɛ kpene keŋ ke taŋ doo nyi maa kolosi nombia kpaakpaa ŋenaŋ. Akpaa ma te yako nombia ŋenaŋ ya na, maa laako.

eng: If I do this voluntarily, I get rewarded for it. But if Im forced to do it, then Ive been charged with a responsibility.
local: Nyi akpaa maŋ gbagba kegyaebii man te mee kolosi nombia kpaakpaa kɛŋa na, maa nyiŋ kom ketɔ. Mɔna akpaa te yɛɛ maŋ gbagba kegyaebii man ya na, kenaŋ na mɛɛ yɛɛ tom keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ba mo fa maŋ nyi kaboena nyi maa yɛɛ nɛ bo.

eng: What reward do I get? That when I preach, I offer the good news free of charge. Thats why I dont use the rights to which Im entitled through the gospel.
local: Woŋ yɛna kom keŋ baa tɔ maŋ? Keŋ yɛna nyi maa yako nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa balaŋ keŋ nyi me na lɛɛ kolo ya. Te mɔɔ gyae maa mo gbɛɛ keŋ me dana nombia kpaakpaa kɛŋa keyako man nɛ, mo gyae kolo fa ma wose ya.

eng: Although Im free from all people, I make myself a slave to all people, to recruit more of them.
local: Doo nyi me kyae ma wose dɔɔ keŋ ma te yɛɛ ŋolo gbeŋi e koraŋ ya nɛ, me ta mo ma wose besena gbeŋi fa balaŋ pou, na maa nyiŋ yela be man balaŋ burum kekɔŋ Kristo gyaŋ.

eng: I act like a Jew to the Jews, so I can recruit Jews. I act like Im under the Law to those under the Law, so I can recruit those who are under the Law .
local: Akpaa me gyu Gyudatena gyaŋ na, mɛɛ yɛɛ ma wose ŋgba Gyuda baale nɛ, na maa nyiŋ yela be man balaŋ burum kekɔŋ Kristo gyaŋ. Te akpaa me gyu balaŋ baŋ be doo Mosesi mmaraa keŋ tɛɛ na, mɛɛ yɛɛ ma wose ŋgba walaŋ ŋon ɔ doo mmaraa keŋ tɛɛ nɛ, mɔna mɔɔ doo ke tɛɛ ya. Mɛɛ yɛɛ mena na maa nyiŋ yela be man balaŋ burum kekɔŋ Kristo gyaŋ.

eng: I act like Im outside the Law to those who are outside the Law, so I can recruit those outside the Law .
local: Mena mɔ te akpaa me gyu balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ doo Gyudatena mmaraa keŋ tɛɛ ya nɛ gyaŋ na, mɛɛ yɛɛ ma wose ŋgba walaŋ ŋon ɔ be doo mmaraa kenaŋ tɛɛ ya nɛ. Mɔna ma te yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon mɔɔ doo Wurubuarɛ mmaraa tɛɛ ya. Me doo Kristo mmaraa tɛɛ. Mɛɛ yɛɛ mena na maa nyiŋ yela be man balaŋ burum baŋ bɔɔ doo mmaraa kenaŋ tɛɛ ya nɛ kekɔŋ Kristo gyaŋ.

eng: I act weak to the weak, so I can recruit the weak. I have become all things to all people, so I could save some by all possible means.
local: Akpaa me lii balaŋ baŋ ba kelɛɛdi taŋ doŋ ya nɛ gyaŋ na, mɛɛ yɛɛ ma wose ŋgba baŋ nɛ, na maa nyiŋ yela be man balaŋ burum kekɔŋ Kristo gyaŋ. Me ta mo ma wose yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ fa balaŋ pou, na maa yɛɛ mena keŋ maa tale nɛ, na maa nyiŋ yela be man balaŋ burum kekɔŋ Kristo gyaŋ.

eng: All the things I do are for the sake of the gospel, so I can be a partner with it.
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan dɔɔ te mɛɛ yɛɛ kɛŋa pou, na maŋ mɔ kenyiŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan gyae ka lee ŋe man kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: Dont you know that all the runners in the stadium run, but only one gets the prize? So run to win.
local: Ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi balaŋ burum ne yeu baa lɛɛ yele na, be popou ne yeu mɔna walaŋ dokoloŋ ne lɛɛ na yele yee? Mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ kyaŋ ɛ wose na ɛ ke yeu nideli, na ɛ ke nyiŋ yele keyeu keŋ man.

eng: Everyone who competes practices self-discipline in everything. The runners do this to get a crown of leaves that shrivel up and die, but we do it to receive a crown that never dies.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne desina ɔ wose fa keyeu na, ɔ ne di keyeu mmaraase ŋan pou dɔɔ. Ɔ ne yɛɛ kɛŋa pou bo fa yele keŋ be gyae ke kyare ya nɛ. Mɔna daa ne yɛɛ Kristo tom keŋ dɛɛ mo nyiŋ yele keŋ gyae ke doo kekpaakekpaa nɛ bo.

eng: So now this is how I run—not without a clear goal in sight. I fight like a boxer in the ring, not like someone who is shadowboxing.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɔɔ ne yeu ŋgba walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba gyeŋ keŋ dɔɔ te ɔ ne yeu nɛ ya, te mɔɔ ne yoo mɔ ŋgba walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne fuŋii wa akɔta do feliŋ man yakaa yakaa nɛ ya.

eng: Rather, Im landing punches on my own body and subduing it like a slave. I do this to be sure that I myself wont be disqualified after preaching to others.
local: Mɔna maŋ dɔɔ mɛɛ naase ma wose diyem, te mɛɛ yɛɛ ke ŋgba me gbeŋi nɛ. Na wee daale dekɔŋ nyi ma kekolosi Kristo nombia ŋan fa balaŋ taŋ wɔle nɛ, na bɔɔ lese maŋ see yenaŋ keŋ mɔɔ nyiŋ yele ya.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to be sure of the fact that our ancestors were all under the cloud and they all went through the sea.
local: Me tebia, mɛɛ gyae ɛ ke tɔɔse kpene keŋ be tuŋ de naanaɔ baŋ bɔɔ sila Mosesi nɛ. Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ debuŋta wuu wɔ, te ɔ yela ba gyoowɔ ɛpo yayam keŋ man lee.

eng: All were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.
local: Wurubuarɛ ka mo ɔ debuŋta keŋ wuu wɔ, na ɔ ke yela be ka gyoo ɛpo yayam keŋ man lee nɛ, nawolo baa nyi be sɔɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ baa te ba bese Mosesi silala.

eng: All ate the same spiritual food.
local: Be popou dii weenɛɛ ŋan ba lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ kaa yala nɛ.

eng: and all drank the same spiritual drink. They drank from a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.
local: te be popou nyɔɔ loŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yeli kɔɔ lee boe keŋ man nɛ. Mena boe kenaŋ yɛna Kristo gbagba, ŋon na ɔ kpuɛ na wɔ ba keta man nɛ.

eng: However, God was unhappy with most of them, and they were struck down in the wilderness.
local: Mɔna be man balaŋ burum nombia ta gyoo Wurubuarɛ sia ya, mena dɔɔ ba yekeewɔ leki depampaa dinaa keŋ man.

eng: These things were examples for us, so we wont crave evil things like they did.
local: Be nombia kɛŋa pou yɛɛ kpene keŋ dɛɛ kɛɛ na dɛɛ kasee, na dɔɔ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ ŋgba baŋ nɛ ya.

eng: Dont worship false gods like some of them did, as it is written, The people sat down to eat and drink and they got up to play .
local: Ɛ na som bɛɛneŋ ŋgba mena keŋ be man baale be yɛɛ nɛ ya. Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see yakowɔ nyiaa, Balaŋ baŋ tisiwɔ kyakee na bee dii na bɛɛ nyɔɔ, te ba korowɔ bɛɛ yɛɛ nombia nkaraŋkaraŋ.

eng: Lets not practice sexual immorality, like some of them did, and twenty-three thousand died in one day.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na yɛɛ kafoŋ ŋgba mena keŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ kafoŋ nombia, te be man balaŋ kakpoŋse saobatooro ba yem wee dokoloŋ nɛ ya.

eng: Lets not test Christ, like some of them did, and were killed by the snakes.
local: Tekaboena nyi dɛɛ teese Wurubuarɛ nɔɔman, ŋgba mena keŋ be man baale be yɛɛ te ɔ be yeli doma be kaa koe wɔ nɛ ya.

eng: Lets not grumble, like some of them did, and were killed by the destroyer.
local: Tekaboena nyi dɛɛ toroŋ ŋgba mena keŋ be man baale ba toroŋ, te Wurubuarɛ be yeli ɔ kpilale ŋon ɔ na koe balaŋ nɛ be kaa koe wɔ nɛ ya.

eng: These things happened to them as an example and were written as a warning for us to whom the end of time has come.
local: Mena nombia kɛŋa pou kɔŋae be dɔɔ na balaŋ ka mo kasee kolo. Te be ŋmarase ŋa bo see na baa mo faree daa, nawolo nyi de kyaa debaŋ keŋ tɛɛle kei ne gyu kɔ ɔto nɛ man.

eng: So those who think they are standing need to watch out or else they may fall.
local: Mena dɔɔ nyaŋ walaŋ ŋon ne yɛɛ neŋ nyi n seŋɛɛ keŋkeŋ nɛ, kɛɛ n wose nideli keŋ nyi n ba lee yala ya.

eng: No temptation has seized you that isnt common for people. But God is faithful. He wont allow you to be tempted beyond your abilities. Instead, with the temptation, God will also supply a way out so that you will be able to endure it.
local: Kekɛɛsekɛɛ daale te kɔŋa ta ɛ dɔɔ, keŋ nyi ke dinɔɔ te kɔŋa ta ŋolo dɔɔ see ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ ne di ɔ nombia dɔɔ, mena dɔɔ ɔ be gyae waa yela kekɛɛsekɛɛ keŋ kela ŋon nɛ kekɔŋ ɛ dɔɔ ya. Te akpaa nyi kekɛɛsekɛɛ de kɔŋ ɛ dɔɔ na, waa wola ŋon gbɛɛ keŋ ɛ ka mo seŋ keŋkeŋ na ɛ ke tale di ke dɔɔ.

eng: So then, my dear friends, run away from the worship of false gods!.
local: Mena dɔɔ me gyoona kpaakpaa, ɛ lese ɛ wose lee bɛɛneŋ ɔsom man.

eng: Im talking to you like you are sensible people. Think about what Im saying.
local: Mee kolosi baa fa ɛmɛɛ nyansatena. Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ gyoo me nombia ŋan man na ɛ ke kɛɛ kpene keŋ mɛɛ yako nɛ.

eng: Isnt the cup of blessing that we bless a sharing in the blood of Christ? Isnt the loaf of bread that we break a sharing in the body of Christ?.
local: Debaŋ keŋ dee di de Gbeŋgyoo weenɛɛ ŋan ɔ kpa dee di na dɛɛ mo tɔɔse e nɛ, leenɔɔ keŋ dɛɛ sola Wurubuarɛ wosefɛɛreŋ gyakaa ke dɔɔ pɛte dɛɛ nyɔɔ lee ke man nɛ, nawolo baa nyi daa na Kristo te yɛɛ dokoloŋ lee ɔ fatabo keŋ dɔɔ. Mena mɔ te bodobodo keŋ dɛɛ kpɛlɛɛ fa dɔŋa nɛ, nawolo baa nyi daa na Kristo te yɛɛ dokoloŋ lee ɔ wosenaane man.

eng: Since there is one loaf of bread, we who are many are one body, because we all share the one loaf of bread.
local: Bodobodo dokoloŋ te dɛɛ mo kpala fa dɔŋa, mena dɔɔ daa balaŋ burum baŋ pou ta bese walaŋ dokoloŋ. Nawolo nyi bodobodo dokoloŋ kenaŋ te de popou diiwɔ.

eng: Look at the people of Israel. Dont those who eat the sacrifices share from the altar?.
local: Ɛ mo kɛɛ Iseraetena. Balaŋ baŋ bee di weenɛɛ ŋan bɔɔ mo deesi fa Wurubuarɛ nɛ, baŋ na Wurubuarɛ na bese na walaŋ dokoloŋ.

eng: What am I saying then? That food sacrificed to a false god is anything, or that a false god is anything?.
local: Mena dɔɔ sena yaa me nombia kɛŋa na wolo baa? Bane yɛɛ kolo ke, yaa weenɛɛ ŋan bɛɛ mo deesi fa bane nɛ yɛna kolo?.

eng: No, but this kind of sacrifice is sacrificed to demons and not to God. I dont want you to be sharing in demons.
local: Koa, kpene keŋ mɛɛ wolo yɛna nyiaa, weenɛɛ ŋan bane somra na mo deesi fa banɛɛ nɛ, te yɛɛ nyi Wurubuarɛ te bɛɛ mo deesi fa ya. Feliŋkumɛɛ te bee deesi fa, mena dɔɔ mɔɔ ne gyae nyi ɛmɛɛ na feliŋkumɛɛ ke yɛɛ dokoloŋ ya.

eng: You cant drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cant participate in the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
local: Ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale mo de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo leenɔɔ nyɔɔ, na ɛ ka bese mo feliŋkumɛɛ mɔ leenɔɔ nyɔɔ ya. Te be gyae ke de nyi ɛ ke di de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo nyefale man, na ɛ ka bese ke di feliŋkumɛɛ mɔ nyefale man ya.

eng: Or should we make the Lord jealous? We arent stronger than he is, are we?.
local: Dɛɛ gyae baa nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ka gyeŋ baŋ? Yaa dɛɛ kɛɛ da wose baa nyi de dana doŋ kela e?.

eng: Everything is permitted, but everything isnt beneficial. Everything is permitted, but everything doesnt build others up.
local: Gbɛɛ doo nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ mɔna na ŋe popou danaana tɔnɔɔ ya. Gbɛɛ doo nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ mɔna na ŋe popou ne gyae la ke yela dɛɛ bee ya.

eng: No one should look out for their own advantage, but they should look out for each other.
local: Ŋolo na gyae ŋon gbagba wui deeli ya, mɔna waa gyae ɔ tebia wui deeli bo.

eng: Eat everything that is sold in the marketplace, without asking questions about it because of your conscience.
local: Naane kamasɛ keŋ bɛɛ yɔɔ kebu man nɛ ɛ ke tale lɛɛ di, mɔna na bɔɔse naane keŋ dalee na ke yela ŋolo konɔɔ man nombia ke yɔkɔse e ya.

eng: The earth and all that is in it belong to the Lord .
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nyiaa Tɛɛle kei na kpene kamasɛ keŋ doo ke man nɛ pou, yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ wui.

eng: If an unbeliever invites you to eat with them and you want to go, eat whatever is served, without asking questions because of your conscience.
local: Akpaa walaŋ ŋon ɔ te yɛɛ Kristo baale e ya de baake ŋon nyi ɛ kaa di, te ke yɛɛ keŋ ɛ ke di na, ɛ gyu ke di mɔna ɛ na bɔɔse weenɛɛ ŋan dalee na ŋolo konɔɔ man nombia ke yɔkɔse e ya.

eng: But if someone says to you, This meat was sacrificed in a temple, then dont eat it for the sake of the one who told you and for the sake of conscience.
local: Mɔna akpaa ŋolo de yako ŋon nyi, Weenɛɛ kɛŋa yɛɛ bane dɛɛ weenɛɛ ŋa na, lee walaŋ ŋon ɔ be yako ŋon nɛ, na balaŋ akaŋ baŋ konɔɔ man nombia dɔɔ ɛ na di ŋa ya.

eng: Now when I say conscience I dont mean yours but the other persons. Why should my freedom be judged by someone elses conscience?.
local: Na ɛmɛɛ gbagba konɔɔ man nombia dɔɔ ya, mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ be yako ŋon nɛ konɔɔ man nombia dɔɔ. Nawolo nyi ŋolo konɔɔ man nombia be gyae ke tale tɔ maŋ gbɛɛ, nyi me na yɛɛ kpene keŋ me dana gbɛɛ nyi maa yɛɛ nɛ ya.

eng: If I participate with gratitude, why should I be blamed for food I thank God for?.
local: Mɔna akpaa me fane fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ te me di weenɛɛ kɛŋa na, woŋ dɔɔ te ŋolo waa bu maŋ fɔɔ lee weenɛɛ kɛŋa kedi dɔɔ?.

eng: So, whether you eat or drink or whatever you do, you should do it all for Gods glory.
local: Mena dɔɔ kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ ne dii yaa ɛ ne nyɔɔ, yaa kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ ne yɛɛ na, ɛ yɛɛ ŋe popou mo lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

eng: Dont offend either Jews or Greeks, or Gods church.
local: Ɛ na yeli ɛ nombia ayɛɛsa ke tɔ Gyuda baale, yaa Giriki baale yaa Kristo walaŋ kamasɛ gbɛɛ ya.

eng: This is the same thing that I do. I please everyone in everything I do. I dont look out for my own advantage, but I look out for many people so that they can be saved.
local: Mena mɔ te mɛɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ maa yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ, na ka gyoo walaŋ kamasɛ sia lee gbɛɛ kamasɛ man. Nawolo nyi mɔɔ ne gyae maŋ gbagba me deeli ya, mɔna balaŋ pou deeli te mɛɛ gyae, na de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo ke nyiŋ lɛɛ ba nyee.

eng: Follow my example, just like I follow Christs.
local: Ɛ kasee me dinɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ maŋ mɔ ne kasee Kristo dinɔɔ nɛ.

eng: I praise you because you remember all my instructions, and you hold on to the traditions exactly as I handed them on to you.
local: Mɛɛ kɛlɛɛ ŋon ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ ne tɔɔse maŋ ɛ nombia pou man, te ɛ dana me nombia ŋan mɔɔ wola ŋon man nideli nɛ.

eng: Now I want you to know that the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is the man, and the head of Christ is God.
local: Mɔna mɛɛ gyae ɛ ka gyeŋ nyi baale kamasɛ nyee yɛna Kristo, te alo mɔ nyee yɛna ɔ baale te Kristo mɔ nyee yɛna Wurubuarɛ.

eng: Every man who prays or prophesies with his head covered shames his head.
local: Mena dɔɔ baale kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne fane yaa ɔ ne kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia balaŋ man, te ɔ mo kolo wu ɔ nyee na ɔ ta do ɔ nyee desɛŋ.

eng: Every woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered disgraces her head. It is the same thing as having her head shaved.
local: Mena mɔ te alo kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne fane yaa ɔ ne kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia balaŋ man, te ɔ ta mo kolo wu ɔ nyee ya na ɔ ta do ɔ nyee desɛŋ. Na yɛɛ yaa ŋgba ɔ gbee ɔ nyee baa nɛ.

eng: If a woman doesnt cover her head, then she should have her hair cut off. If it is disgraceful for a woman to have short hair or to be shaved, then she should keep her head covered.
local: Nyi akpaa alo bɛɛ gyae nyi waa mo kolo wu ɔ nyee ya na, kenaŋ na kaboena nyi waa tisi ɔ nyee. Te akpaa desɛŋ dana alo ŋon nyi waa tisi ɔ nyee yaa ɔ be tale gbee ɔ nyee ya nɛ, kenaŋ na kaboena nyi waa mo kolo wu ɔ nyee.

eng: A man shouldnt have his head covered, because he is the image and glory of God; but the woman is mans glory.
local: Tekaboena nyi baale waa mo kolo wu ɔ nyee ya, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ baale bo ŋon gbagba dinɔɔ man na waa mo lese ŋon Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri wola. Mɔna alo dɔɔ ɔ na lese baale gyoorobiiri bo wola.

eng: Man didnt have his origin from woman, but woman from man.
local: Nawolo nyi na baale te Wurubuarɛ lesewɔ lee alo man ya, mɔna alo te ɔ lesewɔ lee baale man.

eng: and man wasnt created for the sake of the woman, but the woman for the sake of the man.
local: Na alo dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ baale ya, mɔna baale dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ alo.

eng: Because of this a woman should have authority over her head, because of the angels.
local: Nombia kɛŋa na Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ mɔ dɔɔ nɛ, kaboena nyi alo waa mo kolo wu ɔ nyee na ka wola nyi ɔ doe ɔ baale tɛɛ.

eng: However, woman isnt independent from man, and man isnt independent from woman in the Lord.
local: De Gbeŋgyoo Yesu siaman na alo be kyaa ɔ wose dɔɔ ya, te baale mɔ be kyaa ɔ wose dɔɔ ya. Be popou kyaa bo fa dɔŋa.

eng: As woman came from man so also man comes from woman. But everything comes from God.
local: Nawolo nyi ŋgba mena keŋ alo ba lee baale man nɛ, mena mɔ te alo na lolana baale. Te kpene kamasɛ mɔ na lee bo Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

eng: Judge for yourselves: Is it appropriate for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?.
local: Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ gyoo nombia kɛŋa man kɛɛ. Dei nyi alo waa fane fa Wurubuarɛ keŋ nyi ɔ na mo kolo wu ɔ nyee yee?.

eng: Doesnt nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a disgrace to him.
local: Daa gbagba de nombia ayɛɛsa bɛɛ wola daa nyi akpaa baale de tina ɔ nyeenyɔɔse yela te da gyoo kusuu na, ke yɛɛ desɛŋ ke yee?.

eng: but if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? This is because her long hair is given to her for a covering.
local: Mɔna akpaa alo dana nyeenyɔɔse burum na, ne yeli ɔ ne yɛɛ kyeo. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ faa e nyeenyɔɔse dɔɔloŋ bo nyi ŋe dee wu ɔ nyee.

eng: But if someone wants to argue about this, we dont have such a custom, nor do Gods churches.
local: Akpaa ŋolo ne gyae waa lɛɛ nombia kɛŋa aŋmaareŋ na, daa na Kristotena akaŋ baŋ pou nombia ayɛɛsa bɛɛ wolo nyi dɛɛ lɛɛ nombia kɛŋa aŋmaareŋ ya.

eng: Now I dont praise you as I give the following instruction because when you meet together, it does more harm than good.
local: Nɛnɛɛ lee nombia ŋan mɛɛ gyae maa kolosi fa ŋon debokeiman nɛ dɔɔ, mɔɔ ne kɛlɛɛ ŋon ya. Nawolo nyi ɛ gyaŋee ɛ ka som na, bɛɛ mo kolo kpaakpaa kɔŋ ya. Na bese wɔlɛɛ abɔɔ bo kpu.

eng: First of all, when you meet together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you, and I partly believe it.
local: Nombii gyaŋgbate yɛna nyi, mee nyii nyi ɛmɛɛ Kristotena dikpii keŋ de gyaŋee na ɛ ne kpase kpookpoo, te mɛɛ lɛɛ nombia kɛŋa ŋaale di.

eng: Its necessary that there are groups among you, to make it clear who is genuine.
local: Nawolo nyi kaboena nyi kekpase kpookpoo kei dɛɛ kɔŋ ɛ man, na ke nyiŋ lese baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ wola.

eng: So when you get together in one place, it isnt to eat the Lords meal.
local: Ɛ kyaa gyaŋee ɛ ke di na, te yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo weenɛɛ te ɛ ne dii ya.

eng: Each of you goes ahead and eats a private meal. One person goes hungry while another is drunk.
local: Nawolo nyi ɛ gyaŋee ɛ ke di nɛ, walaŋ kamasɛ ne di ɔ wui weenɛɛ bo keŋ ɛ bɛɛ kyaa daa dɔŋa ya. Tanam na ko baale, te baale mɔ ne dii baa dɛɛse.

eng: Dont you have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you look down on Gods churches and humiliate those who have nothing? What can I say to you? Will I praise you? No, I dont praise you in this.
local: Ɛ ne yako baa nyi ɛmɛɛ gbagba be dana akpaŋalaŋse ŋan man ɛ ke di na ɛ ke nyɔɔ yee? Yaa ɛ ne gyae ɛ ke fulu Kristotena dikpii keŋ, na ɛ ka do balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ dana kolo ya nɛ desɛŋ? Woŋ nombia yaa ɛ ne gyae maa yako ŋon baa? Ɛ ne gyae nyi maa kɛlɛɛ ŋon? Koa, kɛŋa man dɔɔ mɔɔ gyae maa kɛlɛɛ ŋon ya.

eng: I received a tradition from the Lord, which I also handed on to you: on the night on which he was betrayed, the Lord Jesus took bread.
local: Nawolo nyi kpene keŋ mɔɔ kasee lee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu gyaŋ nɛ, keŋ yaa maŋ mɔ moowɔ mɛɛ wola ŋon nɛ. Nelim keŋ baa lese de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu fa nɛ ɔ moo bodobodo.

eng: After giving thanks, he broke it and said, This is my body, which is for you; do this to remember me.
local: te ɔ kefane fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ taŋ nɛ ɔ kɛrɛɛ ke te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Kei yɛna ma wosenaane keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo fa ŋon nɛ. Ɛ kyaa yɛɛ kei na ɛ ka mo tɔɔse maŋ.

eng: He did the same thing with the cup, after they had eaten, saying, This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Every time you drink it, do this to remember me.
local: Mena mɔ te be ke di weenɛɛ ŋan taŋ nɛ, ɔ moo leenɔɔ na soloŋ keŋ te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Kei yɛna me fatabo keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo yɛɛ nɔɔkebakesee wɔle. Ɛ kyaa yɛɛ kei debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ ɛ ke nyɔɔ ke nɛ, na ɛ ka mo tɔɔse maŋ.

eng: Every time you eat this bread and drink this cup, you broadcast the death of the Lord until he comes.
local: Kei nawolo nyi debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ ɛ ke di bodobodo kei na ɛ ke nyɔɔ lee leenɔɔ kei man na, ɛ ne di de Gbeŋgyoo yeŋ keŋ adansɛɛ baa kelii wee keŋ waa bese kɔŋ.

eng: This is why those who eat the bread or drink the cup of the Lord inappropriately will be guilty of the Lords body and blood.
local: Mena dɔɔ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ta mo gbɛɛ kpaakpaa di bodobodo kei, na waa nyɔɔ lee de Gbeŋgyoo leenɔɔ kei man ya nɛ, na ɔtenate te yɛɛ dukum baa. Nawolo nyi ɔ te bu de Gbeŋgyoo wosenaane na ɔ fatabo keŋ ya.

eng: Each individual should test himself or herself, and eat from the bread and drink from the cup in that way.
local: Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi walaŋ kamasɛ waa gyoo ɔ wose man nideli, pɛna waa di bodobodo kei na waa nyɔɔ lee de Gbeŋgyoo leenɔɔ kei man.

eng: Those who eat and drink without correctly understanding the body are eating and drinking their own judgment.
local: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ dii te ɔ nyɔɔ, te ɔ te lɛɛ do nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu wosenaane na ɔ fatabo kenaŋ ya nɛ, na ɔtenate falaa ɔ wose baa.

eng: Because of this, many of you are weak and sick, and quite a few have died.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ te ɛ man balaŋ burum baale wose yɔkɔsewɔ, te baale mɔ gyoo kaweese te baale mɔ yekeewɔ nɛ.

eng: But if we had judged ourselves, we wouldnt be judged.
local: Mɔna nyi akpaa da gyoo da wose man nideli pɛte de di de Gbeŋgyoo weenɛɛ ŋan na, Wurubuarɛ be gyae waa bu daa fɔɔ ya.

eng: However, we are disciplined by the Lord when we are judged so that we wont be judged and condemned along with the whole world.
local: Akpaa de Gbeŋgyoo de bu daa fɔɔ na ɔ ne wɔŋ da deŋele bo, na bɔɔ nyiŋ wɔlɛɛ daa kpu na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ akaŋ baŋ ya.

eng: For these reasons, my brothers and sisters, when you get together to eat, wait for each other.
local: Mena dɔɔ me tebia, akpaa ɛ kɔŋ kaa gyaŋee ɛ ke di na ɛ kyaa daa dɔŋa.

eng: If some of you are hungry, they should eat at home so that getting together doesnt lead to judgment. I will give directions about the other things when I come.
local: Mɔna akpaa nyi tanam na ko ŋolo na waa di lee ɔ dɛɛ pɛna waa kɔŋ, na ɛ kɔŋ kaa gyaŋee na bɔɔ nyiŋ bu ŋolo fɔɔ ya. Nyi akpaa me kɔŋ na, maa kaa wola ŋon nombia akaŋ ŋan man kpu.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I dont want you to be ignorant about spiritual gifts.
local: Me tebia, mɔɔ ne gyae ɛ ke kyaa keŋ nyi ɛ ba gyeŋ kolo lee Wurubuarɛ feliŋ abɔɔ anefaŋ ŋan wose man ya.

eng: You know that when you were Gentiles you were often misled by false gods that cant even speak.
local: Ɛ gyeŋ nyi debaŋ keŋ ɛ doo wiase man keŋ ɛ te lɛɛ ta Wurubuarɛ di ya nɛ, ɛ yela ba beo ŋon gyu bɛɛneŋ gyaŋ. Bɛɛneŋ kɛwɔ mɔ bɛɛ fa nyeedoŋ ya.

eng: So I want to make it clear to you that no one says, Jesus is cursed! when speaking by Gods Spirit, and no one can say, Jesus is Lord, except by the Holy Spirit.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ gyae ɛ ka gyeŋ nyi walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ feliŋ doo ɔ man nɛ, ɔ be gyae waa tale yako mmusuo nombia gyakaa Yesu dɔɔ ya. Te ŋolo mɔ be kyaa waa tale yako nyi Yesu yɛna ɔ Gbeŋgyoo, keŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ feliŋ be doo ɔtenate man ya.

eng: There are different spiritual gifts but the same Spirit.
local: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon abɔɔ anefaŋ kyaa kpookpoo, mɔna ŋe popou lee Wurubuarɛ feliŋ dokoloŋ ŋonaŋ gyaŋ.

eng: and there are different ministries and the same Lord.
local: Wurubuarɛ ɔsom tomse kpookpoo kyaa, mɔna de Gbeŋgyoo dokoloŋ ŋonaŋ yaa dɛɛ yɛɛ fa.

eng: and there are different activities but the same God who produces all of them in everyone.
local: Tomse kpookpoo mɔ kyaa, mɔna Wurubuarɛ dokoloŋ ŋonaŋ ne faa na ɔ balaŋ doŋ te bɛɛ mo yɛɛ ŋe popou.

eng: A demonstration of the Spirit is given to each person for the common good.
local: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne faa na Kristo walaŋ kamasɛ doŋ daale dinɔɔ, na waa mo yɛɛ tom Kristotena dikpii keŋ man na ke de fa be popou.

eng: A word of wisdom is given by the Spirit to one person, a word of knowledge to another according to the same Spirit.
local: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋonaŋ ne fa ŋolo nyansa keŋ waa mo kolosi nombia, te ɔ ne fa ŋolo mɔ nombia kagyeŋ.

eng: faith to still another by the same Spirit, gifts of healing to another in the one Spirit.
local: Mena Wurubuarɛ feliŋ dokoloŋ ŋonaŋ ne yele la ŋolo Wurubuarɛ kelɛɛdi ne yɛɛ doŋ, te ɔ ne fa ŋolo mɔ doŋ keŋ waa mo kyɔ kaweese.

eng: performance of miracles to another, prophecy to another, the ability to tell spirits apart to another, different kinds of tongues to another, and the interpretation of the tongues to another.
local: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋonaŋ ne faa na ŋolo doŋ keŋ waa mo yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia, te ɔ ne fa ŋolo mɔ doŋ keŋ waa mo kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia. Ŋon ne yele la ŋolo ne tale gyeŋ nyi nombii daale lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ yaa ke ta lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya. Ŋon ne faa na ŋolo deise kpookpoo kekolosi, te ŋon ke ne faa na ŋolo mɔ deise kpookpoo man kalese.

eng: All these things are produced by the one and same Spirit who gives what he wants to each person.
local: Mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ dokoloŋ ŋonaŋ tom ke, te ɔ ne fa walaŋ kamasɛ lee ŋon feliŋ ŋonaŋ gbagba kegyaebii man.
