eng: Christ is just like the human body—a body is a unit and has many parts; and all the parts of the body are one body, even though there are many.
local: Deniwalaŋ wose yɛɛ dokoloŋ, mɔna ke dana yenaase kpookpoo burum. Doo nyi ke dana yenaase kpookpoo burum koraŋ nɛ, ŋe popou ne bom la yɛɛ deniwalaŋ wosenaane dokoloŋ. Mena ke te Kristo mɔ doo.

eng: We were all baptized by one Spirit into one body, whether Jew or Greek, or slave or free, and we all were given one Spirit to drink.
local: Daa Kristotena pou na Gyudatena na Girikitena na gbeŋa na baŋ be kyaa ba wose dɔɔ nɛ pou, Wurubuarɛ feliŋ dokoloŋ ŋonaŋ te ba moowɔ sɔ de popou Wurubuarɛ loŋ. Ŋon moona daa bom te de popou bese Kristo wosenaane dokoloŋ keŋ. Keŋte de nyiŋa feliŋ dokoloŋ ŋonaŋ, te ɔ ne yɛɛ tom de popou man.

eng: Certainly the body isnt one part but many.
local: Na yenaŋ dokoloŋ ne yɛɛ na deniwalaŋ wose keŋ pou ya, mɔna yenaase kpookpoo ŋan pou bom la yɛɛ wosenaane dokoloŋ.

eng: If the foot says, Im not part of the body because Im not a hand, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Akpaa nae de yako nyi, Ma te yɛɛ nyiŋmaa ya mena dɔɔ mɔɔ kpuɛ wosenaane keŋ man ya na, kei gyae ke tale lese nae keŋ lee wosenaane kenaŋ man?.

eng: If the ear says, Im not part of the body because Im not an eye, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Te akpaa deŋele de yako nyi, Na maŋ yɛna sibii ya mena dɔɔ mɔɔ kpuɛ wosenaane keŋ man ya na, keŋ nawolo baa nyi kɔɔ kpuɛ wosenaane keŋ man yee? Kei gyae ke tale lese deŋele kenaŋ lee wosenaane man?.

eng: If the whole body were an eye, what would happen to the hearing? And if the whole body were an ear, what would happen to the sense of smell?.
local: Nyi akpaa wosenaane keŋ pou deŋ yɛɛ sibii ke na, sena te wosenaane keŋ gyae ke yɛɛ na ke nyii kolo? Te akpaa wosenaane keŋ pou deŋ yɛɛ deŋele ke na, sena te wosenaane keŋ gyae ke yɛɛ na ke wɔŋ nyii?.

eng: But as it is, God has placed each one of the parts in the body just like he wanted.
local: Mɔ n kɛɛ na, Wurubuarɛ moo wosenaane kpookpoo ŋan pou bo bom yɛɛ wosenaane dokoloŋ, lee ŋon gbagba kegyaebii man.

eng: If all were one and the same body part, what would happen to the body?.
local: Nyi wosenaane yenaase kpookpoo ŋan pou deŋ yɛɛ wosenaane yenaŋ dokoloŋ na, nafɔ ke gyae ke yɛɛ gyakoloŋ.

eng: But as it is, there are many parts but one body.
local: Mɔna ŋgba mena keŋ ke doo nɛ, wosenaane yenaase ŋan pou boe mɔna wosenaane keŋ pou yɛɛ dokoloŋ.

eng: So the eye cant say to the hand, I dont need you, or in turn, the head cant say to the feet, I dont need you.
local: Sibii be gyae ke tale yako nyiŋmaa nyi, N be dana tɔnɔɔ fa maŋ ya, te nyee mɔ be gyae ke tale yako nae nyi, N be dana tɔnɔɔ fa maŋ ya.

eng: Instead, the parts of the body that people think are the weakest are the most necessary.
local: Mɔna wosenaane yenaase ŋan dɛɛ kɛɛ nyi ŋe taŋ doŋ ya nɛ, nyi ŋɔɔ doo wosenaane man ya nɛ nafɔ wosenaane be gyae ke tale yɛɛ kolo ya.

eng: The parts of the body that we think are less honorable are the ones we honor the most. The private parts of our body that arent presentable are the ones that are given the most dignity.
local: Te wosenaane yenaŋ keŋ de bee bu ke ya nɛ, keŋ gba te dɛɛ ko da wose na ke. Wosenaane yenaŋ keŋ daa ne kɛɛ nyi ke bee hia nideli ya nɛ, keŋ gba te dɛɛ kɛɛ ke dɔɔ nideli.

eng: The parts of our body that are presentable dont need this. But God has put the body together, giving greater honor to the part with less honor.
local: Da wosenaane yenaase ŋan dɛɛ kɛɛ nyi ŋe dana kyeo nɛ, be doo nyi dɛɛ kɛɛ ŋe dɔɔ mena ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ ta mo wosenaane yenaase ŋan pou towaa, te ɔ moo obuo fa ŋan nafɔ ŋɔɔ taŋ obuo ya nɛ.

eng: so that there wont be division in the body and so the parts might have mutual concern for each other.
local: Ɔ yɛɛ ke mena na kekpase be nyiŋ doo wosenaane yenaase kpookpoo ŋan man ya, mɔna wosenaane yenaŋ kamasɛ dɛɛ kɛɛ ke dɔɔ dɔɔ nideli.

eng: If one part suffers, all the parts suffer with it; if one part gets the glory, all the parts celebrate with it.
local: Akpaa de kɔŋ nyi wosenaane yenaŋ daale ne naa diyem na, akaŋ ŋan pou mɔ ne naa diyem kpu na ke. Mɔna akpaa de kɔŋ nyi wosenaane yenaŋ daale de nyiŋ yele na, ne yɛɛ yenaase akaŋ ŋan mɔ pou gyoŋ.

eng: You are the body of Christ and parts of each other.
local: Nɛnɛɛ ɛ popou bom la yɛɛ Kristo wosenaane keŋ, te ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ yɛɛ ɔ yenaŋ daale ke.

eng: In the church, God has appointed first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healing, the ability to help others, leadership skills, different kinds of tongues.
local: Kristotena dikpii keŋ man nɛ, Wurubuarɛ ta see dibiase ŋaale. Gyaŋgba nyibaŋ yɛna Kristo kpilala baŋ. Baŋ be gyakaa balate yɛna Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra, te baŋ be gyakaa batoorote yɛna baŋ bɛɛ wola Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan nɛ. Baale mɔ yɛna baŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia, te baale mɔ yɛna baŋ bɛɛ kyɔ kaweese, te baale mɔ yɛna baŋ bɛɛ kyɔ be tebia. Baale mɔ yɛna siamantena baŋ bɛɛ kɛɛ nyi nombia ŋan pou bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ ne gyu nideli. Te akaŋ baŋ yɛna baŋ bee kolosi deise kpookpoo nɛ.

eng: All arent apostles, are they? All arent prophets, are they? All arent teachers, are they? All dont perform miracles, do they?.
local: N kɛɛ na, na be popou yɛna Kristo kpilala ya. Na be popou yɛna Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra ya. Na be popou yɛna wolala ya. Na be popou ne yɛɛ na gyakoloŋ nombia ya.

eng: All dont have gifts of healing, do they? All dont speak in different tongues, do they? All dont interpret, do they?.
local: Na be popou ne kyɔɔna kaweese ya. Na be popou ne kolosina deise kpookpoo ya. Na be popou mɔ na lesena deise kpookpoo man ya.

eng: Use your ambition to try to get the greater gifts. And Im going to show you an even better way.
local: Mɔna ɛ yela ɛ sia keyase Wurubuarɛ abɔɔ anefaŋ dinaana ŋan ŋa kela kɛŋa nɛ dɔɔ. Nɛnɛɛ maa wola ŋon nombia kpaakpaa ŋan ŋa kela kɛŋa nɛ pou.

eng: If I speak in tongues of human beings and of angels but I dont have love, Im a clanging gong or a clashing cymbal.
local: Nyi akpaa mɛɛ tale kolosi deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo yaa Wurubuarɛ kpilala dei, te balaŋ kegyae be doo me man ya na, kenaŋ na ma kekolosi pou yɛɛ ŋgba dowuro yaa akpaakpaase ŋan ŋɛɛ yɛɛ yeesa yakaa bo nɛ.

eng: If I have the gift of prophecy and I know all the mysteries and everything else, and if I have such complete faith that I can move mountains but I dont have love, Im nothing.
local: Akpaa me yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e, te maa tale naa Wurubuarɛ nombia aweeseŋ ŋan na maa yako, te ma gyeŋ nombia pou te me dana kelɛɛdi kpaakpaate keŋ maa tale mo kulii bulase, te balaŋ kegyae be doo me man ya na me yɛɛ yakaa.

eng: If I give away everything that I have and hand over my own body to feel good about what Ive done but I dont have love, I receive no benefit whatsoever.
local: Mena mɔ te akpaa ma mo abɔɔ ŋan pou me dana nɛ fa ayematena, te ma mo ma wosenaane fa nyi baa too, te balaŋ kegyae be doo me man ya na ŋan pou mɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ yɛɛ yakaa.

eng: Love is patient, love is kind, it isnt jealous, it doesnt brag, it isnt arrogant.
local: Nyi n ne gyae walaŋ na n ne nyiŋ konɔɔ fa e, te n ne wii ɔ waraŋase mɔ. Ɔ kolo bɛɛ bere n sia ya te n bɛɛ wola n wose ya, te n bɛɛ kɛɛ n wose nyi n kela e ya.

eng: it isnt rude, it doesnt seek its own advantage, it isnt irritable, it doesnt keep a record of complaints.
local: Nyi akpaa n ne gyae walaŋ na, n bɛɛ yɛɛ e abɔɔ ŋan tekaboena nɛ ya, te n bɛɛ gyae nyi nyaŋ nyaageŋ wui ke de na ŋon wui ke wɔlɛɛ ya. N bɛɛ gyeŋ baŋ bileŋbileŋ na e ya, te n bɛɛ mo ɔ nombia do n tɔɔman ya.

eng: it isnt happy with injustice, but it is happy with the truth.
local: Nyi n ne gyae walaŋ nɛ ɔ yɛɛ nombiikum na bɛɛ yɛɛ neŋ gyoŋ ya, mɔna akpaa ɔ yɛɛ nombii kpaakpaa na ne yɛɛ neŋ gyoŋ.

eng: Love puts up with all things, trusts in all things, hopes for all things, endures all things.
local: Nyi akpaa balaŋ kegyae doo n man nɛ, debaŋ kamasɛ na n ne tale seŋ keŋkeŋ abɔɔ pou man, te ne yeli n kelɛɛdi na sem nideli. N ne nyiŋ konɔɔ abɔɔ pou man, te ne yeli n ne tale seŋ keŋkeŋ abɔɔ ŋan n loo gyakaa ŋe dɔɔ nɛ man, te n ne yɛɛ kakyeŋ seŋ keŋkeŋ abɔɔ pou man.

eng: Love never fails. As for prophecies, they will be brought to an end. As for tongues, they will stop. As for knowledge, it will be brought to an end.
local: Nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra ne kolosi see, na deise kpookpoo kekolosi na nombia kagyeŋ pou gyae ke gyu ɔto. Mɔna balaŋ kegyae dɔɔ kɔɔ taŋ ɔto ya.

eng: We know in part and we prophesy in part.
local: De nombia kagyeŋ na doŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo fa daa te dɛɛ tale kolosi ɔ nombia nɛ yɛɛ yaale yaale.

eng: but when the perfect comes, what is partial will be brought to an end.
local: Mɔna akpaa nyi kpene keŋ ke yɛɛ debiliŋ nɛ de kɔŋ na, ŋan ŋe yɛɛ yaale yaale nɛ gyae ke ŋaalaa.

eng: When I was a child, I used to speak like a child, reason like a child, think like a child. But now that I have become a man, Ive put an end to childish things.
local: Debaŋ keŋ me yɛɛ bu e na mee kolosi bo ŋgba biikyaabii nɛ, te mɛɛ gyueŋ mɔ ŋgba biikyaabii nɛ, te me yɛɛ ma kpene kamasɛ mɔ bo ŋgba biikyaabii nɛ. Mɔna me ka bee kei nɛ, me te tina biatɛɛ nombia ŋan pou yela.

eng: Now we see a reflection in a mirror; then we will see face-to-face. Now I know partially, but then I will know completely in the same way that I have been completely known.
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan dɛɛ naa debokeiman nɛ, dɔɔ ne naa ŋa nideli ya. Ŋe yɛɛ fulaa de siaman ŋgba mena keŋ n ne kɛɛ kolo diiku bene man te n bɛɛ naa ke nideli ya. Mɔna debaŋ daale gyae kelii keŋ dɛɛ naa de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ na de sia fonɛɛ, na waa yela dɛɛ gyeŋ ɔ nombia ŋan man nideli. Debaŋ kei mɔ nɛ me nombia kagyeŋ yɛɛ kyomii nɛ, mɔna debaŋ daale gyae kelii na, maa gyeŋ nombia nideli ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ gyeŋ maŋ nideli nɛ.

eng: Now faith, hope, and love remain—these three things—and the greatest of these is love.
local: Abɔɔ kɛŋa pou man nɛ nombia atooro kyaa la keŋ ŋɔɔ taŋ ɔto ya. Ŋan yɛna kelɛɛdi na lookegyakaa na kegyae. Mɔna ŋe popou man na, kegyae yɛna ŋa nyee.

eng: Pursue love, and use your ambition to try to get spiritual gifts but especially so that you might prophesy.
local: Ɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ na ɛ ke gyae dɔŋa, na ɛ ke yase ɛ sia gyae Wurubuarɛ feliŋ abɔɔ anefaŋ ŋan gbɛɛ. Tuturu na, ɛ yase ɛ sia gyae keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa ta ɛ dɔɔ kolosi nɛ gbɛɛ.

eng: This is because those who speak in a tongue dont speak to people but to God; no one understands it—they speak mysteries by the Spirit.
local: Nawolo nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kolosi deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo nɛ, ɔ bɛɛ kolosi fa denibalaŋ ya mɔna ɔ ne kolosi bo fa Wurubuarɛ. N kɛɛ na ŋolo bee nyii ɔ nombia ŋan man ya, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne tɛɛ la ɔ dɔɔ kolosi nombia aweeseŋ ŋenaŋ.

eng: Those who prophesy speak to people, building them up, and giving them encouragement and comfort.
local: Mɔna walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ ne tɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ kolosi nɛ, ɔ nombia ŋan na do balaŋ doŋ na kakyeŋ te ne yeli bɛɛ tale kyaŋ ba wose.

eng: People who speak in a tongue build up themselves; those who prophesy build up the church.
local: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kolosi deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo nɛ, ŋon waageŋ te nombia ŋan ɔ ne kolosi nɛ dana tɔnɔɔ fa. Mɔna ŋon Wurubuarɛ ne tɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ kolosi nɛ, ɔ nombia ŋan ɔ ne kolosi nɛ dana tɔnɔɔ fa Kristotena dikpii keŋ pou.

eng: I wish that all of you spoke in tongues, but Id rather you could prophesy. Those who prophesy are more important than those who speak in tongues, unless they are able to interpret them so that the church might be built up.
local: Nafɔ ma kegyae yɛna nyi ɛ popou yaa kolosi deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo, mɔna keŋ mɛɛ gyae kela yɛna nyi, Wurubuarɛ ke ta ɛ dɔɔ kolosi. Nawolo nyi walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ ne tɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ kolosi na, ɔ kela walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kolosi deise kpookpoo. Gyisɛ ŋolo kyaa botɔɔ keŋ ɔ ne tale lese deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo ŋan man, na kekyɔ Kristotena dikpii keŋ pou, na baa bee bɔ ɔsom keŋ man.

eng: After all, brothers and sisters, if I come to you speaking in tongues, how will I help you unless I speak to you with a revelation, some knowledge, a prophecy, or a teaching?.
local: Me tebia, nyi akpaa me kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ te me kolosi deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo na, woŋ tɔnɔɔ yaa ɛ ke nyiŋ lee ŋe man? Mɔna akpaa me kaa yako ŋon nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ ba lese wola man, yaa nombia kagyeŋ wose man nombia, yaa nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ ba mo fa maŋ nyi me kaa yako ŋon, yaa me kaa wola ŋon nombia na, na kɛŋa be dana tɔnɔɔ fa ŋon yee?.

eng: Likewise, things that arent alive like a harp or a lyre can make a sound, but if there arent different notes in the sounds they make, how will the tune from the harp or the lyre be recognized?.
local: Mena mɔ te abɔɔ ŋan nyeedoŋ be doo ŋe man ya ŋgba kefereese na kaberɛse nɛ, akpaa be ta wola ŋa nideli ya na amɔte ne gyae la waa nyii ŋe man?.

eng: And if a trumpet call is unrecognizable, then who will prepare for battle?.
local: Te akpaa yoonɔɔte ŋon ta wola ɔ kaberɛ nideli ya na, sena te ŋolo waa nyii ke man na waa koro desina ɔ wose fa yoo?.

eng: Its the same way with you: If you dont use language that is easy to understand when you speak in a tongue, then how will anyone understand what is said?.
local: Mena te doo fa ɛmɛɛ mɔ. Akpaa ŋolo de kolosi dei wɔle kpoo daale, te ŋolo te nyii ke man ya na sena te ŋolo waa yɛɛ na waa tale nyii nombia ŋan ɔ ne yako nɛ man? Dekɔŋ mena na, ɔ ne kolosi bo do feliŋ man yakaa.

eng: It will be as if you are speaking into the air! There are probably many language families in the world, and none of them are without meaning.
local: Deise kpookpoo burum kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, mɔna dei daale be kyaa keŋ ŋolo bee nyii ke man ya.

eng: So if I dont know the meaning of the language, then I will be like a foreigner to those who speak it, and they will be like foreigners to me.
local: Akpaa nyi mɔɔ ne nyii dei keŋ ŋolo ne kolosi ya na me yɛɛ ŋɔɔle e ɔ siaman, te mena mɔ te ŋon mɔ yɛɛ ŋɔɔle e me siaman.

eng: The same holds true for you: since you are ambitious for spiritual gifts, use your ambition to try to work toward being the best at building up the church.
local: Mena te doo fa ɛmɛɛ mɔ. Debaŋ keŋ ɛ sia be yɛɛ ɛ ne kɛo Wurubuarɛ feliŋ abɔɔ anefaŋ ŋan nɛ, ɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ kɛo keŋ gyae ke yela Kristotena dikpii keŋ ka bee bɔ ɔsom keŋ man nɛ.

eng: Therefore, those who speak in a tongue should pray to be able to interpret.
local: Mena dɔɔ akpaa nyi ŋolo ne kolosi dei wɔle kpoo daale na, kaboena nyi waa fane na waa nyiŋ tale lese ke man.

eng: If I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays but my mind isnt productive.
local: Nawolo nyi akpaa me fane dei kpoo man na, feliŋ ŋon ɔ doo me man nɛ ne fane la, mɔna ma agyueŋ be doo botɔɔ ya.

eng: What should I do? Ill pray in the Spirit, but Ill pray with my mind too; Ill sing a psalm in the Spirit, but Ill sing the psalm with my mind too.
local: Mena dɔɔ sena yaa maa yɛɛ? Maa mo feliŋ ŋon ɔ doo me man nɛ fane, na maa mo ma agyueŋ mɔ fane. Maa mo feliŋ ŋon ɔ doo me man nɛ kpee nɔɔneŋ, na maa mo ma agyueŋ mɔ kpee nɔɔneŋ.

eng: After all, if you praise God in the Spirit, how will the people who arent trained in that language say Amen! to your thanksgiving, when they dont know what you are saying?.
local: Nyi ɛ gyaŋee ɛ ne fane te n mo dei daale kpoo n na lese Wurubuarɛ yele, te walaŋ yakaa ŋolo kyaa ɛ man keŋ ɔ bee nyii n dei keŋ man ya na, sena te waa tale sɛɛ neŋ nyi, Dɛɛkɔŋ Mena, keŋ nyi ɔ te nyii kpene keŋ n be yako nɛ man ya?.

eng: You may offer a beautiful prayer of thanksgiving, but the other person is not being built up.
local: Mberem na n fanewɔ nideli lese Wurubuarɛ yele, mɔna ke taŋ tɔnɔɔ fa n balate ŋon ya.

eng: I thank God that I speak in tongues more than all of you.
local: Mɛɛ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ ŋgba mɛɛ tale kolosi deise kpookpoo kela ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ nɛ.

eng: But in the church Id rather speak five words in my right mind than speak thousands of words in a tongue so that I can teach others.
local: Mɔna akpaa me kpuɛ Kristotena dikpii keŋ man na, mɛɛ gyae nyi maa kolosi nyansa nombia kyomii bo mo wola balaŋ nombia, kela keŋ nyi maa kolosi nombia burum deise kpookpoo man keŋ bɔɔ gyae baa nyii ŋe man ya.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont be like children in the way you think. Well, be babies when it comes to evil, but be adults in your thinking.
local: Me tebia, ɛ na gyueŋ ŋgba bia nɛ ya. Lee dukum keyɛɛ man na, ɛ yɛɛ ɛ wose ŋgba kyokyoosebia baŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ dukum ya nɛ. Mɔna lee ɛ kɛɛ gyueŋ man na, ɛ gyueŋ ŋgba balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ bee Wurubuarɛ nombia man nɛ.

eng: In the Law it is written: I will speak to this people with foreign languages and foreigners lips, but they will not even listen to me this way , says the Lord.
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa, Maa ta balaŋ baŋ bee kolosi deise kpookpoo nɛ dɔɔ kolosi fa me balaŋ. Maa ta ŋɔɔla dɔɔ kolosi fa me balaŋ. Mɔna kɛŋa wɔle koraŋ na, bɔɔ gyae baa nyii me dei ya.

eng: So then, tongues are a sign for those who dont believe, not for those who believe. But prophecy is a sign for believers, not for those who dont believe.
local: Deise kpookpoo kekolosi te yɛɛ kpene keŋ Kristotena na mo gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ doŋ ya, mɔna baŋ ba te lɛɛ ta Kristo di ya nɛ, bɛɛ mo ke gyene Wurubuarɛ doŋ. Wurubuarɛ nombia keyako see yɛɛ kpene keŋ ke dana tɔnɔɔ fa baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Kristo di nɛ. Te baŋ ba te lɛɛ ta Kristo di ya nɛ dɔɔ, ka be dana tɔnɔɔ fa wɔ ya.

eng: So suppose that the whole church is meeting and everyone is speaking in tongues. If people come in who are outsiders or unbelievers, wont they say that you are out of your minds?.
local: Mena dɔɔ akpaa Kristotena dikpii keŋ de gyaŋee te walaŋ kamasɛ ne kolosi deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo, te akpaa balaŋ wɔlɛɛ yaa baŋ ba te yɛɛ Kristotenawɔ ya nɛ de kɔŋ botɔɔ na, bɔɔ gyae baa yako nyi ba nyeeya ne wɔlɛɛ baa yee?.

eng: But if everyone is prophesying when an unbeliever or outsider comes in, they are tested by all and called to account by all.
local: Mɔna nyi akpaa Wurubuarɛ de tɛɛ ɛ popou dɔɔ ɔ ne kolosi, te walaŋ yakaate yaa ŋon ɔ te yɛɛ Kristo walaŋ e ya nɛ de kaa gyoo ɛ man na, nombia ŋan ɔ ne nyii nɛ gyae ka lese ɔ dukum keyɛɛ debɔɔ, na ke yela waa gyeŋ nyi ɔ yɛɛ dukumyɛɛre e.

eng: The secrets of their hearts are brought to light. When that happens, they will fall on their faces and worship God, proclaiming out loud that truly God is among you!.
local: Nombia ŋenaŋ gyae ka lese ɔ konɔɔ man nombia ŋan pou weesɛɛ nɛ debɔɔ, na waa buŋ som Wurubuarɛ na waa yako nyiaa, Ampaŋ Wurubuarɛ kpuɛ ɛ man.

eng: What is the outcome of this, brothers and sisters? When you meet together, each one has a psalm, a teaching, a revelation, a tongue, or an interpretation. All these things must be done to build up the church.
local: Me tebia, nombia ŋan mɛɛ yako nɛ mɛɛ wolo baa nyi akpaa ɛ kɔŋ kaa gyaŋee nɛ, mberem na ŋolo dana nɔɔneŋ keŋ waa kpee na ŋolo mɔ dana kolo waa wola, yaa ŋolo mɔ dana nombii keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba lese wola e. Mberem na ŋolo mɔ ne gyae waa mo deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo kolosi, yaa ŋolo mɔ kyaa keŋ waa lese dei wɔle kei man nɛ, kaboena nyi yaa yɛɛ kɛŋa pou na ɛ ka mo do Kristotena baŋ kakyeŋ.

eng: If some speak in a tongue, then let two or at most three speak, one at a time, and someone must interpret.
local: Akpaa ɛ man baale baa kolosi deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo na, baa yɛɛ balaŋ bala te ba boo paa na baa yɛɛ balaŋ batooro. Mɔna kaboena nyi baa kolosi dokoloŋ dokoloŋ, na ŋolo mɔ ka lese ŋe man.

eng: However, if there is no interpreter, then they should keep quiet in the meeting. They should speak privately to themselves and to God.
local: Nyi akpaa ŋolo be kyaa keŋ waa lese nombia ŋan man ya nɛ, kenaŋ na ŋon ɔ ne kolosi ŋa nɛ waa kyaŋ ɔ nɔɔ wu Kristotena dikpii keŋ man, na waa kolosi fa Wurubuarɛ na ŋon gbagba.

eng: In the case of prophets, let two or three speak and have the rest evaluate what is said.
local: Ɛ yela Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra bala yaa batooro kekolosi, na balaŋ akaŋ baŋ ka gyoo be nombia ŋan bee kolosi nɛ man nideli.

eng: And if some revelation comes to someone else who is sitting down, the first one should be quiet.
local: Te akpaa ŋolo kyaa te Wurubuarɛ da lese nombia ŋaale wola e nɛ, ɛ yela walaŋ ŋon ɔ be taŋgbɛɛ ɔ ne kolosi nɛ ke tina kekolosi yela.

eng: You can all prophesy one at a time so that everyone can learn and be encouraged.
local: Nawolo nyi ɛ yɛɛ mena na Wurubuarɛ waa tale ta ɛ dɔɔ dokoloŋ dokoloŋ kolosi, na ke yela ɛ popou ke kasee kolo lee ke man na ka do ŋon kakyeŋ.

eng: The spirits of prophets are under the control of the prophets.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon Wurubuarɛ ne tɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ kolosi nɛ, kaboena nyi waa tale kyaŋ ɔ wose lee ɔ kekolosi keŋ man.

eng: God isnt a God of disorder but of peace. Like in all the churches of Gods people.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ te yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ abɔɔ basabasa ya, mɔna ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ wa abɔɔ na ŋe gbɛɛ.

eng: the women should be quiet during the meeting. They are not allowed to talk. Instead, they need to get under control, just as the Law says.
local: Ŋgba mena keŋ doo Kristotena akpiise ŋan pou man nɛ, kaboena nyi ala baa wu be nɔɔ. Be gyaŋee ɔsom man na gbɛɛ be doo nyi baa kolosi ya, mɔna kaboena nyi baa yɔkɔse ba wose ŋgba mena keŋ Mosesi mmaraa keŋ na wolo nɛ.

eng: If they want to learn something, they should ask their husbands at home. It is disgraceful for a woman to talk during the meeting.
local: Nyi akpaa bɛɛ gyae baa nyii nombii daale man nideli na, kaboena nyi baa bɔɔse be baala deni man. Nawolo nyi ke yɛɛ desɛŋ ke nyi alo ke yɛɛ nɔɔdoŋ Kristotena dɛɛgyaŋee man.

eng: Did the word of God originate with you? Has it come only to you?.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na wolo baa nyi ɛ gyaŋ te Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan leewɔ kɔŋ, yaa ɛmɛɛ yaageŋ nyii na ŋa?.

eng: If anyone thinks that they are prophets or spiritual people, then let them recognize that what Im writing to you is the Lords command.
local: Akpaa ŋolo ne baake ɔ wose nyi ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e, yaa ɔ ne kɛɛ ɔ wose nyi ɔ dana Wurubuarɛ feliŋ abɔɔ anefaŋ ŋan na, kaboena waa gyeŋ nyi me nombia ŋan mee kolosi nɛ pou lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

eng: If someone doesnt recognize this, they arent recognized.
local: Te akpaa nyi ŋolo te lɛɛ me nombia kɛŋa di ya nɛ, ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ na mo e yɛɛ kolo ya.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, use your ambition to try to get the gift of prophecy, but dont prevent speaking in tongues.
local: Mena dɔɔ me tebia, ɛ yase ɛ sia na Wurubuarɛ ke ta ɛ dɔɔ kolosi, mɔ ɛ na tɔ baŋ bee kolosi deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo nɛ gbɛɛ ya.

eng: Everything should be done with dignity and in proper order.
local: Mɔna kaboena nyi yaa yɛɛ nombia pou na ŋe gbɛɛ, ŋgba mena keŋ kaboena nɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want to call your attention to the good news that I preached to you, which you also received and in which you stand.
local: Me tebia, nɛnɛɛ mɛɛ gyae maa tɔɔse ŋon nyeedoŋ nombia ŋan mɔɔ yako ŋon te ɛ be lɛɛ di te ɛ seŋɛɛ ŋe man keŋkeŋ nɛ.

eng: You are being saved through it if you hold on to the message I preached to you, unless somehow you believed it for nothing.
local: Akpaa ɛ kyaŋ nombia ŋan mɔɔ yako ŋon nɛ man keŋkeŋ na, Wurubuarɛ waa ta ŋe dɔɔ lɛɛ ɛ nyee. Mɔna akpaa ɛ te kyaŋ ŋa keŋkeŋ ya na ɛ kelɛɛdi keŋ yɛɛ yakaa.

eng: I passed on to you as most important what I also received: Christ died for our sins in line with the scriptures.
local: Nawolo nyi kpene keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ba wola maŋ te ke dana tɔnɔɔ kela kpene kamasɛ nɛ, keŋ yaa ma moowɔ mɛɛ wola ŋon nɛ. Nombia ŋenaŋ yɛna nyi, De nombiakumɛɛ dɔɔ te Kristo kaa yeŋawɔ, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nɛ.

eng: he was buried, and he rose on the third day in line with the scriptures.
local: Be wuu e te weeya atoorote keŋ na Wurubuarɛ gyuusu e lee yeŋ man, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nɛ.

eng: He appeared to Cephas, then to the Twelve.
local: Ɔ lese ɔ wose wola Pita. Kenaŋ wɔle na te ɔ lese ɔ wose wola ɔ kpilala kufu bala baŋ mɔ.

eng: and then he appeared to more than five hundred brothers and sisters at once—most of them are still alive to this day, though some have died.
local: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ ɔ besewɔ lese ɔ wose wola ɔ kaseela baŋ be yaanɔɔle kela balaŋ sɔŋonoŋ akpeŋ anoŋ nɛ debaŋ dokoloŋ man. Be man balaŋ burum sɛɛ be kyaa na be sia te baale mɔ ta yekee.

eng: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
local: Keŋte ɔ lese ɔ wose wola Gyemisi, te ɔ besewɔ lese ɔ wose wola kpilala baŋ pou.

eng: and last of all he appeared to me, as if I were born at the wrong time.
local: Wɔle wɔle nɛ, ɔ kaa lese ɔ wose wola maŋ Pɔɔl ŋon nafɔ ŋolo te gyakaa loo nyi maa kaa bese Kristo walaŋ ya nɛ.

eng: Im the least important of the apostles. I dont deserve to be called an apostle, because I harassed Gods church.
local: Maŋ yɛna Kristo kpilala baŋ pou man kemaasa, te ma tekaboena gba nyi nafɔ baa baake maŋ nyi Kristo kpilale ya. Nawolo nyi me naase Kristotena baŋ diyem nideli.

eng: I am what I am by Gods grace, and Gods grace hasnt been for nothing. In fact, I have worked harder than all the others—that is, it wasnt me but the grace of God that is with me.
local: Mɔna lee Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ dɔɔ te gyɛŋ me kaa bese walaŋ nɛ. Ɔ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba mo boele maŋ nɛ te yɛɛ yakaa ya, nawolo nyi me yɛɛ tom kela Kristo kpilala akaŋ baŋ pou. Mɔna na maŋ gbagba doŋ te ma moowɔ yɛɛ ya, Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba mo boele maŋ nɛ yɛɛ la.

eng: So then, whether you heard the message from me or them, this is what we preach and this is what you have believed.
local: Mena dɔɔ Kristo nombia kɛŋa lee me gyaŋ o ŋa lee be gyaŋ bo o, ŋan te de popou ne kolosi te ŋan mɔ te ɛ lɛɛwɔ di.

eng: So if the message that is preached says that Christ has been raised from the dead, then how can some of you say, Theres no resurrection of the dead?.
local: Mɔna akpaa dee kolosi nyeekelɛɛ nombia ŋan nyi Kristo yeŋawɔ te Wurubuarɛ gyuusu e lee yeŋ man na, woŋ dɔɔ te ɛmɛɛ baale ne yako nyi kefoŋ lee yeŋ man be kyaa ya?.

eng: If theres no resurrection of the dead, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Nyi akpaa kefoŋ lee yeŋ man be kyaa ya na, kenaŋ na nawolo baa nyi Wurubuarɛ te gyuusu Kristo lee yeŋ man ya.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then our preaching is useless and your faith is useless.
local: Te akpaa Wurubuarɛ te gyuusu Kristo lee yeŋ man ya na, kenaŋ na Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan dee kolosi na ɛ kelɛɛdi keŋ mɔ yɛɛ yakaa.

eng: We are found to be false witnesses about God, because we testified against God that he raised Christ, when he didnt raise him if its the case that the dead arent raised.
local: Nyi akpaa be doo mena ya na kenaŋ na daa Kristo kpilala baŋ te di dekara gyakaa Wurubuarɛ dɔɔ, nawolo nyi dɛɛ yako nyi Wurubuarɛ gyuusu Kristo lee yeŋ man. Nyi akpaa woya be gyae baa foŋ lee yeŋ man ya na, kenaŋ na Wurubuarɛ te gyuusu Kristo lee yeŋ man ya.

eng: If the dead arent raised, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Mɔ akpaa kefoŋ lee yeŋ man be kyaa ya na, kenaŋ na Wurubuarɛ te gyuusu Kristo lee yeŋ man ya.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then your faith is worthless; you are still in your sins.
local: Te akpaa nyi Wurubuarɛ te gyuusu Kristo lee yeŋ man ya nɛ, kenaŋ na ɛ kelɛɛdi keŋ yɛɛ yakaa te ɛ sɛɛ ɛ doo ɛ nombiakumɛɛ man.

eng: and whats more, those who have died in Christ are gone forever.
local: Kenaŋ na Kristotena baŋ mɔ bɔɔ yekee nɛ ta yekee leki.

eng: If we have a hope in Christ only in this life, then we deserve to be pitied more than anyone else.
local: Akpaa nyi tɛɛle kei dɔɔ kaageŋ kekyaa dɔɔ te de loo gyakaa Kristo dɔɔ na, kenaŋ na de nombia dana waraŋase kela walaŋ kamasɛ.

eng: But in fact Christ has been raised from the dead. Hes the first crop of the harvest of those who have died.
local: Mɔna anokoare keŋ doo ke man yɛna nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ gyuusu Kristo lee yeŋ man. Ŋon Kristo yɛna woya baŋ pou Wurubuarɛ waa kaa gyuusu wɔ nɛ be gyaŋgbate.

eng: Since death came through a human being, the resurrection of the dead came through one too.
local: Nawolo nyi mena keŋ yeŋ be tɛɛ walaŋ dokoloŋ dɔɔ kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, mena mɔ te kefoŋ lee yeŋ man mɔ tɛɛwɔ walaŋ dokoloŋ dɔɔ kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ.

eng: In the same way that everyone dies in Adam, so also everyone will be given life in Christ.
local: Ŋgba mena keŋ balaŋ pou ba lee Adam man te ɔ nombiakumɛɛ dɔɔ bɛɛ yekee nɛ, mena mɔ te balaŋ baŋ pou be yɛɛ Kristo wui nɛ baa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa lee Kristo yeŋ keŋ man.

eng: Each event will happen in the right order: Christ, the first crop of the harvest, then those who belong to Christ at his coming.
local: Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ dana ɔ debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu e lee yeŋ man. Kristo taŋgbɛɛ la foŋ lee yeŋ man, te wee keŋ waa bese kɔŋ na balaŋ baŋ be yɛɛ ɔ wui nɛ mɔ baa foŋ lee yeŋ man.

eng: and then the end, when Christ hands over the kingdom to God the Father, when he brings every form of rule, every authority and power to an end.
local: Kei wɔle na kpene kamasɛ gyae ke kɔŋ kɔ ɔto. Debaŋ kenaŋ te Kristo waa di doŋse pou na gyooneŋ na ŋe gyoorobiiri pou dɔɔ, na waa mo gyoori keŋ do ɔ kya Wurubuarɛ nyiŋmaa man.

eng: It is necessary for him to rule until he puts all enemies under his feet .
local: Nawolo nyi kaboena nyi Kristo waa di gyoori, kelii debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ kɔlala pou do ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ.

eng: Death is the last enemy to be brought to an end.
local: Te kɔlare ɔtomante ŋon Wurubuarɛ waa wɔlɛɛ e nɛ yɛna yeŋ.

eng: since he has brought everything under control under his feet. When it says that everything has been brought under his control, this clearly means everything except for the one who placed everything under his control.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako lee Kristo wose man nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ ta mo kpene kamasɛ do Kristo tɛɛ. Mɔna keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yako nyi ɔ ta mo kpene kamasɛ do ɔ tɛɛ nɛ, keŋ bɛɛ wolo nyi ŋon Wurubuarɛ gbagba mɔ doo ɔ tɛɛ ya. Nawolo nyi ŋon Wurubuarɛ moona kpene kamasɛ do Kristo tɛɛ.

eng: But when all things have been brought under his control, then the Son himself will also be under the control of the one who gave him control over everything so that God may be all in all.
local: Debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ da mo abɔɔ kɛŋa pou do ɔ bu ŋon tɛɛ taŋ nɛ, ɔ bu ŋon mɔ waa mo ɔ wose do ɔ kya Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ba mo kpene kamasɛ do ɔ tɛɛ nɛ tɛɛ. Na balaŋ pou kena nyi Wurubuarɛ yɛna kpene kamasɛ nyee.

eng: Otherwise, what are those who are getting baptized for the dead doing? If the dead arent raised, then why are they being baptized for them?.
local: Te akpaa kefoŋ lee yeŋ man be kyaa ya na, woŋ dɔɔ yaa balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ lɛɛ ba woya nɔɔ seŋ nyi baa sɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ nɛ loo gyakaa? Yaa akpaa nyi bɔɔ gyae baa gyuusu balaŋ lee yeŋ man korakora ya na, weera te baale ne lɛɛ ba loŋtona baŋ bɔɔ yekee nɛ nɔɔ seŋ nyi baa sɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ fa wɔ?.

eng: And what about us? Why are we in danger all day every day?.
local: Te daa gbagba mɔ de? Nyi akpaa kefoŋ lee yeŋ man be kyaa ya na, woŋ dɔɔ te wee kamasɛ dɛɛ mo da wose fa yeŋ?.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I swear by the pride I have in you in Christ Jesus our Lord, Im facing death every day.
local: Me tebia, mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi yeŋ nombia ne tuŋ maŋ wee kamasɛ. Lee mena keŋ daa na ŋon na Kristo yɛɛ dokoloŋ dɔɔ, mɛɛ tale mo ŋon kyɔ poaa. Mena dɔɔ te mee kolosi nombii kei.

eng: From a human point of view, what good does it do me if I fought wild animals in Ephesus? If the dead arent raised, lets eat and drink because tomorrow well die .
local: Nyi tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nombia dɔɔ te daa na balaŋ baŋ be yɛɛ ŋgba wonembia baŋ bɛɛ kyaŋ balaŋ nɛ yoo yoo dinaa kei Efeso donɔɔ man na, woŋ tɔnɔɔ yaa maa nyiŋ lee ke man? Akpaa nyi kefoŋ lee yeŋ man be kyaa ya na, ɛ yela dɛɛ yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ dudu daale ne yako nɛ nyiaa, Ɛ yela dee di na dɛɛ nyɔɔ, nawolo nyi kyoo kaageŋ na de ta yem.

eng: Dont be deceived, bad company corrupts good character.
local: Ɛ na yeli ŋolo ka beo ŋon ya, Nawolo nyi dɔɔre kum ne wɔlɛɛ dinɔɔ kpaakpaate.

eng: Sober up by acting like you should and dont sin. Some of you are ignorant about God—I say this because you should be ashamed of yourselves!.
local: Ɛ bese gyoo ɛ wose man nideli na ɛ ke tina nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ yela. Nawolo nyi dana desɛŋ ŋgba ɛ man baale sɛɛ ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia korakora ya nɛ.

eng: But someone will say, How are the dead raised? What kind of body will they have when they come back.
local: Mberem na ŋolo waa bɔɔse nyiaa, Sena te woya baa tale foŋ lee yeŋ man bela, te wosenaane woŋti yaa baa nyiŋ.

eng: Look, fool! When you put a seed into the ground, it doesnt come back to life unless it dies.
local: Diyiŋ nombia kebɔɔse yaa kei. Nawolo nyi kpene kamasɛ keŋ n duu tɛɛle man nɛ, kɛɛ daale pɛte kolo wɔle ne tuu lee ke man.

eng: What you put in the ground doesnt have the shape that it will have, but its a bare grain of wheat or some other seed.
local: Akpaa n duu kolo bii nɛ, keŋ n ba mo duu na keŋ nyaŋ ke kɔ nɛ te yɛɛ kyɛɛkyɛɛ ya.

eng: God gives it the sort of shape that he chooses, and he gives each of the seeds its own shape.
local: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ ne faa na ke ke dinɔɔ, ŋgba mena keŋ ŋon gbagba ne gyae nɛ. Kpene kamasɛ keŋ n duu na, Wurubuarɛ ne yeli kɛɛ bee nyiŋ ke dinɔɔ.

eng: All flesh isnt alike. Humans have one kind of flesh, animals have another kind of flesh, birds have another kind of flesh, and fish have another kind.
local: Wosenaane pou te yɛɛ dokoloŋ ya. Denibalaŋ wui kpoo te wonembia wui kpoo te gyebuse wui kpoo te kpebia mɔ wui kpoo.

eng: There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies. The heavenly bodies have one kind of glory, and the earthly bodies have another kind of glory.
local: Mena mɔ te abɔɔ ŋan pou Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ nɛ, ŋan ŋe kyaa adido nɛ ŋa kyeo kpoo te ŋan ŋe kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ mɔ nɛ kyeo kpoo.

eng: The sun has one kind of glory, the moon has another kind of glory, and the stars have another kind of glory .
local: Weese kɛɛ ŋalakee kpoo te gyaalaŋ mɔ wui keŋalakee kpoo te kyolobiise mɔ kɛɛ ŋalakee kpoo. Kyolobii kamasɛ mɔ dana mena keŋ ɔ ne ŋalakee.

eng: Its the same with the resurrection of the dead: a rotting body is put into the ground, but what is raised wont ever decay.
local: Mena ke te gyae ke yɛɛ debaŋ keŋ balaŋ baa foŋ lee yeŋ man nɛ. Akpaa be wuu walaŋ na waa dala, mɔna Wurubuarɛ de gyuusu e na waa fa e wosenaane wɔle keŋ be gyae ke dala ya.

eng: Its degraded when its put into the ground, but its raised in glory. Its weak when its put into the ground, but its raised in power.
local: Nyi akpaa be wuu ke na kɔɔ dana tɔnɔɔ bela ya, mɔna akpaa be gyuusu ke lee yeŋ man na ke dana obuo. Nyi be wuu ke na kɔɔ dana doŋ ya, mɔna akpaa be gyuusu ke na ke dana doŋ.

eng: Its a physical body when its put into the ground, but its raised as a spiritual body. If theres a physical body, theres also a spiritual body.
local: Deniwalaŋ wosenaane keŋ bɛɛ mo wuu nɛ, ke yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ wosenaane ke. Mɔna akpaa Wurubuarɛ de gyuusu ke na ke ta bese Wurubuarɛ man wosenaane. Tɛɛle kei dɔɔ wosenaane kyaa, mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi Wurubuarɛ man wosenaane mɔ dɛɛ kyaa.

eng: So it is also written, The first human, Adam, became a living person, and the last Adam became a spirit that gives life.
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see yakowɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ feliŋ do Adam gyaŋgbate ŋon man te ɔ nyiŋa nyeedoŋ. Mɔna Yesu Kristo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Adam balate ŋon nɛ, ŋon yɛna Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ɔ ne fa nyeedoŋ kekpaa.

eng: But the physical body comes first, not the spiritual one—the spiritual body comes afterward.
local: Na ŋon ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ e nɛ taŋgbɛɛ la kɔŋ ya, mɔna ŋon ɔ yɛɛ deniwalaŋ e nɛ taŋgbɛɛ la kɔŋ. Kenaŋ wɔle na pɛte ŋon ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ e nɛ kɔŋawɔ.

eng: The first human was from the earth made from dust; the second human is from heaven.
local: Adam gyaŋgbate ŋon lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nawolo nyi tɛɛle te Wurubuarɛ moowɔ yɛɛ e, mɔna Adam balate ŋon dɔɔ ŋon lee Wurubuarɛ man adido.

eng: The nature of the person made of dust is shared by people who are made of dust, and the nature of the heavenly person is shared by heavenly people.
local: Mena keŋ Adam ŋon ɔ ba lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ doo nɛ, mena mɔ te tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ doo. Te mena keŋ Adam ŋon ɔ ba lee Wurubuarɛ man doo nɛ, mena mɔ te Wurubuarɛ man balaŋ doo.

eng: We will look like the heavenly person in the same way as we have looked like the person made from dust.
local: Mena keŋ walaŋ ŋon bɔɔ mo tɛɛle ma e dinɔɔ doo de man nɛ, mena mɔ te walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba lee adido nɛ dinɔɔ gyae ke doo de man.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: Flesh and blood cant inherit Gods kingdom. Something that rots cant inherit something that doesnt decay.
local: Me tebia, mɛɛ gyae ɛ ka gyeŋ nyi kpene keŋ ke yɛɛ wosenaane na fatabo ke nɛ, be gyae ke tale gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man ya. Mena mɔ te wosenaane kei keŋ gyae ke dala nɛ, be gyae ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa ya.

eng: Listen, Im telling you a secret: All of us wont die, but we will all be changed.
local: Ɛ see deŋele na ɛ ke tei nombia aweeseŋ kɛŋa. Nombia ŋenaŋ yɛna nyiaa, na de popou ne gyae la dɛɛ yeŋ ya. Mɔna debaŋ keŋ ba wola kaberɛ ɔtomante keŋ na, Wurubuarɛ waa kyɛɛkee de popou karatetee dokoloŋ ŋgba mena keŋ ɔga ne ŋmaale nɛ.

eng: in an instant, in the blink of an eye, at the final trumpet. The trumpet will blast, and the dead will be raised with bodies that wont decay, and we will be changed.
local: Ba wola kaberɛ ɔtomante keŋ na Wurubuarɛ waa kyɛɛkee de popou, te baŋ bɔɔ yekee nɛ baa foŋ lee yeŋ man keŋ bɔɔ gyae baa yeŋ bela ya.

eng: Its necessary for this rotting body to be clothed with what cant decay, and for the body that is dying to be clothed in what cant die.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ waa besena wosenaane keŋ kɛɛ dale nɛ yɛɛ wosenaane keŋ ke bɛɛ dale ya. Na waa besena wosenaane keŋ kɛɛ yem nɛ yɛɛ wosenaane keŋ ke bɛɛ yem ya.

eng: And when the rotting body has been clothed in what cant decay, and the dying body has been clothed in what cant die, then this statement in scripture will happen: Death has been swallowed up by a victory .
local: Akpaa wosenaane keŋ kɛɛ dale nɛ da bese wosenaane keŋ ke bɛɛ dale ya, te wosenaane keŋ kɛɛ yem nɛ da bese wosenaane keŋ ke bɛɛ yem ya nɛ, kenaŋ na Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan bɔɔ ŋmarase see nɛ te kɔŋ mena. Nombia ŋenaŋ yɛna nyiaa, Da te yoo di yeŋ dɔɔ. Yeŋ be taŋ doŋ de dɔɔ bela ya.

eng: Where is your victory, Death? Where is your sting, Death? .
local: Yeŋ, n doŋ keŋ n dana n ne di balaŋ dɔɔ nɛ doo aleŋ? Yeŋ, n doŋ keŋ n dana n na mo do balaŋ kayeyɛɛ nɛ doo aleŋ.

eng: Deaths sting is sin, and the power of sin is the Law.
local: Yeŋ doŋ keŋ ɔ dana ɔ na do balaŋ kayeyɛɛ nɛ yɛna dukum, te mmaraa mɔ ne yele la dukum ne nyiŋ doŋ.

eng: Thanks be to God, who gives us this victory through our Lord Jesus Christ!.
local: Mɔna dɛɛ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ, nawolo nyi ŋon yele la te de tɛɛwɔ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo dɔɔ di kpene kamasɛ dɔɔ.

eng: As a result of all this, my loved brothers and sisters, you must stand firm, unshakable, excelling in the work of the Lord as always, because you know that your labor isnt going to be for nothing in the Lord.
local: Mena dɔɔ me tebia kpaakpaa ɛ seŋ keŋkeŋ, ɛ na yeli kolo ke wosi ŋon ya. Te debaŋ kamasɛ na, ɛ mo ɛ wose pou do de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu tom keŋ man. Nawolo nyi ɛ gyeŋ nyi tom keŋ ɛ ne yɛɛ lee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu doŋ dɔɔ nɛ be gyae ke yɛɛ yakaa ya.

eng: Concerning the collection of money for Gods people: You should do what I have directed the churches in Galatia to do.
local: Nɛnɛɛ toto keŋ ɛ ka do na baa mo kyɔ Wurubuarɛ balaŋ baŋ be kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ, ɛ yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ mɔɔ wola Kristotena akpiise ŋan kya Galatia tɛɛle man nɛ.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will, and Timothy our brother. To Gods church that is in Corinth, along with all of Gods people throughout Achaia.
local: Tɔne kei lee maŋ Pɔɔl ŋon me yɛɛ Yesu Kristo kpilale e lee Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii man nɛ, na de dɔɔ Timoti gyaŋ. Dɛɛ ŋmarase ke dee yelee ɛmɛɛ Kristotena dikpii keŋ ɛ kyaa Korinto donɔɔ man, na Wurubuarɛ balaŋ baŋ ɔ be baake te ɔ be desina wɔ see fa ɔ wose te be kyaa Akaya tɛɛle dɔɔ pou nɛ.

eng: On the first day of the week, each of you should set aside whatever you can afford from what you earn so that the collection wont be delayed until I come.
local: Kosida kamasɛ keŋ ɛ gyaŋee nɛ, kɔba keŋ walaŋ kamasɛ be yɛɛ tom Kosida kenaŋ man te ɔ be nyim nɛ, waa lese ke man ɔ doŋ ketalebii see yenaŋ. Na kɔbase ŋan pou kegyaŋee pɛna maa kɔŋ.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and from our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: De kya Wurubuarɛ na de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo baa mo be bɛrɛɛ boele ŋon, na baa fa ŋon wosefɛɛreŋ.

eng: Then when I get there, Ill send whomever you approve to Jerusalem with letters of recommendation to bring your gift.
local: Na akpaa me kaa gyoo ɛ gyaŋ na, ɛ ka mo ya abɔɔ ŋan do balaŋ baŋ ɛmɛɛ gbagba ba lese wɔ nɛ nyiŋmaa man, na maa ŋmarase me nyiŋmaa man tɔne kpu na ya abɔɔ ŋan fa wɔ, na baa mo gyina Kristotena baŋ be kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! He is the compassionate Father and God of all comfort.
local: Ɛ yela dɛɛ do Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo ɔ kya e nɛ nɔɔ. Ŋon yɛna Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ne wii balaŋ waraŋase te ɔ ne yako do wɔ.

eng: If it seems right for me to go too, theyll travel with me.
local: Te akpaa de kɔŋ nyi maŋ gbagba maa gyu na, maa kpu na wɔ na de popou ke gyu.

eng: Hes the one who comforts us in all our trouble so that we can comfort other people who are in every kind of trouble. We offer the same comfort that we ourselves received from God.
local: Ŋon ne yakola do daa de kayeyɛɛ man, na ke yela daa mɔ ke tale mo keyakodo keŋ ɔ be yako do daa nɛ yako do de tebia baŋ bɛɛ yeyɛɛ nɛ.

eng: Ill come to you after I go through Macedonia, and because Im going through Macedonia.
local: Nyi akpaa ma lee Masedonia tɛɛle man na maa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ, nawolo nyi ma te yɛɛ ma agyueŋ nyi maa mo na Masedonia tɛɛle man pɛ maa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ.

eng: That is because we receive so much comfort through Christ in the same way that we share so many of Christs sufferings.
local: De kɛɛ yeyɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ Kristo mɔ ba yeyɛɛ nɛ, mena mɔ te Wurubuarɛ waa ta Kristo dɔɔ yako do daa ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yako do Kristo nɛ.

eng: I may stay with you or even spend the winter there in Corinth so that you can send me on my way to wherever Im off to next.
local: Me kɔŋ na, mberem maa di weeya ala ŋaale ɛ gyaŋ, yaa maa kyaa kelii waare tɛɛ na ɛ ke nyiŋ kyɔ maŋ me gbɛɛneŋ ŋan maa gyu nɛ man.

eng: So if we have trouble, it is to bring you comfort and salvation. If we are comforted, it is to bring you comfort from the experience of endurance while you go through the same sufferings that we also suffer.
local: Nyi daa Kristo kpilala ne naa diyem na, ɛ nyeekelɛɛ na ɛ keyakodo dɔɔ te dɛɛ naa diyem. Mena keŋ dee nyiŋ keyakodo nɛ, ne yeli ɛmɛɛ mɔ ne nyiŋ keyakodo. Kei ne yeli ɛ ne tale nyiŋ konɔɔ naa diyem ŋgba mena keŋ daa mɔ ne naa nɛ.

eng: I dont want to make a quick visit to you, since I hope to spend some time with you if the Lord lets it happen.
local: Me gbɛɛ kei man dɔɔ, mɔɔ ne gyae nyi maa kpase kɔŋ kaa do ŋon nɔɔ yakaa na maa bese ya, mɔna akpaa Wurubuarɛ de sɛɛ na, maa kaa di weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale ɛ gyaŋ.

eng: Our hope for you is certain, because we know that as you are partners in suffering, so also you are partners in comfort.
local: Mena dɔɔ de loo gyakaa Wurubuarɛ dɔɔ nideli nyi waa yela ɛ ke tale seŋ keŋkeŋ, nawolo nyi da gyeŋ nyi mena keŋ ɛ ne naa diyem ŋgba mena keŋ daa mɔ ne naa ŋaale nɛ, mena te ɛ ke nyiŋ keyakodo ŋgba mena keŋ daa mɔ be nyiŋ keyakodo nɛ.

eng: Ill stay here in Ephesus until the Festival of Pentecost.
local: Mɔna maa kyaa Efeso donɔɔ man kɛbo kelii Pentekosite wee keŋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we dont want you to be unaware of the troubles that we went through in Asia. We were weighed down with a load of suffering that was so far beyond our strength that we were afraid we might not survive.
local: Me tebia, dɔɔ ne gyae ɛ ke kyaa ditiŋtɛɛ man lee diyem keŋ dɔɔ naa Esia tɛɛle man nɛ ya. Balaŋ yela da wose kyaŋa daa nideli, keŋ nyi da te gyakaa loo gba nyi dee nyiŋ nyeedoŋ ya.

eng: In spite of the fact that there are many opponents, a big and productive opportunity has opened up for my mission here.
local: Doo nyi balaŋ burum ta koro seŋ tia maŋ, mɔna ma te naa nyi Wurubuarɛ ta toro gbɛɛ dinaa daale fa maŋ, nyi maa yɛɛ tom keŋ ke gyae ke yɛɛ tɔnɔɔ nɛ kɛbo.

eng: It certainly seemed to us as if we had gotten the death penalty. This was so that we would have confidence in God, who raises the dead, instead of ourselves.
local: De konɔɔse man na, yɛɛ daa ŋgba ba te yako daa nyi baa ko daa nɛ. Kei yela de ta mo da wose lɔ daa gbagba de doŋ dɔɔ ya, mɔna da moo da wose bo lɔ Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyuusu balaŋ lee yeŋ man nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: If Timothy comes to you, be sure that he has no reason to be afraid while hes with you, because he does the work of the Lord just like I do.
local: Akpaa Timoti de kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ na ɛ lɛɛ e nideli na ɔ wɔe ke fɛɛ e, nawolo nyi ŋon mɔ ne yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu tom bo ŋgba maŋ nɛ.

eng: God rescued us from a terrible death, and he will rescue us. We have set our hope on him that he will rescue us again.
local: Ŋon lesena daa lee yeŋ nombia kɛŋa man, te ɔ gyakaa ɔ na lese daa kaa lii gyɛŋ. De loo gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ nyi waa lese daa debaŋ kamasɛ.

eng: So dont let anyone disrespect him, but send him on in peace so he can join me. Im waiting for him along with the brothers and sisters.
local: Ɛ man ŋolo na ŋolo na bɛɛ nyi ɔ be lɛɛ e ya, mɔna ɛ lɔ e gbɛɛ wosefɛɛreŋ man na waa nyiŋ bese kɔŋ me gyaŋ. Mɛɛ kɛɛ ɔ gbɛɛ nyi de tebia Kristotena baale baa kpu na e na baa kɔŋ.

eng: since you are helping with your prayer for us. Then many people can thank God on our behalf for the gift that was given to us through the prayers of many people.
local: Nawolo nyi ɛ ne fane fa daa dɔɔ. Dekɔŋ mena na, Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ de dɔɔ lee balaŋ burum kefane dɔɔ nɛ, gyae ke yela balaŋ burum ke fane fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ.

eng: Concerning Apollos our brother: I strongly encouraged him to visit you with the brothers and sisters, but he didnt want to go now. Hell come when he has an opportunity.
local: De gyoo Apolo dɔɔ ma doo e kakyeŋ nideli nyi waa kpu na de tebia Kristotena akaŋ baŋ na baa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ, mɔna ɔ te sɛɛ nyi waa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ debokeiman ya. Akpaa Wurubuarɛ de fa e gbɛɛ na, waa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ.

eng: We have conducted ourselves with godly sincerity and pure motives in the world, and especially toward you. This is why we are confident, and our conscience confirms this. We didnt act with human wisdom but we relied on the grace of God.
local: Nombii dokoloŋ keŋ dɔɔ te dɛɛ wola da wose nɛ yɛna nyiaa, de konɔɔ man nombia ne di daa adansɛɛ nyi, dɛɛ mo de konɔɔ pou yɛɛ nombia ŋan ayimɛɛ be doo ŋe man ya nɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, te dee di anokoare ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne gyae nɛ. Tuturu na, lee daa na ŋon kekyaa man. Na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyansa te dɛɛ mo yɛɛ nombia kɛŋa ya, mɔna Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba mo boele daa nɛ te dɛɛ mo yɛɛ.

eng: Stay awake, stand firm in your faith, be brave, be strong.
local: Ɛ na wolee ɛ wose ya. Ɛ kyaŋ ɛ kelɛɛdi keŋ man keŋkeŋ. Ɛ seŋ ŋgba baala nɛ, na ɛ ke yɛɛ doŋ.

eng: We dont write anything to you except what you can read and also understand. I hope that you will understand totally.
local: Nombia ŋan pou dɛɛ ŋmarase yelee ŋon nɛ, yɛɛ nombia ŋan ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ ke tale kala na ɛ ke nyii ŋe man.

eng: Everything should be done in love.
local: Ɛ yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ dɔŋa kegyae man.

eng: since you have already understood us partly. Understand that in the day of our Lord Jesus, we will make you proud as you will also make us proud.
local: Ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ be nyii de nombia ŋan man kyomii debaŋ kei nɛ, de loo gyakaa nyi ɛ ke kɔŋ kaa nyii ŋe man nideli wee daale. Daa dɔɔ te ɛ ke tale wola ɛ wose, ŋgba mena keŋ lee ɛmɛɛ mɔ dɔɔ, daa mɔ dɛɛ wola da wose wee keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu waa bese kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I encourage you to do something else. You know that the people in Stephanas household were the first crop of the harvest to come from the mission to Achaia. They have dedicated themselves to the service of Gods people.
local: Setefanase dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ gbagba gyeŋ nyi ŋon na ɔ dekpaŋalaŋmantena yɛna balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ taŋgbɛɛ lɛɛ Kristo di Akaya tɛɛle man. Be ta mo ba wose fa ɔsom keŋ, te bɛɛ kyɔ Kristotena baŋ nideli. Me tebia, mɛɛ waase ŋon bo nyi ɛ yɔkɔse ɛ wose fa mena balaŋ kɛwɔ dinɔɔ, na baŋ mɔ bɛɛ yɛɛ mena tom kei nɛ.

eng: Because I was sure of this, I wanted to visit you first so that you could have a second opportunity to see me.
local: Me loo gyakaa nyi ɛ ke nyii nombia kɛŋa man nideli, mena dɔɔ te nafɔ me yɛɛ ma agyueŋ nyi maa taŋgbɛɛ kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ, na ɛ ke nyiŋ di gyoŋ bola bola.

eng: Im so happy that Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus have arrived; theyve made up for my missing you.
local: Setefanase na Fɔtunatuse na Akayakuse ke kɔŋ me gyaŋ nɛ yɛɛ maŋ gyoŋ nideli. Nawolo nyi baŋ kɔŋala kaa seŋ ɛ nawɔɔ man.

eng: I wanted to visit you on my way to Macedonia, and then come to you again on my way back from Macedonia, at which point I was hoping you would help me on my way to Judea.
local: Nawolo nyi me yɛɛ ma agyueŋ nyi mee gyu Masedonia tɛɛle man na maa mo ɛ gyaŋ, te ma bese lee botɔɔ mɛɛ kɔŋ na maa mo ɛ gyaŋ bela, na ɛ ke nyiŋ lɔ maŋ gbɛɛ na maa gyu Gyudia tɛɛle man. Mɔna ta tekaa de ya.

eng: Indeed theyve provided my spirit and yours with a much-needed rest. Therefore, give them proper recognition.
local: Nawolo nyi ba te yeli ma nyee man nombia te yɛɛ maŋ dokoloŋ, ŋgba mena keŋ ɛmɛɛ mɔ wui be yɛɛ ŋon dokoloŋ nɛ. Kaboena nyi dɛɛ lese mena balaŋ kɛwɔ dinɔɔ yele.

eng: So I wasnt unreliable when I planned to do this, was I? Or do I make decisions with a substandard human process so that I say Yes, yes and No, no at the same time?.
local: Ma ke kyɛɛkee ma agyueŋ kei na, ke yɛɛ biatɛɛ agyueŋ ke? Yaa mɛɛ yɛɛ me nombia baa ŋgba tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ nɛ? Yaa nawolo baa nyi me dana nɔɔse ala ala?.

eng: The churches in the province of Asia greet you. Aquila and Prisca greet you warmly in the Lord, together with the church that meets in their house.
local: Kristotena akpiise ŋan kyaa Esia tɛɛle man nɛ na do ŋon nɔɔ. Akuila na Pirisila na Kristotena dikpii keŋ ne gyaŋee be dɛɛ nɛ, na do ŋon nɔɔ nideli lee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu yele man.

eng: But as God is faithful, our message to you isnt both yes and no.
local: Mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ anokoarete e keŋ ɔ bɛɛ lee ɔ nombii wɔle ya nɛ, mena mɔ yaa daa mɔ de nombia ŋan dɛɛ yako nɛ be yɛɛ nɔɔse ala ala ya.

eng: All the brothers and sisters greet you. You in turn should greet each other with a holy kiss.
local: De tebia Kristotena baŋ pou be kyaa kɛbo nɛ na do ŋon nɔɔ. Ɛ do dɔŋa nɔɔ gyoŋ man.

eng: Gods Son, Jesus Christ, is the one who was preached among you by us—through me, Silvanus, and Timothy—he wasnt yes and no. In him it is always yes.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ bu Yesu Kristo ŋon daa na Silase na Timoti be kolosi ɔ nombia fa ŋon nɛ, ɔ te yɛɛ nɔɔse ala ala walaŋ e ya, keŋ waa Sɛɛ na waa bese Bɛɛ ya. Mɔna ŋon dɔɔ, ɔ sɛɛ na ɔ te sɛɛ.

eng: Here is my greeting in my own handwriting—Paul.
local: Maŋ Pɔɔl gbagba moona me nyiŋmaa ŋmarase nɔɔkado tɔne kei.

eng: All of Gods promises have their yes in him. That is why we say Amen through him to the glory of God.
local: Lee Kristo dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ɔ be yako see nɛ pou kɔŋawɔ mena. Lee ŋon mɔ dɔɔ te de fane taŋ na, dɛɛ yako nyi Amɛn mo lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

eng: A curse on anyone who doesnt love the Lord. Come, Lord!.
local: Akpaa nyi ŋolo bɛɛ gyae de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nombia ya na, Wurubuarɛ waa falaa e. Maranata .

eng: God is the one who establishes us with you in Christ and who anointed us.
local: Wurubuarɛ gbagba ne yɛɛ la te daa na ŋon pou na sem keŋkeŋ Kristo kelɛɛdi keŋ man. Ŋon lesena daa te ɔ kpila daa nyi dee gyu ke yɛɛ ɔ kegyaebii.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
local: De Gbeŋgyoo Yesu waa mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele ŋon, na waa seŋ ɛ wɔle.

eng: God also sealed us and gave the Spirit as a down payment in our hearts.
local: Ŋon moona ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa de dɔɔ, te ɔ moo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do de konɔɔse man mo kyaŋ nae, na ke di adansɛɛ nyi de yɛɛ ɔ wui, te dee nyiŋ abɔɔ ŋan pou ɔ be desina see fa daa nɛ.

eng: My love is with all of you in Christ Jesus.
local: Mɛɛ gyae ɛ popou, te mɛɛ do ŋon nɔɔ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo yele man. Amɛn.

eng: I call on God as my witness—I didnt come again to Corinth because I wanted to spare you.
local: Wurubuarɛ yɛna ma adansɛɛ nyi ma te gyae nyi maa do ŋon kaale ya, mena dɔɔ te me kyɛɛkee ma agyueŋ nyi mɔɔ bese kɔŋ ɛmɛɛ Korintotena gyaŋ ya.

eng: So I decided that, for my own sake, I wouldnt visit you again while I was upset.
local: Mena dɔɔ ma te yɛɛ ma agyueŋ nyi, mɔɔ gyae maa bese kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ na maa yela nombia ke takaa ŋon ya.

eng: It isnt that we are trying to control your faith, but we are working with you for your happiness, because you stand firm in your faith.
local: Dɔɔ ne gyae dɛɛ ŋere ŋon nyi yaa lɛɛ Kristo nombia ŋan dɛɛ yako ŋon nɛ di ya. Nawolo nyi ɛ wulaa ɛ seŋɛɛ ɛ kelɛɛdi keŋ man keŋkeŋ. Mɔna keŋ dɛɛ gyae nyi ke yɛɛ ŋon gyoŋ dɔɔ te daa na ŋon ne yɛɛ tom bom nɛ.

eng: If I make you sad, who will be there to make me glad when you are sad because of me?.
local: Nawolo nyi akpaa me kɔŋ te me yeli nombia de takaa ŋon na, amɔte ne gyae la waa yela me sia kagye. Na ɛmɛɛ baŋ mɔɔ yeli nombia be takaa ŋon nɛ yee?.

eng: Thats why I wrote this very thing to you, so that when I came I wouldnt be made sad by the ones who ought to make me happy. I have confidence in you, that my happiness means your happiness.
local: Keŋ dɔɔ te mɔɔ ŋmarase ŋon tɔne kenaŋ yɛna nyiaa, mɔɔ ne gyae nyi me kɔŋ na kolo ke takaa ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ke yela me sia kagye nɛ ya. Nawolo nyi mɛɛ lɛɛ ɛ popou di nideli nyi akpaa me sia da gye na, ɛmɛɛ mɔ sia gyae ka gye.

eng: I wrote to you in tears, with a very troubled and anxious heart. I didnt write to make you sad but so you would know the overwhelming love that I have for you.
local: Debaŋ keŋ mɛɛ ŋmarase tɔne kei nɛ, na me tɔɔ ne kyoŋii maŋ. Nombia atakaasa na siasaraŋ te ma moowɔ ŋmarase ŋon tɔne kenaŋ. Na me ŋmarase ŋon tɔne kenaŋ bo na ɛ wose ka yeŋ ŋon ya, mɔna mɛɛ gyae nyi ɛ ka gyeŋ mena keŋ mɛɛ gyae ŋon nɛ nideli.

eng: But if someone has made anyone sad, that person hasnt hurt me but all of you to some degree .
local: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ be yɛɛ nombiikum te ɔ be yeli nombia be takaa ŋon nɛ, na maŋ maageŋ te ɔ yɛɛ ke tia ya, mɔna gbɛɛ daale man na ɛ popou yaa ɔ yɛɛ ke tia. Kei te yɛɛ nyi mɛɛ wɔŋee nombia ŋan man bo ya.

eng: The punishment handed out by the majority is enough for this person.
local: Mena keŋ ɛ man balaŋ burum be wɔŋ ɔ deŋele nɛ, keŋ kaageŋ tekaboe.

eng: This is why you should try your best to forgive and to comfort this person now instead, so that this person isnt overwhelmed by too much sorrow.
local: Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi yaa mo ɔ nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ e na ɛ kekolosi do e, na mena ya na gyae ke yela waa mutii yeŋ.

eng: So I encourage you to show your love for this person.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ waase ŋon nyiaa, ɛ lese ɛ kegyae keŋ wola e nideli.

eng: This is another reason why I wrote you. I wanted to test you and see if you are obedient in everything.
local: Kpene keŋ dɔɔ te mɔɔ ŋmarase ŋon tɔne keŋ yɛna nyiaa, mɛɛ gyae maa kɛɛ nyi ɛ ka seŋ keŋkeŋ kekɛɛsekɛɛ man, te ɛ ne nyii me dei debaŋ kamasɛ?.

eng: If you forgive anyone for anything, I do too. And whatever Ive forgiven , I did it for you in the presence of Christ.
local: Nyi akpaa ɛ mo ŋolo nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ e na, maŋ mɔ maa mo kyɛɛ e. Mɔna nyi akpaa nombii daale doo keŋ kaboena nyi maa mo kyɛɛ e koraŋ gba na, lee ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ te maa mo kyɛɛ e lee Kristo yele man.

eng: This is so that we wont be taken advantage of by Satan, because we are well aware of his schemes.
local: Kei gyae ke yela na ɔbɔnsam be nyiŋ tale di de dɔɔ ya. Nawolo nyi da gyeŋ wa adaose ŋan pou.

eng: When I came to Troas to preach Christs gospel, the Lord gave me an opportunity to preach.
local: Debaŋ keŋ mɔɔ gyu ka gyoo Torase donɔɔ man me kaa kolosi de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan nɛ, me naawɔ nyi de Gbeŋgyoo ta toro gbɛɛ fa maŋ nyi maa kolosi ɔ nombia ŋan botɔɔ.

eng: But I was worried because I couldnt find my brother Titus there. So I said good-bye to them and went on to Macedonia.
local: Mɔna me wɔe te fɛɛ maŋ ya, nawolo nyi ma te naa me dɔɔ Tituse botɔɔ ya. Mena dɔɔ me kalaa Torasetena baŋ te me laŋa Masedonia tɛɛle man.

eng: But thank God, who is always leading us around through Christ as if we were in a parade. He releases the fragrance of the knowledge of him everywhere through us.
local: Mɔna mɛɛ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ nawolo nyi Kristo te di ɔ kɔlala pou dɔɔ. Mena mɔ te lee Kristo dɔɔ, daa mɔ ne di de kɔlala baŋ dɔɔ debaŋ kamasɛ. Kristo te tɛɛ de dɔɔ yela ɔ nombia ŋan man kagyeŋ te yaasee gyu yenaŋ kamasɛ ŋgba kolo ofoŋte nɛ.

eng: We smell like the aroma of Christs offering to God, both to those who are being saved and to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ siaman na, daa Kristotena yɛɛ ŋgba kolo ofoŋte nɛ balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ne lɛɛ ba nyee na balaŋ baŋ baa yeŋ leki nɛ siaman.

eng: We smell like a contagious dead person to those who are dying, but we smell like the fountain of life to those who are being saved. Who is qualified for this kind of ministry?.
local: Balaŋ baŋ baa yeŋ leki nɛ gyaŋ na, ofoŋ kei yɛɛ ŋgba kolo wɔŋte keŋ gyae kako wɔ nɛ. Mɔna balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ne lɛɛ ba nyee nɛ dɔɔ, ofoŋ kei yɛɛ ŋgba kpene keŋ ne fa wɔ nyeedoŋ nɛ. Te amɔte kaboela na Kristo tom kei keyɛɛ?.

eng: We arent like so many people who hustle the word of God to make a profit. We are speaking through Christ in the presence of God, as those who are sincere and as those who are sent from God.
local: Daa be doo ŋgba balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ mo Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan di kebu nɛ ya. Mɔna daa dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ siaman na, de yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ ba lese te ɔ be kpila daa, te dɛɛ tɛɛ Kristo dɔɔ mo de konɔɔ pou yako ɔ nombia ŋan anokoare man nɛ.

eng: Are we starting to commend ourselves again? We dont need letters of introduction to you or from you like other people, do we?.
local: Nombia da ayakoŋ kɛŋa nɛ, nawolo nyi da bese yaa dɛɛ kɛlɛɛ da wose baa yaa? Yaa kaboena nyi dɛɛ tela tɔne keŋ gyae ka lese daa wola nɛ kɔna ŋon? Yaa dɛɛ lɛɛ tɔne keŋ gyae ka lese daa wola nɛ lee ɛ gyaŋ, ŋgba mena keŋ baale ne yɛɛ nɛ?.

eng: You are our letter, written on our hearts, known and read by everyone.
local: Tekaboena nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ mena ya, nawolo nyi ɛmɛɛ gbagba yɛna tɔne keŋ na lese de dinɔɔ wola, keŋ Kristo be ŋmarase do de konɔɔse man nɛ. Mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ yɛna tɔne keŋ walaŋ kamasɛ waa kala na waa naa de dinɔɔ.

eng: You show that you are Christs letter, delivered by us. You werent written with ink but with the Spirit of the living God. You werent written on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ na wola la nyi, ɛmɛɛ yɛna Kristo tɔne keŋ be tɛɛ de dɔɔ kɔŋ nɛ. Kei te yɛɛ tɔne keŋ bɔɔ mo nyiŋmaa yakaa ŋmarase ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ feliŋ te ba moowɔ ŋmarase ke. Ka te yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ gyaŋgbatena ne ŋmarase gyakaa boya paŋsapaŋsa dɔɔ nɛ ya, mɔna be ŋmarase ŋa bo do deniwalaŋ konɔɔ man.

eng: This is the confidence that we have through Christ in the presence of God.
local: Dɛɛ yako mena nombia kɛŋa pou nawolo nyi, dɛɛ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di lee Kristo yele man.

eng: It isnt that we ourselves are qualified to claim that anything came from us. No, our qualification is from God.
local: Daa gbagba de doŋ man na, nafɔ da tekaboena tom kei keyɛɛ ya. Nawolo nyi daa gbagba bɛɛ tale yɛɛ kolo de baam dɔɔ ya. De doŋ pou lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

eng: He has qualified us as ministers of a new covenant, not based on what is written but on the Spirit, because what is written kills, but the Spirit gives life.
local: Ŋon faa na daa doŋ te de kaa bese Wurubuarɛ nɔɔkebakesee wɔle keŋ somra. Nɔɔkebakesee wɔle kei doŋ ta lee mmaraase ŋan bɔɔ ŋmarase see nɛ man ya, mɔna doŋ kenaŋ lee Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon gyaŋ. Nawolo nyi mmaraase ŋan bɔɔ ŋmarase see nɛ ne kɔna yeŋ. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon dɔɔ, ŋon ne fa nyeedoŋ bo.

eng: The ministry that brought death was carved in letters on stone tablets. It came with such glory that the Israelites couldnt look for long at Moses face because his face was shining with glory, even though it was a fading glory.
local: Mosesi mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ, be ŋmarase ŋa bo gyakaa boya patalapatala dɔɔ. Te debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo mmaraase ŋan fa ŋon Mosesi nɛ, ŋe kɔŋae Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri man te ke yela Mosesi siagbɛɛ ŋalakeewɔ keŋ Iseraetena te tale kɛɛ ɔ siagbɛɛ ya. Mɔna mena keŋalakee kenaŋ dɔɔ, na ke nɔɔ na yem. Mena dɔɔ akpaa nyi Mosesi mmaraase ŋan na mo yeŋ kɔŋ nɛ, kɔŋae na Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ na.

eng: Wont the ministry of the Spirit be much more glorious?.
local: kenaŋ na Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ɔ ne fa nyeedoŋ nɛ, wui gyoorobiiri be gyae ka kela kei yee?.

eng: If the ministry that brought condemnation has glory, how much more glorious is the ministry that brings righteousness?.
local: Nyi akpaa ɔsom keŋ ne bu balaŋ fɔɔ nɛ dana gyoorobiiri mena na, kenaŋ na ɔ som keŋ ne yele balaŋ gbɛɛneŋ na tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ, wui gyoorobiiri be gyae ka kela kenaŋ yee?.

eng: In fact, what was glorious isnt glorious now, because of the glory that is brighter.
local: Gyoorobiiri keŋ ke kyaa debaŋ kei nɛ dɔɔ, te yeli gyoorobiiri keŋ nafɔ ke kyaa gyaŋgba keŋ nɛ te ŋaalaa.

eng: If the glory that fades away was glorious, how much more glorious is the one that lasts!.
local: Akpaa nyi keŋ ka te kyaa kekpaa ya nɛ kɔŋawɔ na gyoorobiiri na, kenaŋ na keŋ gyae ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ wui gyoorobiiri gyae ka bo kela ke.

eng: So, since we have such a hope, we act with great confidence.
local: Keŋ de dana mena loo kegyakaa kei dɔɔ, te dɛɛ tale gyɔ de konɔɔ kolosi.

eng: We arent like Moses, who used to put a veil over his face so that the Israelites couldnt watch the end of what was fading away.
local: Daa be doo ŋgba Mosesi ŋon ɔ ba mo kolo wu ɔ siagbɛɛ, na Iseraetena be nyiŋ naa ɔ siagbɛɛ debaŋ keŋ keŋalakee keŋ nɔɔ na yem nɛ ya.

eng: But their minds were closed. Right up to the present day the same veil remains when the old covenant is read. The veil is not removed because it is taken away by Christ.
local: Ke yɛɛ anokoare ke nyi, na ba agyueŋse ta toro ta na baa nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan man ya. Te kaa lii na gyɛŋ kei nɛ, akpaa bɛɛ kala Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan doo nɔɔkebakesee bene keŋ man nɛ, bɔɔ ne nyii ŋe man ya. Na yɛɛ yaa ŋgba kolo sɛɛ wuɛ ba agyueŋse dɔɔ nɛ. Nawolo nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo kelɛɛdi kaageŋ ne gyae la ke tale gyuusu walaŋ agyueŋ, na ɔ kala Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan na, waa tale nyii ŋe man.

eng: Even today, whenever Moses is read, a veil lies over their hearts.
local: Kaa lii na gyɛŋ kei koraŋ nɛ, akpaa Iseraetena de kala Mosesi mmaraase ŋan na, bɔɔ ne nyii ŋe man ya. Yɛɛ yaa ŋgba kolo sɛɛ wuɛ ba agyueŋse dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: But whenever someone turns back to the Lord, the veil is removed.
local: Mɔna akpaa nyi ŋolo da mo ɔ wose fa de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo na, waa gyuusu kpene keŋ wuɛ walaŋ ŋonaŋ agyueŋ dɔɔ nɛ, na waa nyii ɔ nombia ŋan man.

eng: The Lord is the Spirit, and where the Lords Spirit is, there is freedom.
local: Nawolo nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo na Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon yɛɛ dokoloŋ. Te botɔɔ keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo feliŋ ŋon kyaa na, walaŋ ne nyiŋ ɔ wose lee mmaraase ŋan tɛɛ.

eng: All of us are looking with unveiled faces at the glory of the Lord as if we were looking in a mirror. We are being transformed into that same image from one degree of glory to the next degree of glory. This comes from the Lord, who is the Spirit.
local: Te daa baŋ kolo be wuɛ de siagbɛɛ ya nɛ, dɛɛ lese de Gbeŋgyoo gyoorobiiri keŋ wola. Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne kyɛɛkee de dinɔɔ na ke yɛɛ ŋgba Kristo dinɔɔ nɛ, te debaŋ kamasɛ mɔ na dee nyiŋ ɔ gyoorobiiri keŋ kpu. Mena nombia kɛŋa lee de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo gyaŋ. Ŋon mɔ yɛna Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon.

eng: This is why we dont get discouraged, given that we received this ministry in the same way that we received Gods mercy.
local: Lee mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ be wii de waraŋase, te ɔ ba mo tom kei do daa de nyiŋmaa man dɔɔ nɛ, dɔɔ ne yee ya.

eng: Instead, we reject secrecy and shameful actions. We dont use deception, and we dont tamper with Gods word. Instead, we commend ourselves to everyones conscience in the sight of God by the public announcement of the truth.
local: Mena dɔɔ te da lese da wose lee desɛŋ nombia man, te dɔɔ ne yɛɛ kolo mɔ weese ya. Dɔɔ na mo adao gbɛɛneŋ man mo kolo kyakale na Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ya. Mɔna dɛɛ lese Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ŋe yɛɛ anokoare nɛ man nideli, na walaŋ kamasɛ kagyeŋ nyi kpene keŋ dɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ, te yɛɛ dekara Wurubuarɛ siaman ya.

eng: And even if our gospel is veiled, it is veiled to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: Te akpaa Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan yɛɛ nombia aweeseŋ ŋa na, kenaŋ na ŋe yɛɛ nombia aweeseŋ ŋa bo fa balaŋ baŋ baa yo nɛ baageŋ.

eng: The god of this age has blinded the minds of those who dont have faith so they couldnt see the light of the gospel that reveals Christs glory. Christ is the image of God.
local: Mena balaŋ kɛwɔ bɛɛ lɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan di ya, nawolo nyi ɔbɔnsam ŋon ɔ yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyoo e nɛ, ta mo wɔ do ditiŋtɛɛ man. Mena dɔɔ bɔɔ ne naa keŋmaŋee keŋ doo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan na lese ɔ gyoorobiiri wola nɛ ya. Te bɔɔ ne naa, na baa gyeŋ nyi Kristo yɛna Wurubuarɛ dinɔɔ gbagba ŋon ya.

eng: We dont preach about ourselves. Instead, we preach about Jesus Christ as Lord, and we describe ourselves as your slaves for Jesus sake.
local: Nombia ŋan dee kolosi fa ŋon nɛ, te yɛɛ daa gbagba da wose man nombia yaa dee kolosi fa ŋon ya. Mɔna Yesu Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan yaa dee kolosi fa ŋon, nyi ŋon yɛna de Gbeŋgyoo te ŋon mɔ dɔɔ te dɛɛ som ŋon.

eng: God said that light should shine out of the darkness. He is the same one who shone in our hearts to give us the light of the knowledge of Gods glory in the face of Jesus Christ.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be yako nyi, Keŋmaŋee dɛɛ lee ditiŋtɛɛ man ŋmaŋee nɛ, ŋon ke yela la te keŋmaŋee ŋmaŋeewɔ de konɔɔse man, na ke yela dɛɛ gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ doo Kristo man nɛ.

eng: But we have this treasure in clay pots so that the awesome power belongs to God and doesnt come from us.
local: Mɔna daa baŋ Wurubuarɛ gyooro abɔɔ kɛŋa doo de man nɛ, de yɛɛ ŋgba kpene keŋ bɔɔ mo tɛɛle ma ke nɛ. Na ka wola nyi doŋ dinaa keŋ doo de man nɛ, lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ. Ke ta lee daa gbagba gyaŋ ya.

eng: We are experiencing all kinds of trouble, but we arent crushed. We are confused, but we arent depressed.
local: Dɛɛ naa diyem lee gbɛɛ kamasɛ man, mɔna da seŋɛɛ keŋkeŋ. Debaŋ ŋaale man na da agyueŋ bee doo debodokoloŋ ya, mɔna da wose bee gbirisi daa ya.

eng: We are harassed, but we arent abandoned. We are knocked down, but we arent knocked out.
local: Bɛɛ naase daa diyem, mɔna Wurubuarɛ bɛɛ lese ɔ sia yela daa ya. Bɛɛ gyɔ daa lɔ yeŋ yeŋ, mɔna dɔɔ na yem ya.

eng: We always carry Jesus death around in our bodies so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies.
local: Wee na wee na dɛɛ naa diyem yeŋ yeŋ da wose man, ŋgba mena keŋ Yesu mɔ be naa nɛ. Na kawola nyi nyeedoŋ keŋ Yesu dana nɛ, keŋ ke te daa mɔ dana.

eng: We who are alive are always being handed over to death for Jesus sake so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies that are dying.
local: Daa baŋ de kyaa na de sia nɛ, wee na wee na lee Yesu dɔɔ bɛɛ lese daa fa yeŋ. Na ke yela walaŋ kamasɛ kena nyi, Yesu nyeedoŋ keŋ doo daa baŋ da wosenaane kei gyae ka yeŋ nɛ mɔ man.

eng: So death is at work in us, but life is at work in you.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ daa ne gyaŋee na yeŋ nombia wee kamasɛ, na ɛmɛɛ kenyiŋ nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa.

eng: We have the same faithful spirit as what is written in scripture: I had faith, and so I spoke. We also have faith, and so we also speak.
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see yenaŋ daale ne yako nyiaa, Me lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia di dɔɔ, te me kolosi ɔ nombia. Kelɛɛdi dokoloŋ kenaŋ te daa mɔ lɛɛwɔ di, mena dɔɔ te dee kolosi ɔ nombia nɛ.

eng: We do this because we know that the one who raised the Lord Jesus will also raise us with Jesus, and he will bring us into his presence along with you.
local: Da gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be gyuusu de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu lee yeŋ man nɛ, waa ta Yesu dɔɔ gyuusu daa na waa mo ŋon kpu na daa kaa seŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ siaman.

eng: All these things are for your benefit. As grace increases to benefit more and more people, it will cause gratitude to increase, which results in Gods glory.
local: Ɛ deeli dɔɔ te nombia kɛŋa pou kɔŋawɔ mena, na ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele balaŋ burum te ɔ ne lɛɛ ba nyee nɛ, ke yela baa fane fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ nideli na baa lese ɔ yele.

eng: So we arent depressed. But even if our bodies are breaking down on the outside, the person that we are on the inside is being renewed every day.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ da wose bɛɛ yɔkɔse daa ya. Nawolo nyi keŋ na da wosenaane kɛɛ yɔkɔse kei koraŋ nɛ, de dinɔɔ wɔle keŋ dɔɔ kee nyiŋ doŋ wee kamasɛ.

eng: Our temporary minor problems are producing an eternal stockpile of glory for us that is beyond all comparison.
local: Diyem keŋ dɛɛ naa debaŋ kei nɛ, ka be gyae ke kyare ya na ka te lam. Ke gyae ke yela dee nyiŋ gyoorobiiri keŋ gyae ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa, keŋ ka dokoso be kyaa ya te ka kela diyem keŋ dɛɛ naa nɛ.

eng: We dont focus on the things that can be seen but on the things that cant be seen. The things that can be seen dont last, but the things that cant be seen are eternal.
local: Mena dɔɔ, dɔɔ na mo de loo gyakaa abɔɔ ŋan dɛɛ naa na de sia fonɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ ya, mɔna dɛɛ mo de loo bo gyakaa abɔɔ ŋan dɔɔ ne naa na de sia ya nɛ dɔɔ. Nawolo nyi abɔɔ ŋan dɛɛ mo de sia naa ŋa nɛ, ŋe bɛɛ kyare ya na ŋa te lam. Mɔna abɔɔ ŋan dɔɔ na mo de sia naa ŋa ya nɛ, ŋe gyae ke doo kekpaakekpaa.

eng: We know that if the tent that we live in on earth is torn down, we have a building from God. Its a house that isnt handmade, which is eternal and located in heaven.
local: Da gyeŋ nyi tɛɛle kei dɔɔ de kyaa nɛ, dɔɔ gyae dɛɛ kyare ya na de ta yem. Mɔna akpaa da yem na, Wurubuarɛ waa fa daa wosenaane wɔle adido, keŋ ka te yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ wosenaane ke ya. Wurubuarɛ gbagba yɛɛ na ke, te ke gyae ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa.

eng: We groan while we live in this residence. We really want to dress ourselves with our building from heaven.
local: Debaŋ kei na dɛɛ keketee da wose man, nawolo nyi dɛɛ gyae nideli nyi Wurubuarɛ waa faa daa mena wosenaane wɔle kei.

eng: since we assume that when we take off this tent, we wont find out that we are naked.
local: Nyi akpaa Wurubuarɛ da mo ke do daa na, dɔɔ gyae dɛɛ ta diteŋteli ya.

eng: Yes, while we are in this tent we groan, because we are weighed down. We want to be dressed not undressed, so that what is dying can be swallowed up by life.
local: Debaŋ keŋ de sɛɛ de doo wosenaane kei man nɛ, dɛɛ yeyɛɛ ŋgba kasolɔ daale na ŋere na daa nɛ. Na dɛɛ yako baa nyi yeŋ yaa dɛɛ gyae debokeiman ya. Mɔna nafɔ da kegyaebii yɛna nyi Wurubuarɛ waa mo wosenaane wɔle keŋ kyaa adido nɛ do daa. Na wosenaane wɔle keŋ kaa kyaa wosenaane bene keŋ na yem nɛ nawɔɔ man.

eng: Now the one who prepared us for this very thing is God, and God gave us the Spirit as a down payment for our home.
local: Wurubuarɛ gbagba desina na daa fa mena kekyɛɛkee kenaŋ, te ɔ moo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do de man. Ŋon te ɔ moowɔ see ateta, mo kyaŋ abɔɔ ŋan pou waa kaa yɛɛ fa daa nɛ nae.

eng: So we are always confident, because we know that while we are living in the body, we are away from our home with the Lord.
local: Mena dɔɔ debaŋ kamasɛ na da wose bɛɛ yɔkɔse daa ya. Da gyeŋ nyi debaŋ keŋ de sɛɛ de kyaa wosenaane kei man kɛbo nɛ, de ta gyoo ta botɔɔ keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu kyaa nɛ ya.

eng: We live by faith and not by sight.
local: Da ke kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, nombia ŋan dɔɔ lɛɛ di nɛ ŋan te de silana. Na abɔɔ ŋan dɛɛ naa na de sia nɛ, te de silana ya.

eng: We are confident, and we would prefer to leave the body and to be at home with the Lord.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ de dana lookegyakaa nideli. Nafɔ da kegyaebii yɛna nyi, dɛɛ lee wosenaane kei man na de kaa kyaa de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu gyaŋ.

eng: So our goal is to be acceptable to him, whether we are at home or away from home.
local: Mena dɔɔ nyi de kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ bo o, yaa da yeŋae o, kpene keŋ dɔɔ dɛɛ yase de sia yɛna nyi, dɛɛ yɛɛ kpene keŋ gyae ka gyoo de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu sia.

eng: We all must appear before Christ in court so that each person can be paid back for the things that were done while in the body, whether they were good or bad.
local: Nawolo nyi kaboena nyi walaŋ kamasɛ waa kɔŋ kaa seŋ Kristo siaman na waa di ɔ nombia, na waa tɔ walaŋ kamasɛ kom ŋgba mena keŋ ɔtenate be yɛɛ ɔ tom tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ. Dukum dana ka kom ketɔ, te debam mɔ dana ka kom ketɔ.

eng: So we try to persuade people, since we know what it means to fear the Lord. We are well known by God, and I hope that in your heart we are well known by you as well.
local: Da gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ keyee kedoobii man dɔɔ, te dɛɛ do balaŋ kakyeŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom man. Wurubuarɛ gyeŋ de dinɔɔ, te dɛɛ lɛɛdi nyi ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ gyeŋ de dinɔɔ lee ɛ konɔɔse man.

eng: We arent trying to commend ourselves to you again. Instead, we are giving you an opportunity to be proud of us so that you could answer those who take pride in superficial appearance, and not in what is in the heart.
local: Te yɛɛ nyi da bese yaa dɛɛ kɛlɛɛ da wose bo fa ŋon ya. Mɔna dɛɛ tɔɔse ŋon nombia ŋan gyae ke yela ɛ ke tale gyina ɛ wose adido lee daa dɔɔ nɛ bo. Dekɔŋ mena na, ɛ ke tale nyiŋ nombii yako balaŋ baŋ bee gyina ba wose adido lee abɔɔ ŋan bɛɛ mo be sia naa ŋa nɛ dɔɔ, kela ŋan ŋe doo deniwalaŋ konɔɔ man nɛ.

eng: If we are crazy, its for Gods sake. If we are rational, its for your sake.
local: Akpaa nombia kɛŋa dɔɔ te ɛ yako nyi da nyeeya ne wɔlɛɛ na, ŋe wɔlɛe fa Wurubuarɛ. Te akpaa da nyeeya doo mɔ na, kenaŋ na ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ te da nyeeya doo.

eng: The love of Christ controls us, because we have concluded this: one died for the sake of all; therefore, all died.
local: Kristo kegyae keŋ te di da nyee, nawolo nyi da gyeŋ nyi ŋon lɛɛ na balaŋ pou yeŋ yeŋ. Mena dɔɔ yɛɛ yaa ŋgba balaŋ pou yeŋa la nɛ.

eng: He died for the sake of all so that those who are alive should live not for themselves but for the one who died for them and was raised.
local: Kristo yeŋa la fa balaŋ pou, na ke yela baŋ be kyaa na be sia nɛ be nyiŋ kyaa fa ba wose ya. Mɔna baa kyaa fa Yesu Kristo ŋon ɔ ba yem fa wɔ, te Wurubuarɛ be gyuusu e nɛ.

eng: So then, from this point on we wont recognize people by human standards. Even though we used to know Christ by human standards, that isnt how we know him now.
local: Mena dɔɔ lee nɛnɛɛ ne gyu na, dɔɔ ne kɛɛ balaŋ ŋgba mena keŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ ne kɛɛ wɔ nɛ ya. Gyaŋgba keŋ na, nafɔ de kɛɛ Kristo mɔ mena. Mɔna nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ, dɔɔ na tekaa kɛɛ e mena bela ya.

eng: So then, if anyone is in Christ, that person is part of the new creation. The old things have gone away, and look, new things have arrived!.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ akpaa nyi ŋolo de lɛɛ Kristo di na, ɔ ta bese walaŋ wɔle. Ɔ dinɔɔ bene keŋ te lam, te nɛnɛɛ ɔ te nyiŋ dinɔɔ wɔle.

eng: All of these new things are from God, who reconciled us to himself through Christ and who gave us the ministry of reconciliation.
local: Nombia kɛŋa pou lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ. Ŋon tɛɛ la Yesu Kristo dɔɔ desina daa na ŋon Wurubuarɛ nsana, te ɔ faa daa tom nyi dɛɛ yako ɔ nombia kɛŋa, na dɛɛ mo desina ŋon na balaŋ nsana.

eng: In other words, God was reconciling the world to himself through Christ, by not counting peoples sins against them. He has trusted us with this message of reconciliation.
local: Nombii keŋ dɛɛ yako ŋon yɛna nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ tɛɛ la Kristo dɔɔ desina ŋon na balaŋ pou nsana, te ɔ moo be nombiakumɛɛ pou kyɛɛ wɔ. Te ɔ moo ŋon na balaŋ nsana kedesina tom kei do daa de nyiŋmaa man.

eng: So we are ambassadors who represent Christ. God is negotiating with you through us. We beg you as Christs representatives, Be reconciled to God.
local: Mena dɔɔ daa yɛna Kristo kpilala baŋ da seŋɛɛ ɔ nawɔɔ man, keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne tɛɛ de dɔɔ kolosi sola ŋon. Da seŋɛɛ Kristo nawɔɔ man dɛɛ sola ŋon nyi, ɛ yela ɛmɛɛ na Wurubuarɛ nsana keyɛɛ gyoŋ.

eng: God caused the one who didnt know sin to be sin for our sake so that through him we could become the righteousness of God.
local: Kristo te yɛɛ nombiikum kamasɛ ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ yela ɔ kaa moo de nombiakumɛɛ pou seele. Te ɔ yeŋawɔ ŋgba nombiakumɛɛyɛɛre nɛ, na ke yela de nombia ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman lee ŋon Kristo dɔɔ.

eng: Since we work together with him, we are also begging you not to receive the grace of God in vain.
local: De yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ daa na Wurubuarɛ ne yɛɛ na tom. Lee mena dɔɔ dɛɛ waase ŋon bo nyiaa, ɛ na yeli Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba mo boele ŋon nɛ ke yɛɛ yakaa ya.

eng: He says, I listened to you at the right time, and I helped you on the day of salvation. Look, now is the right time! Look, now is the day of salvation!.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see yakowɔ yenaŋ daale nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa debaŋ keŋ kaboena nyi maa wii ŋon nɛ, me nyii ɛ ke fane. Debaŋ keŋ mɔ kaboena nyi maa lɛɛ ɛ nyee nɛ, me lɛɛ ŋon. Te maŋ Pɔɔl ne yako ŋon baa ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, nɛnɛɛ yɛna debaŋ keŋ kaboena nyi Wurubuarɛ waa wii ŋon. Gyɛŋ wee kei yɛna nyeekelɛɛwee keŋ.

eng: We dont give anyone any reason to be offended about anything so that our ministry wont be criticized.
local: De bɛɛ gyae nyi ŋolo ke wɔlɛɛ da yele lee tom keŋ dɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ man ya. Mena dɔɔ dɛɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ nyi dɔɔ yɛɛ kolo, na ŋolo kenyiŋ gbɛɛ mo ayimɛɛ wuu daa ya.

eng: Instead, we commend ourselves as ministers of God in every way. We did this with our great endurance through problems, disasters, and stressful situations.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ de nombia ayɛɛsa pou man na, dɛɛ lese wola ŋon nyi de yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ tewulɛɛwɔ. Nawolo nyi de nyiŋa konɔɔ seŋ keŋkeŋ de kayeyɛɛ, na debaŋ keŋ da wose be kyaŋ daa nɛ, na de kaale burum man.

eng: We went through beatings, imprisonments, and riots. We experienced hard work, sleepless nights, and hunger.
local: Be tuku daa nideli wɔŋee daa balaŋ man, te be kyaŋa daa tɔ deni. De kyaŋa da wose yɛɛ tom nideli keŋ nyi dɔɔ ne nyiŋ dosoroŋ gba dee doo ya, te de tɛɛwɔ mɔ na tanam.

eng: We displayed purity, knowledge, patience, and generosity. We served with the Holy Spirit, genuine love.
local: Dɛɛ lese wola nyi de yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ tewulɛɛwɔ, mo lee da kekyaabii keŋ ayimɛɛ be doo ke man ya nɛ man. Da gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan, te dee nyiŋ konɔɔ te dɛɛ yɛɛ deeli. Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon doo de man, te dɛɛ gyae balaŋ anokoare man.

eng: telling the truth, and Gods power. We carried the weapons of righteousness in our right hand and our left hand.
local: Nombii na anokoare te dee kolosi, te dɛɛ yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ mɔ bo lee Wurubuarɛ doŋ man. De nombia ŋan tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ, te de dana demena na dunoluŋ ŋgba yoo abɔɔ nɛ. Ŋan te dɛɛ mo yoo lɛɛ da wose.

eng: We were treated with honor and dishonor and with verbal abuse and good evaluation. We were seen as both fake and real.
local: Baale na lese da yele, te baale mɔ na do daa desɛŋ. Baale na tee daa, mɔna baale mɔ ne kɛlɛɛ daa. Baale ne kɛɛ daa nyi de yɛɛ akɛrɛɛtenawɔ, mɔna de nombia doo na ke gbɛɛ.

eng: as unknown and well known, as dying—and look, we are alive! We were seen as punished but not killed.
local: Baale gyaŋ na, da te yɛɛ balaŋwɔ ya, mɔna da yele ta lee nideli. Kayeyɛɛ dɔɔ akpaa n naa daa na n kpa dɛɛ yem baa, mɔna de dana nyeedoŋ. Bɛɛ naase daa diyem nideli, mɔna de bɛɛ yem ya.

eng: as going through pain but always happy, as poor but making many rich, and as having nothing but owning everything.
local: De dɔŋ waraŋase man, mɔna Kristo dɔɔ dee di gyoŋ debaŋ kamasɛ. De dɔŋ na diyem, mɔna dee yeli balaŋ burum ne nyiŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ. De dɔŋ ŋgba balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ dana kolo ya nɛ, mɔna Kristo dɔɔ kolo na kolo bee hia daa ya.

eng: Corinthians, we have spoken openly to you, and our hearts are wide open.
local: Me tebia Korintotena, da te kolosi nombii na anokoare keŋ fa ŋon, keŋ de ta mo kolo weese ŋon ya. Te dɛɛ gyae ŋon nideli lee de konɔɔ man.

eng: There are no limits to the affection that we feel for you. You are the ones who placed boundaries on your affection for us.
local: De bɛɛ wɔŋ da wose lee ɛ man ya, mɔna ɛmɛɛ ne wɔŋ ɛ wose lee de man.

eng: But as a fair trade—Im talking to you like you are children—open your hearts wide too.
