eng: You know that I first preached the gospel to you because of an illness.
local: Ɛ tɔɔse nyi ma kekɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ gyaŋgbate keŋ nɛ, kawee yele la te me nyiŋa gbɛɛ kolosi Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa ŋon.

eng: Though my poor health burdened you, you didnt look down on me or reject me, but you welcomed me as if I were an angel from God, or as if I were Christ Jesus!.
local: Doo nyi na me kawee kenaŋ yɛɛ kateesa ke fa ŋon, mɔna ɛ te ŋmanyaŋ maŋ ya te ɛ te bɛɛ maŋ mɔ ya. Ɛ lɛɛ maŋ ŋgba Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo lee la adido kɔŋ nɛ, yaa ŋgba maŋ yɛna Yesu Kristo ŋon gbagba nɛ.

eng: Where then is the great attitude that you had? I swear that, if possible, you would have dug out your eyes and given them to me.
local: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ na me naawɔ nyi gyoŋ daale doo ɛ man keŋ nyi nafɔ yeŋ nyiŋa gbɛɛ koraŋ na, yeŋ kulii ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ siabia do maŋ. Nɛnɛɛ mena gyoŋ kenaŋ laŋa aleŋ?.

eng: So then, have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?.
local: Keŋ mɔɔ yako ŋon anokoare keŋ dɔɔ na, me ta bese ɛ kɔlare?.

eng: They are so concerned about you, though not with good intentions. Rather, they want to shut you out so that you would run after them.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ beo yɛɛ ba wose nyi be yɛɛ Kristotenawɔ nɛ, bɛɛ gyae bo nyi baa beo ŋon na ɛ kaa di Gyudatena mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ, mɔna ŋa be gyae ke yɛɛ tɔnɔɔ kamasɛ fa ŋon ya. Bɛɛ gyae bo nyi baa tira daa na ŋon nsana, na ɛ ke nyiŋ mo ɛ loo pou gyakaa be dɔɔ.

eng: However, its always good to have people concerned about you with good intentions, and not just when Im there with you.
local: Akpaa ɛ yase ɛ sia te ɛ ne yɛɛ kolo kpaakpaate na ke dei. Mɔna kaboena nyi yaa yɛɛ ke debaŋ kamasɛ. Te yɛɛ nyi me kyaa ɛ gyaŋ kaageŋ te ɛ ke yɛɛ ke ya.

eng: My little children, Im going through labor pains again until Christ is formed in you.
local: Me bia kpaakpaa, mena keŋ alo na doŋee te ɔ ne naa diyem nɛ, mena yaa mɛɛ naa diyem lee ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ. Te maa naa mena diyem kei kelii debaŋ keŋ ɛ ke nyiŋ Kristo dinɔɔ keŋ.

eng: But I wish I could be with you now and change how I sound, because Im at a loss about you.
local: Ma kegyaebii yɛna nyi nafɔ na me kyaa ɛ gyaŋ, na maa kyɛɛkee ma kekolosi na ke kyaa na ɛ kegyaebii. Nawolo nyi ɛ nombia ayɛɛsa dana maŋ gyakoloŋ.

eng: Tell me—those of you who want to be under the Law—dont you listen to the Law?.
local: Ɛ yela maa bɔɔse ŋon nombii kei. Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ne gyae nyi ɛ ke doo Mosesi mmaraase ŋan tɛɛ nɛ, gbagbaagba na ɛ gyeŋ kpene keŋ mmaraase ŋan ne yako?.

eng: Its written that Abraham had two sons, one by the slave woman and one by the free woman.
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nyiaa, Aberaham lola bia baala bala. Be man ŋolo ɔ naa yɛɛ gbeŋi e, te ŋolo mɔ ɔ naa yɛɛ gyoorobu e.

eng: The son by the slave woman was conceived the normal way, but the son by the free woman was conceived through a promise.
local: Aberaham bu ŋon gbeŋi ŋon ba lolo fa e nɛ, ba lola e bo ŋgba mena keŋ alo kamasɛ ne taŋ lolo nɛ. Mɔna ŋon gyooro bu ŋon ba lolo fa e nɛ dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ wulaa yakowɔ see nyi baa lola e pɛte be kaa lola e.

eng: These things are an allegory: the women are two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai, which gives birth to slave children; this is Hagar.
local: Mena nombia kɛŋa yɛɛ dudu ke keŋ na lese nombia ŋaale wola. Ala bala kɛwɔ seŋɛɛ fa nɔɔkebakesee ala ŋan Wurubuarɛ be bake see fa ɔ balaŋ nɛ. Nɔɔkebakesee keŋ ba lee Sinai bula dɔɔ nɛ, keŋ seŋɛɛ la fa alo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Haga nɛ. Ɔ bia yɛna balaŋ baŋ Mosesi mmaraase ŋan ba besena wɔ yɛɛ gbeŋa nɛ.

eng: Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia, and she corresponds to the present-day Jerusalem, because the city is in slavery with her children.
local: Haga seŋɛɛ fa Sinai bula keŋ ke kyaa Arebia tɛɛle man nɛ. Ɔ yɛɛ ŋgba gyɛŋ wee kei Gyerusalɛm nɛ. Mosesi mmaraase ŋan ta besena ke man balaŋ baŋ pou yɛɛ gbeŋa.

eng: But the Jerusalem that is above is free, and she is our mother.
local: Mɔna Gyerusalɛm keŋ ke kyaa adido nɛ, keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba gyooro bu ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Saara nɛ. Ke man bia be doo mmaraase kamasɛ tɛɛ ŋgba gbeŋa nɛ ya. Saara ŋonaŋ yɛna daa baŋ dɔɔ doo Gyudatena mmaraase tɛɛ ya nɛ de naa.

eng: Its written: Rejoice, barren woman, you who have not given birth . Break out with a shout, you who have not suffered labor pains; because the woman who has been deserted will have many more children than the woman who has a husband .
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nyiaa, Nyaŋ ɔlamanbo ŋon n ta lola ta see ya nɛ, yela ke yɛɛ neŋ gyoŋ. Nyaŋ ŋon n ta doŋee ta see ya nɛ, yela ke yɛɛ neŋ gyoŋ na n ke lɔ akelelee. Doo nyi n baale ta lese ɔ sia lee n dɔɔ, mɔna maa yela waa bese kɔŋ n gyaŋ na n bia ke wuku kela ŋon ɔ kyaa yale tɛɛ nɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, you are children of the promise like Isaac.
local: Me tebia Kristotena, ɛ yɛɛ ŋgba Asiki nɛ. Nawolo nyi mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ be wulaa yako ɔ nombia see pɛte bɔɔ kaa lola e nɛ, mena yaa ɔ yakowɔ see te daa mɔ kaa bese ɔ bia nɛ.

eng: But just as it was then, so it is now also: the one who was conceived the normal way harassed the one who was conceived by the Spirit.
local: Debaŋ kenaŋ man na, bu ŋon bɔɔ lola e ŋgba mena keŋ bɛɛ lola walaŋ kamasɛ nɛ, naase bu ŋon bɔɔ tɛɛ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon doŋ man lola e nɛ diyem. Mena ke yaa sɛɛ doo kaa lii gyɛŋ wee kei nɛ.

eng: Christ has set us free for freedom. Therefore, stand firm and dont submit to the bondage of slavery again.
local: Kristo te lɛɛ da nyeeya yela de te nyiŋ da wose bese gyoorobia. Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi yaa seŋ ɛ kelɛɛdi keŋ man keŋkeŋ. Ɛ na fa ŋolo gbɛɛ na waa besena ŋon yɛɛ ɔ gbeŋa bela ya.

eng: But what does the scripture say? Throw out the slave woman and her son, because the slave womans son wont share the inheritance with the free womans son .
local: Mɔna sena te Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako? Nombia ŋan yakowɔ nyiaa, Gegi gbeŋi ŋon na ɔ bu, nawolo nyi tekaboena nyi gbeŋi bu ŋon waa di ɔ kya abɔɔ kpu na gyooro bu ŋon ya.

eng: Look, I, Paul, am telling you that if you have yourselves circumcised, having Christ wont help you.
local: Ɛ tei me nɔɔwoya nideli. Maŋ Pɔɔl ne yako na ŋon nombia kɛŋa nɛ nyiaa, akpaa ɛ fa gbɛɛ nyi baa kara ŋon ɛ baala wose ŋgba mena keŋ Mosesi mmaraase ŋan nawolo nɛ, na nawolo baa nyi kpene keŋ Kristo be yɛɛ nɛ be taŋ tɔnɔɔ fa ŋon korakora ya.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, we arent the slave womans children, but we are the free womans children.
local: Mena dɔɔ me tebia, da te yɛɛ gbeŋi ŋon biawɔ ya. De yɛɛ gyooro bu ŋon biawɔ.

eng: Again I swear to every man who has himself circumcised that he is required to do the whole Law.
local: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nideli bela nyiaa, akpaa ɛ man ŋolo de fa gbɛɛ nyi baa kara e ɔ baala wose nɛ, kenaŋ na kaboena nyi ɔtenate dee di Mosesi mmaraase ŋan pou dɔɔ.

eng: You people who are trying to be made righteous by the Law have been estranged from Christ. You have fallen away from grace!.
local: Akpaa ɛ man ŋolo de yɛɛ kakyeŋ nyi ɔ ne di Mosesi mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ na Wurubuarɛ ke nyiŋ lɛɛ e do nɛ, kenaŋ na nawolo baa nyi ɔtenate te tira ɔ wose lee Kristo man. Mena ke mɔ yaa ɔtenate te tira ɔ wose lee Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ na mo boele balaŋ nɛ man.

eng: We eagerly wait for the hope of righteousness through the Spirit by faith.
local: Daa dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon doŋ man dɔɔ de loo gyakaa nyi Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ daa do nyi de gbɛɛneŋ tenee ɔ siaman, nawolo nyi dɛɛ lɛɛ Kristo di.

eng: Being circumcised or not being circumcised doesnt matter in Christ Jesus, but faith working through love does matter.
local: Yesu Kristo kelɛɛdi be famenɛ nyi ŋolo kara ɔ baala wose yaa ɔ te kare ya. Mɔna kpene keŋ ne hia yɛna nyi dɛɛ lɛɛ Yesu Kristo di, na dɛɛ gyae Wurubuarɛ na de tebia.

eng: You were running well—who stopped you from obeying the truth?.
local: Kewalaŋ keŋ na, nafɔ ɛ ne yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa lee ɛ kelɛɛdi keŋ man. Nɛnɛɛ amɔte beo na ŋon te ɛ tinaa anokoare keŋ kesila yela?.

eng: This line of reasoning doesnt come from the one who calls you.
local: Ma gyeŋ nyi mena kabeo kei ta lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya. Nawolo nyi ŋon baake na ŋon nyi ɛ kaa bese ɔ bia.

eng: A little yeast works through the whole lump of dough.
local: Mena keŋ bɛɛ mo bodobodo tee kyomii do bodobodo biliŋ man te ne yeli kɛɛ koro nɛ, mena ke mɔ te nombiikum bii gyae ke tale beo balaŋ burum.

eng: Im convinced about you in the Lord that you wont think any other way. But the one who is confusing you will pay the penalty, whoever that may be.
local: Mɔna mɛɛ lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu di nyiaa, ɛ be gyae ɛ ka lee me wɔle ke lɛɛ nombii wɔle daale di bela ya. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa yela ɛ ka lee me wɔle ke lɛɛ nombii wɔle daale di na, ɔtenate yɛɛ woŋ e o, ɔ te yɛɛ woŋ e ya o nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa wɔŋ ɔ deŋele.

eng: Brothers and sisters, if Im still preaching circumcision, why am I still being harassed? In that case, the offense of the cross would be canceled.
local: Me tebia, nyi akpaa me gyakaa mee kolosi nyi yaa kara ɛ baala wose pɛte Wurubuarɛ ke lɛɛ ŋon do ŋgba mena keŋ Gyudatena mmaraase ŋan nawolo na, woŋ dɔɔ te bɛɛ naase maŋ diyem? Nyi meŋ ne kolosi baa nyi, kaboena nyi walaŋ waa kara ɔ baala wose pɛna Wurubuarɛ ke lɛɛ e do nɛ, nafɔ Gyudatena be gyae baa naase maŋ diyem nyi mee kolosi Kristo yeŋ keŋ ɔ ba yem ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ nombia ya.

eng: I wish that the ones who are upsetting you would castrate themselves!.
local: Balaŋ baŋ be dana baala wose kekara nombia bɛɛ naase ŋon diyem nɛ, nafɔ maŋ kegyaebii yɛna nyi baa gyeŋ gyu ka lese ba wose saayɛ gba.

eng: You were called to freedom, brothers and sisters; only dont let this freedom be an opportunity to indulge your selfish impulses, but serve each other through love.
local: Me tebia, ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ baake ŋon bo nyi ɛ kaa bese gyooro bia. Mɔna ɛ na yeli ɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ ke fa ŋon gbɛɛ na ɛ ke yela ɛ lookum ke di ɛ dɔɔ ya. Ɛ mo balaŋ kegyae gyae dɔŋa na ɛ ke kyɔ dɔŋa.

eng: All the Law has been fulfilled in a single statement: Love your neighbor as yourself .
local: Mosesi mmaraase ŋan pou seŋɛɛ bo nombii dokoloŋ dɔɔ. Keŋ yɛna nyiaa, Gyae n dɔɔ ŋgba nyaŋ gbagba n wose nɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, if a person is caught doing something wrong, you who are spiritual should restore someone like this with a spirit of gentleness. Watch out for yourselves so you wont be tempted too.
local: Me tebia, akpaa ɛ naa nyi ɛ man ŋolo ne yɛɛ dukum nɛ ɛmɛɛ baŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon doo ɛ man nideli nɛ, ɛ gyu kekolosi fa e wose keyɔkɔse man na waa nyiŋ bese kɔŋ gbɛɛ kpaakpaate keŋ man. Mɔna ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose nideli, keŋ nyi ɛmɛɛ mɔ be yɛɛ dukum kenaŋ ya.

eng: But if you bite and devour each other, be careful that you dont get eaten up by each other!.
local: Mɔna nyi akpaa ɛ sɛɛ ɛ ne yoo na dɔŋa ŋgba wonembia kumɛɛ na, kenaŋ na ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose nideli. Na mena ya na, ɛ ka koe dɔŋa munu.

eng: Carry each others burdens and so you will fulfill the law of Christ.
local: Ɛ yela ɛ tebia nombia ke yɛɛ ɛ nombia. Akpaa ɛ yɛɛ mena na, na nawolo baa nyi Kristo kegyaebii yaa ɛ ne yɛɛ.

eng: I say be guided by the Spirit and you wont carry out your selfish desires.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyiaa, ɛ yela Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ke ta ɛ siaman lee kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ ne yɛɛ nɛ man. Akpaa ɛ yɛɛ mena na, ɛ ba tekaa sila ɛmɛɛ gbagba nombiakumɛɛ ŋan ɛ ne gyae nɛ ya.

eng: If anyone thinks they are important when they arent, theyre fooling themselves.
local: Akpaa ŋolo ne kɛɛ ɔ wose nyi ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋolo e, mɔna kegbagba na ɔ te yɛɛ ŋolo e ya nɛ kenaŋ na ɔ na beo ɔ wose bo.

eng: A persons selfish desires are set against the Spirit, and the Spirit is set against ones selfish desires. They are opposed to each other, so you shouldnt do whatever you want to do.
local: Nawolo nyiaa, abɔɔ ŋan deniwalaŋ kegyaebii keŋ ne gyae waa yɛɛ nɛ, na ŋan te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne gyae nyi waa yɛɛ ya. Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon na deniwalaŋ kegyaebii keŋ bɛɛ gyoo ya. Lee mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale yɛɛ ɛ kegyaebii ya.

eng: Each person should test their own work and be happy with doing a good job and not compare themselves with others.
local: Kaboena nyi walaŋ kamasɛ waa gyoo ɔ wose man nideli kɛɛ nyi ɔ nombia ayɛɛsa dei. Akpaa ɔ naa nyi ɔ nombia ayɛɛsa dei na, kenaŋ na waa tale gyina ɔ wose adido lee ŋon gbagba ɔ nombia ayɛɛsa dɔɔ, keŋ nyi ɔ be gyae waa mo ɔ wose maa na ŋolo ya.

eng: But if you are being led by the Spirit, you arent under the Law.
local: Mɔna akpaa ɛ yɛɛ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne gyae nyi yaa yɛɛ na, kenaŋ na ɛ ba tekaa doo Gyudatena mmaraase ŋan tɛɛ bela ya.

eng: Each person will have to carry their own load.
local: Nawolo nyi, kaboena nyi walaŋ kamasɛ waa seele ŋon gbagba ɔ kasolɔ.

eng: The actions that are produced by selfish motives are obvious, since they include sexual immorality, moral corruption, doing whatever feels good.
local: Abɔɔ ŋan deniwalaŋ kegyaebii ne gyae nyi waa yɛɛ nɛ, ŋe bɛɛ weese ya. Ŋan yɛna nyi n ka lee n wɛɛle yaa n baale wɔle, yaa n ka mo ayimɛɛ wuu n wose, yaa desɛŋ nombia keyɛɛ.

eng: Those who are taught the word should share all good things with their teacher.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon bɛɛ wola e Wurubuarɛ nombia nɛ, abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan ɔ dana na kaboena nyi waa yela ŋaale ke tuŋ walaŋ ŋon ɔ na wola e nombia ŋan nɛ.

eng: idolatry, drug use and casting spells, hate, fighting, obsession, losing your temper, competitive opposition, conflict, selfishness, group rivalry.
local: Ŋaale mɔ yɛna bane ɔsom, na yolo nombia, na balaŋ kekɔla, na kale kegyae, na daaworo nombia, na baŋ kagyeŋ, na kedikum, na aŋmaareŋ nombia, na dɔŋa wɔle kalee.

eng: Make no mistake, God is not mocked. A person will harvest what they plant.
local: Ɛ na beo ɛ wose ya. Ŋolo be gyae waa tale kulu Wurubuarɛ ya, nawolo nyi kpene kamasɛ keŋ walaŋ de duu na, keŋ ke te waa kɔ.

eng: jealousy, drunkenness, partying, and other things like that. I warn you as I have already warned you, that those who do these kinds of things wont inherit Gods kingdom.
local: Ŋaale mɔ yɛna sibii, na soloŋ kenyɔɔ dɛɛse, na nɔɔkedi keŋ ayoi kumɛɛ doo ke man, na nombia nkaraŋkaraŋ keyɛɛ, na nombiakumɛɛ ŋan pou ŋe yɛɛ ŋgba kɛŋa dinɔɔ nɛ. Mɛɛ faree ŋon baa bela ŋgba mena keŋ mɔɔ wulaa faree ŋon kaalaŋ nɛ nyiaa, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa na, ɔ be gyae waa nyiŋ gbɛɛ gyu Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ man ya.

eng: Those who plant only for their own benefit will harvest devastation from their selfishness, but those who plant for the benefit of the Spirit will harvest eternal life from the Spirit.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ kpene keŋ ɔ dinɔɔ kum keŋ ne gyae na, gyae ke yela waa wɔlɛɛ kekpaakekpaa lee ɔ dinɔɔ kum kenaŋ man. Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne gyae na, gyae ke yela Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ke fa e nyeedoŋ kekpaakekpaa.

eng: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness.
local: Mɔna nyi Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon de kɔŋ kaa doo n man na, abɔɔ ŋan ɔ ne yeli n ne nyim yɛna, balaŋ kegyae, na kenyaŋee, na wosefɛɛreŋ, na konɔɔ kenyiŋ, na wɔe keyɛɛ, na deeli keyɛɛ, na anokoare kedi.

eng: Lets not get tired of doing good, because in time well have a harvest if we dont give up.
local: Ɛ na yeli deeli keyɛɛ ke takaa daa ya. Nawolo nyi akpaa deeli keyɛɛ te takaa daa ya nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa tɔ daa kom debaŋ kpaakpaate keŋ man.

eng: gentleness, and self-control. There is no law against things like this.
local: na wose keyɔkɔse, na wose dɔɔ kedi. Mmaraa kamasɛ be kyaa keŋ ne tɔ mena abɔɔ kɔŋa keyɛɛ ya.

eng: So then, lets work for the good of all whenever we have an opportunity, and especially for those in the household of faith.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ akpaa de nyiŋ gbɛɛ nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ deeli na, kaboena nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ ke fa walaŋ kamasɛ. Tuturu na ɛ yela dɛɛ yɛɛ deeli fa de tebia Kristotena.

eng: Those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the self with its passions and its desires.
local: Baŋ pou be yɛɛ Yesu Kristo balaŋwɔ nɛ, be te tina be nombiakumɛɛ na lookum keŋ pou doo be man nɛ yela.

eng: Look at the large letters Im making with my own handwriting!.
local: Ɛ kɛɛ me nɔɔwoya ɔtoman nyiŋan kɛŋa na ɛ kena nyi maŋ gbagba moona me nyiŋmaa ŋmarase keŋmarasebia dinaana kɛŋa yelee ŋon nɛ.

eng: If we live by the Spirit, lets follow the Spirit.
local: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon te fa daa nyeedoŋ wɔle. Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi dɛɛ yela feliŋ ŋonaŋ ka wola daa gbɛɛ keŋ man dɛɛ kyaa de dinɔɔ wɔle kenaŋ man.

eng: Whoever wants to look good by human standards will try to get you to be circumcised, but only so they wont be harassed for the cross of Christ.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ gyae nyi be nombia ka gyoo Gyudatena sia nɛ, baŋ na ŋere na ŋon nyi yaa kara ɛ baala wose. Mena balaŋ benaŋ ne yee nyi akpaa be kolosi Kristo yeŋ keŋ ɔ ba yem ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ wose man nombia na, balaŋ baa naase wɔ diyem.

eng: Lets not become arrogant, make each other angry, or be jealous of each other.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na yeli dɛɛ gate da wose adido, yaa dɛɛ do dɔŋa baŋ yaa de sia keyɛɛ dɔŋa abɔɔ ya.

eng: Those who are circumcised dont observe the Law themselves, but they want you to be circumcised, so they can boast about your physical body.
local: N kɛɛ na, balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ kara be baala wose nɛ koraŋ bɛɛ tale di mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ ya. Mɔna bɛɛ gyae bo nyi ɛ ke kara ɛ baala wose, na baa nyiŋ gyina ba wose adido nyi baŋ yele la te ɛ kara ɛ baala wose te ɛ bese be balaŋ.

eng: But as for me, God forbid that I should boast about anything except for the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. The world has been crucified to me through him, and I have been crucified to the world.
local: Te maŋ dɔɔ nyi maa gyina ma wose adido koraŋ na, kenaŋ na de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo yeŋ keŋ ɔ ba yem ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ, ke nombia dɔɔ te maa gyina ma wose adido. Nawolo nyi lee Kristo yeŋ keŋ dɔɔ, tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ pou ta tekaa yɛɛ kolo ke me siaman ya. Mena ke mɔ yaa lee Kristo yeŋ keŋ dɔɔ, tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ kegyae loo ta lee me man.

eng: Being circumcised or not being circumcised doesnt mean anything. What matters is a new creation.
local: Nyi ŋolo kara ɔ baala wose yaa ɔ te kare ya o nɛ, keŋ bee hia ya. Kpene keŋ ne hia yɛna nyi lee Kristo dɔɔ ɔtenate te nyiŋ dinɔɔ wɔle.

eng: May peace and mercy be on whoever follows this rule and on Gods Israel.
local: Balaŋ baŋ pou bee nyii nombia kɛŋa te bee di ŋe dɔɔ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa wii be waraŋase na waa fa wɔ wosefɛɛreŋ. Mena balaŋ kɛwɔ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bia gbagba baŋ ɔ ba lese nɛ.

eng: From now on, no one should bother me because I bear the marks of Jesus on my body.
local: Lee gyɛŋ ne gyu nɛ, mɛɛ fane nyi ŋolo na tekaa naase maŋ diyem bela ya. Nawolo nyi gyaŋtarɛɛ ŋan mɔɔ nyim lee ketuku na boya kefuŋii keŋ me yɛɛ Kristo kpilale e dɔɔ nɛ tekaboe maŋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, may the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
local: Me tebia, de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo waa mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele ɛ popou na waa seŋ ɛ wɔle Amɛn.

eng: At one time you were like a dead person because of the things you did wrong and your offenses against God.
local: Gyaŋgba keŋ na nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ te tira ɛmɛɛ na Wurubuarɛ nsana.

eng: You used to live like people of this world. You followed the rule of a destructive spiritual power. This is the spirit of disobedience to Gods will that is now at work in persons whose lives are characterized by disobedience.
local: gyaŋgba kenaŋ na, ɛ silana tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nombiakumɛɛ. Ɛ silana ɔbɔnsam ŋon ɔ yɛɛ dukum pou nyee e nɛ. Te ŋon mɔ ne diina baŋ pou bɔɔ ne tɛɛ Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛneŋ dɔɔ ya nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: At one time you were like those persons. All of you used to do whatever felt good and whatever you thought you wanted so that you were children headed for punishment just like everyone else.
local: Nafɔ nnɛ te daa na wɔ mɔ pou kyaawɔ, na dɛɛ yɛɛ kpene keŋ dɛɛ gyae. Dee sila de dinɔɔ benɛɛ ŋan te dɛɛ yɛɛ kpene keŋ da wose na de konɔɔ man nombia na wola daa nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ. Nafɔ nnɛ yaa Wurubuarɛ baŋ keŋ ne fa deŋele kewɔŋ nɛ doo de dɔɔ, ŋgba balaŋ akaŋ baŋ nɛ.

eng: However, God is rich in mercy. He brought us to life with Christ while we were dead as a result of those things that we did wrong. He did this because of the great love that he has for us. You are saved by Gods grace!.
local: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ wii de waraŋase te lee ɔ kegyae dinaa keŋ ɔ ne gyae de nombia nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: However, God is rich in mercy. He brought us to life with Christ while we were dead as a result of those things that we did wrong. He did this because of the great love that he has for us. You are saved by Gods grace!.
local: ɔ tɛɛwɔ Kristo dɔɔ fa daa baŋ nafɔ debaŋ kenaŋ man dɔɔ yem de dukum man nɛ nyeedoŋ. Mena dɔɔ ɛ tɔɔse nyi Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba mo boele daa dɔɔ te ɛ nyiŋa nyeedoŋ.

eng: And God raised us up and seated us in the heavens with Christ Jesus.
local: Te mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ be gyuusu Kristo lee yeŋ man nɛ, mena mɔ yaa ɔ gyuusu daa mɔ lee mena yeŋ kei man, te ɔ moo daa kekyaa Yesu Kristo gyaŋ adido na daa na e kaa di gyoori.

eng: God did this to show future generations the greatness of his grace by the goodness that God has shown us in Christ Jesus.
local: Ɔ yɛɛ nnɛ kei na debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ kɛŋa pou gyae kekɔŋ na, kenyiŋ lese ɔ bɛrɛɛ na ɔ wɔe keŋ doo Kristo man te ka kela kpene kamasɛ nɛ wola.

eng: You are saved by Gods grace because of your faith. This salvation is Gods gift. Its not something you possessed.
local: Nawolo nyi, bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba moo boele daa, na da kelɛɛdi keŋ dɔɔ lɛɛdi dɔɔ te ɔ fa daa nyeedoŋ. Kɛŋa pou ta lee daa gbagba gyaŋ ya, ke yɛɛ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo fa daa.

eng: Its not something you did that you can be proud of.
local: Ta lee tomse ŋan dɔɔ yɛɛ dɔɔ ya, mena dɔɔ ŋolo na wola ɔ wose ŋe dɔɔ ya.

eng: Instead, we are Gods accomplishment, created in Christ Jesus to do good things. God planned for these good things to be the way that we live our lives.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ na daa mena keŋ gyɛŋ de doo nɛ. Keŋ daa na Kristo be yɛɛ dokoloŋ dɔɔ, te ɔ lese daa nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ tomse kpaakpaa ŋan ɔ be wulaa desina see nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ.

eng: So remember that once you were Gentiles by physical descent, who were called uncircumcised by Jews who are physically circumcised.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ tɔɔse mena keŋ nafɔ lolo man na, ɛ te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ. Ɛmɛɛ te Gyudatena ne baake nyi, baŋ ba te kara be baala wose ya nɛ. Be wui baala wose keŋ bɔɔ kare nɛ, nyiŋmaa te ba moowɔ kara.

eng: At that time you were without Christ. You were aliens rather than citizens of Israel, and strangers to the covenants of Gods promise. In this world you had no hope and no God.
local: Ɛ tɔɔse nyi debaŋ kenaŋ na, ɛmɛɛ na Kristo betaŋ loŋtoro ya. Na ɛ yɛɛ yenaasetenawɔ, ɛ te yɛɛ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ba lese nɛ ya. Na ɛ yɛɛ ŋɔɔla baŋ ɛ be kpuɛ kebakesee keŋ Wurubuarɛ na ɔ balaŋ be bake see nɛ man ya. Na ɛ sia be gyakaa kolo mɔ dɔɔ ya, te ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ ya.

eng: But now, thanks to Christ Jesus, you who once were so far away have been brought near by the blood of Christ.
local: Te nɛnɛɛ keŋ ɛ doo Kristo man dɔɔ, ɛmɛɛ baŋ nafɔ ɛ kyaa lemlem, te ɛ ba benaa na Kristo ya nɛ, lee ɔ yeŋ keŋ ɔ ba yem nɛ dɔɔ, ɔ te tuu ŋon benaa na ɔ wose.

eng: Christ is our peace. He made both Jews and Gentiles into one group. With his body, he broke down the barrier of hatred that divided us.
local: Ŋon gbagba yɛna da wosefɛɛreŋ. Ɔ te yɛɛ Gyudatena na baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ walaŋ dokoloŋ. Lee ɔ yeŋ dɔɔ, ɔ tekerii dekyaŋ keŋ nafɔ ke yɛɛ kekɔla dekyaŋ te ne kpase mena balaŋ akpeŋ ala kɛwɔ nsana nɛ.

eng: He canceled the detailed rules of the Law so that he could create one new person out of the two groups, making peace.
local: Nawolo nyi, ɔ wose te ɔ moowɔ lese Gyudatena mmaraase, na gbɛɛneŋ, na nombia burum ŋan. Kpene keŋ dɔɔ ɔ be yɛɛ kei yɛna nyi, na waa nyiŋ mo mena balaŋ akpeŋ ala kɛwɔ yɛɛ dokoloŋ kɔŋ ŋon gbagba man. Nnɛ te ɔ yela ɔ wosefɛɛreŋ kɔŋawɔ be man.

eng: He reconciled them both as one body to God by the cross, which ended the hostility to God.
local: Ɔ tɛɛwɔ ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ dɔɔ pataa akpiise ala kɛwɔ, kyaŋ wɔ mo do Wurubuarɛ man, yɛɛ wɔ walaŋ dokoloŋ. Nawolo nyi, ɔ ta mo ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ dɔɔ lese kpene keŋ ne yele bɛɛ kɔla dɔŋa nɛ.

eng: When he came, he announced the good news of peace to you who were far away from God and to those who were near.
local: Ɔ kɔŋawɔ kaa yako wosefɛɛreŋ nombia ŋan fa ɛmɛɛ Gyudatena baŋ ɛ benaa na Wurubuarɛ, na baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ.

eng: We both have access to the Father through Christ by the one Spirit.
local: Ŋon dɔɔ te Gyudatena na baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ pou nyiŋa gbɛɛ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ dokoloŋ man, mo gyu kya ŋon gyaŋ.

eng: So now you are no longer strangers and aliens. Rather, you are fellow citizens with Gods people, and you belong to Gods household.
local: Mena dɔɔ nɛnɛɛ nafɔ ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ba benaa na e ya nɛ, ɛ ta tekaa yɛɛ ŋɔɔla na balaŋ yenaasetenawɔ bela ya. Ɛ yɛɛ baŋ Wurubuarɛ be hiraa wɔ nɛ be dɔɔna na be tebia na Wurubuarɛ dekpaŋalaŋmantenawɔ.

eng: As Gods household, you are built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets with Christ Jesus himself as the cornerstone.
local: Ɛmɛɛ mɔ, be ta mo ŋon ŋgba deni nɛ, Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra na Yesu kpilala baŋ yɛna dekyaŋ keŋ mokɔɔ kasee. Ɛmɛɛ te bɛɛ mo ma gyakaa ke dɔɔ. Yesu Kristo gbagba yɛna boe keŋ kɛɛ fa deni keŋ pou doŋ nɛ.

eng: The whole building is joined together in him, and it grows up into a temple that is dedicated to the Lord.
local: Ŋon dɔɔ te deni keŋ boe kamasɛ na sem nideli, te kɛɛ bee bese Wurubuarɛ kedeesa deni keŋ bɔɔ maa see fa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom, te ayimɛɛ be doo ke man ya nɛ.

eng: Christ is building you into a place where God lives through the Spirit.
local: Ɛmɛɛ na Yesu te yɛɛ dokoloŋ dɔɔ, te yela ɛmɛɛ na akaŋ baŋ pou bese dekyae keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne tɛɛ ɔ feliŋ ŋon dɔɔ kyaa.

eng: This is why I, Paul, am a prisoner of Christ for you Gentiles.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ fane fa Wurubuarɛ. Maŋ Pɔɔl ŋon bɔɔ kyaŋ maŋ tɔ deni lee mena keŋ mɛɛ yako Yesu Kristo nombia ŋan mɛɛ fa ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: Youve heard, of course, about the responsibility to distribute Gods grace, which God gave to me for you, right?.
local: Ma gyeŋ nyi ɛ te nyii nyi ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ bɛrɛɛ keŋ boele maŋ, na maa nyiŋ tale yako Yesu Kristo nombia ŋan fa ŋon lee ɛ deeli dɔɔ.

eng: God showed me his secret plan in a revelation, as I mentioned briefly before .
local: Wurubuarɛ ta lese nombia ŋan weesɛɛ nɛ wola maŋ, ŋgba mena keŋ mɔɔ taŋgbɛɛ ŋmarase fa ŋon nɛ.

eng: when you read this, youll understand my insight into the secret plan about Christ.
local: Akpaa ɛ kala ke na, ɛ kena mena keŋ mɔɔ nyii Kristo nombia ŋan nafɔ ŋa weesɛɛ nɛ man.

eng: Earlier generations didnt know this hidden plan that God has now revealed to his holy apostles and prophets through the Spirit.
local: Gyaŋgba keŋ na, ɔ ta lese mena nombia aweeseŋ kɛŋa wola deniwalaŋ ya, mɔna Wurubuarɛ te tɛɛ ɔ feliŋ ŋon dɔɔ lese ŋa wola Yesu Kristo kpilala na Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ ayimɛɛ be doo be man ya nɛ.

eng: This plan is that the Gentiles would be coheirs and parts of the same body, and that they would share with the Jews in the promises of God in Christ Jesus through the gospel.
local: Mena nombia aweeseŋ kɛŋa yɛna nyi; lee Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan dɔɔ balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, ta bese Wurubuarɛ wui ŋgba Gyudatena nɛ. Be popou ta bese walaŋ dokoloŋ na baŋ baa di abɔɔ ŋan Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa ta Yesu Kristo dɔɔ fa wɔ nɛ.

eng: I became a servant of the gospel because of the grace that God showed me through the exercise of his power.
local: Keŋ dɔɔ te ɔ moo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele maŋ, te ɔ moo maŋ yɛɛ Yesu Kristo nombia ŋan kegbetibii, lee ŋon gbagba ɔ doŋ keŋ dɔɔ.

eng: God gave his grace to me, the least of all Gods people, to preach the good news about the immeasurable riches of Christ to the Gentiles.
local: N kɛɛ na, maŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ balaŋ baŋ man pou kemaasa, mɔna maŋ mɔ te ɔ moo ɔ bɛrɛɛ keŋ boele, nyi maa yako Kristo wose man nombia ŋan ŋɛɛ lee koya te ŋa betaŋ ɔto ya nɛ fa baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ.

eng: God sent me to reveal the secret plan that had been hidden since the beginning of time by God, who created everything.
local: Na maa yela balaŋ pou kena Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ nɛ, gbɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba weese lee kewalaŋ keŋ nɛ.

eng: Gods purpose is now to show the rulers and powers in the heavens the many different varieties of his wisdom through the church.
local: Te ɔ kegyae yɛna nyi, debaŋ kei man nɛ waa ta Kristotena dikpii keŋ dɔɔ yela feliŋ man gyooneŋ na doŋse ŋan pou ŋe kyaa adido nɛ ka gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ sia kekara keŋ betaŋ ɔto ya nɛ kpene kamasɛ man.

eng: This was consistent with the plan he had from the beginning of time that he accomplished through Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ kei, lee kebake keŋ ɔ be bake see ɔ nyeeman, te betaŋ ɔto ya nɛ dɔɔ. Ɔ tɛe de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo dɔɔ te ɔ yɛɛ kei.

eng: In Christ we have bold and confident access to God through faith in him.
local: Keŋ dɔɔ lɛɛ e di te daa na e be yɛɛ dokoloŋ dɔɔ, te dɛɛ tale gyɔ de konɔɔ gyu ŋon Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ keŋ dɔɔ ne yee ya.

eng: So then, I ask you not to become discouraged by what Im suffering for you, which is your glory.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ waase ŋon nyi, ɛ na yela ɛ wose ke tekerii ŋon lee diyem keŋ mɛɛ naa fa ŋon nɛ dɔɔ ya. Kɛŋa pou yɛɛ fa ɛ deeli dɔɔ.

eng: This is why I kneel before the Father.
local: Kei dɔɔ te mee buŋ ma doona dɔɔ de kya Wurubuarɛ siaman.

eng: Every ethnic group in heaven or on earth is recognized by him.
local: Lee ŋon dɔɔ te loŋtoro kamasɛ keŋ kyaa adido na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ ne nyiŋ ka yele.

eng: I ask that he will strengthen you in your inner selves from the riches of his glory through the Spirit.
local: Mɛɛ sola Wurubuarɛ nyi, waa mo ɔ gyooro wose deeli keŋ na waa ta ɔ feliŋ ŋon dɔɔ, na waa fa ŋon doŋ, na waa do ŋon kakyeŋ.

eng: I ask that Christ will live in your hearts through faith. As a result of having strong roots in love.
local: Waa yela Kristo kekyaa ɛ konɔɔ man ɛ kelɛɛdi dɔɔ, na kegyae keŋ ɛ ne gyae Wurubuarɛ na balaŋ nɛ kefa ŋon doŋ na ɛ ka seŋ keŋkeŋ.

eng: I ask that youll have the power to grasp loves width and length, height and depth, together with all believers.
local: Na ɛmɛɛ na Kristotena akaŋ baŋ pou kenyiŋ gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ kegyae keŋ ɔ ne gyae daa nɛ boe nideli te ka betaŋ ɔto ya.

eng: I ask that youll know the love of Christ that is beyond knowledge so that you will be filled entirely with the fullness of God.
local: Na ɛ ka gyeŋ nyi kegyae keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne gyae daa nɛ boe, keŋ dɔɔ dɔɔ gyae dɛɛ tale naa ke man taŋ ya. Na Wurubuarɛ ka mo ɔ dinɔɔ pou do ɛ man.

eng: Glory to God, who is able to do far beyond all that we could ask or imagine by his power at work within us.
local: Ɛ yela dɛɛ ta Yesu Kristo na Kristotena dikpii keŋ dɔɔ lese de Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ yele debaŋ kamasɛ. Nawolo nyi ŋon ne tale la yɛɛ abɔɔ burum kela mena keŋ dɛɛ bɔɔse yaa dɛɛ gyueŋ lee ɔ doŋ keŋ ne yɛɛ tom de man nɛ dɔɔ. Daa balaŋ baŋ pou dɔɔ lɛɛ e di te daa na e yɛɛ dokoloŋ nɛ, ɛ yela dɛɛ lese ɔ yele kekpaakekpaa. Amɛn.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will. To the holy and faithful people in Christ Jesus in Ephesus.
local: Tɔne kei lee maŋ Pɔɔl ŋon Wurubuarɛ be gyae nyi maa bese Yesu Kristo kpilale nɛ gyaŋ. Mɛɛ ŋmarase ke mee yelee ɛmɛɛ Kristotena baŋ ɛ kyaa Efeso donɔɔ man, te Wurubuarɛ ba mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɛ dɔɔ te ɛ ne di anokoare ɛ gbɛɛneŋ man fa Yesu Kristo nɛ.

eng: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ de kya e, na de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo baa mo be bɛrɛɛ boele ŋon, na baa fa ŋon wosefɛɛreŋ.

eng: Bless the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! He has blessed us in Christ with every spiritual blessing that comes from heaven.
local: Ɛ yela dɛɛ lese de kya Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo Wurubuarɛ na ɔ kya e nɛ yele. Nawolo nyi keŋ daa na de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo yɛɛ dokoloŋ dɔɔ, ɔ ta mo abɔɔ ŋan pou kyaa adido nɛ boele daa.

eng: God chose us in Christ to be holy and blameless in Gods presence before the creation of the world.
local: Yesu Kristo dɔɔ te ɔ lese daa pɛte ɔ yɛɛ tɛɛle kei na abɔɔ pou, nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ de yɛɛ ŋon gbagba wui ɔ siaman keŋ ayimɛɛ be doo de man ya, lee mena keŋ daa na Kristo yɛɛ dokoloŋ dɔɔ.

eng: God destined us to be his adopted children through Jesus Christ because of his love. This was according to his goodwill and plan.
local: Lee kegyae keŋ ɔ ne gyae daa dɔɔ ɔ taŋgbɛɛwɔ yako see nyi waa ta Yesu Kristo dɔɔ mo daa yɛɛ ɔ bia lee ŋon gbagba kegyaebii man.

eng: and to honor his glorious grace that he has given to us freely through the Son whom he loves.
local: Ɔ lese daa mena na dee nyiŋ lese ɔ gyooro bɛrɛɛ yele keŋ ɔ ba moo fa daa, nawolo nyi de yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon ɔ ne gyae daa nɛ wui.

eng: We have been ransomed through his Sons blood, and we have forgiveness for our failures based on his overflowing grace.
local: Keŋ Kristo ba yem kyɛɛkee ɔ fatabo leki dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ lɛɛ da nyee te ɔ moo de dukum kyɛɛ daa. Kei ne yele la dɛɛ naa nyi Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba mo boele daa nɛ boe nideli. Wurubuarɛ gbagba nyansa na ɔ nombia kagyeŋ man te ɔ moowɔ yɛɛ mena.

eng: God revealed his hidden design to us, which is according to his goodwill and the plan that he intended to accomplish through his Son.
local: Te kpene keŋ ɔ be wulaa yako see nyi waa yɛɛ nɛ, ɔ tɛɛwɔ Kristo dɔɔ lese ɔ nombia ŋan weesɛɛ nɛ wola daa ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ ne gyae nɛ.

eng: This is what God planned for the climax of all times: to bring all things together in Christ, the things in heaven along with the things on earth.
local: Kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa yɛɛ nɛ yɛna nyi, debaŋ keŋ de lii nɛ waa yilaa kpene kamasɛ keŋ kyaa adido na keŋ kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ pou do Kristo tɛɛ.

eng: We have also received an inheritance in Christ. We were destined by the plan of God, who accomplishes everything according to his design.
local: Te keŋ de doo Kristo man dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ ta mo daa yɛɛ ɔ balaŋ mɔ, ŋgba mena keŋ ŋon ɔ be yɛɛ abɔɔ pou be yako ɔ nyeeman see nyi waa yɛɛ daa nɛ.

eng: We are called to be an honor to Gods glory because we were the first to hope in Christ.
local: Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ kɛŋa pou na daa baŋ dɔɔ taŋgbɛɛ lɛɛ Kristo nɛ, ka lese ɔ gyoorobiiri keŋ yele.

eng: You too heard the word of truth in Christ, which is the good news of your salvation. You were sealed with the promised Holy Spirit because you believed in Christ.
local: Mena te doo fa ɛmɛɛ mɔ. Ɛ ke nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ŋe doo ŋe gbɛɛ dɔɔ te ŋɛɛ fa nyeedoŋ nɛ, ɛ lɛɛwɔ di. Lee mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ moo ŋon yɛɛ ɔ wui, te ɔ moo ɔ feliŋ ŋon ɔ be yako see nyi waa mo do ɛ man tɔ nɔɔ nɛ see ŋon. Kei nawola la nyi ɔ ta mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɛ dɔɔ nyi ɛ yɛɛ ɔ wui.

eng: The Holy Spirit is the down payment on our inheritance, which is applied toward our redemption as Gods own people, resulting in the honor of Gods glory.
local: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ kei yɛna kpene keŋ doo ɛ man, ŋgba kpene keŋ ɔ ba mo see ateta nɛ. Kei nawolo nyi dee nyiŋ abɔɔ ŋan pou Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa mo fa ɔ balaŋ nɛ. Kei ne yele la dɛɛ gyem nyi Wurubuarɛ waa lese balaŋ baŋ pou be yɛɛ ɔ wui nɛ lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man. Mena dɔɔ ɛ yela dɛɛ lese Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ yele.

eng: Since I heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all Gods people, this is the reason that.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ me ke nyii mena keŋ ɛ be lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di, na mena keŋ ɛ ne gyae balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ lɛɛ Kristo nombia ŋan di nɛ.

eng: I dont stop giving thanks to God for you when I remember you in my prayers.
local: me bee tina Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ ka do yela ya. Mɛɛ tɔɔse ɛ yela debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ mɛɛ fane fa Wurubuarɛ nɛ.

eng: I pray that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, will give you a spirit of wisdom and revelation that makes God known to you.
local: Mɛɛ fane mɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo na gyoorobia pou be kya e nɛ, waa mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do ɛ man na waa yela ɛ sia kekara, na ɔ feliŋ ŋon ka lese Wurubuarɛ ŋon wola ŋon na ɛ ka gyeŋ e nideli.

eng: I pray that the eyes of your heart will have enough light to see what is the hope of Gods call, what is the richness of Gods glorious inheritance among believers.
local: Mɛɛ solo nyi waa gyuusu ɛ konɔɔse, na ɛ ke nyiŋ gyeŋ kpene keŋ dɔɔ ɔ ba lese ŋon na kpene keŋ dɔɔ ɛ loo gyakaa nɛ, na ɛ ke nyiŋ gyeŋ abɔɔ ŋan Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa mo fa baŋ bɔɔ mo ba wose fa Kristo nɛ. Ŋe yɛɛ abɔɔ ŋan ŋa koya dana doŋ nideli.

eng: and what is the overwhelming greatness of Gods power that is working among us believers. This power is conferred by the energy of Gods powerful strength.
local: Dɛɛ fane nyi yaa gyeŋ ɔ doŋ dinaa keŋ ka dokoso be kyaa ya nɛ. Ɔ ta mo mena doŋ kei fa daa baŋ dɛɛ lɛɛ e di nɛ. Mena doŋ kei te ɔ moowɔ gyuusu Kristo lee yeŋ man, te ɔ moo e kyaase ɔ dunoluŋ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ man.

eng: far above every ruler and authority and power and angelic power, any power that might be named not only now but in the future.
local: Wurubuarɛ te yeli Kristo te di doŋ kela yela pou na gyoorise pou na bɛɛneŋ pou na yola pou na woya pou na ŋmeeseŋ pou na kpene kamasɛ keŋ ne di doŋ deniwalaŋ dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: God put everything under Christs feet and made him head of everything in the church.
local: Wurubuarɛ ta mo kpene kamasɛ do Kristo tɛɛ, te ɔ moo e yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ nyee fa Kristo balaŋ baŋ pou.

eng: which is his body. His body, the church, is the fullness of Christ, who fills everything in every way.
local: Kristo balaŋ kɛwɔ yɛna Kristotena dikpii keŋ. Te wose kei man te Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ, te ɔ kyaa yenaŋ kamasɛ te ɔ ne yeli abɔɔ kamasɛ na sem nideli gbɛɛ kamasɛ man nɛ kyaa.

eng: Therefore, imitate God like dearly loved children.
local: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bia baŋ ɔ ne gyae ɛ nombia nɛ, ɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ na ɛ kekasee e.

eng: Live your life with love, following the example of Christ, who loved us and gave himself for us. He was a sacrificial offering that smelled sweet to God.
local: Ɛ kyaa na dɔŋa gyoŋ man, ŋgba mena keŋ Kristo be gyae daa, te ɔ ba mo ɔ wose yɛɛ ŋgba kedeesa nam, te ba gyoo Wurubuarɛ sia nɛ.

eng: Sexual immorality, and any kind of impurity or greed, shouldnt even be mentioned among you, which is right for holy persons.
local: Te kafoŋ na dinɔɔse kumɛɛ ŋan tekaboena ya, yaa sibii dɔɔ, ɛ na yeli baa baake ŋa yela ɛ man ya. Nawolo nyi ŋan tekaboena Kristotena ya.

eng: Obscene language, silly talk, or vulgar jokes arent acceptable for believers. Instead, there should be thanksgiving.
local: Mena yaa desɛŋ nombia, na diyiŋ ayɛɛsa, na nsendeekum tekaboena ŋon ya, mɔna ɛ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ debaŋ kamasɛ.

eng: Because you know for sure that persons who are sexually immoral, impure, or greedy—which happens when things become gods—those persons wont inherit the kingdom of Christ and God.
local: Mɛɛ gyae ɛ ka gyeŋ nyi, kafoŋtena yaa baŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ ayimɛɛ nombia na baŋ be dana sibii nɛ betaŋ dekyae Kristo na Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man ya. Nawolo nyi n dana sibii na, abɔɔ na bese bɛɛneŋ bo fa neŋ.

eng: Nobody should deceive you with stupid ideas. Gods anger comes down on those who are disobedient because of this kind of thing.
local: Ɛ na yeli ŋolo ka mo akɛrɛɛ nɔɔwoya ŋan betaŋ tɔnɔɔ ya nɛ beo ŋon ya, nawolo nyi nombia kɛŋa dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ baŋ ne kɔŋ balaŋ baŋ ba deŋela dana doŋ nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: So you shouldnt have anything to do with them.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na mo ɛ wose do na balaŋ kɛwɔ dinɔɔ ya.

eng: You were once darkness, but now you are light in the Lord, so live your life as children of light.
local: Nafɔ gyaŋgba keŋ na ɛmɛɛ mɔ kyaa ditiŋtɛɛ man, te nɛnɛɛ keŋ ɛ ba bese Kristotena dɔɔ ɛ kyaa keŋmaŋee keŋ man. Lee mena dɔɔ ɛ ta ŋgba balaŋ baŋ be kyaa keŋmaŋee keŋ man nɛ.

eng: Light produces fruit that consists of every sort of goodness, justice, and truth.
local: Keŋmaŋee kei tɔnɔɔ yɛna nyi, balaŋ ne yɛɛ abɔɔ kpaakpaa na abɔɔ ŋan tenɛɛ te bee di anokoare mɔ.

eng: Therefore, test everything to see whats pleasing to the Lord.
local: Ɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ na ɛ ke yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ keŋ gyae ke yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoŋ.

eng: and dont participate in the unfruitful actions of darkness. Instead, you should reveal the truth about them.
local: Ɛ na mo ɛ wose do na balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ ditiŋtɛɛ man abɔɔ ŋan betaŋ tɔnɔɔ nɛ ya. Mɔna ɛ lese mena ditiŋtɛɛ man abɔɔ kumɛɛ ŋenaŋ debɔɔ.

eng: Its embarrassing to even talk about what certain persons do in secret.
local: Yɛɛ desɛŋ ke gba nyi ɛmɛɛ mɔ yaa yɔɔ nombiakumɛɛ ŋan balaŋ ne yɛɛ ditiŋtɛɛ man nɛ afa.

eng: But everything exposed to the light is revealed by the light.
local: Te kpene kamasɛ keŋ keŋmaŋee keŋ da lee ke dɔɔ na, kɛɛ lese ke dinɔɔ gbagba wola.

eng: Everything that is revealed by the light is light. Therefore, it says, Wake up, sleeper! Get up from the dead, and Christ will shine on you. .
local: Nawolo nyi keŋmaŋee keŋ ne yɛɛ la balaŋ ne naa kpene kamasɛ keŋ nafɔ weesɛɛ nɛ dinɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ te be Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nyiaa, Nyaŋ ŋon n be doo nɛ foŋ lee n dosoroŋ man, Foŋ lee n yeŋ man. Na Kristo keŋmaŋee keŋ ke kɔŋ n man.

eng: So be careful to live your life wisely, not foolishly.
local: Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ keta man nideli. Ɛ na tɛɛ ŋgba ayiŋtena ya, mɔna ɛ ta ŋgba siakaralatena nɛ.

eng: Take advantage of every opportunity because these are evil times.
local: Te debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ ɛ nyim na, ɛ mo yɛɛ kolo kpaakpaa. Ɛ na yeli debaŋ ke laŋ ŋon ya, nawolo nyi nɛnɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ ta boo.

eng: Because of this, dont be ignorant, but understand the Lords will.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na gyim ya, ɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ na ɛ ka gyeŋ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne gyae ɛ ke yɛɛ nɛ.

eng: Dont get drunk on wine, which produces depravity. Instead, be filled with the Spirit in the following ways.
local: Ɛ na dɛɛse ya, nawolo nyi soloŋ kedɛɛse ne wɔlɛɛ walaŋ bo. Mɔna ɛ yela Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ke doo ɛ man nideli.

eng: speak to each other with psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs; sing and make music to the Lord in your hearts.
local: Ɛ mo nombia ŋan doo Wurubuarɛ nɔɔneŋse man nɛ do dɔŋa kakyeŋ. Ɛ mo nɔɔneŋ kekpee na suya kegyɔ keŋ ba lee ɛ konɔɔse man nɛ lese de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ yele.

eng: always give thanks to God the Father for everything in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Debaŋ kamasɛ na ɛ ta de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo dɔɔ fa de kya Wurubuarɛ karaŋ lee kpene kamasɛ man.

eng: and submit to each other out of respect for Christ.
local: Keŋ Wurubuarɛ keyee doo ɛ man dɔɔ, ɛ yɔkɔse ɛ wose fa dɔŋa debaŋ kamasɛ.

eng: For example, wives should submit to their husbands as if to the Lord.
local: Ala, ɛ yɔkɔse ɛ wose fa ɛ baala kpene kamasɛ man, ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ ne yɔkɔse ɛ wose fa de Gbeŋgyoo nɛ.

eng: A husband is the head of his wife like Christ is head of the church, that is, the savior of the body.
local: Nawolo nyi baale ŋon ne diina doŋ ɔ wɛɛle ŋon dɔɔ, ŋgba mena keŋ Kristo ne di doŋ Kristotena dikpii keŋ dɔɔ nɛ. Te Kristo kei gbagba lɛɛ na balaŋ baŋ be doo Kristotena dikpii keŋ yɛɛ ɔ wose ke nɛ nyee.

eng: So wives submit to their husbands in everything like the church submits to Christ.
local: Mena dɔɔ alo kamasɛ waa yɔkɔse ɔ wose fa ɔ baale kpene kamasɛ man, ŋgba mena keŋ Kristotena dikpii keŋ ne yɔkɔse ka wose fa Kristo nɛ.

eng: As for husbands, love your wives just like Christ loved the church and gave himself for her.
local: Baala, ɛ gyae ɛ wɛɛla nombia ŋgba mena keŋ Kristo ne gyae Kristotena dikpii keŋ nombia, te ɔ ba yem fa ke nɛ.

eng: He did this to make her holy by washing her in a bath of water with the word.
local: Kristo yeŋawɔ fa Kristotena dikpii keŋ, te ɔ moo ɔ nombia ŋan yɛɛ ŋgba loŋ nɛ, faafo balaŋ baŋ be doo Kristotena dikpii keŋ man lese ba ayimɛɛ te ɔ moo wɔ see fa Wurubuarɛ.

eng: He did this to present himself with a splendid church, one without any sort of stain or wrinkle on her clothes, but rather one that is holy and blameless.
local: Ɔ yɛɛ kei na waa nyiŋ mo Kristotena dikpii kei kɔŋ ŋon gbagba ɔ siaman kaa yɛɛ ɔ wui, keŋ gyaŋtare yaa ayimɛɛ, yaa nombiikum kamasɛ be doo ka wose man ya.

eng: Thats how husbands ought to love their wives—in the same way as they do their own bodies. Anyone who loves his wife loves himself.
local: Mena ke mɔ te kaboena nyi baala baa gyae be wɛɛla nombia, ŋgba mena keŋ bɛɛ gyae baŋ gbagba ba wose nɛ. Baale ŋon ɔ ne gyae ɔ wɛɛle nombia nɛ, ne gyae ŋon gbagba ɔ wose baa.

eng: No one ever hates his own body, but feeds it and takes care of it just like Christ does for the church.
local: Ŋolo be kyaa keŋ ɔ ne kɔla ŋon gbagba ɔ wose ya, mɔna ɔ ne desina ke te ɔ ne kɛɛ ke dɔɔ mɔ. Nnɛ mɔ yaa Kristo mɔ ne desina, te ɔ ne kɛɛ Kristotena dikpii keŋ dɔɔ, nawolo nyi de yɛɛ ɔ wose man balaŋwɔ.

eng: This is why a man will leave his father and mother and be united with his wife, and the two of them will be one body .
local: Keŋ dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nyiaa, Lee mena dɔɔ baale waa lee ɔ kya na ɔ naa gyaŋ, na waa gyu ke kpu na ɔ wɛɛle na ŋon na e ka bese walaŋ dokoloŋ.

eng: Marriage is a significant allegory, and Im applying it to Christ and the church.
local: Mena nombii kei yɛɛ nombii keŋ ke dana yuŋ te ŋolo mɔ be tale nyii ka asɛɛ ya. Te maŋ dɔɔ ma gyeŋ nyi Kristo na balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ e di nɛ wose man nombia yaa bɛɛ yako.

eng: In any case, as for you individually, each one of you should love his wife as himself, and wives should respect their husbands.
local: Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi baale kamasɛ mɔ waa gyae ɔ wɛɛle ŋgba ŋon gbagba ɔ wose nɛ, na alo kamasɛ mɔ ke yɔkɔse ɔ wose fa ɔ baale.

eng: Therefore, as a prisoner for the Lord, I encourage you to live as people worthy of the call you received from God.
local: Mena dɔɔ nɛnɛɛ maŋ Pɔɔl ŋon me doo deni man keŋ mɛɛ yako Kristo nombia ŋan dɔɔ nɛ, mɛɛ waase ŋon nyi ɛ yela ɛ dinɔɔse ke kaboena kebaake kpaakpaa keŋ Wurubuarɛ be baake ŋon nɛ.

eng: Conduct yourselves with all humility, gentleness, and patience. Accept each other with love.
local: Ɛ yɔkɔse ɛ wose. Ɛ woo na ɛ kenyiŋ konɔɔ. Ɛ gyae dɔŋa nombia na ɛ kewii fa dɔŋa. Ɛ na mo ŋolo mɔ nombia do ɛ tɔɔ man ya.

eng: and make an effort to preserve the unity of the Spirit with the peace that ties you together.
local: Ɛ kyaŋ ɛ wose na nɔɔdokoloŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ne tɛɛ wosefɛɛreŋ dɔɔ fa daa nɛ kekyaŋ ŋon bom.

eng: You are one body and one spirit, just as God also called you in one hope.
local: De popou yɛɛ dokoloŋ. Wurubuarɛ feliŋ dokoloŋ kyaa la mena dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ baake daa te de sia gyakaa kolo dokoloŋ kooŋ dɔɔ.

eng: There is one Lord, one faith, one baptism.
local: De Gbeŋgyoo yɛɛ dokoloŋ te Wurubuarɛ loŋkesɔ mɔ dokoloŋ kooŋ kyaa, la te gbɛɛ dokoloŋ mɔ dɔɔ te dɛɛ tɛɛ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di.

eng: and one God and Father of all, who is over all, through all, and in all.
local: De popou dana Wurubuarɛ dokoloŋ na kya dokoloŋ. Ŋon yɛna de popou de Gbeŋgyoo ŋon ɔ kela kpene kamasɛ, te ɔ doo de popou man te ɔ ne yɛɛ tom de popou man nɛ.

eng: God has given his grace to each one of us measured out by the gift that is given by Christ.
local: Yesu gbagba kegyaebii te ɔ moo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele daa dokoloŋ dokoloŋ, walaŋ kamasɛ na ɔ wui.

eng: Thats why scripture says, When he climbed up to the heights, he captured prisoners, and he gave gifts to people .
local: Mena dɔɔ te be ŋmarasewɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan man nyiaa, Debaŋ keŋ ɔ ne gyu adido nɛ ɔ moo baŋ ɔ be yoo dii be dɔɔ nɛ kpu na ɔ wose laŋ, te ɔ moo abɔɔ fa balaŋ.

eng: What does the phrase he climbed up mean if it doesnt mean that he had first gone down into the lower regions, the earth?.
local: Te akpaa be yako nyi ɔ gyeŋawɔ gyu adido na nawolo nyi sena? Kei nawolo baa nyi, ɔ taŋgbɛɛwɔ tisi gyoo tɛɛle kei ateta pɛ.

eng: The one who went down is the same one who climbed up above all the heavens so that he might fill everything.
local: Te ŋon ɔ ba gyoo tɛɛle man nɛ, ŋon ke yɛna walaŋ ŋon mɔ ɔ ba koro gbaa ŋɔma pou na waa nyiŋ kyaa yenaŋ kamasɛ.

eng: He gave some apostles, some prophets, some evangelists, and some pastors and teachers.
local: Abɔɔ ŋan ɔ ba mo boele ɔ balaŋ yɛna nyi, baale baa bese Kristo kpilala, na baale ka bese Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra, na baale mɔ ka bese baŋ bɛɛ tɛɛ kolosi nombia kpaakpaa ŋan nɛ, te baale mɔ baa yɛɛ ɔsɔfɔɔse na wolala.

eng: His purpose was to equip Gods people for the work of serving and building up the body of Christ.
local: Ɔ moo tomse kɛŋa boele ɔ balaŋ, na waa desina wɔ fa wɔ ɔsom keŋ, na kenyiŋ yela Kristotena dikpii keŋ ka bee nideli.

eng: until we all reach the unity of faith and knowledge of Gods Son. Gods goal is for us to become mature adults—to be fully grown, measured by the standard of the fullness of Christ.
local: kelii debaŋ keŋ de popou dɛɛ bee yɛɛ dokoloŋ, na dɛɛ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon di nideli na dɛɛ lee Kristo de dinɔɔ man, da kelɛɛdi na de kagyeŋ keŋ da gyeŋ e nideli nɛ man.

eng: As a result, we arent supposed to be infants any longer who can be tossed and blown around by every wind that comes from teaching with deceitful scheming and the tricks people play to deliberately mislead others.
local: Na dɔɔ tekaa bese bia wɔlɛɛ keŋ balaŋ ka mo kawola kumɛɛ kamasɛ na ba adao gbɛɛneŋ kaa beo daa, na kegyɔ daa gyu sia na wɔle ŋgba mena keŋ feliŋ ne gyɔ degbele loŋ dɔɔ nɛ ya.

eng: Instead, by speaking the truth with love, lets grow in every way into Christ.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ di anokoare fa dɔŋa lee ɛ konɔɔse man, na ɛ kegyae dɔŋa nombia, na ɛ ka mo gbɛɛ kamasɛ man bee Kristo ŋon ɔ yɛɛ dikpii keŋ nyee nɛ man.

eng: who is the head. The whole body grows from him, as it is joined and held together by all the supporting ligaments. The body makes itself grow in that it builds itself up with love as each one does its part.
local: Yesu Kristo man te wose man yenaase kpookpoo ŋan pou ne gyaŋee nkyakaase man kyaŋ dɔŋa nideli. Te akpaa yenaase kpookpoo kamasɛ kɛŋa ne gyae dɔŋa, te ŋɛɛ yɛɛ tomse ŋan ne kaboena ŋa na, ne yeli Kristotena dikpii keŋ na bee nideli.

eng: So Im telling you this, and I insist on it in the Lord: you shouldnt live your life like the Gentiles anymore. They base their lives on pointless thinking.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ faree ŋon de Gbeŋgyoo ŋon yele man nyi, ɛ na kyaa bela ŋgba balaŋ akaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ Kristo ya, te be gbɛɛneŋ betaŋ tɔnɔɔ nɛ ya.

eng: and they are in the dark in their reasoning. They are disconnected from Gods life because of their ignorance and their closed hearts.
local: Ba nyeeya man dana ditiŋtɛɛ, te bɔɔ taŋ nyeedoŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be fa daa nɛ ya, nawolo nyi ba te tɔ be konɔɔse bɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan kenyii.

eng: They are people who lack all sense of right and wrong, and who have turned themselves over to doing whatever feels good and to practicing every sort of corruption along with greed.
local: Te keŋ bɔɔ taŋ desɛŋ bela dɔɔ ya nɛ, be ta mo ba wose bɛɛ yɛɛ nombia akarankaraŋ ŋan tekaboena ya nɛ, te ŋolo bɛɛ tɔ wɔ gbɛɛ ya.

eng: But you didnt learn that sort of thing from Christ.
local: Mɔ ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ, ɛ te kasee kei lee Kristo wose man ya.

eng: Since you really listened to him and you were taught how the truth is in Jesus.
local: Ampaŋ te ɛ nyii Kristo wose man nombia ŋan. Te keŋ ɛ yɛɛ ɔ silalawɔ dɔɔ, be ta wola ŋon anokoare keŋ doo Yesu man nɛ.

eng: change the former way of life that was part of the person you once were, corrupted by deceitful desires.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ tina ɛ dinɔɔ benɛɛ ŋan na beo ŋon, te ne wɔlɛɛ ŋon nɛ yela.

eng: Instead, renew the thinking in your mind by the Spirit.
local: Ɛ yela ɛ nyeeya na ɛ konɔɔse man nombia ke yɛɛ wɔlɛɛ.

eng: and clothe yourself with the new person created according to Gods image in justice and true holiness.
local: Ɛ yela Wurubuarɛ dinɔɔ keŋ tenɛɛ, te ka betaŋ ayimɛɛ ya nɛ ke yɛɛ dinɔɔ wɔlɛɛ ɛ man.

eng: Therefore, after you have gotten rid of lying, Each of you must tell the truth to your neighbor because we are parts of each other in the same body.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ tina akɛrɛɛ kedi yela, na ɛ kedi anokoare fa dɔŋa. Nawolo nyi de man walaŋ kamasɛ yɛɛ Kristotena dikpii keŋ yenaŋ daale ke.

eng: Be angry without sinning. Dont let the sun set on your anger.
local: Te akpaa ɛ gyeŋ baŋ na, ɛ na yeli ɛ baŋ keŋ ka mo ŋon gyu dukum man ya. Ɛ na yeli weese ka gyoo na baŋ keŋ sɛɛ doo ɛ man ya. Na mena ya na ɛ ke fa ɔbɔnsam nae daseŋae na waa kɛɛse ŋon kɛɛ.

eng: Thieves should no longer steal. Instead, they should go to work, using their hands to do good so that they will have something to share with whoever is in need.
local: Walaŋ ŋon nafɔ ɔ yɛɛ ŋmɛɛlate e na, waa tina ŋmɛɛleŋ yela, na waa mo ɔ nyiŋmaase yɛɛ tom gbɛɛ kpaakpaa dɔɔ, na waa nyiŋ kolo na waa fa ayematena mɔ.

eng: Dont let any foul words come out of your mouth. Only say what is helpful when it is needed for building up the community so that it benefits those who hear what you say.
local: Ɛ na yeli nɔɔwoya ŋan tekaboena ya nɛ ka lee ɛ nɔɔse man ya, mɔna ɛ yela ɛ kekolosi ka do balaŋ kakyeŋ na baa bee.

eng: Dont make the Holy Spirit of God unhappy—you were sealed by him for the day of redemption.
local: Ɛ na yɛɛ kpene keŋ gyae ka wee Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ya, nawolo nyi ŋon te Wurubuarɛ moowɔ see ŋon nso, keŋ nawolo nyi ɛ yɛɛ ɔ wui. Kei nawola la nyi, wee daale waa kɔŋ kaa lese ŋon na ɛ kenyiŋ ɛ wose.

eng: Put aside all bitterness, losing your temper, anger, shouting, and slander, along with every other evil.
local: Ɛ tina nombia akarankaraŋ kɛŋa pou, ŋgba tɔɔman ŋaŋ na akpanyaŋnyaŋ na baŋ na nyeeman boalaŋ na balaŋ kewɔlɛɛ na kale na balaŋ katee na kelabereketao na dinɔɔ kumɛɛ ŋan pou keyɛɛ yela.

eng: Be kind, compassionate, and forgiving to each other, in the same way God forgave you in Christ.
local: Mɔna ɛ yɛɛ wɔe, na ɛ ke wii fa dɔŋa. Ɛ mo walaŋ kamasɛ dukum kyɛɛ e, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ Kristo dɔɔ mo ɛ nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ ŋon nɛ.

eng: As for children, obey your parents in the Lord, because it is right.
local: Bia, keŋ ɛ yɛɛ Kristotenawɔ dɔɔ kaboena nyi yaa tei ɛ kya na ɛ naa dei. Nawolo nyi ɛ ne yɛɛ kei na, ɛ ne yɛɛ kpene keŋ dei.

eng: The commandment Honor your father and mother is the first one with a promise attached.
local: Tei n kya na n naa dei, nawolo nyi kei yɛna Wurubuarɛ mmaraa gyaŋgbate keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yako nyi n di ke dɔɔ na, nyaŋ kenyiŋ ke tɔnɔɔ.

eng: so that things will go well for you, and you will live for a long time in the land .
local: Tɔnɔɔ kenaŋ yɛna nyi akpaa n di mmaraa kei dɔɔ na, kpene kamasɛ gyae ke de fa neŋ na n kenyiŋ kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ kyare.

eng: As for parents, dont provoke your children to anger, but raise them with discipline and instruction about the Lord.
local: Kyaɔ, ɛ na do ɛ bia baŋ lee kpene keŋ ɛ ne yɛɛ wɔ nɛ dɔɔ ya. Mɔna kaboena nyi yaa kɛɛ be dɔɔ na ɛ ka wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia na baa di ŋe dɔɔ.

eng: As for slaves, obey your human masters with fear and trembling and with sincere devotion to Christ.
local: Mee kolosi mɛɛ fa ɛmɛɛ gbeŋa mɔ nyi, ɛ tei ɛ gbeŋgyoona baŋ ɛ kyaa be gyaŋ nɛ dei lee ɛ konɔɔse man, ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ ne tei Kristo nɛ.

eng: Dont work to make yourself look good and try to flatter people, but act like slaves of Christ carrying out Gods will from the heart.
local: Ɛ na yako nyi, debaŋ keŋ be kyaa ɛ gyaŋ te bɛɛ kɛɛ ŋon dɔɔ te ɛ ke yɛɛ tom ka gyoo be sia na baa kɛlɛɛ ŋon ya.

eng: Serve your owners enthusiastically, as though you were serving the Lord and not human beings.
local: Ɛ na yɛɛ ɛ tomse ŋgba denibalaŋ yaa ɛ ne yɛɛ fa nɛ ya, mɔna ɛ yɛɛ ŋa na gyoŋ ŋgba ɛ Gbeŋgyoo Yesu yaa ɛ ne yɛɛ fa nɛ.

eng: You know that the Lord will reward every person who does what is right, whether that person is a slave or a free person.
local: Nyi n yɛɛ gbeŋi o, n yɛɛ gyooro bu e o, ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi, Wurubuarɛ waa tɔ walaŋ kamasɛ kom, lee ɔ tom kpaakpaa keŋ ɔ ne yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: As for masters, treat your slaves in the same way. Stop threatening them, because you know that both you and your slaves have a master in heaven. He doesnt distinguish between people on the basis of status.
local: Ɛmɛɛ gbeŋgyoona baŋ mɔ, ɛ tela ɛ gbeŋa ŋgba mena keŋ kaboena nɛ, ɛ naa ŋmaase wɔ ya. Ɛ tɔɔse nyi, ɛmɛɛ na ɛ gbeŋa baŋ pou yɛɛ ɛ Gbeŋgyoo ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ wui. Ŋon ne diina nombia keŋ ɔ bɛɛ kɛɛ ŋolo siaman ya.

eng: Finally, be strengthened by the Lord and his powerful strength.
local: Pɛ me nɔɔwoya kesi na, me nɔɔwoya akaŋ yɛna nyi, ɛ seŋ de Gbeŋgyoo wɔɔle dinaa keŋ man keŋkeŋ.

eng: Put on Gods armor so that you can make a stand against the tricks of the devil.
local: Ɛ mo Wurubuarɛ yoo abɔɔ ŋan pou do ŋgba yoo kegbase nɛ, na ɛ ke tale di ɔbɔnsam ŋon adao gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ ŋan pou dɔɔ.

eng: We arent fighting against human enemies but against rulers, authorities, forces of cosmic darkness, and spiritual powers of evil in the heavens.
local: Nawolo nyi de yoo keŋ dee yoo nɛ, te yɛɛ denibalaŋ yaa dɛɛ yoo na ya. Mɔna dee yoo bo na feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan ŋee di doŋ ŋɔma man nɛ, na feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan ŋee di doŋ balaŋ dɔɔ, te bɛɛ som ŋa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ. Feliŋkumɛɛ kɛŋa ŋaale yɛna ŋgba woya, na bɛɛneŋ, na yola na ŋmeeseŋ, na gyabunise na gaose na doŋse ŋan pou ŋe yɛɛ feliŋkumɛɛ ŋa, te ŋe kyaa ditiŋtɛɛ man adido na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ keŋ de bɛɛ mo de sia fonɛɛ naa ŋa ya nɛ.

eng: Therefore, pick up the full armor of God so that you can stand your ground on the evil day and after you have done everything possible to still stand.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ do Wurubuarɛ yoo abɔɔ ŋan pou, na ɛ kenyiŋ tale seŋ keŋkeŋ ŋmanyaŋ ɔbɔnsam ŋon nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ debaŋ kei man. Te akpaa ɛ yoo keŋ de taŋ na, baa naa nyi ɛ sɛɛ ɛ seŋɛɛ ɛ da seŋae keŋ man.

eng: So stand with the belt of truth around your waist, justice as your breastplate.
local: Keŋ dɔɔ ɛ desina ɛ wose na ɛ ka seŋ keŋkeŋ. Ɛ yela anokoare keŋ ɛ ne dii fa Wurubuarɛ nɛ ke yɛɛ ŋgba abɔsɔɔ nɛ, na ɛ ka mo kpaŋ ɛ teŋase man, na ɛ gbɛɛneŋ katenee mɔ ke yɛɛ ŋgba nyeele pampasaŋ keŋ yoonɔɔtena na mo gyee be konɔɔ nɛ.

eng: and put shoes on your feet so that you are ready to spread the good news of peace.
local: Te kegyae keŋ ɛ ne gyae nyi ɛ ke yako Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ne fa balaŋ wosefɛɛreŋ nɛ, ke yɛɛ ŋgba nkyokotase ŋan ɛ na doo nɛ.

eng: Above all, carry the shield of faith so that you can extinguish the flaming arrows of the evil one.
local: Kɛŋa pou wɔle nɛ, ɛ yela ɛ kelɛɛdi ke yɛɛ ŋgba nyeele pampasaŋ keŋ nɛ. Kei te ɛ ke tale mo lɛɛ ɔbɔnsam ŋon apiase ŋan ŋe nɔɔse dana boalaŋ nɛ.

eng: Take the helmet of salvation and the sword of the Spirit, which is Gods word.
local: Ɛ yela nyeekelɛɛ keŋ Kristo be lɛɛ ŋon nɛ, ke yɛɛ ŋgba nyeele kotɔtɔɔ keŋ yoonɔɔtena na mo wuu lɛɛ ba nyeeya nɛ. Na ɛ ke yela Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan, ke yɛɛ ŋgba yoosila keŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ne fa ŋon nyi yaa mo tela gyu yoo nɛ.

eng: Offer prayers and petitions in the Spirit all the time. Stay alert by hanging in there and praying for all believers.
local: Ɛ mo kefane yɛɛ abɔɔ kɛŋa pou. Ɛ sola Wurubuarɛ na waa fa ŋon doŋ. Ɛ fane debaŋ kamasɛ, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon na wola ŋon nɛ. Lee nnɛ dɔɔ ɛ doo ɛ wose dɔɔ nideli, ɛ na yeli ɛ wose keyɔkɔse ŋon ya. Na ɛ ke fane debaŋ kamasɛ fa Wurubuarɛ balaŋ pou.

eng: As for me, pray that when I open my mouth, Ill get a message that confidently makes this secret plan of the gospel known.
local: Ɛ fane fa maŋ mɔ, nyi me na yee ya. Te me gyu mɛɛ yako Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan na, Wurubuarɛ gbagba ka mo ɔ nɔɔwoya ŋan do me nɔɔman, na maa tale lese nombia aweeseŋ ŋan doo Wurubuarɛ nombia man nɛ wola.

eng: Im an ambassador in chains for the sake of the gospel. Pray so that the Lord will give me the confidence to say what I have to say.
local: Lee mena keŋ ma seŋɛɛ fa nombia kɛŋa dɔɔ, te be kyaŋa maŋ do deni man. Ɛ fane fa maŋ, na maa gyɔ me konɔɔ yako nombia ŋan ŋgba mena keŋ kaboena nyi maa yako nɛ.

eng: Tychicus, my loved brother and faithful servant of the Lord, can inform you about my situation and what Im doing.
local: Mee kpila Tikikuse, de gyoo ŋon dɛɛ gyae ɔ nombia te ɔ ne yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo tom keŋ ke gbɛɛ dɔɔ nɛ ɛ gyaŋ. Ɔ kɔŋ na waa yako ŋon ma wose man nombia pou, na ɛ ka gyeŋ ma kekyaabii man.

eng: Ive sent him for this reason—so that you will know about us. He can reassure you.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ te mee kpila e ɛ gyaŋ, na waa yako ŋon da kekyaabii man, na waa do ŋon kakyeŋ mɔ.

eng: May there be peace with the brothers and sisters as well as love with the faith that comes from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: De kya Wurubuarɛ na de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo baa fa ɛmɛɛ de tebia baŋ ɛ silana Kristo nɛ wosefɛɛreŋ. Na baa yela ɛ ke gyae dɔŋa na ɛ ke lɛɛ Kristo di nideli.

eng: May grace be with all those who love our Lord Jesus Christ forever.
local: Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele baŋ pou bɛɛ gyae de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo kegyae keŋ betaŋ ɔto ya nɛ.

eng: From Paul and Timothy, slaves of Christ Jesus. To all those in Philippi who are Gods people in Christ Jesus, along with your supervisors and servants.
local: Tɔne kei lee maŋ Pɔɔl na Timoti baŋ de yɛɛ Yesu Kristo tewulɛɛwɔ nɛ gyaŋ. Dɛɛ ŋmarase ke dee yelee ɛmɛɛ Wurubuarɛ balaŋ baŋ pou ɛmɛɛ na Yesu Kristo be yɛɛ dokoloŋ, te ɛ kyaa Filipi donɔɔ man nɛ na Kristotena kegyiise na baŋ pou bɛɛ kyɔ wɔ nɛ.

eng: May the grace and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
local: De kya Wurubuarɛ na de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo baa mo be bɛrɛɛ boele ŋon na baa fa ŋon wosefɛɛreŋ.

eng: I thank my God every time I mention you in my prayers.
local: Debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ me tɔɔse ŋon na, mɛɛ fane fa me Wurubuarɛ karaŋ lee ɛ dɔɔ.

eng: Im thankful for all of you every time I pray, and its always a prayer full of joy.
local: Mena mɔ te debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ mɛɛ fane fa ŋon na mɛɛ mo gyoŋ fane.

eng: Im glad because of the way you have been my partners in the ministry of the gospel from the time you first believed it until now.
local: Nawolo nyi lee debaŋ keŋ ɛ be lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan di nɛ, te ɛ gyakaa ɛ ne kyɔ maŋ lee nombia ŋenaŋ kekolosi man kaa lii naŋ gyɛŋ.

eng: Im sure about this: the one who started a good work in you will stay with you to complete the job by the day of Christ Jesus.
local: Te mɛɛ lɛɛ di nideli nyi Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be fi tom kpaakpaa kei asɛɛ ɛ man nɛ, waa yɛɛ ke tɔɔle kelii wee keŋ Yesu Kristo waa bese kɔŋ nɛ.

eng: I have good reason to think this way about all of you because I keep you in my heart. You are all my partners in Gods grace, both during my time in prison and in the defense and support of the gospel.
local: Mɛɛ gyae ŋon nideli lee me konɔɔ man. Mena dɔɔ ma gyeŋ nyi ke dei nyi maa gyueŋ ɛ wose man ŋgba mena keŋ mɛɛ gyueŋ nɛ. Nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ bɔɔ kyaŋ maŋ tɔ deni, na debaŋ keŋ mee yoo lee Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan dɔɔ, na debaŋ keŋ mɛɛ lese Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋenaŋ man wola balaŋ nyi ŋe yɛɛ ampaŋ nɛ, ɛ popou kyɔɔ maŋ lee tom keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo boele maŋ nɛ keyɛɛ man.

eng: God is my witness that I feel affection for all of you with the compassion of Christ Jesus.
local: Wurubuarɛ gbagba gyeŋ nyi ɛ nombia doo me loo man, te mɛɛ gyae ŋon nideli ŋgba mena keŋ Yesu Kristo ne gyae daa nɛ.

eng: This is my prayer: that your love might become even more and more rich with knowledge and all kinds of insight.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ mɛɛ fane fa ŋon nyiaa, ɛ balaŋ kegyae keŋ dɛɛ bee kpu nideli na ɛ ke nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan man, na ɛ ka gyeŋ kpene keŋ kaboena nyi yaa yɛɛ lee ɛ kekyaabii man.

eng: I pray this so that you will be able to decide what really matters and so you will be sincere and blameless on the day of Christ.
local: Kei gyae ke yela ɛ ke tale gyeŋ kpene keŋ dei nɛ nideli, na ɛ ke kyaa keŋ nombiikum kamasɛ yaa ayimɛɛ kamasɛ be doo ɛ wose man ya, kelii wee keŋ Kristo waa bese kɔŋ.

eng: I pray that you will then be filled with the fruit of righteousness, which comes from Jesus Christ, in order to give glory and praise to God.
local: Mɛɛ fane mɔ nyi Yesu Kristo waa desina ŋon na ɛ ke tale yɛɛ abɔɔ ŋan ŋa tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ, na ke yela balaŋ ka mo obuo fa Wurubuarɛ na baa lese ɔ yele.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to know that the things that have happened to me have actually advanced the gospel.
local: Me tebia, mɛɛ gyae ɛ ka gyeŋ nyi nombia ŋan be tuŋ maŋ nɛ gba te yeli Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan te yaasee.

eng: The whole Praetorian Guard and everyone else knows that Im in prison for Christ.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ yoonɔɔtena baŋ bɛɛ deke gyoo dɛɛ, na balaŋ akaŋ baŋ pou te nyii nyi keŋ mɛɛ som Kristo dɔɔ te be kyaŋa maŋ tɔ deni nɛ.

eng: Most of the brothers and sisters have had more confidence through the Lord to speak the word boldly and bravely because of my jail time.
local: Ba ke kyaŋ maŋ tɔ deni keŋ ta do me tebia Kristotena burum kakyeŋ ba kelɛɛdi man. Ka te yeli bɛɛ gyɔ be konɔɔ kolosi nyeekelɛɛ nombia ŋan keŋ bɔɔ ne yee kolo bela ya.

eng: Some certainly preach Christ with jealous and competitive motives, but others preach with good motives.
local: Ma gyeŋ nyi sibii na daaworo dɔɔ te balaŋ baale dɔŋɛɛ bee kolosi Kristo nombia ŋan. Mɔna baale mɔ na, baŋ ne kolosi nombia ŋan bo lee be konɔɔ kpaakpaa man.

eng: They are motivated by love, because they know that Im put here to give a defense of the gospel.
local: Mena balaŋ wɔle nyibaŋ kɛwɔ dɔɔ, keŋ bɛɛ gyae maŋ dɔɔ te bee kolosi nyeekelɛɛ nombia ŋan. Nawolo nyi ba gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ lese na maŋ, na maa nyiŋ lese wola balaŋ nyi ɔ nyeedoŋ nombia ŋan yɛɛ ampaŋ.

eng: the others preach Christ because of their selfish ambition. They are insincere, hoping to cause me more pain while Im in prison.
local: Balaŋ gyaŋgba nyibaŋ baŋ dɔɔ, baŋ gbagba deeli dɔɔ te bee kolosi Kristo nombia ŋan. Bɔɔ na lese be konɔɔ man kolosi nyeekelɛɛ nombia ŋan ya, te ba gyeŋ nyi be yɛɛ mena na baa tale yela diyem keŋ mɛɛ naa nɛ, ka bo kpu debaŋ keŋ me doo deni man nɛ.

eng: What do I think about this? Just this: since Christ is proclaimed in every possible way, whether from dishonest or true motives, Im glad and Ill continue to be glad.
local: Mɔna maŋ dɔɔ kei bee hia maŋ ya. Kpene keŋ ne hia maŋ yɛna nyi bee kolosi Kristo nombia ŋan. Nyi ba moo konɔɔ kpaakpaa bo kolosi ŋa o, be ta mo konɔɔ kpaakpaa kolosi ŋa ya o nɛ, gbɛɛ kamasɛ man na bee kolosi Kristo nombia ŋan. Kei dɔɔ te dana maŋ gyoŋ nɛ, te gyae ke yɛɛ maŋ gyoŋ menɛɛ kelii ɔto.

eng: Im glad because I know that this will result in my release through your prayers and the help of the Spirit of Jesus Christ.
local: Nawolo nyi ma gyeŋ nyi ɛ ne fane fa maŋ, te Yesu Kristo feliŋ ŋon mɔ ne kyɔ maŋ. Mena dɔɔ ma gyeŋ nyi kɛŋa pou gyae ke yela maa kaa nyiŋ ma wose.

eng: It is my expectation and hope that I wont be put to shame in anything. Rather, I hope with daring courage that Christs greatness will be seen in my body, now as always, whether I live or die.
local: Mɛɛ gyae me konɔɔ man nideli te me loo mɔ gyakaa nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ be gyae waa yela desɛŋ ke kyaŋ maŋ lee gbɛɛ kamasɛ man ya. Waa yela maa gyɔ me konɔɔ kolosi ŋgba mena keŋ mɛɛ taŋ yɛɛ nɛ, na nyi me kyaa na me sia bo o, yaa ma yeŋae o nɛ, na maa nyiŋ lese Kristo yele lee ma kekyaabii man.

eng: Because for me, living serves Christ and dying is even better.
local: Maŋ dɔɔ, akpaa me kyaa na me kyaa bo fa Kristo. Te akpaa ma yem mɔ na, ke yɛɛ tɔnɔɔ ke fa maŋ.

eng: If I continue to live in this world, I get results from my work.
local: Akpaa me sɛɛ me kyaa nyeedoŋ man na, ke yɛɛ gbɛɛ daale ke fa maŋ na maa nyiŋ yɛɛ tom kpaakpaa fa Wurubuarɛ. Mena dɔɔ mɔɔ gyeŋ keŋ maa lese ya. Nyi maa lese nyeedoŋ bo o, yaa maa lese yeŋ bo o, mɔɔ gyeŋ ya.

eng: But I dont know what I prefer. Im torn between the two because I want to leave this life and be with Christ, which is far better.
local: Ma te dɔɔ abɔɔ ala kɛŋa nsana. Nafɔ ma kegyae yɛna nyi maa yeŋ lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ na me kaa kyaa Kristo gyaŋ adido, nawolo nyi keŋ dei kela ma kekyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ.

eng: However, its more important for me to stay in this world for your sake.
local: Mɔna lee ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ, dei nyi maa kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ na maa nyiŋ kyɔ ŋon.

eng: Im sure of this: I will stay alive and remain with all of you to help your progress and the joy of your faith.
local: Mɛɛ lɛɛ di nideli nyi daa na ŋon pou dɛɛ kyaa, na maa nyiŋ kyɔ ŋon na ɛ ka bee ɛ kelɛɛdi man na ɛ ke nyiŋ di gyoŋ lee ɛ Wurubuarɛ kelɛɛdi man.

eng: and to increase your pride in Christ Jesus through my presence when I visit you again.
local: Kei gyae ke yela nyi akpaa ma bese kaa kyaa na ŋon na, lee maŋ dɔɔ gyoŋ keŋ ɛ ne dii lee Yesu Kristo dɔɔ nɛ, gyae ka bese gbaa.

eng: Most important, live together in a manner worthy of Christs gospel. Do this, whether I come and see you or Im absent and hear about you. Do this so that you stand firm, united in one spirit and mind as you struggle together to remain faithful to the gospel.
local: Nɛnɛɛ kpene keŋ be kaa ɛ ke yɛɛ yɛna nyiaa, ɛ yela ɛ kekyaabii ke kyaa na mena keŋ nyeekelɛɛ nombia ŋan nawolo nɛ. Akpaa ɛ yɛɛ mena nɛ, kenaŋ na nyi me kyaa ɛ gyaŋ yaa mɔɔ kyaa ɛ gyaŋ koraŋ ya na, maa nyii nyi ɛ dana Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan keŋkeŋ, te ɛ seŋɛɛ na ɛ tebia nɔɔdokoloŋ na ɛ nombia ayɛɛsa pou man, te ɛ ta mo nɔɔdokoloŋ ɛ ne kyɔ balaŋ na baa lɛɛ Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan di.

eng: That way, you wont be afraid of anything your enemies do. Your faithfulness and courage are a sign of their coming destruction and your salvation, which is from God.
local: Te maa nyii mɔ nyi ɛ bee yee balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ koro seŋ tia ŋon nɛ lee gbɛɛ kamasɛ man ya. Ɛ yɛɛ mena na gyae ka lese wola wɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ waa wɔlɛɛ wɔ, mɔna ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ ɛ nyee.

eng: God has generously granted you the privilege, not only of believing in Christ but also of suffering for Christs sake.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ te fa ŋon gbɛɛ nyi yaa lɛɛ Kristo di kaageŋ bo ya, mɔna ɔ faa ŋon gbɛɛ nyi lee ŋon Kristo dɔɔ yaa naa diyem mɔ.

eng: You are having the same struggle that you saw me face and now hear that Im still facing.
local: Ɛ naa diyem keŋ mɔɔ naa debaŋ keŋ me kyaa ɛ gyaŋ nɛ, te ɛ ne nyii mena keŋ me gyakaa mɛɛ naa diyem nɛ. Mena diyem kenaŋ ŋaale yaa ɛmɛɛ mɔ ne naa nɛ.

eng: Therefore, if there is any encouragement in Christ, any comfort in love, any sharing in the Spirit, any sympathy.
local: Lee nɔɔdokoloŋ keŋ ɛmɛɛ na Kristo be yɛɛ ɛ kekyaabii man dɔɔ ɔ na do ŋon doŋ, te ɔ balaŋ kegyae keŋ mɔ na do ŋon kakyeŋ. Mena mɔ te lee nɔɔdokoloŋ keŋ doo ɛmɛɛ na Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon man dɔɔ, ne yeli ɛmɛɛ na ɛ tebia ne yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ te ɛ ne yɛɛ deeli fa dɔŋa te ɛ ne wii dɔŋa waraŋase.

eng: complete my joy by thinking the same way, having the same love, being united, and agreeing with each other.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ yela ya agyueŋ ke yɛɛ dokoloŋ, na ɛ ke gyae dɔŋa kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Ɛ yela ɛmɛɛ na ɛ tebia pou ke yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ na ɛ ka seŋ nombii dokoloŋ dɔɔ. Akpaa ɛ yɛɛ mena na, ɛ ke yela gyoŋ keŋ doo me man nɛ ka bo kpu.

eng: Dont do anything for selfish purposes, but with humility think of others as better than yourselves.
local: Ɛ na yɛɛ sibiikum ya, te ɛ na wola ɛ wose lee gbɛɛ kamasɛ man ya. Mɔna ɛ yɔkɔse ɛ wose fa dɔŋa, na ɛ ke bu ɛ tebia nyi ba kela ŋon.

eng: Instead of each person watching out for their own good, watch out for what is better for others.
local: Ɛ na yeli ɛ sia keyɛɛ ɛmɛɛ yaageŋ wui abɔɔ dɔɔ ya, mɔna ɛ gyae ɛ tebia mɔ deeli.

eng: Adopt the attitude that was in Christ Jesus.
local: Ɛ yela ɛ dinɔɔ ke kyaa na Yesu Kristo wui.

eng: Though he was in the form of God, he did not consider being equal with God something to exploit.
local: Ɛ tɔɔse nyiaa, Yesu na Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ dokoloŋ lee gbɛɛ kamasɛ man. Mɔna ɔ te kɛɛ ɔ wose nyi ŋon na Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ kyɛɛkyɛɛ ya.

eng: But he emptied himself by taking the form of a slave and by becoming like human beings. When he found himself in the form of a human.
local: Ɔ te kɛɛ ɔ wose nyi ɔ yɛɛ ŋolo e ya. Mɔna ɔ moo ɔ wose yɛɛ ŋgba gbeŋi nɛ, te ɔ yela ba lola e ŋgba deniwalaŋ nɛ.

eng: he humbled himself by becoming obedient to the point of death, even death on a cross.
local: Be ka lola e ŋgba deniwalaŋ nɛ, ɔ yɔkɔse ɔ wose te ɔ nyii Wurubuarɛ dei kelii ɔ yeŋ man. Yeŋ keŋ ɔ ba yem gba nɛ, yɛɛ keŋ bɔɔ gyɔ e mataa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ ŋgba dukumyɛɛre nɛ.

eng: Therefore, God highly honored him and gave him a name above all names.
local: Mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ gyina e adido adido te ɔ faa e gyooro dekyae, te ɔ fa e yele keŋ kela yele kamasɛ.

eng: so that at the name of Jesus everyone in heaven, on earth, and under the earth might bow.
local: Lee mena yele kenaŋ dɔɔ, balaŋ na kpene kamasɛ keŋ kyaa adido na ŋan pou ŋe kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, na ŋan pou ŋe doo tɛɛle kei ateta nɛ gyae ke buŋ som Yesu.

eng: and every tongue confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.
local: Mena ke te walaŋ kamasɛ waa gyuusu ɔ nɔɔ yako nyi Yesu Kristo yɛna de Gbeŋgyoo. Kei gyae ke yela balaŋ ka lese de kya Wurubuarɛ yele.

eng: Therefore, my loved ones, just as you always obey me, not just when I am present but now even more while I am away, carry out your own salvation with fear and trembling.
local: Mena dɔɔ me tebia kpaakpaa, debaŋ keŋ me kyaa ɛ gyaŋ na ɛ ne di nyeekelɛɛ nombia ŋan dɔɔ. Mena mɔ yaa mɛɛ gyae nyi nɛnɛɛ keŋ mɔɔ kyaa ɛ gyaŋ ya nɛ, ɛ kyaŋ ɛ wose na ɛ ke di ŋe dɔɔ kela gyaŋgba keŋ. Ɛ mo Wurubuarɛ keyee deke ɛ wose, na ɛ ke sila nyeekelɛɛ nombia ŋan nideli.

eng: God is the one who enables you both to want and to actually live out his good purposes.
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ ne yɛɛ na tom ɛ man, mena dɔɔ te ɔ ne yeli ɛ ne gyae nyi ɛ ke yɛɛ ɔ kegyaebii te ŋon mɔ na doo na ŋon kakyeŋ te ɛ ne tale yɛɛ ɔ kegyaebii.

eng: Do everything without grumbling and arguing.
local: Kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ ne yɛɛ na ɛ na toroŋ ya, te ɛ na mo gyoo dɔŋa mɔ ya.

eng: so that you may be blameless and pure, innocent children of God surrounded by people who are crooked and corrupt. Among these people you shine like stars in the world.
local: Ɛ yɛɛ mena na ayimɛɛ kamasɛ be gyae ke doo ɛ wose man ya, te ŋolo mɔ be gyae waa nyiŋ nombiikum daale yako lee ɛ wose man ya. Na ɛ ke nyiŋ bese Wurubuarɛ bia baŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa balaŋ kumɛɛ man. Ɛ yela ɛ nombia kpaakpaa keyɛɛ ka lee debɔɔ be man, ŋgba mena keŋ kyolobiise ne ŋalakee ditiŋtɛɛ man nɛ.

eng: because you hold on to the word of life. This will allow me to say on the day of Christ that I havent run for nothing or worked for nothing.
local: Ɛ kyaŋ nyeedoŋ nombia ŋan man nideli. Akpaa ɛ yɛɛ mena na, lee ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ maa tale gyɔ me konɔɔ wee keŋ Kristo waa kɔŋ nɛ, na ka wola nyi me kaale na ma tom keŋ mɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ pou te yɛɛ yakaa ya.

eng: But even if I am poured out like a drink offering upon the altar of service for your faith, I am glad. Im glad with all of you.
local: N te kɛɛ ya na baa ko maŋ. Nyi de kɔŋ mena na maa yɛɛ ŋgba wonembu ŋon ɛ ba mo deesi fa Wurubuarɛ lee mena keŋ ɛ be lɛɛ e di dɔɔ nɛ. Dekɔŋ mena na gyae ke yɛɛ maŋ gyoŋ, na ke yela de popou ke nyaŋee.

eng: You should be glad about this in the same way. Be glad with me!.
local: Mena ke te kaboena nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ ɛmɛɛ mɔ gyoŋ, na daa na ŋon pou ke kyaa gyoŋ man.

eng: I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to see you soon so that I may be encouraged by hearing about you.
local: Me loo gyakaa de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu dɔɔ nyi ɔ fa maŋ gbɛɛ na, be gyae ke kyare ya maa kpila Timoti ɛ gyaŋ. Na ke yela maa nyii ɛ kekyaabii man, na ɛmɛɛ mɔ ke nyii ma kekyaabii man na ka do maŋ kakyeŋ.

eng: I have no one like him. He is a person who genuinely cares about your well-being.
local: Ma be dana ŋolo keŋ ɔ na gyueŋ ɛ deeli dɔɔ kpu na ŋon Timoti ya.

eng: All the others put their own business ahead of Jesus Christs business.
local: Nawolo nyi balaŋ akaŋ baŋ pou dɔɔ, be sia gyakaa baŋ gbagba abɔɔ dɔɔ bo. Bɔɔ na gyueŋ Yesu Kristo nombia ŋan dɔɔ ya.

eng: You know his character, how he labors with me for the gospel like a son works with his father.
local: Mɔna ɛ gyeŋ nyi Timoti ta lese wola nyi, ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon n ke tale mo n wose lɔ ɔ dɔɔ. Nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ mee kolosi Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan nɛ, ɔ seŋawɔ me gyaŋ ŋgba mena keŋ bu na sem ɔ kya gyaŋ nɛ.

eng: So he is the one that I hope to send as soon as I find out how things turn out here for me.
local: Mena dɔɔ akpaa ma betaa naa mena keŋ ma kekyaabii man doo na, maa kpila e ɛ gyaŋ.

eng: I trust in the Lord that I also will visit you soon.
local: Mena mɔ te me loo gyakaa nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu de sɛɛ na, be gyae ke kyare ya na maŋ gbagba maa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ.

eng: I think it is also necessary to send Epaphroditus to you. He is my brother, coworker, and fellow soldier; and he is your representative who serves my needs.
local: Mɔna me naawɔ nyi kaboena nyi maa kpila me dɔɔ Kristo baale Epaferadituse ŋon daa na e ne yɛɛ tom dokoloŋ, te ɛ be kpila e nyi waa kɔŋ kaa kyɔ maŋ nɛ nyi waa bese kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ. Daa na e ne yɛɛ na tom dokoloŋ, te dee yoo na nyeedoŋ nombia ŋan keyako ŋgba yoonɔɔtena nɛ.

eng: He misses you all, and he was upset because you heard he was sick.
local: Ɔ ne gyae lee ɔ konɔɔ man nyi waa naa ɛ popou. Nawolo nyi ɛ ke nyii nyi ɔ na wee nɛ ke takaa e nideli.

eng: In fact, he was so sick that he nearly died. But God had mercy on him—and not just on him but also on me, because his death would have caused me great sorrow.
local: Kawee gbagba na ɔ weewɔ keŋ koraŋ ɔ yela waa yeŋ, mɔna Wurubuarɛ wii ɔ waraŋase. Na ŋon waageŋ waraŋase te Wurubuarɛ wiiwɔ ya, mɔna ɔ wii maŋ gbagba mɔ waraŋase. Na mena ya na nafɔ meŋ yeyɛɛwɔ nideli.

eng: Therefore, I am sending him immediately so that when you see him again you can be glad and I wont worry.
local: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ gyae nyi waa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ bileŋ, na ɛ naa e na ke yɛɛ ŋon gyoŋ na maŋ mɔ wɔe ke fɛɛ maŋ.

eng: So welcome him in the Lord with great joy and show great respect for people like him.
local: Nyi akpaa ɔ kɔŋ na ɛ lɛɛ e gyoŋ man, nawolo nyi de popou yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo balaŋwɔ. Ɛ mo obuo fa mena balaŋ kɛwɔ dinɔɔ.

eng: He risked his life and almost died for the work of Christ, and he did this to make up for the help you couldnt give me.
local: Nawolo nyi Kristo tom keŋ dɔɔ te ɔ yela waa yeŋ nɛ. Ŋon moona ɔ wose fa yeŋ kaa seŋ ɛ nawɔɔ man kyɔ maŋ tom keŋ keyɛɛ man.

eng: So then, my brothers and sisters, be glad in the Lord. Its no trouble for me to repeat the same things to you because they will help keep you on track.
local: Me tebia, nombii keŋ be kaa maa yako ŋon yɛna nyi ɛ yela ke yɛɛ ŋon gyoŋ lee kpene keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ne yɛɛ fa ŋon nɛ dɔɔ. Maŋ dɔɔ bɛɛ takaa maŋ nyi me ta bese mɛɛ ŋmarase ŋon nombia ŋan mɔɔ wulaa ŋmarase ŋon kaalaŋ nɛ ya. Nawolo nyi ma gyeŋ nyi nombia ŋenaŋ gyae ke kyɔ ŋon, na ɛ ke tale seŋ keŋkeŋ ɛ kelɛɛdi man.

eng: Watch out for the dogs. Watch out for people who do evil things. Watch out for those who insist on circumcision, which is really mutilation.
local: Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose man nideli na balaŋ baŋ be dana nombiakumɛɛ te bɛɛ gyae baa wɔlɛɛ ɛ kelɛɛdi, te bɛɛ ŋere balaŋ nyi baa kara be baala wose nɛ.

eng: We are the circumcision. We are the ones who serve by Gods Spirit and who boast in Christ Jesus. We dont put our confidence in rituals performed on the body.
local: Daa yɛna balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ kara de baala wose gbagba. Nawolo nyi daa ne tɛɛ bo Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon dɔɔ som Wurubuarɛ. Lee Yesu Kristo mɔ dɔɔ dɛɛ gyɔ de konɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ bɛɛ tekaa hia nyi dɛɛ mo de sia gyakaa baala wose kekara keŋ bɛɛ mo nyiŋmaa kara nɛ nombia dɔɔ ya.

eng: though I have good reason to have this kind of confidence. If anyone else has reason to put their confidence in physical advantages, I have even more.
local: Nafɔ maŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋon kaboena nyi maa wola ma wose lee mena abɔɔ kɛŋa dinɔɔ man. Akpaa nyi ŋolo dana kpene keŋ dɔɔ te waa tale mo ɔ wose lɔ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa dinɔɔ dɔɔ na, nafɔ ŋolo be kyaa me wɔle ya.

eng: I was circumcised on the eighth day. I am from the people of Israel and the tribe of Benjamin. I am a Hebrew of the Hebrews. With respect to observing the Law, Im a Pharisee.
local: Kpene keŋ dɔɔ te mɛɛ yako mena yɛna nyiaa, be ka lola maŋ weeya gyanaarate na te be kara maŋ me baala wose. Me yɛɛ Iserae bu gbagba e ŋon mɔɔ lee Gyekɔpo bu Bɛngyamɛn dekpaŋalaŋ man. Me naanaɔ yɛɛ Hiburutenawɔ mena dɔɔ maŋ gbagba mɔ me yɛɛ Hiburutena fatabo man walaŋ e. Nyi akpaa de kɛɛ Gyudatena mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ kedi na maa tale yako nyi me yɛɛ Farasii baale e, nawolo nyi me dii ba mmaraase ŋan pou dɔɔ nideli.

eng: With respect to devotion to the faith, I harassed the church. With respect to righteousness under the Law, Im blameless.
local: Ma doo kakyeŋ Gyudatena ɔsom keŋ man nideli, te me naase Kristo silala baŋ diyem nideli. Akpaa nyi nafɔ kaboena bo nyi walaŋ waa di mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ pɛna Wurubuarɛ ke lɛɛ e do na, nafɔ maŋ dɔɔ nombiikum daale be doo ma wose man ya.

eng: These things were my assets, but I wrote them off as a loss for the sake of Christ.
local: Abɔɔ ŋan pou nafɔ ŋe dana tɔnɔɔ fa maŋ nɛ, nɛnɛɛ lee Kristo dɔɔ ŋɔɔ taŋ tɔnɔɔ fa maŋ bela ya.
