eng: I, Paul, will pay it back to you . Of course, I wont mention that you owe me your life.
local: Maŋ Pɔɔl gbagba moona me nyiŋmaa mɛɛ ŋmarase mena nombii kei nɛ. Maa tɔ neŋ kom keŋ ɔ dana neŋ nɛ, mɔna na wolee nyi nyaŋ gbagba koraŋ dana maŋ kom ya. Nawolo nyi maŋ yele la te n nyiŋa nyeedoŋ wɔle Kristo man. Kei na wolo baa nyi n dibiliŋ koraŋ yɛɛ me wui.

eng: Yes, brother, I want this favor from you in the Lord! Refresh my heart in Christ.
local: Mena dɔɔ me dɔɔ kpaakpaa yɛɛ nombii kei fa maŋ lee de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ yele man. Kristo dɔɔ yɛɛ ke fa maŋ, na n ke yela me wɔe ke fɛɛ maŋ.

eng: Im writing to you, confident of your obedience and knowing that you will do more than what I ask.
local: Mɛɛ lɛɛdi nyi me tɔne kei gyae ke yela n ke yɛɛ kpene keŋ mɔɔ yako neŋ nɛ, te ma gyeŋ nyi nyaŋ ke yɛɛ gba kela mena.

eng: Also, one more thing—prepare a guest room for me. I hope that I will be released from prison to be with you because of your prayers.
local: Mɛɛ ŋmarase neŋ tɔne kei nɛ, mɛɛ gyae nyi n ke desina dekyae see maŋ nawolo nyi ma gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ waa nyii ɛ kefane keŋ pou, na waa besena maŋ kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ.

eng: Epaphras, who is in prison with me for the cause of Christ Jesus, greets you.
local: Epafirase ŋon lee Kristo Yesu dɔɔ te bɔɔ kyaŋ daa na e tɔ deni nɛ, na do neŋ nɔɔ.

eng: as well as my coworkers Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke.
local: Mena ke te Maake na Arisitakuse na Demase na Luka baŋ daa na wɔ ne yɛɛ tom dokoloŋ nɛ mɔ na do neŋ nɔɔ.

eng: May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
local: De Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo waa mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele ɛ popou na waa seŋ ɛ wɔle.

eng: In the past, God spoke through the prophets to our ancestors in many times and many ways.
local: Gyaŋgba gyaŋgba keŋ na, Wurubuarɛ tɛɛwɔ wa akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra dɔɔ kolosi agbaatee agbaatee fa de naanaɔ lee gbɛɛneŋ kpookpoo man.

eng: In these final days, though, he spoke to us through a Son. God made his Son the heir of everything and created the world through him.
local: Mɔna debaŋ kei man keŋ tɛɛle kei ne gyu kɔ ɔto nɛ, ɔ tɛe ɔ bu Yesu dɔɔ kolosi fa daa. Ɔ bu ŋonaŋ dɔɔ te ɔ tɛɛwɔ yɛɛ tɛɛle kei pou. Ŋon te Wurubuarɛ lesewɔ see nyi tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ pou dee doo ɔ tɛɛ.

eng: The Son is the light of Gods glory and the imprint of Gods being. He maintains everything with his powerful message. After he carried out the cleansing of people from their sins, he sat down at the right side of the highest majesty.
local: Ɔ bu ŋonaŋ dɔɔ te ŋon Wurubuarɛ tɛɛwɔ lese ɔ gyoorobiiri wola, te ɔ bu ŋonaŋ mɔ lesena Wurubuarɛ dinɔɔ gbagba keŋ wola. Ɔ bu kei nɔɔwoya ŋan ŋe dana doŋ nɛ, te ɔ moowɔ ɔ dana tɛɛle kei pou. Ɔ ka yeŋ lɛɛ denibalaŋ lee ba dukum man wɔle na, te ɔ gyuuwɔ ke kyaa adido ɔ kya Wurubuarɛ dekpatakpere ŋon dunoluŋ dɔɔ.

eng: And the Son became so much greater than the other messengers, such as angels, that he received a more important title than theirs.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ bu kei kela Wurubuarɛ kpilala pou, te yele keŋ mɔ Wurubuarɛ be fa e nɛ kela Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ wui.

eng: After all, when did God ever say to any of the angels: You are my Son. Today I have become your Father? Or, even, I will be his Father, and he will be my Son? .
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see man nɛ, Wurubuarɛ te yako ta ɔ kpilala baŋ man ŋolo see nyi, N yɛɛ me bu e, te gyɛŋ maŋ yɛna n kya ya. Mena mɔ te ɔ te yako ta be man ŋolo see nyi, Me ta bese n kya, te nyaŋ mɔ n ta bese me bu ya.

eng: But then, when he brought his firstborn into the world, he said, All of Gods angels must worship him. .
local: Mɔna debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne gyae waa kpila ɔ bu kegyia nyi waa kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Kaboena nyi Wurubuarɛ kpilala pou baa som e.

eng: He talks about the angels: Hes the one who uses the spirits for his messengers and who uses flames of fire as ministers. .
local: Te nnɛ te Wurubuarɛ kolosiwɔ lee ɔ kpilala baŋ wose man. Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Maa yela me kpilala ke yɛɛ ŋgba feliŋ nɛ, na maa yela me tewulɛɛ ke yɛɛ ŋgba boalaŋ nɛ.

eng: But he says to his Son, God, your throne is forever and your kingdoms scepter is a rod of justice. .
local: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ yakowɔ lee ɔ bu ŋon wose man nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ, n ke di gyoori kekpaakekpaa, te nyaŋ ke di ke ke gbɛɛ dɔɔ.

eng: You loved righteousness and hated lawless behavior. That is why God, your God, has anointed you more than your companions with the oil of joy. .
local: N ne gyae nombia ŋan ŋe doo na ŋe gbɛɛ, te n ne kɔla nombiakumɛɛ. Mena dɔɔ maŋ n Wurubuarɛ ŋon ta lese neŋ yele kela n tebia pou, te me yela n dii gyoŋ mɔ kela wɔ.

eng: And he says, You, Lord, laid the earths foundations in the beginning, and the heavens are made by your hands. .
local: Wurubuarɛ besewɔ yako nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo, lee kewalaŋ keŋ na nyaŋ see na tɛɛle kei mokɔɔ, te nyaŋ yɛɛna abɔɔ ŋan pou ŋe kyaa adido nɛ.

eng: They will pass away, but you remain. They will all wear out like old clothes. .
local: Adido na ateta pou gyae kaa ŋaalaa, mɔna nyaŋ dɔɔ nyaŋ ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa. Ŋe popou gyae kaa lɔɔree ŋgba kanyaŋ nɛ.

eng: You will fold them up like a coat. They will be changed like a person changes clothes, but you stay the same, and the years of your life wont come to an end. .
local: Nyaŋ ke bɔ adido na ateta ŋgba kanyaŋ nɛ na n ke kyɛɛkee ŋa. Mɔna nyaŋ dɔɔ n kedoobii te n doo, te nyaŋ ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa.

eng: When has he ever said to any of the angels, Sit at my right side until I put your enemies under your feet like a footstool? .
local: Nombii keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba bese yako ɔ bu ŋon yɛna nyi, Kɔŋ kaa kyaa me dunoluŋ dɔɔ, kelii debaŋ keŋ maa yoo di n kɔlala pou dɔɔ. Kpilala baŋ man woŋti te Wurubuarɛ yako e mena nombii kei see?.

eng: Arent all the angels ministering spirits who are sent to serve those who are going to inherit salvation?.
local: N kɛɛ na kpilala baŋ dɔɔ, be yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ tewulɛɛwɔ te be dɔŋ bo feliŋ man. Wurubuarɛ ne kpilii wɔ bo nyi be kaa kyɔ balaŋ baŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ ba nyee wee daale nɛ.

eng: This is why its necessary for us to pay more attention to what we have heard, or else we may drift away from it.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi dɛɛ kyaŋ nombia ŋan dɔɔ nyii nɛ man nideli na dɔɔ nyiŋ yo gbɛɛ ya.

eng: If the message that was spoken by angels was reliable, and every offense and act of disobedience received an appropriate consequence.
local: Nawolo nyi nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ ɔ kpilala dɔɔ kolosi nɛ doo na ŋe gbɛɛ. Mena dɔɔ debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ balaŋ be yɛɛ deŋele doŋ te be te di ɔ nombia ŋan dɔɔ ya na, ɔ wɔŋa ba deŋele ŋgba mena keŋ kaboena nɛ.

eng: how will we escape if we ignore such a great salvation? It was first announced through the Lord, and then it was confirmed by those who heard him.
local: Akpaa nnɛ te doo na, kenaŋ na nyi de bɛɛ nyeekelɛɛ dinaa kei nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ be fa daa debaŋ kei na, sena te dɛɛ yɛɛ na dɛɛ tale lee deŋele kewɔŋ keŋ waa wɔŋ daa nɛ man? De Gbeŋgyoo Yesu gbagba taŋgbɛɛ la yako nyeekelɛɛ nombia kɛŋa fa balaŋ. Kenaŋ wɔle na balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ taŋgbɛɛ nyii ŋa nɛ, mɔ dii nombia kɛŋa adansɛɛ fa daa nyi ŋe yɛɛ ampaŋ.

eng: God also vouched for their message with signs, amazing things, various miracles, and gifts from the Holy Spirit, which were handed out the way he wanted.
local: Wurubuarɛ gbagba mɔ lesewɔ wola nyi nombia ŋan bɔɔ kolosi nɛ doo na ŋe gbɛɛ. Nawolo nyi ɔ faa wɔ doŋ te be yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana, te ɔ moo ɔ feliŋ ŋon abɔɔ ŋan ɔ na mo boele balaŋ nɛ boele wɔ ɔ kegyaebii man.

eng: God didnt put the world that is coming under the angels control.
local: Tɛɛle wɔle keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ wee daale te dee kolosi ka wose man nombia nɛ, Wurubuarɛ te yako nyi waa mo ke do ɔ kpilala baŋ nyiŋmaa man bo ya.

eng: Instead, someone declared somewhere, What is humanity that you think about them? Or what are human beings that you care about them? .
local: Gyoo Defidi yakowɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man yenaŋ daale nyiaa, Deniwalaŋ yɛna amɔte te n na gyueŋ ɔ dɔɔ, yaa tɔnɔɔ woŋti doo la deniwalaŋ bu man te n ne kɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ mena?.

eng: For a while you made them lower than angels. You crowned human beings with glory and honor. .
local: N ke yɛɛ deniwalaŋ na, n yɔkɔse e te n yela n kpilala baŋ kela e debaŋ kyomii daale, kenaŋ wɔle te n kaa faa e gyoorobiiri na obuo.

eng: You put everything under their control. When he puts everything under their control, he doesnt leave anything out of control. But right now, we dont see everything under their control yet.
local: te n moo kpene kamasɛ do ɔ tɛɛ. Kei na wolo baa nyi Wurubuarɛ moo kpene kamasɛ do ɔ tɛɛ keŋ ɔ te kaase kolo ya. Mɔna debaŋ kei man nɛ, de bɛɛ naa nyi abɔɔ ŋan pou doo ɔ tɛɛ ya.

eng: However, we do see the one who was made lower in order than the angels for a little while—its Jesus! Hes the one who is now crowned with glory and honor because of the suffering of his death. He suffered death so that he could taste death for everyone through Gods grace.
local: Mɔna da te naa nyi Yesu ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yɔkɔse e kyomii kela ɔ kpilala baŋ debaŋ kyomii nɛ, nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ ta mo gyoorobiiri na obuo fa e nawolo nyi ɔ naa diyem te ɔ yeŋawɔ. Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ na mo boele balaŋ nɛ dɔɔ, te Yesu lɛɛ walaŋ kamasɛ yeŋ yeŋ.

eng: It was appropriate for God, for whom and through whom everything exists, to use experiences of suffering to make perfect the pioneer of salvation. This salvation belongs to many sons and daughters whom hes leading to glory.
local: Wurubuarɛ yɛɛna kpene kamasɛ te ɔ moo ɔ doŋ ɔ dana ŋa na ŋe gbɛɛ. Mena dɔɔ ke yɛɛ kpene keŋ doo na ke gbɛɛ nyi waa yela ɔ bia burum ke kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ na baŋ mɔ kaa di gyoori kpu na e. Lee mena dɔɔ te ɔ yela Yesu Kristo nyeelɛɛre ŋon baa ta ɔ dɔɔ nyiŋ nyeekelɛɛ nɛ, nyiŋa daseŋae keŋ kaboena nɛ ŋon Wurubuarɛ siaman te ɔ naa diyem yeŋ. Kei nawolo baa nyi Yesu dɔɔ te baa ta, na baa nyiŋ nyeekelɛɛ.

eng: This is because the one who makes people holy and the people who are being made holy all come from one source. That is why Jesus isnt ashamed to call them brothers and sisters when he says.
local: Yesu ne gberaa na ayimɛɛ lee walaŋ wose man na ɔ gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman. Walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne gberaa ayimɛɛ lee balaŋ wose man, na balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gberaa ayimɛɛ lee ba wose man nɛ, be popou be kya yɛɛ dokoloŋ. Mena dɔɔ desɛŋ be dana e nyi waa baake wɔ nyi ɔ loŋtona ya.

eng: I will publicly announce your name to my brothers and sisters. I will praise you in the middle of the assembly. .
local: Yesu yako Wurubuarɛ nyiaa, Maa lese n nombia ŋan n be yɛɛ nɛ wola ma loŋtona. Botɔɔ keŋ balaŋ de gyaŋee nyi baa som neŋ na, maa kɛlɛɛ neŋ be siaman.

eng: He also says, I will rely on him. And also, Here I am with the children whom God has given to me. .
local: Ɔ besewɔ yako nyiaa, Maa mo me loo pou gyakaa n dɔɔ. Te ɔ besewɔ yako bela nyiaa, Kɛɛ, daa na balaŋ baŋ n ba mo wɔ do me nyiŋmaa man nɛ yaa.

eng: Therefore, since the children share in flesh and blood, he also shared the same things in the same way. He did this to destroy the one who holds the power over death—the devil—by dying.
local: Balaŋ baŋ Yesu be baake wɔ nyi ɔ loŋtona nɛ be yɛɛ denibalaŋwɔ keŋ be dana wosenaane na fatabo. Mena dɔɔ te Yesu mɔ kaa bese deniwalaŋ ŋgba baŋ nɛ, na waa ta ɔ yeŋ keŋ dɔɔ di ɔbɔnsam ŋon ɔ dana doŋ yeŋ dɔɔ nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: He set free those who were held in slavery their entire lives by their fear of death.
local: Ɔ yɛe mena na waa nyiŋ lɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bee yee yeŋ, te debaŋ kamasɛ na ba agyueŋ pou gyakaa bo yeŋ dɔɔ nɛ nyee.

eng: Of course, he isnt trying to help angels, but rather hes helping Abrahams descendants.
local: Kei nawolo baa nyi na Wurubuarɛ kpilala te Yesu kɔŋawɔ ɔ kaa kyɔ ya, mɔna ɔ kɔŋae ɔ kaa kyɔ Aberaham duuluŋ man balaŋ.

eng: Therefore, he had to be made like his brothers and sisters in every way. This was so that he could become a merciful and faithful high priest in things relating to God, in order to wipe away the sins of the people.
local: Lee kei dɔɔ te ɔ bese deniwalaŋ ŋgba ɔ loŋtona nɛ gbɛɛ kamasɛ man, na waa nyiŋ bese Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia ŋon ɔ ne wii balaŋ waraŋase, te ɔ ne di anokoare Wurubuarɛ ɔsom keŋ man. Na waa nyiŋ yeŋ pataa denibalaŋ na Wurubuarɛ nsana.

eng: Hes able to help those who are being tempted, since he himself experienced suffering when he was tempted.
local: Ɔbɔnsam kɛɛse ŋon gbagba kɛɛ te ɔ naa diyem nideli, mena dɔɔ waa tale kyɔ balaŋ baŋ pou ɔbɔnsam ne kɛɛse wɔ kɛɛ nɛ.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters who are partners in the heavenly calling, think about Jesus, the apostle and high priest of our confession.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ me tebia Kristotena baŋ mɔ ɛ be lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di nɛ, ɛ mo ya agyueŋ pou gyakaa Yesu dɔɔ. Ŋon yɛna Wurubuarɛ kpilale na Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia ŋon dɔɔ lɛɛ e di nɛ.

eng: Jesus was faithful to the one who appointed him just like Moses was faithful in Gods house.
local: Ɔ moo ɔ konɔɔ pou fa Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ba lese e nɛ, ŋgba mena keŋ Mosesi mɔ ba mo ɔ konɔɔ pou fa Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ba lese e nyi ɔ kaa kɛɛ ɔ balaŋ dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: But he deserves greater glory than Moses in the same way that the builder of the house deserves more honor than the house itself.
local: Da gyeŋ nyi walaŋ de baŋ deni na, ŋon ne nyiŋa na yele kela deni keŋ ɔ be bam nɛ. Mena mɔ te Yesu dana yele kela Mosesi.

eng: Every house is built by someone, but God is the builder of everything.
local: Walaŋ ne baŋa na deni, mɔna Wurubuarɛ yɛɛna walaŋ na kpene kamasɛ.

eng: Moses was faithful in all Gods house as a servant in order to affirm the things that would be spoken later.
local: Mosesi moo ɔ konɔɔ pou yɛɛ tom Wurubuarɛ balaŋ man ŋgba Wurubuarɛ tobaale nɛ, te ɔ taŋgbɛɛwɔ kolosi nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ waa kaa kolosi wee daale nɛ.

eng: But Jesus was faithful over Gods house as a Son. We are his house if we hold on to the confidence and the pride that our hope gives us.
local: Mɔna Kristo dɔɔ ŋon yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bu e, te ɔ lese ɔ konɔɔ pou kɛɛ Wurubuarɛ balaŋ dɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ akpaa de nyiŋ konɔɔ te da mo de sia gyakaa abɔɔ ŋan ɔ be yako see nyi waa fa daa nɛ dɔɔ na, keŋ ne gyae la ke yela dɛɛ tale bese ɔ balaŋ.

eng: So, as the Holy Spirit says, Today, if you hear his voice, .
local: Lee mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon yakowɔ nyiaa, Gyɛŋ kei akpaa nyaŋ ŋon n nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan na.

eng: dont have stubborn hearts as they did in the rebellion, on the day when they tested me in the desert. .
local: na yɛɛ deŋele doŋ ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ naanaɔ be yɛɛ te bɔɔ koro seŋ tia Wurubuarɛ depampaa dinaa keŋ man, te bɔɔ teese ɔ nɔɔman nɛ ya.

eng: That is where your ancestors challenged and tested me, though they had seen my work for forty years. .
local: Ɛ naanaɔ naa kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ kulutooneŋ sɔŋola ŋan man, mɔ kenaŋ wɔle na ba teese ɔ nɔɔman kɛɛse e kɛɛ.

eng: So I was angry with them. I said, Their hearts always go off course, and they dont know my ways. .
local: Mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ gyeŋa baŋ ɔ balaŋ benaŋ dɔɔ te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, Debaŋ kamasɛ na bɛɛ yo gbɛɛ nawolo nyi bɔɔ ne sila me nɔɔwoya ya.

eng: Because of my anger I swore: They will never enter my rest! .
local: Keŋte ma gyeŋa baŋ kaŋ ntam nyiaa, Bɔɔ gyae baa nyiŋ gbɛɛ gyoo botɔɔ keŋ mɔɔ desina see wɔ nyi be kaa kyaa na baa fɛɛfo nɛ korakora ya. .

eng: Watch out, brothers and sisters, so that none of you have an evil, unfaithful heart that abandons the living God.
local: Mena dɔɔ me tebia, ɛ kɛɛ nideli nyi ɛ man ŋolo be nyiŋ agyueŋ kum na waa lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ na waa lee Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ wɔle ya.

eng: Instead, encourage each other every day, as long as its called today, so that none of you become insensitive to God because of sins deception.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ nyiŋaa ɛ kyaa na ɛ sia nɛ, ɛ kolosi do dɔŋa kakyeŋ wee na wee. Na nombiakumɛɛ ba beo ɛ man ŋolo na waa yɛɛ deŋele doŋ ya.

eng: We are partners with Christ, but only if we hold on to the confidence we had in the beginning until the end.
local: Akpaa de kyaŋ da kelɛɛdi keŋ dɔɔ taŋgbɛɛ lɛɛ Kristo di nɛ man keŋkeŋ kelii da yeŋ wee na, na daa na Kristo yɛɛ dokoloŋ baa.

eng: When it says, Today, if you hear his voice, dont have stubborn hearts as they did in the rebellion. .
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan yakowɔ nyiaa, Gyɛŋ akpaa nyaŋ ŋon n nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan na, na yɛɛ deŋele doŋ ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ naanaɔ be yɛɛ deŋele doŋ tia Wurubuarɛ nɛ ya.

eng: Who was it who rebelled when they heard his voice? Wasnt it all of those who were brought out of Egypt by Moses?.
local: Balaŋ bomɔ nyiina Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan te ba korowɔ seŋ tia e? Na baŋ yɛna balaŋ baŋ Mosesi be tɛɛ be siaman mo wɔ lee Igyipite tɛɛle man nɛ yee?.

eng: And with whom was God angry for forty years? Wasnt it with the ones who sinned, whose bodies fell in the desert?.
local: Balaŋ bomɔ te Wurubuarɛ gyeŋa baŋ do kulutooneŋ sɔŋola ŋan man? Na baŋ yɛna balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ, te ɔ be yeli bɔɔ yekee depampaa dinaa keŋ man nɛ yee?.

eng: And against whom did he swear that they would never enter his rest, if not against the ones who were disobedient?.
local: Balaŋ bomɔ te Wurubuarɛ gyeŋa baŋ kaŋ ntam do nyi bɔɔ gyae baa gyoo botɔɔ keŋ ɔ be desina see wɔ nyi be kaa fɛɛfo nɛ ya? Na baŋ yɛna balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ deŋele doŋ te ba te lɛɛ e di nɛ yee?.

eng: We see that they couldnt enter because of their lack of faith.
local: Mena dɔɔ da te naa nyi keŋ ba te lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di dɔɔ ya, te ba te tale ka gyoo botɔɔ keŋ ɔ be desina see wɔ nyi be kaa kyaa fɛɛfo nɛ ya.

eng: Therefore, since the promise that we can enter into rest is still open, lets be careful so that none of you will appear to miss it.
local: Wurubuarɛ keyako see keŋ ɔ be yako nyi ɔ te desina defɛɛfoe daale see fa ɔ balaŋ nɛ, ke sɛɛ ka seŋɛɛ mena. Mena dɔɔ ɛ yela dɛɛ kɛɛ nideli keŋ de man ŋolo be yɛɛ kolo kum daale, na ka ke tɔ e gbɛɛ keŋ ɔ be nyiŋ gyoo botɔɔ ya.

eng: We also had the good news preached to us, just as the Israelites did. However, the message they heard didnt help them because they werent united in faith with the ones who listened to it.
local: Nawolo nyi nombia kpaakpaa ŋan bɔɔ kolosi fa de naanaɔ nɛ, ŋan ke te be kolosiwɔ fa daa mɔ. Mɔna nombia ŋan bɔɔ nyii nɛ te yɛɛ tɔnɔɔ fa wɔ ya, nawolo nyi ba te lɛɛ ŋa di ya.

eng: We who have faith are entering the rest. As God said, And because of my anger I swore: They will never enter into my rest! And yet Gods works were completed at the foundation of the world.
local: Mena dɔɔ daa baŋ dɔɔ lɛɛ ɔ nombia ŋan di nɛ, daa daageŋ ne gyae la dee nyiŋ gbɛɛ gyoo botɔɔ keŋ ɔ be desina see ɔ balaŋ nyi be kaa kyaa fɛɛfo nɛ. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ yakowɔ nyiaa, Me ta mo baŋ kaŋ ntam nyi, Bɔɔ gyae baa gyoo botɔɔ keŋ mɔɔ desina see me balaŋ nyi be kaa kyaa fɛɛfo nɛ korakora ya. Mɔna da gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ wulaa bake ɔ nombia see leenaŋ debaŋ keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ taŋ nɛ.

eng: Then somewhere he said this about the seventh day of creation: God rested on the seventh day from all his works. .
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan yakowɔ yenaŋ daale lee wee nyetoorote keŋ man nyiaa, Weeya looro te Wurubuarɛ moowɔ yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ, te ɔ fɛɛfowɔ wee nyetoorote keŋ man.

eng: But again, in the passage above, God said, They will never enter my rest! .
local: Wurubuarɛ besewɔ yako lee kefɛɛfo kei wose man bela nyiaa, Bɔɔ gyae baa gyoo botɔɔ keŋ mɔɔ desina see me balaŋ nyi be kaa kyaa fɛɛfo nɛ ya.

eng: Therefore, its left open for some to enter it, and the ones who had the good news preached to them before didnt enter because of disobedience.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ taŋgbɛɛ nyii Wurubuarɛ kefɛɛfo kei wose man nombia nɛ, lee ba deŋele doŋ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ te fa wɔ gbɛɛ na baa gyoo botɔɔ keŋ ɔ be desina see wɔ nyi be kaa kyaa fɛɛfo nɛ ya. Kei nawolo baa nyi balaŋ wɔlɛɛ baale te Wurubuarɛ waa fa gbɛɛ na baa gyoo botɔɔ ke kyaa fɛɛfo.

eng: Just as it says in the passage above, God designates a certain day as today, when he says through David much later, Today, if you hear his voice, dont have stubborn hearts. .
local: Mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ ta bese see wee daale keŋ ɔ ne baake ke nyi Gyɛŋ, na ke yela balaŋ ke nyiŋ gbɛɛ gyoo ɔ defɛɛfoe keŋ man. Kulutooneŋ burum ŋaale wɔle nɛ, ɔ tɛɛwɔ gyoo Defidi dɔɔ bese kolosi lee wee kenaŋ wose man, ŋgba mena keŋ bɔɔ wulaa kolosi ka wose man nombia tɔne kei man nɛ nyiaa, Gyɛŋ akpaa nyaŋ ŋon n nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan na, na yɛɛ deŋele doŋ ya.

eng: If Joshua gave the Israelites rest, God wouldnt have spoken about another day later on.
local: Debaŋ keŋ de naana Gyosua be tɛɛ Iseraetena siaman nɛ, ɔ te tale yela baa nyiŋ mena kefɛɛfo kei ya. Nyi ŋon talewɔ yela baa nyiŋ mena kefɛɛfo kei nɛ, nafɔ Wurubuarɛ be gyae waa kolosi kefɛɛfowee wɔle daale wose man nombia bela ya.

eng: So you see that a sabbath rest is left open for Gods people.
local: Kei nawolo baa nyi wee daale kyaa keŋ Wurubuarɛ balaŋ baa fɛɛfo ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ be fɛɛfo wee nyetoorote keŋ man nɛ.

eng: The one who entered Gods rest also rested from his works, just as God rested from his own.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon Wurubuarɛ waa yela waa gyoo ɔ defɛɛfoe keŋ man nɛ, nyi ɔtenate de yɛɛ ɔ tom taŋ na waa fɛɛfo ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ mɔ be yɛɛ ɔ tom taŋ te ɔ be fɛɛfo nɛ.

eng: Therefore, lets make every effort to enter that rest so that no one will fall by following the same example of disobedience.
local: Gyaŋgba gyaŋgba balaŋ baŋ yɛɛ deŋele doŋ, mena dɔɔ be te nyiŋ gbɛɛ gyoo Wurubuarɛ defɛɛfoe keŋ man ya. Lee mena dɔɔ ɛ na yeli daa mɔ ke yɛɛ deŋele doŋ na dɛɛ yo gbɛɛ ya. Mɔna ɛ yela dɛɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ na dɛɛ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii, na dee nyiŋ gbɛɛ gyoo ɔ defɛɛfoe keŋ man.

eng: because Gods word is living, active, and sharper than any two-edged sword. It penetrates to the point that it separates the soul from the spirit and the joints from the marrow. Its able to judge the hearts thoughts and intentions.
local: Wurubuarɛ nombii keŋ nɔɔ dana boalaŋ te kɛɛ fa nyeedoŋ kekpaa. Ke yɛɛ ŋgba sila sia na wɔlete nɛ, te ke dana beeleŋ kela sila sia na wɔlete kamasɛ. Kɛɛ tale kara gyoo deniwalaŋ yenaŋ kamasɛ, lese deniwalaŋ dinɔɔ na ɔ nyeeman na ɔ konɔɔ man nombia pou debɔɔ.

eng: No creature is hidden from it, but rather everything is naked and exposed to the eyes of the one to whom we have to give an answer.
local: Abɔɔ ŋan pou Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ nɛ, ŋe man dokoloŋ be kyaa keŋ ka weesɛɛ ɔ siaman ya. Kpene kamasɛ doo ɔ siaman peŋteŋpereiŋ. Ŋon Wurubuarɛ siaman te de popou dɛɛ kaa seŋ tɔlɛɛ de nɔɔwoya wee daale.

eng: Also, lets hold on to the confession since we have a great high priest who passed through the heavens, who is Jesus, Gods Son.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ yela dɛɛ kyaŋ da kelɛɛdi keŋ de dana nɛ man keŋkeŋ. Nawolo nyi de dana Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia kpaakpaate ŋolo. Ŋon yɛna Yesu, Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon ɔ be lam Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ adido nɛ.

eng: because we dont have a high priest who cant sympathize with our weaknesses but instead one who was tempted in every way that we are, except without sin.
local: Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia ŋon de dana nɛ, ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne wii daa te ɔ ne yeli dɛɛ tale yɛɛ abɔɔ ŋan kaboena nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ te dɔɔ ne tale yɛɛ ya nɛ. Nawolo nyi ɔbɔnsam kɛɛse e kɛɛ lee gbɛɛ kamasɛ man ŋgba daa nɛ, mɔna ŋon dɔɔ ɔ te yɛɛ dukum ya.

eng: Finally, lets draw near to the throne of favor with confidence so that we can receive mercy and find grace when we need help.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na yeli dee yee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ kegyu ya. Nawolo nyi da gyeŋ nyi waa wii de waraŋase na waa mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele daa, na waa kyɔ daa debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ da wose de kyaŋ daa na.

eng: Every high priest is taken from the people and put in charge of things that relate to God for their sake, in order to offer gifts and sacrifices for sins.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ba lese e nyi waa yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia na, bɛɛ lese e bo lee ŋon gbagba ɔ balaŋ man na waa seŋ be nawɔɔ man lee be Wurubuarɛ ɔsom man. Na waa lɛɛ ba kedeesa abɔɔ mo gyu ke deesi fa Wurubuarɛ, na Wurubuarɛ ke nyiŋ mo be nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ.

eng: The high priest is able to deal gently with the ignorant and those who are misled since he himself is prone to weakness.
local: Ŋon gbagba mɔ yɛɛ deniwalaŋ e te ɔ ne tale yɛɛ kpene keŋ be dei ya, mena dɔɔ ɔ ne nyiŋ konɔɔ fa balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ ya te bɛɛ yɛɛ abɔɔ ŋan be dei ya te ne wɔŋee wɔ lee Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛneŋ man nɛ.

eng: Because of his weakness, he must offer sacrifices for his own sins as well as for the people.
local: Mena dɔɔ te kaboena nyi waa taŋgbɛɛ deesi fa Wurubuarɛ lee ŋon gbagba wui nombiakumɛɛ dɔɔ, pɛna waa tale deesi fa ɔ balaŋ baŋ mɔ wui nombiakumɛɛ ŋan bɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ.

eng: No one takes this honor for themselves but takes it only when they are called by God, just like Aaron.
local: Akpaa Wurubuarɛ te baake ŋolo nyi ɔ kaa yɛɛ ɔ sae kegyia ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be baake Aarɔn ya nɛ, walaŋ ŋonaŋ gbagba be tale see ɔ wose Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia ya.

eng: In the same way Christ also didnt promote himself to become high priest. Instead, it was the one who said to him, You are my Son. Today I have become your Father .
local: Mena te Kristo mɔ ta see ɔ wose Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ lese na e te ɔ yako e nyiaa, N yɛɛ me bu e, gyɛŋ me ta bese n kya.

eng: as he also says in another place, You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek. .
local: Wurubuarɛ besewɔ yako ɔ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see man yenaŋ daale bela nyiaa, N yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ sae e kekpaakekpaa, ŋgba mena keŋ Mɛkesɛdɛke be di Wurubuarɛ saere kekpaakekpaa nɛ.

eng: During his days on earth, Christ offered prayers and requests with loud cries and tears as his sacrifices to the one who was able to save him from death. He was heard because of his godly devotion.
local: Yesu ke kyaa tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ moo yeesa wii lese siasaraŋ fane fa Wurubuarɛ. Nawolo nyi ŋon waageŋ kooŋ ne gyae la waa tale lese e lee yeŋ man. Lee mena keŋ ɔ be yɔkɔse ɔ wose mo obuo fa Wurubuarɛ dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ nyii ɔ kefane keŋ.

eng: Although he was a Son, he learned obedience from what he suffered.
local: Keŋ na keŋ Yesu yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bu e koraŋ na, ɔ naa diyem nideli. Lee ɔ diyem kena kenaŋ man nɛ, ɔ kasee mena keŋ waa nyii ɔ kya dei.

eng: After he had been made perfect, he became the source of eternal salvation for everyone who obeys him.
local: Yesu ke tei Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɔ be yako e nyi waa yɛɛ taŋ nɛ, keŋ yele la te ɔ kaa bese nyeelɛɛre na waa yela baŋ pou bee nyii ɔ dei nɛ ke nyiŋ nyeekelɛɛ kekpaakekpaa.

eng: He was appointed by God to be a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
local: Mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ lese e nyi waa yɛɛ ɔ sae kegyia, ŋgba mena keŋ Mɛkesɛdɛke be yɛɛ ɔ sae kegyia nɛ.

eng: We have a lot to say about this topic, and its difficult to explain, because you have been lazy and you havent been listening.
local: Nafɔ de dana nombia burum dɛɛ yako lee Mɛkesɛdɛke na Yesu saere kedi kei wose man, mɔna nombia ŋan man kalese dana baŋ nawolo nyi ɛ nombia man kenyii dana doŋ.

eng: Although you should have been teachers by now, you need someone to teach you an introduction to the basics about Gods message. You have come to the place where you need milk instead of solid food.
local: Debaŋ kei nɛ nafɔ kaboena nyi ɛmɛɛ mɔ yaa bese Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan wolala. Mɔna ɛ sɛɛ ɛ yɛɛ ŋgba bia baŋ be kaboena bo nyi ŋolo waa bese wola wɔ Yesu Kristo ɔsom nombia ŋan kewalaŋ nɛ. Ɛ yɛɛ yaa ŋgba bia baŋ be sɛɛ bɛɛ ŋmase dide nɛ. Ɛ ta bee ta na weenɛɛ doŋ nyiŋan kedi ya.

eng: Everyone who lives on milk is not used to the word of righteousness, because they are babies.
local: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ sɛɛ ɔ ne ŋmase dide nɛ, na ɔ yɛɛ bu e. Ɔ ta gyeŋa ta kpene keŋ yɛɛ dukum yaa debam ya.

eng: But solid food is for the mature, whose senses are trained by practice to distinguish between good and evil.
local: Mɔna kegyiise ne tale la di weenɛɛ doŋ nyiŋan. Nawolo nyi lee mena keŋ nombia be yala wɔ te bɔɔ gyoo ŋe man nideli te bɔɔ naa dɔɔ, ba gyeŋ kpene keŋ yɛɛ dukum na kpene keŋ yɛɛ debam.

eng: So lets press on to maturity, by moving on from the basics about Christs word. Lets not lay a foundation of turning away from dead works, of faith in God.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ ɛ yela dee tina Kristo ɔsom nombia ŋan dɔɔ kasee kewalaŋ nɛ yela mena, na dɛɛ kasee abɔɔ ŋan ŋe doo de siaman te ŋe gyae ke yela da Kristo kelɛɛdi ke yɛɛ doŋ nɛ. Ta tekaa kaboena nyi dɛɛ bese kasee nombia kewalaŋ ŋan dɔɔ kasee kaalaŋ, ŋgba kekyɛɛkee lee dinɔɔ ŋan be dana tɔnɔɔ ya te ŋɛɛ mo daa gyu yeŋ man, na Wurubuarɛ kelɛɛdi nɛ ya.

eng: of teaching about ritual ways to wash with water, laying on of hands, the resurrection from the dead, and eternal judgment—all over again.
local: Ɛ na yeli dɛɛ tekaa kasee kolo lee Wurubuarɛ loŋkesɔ, na nyiŋmaa kamo gyakaa balaŋ dɔɔ, na woya kefoŋ lee yeŋ man, na Wurubuarɛ nombia kedi debaŋ ɔtomante keŋ man bela ya.

eng: Were going to press on, if God allows it.
local: Te nyi akpaa Wurubuarɛ de sɛɛ na dee tina mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou keyɛɛ yela, na dɛɛ bee gyu de siaman.

eng: Because its impossible to restore people to changed hearts and lives who turn away once they have seen the light, tasted the heavenly gift, become partners with the Holy Spirit.
local: Nyi Wurubuarɛ da toro walaŋ agyueŋ te ɔtenate da gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia, te Wurubuarɛ da mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele e te ɔ mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do ɔ man te ɔ nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia te ɔ naa nyi ŋe dei te ɔ gyeŋ doŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa kaa lese wola debaŋ ɔtomante keŋ man nɛ, lee kɛŋa pou wɔle te ɔtenate da besena ɔ wɔle tee Wurubuarɛ na, kolo be kyaa keŋ deniwalaŋ waa yɛɛ na kabesena ɔtenate kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya.

eng: They are crucifying Gods Son all over again and exposing him to public shame.
local: Mena dɔɔ nyi akpaa ba lee mena nombia kɛŋa wɔle nɛ, gyae ke yɛɛ doŋ fa wɔ nyi baa tina be nombiakumɛɛ ŋan bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ yela na baa kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ bela. Be nombia ayɛɛsa ŋenaŋ nawolo baa nyi, na yɛɛ yaa ŋgba ba bese yaa bɛɛ gyɔ Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon baa sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ fulu e balaŋ man nɛ.

eng: The ground receives a blessing from God when it drinks up the rain that regularly comes and falls on it and yields a useful crop for those people for whom it is being farmed.
local: Tɛɛle keŋ ɔga ne ŋɛɛ woli ke te ne yeli wɔɔfarale ne nyiŋ weenɛɛ kpaakpaa lee ke man nɛ, Wurubuarɛ ne hiraa na ke.

eng: But if it produces thorns and thistles, its useless and close to being cursed. It ends up being burned.
local: Mɔna akpaa sɔɔse na ŋalese ne tuu la ke dɔɔ na, na mena tɛɛle kenaŋ be dana tɔnɔɔ ya. Te n te kɛɛ ya na Wurubuarɛ waa tale falaa ke, na waa yela boalaŋ ke kpa ke.

eng: But we are convinced of better things in your case, brothers and sisters, even though we are talking this way—things that go together with salvation.
local: Me gyoona kpaakpaa, keŋ na de kɛɛ yako kei koraŋ nɛ, da gyeŋ nyi ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ nombia kpaakpaa ŋaale doo ɛ man, keŋ ŋɛɛ kyaa na nyeekelɛɛ nombia ŋan.

eng: God isnt unjust so that he forgets your efforts and the love you have shown for his names sake when you served and continue to serve Gods holy people.
local: Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne di ɔ nombia ke gbɛɛ dɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ ɔ be gyae waa wolee ɛ tom keŋ ɛ be yɛɛ, na ɛ balaŋ kegyae keŋ ɛ na lese wola e nɛ ya. Nawolo nyi lee ŋon dɔɔ ɛ te kyɔ ɛ tebia Kristotena, te ɛ gyakaa ɛ ne kyɔ wɔ.

eng: But we desperately want each of you to show the same effort to make your hope sure until the end.
local: Da kegyaebii yɛna nyi, ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ waa kyaŋ nombia kpaakpaa kɛŋa man nideli kelii ɔto. Kei gyae ke yela ɛ ka gyeŋ nideli nyi ɛ ke nyiŋ kpene keŋ dɔɔ ɛ loo gyakaa nɛ.

eng: This is so you wont be lazy but follow the example of the ones who inherit the promises through faith and patience.
local: Dɔɔ ne gyae nyi ɛ man ŋolo waa yɛɛ kebɔŋale Wurubuarɛ ɔsom keŋ man ya. Mɔna dɛɛ gyae bo nyi yaa kasee balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di te bɔɔ nyiŋ konɔɔ yɛɛ kakyeŋ seŋ ba kelɛɛdi keŋ man kelii ɔto, te Wurubuarɛ be fa wɔ kpene keŋ ɔ be yako see nyi waa fa wɔ nɛ.

eng: When God gave Abraham his promise, he swore by himself since he couldnt swear by anyone greater.
local: Debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yako ɔ nombii see fa de naana Aberaham nɛ, ɔ moo ŋon Wurubuarɛ gbagba yele bo kaŋ ntam. Nawolo nyi na ŋolo be kyaa keŋ ɔ kela ŋon Wurubuarɛ keŋ waa mo walaŋ ŋonaŋ yele kaŋ ntam ya.

eng: He said, I will certainly bless you and multiply your descendants. .
local: Wurubuarɛ yako de naana Aberaham nyiaa, Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako neŋ nyi maa hiraa neŋ, na maa yela n duuluŋ man balaŋ ka bo nideli.

eng: So Abraham obtained the promise by showing patience.
local: Mena dɔɔ de naana Aberaham mɔ nyiŋa konɔɔ kyaa daa kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa fa e nɛ, te ɔ nyiŋmaa kaŋa ke.

eng: People pledge by something greater than themselves. A solemn pledge guarantees what they say and shuts down any argument.
local: Nyi akpaa ŋolo ne gyae waa kaŋ ntam na, ɔ na mo walaŋ ŋon ɔ kela e nɛ yele bo kaŋ ntam. Ntam kekaŋ kenaŋ na wola la nyi, nombia ŋan ɔtenate be yako nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ keŋ aŋmaareŋ kelɛɛ be doo ke man ya.

eng: When God wanted to further demonstrate to the heirs of the promise that his purpose doesnt change, he guaranteed it with a solemn pledge.
local: Keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne gyae nyi balaŋ baŋ baa nyiŋ kpene keŋ ɔ be yako see nyi waa fa wɔ nɛ, ka gyeŋ nideli nyi ɔ be gyae waa kyɛɛkee wa agyueŋ ya dɔɔ nɛ, mena dɔɔ te ɔ kaŋa ntam kpu na nombii keŋ ɔ be yako see nɛ.

eng: So these are two things that dont change, because its impossible for God to lie. He did this so that we, who have taken refuge in him, can be encouraged to grasp the hope that is lying in front of us.
local: Nombii keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yako see na ntam keŋ ɔ be kam nɛ, ɔ be gyae waa kyɛɛkee nombia ala kɛŋa man daale ya nawolo nyi ɔ bee di dekara ya. Mena dɔɔ nombii kei na do daa baŋ dɔɔ yeu kaa gyoo e nɛ kakyeŋ da kelɛɛdi man, na dɛɛ tale kyaŋ kpene keŋ dɔɔ ɔ be yeli de loo gyakaa nɛ man nideli.

eng: This hope, which is a safe and secure anchor for our whole being, enters the sanctuary behind the curtain.
local: Da nyeedoŋ pou seŋɛe mena lookegyakaa kei dɔɔ. Keŋ yele la te de nyiŋa gbɛɛ gyoo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa tɔɔmante keŋ man, botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ mo kanyaŋ kpase ke keŋ nafɔ gyaŋgba na walaŋ yakaate bee nyiŋ gbɛɛ gyoo botɔɔ ya nɛ.

eng: Thats where Jesus went in advance and entered for us, since he became a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
local: Yesu te kaa bese Wurubuarɛ saese pou kegyia kekpaakekpaa, ŋgba mena keŋ Mɛkesɛdɛke be di Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia kekpaakekpaa nɛ. Te ɔ te kyaŋ daa gbɛɛ ka gyoo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa tɔɔmante keŋ man, na daa mɔ ke sila e.

eng: This Melchizedek, who was king of Salem and priest of the Most High God, met Abraham as he returned from the defeat of the kings, and Melchizedek blessed him.
local: Na Mɛkesɛdɛke kei yɛɛ donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Saalɛm nɛ gyoo e, te ɔ besewɔ ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpatakpere ŋon sae e. Debaŋ keŋ de naana Aberaham be gyu ke yoo gyooneŋ baale di be dɔɔ bese ɔ ne kɔŋ nɛ, ɔ kaa gyaŋeewɔ na gyoo Mɛkesɛdɛke kei. Botɔɔ te Mɛkesɛdɛke hiraa ŋon de naana Aberaham.

eng: Abraham gave a tenth of everything to him. His name means first king of righteousness, and then king of Salem, that is, king of peace.
local: Keŋte de naana Aberaham kpase abɔɔ ŋan pou ɔ be nyim lee yoo keŋ man nɛ akpeŋ kufu, te ɔ moo dekpeŋ daale fa Mɛkesɛdɛke ŋonaŋ. Mɛkesɛdɛke yele kei asɛɛ yɛna nyi Gyoo ŋon ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman, te Saalɛm gyoo mɔ nawolo nyi Wosefɛɛreŋ gyoo.

eng: He is without father or mother or any family. He has no beginning or end of life, but hes like Gods Son and remains a priest for all time.
local: Ŋolo ba gyeŋ Mɛkesɛdɛke kei ɔ kya yaa ɔ naa yaa ɔ duuluŋ man dalee ya. Ba te yako kolo mɔ lee ɔ kalola yaa ɔ yeŋ wose man ya. Ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ sae e kekpaakekpaa ŋgba Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon nɛ.

eng: See how great Melchizedek was! Abraham, the father of the people, gave him a tenth of everything he captured.
local: Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ kɛɛ na, ɛ kena nyi ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ dinaa e te ɔ dana yele mɔ. De naana Aberaham ŋon ɔ dana yele Wurubuarɛ siaman koraŋ nɛ kpase abɔɔ ŋan ɔ be nyim lee yoo man nɛ akpeŋ kufu, te ɔ moo ŋe man dekpeŋ fa Mɛkesɛdɛke ŋonaŋ.

eng: The descendants of Levi who receive the office of priest have a commandment under the Law to collect a tenth of everything from the people who are their brothers and sisters, though they also are descended from Abraham.
local: Mosesi mmaraase ŋan nawolo nyiaa, kaboena nyi Gyudatena baŋ be yɛɛ de naana Aberaham ɔ naanasewɔ nɛ, baa kpala ba abɔɔ ŋan be dana nɛ akpeŋ kufu na baa mo ŋe man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ fa Lewitena baŋ be yɛɛ saesewɔ nɛ. Da gyeŋ nyi Lewitena kɛwɔ mɔ yɛɛ de naana Aberaham ɔ naanasewɔ, mɔna Wurubuarɛ faa Gyudatena mena mmaraase ŋenaŋ nyi baa tɔ mena abɔɔ ŋenaŋ fa baŋ gbagba be tebia Lewitena baŋ.

eng: But Melchizedek, who isnt related to them, received a tenth of everything from Abraham and blessed the one who had received the promises.
local: Mɛkesɛdɛke kei ta lee Lewi duuluŋ man gba ya, mɔna ɔ talewɔ lɛɛ abɔɔ kekpase kufu man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ kei lee de naana Aberaham ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa yɛɛ kolo fa e nɛ gyaŋ te ɔ hiraa e.

eng: Without question, the less important person is blessed by the more important person.
local: Walaŋ kamasɛ taŋ gyeŋ nyi kegyia ne hiraa na kemaasa keŋ aŋmaareŋ be doo ke man ya.

eng: In addition, in one case a tenth is received by people who die, and in the other case, the tenth is received by someone who continues to live, according to the record.
local: Lewitena baŋ bɛɛ lɛɛ abɔɔ kekpala kufu man dekpeŋ kei nɛ be yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yem, mɔna Mɛkesɛdɛke ŋon de naana Aberaham be tɔ abɔɔ kɛŋa fa nɛ dɔɔ, ɔ ta yem ya ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia be yako nɛ.

eng: It could be said that Levi, who received a tenth, paid a tenth through Abraham.
local: Dɛɛ tale yako nyi Lewi mɔ tɛɛwɔ de naana Aberaham dɔɔ tɔ abɔɔ kufu man dekpeŋ kei fa Mɛkesɛdɛke.

eng: because he was still in his ancestors body when Abraham paid the tenth to Melchizedek.
local: Nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ de naana Aberaham be gyaŋee na Mɛkesɛdɛke nɛ, na be ta lola ta Lewi ya. Debaŋ kenaŋ na ɔ sɛɛ ɔ doo ɔ naana Aberaham teŋa man.

eng: So if perfection came through the levitical office of priest , why was there still a need to speak about raising up another priest according to the order of Melchizedek rather than one according to the order of Aaron?.
local: Debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ de naana Mosesi dɔɔ mo mmaraase ŋan fa Gyudatena nɛ, ɔ lese balaŋ lee Lewitena man nyi baa di saere. Nyi Lewitena saere tom keŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ deŋ talewɔ yela balaŋ gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman na, nafɔ bɛɛ tekaa hia nyi Wurubuarɛ waa bese lese sae ŋolo ke kɔŋ ŋgba Mɛkesɛdɛke nɛ ya. Te nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ te kpila ŋolo nyi ɔ kaa di saere ŋgba Mɛkesɛdɛke nɛ. Mɔna ɔ ta lee Aarɔn ŋon ɔ yɛɛ Lewi baale e nɛ duuluŋ man ya.

eng: When the order of the priest changes, there has to be a change in the Law as well.
local: Nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ te yɛɛ Lewi baale e ya nɛ de kaa di Wurubuarɛ saere na, na kaboena nyi baa kyɛɛkee mmaraa keŋ, nawolo nyi Lewitena baageŋ te kaboena nyi baa di Wurubuarɛ saere.

eng: The person we are talking about belongs to another tribe, and no one ever served at the altar from that tribe.
local: Mena nombia kɛŋa dɛɛ yako nɛ, na Yesu Kristo wose man nombia yaa dɛɛ yako nɛ. Nawolo nyi ɔ ta lee Lewi dekpaŋalaŋ man ya te ɔ kaa dii Wurubuarɛ saere. Ɔ lee dekpaŋalaŋ kpoo daale man, te ŋolo mɔ te dii ta Wurubuarɛ saere lee mena dekpaŋalaŋ kenaŋ man see ya.

eng: Its clear that our Lord came from the tribe of Judah, but Moses never said anything about priests from that tribe.
local: De popou gyeŋ nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu lee Gyuda dekpaŋalaŋ man, te de naana Mosesi mɔ te yako kolo lee Gyuda dekpaŋalaŋmantena benaŋ saere kedi wose man ya.

eng: And its even clearer if another priest appears who is like Melchizedek.
local: Nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ nombii kei man kenyii be dana baŋ bela ya. Nawolo nyi walaŋ wɔle kpoo kɔŋala kaa di Wurubuarɛ saere ŋgba Mɛkesɛdɛke nɛ.

eng: He has become a priest by the power of a life that cant be destroyed, rather than a legal requirement about physical descent.
local: Ɔ saere keŋ ɔ be kaa dii nɛ, te yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ mmaraase yaa dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man ɔ ba lee nɛ dɔɔ te ɔ dii ke ya. Mɔna ɔ dii saere kenaŋ bo lee ɔ doŋ keŋ ɔ dana, keŋ yeŋ be tale di ɔ dɔɔ ya nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: This is confirmed: You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek. .
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see yakowɔ nyi, Wurubuarɛ yako Kristo nyiaa, N yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ sae e kekpaakekpaa, ŋgba mena keŋ Mɛkesɛdɛke mɔ be di Wurubuarɛ saere kekpaakekpaa nɛ.

eng: On the one hand, an earlier command is set aside because it was weak and useless.
local: Mena dɔɔ lee gbɛɛ daale man na, dɛɛ tale yako nyi Wurubuarɛ ta mo Mosesi mmaraase benɛɛ ŋan see yenaŋ. Nawolo nyi ŋɔɔ dana tɔnɔɔ ya, te ŋɔɔ dana nɔɔdoŋ mɔ ya.

eng: . On the other hand, a better hope is introduced, through which we draw near to God.
local: Mosesi mmaraase ŋan te tale yela de gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne gyae nɛ ya. Mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ te fa daa lookegyakaa kpaakpaate keŋ ka kela bene keŋ. Lee mena dɔɔ nɛnɛɛ dɛɛ tale tuu benaa Wurubuarɛ.

eng: And this was not done without a solemn pledge! The others have become priests without a solemn pledge.
local: Wurubuarɛ kaŋa ntam pɛte ɔ see Yesu saere kekpaakekpaa, mɔna saese akaŋ baŋ dɔɔ ɔ bɛɛ kaŋ ntam pɛte bɛɛ see wɔ saere ya.

eng: but this priest was affirmed with a solemn pledge by the one who said, The Lord has made a solemn pledge and will not change his mind: You are a priest forever. .
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ ne yako nyi, pɛ Wurubuarɛ ka see Yesu saere na ɔ kaŋa ntam nyiaa, Maŋ Wurubuarɛ te kaŋ ntam te mɔɔ gyae maa kyɛɛkee ma agyueŋ ya. Nyaŋ ke di me saere kekpaakekpaa. .

eng: As a result, Jesus has become the guarantee of a better covenant.
local: Wurubuarɛ ntam keŋ ɔ be kam nɛ, keŋ yele la te Yesu kaa bese walaŋ ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ ntam kenaŋ wɔle, na ka wola nyi nɔɔkebakesee wɔle kei dei kela bene keŋ.

eng: The others who became priests are numerous because death prevented them from continuing to serve.
local: Gyaŋgba keŋ na Wurubuarɛ saese baŋ boe, nawolo nyi yeŋ bee yeli baa yɛɛ be saere tom keŋ kelii ɔto ya.

eng: In contrast, he holds the office of priest permanently because he continues to serve forever.
local: Mɔna Yesu dɔɔ ɔ saere kedi keŋ be gyae ka lee ɔ gyaŋ gyu ŋolo gyaŋ ya, nawolo nyi ŋon kyaa kekpaakekpaa.

eng: This is why he can completely save those who are approaching God through him, because he always lives to speak with God for them.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ waa tale lɛɛ balaŋ baŋ pou bɛɛ tɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ nɛ nyee, nawolo nyi ɔ kyaa kekpaakekpaa te ɔ ne fane fa Wurubuarɛ lee be dɔɔ.

eng: Its appropriate for us to have this kind of high priest: holy, innocent, incorrupt, separate from sinners, and raised high above the heavens.
local: Yesu Kristo yɛna Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia ŋon ɔ ne tale yɛɛ kpene keŋ dɛɛ gyae nɛ fa daa. Nawolo nyi ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman, te nombiikum kamasɛ na ayimɛɛ kamasɛ mɔ be doo ɔ man ya. Wurubuarɛ lese e lee nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra man te ɔ gyinaa e adido kela walaŋ kamasɛ.

eng: He doesnt need to offer sacrifices every day like the other high priests, first for their own sins and then for the sins of the people. He did this once for all when he offered himself.
local: Yesu be doo ŋgba Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise baŋ bɔɔ kyaa kaalaŋ nɛ ya. Baŋ na wee na wee na kaboena nyi baa deesi gberaa ba wose ayimɛɛ, pɛna be kaa deesi gberaa be balaŋ mɔ wui ayimɛɛ na Wurubuarɛ ka mo be nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ. Mɔna Yesu dɔɔ tekaboena nyi waa yɛɛ mena ya, ɔ kedeesa keŋ yɛɛ dokoloŋ kooŋ. Debaŋ keŋ ɔ ba yem ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ, te ɔ moo ɔ wose fa ŋgba kedeesa kolo nɛ lee balaŋ nombiakumɛɛ dɔɔ, na Wurubuarɛ ka mo be nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ.

eng: The Law appoints people who are prone to weakness as high priests, but the content of the solemn pledge, which came after the Law, appointed a Son who has been made perfect forever.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ tɛɛ Mosesi mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ lese wɔ nyi baa yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise nɛ, ba te yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman kelii ɔto ya. Mɔna mmaraase ŋan wɔle na Wurubuarɛ kaŋa ntam kpu na nombii keŋ ɔ be yako see nɛ, te ɔ lese ɔ bu Yesu Kristo ŋon dukum kamasɛ be doo ɔ man ya nɛ nyi waa bese ɔ sae kegyia kekpaakekpaa.

eng: Now the main point of what we are saying is this: We have this kind of high priest. He sat down at the right side of the throne of the majesty in the heavens.
local: Nombia ŋan dɛɛ yako nɛ pou nyee yɛna nyiaa, de dana Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia ŋon ɔ kyaa Wurubuarɛ dekpatakpere ŋon gyoorogbeli keŋ dunoluŋ dɔɔ.

eng: Hes serving as a priest in the holy place, which is the true meeting tent that God, not any human being, set up.
local: Ɔ ne yɛɛ ɔ saere tom keŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa tɔɔmante keŋ man adido botɔɔ. Kei yɛna Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni gbagba keŋ. Na denibalaŋ baŋa na mena deni kenaŋ ya, mɔna Wurubuarɛ gbagba baŋa na ke.

eng: Every high priest is appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices. So its necessary for this high priest also to have something to offer.
local: Akpaa ba see sae kegyia kamasɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ na, ɔ tom yɛna nyi waa mo kedeesa abɔɔ na wonembia baŋ balaŋ ne faa nɛ deesi fa Wurubuarɛ. Mena dɔɔ te kaboena nyi sae kegyia Yesu mɔ, waa nyiŋ kpene keŋ waa mo gyu ke deesi fa Wurubuarɛ.

eng: If he was located on earth, he wouldnt be a priest because there are already others who offer gifts based on the Law.
local: Nyi Yesu deŋ kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, nafɔ ŋon te nyiŋ gbɛɛ di saere kei ya. Nawolo nyi na be te wulaa lese balaŋ baŋ baa di saere nɛ see, ŋgba mena keŋ Mosesi mmaraase ŋan ba wola wɔ nɛ.

eng: They serve in a place that is a copy and shadow of the heavenly meeting tent. This is indicated when Moses was warned by God when he was about to set up the meeting tent: See that you follow the pattern that I showed you on the mountain in every detail .
local: Tom keŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man adido nɛ, keŋ yaa saese baŋ ne kasee yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ. Mena mɔ ke te debaŋ keŋ Mosesi ne gyae waa baŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni keŋ bɔɔ mo kanyaŋ yɛɛ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ faree e nyiaa, Kɛɛ na n ke yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ nideli, na ke kyaa na keŋ mɔɔ lese wola neŋ bula keŋ dɔɔ nɛ pɛpɛɛpɛ.

eng: But now, Jesus has received a superior priestly service just as he arranged a better covenant that is enacted with better promises.
local: Yesu saere tom keŋ ɔ be kaa yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ dei kela saese benɛɛ baŋ bɔɔ kyaa kaalaŋ nɛ wui. Mena mɔ te nɔɔkebakesee wɔle keŋ dɔɔ ɔ ba moo, na ke yela balaŋ gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ dei kela nɔɔkebakesee bene keŋ. Nawolo nyi abɔɔ ŋan ɔ be yako nyi waa ta nɔɔkebakesee wɔle kei dɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ, dei kela abɔɔ ŋan ɔ be yako nyi waa ta nɔɔkebakesee bene keŋ dɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ.

eng: If the first covenant had been without fault, it wouldnt have made sense to expect a second.
local: Nyi nɔɔkebakesee gyaŋgbate keŋ wose man deŋ be dana nombii ya nɛ, nafɔ nɔɔkebakesee wɔle ba tekaa hia bela ya.

eng: But God did find fault with them, since he says, Look, the days are coming, says the Lord, when I will make a covenant with the house of Israel, and I will make a new covenant with the house of Judah. .
local: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ naawɔ nyi nɔɔkebakesee bene keŋ te yeli ɔ balaŋ gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee ɔ siaman ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ ne gyae nɛ ya. Mena dɔɔ te ɔ yako ba wose man nombia nyiaa, Debaŋ daale ne kɔŋ, keŋ maa bake nɔɔ wɔle see fa me balaŋ Iseraetena na Gyuda duuluŋ man balaŋ.

eng: It will not be like the covenant that I made with their ancestors on the day I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt, because they did not continue to keep my covenant, and I lost interest in them, says the Lord. .
local: Kei be gyae ke yɛɛ ŋgba nɔɔkebakesee keŋ daa na be naanaɔ be bake see, debaŋ keŋ mɔɔ mo me doŋ lese wɔ lee Igyipite tɛɛle man nɛ ya. Nawolo nyi keŋ be te di me nɔɔkebakesee kenaŋ dɔɔ kelii ɔto dɔɔ ya nɛ, te ma lese me sia yela wɔ.

eng: This is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days, says the Lord. I will place my laws in their minds, and write them on their hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people. .
local: Nɔɔkebakesee wɔle keŋ maa bake na me balaŋ Iseraetena yɛna nyiaa, Maa mo me nombia ŋan do ba agyueŋse man, na maa ŋmarase ŋa do be konɔɔse man. Maŋ ne gyae la maa yɛɛ be Wurubuarɛ, na baŋ mɔ ke yɛɛ me balaŋ.

eng: And each person wont ever teach a neighbor or their brother or sister, saying, Know the Lord, because they will all know me, from the least important of them to the most important; .
local: Ŋolo be gyae waa tekaa wola ɔ dɔɔ nombia bela, yaa ŋolo mɔ ke yako ɔ loŋto nyi Maa wola neŋ Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛneŋ ya! Nawolo nyi be popou baa gyeŋ maŋ lee ba kemaasa dɔɔ kelii be kegyia dɔɔ.

eng: because I will be lenient toward their unjust actions, and I wont remember their sins anymore. .
local: Maa mo be nombiakumɛɛ pou kyɛɛ wɔ, keŋ mɔɔ gyae maa tekaa tɔɔse abɔɔ ŋan bɔɔ yɛɛ tia maŋ nɛ bela ya.

eng: When it says new, it makes the first obsolete. And if something is old and outdated, its close to disappearing.
local: Wurubuarɛ ke baake nɔɔkebakesee kei nyi nɔɔkebakesee wɔle nɛ, na ɔ na wolo baa nyi gyaŋgbate keŋ te yɛɛ bene. Te kpene kamasɛ keŋ ka de yɛɛ bene na, kɔɔ doo kolo man bela ya. Nawolo nyi be te nyiŋ kolo wɔle see ke nawɔɔ man.

eng: So then the first covenant had regulations for the priests service and the holy place on earth.
local: Nɔɔkebakesee gyaŋgbate keŋ man nɛ Wurubuarɛ wola wɔ gbɛɛ keŋ man baa mo som e, na botɔɔ keŋ baa som e tɛɛle kei dɔɔ.

eng: They pitched the first tent called the holy place. It contained the lampstand, the table, and the loaves of bread presented to God.
local: Be baŋa ɔsom deni dinaa te ba moo kanyaŋ dinaa daale kpase ke man ala. Siamante keŋ man te ba moo kanea na tebulu keŋ dɔɔ bɛɛ mo bodobodo gyakaa fa Wurubuarɛ kedi nɛ see. Botɔɔ te bɛɛ baake nyi Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa siamante.

eng: There was a tent behind the second curtain called the holy of holies.
local: Wɔlete keŋ mɔ te bɛɛ baake nyi Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa tɔɔmante.

eng: It had the gold altar for incense and the chest containing the covenant, which was covered with gold on all sides. In the chest there was a gold jar containing manna, Aarons rod that budded, and the stone tablets of the covenant.
local: Abɔɔ ŋan doo botɔɔ yɛna kedeesa boe keŋ bɔɔ mo kɔba yayam yɛɛ ke te bɛɛ too oliifi ke dɔɔ nɛ, na adakaa keŋ bɔɔ mo kɔba yayam mataa mataa ke pou kilisi nɛ. Adakaa kenaŋ seŋɛɛ la fa nɔɔkebakesee wɔle keŋ. Abɔɔ ŋan doo adakaa kenaŋ man yɛna, kɔba yayam nyefale keŋ weenɛɛ ŋan bɛɛ baake ŋa nyi maana doo ke man nɛ, na Aarɔn gbutu keŋ ba potee nɛ na boya patalapatala ala ŋan bɔɔ ŋmarase mmaraase ŋan Wurubuarɛ ba mo fa Mosesi nɛ gyakaa ŋe dɔɔ nɛ.

eng: Above the chest there were magnificent winged creatures casting their shadow over the seat of the chest, where sin is taken care of. Right now we cant talk about these things in detail.
local: Ba te yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ kpilala bala baŋ bɔɔ fɛlɛɛ be beŋkelɛɛ nɛ gyakaa adakaa keŋ dɔɔ. Kei na wola la nyi Wurubuarɛ kyaa botɔɔ kenaŋ. Bɛɛ baake Wurubuarɛ kpilala benaŋ nyi Kerubimse. Kerubimse baŋ nsana nsana te Wurubuarɛ ne wii balaŋ waraŋase mo be nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ. Kei yɛna kpene keŋ maa tale yako ŋon debaŋ kei, nawolo nyi mɔɔ gyae maa tale lese nombia kɛŋa man pou wola ŋon debokeiman ya.

eng: When these things have been prepared in this way, priests enter the first tent all the time as they perform their service.
local: Nyi akpaa ba see abɔɔ kɛŋa pou deni keŋ man taŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ saese baŋ na gyoo deni siamante keŋ man ke yɛɛ be saere tom wee kamasɛ.

eng: But only the high priest enters the second tent once a year. He never does this without blood, which he offers for himself and for the sins the people committed in ignorance.
local: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia ŋon waageŋ te kaboena nyi waa gyoo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa tɔɔmante keŋ man. Kulutoo dokoloŋ man na, dokoloŋ kooŋ te ɔ na gyoo ke man. Pɛte waa gyoo ke man mɔ na, kaboena nyi waa nyiŋ wonembu fatabo keŋ waa mo gyoraa ɔ wose na ɔ balaŋ baŋ pou lee be nombiakumɛɛ ŋan be ta gyem ya te bɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: With this, the Holy Spirit is showing that the way into the holy place hadnt been revealed yet while the first tent was standing.
local: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon dɔŋ nombia kɛŋa dɔɔ ɔ na wola daa lee abɔɔ kɛŋa man nideli nyiaa, debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ kanyaŋ ɔsom deni dinaa kei nyiŋa ka seŋɛɛ nɛ, na gbɛɛ ta toro ta fa balaŋ yakaa nyibaŋ nyi baa gyoo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa tɔɔmante keŋ man ya.

eng: This is a symbol for the present time. It shows that the gifts and sacrifices that are being offered cant perfect the conscience of the one who is serving.
local: Kei yɛɛ nombia ŋan doo fa daa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ, na dɛɛ kasee kolo lee ke man. Nombia kɛŋa na wola daa baa nyi, wonembia na abɔɔ ŋan balaŋ ba mo deesi pataa Wurubuarɛ nɛ, te tale gberaa ba ayimɛɛ na baa gyeŋ be konɔɔse man nyi Wurubuarɛ ta mo be nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ ya.

eng: These are superficial regulations that are only about food, drink, and various ritual ways to wash with water. They are regulations that have been imposed until the time of the new order.
local: Nawolo nyi bɔ ɔsom pou yɛɛ wosenaane man ɔsom ke. Te ba kpene kamasɛ gyakae kedi na kenyɔɔ na wose kefaafo mmaraase dɔɔ, ŋgba mena keŋ ba mmaraase na wolo nɛ. Kɛŋa doowɔ menɛɛ kelii debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be kaa wola wɔ gbɛɛ wɔle keŋ baa mo som e lee nɔɔkebakesee wɔle keŋ man nɛ.

eng: But Christ has appeared as the high priest of the good things that have happened. He passed through the greater and more perfect meeting tent, which isnt made by human hands .
local: Nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ Kristo yɛna de Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia, te ɔ ta mo abɔɔ kpaakpaa kɔŋ ɔsom wɔle keŋ nɛnɛɛ de dana nɛ man. Mena abɔɔ kɛŋa kela abɔɔ ŋan nafɔ ŋe doo ɔsom bene keŋ man nɛ. Te Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ɔ ne yɛɛ ɔ tom nɛ, kela bene keŋ lee gbɛɛ kamasɛ man. Ka te yɛɛ ɔsom deni keŋ denibalaŋ ba mo be nyiŋmaase ma ke ya. Mena dɔɔ ka te yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ ɔsom deni ke ya.

eng: He entered the holy of holies once for all by his own blood, not by the blood of goats or calves, securing our deliverance for all time.
local: Debaŋ keŋ ɔ ba gyoo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa tɔɔmante keŋ man nɛ, na asoose na nunkyuese bia fatabo te ɔ moowɔ gyoo botɔɔ ya. Mɔna ŋon gbagba fatabo te ɔ moowɔ gyoo botɔɔ ke deesi karatetee dokoloŋ mo tɔ nombiakumɛɛ kom kekpaakekpaa. Lee mena dɔɔ, te yeli Wurubuarɛ ta mo de nombiakumɛɛ pou kyɛɛ daa kekpaakekpaa.

eng: If the blood of goats and bulls and the sprinkled ashes of cows made spiritually contaminated people holy and clean.
local: Gyaŋgba gyaŋgba keŋ na, nyi akpaa ŋolo da mo ayimɛɛ tina ɔ wose nɛ, bɛɛ mo asoo na nunkyue baale fatabo, na nunkyue bu ŋon bɔɔ too e nɛ tɔŋ ŋmatee ɔtenate mo gberaa wa ayimɛɛ ŋan lee ɔ wose man.

eng: how much more will the blood of Jesus wash our consciences clean from dead works in order to serve the living God? He offered himself to God through the eternal Spirit as a sacrifice without any flaw.
local: Nyi akpaa nunkyuese na asoose fatabo ne tale gberaa ayimɛɛ lee balaŋ wose man nɛ, kenaŋ na Kristo fatabo gyae ke tale yɛɛ kela mena na ke kyɛɛkee de dinɔɔ. Nawolo nyi na ayimɛɛ kamasɛ be doo ɔ man ya, te ɔ tɛe Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ɔ kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ doŋ dɔɔ mo ɔ wose deesi fa Wurubuarɛ lee de nombiakumɛɛ dɔɔ. Ɔ fatabo keŋ ne faafo na de nombiakumɛɛ lee de konɔɔse man, na dɛɛ tale som Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ nideli.

eng: This is why hes the mediator of a new covenant : so that those who are called might receive the promise of the eternal inheritance on the basis of his death. His death occurred to set them free from the offenses committed under the first covenant.
local: Lee kei dɔɔ Kristo te kaa bese walaŋ ŋon ɔ doo daa na Wurubuarɛ nsana lee nɔɔkebakesee wɔle keŋ man, na ke yela balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ be baake wɔ nɛ ke nyiŋ kpene keŋ ɔ be yako see nyi waa fa wɔ kekpaakekpaa nɛ. Te lee Kristo yeŋ kei dɔɔ, nombiakumɛɛ ŋan balaŋ be yɛɛ debaŋ keŋ be doo nɔɔkebakesee gyaŋgbate keŋ man nɛ, Wurubuarɛ ta mo ŋe popou kyɛɛ wɔ.

eng: When there is a will, you need to confirm the death of the one who made the will.
local: Nyi akpaa ŋolo de yɛɛ ɔ yeŋ wɔle abɔɔ kekpala tɔne see nɛ, kaboena nyi ɔtenate waa yeŋ pɛna ŋolo ke di wa abɔɔ. Nawolo nyi walaŋ ta yeŋa ta ya na, bɔɔ ne di wa abɔɔ ya.

eng: This is because a will takes effect only after a death, since its not in force while the one who made the will is alive.
local: Nyi ŋolo da yem na, pɛte ɔ yeŋ wɔle abɔɔ kekpala tɔne keŋ ne yɛɛ tom. Nyi akpaa walaŋ ŋon ɔ be yɛɛ ke nɛ ta yeŋa ta ya na, tɔne kenaŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ tom ya.

eng: So not even the first covenant was put into effect without blood.
local: Lee kei dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ moo fatabo tɔ nɔɔkebakesee gyaŋgbate keŋ nɔɔ.

eng: Moses took the blood of calves and goats, along with water, scarlet wool, and hyssop, and sprinkled both the Law scroll itself and all the people after he had proclaimed every command of the Law to all the people.
local: Mena dɔɔ te debaŋ keŋ Mosesi be yako balaŋ baŋ pou Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan taŋ nɛ, ɔ moo nunkyuese bia na asoose fatabo mo kyakale na loŋ. Kenaŋ wɔle te ɔ moo wona ŋaale ŋgba kekuŋ nɛ te ɔ kpera daŋ daale tombii, te ɔ moo ŋa bom sɔɔ loŋ na fatabo keŋ man te ɔ moowɔ ŋmatee mmaraa tɔne keŋ na balaŋ baŋ pou mo gyoraa wɔ.

eng: While he did it, he said, This is the blood of the covenant that God established for you. .
local: Keŋte Mosesi yako balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, kei yɛna fatabo keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo tɔ nombia ŋan ɛmɛɛ na e be bake see nyi yaa di ŋe dɔɔ nɛ nɔɔ.

eng: And in the same way he sprinkled the meeting tent and also all the equipment that would be used in the priests service with blood.
local: Mena mɔ te Mosesi moo fatabo keŋ ŋmatee Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ, na ɔsom abɔɔ ŋan pou.

eng: Almost everything is cleansed by blood, according to the Laws regulations, and there is no forgiveness without blood being shed.
local: Ke yɛɛ ampaŋ nyi Mosesi mmaraase ŋan nawolo nyi, fatabo te bɛɛ mo gberaa kpene kamasɛ lese ka ayimɛɛ. Mena dɔɔ nyi akpaa fatabo ta lee ya na, kenaŋ na dukum ka mo kyɛɛ mɔ be kyaa ya.

eng: So it was necessary for the copies of the heavenly things to be cleansed with these sacrifices, but the heavenly things had to be cleansed with better sacrifices than these.
local: Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi baa mo wonembia deesi, na baa mo gberaa abɔɔ ŋan ŋe kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ te ŋa seŋɛɛ fa adido wui ŋan nɛ. Mɔna adido wui ŋan gbagba dɔɔ, kaboena nyi baa mo kpene keŋ kɛɛ gyoo sibii kela wonembia fatabo nɛ deesi bo mo gyoraa ŋa.

eng: Christ didnt enter the holy place made by human hands, but into heaven itself, so that he now appears in Gods presence for us.
local: Nawolo nyi na ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ bɔɔ kasee adido wui keŋ yɛɛ nɛ tɔɔman te Kristo gyoowɔ ya, mɔna ɔ gyoe adido gbagba botɔɔ keŋ Wurubuarɛ kyaa nɛ. Te nɛnɛɛ ɔ seŋɛɛ de nawɔɔ man Wurubuarɛ siaman ɔ na sola e fa daa.

eng: He didnt enter to offer himself over and over again, like the high priest enters the earthly holy place every year with blood that isnt his.
local: Kulutoo kamasɛ na, Gyudatena saese kegyia ŋon na mo wonembu fatabo gyu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa tɔɔmante keŋ man ke deesi. Mɔna Kristo dɔɔ ŋon ta mo ɔ wose gyu botɔɔ ke deesi fa Wurubuarɛ kulutoo na kulutoo ya.

eng: If that were so, then Jesus would have to suffer many times since the foundation of the world. Instead, he has now appeared once at the end of the ages to get rid of sin by sacrificing himself.
local: Nyi Kristo deŋ yɛɛwɔ mena nɛ, nafɔ ŋon naa diyem agbaatee agbaatee mo leenaŋ debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ tɛɛle kei kewalaŋ nɛ. Mɔna tɛɛle kei kee gyu kɔ ɔto nɛ, ɔ lese ɔ wose wola te ɔ moo ɔ wose deesi karatetee dokoloŋ na waa mo lese de nombiakumɛɛ.

eng: People are destined to die once and then face judgment.
local: Doo fa deniwalaŋ kamasɛ nyi yeŋ dokoloŋ te waa yeŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ. Lee kenaŋ wɔle na Wurubuarɛ waa di ɔ nombia.

eng: In the same way, Christ was also offered once to take on himself the sins of many people. He will appear a second time, not to take away sin but to save those who are eagerly waiting for him.
local: Mena yeŋ dokoloŋ kenaŋ ke te Kristo mɔ yeŋawɔ mo ɔ wose fa, na waa nyiŋ lese balaŋ burum lee ba dukum man. Yesu waa bese kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ bela. Ɔ kɛɛ kɔŋ kei dɔɔ, na ɔ ne kɔŋ bo ɔ kaa lese balaŋ lee be nombiakumɛɛ man ya. Mɔna ɔ ne kɔŋ bo ɔ kaa lɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ kakyeŋ be kyaa be daa na e nɛ nyee.

eng: The Law is a shadow of the good things that are coming, not the real things themselves. It never can perfect the ones who are trying to draw near to God through the same sacrifices that are offered continually every year.
local: Mosesi mmaraase ŋan seŋɛɛ bo fa abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan dɔŋ wɔle kaa kɔŋ nɛ, ŋa te yɛɛ abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋenaŋ gbagba ya. Lee kei dɔɔ kedeesase ŋan balaŋ ne deesi kulutoo kamasɛ ŋgba mena keŋ mmaraase ŋan ba wola wɔ nɛ, be gyae ke tale yela walaŋ gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman ya.

eng: Otherwise, wouldnt they have stopped being offered? If the people carrying out their religious duties had been completely cleansed once, no one would have been aware of sin anymore.
local: Nyi ba kedeesase ŋenaŋ deŋ talewɔ yela be gbɛɛneŋ deŋ teneewɔ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ, nafɔ beŋ wulaa tinaa kedeesase ŋenaŋ yela. Nyi ŋe deŋ talewɔ lɛɛ wɔ nɛ, nafɔ bɔɔ tekaa wɔlɛɛ nyi Wurubuarɛ waa kaa wɔŋ ba deŋele lee be nombiakumɛɛ ŋenaŋ dɔɔ ya.

eng: Instead, these sacrifices are a reminder of sin every year.
local: Mɔna anokoare keŋ doo nombii kei man yɛna nyiaa, kulutoo kamasɛ na mena kedeesase ŋan bee deesi agbaatee agbaatee nɛ, na bese bo yela bɛɛ tɔɔse be nombiakumɛɛ ŋan bɔɔ yɛɛ kaalaŋ nɛ.

eng: because its impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
local: Nawolo nyi nunkyuese na asoose fatabo, be gyae ke tale faafo dukum lee deniwalaŋ wose man ya.

eng: Therefore, when he comes into the world he says, You didnt want a sacrifice or an offering, but you prepared a body for me; .
local: Lee mena dɔɔ debaŋ keŋ Kristo ne gyae waa kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ yako ɔ kya Wurubuarɛ nyiaa, Na kedeesa na abɔɔ ŋan bɛɛ mo sola neŋ nɛ na gyoo na n sia ya. Wonembia katoo kedeesa, na dukum kasola kedeesa koraŋ bɛɛ gyoo n sia ya. Mɔna n te desina ma wosenaane na maa mo ke deesi fa neŋ.

eng: So then I said, Look, Ive come to do your will, God. This has been written about me in the scroll. .
local: Mena dɔɔ, Me Gbeŋgyoo maŋ yaa. Me kɔŋae me kaa yɛɛ n kegyaebii, ŋgba mena keŋ bɔɔ ŋmarase ma wose man nombia do n nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man nɛ. .

eng: He says above, You didnt want and you werent pleased with a sacrifice or an offering or with entirely burned offerings or a purification offering, which are offered because the Law requires them.
local: Kristo taŋ wulaa yako kaalaŋ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ bɛɛ gyae kedeesa na abɔɔ ŋan bɛɛ mo sola e na wonembia katoo kedeesa na dukum kasola kedeesa ya, te ŋe bɛɛ gyoo ɔ sia mɔ ya. Mɔna kɛŋa pou yɛɛ kpene keŋ Mosesi mmaraase ŋan na wolo nyi baa yɛɛ.

eng: Then he said, Look, Ive come to do your will . He puts an end to the first to establish the second.
local: Botɔɔ te Kristo besewɔ yako nyiaa, Me Gbeŋgyoo maŋ yaa. Me kɔŋae me kaa yɛɛ n kegyaebii. Kei nawolo baa nyi Wurubuarɛ te bɛɛ kedeesa gyaŋgbate keŋ, te ɔ moo Kristo wui keŋ ɔ ba mo ɔ wose deesi nɛ kaa see ke nawɔɔ man.

eng: We have been made holy by Gods will through the offering of Jesus Christs body once for all.
local: Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii keŋ Kristo be kɔŋ kaa yɛɛ mo ɔ wose deesi karatetee dokoloŋ kekpaakekpaa nɛ, keŋ lese na da ayimɛɛ desina daa see fa Wurubuarɛ.

eng: Every priest stands every day serving and offering the same sacrifices over and over, sacrifices that can never take away sins.
local: Wee na wee na, Gyudatena saese baŋ gyakaa bee deesi kedeesa dokoloŋ kenaŋ agbaatee agbaatee. Mɔna ŋe bɛɛ tale lese dukum lee deniwalaŋ wose man ya.

eng: But when this priest offered one sacrifice for sins for all time, he sat down at the right side of God.
local: Mɔ Kristo dɔɔ ŋon moo ɔ wose deesi fa Wurubuarɛ karatetee dokoloŋ kekpaakekpaa, na waa mo lese de nombiakumɛɛ. Kenaŋ wɔle na te ɔ ke kyaawɔ Wurubuarɛ dunoluŋ dɔɔ adido.

eng: Since then, hes waiting until his enemies are made into a footstool for his feet.
local: Lee mena debaŋ kenaŋ pou nɛ, ɔ sɛɛ ɔ kyaa botɔɔ ɔ ne daa wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ kɔlala pou do e ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ nɛ.

eng: because he perfected the people who are being made holy with one offering for all time.
local: Kristo kedeesa keŋ ɔ ba mo ɔ wose deesi fa Wurubuarɛ dɔɔ nɛ, te yeli balaŋ baŋ pou ɔ be baake wɔ te ɔ be desina wɔ see fa ɔ wose nɛ, ta bese balaŋ baŋ ayimɛɛ be doo be man ya nɛ kekpaakekpaa.

eng: The Holy Spirit affirms this when saying.
local: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon gbagba mɔ ne di adansɛɛ lee mena nombia kɛŋa wose man, ŋgba mena keŋ bɔɔ ŋmarase Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see man nɛ nyiaa.

eng: This is the covenant that I will make with them. After these days, says the Lord, I will place my laws in their hearts and write them on their minds. .
local: Wurubuarɛ kpa, Nɔɔkebakesee keŋ maŋ ɛ Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ na me balaŋ be bake see yɛna nyiaa, Debaŋ daale de lii na, maa mo ma mmaraase ŋan do be konɔɔse na ba nyeeya man.

eng: And I wont remember their sins and their lawless behavior anymore. .
local: Keŋte ɔ besewɔ yako nyiaa, Mɔɔ gyae maa tɔɔse be nombiakumɛɛ na be nombia nkaraŋkaraŋ ŋan bɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ bela ya.

eng: When there is forgiveness for these things, there is no longer an offering for sin.
local: Te nyi akpaa Wurubuarɛ ta mo de nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ daa na, ta tekaa kaboena nyi dɛɛ bese deesi fa e lee de nombiakumɛɛ dɔɔ bela ya.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we have confidence that we can enter the holy of holies by means of Jesus blood.
local: Me tebia, lee Yesu yeŋ keŋ dɔɔ de te nyiŋ gbɛɛ keŋ dɛɛ tale gyoo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa tɔɔmante keŋ man yakaa.

eng: through a new and living way that he opened up for us through the curtain, which is his body.
local: Nɛnɛɛ dɛɛ tale gbaa kanyaŋ keŋ bɔɔ mo kpase deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ. Nawolo nyi Yesu ta mo ɔ wose deesi mo toro gbɛɛ wɔle keŋ na mo daa gyu nyeedoŋ wɔle keŋ man nɛ fa daa.

eng: and we have a great high priest over Gods house.
local: Nɛnɛɛ de te nyiŋ Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia dekpatakpere ŋon daa Wurubuarɛ balaŋ pou doo ɔ nyiŋmaa man.

eng: Therefore, lets draw near with a genuine heart with the certainty that our faith gives us, since our hearts are sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies are washed with pure water.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ yela dɛɛ mo konɔɔ kpaakpaa tuu benaa Wurubuarɛ, na dɛɛ kyaŋ kelɛɛdi keŋ dɔɔ lɛɛdi nɛ man keŋkeŋ. Ɛ yela dɛɛ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gberaa be nombiakumɛɛ lee ba wose man keŋ bɔɔ tekaa wɔlɛɛ na ŋa bela ya, na dɛɛ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ mo Wurubuarɛ loŋ faafo da ayimɛɛ pou leki nɛ.

eng: Lets hold on to the confession of our hope without wavering, because the one who made the promises is reliable.
local: Ɛ yela dɛɛ kyaŋ nombia ŋan dɔɔ lɛɛdi te de loo gyakaa ŋe dɔɔ nɛ man keŋkeŋ keŋ dɔɔ lee de wɔle ya. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ de yako nombii see na, ɔ bɛɛ lee ke wɔle ya. Mena dɔɔ abɔɔ ŋan ɔ be yako see nyi waa yɛɛ fa daa nɛ, waa yɛɛ ŋa fa daa.

eng: And let us consider each other carefully for the purpose of sparking love and good deeds.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ yela dɛɛ gyueŋ mena keŋ dɛɛ do dɔŋa kakyeŋ, na dɛɛ gyae dɔŋa na dɛɛ yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa fa dɔŋa.

eng: Dont stop meeting together with other believers, which some people have gotten into the habit of doing. Instead, encourage each other, especially as you see the day drawing near.
local: Ɛ na yeli dee tina kegyaŋee som ŋgba Kristotena nɛ yela, ŋgba mena keŋ baale ba mo ke yɛɛ be dinɔɔ nɛ ya. Mɔna ɛ yela dɛɛ kyaa gyaŋee na dɛɛ do dɔŋa kakyeŋ nideli, tuturu na nɛnɛɛ keŋ dɔɔ naa nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu wee dinaa keŋ ɔ be yako see nyi waa bese kɔŋ na benaa nɛ.

eng: If we make the decision to sin after we receive the knowledge of the truth, there isnt a sacrifice for sins left any longer.
local: Akpaa nyi ŋolo gyeŋ nyeedoŋ nombia gbagba ŋan te ɔ bese naa na sia yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ nɛ, kenaŋ na kedeesa daale be kyaa keŋ ɔtenate waa deesi fa Wurubuarɛ, na Wurubuarɛ ka mo ɔ nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ e bela ya.

eng: Theres only a scary expectation of judgment and of a burning fire thats going to devour Gods opponents.
local: Mɔna ɔtenate waa kyaa bo na wɔe kelɔ ɔ ne daa nombia kedi wee, na boalaŋ degyapaa keŋ gyae kaa kpa balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ koro seŋ tia Wurubuarɛ nɛ.

eng: When someone rejected the Law from Moses, they were put to death without mercy on the basis of the testimony of two or three witnesses.
local: Gyaŋgba gyaŋgba keŋ nɛ, akpaa ŋolo te di Mosesi mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ ya te balaŋ bala yaa batooro de di adansɛɛ tia e nɛ, bɛɛ ko ɔtenate keŋ bɔɔ ne wii ɔ waraŋase ya.

eng: How much worse punishment do you think is deserved by the person who walks all over Gods Son, who acts as if the blood of the covenant that made us holy is just ordinary blood, and who insults the Spirit of grace?.
local: Akpaa mena te doo nɛ, kenaŋ na deŋele keŋ baa wɔŋ walaŋ ŋon ɔ ta mo Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon nombia ŋan yɛɛ kolo ya nɛ, ka dokoso be kyaa ya. Baa wɔŋ ɔtenate deŋele nideli, nawolo nyi ɔ nombia ayɛɛsa nawolo baa nyiaa Yesu fatabo keŋ ɔ ba mo tɔ nɔɔkebakesee wɔle keŋ nɔɔ, te ɔ ba mo gberaa da ayimɛɛ lee da wose man nɛ be dana tɔnɔɔ fa e ya, te ɔ ta mo Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ɔ na mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele balaŋ mɔ nɛ yɛɛ kolo ya.

eng: We know the one who said, Judgment is mine; I will pay people back. And he also said, The Lord will judge his people. .
local: Nawolo nyi da gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ yakowɔ nyiaa, Maŋ ne gyae la maa tɔ me balaŋ kom lee be nombiakumɛɛ dɔɔ. Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see na bese yako nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ne gyae la waa di ɔ balaŋ nombia.

eng: Its scary to fall into the hands of the living God!.
local: Ke gyae ke yɛɛ gyakoloŋ, nyi ŋolo ke yala Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ nyiŋmaa man.

eng: But remember the earlier days, after you saw the light. You stood your ground while you were suffering from an enormous amount of pressure.
local: Ɛ tɔɔse debaŋ keŋ ɛ be taŋgbɛɛ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan di kewalaŋ nɛ. Nombia dinaana yala ŋon, mɔna ɛ kyaŋa ɛ wose seŋ keŋkeŋ di ŋe dɔɔ.

eng: Sometimes you were exposed to insults and abuse in public. Other times you became partners with those who were treated that way.
local: Weeya ŋaale man na bɛɛ tee ŋon, fulu ŋon balaŋ man. Ŋaale man mɔ na, ɛ na sem ɛ tebia baŋ bɛɛ naase wɔ diyem nɛ wɔle, naa diyem ŋgba baŋ mɔ nɛ.

eng: You even showed sympathy toward people in prison and accepted the confiscation of your possessions with joy, since you knew that you had better and lasting possessions.
local: Ɛ wii ɛ tebia baŋ bɔɔ kyaŋ wɔ tɔ deni nɛ waraŋase. Be lɛo ya abɔɔ koraŋ na ɛ ta ko ɛ wose ya, mɔna ke yɛɛ ŋon gyoŋ bo, nawolo nyi ɛ gyeŋ nyi ɛ dana abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan ŋa kela tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ, te ŋe doo fa ŋon kekpaakekpaa nɛ.

eng: So dont throw away your confidence—it brings a great reward.
local: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na yeli ɛ wose ke yɔkɔse ŋon Wurubuarɛ ɔsom keŋ man ya. Ɛ kyaŋ nombia ŋan ɛ be lɛɛdi nɛ man keŋkeŋ, nawolo nyi wee daale Wurubuarɛ waa tɔ ŋon kom.

eng: You need to endure so that you can receive the promises after you do Gods will.
local: Kaboena nyi yaa yɛɛ kakyeŋ seŋ keŋkeŋ na ɛ ke nyiŋ tale yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii. Ɛ yɛɛ mena na ɛ ke nyiŋ kpene keŋ ɔ be yako see nyi waa fa ŋon nɛ.

eng: In a little while longer, the one who is coming will come and wont delay; .
local: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyiaa, Be gyae ke kyare ya, walaŋ ŋon kaboena nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ waa kɔŋ. Ɔ be gyae waa dɔɔ wɔle na kekɔŋ ya.

eng: but my righteous one will live by faith, and my whole being wont be pleased with anyone who shrinks back. .
local: Me balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ me siaman nɛ, lee ba kelɛɛdi dɔɔ te baa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kyaa. Mɔna akpaa be man ŋolo ta seŋ ɔ kelɛɛdi man keŋkeŋ ya te ɔ lee ɔ wɔle na, ɔ nombia be gyae ka gyoo me sia ya.

eng: But we arent the sort of people who timidly draw back and end up being destroyed. Were the sort of people who have faith so that our whole beings are preserved.
local: Daa te yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ lee Wurubuarɛ wɔle, te baŋ na e nsana be tiri kekpaakekpaa nɛ ya. Mɔna daa yɛna balaŋ baŋ dɔɔ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di, te ɔ be lɛɛ da nyee nɛ.

eng: Faith is the reality of what we hope for, the proof of what we dont see.
local: Woŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ kelɛɛdi? Keŋ yɛna nyi deniwalaŋ ka gyeŋ ɔ konɔɔ man nideli nyi, abɔɔ ŋan ɔ loo gyakaa ŋe dɔɔ nɛ waa nyiŋ ŋa. Doo nyi ɔ bɛɛ naa mena abɔɔ ŋenaŋ na ɔ sia ya, mɔna ɔ gyeŋ ɔ konɔɔ man nideli nyi mena abɔɔ ŋenaŋ kyaa, te wee daale waa kaa nyiŋ ŋa.

eng: The elders in the past were approved because they showed faith.
local: Mena ke te beneŋtena baŋ mɔ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di, te lee ba kelɛɛdi dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ kɛlɛɛ wɔ.

eng: By faith we understand that the universe has been created by a word from God so that the visible came into existence from the invisible.
local: Lee mena keŋ dɔɔ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di dɔɔ te da gyeŋ nyi nɔɔwoya ŋaageŋ te ɔ kolosiwɔ, te abɔɔ ŋan pou ŋe kyaa adido na ateta nɛ kɔŋawɔ. Kei nawolo baa nyi abɔɔ ŋan pou deniwalaŋ bɛɛ naa ŋa ɔ sia ya nɛ, ŋan te Wurubuarɛ moowɔ yɛɛ abɔɔ ŋan dɛɛ naa na de sia nɛ.

eng: By faith Abel offered a better sacrifice to God than Cain, which showed that he was righteous, since God gave approval to him for his gift. Though he died, hes still speaking through faith.
local: Abɛle kelɛɛ keŋ ɔ be lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di dɔɔ, abɔɔ ŋan ɔ ba mo deesi fa Wurubuarɛ nɛ gyoo Wurubuarɛ sia kela ɔ kegyia Kani wui. Mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ mɔ lɛɛ e do nyi ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ŋon Wurubuarɛ siaman. Abɛle wulaa yeŋawɔ mɔna ɔ kelɛɛdi nombia ŋan ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ, ŋe sɛɛ ŋe doo fa daa.

eng: By faith Enoch was taken up so that he didnt see death, and he wasnt found because God took him up. He was given approval for having pleased God before he was taken up.
local: Enɔke mɔ kelɛɛ keŋ ɔ be lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ gate e gyu adido na ɔ sia fonɛɛ. Ŋolo te naa e ya nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ moo e gyu adido. Pɛ Wurubuarɛ ka mo Enɔke gyu adido nɛ, Wurubuarɛ dii adansɛɛ lee ɔ wose man nyi ɔ nombia na gyoo ŋon Wurubuarɛ sia.

eng: Its impossible to please God without faith because the one who draws near to God must believe that he exists and that he rewards people who try to find him.
local: Nyi akpaa ŋolo bɛɛ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di ya nɛ, ɔtenate nombia be gyae ka gyoo Wurubuarɛ sia korakora ya. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae nyi waa kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ nɛ, kaboena nyi ɔtenate dɛɛ lɛɛ di nyi Wurubuarɛ kyaa, te waa mo abɔɔ kpaakpaa tɔ balaŋ baŋ pou bɛɛ yase be sia kɛo ɔ gbɛɛneŋ man nideli nɛ kom.

eng: By faith Noah responded with godly fear when he was warned about events he hadnt seen yet. He built an ark to deliver his household. With his faith, he criticized the world and became an heir of the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Nowa mɔ kelɛɛ keŋ ɔ be lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di dɔɔ, debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be faree e lee kayeyɛɛ dinaa keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ, doo nyi na ɔ ba gyeŋ kayeyɛɛ kenaŋ dinɔɔ ya, mɔna ɔ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di te ɔ yae adakaa dinaa. Keŋte ŋon na ɔ dekpaŋalaŋmantena pou gyoowɔ ke man, te be nyiŋa nyeedoŋ debaŋ keŋ loŋ be waale tɛɛle pou nɛ. Ɔ kelɛɛdi kei te yeli ɔ te bu balaŋ akaŋ baŋ pou fɔɔ nyi be yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ, keŋ kaboena nyi Wurubuarɛ waa wɔlɛɛ wɔ. Lee Nowa kelɛɛdi kei dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ lɛɛ e do nyi ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ɔ siaman.

eng: By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to a place that he was going to receive as an inheritance. He went out without knowing where he was going.
local: Aberaham mɔ kelɛɛ keŋ ɔ be lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di dɔɔ, debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be baake e yako e nyi waa koro lee ɔ tɛɛle man, na waa gyu tɛɛle wɔle daale keŋ ɔ be yako see nyi wee daale ke gyae kaa bese ŋon na ɔ duuluŋ man balaŋ pou wui dɔɔ nɛ ɔ nyii Wurubuarɛ dei. Debaŋ kenaŋ na Aberaham ba gyeŋ tɛɛle keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne yako nɛ wose man kolo ya, mɔna ɔ korowɔ tina ɔ donɔɔ yela gyu botɔɔ.

eng: By faith he lived in the land he had been promised as a stranger. He lived in tents along with Isaac and Jacob, who were coheirs of the same promise.
local: Lee Aberaham kelɛɛdi dɔɔ ɔ gyuuwɔ kekyaa tɛɛle keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi ke gyae kaa bese ɔ wui nɛ dɔɔ ŋgba ŋɔɔle nɛ. Ɔ ke kyaawɔ kanyaŋ denɛɛ man ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ bu Asiki na ɔ naanabii Gyekɔpo mɔ ba ke kyaa nɛ. Wurubuarɛ yakowɔ see fa Asiki na Gyekɔpo mɔ nyi wee daale mena tɛɛle kenaŋ gyae kaa bese be wui, ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yako see fa Aberaham nɛ.

eng: He was looking forward to a city that has foundations, whose architect and builder is God.
local: Na Aberaham dɔɔ, ŋon sia gyakaa bo donɔɔ keŋ Wurubuarɛ gbagba ba mo adao lese ke, te ka seŋɛɛ nideli te ke dana doŋ te ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: By faith even Sarah received the ability to have a child, though she herself was barren and past the age for having children, because she believed that the one who promised was faithful.
local: Doo nyi na Aberaham te ŋmɛɛ gbaa lolo te ɔ wɛɛle Saara mɔ yɛɛ ɔlamanbo e, mɔna lee kelɛɛ keŋ ɔ be lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ yela ɔ lola bu. Nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa fa wɔ bu nɛ, ɔ lɛɛwɔ di nyi Wurubuarɛ waa di ɔ nombia ŋan ɔ be yako see nɛ dɔɔ.

eng: So descendants were born from one man . They were as many as the number of the stars in the sky and as countless as the grains of sand on the seashore.
local: Lee mena dɔɔ Aberaham ŋon ɔ be ŋmɛɛ gbaa lolo te ɔ loo be gyakaa nyi nafɔ waa tale lola bela ya nɛ, ɔ kaa lola bu te ɔ duuluŋ man balaŋ kaa wukuwɔ ŋgba kyolobiise ŋan ŋe kyaa adido, yaa ɛpo nɔɔman aŋasa nɛ.

eng: All these people died in faith without receiving the promises, but they saw the promises from a distance and welcomed them. They confessed that they were strangers and immigrants on earth.
local: Mena balaŋ kɛwɔ pou te nyiŋ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa fa wɔ nɛ ya, mɔna be gyakaa bɛɛ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di pɛte ba yekeewɔ. Nawolo nyi lee ba kelɛɛdi dɔɔ yɛɛ yaa ŋgba be wulaa naa abɔɔ ŋan na lem keŋ wee daale baa kaa nyiŋ ŋa nɛ. Mena dɔɔ be nyaŋeewɔ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ, te be lɛɛwɔ do nyi be yɛɛ ŋɔɔlawɔ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ.

eng: People who say this kind of thing make it clear that they are looking for a homeland.
local: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyuusu be nɔɔ yako mena nombia kɛŋa nɛ, na bɛɛ wolo baa nyi bɛɛ kɛo baŋ gbagba be tɛɛle na baa kyaa ke dɔɔ.

eng: If they had been thinking about the country that they had left, they would have had the opportunity to return to it.
local: Kei nawolo baa nyi na be loo be gyakaa tɛɛle keŋ bɔɔ koro tina ke yela nɛ dɔɔ ya. Nawolo nyi, nyi be loo deŋ gyakaa ke dɔɔ nɛ, nafɔ be dana gbɛɛ nyi baa bese gyu botɔɔ.

eng: But at this point in time, they are longing for a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore, God isnt ashamed to be called their God—he has prepared a city for them.
local: Mɔna ba moo be loo bo gyakaa tɛɛle keŋ ke dei kela keŋ bɔɔ koro yela ke nɛ dɔɔ. Mena tɛɛle kenaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ adido. Mena dɔɔ be dana Wurubuarɛ desɛŋ nyi mena balaŋ benaŋ baa baake e nyi be Wurubuarɛ ya. Nawolo nyi ɔ te desina tɛɛle kpaakpaa daale see wɔ.

eng: By faith Abraham offered Isaac when he was tested. The one who received the promises was offering his only son.
local: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ yako Aberaham nyi, N bu Asiki dɔɔ te maa ta na maa yela n duuluŋ man balaŋ ke wuku. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ kɛɛse Aberaham kelɛɛdi kɛɛ te ɔ yako e nyi waa mo ɔ bu Asiki ŋonaŋ deesi fa e. Te lee kelɛɛ keŋ Aberaham be lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di dɔɔ, ɔ desina ɔ wose nyi waa mo ɔ bu Asiki deesi fa Wurubuarɛ.

eng: He figured that God could even raise him from the dead. So in a way he did receive him back from the dead.
local: Aberaham sɛɛwɔ nyi waa mo ɔ bu Asiki deesi fa Wurubuarɛ, nawolo nyi wa agyueŋ man na ɔ gyeŋ nyi ɔ bu ŋon da yem koraŋ na, Wurubuarɛ waa tale gyuusu e lee yeŋ man. N kɛɛ gba na, na yɛɛ yaa ŋgba ɔ moo e baa deesi fa Wurubuarɛ ampaŋ, te Wurubuarɛ gyuusu e lee yeŋ man nɛ.

eng: By faith Isaac also blessed Jacob and Esau concerning their future.
local: Asiki mɔ kelɛɛ keŋ ɔ be lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di dɔɔ, te ɔ talewɔ yako ɔ bia Gyekɔpo na Esawu abɔɔ ŋan Wurubuarɛ waa yɛɛ fa wɔ wee daale nɛ.

eng: By faith Jacob blessed each of Josephs sons as he was dying and bowed in worship over the head of his staff. .
local: Gyekɔpo mɔ kelɛɛ keŋ ɔ be lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di dɔɔ debaŋ keŋ ɔ yeŋ ba benaa nɛ, ɔ seŋawɔ teeli na ɔ gbutu te ɔ doo Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ, te ɔ sola e nyi waa hiraa ɔ naanasebia Gyosɛfo bia baala bala baŋ.
